Looking down at the northeast corner, there was a long river, and three shepherds were tending sheep in the river; the sheep were white, fat, and as large as horses. I asked, 'Where is my home?' The shepherds did not answer. After walking about dozens of miles, I saw a distant palace, its tiles all yellow glazed tiles, like a royal residence. Approaching, there were two men in boots, hats, robes, and sashes standing by the palace steps, resembling the historical figures Gao Lishi and Tong Guan."

    39

    殿前有黄金扁额，书‘地穷宫’三字。我玩视良久，袍带者怒，来逐我曰：“此何地，容尔立耶？‘我素刚，不肯去，与之争。殿内传呼曰：”外何喧嚷？’袍带者入，良久出曰：“汝毋去，听候谕旨。‘二人环而守之。天渐暮，阴风四起，霜片如瓦。我冻久战栗，两守者亦瑟缩流涕，指我怨曰：”微汝来作闹，我辈岂受此冷夜之苦哉！’天稍明，殿内钟动，风霜亦霁。又一人出曰：“昨所留人，著送归本处。‘袍带者拉以行。仍过原处，见牧羊人尚在。袍带者以我授之曰：’奉旨交此人与汝，送他还家，我去矣。‘牧羊人殴我以拳。惧而坠河，饮水腹胀，一溺遂苏。”言毕后，盥手沐面，饮食如常。后十日余，仍卒。

    Explanation

    39

    In front of the palace stood a golden plaque inscribed with the words "Dì Qióng Gōng" (the Palace of the Endless Earth or the Palace at the Edge of the World). I gazed at it for a long time when an official, dressed in formal robes and sashes, became angry and came to chase me away, saying, "What place is this? Can you stand here?" I, being usually stubborn, refused to leave and argued with him. Inside the palace, someone announced, "What is the commotion outside?" The official entered and returned after a long time, saying, "Do not leave; await the imperial decree." Two people then surrounded and guarded me. As the sky gradually darkened, gale winds blew from all sides, and frost flakes as thick as tiles fell. I was frozen for a long time and shivering, while the two guards also trembled, their tears streaming, pointing at me and complaining, "If it weren't for you causing trouble, we wouldn't be suffering through this cold night!" As the sky began to brighten, the bell inside the palace rang, and the wind and frost also cleared up. Another person came out and said, "The person left yesterday should be sent back to his original place." The official led me away, and we returned to the original place where the shepherd was still waiting. The official handed me over to the shepherd, saying, "By imperial order, hand this person over to you, send him back home, and I will leave." The shepherd hit me with his fists. Out of fear, I fell into the river, drank water, and my stomach swelled. After one bout of urination, I revived. Afterward, I washed my hands and face, ate and drank as usual, but ten days later, I died.

    40

    先是，李之邻张姓者，睡至三更，床侧闻人呼声。惊起，见黑衣四人，各长丈余，曰：“为我引路至李守备家。”张不肯，黑衣人欲殴之，惧而同行。至李门，先有二人蹲于门上，貌更狞恶。四人不敢仰视，偕张穿篱笆侧路以入，俄而哭声内作。此事傅卓园提督所言，李其友也。

    　　○狱中石匣越州周道沣以难荫选陕西陇州知州，抵署后，循例按狱。狱中有石匣，长尺许，封锁甚固。周欲开视。狱吏固持不可，曰：“相传自明季即有此匣，不知所藏何物，但记有道人云：”开则不利于官。‘“周素愎，必欲开视。乃斧其匣，得人影半幅，赤身带血，面目模糊，冷气袭人。周谛视未毕，有硫黄气自匣中起，卷幅烧毁，纸灰腾空而去。周大悸得病，卒于陇。竟不知何怪。周兰坡学士为余言，州牧即其从孙也。

    Explanation

    40

    Previously, a neighbor of Li Shoubi named Zhang, while sleeping until the third watch, heard voices calling out beside his bed. Startled, he saw four black-clad figures, each about ten feet tall, who said, "Guide us to the home of Li Shoubi." Zhang refused, but the black-clad figures threatened to assault him, so he complied out of fear. They arrived at Li's gate, where two more figures were crouching on the roof, looking even more fearsome. The four figures dared not look up and led Zhang through a side path through the fence into the house. Suddenly, cries began inside. This story was recounted by General Fu Zhuoyuan, who was a friend of Li.

    Later, Zhou Daofeng, who was appointed as the magistrate of Longzhou through his father's military merit, followed the usual procedure in reviewing cases. In the prison, there was a stone box, about a foot long, tightly sealed. Zhou insisted on opening it, despite the prison officials' strong opposition, saying, "It is said that this box has existed since the late Ming dynasty. We do not know what it contains, but there is a tradition that a Taoist said, 'Opening it would be harmful to the official.'" Zhou, known for his stubbornness, insisted on opening it and cut the box open. Inside, he found a half-length image of a person, naked and bloody, with a blurred face, and a cold chill emanated from it. Zhou was still examining it when sulfur fumes rose from the box, burning the contents, and the ashes flew into the air. Zhou became extremely frightened, fell ill, and died in Longzhou. The mystery of what caused this remained unsolved. Scholar Zhou Lanpo recounted this to me, and he is the grandson of Zhou Daofeng.

    41

    ●卷二○张元妻河南偃师县乡人张元妻薛氏归宁母家返，小叔迎之。路过古墓，树木阴森，薛氏将溲焉。牵所乘驴与小叔，使视之，而挂所衣红布裙于树。溲毕返，裙失所在。归家，与夫宿，侵晨不起。家人撞门入，窗牖宛然，而夫妇有身无首。告之官，不能理。拘小叔讯之，具道昨日失裙事迹。至墓所，墓旁有穴，滑溜如常有物出入者。窥之，红布裙带在外，即其嫂物。掘之，两首具在，并无棺椁。穴甚小，仅容一手。官竟不能谳也。

    Explanation

    41

    Final Translation

    In Volume Twenty, Chapter Twenty, there is the story of Zhang Yuan's wife, Xue, a native of Yanshi County in Henan Province. After returning to her parents' home for a visit, she was greeted by her younger brother-in-law upon her return. As they passed by an ancient tomb, the dense and dark trees created an eerie atmosphere. Xue, needing to relieve herself, tied her donkey to her younger brother-in-law and asked him to watch it, then hung her red cloth skirt on a tree before proceeding. Upon finishing, she returned, only to find her skirt missing. She went home and slept with her husband, but did not get up in the morning. Her family broke down the door, and though the windows and doors were intact, they found the couple with bodies but no heads. They reported the incident to the authorities, who were unable to handle it. The younger brother-in-law was arrested for questioning, and he fully described the incident of losing the skirt the previous day. The investigators went to the tomb, where they found a small, smooth hole beside it, as if something frequently passed through it. Looking inside, they saw the red cloth skirt's belt outside, confirming it belonged to her sister-in-law. They dug up the hole and found two heads, with no coffins or burial boxes. The hole was very small, just big enough for one hand. Ultimately, the officials could not resolve the case.

    42

    ○蝴蝶怪京师叶某，与易州王四相善。王以七月七日为六旬寿期，叶骑驴往祝。过房山，天将暮矣。一伟丈夫跃马至，问：“将何往？”叶告以故。丈夫喜曰：“王四，吾中表也。吾将往祝，盍同行乎？”叶大喜，与之偕行。丈夫屡蹑其背，叶固让前行，伪许，而仍落后。叶疑为盗，屡回顾之。时天已黑，不甚辨其状貌，但见电光所烛，丈夫悬首马下，以两脚踏空而行。一路雷与之俱。丈夫口吐黑气，与雷相触，舌长丈余，色如朱砂。叶大骇，卒无奈何，且隐忍之，疾驱至王四家。

    Explanation

    42

    A Strange Butterfly Incident in the Capital

    Ye, a resident of Beijing, was on good terms with Wang Si from Yizhou. On the seventh day of the seventh lunar month, Wang Si was celebrating his sixtieth birthday. Ye rode a donkey to wish him well. As they passed through Fangshan, it was already dark. Suddenly, a powerful man on horseback appeared and asked, "Where are you going?" Ye told him about his purpose. The man was delighted and said, "Wang Si is my distant relative. I am also going to wish him well. Why don’t we go together?" Ye was very pleased and agreed to travel with him. The man repeatedly touched the back of the donkey. Although Ye insisted on leading, he pretended to agree but actually fell behind. Ye suspected the man might be a thief and kept looking back at him. By then, it was already dark, and he could not clearly see the man’s appearance. However, he saw that the man’s head hung down from the horse, and his feet seemed to be floating in mid-air. Thunder accompanied them throughout the journey. The man’s mouth emitted black smoke that interacted with the thunder, and his tongue was over ten feet long, the color of cinnabar. Ye was greatly frightened, but in the end, there was nothing he could do about it. He endured it silently and hurriedly reached Wang Si’s house.

    43

    王出与相见，欢然置酒。叶私问：“与路上丈夫何亲？”曰：“此吾中表张某也，现居京师绳匠胡同，以熔银为业。”叶稍自安，且疑路上所见眼花耳。酒毕，叶就寝，心悸，不肯与同宿。丈夫固要之，不得已，请一苍头伴焉。叶彻夜不寐，而苍头酣寝矣。三鼓灯灭，丈夫起坐，复吐其舌，一室光明。以鼻嗅叶之帐，涎流不已。伸两手，持苍头啖之，骨星星坠地。叶素奉关神，急呼曰：“伏魔大帝何在？”忽訇然有钟鼓声，关帝持巨刃排梁而下，直击此怪。怪化一蝴蝶，大如车轮，张翅拒刃。盘旋片时，又霹雳一震，蝴蝶与关神俱无所见。叶昏晕仆地，日午不起。王四启门视之，具道所以。地有鲜血数斗，床上失一张某与一苍头矣。

    Explanation

    43

    The king came out to meet him, and they joyfully set up wine. Ye privately asked, "What relation do you have with the man on the road?" The king replied, "He is my cousin Zhang, who currently resides in the Rope Maker Alley in Beijing and makes a living by melting silver." Ye felt somewhat reassured but still suspected that what he saw on the road was an illusion. After the wine was finished, Ye went to bed, feeling anxious and unwilling to share the same room. The husband insisted, and Ye reluctantly asked a servant to accompany him. Ye stayed awake all night, while the servant slept soundly. At the third drum strike (midnight), the husband stood up, spitted out his tongue, and the room was filled with light. He sniffed at Ye's bed and drooled uncontrollably. He stretched out his hands, grabbed the servant, and devoured him, with bones falling to the ground. Ye, who usually worshipped Guan Yu, desperately called out, "Where is the God of War?" Suddenly, there was a loud sound of bells and drums, and Guan Yu descended from the ceiling with a large sword, attacking the monster. The monster transformed into a large butterfly, as big as a cart wheel, and flapped its wings to resist the sword. They circled for a moment, then a thunderclap occurred, and both the butterfly and Guan Yu disappeared. Ye fainted and fell to the ground, remaining unconscious until noon. The king opened the door four times to check on him, and Ye recounted everything that had happened. There were several buckets of fresh blood on the ground, and the servant Zhang and the servant were missing from the bed.

    44

    所骑马宛然在厩。急遣人至绳匠胡同踪迹张某，张方踞炉烧银，并无往易州祝寿之事。

    　　○白二官常州王姓者，以幕游为业。岁暮归里，慕张氏青山庄园林之美，袱被往游。

    　　遇白二官于园中——素所狎戏旦也，甚喜。游毕，同宿于园。王神思恍惚，不能成寝，见白二官伸头吹灯。灯离白所卧处二丈馀，而白伸头亦长二丈馀，吹灯而灭。王大骇，以被裹首而寝。白至其床前揭被，以手上下量之，所按处其冷如铁。

    　　王惊呼，无人答应。忽窗西有一黑物，猪脸毛爪，从外跳入，与白二官对搏甚凶，不知胜负。俄而天明，地上见鲜血一片，死蟒一条。急往白二官家询之：二官得蛊疾半年，一旦而愈。其疾愈之时，即王姓遇白二官之时也。

    Explanation

    44

    The horse was still in the stable, exactly as it had been. Hastily, someone was sent to trace Zhang in Rope Maker's Lane. Zhang was just sitting by the furnace melting silver, and there was no mention of him going to Yizhou to celebrate a birthday.

    ○ Bai Er Guan, a man from Changzhou surnamed Wang, made his living as a retainer. At the end of the year, he returned to his hometown, admiring the beauty of Zhang's Green Mountain Villa and its gardens, and he went to visit with his bedding.

    In the garden, he met Bai Er Guan, a male performer he often frequented, and he was very happy. After the tour, they stayed together in the garden. Wang was mentally confused and unable to sleep, when he saw Bai Er Guan extend his head and blow out the lamp. The lamp was more than two zhang away from where Bai was lying, and when Bai extended his head, it also reached more than two zhang, and the lamp went out. Wang was greatly startled and covered his head with a quilt to sleep. Bai came to his bed and lifted the quilt, measuring up and down with his hand, and the place where he pressed was as cold as iron.

    Wang shouted in surprise, but no one responded. Suddenly, a black object appeared west of the window, with a pig's face and hairy claws, jumping in from outside and fighting fiercely with Bai Er Guan, but it was unknown who won. Soon after, dawn broke, and on the ground, there was a pool of fresh blood and a dead python. Hastily, someone went to Bai Er Guan's house to inquire: Bai had contracted a venomous illness for half a year and suddenly recovered. The time when he recovered was the same time when Wang met Bai Er Guan.

    45

    ○关东毛人以人为饵关东人许善根，以掘人参为业。故事：掘参者须黑夜往掘。许夜行劳倦，宿沙上。及醒，其身为一长人所抱，身长二丈许，遍体红毛。以左手抚许之身，又以许身摩擦其毛，如玩珠玉者。然每一摩抚，则狂笑不止。许自分将果其腹矣。

    　　俄而抱至一洞，虎筋、鹿尾、象牙之类，森森山积。置许石榻上，取虎鹿进而奉之。许喜出望外，然不能食也。长人俯而若有所思，既而点首若有所得，敲石为火，汲水焚锅，为烹熟而进之。许大啖。黎明，长人复抱而出，身挟五矢，至绝壁之上，缚许于高树。许复大骇，疑将射己。俄而，群虎闻生人气，尽出穴，争来搏许。长人抽矢毙虎，复解缚抱许，曳死虎而返，烹献如故。许始心悟：长人养己以饵虎也。如是月余，许无恙，而长人竟以大肥。

    Explanation

    45

    A Hairy Man from Guandong Using Humans as Bait

    A man from Guandong named Xu Shangen made a living by digging ginseng. According to tradition, those who dug ginseng had to go at night. One night, Xu was exhausted from his journey and slept on the sand. When he woke up, he found himself embraced by a tall, hairy man, who was about two zhang (approximately 6 meters) tall and covered in red hair. The tall man stroked Xu's body with his left hand and rubbed his own hair against Xu's body, as if playing with precious jewels. Each time he stroked, he laughed uncontrollably. Xu thought that the tall man would surely devour him.

    Soon after, the tall man carried Xu to a cave filled with tiger bones, deer tails, and ivory, arranged in dense piles like mountains. He placed Xu on a stone couch and brought him tiger and deer meat to offer to him. Xu was overjoyed beyond expectation, but he could not eat it. The tall man looked down as if deep in thought, then nodded as if he had gained something. He struck stones to make fire, drew water, and cooked the meat thoroughly before offering it to Xu. Xu ate heartily. At dawn, the tall man carried Xu out again, carrying five arrows with him. They went to a steep cliff, where the tall man tied Xu to a tall tree. Xu was terrified again, suspecting that the tall man would shoot him. Soon, a group of tigers heard the scent of living humans and came out of their dens, attacking Xu. The tall man drew an arrow and killed the tigers, then untied Xu and carried him back, dragging the dead tigers with him. He cooked and offered the meat as before. Xu finally realized in his heart: the tall man was nourishing himself to use as bait for the tigers. For a month or so, Xu remained unharmed, while the tall man eventually became very fat.

    46

    许一日思家，跪长人前涕泣再拜，以手指东方不已。长人亦潸然。复抱至采参处，示以归路，并为历指产参地，示相报意。许从此富矣。

    　　○平阳令平阳令朱铄，性惨刻，所宰邑，别造厚枷巨梃。案涉妇女，必引入奸情讯之。

    　　杖妓，去小衣，以杖抵其阴，使肿溃数月，曰：“看渠如何接客！”以臀血涂嫖客面。妓之美者加酷焉，髡其发，以刀开其两鼻孔，曰：“使美者不美，则妓风绝矣。”逢同寅官，必自诧曰：“见色不动，非吾铁面冰心，何能如此！”以俸满迁山东别驾。

    Explanation

    46

    One day, Xu thought of his home and knelt before the tall figure, weeping and bowing twice, pointing eastward repeatedly. The tall figure also shed tears. Then, he took Xu to the ginseng harvesting area, showed him the way back, and pointed out the ginseng-producing areas to indicate his gratitude. Xu became wealthy from then on.

    The magistrate of Pingyang, Zhu Shuo, was cruel and harsh. In the town he governed, he made thick stocks and heavy sticks. When women were involved in cases, he would inevitably bring up adultery to interrogate them. He beat the prostitutes, took off their undergarments, and hit their private parts with a stick, causing them to swell and ulcerate for months, saying, "To see how they would receive customers." He smeared the buttocks' blood on the faces of the customers. For the beautiful prostitutes, he was even more cruel, shaved their heads, and cut two holes in their nostrils, saying, "To make the beautiful ones ugly, then the beauty of the prostitutes would be gone." When he met fellow officials, he would boast to himself, "Not moved by beauty, without my iron face and icy heart, how could I do this!" After his term was up, he was transferred to be the Deputy Governor of Shandong.

    47

    挈眷至茌平旅店，店楼封锁甚固，朱问故。店主曰：“楼中有怪，历年不启。”

    　　朱素愎，曰：“何害！怪闻吾威名，早当自退！”妻子苦劝不听。乃置妻子于别室，己独携剑秉烛坐至三鼓，有扣门进者，白须绛冠，见朱长揖。朱叱：“何怪？”

    　　老人曰：“某非怪，乃此方土地神也。闻贵人至此，正群怪殄灭之时，故喜而相迎。”且嘱曰：“公，少顷怪至，但须以宝剑挥之，某更相助，无不授首矣。”

    　　朱大喜，谢而遣之。

    　　须臾，青面者、白面者以次第至。朱以剑斫，应手而倒。最后有长牙黑嘴者来，朱以剑击，亦呼痛而陨。朱喜自负，急呼店主告之。时鸡已鸣，家人秉烛来照，横尸满地，悉其妻妾子女也。朱大叫曰：“吾乃为妖鬼所弄乎！”一恸而绝。

    Explanation

    47

    Zhu took his family to an inn in Ciping. The storey of the hotel was tightly sealed, and Zhu asked the reason. The innkeeper said, "There is a strange phenomenon in the building, and it has not been opened for many years."

    Zhu, who was usually obstinate, said, "What harm? The strange phenomenon will hear of my reputation and should retreat early!" Despite his wife's and children's earnest pleas, he refused to listen. He placed his family in a separate room and, with only his sword and a candle, sat until the third drumbeat of the night. At that moment, someone knocked on the door and entered. An old man with a white beard and a red cap bowed deeply to Zhu. Zhu shouted, "What strange phenomenon are you?"

    The old man replied, "I am not a strange phenomenon; I am the local land god. I heard that a noble person has come here, and it is the time when all strange phenomena will be destroyed, so I am happy to welcome you." He added, "Sir, shortly, the strange phenomena will come. You must strike them with your magical sword, and I will assist you. None of them will escape."

    Zhu was greatly pleased and thanked the old man before dismissing him.

    Soon, the green-faced and white-faced ones came one after another. Zhu struck them with his sword, and they fell with a single blow. Finally, a being with long fangs and a black beak came. Zhu struck it with his sword, and it also cried out in pain and fell dead. Zhu was pleased with himself and hurriedly called the innkeeper to inform him. By then, the rooster had crowed, and his family came with candles to see, finding dead bodies all over the ground, which were all his wives, concubines, and children. Zhu shouted, "Am I being toyed with by evil spirits?" and cried out in despair, then died.

    48

    ○不倒翁蒋生某往河南，过巩县，宿焉。店家有西楼，洒扫极净，蒋爱之，以行李往。

    　　店主笑曰：“公胆大否？此楼不甚安。”蒋曰：“椒山自有胆。”秉烛坐至夜深，闻几下如竹桶泛水声，有跃出者：青衣皂冠，长三寸许，类世间差役状。睨蒋许久，叱叱而退。

    　　少顷，数短人舁一官至，旗帜马车之类，历历如豆。官乌纱冠危坐，指蒋大詈，声细如蜂虿。蒋天怖色。官愈怒，小手拍地，麾众短人拘蒋。众短人牵鞋扯袜，竟不能动。官嫌其无勇，攘臂自起。蒋以手摄之，置于几上，细视之，世所卖不倒翁也。块然僵仆，一土偶耳。其舆从俯伏罗拜，乞还其主。蒋戏曰：“尔须以物赎。”应声曰：“诺。”墙穴中嗡嗡有声，或四人辇一钗，或二人扛一簪。

    Explanation

    48

    A Never-Falling Figurine

    A certain Mr. Jiang from Jiaozhou was traveling to Henan, passing through Gong County, where he stayed overnight. The inn had a western tower that was swept and cleaned to perfection. Mr. Jiang liked it and moved his belongings there.

    The innkeeper smiled and said, "Are you brave enough? This tower is not very safe." Mr. Jiang replied, "Jiaozhou has its own courage." He lit a candle and sat until late into the night. He heard a sound like water gurgling in a bamboo bucket beneath the table. Suddenly, something leaped out: a figure dressed in a black robe and wearing a black cap, about three inches tall, resembling a worldly servant. It glared at Mr. Jiang for a long time before retreating with a hiss.

    A short while later, several short people carried an official to the tower. Flags, banners, and carriages were visible, though they looked like beans. The official, wearing a black silk cap, sat upright and pointed at Mr. Jiang, scolding him in a high-pitched voice like that of a bee or scorpion. Mr. Jiang was terrified. The official grew angrier and slapped the ground with his tiny hand, ordering the short people to arrest Mr. Jiang. The short people tried to pull off his shoes and socks, but could not move him. The official, displeased with his lack of courage, stood up on his own. Mr. Jiang grabbed him and placed him on the table, examining him closely—it was a traditional "never-falling doll" sold in the world. It lay motionless and lifeless—just a clay figurine. Its attendants prostrated themselves in obeisance, begging to return it to its owner. Mr. Jiang teased, "You must redeem it with an object." They replied, "Yes." There was a buzzing sound from a wall hole, and sometimes four people carried a hairpin, sometimes two people carried a hairpin.

    49

    顷刻，首饰金帛之属布散于地。蒋取不倒翁掷与之，复能举动如初。然队伍不复整矣，奔窜而散。

    　　天渐明，店主大呼：“失贼！”问之，则楼上赎官之物，皆三寸短人所偷店主物也。

    　　○算命先生鬼平望周姓，以撑舟为业。舟过湖州桥下，篙触骨坛落水，至家而妹病，呼曰：“我湖州算命先生徐某。在生时，督抚司道贵人，谁不敬我！汝何人，敢投我骨于水！”女素不识字，病后能读书，喜为人算命。写八字与之，其推排悉合世上五行之说，亦不甚验也。周具牒诉于城隍。女卧一日醒曰：“见二青衣拘一鬼与我质于神前，鬼跪诉毁骨之事。神曰：”其兄触汝而责之于妹，何畏强欺弱耶！

    Explanation

    49

    In an instant, jewelry, gold, and silk scattered across the ground. Jiang picked up a "topsy-turvy" toy and threw it to them, which then moved as it had before. However, the group was no longer in order and scattered chaotically.

    As dawn approached, the shopkeeper shouted, "The thieves have escaped!" Upon investigation, it was found that the valuables being redeemed by officials on the upper floor had all been stolen by three-inch-tall figures, who were the store's property.

    A fortune-teller named Zhou, who was known as "Ghost Pingwang," made a living by rowing boats. One day, while passing under the bridge in Huzhou, his pole struck a bone altar and caused it to fall into the water. When he returned home, his sister fell ill and cried out, "I am Xu, the fortune-teller from Huzhou! In my lifetime, I was respected by high-ranking officials like governors and military commanders. Who are you to dare to throw my bones into the water?" The girl, who had never been able to read before, could now read after her illness and enjoyed predicting fortunes for others. She wrote down their birth dates and, according to the arrangement, they all matched the traditional Chinese elements. However, her predictions were not always accurate. Zhou prepared a petition and took it to the City God. After a day, the girl woke up and said, "I saw two officials in green robes arrest a ghost and bring it to trial before the gods. The ghost knelt and pleaded the case of destroying the bones. The gods said, 'Your brother hit you and blamed you on your sister. How dare you bully the weak!'"

    50

    汝自称能算命，而为能自护其朽骨，其算法不灵可知。生前哄骗人财物，不知多少矣！笞二十，押赴湖州。‘“女自此不复识字，亦不能算命矣。

    　　○鬼借力制凶人俗传凶人之终，必有恶鬼，以其力能相制也。扬州唐氏妻某，素悍妒，妾婢死其手者无数。亡何，暴病，口喃喃詈骂，如平日撒泼状。邻有徐元，膂力绝人，先一日昏晕，鼾呼叫骂，如与人角斗者，逾日始苏。或问故，曰：“吾为群鬼所借用耳。鬼奉阎罗命拘唐妻，而唐妻力强，群鬼不能制，故来假吾力缚之。吾与斗三日，昨被吾拉倒其足，缚交群鬼，吾才归耳。”往视唐妻，果气绝，而左足有青伤。

    Explanation

    50

    You claim to be able to tell fortunes, yet you cannot protect your own decaying bones, which shows that your fortune-telling method is not effective. Before your death, you deceived people out of their money and property, an amount that is unknown! You will receive twenty lashes and be sent to Huzhou. "From now on, she will no longer be able to read or write, nor will she be able to tell fortunes."

    ○ Ghosts Borrow Strength to Subdue Evil People

    According to popular belief, the end of an evil person's life is marked by a wicked ghost, who has the power to subdue them. In Yangzhou, there was a woman named Tang, who was always fierce and jealous, and countless maidservants and concubines died by her hands. Soon after, she suffered from a sudden illness, muttering curses and insults, as if she was acting out in her usual temperamental manner. There was a neighbor named Xu Yuan, whose strength was unmatched. The day before, he fainted and called out in his sleep, cursing, as if he was fighting someone. After a day, he regained consciousness. Someone asked why, and he said, "I was borrowed by the ghosts. The ghosts were ordered by Yama to arrest Tang's wife, but Tang's wife was very strong, and the ghosts could not subdue her. So they came to borrow my strength to bind her. I fought with her for three days. Yesterday, I pulled down her leg and tied her up for the ghosts, and then I returned." When he went to see Tang's wife, she was indeed dead, and her left foot had a bruise.

    51

    ○马盼盼寿州刺史刘介石，好扶乩。牧泰州时，请仙西厅。一日，乩盘大动，书“盼盼”二字，又书有“两世缘”三字。刘大骇，以为关盼盼也。问：“两世何缘？”

    　　曰：“事载《西湖佳话》。”刘书纸焚之曰：“可得见面否？”曰：“在今晚。”

    　　果薄暮而病，目定神昏。姜妾大骇，围坐守之。灯上片时，阴风飒然，一女子容色绝世，遍身衣履甚华，手执红纱灯，从户外入，向刘直扑。刘冷汗如雨下，心有悔意。女子曰：“君怖我乎？缘尚未到故也。”复从户外出，刘病稍差。嗣后意有所动，女子辄来。

    　　刘一日寓扬州天宁寺，秋雨闷坐，复思此女，取乩纸焚。乩盘大书曰：“我韦驮佛也。念汝为妖孽所缠，特来相救。汝可知天条否？上帝最恶者，以生人而好与鬼神交接，其孽在淫、嗔以上。汝嗣后速宜改悔，毋得邀仙媚鬼，自戕其命。”

    Explanation

    51

    Translation

    ○Ma Panpan, the governor of Shouzhou, Liu Jieshi, was fond of divination through the use of a divining board. While serving as the governor of Taizhou, he invited a spirit to appear in the west hall. One day, the divining board moved vigorously, writing the characters "Panpan" and then "two-life affinity." Liu Jieshi was greatly startled, thinking it referred to Guan Panpan. He asked, "What is the affinity of two lives?"

    The spirit replied, "It is recorded in The West Lake Tales." Liu Jieshi wrote a paper and burned it, asking, "Can I see you?" The spirit replied, "Tonight."

    Indeed, in the late afternoon, Liu Jieshi fell ill, with a fixed gaze and dazed consciousness. His concubine, Jiang, was greatly alarmed and sat around him to guard him. After a while, a sudden, chilling wind blew, and a woman of extraordinary beauty entered from outside, dressed in very splendid attire and holding a red silk lantern. She rushed toward Liu Jieshi. Liu Jieshi broke out in cold sweat, feeling regret. The woman said, "Are you afraid of me? It's because the time for our meeting has not yet come." She then left from outside, and Liu Jieshi's illness slightly improved. Afterward, whenever he had certain thoughts or desires, the woman would appear.

    One day, Liu Jieshi stayed at the Tianning Temple in Yangzhou, sitting in boredom during a rainy autumn day and again thinking of this woman. He took some divining paper and burned it. The divining board wrote large characters, saying, "I am the Bodhisattva Vajrapani. I think you are being entangled by evil spirits, so I especially came to save you. Do you know the laws of heaven? The Heavenly Emperor most detests a living person who likes to interact with ghosts and spirits. The sin is worse than lust and anger. You should quickly repent afterward, and do not invite spirits or flatter ghosts, for you are harming your own life."

    52

    刘悚然叩头，焚乩盘，烧符纸，自此妖绝。

    　　数年后，阅《西湖佳话》：“泰州有宋时营妓马盼盼墓，在州署之左偏。”

    　　《青箱杂志》载：“盼盼机巧，能学东坡书法。”始悟现形之妖，非关盼盼也。

    　　○滇绵谷秀才半世女妆蜀人滇谦六，富而无子，屡得屡亡。有星家教以压胜之法，云：“足下两世命中所照临者多是雌宿，虽获雄，无益也。惟获雄而以雌畜之，庶可补救。”已而绵谷生，谦六教以穿耳、梳头、裹足，呼为“小七娘”；娶不梳头、不裹足、不穿耳之女以妻之；果长大，入胖。生二孙，偶以郎名孙，即死。于是每孙生，亦以女畜之。绵谷韶秀无须，颇以女自居，有《绣针词》行世。吾友杨刺史潮观与之交好，为序其颠末。

    Explanation

    52

    Liu was startled and bowed deeply, burning the divination board and the talisman papers, and from then on, the evil spirits ceased.

    Several years later, he read The Tales of West Lake: "There is a tomb of Ma Pan Pan, a courtesan from the Song Dynasty, in Taizhou, to the left of the prefectural office."

    The Qingxiang Magazine records: "Pan Pan was clever and could imitate Su Dongpo's calligraphy." He then realized that the evil spirit that appeared in its true form was not related to Pan Pan.

    ○ Dian Qian Gu, a scholar from Sichuan who dressed as a woman for half his life, was wealthy but childless, repeatedly losing his children. An astrologer taught him a method to suppress misfortune, saying: "In both of your lifetimes, the stars that illuminate your fate are mostly female stars. Even if you gain a male child, it would be of no benefit. Only if you gain a male child and raise him as a girl can you perhaps make up for it." After that, Qian Gu was born, and Qian Liu taught him to pierce his ears, comb his hair, and bind his feet, calling him "Little Seventh Miss." He married a woman who did not comb her hair, bind her feet, or pierce her ears, and raised her as his wife. Qian Gu indeed grew up, becoming plump. He had two grandsons, and when he named one "Lang," he died. From then on, every time a grandson was born, he was also raised as a girl. Qian Gu was graceful and handsome without a beard, often identifying himself as a woman, and he had The Poems of the Needle in circulation. My friend Yang Chao Guan, the prefect, was friendly with him and wrote a preface to his life story.

    53

    ○炼丹道士楚中大宗伯张履昊好道。予告归，寄居江宁。入城时，拥朱提一百六十万。

    　　有郎总兵者，公门下士也，荐朱道士善黄白之术，寿九百余岁，烧杏核成银，屡试若神。道士说公烧丹，以白银百万，炼丹一枚，则长生可致。公惑之，斋戒三日，定坎离之位。每一炉，辄下银五万两，炭百担。昼则公亲监之，夜则使人守之。银登时化为水。炼三月，费银八十万，丹无消息。诘之，道士曰：“满百万则丹成。成后含之：不饥不寒，可南可北，随意所之，无不可到。”公无奈何，复与十余万，然已觉其妄，道士溲溺，必遣人尾之。

    Explanation

    53

    A Daoist Alchemist from the Chu Region: Zhang Luhao, the High Official, Was Devoted to Daoist Practices. I was granted leave to return home and temporarily lodged in Jiangning. Upon entering the city, I possessed 1.6 million zhūtí (ancient silver coins).

    There was a military official named Lang Zongbing, who was a subordinate of the official. He recommended a Daoist named Zhu, who was skilled in alchemy, claimed to live for over nine hundred years, and demonstrated the ability to turn almond kernels into silver. The Daoist repeatedly tested his skills with divine results. The Daoist advised the official to burn alchemical elixirs using one million liang of silver, and that by consuming the elixir, he could achieve immortality. The official was convinced and fasted for three days, setting the positions of Kan (water) and Li (fire) in the alchemical process. For each furnace, five million liang of silver and one hundred loads of charcoal were used. During the day, the official personally supervised the process, and at night, he had someone guard it. The silver instantly turned into water. After three months of alchemy, eight million liang of silver were spent, but no elixir was produced. When questioned, the Daoist said, "Once one million is reached, the elixir will be completed. After consuming it, one will not feel hunger or cold, can go south or north, and can go wherever one wishes, with no place unreachable." The official had no choice but to give him another ten thousand liang of silver, though he had already begun to doubt the Daoist's claims. The Daoist's urination and defecation were closely monitored by someone.

    54

    清晨，道士溲于园，尾者回顾，忽失道士所在。往视其炉，百万俱空矣。启道士行李，得书一封，云：“公此种财，皆非义物也。吾与公有宿缘，特来取去，为公打点阴间赎罪费用，日后自有效验。幸毋相怪。”家人觇道士者皆云：每五万银下炉时，屋上隐隐有雷声，道士惶恐伏地，以朱符盖其头。其搬运实无痕迹。

    　　○叶老脱有叶老脱者，不知其由来，科头跣足，冬夏一布袍，手挈竹席而行。尝投维扬旅店，嫌房客嘈杂，欲择洁地。店主指一室曰：“此最静僻，但有鬼，不可宿。”

    Explanation

    54

    Translation

    In the early morning, the Taoist urinated in the garden, and the follower looked back, only to suddenly find the Taoist had vanished. Upon checking the furnace, a million were all gone. Opening the Taoist's luggage, they found a letter that read: "Your kind of wealth, all are unjust gains. I have a predestined connection with you, hence I come to take it away, to arrange for your expenses in the afterlife for redemption. In the future, there will be proof. Please do not blame me." Those who observed the Taoist among the family members all said: every time five million silver was put into the furnace, there was a faint thunder sound above the house. The Taoist was terrified and knelt on the ground, covering his head with a red talisman. The transportation was actually without any trace.

    ○ Ye Laotuo

    There was a man named Ye Laotuo, whose origin was unknown. He wore no hat and went barefoot, wearing a single cloth robe throughout the year, carrying a bamboo mat as he traveled. Once, he stayed at a hotel in Weiyang, finding the other guests noisy, and wished to find a clean place. The innkeeper pointed to a room and said: "This is the quietest and most secluded, but there is a ghost, so you cannot stay here."

    55

    叶曰：“无害。”径自扫除，摊竹席于地。

    　　夜，卧至三鼓，门忽开，见有妇人系帛于项，双眸抉出，悬两颐下，伸舌长数尺，彳亍而来。旁有无头鬼，手提两头继至。尾其后者：一鬼遍体皆黑，耳目口鼻甚模糊；一鬼四肢黄肿，腹大于五石匏。相诧曰：“此间有生人气，当共攫之。”群作搜捕状，卒不得近叶。一鬼曰：“明明在此，而搜之不得，奈何？”

    　　黄胖者曰：“凡吾辈之所以能摄人者，以其心怖而魂先出也。此人盖有道之士，心不怖，魂不离体，故仓猝不易得。”群鬼方徨四顾，叶乃起，坐席上，以手自表曰：“我在此。”群鬼惊悸，齐跪地下。叶一一讯之。妇人指三鬼曰：“此死于水者，此死于火者，此盗杀人而被刑者，我则缢死此室者也。”叶曰：“若辈服我乎？”皆曰：“然。”曰：“然则各自投生，勿在此作祟。”各罗拜去。

    Explanation

    55

    Ye said, "No harm." He proceeded to sweep and clean, and spread a bamboo mat on the ground.

    At night, he lay down until the third watch when the door suddenly opened. He saw a woman with a silk cloth tied around her neck, her eyes plucked out and hanging from both cheeks, her tongue extended several feet, and she walked slowly and hesitantly. Beside her was a headless ghost, holding two heads in its hands and following. The one following behind: one ghost was entirely black, with very blurred ears, eyes, mouth, and nose; another ghost had yellow and swollen limbs, and a belly larger than a five-stone gourd. They said in amazement, "There is living human energy here; we should seize it together." The group made a searching and capturing gesture, but finally could not get close to Ye. One ghost said, "It is clearly here, but we cannot find it, what should we do?"

    The yellow and fat one said, "The reason why we can capture people is because their hearts are terrified and their souls come out first. This person is probably a Daoist, with a heart that is not terrified and a soul that does not leave the body, so it is not easy to get in a hurry." The group of ghosts were hesitating and looking around, and Ye then got up, sat on the mat, and with his hand pointing to himself and said, "I am here." The group of ghosts were startled and terrified, and knelt down together on the ground. Ye questioned them one by one. The woman pointed to three ghosts and said, "This one died in water, this one died in fire, this one was a thief who killed people and was punished, I am the one who hanged myself in this room." Ye said, "Do you all submit to me?" They all said, "Yes." He said, "Then each of you should reincarnate, do not cause trouble here." They all knelt and bowed and left.

    56

    迨晓，为主人道其事，嗣后此室宴然。

    　　○苏耽老饮疫神杭州苏耽老，性滑稽，善嘲人。人恶之，元旦，画疫神一纸压其门。耽老晨出开门，见而大笑，迎疫神归，延之上座，与共饮酒而烧化之。是年大疫，四邻病者为祀疫神。其病人辄作神语曰：“我元旦受苏耽老礼敬，愧无以报。欲禳我者，必请苏君陪我，我方去。”于是祀疫神者争先请苏，苏逐日奔忙，困于酒食。

    　　其家大小十馀口，无一病者。

    　　○刘刺史奇梦陕西刘刺史介石补官江南，寓苏州虎丘。夜二鼓，梦乘轻风归陕，未至乡里，路遇一鬼尾之，长三尺许，囚首丧面，狞丑可憎，与刘对搏。良久，鬼败，刘挟鬼于腋下而趋，将投之河。路遇余姓者，故邻也，谓曰：“城西有观音庙，何不挟此鬼诉于观音以杜后患？”刘然其言，挟鬼入庙。

    Explanation

    56

    By dawn, the owner was told about the matter, and afterward, the room was peaceful.

    ○ Su Danlao and the Plague God

    In Hangzhou, there was a man named Su Danlao, who was jovial and skilled at mocking others. People disliked him, so on New Year's Day, they drew a picture of the plague god and placed it at his door. One morning, Su Danlao opened the door and saw the picture, and he laughed heartily. He welcomed the plague god back, invited him to sit in the seat of honor, shared a drink with him, and then burned the picture to ashes. That year, there was a great plague, and the sick neighbors began to worship the plague god. The sick people would speak as if they were the god, saying, "On New Year's Day, I received respectful treatment from Su Danlao, and I am ashamed that I have nothing to repay. Those who wish to rid themselves of me must invite Su Danlao to accompany me, and I will leave." As a result, those who worshiped the plague god rushed to invite Su Danlao, and Su Danlao ran around every day, tired from alcohol and food.

    All the family members, more than ten in number, remained healthy.

    ○ A Strange Dream of Cishi Liu

    In Shaanxi, Cishi Liu Jieshi was appointed to a new post in Jiangnan and stayed in Suzhou at Tiger Hill. One night, at the second drumbeat, he dreamed that he was riding a light breeze back to Shaanxi. Before reaching his hometown, he encountered a ghost following him. The ghost was about three feet tall, with a disheveled head and a mournful face, and looked extremely ugly and terrifying. The ghost challenged Liu to a fight. After a while, the ghost was defeated, and Liu took the ghost under his arm and hurried to throw it into the river. On the way, he met someone surnamed Yu, an old neighbor, who said, "There is a temple of Guanyin to the west of the city. Why not take this ghost to complain to Guanyin to prevent future troubles?" Liu agreed, and he took the ghost to the temple.

    57

    庙门外韦驮金刚神皆怒目视鬼，各举所持兵器作击鬼状，鬼亦悚惧。观音望见，呼曰：“此阴府之鬼，须押回阴府。”刘拜谢。观音目金刚押解。金刚跪辞，语不甚解，似不屑押解者。现音笑目刘曰：“即着汝押往阴府。”刘跪曰：“弟子凡身，何能到阴府？”观音曰：“易耳。”捧刘面呵气者三，即遣出。鬼俯伏无语，相随而行。

    　　刘自念虽有观音之命，然阴府未知在何处，正徘徊间，复遇余姓者。曰：“君欲往阴府，前路有竹笠覆地者是也。”刘望路北有笠，如俗所用酱缸篷状，以手起之，洼然一井。鬼见大喜，跃而入。刘随之，冷不可耐。每坠丈许，必为井所夹，有温气自上而下，则又坠矣。

    　　三坠后，豁然有声，乃落于瓦上。张目视之，别有天地，白日丽空，所坠之瓦上，即王者之殿角也。闻殿中群神震怒，大呼曰：“何处生人气？”有金甲者擒刘至王前。王衮龙衣，冕旒，须白如银，上坐，问：“尔生人，胡为至此？”

    Explanation

    57

    Outside the temple, the Bodhisattva Vajrapani and the Dharma Protector gods all glared at the ghost with anger, each raising their held weapons as if to strike it. The ghost was also terrified. Guanyin saw this and called out, "This is a ghost from the Underworld, who must be taken back to the Underworld." Liu bowed in gratitude. Guanyin looked at the Dharma Protector to carry out the arrest. The Dharma Protector knelt to decline, speaking in a way that was not fully understood, seeming reluctant to carry out the arrest. Guanyin smiled at Liu and said, "You shall take him to the Underworld." Liu knelt and said, "As a mere mortal, how can I go to the Underworld?" Guanyin replied, "It is easy." She held Liu's face and blew three times, then sent him out. The ghost knelt silently and followed.

    Liu thought to himself that although he had Guanyin's command, he did not know where the Underworld was. As he hesitated, he encountered someone named Yu. Yu said, "If you wish to go to the Underworld, the path ahead where there is a bamboo hat covering the ground is the way." Liu saw a bamboo hat on the north side of the road, shaped like a cover for a jar of soy sauce, and lifted it with his hand, revealing a deep well. The ghost saw this and was greatly pleased, leaping into the well. Liu followed, finding it unbearably cold. Every time he fell about a meter, he was caught by the well, and warm air would descend from above, causing him to fall again.

    After falling three times, he heard a loud sound and landed on tiles. Opening his eyes, he saw a different world, with the sun shining brightly in the sky. The tiles he had landed on were part of the corner of a king's palace. He heard the gods in the hall roar in anger, shouting, "Where is the smell of life coming from?" A god in golden armor seized Liu and brought him before the king. The king wore a dragon robe, a crown with tassels, and had a beard white as silver, sitting high above. He asked, "You, a living person, why are you here?"

    58

    刘具道观音遣解之事。王目金甲神ㄏ其面仰天，谛视之，曰：“面有红光，果然佛遣来。”问：“鬼安在？”曰：“在墙脚下。”王厉声曰：“恶鬼难留！着押归原处。”群神叉戟交集，将鬼叉戟上投池，池中毒蛇怪鳖争脔食之。

    　　刘自念：已到阴府，何不一问前生事？揖金甲神曰：“某愿知前生事。”金甲神首肯，引至廊下，抽簿示之曰：“汝前生九岁时，曾盗人卖儿银八两，卖儿父母懊恨而亡，汝以此孽夭死。今再世矣，犹应为瞽，以偿前愆。”刘大惊曰：“作善可禳乎？”神曰：“视汝善何如耳。”语未毕，殿中呼曰：“天符至矣，速令刘某回阳，毋致泄漏阴司案件。”金甲神掖至王前。刘复跪求曰：“某凡身，何能出此阴界？”王持刘背吸气者三，遂耸身于井。三耸三夹如前，有温气自下而上，身从井出。

    Explanation

    58

    Liu detailed the events of being sent to resolve the situation. The king looked at the golden-armored deity, who was looking up to the sky, and examined him closely, saying, "There is a red light on his face; indeed, he was sent by the Buddha." The king asked, "Where is the ghost?" The deity replied, "It is at the foot of the wall." The king sternly said, "It is difficult to keep an evil ghost! Send it back to its original place." The gods, armed with spears and halberds, threw the ghost into the pool, where the venomous snakes and strange turtles fought over and ate it.

    Liu thought to himself: "I have already arrived in the underworld; why not ask about my past-life events?" He bowed respectfully to the golden-armored deity and said, "I wish to know about my past-life events." The golden-armored deity nodded, led him to the hall, and pulled out a register to show him, saying, "In your past life, when you were nine years old, you stole eight taels of silver from someone selling their child. The parents of the child, filled with sorrow and anger, died, and you died prematurely due to this sin. Now you have been reincarnated, but you should still be blind to repay your past sins." Liu was greatly shocked and said, "Can good deeds mitigate this?" The deity replied, "It depends on how good your deeds are." Before the deity finished speaking, a voice from the hall called out, "The heavenly decree has arrived! Quickly send Liu back to the mortal world, do not allow the secrets of the underworld to leak out." The golden-armored deity led Liu back to the king. Liu knelt and begged again, "I am a mortal, how can I leave this realm of the underworld?" The king held Liu's back and inhaled three times, and Liu rose up into the well. Three times he rose and was held, and there was a warm, ascending vapor. His body rose from the well.

    59

    至长安道上，复命于观音庙，跪陈阴府本末。旁一童子嚅嚅不已，所陈语与刘同。刘骇视之，耳目口鼻俨然己之本身也，但缩小如婴儿。刘大惊，指童子呼曰：“此妖也！”童子亦指刘呼曰：“此妖也！”观音谓刘曰：“汝毋恐，此汝魂也。汝魂恶而魄善，故作事坚强而不甚透彻，今为汝易之。”刘拜谢，童子不谢，曰：“我在彼上，今欲易我，必先去我。我去，独不于彼有伤乎？”观音笑曰：“毋伤也。”手金簪长尺许，自刘之左胁插入，剔一肠出，以腕绕之。每绕尺许，则童子身渐缩小。绕毕，掷于梁上，童子不复见矣。观音以掌扑案，刘悸而醒，仍在苏州枕席间，胁下红痕，犹隐然在焉。月余，陕信至，其邻人余姓者亡矣。此事介石亲为余言。

    Explanation

    59

    On the road to Chang'an, Liu reported back at the Guanyin temple, kneeling to recount the entire story of the underworld. Beside him, a child kept mumbling persistently, and the words he spoke were the same as Liu's. Startled, Liu looked at the child, who had ears, eyes, nose, and mouth that resembled his own, but was only as small as a baby. Liu was greatly alarmed and pointed at the child, shouting, "This is a demon!" The child also pointed at Liu and shouted, "This is a demon!" Guanyin said to Liu, "Do not be afraid; this is your soul. Your soul is evil, but your spirit is good, therefore, you do things strongly but not very thoroughly. Now, I will change it for you." Liu bowed and thanked her, but the child did not thank her. The child said, "I am there, now I want to change me, I must first remove me. If I go, will I not cause harm to him?" Guanyin smiled and said, "Do not worry." She took a golden hairpin about a foot long, inserted it into Liu's left side, pulled out an intestine, and wrapped it around with her wrist. Each time she wrapped about a foot, the child's body gradually shrank. After wrapping it all, she threw it onto the beam, and the child was no longer seen. Guanyin slapped the table with her hand, and Liu, frightened, woke up. He was still in his bed in Suzhou, with a faint red mark under his ribs. A month later, a letter from Shaanxi arrived, informing him that his neighbor, surnamed Yu, had died. This matter was personally told to me by Jieshi.

    60

    ○赵李二生广东赵、李二生，读书番禺山中。端阳节日，赵氏父母馈酒肴为两生庆节，两生同饮甚乐。至二鼓，闻扣门声，启之，亦书生也，衣冠楚楚。自云：相离十里许，慕两生高义，愿来纳交。邀入坐，言论风生。先论举业，后及古文词赋，元元本本，两生自以为弗及。最后论及仙佛，赵素不乐闻而李颇信之，书生因力辨其有，且曰：“欲见佛乎？此顷刻事也。”李欣然欲试之。书生取案几叠高五尺许，身踞其上，登时有旃檀之气氤氲四至，随取身上绢带作圈，谓二生曰：“从圈入，即佛地也，可以见佛。”李信之既笃，见圈中观音、韦驮，香烟飘渺，即欲以头入圈；而赵望之则獠牙青面、吐舌丈馀者在圈中矣。遂大呼。家人共进，李如梦醒者，虽挣脱，而颈已有伤，书生杳然不复可见。两生家俱以此山有邪，不可读书，各令还家。明年，李举孝廉，会试连捷，出授庐江知县。卒以被劾，自缢而亡。

    Explanation

    60

    ○ Zhao and Li, two scholars from Guangdong, were studying in the mountains of Panyu. On the Dragon Boat Festival, Zhao's parents sent wine and food to celebrate the festival with the two scholars, and they enjoyed the feast together. Around the second watch of the night (around 2 AM), they heard a knock on the door. When they opened it, they found another scholar, well-dressed and elegant. He said, "I am about ten miles away and have admired the noble character of the two scholars. I wish to make friends with you." He was invited in and sat down, and they engaged in lively conversation. They first discussed the imperial examinations, then moved on to classical literature and poetry, delving into the fundamental principles. The two scholars felt that they could not match his knowledge. Finally, they turned to the topic of deities and spirits. Zhao had never been interested in this subject, but Li was quite believing. The scholar then vigorously argued that these deities existed and said, "Do you want to see a deity? This can be done in an instant." Li eagerly wanted to try it. The scholar took a table and stacked it five feet high, then stood on it. Suddenly, the air was filled with the fragrant aroma of sandalwood. He then took a silk belt from his body and made a circle, saying to the two scholars, "Enter the circle, and you will be in the realm of the Buddha, where you can see the Buddha." Li, who was very convinced, saw the Bodhisattva Guanyin and the Bodhisattva Wenshu, with incense smoke swirling around. He was about to put his head into the circle, but Zhao saw a fierce, demonic figure with sharp teeth, a blue face, and a long tongue inside the circle. Zhao shouted in alarm. The family rushed in, and Li, like someone waking from a dream, struggled to free himself, but his neck was already injured. The scholar vanished without a trace.

    The families of the two scholars both believed that the mountain was haunted and that it was not safe to study there, so they each sent their children back home. The following year, Li passed the imperial examination and the provincial examination consecutively, and was appointed as the magistrate of Lujiang County. However, he was eventually impeached and hanged himself and died.

    61

    ○山东林秀才山东林秀才长康，四十不第。一日，有改业之想，闻旁有呼者曰：“莫灰心。”

    　　林惊问：“何人？”曰：“我鬼也，守公而行，并为公护驾者数年矣。”林欲见其形，鬼不可。再四言，鬼曰：“公必欲见我，无怖而后可。”林许之，遂跪于前，丧面流血，曰：“某蓝城县市布者也，为掖县张某某害，以尸压东城门石磨盘之下。公异日当宰掖县，故常侍公，求为伸冤。”且言公某年举乡试，某年成进士，言毕不复见。至期，果举孝廉，惟进士之期爽焉。林叹曰：“世间功名之事，鬼亦有不知者乎！”言未毕，空中又呼曰：“公自行有亏耳，非我误报也！

    Explanation

    61

    A Scholar from Shandong

    A scholar named Lin Changkang from Shandong had failed the imperial examinations at the age of forty. One day, he had thoughts of changing careers when he heard someone calling out, "Don't lose heart."

    Lin was startled and asked, "Who is there?" The voice replied, "I am a ghost, guarding your actions and protecting you for several years." Lin wanted to see the ghost's form, but the ghost refused. After much insistence, the ghost said, "If you insist on seeing me, it will be possible without fear." Lin agreed, and the ghost appeared before him, with a disfigured face and bleeding. "I was a cloth merchant from Lancheng County, killed by someone from Yexian, and my body was pressed under the stone millstone at the east city gate. You will one day govern Yexian, so I have always served you, seeking justice for me." The ghost also mentioned that Lin would pass the provincial examination in a certain year and become a Jinshi in another year. After speaking, the ghost disappeared. As predicted, Lin indeed passed the examination for filial piety and integrity, but the date for becoming a Jinshi did not come to pass. Lin sighed, "Is there anything even ghosts don't know about the affairs of official success?" Before he finished speaking, a voice from the air called out, "You have your own faults; it is not I who have misled you!"

    62

    公于某月日私通孀妇某，幸不成胎，无人知觉。阴司记其恶而宽其罪，罚迟二科。“

    　　林悚然，谨身修善，逾二科而成进士，授官掖县。抵任进城，见一石磨，启之，果得尸；立拘张某，讯之，尽吐杀人情实，置之于法。

    　　○秦中墓道秦中土地极厚，有掘三五丈而未及泉者。凤翔以西，其俗：人死不即葬，多暴露之，俟其血肉化尽，然后葬埋，否则有发凶之说。尸未消化而葬者，一得地气，三月之后，遍体生毛，白者号白凶，黑者号黑凶，便入人家为孽。

    　　刘刺史之邻孙姓者掘沟得一石门，开之，隧道宛然。陈设鸡犬尊，皆瓦为之。中悬二棺，旁列男女数人，钉身于墙。盖古之为殉者，惧其仆，故钉之也。

    Explanation

    62

    In a certain month, a man had illicit relations with a widow, fortunately without resulting in pregnancy, and no one was aware of it. The underworld recorded his evil deed but showed leniency in his punishment, delaying it by two periods of "ke" (a unit of time or punishment).

    Frightened, the man became cautious in his conduct and cultivated goodness. After passing two periods of "ke," he passed the imperial examinations and became a Jinshi. He was appointed as an official in Yexian County. Upon arriving at his post and entering the city, he saw a stone mill. When he opened it, he indeed found a corpse. He immediately arrested Zhang, interrogated him, and he fully confessed to the details of the murder. Zhang was dealt with according to the law.

    In the region of Qin, the land is extremely thick, and there are places where digging three to five zhang (a unit of length) does not reach the water table. In the area west of Fengxiang, the custom is: when people die, they are not immediately buried, often left exposed. They wait until their flesh has completely decayed before burying them, otherwise, there is a belief that it will bring bad luck. Those who are buried before their bodies have decomposed, once they acquire the earth's energy, will grow hair all over their bodies three months later. The white ones are called "white evil," and the black ones are called "black evil," and they enter people's homes as a source of misfortune.

    A neighbor of Cishi Liu, a man surnamed Sun, dug a trench and found a stone door. When he opened it, a tunnel was exactly as it was. Inside, there were arrangements of chickens, dogs, and wine vessels, all made of clay. In the middle hung two coffins, and on the sides were arranged several men and women, their bodies nailed to the walls. This was probably the ancient practice of burying people alive as sacrifices, fearing that they might fall down or collapse, so they were nailed to the walls.

    63

    衣冠状貌，约略可睹。稍逼视之，风起于穴，悉化为灰，并骨如白尘矣，其钉犹在左右墙上。不知何王之墓。亦有掘得土人作卧形者，有头角四肢而无耳目，疑皆古尸之所化也。

    　　○夏侯墓本朝松江提督张勇生时，其父梦有金甲神，自称汉将军夏侯氏，入门，随即生勇。后封侯归葬，掘地得古碑，隶书“魏将军夏侯墓”，字如碗大。阅二千年而骨肉复归其故处，亦奇。

    　　○塞外二事雍正时，定西大将军纪成斌以失律诛，在塞外颇为祟。后接任将军查公辕下兵某，白日仆地，自称“纪大将军，求索饮食”。众皆罗拜，代为乞命。幕客陈对轩，豪士也，直前批其颊，骂曰：“纪成斌，尔征阿拉蒲坦，临陈退缩，以王法伏诛。鬼若有灵，尚宜自愧，何敢忝为厉鬼，作屠沽儿乞食状耶！”骂毕，兵蹶然起，不复┲语矣。自后凡有疫疠自称纪大将军者，称“陈相公来了”骇之，无不立愈。

    Explanation

    63

    The appearance of clothing and posture can be vaguely seen. Upon closer inspection, a wind arose from a hole, and everything turned into ash, with the bones becoming like white dust. The nails remained on the left and right walls. It is unknown whose king's tomb it is. There were also cases where figures made of earth in a lying position were dug up, with heads, horns, and limbs but no eyes or ears. It is suspected that they are all transformed from ancient corpses.

    ○ Tomb of Xiahou Dun
    In the early Qing dynasty, when Zhang Yong, the governor of Songjiang, was born, his father dreamed of a golden-armored deity who identified himself as General Xiahou of Han and entered the house. Shortly after, Zhang Yong was born. After being ennobled and returning to be buried, an ancient stone tablet was unearthed, written in clerical script: "Tomb of General Xiahou Dun of Wei," with characters as large as bowls. After two thousand years, the bones and flesh returned to their original place, which is also remarkable.

    ○ Two Matters in the Frontier
    During the reign of Emperor Yongzheng, the great general of Dingxi, Ji Chengbin, was executed for failing to follow military regulations. In the frontier, he became quite troublesome. Later, a soldier under the new general, Cai, fell to the ground during the day and claimed to be "General Ji, seeking food." Everyone knelt and begged for his life. The guest Tanchuoxuan, a bold man, walked up and slapped him across the face, saying, "Ji Chengbin, you retreated in battle when attacking Alaputan, and were executed by imperial decree. If you have any spirit, you should be ashamed of yourself. How dare you disgrace yourself as a malevolent ghost, begging for food like a butcher or peddler?" After finishing his scolding, the soldier suddenly got up and no longer spoke. From then on, anyone who claimed to be General Ji during an epidemic would be frightened by the mention of "Tanchuoxuan has come," and they would all immediately recover.

    64

    纪受诛时，家奴尽散，一厨者收其尸。亡何病死，常附病者身，自称“厨神”，曰：“上帝怜我忠心葬主，故命为群鬼长。”问：“纪将军何在？”曰：“上帝怒其失律，使兵民受伤数万，罚为疫鬼，受我驱遣。我以主人故，终不敢。然我所言无不听。”嗣后，塞外遇将军为祟，先请陈相公，如陈不来，便呼厨神，纪亦去矣。

    　　○关神断狱溧阳马孝廉丰，未第时，馆于邑之西村李家。邻有王某，性凶恶，素捶其妻。

    　　妻饥饿，无以自存，窃李家鸡烹食之。李知之，告其夫。夫方被酒，大怒，持刀牵妻至。审问得实，将杀之。妻大惧，诬鸡为孝廉所窃。孝廉与争，无以自明，曰：“村有关神庙，请往掷杯交卜之。卦阴者妇人窃，卦阳者男子窃。”如其言，三掷皆阳。王投刀放妻归，而孝廉以窃鸡故，为村人所薄，失馆数年。

    Explanation

    64

    When Ji was executed, all his servants scattered, except for one kitchen worker who buried his body. Not long afterward, the kitchen worker died and began to haunt the sick, calling himself the "Kitchen Deity" and saying, "The Heavenly Deity pities my loyal heart in burying the master, so I am appointed as the leader of all ghosts." When asked, "Where is General Ji now?" he replied, "The Heavenly Deity is angry that he lost the rhythm, causing tens of thousands of soldiers and civilians to be injured, and has punished him to be a plague spirit, subject to my command. However, I ultimately dare not do so. Nevertheless, whatever I say, he listens to." Afterward, in the borderlands, when encountering the general causing trouble, one should first call upon Master Chen; if Master Chen does not come, then call upon the Kitchen Deity, and the general will depart as well.

    ○ The God of Guan Resolves a Case

    In Lichang, there was a scholar named Ma Feng who had not yet passed the imperial examinations. He was staying as a tutor in the western village of a town at the Li family's house. There was a man named Wang who lived nearby and was known for his cruelty. He often beat his wife.

    The wife, hungry and unable to survive on her own, stole a chicken from the Li family to cook and eat. The Li family discovered this and told Wang. Wang, who was drunk, became extremely angry and took a knife to bring his wife to the Li family. After questioning her, he confirmed the truth and was about to kill her. The wife was greatly afraid and falsely accused the scholar of stealing the chicken. The scholar argued with her but could not prove his innocence. He said, "There is a temple of the God of Guan in the village. Let us go there to cast the cup and divination. If the divination shows yin, then a woman stole it; if it shows yang, then a man stole it." They followed his suggestion, and three casts all showed yang. Wang threw away his knife and let his wife return home, but the scholar, because of the chicken theft, was looked down upon by the villagers and lost his position as a tutor for several years.

    65

    他日，有扶乩者方登坛，自称关神。孝廉记前事，大骂神之不灵。乩书灰盘曰：“马孝廉，汝将来有临民之职，亦知事有缓急重耶？汝窃鸡，不过失馆；某妻窃鸡，立死刀下矣。我宁受不灵之名，以救生人之命。上帝念我能识政体，故超升三级。汝乃怨我耶？”孝廉曰：“关神既封帝矣，何级之升？”乩神曰：“今四海九州皆有关神庙，焉得有许多关神分享血食。凡村乡所立关庙，皆奉上帝命，择里中鬼平生正直者代司其事，真关神在帝左右，何能降凡耶？”孝廉乃服。

    　　○紫清烟语苏州杨大瓢讳宾者，工书法，年六十时，病死而苏，曰：“天上书府唤我赴试耳。近日玉帝制《紫清烟语》一部，缮写者少，故召试诸善书人。我未知中式否。如中式，则不能复生矣。”越三日，空中有鸾鹤之声，杨愀然曰：“吾不能学王僧虔，以秃笔自累，致损其生。”瞑目而逝。或问天府书家姓名，曰：“索靖一等第一人，右军一等第十人。”

    Explanation

    65

    One day, a diviner ascended the altar and claimed to be the God of Guan. The scholar, remembering his previous encounter, angrily accused the deity of being ineffective. The divining tablet inscribed on the ash tray: "Mr. Ma, you will soon hold a position of governing the people. Do you understand the varying degrees of urgency and importance in matters? You stole a chicken, and lost your position; someone else’s wife stole a chicken, and she was executed immediately. I would rather bear the name of being ineffective to save a life. The Supreme Deity appreciates my understanding of governance and has promoted me three levels. Do you blame me?" The scholar retorted, "Since Guan has already been enfeoffed as an emperor, how can there be further promotion?" The divining tablet replied, "Now, there are Guan temples in all corners of the world. How could there be so many Guans sharing the sacrificial offerings? All village and town Guan temples are established by the command of the Supreme Deity, selecting righteous spirits from the local dead to serve as proxies. The true Guan is in the presence of the emperor, and cannot descend to the mortal world." The scholar then accepted the explanation.

    ○ Purple Smoke Language

    In Suzhou, there was a calligrapher named Yang Dapiao, who was skilled in calligraphy. At the age of sixty, he died from illness but revived, saying, "The calligraphy office in heaven has summoned me for a test. Recently, the Jade Emperor has compiled a book called 'Purple Smoke Language,' and there are few who can transcribe it. Therefore, he has called upon all skilled calligraphers for a test. I do not know if I will pass. If I do, I will not be able to return to life." Three days later, there were sounds of phoenixes and cranes in the air. Yang said solemnly, "I cannot emulate Wang Sengqian, burdening myself with a worn-out brush, which would harm my life." He closed his eyes and passed away. Someone asked about the names of calligraphers in heaven, and was told, "Suo Jing is the first among the first rank, and Yongjun (Wang Xizhi) is the tenth among the first rank."

    66

    ○顾尧年乾隆十五年，余寓苏州江雨峰家。其子宝臣赴金陵乡试，归家病剧。雨峰遍召名医，均有难色。知余与薛征君一瓢交好，强余作札邀之。未至，余与雨峰候于门。病者在室呼曰：“顾尧年来矣！”连称：“顾叟请坐。”顾尧年者，苏市布衣，先以请平米价、倡众殴官为苏抚安公所诛者也。坐定，语江曰：“江相公，你已中乡试三十八名矣，病亦无恙，可自宽解。赐我酒肉，我便去。”雨峰闻之，急入房相慰曰：“顾叟速去，当即祭叟。”病者曰：“外有钱塘袁某官，喧聒于门，我怖之，不能去。”又曰：“薛先生到门矣。其人良医也，我当避之。”

    Explanation

    66

    In the 15th year of the Qianlong era, I was staying at Jiang Yufeng's house in Suzhou. His son, Baocen, went to Jinling for the provincial examination, returned home, and became severely ill. Jiang Yufeng summoned many famous doctors, but all of them had a difficult expression. Knowing that I was friends with Xue Zhengjun Yipiao, he forced me to write a letter inviting him. Before Xue arrived, I and Jiang Yufeng waited at the door. The patient, who was in the room, called out, "Con Yiannian has come!" He repeatedly said, "Mr. Con, please sit down." Con Yiannian was a man from Suzhou who was executed by Governor An of Suzhou for leading a crowd to beat officials over the price of grain. After sitting down, he spoke to Jiang, saying, "Mr. Jiang, you have passed the provincial examination as the 38th candidate, and your illness is also not serious, so you can be at ease. Give me some wine and meat, and I will leave." Jiang Yufeng, hearing this, quickly entered the room to comfort him, saying, "I will immediately offer a sacrifice to you." The patient replied, "There is a certain official from Qiantang outside the door, making a lot of noise, and I am afraid of him, so I cannot leave." He then said again, "Mr. Xue has arrived at the door. He is a good doctor, so I should avoid him."

    67

    雨峰急出，拉余让路，而一瓢果自外入。即告以故。一瓢大笑曰：“鬼既避我二人，请与公同入逐之。”遂入房。薛按脉，余帚扫床前，一药而愈。其年宝臣登第，果如所报之名次。

    　　○妖道乞鱼余姊夫王贡南，居杭州之横河桥。晨出，遇道士于门，拱手曰：“乞公一鱼。”

    　　贡南嗔曰：“汝出家人吃素，乃索鱼肉耶？”曰：“木鱼也。”贡南拒之。道士曰：“公吝于前，必悔于后。”遂去。是夜，闻落瓦声。旦视之，瓦集于庭。次夜，衣服尽入厕溷中。

    Explanation

    67

    Rain Peak hurriedly stepped out, pulling me aside to make way, and indeed, a ladle came from outside. He then told him the reason. The ladle laughed loudly and said, "The ghost has already avoided us two. Let's go in together to chase it away." So we entered the room. Xue examined the pulse, and I swept in front of the bed. One medicine and he was cured. That year, Baochen passed the imperial examination, indeed as reported in the name list.

    ○ A Sorcerer Asks for Fish

    My brother-in-law Wang Gongnan lived in Hangzhou by the Henghe Bridge. In the morning, he met a Taoist priest at the door, who bowed and said, "May I ask you for a fish?"

    Gongnan scolded, "You, as an outcast, eat vegetarian food, yet you ask for fish and meat?" The priest replied, "It's a wooden fish." Gongnan refused him. The Taoist priest said, "You will regret your stinginess later." So he left. That night, there was a sound of tiles falling. In the morning, he saw the tiles piled up in the courtyard. The next night, his clothes were all in the toilet.

    68

    贡南乞符于张有虔秀才家。张曰：“我有二符，其价一贱一贵。贱者张之，可制之于旦夕；贵者张之，现神获怪。”贡南取贱者归，悬中堂。是夜，果安。

    　　越三日，又有老道士，形容古怪，来叩门，适贡南他适，次子后文出见。道士曰：“汝家日前为某道所苦，其人即我之弟子也。汝索救于符，不如索救于我。可嘱汝父，明日到西湖之冷泉亭，大呼‘铁冠’三声，我即至矣。否则，符且为鬼窃去。”贡南归，后文告之。贡南侵晨至冷泉亭，大呼“铁冠”数百声，杳无应者。

    　　适钱塘令王嘉会路过，贡南拦舆，口诉原委。王疑其痴，大被诟辱。是夜，集家丁雄健者数人护守此符。五更，砉然有声，符已不见。旦视之，几有巨人迹，长尺许。从此，每夜群鬼毕集，撞门掷碗。贡南大骇，以五十金重索符于张氏。悬后，鬼果寂然。

    Explanation

    68

    Gongnan went to Zhang Youqian's house to request a talisman. Zhang said, "I have two talismans, one cheap and one expensive. The cheaper one, when hung, can provide protection within a day or night; the more expensive one, when hung, may appear as a deity and attract strange phenomena." Gongnan took the cheaper one and returned home, hanging it in the hall. That night, it was indeed safe.

    Three days later, another old Taoist priest, with a strange appearance, came to knock on the door. Just as Gongnan was away, his younger son Houwen went out to see. The Taoist priest said, "Your family has been troubled by a certain Taoist, who is my disciple. You seek salvation through the talisman, but it would be better to seek salvation through me. Tell your father to come to Cold Spring Pavilion on West Lake tomorrow and shout 'Iron Crown' three times. I will come then. Otherwise, the talisman will be stolen by ghosts." Gongnan returned, and Houwen told him. The next morning, Gongnan went to Cold Spring Pavilion on West Lake and shouted "Iron Crown" hundreds of times, but there was no response.

    Just as the magistrate of Qiantang, Wang Jiawei, passed by, Gongnan stopped his carriage and explained the situation. Wang Jiawei suspected him of being foolish and severely insulted him. That night, Gongnan gathered several strong household servants to guard the talisman. At the fifth watch, there was a sudden, sharp sound, and the talisman was no longer there. In the morning, they saw footprints of a giant, about a foot long. From then on, every night, all the ghosts gathered, knocking on the door and throwing bowls. Gongnan was greatly frightened and paid fifty gold pieces to Zhang Youqian for the more expensive talisman. After hanging it, the ghosts indeed became silent.

    69

    一日，王怒其长男后曾，将杖之。后曾逃，三日不归。余姊泣不已。贡南亲自寻求，见后曾徨于河，将溺焉，急拉上肩舆，其重倍他日。到家，两眼瞪视，语喃喃不可辨。卧席下，忽惊呼曰：“要审！要审我即去。”贡南曰：“儿何去？

    　　我当偕去。“后曾起，具衣冠，跪符下，贡南与俱。贡南无所见，后曾见一神上坐，眉间三目，金面红须，旁跪者皆渺小丈夫。神曰：”王某阳寿未终，尔何得以其有畏惧之心便惑之以死？“又曰：”尔等五方小吏，不受上清敕令，乃为妖道奴仆耶！“各谢罪，神予杖三十，鬼啾啾乞哀。视其臀，作青泥色。事毕，以靴脚踢后曾，如梦之初醒，汗浃于背。嗣后，家亦安宁。

    Explanation

    69

    One day, the king was angry at his eldest son Hou Zeng and was about to beat him. Hou Zeng fled and did not return for three days. My elder sister wept continuously. Gongnan personally searched for him and saw Hou Zeng wandering aimlessly by the river, about to drown. He quickly pulled him onto a sedan chair, which was much heavier than usual. When they arrived home, Hou Zeng's eyes were wide open, and he spoke incoherently. Lying under the mat, he suddenly shouted, "I must be questioned! If I must be questioned, I will leave." Gongnan said, "What are you going to do? I will go with you." Hou Zeng stood up, dressed in formal attire, and knelt in front of the tablet. Gongnan went with him. Gongnan saw nothing, but Hou Zeng saw a god sitting above, with three eyes between the eyebrows, a golden face, and a red beard. Those kneeling beside the god were all small men. The god said, "Mr. Wang's earthly life has not ended. Why do you deceive him with fear to make him die?" He also said, "You five minor officials, do not obey the orders of the Supreme Clarity. Are you servants of evil Taoists?" Each one apologized. The god gave them thirty strokes, and the ghosts cried out in distress. Looking at their buttocks, they were of a greenish mud color. After the matter was concluded, the god kicked Hou Zeng with his boot, as if he had just woken from a dream. Sweat drenched his back. Thereafter, the family also became peaceful.

    70

    ○尸行诉冤常州西乡有顾姓者，日暮郊行，借宿古庙。庙僧曰：“今晚为某家送殓，生徒尽行，庙中无人，君为我看庙。”顾允之，为闭庙门，吹灯卧。

    　　至三鼓，有人撞门，声甚厉。顾喝问：“何人？”外应曰：“沈定兰也。”

    　　沈定兰者，顾之旧交，已死十年之人也。顾大怖，不肯开。门外大呼曰：“尔无怖，我有事托君。若迟迟不开，我既为鬼，独不能冲门而进乎？所以唤尔开门者，正以照常行事，存故人之情耳。”顾不得已为启其钥，砉然有声，如人坠地。顾手忙眼颤，意欲举烛。忽地上又大呼曰：“我非沈定兰也。我乃东家新死李某，被奸妇毒死，故托名沈定兰，求汝伸冤。”顾曰：“我非官府，冤何能伸？”鬼曰：“尸伤可验。”问：“尸在何处？”曰：“灯至即见。但见灯，我便不能言矣。”

    Explanation

    70

    A Ghost Appeals for Justice

    In the western outskirts of Changzhou, there was a man surnamed Gu. One evening, as he was traveling through the countryside, he sought lodging in an ancient temple. The temple monk said, "Tonight we are sending a funeral procession for someone, and all the students have left, so the temple is empty. You can watch over the temple for us." Gu agreed and closed the temple door, blew out the lamp, and lay down to rest.

    At the third watch of the night, someone knocked on the door with great force. Gu shouted, "Who is there?" The person outside replied, "It is Shen Dinglan."

    Shen Dinglan was an old friend of Gu's, a man who had died ten years ago. Gu was greatly frightened and refused to open the door. The person outside shouted, "Do not be afraid. I have something to ask of you. If you delay opening the door, I am a ghost, and can I not rush in through the door? I am calling you to open the door because I want to act as usual, to maintain the feelings of old friends." Gu, having no choice, opened the lock. There was a loud sound, like a person falling to the ground. Gu's hands trembled, and he wanted to light a candle. Suddenly, the person on the ground shouted again, "I am not Shen Dinglan. I am Li, the recently deceased from the east, who was poisoned by his wife. I am using Shen Dinglan's name to ask you to seek justice for me." Gu said, "I am not an official, how can I seek justice for you?" The ghost said, "The injuries on the body can be examined." Gu asked, "Where is the body?" The ghost replied, "When the light comes, you will see. But once you see the light, I will not be able to speak anymore."

    71

    正匆遽间，外扣门者人声甚众，顾迎出，则群僧归庙，各有骇色，曰：“正诵经送尸，尸隐不见，故各自罢归。”顾告以故，同举火照尸，有七窍流血者奄然在地。次日，同报有司，为理其冤。

    　　○沭阳洪氏狱乾隆甲子，余宰沭阳。有淮安吴秀才者，馆于洪氏。洪故村民，饶于财。吴挈一妻一子，居其外舍。洪氏主人偶馔先生并其子，妻独居于室。夜二更返，妻被杀死，刀掷墙外，即先生家切菜刀也。余往验尸，见妇人颈上三创，粥流喉外，为之惨然。根究凶手，无可踪迹。洪家有奴洪安者，素以左手持物，而刀痕左重右轻，遂刑讯之。初即承认，既而诉为家主洪生某指使，为奸师母不遂，故杀之。

    　　生即吴之学徒也。及讯洪生，则又以奴曾被笞，故仇诬耳。狱未具，余调江宁。

    　　后任魏公廷会，竟坐洪安，以状上。臬司翁公藻嫌供情未确，均释之，别缉正凶。

    Explanation

    71

    In the midst of haste, there was a loud knocking on the door, and upon opening it, they found a group of monks returning to the temple, each with a look of shock on their faces. They said, "We were reciting scriptures and escorting the corpse, but the corpse disappeared, so we all returned." The reason was explained, and together they lit a fire to inspect the corpse, which was found lying motionless on the ground with blood flowing from its seven orifices. The next day, they reported the matter to the authorities.

    In the year 1744 during the reign of Emperor Qianlong, I served as the magistrate of Shuyang. There was a scholar named Wu from Huai'an who was a guest teacher in the Hong family. The Hong family was originally a farming family but was wealthy. Wu brought his wife and son to live in a separate house. One evening, the host of the Hong family happened to prepare a meal for Mr. Wu and his son, while his wife stayed alone in the room. At midnight, she returned to find her wife killed, with the knife thrown outside the wall, which was the same knife used in Wu's household for cutting vegetables. I went to examine the body and saw three wounds on the woman's neck, with blood flowing from her throat, which made me feel deeply sorrowful. Upon investigating the murderer, there were no traces to follow. In the Hong family, there was a servant named Hong An, who usually held things with his left hand, and the knife marks were heavier on the left side and lighter on the right side. He was then subjected to torture. Initially, he admitted to the crime, but later claimed that he was ordered by the master of the Hong family, Mr. Hong, to commit the murder because he failed to seduce the mistress of the household.

    The student in question was indeed Wu's disciple. When interrogating Mr. Hong, he claimed that the servant had previously been beaten, so he harbored resentment and framed him. The case was not yet resolved, and I was transferred to Jiangning.

    The subsequent governor, Wei Tinghui, ultimately blamed Hong An and submitted the case report. The provincial governor, Wang Zao, considered the evidence unreliable and released all the suspects, deciding to separately investigate the real culprit.

    72

    十二年来，未得也。

    　　丙子六月，余从弟凤仪自沭阳来，道“有洪某者，系武生员，去年病死，尸柩未出，见梦于其妻曰：某年某月奸杀吴先生妇者我也。漏网十余载，今被冤魂诉于天。明午雷来击棺，可速为我迁棺避之。其妻惊觉，方议引盾之事，而棺前失火，并骨为灰烬矣。其余草屋木器俱完好也。余方愧身为县令，妇冤不能雪，又加刑于无罪之人，深为作吏之累。然天报必迟至十年后，又不于其身而于其无知之骸骨，何耶？此等凶徒，其身已死，其鬼不灵，何以尚存精爽于梦寐而又自惜其躯壳者，何耶？

    　　○雷公被绐南丰征士赵黎村言：其祖某，为一乡豪士。明季乱时，有匪类某，武断乡曲，惯为纠钱作社之事，穷氓苦之。赵为告官，逐散其党。诸匪无所得，积怨者众。

    　　赵有膂力，群匪不敢私报，每天阴雷起，则聚其妻孥，具豚蹄祷曰：“何不击恶人赵某耶？”一日，赵方采花园中，见尖嘴毛人从空而下，响轰然，有硫黄气。

    Explanation

    72

    For twelve years, I have not succeeded.

    In the sixth month of the year Bing Zi, my younger brother Fengyi came from Shuyang and told me: "There was a person named Hong, who was a military student. He died of illness last year, and his coffin has not been moved out. In a dream, he appeared to his wife and said, 'I am the one who raped and killed Mr. Wu's wife in a certain year and month. I escaped punishment for over ten years, but now the wronged spirit has reported me to heaven. Tomorrow at noon, lightning will strike the coffin. Please quickly move my coffin to avoid it.' His wife was startled awake and was just discussing the matter when a fire broke out in front of the coffin, turning the bones to ashes. The rest of the thatched houses and wooden utensils were all intact. I am ashamed to be a county magistrate, as I could not clear the widow's grievance and also imposed punishment on an innocent person, deeply burdening me as an official. Yet, heaven's retribution would certainly come ten years later, and not on his body but on his unaware remains, why is that? Such villains, their bodies are dead, and their ghosts are not active, why do they still exist in dreams and care about their physical bodies, why is that?"

    ○ A man from Nanfeng, a recluse named Zhao Lichun, said: His ancestor was a powerful figure in a village. During the late Ming chaos, there was a bandit named someone who dominated the village and was accustomed to raising money and forming gangs, causing great suffering to the poor. Zhao reported him to the authorities, who dispersed his gang. The bandits had no gains and accumulated many enemies.

    Zhao had great strength, and the bandits dared not privately seek revenge. Whenever dark thunderstorms arose, they would gather their wives and children, prepare pig trotters, and pray, saying, "Why don't you strike the evil man Zhao?" One day, Zhao was picking flowers in the garden when he saw a creature with a sharp beak and fur flying down from the sky, making a loud noise and emitting a sulfur smell.

    73

    赵知雷公为匪所绐，手溺器掷之曰：“雷公！雷公！吾生五十年，从未见公之击虎，而屡见公之击牛也。欺善怕恶 ，何至于此！公能答我，虽枉死不恨。”雷噤不发声，怒目闪闪，如有惭色。又为溺所污，竟坠田中，苦吼三日。其群匪曰：“吾累雷公！吾累雷公！”为设醮超度之，始去。

    　　○鬼冒名索祭某侍卫好驰射，逐兔东直门。有翁蹲而汲水，马逸不止，挤翁于井。某大惧，急奔归家。是夜，即见此翁排闼入，骂云：“尔虽无心杀我，然见我落井，唤人救我，尚有活埋，何乃忍心潜逃，竟归家耶？”某无以答。翁即毁器坏户，作祟不已。举家跪求，为设斋醮。鬼曰：“无益也。欲我安宁，须刻木为主，写我姓名于上，每日以豚蹄享我，当作祖宗待我，方饶汝。”如其言，祟为之止。自此，过东直门，必纡道而避此井。

    Explanation

    73

    Translation

    Zhao Zhi tricked the bandits into believing that the Thunder God had been deceived. He threw a urinal at the Thunder God and said, "Thunder God! Thunder God! I have lived for fifty years, and I have never seen you strike a tiger, but I have often seen you strike a bull. How can you bully the good and fear the evil to this extent! If you can answer me, I will not regret dying unjustly." The Thunder God remained silent, his eyes flashing with anger, as if showing signs of shame. The urinal hit him, causing him to fall into the field and suffer for three days. The bandits, frustrated, exclaimed, "We have troubled the Thunder God! We have troubled the Thunder God!" They set up a ritual to help the Thunder God's soul pass on and then left.

    ○ Ghost Disguised as the Old Man Demanding Sacrifice

    A certain imperial guard enjoyed hunting and chasing rabbits near the East Straight Gate. An old man was squatting by a well, drawing water, when the guard's horse galloped out of control and pushed the old man into the well. The guard was terrified and rushed home. That night, the old man appeared at the door and shouted, "Although you did not intend to kill me, seeing me fall into the well, you did not call for help to save me, and even let me be buried alive. Why did you have the heart to escape and return home?" The guard had no reply. The old man then destroyed household items and continued to cause trouble. The entire family knelt and begged, and a ritual was set up to appease the spirit. The ghost said, "It is of no use. To make me peaceful, you must carve a wooden figure, write my name on it, and offer it to me every day with pig trotters, treating me as if I were an ancestor. Only then will I spare you." Following his words, the disturbances ceased. From then on, when passing by the East Straight Gate, they always took a detour to avoid the well.

    74

    后扈从圣驾，当过东直门，仍欲纡道走。其总管斥之曰：“倘上问汝何在，将何词以对？况青天白日，千乘马骑，何畏鬼耶？”某不得已，仍过井所，则见老翁宛然立井边，奔前牵衣骂曰：“我今日寻着汝矣！汝前年马冲我而不救，何忍心耶？”且詈且殴之。某惊遽哀恳曰：“我罪何辞，但翁已在我家受祭数年，曾面许宽我，何以又改前言？”翁更怒曰：“吾未死，何需汝祭？我虽为马所冲，失脚落井，后有过者闻我呼救，登时曳出。尔何得疑我为鬼？”某大骇，即拉翁同至其家，共观木主所书者，非其姓名。翁攘臂骂，取木主掷之，撒所供物于地。

    Explanation

    74

    After accompanying the imperial entourage, when passing through the Eastern Straight Gate, the individual still wanted to take a detour. The supervisor rebuked him, saying, "If the emperor asks where you are, what excuse will you give? Moreover, under the clear sky and bright sun, with a thousand horses and riders, why fear ghosts?" The individual had no choice but to continue on to the well, where he saw an old man standing by the well. The old man ran forward, grabbed his clothes, and scolded, "I have found you today! Last year, your horse trampled me and you did not help me, how could you be so heartless?" He continued to scold and beat the individual. The individual was shocked and pleaded, "What can I say for my sins, but the old man has been worshipped in my home for several years, and he promised to forgive me. Why did you change your mind?" The old man became even angrier, saying, "I am not dead, why do you need to worship me? Although I was trampled by a horse and fell into the well, someone later heard my cries for help and pulled me out. How could you doubt that I am a ghost?" The individual was greatly frightened and immediately took the old man to his home. They examined the wooden tablet, and it was not inscribed with the old man's name. The old man raised his arms and scolded, took the wooden tablet and threw it, and scattered the offerings on the ground.

    75

    举家惶愕，不解其故，闻空中有声大笑而去。

    　　○鬼畏人拼命介侍郎有族兄某，强悍，憎人言鬼神事。每所居，喜择其素号不祥者而居之。

    　　过山东一旅店，人言西厢有怪，介大喜，开户直入。坐至二鼓，瓦坠于梁。介骂曰：“若鬼耶，须择吾屋上所无者而掷焉，吾方畏汝。”果坠一磨石。介又骂曰：“若厉鬼耶，须能碎吾之几，吾方畏汝。”则坠一巨石，碎几之半。介大怒，骂曰：“鬼狗奴！敢碎吾之首，吾方服汝！”起立掷冠于地，昂首而待。自此，寂然无声，怪亦永断矣。

    Explanation

    75

    The whole family was filled with shock and fear, unable to comprehend the reason, as they heard a voice laughing loudly and then leaving.

    ○ Ghosts Fear Those Who Are Not Afraid of Death

    Jie, the high-ranking official, had a cousin-brother who was strong and fearless, and he hated people talking about ghostly or supernatural matters. Wherever he lived, he liked to choose places that were traditionally considered unlucky to reside in.

    One day, he passed by a tavern in Shandong, where people said there was a strange occurrence in the west wing. Jie was very pleased and opened the door, entering directly. He sat until the second drumbeat, when tiles fell from the beam. Jie shouted, "If it's a ghost, it should throw something that isn't on my roof. I will be afraid of you." Indeed, a millstone fell. Jie then shouted again, "If it's a fierce ghost, it should be able to break my table. I will be afraid of you." Then a large stone fell, breaking half of the table. Jie became very angry and shouted, "Ghost dog slave! Dare to break my head? I will finally be afraid of you!" He stood up, threw his hat on the ground, and stood tall and waited. From then on, there was complete silence, and the strange occurrences also ceased forever.

    76

    ○天壳浑天之说：天地如鸡卵，卵中之黄白未分，是混沌也；卵中之黄白既分，是开辟也。人不能游于卵壳之外。则道家三十三天之说，终属渺茫。秦中地厚，往往崩裂，全村皆陷。有冲起黑水者，有冒出烟火者，有裂而仍合者，惟所陷之人民家室，从无再出土者，亦不知何往矣。

    　　顺治三年，武威地陷。有董遇者，学炼形之术，能伏气沉海中不死。全家遭此劫。九日后，竟一身自地下起，云：“初陷时，沉沉然。一日一夜，坠至于泉。

    　　其坠下之势，似飞非飞，似晕非晕，颇为顺适，犹与家人答问。一至于泉，则家口尽溺死，董伏气入水底千余丈，乃复干燥，觉四面纯黄色。已而渐明，下视苍苍然，有天在下。细听之，人民鸡犬之声，因风而至。我意“此是天壳之外天也，得落第二层天宫固佳，即落在人家瓦上，岂不敬我为天上人耶？”因极力将身挣坠。为罡风所勒，兜卷空中，终不得下。

    Explanation

    76

    The Cosmology of the Eggshell Heaven

    According to the theory of the eggshell heaven, the universe is like a chicken egg, with the yolk and white representing the unformed and formed states of the cosmos. When the yolk and white are undifferentiated, it is chaos; when they are separated, it is the beginning of creation. Humans cannot exist outside the outer shell of the universe. Thus, the Daoist concept of the thirty-three heavens is ultimately vague and uncertain.

    In the region of Qin, the soil is thick and often cracks and collapses, swallowing entire villages. Sometimes black water rises, sometimes smoke and fire appear, and sometimes the cracks reseal. However, once people and their homes are swallowed, they never reappear, and their fate is unknown.

    In the third year of the Shunzhi era, the ground collapsed in Wuwei. There was a man named Dong Yu, who practiced techniques to control his breath and survive underwater. His entire family was affected by this disaster. Nine days later, Dong Yu emerged from the ground alone, saying, "At first, it felt heavy and sinking. It took one day and night to fall to the water. The sensation of falling was neither flying nor dizziness, but rather smooth and comfortable. I could still communicate with my family during the fall. When we reached the water, my family all drowned. I submerged myself and emerged dry from the water, feeling surrounded by a pure yellow color. Then the surroundings became clearer, revealing a sky below. I heard the sounds of people and animals carried by the wind. I thought, 'This must be the outer layer of the heavens. To fall into the second layer of heaven would be ideal, or even landing on a rooftop, where I would be revered as a heavenly being.' I tried to fall further, but strong winds kept me suspended in the air, preventing me from descending."

    77

    俄而，有古衣冠人，长二丈余，叱曰：“此两天分界处，万古神圣不破此关。汝何人，作此妄想？速趁地未合时，仍归汝世界，否则大地一合百万丈。汝能穿水，不能穿土，死矣！”语未毕，忽金光万道，自远而来，热不可耐。古衣冠者抚其背曰：“速行！速行！日轮至矣！我且避去，汝血肉之身，不走，将炽为飞灰。”董闻之悚然，即运气腾身而上。面目为水土所蚀，黑如焦炭；衣服、肌肤，粘结一片。逾月，始复人形，自称“劫外叟”。余按《淮南子》曰：温带之下，无血气之伦。日轮所近，即温带矣。

    Explanation

    77

    Soon after, an anciently dressed man, more than two zhang tall, shouted, "This is the boundary between two worlds, a place that even the ancient and revered cannot breach. Who are you, to entertain such wild thoughts? Quickly, while the ground is not yet sealed, return to your world. Otherwise, when the earth closes, it will be a hundred thousand feet thick. You can pass through water, but not through earth; you will die!" As he finished speaking, suddenly, myriad rays of golden light appeared, coming from afar, so intense that it was unbearable. The anciently dressed man patted his back and said, "Hurry! Hurry! The sun wheel is coming! I will take my leave. If you do not move, your mortal body will be consumed by the intense heat." Dong was filled with dread and immediately used his supernatural abilities to ascend. His face was eroded by water and soil, black as charcoal; his clothes and skin clung together. After a month, he regained his human form and called himself "The Man Outside the Cycle." According to the Huainanzi, "Below the temperate zone, there are no beings with blood and flesh. The region near the sun wheel is the temperate zone."

    78

    ○董贤为神康熙间，从叔祖弓韬公为西安同知，求雨终南山。山侧有古庙，中塑美少年，金貂龙衮，服饰如汉公侯。问道士何神，道士指为孙策。弓韬公以为孙策横行江东，未尝至长安。且以策才武，当有英锐之气，而神状妍媚如妇女，疑为邪神。

    　　会建修太白山龙王祠，意欲毁庙，拆其木瓦，移而用之。

    　　是夕，梦神召见，曰：“余非孙郎，乃汉大司马董圣卿也。我为王莽所害，死甚惨。上帝怜我无罪，虽居高位、蒙盛宠，而在朝未尝害一士大夫，故封我为大郎神，管此方晴雨。”弓韬公知是董贤，记《贤传》中有“美丽自喜”之语，谛视不已。神有不悦之色，曰：“汝毋为班固所欺也，固作《哀皇帝本纪》，既言帝病痿，不能生子，又安能幸我耶？此自相矛盾语也。我当日君臣相得，与帝同卧起，事实有之。

    Explanation

    78
