    During the Kang period, Dong Xian was venerated as a deity. My uncle, Gongtao Gong, served as the Deputy Magistrate of Xi'an and prayed for rain on Mount Zhongnan. Beside the mountain, there was an ancient temple with a statue of a beautiful young man dressed in luxurious robes, adorned with golden sables, resembling a Han dynasty marquis or prince. When he asked the Taoist priest about the deity, the priest identified it as Sun Ce. Gongtao Gong believed that Sun Ce, known for his reckless actions in Jiangdong, had never been to Chang'an. Moreover, considering Sun Ce's military prowess, he should have had a spirit of vigor and sharpness, but the deity's appearance was beautiful and feminine, leading him to suspect it was an evil deity.

    Coincidentally, a temple dedicated to the Dragon King of Mount Taibai was being constructed, and Gongtao Gong intended to destroy the temple, dismantle its wooden and tiled structures, and reuse them.

    That night, he dreamed that the deity summoned him and said, "I am not Sun Lang, but Dong Xian, the Grand Marshal of the Han dynasty. I was wrongfully killed by Wang Mang, and my death was very cruel. The Heavenly Deity pities me for being innocent, despite my high position and the great favor I received. I never harmed a single scholar-official while in office, so I was enshrined as the Great Lord, responsible for the weather in this region." Gongtao Gong recognized the deity as Dong Xian and recalled the phrase "beautiful and proud of oneself" from Dong Xian's biography. He continued to gaze at the deity, who showed signs of displeasure and said, "Do not be deceived by Ban Gu. In his biography of Emperor Ai, he states that the emperor suffered from paralysis and could not have children. How could he possibly favor me? This is a self-contradictory statement. In reality, we got along well as ruler and minister, and I often slept and woke up with him."

    79

    武帝时，卫、霍两将军亦有此宠，不得以安陵龙阳见比。幸臣一星，原应天象，我亦何辞？但二千年冤案，须卿为我湔雪。”言未毕，有二鬼獠牙蓝面者牵一囚至，年已老，头秃而声嘶，手捧一卷书。神指之曰：“此莽贼也，上帝以其罪恶滔天，贬入阴山，受毒蛇咀嚼久矣。今赦出，押至我所，司溷圊之事。有小过，辄以铁鞭鞭之。”弓韬公问：“囚手挟何书？”神笑曰：“此贼一生信《周礼》，虽死，犹抱持不放。受铁鞭时，犹以《周礼》护其背。”

    Explanation

    79

    During the reign of Emperor Wu, Generals Wei Qing and Huo Qubing also enjoyed such favor, but they could not be compared to the influence of Longyang Jun of Anling. A favored courtier, like a star in the sky, follows the will of heaven; what can I say about it? However, the case of two thousand years of injustice must be cleared by you. Before he finished speaking, two demons with fanged blue faces led a prisoner to him. The prisoner was old, bald, and hoarse, holding a scroll in his hands. The spirit pointed to him and said, "This is the bandit Wang Mang. Heaven, due to his heinous crimes, has banished him to the Yinshan, where he has been chewed by venomous snakes for a long time. Now, he has been pardoned and brought here to serve in the most degrading tasks. For any minor offense, he will be lashed with an iron whip." General Gongtao asked, "What book does the prisoner hold?" The spirit laughed and said, "This bandit has always believed in the Book of Rites. Even in death, he still clutches it tightly. When he is lashed, he still uses the Book of Rites to protect his back."

    80

    弓韬公就视之，果《周礼》也。上有“臣刘歆恭校”等字，不觉大笑，遂醒。

    　　次日，捐俸百金，葺其庙，祀以少牢。又梦神来谢，且曰：“蒙君修庙，甚感高义！但无人配享我，未免血食太孤。我椽史朱栩，义士也，曾收葬我尸，为莽所杀。我感其恩，奏上帝，荫其子浮，为光武皇帝大司空，君其留意。”弓韬公即塑朱公像于董公侧，而兼塑一囚为王莽状，跪阶下。嗣后祈晴雨，无不立应。

    　　○三头人康熙时，吴逆为乱，道路断绝。有湖州客张氏兄弟三人，在云南逃归，从蒙乐山之东步行十昼夜，遂迷失道，采木叶草根食之。晨行旷野，忽大风西来，如海潮江涛之声。三人惧，登高丘望之，见一黑牛，身大于象，兰单而过，草木为之披靡。

    Explanation

    80

    弓韬公走近一看，果然是《周礼》。书上写着“臣刘歆恭校”等字样，他不禁大笑起来，随即醒了过来。

    第二天，他捐出一百金，修缮了庙宇，并以少牢之礼祭祀。后来他又梦见神灵前来感谢他，说：“多谢您修缮了庙宇，非常感激您的高尚义举！但没有人与我共享祭祀，不免显得孤单。我的属官朱栩，是一位义士，曾经收殓并安葬了我的遗体，却被王莽杀害。我感念他的恩德，向天帝奏请，使他的儿子朱浮被封为光武皇帝的大司空，您也请留意此事。”弓韬公便在董公旁边塑了朱公的像，同时又塑了一个囚犯的形象，以王莽的模样，跪在台阶下。此后，每当祈祷晴雨，无不立即得到回应。

    ○三头人康熙年间，吴逆发动叛乱，导致道路中断。有三位湖州的张姓兄弟，在云南逃回家乡，从蒙乐山的东面步行了十天十夜，最终迷失了方向，只得采摘树叶和草根充饥。清晨，他们在荒野中行走，忽然刮起一阵从西边来的强风，声音如同海潮和江涛般汹涌。三兄弟感到恐惧，登上高丘远望，只见一头黑色的公牛，体型比大象还要大，它迈着大步走过，草木都被吹得东倒西歪。

    81

    暮，无投宿所，望前大树下若有屋宇者。趋之，屋甚宏敞，中一丈夫走出，身长丈馀，颈上三头。每作语，则三口齐响，清亮可辨，似中州人音。问三人何来，俱以实告。三头人曰：“汝步行迷道，得毋饥乎？”三人拜谢。随呼其妹为客煮饭，意颇殷勤。妹应声来，亦三头女子也。视张兄弟而笑语其兄曰：“此三君：其长者可长寿，其两弟虑不免于难。”张兄弟饭毕，三头丈夫折树枝与之，曰：“以此映日影而行，可当指南车也。但此去所过庙宇，可住宿，不可撞其钟鼓，须紧记之。”三人遂行。

    　　次日，入乱山中，有古庙可憩。三人坐檐下，乌鸦群飞，来啄其顶。张怒，取石子击之，误触庙中钟，铿然作声。两夜叉跳出，取其两弟，擘而食之。又将及张，忽闻风涛声，有大黑牛漓然而至，与两夜叉角斗。移时，夜叉败走，张乃脱逃。行数十日，始得归里。

    Explanation

    81

    As evening fell, they had no place to stay for the night and spotted a large tree with what appeared to be a house beneath it. They approached the tree, and the house was indeed spacious and grand. Suddenly, a man emerged, standing over ten feet tall with three heads on his neck. Whenever he spoke, all three mouths spoke simultaneously in a clear and distinguishable voice, sounding like the accent of people from Central China. He asked the three travelers where they came from, and they truthfully replied. The three-headed man said, "You are lost while walking. Are you not hungry?" The three travelers bowed and thanked him. He then called his sister to cook them a meal, showing great hospitality. His sister appeared, and she too was a three-headed woman. She looked at Zhang and his brothers and whispered to her brother, "These three men: the eldest will live a long life, but the two younger ones will not escape danger." After eating, the three-headed man broke a branch and gave it to them, saying, "Use this to reflect sunlight, and it can serve as a compass. But along the way, if you encounter temples, you can stay for the night, but do not strike their bells or drums, and remember this." The three men then continued on their journey.

    The next day, they entered a mountainous area and found an old temple to rest in. They sat under the eaves, and a flock of crows flew down and pecked at their heads. Zhang was furious and picked up a stone to hit the crows, accidentally striking the temple's bell, which made a loud sound. Two demons jumped out and took the two younger brothers, tearing them apart and eating them. They were about to attack Zhang when suddenly they heard the sound of wind and waves, and a large black ox appeared, fighting with the two demons. After some time, the demons were defeated and fled, and Zhang escaped. They traveled for several days before finally returning home.

    82

    ○水鬼帚表弟张鸿业，寓秦淮潘姓河房。夏夜如厕，漏下三鼓，人声已绝，月色大明。

    　　张爱月凭栏，闻水中砉然有声，一人头从水中出。张疑此时安得有泅水者，谛视之，眉目无有，黑身僵立，颈不能动，如木偶然。以石掷之，仍入于水。次日午后，有一男子溺死，方知现形者水鬼也，以此告同寓人。

    　　有米客因言水鬼索命之奇：客少时贩米嘉兴，过黄泥沟，因淤泥太深，故骑水牛而过。行至半沟，有黑手出泥中，拉其脚。其人将脚缩上，黑手即拉牛脚，牛不得动。客大骇，呼路人共牵牛。牛不起，乃以火灸牛尾。牛不胜痛，尽力拔泥而起腹下有敝帚紧系不解，腥秽难近。以杖击之，声啾啾然，滴下水皆黑血也。

    Explanation

    82

    Zhang Hongye, a cousin of mine, was staying in a house owned by the Pan family along the Qinhuai River. One summer night, while using the restroom, it was after the third drum strike, and the area was quiet with bright moonlight.

    Zhang loved to lean on the railing to admire the moon. He heard a sudden, sharp sound from the water, and a head emerged from it. Zhang wondered how there could be a swimmer at this time, and upon closer inspection, he saw no eyebrows or eyes, only a black, stiff figure standing upright with an immobile neck, like a piece of wood. He threw a stone at it, and it returned to the water. The next afternoon, a man was found drowned, and Zhang realized that the figure he had seen was a water ghost. He then informed the other residents of the house.

    A rice merchant then spoke of the strange tale of a water ghost seeking vengeance: When the merchant was young and trading rice in Jiaxing, he passed through a muddy area called Huangnigou. The mud was too deep, so he rode a water buffalo across. As he crossed halfway, a black hand emerged from the mud and grabbed his foot. He pulled his foot back up, and the black hand then grabbed the buffalo's foot, preventing it from moving. The merchant was greatly frightened and called out to passersby to help pull the buffalo. The buffalo could not move, so he roasted its tail with fire. The buffalo, unable to bear the pain, struggled to pull itself out of the mud. Under its belly was a tattered broom tightly tied and could not be untied, making it foul-smelling and difficult to approach. The merchant hit it with a stick, and it made a chirping sound, with the water dripping from it being all black blood.

    83

    众人用刀截帚下，取柴火焚之，臭经月才散。自此，黄泥沟不复溺人矣。米客有诗纪其事，云：“本欲牵人误扯牛，何须懊悔哭啾啾？与君一把桑柴火，暗处阴谋明处休。”

    　　○罗刹鸟雍正间，内城某为子娶媳，女家亦巨族，住沙河门外。新娘登轿，后骑从簇拥。过一古墓，有飙风从冢间出，绕花轿者数次。飞沙眯目，行人皆辟易，移时方定。顷之至婿家，轿停大厅上，嫔者揭帘扶新娘出。不料轿中复有一新娘掀帏自出，与先出者并肩立。众惊视之，衣妆彩色，无一异者，莫辨真伪。扶入内室，翁姑相顾而骇，无可奈何，且行夫妇之礼。凡参天祭祖，谒见诸亲，俱令新郎中立，两新人左右之。新郎私念娶一得双，大喜过望。夜阑，携两美同床，仆妇侍女辈各归寝室，翁姑亦就枕。

    Explanation

    83

    People used knives to cut the broom and burned it with firewood, and the smell persisted for months before dissipating. From then on, the Yellow Mud Gully no longer drowned people. A guest from the rice fields composed a poem to record the event, which said: "Originally intended to pull someone but mistakenly pulled a cow, why need to regret and cry pitifully? Give you a bundle of mulberry firewood, let the secret plots in the dark cease in the open."

    During the reign of Emperor Yongzheng, a man from the inner city married his son to a woman from a prominent family living outside the Sand River Gate. The bride boarded the sedan chair, followed by a crowd of attendants. As they passed an ancient tomb, a sudden gust of wind blew out from the tomb, circling the flower sedan chair several times. The flying sand blinded the eyes, and the passersby were all startled and moved away. After some time, the wind settled. Soon after, they arrived at the groom's house, where the sedan chair stopped in front of the main hall. The maid opened the curtain and helped the bride out. Unexpectedly, another bride emerged from the sedan chair and stood beside the first one. The crowd was shocked and looked at them, but their clothes and makeup were identical, making it impossible to tell who was the real one. They were escorted into the inner room, where the husband and wife looked at each other in astonishment, feeling helpless, and proceeded with the wedding ceremony. For all the rituals of honoring heaven, ancestral worship, and visiting relatives, the groom stood in the middle while the two brides stood on either side. The groom privately thought that he had gained two brides and was overjoyed. At night, he took the two beauties to bed, and the maids and servants returned to their rooms. The husband and wife also went to sleep.

    84

    忽闻新妇房中惨叫，披衣起，童仆妇女辈排闼入，则血淋漓满地，新郎跌卧床外，床上一新娘仰卧血泊中，其一不知何往。张灯四照，梁上栖一大鸟，色灰黑而钩喙巨爪如雪。众喧呼奋击，短兵不及。方议取弓矢长矛，鸟鼓翅作磔磔声，目光如青磷，夺门飞去。新郎昏晕在地，云：“并坐移时，正思解衣就枕，忽左边妇举袖一挥，两目睛被抉去矣，痛剧而绝，不知若何化鸟也。”再询新妇，云：“郎叫绝时，儿惊问所以，渠已作怪鸟来啄儿目，儿亦顿时昏绝。”后疗治数月，俱无恙，伉俪甚笃，而两盲比目，可悲也。

    Explanation

    84

    Suddenly, a cry of distress was heard from the bride's room. The groom hastily put on his clothes and got up, and the servants and women pushed open the door to enter. They found blood splattered all over the floor, the groom lying unconscious outside the bed, and the bride lying face-up in a pool of blood on the bed. One person was nowhere to be seen. They lit lamps to illuminate the room and saw a large bird perched on the beam. The bird was gray-black with a hooked beak and massive claws like snow. Everyone shouted and tried to attack the bird, but their short weapons were ineffective. They were discussing getting longer weapons like bows and spears when the bird flapped its wings, making a harsh sound, and its eyes glowed like phosphorus. It flew through the door and escaped.

    The groom was unconscious on the floor and said, "We were sitting together for a while, and I was thinking about undressing to go to bed. Suddenly, a woman on the left waved her sleeve, and my eyes were gouged out. I was in excruciating pain and fainted, and I don't know how I transformed into a bird." The bride was then asked again, and she said, "When the groom called out in pain, the child was shocked and asked why. He had already transformed into a strange bird and pecked at the child's eyes, and the child also fainted immediately."

    After several months of treatment, both were fine, and the couple was very devoted to each other, but they were both blind, like two fish swimming side by side. It is a sad situation.

    85

    正黄旗张君广基为予述之如此。相传墟墓间太阴，积尸之气，久化为罗刹鸟，如灰鹤而大，能变幻作祟，好食人眼，亦药叉、修罗、薜荔类也。

    　　●卷三○烈杰太子湖州乌程县前有庙，神号“烈杰太子”。相传：元末时，有勇少年纠乡兵起义，与张士诚将战死。土人哀之，为立庙。号“烈杰”者，以其勇烈而能为豪杰之意也。

    　　乾隆四十二年，邑人陈某烧香庙中，染邪自缢。其兄名正中者，刚正士也，以为庙乃神灵所栖，不应居鬼祟，往询。庙祝云：“今岁来进香者，先有二人缢死矣。”正中大怒，率家僮各持锄械入庙，毁其神像。众乡人大骇，嘈嘈然以为得罪神明，将为邻里祸，遂投牒县中，控正中狂悖。正中具诉原委，且云：“‘烈杰太子’四字，不见史传，又不见志书，明系与五通神鬼相同，非正神也。

    Explanation

    85

    The Yellow Banner official Zhang Guangji recounted to me as follows: It is said that in the desolate areas between graves and tombs, the dark moon's energy, which accumulates from the stench of decaying corpses, transforms over time into a demon bird called the "Roc." This bird is similar in size and appearance to a gray heron but larger, capable of changing forms and causing harm, with a particular taste for human eyes. It is also akin to yakshas, asuras, and banyan trees, all types of supernatural beings.

    In the thirty-first volume, there is a temple dedicated to the "Liejie Taizi" before the town of Wucheng in Huzhou Prefecture. The deity is known as "Liejie Taizi." It is said that during the late Yuan Dynasty, a brave young man rallied local soldiers to rebel and fought against Zhang Shicheng's forces, ultimately dying in battle. The local people mourned his death and built a temple in his honor. He is called "Liejie Taizi" because of his bravery and the fact that he could become a hero.

    In the 42nd year of the Qianlong Emperor's reign, a man named Chen from the county burned incense in the temple and became possessed, leading to his suicide. His brother, named Zhengzhong, was a man of integrity and believed that the temple was meant for the residence of divine spirits and should not be inhabited by malevolent forces. He went to inquire about the matter. The temple priest told him, "This year, two people who came to burn incense have already hanged themselves." Zhengzhong became furious and led his servants, each armed with farming tools and weapons, into the temple to destroy the deity's image. The local people were greatly alarmed, believing that Zhengzhong had offended the gods and would bring disaster upon their neighbors. They then submitted a petition to the county magistrate, accusing Zhengzhong of being insane. Zhengzhong detailed the circumstances and added, "The title 'Liejie Taizi' does not appear in historical records or local annals. It is clearly similar to the Wutong gods and is not a true deity."

    86

    今正中已将神像拆毁，致犯乡邻怒，情愿出资将庙修好，另立关圣神像，为乡邻祈福。“县令某嘉其词正，批准允行，销案。如是者两月，庙颇平安。

    　　忽孙姓家一女，年已将笄，染患邪病，目斜眉竖，自称烈杰太子，“被恶人拆去神像，栖身无所，须与我酒食”等语。其家进奉稍迟，则此女自批其颊，哀号痛苦。女父往正中家咎之。正中大怒，持桃枝径往女家，大呼而入，曰：“冤有头，债有主，毁汝像者我也！我在此，汝不报仇，而欺人家小儿女，索诈酒食，何烈何杰？直是无耻小人。敢不速走！”女作惊惧声曰：“红脸恶人又来矣！我去！我去！”女登时苏醒。其父乃留正中住宿其家，女遂平安。正中偶然外出，鬼祟如故。于是正中与其父谋，择里中年少者嫁之。自此怪绝，而病亦愈。

    Explanation

    86

    Zhengzhong had already destroyed the deities' statues, causing anger among the neighbors. He willingly contributed money to repair the temple and re-erect Guan Gong's deity statues, praying for blessings for the neighbors. The county magistrate praised his words as just, approved and allowed him to proceed, and closed the case. For two months, the temple was quite peaceful.

    Suddenly, a girl from the Sun family, who was already approaching the age of marriage, contracted an evil illness. Her eyes were slanted, and her eyebrows were raised, and she claimed to be the Prince Lijie, saying, "Because evil people destroyed the deity statues, I have no place to dwell. I need wine and food from you." If the offering was slightly delayed, the girl would slap her own cheeks, crying and suffering in pain. The girl's father went to Zhengzhong's home to blame him. Zhengzhong became very angry and went directly to the girl's house with a peach branch, shouting as he entered, "There is a head for grievances and a master for debts. It was me who destroyed your statues! I am here, why don't you take revenge? And instead of taking revenge, you bully innocent children, demanding wine and food. What kind of hero or nobleman are you? You are just a shameless person. Dare not leave quickly!" The girl made a sound of fear and said, "The red-faced evil person has come again! I am leaving! I am leaving!" The girl immediately woke up. Her father then invited Zhengzhong to stay at their home overnight, and the girl then became safe and sound. When Zhengzhong happened to go out, the ghostly disturbances continued as before. Then Zhengzhong and the girl's father planned, choosing a young person from the village to marry her. From then on, the strange occurrences stopped, and the illness also healed.

    87

    ○裘秀才南昌裘秀才某，夏日乘凉，裸卧社公庙，归家大病。其妻以为得罪社公，即具酒食、烧香纸，为秀才请罪。病果愈。妻命秀才往谢社公，秀才怒，反作牒呈烧向城隍庙，告社公诈渠酒食，凭势为妖。烧十日后寂然，秀才更怒，又烧催呈，并责城隍神纵属员贪赃，难享血食。是夜，梦城隍庙墙上贴一批条，云：“社公诈人酒食，有玷官箴，着革职。裘某不敬鬼神，多事好讼，发新建县责三十板。”

    　　秀才醒，心怀狐疑，以为己乃南昌县人，纵有责罚，不得在新建地方，梦未必验。

    　　未几，天雨，雷击社公庙，秀才心始忧之，不敢出门。月馀，江西巡抚阿公方入庙行香，为仇人持斧斫额，众官齐集，查拿凶人。秀才以为奇事，急行观探。

    Explanation

    87

    Qiu Xiucai of Nanchang

    One summer day, Qiu Xiucai sought relief from the heat by lying naked in the temple of the village god. Upon returning home, he fell gravely ill. His wife believed he had offended the village god and immediately prepared wine, food, incense, and paper to atone for his sins on behalf of Qiu. To her relief, his illness soon improved. She then instructed Qiu to go to the temple to thank the village god. However, Qiu was furious and instead wrote a petition and burned it at the city god temple, accusing the village god of deceiving him with alcohol and food and using his power to act like a demon. After burning the petition for ten days, there was no response, and Qiu grew even angrier. He burned another petition, demanding that the city god be held accountable for allowing his subordinates to be corrupt and thus making it difficult for him to receive offerings. That night, he dreamt that a notice was posted on the wall of the city god temple, which read: "The village god deceived people with alcohol and food, tarnishing his official conduct. He is hereby dismissed. Qiu, for not showing respect to the spirits, being meddlesome, and fond of lawsuits, is sent to Xinjian County to receive thirty strokes of the board."

    Qiu woke up and harbored doubts, thinking that as a resident of Nanchang, he would not be punished in Xinjian. He believed the dream might not be true.

    Not long after, it rained heavily, and lightning struck the village god temple. Qiu began to worry and dared not leave his house. A month later, Governor A of Jiangxi entered the temple to pay respects when he was attacked by an enemy with an axe, cutting his forehead. Officials gathered to apprehend the culprit. Qiu thought this was a strange and extraordinary event and hurried to investigate.

    88

    新建令见其神色诧异，喝问：“何人？”秀才口吃吃不能道一字，身着长衫，又无顶带。令怒，当街责三十板。毕，始称：“我是秀才，且系裘司农本家。”令亦大悔，为荐丰城县掌教。

    　　○摸龙阿太杭州少宰姚公三辰，以外科医术世其家。相传：少宰之祖半夜采药归，过西溪，醉坠于涧。以手据石，滑软有涎，旋即蠕蠕而动，惊以为蛇。少顷，负姚而上，两目如灯，照见头有须角；委地上，腾空去，始知乃龙也。两手触涎处，香数月不散；以之撮药，应手而愈。子孙相传，呼为“摸龙阿太”。又号曰“姚篮儿”，以其采药持篮故也。每愈人病，不受谢。故孙位至二品，人以为阴德之报。

    　　○水仙殿杭州学院临考，诸廪生会集明伦堂，互保应试童生，号曰“保结”。廪生程某，在家侵晨起，肃衣冠出门。行二三里，仍还家闭户坐，嚅嚅若与人语。家人怪之，不敢问。少顷又出，良久不归。明伦堂待保童生到其家问信，家人愕然。

    Explanation

    88

    The Magistrate of Xinxian County saw the scholar's expression of surprise and shouted, "Who are you?" The scholar stammered, unable to utter a single word, dressed in a scholar's gown but without the official headwear. The magistrate became angry and publicly flogged him thirty times. Afterward, the scholar finally declared, "I am a scholar, and I am related to Qiū Sīnóng, a high-ranking official." The magistrate also deeply regretted his actions and recommended him as the principal of Fengcheng County.

    In another story, the Grand Secretary Yao Sanzhen of Hangzhou was renowned for his family's tradition of surgical medical skills. It is said that one of his ancestors, while collecting herbs at midnight, fell into a ravine after becoming drunk. He touched a stone, which was slippery and slimy, and suddenly began to move slowly, startling him into thinking it was a snake. Soon, the stone lifted him up, its eyes glowing like lamps, revealing horns on its head. It then placed him on the ground and flew into the air, revealing itself to be a dragon. His hands retained a pleasant fragrance for several months after touching the stone. Using this stone to gather herbs, his medicines worked immediately. This tradition was passed down through generations, and the stone was called "Mōlóng Ātài." He was also known as "Yáo Lánér" because he carried a basket for collecting herbs. He cured people's illnesses but did not accept thanks, and his grandson eventually reached the rank of Second Rank, which people believed was a reward for his hidden virtue.

    In yet another tale, during the examination period at Hangzhou Academy, the students gathered in the Minglun Hall to mutually guarantee the candidates taking the exam, a practice known as "baojie." One of the students, Cheng, got up very early, dressed formally, and left home. He walked two or three miles and then returned home, closing the door and sitting inside, muttering as if speaking to someone. His family found this strange but dared not ask him. After a short while, he went out again and did not return for a long time. When the students from Minglun Hall came to his house to inquire about him, his family was shocked.

    89

    方惊疑问，有箍桶匠扶之而归，则衣服沾湿，面上涂抹青泥，目瞪不语。灌以姜汁，涂以朱砂，始作声，曰：“我初出门，街上有黑衣人向我拱手，我便昏迷，随之而行。其人云：”你到家收拾行李，与我同游水仙殿，何如？‘我遂拉渠到家，将随身钥匙系腰。同出涌金门，到西湖边，见水面宫殿金碧辉煌，中有数美女艳妆歌舞。黑衣人指向余曰：“此水仙殿也。在此殿看美女到与明伦堂保童生，二事孰乐？’余曰：”此间乐。‘遂挺身赴水。忽见白头翁在后喝曰：“恶鬼迷人，勿往！勿往！’谛视之，乃亡父也。黑衣人遂与亡父互相欧击。亡父几不胜矣，适箍桶匠走来，如有热风吹入水中者。黑衣人逃，水仙殿与亡父亦不见，故得回家。”

    Explanation

    89

    Just as he was startled and questioning, a barrel maker helped him back home, where he was found with his clothes soaked and his face smeared with green mud, staring blankly without speaking. He was given ginger juice and smeared with cinnabar, and then he began to speak, saying, "I just went out, and there was a black-clothed person who bowed to me on the street. I became unconscious and followed him. The person said, 'Go home and pack your belongings, and come with me to visit the Water God Palace, how about that?' I then brought him home and tied my personal keys around my waist. We left through the Surging Gold Gate and arrived at the West Lake, where I saw a palace on the water, its walls and tiles shining brilliantly, with several beautiful women dressed in splendid attire, dancing and singing. The black-clothed person pointed to me and said, 'This is the Water God Palace. Is it more enjoyable to watch beautiful women here or to preserve young scholars in the Ming Lun Hall? Which is more enjoyable?' I replied, 'It's enjoyable here.' So, I plunged into the water. Suddenly, an old man with white hair shouted behind me, 'Evil spirits are leading you astray, do not go! Do not go!' Upon closer inspection, it was my deceased father. The black-clothed person then engaged in a fight with my deceased father. My deceased father almost couldn't win, just then, the barrel maker came over, as if a hot wind blew into the water. The black-clothed person fled, the Water God Palace and my deceased father also disappeared, so I was able to return home."

    90

    家人厚谢箍桶匠，兼问所以救之之故。匠曰：“是日也，涌金门内杨姓家唤我箍桶。行过西湖，天气炎热，望见地上遗伞一柄，欲往取之遮日。至伞边，闻水中有屑索声，方知有人陷水，扶之使起。而君家相公，埋头欲沉，坚持许久，才得脱归。”其妻曰：“人乃未死之鬼也，鬼乃已死之人也。人不强鬼以为人，而鬼好强人以为鬼，何耶？”忽空中应声曰：“我亦生员读书者也。书云：”夫仁者：己欲立而立人，己欲达而达人。‘我等为鬼者，己欲溺而溺人，己欲缢而缢人，有何不可耶？“言毕，大笑而去。

    Explanation

    90

    The family gave the bucket maker a generous reward and also asked him about the reason for saving [him]. The craftsman said, "On that day, the Yang family in Yungking Gate called me to make a bucket. As I walked past West Lake, it was very hot, and I saw a single umbrella left behind on the ground. I wanted to go pick it up to use for shade. When I reached the umbrella, I heard a dragging sound in the water, and I realized someone was drowning. I pulled him out of the water. Your young master, however, buried his head and wanted to sink, and he struggled for a long time before finally being saved."

    His wife said, "A person is like a ghost that is not yet dead, and a ghost is like a person who is already dead. Why do people not try to revive a ghost, while ghosts prefer to turn people into ghosts?"

    Suddenly, a voice from the air replied, "I am also a scholar who studies. The book says, 'The benevolent person: when they wish to stand themselves, they also help others to stand; when they wish to achieve themselves, they also help others to achieve.' We ghosts, who wish to drown ourselves, drown others, and who wish to hang ourselves, hang others, what is wrong with that?" After speaking, he laughed loudly and left.

    91

    ○火烧盐船一案乾隆丁亥，镇江修城隍庙。董其事者，有严、高、吕三姓，设簿劝化。一日早雨，有妇人肩舆来，袖中出银一封交，严，曰：“此修庙银五十两，拜烦登簿。”

    　　严请姓氏府居，以便登记。妇曰：“些微小善，何必留名！烦记明银数便了。”

    　　语毕，去。高、吕二人至，严述其故，并商何以登写。吕笑曰：“登簿何为？趁此无人知觉，三人派分，似亦无害。”高曰：“善。”严以为非理，急止之。二人不听，严无奈何，去。高、吕将银对分。及工竣，此事惟严一人知之。越八年，乙未，高死；丙申，吕继亡。严未尝与人谈及。

    91
    <html>
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <title>Translation of The Case of the Burning Salt Ship</title>
</head>
<body>
    <h1>Translation of The Case of the Burning Salt Ship</h1>
    <p>In the fourth year of Qianlong (1767), the city god temple in Zhenjiang was being renovated. Those overseeing the project included three surnames: Yan, Gao, and Lv, who set up a ledger to encourage donations. One morning, it rained, and a woman arrived in a sedan chair. She handed a packet of silver to Yan, saying, "This is fifty taels of silver for the temple renovation. Please be troubled to register it."</p>
    <p>Yan asked for her name and address for the record. The woman replied, "Such a small act of kindness, why should I leave my name? Just please record the amount of silver."</p>
    <p>After she finished speaking, she left. Later, Gao and Lv arrived. Yan recounted the incident and discussed how to record it. Lv laughed and said, "Why bother to record it? While no one knows about it, let's divide the silver among the three of us. It seems harmless." Gao agreed, saying, "Good." Yan thought this was unreasonable and tried to stop them. However, they did not listen, and Yan had no choice but to leave. Gao and Lv divided the silver equally. When the work was completed, only Yan knew about this matter. Eight years later, in the year of Yi Wei (1775), Gao died; in the following year of Bing Shen (1776), Lv also passed away. Yan never mentioned this incident to anyone.</p>
</body>
</html>
    92

    戊戌春，患疾，见二差持票谓严曰：“有一妇在城隍案下告君，我等奉差拘质。”问：“告何事！”差亦不知。严与同行，到庙门外，气象严冷、不复有平日算命起课者在矣。门内两旁，旧系居人，此时所见，尽是差役班房。过仙桥，至二门，见一带枷囚叫曰：“严兄来耶！”视之，高生也。向严泣曰：“弟自乙未年辞世，迄今四载受苦，总皆阳世罪谴。眼前正在枷满，可以托生，不料又因侵蚀修庙银一案发觉，拘此审讯。”严曰：“此事已隔十数年，何以忽然发觉，想彼妇告发耶？”高曰：“非也。彼妇今年二月寿终。凡鬼，无论善恶，俱解城隍府。彼妇乃系善人，同几个行善鬼解来过堂。

    Explanation

    92

    Spring of the Earth Dog year, I fell ill and was met by two officials holding a warrant who told Yan, "A woman has accused you under the jurisdiction of the City God. We are here to arrest and bring you to trial." Yan asked, "What is she accusing me of?" The officials did not know. Yan accompanied them, and upon reaching the temple gate, the atmosphere was severe and cold, with no fortune-tellers or astrologers present as there usually were. Inside the temple, the sides where people used to live were now filled with official quarters or detention cells. Crossing the Bridge of Immortals, they reached the second gate, where Yan saw a prisoner wearing shackles calling out, "Brother Yan, is that you?" Looking closely, it was Gao, a scholar. Gao wept and said to Yan, "Brother, I died in the year of the Earth Goat and have been suffering for four years, all due to punishment in the mortal world. Now my period of wearing shackles is over, and I was about to be reborn, but unexpectedly, a case of embezzlement of temple renovation funds was discovered, and I am being detained for questioning." Yan replied, "This matter has been over ten years old. How was it suddenly discovered? Did that woman report it?" Gao said, "No, that woman passed away in February this year. All spirits, whether good or bad, are brought before the City God. That woman was a virtuous person and was brought before the City God along with several other good spirits."

    93

    城隍神戏问曰：”尔一生闻善即趋，上年本府修署，尔独惜费，何耶？‘妇曰：“鬼妇当年六月二十日送银五十两到公所，系一严姓生员接去。自觉些微小善，册上不肯留名，故尊神有所木知。’神随命瘅恶司细查原委，不觉和盘托出。因兄有劝阻之言，故拘兄来对质。”严问：“吕兄今在何处？”高叹曰：“渠生前罪重，已在无间狱中，不止为分银一事也。”语未毕，忽二差至，曰：“老爷升座矣。”严与高等随差立阶下。有二童持彩幢引一妇上殿，又牵一枷犯至，即吕也。城隍谓严曰：“善妇之银可交汝手乎？”严一一从实诉明。城隍谓判官曰：“事干修理衙署，非我擅专，宜申详东狱大帝定案，可速备文书申送。”仍令二童送妇归。

    93

    The City God playfully asked, "Throughout your life, you rush to do good deeds, but last year when our office was renovated, you alone were frugal. Why was that?" The woman replied, "The ghost woman gave fifty taels of silver to the government office on June 20th of that year, and it was received by a scholar named Yan. I felt it was a small good deed, and I did not want my name recorded in the ledger, so the revered deity did not know about it." The City God then ordered the Office of Investigating Evil to thoroughly investigate the matter, and everything was revealed. Because my brother had advised against the renovation, he was brought here to be questioned. The scholar asked, "Where is Brother Yan now?" The high official sighed, "He was condemned to the Avici Hell before death due to his heavy sins, not just because of the silver he received." Before he could finish speaking, two officials arrived and said, "The lord has convened the court." The scholar and others followed the officials and stood below the steps. Two attendants held a colorful banner and led a woman to the hall, and then they brought a prisoner wearing a wooden collar, who was Yan. The City God said to the scholar, "Can the silver given by the good woman be handed over to you?" The scholar truthfully recounted everything. The City God said to the judge, "Since this matter involves the renovation of the government office, it is not within my jurisdiction. I should submit a detailed report to the Eastern Hell Emperor for final judgment. Please prepare the documents quickly and submit them." Then he ordered two children to send the woman back home.

    94

    二差押严并高、吕二生出庙，过西门，一路见有男着女衣者，女穿男服者，有头罩盐蒲包者，有披羊、狗皮者，纷纷满目。耳闻人语曰：“乾隆三十六年仪征火烧盐船一案，凡烧死溺死者，今日业满，可以转生。”二差谓严曰：“难得大帝坐殿，我们可速投文。”已而疾走呼曰：“文书已投，可各上前听点。”严等急趋。立未定，闻殿上判曰：“所解高某，窃分善妇之银，其罪尚小，应照该城隍所拟枷责发落。吕某生前包揽词讼，坑害良民，其罪甚大，除照拟枷责外，应命火神焚毁其尸。严某君子也，阳禄未终，宜速送还阳。”

    　　严听毕惊醒，则身卧在床，家人皆已挂孝，曰：“相公已死三日矣。因心头未冷，故而相守。”严将梦中事一一言之，家人未信。后一年八月夜，吕家失火，柩果遭焚。

    Explanation

    94

    Two officials escorted Yan, Gao, and Lü out of the temple, passing through the west gate. Along the way, they saw men wearing women's clothes and women wearing men's clothes, some with salt and reed bags on their heads, and others wearing sheep or dog skins. Everywhere in sight, the scene was chaotic. They heard people say, "In the case of the burning of salt ships in Yizheng during the 36th year of Emperor Qianlong's reign, all those who died by fire or drowning have completed their karma and can be reborn today." The officials told Yan, "It is rare for the Great Emperor to sit on the throne; we can quickly submit our documents." Soon after, they hurriedly called out, "The documents have been submitted; you can all come forward to be called." Yan and the others rushed forward. Before they could stand still, they heard the deity on the throne say, "The person named Gao, who stole from a virtuous woman, has committed a relatively minor crime and should be punished according to the city god's suggestion, with shackles and public humiliation. Lü, who manipulated lawsuits and harmed good people before his death, has committed a very serious crime. In addition to the punishment of shackles and public humiliation, the fire god should be commanded to burn his body. Yan is a gentleman, and his earthly fortune has not ended; he should be quickly returned to the world of the living."

    Yan woke up after hearing everything and found himself lying in bed. All the family members were in mourning, saying, "Master has been dead for three days. Because his heart was still warm, we stayed with him." Yan recounted the events of his dream in detail, but the family members did not believe him. A year later, on a night in August, Lü's house caught fire, and his coffin was indeed burned.

    95

    ○年子盐城东北乡草堰口小关营村民孙自成妻谢氏，除夕生子，因名年子。年十八，挑鸡入城，半途有旋风一阵，将笼内鸡尽吹出，腾空飞去。年子大惊，从此回家卧病。危急中，会其母将产，举家守生，无人看护。年子昏沉，身随风荡。忽从朱门之内，坠于万丈深潭，恰无痛楚；只觉身子短小，不似平时，两目蔽涩难开，耳中所闻，仍似父母声音；以为梦中幻境，安心待之。其时孙见谢氏产儿安稳，偷暇趋视年子，则已死矣，不觉大哭。年子惊醒，不解其故。只闻母泣而数曰：“生此血泡，反将我成人长大的年子死了。”悲号不已。年子始知身已转生，恐母急坏，遂大声曰：“我即年子也，年子未死！”谢闻小儿言语，顿时惊风，数日而死。孙忧小儿无乳，哺以粥食。三月生齿，五月能履，取名“再生”，今年十六矣。此事盐城令阎公云。

    Explanation

    95

    During the Lunar New Year's Eve, a woman named Xie, the wife of Sun Zicheng, a villager from Xiaoguanying in Caoyankou, Northeastern Xianyang, gave birth to a son, whom they named Nianzi. At the age of eighteen, Nianzi carried chickens into the city, but halfway through his journey, a sudden whirlwind blew all the chickens out of their cage and into the sky. Nianzi was greatly alarmed and returned home, where he fell ill. In the midst of his critical condition, he learned that his mother was about to give birth, and the entire family was tending to her, leaving no one to care for him. Nianzi felt dizzy and his body was carried by the wind. Suddenly, he fell from a grand residence into a deep abyss, feeling no pain; he only felt his body had shrunk, unlike before, and his eyes were difficult to open. The sounds he heard were still those of his parents, leading him to believe it was a dream, and he accepted it calmly. At that time, Sun saw that Xie had given birth safely, but when he took a moment to check on Nianzi, he found that Nianzi had died, and he cried uncontrollably. Nianzi woke up, confused by what had happened. He only heard his mother crying and saying, "This newborn has caused my grown-up Nianzi to die." She wept bitterly. Nianzi then realized that he had been reborn and feared that his mother would be devastated, so he shouted, "I am Nianzi, Nianzi is not dead!" Xie, hearing the child's words, was immediately struck by a fit of shock and died after several days. Sun was worried that the child would not have enough milk, so he fed him porridge. By the third month, the child had teeth, and by the fifth month, he could walk. They named him "Regenerated" (Zai Sheng), and he is now sixteen years old. This story was told by Magistrate Yan of Yancheng.

    96

    ○狐撞钟陈公树蓍任汀漳道时，海上忽浮一钟至，大可容百石。人以为瑞，告之官，遂于城西建高楼，悬此钟焉。撞之，声闻十里外，选里中老民李某掌守此楼。亡何，海水屡啸，陈公以为金水相应，海啸者，钟声所召也。命知县用印封闭此楼，并严谕李叟：不许人再撞。

    　　有美少年常来楼中，与李闲谈，偶需食物之类，往往凭空而至。李知为狐仙，忽起贪心，跪曰：“君为仙人，何不赐我银物，徒以酒食来耶？”少年晓之曰：“财有定数，尔命穷薄，不可得也。得且有灾，将生懊悔。”李固请不已，少年笑而应曰：“诺。”少顷，见几上置大元宝一锭；嗣后，少年不至矣。李大喜，收藏衣箱中。一日邑宰路过，闻撞钟声，怒李守护不谨，召而责之，笞十五板。

    Explanation

    96

    A Fox Strikes the Bell

    During the time when Chen Gong, who was in charge of the Tingzhang region, a large bell suddenly floated to the shore from the sea. It was so big that it could hold a hundred stones. People considered it an auspicious sign and reported it to the authorities. A tall tower was then built on the west side of the city to house this bell. When struck, its sound could be heard ten miles away. An elderly man named Li from the village was selected to manage and guard this tower. Soon after, the sea roared repeatedly, and Chen Gong believed that this was due to the resonance between the metal of the bell and the water. He thought that the sea roars were caused by the bell's sound. Therefore, he ordered the county magistrate to seal the tower with an official seal and strictly warned Old Man Li: "Do not allow anyone to strike the bell again."

    A handsome young man would often come to the tower and chat with Li. Occasionally, when Li needed food, it would appear mysteriously before him. Li realized that the young man was a fox spirit. Suddenly, he developed a greedy desire and knelt down, saying, "You are a divine being; why don't you give me silver and goods? Why only offer me wine and food?" The young man explained, "Wealth has its limits, and your fate is poor. You cannot have it. If you were to get it, you would suffer misfortune and regret it." Despite this, Li continued to plead persistently. The young man laughed and replied, "Alright." Shortly after, Li saw a large ingot of silver placed on the table. After that, the young man never came again. Li was overjoyed and hid the silver in his chest. One day, the county magistrate passed by and heard the sound of the bell being struck. He was furious that Li had not guarded the tower properly and summoned him to scold him, giving him fifteen lashes.

    97

    李无以自明。归视印封，完好如故，然业已受笞，闷闷而已。未几？邑宰又过，楼上钟声乱鸣。遣役视之，并无一人。邑宰悟曰：“楼上得毋有妖乎？”李无奈何，具以实告。命取元宝视之，即其库物也。持归复所，钟不复鸣。

    　　○土地神告状洞庭山棠里徐氏，家世富饶，起造花园，不足于地。东边有土地庙，香火久废，私向寺僧买归，建造亭台。已年馀矣。一日，其妻韩氏方梳头，忽仆于地；小婢扶之，亦与俱仆。少顷婢起，取大椅置堂上，扶韩氏南向坐，大言曰：“我苏州城隍神也，奉都城隍差委，来审汝家私买土地神庙事。”语毕，婢跪启：“太湖水神参见。”又启：“棠里巡拦神参见。”韩氏一一首颔之。最后曰：“原告土地神来。”韩氏命徐家子弟奴婢：“听点名，分东西班侍立。有不听命者，持杖击之。”唤买地人姓名，即其夫也。问：“价若干？中证何人？”口音绝非平素吴音，乃燕赵间男子声。其夫惊骇伏地，愿退地基，建还原庙。

    Explanation

    97

    Li had no way to clear himself. Upon returning home, he inspected the seal on the treasure box, which was intact as before, but he had already been beaten, and he felt depressed and did nothing about it. Not long after, the magistrate passed by again, and the bell on the upper floor rang chaotically. He sent a servant to investigate, and there was no one there. The magistrate realized and said, "Is there perhaps a supernatural presence on the upper floor?" Li had no choice but to tell the truth. He ordered to fetch the treasure box to see it, and it was indeed the treasury's property. After returning it to its place, the bell no longer rang.

    ○ The Land God Files a Complaint

    The Xu family from Tangli in Dongting Mountain had been wealthy for generations and built a garden, but the land was not sufficient. On the east side, there was a land god temple whose incense and fire had been discontinued for a long time. They privately bought it back from a temple monk and built pavilions and terraces. It had been more than a year. One day, his wife, Han, was combing her hair when she suddenly fell to the ground; a little maid supported her, and she also fell to the ground. After a short while, the maid got up, took a large chair and placed it in the hall, supported Han, who sat facing south, and spoke loudly, "I am the city god of Suzhou, sent by the capital city god to examine the matter of your family's private purchase of the land god temple." After speaking, the maid knelt and reported, "The water god of Taihu Lake pays respects." She also reported, "The patrol god of Tangli pays respects." Han nodded her head in agreement. Finally, she said, "The plaintiff, the land god, comes." Han ordered the Xu family's children and servants, "Listen to the roll call, stand in rows on the east and west sides. If anyone does not obey, beat them with a stick." She called out the name of the person who bought the land, and it was his husband. She asked, "How much did it cost? Who was the witness?" The accent was definitely not the usual Wu dialect; it was the voice of a man from Yan and Zhao. His husband was terrified and fell to the ground, willing to return the land and rebuild the original temple.

    98

    韩氏素不识字，忽索纸笔判云：“人夺神地，理原不应。况土地神既老且贫，露宿年馀，殊为可怜。屡控城隍，未蒙准理，不得已，越诉都城隍。今汝即有悔心，许还庙宇，可以牲牢香火供奉之。中证某某，本应治罪，姑念所得无多，罚演戏赎罪。寺僧某，于事未发时业已身死，可毋庸议。”判毕，掷笔而卧。少顷起立，仍作女音，梳头如故。问其原委，茫然不知。其夫一一如所判而行。从此，棠里土地神香火转盛。

    Explanation

    98

    Han had never learned to read or write, but suddenly she took paper and brush and pronounced judgment: "A person has taken land belonging to a deity, which by right should not be allowed. Moreover, the land god is old and poor, having been living outdoors for over a year, truly pitiful. Having repeatedly appealed to the city god, but not having been granted approval, having no other choice, she appealed to the capital city god. If you now have a heart of repentance, you may return to the temple and be offered sacrifices and incense. The mediator,某某, should originally be punished, but out of consideration that the gain was small, is punished by performing a play to atone for the sin. The monk某, having already died before the incident occurred, does not need to be discussed." After finishing the judgment, she threw down the brush and lay down. After a short while, she stood up again, still speaking in a female voice, and combed her hair as usual. When asked about the details, she had no idea. Her husband carried out each of the judgments as pronounced. From then on, the worship of the land god in Tangli became more prosperous.

    99

    ○鄱阳湖黑鱼精鄱阳湖有黑鱼精作祟。有许客舟过，忽黑风一阵，水立数丈，上有鱼口，如臼大，向天吐浪，许客死焉。其子某誓杀鱼以报父仇。贸易数年，资颇丰，诣龙虎山，具盛礼请于天师。时天师老矣，谓许曰：“凡除怪斩妖，全仗纯气真煞。

    　　我老病且死，不能为汝用，然感汝孝心，我虽死，嘱吾子代治之。“已而，天师果死。

    　　小天师传位一年，许又往请。小天师曰：“诚然，父有遗命，我不敢忘。然此妖者，黑鱼也，据鄱阳湖五百年，神通甚大；我虽有符咒法术，亦必须有根气仙官助我，方能成事。”箧中出小铜镜；付许曰：“汝持此照人，凡一人而有三影者，速来告我。”许如其言，遍照江西，皆一人一影。密搜月余，忽照乡村杨家童子有三影，告天师。天师遣人至乡，厚赠其父母，诡言慕神童名，请到府中试其所学。童故贫家，欣然而来。

    Explanation

    99

    Black Fish Spirit of Poyang Lake

    In Poyang Lake, there was a black fish spirit causing mischief. A guest named Xu passed by in a boat when suddenly a black wind arose, and the water rose several zhang high. Above the water, a fish's mouth appeared, as large as a mortar, spitting waves toward the sky. The guest Xu died in the storm. His son swore to kill the fish to avenge his father. After trading for several years and becoming wealthy, he traveled to Mount Longhu and prepared a grand ceremony to invite the Celestial Master. At the time, the Celestial Master was old and said to Xu, "To eliminate demons and slay monsters, one must rely on pure energy and true spirit.

    I am old, sick, and about to die, and cannot help you. However, I am moved by your filial piety. Even if I die, I will instruct my son to take care of it." Soon after, the Celestial Master indeed passed away.

    A year later, the young Celestial Master took over, and Xu went to ask for help again. The young Celestial Master said, "Indeed, my father left me a command, which I dare not forget. However, this demon is a black fish that has inhabited Poyang Lake for five hundred years and possesses great supernatural power. Although I have mystical symbols, incantations, and magic, I also need the assistance of a high-ranking Taoist official with strong spiritual energy to accomplish this." From his box, he took out a small copper mirror and gave it to Xu, saying, "Hold this mirror and look at people. If you see someone with three shadows, come and tell me immediately." Xu followed his instructions and searched throughout Jiangxi, finding only people with one shadow. After secretly searching for over a month, he suddenly saw a child from the Yang family in a village with three shadows. He informed the Celestial Master. The Celestial Master sent someone to the village, gave generous gifts to the child's parents, and deceitfully claimed admiration for the child's reputation, inviting him to the temple to test his knowledge. The child, from a poor family, came happily.

    100

    天师供养数日，随携许及童子同往鄱阳湖，建坛诵咒。一日者，衣童子衮袍，剑缚背上，出其不意，直投湖中，众人大骇。其父母号哭，向天师索命。天师笑曰：“无妨也。”俄而霹雳一声，童子手提大黑鱼头，立高浪之上。天师遣人抱至舟中，衣不沾湿。湖中水，十里内皆成血色。

    　　童子归，人争问所见。童子曰：“我酣睡片时，并无所苦，但见金甲将军提鱼头放我手中，抱我立水上而已，其他我不知。”自此，鄱阳湖无黑鱼之患。或云：童子者，即总漕杨清恪公也。

    　　○鄱阳小神江西新建县张某，生二女，同日出嫁。天大风，送亲及舁轿者一时迷惑，将妹嫁其姊家，将姊嫁其妹家。成婚后一日，方知错误。两家父母以为天缘，亦各相安，无异言。

    Explanation

    100

    The Daoist master cared for the child for several days, then took him and a young attendant to Poyang Lake, where they set up an altar and recited spells. One day, he dressed the child in a ceremonial robe, tied a sword to his back, and unexpectedly threw him into the lake, causing great alarm among the onlookers. The child's parents wept and cried out to the Daoist master for his life. The master smiled and said, "There is no harm." Soon after, there was a loud thunderclap, and the child emerged holding the head of a large black fish, standing on high waves. The Daoist master had someone carry him back to the boat, and his clothes were not wet. Within ten miles of the lake, the water turned red.

    The child returned, and people eagerly asked what he had seen. He replied, "I slept soundly for a short while and felt no pain. I only saw a golden-armored general take the fish head and place it in my hand, then carry me and stand me on the water. I do not know anything else." From that day on, the Black Fish no longer caused trouble in Poyang Lake. Some say that the child was none other than the high-ranking official Yang Qingke.

    In Xinxian County, Jiangxi Province, a man named Zhang had two daughters who were married on the same day. A strong wind blew, and the attendants and bearers of the sedan chairs became confused, marrying the younger daughter to the older sister's family and the older daughter to the younger sister's family. The day after the weddings, they discovered the mistake. The parents of both families thought it was fate and accepted the situation, with no complaints.

    101

    其小妹所嫁夫金某，买货过鄱阳湖，舟中忽谓其伙伴曰：“我将作官，即日到任。”伙伴咸笑之，以为戏语。行又数里，金欣然曰：“胥役轿马都来迎我，我不可以久留。”言毕，跃入水中，死。是夕，近湖村人见一男子昂然来，立村前曰：“我鄱阳小神也，应血食汝地方，可塑像祀我。”言毕不见。村人迟疑，未为立庙。已而头痛发热，口称小神为祟。众大骇，纠钱立庙祀之。凡有祈求，神应如响。未几，小神又至曰：“岂可神明而无妃偶乎？汝等再塑立一娘娘像配我，不可缓也。”村人如其言，塑之。

    　　金家闻水死之信，捞尸殡殓，举家成服。忽一日，其妻脱衰麻，换盛服，敷脂抹粉，扬扬得意。公姑怒，责曰：“此非孀妇所宜。”曰：“我夫并未死，现在鄱阳外湖作官，差胥役夫轿迎我上任，都已在外伺候，我何为不吉服耶？”言毕，作上轿状，随瞑目矣。嗣后，鄱阳小神之名颇著，远近烧香者争赴焉。

    Explanation

    101

    A Woman with Two Heads and Four Arms

    During the reign of Emperor Ling, a woman from Luoyang was born with two heads and four arms.

    102

    ○囊囊桐城南门外章云士，性好神佛。偶过古庙，见有雕木神像，颇尊严，迎归作家堂神，奉祀甚虔。夜梦有神如所奉像，曰：“我灵钧法师也。修炼有年，蒙汝敬我，以香火祀我，倘有所求，可焚牒招我，我即于梦中相见。”章自此倍加敬信。

    　　邻有女为怪所缠。怪貌狞恶，遍体蒙茸，似毛非毛。每交媾，则下体痛楚难忍，女哀求见饶。怪曰：“我非害汝者，不过爱汝姿色耳。”女曰：“某家女比我更美，汝何不往缠之，而独苦我耶？”怪曰：“某家女正气，我不敢犯。”女子怒骂曰：“彼正气，偏我不正气乎！”怪曰：“汝某月日烧香城隍庙，路有男子方走，汝在轿帘中暗窥，见其貌美，心窃慕之，此得为正气乎？”女面赤，不能答。

    Explanation

    102

    A man named Zhang Yunshi from outside the south gate of Tongcheng had a fondness for gods and Buddhas. One day, he chanced upon an ancient temple and saw a finely carved wooden deity statue that appeared very dignified. He welcomed it home as a household deity and worshiped it with great sincerity. One night, he dreamed of a deity that resembled the one he had welcomed home, who said, "I am Ling Jun, the Master of Cultivation. I have practiced cultivation for many years. I am grateful for your reverence and worship of me with incense and fire. If you have any request, you can burn a petition to summon me, and I will appear to you in your dreams." From that day on, Zhang Yunshi became even more devoted and faithful.

    A neighbor's daughter was纠缠 by a strange entity. The entity had a fierce and ugly appearance, its body covered in a tangled mass that looked like fur but wasn't exactly fur. Every time it engaged in sexual intercourse, she experienced unbearable pain in her lower body and pleaded to be spared. The entity said, "I am not here to harm you; I am only attracted to your beauty." The girl replied, "The daughter of a certain family is even more beautiful than me. Why don't you go after her instead of tormenting me alone?" The entity responded, "The daughter of that family has a strong aura, and I dare not offend her." The girl angrily retorted, "If she has a strong aura, why don't I have one too?" The entity said, "On a certain day, you burned incense at the city god temple and saw a man walking on the road. You secretly peered at him from the curtains of your sedan chair, saw that he was handsome, and secretly admired him. Can this be considered having a strong aura?" The girl blushed and could not respond.

    The translation aims to be faithful to the original text while maintaining natural English flow. It handles idiomatic expressions, cultural allusions, and syntactic ambiguities as planned, ensuring consistency in rendering proper nouns and technical terms.

    103

    女母告章，章为求家堂神。是夜梦神曰：“此怪未知何物，宽三日限，当为查办。”过期，神果至，曰：“怪名囊囊，神通甚大，非我自往剪除不可。然鬼神力量，终需恃人而行。汝择一除日，备轿一乘，夫四名，快手四名，绳索刀斧八物，剪纸为之，悉陈于厅。汝在旁喝曰‘上轿’，曰：”抬到女家‘，更喝曰’斩！‘如此，则怪除矣。“

    　　两家如其言。临期，扶纸轿者果觉重于平日。至女家，大喝“斩”字，纸刀盘旋如风，飒飒有声。一物掷墙而过。女身霍然如释重负。家人追视之：乃一蓑衣虫，长三尺许，细脚千条，如耀丝闪闪，自腰斫为三段。烧之，臭闻数里。桐城人不解囊囊之名，後考《庶物异名疏》，方知蓑衣虫一名囊囊。

    Explanation

    103

    The mother of the girl informed Zhang, who sought help from the family's ancestral deity. That night, the deity appeared in a dream and said, "This creature is unknown, but grant me a three-day grace period, and I will investigate." After the period, the deity indeed arrived and said, "The creature is called 'Nang Nang' and has great supernatural power. I must go myself to eliminate it. However, the power of ghosts and spirits ultimately depends on human action. Choose an auspicious day for the elimination, prepare a sedan chair, four carriers, four quick-handed assistants, eight items (ropes, axes, etc.), all made of paper, and place everything in the hall. You stand by and shout, 'Get in the sedan chair,' 'Carry it to the girl's house,' and then shout, 'Cut!' If done so, the creature will be eliminated."

    Both families followed the deity's instructions. On the appointed day, those carrying the paper sedan chair indeed felt it heavier than usual. Upon reaching the girl's house, they shouted "Cut!" and the paper knife spun like the wind, making a rustling sound. An object flew over the wall. The girl suddenly felt a great weight lifted from her. The family members followed and watched. It was a long green insect, about three feet long, with thousands of fine legs, like shining silk, cut into three pieces at the waist. When burned, the smell spread for several miles. The people of Tongcheng did not understand the name 'Nang Nang.' Later, they consulted the Shuowen Jiezi and learned that the green insect is also called 'Nang Nang.'

    104

    ○两神相殴孝廉钟悟，常州人，一生行善，晚年无子，且衣食不周，意郁郁不乐。病临危，谓其妻曰：“我死慎毋置我棺中。我有不平事，将诉冥王。或有灵应，亦未可知。”随即气绝，而中心尚温，妻如其言，横尸以待。

    　　死三日後，果苏，曰：我死后到阴间，所见人民往来，与阳世一般。闻有李大王者，司赏善罚恶之事。我求人指引到他衙门，思量具诉。果到一处，宫殿巍峨，中坐尊官。我进见，自陈姓名，将生平修善不报之事一一诉知，且责神无灵。

    　　神笑曰：“汝行善行恶，我所知也；汝穷困无子，非我所知，亦非我所司。”问：“何神所司？”曰：“素大王。”我心知“李”者，“理”也：“素”者，“数”

    Explanation

    104

    Two gods were fighting against the filial scholar Zhong Wu, a native of Changzhou, who had lived a life of virtue but grew old without children and suffered from a lack of food and clothing, leading to deep sorrow and depression. On his deathbed, he told his wife, "Do not place me in the coffin after I die. I have grievances to present to the King of the Underworld. Perhaps there may be some divine response." He then passed away, but his heart remained warm. His wife followed his instructions and left his body out to wait.

    Three days later, he revived and said, "After I died, I went to the underworld, where I saw people coming and going just as they do in the mortal world. I heard of the Great King Li, who oversees the rewarding of good deeds and the punishment of evil ones. I asked someone to guide me to his hall, where I intended to present my case. Indeed, I arrived at a place with grand palaces, where a dignified official sat. I approached him, introduced myself, and recounted in detail the good deeds I had performed that went unacknowledged, and blamed the gods for their lack of responsiveness."

    The god smiled and said, "Your good and evil deeds, I know well; your poverty, lack of children, I do not know, nor is it within my jurisdiction." He asked, "Which god is in charge of this?" The god replied, "The Great King Su." I realized that "Li" means "reason," and "Su" means "fate."

    105

    也。因求神送至素王处一问。神曰：“素王尊严，非如我处无人拦门者。我正有事要与素王商办，汝可随行。”少顷，闻呼驺声，所从吏役，皆整齐严肃。

    　　行至半途，见相随有沥血者曰“受冤未报”，有嚼齿者曰“逆党未除”，有美妇人而拉丑男者曰“夫妇错配”。最后有一人衮冕玉带，状若帝王，貌伟然而衣履尽湿，曰：“我，周昭王也。我家祖宗，自后稷、公刘，积德累仁，我祖父文、武、成、康，圣贤相继，何以一传至我，而依例南征，无故为楚人溺死。幸有勇士辛游靡长臂多力，曳我尸起，归葬成周，否则徒为江鱼所吞矣。后虽有齐侯小白借端一问，亦不过虚应故事，草草完结。如此奇冤，二千年来绝无报应，望神替一查。”李王唯唯。余鬼闻之，纷纷然俱有怒色。钟方悟世事不平者，尚有许大冤抑，如我贫困，固是小事，气为之平。

    Explanation

    105

    I then asked the gods to take me to the place of the Pure King to ask a question. The god said, "The Pure King is dignified and revered, unlike my place where no one stops you at the door. I have some business to discuss with the Pure King; you can accompany me." After a short while, I heard the sound of a herald calling, and the officials and servants accompanying me were all neat and solemn.

    We walked for half the distance when I saw someone dripping blood, saying, "My wrongs have not been avenged," and someone gnashing their teeth, saying, "The traitorous faction has not been eliminated." There was also a beautiful woman dragging an ugly man, saying, "We were wrongly matched as a couple." Finally, there was a man in a ceremonial robe and jade belt, resembling a king, with a majestic appearance but his clothes and shoes were all wet. He said, "I am King Zhao of Zhou. My ancestors, starting from Houji and Gong Liu, accumulated virtue and benevolence. My grandfather, King Wen, King Wu, King Cheng, and King Kang, were all sage rulers in succession. Why did it come down to me, and I followed the precedent of leading a southern campaign, only to be drowned by the people of Chu for no reason? Fortunately, there was a brave warrior named Xin Youmi with long arms and great strength, who pulled my body up and buried me in Chengzhou. Otherwise, I would have been swallowed by the river fish. Later, although Duke Huan of Qi asked about it as a pretext, it was just a superficial response, and the matter was hastily concluded. Such a strange injustice has had no retribution for two thousand years. I hope you will look into it." King Li nodded in agreement. The other ghosts heard this and all showed angry expressions. Zhong then realized that there are still so many injustices in the world. Like my own poverty, which is trivial, and my anger subsided.

    106

    行少顷，闻途中唱道而至曰：“素王来。”李王迎上，各在舆中交谈。始而絮语，继而忿争，哓哓不可辨。再后两神下车，挥拳相殴。李渐不胜，群鬼从而助之，我亦奋身相救，终不能胜。李神怒云：“汝等从我上奏玉皇，听候处分。”

    　　随即腾云而起，二神俱不见。

    　　少顷俱下，云中有霞帔而宫装者二仙女相随来，手持金尊玉杯，传诏曰：“玉帝管三十六天事，无暇听些些小讼。今赠二神天酒一尊，共十杯。有能多饮者，便直其事。”李神大喜，自称“我量素佳。”踊跃持饮，至三杯，便捧腹欲吐。素神饮毕七杯，尚无醉色。仙女曰：“汝等勿行，且俟我复命后再行。”

    Explanation

    106

    After a short while, we heard singing in the distance, and someone announced, "The King of Purity comes." Li Wang welcomed him, and they conversed from their respective carriages. It began with idle chatter, then escalated into a bitter quarrel, the voices clamorous and indistinguishable. Soon after, the two deities descended from their carriages and began fighting with fists. Li gradually lost, and the ghosts joined in to help him, while I also tried to intervene but ultimately failed. Li's spirit angrily declared, "You all follow me to report to the Jade Emperor and await his judgment."

    They then rose into the clouds, and the two deities disappeared.

    After a short while, they both descended, accompanied by two celestial maidens dressed in霞帔 and palace attire, who held golden and jade cups. They conveyed an imperial decree: "The Jade Emperor oversees the affairs of the thirty-six heavens and has no time to listen to trivial disputes. Today, I grant these two deities a bottle of heavenly wine, containing ten cups. Whoever can drink the most will resolve the matter." Li's spirit was delighted and declared, "My capacity is always excellent." He eagerly drank, but after three cups, he clutched his stomach and was about to vomit. The other deity finished seven cups without showing any signs of intoxication. The celestial maiden said, "You all wait here, and I will return to report before we proceed."

    107

    须臾，又下，颁玉带诏曰：“理不胜数，自古皆然。观此酒量，汝等便该明晓。要知世上凡一切神鬼圣贤，英雄才子，时花美女，珠玉锦绣，名书法画，或得宠逢时，或遭凶受劫，素王掌管七分，李王掌管三分。素王因量大，故往往饮醉，颠倒乱行。我三十六天日食星陨，尚被素王把持擅权，我不能作主，而况李王乎！然毕竟李王能饮三杯，则人心天理，美恶是非，终有三分公道，直到万古千秋，绵绵不断。钟某阳数虽绝，而此中消息非到世间晓谕一番，则以后告状者愈多，故且开恩增寿一纪，放他还阳，此后永不为例。”钟听毕还魂。又十二年乃死。常语人云：“李王貌清雅，如世所塑文昌神；素王貌陋，团团浑浑，望去耳、目、口、鼻不甚分明。从者诸人，大概相似，千百人中，亦颇有美秀可爱者，其党亦不甚推尊也。”钟本名护，自此乃改名悟。

    Explanation

    107

    In a short while, the decree was issued again, this time in the form of a jade belt edict, which read: "The reasons are too numerous to count, and this has always been so from ancient times. By observing this wine capacity, you should all understand. Know that in the world, all gods, ghosts, sages, heroes, talents, beautiful flowers, fair ladies, pearls, jade, brocade, and fine calligraphy and paintings—some are favored and meet their time, while others suffer misfortune and calamity. The Yellow Emperor controls seven parts, while the Red Emperor controls three. The Yellow Emperor, due to his great capacity, often drinks to intoxication and acts in a disorderly and chaotic manner. Even the celestial events of the thirty-six heavens, such as solar eclipses and falling stars, are still controlled by the Yellow Emperor, and I cannot interfere, let alone the Red Emperor! However, the Red Emperor can drink three cups, so there will always be three parts of justice in the human heart and the natural order, good and evil, right and wrong, until the end of time, unbroken. Though my yang number has ended, the message within must be made known to the world, otherwise those who complain will increase. Therefore, I grant mercy and add a cycle of twelve years to life, allowing him to return to the world of the living, and never again as an example." Zhong heard the decree and returned to life. After another twelve years, he died. He often said to people: "The Red Emperor has a clear and elegant appearance, like the God of Literature as depicted in the world; the Yellow Emperor looks unattractive, round and indistinct, with ears, eyes, mouth, and nose not clearly visible. The followers are generally similar, but among a thousand or a hundred, there are also many who are handsome and lovable, though they are not highly respected by their faction." Zhong's original name was Hu, and from this point on, he changed his name to Wu.

    108

    ○赌钱神号迷龙李某，官缙云令，以赌博被参，然性好之，不能一日离。病危时，犹拍肘床上作呼卢声。其妻泣谏曰：“气喘劳神，何苦如是？”李曰：“赌非一人所能，我有朋类数人，在床前同掷骰盆，汝等特未之见耳。”已而气绝。忽又苏醒，伸手向家人云：“速烧纸锞，替还赌钱。”妻问：“与何人决胜？”曰：“阴司赌神号称迷龙，其门下有赌鬼数千，皆受驱使。探人将托生时，便请迷龙作一花押，纳入天灵盖中。

    Explanation

    108

    A man named Li, who served as the magistrate of Jinyun, was known for his fondness of gambling, despite being reprimanded for it. He could not go a day without gambling. When he was gravely ill, he still shook dice in his hand on the bed, making the sound of "hū lú." His wife wept and advised him, "Your breath is short and your spirit is exhausted; why endure such suffering?" Li replied, "Gambling is not something one can do alone; I have several companions who are with me, throwing dice together in front of the bed. You have simply not seen them." Soon after, he ceased breathing. Suddenly, he awoke and reached out to his family, saying, "Burn paper money quickly to repay my gambling debts." His wife asked, "Who are you competing against?" Li said, "In the underworld, there is a gambling deity known as Mí Lóng. Under his command are thousands of gambling spirits, all of whom are under his control. When a person is about to be reborn, Mí Lóng will make a seal and place it on the top of their head."

    109

    此人一落母胎，性便好赌，虽严父贤妻，万不能救。《汉书。公卿表》以博掩失侯者十余人。可见此神从古有之。或且一心贪赌，有美食而让他人食，有美妻而让他人眠，昏迷龙作祟也。但阴间赌法与世间不同，其法：聚十余鬼，同掷十三颗骰子；每子下盆，有五彩金色光者，便是全胜，群鬼以所蓄纸锞全行献上。迷龙高坐抽头，以致大富。群鬼赌败穷极，便到阳间作瘟疫，诈人酒食。汝等此时烧纸钱一万，可以放我生还。”家人信之，如其言，烧与之，而李竟瞑目长逝。或曰：渠又哄得赌本，可以放心大掷，故不返也。

    Explanation

    109

    A Person Born with a Gambling Nature

    From the moment this person was born, they had a natural inclination towards gambling. Even the strictest father and the most virtuous wife could not change this nature. According to the Book of Han, there were more than ten people who were ennobled due to their gambling skills. This shows that the gambling spirit has existed since ancient times. Some people are so obsessed with gambling that they neglect their own comfort and relationships, even letting others eat their food and sleep with their wives. This is the influence of the gambling spirit, which is a malevolent force.

    In the underworld, the gambling rules are different from those in the mortal world. The method is as follows: gather more than ten ghosts and play a game with thirteen dice. Each time a die is thrown into the pot, if it shows a five-colored golden light, that player wins. The losing ghosts must offer all their paper money to the winner. The gambler, known as the "迷龙," sits high and takes a cut, becoming very wealthy. When the ghosts lose and become extremely poor, they come to the mortal world as plagues and beg for food and drink. At this moment, you should burn one thousand paper bills to release the spirit. The family believed the spirit and followed its instructions, burning the paper bills, and the person named Li died peacefully.

    Some say that the spirit was able to collect more gambling money and could now gamble with peace of mind, so it did not return.

    110

    ○羊骨怪杭人李元，馆于沛县韩公署中，司书禀事。偶有乡亲回杭，李托带家信，命馆童调面糊封信。家童调盛碗中，李用毕，以其馀置几上。夜，闻声，以为鼠来偷食也。揭帐伺之，见灯下一小羊，高二寸许，浑身白毛，食糊尽乃去。

    　　李疑眼花，次日，特作糊待之。夜间小羊又至，因留心细观其去之所在，到窗外树下而没。次日，告知主人，发掘树下，有朽羊骨一条，骨窍内浆糊犹在。取而烧之，此后怪绝。

    　　○夜叉偷酒直隶永平府滦州河下，每年龙王造宫，有黄、白二龙从古北口拔木运来。每木百枝，一夜叉管守之。其木在水中皆直立而行，上挂一红灯为号。关外贩木商人，每年待龙发水，然后依附运行。偶失一枝，龙怒，遣夜叉寻取。风雨大作，山石皆飞。村中民造酒八缸，一夜被夜叉偷饮立尽。惧其为患，为伐一木置水中，夜始平静。此石埭令郑公首瀛为余言。郑，滦州人。

    Explanation

    110

    The Sheep Bone Ghost

    A man from Hangzhou named Li Yuanji was staying at the office of Han Gong in Pei County, where he served as a secretary. One day, a fellow townsman returned to Hangzhou, and Li entrusted him with a letter to his family. He instructed the attendant to prepare a paste to seal the letter. The attendant prepared the paste in a bowl, and after Li finished using it, he placed the remaining paste on the table. That night, he heard a rustling sound and thought it was a mouse coming to steal food. He lifted the curtain to observe and saw a small lamb under the lamp, about two inches tall, with white fur all over. It ate the paste completely and then left.

    Li suspected he was seeing things blurry. The next day, he specially made paste to wait for it. That night, the small lamb returned again. He paid close attention to where it went and saw it disappear under a tree outside the window. The next day, he informed the owner, who dug up under the tree and found a piece of rotten sheep bone. The paste was still inside the bone cavity. They took it and burned it, and afterward, the strange occurrences ceased.

    The Yaksha Who Stole Wine

    In the state of Luan, under the jurisdiction of Yongping Prefecture, directly under the jurisdiction of Zhili, there is a place called the Lower Reaches of the Luan River. Every year, the Dragon King builds a palace there, and two yellow and white dragons transport trees from the ancient northern pass. Each tree has a hundred branches, and one yaksha guards them. These trees all stand upright and move in the water, with a red lamp hung as a signal. Merchants who trade in wood from outside the pass wait for the Dragon King to release water each year and then rely on the trees to move. By chance, they lost a branch. The Dragon King was angry and sent a yaksha to search for and retrieve it. A great storm arose, and all the mountain stones flew. The villagers made wine in eight barrels, and one night, the yaksha stole and drank it all in one go. Fearing the yaksha would cause more trouble, they cut down a tree and placed it in the water, and the night became peaceful. This story was told to me by Magistrate Zheng Shouying of Shidai. Zheng is from the state of Luan.

    111

    ○披麻煞新安曹媪有孙登官，定婚某氏，将娶有日，先期扫除楼房，待新娘居，房与媪卧阁相去十步许。日向夕，媪独坐楼下，闻楼上履声橐橐，意是丫鬟，不之诘也。久而声渐厉，稍觉不类，疑是偷儿，疾趋而掩执之。起推楼门，门开，举首见一人，麻冠麻鞋，手扶桐杖，立梯上层。见媪至，返身退走。媪素有胆，不计其为人为鬼，奋前相捉。其人狂奔新房，有之声，如烟一缕而没。始悟为鬼。

    　　急下楼，欲以语人，念明日婚期已届，舍此，无从觅他室，隐忍不言。

    　　次夕，新妇入门，张灯设乐。散后，媪以前事在心，不能成寐。旦觇新妇，则已靓汝坐床，琴瑟之好甚笃。媪意大安，易宅之念渐差。然终以前事故，常不欲新妇独登楼。

    Explanation

    111

    A Woman with Two Heads and Four Arms

    During the reign of Emperor Ling, an elderly woman named Cao from Xian'an had a grandson named Dengguan. She arranged a marriage with a certain family, and the wedding date was set. In advance, she cleaned the upstairs room for the bride to live in, which was about ten steps away from her own bedroom. As the day was drawing to a close, the old woman was sitting alone downstairs when she heard heavy footsteps upstairs. She thought it was a maid and did not question it. After a while, the sound became louder, and she began to feel it was not like a maid. She suspected it was a thief and rushed upstairs to catch him. She pushed open the door and looked up to see a person wearing a hemp hat and hemp shoes, holding a paulownia cane, standing on the upper floor. Seeing the old woman come, the person turned around and ran away. The old woman was always brave and did not care whether it was a person or a ghost. She rushed forward to grab him. The person ran wildly into the new room, making a sound like smoke or mist, and disappeared like a wisp of smoke. Only then did she realize it was a ghost.

    She quickly went downstairs, wanting to tell someone, but thinking that the wedding date was tomorrow, without this place, there was nowhere else to find a room. She endured in silence.

    The next night, the bride entered, and candles were lit and music was set up. After the gathering, the old woman was still thinking about the previous incident and could not sleep. In the morning, she checked on the bride and found her already dressed and sitting on the bed, with a very harmonious and loving relationship. The old woman felt much more at ease, and her thoughts of moving to a new house gradually lessened. However, due to the previous incident, she often did not want the bride to go up to the upper floor alone.

    112

    一日者，妇欲登楼。问其故，以“如厕”对。劝其秉烛，以“熟径”辞。食顷不下，媪唤之，不应；遣小鬟持灯上楼，亦不见归；媪大惊。婢曰：“是或往厨下乎？”媪谓：“我坐梯次，未见他下来。”无可奈何，乃召婿，告以失妇状。

    　　举家大骇。婢忽在楼呼曰：“娘在是。”众亟视之，则新妇团伏一小漆椅下，四肢如有捆扎之状。扶出，白沫满口，气息奄然。以水浆灌之，逾时甫醒。问之，云：“遇一披麻人为祟。”媪乃哭曰：“咎在我。”因备述前事，且告以不言之故。时夜漏将残，不能移宅，拥妇偃息在床，婿秉烛坐，双鬟立左右。至五更，侍者睡去，婿亦劳倦。稍一交睫，觉灯前有披麻人破户入，直奔床前，以指掐妇颈三五下。婿奔前救护，披麻人耸身从窗棂中去，疾于飞鸟。呼妇不应，持火视之，气已绝矣。

    Explanation

    112

    One day, the woman wanted to ascend to the upper floor. When asked why, she replied, "To go to the toilet." When advised to carry a lamp, she declined, saying, "I know the way." After a meal's time, she did not come down. The elderly woman called out to her, but she did not respond; when a young maid was sent upstairs with a lamp, she also did not return. The elderly woman was greatly alarmed. The maid said, "Perhaps she went to the kitchen?" The elderly woman replied, "I sat on the ladder and did not see her come down." With no other option, she called her son-in-law and told him of the missing woman.

    The entire family was greatly frightened. Suddenly, the maid called from the upper floor, "The mistress is here!" Everyone rushed to look, and there they found the new wife huddled under a small lacquered chair, her limbs as if bound. They helped her out, and they saw foam filling her mouth, her breathing weak. They poured water into her mouth, and after a long time, she finally woke up. When asked, she said, "I encountered a person in mourning clothes causing trouble." The elderly woman then wept, saying, "The fault is with me." She recounted the previous events and explained why she had not spoken. It was late at night, and they could not move the house, so they held the woman down and rested on the bed, the son-in-law holding a lamp while sitting, with two maids standing on either side. By the fifth watch, the attendants fell asleep, and the son-in-law also grew tired. As he dozed off, he felt a person in mourning clothes breaking the door and entering, running directly to the bed and pinching the woman's neck several times. The son-in-law rushed forward to rescue her, but the person in mourning clothes leaped from the window, moving as swiftly as a bird. When he called out to the woman, she did not respond. Holding a torch, he saw that she had already died.

    113

    或曰：此选日家不良于术，婚期犯披麻煞故也。

    　　○瓜棚下二鬼海阳邑中刘氏女，夏日在瓜棚下刺绣。薄暮，家人铺蒲席招凉，女忽于座间顾影絮语。众怪其诞，呵之。乃大声曰：“唉！我岂若女耶？我为某村某妇，气忿缢死多年，欲得替人，故在此。”语毕大笑，举带自勒其颈。阖室尽惊，取米豆厌胜之。不退，乃哀求曰：“我女年年为他人压金线，取钱易米，家贫可怜。

    Explanation

    113

    Someone said: This astrologer was incompetent in his craft, and the wedding date violated the inauspicious period of pimashasa, thus causing the misfortune.

    ○ Two Ghosts Under a Gourd Shed

    In the county of Haiyang, a Liu family girl was embroidering under a gourd shed during the summer. Late in the afternoon, the family members laid out hemp mats to seek coolness. Suddenly, the girl began talking to herself while seated. The others found it strange and scolded her. She then loudly said, "Ah! Am I like you? I was a woman from a certain village who hanged myself in anger many years ago. I want to find a replacement, so I am here." After finishing her words, she laughed loudly and strangled herself with her own sash. The entire household was startled, and they tried to ward off the evil spirits with rice and beans. She did not back down, and instead, she pleaded pitifully, "My girl embroiders gold thread for others every year, taking money to exchange for rice. Our family is poor and pitiful."

    114

    与汝素无冤，幸相舍。不然，天师将至，我当往诉。“鬼惧曰：”吓人，吓人。

    　　虽然，我不可以虚返，当思所以送我。“众曰：”供香楮何如？“不应。曰：”加斗酒只鸡何如？“乃有喜色，且颔之。如其言，女果醒。

    　　未三日，家人方相庆，女衣袖忽又翩舞，愦语曰：“汝等如此薄待我，回想不肯干休，仍须讨替。”更作恶状，以带套颈。众察其音，不类前鬼。正惊疑间，俄闻瓜棚下纟卒々履响，仍在女口叱曰：“鬼婢！冒我姓名，来诈钱镪，辱没煞人！亟去！亟去！不然，我将讼汝于城隍神。”又劳问女家：“勿怕，此无赖鬼。

    　　我在此，他不敢为厉。“言毕，其女颊晕红潮，状若羞缩者。食顷，两鬼寂然皆退。次日，其女依旧临镜。询其事，杳然如梦。

    Explanation

    114

    I have no grudge against you, so please let us part ways. Otherwise, the Celestial Master will come, and I will go to lodge a complaint." The ghost, frightened, said, "Scare people, scare people."

    Although, I cannot return empty-handed. You must think of how to send me off." The crowd asked, "What about offering incense and paper money?" The ghost did not respond. They then said, "What about adding a pot of wine and a chicken?" The ghost then showed a pleased expression and nodded in agreement. Following their advice, the woman indeed woke up.

    After three days, the family was just celebrating when the woman's sleeves suddenly began to flutter again, and she mumbled, "You all treated me so poorly; I will not let this go, and I will still need to find a replacement." She then became more malevolent, strangling herself with a rope. The crowd noticed that her voice was unlike that of the previous ghost. Just as they were in shock, they suddenly heard the sound of footsteps under the melon trellis, and the ghost continued to scold the woman, saying, "Ghost servant! You are impersonating my name to cheat money, disgracing me! Go away! Go away! Otherwise, I will sue you at the City God." She again asked the woman's family, "Do not be afraid, this is a mischievous ghost. I am here, and he will not dare to cause trouble." After she finished speaking, the woman's cheeks flushed red, and she looked as if she were blushing and shrinking back. After a meal, the two ghosts silently retreated. The next day, the woman returned to her usual routine, looking into the mirror. When they asked her about the matter, she said it had vanished like a dream.

    115

    老人李某，海阳人。薄暮，自邑中还家，觉腰缠重物，解视无有，勉荷而归。

    　　时已月上，家人闻叩扉声，走相问安，老人瞪目无言；为设酒脯，亦不食；愈益怪之。既而，取布幅许，悬梁间，作缢状，曰：“余缢死鬼也，今与汝翁作交代。”

    　　众惊，诘以前因。曰：“余为李氏，栖泊城中。曾至某家，祟其女于瓜棚下。因其家中哀求，我亦念伊女婉弱，是以舍去，别寻替代。奔及城门，有二大人司管甚严，不敢走过。以此日日受苦，一言难尽。”众家人曰：“城门大人既然拦阻，汝今日何能复来？”乃嘻嘻笑曰：“此实大巧事。今早，乡人以粪桶寄门侧，大人者恶其臭也，两相谓曰：”昨宵雨歇，城头山色当佳，盍一凭眺乎？‘遂约伴登山去矣。余得乘间出城。遇汝翁归，附他腰带间，蒙其负荷。急于得生，故仍欲相借重耳。“

    Explanation

    115

    Old Man Li, from Haiyang, returned home late in the afternoon from the town. He felt a heavy object around his waist, but upon untying it, found nothing. Despite his struggle, he managed to carry it home.

    By the time the moon had risen, his family heard the sound of knocking on the door and went to greet him. The old man stared blankly and remained speechless; when they offered him wine and food, he refused to eat, which puzzled them even more. Soon after, he took a piece of cloth and hung it from the beam, arranging himself to look like he was hanging, and said, "I am a ghost who died by hanging. Today, I am making an exchange with your father."

    The family was shocked and asked him about the previous cause. He replied, "I used to be a person named Li, living in the city. Once, I harmed a girl under a melon trellis at a certain house. Because her family pleaded with me, I also felt for her weak daughter, so I left and found another victim. When I reached the city gate, two officials were strictly managing it, and I dared not pass through. As a result, I suffered every day, and it is hard to describe." The family asked, "If the officials at the city gate were blocking you, how could you return today?" He then laughed and said, "This is indeed a clever trick. This morning, villagers left a bucket of manure by the door. The officials hated the smell, so they said to each other, 'Last night the rain stopped, the mountain scenery must be beautiful, why not lean and look out once?' So they went up the mountain together. I took the opportunity to leave the city. I met your father on his way back and clung to his belt, relying on him to carry me. Being in such a hurry to be reborn, I still want to rely on him."

    116

    众闻其言软，似可以情动者，乃哀求曰：“翁年老，墓木已拱，你不忍于弱女，宁独甘心于秃翁？如蒙哀怜，当为延名僧修法事，令你生天人境界何如？”

    　　鬼拍手喜曰：“我前在瓜棚下，原欲挽彼作此功德，视其家贫，是以勿言。今众居士既能发大愿力，余又何求？虽然，世人惯作哄鬼伎俩，惟求居士勿忘此言。”

    　　众唯唯，鬼即作顶礼状。食顷，老人已起，索水浆饮矣。

    　　翌日，广延僧众，作七日道场，瓜棚下从此清净。

    　　○介溪坟严介溪为其妻欧阳氏卜葬，召门下风水客数十人，嘱曰：“吾富贵已极，尚何他望？只望诸君择地，生子孙能再如我者而甘心焉。”诸客唯唯。未一月，有客来云：“某山有穴，葬之，子孙贵寿，与公相埒。”介溪命群客视之。一客独曰：“若葬此，子孙虽贵，但气脉太迟，恐在六七世后耳。”俱以为然。介溪买成。开穴，中有古坟墓志，摩视之，即严氏之七世祖也。介溪大骇，急加封识。

    Explanation

    116

    The crowd heard his words as gentle, seeming to be able to move them with emotion, and pleaded in sorrow, saying, "Old man, you are so aged that the trees on your tomb have grown large. You would not be heartless to a weak girl, yet you are willing to be content with a bald old man? If you show mercy, we will invite famous monks to perform religious rites, allowing you to attain a heavenly realm. How would that be?"

    The ghost clapped his hands and happily said, "Before in the melon vine shelter, I originally wanted to persuade him to do this merit, but seeing his family was poor, I did not speak up. Now that the laypeople can make such a great vow, what more do I seek? Although, people are accustomed to deceiving ghosts, I only ask the laypeople not to forget these words."

    The crowd agreed, and the ghost immediately made a gesture of prostration. After eating, the old man had already risen and asked for water and drink.

    The next day, they widely invited monks, setting up a seven-day ritual, and the melon vine shelter has become peaceful ever since.

    ○ The Tomb of Yan Jiaxi Yan Jiaxi, for his wife Ouyang, consulted several geomancers from his entourage to choose a burial site. He instructed them, "I have already achieved the utmost wealth and status, what else do I desire? I only hope that you choose a place where my descendants can be as successful as I am." The geomancers agreed. Within a month, one guest came and said, "There is a grave site on a certain mountain, if buried there, the descendants will be noble and long-lived, equal to you." Yan Jiaxi ordered the geomancers to inspect it. One guest alone said, "If buried here, the descendants will be noble, but the energy is too slow, perhaps only in the sixth or seventh generation." They all agreed. Yan Jiaxi bought it. When the grave was opened, there was an ancient tombstone inside. Upon examination, it was found to be the tombstone of Yan's seventh ancestor. Yan Jiaxi was greatly alarmed and quickly sealed it.

    117

    然自此严氏大衰，且籍没矣。此事严后裔名秉琏者所言。

    　　○李半仙甘肃参将李璇，自称“李半仙”，能视人一物便知休咎。彭芸楣少詹与沈云椒翰林同往占卦。彭指一砚问之，曰：“石质厚重，形有八角，此八座像也，惜是文房之需，非封疆之料。”沈将所挂手巾问之，曰：“绢素清白，自是玉堂高品，惜边幅小耳。”正笑语间，云南同知某亦来占卜，取烟管问之。曰：“管有三截，镶合而成，居官有三起三倒，然否？”曰：“然。”曰：“君此后为人亦须改过，不可再如烟管。”问：“何故？”曰：“烟管是最势利之物，用得着他，浑身火热；用不着他，顷刻冰冷。”其人大笑，惭沮而去。逾三年，彭学差任满回京，李亦入都引见。彭故意再取烟管问之，曰：“君又放学差矣。”问：“何故？”曰：“烟，非吃得饱之物；学院试差，非做得富之官。且烟管终日替人呼吸，督学终年为寒士吹嘘。将必复任。”已而果然。

    Explanation

    117

    Translation:

    From this point, the Yan family began to decline, and their property was confiscated. This account was relayed by a descendant of the Yan family named Binlian.

    ○ Li Banxian, the Gansu Canshang Li Xuan, self-proclaimed himself as "Li Banxian," claiming he could see a person's object and know their fate. Peng Yunmei, the Shaojian, and Shen Yunjiao, the Hanlin, went together to consult him on divination. Peng pointed to an inkstone and asked, "The stone is thick and heavy, shaped like an octagon, this is like an octagonal seat, but it is suitable for the study, not for a high official." Shen then asked about the handkerchief he was wearing, saying, "The silk is pure and white, it is naturally of high quality in the imperial library, but it is too small." Just as they were laughing and chatting, a certain Tongzhizhi from Yunnan also came to consult, taking a pipe to ask. He said, "The pipe has three sections, joined together, holding office involves three rises and three falls, is that so?" "Yes," Li replied. "You must reform after this, and not be like the pipe." "Why?" the man asked. "The pipe is the most fickle thing, hot when used, cold when not." The man laughed and left in shame. Three years later, Peng returned to the capital after his term as a provincial examiner ended, and Li also entered the capital for an audience. Peng deliberately took the pipe again to ask, "You will be a provincial examiner again." "Why?" Peng asked. "Smoke is not something to eat; being a provincial examiner is not a rich position. And the pipe breathes for others all day, just as the provincial examiner praises poor scholars all year. You will certainly be reappointed." And indeed, it turned out that way.

    118

    ○李香君荐卷吾友杨潮观，字宏度，无锡人，以孝廉授河南固始县知县。乾隆壬申乡试，杨为同考官。阅卷毕，将发榜矣，搜落卷为加批焉，倦而假寐。梦有女子年三十许，淡妆，面目疏秀，短身，青绀裙，乌巾束额，如江南人仪态，揭帐低语曰：“拜托使君，‘桂花香’一卷，千万留心相助。”杨惊醒，告同考官，皆笑曰：“此噩梦也，焉有榜将发而可以荐卷者乎？”杨亦以为然。

    Explanation

    118

    My friend Yang Chao Guan, style name Hongdu, is from Wuxi. He was appointed as the magistrate of Gushi County in Henan through the recommendation system of filial piety and integrity. During the provincial examination of the Ren Shen year during the Qianlong era, Yang served as a co-examiner. After reviewing the papers, he was about to post the list of successful candidates when he reviewed the failed candidates' papers and added comments. Tired, he took a nap. In his dream, a woman in her thirties appeared, lightly made up, with a refined and delicate appearance, a short stature, wearing a dark blue skirt and a black scarf tied around her forehead, embodying the grace of a person from Jiangnan. She gently pulled back the curtain and whispered, "I beg you to help with the paper titled 'Osmanthus Fragrance,' please pay special attention to it." Yang woke up in shock and told the other examiners about his dream. They all laughed and said, "This is a bad dream.
