How can there be a situation where the list is about to be posted and one can still recommend papers?" Yang also agreed with them.

    119

    偶阅一落卷，表联有“杏花时节桂花香”之句，盖壬申二月表，题即《谢开科事》也。杨大惊，加意翻阅。表颇华赡，五策尤详明，真饱学者也以时艺不甚佳，故置之孙山外。杨既感梦兆，又难直告主司，欲荐未荐，方徘徊间，适正主试钱少司农东麓先生嫌进呈策通场未得佳者，命各房搜索。杨喜，即以“桂花香”

    　　卷荐上。钱公如得至宝，取中八十三名。拆卷填榜，乃商丘老贡生侯元标，其祖侯朝宗也。方疑女子来托者，即李香君。杨自以得见香君，夸于人前，以为奇事。

    　　○道士取葫芦秀水祝宣臣，名维诰，余戊午同年也。其尊人某，饶于财。一日，有长髯道士叩门求见，主人问：“法师何为来？”曰：“我有一友，现住君家，故来相访。”

    Explanation

    119

    Once upon a time, I chanced upon a discarded scroll. The memorial contained a couplet that read, "杏花时节桂花香" (The fragrance of osmanthus in the season of apricot blossoms). This was a memorial written in the second month of the year Ren Shen, and its subject was "谢开科事" (the opening of the imperial examinations). Yang was greatly surprised and carefully reviewed the scroll. The memorial was quite elegant and rich, and the five strategies were especially detailed and clear. Truly, it was written by a well-read scholar. However, because his contemporary works were not very good, he was placed outside the list of successful candidates. Yang felt a sense of foreboding from a dream and found it difficult to directly inform the chief examiner. He wanted to recommend the candidate but hesitated, and while he was in a state of indecision, just as the chief examiner was in charge, Mr. Qian, the junior minister of agriculture from Donglu, was dissatisfied because no good essays had been submitted throughout the examination. He ordered each room to search. Yang was pleased and immediately recommended the scroll with the phrase "桂花香." Mr. Qian regarded it as a treasure and selected eighty-three candidates. When the scroll was unsealed and the list was filled, it turned out to be the old scholar Hou Yuantiao from Shangqiu, whose ancestor was Hou Chaoyong. Yang then suspected that the woman who came to entrust him was Li Xiangjun. Yang boasted in front of others that he had met Li Xiangjun, considering it a strange event.

    ○ A Taoist priest named Xiu Shui ZHU Xuan Chen, whose name was Wei Gao, was my contemporary from the year Wu Wu. His father was well-off. One day, a long-bearded Taoist priest knocked on the door and asked to be received. The master of the house asked, "Why have you come, Master?" The priest replied, "I have a friend who is currently staying at your house, so I have come to visit him."

    120

    祝曰：“此间并无道人，谁为君友？”道士曰：“现在观稼书房之第三间，如不信，烦主人同往寻之。”

    　　祝与同往，则书房挂吕纯阳像。道士指笑曰：“此吾师兄也，偷我葫芦，久不见还，故我来索债。”言毕，伸手向画上作取状。吕仙亦笑，以葫芦掷还之。

    　　主人视画上，果无葫芦矣。大惊，问：“取葫芦何用？”道士曰：“此间一府四县，夏间将有大疫，鸡犬不留。我取葫芦炼仙丹，救此方人。能行善者，以千金买药备用，不特自活，兼可救世，立大功德。”因出囊中药数丸示主人，芬芳扑鼻，且曰：“今年八月中秋月色大明时，我仍来汝家，可设瓜果待我。此间人民，恐少一半矣。”祝心动，曰：“如弟子者可行功德乎？”曰：“可。”乃命家僮以千金与之。道士束负腰间，如匹布然，不觉其重。留药十丸，拱手别去。祝举家敬若神明，早晚礼拜。

    Explanation

    120

    Zhu said, "There are no Taoists here, who is your friend?" The Taoist replied, "In the third room of the Gua Jia Study, if you don't believe me, please have the master come with you to find it."

    Zhu went with the master, and upon entering the study, they saw a painting of Lü Chunyang. The Taoist pointed and laughed, saying, "This is my senior brother, who stole my gourd and has not returned it for a long time, so I have come to collect the debt." After speaking, he extended his hand towards the painting as if to take it. Lü Xian also laughed and threw the gourd back to him.

    The master looked at the painting and indeed saw no gourd there. He was greatly surprised and asked, "What is the use of taking the gourd?" The Taoist said, "In this prefecture and its four counties, there will be a great epidemic in the summer, not even chickens and dogs will be spared. I take the gourd to refine elixirs and save the people of this area. Those who can do good deeds should buy the medicine with a thousand gold coins to use as needed, not only to save themselves but also to save the world and establish great merit." Then he took out several pills from his bag and showed them to the master, which were fragrant and filled the nose. He added, "When the moon is very bright during the Mid-Autumn Festival this year, I will still come to your house. You can set up melons and fruits to welcome me. Perhaps half of the people here will be lost." Zhu was moved and asked, "Can someone like me perform good deeds?" The Taoist replied, "Yes." Then he ordered the servant to give him a thousand gold coins. The Taoist tied the gourd to his waist, as if it were a piece of cloth, and did not feel its weight. He left ten pills and bowed before departing. Zhu's whole family revered him as a god and worshiped him morning and evening.

    121

    是年，夏间无疫，中秋无月，且风雨交加，道士亦杳不至。

    　　○火焚人不当水死泾县叶某，与人贸易安庆。江行遇风，同船十馀人半溺死矣，独叶坠水中，见红袍人抱而起之，因以得免。自以为获神人之助，后必大贵。亡何，家居不戒于火，竟烧死。

    　　○城隍杀鬼不许为{渐耳}台州朱始女，已嫁矣，夫外出为贾。忽一日，灯下见赤脚人，披红布袍，貌丑恶，来与亵狎，且云：“娶汝为妻。”妇力不能拒，因之痴迷，日渐黄瘦。当怪未来时，言笑如常；来，则有风肃然。他人不见，惟妇见之。

    　　妇姊夫袁承栋，素有拳勇，妇父母将女匿袁家。数日，怪不来。月余，踪迹而至。曰：“汝乃藏此处乎！累我各处寻觅。及访知汝在此处，我要来，又隔一桥。桥神持棒打我，我不能过。昨日将身坐在担粪者周四桶中，才能过来。此后汝虽藏石柜中，吾能取汝。”

    Explanation

    121

    In that year, there was no epidemic in the summer, the Mid-Autumn Festival saw no moon, and there was a violent storm with wind and rain, and the Taoist priest did not arrive either.

    ○ A man from Jing County, Ye, who traded in Anqing, encountered a storm while traveling by river. More than half of the people on the boat drowned, but Ye fell into the water and was saved by a figure in a red robe, who lifted him up, thus sparing him. He believed that he had received divine assistance and would surely achieve great wealth or status. Not long after, at home, he failed to be cautious about fire and was burned to death.

    ○ The city god killed a ghost and prevented it from becoming a {渐耳}. A woman from Taizhou, who had already married, had her husband away trading. One day, while lighting a lamp, she saw a barefoot figure in a red cloth robe, which was ugly and tried to molest her, saying, "I will marry you." The woman could not resist and became entranced, gradually turning pale and thin. When the figure was not present, she behaved normally; when it appeared, there was a sudden, eerie presence. Others could not see it, only the woman could.

    Her brother-in-law, Yuan Chengdong, who was known for his martial prowess, hid her in his house. After several days, the figure did not come. After a month, it tracked her down and said, "Are you hiding here! It has made me search everywhere. After finding out you are here, I want to come, but there is a bridge. The bridge god beat me with a stick, and I could not cross. Yesterday, I sat in the four buckets carried by the man named Zhou who carries manure, and only then could I come over. Even if you hide in a stone cabinet, I can still get you."

    122

    袁与妇商量持刀斫之，妇指怪在西则西斫，指怪在东则东斫。一日，妇喜拍手曰：“斫中此怪额角矣。”果数日不至。已而布缠其额，仍来为祟。袁发鸟枪击之，怪善于闪躲，屡击不中。一日，妇又喜曰：“中怪臂矣。”果数日不来。

    　　已而布缠其臂又来，入门骂曰：“汝如此无情，吾将索汝性命。”殴撞此妇，满身青肿，哀号欲绝。

    　　女父与袁连名作状焚城隍庙。是夜，女梦有青衣二人持牌唤妇听审，且索差钱曰：“此场官司，我包汝必胜，可烧锡锞二千谢我。你莫赚多，阴间只算九七银二十两。此项非我独享，将替你为铺堂之用，凭汝叔绍先一同分散，他日可见个分明。”绍先者，朱家已死之族叔也。如其言，烧与之。五更，女醒，曰：“事已审明，此怪是东埠头轿夫，名马大。城隍怒其生前作恶，死尚如此，用大杖打四十，戴长枷在庙前示众。”从此，妇果康健，合家欢喜。

    Explanation

    122

    Mr. Yuan discussed with his wife about using a knife to attack the ghost. The wife indicated that if the ghost was to the west, they should chop at the west, and if the ghost was to the east, they should chop at the east. One day, the wife happily clapped her hands and said, "I have hit the ghost's forehead!" Indeed, the ghost did not appear for several days. However, it soon returned with a bandage on its forehead and continued to cause trouble. Mr. Yuan then shot at the ghost with a bird gun, but the ghost was skilled at dodging and avoided being hit. One day, the wife again happily said, "I have hit the ghost's arm!" Indeed, the ghost did not appear for several days.

    Soon after, the ghost returned with a bandage on its arm and entered the house, cursing, "You are so heartless, I will take your life!" It beat and collided with the wife, leaving her covered in bruises and swelling, crying in pain, almost to the point of death.

    The girl's father and Mr. Yuan jointly wrote a petition and burned it at the city god temple. That night, the girl dreamed of two men in blue robes holding plaques, calling the wife to be tried, and demanding court fees. They said, "In this case, I guarantee you will win. You can burn two thousand silver ingots to thank me. Do not overpay, as in the afterlife, it is calculated as nine-tenths of twenty taels of silver. This amount is not for my sole benefit; it will serve as the offering for the temple hall. Your uncle Shaoxian will distribute it together with you. One day, you will see it clearly." Shaoxian was the deceased clan uncle of the Zhu family. They followed his instructions and burned the silver ingots for him. At the fifth watch, the girl woke up and said, "The matter has been clarified. This ghost is a sedan chair carrier from the eastern pier, named Ma Da. The city god was angry at his past misdeeds and his continued malevolent behavior after death, so he was beaten with a large stick forty times and publicly displayed in shackles in front of the temple." From then on, the wife indeed recovered her health, and the whole family was happy.

    123

    未三日，又痴迷如前，口称：“我是轿夫之妻张氏。汝父、汝姊夫将我夫告城隍枷责，害我忍饥独宿，我今日要为夫报仇。”以手爪掐妇眼，眼几瞎。女父与承栋无奈何，再焚一牒与城隍。是夕，女又梦鬼隶召往，怪亦在焉。城隍置所焚牒于案前，慎目厉声曰：“夫妻一般凶恶，可谓‘一床不出两样人’矣，非腰斩不可。”命两隶缚鬼持刀截之，分为两段，有黑气流出，不见肠胃，亦不见有血。旁二隶请曰：“可准押往鸦鸣国为{渐耳}否？”城隍不许，曰：“此奴作鬼便害人，若作{渐耳}必又害鬼。可扬灭恶气，以断其根。”两隶呼长须者二人，各持大扇扇其尸，顷刻化为黑烟，散尽不见。囚其妻，械手足，充发黑云山罗刹神处充当苦差。命原差送妇还阳。女惊而醒。

    Explanation

    123

    Not even three days had passed when she became obsessed again, saying, "I am Zhang, the wife of the sedan chair bearer. Your father and your brother-in-law reported my husband to the City God and had him imprisoned, forcing me to endure hunger and sleep alone. Today, I must avenge my husband." She scratched the woman's eyes with her claws, nearly blinding them. The girl's father and Chengdong could do nothing about it, so they burned another petition to the City God. That night, the girl dreamed of being summoned by ghost servants, and the strange creature was also there. The City God placed the burned petition on the table, looked sternly, and spoke in a harsh voice, "Truly, 'one bed cannot produce two different people'; this cannot be avoided but by decapitation." He ordered two servants to bind the ghost and cut it in half with a knife, dividing it into two parts. Black vapor flowed out, but there were no visible intestines or blood. The two servants beside him asked, "Can we allow it to be sent to the Land of Crows to become a {gradual ear}?" The City God refused, saying, "This slave, even as a ghost, harms people, and if made into a {gradual ear}, it will certainly harm ghosts. We can dispel the evil vapor and cut off its root." The two servants called two long-bearded men, who each held a large fan and fanned the corpse. In an instant, it turned into black smoke and dissipated. They imprisoned her wife, shackled her hands and feet, and sent her to Black Cloud Mountain to serve the demon god as a laborer. The City God ordered the original messenger to send the woman back to the living world. The girl woke up in shock.

    124

    从此，朱妇安然，仍回夫家，生二子一女，至今犹存。鬼所云“担粪周四”

    　　者，其邻也。问之，曰：“果然可疑，我某日担空桶归，压肩甚重。”

    　　●卷四○吕蒙涂脸湖北秀才钟某，唐太史赤子之表戚也。将赴秋试，梦文昌神召，跪殿下。不发一言，但呼之近前，取笔向砚上蘸极浓墨涂其脸几满。大惊而醒，虑有污卷之事，意忽忽不乐。随入场，倦，在号檐中假寐。见有伟丈夫掀其号帘，长髯绿袍，乃关帝也。骂曰：“吕蒙老贼！你道涂抹面孔，我便不认得你么！”言毕不见，钟方悟前生是吕蒙，心甚惶悚。是年，获隽。后十年，选山西解梁知县。到任三日，往谒武庙，一拜不起。家人视之，业已死矣。

    Explanation

    124

    From then on, the woman surnamed Zhu lived peacefully and returned to her husband's home. She gave birth to two sons and one daughter, who still exist to this day. The ghost mentioned "the person who carries manure on Thursday," which refers to his neighbor. When asked, the neighbor said, "Indeed, it was suspicious. On a certain day, I carried an empty bucket back, and the weight was very heavy on my shoulders."

    ● Chapter Four, Section Zero: Lu Meng's Blackened Face

    Hubei scholar Zhong, a distant relative of Tang Taishi's son, was about to take the provincial examination. He dreamed that the God of Literature summoned him, and he knelt before the throne. The god did not speak but called him forward, took a brush, dipped it in extremely thick ink from the inkstone, and nearly covered his face. He woke up in great shock, fearing that his examination papers would be stained, and felt gloomy and unhappy. After entering the examination hall, he was tired and dozed off under the canopy. He saw a tall man with a long beard and green robe lift his canopy curtain. It was Guan Yu, the God of War. Guan Yu shouted, "Old traitor Lu Meng! Do you think I won't recognize you even if you blacken your face?" After saying this, he disappeared. Zhong realized that he had been Lu Meng in a past life and was filled with dread. That year, he passed the examination. Ten years later, he was appointed as the magistrate of Jieyang in Shanxi. Three days after taking office, he went to pay respects at the Temple of War, and after one bow, he did not rise again. His family saw him and found that he had already died.

    125

    ○郑细九扬州名奴，多以细称。细九者，商人郑氏奴也。郑家主母病革，忽苏，矍然而起，曰：“事大可笑。我死何妨，不应托生于细九家为儿，以故我魂已出户，到半途得此消息，将送我者打脱而返。”言毕，道“口喝”，索青菜汤。家人煮之。咽少许，仍仆于床，瞑目而逝。须叟，郑细九来报，家中产一儿，口含菜叶，啼声甚厉。嗣后，郑氏颇加恩养，不敢以奴产子待也。

    　　○替鬼做媒江浦南乡有女张氏，嫁陈某，七年而寡，日食不周，改适张姓。张亦丧妻七年，作媒者以为天缘巧合。婚甫半月，张之前夫附魂妻身曰：“汝太无良！竟不替我守节，转嫁庸奴！”以手自批其颊。张家人为烧纸钱，再三劝慰，作厉如故。

    Explanation

    125

    Translation

    V. Zhen Xiujiu, a Famous Slave from Yangzhou

    Zhen Xiujiu was a famous slave from Yangzhou, often referred to as "fine" or "delicate." He was a slave of the merchant Zheng. Zheng's master's wife was on her deathbed. She suddenly came to life, sat up in a startled manner, and said, "It's very amusing. What harm is there in my death? I should not have been reborn in the family of Zhen Xiujiu as a child. Therefore, my soul has already left the house, and halfway there, I received this message, and the person who was supposed to escort me was beaten and returned." After saying this, she said, "I am thirsty," and asked for vegetable soup. The family cooked it for her. She swallowed a little, then fell back onto the bed, closed her eyes, and passed away. A short while later, Zhen Xiujiu came to report that a child had been born at home, with a leaf in his mouth, and he cried very loudly. Thereafter, the Zheng family treated him with great kindness and did not treat him as a slave's child.

    VI. A Ghost Acting as a Matchmaker

    In the southern part of Jiangpu, there was a woman named Zhang, who married a man named Chen. After seven years, she became a widow, and her daily food was insufficient. She remarried a man surnamed Zhang. Zhang had also lost his wife seven years ago. The matchmaker believed that this was a matter of heavenly fate. Just half a month after their marriage, Zhang's previous husband's spirit attached itself to his wife's body and said, "You are too unkind! You did not remain faithful to me, and instead married a common slave!" He slapped himself across the face with his hand. Zhang's family burned paper money for him and repeatedly tried to console him, but he remained furious as before.

    126

    未几，张之前妻又附魂于其夫之身，骂曰：“汝太薄情！但知有新人，不知有旧人！”亦以手自击撞。举家惊惶。

    　　适其时原作媒者秦某在旁，戏曰：“我从前既替活人作媒，我今日何妨替死鬼作媒。陈某既在此索妻，汝又在此索夫，何不彼此交配而退；则阴间不寂寞，而两家活夫妻亦平安矣。何必在此吵闹耶？”张面作羞缩状，曰：“我亦有此意，但我貌丑，未知陈某肯要我否？我不便自言。先生既有此好意，即求先生一说，何如？”秦乃向两处通陈，俱唯唯。忽又笑曰：“此事极好，但我辈虽鬼，不可野合，为群鬼所轻。必须媒人替我剪纸人作舆从，具锣鼓音乐，摆酒席，送合欢杯，使男女二人成礼而退，我辈才去。”张家如其言，从此，两人之身安然无恙。

    Explanation

    126

    After a short while, Zhang's previous wife possessed his body again and scolded him, saying, "You are too heartless! You only know about the new one, not the old one!" She also struck herself with her hands. The entire family was frightened and alarmed.

    At that moment, the original matchmaker, Qin, was present and jokingly said, "I have previously served as a matchmaker for the living, so why can't I serve as a matchmaker for the dead today? Teng has come here to seek a wife, and you have come here to seek a husband. Why don't you both pair up and leave? Then the underworld won't be lonely, and the living couples in both families will be safe. Why are you causing such a commotion here?" Zhang's face turned red with embarrassment and he said, "I also had this idea, but I am unattractive, and I don't know if Teng would want me. I cannot speak for myself. Since you have such good intentions, please ask on my behalf. What do you think?" Qin then went to both parties and they both agreed. Suddenly, Qin laughed and said, "This is a very good idea, but even though we are ghosts, we cannot engage in wild unions, which would be looked down upon by other spirits. The matchmaker must cut out paper figures to serve as attendants, prepare drums and gongs music, set up a banquet, and present a cup of joy, so that the two of you can complete the ceremony and depart. Only then will we leave." Zhang's family followed Qin's instructions, and from then on, both of their bodies were safe and sound.

    127

    乡邻哄传某村替鬼做媒，替鬼做亲。

    　　○鬼有三技过此鬼道乃穷蔡魏公孝廉常言：“鬼有三技：一迷二遮三吓。”或问：“三技云何？”曰：我表弟吕某，松江廪生，性豪放，自号豁达先生。尝过泖湖西乡，天渐黑，见妇人面施粉黛，贸贸然持绳索而奔。望见吕，走避大树下，而所持绳则遗坠地上。

    　　吕取观，乃一条草索。嗅之，有阴霾之气。心知为缢死鬼。取藏怀中，径向前行。

    Explanation

    127

    The villagers spread rumors that a certain village was arranging a marriage for a ghost and conducting a wedding for a ghost.

    ○ Ghosts have three skills; beyond these, their path is difficult. Cai Weigong, a scholar, once said, "Ghosts have three skills: to bewilder, to obscure, and to frighten." Someone asked, "What are these three skills?" He replied: "My cousin Lu, a scholar from Songjiang, was bold and unrestrained, and called himself the Open-Minded Master. Once, he passed through the western villages of Maohu Lake, and as it grew dark, he saw a woman with makeup on her face, rushing around holding a rope. When she saw Lu, she ran away to hide under a big tree, and the rope she held dropped to the ground.

    Lu picked it up to examine it, and it was a piece of grass rope. He smelled it and found it had a gloomy, damp smell. He knew it was the ghost of someone who had hanged themselves. He put it in his bosom and continued on his way."

    128

    其女出树中，往前遮拦，左行则左拦，右行则右拦。吕心知俗所称‘鬼打墙’是也，直冲而行。鬼无奈何，长啸一声，变作披发流血状，伸舌尺许，向之跳跃。

    　　吕曰：“‘汝前之涂眉画粉，迷我也；向前阻拒，遮我也；今作此恶状，吓我也。

    　　三技毕矣，我总不怕，想无他技可施。尔亦知我素名豁达先生乎？‘鬼仍复原形跪地曰：“我城中施姓女子，与夫口角，一时短见自缢。今闻泖东某家妇亦与其夫不睦，故我往取替代。不料半路被先生截住，又将我绳夺去。我实在计穷，只求先生超生。’吕问：”作何超法？‘曰：“替我告知城中施家，作道场，请高僧，多念《往生咒》，我便可托生。’吕笑曰：”我即高僧也。我有《往生咒》，为汝一通。‘即高唱曰：“好大世界，无遮无碍。死去生来，有何替代？要走便走，岂不爽快！’鬼听毕，恍然大悟，伏地再拜，奔趋而去。”后土人云：此处向不平静，自豁达先生过后，永无为祟者。

    Explanation

    128

    From the tree, the woman emerged, blocking the way forward, obstructing to the left when he turned left, and to the right when he turned right. Lü realized that this was what the common folk called "ghost wall," and he charged straight ahead. The ghost had no choice but to let out a long howl, transforming into a ghastly figure with disheveled hair and flowing blood, extending its tongue about a foot long, and leaping towards him.

    Lü said, "Your previous attempts to paint your eyebrows and apply powder to bewilder me, to block my path and obscure me, and now this terrible appearance to frighten me—these three tricks are exhausted. I am not afraid of any of them, and I doubt you have any other skills left. Do you know that I am known as the 'Hedda Master'?" The ghost returned to its original form and knelt on the ground, saying, "I am a woman of the Shi family from the city. I hanged myself after a quarrel with my husband. Now I have heard that a woman in the eastern part of Miao is also having marital troubles, so I went to take her place. Unexpectedly, I was intercepted by you, and you also took my rope away. I am truly at a loss; I only ask you to help me transcend this life." Lü asked, "What method should I use to help you transcend?" The ghost replied, "Tell the Shi family in the city to hold a ritual, invite a high monk, and recite the 'Rebirth Mantra' many times, and I will be able to be reborn." Lü laughed and said, "I am the high monk myself. I have the 'Rebirth Mantra' and will recite it for you." He then chanted, "What a vast world, with no obstructions or hindrances. Death and rebirth, what need for substitutes? If you wish to go, go; isn't that delightful!" The ghost, upon hearing this, suddenly realized the truth, bowed down again, and rushed away. Later, the locals said that this place had never been peaceful until after the 'Hedda Master' passed through, and it would never be plagued by ghosts again.

    129

    ○鬼多变苍蝇徽州状元戴有祺，与友夜醉，玩月出城，步回龙桥上。有蓝衣人持伞从西乡来，见戴公，欲前不前。疑为窃贼，直前擒问。曰：“我差役也，奉本官拘人。”

    　　戴曰：“汝太说谎。世上只有城里差人向地外拘人者，断无城外差人向城里拘人之理！”蓝衣者不得已，跪曰：“我非人，乃鬼也，奉阴官命，就城里拘人是实。”

    Explanation

    129

    Translation:

    ○ A ghost transformed into a fly and approached Dai Youqi, the zhuangyuan from Huizhou, who was out with a friend, enjoying the moon and walking on the Bridge of the Dragon Returning. A man in blue clothing, holding an umbrella, came from the west and saw Dai Youqi, hesitating to approach. Suspecting him of being a thief, Dai Youqi grabbed and questioned him. The man said, "I am a servant, serving the official's order to arrest someone."

    Dai Youqi replied, "You are too much of a liar. In the world, there are only city officials who go outside to arrest people, not officials from outside the city coming into the city to arrest people!" The man in blue clothing, unable to deny it, knelt down and said, "I am not human, but a ghost. I am following an order from the underworld to arrest someone in the city."

    130

    问：“有牌票乎？”曰：“有。”取而视之，其第三名即戴之表兄某也。戴欲救表兄，心疑所言不实，乃放之行，而坚坐桥上待之。四鼓，蓝衣者果至。戴问：“人可拘齐乎？”曰：“齐矣。”问：“何在？”曰：“在我所持伞上。”戴视之，有线缚五苍蝇在焉，嘶嘶有声。戴大笑，取而放之。其人惶急，踉跄走去。

    　　天色渐明，戴入城，至表兄处探问。其家人云：“家主病久，三更已死，四更复活，天明则又死矣。”

    　　江宁刘某，年七岁，肾囊红肿，医药罔效。邻有饶氏妇，当阴司差役之事，到期，便与夫异床而寝，不饮不食，若痴迷者。刘母托往阴司一查。去三日，来报曰：“无妨也。二郎前世好食田鸡，剥杀太多，故今世群鸡来啮，相与报仇。

    　　然天生田鸡，原系供人食者，虫鱼皆八蜡神所管，只须向刘猛将军处烧香求祷，便可无恙。“如其言，予疾果痊。

    Explanation

    130

    A man asked, "Do you have a warrant?" The official replied, "Yes." Taking it and looking at it, he saw that the third name was that of Duan's cousin. Duan wanted to save his cousin but was suspicious of the official's claims. He let the official go and sat firmly on the bridge waiting for him. At the fourth drumbeat of the night, the blue-clad official indeed arrived. Duan asked, "Can the person be arrested?" The official replied, "Yes, he is here." Duan asked, "Where is he?" The official said, "He is on the umbrella I am holding." Duan looked and saw five flies tied with a string, making a whistling sound. Duan laughed and released them. The official was frightened and hurriedly left.

    As the sky gradually became light, Duan entered the city and went to his cousin's house to inquire. His cousin's family said, "Our master has been ill for a long time, dying at the third watch, reviving at the fourth watch, and dying again at dawn."

    In Jiangning, there was a seven-year-old boy named Liu whose scrotum was swollen and red, and medicines were ineffective. A neighbor, a woman surnamed Rao, who performed duties in the underworld, would sleep separately from her husband when her time came, not eating or drinking, as if in a trance. Liu's mother asked her to go to the underworld to investigate. After three days, she returned and reported, "There is no harm. In a past life, the second son liked to eat frogs and killed too many, so in this life, the frogs are coming to bite him, seeking revenge together.

    However, frogs are naturally meant to be eaten by people, and insects and fish are under the jurisdiction of the Eight Wax Gods. One only needs to burn incense and pray to General Liu Meng, and the illness will be cured." Following her advice, the boy's illness indeed healed.

    131

    一日者，饶氏睡两日夜方醒；醒后满身流汗，口去喘不已。其嫂问故，曰：“邻妇某氏，凶恶难捉，冥王差我拘拿。不料他临时尚强有力，与我斗多时。幸亏我解下缠足布捆缚其手，裁得牵来。”嫂曰：“现在何处？”曰：“在窗外梧桐树上。”嫂往观之，见无别物，只头发拴一苍蝇。嫂戏取蝇夹入针线箱中。未几，闻饶氏在床上有呼号声，良久乃苏，曰：“嫂为戏太虐！阴司因我拿某妇不到，重责三十板，勒限再拿。嫂速还我苍蝇，为免再责。”嫂视其臀，果有杖痕，始大悔，取苍蝇付之。饶氏取含口中睡去，遂亦平静。自此，不肯替人间查阴司事矣。

    　　○严秉严秉，作云南禄劝县。县署东偏有屋三间，封锁甚严。相传狐仙所居，官到必祭。严循例致祭。其妻某必欲观之，屡伺门侧，不得见。一日，见美妇人倚窗梳头。妻素悍妒，虑惑其夫，率奴婢持棒冲入乱殴。美妇化作白鹅，绕地哀鸣。

    Explanation

    131

    One day, Rao Zhi slept for two days and nights before waking. After waking, she was drenched in sweat and panting heavily. Her sister-in-law asked why, and Rao Zhi replied, "A neighbor's wife, surnamed Zhao, is ferocious and hard to catch. The King of Hell sent me to apprehend her. Unbeknownst to me, she was still strong and powerful at the time, and we fought for a long time. Fortunately, I managed to bind her hands with the bound foot cloth and finally brought her here." Her sister-in-law asked, "Where is she now?" Rao Zhi said, "She is on the wutong tree outside the window." The sister-in-law went to look and saw nothing but a fly tied to a strand of hair. She playfully took the fly and put it in the needlework box. Soon after, Rao Zhi was heard shouting in bed. After a long time, she came to and said, "Sister-in-law, you are too cruel in your play! Because I failed to apprehend that woman, the underworld severely punished me with thirty strokes and set a deadline for me to apprehend her again. Quickly return my fly to me, so I can avoid further punishment." Her sister-in-law saw the marks of the beating on her buttocks and deeply regretted her actions. She took out the fly and gave it to Rao Zhi. Rao Zhi put the fly in her mouth and fell asleep again, and she also calmed down. From then on, she no longer wished to investigate matters of the underworld for the world.

    Regarding Yan Bingyi, who served in Luchuan County, Yunnan Province, there was a small house on the eastern side of the county office, locked very tightly. It was said to be the dwelling of a fox spirit, and officials were required to offer sacrifices upon their arrival. Yan Bingyi followed the tradition and offered sacrifices. His wife, however, insisted on seeing it. She waited by the door many times but could not see anything. One day, she saw a beautiful woman leaning by the window, combing her hair. The wife, who was naturally jealous and fierce, feared that the woman might bewitch her husband. She led her servants and maids with sticks and rushed in, beating wildly. The beautiful woman transformed into a white goose and cried mournfully around the ground.

    132

    秉取印印其背，遂现原形委地，堕胎而死，胎中两小狐也。严取朱笔点其额，两小狐亦死。取大小狐投之火中，自此署中无狐，而严氏亦无恙。又一年，其妻怀孕，生双胞，头上各有一点红，如朱笔所点。妻大惊而陨。严以痛妻故，未几，亦病亡。小儿终不育。

    　　○奉新奇事江西奉新村民李氏妇，生产三日，胎不下，其姑率三女守之。以倦故，又请邻妇三人轮流守护。一妇姓孙，有儿尚襁褓，不能同往，乃交托外婆家而率长子名钟者同往。钟已弱冠入学，虑夜间寂寞，乃持书一卷往。次日将午，其门内绝无人声，戚里疑之，打门入，则产妇死于床，七人死于地。七人中，六人衣服面目无他异，惟气绝而已，独孙秀才身尚端坐，右手执书如故。其左臂自肩以下，全身烧毁，直至脚底，黑如煤炭。合村大噪，鸣于官。急相验，命且掩埋，亦无从申报也。此事彭芸楣少司马为余言。

    Explanation

    132

    The Jade Seal was held and stamped on its back, and it then revealed its true form, falling to the ground. The fetus died, and within it were two small foxes. The strict one took a red pen to mark their foreheads, and the two small foxes also died. The large and small foxes were thrown into the fire, and from then on, there were no foxes in the office, and the strict one was also unharmed. A year later, his wife became pregnant and gave birth to twins, each with a red dot on their head, as if marked by a red pen. The wife was greatly shocked and died. The strict one, out of grief for his wife, not long after, also died of illness. The children ultimately did not have offspring.

    ○ A Strange Event in Fengxin, Jiangxi

    In a village in Fengxin, Jiangxi, a woman from the Li family had been in labor for three days, but the fetus did not come out. Her mother-in-law took charge of three daughters to guard her. Due to exhaustion, they also invited three neighboring women to take turns guarding. One of these women, named Sun, had a child still in swaddling clothes and could not go along, so she entrusted her child to her mother-in-law and took her eldest son, named Zhong, with her. Zhong was already a young man and had entered school, and he was concerned about the loneliness at night, so he took a scroll of books with him. The next day, just before noon, there was no sound inside the door. The relatives and neighbors suspected something and knocked on the door, only to find that the woman had died in bed, and seven people had died on the ground. Among the seven, six had no other abnormalities in their clothes or appearance, only that they were dead. The only exception was Mr. Sun, who was still sitting upright, holding a book in his right hand as usual. His left arm from the shoulder down was completely burned, black as coal, all the way to his feet. The entire village was in an uproar, and the matter was reported to the authorities. An urgent investigation was conducted, and they were ordered to bury them, but there was no way to report it. This matter was told to me by Vice-Marshal Peng Yunmei.

    133

    ○智恒僧苏州陈国鸿，彭芸楣先生丁酉乡试所取孝廉，性好古玩。家园内有种荷花缸，年久不起，陈命扛起，阅其款识。缸下又得一坛，黄碧色，花纹甚古，中有淤泥朽骨数片。陈投骨于水，携坛入室。夜，梦一僧来曰：“我唐时僧智恒也。汝所取磁坛，乃我埋骨坛，速还我骨而土掩焉。”陈素豪，告友朋，不以为意。又三日，其母梦一长眉僧挟一恶状僧至，曰：“汝子无礼，贪我磁坛，抛撒我骨，我诉之不理，欺我老耳。我师兄大千闻之不平，故同来索汝子之命。”母惊醒，命家人遍寻所弃之骨，仅存一片。问孝廉，则已迷闷，不省人事矣。未十日而病亡。

    Explanation

    133

    A Monk Named Zhi Heng from Suzhou

    Mr. Chen Guohong of Suzhou, who passed the provincial examination in the year Ding You under the tutelage of Mr. Peng Yunmei, had a fondness for ancient artifacts. In his garden, there was an old lotus pond vase that had not been moved for years. Chen ordered it to be lifted, and upon examining its inscriptions, he discovered another jar beneath it. The jar was a yellowish-green color, with ancient patterns, and contained some decayed bones amidst mud. Chen threw the bones into the water and brought the jar into his room. That night, he dreamed of a monk who said, “I am the monk Zhi Heng from the Tang Dynasty. The jar you took is my burial urn. Quickly return my bones and bury them.” Chen, who was usually bold, told his friends about the matter but did not take it seriously. Three days later, his mother dreamed of a monk with long eyebrows accompanied by an evil-looking monk, who said, “Your son was disrespectful, took my jar, and scattered my bones. I complained but was ignored, thinking I was old. My senior brother, Daqian, heard of this and was not pleased, so he came to seek my son’s life.” Chen’s mother woke up in shock and ordered her servants to search for the scattered bones, but only one piece was found. When she asked her son, he was confused and unconscious. Within ten days, he fell ill and died.

    134

    ○三斗汉三斗汉者，粤之鄙人也，其饭须三斗粟乃饱，人故呼为“三斗汉”。身长一丈，围抱不周。须虬面黑，乞食于市，所得莫能果腹。一日，之惠州，戏于提督军门外，双手挈二石狮去。提督召之，则仍挈双石狮而来。提督命五牛曳横木于前，三斗汉挽其后，用鞭鞭牛，牛奋欲奔，终不能移尺寸。提督奇其力，赏食马粮，使入伍学武。乃跪求云：“小人食须三斗粟，愿倍其粮。”提督许之。习武有年，驰马辄坠，箭发不中，乃改步卒。郁郁不得志而归，游于潮州。值潮之东门修湘子桥。桥梁石长三丈余，宽厚皆尺五。众工构天架，数十人挽之，莫能上。

    　　三斗汉从旁笑曰：“如许众人，面汗背，犹不能升一条石块耶！”众怒其妄，命试之。遂登架，独挽而上，众股栗。桥洞故有百数，辛卯年圮其三，郡丞范公捐俸倡修，见此人能独挽巨石，费省工速，遂命尽挽其余，赏钱数十千。不一月，食尽去，莫知所之。或云饿死于澄江。

    Explanation

    134

    A Man Who Needs Three Pecks of Rice

    A man known as "Three Pecks of Rice" was a rustic from Guangdong. He required three pecks of rice to feel full, and thus people called him "Three Pecks of Rice." He was a towering figure, over ten feet tall, so large that his body could not be embraced. With curly hair and a dark face, he begged for food in the market, but what he received was never enough to fill his stomach. One day, he went to Huizhou and played around the gate of the governor's military camp, carrying away two stone lions with his bare hands. The governor summoned him, and he returned carrying the two stone lions. The governor ordered five oxen to pull a horizontal log in front of him, and Three Pecks of Rice pulled from behind. The governor whipped the oxen, which struggled to run, but they could not move even an inch. Impressed by his strength, the governor rewarded him with horse feed and enlisted him into the army to learn martial arts. Three Pecks of Rice then knelt and asked, "I need three pecks of rice to eat, and I wish to have double the rations." The governor agreed. After years of training, he fell off his horse every time he rode it and missed his shots with the bow. He then switched to being a foot soldier. Disappointed and frustrated, he returned home and wandered to Chaozhou. Just as the eastern gate of Chaozhou was repairing the Xiangzi Bridge, which had stones over thirty feet long and fifteen inches wide and thick. Many workers constructed a high platform and tried to lift the stones, but they could not manage it.

    Three Pecks of Rice laughed from the side, saying, "With so many people sweating and straining, they still can't lift a single stone block!" The crowd was angry at his audacity and ordered him to try. He then climbed the platform and lifted the stone alone, causing the crowd to tremble. The bridge had originally had over a hundred arches, but three had collapsed in the year Xinhao. The county magistrate Fan, who had donated his salary to lead the repairs, saw that this man could lift such a large stone alone, saving both money and time. He then ordered him to lift the rest of the stones and rewarded him with tens of thousands of coins. Within a month, he ate up everything and left, and no one knows where he went. Some say he starved to death in Chengjiang.

    135

    ○苏南村桐邑有苏南村者，病笃昏迷，问其家人曰：“李耕野、魏兆芳可曾来否？”

    　　家人莫知，漫应之。顷又问，答以：“未曾来”。曰：“尔等当着人唤他速来。”

    　　家人以为谩语，不应。乃长叹欲逝。家人仓皇遣健足奔市，购纸轿一乘。至，则见舆夫背有“李耕野”、“魏兆芳”字样，乃恍然悟，急焚之，而其气始绝。舆夫姓字，乃好事者戏书也，竟成为真，亦奇。

    　　○叶生妻桐城邑西牛栏铺界叶生，笔耕糊口，父兄业农。乾隆癸卯春，佃其族人田于牌门庄，阖室移居于是。其妻年十八，素端重寡言，忽发颠谩骂，其音不一，惟骂李某“丧绝天良，毁我辈十人冢，盖造房屋，好生受用，将我等骸骨践踏污秽。”

    Explanation

    135

    Translation

    In the village of Su Nan in Tong County, there was a man named Su Nan who was gravely ill and delirious. He asked his family, "Has Li Gengye or Wei Zhao Fang come yet?"

    The family did not know and responded vaguely. After a while, he asked again, and they replied, "They have not come." He said, "You should send someone to call them immediately."

    The family thought he was speaking idly and did not respond. He then took a deep sigh and was about to die. The family hastily sent a strong man to the market to buy a sedan chair. When they arrived, they saw that the bearers of the sedan chair had the names "Li Gengye" and "Wei Zhao Fang" written on their backs. They suddenly realized the truth and quickly burned the sedan chair, and the man's breath finally ceased. The names on the bearers were written by someone for fun, but they unexpectedly turned out to be true, which was indeed strange.

    In the western part of Tong County, near the cowshed boundary, there lived a man named Ye Sheng who made a living by writing. His father and brothers were farmers. In the spring of 1743 during the reign of Emperor Qianlong, Ye Sheng rented land from his clan members in Pai Men Village and moved his entire family there. His wife was eighteen years old, usually dignified and rarely spoke. Suddenly, she went mad and began to curse, her voice varied, but she only cursed Li, saying, "You have lost all sense of morality, destroyed the tombs of ten people, built houses, enjoyed the benefits, and trampled and defiled our bones."

    136

    叶生不解，询邻老，始知房主李某于康熙时平坟架屋，事实有之。乃诘其妻云：“平坟做屋，实李某事，于我何干？”妻答云：“当时李某气焰甚高，我等忍气不言，多出游避之。今看尔家运低，故在此泄忿。”骂音中惟此厉声者最恶，其九音偶尔相间，亦略平和。生许以拆屋培冢，答云：“屋有主人，尔不能擅拆，盍往商量？”生奔请李姓来，其妻引至堂西两正屋内指示曰：“此二椁也。此四坟也，其牖旁乃二女坟，我坟在床后墙下。”李问：“尔何人？”答云：“我阮姓孚名，年二十二，前明正德间儒生。读书白鹤观，戏习道教，竟成羽士。偶为贪色逾墙，被辱自缢。葬此十人中，惟我受践踏污秽更苦，故我纠合伊等同来。”

    Explanation

    136

    叶生不解，询问邻里的老人，才得知房主李某在康熙年间平坟建屋，此事确实发生过。于是他质问他的妻子说：“平坟建屋，确实是李某的事，与我有什么关系？”妻子回答说：“当时李某气焰嚣张，我们忍气吞声，大多外出躲避。现在看你家运势低落，所以在这里发泄不满。”在这段话中，只有这一句声音最严厉，其余的声音偶尔交替出现，也略显平和。叶生答应拆除房屋并修复坟墓，妻子说：“房屋有主人，你不能擅自拆除，为什么不前去商量？”叶生急忙请李某来，他的妻子带领他到堂屋西侧的两间正房内指着说：“这是两个棺椁。这是四个坟墓，其中两个女儿的坟墓在窗户旁边，我的坟墓在床后墙下。”李某问道：“你是谁？”回答说：“我姓阮名孚，今年二十二岁，是明朝正德年间的一名儒生。我在白鹤观读书，偶尔学习道教，最终成为了一名道士。有一次因为贪恋美色翻墙出去，被侮辱后自缢而死。葬在这里的十个人中，只有我遭受了更多的践踏和污染，所以我召集他们一起来。”

    137

    李云：“汝骨在何处？”答曰：“正中一冢掘下三尺，见棺黑色者，是我也。”

    　　李踌躇不敢掘，鬼骂不息。远近劝者络绎而至，在问必答。或烧纸钱求之，其九鬼亦从旁劝解，音皆自其妻口中出。缢鬼骂曰：“汝等九个赌贼！得受叶家纸钱，彼此赶老羊快活，便来劝我么？”自是九鬼无声，惟缢鬼独闹。生请羽士禳解，属塾师陈某作荐送文。鬼大笑曰：“不通之极！某故事用错，某处文词鄙俗。况送我文，当求我，不应以威胁我。”塾师惭赧，唯唯而已。道士诵经略错，必加切责。

    Explanation

    137

    Li Yun asked, "Where are your bones?" The ghost replied, "They are in a central grave, three feet below the surface, in a black coffin."

    Li hesitated and dared not dig. The ghost kept cursing. People from far and near came to persuade, and for every question, there was an answer. Some burned paper money to appeal to it, and the nine ghosts also tried to mediate, with their voices all coming from the wife's mouth. The hanged ghost cursed, saying, "You nine gambling thieves! Having received paper money from the Ye family, you chase old sheep for fun, and now you come to persuade me?" From then on, the nine ghosts were silent, leaving only the hanged ghost to cause trouble. The living person asked a Taoist priest to perform a ritual to pacify and entrusted the village teacher Mr. T to write a memorial text. The ghost laughed loudly, saying, "Utterly incorrect! The story is used wrongly, and the language is vulgar and common. Besides, when sending me a text, one should seek me, not threaten me." The village teacher felt ashamed and nodded in agreement. The Taoist priest, having slightly misread the scripture, was severely reprimanded.

    138

    生之戚有程氏者，家素丰，方到门，鬼曰：“富翁来矣，当备好茶。”章孝廉甫与生有姻，将到，鬼曰：“文星至矣，求为我作墓志。”章口占一律赠之，曰：“当年底事竟投环？遗体飘零瘗此间。茅屋妄成将拆去，高封误毁已培还。

    　　从兹独乐安黄壤，还望垂怜放翠鬟。他日超升借法力，直排阊阖列仙班。“鬼谢曰：”蒙奖太过。孚有风流罪过，安能排阊阖列仙班乎！惟五、六二语见教极是，吾遵命去矣。“临去，呼叶生字，告之曰：”吾不受道士忏悔，受文人忏悔，亦未忘结习故也。尔盍镌诗墓石以光泉壤？“生妻瞑目无言。越一日，乃醒。

    Explanation

    138

    Among Sheng's relatives was a man surnamed Cheng, who had always been wealthy. Just as he arrived at the door, the ghost said, "The rich man has come, so prepare good tea." Zhang, the scholar-official related to Sheng by marriage, was about to arrive, and the ghost said, "The literary star has come, so ask him to write a tombstone inscription for me." Zhang composed a poem on the spot and gave it to him, saying:

    "At that time, what happened led to the end? The remains were scattered and buried here. The thatched cottage was mistakenly built and will be torn down, the high tomb was mistakenly destroyed and has been restored.

    From now on, I will enjoy peace in the yellow soil, hoping for your compassion to release the green hair. One day, I will ascend to the celestial gate with the help of divine power, directly joining the ranks of immortals."

    The ghost replied, "Your praise is too much. I have a sin of indulgence in pleasure, how can I ascend to the celestial gate and join the ranks of immortals! Only the fifth and sixth lines of your advice are extremely correct, and I will obey your command and leave now." On his way out, he called out the name of Ye Sheng and told him, "I do not accept repentance from a Taoist priest, but accept repentance from a literatus, and I have not forgotten my habits. Why not carve this poem on the tombstone to honor the grave?" Sheng's wife closed her eyes and remained silent. After a day, she woke up.

    139

    ○七盗索命杭州汤秀才世坤，年三十馀，馆于范家。一日晚坐，生徒四散。时冬月，畏风，书斋窗户尽闭。夜交三鼓，一灯荧然，汤方看书，窗外有无头人跳入，随其后者六人，皆无头，其头悉用带挂腰间，围汤，而各以头血滴之，涔涔冷湿，汤惊迷不能声。适馆僮持溺器来，一冲而散。汤陨地不醒，僮告主人，急来救起，灌姜汤数瓯，醒，具道所以，因乞回家。主人唤肩舆送之，天已大明。家住城隍山脚下，将近山，汤告舆夫不肯归家，愿仍至馆。云：未至山脚下，望见夜中七断头鬼昂然高坐，似有相待之意。主人无奈何，仍延馆中。遂大病，身热如焚。

    Explanation

    139

    Seven Robbers Seeking Vengeance

    In Hangzhou, there was a scholar named Tang Shikun, in his early thirties, who stayed at the Fan family's residence. One evening, he sat alone in his study as his students had scattered. It was winter, and he was afraid of the cold, so he had closed all the windows and doors of his study. As the third watch of the night approached, a faint light from a lamp illuminated the room. Tang was reading when suddenly a headless figure leaped into the room, followed by six others, all of whom were also headless. Their heads were tied around their waists with cords, and they surrounded Tang, each dripping blood from their heads, which fell in a cold, wet stream. Tang was stunned and could not speak. Just then, the servant came with a chamber pot, and upon seeing him, the figures scattered. Tang fell to the ground unconscious. The servant informed the master, who quickly came to rescue him and gave him several bowls of ginger soup. Tang awoke and fully explained the reason, and then requested to return home. The host called for a sedan chair to send him, and by then, the sky was already bright. His home was at the foot of City God Mountain. As they approached the mountain, Tang told the sedan chair bearers not to return home and wished to return to the academy instead. He said that before reaching the foot of the mountain, he saw seven headless ghosts sitting upright in the night, as if waiting for him. The host had no choice but to continue inviting him to stay. Soon after, Tang fell seriously ill, his body burning with fever.

    140

    主人素贤，为迎其妻来侍汤药。未三日，卒。已而苏，谓妻曰：“吾不活矣，所以复苏者，冥府宽恩，许来相诀故也。昨病重时，见青衣四人拉吾同行，云‘有人告发索命事’。所到，黄沙茫茫，心知阴界，因问：”吾何罪？‘青衣曰：’相公请自观其容便晓矣。‘吾云：“人不能自见其容，作何观法？’四青衣各赠有柄小镜，曰：”请相公照。‘如其言，便觉庞然魁梧，须长七八寸，非今生清瘦面貌。前生姓吴，名锵，乃明季娄县知县。七人者，七盗也，埋四万金于某所，被获后，谋以此金贿官免死，托娄县典史许某转请于我。许匿取二万，以二万说我。我彼时明知盗罪难逭，拒之。许典史引《左氏》’杀汝，壁将焉往‘之说，请掘取其金而仍杀之。

    Explanation

    140

    The host, who was always virtuous, brought his wife to care for him while he was ill. Not even three days had passed when he died. Afterward, he revived and said to his wife, "I will not survive, but the reason I revived is because of the leniency of the underworld, which allowed me to come and say goodbye. Yesterday, when I was gravely ill, I saw four men in green robes pulling me along and said, 'Someone has accused us of demanding a life.' The place we arrived at was shrouded in yellow sand, and I knew I was in the underworld. I asked, 'What crime have I committed?' The men in green robes replied, 'Sir, please look at your own appearance to understand.' I said, 'A person cannot see their own appearance; how can I look?' The four men in green robes each gave me a small mirror with a handle and said, 'Please, sir, look into the mirror.' As they said, I immediately felt myself as a huge and imposing figure, with a beard seven or eight inches long, not the thin and gaunt face I had in this life. In my previous life, I was named Wu Qiang, the magistrate of Loucheng during the late Ming dynasty. The seven people were seven bandits who buried four million taels of gold in a certain place. After being captured, they plotted to bribe officials with this gold to avoid death, entrusting the county magistrate Xu of Loucheng to request it from me. Xu secretly took two million and offered two million to me. At that time, I knew that the crime of theft was difficult to escape, so I refused it. County magistrate Xu cited the saying from the Zuo Zhuan 'If you kill you, where will the wall go?' and asked me to dig up the gold and still kill them."

    141

    我一时心贪，竟从许计，此时悔之无及。乃随四人行至一处，宫阙壮丽，中坐衮袍阴官，色颇和。吾拜伏阶下，七鬼者捧头于肩，若有所诉。诉毕，仍挂头腰间。吾哀乞阴官。官曰：“我无成见，汝自向七鬼求情。’吾因转向七鬼叩头云：”请高僧超度，多烧纸钱。‘鬼俱不肯，其头摇于腰间，狞恶殊甚。开口露牙，就近来咬我颈。阴官喝曰：“盗休无礼。汝等罪应死，非某枉法。某之不良，有取尔等财耳。但起意者典史，非吴令，似可缓索渠命。’七鬼者又各以头装颈，哭曰：”我等向伊索债，非常命也。彼食朝廷俸而贪盗财，是亦一资也。许典史久已被我等咀嚼矣。困吴令初转世为美女，嫁宋尚书牧仲为妾，宋贵人有文名，某等不敢近。今又托生汤家，汤祖宗素积德，家中应有科目。

    Explanation

    141

    I was momentarily tempted by greed and ultimately followed his plan. Now it is too late to regret. So I followed the four people to a place, where the palace was magnificent, and in the center sat an official in a ceremonial robe with a quite amiable expression. I knelt and bowed at the foot of the steps, and the seven ghosts held their heads on their shoulders, as if they had something to report. After finishing, they hung their heads back on their shoulders. I pleaded with the official. The official said, "I have no bias; you must plead your case to the seven ghosts yourself." So I turned to the seven ghosts and knelt, saying, "Please let a high monk perform a ritual to liberate them, and burn many paper money." The ghosts all refused, and their heads swayed on their shoulders, indicating they were extremely fierce and cruel. They opened their mouths, showing their teeth, and came close to bite my neck. The official shouted, "Thief, cease this impudence! Your crimes warrant death; I am not acting unjustly. My own misconduct is only in taking your money. However, the one who conceived the idea was the magistrate, not the local official; perhaps we can delay the execution of his sentence." The seven ghosts, each holding their heads on their necks, cried, "We are collecting debts from him; this is not a normal life." He receives salary from the court but is greedy and steals; this is also a form of income. The magistrate has been devoured by us for a long time. The local official was reborn as a beautiful woman, married to Song Shangren's concubine, and since Song is a prominent scholar with literary fame, we dared not approach her. Now she has been reborn into the Tang family, whose ancestors have always accumulated virtue, so there should be a scholar in the family.

    142

    今年除夕，渠之姓名将被文昌君送上天榜，一入天榜，则邪魔不敢近，我等又休矣。千载一时，寻捉非易，愿官勿行妇人之仁。‘阴官听毕蹙额曰：“盗亦有道，吾无如何。汝姑回阳间，一别妻孥可也。’以此，我得暂苏。”语毕，不复开口。

    Explanation

    142

    On New Year's Eve, his name will be placed on the heavenly list by the Lord of Literature, and once it is, demons will not dare to approach, and we will be at rest again. This is a once-in-a-thousand-years opportunity, and to find and catch him is not easy. I wish the official would not act with soft-heartedness. The underworld official, after hearing, frowned and said, "Thieves also have their ways, I can do nothing. Temporarily return to the mortal world, bid farewell to your wife and children." For this reason, I was able to temporarily revive. After speaking, he no longer opened his mouth.

    143

    妻为焚烧黄白纸钱千百万，竟无言而卒。

    　　汤氏别房讳世昌者，次年乡试及第，中进士，入词林，人皆以为填天榜者所抽换矣。

    　　○陈清恪公吹气退鬼陈公鹏年未遇时，与乡人李孚相喜。秋夕，乘月色过李闲话。李故寒士，谓陈曰：“与妇谋酒不得，子少坐，我外出沽酒，与子赏月。”陈持其诗卷坐观待之。门外有妇人蓝衣蓬首开户入，见陈，便却去。陈疑李氏戚也，避客，故不入，乃侧坐避妇人。妇人袖物来，藏门槛下，身走入内。陈心疑何物，就槛视之，一绳也，臭，有血痕。陈悟此乃缢鬼，取其绳置靴中，坐如故。

    　　少顷，蓬首妇出，探藏处，失绳，怒，直奔陈前，呼曰：“还我物！”陈曰：“何物？”妇不答，但耸立张口吹陈，冷风一阵如冰，毛发噤<齿介>，灯荧荧青色将灭。陈私念：鬼尚有气，我独无气乎？乃亦鼓气吹妇。妇当公吹处，成一空洞，始而腹穿，继而胸穿，终乃头灭。顷刻，如轻烟散尽，不复见矣。

    Explanation

    143

    The Wife Burned Countless Millions of Yellow and White Paper Money, and She Died Without Uttering a Word.

    The one surnamed Shang, from a branch family, whose name was Shichang, passed the provincial examination the following year, became a Jinshi, and entered the Hanlin Academy. People all believed that he had replaced the one who filled the imperial list.

    ○ Grand Master Chen Qingke Blows Away a Ghost

    Master Chen Pengnian, before he achieved success, befriended a fellow villager named Li Fu. On an autumn evening, he visited Li under the moonlight to chat. Li was a poor scholar and told Chen, "I couldn't get my wife to prepare wine, so you wait a moment while I go out to buy wine to enjoy the moon together." Chen held his poetry scroll and sat to wait for him. A woman in blue clothing and with disheveled hair opened the door and entered. Upon seeing Chen, she turned and left. Chen suspected that the woman was a relative of Li, so to avoid causing trouble, he sat aside to avoid her. The woman came with something in her sleeve and hid it under the threshold, then walked inside. Chen wondered what the object was and went to the threshold to look, and saw it was a rope, which smelled and had bloodstains. Chen realized this was a hanging ghost and took the rope and put it in his boot, then sat as before.

    A short while later, the disheveled woman came out, searched for the place where the rope was hidden, and found it missing. She became angry, rushed to Chen's front, and shouted, "Give back my property!" Chen asked, "What property?" The woman did not answer, but stood up on tiptoe and blew at Chen. A cold wind like ice blew, causing Chen's hair to stand on end, and the lamp flickered with a greenish light, about to go out. Chen privately thought, "Even the ghost has breath; do I alone have no breath?" He then also blew air at the woman. Where Chen blew, the woman turned into a hollow space; first her abdomen was pierced, then her chest, and finally her head disappeared. In a moment, she dissipated like light smoke and was no longer visible.

    144

    少顷，李持酒入，大呼：“妇缢于床！”陈笑曰：“无伤也，鬼绳尚在我靴。”

    　　告之故，乃共入解救，灌以姜汤，苏，问：“何故寻死？”其妻曰：“家贫甚，夫君好客不已。头止一钗，拔去沽酒。心闷甚，客又在外，未便声张。旁忽有蓬首妇人，自称左邻，告我以夫非为客拔钗也，将赴赌钱场耳。我愈郁恨，且念夜深，夫不归，客不去，无面目辞客。蓬首妇手作圈曰：”从此入即佛国，欢喜无量。‘余从此圈入，而手套不紧，圈屡散。妇人曰：“取吾佛带来，则成佛矣。’走出取带，良久不来。余方冥然若梦，而君来救我矣。”访之邻，数月前果缢死一村妇。

    Explanation

    144

    After a short while, Li brought in the wine and shouted, "The wife has hanged herself on the bed!" Chen laughed and said, "No harm done, the ghost rope is still in my boot."

    He explained the reason, and they went in to save her. They poured ginger soup on her, and she revived. They asked, "Why did you want to die?" Her wife said, "Our family is very poor, and my husband keeps entertaining guests. There is only one hairpin on my head, and I took it off to buy wine. My heart was very troubled, and it was not convenient to make a fuss. Suddenly, there was a woman with disheveled hair who called herself the neighbor to the left. She told me that my husband was not going to entertain guests but was going to the gambling den. I became even more depressed and resentful. She said, 'Take my Buddha's belt, and you will become a Buddha.' I went out to get the belt, and she said, 'Enter through this circle.' The circle kept falling apart, and I was in a state of dazedness like a dream. Then you came to save me." They went to the neighbor, and a few months ago, a village woman did indeed hang herself.

    145

    ○陈圣涛遇狐绍兴陈圣涛者，贫士也，丧偶。游扬州，寓天宁寺侧一小庙，庙僧遇之甚薄。

    　　陈见庙有小楼扃闭，问僧何故。僧曰：“楼有怪。”陈必欲登，乃开户入。见几上无丝毫尘，有镜架梳篦等物。大疑，以为僧藏妇人，不语出。过数日，望见美妇倚楼窥，陈亦目挑之。妇腾身下，已至陈所。陈始惊以为非人。其妇曰：“我仙也，汝毋怖，为有夙缘故耳。”款接甚殷，竟成夫妇。

    　　每月朔，妇告假七日，云：“往泰山娘娘处听差。”陈乘妇去，启其箱，金玉灿然。陈一丝不取，代扃锁如初。妇归，陈私谓曰：“我贫甚，而君颇有余资，盍假我屯货为生业乎？”妇曰：“君骨相贫，不能富，虽作商贾无益。且喜君行义甚高，开我之箱，分文不取，亦足敬也。请资君衣食。”自后，陈不起炊，中馈之事，妇主之。

    Explanation

    145

    Chen Shengtao Encounters a Fox

    In Shaoxing, there was a poor scholar named Chen Shengtao, who had lost his wife. He traveled to Yangzhou and lodged in a small temple beside Tianning Temple, where the temple monk treated him very poorly.

    Chen saw a small, locked room in the temple and asked the monk why it was locked. The monk replied, "There is a spirit in the room." Despite this, Chen insisted on entering and had the monk unlock the door. Inside, he found no dust on the table and saw items such as a mirror frame and hair comb. He was greatly puzzled and suspected that the monk was hiding a woman, so he left without saying a word. After several days, he saw a beautiful woman leaning against the building, peeking at him. Chen also flirted with her. The woman leaped down and appeared before Chen. Chen was startled to think she was not human. The woman said, "I am a fairy. Do not be afraid, for we have a predestined connection." She treated him very warmly, and they eventually became husband and wife.

    Every month on the first day, the woman would take a seven-day leave, saying, "I am going to the Queen Mother of Mount Tai to report." When she was away, Chen opened her box and found it filled with golden and jade treasures. Chen did not take a single thread of it and locked the box again as before. When the woman returned, Chen privately said to her, "I am very poor, while you have plenty of wealth. Why don't you lend me some goods to start a business?" The woman replied, "Your bone structure indicates poverty, and you cannot become wealthy, even if you become a merchant. I admire your high moral character, as you did not take a single thing from my box, which is also worthy of respect. Please provide for your food and clothing." From then on, Chen did not cook, and the woman took charge of household affairs.

    146

    居年余，妇谓陈曰：“妾所蓄金已为君捐纳飞班通判，赴京投供，即可选也。

    　　妾请先入京师置屋待君。“陈曰：”娘子去，我从何处访寻？“曰：”君第入都，到彰义门，妾自遣人相迎。“陈如其言，后妇人两月入都，至彰义门，果有苍头跪曰：”主君到迟，娘娘相待久矣。“引至米市胡同，则崇垣大厦，奴婢数十人皆跪迎叩头如旧曾服侍者。陈亦不解其故。登堂，妇人盛服出迎，携手入房。陈问：”诸奴婢何以识我？“曰：”勿声张。妾假君形貌赴部投捐，又假君形貌买宅立契，诸奴婢投身时，亦假君形貌以临之，故皆认识君。“因私教陈曰：”若何姓，若何名，唤遣时须如我所嘱，毋为若辈所疑。“陈喜甚，因通书于家。

    Explanation

    146

    After living for more than a year, the woman told Chen, "I have used my accumulated wealth to secure a rapid promotion for you to the position of Tongpan. You should submit your application and present yourself for selection.

    I will go to the capital first to buy a house and wait for you."

    Chen asked, "If you go, where will I look for you?"

    She replied, "Just go to the capital. When you arrive at Zhangyi Gate, I will send someone to meet you."

    Chen followed her instructions. Two months later, the woman arrived in the capital and went to Zhangyi Gate. Sure enough, there was a servant kneeling and saying, "Master, you arrived late. Lady has been waiting for you a long time." He led Chen to Mishi Hutong, where they found a grand house with high walls and large buildings. Dozens of servants knelt and bowed in respect.

    The woman, dressed in fine clothing, came out to greet Chen and led him into the house. Chen asked, "Why do these servants recognize me?"

    She explained, "Don't make a fuss. I impersonated you when I submitted the application and bought the house. I also took on your appearance when I hired the servants. That's why they recognize you."

    She then privately instructed Chen, "When you call or dismiss them, follow my instructions. Do not be suspected by them."

    Chen was very pleased and sent a letter home.

    147

    明年，陈之长子来，知父已续娶后母，入房拜见。母慈恤倍至，如所生。子亦孝敬不违。妇人曰：“闻儿有妇，何不偕来？明年可同至别驾任所。”长子唯唯。妇人赠舟车费，迎其妻入京同居。忽一日，门外有少年求见。陈问：“何人？”

    　　少年曰：“吾母在此。”陈问妇人，妇人曰：“是吾儿，妾前夫所生也。”唤入，拜陈，并拜陈之长子，呼为兄。

    　　居亡何，妇假日也，不在家；长子亦外出。妻王氏方梳妆，少年窥嫂有色，排窗入，拥抱求欢。王不可，少年强之，弛下衣，以阴示嫂，茎头无肉而有毛，尖挺如立锥。王愈畏恶，大呼乞命。少年惧，奔出。王之裙褶已毁裂矣。长子夜归被酒，见妻容色有异，问之，具道所以。长子不胜忿，拔几上刀寻少年。少年已卧，就帐中斫之。烛照，一狐断首而毙。陈知其事，惊骇。惧妇人假满归，必索其子命，乃即夜父子逃归绍兴。官不赴选，一钱不得着身，贫如故。

    Explanation

    147

    The following year, Chen's eldest son came, knowing that his father had remarried a stepmother, he entered the room to pay respects. The stepmother's kindness and care were doubled, treating him like a biological son. The son was also filial and obedient. The woman said, "I heard your son has a wife, why didn't you bring her along? Next year, you can both come to the new position." The eldest son agreed. The woman gave money for travel expenses, welcoming his wife to the capital to live together. One day, a young man appeared at the door to request an audience. Chen asked, "Who are you?"

    The young man said, "My mother is here." Chen asked the woman, who replied, "This is my son, born of my former husband." She called him in, and he bowed to Chen, and also bowed to Chen's eldest son, calling him brother.

    Not long after, the woman was on leave and not at home; the eldest son was also out. Wang, the wife, was just dressing when the young man saw his sister-in-law and thought she was beautiful. He pushed through the window to enter, embraced her, and sought intimacy. Wang refused, but the young man forced her. He undressed himself and showed his genitals to his sister-in-law, the tip was hairless but had hair, sharp and pointed like a cone. Wang was even more afraid and cried out for her life. The young man was afraid and ran out. Wang's skirt was already torn. The eldest son returned home drunk at night, saw his wife looking different, asked her, and she explained everything. The eldest son was too angry and picked up a knife from the table to look for the young man. The young man was already asleep, and the eldest son attacked him in the tent. Under the candlelight, a fox with its head cut off lay dead. Chen learned of the incident and was shocked. Fearing that the woman would return after her leave was up and demand her son's life, they fled back to Shaoxing that very night. They did not attend the official selection process, and they were as poor as ever.

    148

    ○长鬼被缚竹墩沈翰林厚余，少与友张姓同学读书。数日张不至，问之，张患伤寒甚剧，因往问候。入门悄然，将升堂，见堂上先有一长人端坐，仰面视堂上题额。沈疑非人，戏解腰带，潜缚其两腿。长人惊，转面相视。沈叩以“何处来？”长人云：“张某当死，余为勾差，当先来与其家堂神说明，再动手勾捉。”沈以张“寡母在堂，未娶无子，胡可以死？”恳画计缓之。长人亦有怜色，而谢以无术。沈代恳再三，长人曰：“只一法耳。张明日午时当死，先期有冥使五人偕余自其门外柳树下入。冥中鬼饥渴久，得饮食即忘事。君可预设两席，置六人座，君候于门外柳树边。有旋风自上而下，即拱揖入门，延之入座，勤为劝酬。视日影逾午，则起散。张可以免。”沈允诺，即入语张家人。届期，一一如所教。张至巳刻，已昏晕；当午，惟存一息；外席散，而神气渐复。沈大喜。

    Explanation

    148

    A Tall Ghost Bound to a Bamboo Stand: The Scholar Se Houyu

    In his youth, Se Houyu, a翰林, studied with a friend named Zhang. After several days, Zhang did not come. Se inquired and learned that Zhang was suffering from a severe case of typhoid fever, so he went to visit him. Upon entering the house quietly, he was about to ascend to the main hall when he saw a tall figure sitting there, looking up at the inscription on the hall. Se suspected that it was not a human and, in a playful mood, untied his own belt and secretly bound the ghost's legs. The tall figure was startled and turned to look at Se. Se asked, "Where are you from?" The tall figure replied, "Zhang is about to die. I am here to deliver a summons from the underworld. I must first explain this to the household's ancestral spirits before I can proceed to capture him." Se said, "Zhang's mother is still alive, he has not married, and he has no children. How can he die?" He then requested a plan to delay Zhang's fate. The tall figure showed a sympathetic expression but apologized, saying he had no means to do so. Se pleaded repeatedly, and the tall figure said, "There is only one method. Zhang will die tomorrow at noon. Before that, five messengers from the underworld will enter through a willow tree outside his house. Ghosts in the underworld are very hungry and thirsty; if they eat or drink, they will forget their tasks. You can set up two tables with six seats and wait for me at the willow tree outside the house. When a whirlwind descends from above, bow and invite them to enter, seat them, and serve them food and wine frequently. Once the sun passes noon, you can dismiss them. Zhang can be saved." Se agreed and immediately went to inform Zhang's family. On the appointed day, everything was done as instructed. Zhang, by the fourth watch, was already in a faint; at noon, he was barely breathing. When the guests left, his spirit gradually returned. Se was overjoyed.

    149

    归月余，夜梦前长人作痛楚状攒眉告曰：“前为君画策，张君得延一纪，入学，且当中某科副车，举二子。而余以泄冥事，为同辈所告，责四十板革役矣。

    　　余本非鬼，乃峡石镇挑脚夫刘先。今遭冥责，不复能行起。尚有三年阳数未终，须君语张君给日用费，终我余年。“沈语张，张即持数十金偕沈买舟访之，果得其人，方以瘫疾卧床。乃拜谢床下，以所携金赠之而返。张后一如梦中所语。

    　　○西园女怪杭郡周姓者，与友陈某游邗上，住某绅家。时初秋，尚有馀暑，所居屋颇隘。

    　　主人西园精舍数间，颇幽静，面山临池。二人移榻其中，数夜安然。

    Explanation

    149

    After returning for a month, I had a dream where a tall figure appeared in pain, frowning and telling me, "Previously, I helped you plan, and Zhang was granted an extension of his life by twelve years, entered the academy, and was placed as a reserve candidate in a certain examination, and also had two sons. However, I revealed secrets of the underworld, and was reported by my peers, resulting in a punishment of forty strokes and dismissal from my post.

    I am not actually a ghost; I am Liu Xian, a porter from Xiaoshi Town. Now, I have been punished by the underworld and can no longer move. I still have three years left of my earthly life, and I need you to tell Zhang to provide me with daily expenses to sustain my remaining years." Shen told Zhang, and Zhang immediately took several dozen gold pieces and accompanied Shen to visit him by boat. Indeed, they found the person, who was lying in bed paralyzed. They bowed in gratitude at the bedside and gave him the money they had brought, then returned. Later, Zhang experienced everything just as described in the dream.

    ○ A Strange Occurrence in the West Garden

    A man surnamed Zhou from Hangzhou, traveling with his friend Chen to Yangzhou, stayed at the house of a certain gentry. It was early autumn, and there was still some residual heat. The house they were staying in was quite narrow.

    The master's West Garden had several refined dwellings, which were very secluded, facing a mountain and overlooking a pond. The two of them moved their beds into the garden, and spent several nights there peacefully.

    150

    一夕，步月至二鼓，入室将寝，闻庭外步さ声，徐徐吟曰：“春花成往事，秋月又今宵。回首巫山远，空将两鬓凋。”两人初疑主人出游，既而语气不类，披衣窃视，见一美女背栏干立。两人私语：未闻主人家有此人，且装束殊不似近时，得毋贡所谓鬼魅者此乎？陈少年情动，曰：“有此丽质，魅亦何妨？”因呼曰：“美女何不入室一谈？”庭外应声曰：“妾可入，君独不可出耶？”陈拉周启户出，不复见人。呼之，随呼随应，而人不可得。寻声以往，若在树间，审视之，则柳枝下倒悬一妇人首。二人骇极大呼。首坠地，跳跃而来。二人急奔避入室，首已随至。两人关门，尽力抵之；首啮门限，咋咋有声。俄闻鸡鸣，首跳跃去，至池而投。两人迨天明，急移住旧所，各病虐数十日。

    　　○雷诛营卒乾隆三年二月间，雷震死一营卒。卒素无恶迹，人咸怪之。有同营老卒告于众曰：“某顷已改行为善，二十年前披甲时曾有一事，我因同为班卒，稔知之。

    Explanation

    150

    One night, walking under the moon until the second drumbeat, I entered the room to sleep and heard footsteps outside the courtyard. Slowly reciting, she said, "Spring flowers become past events, autumn moon is once again tonight. Looking back, the Wuchang Mountains are far away, in vain, my temples are turning gray." The two of us initially thought the master had gone out, but then the tone was unlike anything we had heard before. We put on our clothes and peeped out, seeing a beautiful woman leaning against the railing. We whispered to each other: we had never heard of anyone like this in the master's household, and her attire was quite unlike the current fashion. Is this not the so-called ghost or demon? The young man was moved by emotion and said, "With such beauty, even a demon would be fine." He called out, "Why doesn't the beautiful lady come in for a talk?" The voice outside the courtyard responded, "I can come in, but why can't you come out?" The young man pulled Zhou and opened the door to go out, but no one was there. We called out, and she responded to every call, yet the person could not be found. Following the sound, it seemed she was between the trees. Upon closer inspection, a woman's head was hanging upside down from a willow branch. The two of us were shocked to the point of screaming. The head fell to the ground, jumping toward us. We quickly ran back into the room, but the head had already followed us. We closed the door and tried our best to block it; the head bit at the threshold, making a gnashing sound. Soon after hearing the rooster crow, the head jumped away, heading toward the pool. By dawn, the two of us quickly moved to the old place, each falling ill with malaria for dozens of days.

    ○ Lightning Strikes a Soldier During the second month of the third year of Emperor Qianlong's reign, a soldier was struck by lightning and killed. The soldier had no evil deeds, and people were all surprised. An old soldier from the same camp told the others, "He had recently changed his ways and become good. Twenty years ago, when he was in armor, there was an incident. I, being the same class as him, knew it well."

    151

    某将军猎皋亭山下，某立帐房于路旁。薄暮，有小尼过帐外。见前后无人，拉入行奸。尼再四抵拦，遗其裤而逸。某追半里许，尼避入一田家，某怅怅而返。尼所避之家仅一少妇，一小儿，其夫外出佣工。见尼入，拒之。尼语之故，哀求假宿。妇怜而许之，借以己裤。尼约以“三日后，当来归还”，未明即去。夫归，脱垢衣欲换。妇启箧，求之不得，而己裤故在，因悟前仓卒中误以夫裤借去。方自咎未言，而小儿在旁曰：“昨夜和尚来穿去耳。”夫疑之，细叩踪迹。儿具告：和尚夜来哀求阿娘，如何留宿，如何借裤，如何带黑出门。妇力辩是尼非僧，夫不信，始以詈骂，继加捶楚。遍告邻佑。邻佑以事在昏夜，各推不知。妇不胜其冤，竟缢死。次早，其夫启门，见女尼持裤来还，并篮贮糕饵为谢。其子指以告父曰：“此即前夜借宿之和尚也。”夫悔，痛杖其子，毙于妇柩前，己亦自缢。

    Explanation

    151

    A certain general was hunting in the foothills of Gao Ting Mountain. He set up a tent by the roadside. As dusk fell, a young nun passed by the tent. Seeing no one around, he pulled her into the tent for sexual intercourse. The nun repeatedly resisted, but he took her pants and she escaped. The general chased her for half a mile, but she hid in a peasant's house and he returned dejectedly. The peasant's house was occupied by a young woman and a child, whose husband was working outside as a laborer. When the nun entered, the wife initially resisted. The nun explained the situation and begged to be allowed to stay overnight. The wife pitied her and lent her her own pants. The nun promised to return the pants in three days and left before dawn. The husband returned, took off his dirty clothes, and wanted to change. The wife searched her chest but could not find the pants, though hers were still there. She realized that she had mistakenly lent her husband's pants to the nun in the hurry. She blamed herself for not speaking up and the child, who was nearby, said, "Last night, the monk came and took them." The husband suspected the child and questioned him closely about her whereabouts. The child told everything: the monk came at night and begged the mother, how he stayed the night, how he borrowed the pants, and how he left in the dark. The wife strongly defended that it was a nun, not a monk, but the husband did not believe her, so he started scolding her and then beat her. He informed all the neighbors. The neighbors, considering it happened at night, each claimed they did not know. The wife could not bear the injustice and ultimately hanged herself. The next morning, the husband opened the door and saw a nun returning the pants, carrying a basket of cakes and sweets as a gift. The child pointed and told his father, "This is the same monk who stayed the night before." The husband regretted, severely beat his son, killed him in front of his wife's coffin, and then hanged himself.

    152

    邻里以经官不无多累，相与殡殓，寝其事。

    　　次冬，将军又猎其地。土人有言之者，余虽心识为某卒，而事既寝息，遂不复言。曾密语某，某亦心动，自是改行为善，冀以盖愆，而不虞天诛之必不可逭也。

    　　○青龙党杭州旧有恶少歃血结盟，刺背为小青龙，号“青龙党”，横行闾里。雍正末年，臬司范国擒治之，死者十之八九，首恶董超，竟以逃免。乾隆某年冬，梦其党数十人走告曰：“子为党首，虽幸逃免，明年当伏天诛。”董惶恐求计，众曰：“计惟投保叔塔草庵僧为徒，力持戒行，或可幸免。”董梦觉，访之塔下，果有老僧结草棚趺坐诵经。董长跪泣涕，自陈罪戾，愿度为弟子。老僧初犹逊谢，既见其情真，乃与剪发为头陀，令日间诵经，夜沿山敲木鱼念佛号。自冬至春，修持颇力。

    Explanation

    152

    The neighbors, considering the involvement of the authorities would bring many troubles, jointly performed the burial rites and laid the matter to rest.

    The following winter, the general hunted in the same area again. Some locals spoke about it, and I recognized in my heart that it was the certain soldier, but since the matter had been laid to rest, I did not mention it again. I once secretly told someone, and that person was also moved, and from then on, he changed his behavior for the better, hoping to atone for his sins, without realizing that the heavenly punishment could not be avoided.

    ○ Qilong Party In old times in Hangzhou, there were some young ruffians who swore an oath by drinking blood, tattooed a small qilong on their backs, and called themselves the "Qilong Party," acting recklessly in the neighborhood. In the late years of Emperor Yongzheng's reign, the governor of Zhejiang, Fan Guo, captured and punished them, killing about eight or nine out of ten. The leader, Dong Chao, managed to escape and avoid punishment. In a certain year of Emperor Qianlong's reign, Dong dreamed that dozens of members of his party came to tell him, "You are the leader of the party, although you have luckily escaped, next year you will be punished by heaven." Dong was filled with fear and sought advice. They said, "The only way is to seek refuge under a monk at the Baoshu Tower hermitage and become his disciple, strictly adhering to monastic discipline, perhaps you can be spared." After waking up from the dream, Dong went to the foot of the tower and found an old monk who had set up a thatched hut and was sitting in meditation, reciting scriptures. Dong knelt down and wept, confessing his sins and expressing his wish to become a disciple. The old monk initially declined, but seeing his sincere repentance, he agreed to shave his head and make him a mendicant monk. He instructed Dong to recite scriptures during the day and to chant the Buddha's name while ringing a wooden fish at night. From winter to spring, Dong practiced with great effort.

    153

    四月某日，从市上化斋归，小憩土地祠。朦胧睡去，见其党来促曰：“速归！

    　　速归！今夕雷至矣！“董惊觉，踉跄归棚，天已昏黑，果有雷声。董以梦告僧。

    　　憎令跪己膝下，两袖蒙其顶而诵经如故。不数刻，电光绕棚，霹雳连下，或中棚左石，或中棚右树，如是者七八击，皆不得中。少顷，风雷俱止，云开见月。老僧谓难已过，掖以起曰：“从此当无事矣。”董惊魂少定，拜谢老憎，出棚外。

    　　忽电光烁然，震霆一声，已毙石上。

    　　○陈州考院河南陈州学院衙堂后有楼三间封锁，相传有鬼物。康熙中，汤西崖先生以给谏视学其他，亦以老吏言，扃其楼如故。时值盛暑，幕中人多屋少，杭州王秀才，中州景秀才考祥，居常以胆气自壮，欲移居高楼。汤告以所闻，不信。断锁登楼，则明窗四敞，梁无点尘，愈疑前言为妄。景榻于楼之外间，王榻于楼之内间，让中一间为起座所。

    Explanation

    153

    On a certain day in April, Dong returned from the market after alms-giving and took a short rest at the Earth God Shrine. He fell into a drowsy sleep and saw his companions urging him, saying, "Hurry back! Hurry back! Tonight the thunder will come!" Dong was startled awake and stumbled back to the shelter. The sky was already dark, and indeed, there was thunder. Dong reported his dream to the monk. The monk made him kneel at his knees, with his sleeves covering the top of Dong's head and continuing to recite scriptures as before. Within a short time, lightning surrounded the shelter, and thunderbolts fell one after another, striking the stone on the left side of the shelter and the tree on the right side of the shelter, in this manner, seven or eight times, none hitting the target. After a short while, the wind and thunder both stopped, and the clouds parted to reveal the moon. The old monk said the danger had passed and helped him up, saying, "From now on, there should be no more trouble." Dong's soul was somewhat calmed, and he bowed and thanked the old monk, exiting the shelter. Suddenly, the lightning flashed brightly, and a deafening thunderclap followed, and he was already dead on the stone.

    In the examination hall of the Chenzhou Academy in Henan, there was a locked three-story building behind the administrative hall. It was said to be haunted by spirits. During the reign of Emperor Kangxi, Mr. Tang Xiyi, a censor, was inspecting the school. He also believed the stories told by old officials and kept the building locked as before. At that time, it was the height of summer, and there were more people than rooms in the examination hall. Wang Xiucai from Hangzhou and Jing Kaoxiang from Zhongzhou, who usually boasted of their courage, wanted to move into the tall building. Mr. Tang warned them about what he had heard, but they did not believe him. They broke the lock and climbed up to the building. They found the windows wide open, and the beams were free of dust. They became even more suspicious that the previous warnings were false. Jing slept in the outer room, while Wang slept in the inner room, leaving the middle room for their meeting place.

    154

    漏下二鼓，景先睡，王从中间持烛归寝，语景曰：“人言楼有祟，今数夕无事，可知前人无胆，为书吏所愚。”景未答，便闻楼梯下有履声徐徐登者。景呼王曰：“楼下何响？”王笑曰：“想楼下人故意来吓我耳。”少顷，其人连步上，景大窘，号呼；王亦起，持烛出。至中间，灯光收缩如萤火。二人惊，急添烧数烛。烛光稍大，而色终青绿。楼门洞开，门外立一青衣人，身长二尺，面长二尺，无目无口无鼻而有发，发直竖，亦长二尺许。二人大声唤楼下人来，此物遂倒身而下。窗外四面啾啾然作百种鬼声，房中什物皆动跃。二人几骇死，至鸡鸣始息。

    Explanation

    154

    At the second drum strike, Jing went to sleep. Wang took a candle and went to bed in the middle of the room. He said to Jing, "People say there is a ghost in the building. Now that several nights have passed without incident, we can see that the previous occupants lacked courage and were deceived by the clerks." Jing had not yet answered when he heard the sound of footsteps slowly ascending from the stairs. Jing called out to Wang, "What is the noise downstairs?" Wang laughed and said, "I think it's someone downstairs deliberately scaring me." After a short while, the person stepped up continuously, causing Jing to become greatly frightened and call out; Wang also got up and took a candle. They reached the middle of the room, where the light contracted like a firefly. The two were shocked and quickly added more candles. The light grew slightly brighter, but it remained greenish-blue. The door of the building was wide open, and a person dressed in blue stood outside, two feet tall with a face two feet long, no eyes, no mouth, no nose, but with hair that stood straight up, about two feet long. The two shouted loudly for someone downstairs to come, and the creature fell down. Outside the window, all around, there were various ghostly sounds, and the objects in the room began to move and jump. The two almost died of fright, and it stopped at the crow of the rooster.

    155

    次日，有老吏言：先是溧阳潘公督学时，岁试毕，明日当发案，潘已就寝。

    　　将二更，忽闻堂上击鼓声。潘遣僮问之，值堂吏曰顷有披发妇人从西考棚中出，上阶求见大人。吏以深夜，不敢传答。曰：“吾有冤，欲见大人陈诉。吾非人，乃鬼也。”吏惊仆，鬼因自击鼓。署中皆惶遽，不知所为。仆人张姓者，稍有胆，乃出问之。鬼曰：“大人见我何碍？今既不出，即烦致语：我，某县某生家仆妇也。主人涎我色奸我，不从，则鞭挞之。我语夫，夫醉后有不逊语，渠夜率家人杀我夫喂马。次早入房，命数人抱我行奸。我肆口詈之，遂大怒，立捶死，埋后园西石槽下。沉冤数载，今特来求申。”言毕大哭。张曰：“尔所告某生，今来就试否？”鬼曰：“来，已取第二等第十三名矣。”张入告潘公。公拆十三名视之，果某生姓名也，因令张出慰之曰：“当为尔檄府县查审。”鬼仰天长啸去。

    Explanation

    155

    The next day, an old official said: Previously, when Pan Gong, the scholar-official from Lixian, was supervising the education, after the annual examination was completed, the results were to be announced the next day, and Pan had already gone to bed. Around the second watch of the night, they suddenly heard drumming on the hall. Pan sent a servant to inquire about it, and the hall official said, "A moment ago, a woman with loose hair came out from the west examination hall and went up the stairs to see the master." The official was so shocked that he fell down, and the ghost then struck the drum herself. Everyone in the office was panic-stricken and did not know what to do. A servant named Zhang, who was somewhat brave, then went out to ask her. The ghost said, "What harm would it be for the master to see me? Since the master does not come out, please convey this message: I am the servant-woman of a certain scholar in a certain county. The master lusted after me and raped me. If I did not comply, he would beat me. I told my husband, and he spoke disrespectfully after drinking. He then killed my husband at night and fed him to the horses. The next morning, he entered the room and ordered several people to rape me. I cursed him loudly. He became very angry and immediately beat me to death, burying me under a stone trough in the west garden. My grievance has been buried for many years. Now I have come specifically to seek justice." After finishing speaking, she cried loudly. Zhang asked, "The scholar you mentioned, is he here for the examination now?" The ghost replied, "He is here, and has already been ranked 13th in the second class." Zhang went in to report to Pan Gong. Pan Gong opened the 13th name and saw that it was indeed the scholar's name. He then ordered Zhang to go out and comfort her, saying, "I will issue an order for the prefecture and county to investigate." The ghost looked up to the sky and roared before leaving.

    156

    潘次日即以访闻檄县，果于石槽下得女尸，遂置生于法。此是衙门一异闻，而楼上之怪，究不知何物也。王后举孝廉，景后官侍御。

    　　○符离楚客康熙十二年冬，有楚客贸易山东，由徐州至符离。约二鼓，北风劲甚，见道旁酒肆灯火方盛。入饮，即假宿焉。店中人似有难色，有老者怜其仓迫，谓曰：“方设馔以待远归之士，无余酒饮君。右有耳房，可以暂宿。”引客进。

    　　客饥渴甚，不能成寐，闻外间人马喧声，心疑之。起，从门隙窥，见店中匝地皆军士，据地饮食，谈说兵间事。皆不甚晓。少顷，众相呼曰：“主将来矣。”

    Explanation

    156

    The next day, Pan immediately issued an order to the county upon hearing the news, and indeed, a female corpse was found under a stone trough. The perpetrator was then put to death according to the law. This is an unusual occurrence in the government office, and the strange phenomenon on the upper floor still does not know what it is. The princess was selected as a scholar-official, and the princess held the position of a certain official.

    ○ A Guest from Chu in Fulü

    In the winter of the twelfth year of the Kangxi reign, a guest from Chu was trading in Shandong and traveled from Xuzhou to Fulü. Around midnight, the northern wind was very strong, and he saw a wine shop by the roadside with its lights very bright. He entered to drink and immediately rented a room for the night. The people in the inn seemed reluctant, but an elderly man, moved by his urgency, said, "We are just setting up a meal to welcome a traveler returning from afar. We have no extra wine for you, but there is a small room to the right where you can stay temporarily." He led the guest inside.

    The guest was very hungry and thirsty and could not sleep. He heard the noise of people and horses outside and became suspicious. He got up and peeked through the crack in the door, seeing that the inn was filled with soldiers sitting on the ground, eating and drinking, and talking about military affairs. None of them fully understood the topic. A short while later, everyone called out to each other, saying, "The leader is coming!"

    157

    远远有呵殿声，咸趋出迎候。见纸灯数十，错落而来，一雄壮长髯者下马，入店上坐，众人伺立门外。店主人具酒食上，啜有声。毕，呼军士入曰：“尔辈远出久矣，各且归队，吾亦少憩，俟文书至，再行未迟。”众诺而退。随呼曰：“阿七，来！”有少年军士从店左门出，店中人闭门避去。阿七引长髯者入左门，门隙有灯射出。客从右耳房潜至左门隙窥之，见门内有竹床，无睡具，灯置地上。

    　　长髯者引手撼其头，头即坠下，放置床上。阿七代捉其左右臂，亦皆坠下，分置床内外。然后倒身卧于床，阿七摇其身，自腰下对裂作两段，倒于地。灯亦旋灭。

    　　客悸甚，飞趋耳房，以袖掩面卧，辗转不能寐。

    　　遥闻鸡鸣一二次，渐觉身冷。启袖，见天色微明，身乃卧乱树中。旷野无屋，亦无坟堆。冒寒行三里许，始有店。店主人方开门，迓客问客来何早？客告以所遇，并问所宿为何地？曰：“此间皆旧战场也。”

    Explanation

    157

    Far in the distance, the sound of a ceremonial procession could be heard, and everyone rushed out to greet it. They saw several paper lanterns scattered as they approached. A strong and bearded man dismounted and entered the store, taking a seat. Everyone waited outside the door. The store owner served wine and food, and the sounds of drinking and eating could be heard. After finishing, he called the soldiers inside and said, "You have been out for a long time; each of you should return to your formation. I will also rest for a while. Wait until the documents arrive, and we will continue our journey." The soldiers nodded and left. He then called out, "Ah Qi, come!" A young soldier emerged from the left door of the store, and the people inside closed the door and stepped back. Ah Qi led the bearded man into the left door, and light shone through the gap. The guest sneaked from the right ear room to peek through the gap and saw a bamboo bed inside the door, with no bedding, and a lamp placed on the ground.

    The bearded man raised his hand and shook the head, which fell off and was placed on the bed. Ah Qi caught the man's left and right arms, which also fell off and were placed on the bed, one inside and one outside. Then the man lay down on the bed, and Ah Qi shook his body, causing him to split into two parts from the waist down, falling to the ground. The lamp also went out.

    The guest was extremely frightened and rushed to the ear room, lying down with his sleeve covering his face, restlessly unable to sleep.

    He heard the rooster crow once or twice, and gradually felt the cold. He uncovered his sleeve and saw the sky lightening. He realized he was lying in a chaotic forest. The open field had no houses, nor any mounds. He braved the cold to walk about three miles before finding a store. The store owner was just opening the door and greeted the guest, asking why he had come so early. The guest told him what he had experienced and asked what place he had stayed in. The store owner replied, "This area is all old battlefields."

    158

    ○徐氏疫亡雍正壬子冬，杭城徐姓嫁女某家。杭俗：弥月行双回门礼。是日，婿饮于徐，徐为设榻厅楼下。婿就帐未寝，闻楼梯有行步声，见四人下楼立灯前：一纱帽朱衣，一方巾道服，馀二人皆暖帽皮袍，相与叹息。少顷，有女装者五人，亦来掩泣于灯前。有高年妇人指帐中曰：“可托此人？”纱帽者摇手曰：“无济。”且泣曰：“吾当求张先生存吾门一线耳。”互相劝慰，或坐或行。婿悸极，不能出声。迨五鼓，方相扶上楼。桌下忽走出一黑面人，急上梯挽红衣者曰：“独不能为我留一线耶！”红衣者唯唯。时鸡已鸣，黑面人奔桌下去。婿候窗微亮，披衣入内，叩楼上何人所居，曰：“新年供祖先神像，无人住也。”婿上楼观像，衣饰状貌与所见不同，心不解所以，秘而不言。

    Explanation

    158

    In the winter of the Renzi year of the Yongzheng era, a family named Xu in Hangzhou City married off their daughter to a certain family. According to the customs of Hangzhou, the bride would return to her parents' home twice after childbirth, a ritual known as the "double return visit." On that day, the groom drank at the Xu family's home, and the Xu family set up a bed in the hall downstairs. The groom lay down in the bed but did not sleep, as he heard footsteps on the stairs.
