Dong then woke up and quickly told his close relatives. He visited the county of Le Ping to verify it, and indeed, a woman was struck dead by lightning, with the time and date all matching. While reading the register, Dong secretly glanced at the names: the first was Cheng Junxian, and the second was Wang Peikui. The next year, they all came true.

    198

    ○捉鬼婺源汪启明，迁居上河之进士第，其族汪进士波故宅也。乾隆甲午四月，一日，夜梦魇良久，寤，见一鬼逼帷立，高与屋齐。汪素勇，突起搏之。鬼急夺门走，而误触墙，状甚狼狈。汪追及之，抱其腰。忽阴风起，残灯灭，不见鬼面目，但觉手甚冷，腰粗如瓮。欲喊集家人，而声噤不能出。久之，极力大叫，家人齐应。鬼形缩小如婴儿。各持炬来照，则所握者坏丝绵一团也。窗外瓦砾乱掷如雨，家人咸怖，劝释之。汪笑曰：“鬼党虚吓人耳，奚能为？倘释之，将助为祟，不如杀一鬼以惩百鬼。”因左手握鬼，右手取家人火炬烧之。膊有声，鲜血迸射，臭气不可闻。迨晓，四邻惊集，闻其臭，无不掩鼻者。地上血厚寸许，腥腻如胶，竟不知何鬼也。王葑亭舍人为作《捉鬼行》纪其事。

    Explanation

    198

    A Ghost-Catching Incident in Wuyuan

    Wang Qiming from Wuyuan moved to a house in Shanghe that was once an imperial examination hall, the former residence of his relative, Wang Jinshi Bo. In the fourth month of the year 1774 during the reign of Emperor Qianlong, one day, Wang Qiming had a nightmare and was troubled by it for a long time. Upon waking, he saw a ghost standing close to the curtains, as tall as the house itself. Wang Qiming, who was naturally brave, suddenly attacked the ghost. The ghost hastily tried to escape through the door but accidentally bumped into the wall, appearing very disheveled. Wang Qiming caught up with the ghost and grabbed its waist. Suddenly, a cold wind arose, and the dim light went out, leaving the ghost's face unseen. He could only feel that the ghost's hands were very cold and its waist was as thick as a jar. He wanted to call for his family, but his voice was paralyzed and could not be heard. After a while, he tried to shout loudly, and his family responded. The ghost's form then shrank to the size of an infant. Each family member held a torch to illuminate the scene, and they found that what they were holding was a clump of damaged silk padding. Debris was being thrown outside like rain, and the family members were all frightened, urging Wang Qiming to release the ghost. Wang Qiming smiled and said, "The ghost's companions are just trying to scare us with false threats; what can they do? If we release it, it might help others cause mischief. It is better to kill one ghost to punish many." He held the ghost with his left hand and burned it with a torch from his family using his right hand. The sound of burning flesh was heard, and blood splattered. The smell was so strong that it could not be ignored. By dawn, neighbors gathered in shock, covering their noses at the smell. The blood on the ground was about an inch thick, sticky and viscous. They did not know what kind of ghost it was. Wang Fengting, a writer, composed a poem titled "The Capture of a Ghost" to record the incident.

    199

    ○某侍郎异梦乾隆二十年，某侍郎督视黄河，驻扎陶庄。岁除夕矣，侍郎素勤，骑匹马，跟从者四人，持悬火巡河。行冰淖中，一望黄茅白苇，自觉凄然。见草中有支布帐而露烛光者，召问，则主簿某也。侍郎爱其勤，大加夸奖。主簿请曰：“大人除夕至此，夜已三鼓，天寒风紧，回馆尚远，某有度岁酒肴，献上一醉何如？”

    　　侍郎笑而受之。饮数觞，仍归公馆，倦，解衣卧。

    　　梦中依旧骑马看河，觉所行处便非前境，最后黄沙茫茫。行二里许，有火光出庐舍间，就之，老妪迎门，细视，即其亡母太夫人也。见侍郎惊曰：“汝何至此？”侍郎告以奉命看河之故。太夫人曰：“此非人间，汝既来，如何能归？”

    Explanation

    199

    During the 20th year of the Qianlong Emperor's reign, a certain vice-minister was overseeing the Yellow River and stationed in Taobranch. It was the eve of the lunar New Year, and the vice-minister, known for his diligence, rode a horse with four attendants, carrying a suspended lamp to patrol the river. They were traveling through a frozen marsh, where they saw vast stretches of yellow grass and white reeds, which made them feel desolate. They noticed a tent set up in the grass with a candlelight visible, and they summoned the person inside. It turned out to be a certain clerk. The vice-minister admired his diligence and praised him highly. The clerk then invited, "Your Excellency, you have come here on New Year's Eve, and it is already the third watch of the night. The weather is cold and windy, and the return to the official residence is still far. I have prepared a New Year's Eve feast. How about offering a drink to get drunk together?"

    The vice-minister smiled and accepted the invitation. After drinking several cups, he returned to the official residence, feeling tired, and took off his clothes to lie down.

    In his dream, he continued to ride his horse and inspect the river. He realized that the place he was going was no longer familiar, and eventually, he found himself in a vast expanse of yellow sand. After walking about two miles, he saw a light coming from a hut. Approaching it, an old woman greeted him at the door. Upon closer inspection, he recognized her as his deceased mother, the Lady Mother. Seeing the vice-minister, she was surprised and asked, "Why are you here?" The vice-minister explained that he was ordered to inspect the river. The Lady Mother replied, "This is not the human world. You have come here, so how can you return?"

    200

    侍郎方悟太夫人已亡，己身已死。遂大哭。太夫人曰：“河西有老和尚，法力甚大，吾带汝往求之。”侍郎随行。

    　　至一庙，庄严如王者居，南面坐一老僧，闭目无言。侍郎跪阶下，再拜，僧不为礼。侍郎问：“我奉天子命看河，因何至此？”僧又无言。侍郎怒曰：“我为天子大臣，纵有罪当死，亦须示我，使我心服，何嘿嘿如哑羊耶？”老僧笑曰：“汝杀人多矣，禄折尽矣，尚何问为。”侍郎曰：“我杀人虽多，皆国法应诛之人，非我罪也。”僧曰：“汝当日办案时，果只知有国法乎，抑贪图迎合固宠迁官乎？”取案上如意，直指其心。侍郎觉冷气一条直逼五脏，心<走吉>々然跳不止，汗如雨下，惶悚不能言。良久，曰：“某知罪矣。嗣后改过何如？”僧曰：“汝非改过之人，今日恰非汝寿尽之日。”顾左右沙弥云：“领他出，放他归。”

    Explanation

    200

    The official suddenly realized that his mother-in-law had passed away and that he himself was dead. He began to weep loudly. His mother-in-law said, "There is an elderly monk in the Western River region with great magical power. I will take you to seek him out." The official followed her.

    Upon arriving at a temple, they found it as grand as the residence of a king. Facing south, there sat an elderly monk, his eyes closed and silent. The official knelt at the bottom of the steps, performed a formal bow twice, but the monk did not return the gesture. The official asked, "I was sent by the emperor to oversee the river, why am I here?" The monk remained silent. The official became angry and said, "As a high-ranking official of the emperor, even if I have committed a crime worthy of death, you should still tell me so that I may be convinced. Why are you silent like a dumb sheep?" The old monk smiled and said, "You have killed many people, your good fortune is exhausted, so why do you still ask?" The official replied, "Although I have killed many, they were all people who deserved to be punished by law, not my fault." The monk said, "When you were handling cases, did you truly only know the law, or were you merely trying to please the emperor to gain favor and promotion?" He took a wish-fulfilling scepter from the table and pointed it directly at the official's heart. The official felt a cold breath pierce his five organs, his heart beating rapidly, and he broke out in a cold sweat, too frightened to speak. After a long while, he said, "I understand my fault. What if I reform from now on?" The monk said, "You are not someone who will reform, today is not the end of your life." Looking around, he told the young monks, "Lead him out, let him go home."

    201

    沙弥同行，昏黑中，开其拳，出一小珠，光照黄河工次一段，直至陶庄公馆，历历如白昼。太夫人迎来，泣曰：“儿虽归，不久即来，无多时别也。”遂依原路归，及门下马而醒，日已午矣。

    　　众河员贺节盈门，疑侍郎最勤，何以元旦不起？侍郎亦不肯明言其故。是年四月病呕血，竟以不起。此事裘文达公为余言。

    Explanation

    201

    A group of novice monks were traveling together in the darkness when one of them opened his fist and produced a small pearl. The light from the pearl illuminated a section of the Yellow River, all the way to the Táo Zhuāng Gongguǎn, clearly visible as if in broad daylight. The elder lady welcomed them and wept, saying, "My son will return, but not for long; we will not be apart for long." They then returned along the original path, and upon reaching the gate and dismounting, he woke up. It was already noon.

    Many officials of the river bureau filled the hall to congratulate, wondering why the Attendant Minister was the most diligent yet did not rise on New Year's Day. The Attendant Minister would not clearly explain the reason. In that April, he fell ill and vomited blood, eventually passing away. This matter was told to me by Qiu Wéndá Gong.

    202

    ○奉行次盘古成案《北史》称“毗骞国王头长三尺，至今不死”，予尝疑其诞。康熙间，浙人方文木泛海，被风吹至一处，宫殿巍峨，上署“毗骞殿”三字，方大惊，俯伏殿外。两霞帔者引之入。有长头王上坐，冕如巨桶，珍珠四垂，须拂拂然相触有声，问文木曰：“汝浙人乎？”曰：“然。”王曰：“离此五十万里矣。”赐文木板，米大如枣。

    　　文木知王神灵，跪拜求归。王顾谓侍臣曰：“取第一次盘古皇帝成案替他一查。”文木大骇，叩头曰：“盘古皇帝有几个乎？”王曰：“天地无始无终，有十二万年，便有一盘古。今来朝天者，已有盘古万万馀人，我安能记明数目？但元会运世之说，已被宋朝人邵尧夫说破。可惜历来开辟总奉行第一次开辟之成案，尚无人说破，故风吹汝来，亦要说破此故，以晓世人耳。”文木不解所谓。王曰：“我且问汝：世间福善祸淫，何以有报有不报耶？天地鬼神，何以有灵有不灵耶？

    Explanation

    202

    Translation:

    In accordance with the precedent set by the first Pangu emperor, the Beishi (Northern Histories) records that the king of Pianqian had a head three feet long and has not died to this day. I once doubted the veracity of this claim. During the Kangxi period, a man from Zhejiang named Fang Wenmu was sailing at sea when he was blown by the wind to a place. The palace was grand, with the words "Pianqian Hall" inscribed above it. Fang was greatly surprised and knelt outside the hall. Two women in霞帔 (ceremonial garments) led him inside. There sat a king with a long head, wearing a crown shaped like a giant bucket, adorned with pearls hanging down, and a long beard that touched and made a sound when it moved. The king asked Fang, "Are you from Zhejiang?" Fang replied, "Yes." The king said, "This place is fifty million miles away from there." He then gave Fang a board and rice grains as large as dates.

    Fang knew that the king was divine and knelt to beg to return home. The king turned to his attendants and said, "Fetch the first case of the first Pangu emperor to examine for him." Fang was greatly startled and knelt, asking, "How many Pangu emperors are there?" The king replied, "The heavens and earth have no beginning and no end. Every twelve thousand years, there is a Pangu. Those who come to pay homage to heaven have already numbered in the millions of Pangu emperors. How could I possibly keep track of the exact number? However, the theory of the cyclical nature of time and the universe, which was already explained by the Song dynasty scholar Shao Yong, has not been broken. It is unfortunate that throughout history, people have always followed the precedent set by the first Pangu emperor without understanding it. That is why the wind brought you here—to reveal this truth and make it known to the people." Fang did not understand what he meant. The king said, "Let me ask you: Why are there cases where good deeds are rewarded and evil deeds are punished, and why are there cases where they are not? Why are there times when the heavens, earth, gods, and spirits are effective, and why are there times when they are not?"

    203

    修仙学佛，何以有成有不成耶？红颜薄命，而何以不薄者亦有耶？才子命穷，可何以不穷者亦多耶？一饮一啄，何以有前定耶？日食山崩，何以有劫数耶？彼善推算者，何以能知而不能免耶？彼怨天尤天者，天胡不降之罚耶？“文木不能答。

    　　王曰：“呜呼！今世上所行，皆成案也。当第一次世界开辟十二万年之中，所有人物事宜，亦非造物者之有心造作，偶然随气化之推迁，半明半暗，忽是忽非，如泻水落地，偶成方圆；如孩童着棋，随手下子。既定之后，竟成一本板板帐簿，生铁铸成矣。乾坤将毁时，天帝将此册交代与第二次开辟之天帝，命其依样奉行，丝毫不许变动，以故人意与天心往往参差不齐。世上人终日忙忙急急，正如木偶傀儡，喑中为之牵丝者。成败巧拙，久已前定，人自不知耳。”文木恍然，曰：“然则今之所谓三皇五帝，即前此之三皇五帝乎？今之二十一史中之事，即前此之二十一史中之事乎？”王曰：“然。”

    Explanation

    203

    Translation:

    Why do some succeed and others fail in seeking immortality or enlightenment? Why do beautiful women often have short lives, yet some do not? Why do talented individuals often face poverty, yet many do not? Why is every event in life seemingly predetermined? Why do natural disasters like solar eclipses and earthquakes signify periods of great calamity? Why can those skilled in divination predict events but cannot escape them? Why does heaven not punish those who blame it? Wenmu could not answer.

    The king said, "Alas! Everything in the world today is predetermined. During the first creation of the world, which lasted twelve million years, all events and circumstances were not deliberately created by the Creator, but rather arose by chance, half in the light and half in the dark, sometimes right and sometimes wrong, like water spilled on the ground, forming circles and squares by chance; like a child playing chess, placing pieces at random. Once set, it became a rigid ledger, cast in solid iron. When the world is about to be destroyed, the Heavenly Emperor will hand over this ledger to the Heavenly Emperor of the second creation, commanding him to follow the same pattern without any change, which is why human intentions often do not align with the will of heaven. People in the world are constantly busy and hurried, just like wooden puppets, being controlled by unseen strings. Success, failure, skill, and clumsiness have long been predetermined, and people are unaware of it." Wenmu suddenly understood and said, "Then is what is called the Three Sovereigns and Five Emperors of today the same as the Three Sovereigns and Five Emperors of the past? Are the events in the Twenty-One Histories of today the same as those of the past?" The king replied, "Yes."

    204

    言未毕，侍臣捧一册至，上书“康熙三年，浙江方水木泛海至毗骞国，应将前定天机漏泄，俾世人共晓，仍送归浙江”云云。文木拜谢，临别泣下。王摇手曰：“子胡然？十二万年之后，我与汝又会于此矣！何必泣为？”既而笑曰：“我错，我错！此一泣，亦是十二万年中原有两条眼泪，故照样誊录，我不必劝止也。”文木问王年寿，左右曰：“王与第一次盘古同生，不与第千万次盘古同死。”文木曰：“王不死，则乾坤毁时，王将安归？”王曰：“我沙身也，历劫不坏。万物毁坏，变为泥沙而极矣。我先居于极坏之处，劫火不能烧，洪水不能淹，惟为恶风所吹荡。上至九天，下至九渊，殊觉劳顿。每每枯坐数万年，等盘古出世，觉日子太多，殊可厌耳。”言毕，口嘘气吹文木，文木乘空而起，仍至海船上。

    Explanation

    204

    While speaking was not yet finished, a courtier holding a scroll came, inscribed on top was "In the third year of the Kangxi era, Fang Shui Mu from Zhejiang sailed to Biquan Kingdom, should disclose the previously determined heavenly secrets, so that all people may understand, still send back to Zhejiang." Wenmu bowed and thanked, at parting wept. The king shook his hand and said, "Why so? After twelve million years, I and you will meet again here! Why cry for?" After a while, he laughed and said, "I was wrong, I was wrong! This one cry is also the two tears that originally existed in twelve million years, so it is copied verbatim, I do not need to stop you." Wenmu asked about the king's lifespan, the attendants said, "The king and the first Pangu were born together, not with the thousandth Pangu dying together." Wenmu said, "If the king does not die, where will he go when heaven and earth are destroyed?" The king said, "I am a body of sand, enduring through calamities without being destroyed. All things are destroyed and become mud and sand, reaching their limit. I first reside in the most desolate place, calamitous fire cannot burn, floods cannot drown, only blown away by evil winds. Up to the ninth heaven, down to the ninth abyss, feels very tiring. Often sit dry for tens of thousands of years, waiting for Pangu to be born, feel the days are too many, very annoying indeed." After saying this, he exhaled breath to blow Wenmu, Wenmu rises into the air, still returns to the ship at sea.

    205

    月馀归浙，以此语毛西河先生。先生曰：“人但知万事前定，而不知所以前定之故，今得是说，方始豁然。”

    　　●卷六○猪道人即郑曼阝明季，华山寺中养一猪，年代甚久，毛尽脱落，能持斋，不食秽物，闻诵经声，则叩首作顶礼状，合寺僧以“道人”呼之。

    　　一夕，老病将死，寺中住持湛一和尚者，素有道行，将往他处说法，召其徒渭曰：“猪道人若死，必碎割之，分其肉啖寺邻。”众僧虽诺之，而心以为非。

    Explanation

    205

    After more than a month, he returned to Zhejiang and told this story to Mr. Mao Xihé. Mr. Mao said, "People only know that all things are predestined, but they do not know the reason why it is predestined. Now that I have heard this explanation, I finally understand clearly."

    Chapter Sixty: The Taoist Pig (Pig Daoist) During the late Ming dynasty, in Huashan Temple, they raised a pig that had lived for a very long time, and all its hair had fallen out. It could fast and did not eat impure things. When it heard the sound of reciting scriptures, it would bow its head and make a gesture of reverence. All the monks in the temple called it 'Taoist.'

    One night, the pig was old and sick, near death. The abbot of the temple, Venerable Zhan Yi, who had always practiced the Way, was about to go to another place to preach. He summoned his disciple Wei and said, "If the Taoist pig dies, it must be cut into pieces, and its meat must be given to the neighbors of the temple to eat." The monks agreed, but in their hearts, they thought it was wrong.

    206

    已而猪死，乃私埋之。湛一归，问猪死作何处分。众僧以实告，且曰：“佛法戒杀，故某等已埋葬之。”湛一大惊，即往埋猪处，以杖击地哭曰：“吾负汝！吾负汝！”众僧问故，曰：“三十年后，某村有一清贵官无辜而受极刑者，即此猪也。猪前生系宰官，有负心事，知恶劫难逃，托生为畜，来求超度。我故立意以刀解法厌胜之，不意为汝辈庸流所误。然此亦大数，无可挽回也。”

    　　崇祯间，某村翰林郑曼阝素行端方，在东林党籍中，为其舅吴某诬其杖母事，凌迟处死，天下冤之。其时湛一业已圆寂，众方服其通因果也。

    　　○徐先生宿松石赞臣家饶于财，兄弟数人，资各数万。宿俗：富饶之家，每日必设一家常饭置外厅堂，不拘来客，皆就食焉，号曰“燕坐”。忽有徐姓者，清瘦微须，亦来就食，指门外青山曰：“君等曾见过山跳乎？”曰：“未也。”徐以手指三撮，山果三跃。众人大奇之，呼为先生。

    Explanation

    206

    After a while, the pig died, and it was secretly buried. Zhan Yi returned home and asked what had been done with the dead pig. The monks told the truth and said, "Buddhist teachings forbid killing, so we have already buried it." Zhan Yi was greatly shocked and immediately went to the place where the pig was buried, striking the ground with his staff and crying out, "I have failed you! I have failed you!" The monks asked him why, and he replied, "Thirty years from now, there will be an upright and noble official in a certain village who will be executed unjustly. That official is this pig. In its previous life, it was a high-ranking official who had committed a betrayal. Knowing that it could not escape its evil fate, it was reborn as an animal to seek salvation. I had intended to use a knife to perform a ritual to subdue it, but I did not expect to be misled by you common folk. However, this is also part of great destiny, which cannot be undone."

    During the reign of Emperor Chongzhen, there was a man named Zheng Man from a certain village who was known for his integrity and was listed among the Donglin Party. He was falsely accused by his uncle, Wu, of beating his mother, and was executed by death by slicing, which the world felt was unjust. At that time, Zhan Yi had already passed away, and people admired his understanding of cause and effect.

    ○ Mr. Xu, who lived in Susong, was a man of considerable wealth. He had several brothers, each of whom had assets amounting to tens of thousands. In the local custom, wealthy families would prepare a simple meal every day in the outer hall for any visitors, who were all welcome to eat, and this was called "Yan Zuo." One day, a man surnamed Xu, who was thin and had a small mustache, also came to eat. He pointed to the green mountains outside and asked, "Have you ever seen a mountain jump?" They replied, "No." Xu then pointed his finger three times, and the mountain indeed leaped three times. Everyone was greatly amazed and called him Mr. Xu.

    207

    先生谓赞臣曰：“君等家资虽富，能炼丹，可加十倍。”群兄弟惑其言，置炉设灶，各出银母数千以求子金。二房弟妇某氏，素黠，暗置铜于银母中，不与先生见。亡何炭炽，风雷起于屋上，劈碎瓦数片。先生骂曰：“此必有假银搀杂，致干鬼神怒。”询之，果然，合家骇服。先生置铜盘于空中，呼曰：“丹来。”

    　　盘中铿然，一锭坠下；连呼之，铿铿之声不已，大锭小锭齐落于盘。先生曰：“炼大丹在深山中人迹不到之所，可致千万，盍随我往江西庐山乎？”石氏兄弟愈喜，即载银数万随先生往。未半途，先生上岸去矣。夜，率大盗数十明火执杖来劫取银，曰：“毋怖，我虽盗魁，然颇有良心。念汝等供养我甚诚，当留下千金，俾汝等还乡。”于是，石家兄弟以全数与之，惘惘然归。

    Explanation

    207

    The master told Zan Chen, saying, "Though you all are wealthy in family assets, if you can refine elixirs, you can multiply your wealth tenfold." The brothers were bewildered by his words and set up furnaces and stoves, each contributing several thousand silver ingots to seek offspring gold. One of the brothers' wives, always cunning, secretly placed copper in the silver ingots without showing it to the master. Soon the charcoal blazed, and a wind and thunder arose on the roof, shattering several tiles. The master shouted, "This must be due to fake silver being mixed in, which has angered the spirits." When asked, it turned out to be true, and the whole family was terrified and convinced. The master placed a copper tray in the air and called out, "The elixir comes."

    A clanging sound was heard in the tray, and a piece of gold fell. Continuing to call, the clanging sounds did not stop, and large and small pieces fell into the tray together. The master said, "Refining the great elixir in a deep mountain where no one goes can yield millions. Why not follow me to Mount Lu in Jiangxi?" The brothers of the Shi family became even happier and immediately loaded tens of thousands of silver and followed the master. Before halfway, the master went ashore and left. At night, leading dozens of bandits with torches and weapons, they came to rob the silver. "Do not be afraid, though I am the leader of the bandits, I still have a conscience. Thinking that you have been very sincere in supporting me, I will leave you a thousand gold pieces so you can return to your hometown." Thus, the brothers of the Shi family gave all the silver and returned home in a daze.

    208

    十年后，安庆按察使衙门役吏差人来召赞臣，曰：“狱有大盗徐某，请君相见。”赞臣不得已往，果见先生。先生曰：“我劫数已尽，死亦何辞。但念我数年交谊，为葬其遗骸。”脱手上金钏四只与赞臣为棺费，且曰：“我大限在七月一日未时，汝可来送。”至期，赞臣往市曹，见先生反接待斩。忽胯下出一小儿作先生音曰：“看杀我！看杀我！”须臾头落，小儿亦不见。其时臬使为祖廷圭，满洲正蓝旗人。

    　　○秦毛人湖广郧阳房县有房山，高险幽远，四面石洞如房。多毛人，长丈馀，遍体生毛，往往出山食人鸡犬，拒之者必遭攫搏。以枪炮击之，铅子皆落地，不能伤。

    　　相传制之法，只须以手合拍，叫曰：“筑长城！筑长城！”则毛人仓皇逃去。余有世好张君名者，曾官其地，试之果然。土人曰：“秦时筑长城，人避入山中，岁久不死，遂成此怪。见人必问：”城修完否？‘以故知其所怯而吓之。“数千年后犹畏秦法，可想见始皇之威。

    208

    Ten years later, a clerk from the office of the Anqing Inspector of Judicial Affairs summoned Zan Chen, saying, "There is a major criminal named Xu in the prison. Please come to meet him." Zan Chen had no choice but to go, and indeed, he met the gentleman. The gentleman said, "My fate has come to an end, and what is there to fear in death? But I think of our years of friendship, and I ask you to bury my remains." He removed four bracelets from his wrist and gave them to Zan Chen as funeral expenses, and also said, "My appointed time is at noon on the first day of July. You may come to see me off." On that day, Zan Chen went to the public square, where he saw the gentleman being executed. Suddenly, a little boy appeared from under his leg and spoke in the gentleman's voice, saying, "Look at me being killed! Look at me being killed!" Soon his head fell off, and the little boy also disappeared. At that time, the Inspector of Judicial Affairs was Zu Tingui, a Manchu from the Blue Banner.

    ○ Qin Hairy People In the region of Fanyang, Yunyang, Huguang, there is a mountain called Fangshan, which is high, dangerous, and remote, surrounded by stone caves like rooms. There are many hairy people, over ten feet long, covered with hair all over their bodies. They often come out of the mountain to eat people's chickens and dogs, and those who resist will surely be grabbed and attacked. When shot with guns, the bullets all fall to the ground and cannot harm them.

    It is said that the method to control them is to clap hands and shout, "Build the Great Wall! Build the Great Wall!" Then the hairy people will flee in panic. I have a friend named Zhang Zong, who is a friend from my generation, and he once served in that place. He tried it and it was indeed so. The locals said, "During the Qin dynasty, the Great Wall was built, and people fled into the mountains. After many years, they did not die, and thus they became these strange beings. When they see people, they always ask, 'Is the wall built yet?' Therefore, knowing their fear, we can scare them." Even thousands of years later, they still fear the Qin laws, one can imagine the might of the First Emperor.

    209

    ○貘房山有貘兽，好食铜铁而不伤人。凡民间犁锄刀斧之类，见则涎流，食之如腐。城门上所包铁皮，尽为所啖。

    　　○人同喀尔喀有兽，似猴非猴，中国人呼为“人同”，番人呼为“噶里”。往往窥探穹庐，乞人饮食，或乞取小刀烟具之属。被人呼喝，即弃而走。有某将军畜养之，唤使豆樵汲等事，颇能服役。居一年，将军任满，归。人同立马前，泪下如雨，相从十馀里，麾之不去。将军曰：“汝之不能从我至中国，犹我之不能从汝居此土也。汝送我可止矣。”人同悲鸣而去，犹屡回头仰视云。

    　　○人虾国初，有前明逸老某欲殉难，而不肯死于刀绳水火。念乐死莫如信陵君，以醇酒妇人自戕。仿而为之，多娶姬妾，终日荒淫。如是数年，卒不得死，但督脉断矣，头弯背驼，伛偻如熟虾，匍匐而行。人戏呼之曰“人虾”。如是者二十馀年，八十四岁方死。王子坚先生言幼时犹见此翁。

    Explanation

    209

    ○ Mòfáng Mountain has a mò beast, which loves eating copper and iron but does not harm people. All farming tools like plows, hoes, knives, and axes, if seen, will drool and eat them as if they were rotten. The iron sheets on the city gates are all eaten by it.

    ○ In the region of Ren Tóng, there is a creature that resembles a monkey but is not a monkey. The Chinese call it "Ren Tóng," and the locals call it "Gālǐ." It often peeks into yurts and asks for food, or asks for small knives and smoking utensils. If called out, it immediately abandons and runs away. A certain general kept it and called it to fetch beans, chop wood, and draw water, and it could serve well. After a year, the general's term was over, and he returned. The Ren Tóng stood in front of him, crying like rain, and followed for over ten miles, unable to be dismissed. The general said, "Your inability to follow me to China is like my inability to follow you here. You can stop now." The Ren Tóng wept and departed, still looking back and up.

    ○ At the beginning of the Qing Dynasty, there was an elderly man from the Ming Dynasty who wanted to die for his country but did not want to die by knife, rope, water, or fire. He thought that dying from excessive drinking and women was the best way, so he imitated the behavior of Lord Xianling, who died from excessive drinking and women. He followed this path, taking many concubines and indulging in debauchery. This went on for several years, but he never died. However, his督脉 (dū mài, a meridian in traditional Chinese medicine) was broken, and his head bent, his back curved, and he hunched over like a cooked shrimp, crawling on the ground. People jokingly called him "Ren Xiā." This went on for over twenty years, and he died at the age of 84. Mr. Wang Zǐjiān said that he still saw this old man when he was young.

    210

    ○鸭嬖江西高安县僮杨贵，年十九岁，微有姿，性柔和。有狎之者，都无所拒。一日夏间，浴于池中，忽一雄鸭飞啮其臀，而以尾扑之作抽叠状，击之不去。须叟死矣，尾后拖下肉茎一缕，臊水涓涓然。合署人大笑，呼杨为“鸭嬖”。

    　　○精无锡华生，美风姿，家住水沟头，密迩圣庙。庙前有桥甚阔，多为游人憩息。

    　　夏日，生上桥纳凉，日将夕，步入学宫，见间道侧一小门，有女徘徊户下。生心动，试前乞火。女笑而与之，亦以目相注。生更欲进词，而女已阖扉，遂记门径而出。次日再往，女已在门相待。生叩姓氏，知为学中门斗女，且曰：“妾舍逼隘，不避耳目；卿家咫尺，但得静僻一室，妾当夜分相就。卿明夕可待我于门。”

    Explanation

    210

    ○Yang Gui, a nineteen-year-old servant from Gao'an County in Jiangxi Province, had a slight beauty and a gentle disposition. Those who were intimate with him did not refuse them. One day during the summer, he bathed in a pond. Suddenly, a male duck flew and bit his buttocks, and with its tail, it made a rhythmic flapping motion. He could not drive it away. Soon after, he died. A piece of flesh trailed from his tail, and smelly water dripped from it. Everyone in the office laughed and called Yang by the name "duck favorite."

    ○Hua Sheng, a man from Wuxi with a beautiful appearance, lived near the water ditch, close to the sacred temple. There was a wide bridge in front of the temple, often used by tourists for resting.

    One summer day, Hua Sheng went up to the bridge to cool off. As the day was nearing its end, he walked into the school. He saw a small door on the side of a side road, and a woman was lingering by the door. Hua Sheng's heart was stirred, and he tried to borrow a light. The woman smiled and gave it to him, also looking at him with her eyes. Hua Sheng wanted to say more, but the woman had already closed the door. He then remembered the path and left. The next day, he went again, and the woman was already waiting at the door. Hua Sheng asked for her name and learned that she was the daughter of the gatekeeper of the school. She said, "My place is cramped and narrow, not avoiding eyes and ears; your home is nearby, but if only there is a quiet and secluded room, I will come to you in the middle of the night. You can wait for me at the door tomorrow night."

    211

    生喜急归，诳妇以畏暑，宜独寝，洒扫外室，潜候于门。女果夜来，携手入室，生喜过望。自是每夕必至。

    　　数月后，生渐羸弱。父母潜窥寝处，见生与女并坐嬉笑，亟排闼入，寂然无人，乃严诘生生备道始末，父母大骇，偕生赴学宫踪迹，绝无向时门径；遍访门斗中，亦并无有女者。共知为妖，乃广延僧道，请符，一无所效。其父研朱砂与生曰：“俟其来时，潜印女身，便可踪迹。”生俟女睡，以朱砂散置发上，而女不知。次日，父母偕人入圣庙遍寻，绝无影响。忽闻邻妇诟小儿曰：“甫换新裤，又染猩红，从何处染来耶？”其父闻而异之，往视，小儿裤上尽朱砂，因究儿所自。曰：“适骑学宫前负碑龟首，不觉染此。”往视之首，朱砂在焉。

    Explanation

    211

    The man was delighted and hurriedly returned, deceiving his wife by saying he was afraid of the heat and should sleep alone. He swept and cleaned the outer room and waited secretly at the door. The woman indeed came at night, held hands with him, and entered the room. The man's joy exceeded his expectations. From then on, she came every night.

    After several months, the man gradually became weak. The parents secretly peered into his sleeping place and saw him sitting with the woman, laughing together. They hurriedly pushed open the door and entered, but there was no one there. They severely questioned the man, who thoroughly explained the beginning and end. The parents were greatly frightened and accompanied the man to the school to track down the woman, but there were no traces of the previous entrance. They visited every door and corner, but there was no woman there. Everyone knew it was a demon, so they widely invited monks and Taoists to bring talismans and charms, but nothing was effective. The father ground cinnabar and told the man, "Wait for her to come, and secretly imprint her body with cinnabar, then you can track her." The man waited for the woman to sleep and spread cinnabar on her hair, but the woman did not know. The next day, the parents accompanied people to the sacred temple and searched everywhere, but there was no trace. Suddenly, they heard a neighbor woman scolding a child, saying, "Just changed into new pants, and again stained with bright red, where did you get this from?" The father heard it and found it strange, went to see, and found the child's pants entirely stained with cinnabar. He therefore investigated where the child got it from. The child said, "Just rode on the back of the turtle-shaped stone at the front of the school, unconsciously got stained with this." They went to see the head of the turtle-shaped stone, and cinnabar was there.

    212

    乃启学宫，碎碑下龟首，石片片有血丝，腹中有小石如卵，坚光若镜，锤之不碎，远投太湖。自是女不复来。

    　　阅半月，女忽直入寝所詈生曰：“我何负卿？竟碎我身体！然我亦不恼也。

    　　卿父母所虑者，为卿病耳。今已乞得仙宫灵药，服之当无恙。“出草叶数茎，强生食。其味香甘，且云：”前者居处相近，可朝夕往返；今稍远，便当长住此矣。“

    Explanation

    212

    A school was established, and a stele was broken, revealing a hidden object. The stone pieces had streaks of blood, and inside a body were found small stones like eggs, hard and bright as mirrors, unbreakable even when struck with a hammer, and were thrown far into Taihu Lake. From then on, the woman did not come back.

    After half a month, the woman suddenly entered the bedroom and cursed the man, saying, "What wrong have I done to you? You even broke my body! But I am not angry.

    Your parents are worried only because you are ill. Now I have obtained miraculous medicine from a heavenly palace, and taking it will make you well." She took out a few stems of herb leaves and forced the man to eat them. The taste was fragrant and sweet, and she said, "Previously, our residences were close, and we could go back and forth every day; now that we are a bit farther apart, I will live long-term here."

    213

    自是白昼见形，惟不饮食，家人大小咸得见之。生妻大骂，女笑而不答。每夕，生妻拥生坐床，不令女上，女亦不强。但一就枕，妻即忄昏々长睡，不知所为，而女独与生寝。生服灵药后，精神顿好，绝不似曩时孱弱。父母无奈，姑听之。

    　　如是年余。

    　　一日，生偶行街市，有一疥道人熟视生曰：“君妖气过重，不实言，死期近矣！”生以实告。疥道人邀入茶肆，取背上葫芦倾酒饮之，出黄纸二符授生曰：“汝持归，一贴寝门，一贴床上，毋令女知。彼缘尚未绝，俟八月十五夜，我当来相见。”时六月中旬也。生归，如约贴符。女至门惊却，大诟曰：“何又薄情若此？然吾岂惧此哉！”词甚厉，而终不敢入。良久，大笑曰：“我有要语告君，凭君自择，君且启符。”如其言，乃入，告生曰：“郎君貌美，妾爱君，道人亦爱君。妾爱君，想君为夫；道人爱君，想君为龙阳耳。二者，郎君择焉。”生大悟，遂相爱如初。

    Explanation

    213

    From that time on, she appeared during the day, but did not eat or drink, and all family members, young and old, could see her. The husband's wife scolded her loudly, and the girl laughed without answering. Every night, the wife hugged the husband and sat on the bed, not allowing the girl to come up, and the girl did not force her way. But as soon as they lay down on the pillow, the wife fell into a deep, prolonged sleep, unaware of what was happening, while the girl alone slept with the husband. After taking the magic medicine, the husband's spirit suddenly improved, and he no longer resembled his former weak state. The parents had no choice but to temporarily allow it.

    For more than a year, it was like this.

    One day, the husband happened to be walking in the street, and a leprous hermit looked at him closely and said, "Your demonic aura is too strong. If you do not tell the truth, your death is near!" The husband told him the truth. The leprous hermit invited him into a teahouse, poured out the wine from the gourd on his back and drank it, and took out two yellow papers with talismans and gave them to the husband, saying, "You take them home, one to stick on the bedroom door and one on the bed, do not let the girl know. Her fate has not yet ended. Wait until the night of the fifteenth day of the eighth month, I will come to meet you." It was mid-June at the time. The husband returned home and followed the agreement to paste the talismans. The girl came to the door and was shocked, scolding loudly, "Why are you so cold-hearted? But I am not afraid of this!" Her words were very harsh, but she still dared not enter. After a long time, she laughed and said, "I have important words to tell you, let you choose yourself. You open the talisman." As he said, he entered and told the husband, "My lord is handsome, I love you, the hermit also loves you. I love you, I want you to be my husband; the hermit loves you, he wants you to be his lover. Between these two, my lord chooses." The husband greatly understood and thus they loved each other as before.

    214

    至中秋望夕，生方与女并坐看月，忽闻唤名声，见一人露半身于短墙外。迫视之，疥道人也。拉生告曰：“妖缘将尽，特来为汝驱除。”生意不欲。道人曰：“妖以秽言谤我，我亦知之，以此愈不饶他。”书二符曰：“速去擒来。”生方逡巡，适家人出，遽将符送至妻所。妻大喜，持符向女，女战栗作噤，乃缚女手，拥之以行。女泣谓生曰：“早知缘尽当去，因一点痴情，淹留受祸。但数年恩爱，卿所深知，今当永诀，乞置我于墙阴，勿令月光照我，或冀须臾缓死。卿能见怜否？”生固不忍绝之也，乃拥女至墙阴，手解其缚。女奋身跃起，化一片黑云，平地飞升。道人亦长啸一声，向东南腾空追去，不知所往。

    Explanation

    214

    On the evening of the Mid-Autumn Festival, the man was sitting with his wife watching the moon when suddenly he heard a call. He saw a person with half of their body exposed outside a short wall. Upon closer inspection, it was the leprous hermit. The hermit pulled the man aside and said, "The demon's connection is coming to an end, so I have come specifically to drive it away for you." The man did not want to. The hermit said, "The demon has slandered me with filthy words, and I also know about it, therefore, I will not spare it." He wrote two talismans and said, "Go quickly and capture it." The man hesitated just as the family members came out, and he quickly brought the talisman to his wife. She was very happy, held the talisman towards the woman, and the woman trembled and made a sound. Then she tied the woman's hands and carried her along. The woman wept and said to the man, "If I had known the connection would end, I should have left. Because of a bit of foolish affection, I stayed and suffered. But for several years of love, which you deeply understand, now we must part forever. Please place me in the shadow of the wall, do not let the moonlight shine on me, perhaps hoping for a momentary delay in death. Can you show mercy to me?" The man could not bear to abandon her, so he carried the woman to the shadow of the wall and untied her hands with his hands. The woman leaped up with all her might, transformed into a dark cloud, and rose from the ground. The hermit also let out a long cry, soared into the sky towards the southeast, and they disappeared, not knowing where they went.

    215

    ○阴间中秋官不办事罗之芳，湖北荆州府监利县举人。辛未会试，有福建浦城县李姓者来拜，曰：“足下今科必中，但恐未能馆选。”罗询其故，李不肯说，云：“俟验后再说。”

    　　榜发，果中进士，竟未馆选，乃往问之。据云：“前得一梦，梦足下将为浦城县老父台，故来相访。”罗还家，选期尚早，乃就馆某氏，自道将来选宫，必得浦城矣。不料处馆三年，一病而殁，家中亦不知李所说梦中事也。

    Explanation

    215

    During the year of Xinwei (1751), Luo Zifang, a scholar from Jiangli County, Jingzhou Prefecture, Hubei Province, took the metropolitan examination. A man surnamed Li from Pucheng County, Fujian Province, came to visit him and said, "You will definitely pass this year, but I fear you may not be selected for the Hanlin Academy." Luo asked him why, but Li refused to say, saying, "Wait until after the examination to discuss further."

    When the results were announced, Luo indeed passed the imperial examination and became a Jinshi (imperial scholar), but he was not selected for the Hanlin Academy. He then went to ask Li about it. Li said, "I had a dream before, in which I saw you becoming a senior official in Pucheng County, so I came to visit you."

    Luo returned home, and since the selection period was still early, he took a teaching position with a certain family, believing that he would surely be selected for the Hanlin Academy. Unexpectedly, he taught for three years and died suddenly due to illness. His family did not know about the dream that Li had mentioned.

    216

    又一年后八月十五日，家中请仙，乩盘大书：“我系罗之芳，今回来了。”

    　　合家不信，乩上书：“你等若不信，有螺蛳湾田契一纸，我当年因殁于馆中，未得清付家中，尚记得夹在《礼记》某篇内。尔等现在与田邻构讼，可查出呈验，则四至分明，讼事可息。”家人当即检查，果得此契，于是合家痛哭。乩上亦写数十“哭”字。问：“现在何处？”乩写：“做浦城县城隍。”且云：“阴间比阳间公事更忙，一刻不暇，惟中秋一日，例不办事。然必月朗风清，英魂方能行远。今适逢此夕，故得闲回家一走。若平常日子，便不得暇回来了。”又吩咐家人：“庭外草木不得摇动，我带回鬼吏鬼卒有十余人，皆依草附木而栖。鬼性畏风，若无所凭借，被风一吹，便不知飘泊何处，岂不是我做城隍的反害了他们么！”

    Explanation

    216

    One year later, on the Mid-Autumn Festival of the eighth month, the family invited spirits, and the jī pán (spirit-writing platform) wrote: "I am Luo Zhi Fang, and I have returned."

    The entire family did not believe it. The jī pán wrote: "If you do not believe, there is a land deed from Luosiwān. I died in a guesthouse last year and was unable to settle accounts with my family. I still remember it was hidden in a certain section of the Liji (Book of Rites). You are currently in a land dispute with your neighbors. You can find and present this deed, and the boundaries will be clearly defined, resolving the dispute." The family immediately checked and indeed found the deed. As a result, the entire family wept bitterly. The jī pán also wrote dozens of "哭" (cry) characters. They asked, "Where are you now?" The jī pán wrote: "I am the city god of Pucheng." It continued, "In the afterlife, official duties are even busier than in the mortal world, with no spare moment. Only on the Mid-Autumn Festival is there a day off. However, it must be a clear and windless night for the spirit to travel far. Tonight happens to meet these conditions, so I have some free time to come home. On ordinary days, I cannot spare the time to return." It then instructed the family: "Do not shake the plants and trees outside. I have brought back over ten spirits, all of whom live among the grass and trees. Spirits fear the wind. If they have no support, they will be blown away by the wind and end up somewhere unknown. Wouldn't that be me harming them as the city god?"

    217

    乩盘书毕，又做长赋一篇乃去。

    　　○缚山魈湖州孙叶飞先生，掌教云南，素豪于饮。中秋夕，招诸生饮于乐志堂，月色大明，忽几上有声，如大石崩压之状。正愕视间，门外有怪，头戴红纬帽，黑瘦如猴，颈下绿毛茸茸然，以一足跳跃而至。见诸客方饮，大笑去，声如裂竹。人皆指为山魈，不敢近前。伺其所往，则闯入右首厨房。厨者醉卧床上，山魈揭帐视之，又笑不止。众大呼，厨人惊醒见怪，即持木棍殴击，山魈亦伸臂作攫搏状。

    　　厨夫素勇，手抱怪腰，同滚地上。众人各持刀棍来助，斫之不入。棍击良久，渐渐缩小，面目模糊，变一肉团；乃以绳捆于柱，拟天明将投之江。

    Explanation

    217

    After the writing on the乩盤 was completed, the spirit wrote a long poem or prose piece and then left.

    ○ Mr. Sun Yefei of Huzhou, who served as the principal in Yunnan, was known for his love of drinking. On the Mid-Autumn Festival, he invited his students to drink at the Lezhi Hall. The moonlight was bright, and suddenly, there was a sound on the table, as if a large stone had collapsed. Just as they were staring in surprise, a strange creature appeared outside the door. It wore a red hat and was black and thin, resembling a monkey, with green, soft hair on its neck. It jumped on one foot and arrived, seeing the guests drinking and laughed loudly before leaving, the sound of its laughter like bamboo being split. Everyone pointed to it and called it a mountain demon, daring not to approach. Watching where it went, it rushed into the right-hand kitchen. The cook, drunk and lying on the bed, the mountain demon lifted the curtain and looked at it, laughing uncontrollably. The crowd shouted loudly, the cook awoke in surprise and saw the creature, immediately grabbing a wooden stick to beat it. The mountain demon also extended its arms in a grasping and attacking posture. The cook, usually brave, grabbed the creature's waist, and they rolled together on the ground. The crowd each holding a knife or stick came to help, but their cuts did not penetrate. They beat it with sticks for a long time, and gradually it shrank, its face becoming blurred, changing into a lump of flesh. They then tied it to a pillar, planning to throw it into the river at dawn.

    218

    至鸡鸣时，又复几上有极大声响，急往视之，怪已不见。地上遗纬帽一顶，乃书院生徒朱某之物。方知院中秀才往往失帽，皆此怪所窃。而此怪好戴纬帽，亦不可解。

    　　○门夹鬼腿尹月恒住杭州艮山门外，自沙河滩归，怀菱半斤。路经钵盂潭，人稀在旷，有义冢数堆，觉怀内轻松，探所买菱，已失去矣。因转身寻至义冢，见菱肉剖碎，并聚冢尖。尹复拾至怀内，踉跄归家。

    　　食未竟而病大作，喊云：“吾等不尝菱肉久矣！欲借以解宿馋。汝必尽数取回，何吝啬若是？今吾等至汝家，非饱食不去。”其家惧，即供饭为主人赎罪。

    　　杭俗例：凡送鬼者，前人送出门，后人把门闭。其家循此例，闭门过急，尹复大声云：“汝请客当恭敬。今吾等犹未走，而汝门骤闭，夹坏我腿，痛苦难禁。非再大烹请我，则吾永不出汝门矣。”因复祈禳，尹病稍安。然旋也旋发不脱体，卒以此亡。

    Explanation

    218

    At the cockcrow, there was again a loud sound on the desk. Hurriedly going to see it, the monster had already disappeared. A hat was left on the ground, belonging to a student named Zhu from the academy. Only then did he realize that scholars in the academy often lost their hats, all stolen by this monster. This monster likes to wear a hat, which is also inexplicable.

    ○ Door Jammed by a Ghost Leg

    Yin Yueheng lived outside the northeast gate of Hangzhou. Returning from the sand river bank, he carried half a catty of lotus seeds. Passing through the bowl-shaped pond, where there were few people in the open area and several burial mounds, he felt light inside his bag. Checking the lotus seeds he bought, they had already disappeared. Turning around and going back to the burial mound, he saw the lotus seeds cut into pieces, gathered at the top of the mound. Yin picked them up and put them back in his bag, staggering home.

    Before he finished eating, his illness became severe. He shouted, saying, "We haven't tasted lotus seeds for a long time! We want to use them to satisfy our long-standing craving. You must take all of them back, how stingy you are! Now we have come to your house, we won't leave until we are full." His family was afraid and immediately prepared food to atone for the master's sins.

    In Hangzhou, it is customary that when sending a ghost, the first person sends them out, and the second person closes the door. His family followed this custom, but closed the door too quickly. Yin shouted again, saying, "You should be respectful when hosting guests. Now we haven't left yet, and you closed the door too quickly, injuring my leg, which is very painful. Unless you prepare a grand feast for me, I will never leave your door." Therefore, they performed another ritual, and Yin's illness slightly improved. But soon it recurred and he could not shake it off, ultimately dying because of it.

    219

    ○祭雷文黄湘舟云：渠田邻某有子，生十五岁，被雷震死，其父作文祭雷云：“雷之神，谁敢侮？雷之击，谁敢阻？虽然，我有一言问雷祖。说是吾儿今生孽，我儿今才十五。说是我儿前生孽，何不使他今生不出土？雷公雷公作何语？”祭毕，写其文于黄纸焚之。忽又霹雳一声，其子活矣。

    　　○王介眉侍读是习凿齿后身吾乡孝廉王介眉，名延年，同荐博学鸿词。少尝梦至一室，秘书古器，盎然横陈。榻坐一叟，短身白须，见客不起，亦不言。又有一人颀而黑，揖介眉而言曰：“余汉之陈寿也，作《三国志》，黜刘帝魏，实出无心，不料后人以为口实。”

    　　指榻上人曰：“赖此彦威先生以《汉晋春秋》正之。汝乃先生之后身，闻方撰《历代编年纪事》，夙根在此，须勉而成之。”言讫，手授一卷书，俾题六绝句而寤。寤后仅记二句，曰：“惭无《汉晋春秋》笔，敢道前生是彦威。”后介眉年八十馀，进呈所撰《编年纪事》，得赐翰林侍读。

    Explanation

    219

    Translation

    ○祭雷文

    According to Huang Xiangzhou: A neighbor of mine had a son who was fifteen years old and was struck by lightning, dying as a result. His father composed an ode to the thunder and said: "The god of thunder, who dares to insult? The strike of thunder, who dares to stop? Although, I have one word to ask of the Thunder Ancestor. Is it that my son's sins are from this life? My son is only fifteen. Or is it that my son's sins are from a previous life, why not let him remain underground in this life? What do you have to say, Thunder God?" After the ritual, he wrote the text on yellow paper and burned it. Suddenly, there was another thunderclap, and his son came back to life.

    ○王介眉侍读是习凿齿后身

    My fellow scholar, Wang Jiemei, whose name is Yanian, was recommended for his extensive learning and literary talent. In his youth, he once dreamed of entering a room filled with ancient artifacts and books, arranged abundantly around him. On a couch sat an old man, short in stature with white hair; he did not rise to greet guests nor speak. Another person, tall and dark, bowed to Jiemei and said: "I am Chen Shou of the Han dynasty, the author of the Records of the Three Kingdoms. I deposed Liu Bei and established Cao Wei, but it was done without malice, yet it was misunderstood by later generations."

    He pointed to the old man on the couch and said: "Thanks to Mr. Yanwei, who corrected it with the History of Han and Jin. You are the reincarnation of the master. I heard that you are currently writing a chronicle of historical events, and your innate talent lies here. You must strive to complete it." He finished speaking and handed him a scroll of books, instructing him to write six quatrains before waking up. Upon waking, he could only remember two lines: "I am ashamed to lack the pen of Han and Jin; how dare I claim to be the reincarnation of Yanwei?" Later, when Wang Jiemei was over eighty, he presented his work on the Chronicle of Historical Events and was granted the position of Reader in the Hanlin Academy.

    220

    ○周若虚慈溪周若虚，久困场屋，在城外谢家店教读四十余年，凡村内长幼，靡不受业。一日，晚膳后在馆独坐，有学生冯某向前作揖，邀若虚至家，有要事相恳。

    　　言毕告别，辞色之间，甚觉惨惋。若虚忆冯某已死，所见者系鬼，不觉大惊，即诣其家。

    　　冯某之父梦兰在门外伫立，见某即挽留小饮。若虚亦不道其所以，闲话家常。

    　　不觉漏下三鼓，不能回家，梦兰留宿楼上；在中间设榻，间壁即冯某之妻王氏住房，隐隐似有哭声。若虚秉烛不寐。见楼梯上有青衣妇人，屡屡伸头窥探，始露半面，继现全身。若虚呵问：“何人？”其妇厉声曰：“周先生，此时应该睡矣。”

    Explanation

    220

    ○Zhou Ruoxu, from Cixi, had long been unsuccessful in the imperial examinations. For over forty years, he taught in Xiejia Store outside the city, and all villagers, young and old, were his students. One day, after dinner, he sat alone in the study hall when a student named Feng bowed respectfully and invited him home, saying there was an urgent matter to discuss.

    After finishing their conversation, Feng bid farewell. In his manner and expression, he seemed very sad. Zhou Ruoxu remembered that Feng was dead, and the one he saw was a ghost. He was greatly startled and immediately went to Feng's house.

    Feng's father, Menglan, was standing outside the door. When he saw Zhou Ruoxu, he invited him in for a small drink. Zhou Ruoxu did not ask about the reason and chatted about daily matters.

    Before he realized it, it was past midnight. Unable to go home, Menglan invited him to stay upstairs. A bed was set up in the middle, and across the wall was the room of Feng's wife, Wang. There seemed to be faint crying. Zhou Ruoxu held a candle and did not sleep. He saw a woman in green clothes on the stairs, who kept peeking out. First, half of her face appeared, then the whole body. Zhou Ruoxu shouted, "Who are you?" The woman shouted sternly, "Mr. Zhou, you should be asleep by now."

    221

    若虚曰：“我睡与不睡，与汝何干？”妇曰：“我是何人！与先生何干？”即披发沥血，持绳奔犯。若虚惊骇欲倒，忽背后有人用手持扶，曰：“先生休怕，学生在此保护。”谛视之，即已故之冯生也。随即不见。

    　　若虚喊叫，其父梦兰持烛上楼，若虚具道所见。梦兰即叫媳妇王氏开门，杳无声息；抉门入，则身已悬梁上矣。若虚协同解救，逾时始苏。因午前王氏与小姑争闹，被翁责骂，短见轻生，恶鬼乘机而至。其夫在泉下知之，故求援于若虚。

    　　○葛道人以风洗手葛道人者，杭州仁和人，家素小康，性好道。年五十外，分家资，半以与子，而挟其半以游。过钱塘江，将取道入天台山，路遇一叟拱手曰：“子有道骨，盍学道？”葛与谈，甚悦。叟曰：“某福建人也，明习天文，曾官于钦天监，辞官归二十年矣。子如不弃，明春当候子于家。”写居址与之。

    Explanation

    221

    **Ruòxū said, "Whether I sleep or not, what does it matter to you?" The woman replied, "Who am I! What business do I have with you?" She immediately tore her hair and bled, holding a rope and rushing at him. Ruòxū was terrified and nearly collapsed. Suddenly, someone appeared behind him, holding him up with their hand and saying, "Sir, do not be afraid. I am here to protect you." He looked closely and saw that it was his deceased friend Fénɡ Shēng. He disappeared immediately.

    Ruòxū shouted, and his father Mèng Lán carried a candle upstairs. Ruòxū explained what he had seen. Mèng Lán then called his wife Wáng Shì to open the door. There was no sound. He forced the door open and saw that she was hanging by the neck. Ruòxū helped to rescue her, and after some time, she regained consciousness. It turned out that earlier in the day, Wáng Shì had quarreled with her younger sister-in-law and was scolded by her father-in-law. She had a narrow view and took her own life, and an evil spirit took advantage of the situation. Her husband, who was in the afterlife, knew about this and sought help from Ruòxū.

    Gě Dàorén, a Taoist practitioner from Rénhé, Hangzhou, came from a modestly prosperous family and had a natural inclination towards Taoism. When he was over fifty, he divided his family's assets, giving half to his son and taking the other half to travel. As he passed the Qiantang River, he planned to take the road to Tiantai Mountain. On the way, he met an old man who greeted him and said, "You have the bone of a Taoist. Why don't you learn the Tao?" Gě Dàorén talked with him and was very pleased. The old man said, "I am from Fujian. I am well-versed in astronomy and served as an official at the Imperial Astronomical Bureau. I resigned twenty years ago. If you don't mind, I will wait for you at my home next spring." He wrote down his address and gave it to him.**

    222

    葛次年如期往访，不遇，怅怅欲回。晚入旅店，又见一道士，貌伟神派，终夕不发一语。葛就而与谈，自陈为访仙故来。道士曰：“子果有志，吾荐子入庐山，见吾师兄云林先生，可以为子师。”葛求荐书而往。行深山中十余日，不见踪迹，心窃疑之。

    　　一日，见山洞中坐一老人，以手招风作盥沐状。葛异之，因陈道人书拜于座下。老人曰：“汝来太早矣！尚有人间未了缘三十年。吾且与汝经一卷，法宝一件，汝出山诵经守宝以济世人，三十年后再入山，吾传汝道可也。”葛问：“以手招风何为？”曰：“修神仙术成者，食不用火，沐不用水，招风所以洗手也。”

    　　因导葛出山。行未半日，已至南昌大路矣。

    　　至家，葛道人学其术，能治鬼服妖。所谓法宝者，乃一鹅子石，有缝，颇似人眼，有光芒，能自动闪闪，如交睫然。葛亦不轻以示人也。

    Explanation

    222

    Ge Zian visited as scheduled, but did not meet anyone, and felt disappointed, wanting to return. Late at night, he entered an inn and saw another Taoist with a majestic appearance and divine aura, who remained silent all night. Ge approached and talked with the Taoist, explaining that he came to seek immortals. The Taoist said, "If you indeed have the resolve, I will recommend you to enter Mount Lu to meet my senior brother, Mr. Yunlin, who can be your teacher." Ge asked for a recommendation letter and went. He traveled deep into the mountains for over ten days, did not see any signs, and secretly doubted it.

    One day, he saw an old man sitting in a cave, waving his hand as if to wash his face. Ge was surprised by it, presented the Taoist's letter, and bowed before the old man. The old man said, "You came too early! You still have thirty years of unfinished affairs in the human world. I will give you a scroll of scripture and a piece of treasure. You should go out of the mountain and recite the scripture, protect the treasure to benefit the world. After thirty years, come back to the mountain, and I will teach you the way." Ge asked, "What is the purpose of waving the hand to the wind?" The old man replied, "Those who have mastered the art of immortality do not need fire to eat, do not need water to bathe, and waving the hand to the wind is to wash the hands."

    The old man led Ge out of the mountain. Within half a day, they had already reached the main road of Nanchang.

    Upon returning home, Ge the Taoist learned the technique, which allowed him to exorcise ghosts and subdue demons. The so-called treasure was a stone resembling a goose egg, with a seam that looked quite like a human eye. It had a glow and could shine automatically, like the blink of an eye. Ge did not show it to others lightly.

    223

    ○沈姓妻杭州有沈姓者，住运司署前，与葛道人善。其长子旭初，妻有娠，询道人说男女。道人命：“取水一碗来。”沈与水，置几上。道人为默念咒语数通，侧耳听片时，蹙额曰：“奈何！奈何！”沈惊问故，曰：“汝妻不久有难，恐伤性命，不暇问男妇也。”沈虽素知道人灵异，然其妻甚健，疑信参半。

    　　未几，沈妻持灯上楼，忽大声呼痛。其翁姑与其夫急走视之，已卧床颠扑，面作笑容曰：“今日乃泄我恨。”其声若绍兴人。沈夫妻环叩之，答曰：“我自报冤，不干汝事。”沈急命次子某往求道人。道人至，取米一碗，口作咒语，手撮米击病者。病者作畏惧状曰：“我奉符命报冤，道人勿打！”道人曰：“汝有何冤？”病者答曰：“予，山阴人也。此女前生乃予邻家妇。予时四岁，偶戏其家，碎其碗。伊詈我母与私夫某往来，故生此恶儿。

    Explanation

    223

    In Hangzhou, there lived a man surnamed Shen who resided in front of the transport office and was friends with a Daoist practitioner named Ge. Shen's eldest son, Xu Chu, had a pregnant wife. Seeking to know the gender of the unborn child, Shen consulted Daoist Ge. The Daoist instructed Shen to bring a bowl of water, which he placed on a table. After muttering incantations several times and listening intently, Daoist Ge furrowed his brow and exclaimed, "What shall we do! What shall we do!" Shen, startled, asked what was wrong. Daoist Ge replied, "Your wife will soon face trouble, and I fear for her life. I have no time to inquire whether it will be a boy or a girl."

    Despite having always known Daoist Ge to be a spiritual master, Shen's wife was in good health, so he was half-believing and half-doubting.

    Not long after, Shen's wife, holding a lamp, ascended the stairs. Suddenly, she let out a loud cry of pain. Her father-in-law, mother-in-law, and husband rushed to her aid. She was already lying on the bed, thrashing about, and smiling as she said, "Today is the day I can vent my anger." Her voice sounded like that of a person from Shaoxing. Shen and his wife surrounded her, asking what was wrong. She answered, "I am avenging myself; this does not concern you." Shen quickly sent his second son to seek out Daoist Ge. When Daoist Ge arrived, he took a bowl of rice, muttered incantations, and began to sprinkle the rice at the patient. The patient looked fearful and said, "I am carrying out a divine command to avenge myself. Monk, do not hit me!" Daoist Ge asked, "What wrong have you suffered?" The patient replied, "I am from Shanyin. In her past life, this woman was my neighbor's wife. At the time, I was four years old and accidentally played in her home, breaking her bowl. She cursed my mother and her lover, someone named [name], for their relationship, therefore she gave birth to this wicked child."

    224

    予诉之母，母恐我泄其事，挞予至死。是致予死者，此妇也。我仇之久矣，今始寻着。”道人告沈曰：“报冤索命事，都是东岳掌管，必须诉于岳帝，允救，方可以法治；否则难救。”沈清晨赴法华山岳帝庙，默诉其事，占得上上签，归告道人。其时妇胎已堕，道人嫌不洁，不肯入房。沈合家哭求，道人乃诣榻前，书召彩云符一纸，问：“好看否？”病妇答曰：“好。”道人曰：“何不出现？”应曰：“诺。”道人即捏诀向空一捉，曰：“得矣。”驰下楼去，病人昏迷若醒，曰：“我为何遍身痛极？

    Explanation

    224

    The narrator complained to his mother, who feared he would reveal the matter and beat him to death. It was this woman who led to his death. He had hated her for a long time and now had finally found her. The Taoist priest told Shen, "Matters of revenge and taking lives are all under the jurisdiction of the Eastern Peak. One must appeal to the Emperor of the Eastern Peak, seek his permission, and then it can be handled according to the law; otherwise, it is difficult to save." Shen went to the Eastern Peak temple in the morning, silently appealed to the matter, and drew the topmost sign. He returned and told the Taoist priest. At that time, the woman's fetus had already fallen, and the Taoist priest disliked the impurity and would not enter the room. Shen's whole family wept and begged, and the Taoist priest then went to the bedside and wrote a summons to the Rainbow Cloud on a piece of paper. He asked, "Is it beautiful?" The sick woman answered, "Yes." The Taoist priest said, "Why don't you appear?" She replied, "Yes." The Taoist priest then made a gesture, reached out into the air, and said, "Got it!" He rushed downstairs. The patient was in a state of unconsciousness but seemed to be awake and said, "Why am I in so much pain all over?"

    225

    腹甚饥。“左右与之食。

    　　安未半刻，又作哭声曰：“汝携我孙去，我在此，亦能索汝命！”言毕，颠狂如故。口中作声甚杂，皆杭音。内有一鬼云：“我辈皆张老头儿邀来，你家若肯斋荐，我等即去。”沈邀僧作道场，众声称谢不已。忽又作张老者声云：“我是正客，如何反轻我？诸人馒头皆是菜心，我独豆沙多而菜心少？”沈视所设张老位前，果如所言，乃换与之。求其去，终不肯，复请道人来。道人授桃枝一束，曰：“吵则打之。”沈持入，向病人作欲打势。妇哀鸣曰：“勿打，我去，我去。”

    　　道人立门外，预设一瓮，向空骂曰：“速入此中！”用符一纸封其口携去，沈妇从此愈矣。

    　　半年后，有人遇道人于理安寺，见众僧扛道人行空室中，七昼夜不着土木，口吐黑汁数升，污沾衣，色如血。告人曰：“我以为童真之身污产妇秽气，幸众长老超度，不然，几堕落矣。”

    Explanation

    225

    A woman was very hungry. Those around her offered her food.

    Not even a moment had passed when she began to cry out, saying, "You take my grandson away, but if I stay here, I can still take your life!" After speaking, she continued to behave as wildly as before. The sounds coming from her mouth were very varied, all in Hangzhou dialect. One of the spirits said, "We were all invited by Old Man Zhang. If your family is willing to offer sacrifices, we will leave." Shen invited monks to perform a ritual, and the crowd kept expressing their gratitude. Suddenly, another spirit spoke in the voice of Old Man Zhang, saying, "I am the main guest, why are you treating me so lightly? Everyone else's steamed buns have more vegetable filling, but mine has more bean paste and less vegetable filling?" Shen looked at the position set up for Old Man Zhang and saw that it was indeed as he had said. He then switched the steamed buns. He asked them to leave, but they refused. He then asked a Taoist priest to come. The Taoist priest gave him a bundle of peach branches and said, "If they make a fuss, hit them." Shen held the peach branches and made a gesture as if he was going to hit the patient. The woman cried out, "Don't hit me, I'll go, I'll go."

    The Taoist priest stood outside and had a jar ready, then shouted into the air, "Get in there quickly!" He sealed the jar with a talisman and took it away. Shen's wife was cured from then on.

    Half a year later, someone met the Taoist priest at Li'an Temple. He saw the monks carrying the Taoist priest through an empty room for seven days and nights without touching the ground or walls. The Taoist priest spat out several liters of black fluid, which stained his clothes red like blood. He told someone, "I thought my pure body was tainted by the impurities of childbirth. Fortunately, the senior monks saved me, otherwise, I would have fallen into a lower realm."

    226

    ○怪弄爆竹自焚绍兴民家有楼，终年闭。一日，有远客来求宿。主人曰：“宅东有楼，君敢居乎？”客问故，曰：“此楼素积辎重，二仆居之。夜半闻叫号声，往视之，见二仆颜色如土，战栗不能言。少顷云：”我二人甫睡，尚未灭烛，见一物长尺许，如人间石敢当状，至榻前，搴帏欲上。我等骇极，不觉大呼狂奔而下。‘所见如此，自是莫敢有楼居者。“客闻笑曰：”仆请身试之。“主人不能挽，为涤尘土，列几席而下榻焉。客登楼，燃烛佩剑以待。

    　　漏三下，有声索索自室北隅起。凝睇窥之，见一怪如主人所言状，跳而登座，翻阅客之书卷。良久，复启其箧，陈物几上，一一审视。箧内有徽州炮竹数枚，怪持向灯前，把玩良久。烟花飞落药线上，轰然一声，响如霹雳，此怪唧唧滚地，遂殁不见。心大异之，虞其复来，待至漏尽，竟匿迹销声矣。

    Explanation

    226

    A Strange Creature and Firecrackers

    In a household in Shaoxing, there was a tower that remained closed all year round. One day, a distant traveler came seeking lodging. The host said, "There is a tower east of the house. Dare you stay there?" The guest asked why, and the host replied, "This tower has always stored heavy goods, and two servants lived there. One night, they heard shouting in the middle of the night. They went to investigate and saw the two servants' faces as pale as earth, trembling and unable to speak. After a short while, they said, 'We had just fallen asleep and the candles were still lit. We saw an object about a foot long, resembling a human stone guardian, approach the bed, lift the curtain, and try to climb up. We were terrified and unconsciously screamed and ran down.' Since then, no one has dared to live in the tower." The guest heard this and laughed, saying, "I will try it myself." The host could not dissuade him, so he cleaned the dust, arranged the tables and chairs, and went to bed. The guest climbed the tower, lit a candle, and drew his sword to wait.

    At the third chime of the night bell, a sound started from the northwest corner of the room. He looked closely and saw a creature as described by the host, jump onto the seat, and flip through the guest's books. After a long time, the creature opened a chest, placed items on the table, and examined them one by one. Inside the chest were several Huizhou firecrackers. The creature held them toward the lamp, playing with them for a long time. Suddenly, the sparks flew onto the fuse, and with a loud bang, the sound was like thunder. The creature squeaked and rolled on the ground, then disappeared without a trace. The guest found this very strange and feared it might return. He waited until the night bell rang again, but the creature finally vanished without a sound.

    227

    晨起告主人，互相惊诧。至夜，客仍宿楼上，杳无所见。此后，楼中怪绝。

    　　○喀雄喀雄者，姓杨，父作守备，早亡。表叔周某，作副将，镇河州，怜其孤，抚养之。周有女，年相若，见雄少年聪秀，颇爱之，时与饮食。周家法甚严，卒无他事。

    　　有务子者，亦周戚也，直宿书斋。夏月，雄苦热，徘徊月下，见周女冉冉而至，遂与成欢。次日入内，见女晓妆，雄目之而笑，女亦笑迎之。自后无日不至。

    　　务子闻其房中笑语，疑而窥之，见雄与周女相狎，而心大妒，密白周公。周入宅让其夫人，夫人曰：“女儿夜夜与我同床，焉有此事？”周终以为疑，借他事杖雄而遣之。雄无所依，栖身兰州古寺中。

    Explanation

    227

    In the morning, they woke up and told the owner, who was surprised and alarmed. At night, the guest still stayed in the upstairs room, but saw nothing. After this, there were no more strange occurrences in the house.

    ○ Kah Xiong, a man named Yang, whose father was a military commander and died early. His uncle, Zhou, served as a deputy general and was stationed in Hezhou. He pitied Kah Xiong's orphaned state and raised him. Zhou had a daughter, who was about the same age as Kah Xiong. She found him to be a young and intelligent boy and loved him, often sharing meals with him. Zhou's household rules were very strict, and there were no other incidents.

    There was also a relative of Zhou named Wu Zi, who stayed in the study overnight. In the summer, Kah Xiong was suffering from the heat and walked around under the moon. He saw Zhou's daughter slowly approaching and they became intimate. The next day, when he entered the inner quarters, he saw the girl in her morning makeup. Kah Xiong looked at her and smiled, and she smiled back at him. From then on, he visited her every day.

    Wu Zi heard laughter and conversation in the room and suspected something. He peeped in and saw Kah Xiong and Zhou's daughter being intimate, which made him very jealous. He secretly reported this to Zhou. Zhou went home and reprimanded his wife, who said, "My daughter sleeps with me every night, how could this happen?" Zhou remained suspicious and, using another pretext, beat Kah Xiong and sent him away. Kah Xiong had nowhere to go and took refuge in an ancient temple in Lanzhou.

    228

    一日者，女忽至，带来辎重甚富。雄惊且喜，问：“从何来？”曰：“与我叔父同来。”盖周公之弟名钅吾者，亦武官也，方升兰州守备。雄深信不疑，与女居半月，扬扬如富人。叔到任后，遇诸涂，喜曰：“侄在此乎？”曰：“然。”

    　　叔策马登其堂，侄妇出拜，乃周女也，大惊问故，雄具言之。钅吾曰：“予来时，不闻署中失女事，岂吾兄讳之耶？”居数日，借公事回河州，备述其事。周大骇，曰：“吾女宛然在室，顷且同饭，哪有此事？或者其狐仙所冒托耶！”夫人曰：“与其使狐狸冒托我女之名，玷我闺门，不如竟以真女妻之，看渠如何？”周兄弟二人大以为然，即招雄归成亲。

    　　合卺之夕，西宁之女先已在房，雄茫然不知所措。女笑而谓之曰：“何事张皇？儿狐也，实为报德而来。令祖作将军时，尝猎于土门关。儿贯矢被擒，令祖拔失纵之。屡欲报恩，无从下手。近知郎爱周女而不得，故来作冰人，以偿汝愿。

    Explanation

    228

    One day, a woman suddenly arrived, bringing with her a great deal of wealth. The man was both surprised and delighted, asking, "Where did you come from?" She replied, "I came with my uncle." It turned out that the woman was the sister of Zhou Gong, whose brother was named Jin Wu, also a military officer, who had just been appointed as the commander of the garrison in Lanzhou. The man believed her without doubt and lived with her for half a month, acting proudly like a wealthy person. After the uncle took up his post, he met him on the road and was overjoyed, saying, "Is my nephew here?" The man replied, "Yes."

    The uncle rode a horse and entered the hall, and the niece-in-law came out to greet him. It was Zhou's daughter, and the uncle was greatly surprised, asking why. The man explained everything. Jin Wu said, "When I came, I did not hear of any incident of a girl being lost in the office. Could my elder brother have been hiding it?" After staying for several days, he returned to Hedzhou on official business and detailed the matter. Zhou was greatly shocked, saying, "My daughter is still in the room, and we just ate together. How could this be? Perhaps it is a fox spirit impersonating her!" His wife said, "Rather than let a fox spirit impersonate my daughter and disgrace our family, it would be better to marry the real daughter to him and see what happens." Zhou's two brothers agreed, and they immediately invited the man to return and get married.

    On the night of the wedding feast, the woman from Xining was already in the room, and the man was completely at a loss, not knowing what to do. The woman smiled and said to him, "Why are you so alarmed? I am a fox, and I came to repay a debt of gratitude. When your grandfather was a general, he once hunted at Tumen Pass. I was pierced by arrows and captured. Your grandfather pulled out the arrow and let me go. I have often wanted to repay the favor, but had no way to do so. Recently, I learned that you love Zhou's daughter but cannot have her, so I came to act as a matchmaker to fulfill your wish."

    229

    亦因子与周女有夙缘，不然，儿亦不能为力也。今媒已成，儿去矣。“倏然不见。

    　　○常熟程生乾隆甲子，江南乡试，常熟程生，年四十许，头场已入号矣，夜忽惊叫，似得疯病者。同号生怜而问之，俯首不答。日未午，即收拾考篮，投白卷求出。同号生不解其意，牵裾强问之，曰：“我有亏心事发觉矣。我年未三十时，馆某绅家，弟子四人，皆主人之子侄也。有柳生者，年十九，貌美，余心慕，欲私之，不得其间，适清明节，诸生俱归家扫墓，惟柳生与余相对，余挑以诗曰：”绣被凭谁寝？相逢自有因。亭亭临玉树，可许凤栖身？‘柳见之脸红，团而嚼之。余以为可动矣，遂强以酒，俟其醉而私焉。五更，柳醒，知已被污，大恸。余劝慰之，沉沉睡去。天明，则柳已缢死床上矣。家人不知其故，余不敢言，饮泣而已。

    Explanation

    229

    Due to a predestined connection between me and Zhou's daughter, otherwise, I would not be able to help. Now that the matchmaker has arranged the marriage, I am leaving. "Suddenly, he disappeared.

    ○Changshu Cheng in 1744, during the Jiangnan provincial examination, a scholar from Changshu named Cheng, around forty years old, had already entered the examination hall for the first session. At night, he suddenly screamed, as if he had gone mad. A fellow examinee pitied him and asked him, but he lowered his head without answering. Before noon, he packed up his exam basket and submitted a blank paper to leave. The fellow examinee did not understand his intentions and pulled his robe to force him to answer, saying, "I have discovered my guilty conscience. When I was not yet thirty, I taught at a gentleman's household. There were four students, all of whom were the master's nephews and nieces. There was a student named Liu, who was nineteen years old and handsome. I admired him and wanted to have an affair with him. I could not find an opportunity. On Qingming Festival, all the students went home to sweep the graves, leaving only Liu and me. I teased him with a poem saying, 'Who will sleep with the embroidered quilt? Meeting has its own reason. Standing tall beside a jade tree, can allow a phoenix to perch?' Liu blushed when he saw it, tore it up, and chewed it. I thought he might be moved, so I forced him to drink, waited for him to get drunk, and then had an affair with him. At dawn, Liu woke up, knowing he had been defiled, and cried bitterly. I comforted him, and he fell into a deep sleep. At dawn, Liu had hanged himself on the bed. The family did not know the reason, and I dared not speak, crying silently.

    230

    不料昨进号，见柳生先坐号中，旁一皂隶，将我与柳齐牵至阴司处。有官府坐堂上，柳诉良久，余亦认罪。神判曰：“律载：鸡奸者照以秽物入人口例，决杖一百。汝为人师，而居心淫邪，应加一等治罪。汝命该两榜，且有禄籍，今尽削去。‘柳生争曰：”渠应抵命，杖太轻。’阴官笑曰：“汝虽死，终非程所杀也。倘程因汝不从而竟杀汝，将何罪以抵之？且汝身为男子，上有老母，此身关系甚大，何得学妇女之见羞忿轻生？《易》称：”窥观女贞，亦可丑也。“从古朝廷旌烈女不旌贞童，圣人立法之意，汝独不三思耶？”柳闻之大悔，两手自搏，泪如雨下。神笑曰：“念汝迂拘，着罚往山西蒋善人家作节妇，替他谨守闺门，享受旌表。‘判毕，将我杖二十放还魂，依然在号中。现在下身痛楚，不能作文；就作文，亦终不中也。不去何为？”遂呻吟颓唐而去。

    Explanation

    230

    Unexpectedly, I entered the examination hall yesterday and saw Liu Sheng already seated in it, with a servant standing beside him. He led me and Liu together to the underworld. An official was sitting on the dais, and Liu pleaded for a long time, while I also confessed to my crime. The god pronounced judgment: "According to the law, those who commit sodomy shall be punished according to the example of forcing filth into the mouth, with a hundred strokes of the stick. As a teacher, you harbor perverse desires, and should be punished one degree more severely. Your fate was meant for two lists, and you have a record of merit, but now all of this is stripped away." Liu argued, "He should be made to atone for his life, but a hundred strokes are too light." The god laughed and said, "Even if you die, it was not Cheng who killed you. If Cheng had killed you because you refused him, what crime would that be? As a man, you have an elderly mother above you; this body is of great importance, how can you act like a woman, ashamed and angry to the point of taking your own life? The Yijing says: 'Peering through a window to see a woman's chastity is also shameful.' Since ancient times, the court has honored loyal women but not chaste children. The intent of the sage's laws, have you not thought about this three times?" Liu heard this and greatly regretted, hitting himself with his hands, tears streaming down his face. The god laughed and said, "Considering your rigid and narrow-mindedness, I will punish you by sending you to the home of Jiang Shan in Shanxi to be a chaste wife, guarding the inner chambers for him and enjoying the commendation." After the judgment, I was beaten twenty times and returned to life, still in the examination hall. Now my lower body is in pain, I cannot write; even if I write, it will not succeed. Why not go?" He then moaned and left in a despondent state.

    231

    ○怪风凉州大靖营有松山者，在沙碛中，古战场也。将军塔思哈因公领兵过其处，白草黄云，一望无际。忽见一山高千仞，中有火星万点，蔽日而来，声若雷霆，人马失色。哈大惊，谓是山移。俄而渐近，不及回避，乃同下马闭目据地，互相抱持。顷之，天地如墨，人人滚地，马亦翻倒，良久始定。麾下三十六人，满面皆血，石子嵌入面皮，深者半寸。回望高山，已在数十里之外。日暮，抵大靖营，告总兵马成龙。马笑曰：“此风怪，非山移也。若山移，公等死矣。此等风，塞外至冬常常有之，不伤性命。但公等为沙石所击，从此尽成麻面，年貌册又须另造矣。”

    Explanation

    231

    A Strange Wind in the Da Jing Camp of Lanzhou

    In the Da Jing Camp of Lanzhou, there is a mountain called Songshan, located in the sandy desert, an ancient battlefield. General Tasi Hainin led his troops through this area, where the white grass and yellow clouds stretched endlessly. Suddenly, they saw a mountain towering thousands of feet high, with countless points of fire inside, blocking the sun as it approached, the sound like thunder, causing both men and horses to pale in fear. General Tasi was greatly alarmed, thinking it was the mountain moving. Soon, it drew closer, and there was no time to avoid it, so he and his men dismounted, closed their eyes, crouched on the ground, and held onto each other. After a short while, the sky and earth turned black, and everyone rolled on the ground, with horses also falling over. It took a long time before everything settled. Among the thirty-six men under his command, all had blood on their faces, with stones embedded in their skin, some as deep as half an inch. Looking back, the high mountain was already dozens of miles away. By dusk, they arrived at the Da Jing Camp and reported to General Ma Chenglong. General Ma laughed and said, "This is a strange wind, not the mountain moving. If the mountain were moving, you would all be dead. Such winds often occur in the border regions during winter, and they do not harm life. However, you have been struck by sand and stones, so from now on, you will all have pockmarked faces, and the register of ages and appearances will need to be updated."

    232

    ○孝女京师崇文门外花儿市居民，皆以制通草花为业。有幼女奉老父居，亦以制花生活。父久病不起，女忘啜废寝，明慰暗忧。适有邻媪纠众妇女往丫环山进香者，女因问：“进香可能疗父病否？”媪曰：“诚心祈祷，灵应如响。”女曰：“此间去山，道里几何？”曰：“百余里。”曰：“一里几何？”媪曰：“二百五十步。”女谨记之。每夜静父寝，持香一炷，自计步数里数，绕院叩头，默祝身为女子不能朝山之故。如是半月有余。向例：丫髻山奉祀碧霞元君，凡王公绅，每至四月，无不进香，以鸡鸣时即上殿拍香者为头香。头香必待大富贵家，庶人无敢僭越。时有太监张某往进头香，甫辟殿门，已有香在炉中。张怒责庙主，庙主曰：“殿不曾开，不识此香何由得上。”张曰：“既往不咎，明日当来上头香，汝可待我，毋许别人先入。”庙主唯唯。

    Explanation

    232

    Translation:

    In the flower market outside the Chongwen Gate in Beijing, all residents make artificial flowers for a living. There was a young girl who took care of her elderly father and also made flowers for a living. Her father had been ill for a long time and could not get up. The daughter forgot to eat and sleep, comforting him openly but worrying secretly. Just then, a neighbor woman gathered other women to go to Ya Huan Mountain for a pilgrimage. The daughter asked, "Can offering incense cure my father's illness?" The neighbor woman said, "If you pray sincerely, the response will be like an echo." The daughter then asked, "How far is it from here to the mountain?" The neighbor woman replied, "About a hundred li." The daughter then asked, "How long is one li?" The neighbor woman said, "Two hundred and fifty steps." The daughter remembered this carefully.

    Every night when her father slept, she would hold a stick of incense, count the steps and the distance, circle the courtyard, bow, and silently pray for the reason why, as a woman, she could not go to the mountain. She did this for more than half a month. By custom, Ya Ji Mountain worships the Goddess Bi Xia Yuan Jun. All nobles and officials, every April, would offer incense, and the first person to offer incense at the temple at the time of the rooster's crow would be the head incense. The head incense must be offered by a wealthy and powerful family, and commoners dare not overstep. At that time, a eunuch named Zhang went to offer the head incense. As soon as he opened the temple door, there was already incense in the incense burner. Zhang became angry and blamed the temple manager. The temple manager said, "The temple was not open, so I don't know how this incense got there." Zhang said, "I will not blame you for what has already happened. Tomorrow, I will come to offer the head incense. You can wait for me, and do not allow anyone else to enter first." The temple manager nodded in agreement.

    233

    次日始四更，张已至；至则炉中香已宛然，一女子方礼拜伏地，闻人声，倏不见。张曰：“岂有神圣之前鬼怪敢公然出现者，此必有因。”坐二山门外，聚香客而告之，并详述所见容态服饰。一媪听良久，曰：“据君所见，乃吾邻女某也。”因说其在家救父礼拜之事。张叹曰：“此孝女，神感也。”进香毕，即策马至女家，厚赐之，认为义女，父病旋愈。因太监周恤故，家渐温饱。女嫁大兴张氏，为富商妻。

    Explanation

    233

    The next day at the fourth watch, Zhang arrived; upon his arrival, the incense in the stove was still fragrant, and a woman was just about to kneel and worship. Upon hearing the sound of a person, she suddenly disappeared. Zhang said, "How could there be any ghosts or monsters daring to appear openly before a deity? There must be a reason for this." He sat outside the two mountain gates, gathered the incense burners, and told them about what he had seen, detailing the woman's appearance and attire. An old woman listened for a long time and said, "According to what you saw, it was my neighbor's daughter." She then told the story of how she had saved her father by worshipping. Zhang sighed and said, "This is a filial daughter, moved by the deity." After finishing the incense offering, he immediately rode to the girl's house, gave her generous gifts, took her as a foster daughter, and her father's illness quickly healed. Because of the eunuch's care, the family gradually became well-fed and comfortable. The girl later married a Zhang from Daxing and became the wife of a wealthy merchant.

    234

    ○老妪变狼广东崖州农民孙姓者，家有母，年七十馀。忽两臂生毛，渐至腹背，再至于掌，皆长寸馀；身渐伛偻，尻后尾生。一日，仆地化作白狼，冲门而去。家人无奈何，听其所之。每隔一月，或半月，必还家视其子孙，照常饮啖。邻里恶之，欲持刀箭杀之。其子妇乃买豚蹄，俟其再至，嘱曰：“婆婆享此，以后不必再来。

    　　我辈儿孙深知婆婆思家，无恶意，彼邻居人那能知道？倘以刀箭相伤，则做儿媳者心上如何忍得？“言毕，狼哀号良久，环视各处，然后走出。自后，竟不来矣。

    　　○义犬附魂京中常公子某，少年貌美，爱一犬，名花儿，出则相随。春日，丰台看花，归迟人散，遇三恶少方坐地轰饮。见公子美，以邪语调之。初而牵衣，继而亲嘴。

    Explanation

    234

    ○ An Old Woman Transforms into a Wolf
    In the Guangdong region of Yanzhou, a farmer surnamed Sun had a mother who was over seventy years old. Suddenly, her arms began to grow hair, which gradually spread to her abdomen, back, and palms, each growing a few inches. Her body gradually became hunched, and a tail grew from her buttocks. One day, she fell to the ground and transformed into a white wolf, rushing out through the door. The family could do nothing but let her go wherever she wanted. Every month, or sometimes every half month, she would return home to visit her descendants, eating and drinking as usual. The neighbors hated her and wanted to kill her with knives and arrows. Her daughter-in-law then bought some pig trotters and waited for the wolf to return, giving her instructions: "Grandma, enjoy this; from now on, you don't need to come back. We, your grandchildren, deeply understand that Grandma misses home and has no ill intentions. How could those neighbors know? If they harm you with knives and arrows, how could I, as your daughter-in-law, bear it?" After she finished speaking, the wolf wailed for a long time, looked around, and then left. After that, she never came back.

    ○ A Loyal Dog Possessed by a Spirit
    In Beijing, there was a young man named Sun who was handsome. He loved a dog named Flower, who followed him wherever he went. One spring day, they went to Fengtai to admire flowers. When they returned late and people had dispersed, they encountered three ruffians who were drinking on the ground. Seeing the young man was handsome, they used lewd words to tease him. They first pulled his clothes and then kissed him.

    235

    公子羞沮遮拦，力不能拒。花儿咆哮，奋前咬噬。恶少怒，取巨石击之，中花儿之头，脑浆迸裂，死于树下。恶少无忌，遂解带缚公子手足，剥去下衣。两恶少踏其背，一恶少褪裤，按其臀，将淫之。忽有癞狗从树林中突出，背后咬其肾囊，两子齐落，血流满地。两恶少大骇，拥伤者归。随后有行人过，解分子缚，以下衣与之，始得归家。心感花儿之义，次日往收其骨，为之立冢。

    　　夜，梦花儿来，作人语曰：“犬受主人恩，正欲图报，而被凶人打死，一灵不昧，附魂于豆腐店癞狗身上，终杀此贼。犬虽死，犬心安矣。”言毕，哀号而去。公子明日访至卖豆腐家，果有癞狗。店主云：“此狗奄奄，既病且老，从不咬人，昨日归家，满口是血，不解何故。”遣人访之，恶少到家死矣。

    Explanation

    235

    The nobleman, feeling ashamed and humiliated, could not resist. The dog roared and charged forward, biting and attacking. The ruffians, angry, took a large stone and struck it, hitting the dog's head, causing its brain to burst out, and it died under the tree. The ruffians, without fear, then tied up the nobleman's hands and feet and stripped him of his lower garments. Two ruffians stepped on his back, one ruffian removed his pants, pressed his buttocks, and was about to sexually assault him. Suddenly, a mangy dog leaped out from the woods, biting his testicles from behind, causing them to fall off, and blood flowed everywhere. The two ruffians were greatly frightened and carried the injured one away. Later, a passerby untied the nobleman, gave him some lower garments, and he finally returned home. He was moved by the dog's loyalty, and the next day went to collect its bones and built a tomb for it.

    At night, the dog appeared in a dream, speaking in human language: "Having received kindness from its master, the dog wanted to repay it, but was killed by the ruffians. Its spirit remained pure and attached itself to the mangy dog at the tofu shop, ultimately killing these ruffians. Although the dog is dead, its heart is at peace." It wailed and left. The next day, the nobleman visited the tofu shop, and indeed, there was a mangy dog. The shopkeeper said, "This dog is very weak, both sick and old, and never bites people. Yesterday, when it came home, its mouth was full of blood, and I don't know why." They sent someone to investigate, and it turned out that the ruffians had died at home.

    236

    ○白虹精浙江塘西镇丁水桥篙工马南箴，撑小舟夜行，有老妇携女呼渡，舟中客拒之，篙工曰：“黑夜妇女无归，渡之亦阴德事。”老妇携女应声上，坐舱中，嘿无言。

    　　时当孟秋，斗柄西指，老妇指而顾其女笑曰：“猪郎又手指西方矣，好趋风气若是乎！”女曰：“非也，七郎君有所不得已也。若不随时为转移，虑世间人不识春秋耳。”舟客怪其语，瞪愕相顾。妇与女夷然，绝不介意。舟近北关门，天已明，老妇出囊中黄豆升许谢篙工，并解麻布一方与之包豆，曰：“我姓白，住西天门，汝他日欲见我，但以足踏麻布上，便升天而行至我家矣。”言讫不见。篙工以为妖，撒豆于野。

    Explanation

    236

    A White Rainbow Spirit from Ding Shui Bridge in Tangxi Town, Zhejiang, named Ma Nanzhen, was rowing a small boat at night when an old woman with her daughter called for a ride. The passengers on the boat refused, but Ma said, "These women have nowhere to go at night; helping them is also an act of hidden virtue." The old woman and her daughter quickly boarded the boat and sat quietly without speaking.

    It was the beginning of autumn, and the handle of the Big Dipper pointed west. The old woman glanced at her daughter and smiled, saying, "The pig boy is pointing west again. Is it good to follow the wind like this?" Her daughter replied, "No, the Seventh Lord has no choice but to do so. If not changing with the times, to prevent people from not understanding the seasons." The boat passengers were surprised by her words and stared at each other in disbelief. The old woman and her daughter remained calm and showed no concern. As the boat approached the northern gate, it was already dawn. The old woman took out a handful of yellow beans from her bag and thanked Ma, also unwrapping a piece of hemp cloth to wrap the beans. She said, "My surname is Bai, and I live at the Western Heaven Gate. If you want to see me in the future, just step on the hemp cloth, and you will ascend to heaven and reach my home." After speaking, she disappeared. Ma thought she was a spirit and scattered the beans in the wild.

    237
