    　　○孛星女身山东有施道士者，善祈晴雨。乾隆十二年，东省大旱，抚军准祈雨不得，锁道士而逼之。道士曰：“雨非不可得也，但须某日孛星下降，公捐锦被一条，白金百两，某捐阳寿十年，方可得雨。”抚军如其言。

    　　至期，道士登坛，呼一童子近前，令其伸手，画三符于掌中，嘱曰：“至某处和田中，见白衣妇人便掷此符，彼必追汝，汝以次符掷之；彼再追，汝以第三符掷之；速归上坛避匿可也。”童子往，果见白衣妇，如其言，掷一符。妇人怒，弃裙追童。童掷次符，妇人益怒，解上衣露两乳奔前。童土掷三符，忽霹雳一声，妇人亵衣全解，赤身狂追。童急趋至坛，而妇人亦至。道人敲令牌喝曰：“雨！

    Explanation

    276

    The Ma family, because of ghostly speech, greatly reveres the worship of the door gods and thus establishes their family legacy.

    In Shandong, there was a Daoist named Shi who was skilled in praying for clear skies and rain. During the 12th year of the Qianlong era, there was a severe drought in the eastern provinces, and the governor allowed a Daoist to pray for rain, but the prayer was unsuccessful, so he locked the Daoist and pressured him. The Daoist said, "Rain is not impossible to obtain, but it requires that on a certain day a comet descends, the governor donates a silk quilt and one hundred taels of silver, and I donate ten years of positive lifespan, only then can rain be obtained." The governor followed his words.

    On the appointed day, the Daoist ascended the altar, called a young boy forward, instructed him to extend his hand, drew three talismans on his palm, and said, "Go to a certain place in the fields, see a white-clad woman and throw this talisman at her; she will surely chase you, then throw the second talisman at her; if she chases again, throw the third talisman at her; quickly return to the altar and hide." The boy went, and indeed saw a white-clad woman, as instructed, and threw the first talisman. The woman became angry, discarded her skirt, and chased the boy. The boy threw the second talisman, and the woman became even angrier, undid her upper clothing, exposed her breasts, and rushed forward. The boy threw the third talisman, and suddenly there was a thunderous clap. The woman's undergarments were completely undone, and she chased him naked. The boy hurried to the altar, and the woman also arrived. The Daoist struck the令牌 (token) and shouted, "Rain!"

    277

    雨！雨！“妇人仰卧坛下，云气自其阴中出，弥漫蔽天，雨五日不止。道士覆以锦被。妇渐苏，大恚耻，曰：”我某家妇，何为赤身卧此？“抚军备衣服令着，遣老妪送归，以百金酬其家。

    　　事后问道士，道士曰：“孛星女身而性淫，能为云雨，居天上亦赤体，惟朝北斗之期始着衣裳。是日下降田间，吾以符摄入某妇之身，使替代而来；又激怒之，使雷雨齐下。然用法太恶，必遭阴遣矣。”不数年，道士暴亡。

    　　○九夫坟句容南门外有九夫坟。相传昔有妇人甚美，夫死，止一幼子，家资甚厚，乃招一夫。生一子，夫又死，即葬于前夫之侧；而又赘一夫，复死如前。凡嫁九夫，生九子，环列九坟。妇人死，葬于九坟之中。每日落时，其地即起阴风，夜有呼啸争斗之声，若相冒而夺此妇者。行路不敢过，邻村为之不安，相率诉于邑。

    Explanation

    277

    Translation:

    Rain! Rain! A woman lay on her back at the altar, with clouds emerging from her crotch, spreading and obscuring the sky, and the rain continued for five days without stopping. The Taoist priest covered her with a brocade quilt. The woman gradually regained consciousness, greatly angry and ashamed, and said, "I am the wife of a certain family. Why am I lying here naked?" The military official prepared clothes for her to wear, sent an old woman to escort her back, and compensated her family with a hundred gold pieces.

    Afterward, he asked the Taoist priest, who replied, "The star maiden has a body and a lustful nature, capable of producing clouds and rain. Even in heaven, she is naked, only putting on clothes during the time of worshiping the Big Dipper. On that day, she descended to the fields, and I used a talisman to capture her body, making her come as a substitute; I also provoked her anger, causing thunder and rain to fall together. However, the method I used was too harsh, and I will surely be punished by the underworld." Not long after, the Taoist priest died suddenly.

    ○ Nine-Husband Grave

    South of the gate of Juzhou, there is a grave known as the Nine-Husband Grave. It is said that there was once a very beautiful woman whose husband died, leaving only a young son and a substantial fortune. She then took a new husband. She gave birth to a son, and her husband died again, so she was buried beside her previous husband. She then took another husband, who also died as before. In total, she married nine husbands, bore nine sons, and their graves were arranged in a circle around hers. When the woman died, she was buried in the center of the nine graves. Every evening at sunset, a gloomy wind would arise from the ground, and at night there were sounds of shouting and fighting, as if they were trying to take this woman from each other. Passersby dared not go by, and the neighboring villages felt uneasy, so they all went to the county office to report the matter.

    278

    令赵天爵随至其他，排衙呼皂隶，于各坟头持大杖重责三十，自此寂然。

    　　○土地奶奶索诈虎踞关名医涂彻儒，与之交好，其子妇吴氏，孝廉讳镇者之妹也。乾隆丙申六月，吴氏夜梦街坊总甲李某持簿化缘，口称“虎踞关将有火灾，纠费演戏以禳之”。簿上姓名，皆里中相识者。正徘徊间，有老妇人黄衫绛裙从门外入，谓吴曰：“今年此处火灾是九月初三日，君家首被其祸，数不可逃。须烧纸钱、买牲牢还愿，庶不至烧伤人命。”吴氏梦醒，方悟总甲李某久已物故，乃往各邻家告以故，并问：“此间可有衣黄衫妇人否？”皆曰：“无之。”吴有戒心，往祷土地庙，见所塑土地奶奶，宛然梦中所见，惊惧异常。诸邻闻之，亦大骇。彼此演戏祭祷，费数百金。

    Explanation

    278

    Translation:

    The official ordered Zhao Tianzhu to accompany him to another place, where he set up an official audience and called the servants. At each grave site, he severely beat someone thirty times, and from then on, everything became quiet.

    As for the Earth Goddess, she sought to deceive the famous doctor Tu Che Ru at Tiger Occupying Pass. He was a close friend of hers. His daughter-in-law, Wu, was the sister of a man named Zhen, who held the title of "孝廉" (a scholar-official). In the sixth month of the year Gengchen during the reign of Emperor Qianlong, Wu had a dream in which the head of the neighborhood, Mr. Li, held a ledger and collected alms. He said, "There will be a fire at Tiger Occupying Pass on the third day of the ninth month this year. Your family will be the first to suffer, and fate cannot be avoided. You must burn paper money, buy animals, and fulfill a vow in order to hopefully avoid burning people." Wu woke up from the dream and only then realized that Mr. Li, the head of the neighborhood, had been dead for a long time. She then went to each of her neighbors' houses and told them the reason, asking, "Is there a woman here wearing a yellow robe?" They all said, "None." Wu had a sense of foreboding and went to pray at the Earth God temple. She saw the statue of the Earth Goddess, which looked exactly like the old woman she had seen in her dream, and was extremely frightened. The neighbors, upon hearing this, were also greatly frightened. They performed a play and worshipped together, spending several hundred gold coins to ward off the disaster.

    279

    将至九月，涂氏一门衣箱器具尽搬移戚里家，自初一日起，不复举炊矣。至期，四邻寂然，竟无焚如之患。涂氏至今安好。

    　　●卷八○鬼闻鸡鸣则缩予门生司马骧，馆溧水林姓家，其所住地名横山乡，僻处也。天盛暑，以其西厅宏敞，乃与群弟子洒扫，为晚间乘凉之处。书籍行李，移床就焉，秉烛而卧。至三鼓，门外啾啾有声，户枢拔矣，烛光渐小，阴风吹来，有矮鬼先入，脸似笑非笑，似哭非哭，绕地而趋。随后一纱帽红袍入，白须飘飘，摇摆而进，徐行数步，坐椅上，观司马所作诗文，屡点头，若领解者。俄顷起立，手携矮鬼步至床前；司马亦起坐，与彼对视。忽鸡叫一声，两鬼缩短一尺，灯光为之一亮。

    　　鸡三四声，鬼三四缩，愈缩愈短，渐渐纱帽两翅擦地而没。

    　　次日，问之士人，云：“此屋是前明林御史父子同葬所也。”主人掘地，未棺宛然，乃为文祭之，起棺迁葬。

    Explanation

    279

    As it approaches the ninth month, the entire family of Tu moved all their clothes, boxes, and utensils to the homes of relatives. From the first day onward, they no longer cooked. By the time the ninth month arrived, the neighbors remained silent, and there was no danger of fire. The Tu family is still safe and well.

    Translation

    As it approaches the ninth month, the entire family of Tu moved all their clothes, boxes, and utensils to the homes of relatives. From the first day onward, they no longer cooked. By the time the ninth month arrived, the neighbors remained silent, and there was no danger of fire. The Tu family is still safe and well.

    Translation

    As it approaches the ninth month, the entire family of Tu moved all their clothes, boxes, and utensils to the homes of relatives. From the first day onward, they no longer cooked. By the time the ninth month arrived, the neighbors remained silent, and there was no danger of fire. The Tu family is still safe and well.

    Translation

    As it approaches the ninth month, the entire family of Tu moved all their clothes, boxes, and utensils to the homes of relatives. From the first day onward, they no longer cooked. By the time the ninth month arrived, the neighbors remained silent, and there was no danger of fire. The Tu family is still safe and well.

    Translation

    As it approaches the ninth month, the entire family of Tu moved all their clothes, boxes, and utensils to the homes of relatives. From the first day onward, they no longer cooked. By the time the ninth month arrived, the neighbors remained silent, and there was no danger of fire. The Tu family is still safe and well.

    Translation

    As it approaches the ninth month, the entire family of Tu moved all their clothes, boxes, and utensils to the homes of relatives. From the first day onward, they no longer cooked. By the time the ninth month arrived, the neighbors remained silent, and there was no danger of fire. The Tu family is still safe and well.

    Translation

    As it approaches the ninth month, the entire family of Tu moved all their clothes, boxes, and utensils to the homes of relatives. From the first day onward, they no longer cooked. By the time the ninth month arrived, the neighbors remained silent, and there was no danger of fire. The Tu family is still safe and well.

    Translation

    As it approaches the ninth month, the entire family of Tu moved all their clothes, boxes, and utensils to the homes of relatives. From the first day onward, they no longer cooked. By the time the ninth month arrived, the neighbors remained silent, and there was no danger of fire. The Tu family is still safe and well.

    Translation

    As it approaches the ninth month, the entire family of Tu moved all their clothes, boxes, and utensils to the homes of relatives. From the first day onward, they no longer cooked. By the time the ninth month arrived, the neighbors remained silent, and there was no danger of fire. The Tu family is still safe and well.

    As it approaches the ninth month, the entire family of Tu moved all their clothes, boxes, and utensils to the homes of relatives. From the first day onward, they no longer cooked. By the time the ninth month arrived, the neighbors remained silent, and there was no danger of fire. The Tu family is still safe and well.

    Translation

    As it approaches the ninth month, the entire family of Tu moved all their clothes, boxes, and utensils to the homes of relatives. From the first day onward, they no longer cooked. By the time the ninth month arrived, the neighbors remained silent, and there was no danger of fire. The Tu family is still safe and well.

    Translation

    As it approaches the ninth month, the entire family of Tu moved all their clothes, boxes, and utensils to the homes of relatives. From the first day onward, they no longer cooked. By the time the ninth month arrived, the neighbors remained silent, and there was no danger of fire. The Tu family is still safe and well.

    Translation

    As it approaches the ninth month, the entire family of Tu moved all their clothes, boxes, and utensils to the homes of relatives. From the first day onward, they no longer cooked. By the time the ninth month arrived, the neighbors remained silent, and there was no danger of fire. The Tu family is still safe and well.

    Translation

    As it approaches the ninth month, the entire family of Tu moved all their clothes, boxes, and utensils to the homes of relatives. From the first day onward, they no longer cooked. By the time the ninth month arrived, the neighbors remained silent, and there was no danger of fire. The Tu family is still safe and well.

    Translation

    As it

    280

    ○蜈蚣吐丹余舅氏章升扶，过温州雁荡山，日方午，独行涧中。忽东北有腥风扑鼻而至，一蟒蛇长数丈，腾空奔迅，其行如箭，若有所避者，后有五六尺长紫金色一蜈蚣逐之。蛇跃入溪中，蜈蚣不能入水，乃舞掉其群脚，飒飒作声，以须钳掉水。良久，口吐一红丸如血色，落水中。少顷，水如沸汤，热气上冲。蛇在水中颠扑不已，未几死矣，横浮水面。蜈蚣乃飞上蛇头，啄其脑，仍向水吸取红丸，纳口中，腾空去。

    　　○雷部三爷杭州施姓者，家居忠清里，六月雷雨后，小便树下。甫解，见有鸡爪尖面者蹲焉，大怖而返。夜即暴病，狂呼：“触犯雷神。”家人环跪求赦。病者曰：“治酒饮我，杀羊食我，我贷其命。”如其言，三日而愈。适有天师法官过杭，施姓与有旧，以其事告之。法官笑曰：“此雷部奴中奴也，小名阿三，惯倚势诈人酒食。如果雷神，其技量宁止此耶？”今长随中有称“三爷”、“四爷”者是矣。

    Explanation

    280

    Translation

    Centipede Spits Out the Elixir

    My maternal uncle, Zhang Shengfu, was passing through Yandang Mountain in Wenzhou. It was midday, and he was walking alone by the stream. Suddenly, a pungent wind came from the northeast, and a giant python, several zhang long, soared through the air with great speed, moving like an arrow, as if it were trying to avoid something. Behind it, a purple-gold centipede, about five or six chi long, pursued it. The snake leaped into the stream, but the centipede could not enter the water. It began to sway its many legs, making a rustling sound, and used its pincers to stir the water. After a while, it spat out a red pill as red as blood, which fell into the water. Shortly after, the water boiled like hot soup, and steam rose up. The snake thrashed in the water, unable to stop, and soon died, floating face up on the surface. The centipede then flew onto the snake's head, pecked at its brain, and returned to the water to suck up the red pill, swallowing it. It then soared away into the sky.

    The Third Official of the Thunder God's Department

    In Hangzhou, there was a man surnamed Shi who lived in Zhongqingli. One June, after a thunderstorm, he relieved himself under a tree. As he was finishing, he saw a figure with chicken-clawed feet and a sharp face squatting there. He was greatly frightened and hurried back home. That night, he fell gravely ill and shouted wildly, "I have offended the Thunder God!" His family surrounded him, kneeling and begging for forgiveness. The sick man said, "Prepare wine to drink with me, kill a sheep to eat with me, and I will spare your lives." They did as he asked, and he recovered in three days. Coincidentally, a Taoist priest and judge passed through Hangzhou, and Shi had known him before. He told the priest about the incident. The priest laughed and said, "This is a subordinate of the subordinates in the Thunder God's department, known as 'Ah San.' He is accustomed to using his power to trick people into giving him food and drink. If he were truly a Thunder God, his skills would not be limited to this!" Today, the long-term attendants who are called "Third Official" and "Fourth Official" are probably descendants of such figures.

    281

    ○鬼乖乖金陵葛某，嗜酒而豪，逢人必狎侮之。清明，与友四五人游雨花台。台旁有败棺，露见红裙，同人戏曰：“汝逢人必狎，敢狎此棺中物乎？”葛笑曰：“何妨。”往棺前以手招曰：“乖乖吃酒。”如是者再。群客服其胆，大笑而散。

    　　葛暮归家，背有黑影尾之，声啾啾曰：“乖乖来吃酒。”葛知为鬼，虑避之则气先馁，乃向后招呼曰：“鬼乖乖，随我来。”径往酒店，上楼置一酒壶两杯，向黑影酬劝。旁人无所见，疑有痴疾，听其所为。共饮良久，乃脱帽置几上，谓黑影曰：“我下楼小便，即来奉陪。”黑影者首肯之。葛急趋出归家。

    　　酒保见客去遗帽，遂窃取之。是夕，为鬼缠绕，口喃喃不绝，天明自缢。店主人笑曰：“认帽不认貌，乖乖不乖。”

    　　○凤凰山崩同年沈永之任云南驿道时，奉制府璋公之命，开凤凰山八十里，通摆夷苗路。

    Explanation

    281

    Ghostly Playfulness in Jinling

    Ge from Jinling, a man who loved drinking and was bold, would tease or insult everyone he met. On the Qingming Festival, he went with four or five friends to visit Yuhuatai. Near the platform, there was a decaying coffin, and a red skirt was exposed. His friends teased him, saying, "You must tease everyone, dare you tease the thing in this coffin?" Ge laughed and said, "What's the problem?" He went up to the coffin and waved his hand, saying, "Come and drink." He did this twice. The group was impressed by his courage and laughed and dispersed.

    That evening, Ge returned home, and a black shadow followed him, chirping, "Come and drink." Ge knew it was a ghost, but feared that if he avoided it, his spirit would weaken first. So, he called out behind him, "Ghosty, follow me." He went straight to a hotel, went upstairs, and placed a wine jar and two cups. Others saw nothing and suspected he had a mental illness, letting him do as he pleased. They drank together for a long time, and then Ge took off his hat and placed it on the table, saying to the black shadow, "I will go downstairs to use the bathroom and come back to join you." The black shadow nodded in agreement. Ge hurriedly left and went home.

    The innkeeper saw the guest leave and left his hat, so he stole it. That night, Ge was tormented by ghosts, muttering continuously, and committed suicide by hanging at dawn. The innkeeper laughed and said, "Recognized the hat but not the face, Ghosty is not so obedient after all."

    Phoenix Mountain Collapse

    Shen Yongzhi, a contemporary, served as the postal route commander in Yunnan. He was ordered by Governor Zhang Gong to open up 80 miles of Phoenix Mountain to connect the path through the territory of the Bai and Miao ethnic groups.

    282

    山径险峭，自汉、唐来人迹未到处也。每斫一树，有白气自其根出，如匹练升天。

    　　蟆虾大如车轮，见人辄瞪目怒视，当之者登时扑地。土人醉烧酒，以雄黄塞鼻，持巨斧砍杀之，烹食可疗三日饥。忽一日，有美女艳装从山洞奔出，役夫数千人，皆出洞追而观之，老成者不动心，操作如故。俄而山崩，不出洞者压死矣。沈公为余述其事，且戏曰：“人之不可不好色也，有如是夫。”

    　　○董金瓯董金瓯者，湖州勇士，能负重，走京师，十日可到。尝为人腰千金入都，过山东开成庙，有盗尾后，将取其金。董知之，挂金树上，下马与搏。盗抵敌不胜，问：“足下拳法，何人所授？”曰：“僧耳。”盗曰：“破僧耳拳，须我妹来，汝敢在此相待否？”董笑曰：“避女子非夫也。”坐以待之。少顷，一美女来，年十八九，貌甚和，相见即格斗，良久曰：“汝拳法非僧耳授也，当别有人。”

    Explanation

    282

    The mountain path is perilous and steep, a place where human footprints have not yet reached since the Han and Tang dynasties. Whenever a tree is cut down, white vapor rises from its roots like a ribbon ascending to heaven.

    The mada (a mythical creature) is as large as a carriage wheel. It glares at people with anger, and those who encounter it fall to the ground immediately. Local people, drunk on rice wine and with realgar stuffed in their noses, wield large axes to kill it. Cooking and eating it can cure hunger for three days.

    One day, a beautiful woman dressed in fine clothes suddenly emerged from a mountain cave. Thousands of laborers came out of the cave to chase and watch her. The experienced ones remained unmoved and continued working as usual. Soon the mountain collapsed, and those who did not come out of the cave were crushed to death.

    Mr. Shen told me about this event and jokingly said, "One cannot help but admire beauty; look at what happened."

    ○ Dong Jinguo

    Dong Jinguo was a brave warrior from Huzhou who could carry heavy loads. He could travel to the capital in ten days. Once, he carried a thousand gold pieces for someone and passed through the Kai Cheng Temple in Shandong. A thief followed him, intending to steal the gold. Dong realized this and hung the gold on a tree, dismounted, and fought the thief. The thief could not defeat him and asked, "Who taught you this martial art?" Dong replied, "A monk did." The thief said, "To break the monk's martial art, my sister must come. Will you wait here for her?" Dong laughed and said, "A man should not avoid a woman." He sat and waited. Shortly after, a beautiful woman arrived, about eighteen or nineteen years old, with a gentle appearance. They immediately began to fight. After a while, she said, "Your martial art was not taught by a monk; there must be another teacher."

    283

    董以实告，曰：“我初学于僧耳，后学于僧耳之师王征南。”女子曰：“若然，须至我家，彼此一饭再斗方决，汝敢往乎？”董恃其勇，径随女子行。

    　　到其家，则其兄已先在家，张灯挂红，率妻欢迎，曰：“妹夫来矣。”以红巾蒙其妹头，强之交拜。董骇然问故，曰：“吾父某亦为人保标，路逢僧耳，与角斗不胜而死。我与妹立志报仇，同习拳法，必须胜僧耳者然后可以杀之。访得僧耳之师为王征南，苦相寻无路。汝是其弟子，则可以引见征南，再学拳法报此仇矣。”董遂赘其家，别遣人赍腰间金赴京师。日后不知所终。

    Explanation

    283

    董 admitted, saying, "I first learned from Seng Er, and later from Seng Er's teacher, Wang Zhengnan." The woman said, "If that's the case, you must come to my home. We will have one meal and then fight again to settle it. Dare you go?" Relying on his courage, Dong followed the woman directly.

    Upon arriving at her home, he found her brother already there. They lit lamps and hung red decorations, and her wife welcomed him, saying, "My brother-in-law has arrived." They covered her sister's head with a red scarf and forced Dong to bow to her. In shock, Dong asked the reason. She said, "My father was also a protector of some sort. He met Seng Er on the road, fought him, and died without winning. My sister and I swore to take revenge. We studied martial arts together and must defeat Seng Er before we can kill him. We found out that Seng Er's teacher is Wang Zhengnan, but we have been searching for him without success. You are his disciple, so you can introduce me to Wang Zhengnan. I will study martial arts again to take revenge." Dong then became part of her family and sent someone else to the capital with the gold in his waistband. Later, his fate was unknown.

    284

    ○蒋厨常州蒋用庵御史家厨李贵，取水厨下，忽中恶仆地。召巫视之，曰：“此人夜行冲犯城隍仪仗，故被鬼卒擒去。须用三牲纸钱祷求城隍庙中西廊之黑面皂隶，便可释放。”如其言，李果苏。家人问之，曰：“我方汲水，忽被两个武进县黑面皂头来拿去，说我冲犯他老爷仪仗，缚我衙门外树上，听候发落。我实不知原委，今日听他二人私地说：”李某业已尽孝敬之礼，可以放他回去，不必禀官。‘将我解去索子，推入水中，我便惊醒。“御史公闻之笑曰：”看此光景，拿时城隍不知，放时城隍不知，都是黑面皂隶诈钱作祟耳。谁谓阴间官清于阳间官乎！“

    Explanation

    284

    A Cook from Changzhou

    In the household of the censor Jiang Yong'an from Changzhou, there was a cook named Li Gui. One day, while fetching water from the kitchen, he suddenly fell ill and collapsed. The family summoned a shaman to examine him, who said, "This person ran into the ceremonial procession of the City God at night, and therefore was captured by ghost soldiers. To release him, you must use three sacrificial animals and paper money to pray to the black-faced servant in the west wing of the City God temple."

    Following the shaman's advice, Li Gui indeed recovered. His family asked him what had happened, and he replied, "I was just drawing water when two black-faced servants from Wujin County came and took me, saying that I had run into their master's procession. They tied me to a tree outside the office and left me there to await judgment. I truly did not know the cause. Today, I heard the two of them privately say that since Mr. Li has already paid his respects, he can be released to go back, and there is no need to report to the authorities. They untied the ropes and pushed me into the water, and I awoke in a panic."

    Upon hearing this, the censor Jiang laughed and said, "Looking at this situation, the City God did not know when they took me, nor did he know when they released me. It is all just the black-faced servants pretending to be possessed to extort money. Who would say that officials in the underworld are cleaner than those in the real world?"

    285

    ○见曹操称晚生江南副榜王芾，梦古衣冠人召往一处：宫阙巍峨，兵卫甚严。有赤帻者从军门出曰：“汉丞相曹公奉屈。”王遂入，见一人皮弁上坐，须眉苍白。芾心知为操，一时心悸，无以自名，乃长揖称：“晚生王某奉谒。”操命旁坐，谓曰：“闻汝好学书，可知楷书先乎？草书先乎？”曰：“楷书先。”操摇首曰：“不然，先有草书，后有楷书。所以召汝者，正谓将此义告知，以便转语世人也。”

    Explanation

    285

    A Dream with Cao Cao

    Wang Bi, a scholar from the southern region who was listed on the supplementary list of successful candidates in an imperial examination, dreamt that an ancient person in formal attire invited him to a place: the palace was grand and imposing, with a very strict military guard. A person wearing a red headgear came out from the military gate and said, "Chancellor Cao of Han respectfully invites you." Wang Bi entered and saw a man sitting above in a ceremonial hat, with white hair and beard. Wang Bi realized it was Cao Cao, and he felt a moment of fear, unable to introduce himself. He then performed a formal bow and said, "A latecomer, Wang, respectfully greets you." Cao Cao gestured for him to sit beside him and said, "I have heard that you enjoy studying calligraphy. Do you know if regular script (楷书) came first or cursive script (草书) came first?" Wang Bi replied, "Regular script came first." Cao Cao shook his head and said, "That is incorrect. Cursive script came first, followed by regular script. The reason I called you here is to inform you of this principle, so you can convey it to the world."

    286

    语毕，仍遣赤帻人送出。甫及门，闻内有呼号声，赤帻者曰：“相王又用五色棒棰人矣。”芾惊而醒。

    　　○武后谢嵇先生无锡嵇侍读受之，余授业弟子也。辛丑冬，过随园，余止而觞之。席间论史事，余极言《通鉴》载杨妃洗儿事之诬。嵇云：“门生在史局时，派修唐鉴，立论颇合先生之意，将《旧唐书》所载武后淫秽事大半删除，同局以为不然。亡何，夜卧书舍，有小黄门来，称：”则天皇太后请嵇先生。‘因随之行。望前面宫殿外有四金柱插空，高数十丈，上书’天枢‘二字。一宫女云鬟霞佩出，引向殿西角，云：“先生少坐，待我奏闻。’语毕便去。殿上门槛甚高，跨殊费力。

    Explanation

    286

    After the conversation ended, they still sent a red-capped person to escort him out. Just as he reached the door, he heard cries inside. The red-capped person said, "The Prince of Qin is using the five-colored stick to beat people again." Fu was startled and woke up.

    ○ Empress Wu thanked Mr. Ji, a reader from Wuxi, who was my teacher. In the winter of XinChou, he passed through Sui Garden, and I stopped him and invited him to drink. During the feast, we discussed historical events, and I strongly criticized the falsehood in the Zizhi Tongjian about Yang Fei bathing her child. Ji said, "When I was in the history bureau, I was assigned to revise the Tang Zhi. My arguments aligned with your views, and I removed most of the lewd stories about Wu Zetian from the Old Book of Tang. My colleagues disagreed." Soon after, while sleeping in the study, a small yellow gate official came and said, "The Empress Wu Zetian requests Mr. Ji." He followed him. Looking ahead, there were four golden pillars inserted into the sky, reaching dozens of feet high, with the words "Tian Zhu" written on them. A palace girl with hair like clouds and a brocade belt appeared and led him to the west corner of the hall, saying, "Please sit for a moment; I will inform her." After the conversation ended, she left. The threshold of the hall was very high, and it was very difficult to step over.

    287

    绣帘中坐冕旒者，相离远，仰视不甚分明。异香从殿上吹来，仿佛莲花气息。旁有虎皮交椅，坐白须人，手执牙笏，口奏事，琅琅数千言，亦不可辨。冕旒者似与驳诘良久，已而大笑，其齿皓然呈露，洁白如玉，面为旒珠所遮，终未见也。少顷，前宫女出谓曰：”今天已暮，太后不及相见，请先生且回。所以奉屈者，谢先生驳删《唐书》之功，先生当自知之。‘语毕，袖中出一玉秤，曰：“此我在长安以此称量天下才者，先生将往长安，敢以奉赠。’门生心知是上官婉儿，逡巡揖谢而醒。其年果有督学陕西之差。”

    Explanation

    287

    Behind the embroidered curtain sat an emperor, distant and not clearly visible when looked upon. A strange fragrance wafted from the hall, reminiscent of the scent of lotus flowers. Beside him was a tiger-skin chair occupied by an elderly man with white hair, who held an ivory tablet and spoke in a clear, ringing voice, delivering thousands of words, though they were indistinguishable. The emperor seemed to debate with him for a long time, then burst into laughter, revealing his teeth, which were as white and lustrous as jade. His face was obscured by the tassels of his crown, and he was never fully seen. After a while, a palace maiden emerged and said, "It is already late, and the Empress Dowager cannot meet you today. Please, sir, return. The reason for your invitation is to thank you for your efforts in revising the Tangshu (Book of Tang), which you should well know. When she finished speaking, she took out a jade scale from her sleeve and said, 'This is the scale I used in Chang'an to measure the talents of people across the land. You are about to go to Chang'an, so I dare to present it to you.' The student knew it was none other than Shangguan Wan'er, and he bowed and thanked her hesitantly before waking up. That year, he indeed received an appointment to oversee education in Shaanxi Province.

    288

    ○冒失鬼相法：瞳神青者，能见妖；白者，能见鬼。杭州三元坊石牌楼旁居老妪沈氏，素能见鬼，常言十年前见一蓬头鬼，匿牌楼上石绣球中，手执纸钱为标，长丈馀，累累若贯珠。伺人过牌楼下，暗掷标打其头。人辄作寒噤，毛孔森然，归家即病，必向空中祈祷，或设野祭方愈。蓬头鬼借此伎俩，往往醉饱。一日，有长大男子，气昂昂然，背负钱镪而过，蓬头鬼掷以标。男子头上忽发火焰，冲烧其标，线层层裂断，蓬头鬼自牌楼上颠仆，滚绣球而下，喷嚏不止，化为黑烟散去。负钱之男子全不知也。自此，三元坊石牌楼无复作祟矣。吾友方子云闻之笑曰：“作鬼害人，亦须看风色。若蓬头鬼者，其即世所称之‘冒失鬼’乎？”

    Explanation

    288

    The Method of Identifying Careless Ghosts: Those with blue irises can see spirits; those with white irises can see ghosts. An elderly woman named Shen, who lived near the stone archway in Sanyuan Lane in Hangzhou, was known for her ability to see ghosts. She often recounted that ten years ago she saw a bald-headed ghost hiding inside a stone ball on the stone archway, holding paper money as a marker, about ten feet long, strung together like beads. The ghost would wait for people passing under the archway and secretly throw the paper money at their heads. People would shudder, their pores standing on end, and upon returning home, they would fall ill, praying to the air or performing wild sacrifices to recover. The bald-headed ghost often got drunk or satisfied using this trick. One day, a tall and proud man passed by, carrying a large amount of money. The bald-headed ghost threw the paper money at him, but suddenly, flames erupted from the man's head, burning the paper money, which broke apart layer by layer. The bald-headed ghost fell from the archway, rolling down the stone ball, sneezing continuously, and then transformed into black smoke and dispersed. The man carrying the money was completely unaware of what had happened. Since then, the stone archway in Sanyuan Lane no longer caused trouble. My friend Fang Ziyun heard this and laughed, saying, "Even ghosts must consider the situation before harming people. Could this bald-headed ghost be the one known as the 'careless ghost' in the world?"

    289

    ○史宫詹改命溧阳宫詹史胄斯，未遇时，赴省乡试，通南门外汤道士谈命甚精，因以年庚求为推算。道士曰：“照丑时算，你终身只一诸生，寿可八十三岁。若照寅时算，便可官登三品，今科便中。汝丑时乎？寅时乎？”曰：“丑时也。”曰：“若然，则今科不中矣。”史怆然不乐。道士曰：“命可改也，但阴司寿算最重，君如肯减寿三十年，当为君改作寅时。”史公欣然愿改。道士曰：“果情愿者，明日早来。”

    Explanation

    289

    A High-Ranking Official Changes His Fate

    During the reign of Emperor Ling, the high-ranking official Shi Guozhi from Liyang was taking the provincial examination. He stopped at the South Gate and met a Taoist priest named Tang, who was very skilled in fortune-telling. Shi presented his birth year and month to the Taoist priest for a calculation.

    The Taoist priest said, "If we calculate based on the second Earthly Branch (丑时), you will remain a mere scholar all your life, and your lifespan will be eighty-three years. If we calculate based on the third Earthly Branch (寅时), you will reach the rank of third-grade official, and you will pass this year's examination. Are you born during the second or third Earthly Branch?"

    Shi replied, "I was born during the second Earthly Branch."

    The Taoist priest said, "If that is the case, then you will not pass this year's examination." Shi was sad and unhappy.

    The Taoist priest continued, "Fate can be changed, but the importance of lifespan in the underworld is greatest. If you are willing to reduce your lifespan by thirty years, I will change your fate to the third Earthly Branch."

    Shi happily agreed to change his fate. The Taoist priest said, "If you truly agree, come early tomorrow."

    290

    次夜，史五鼓熏沐到寺，道士已启户待，曰：“子诚信人，但日后官尊寿短，毋自悔也。”史唯唯，具香烛，对天自陈。道士披发仗剑，口中喃喃诵咒，良久，另书一庚帖与之。史公持，归置箧中。果于是年乡会联捷，官至官詹。

    　　五十二岁，希图降级永年，而任内总无过失。商之吏部，笑而不信。至次年春，精神甚健。五月，偶染微疾。上命太医往视，为药所误，竟不起矣。此事公孙抑堂司马言。司马，余亲家也。

    　　○高相国种须高文端公自言年二十五作山东泗水县令时，吕道士为之相面，曰：“君当贵极人臣，然须不生，官不迁。”相国自摩其颐，曰：“根且未有，何况于须？”

    　　吕曰：“我能种之。”是夕伺公睡熟，以笔蘸墨画颐下如星点。三日而须出矣。

    　　然笔所画，缕缕百十茎，终身不能多也。是年迁州牧，擢迁至总督而入相。

    Explanation

    290

    On the following night, at the fifth watch, the prime minister bathed and groomed himself before arriving at the temple. The Daoist priest had already opened the door to wait for him. The priest said, "You are a trustworthy person, but in the future, you will have a high rank but a short life, so do not regret it later." The prime minister nodded in agreement, prepared incense and candles, and made a declaration to heaven. The Daoist priest, with long hair and a sword, muttered spells in a low voice for a long time, then wrote a talisman and gave it to the prime minister. The prime minister took it, returned home, and placed it in a chest. Indeed, that year, he succeeded in both the provincial and metropolitan examinations and was promoted to a high-ranking official in the imperial court.

    At the age of fifty-two, he hoped for a lower rank to ensure a longer life, but he never made any mistakes in his official duties. He consulted the Ministry of Personnel, who laughed and did not believe him. By the following spring, he was very healthy in spirit. In May, he contracted a minor illness. The emperor ordered a court physician to visit, but the treatment was ineffective, and he could not recover. This story was told by General Gong Sun Yitang, who is the narrator's father-in-law.

    ○ The Story of the Prime Minister Gao Planting His Beard

    Prime Minister Gao himself said that when he was twenty-five, he was the magistrate of Siwater County in Shandong. A Daoist priest named Lü gave him a facial reading and said, "You will become extremely powerful, even surpassing the emperor's ministers, but you will not grow a beard, and your official position will not change." The prime minister rubbed his chin and said, "If there is no root, how can there be a beard?"

    The Daoist priest replied, "I can plant it for you." That night, he waited until the prime minister was asleep, dipped a brush in ink, and drew dots on the prime minister's chin like stars. Three days later, the beard grew. However, the beard only grew in the places where the dots were drawn, with a hundred or so strands, and he could not grow more than that throughout his life. That year, he was promoted to the position of Prefect of Fuzhou, and he was then promoted to Governor-General and eventually became a prime minister.

    291

    ○话官说鬼河东运使吴云从作刑部郎中，公馆外偶有社会，家人妇抱小公子出看，溺尿路旁。公子忽哭不止，家人抱归，不知何故。至夜，公子作北语云：“怎么小孩子这般无礼，溺在我头上！我与你不得开交！”吵闹一夜。吴公怒，次晨作牒焚与本处城隍，云：“我南方人也，无故小儿撞着说官话鬼，猖獗可恨，托为拿究。”

    　　是夜平定。

    　　至第三日晚，公子又病，仍作北语云：“你不过是个官儿罢了，竟这样糟挞我们的老四！咱们兄弟今来替他报仇，要些烧酒喝喝。”夫人不得已，曰：“与你喝，不要闹。”于是，一鬼喝毕，一鬼又要喝，兼讨前门外杨家血贯肠做下酒物，呶呶之声，又复达旦。吴公上前批其颊骂曰：“狗奴！强转舌根，学说官话，再说便打。”然打者自打，说者自说。吴又牒城隍云：“说官话鬼又来了，求神惩治。”是夕，宅中闻鞭挞声。鬼云：“你不要打，咱们去就是了。”公子病随愈。

    Explanation

    291

    ○ Officials and Ghosts

    Wu Yuncong, the River East Transport Commissioner, was appointed as the Director of the Criminal Department. Outside his official residence, there happened to be a gathering. His wife took the young master out to see, and the child urinated on the side of the road. The young master suddenly cried uncontrollably, and the family brought him back inside, not knowing the reason. That night, the young master spoke in northern dialect, saying, "How dare a child be so disrespectful, urinating on me! I can't get away from you!" They argued all night.

    Wu Yuncong was angry. The next morning, he wrote a document and burned it to the local city god, saying, "I am a person from the south, and without cause, a child encountered a ghost speaking official language, which is outrageous and hateful. I ask for them to be caught and investigated."

    That night, the situation calmed down.

    On the third night, the young master fell ill again and still spoke in northern dialect, saying, "You are just an official, and you mistreat our younger brother so much! Our brothers have come to avenge him. Give us some alcohol to drink." The lady of the house had no choice but to say, "I will give you some, but don't cause trouble." One ghost drank, and another ghost wanted to drink, asking for some blood sausage from Yang's shop outside the city gate as a drink. Their noise continued until dawn. Wu Yuncong stepped forward and slapped the ghost, scolding, "Scoundrel! Forcibly changing your tongue, learning to speak official language, if you speak again, I will beat you." But the one who hits hits, and the one who speaks speaks. Wu Yuncong then wrote another document to the city god, saying, "The ghost speaking official language has come again. I ask the god to punish." That night, the household heard the sound of beating. The ghost said, "Don't beat us, we will leave." The young master's illness then improved.

    292

    ○偷雷锥杭州孩儿巷有万姓甚富，高房大厦。一日，雷击怪，过产妇房，受污不能上天，蹲于园中高树之顶，鸡爪尖嘴，手持一锥。人初见，不知为何物：久而不去，知是雷公。万戏谕家人曰：“有能偷得雷公手中锥者，赏银十两。”众奴嘿然，俱称不敢；一瓦匠某应声去。先取高梯置墙侧，日西落，乘黑而上。雷公方睡，匠竟取其锥下。主人视之：非铁非石，光可照人，重五两，长七寸，锋棱甚利，刺石如泥。苦无所用，乃唤铁工至，命改一刀，以便佩带。方下火，化一阵青烟，杳然去矣。俗云：“天火得人火而化。”信然。

    Explanation

    292

    In the child's alley of Hangzhou, there lived a wealthy family surnamed Wan, with tall and grand houses. One day, a thunderbolt monster was struck by lightning, passing over a room where a woman was giving birth. The monster was soiled and could not ascend to heaven, so it squatted on the top of a tall tree in the garden. It had chicken-like claws, a sharp beak, and held a spike in its hand. When people first saw it, they did not know what it was. After a while, they realized it was the thunderbolt monster. The Wan family jokingly told their servants, "Whoever can steal the spike from the thunderbolt monster's hand will be rewarded with ten taels of silver." The servants remained silent, all claiming they dared not. One bricklayer named Wang stepped forward. He first took a high ladder and placed it against the wall. As the sun set, he went up in the darkness. The thunderbolt monster was sleeping, and the bricklayer finally took the spike down. The owner examined it: it was neither iron nor stone, its light could illuminate people, it weighed five taels, was seven inches long, had a very sharp edge, and could pierce stone like mud. Frustrated by its lack of use, the owner called an ironworker and ordered him to change it into a knife for easy carrying. As soon as the fire was lit, the spike turned into a wisp of blue smoke and disappeared mysteriously. It is said, "Heavenly fire transforms into human fire." Indeed, so it is.

    293

    ○土地受饿杭州钱塘邑生张望龄，病疟。热重时，见已故同学顾某者踉跄而来，曰：“兄寿算已绝，幸幼年曾救一女，益寿一纪。前兄所救之女知兄病重，特来奉探，为地方鬼棍所诈，诬以平素有黯昧事。弟大加呵饬，方遣之去，特诣府奉贺。”

    　　张见故人为己事而来，衣裳蓝缕，面有菜色，因谢以金。顾辞不受，曰：“我现为本处土地神，因官职小，地方清苦，我又素讲操守，不肯擅受鬼词，滥作威福，故终年无香火，虽作土地，往往受饿。然非分之财，虽故人见赠，我终不受。”

    Explanation

    293

    ○ The spirit of the land in Qiantang County, Hangzhou, suffered from hunger. A student named Zhang Wangling from Qiantang County was suffering from malaria. When his fever was severe, he saw his deceased classmate, Gu, staggering towards him. Gu said, "Your life span has been cut off. Fortunately, you saved a girl in your youth, and your life was extended by a decade. The girl you saved, knowing you were gravely ill, especially came to visit you. She was deceived by local evil spirits, who falsely accused her of having dark and obscure matters. I sternly reprimanded them and sent her away. I especially went to report this good news."

    Zhang saw that his deceased classmate had come for his sake, and he was dressed in tattered clothes with a gaunt face. He thanked Gu with gold, but Gu refused to accept it, saying, "I am now the land deity of this place. Due to my small office and the poverty of the area, I have always been strict in my conduct and have never accepted false accusations or acted arbitrarily. Therefore, I have no incense or offerings throughout the year, and although I am a land deity, I often suffer from hunger. However, I will not accept any unearned wealth, even if it is a gift from an old friend."

    294

    张大笑。

    　　次日，具牺牲牢祭之，又梦顾来谢曰：“人得一饱，可耐三日；鬼得一饱，可耐一年。我受君恩，可挨到阴司大计，望荐卓异矣。”张问：“如此清官，何以不即升城隍？”曰：“解应酬者，可望格外超升；做清官者，只好大计卓荐。”

    　　○批僵尸颊桐城钱姓者，往仪凤门外。一夕回家，时已二鼓，同事劝以明日早行。钱不肯，提灯上马，乘醉而行。到扫家湾地方，荒冢丛密，见树林内有人跳跃而来，披发跣足，面如粉墙。马惊不前，灯色渐绿。钱倚醉胆壮，手批其颊。其头随披随转，少顷又回，如牵丝于木偶中，阴风袭人；幸后面人至，其物退走，仍至树林而灭。次日，钱手黑如墨。三四年后，黑始退尽。询之土人，曰：“此初做僵尸，未成材料者也。”

    Explanation

    294

    Zhang laughed heartily.

    The next day, he prepared a sacrifice and offered it at the altar, and then dreamed that the ghost returned to thank him, saying, "A person can survive three days with one meal, but a ghost can survive a year with one meal. I have received your kindness, and I can endure until the great reckoning in the underworld. I hope you will recommend me as outstanding."

    Zhang asked, "Why hasn't such a clear official been promoted to the position of city god?"

    The ghost replied, "Those who excel in social duties may be expected to receive extraordinary promotion, but those who are clear officials can only be recommended as outstanding in the annual evaluation."

    One day, a man from Tongcheng was traveling outside the city. One night, when he returned home, it was already past midnight. His colleagues advised him to leave early the next morning. The man refused and, carrying a lantern, mounted his horse and set off while drunk. He reached a place called Saowayan, where there were many desolate tombs. Suddenly, he saw someone jumping out from the woods, with disheveled hair and bare feet, and a face as pale as a wall. The horse was startled and would not move forward, and the lantern's light turned green. The man, relying on his drunken courage, slapped the ghost on the cheek. The ghost's head turned with the slap, and after a short while, it returned, as if controlled by strings, with a cold wind blowing. Fortunately, people arrived from behind, and the ghost retreated and disappeared into the woods.

    The next day, the man's hands were black as ink. It took three or four years for the blackness to fade away. He asked the local people about the incident, and they said, "This was a newly turned zombie, not yet fully formed."

    295

    ○簸箕龟乾隆辛卯春，山阴刘际云舟过镇江，见风覆客船，漂没货物甚多。江边有素谙水性人，俗名“水鬼”，专以打捞货物为生。是日，客舟有覆者，群水鬼皆至，言定价钱，一齐入水。及上岸，忽少一人，众疑其在水藏匿金银，复入水，遍寻不得。但见一龟：赤色，大过浴盆，形扁如簸箕，无头无尾无足。水鬼被其咬住，拉之不开，乃以大铁钩拽龟上岸。通体有小穴数百，皆其口也，人血已经吸尽，而口犹紧咬不放。刺以利刃，龟若不知。不得已，并人与龟烈火焚之，臭闻数里。

    Explanation

    295

    In the spring of the Xinmao year during the Qianlong era, Liu Jiyun of Shan Yin passed by Zhenjiang on a boat, where he saw a passenger boat capsized by the wind, with a lot of goods swept away and sunk. On the riverbank, there were people skilled in water, commonly known as "water ghosts," who devoted themselves to making a living by salvaging goods. On that day, when there were capsized passenger boats, all the water ghosts came, agreed on the price, and went into the water together. Upon returning to the shore, one person was suddenly missing. The crowd suspected that he had hidden gold and silver in the water, so they went back into the water and searched everywhere but could not find him. Only a turtle was seen: red, larger than a bathtub, flat like a winnowing basket, with no head, no tail, no feet. The water ghost was bitten by the turtle and could not pull it open, so they used a large iron hook to drag the turtle onto the shore. There were hundreds of small holes all over its body, all of which were its mouths, and the human blood had been sucked dry, but the mouths still tightly held on. Stabbed with a sharp blade, the turtle seemed unaware. Unavoidably, both the person and the turtle were burned with intense fire, and the smell spread for several miles.

    296

    或曰：“此即锅盖鱼之极大者，严州江中尤多。”

    　　○命该薄棺台州富户张姓家有老仆某，六十无子，自备一棺，嫌材料太薄，访有贫家治丧仓卒不能办棺者，借与用之，还时但索加厚一寸，以为利息。如是数年，居然棺厚九寸矣，藏主有厢房内。一夕，邻家火起，合室仓皇。看火者见张氏宅上立一黑衣人，手执红旗，逆风而挥，挥到处火头便转。张氏正宅无恙，惟厢房烧毁。

    Explanation

    296

    It is said, "This is the largest type of 锅盖鱼, and there are many of them in the Yangzhou River."

    ○ A servant from a wealthy Zhang family in Taizhou, who was sixty years old and had no children, prepared his own coffin. Disliking the thin material, he sought out poor families who were in a hurry to prepare funerals and could not afford a coffin, lending it to them for use. When returned, he simply asked for it to be made one inch thicker, as interest. After several years, the coffin ended up being nine inches thick, stored in the side room. One night, a fire broke out in a neighboring house, and the whole household was in panic. The fire watcher saw a black-clothed person standing on the Zhang family's house, holding a red flag and waving it against the wind. Wherever he waved, the flames turned. The main house of the Zhang family was unharmed, but only the side room was burned down.

    297

    老仆急入扛取棺，业已焚及，忙投水塘中。俟扑灭馀火后拖起刨之，依然可用，但尺寸之薄，亦依然如前矣。

    　　○向狐仙学道云南监生俞寿宁，习仙家符之学，仗一古剑替人驱妖，颇有灵应。一日，其友张某下田收租，遇大风雨，过其门，将借宿焉。俞不可，张忿然而行，必欲探其所以见拒之故，仍往其门，穴墙窥窥焉。见俞张设酒肴有两席，宾客欢呼，男女集沓。张愈怒，斧碎其门，排闼入，则酒席具存而群宾不见。俞惊出，蹋足曰：“君误我！君误我！我好学仙，难得真师传道，不得已，广请狐仙指示。半年以来，所遇男女狐仙甚多，有相约为兄弟者，为夫妇者，为兄妹者，不一而足。

    Explanation

    297

    The old servant hurriedly went in to carry out the coffin, which was already burned to it. He quickly threw it into the pond to extinguish the fire. After waiting for the remaining fire to be extinguished, he dragged it out and dug it up. It was still usable, but the thinness of its dimensions remained unchanged.

    As for Yu Shouning, a student from Yunnan, he learned the art of Taoist talismans and relied on an ancient sword to exorcise demons for others, which had quite a bit of miraculous response. One day, his friend Zhang went to collect rent from the fields and encountered a heavy rainstorm. Passing by Yu's door, he wanted to stay overnight. Yu refused, and Zhang furiously left, determined to find out why he was rejected. He still went to Yu's door, bored a hole in the wall, and peeped through. He saw Yu setting up two tables with wine and food, guests cheering, and men and women gathered. Zhang became even angrier, broke down the door with an axe, and forced his way in. But the table was still set and the guests were nowhere to be seen. Yu exclaimed, "You have misled me! You have misled me!" He explained, "I like to learn Taoism, but it's hard to find a true master to teach me. Unwillingly, I widely invited fox spirits to guide me. In the past half year, I have met many male and female fox spirits. Some became brothers, some became spouses, some became siblings, and so on."

    298

    今日众仙会议，将授长生要诀，故隆其礼文，备馔相延。尚未谈及玄关要旨，而被汝撞破，泄漏天机，致诸仙散去，岂非天哉！前数日紫文真人原说今日是破日，必被凡人冲破，须改日作会；而瑶仙三妹以明日将嫁某郎，故权择今日。果然不利，亦数也。我明日行矣，将别择一洁净之所聚会群仙，不使人知。“此后俞云游于外，不知所往。

    　　○五通神因人而施江宁陈瑶芬之子某，素不良。游普济寺，见寺供五通神坐关帝之上，怒其无礼，呼僧责之，命移五通于关帝之下。游人观者俱以为是，陈傲然自得。夕归，见五通神当门而立，遂仆地，狂叫曰：“我五通大王也，享人间血食久矣，偶然运气不好，撞着江苏巡抚老汤，两江总督小尹，将我诛逐。他两个都是贵人，又是正人，我无可奈何，只得甘受。汝乃市井小人，敢作威福！我不能饶汝矣。”

    Explanation

    298

    Today, the assembly of immortals was convened to impart the secrets of immortality. The ceremonial rites were elevated, and feasts were prepared to welcome them. The meeting had not yet touched upon the profound principles when you inadvertently disrupted it, revealing divine secrets, causing the immortals to disperse. Is it not heaven's will?

    Several days ago, Purple Text True Person had said that today was an inauspicious day, destined to be disrupted by a mortal, necessitating a change of date. However, Jade Immortal's third sister, who was to marry a certain groom the next day, chose today for the meeting. Indeed, it proved unfavorable, as fate would have it. I will depart tomorrow and choose another clean place to gather the immortals, keeping it secret from mortals. After this, Yu Cloud will wander abroad, and no one knows where he has gone.

    The Five-Path God, due to human interference, said to Chen Yao Fen's son, who was always unrighteous. He visited Puji Temple and saw the Five-Path God sitting above Lord Guan, whom he found disrespectful. He called out to the monks to rebuke the Five-Path God and order him to move below Lord Guan. The visitors and onlookers all agreed, and Chen Yao Fen felt proud. In the evening, when he returned home, he saw the Five-Path God standing at the door, and he fell to the ground, shouting wildly: "I am the Great King of the Five Paths! I have enjoyed the blood offerings of humanity for a long time. Unfortunately, I encountered the old Tang, the governor of Jiangsu, and the young Yin, the governor of the two Jiangs, who drove me away. They are both high-ranking officials and upright men, and I could do nothing about it, so I had to accept it. You are just a commoner, daring to wield power and authority! I cannot spare you!"

    299

    其家环拜，具三牲纸课，延僧祷祀，竟不能救而死。

    　　○张奇神湖南张奇神者，能以术摄人魂，崇奉甚众。江陵书生吴某独不信，于众辱之，知其夜必为祟，持《易经》坐灯下。闻瓦上飒飒作声，有金甲神排门入，持枪来刺。生以《易经》掷之，金甲神倒地。视之，一纸人耳，拾置书卷内夹之。有顷，有青面二鬼持斧齐来，亦以《易经》掷之，倒如初，又夹于书卷内。

    　　夜半，其妇号泣叩门曰：“妾夫张某昨日遣两子作祟，不料俱为先生所擒，未知有何神术，乞放时性命。”吴曰：“来者三纸人，并非汝子。‘”妇曰：“妾夫及两儿皆附纸人来，此刻现有三尸在家，过鸡鸣则不能复生矣。”哀告再三。吴曰：“汝害人不少，当有此报。今吾怜汝，还汝一子可也。”妇持一纸人泣而去。明日访之，奇神及长子皆死，惟少子存。

    Explanation

    299

    The family surrounded him in reverence, prepared three sacrificial animals and paper offerings, and invited monks to pray and perform a ritual, but they could not save him, and he died.

    ○ Zhang Qishen, a magician from Hunan, was able to capture people's souls with his magic and was greatly revered by many. A scholar from Jiangling named Wu did not believe in him and publicly insulted him in front of others. Knowing that the magician would cause harm at night, Wu held the I Ching (Book of Changes) and sat by the lamp. He heard a rustling sound on the roof tiles and saw a god in golden armor pushing open the door and entering, holding a spear to stab. Wu threw the I Ching at him, and the god in golden armor fell to the ground. Upon closer inspection, it was just a paper figure. Wu picked it up and placed it inside his book. After a while, two green-faced ghosts came together with axes. Wu also threw the I Ching at them, and they fell just as before. He again placed them inside his book.

    At midnight, his wife cried and knocked on the door, saying, "My husband Zhang sent two sons to cause harm yesterday. Unfortunately, both were captured by you. I don't know what kind of divine power you have. Please release their lives." Wu replied, "Those who came were three paper figures, not your sons." The wife said, "My husband and the two sons are attached to the paper figures. Right now, there are three bodies at home. If it passes midnight, they won't come back to life." She repeatedly pleaded. Wu said, "You have harmed many people, so you deserve this punishment. I will return one of your sons." The wife took one paper figure and left crying. The next day, when Wu checked, the magician and the eldest son were dead, but the youngest son was still alive.

    300

    ○青阳江丫青阳人江丫，处乡馆，教村童五人，长者不过十二三岁，幼者八九岁。一日，字课甫毕，江忽持木棍将五生排头打死；已亦触墙流血，昏晕倒地。各家父母闻之，奔赴喊哭，叩其故。据江云：午间安坐，突见窗外奇鬼六七辈，绀发蓝面，着五色衣，前来搏噬诸生。我惶急，驱之不去，随取木棍将鬼打无踪，自幸诸生得免于难。亡何谛观，始知所打死者非鬼，即弟子五人。横尸在地，痛摧心肝，因自寻死，故触墙脑裂。官验取供，以鬼语难成信谳，质之各家父母。皆云：与江丫平日绝无仇隙，渠作先生，爱惜诸童颇好，亦无疯症，此举不知何故，想系前生冤孽。江脑破垂毙，现在收禁，俟医治痊时再行审抵云云。此乾隆二十一年五月间青阳知县申详总督尹公文书也，余亲见之。半月后，报江丫死于狱。

    Explanation

    300

    In Qingyang, there was a man named Jiang Ya, who taught at a rural school and instructed five village children, the oldest no more than twelve or thirteen years old, and the youngest eight or nine years old. One day, after finishing a character lesson, Jiang suddenly took a wooden stick and beat the five students to death in order. He himself also hit the wall, bleeding, and fainted to the ground. The parents of each child heard about it and rushed over, shouting and crying, asking him the reason. According to Jiang, he said: At noon, he was sitting peacefully when he suddenly saw six or seven strange ghosts outside the window, with dark blue hair and blue faces, wearing clothes of five colors, coming to attack and devour the students. He was in a panic, unable to drive them away, so he took a wooden stick and beat the ghosts until they disappeared, glad that the students were saved from danger. Not long after, upon closer inspection, he realized that those he had beaten to death were not ghosts, but his five disciples. The bodies lay on the ground, the pain piercing his heart and liver. He then sought his own death, so he hit his head against the wall and broke it. The officials examined the scene and took a statement, finding it difficult to establish a credible case based on the ghost story. They questioned the parents of each child, who all said: We had no quarrels or grievances with Jiang Ya. He was a teacher who cared for the children quite well, and he also had no signs of madness, so we don't know why he did this. It must be a karmic debt from a past life. Jiang's head was broken and he was near death, so he is now being detained. He will be examined and punished after he is fully healed. This is a document from the Qingyang County Magistrate, submitted to Governor Yin, during the fifth month of the twenty-first year of the Qianlong era. I saw it with my own eyes. Half a month later, it was reported that Jiang Ya died in prison.

    301

    ○梁武帝第四子杭州汪慎仪家，园亭极佳，园在小粉墙北街，主人将有掘池之举，夜梦美少年：玉冠珠履，仪貌详华；自领以下，悉翠丝环衤颉，袍衫上绣万枝梅花。自称：“我梁武皇帝第四子南康王萧绩也，都督江州病薨，葬此千馀年。闻主人将有池塘之掘，幸勿伤我窀穸。”言毕而逝。主人次日命锹锸试之，未丈许，得梁天监八年所造方砖数十块，遂止掘。今砖藏严侍读冬友家。

    　　○吕城无关庙吕城五十里内无关庙。相传城为吕蒙所筑，至今蒙为土地。一造关庙，每夜必有兵戈角斗声，以故相戒勿立关庙也。有以卜卦行道者借宿土神庙中，夜间雷雨作闹，屋瓦皆飞及旦。不解其故。里人来观：则卜者所肩一布上画帝君像也。

    Explanation

    301

    ○ In the home of Wang Shenyi in Hangzhou, the garden pavilions were extremely beautiful, and the garden was located on the north street of the small powder wall. The owner was planning to dig a pond, and that night, he dreamed of a beautiful young man: wearing a jade crown and pearl shoes, with a dignified and elegant appearance; below the waist, all adorned with green silk rings and embroidered garments, with the robe embroidered with ten thousand branches of plum blossoms. The young man introduced himself as: "I am the fourth son of Emperor Wu of Liang, Prince of Nankang, Xiao Ji. I died while serving as the military governor of Jiangzhou and have been buried here for over a thousand years. I heard that the owner is planning to dig a pond. Please do not disturb my tomb." After speaking, he disappeared. The next day, the owner ordered spades and shovels to test the area, and not even a full zhang (about 3 meters) deep, they found dozens of square bricks made in the eighth year of Emperor Wu's Tianjian reign. They then stopped digging. Now the bricks are kept in the home of Yan Shidu, a scholar.

    ○ There is no Guan Temple in Lücheng within fifty li of the city. It is said that the city was built by Lü Meng, and Lü Meng is still worshipped as the local deity. Every time a Guan Temple is built, there are sounds of battle and fighting every night, so people have been warned not to build a Guan Temple. A diviner traveling on the road stayed overnight in a temple dedicated to the earth god. During the night, there was thunder and rain causing disturbances, and the roof tiles all flew off by morning. He did not understand the reason. Villagers came to see, and it was found that the diviner was carrying a banner on his shoulder with an image of the emperor painted on it.

    302

    乃逐之，不许其再宿吕侯庙中。

    　　○姚剑仙边桂岩为山于通判，构屋洪泽堤畔，集宾客觞咏其中。一夕，觥筹正开，有客闯然入，冠履垢敝，辫发毵毵然，披拂于耳，叉手捐坐诸客上，饮啖无怍。

    　　诸客问名姓，曰：“姓姚，号穆云，浙之萧山人。”问何能，笑曰：“能戏剑。”

    　　口吐铅子一丸，滚掌中成剑，长寸许，火光自剑端出，熠熠如蛇吐舌。诸客悚息，莫敢声。主人虑惊客，再三请收。客谓主人曰：“剑不出则已，既出，则杀气甚盛，必斩一生物而后能敛。”通判曰：“除人外皆可。”姚顾阶下桃树，手指之。

    　　白光飞树下，环绕一匝，树仆地无声。口中复吐一丸如前状，与桃树下白光相击，双虬攫拿，直上青天，满堂灯烛尽灭。姚且弄丸且视诸客，客愈惊惧，有长跪者。

    Explanation

    302

    They drove him away, not allowing him to stay another night in the temple of Lord Lü.

    ○ Yao Jianxian, serving as the magistrate of Shanyuan, built a house by the banks of Hongze Lake, where he gathered guests to drink and compose poetry. One evening, as the wine cups and game pieces were being passed around, a guest suddenly entered, wearing dirty and worn-out clothes, with long, straggly braids hanging down and brushing against his ears. He crossed his hands and sat above the guests, eating and drinking without any shame.

    The guests asked for his name and surname, and he replied, "My surname is Yao, and my courtesy name is Muyun. I am from Xiaoshan, Zhejiang." When asked about his abilities, he smiled and said, "I can perform swordplay."

    He spat out a small ball, which rolled in his palm and transformed into a sword, about an inch long. A flame burst forth from the tip of the sword, flickering like a snake's tongue. The guests were frightened and held their breath, not daring to make a sound. The host, worried about frightening the guests, repeatedly asked him to put the sword away. The guest told the host, "If the sword doesn't come out, then that's it. Once it does, the killing intent is very strong, and I must cut a living thing before I can put it away." The magistrate said, "Except for humans, anything else is fine." Yao looked at the peach tree on the steps and pointed to it.

    A white light flew under the tree, circled around once, and the tree fell to the ground silently. He then spat out another ball like the first one, which clashed with the white light under the peach tree. The two balls were like two dragons grabbing each other, shooting straight up into the sky. All the lights in the hall went out. Yao played with the ball while looking at the guests, and the guests became even more frightened, some of them kneeling down.

    303

    姚微笑起曰：“毕矣。”以手招两光奔掌内，仍作双丸吞口中，了无他物，引满大嚼。群客请受业为弟子，姚曰：“太平之世，用此何为？吾有剑术无点金术，故来。”通判赠以百金。居三日去。

    　　○黑煞神桐城农民汪廷佐，耕双冈圩。发一古墓。得古鼎、铜镜等物。携归家，置镜几上，彻夜通明，以为宝也，与其妻加爱护焉。

    　　亡何，汪入街市，路见狰狞黑面者，长丈余，拳殴之曰：“我黑煞神也，汝盗陆小姐墓，当死。小姐乃元元年安徽太守陆公女。陆作官有善政，小姐夭亡，上帝怜之，嘱我营护其坟，命小姐往徽州司一路痘疫事。汝敢乘我与小姐外出，而盗其所有耶！”言毕，仆地昏迷，路人舁之至家，疽发于背。小姐亦附其妻身大骂。举家哀求，欲延高僧为设斋醮。小姐曰：“不必，汝村农无知。既自知罪，但速将鼎、镜等物送归原所，别买棺安葬我骨，可以恕汝。

    Explanation

    303

    Yao smiled and stood up, saying, "It is finished." He used his hand to summon two lights into his palm, then formed them into two pills and swallowed them into his mouth. There was nothing else in his mouth, and he ate heartily. The guests asked to learn from him and become his disciples. Yao said, "In a peaceful era, what is the use of this? I have swordsmanship but no alchemy, so I came." The prefecture magistrate gave him a hundred gold pieces. He stayed for three days and then left.

    The Black Demon God: Wang Tingzuo, a farmer from Tongcheng, was plowing the Double Ridge embankment. He discovered an ancient tomb. He found an ancient tripod, a bronze mirror, and other objects. He carried them home, placed the mirror on the table, and it remained brightly lit all night, which he considered a treasure. He and his wife cherished it dearly.

    Shortly after, Wang entered the market and saw a fierce, black-faced man over ten feet tall. The man punched him and said, "I am the Black Demon God. You stole Lady Lu's tomb, you shall die. Lady Lu was the daughter of Governor Lu of Anhui in the first year of Yuanyou. Governor Lu had good governance, but Lady Lu died young. God pitied her and entrusted me to protect her grave. He ordered Lady Lu to manage smallpox and plague affairs in Huizhou. How dare you take advantage of my and Lady Lu's absence to steal everything?" After saying this, he fell to the ground unconscious. Passersby carried him home, and he developed a boil on his back. Lady Lu also attached herself to Wang's wife and scolded her. The whole family begged and wanted to invite a high monk to perform a ritual. Lady Lu said, "It's unnecessary, you village farmers are ignorant. Since you know your guilt, just quickly return the tripod, mirror, and other items to their original place, and buy another coffin to bury my bones, and I will forgive you."

    304

    但我已为冥司痘神，应享香火，此段公案，须立一碑，晓示村民，永照灵应。城中贡士姚先生翌佐，人品端方，人所敬信，须往求其作记，方免汝死。”汪叩头曰：“前发墓时，但见鼎镜等物，实不见有骸骨。此时虽买新棺，将从何处检小姐骨耶？”小姐曰：“我年少女子，骨脆，岁又久远，故已化矣。然我骨所化之土，坚洁不污，有金色光。汝往坑中取土，映日视之，便有识别，可以改葬。”汪如其言，试之果然，即为礼葬。往告姚贡生，姚亦夜有所梦，乃作记立碑，而汪疽愈。

    　　此事江宁太守章公攀桂所言。章，桐城人也。

    　　○吴子云康熙初，桐城秀才吴子云春夜玩月，闻空中有人声曰：“今年乡试，吴子云当中四十九名。”诵其文琅琅然，题是“君子之于天下也”一章。吴虽不甚记忆，而觉其文甚佳，因预作此题文以备试。未几入场，果此题，大喜，因书宿构，放榜果中，如其数。旋登进士，官翰林，督学湖南，满载而归。

    Explanation

    304

    But I have become the deity of smallpox in the underworld and should be honored with incense and offerings. This case must be recorded on a stele to inform the villagers and shine forever as a sign of divine response. Mr. Yao, a respected scholar from the city, has an upright and honorable character and is trusted by all. You must go to him to request that he write the inscription, which will save your life. Mr. Wang bowed and said, "When we opened the tomb, we only saw items like a tripod and mirror, but no bones. Even if we buy a new coffin, where will we find the bones of the young lady?" The young lady replied, "I was a young woman with brittle bones, and the passage of time has caused them to dissolve. However, the soil where my bones have dissolved is clean and unsoiled, with a golden glow. Go to the pit and take some soil, hold it up to the sun, and you will be able to identify it for a proper reburial." Mr. Wang followed her instructions, and indeed, he found the soil as described, so he proceeded to bury her with proper rites. He then informed Mr. Yao, who also had a dream that night and wrote the inscription and erected the stele, after which Mr. Wang's boil healed.

    This story was recounted by Governor Zhang Pangu of Jiangning. Zhang was from Tongcheng.

    ○ Wu Ziyun, a talented scholar from Tongcheng, was playing with the moon one spring night during the early years of the Kangxi era. He heard a voice in the air saying, "This year's provincial examination, Wu Ziyun will come in 49th place." The voice recited the text clearly, and the topic was "The Gentleman's Role in the World." Although Wu did not remember the text well, he felt it was very good, so he prepared a draft in advance. Soon after entering the examination hall, the topic came up, and he was delighted. He wrote from his prepared draft, and when the results were announced, he indeed came in 49th place, just as predicted. He then passed the imperial examination, became a Hanlin scholar, and was appointed to oversee education in Hunan, returning home with great success.

    305

    宿旅店中，夜取溺器，忽有人以手奉之，十指纤纤然。吴惊问，曰：“我狐仙也，与公有前缘，故来相伺。”起烛之，嫣然美女，遂偕伉俪。嘱曰：“妾有雷劫，曾匿君车中以免，故来报君。今君亦有大祸，不可不防。”吴问故，曰：“前途君必宿吕姓店，吕有爱女年九岁，君召而爱之抱之，继为干女，重赐珍宝，则免矣。”吴至吕家，果有此女，遂如其言。至三更时，店主拉吴手笑曰：“我响马盗魁也，君出署时，辎重颇富，诸偻罗儿相涎已久。今知君真长者，我不忍害君。”取壁上铃鞭撞壁者三，诸盗齐入，曰：“吴学院，我干亲家也，诸君不得无礼，急为我护送到家。”吴竟得免。

    Explanation

    305

    Staying at an inn, at night he took a night pot, and suddenly someone handed it to him with their slender fingers. Wu was surprised and asked, "I am a fox spirit, and I have a predestined connection with you, so I came to serve you." Upon lighting a candle, he saw a beautiful young lady, and they became husband and wife. She advised, "I have a thunderbolt calamity, and I once hid in your carriage to avoid it, so I came to repay you. Now you also have a great calamity, you must be cautious." Wu asked why, and she said, "Ahead, you will surely stay at a Li's inn. Li has a beloved daughter aged nine. You should call her, love her, and embrace her, and make her your goddaughter, heavily rewarding her with treasures, then you will be saved." Wu went to Li's house, and indeed there was such a daughter, so he followed her advice. At the third watch of the night, the innkeeper pulled Wu's hand and laughed, saying, "I am the leader of the bandits. When you left the office, your baggage was quite rich, and the bandits have been salivating for a long time. Now I know you are a true gentleman, I do not want to harm you." He took the bell and whip from the wall and struck the wall three times, and all the bandits entered, saying, "Mr. Wu, I am your godfather, the rest of you must not be rude, quickly escort me home." Wu was thus saved.

    306

    后吴无子，族人争以子来求继。吴私问狐：“应继何人？”曰：“牧牛儿好。”

    　　次日，果有牧童过，亦本家也，吴拉入嗣为己子，族人皆笑之。吴亡后，儿颇恂谨，能守其业，家日以富，至今人呼为“吴牛”。尝索对联于方处士贞观，方戏书云：“对窗常玩月，独坐自弹琴。”吴甚喜，竟不知暗用牛事嘲之也。

    　　○秃尾龙山东文登县毕氏妇，三月间沤衣池上，见树上有李，大如鸡卵，心异之，以为暮春时不应有李，采而食焉，甘美异常。自此腹中拳然，遂有孕。十四月，产一小龙，长二尺许，坠地即飞去；到清晨，必来饮其母之乳。父恶而持刀逐之，断其尾，小龙从此不来。

    　　后数年，其母死，殡于村中。一夕，雷电风雨，晦冥中若有物蟠旋者。次日视之，棺已葬矣，隆然成一大坟。又数年，其父死，邻人为合葬焉。其夕雷电又作。次日，见其父棺从穴中掀出，若不容其合葬者。嗣后村人呼为“秃尾龙母坟”，祈睛祷雨无不应。

    Explanation

    306

    After Wu had no heir, his family members competed to offer their sons as potential successors. Wu privately asked a fox, "Who should I choose as my heir?" The fox replied, "A cowherd would be a good choice."

    The next day, a cowherd from the same family passed by, and Wu brought him in to be his adopted son. The family members all laughed at this decision. After Wu's death, the adopted son proved to be diligent and managed to maintain and expand the family's wealth. He became known as "Wu Niu." Once, he sought a couplet from the scholar Fang Zhiguan. Fang playfully wrote, "Facing the window, I often enjoy the moon; sitting alone, I play the lute." Wu Niu was very pleased with this, unaware that the couplet was a subtle insult, using the cowherd's occupation as a joke.

    In Wendeng County, Shandong Province, a woman from the Bi family was doing laundry by a pond in late spring. She saw a plum (or possibly a peach) as large as a chicken's egg on a tree and found it unusual, thinking that it was strange for there to be such fruit in late spring. She picked and ate it, and it was exceptionally sweet. Soon after, she became pregnant. After fourteen months, she gave birth to a small dragon, about two feet long. The dragon flew away immediately after being born but returned every morning to drink its mother's milk. Her father, disgusted, chased the dragon with a knife and cut off its tail. The dragon never returned after that.

    Several years later, the mother died and was buried in the village. One night, there was a storm with lightning, and something seemed to be coiling and spinning in the darkness. The next day, they found that the coffin had already been buried, forming a large mound. Several years later, the father died, and his neighbors arranged for a double burial. That night, there was another storm with lightning. The next day, they saw the father's coffin being lifted out of the grave, as if the earth did not want them to be buried together. From then on, the grave came to be known as the "Tuwei Long Mu Feng" (Turtle-tail Dragon Mother's Grave). People prayed for rain or clear skies, and their requests were always granted.

    307

    此事陶悔轩方伯为余言之，且云：“偶阅《群芳谱》云：”天罚乖龙，必割其耳，耳坠于地，辄化为李。‘毕妇所食之李，乃龙耳也，故感气化而生小龙。“

    　　○石灰窑雷湘潭县西二十里，地名石灰窑。某翁家颇小康，无子，有二女，赘婿相依。

    　　翁贩谷粤西，买妾归，腹有孕矣。其次女夫妇私议：“若得男，吾辈岂能分翁家财？”乃阳与妾厚，而阴设计害之。及分娩，得男，落地死。翁大恨，以为命不宜子，不知乃其次女贿稳婆扼吭绝之也。翁痛不已，解衣裹死儿瘗之后圃。次女与稳婆心犹未安，往启视之。忽霹雳一声，女毙而死，儿苏矣；稳婆亦焦烂，犹未死。众问得其故。翌日，稳婆亦亡，若天故迟死之，取有供状以戒世者。某乃葬女逐婿，分给钱粟使归。舟抵中流，怪风起，婿亦溺死，前后乃数日。

    307

    This matter was told to me by Cao Hui Xuan, a high-ranking official, who said, "I once read in The Spectrum of All Flowers that 'When a misbehaving dragon is punished by heaven, its ear is cut off, and when it falls to the ground, it transforms into a plum tree.' The plum eaten by the woman Bi was actually the transformed ear of the dragon, and thus she gave birth to a small dragon due to this transformation."

    ○ In the lime kiln located twenty miles west of Xiangtan County, known as Lime Kiln, there was a certain old man whose family was moderately well-off but had no sons. He had two daughters and a son-in-law who lived with him.

    The old man traded grain in the western part of Guangxi Province and brought a concubine back home, who was pregnant. His second daughter privately discussed with her husband, "If we get a boy, how could we divide the old man's property?" So they pretended to be friendly with the concubine but secretly plotted to harm her. Upon giving birth, she delivered a boy, but the baby died immediately after birth. The old man was greatly angry, thinking that it was not meant for him to have a son, not knowing that it was his second daughter who bribed the midwife to strangle the baby. The old man was in great pain and took off his clothes to wrap the dead baby and buried it in the garden behind the house. The second daughter and the midwife were still not at ease, so they went to check on the burial site. Suddenly, there was a loud thunderclap, and the woman died, but the child revived; the midwife also burned to a crisp but was not yet dead. People asked what had happened. The next day, the midwife also died, as if heaven deliberately delayed her death to obtain a confession to warn the world. The old man then buried the woman and expelled the son-in-law, dividing the money and grain to send him back. When the boat reached the middle of the river, a strange wind arose, and the son-in-law also drowned. All of this happened over several days.

    308

    ○徐巨源南晶徐巨源，字世溥，崇正进士，以善书名。某戚邹某，延之入馆。途遇怪风，摄入云中，见袍笏官吏迎曰：“冥府造宫殿，请君题榜书联。”徐随至一听，如王者居，其匾对皆有成句，但未书耳。扁云：“一切惟心造。”对云：“作事未经成死案，入门犹可望生还。”徐书毕，冥王筹所以谢者，世溥请为母延寿一纪，王许之。徐见判官执簿，因求查己算。判官曰：“此正命簿也。汝非正命死者，不在此簿。”乃别检一“火”字簿，上书云：“某月某日，徐巨源被烧死。”

    Explanation

    308

    Xu Juyuan, from Nanjing, was a scholar-official who passed the imperial examinations during the reign of Emperor Chongzhen. He was known for his skill in calligraphy. A relative named Zou invited him to his study. On the way, they encountered a strange wind that sucked them up into the clouds. They saw officials dressed in formal robes and holding ceremonial tablets welcoming them and saying, "The Underworld is constructing palaces and requests you to inscribe plaques and couplets." Xu followed them to a place that seemed like the residence of a king. The plaques and couplets were already written but not yet inscribed. The plaque read, "Everything is created by the mind." The couplet read, "Cases are not yet closed when they are not resolved, and there is still hope for return when one enters."

    After Xu finished inscribing them, the King of Hell considered what to offer in return. Xu asked to extend his mother's life by one jī (12 years). The King of Hell agreed. Xu then saw an official holding a register and asked to check his own fate. The official said, "This is the register of those who die according to their destined lifespan. You, who do not die according to your destined lifespan, are not listed here." The official then checked a different register marked with the character "fire." It read, "On a certain day in a certain month, Xu Juyuan was burned to death."

    309

    徐大惧，白冥王祈改。冥王曰：“此天定也，姑徇子请，但须记明时日，毋近火可耳。”徐辞谢而还，急至邹家。主人惊曰：“先生期年何往？舆丁以失脱先生故被控于官，久以疑案系县狱矣！”世溥具言其故，并为白于官，事得释。

    　　时同郡熊文纪号雪堂，以少宰家居，招徐饮酒，未阑，熊忽辞入曰：“某以痞发，故不获陪侍。”徐戏曰：“古有太宰，今又有少宰耶！”熊不怿。徐临去书唐人绝句“千山鸟飞绝”一首于壁，将四句逆书之，乃“雪翁灭绝”四字也，熊怀恨于心。徐忆冥府言，惧火，故不近木器，作石室于西山，裹粮避灾。

    Explanation

    309

    Xu was greatly afraid and reported to the King of the Underworld, begging for a change. The King of the Underworld said, "This is fate; I will temporarily comply with your request, but you must remember the exact date and avoid fire." Xu bowed in thanks and returned, hurrying to Master Zou's house. The owner was startled and said, "Where have you been all this time, Master? The carriage driver was accused by the authorities because he lost you and has been held in county prison for a long time as a suspect!" Shi Pu explained the whole story and reported it to the authorities, and the matter was resolved.

    At that time, there was Xiong Wenji, known as Xuetang, from the same prefecture, who lived at home after retiring as a lesser chancellor. He invited Xu to drink. Before the drinking session ended, Xiong suddenly excused himself and said, "I have an abdominal pain, so I cannot accompany you." Xu jokingly said, "In ancient times there was a Great Chancellor who was ill, and now there is a Lesser Chancellor who is ill!" Xiong was displeased. As Xu was leaving, he wrote a Tang dynasty quatrain "In thousands of mountains, not a bird flies" on the wall, writing the four lines backward, which formed the words "Xue翁's extinction." Xiong harbored resentment in his heart. Xu remembered the warning from the underworld and was afraid of fire, so he did not use wooden utensils. He built a stone room in the west mountain and stored food to avoid disaster.

    310

    时劫盗横行，熊遣人流言：“徐进士窟重金于西山”。群盗往劫，竟不得金，乃烙铁遍烧其体而死。

    　　○九天玄女周少司空青原，未遇时，梦人召至一处：长松夹道，朱门径丈，金字榜云：“九天玄女之府”。周入拜见。玄女霞帔珠冠，南面坐，以手平扶之，曰：“无他相属，因小女有小影，求先生题诗。”命侍者出一卷子，汉、魏名人笔墨俱在焉。淮南王刘安隶书最工，自曹子建以下，稍近钟、王风格。周素敏捷，挥笔疾书，得五律四章。玄女喜，命女出拜，年甫及笄，神光照耀，周不敢仰视。女曰：“周先生富贵中人，何以身带暗疾？我无以报，愿为君除此疾作润笔之费。”解裙带，授药一丸，命吞之。周幼时误食铁针着肠胃间，时作隐痛，自此霍然。醒后诗不能记，惟记一联云：“冰雪消无质，星辰系满头。”

    Explanation

    310

    At a time when bandits and robbers were rampant, Xiong spread rumors: "Xu Jìnshì has hidden a large sum of gold in Xishan." The bandits went to rob, but ultimately did not find the gold, and they burned his body with red-hot irons until he died.

    ○ Nine Heavens Immortal Lady, Zhou Shao Sikong Qingyuan, before meeting anyone, dreamed of someone summoning him to a place: pine trees lined the road, a red gate a hundred feet wide, golden characters on the signboard said: "Palace of the Nine Heavens Immortal Lady." Zhou entered and bowed. The Immortal Lady wore a silk robe and a pearl crown, sat facing south, and with her hand gently supported him, saying: "I have no other business, because my daughter has a small portrait, I ask the gentleman to write a poem." She ordered a servant to bring out a scroll, where the calligraphy of famous people from the Han and Wei dynasties was all present. Prince Liu An of Huainan was the best in regular script, and from Cao Zijian onwards, the styles were slightly closer to those of Zhong Yao and Wang Xizhi. Zhou was usually agile, and he wrote quickly with a brush, composing four quatrains in five-character verse. The Immortal Lady was pleased, and she ordered her daughter to come forward and bow. The daughter was just coming of age, her divine light shining brightly, and Zhou dared not look up at her. The daughter said, "Mr. Zhou, as a man of wealth and status, why do you carry a hidden illness? I have nothing to offer in return, but I wish to heal your illness as a fee for your writing." She untied her sash, gave him a pill, and instructed him to swallow it. Zhou had once mistakenly swallowed an iron needle, which had lodged in his intestines, causing occasional pain; from that moment on, he was suddenly cured. After waking up, he could not remember the poems, but he only remembered one couplet, which said: "Ice and snow melt without substance, stars are tied all over the head."

    311

    ○项王显灵无锡张宏九者，贩芜湖，路过乌江，天起暴风，舟冲石上破矣，水灌舟中，舟人泣呼项王求救。忽有银光如一匹布，斜塞船底，水竟停涌，而人得登岸。次早视之，舱底已穿，有大白鱼以身横塞其穿处，故水竟不得入。舟人举船摇橹，则洋洋然去矣。自此，项王香火倍盛于往时。此乾隆四十年事。

    Explanation

    311

    A Woman with Two Heads and Four Arms

    During the reign of Emperor Ling, a woman from Luoyang was born with two heads and four arms.

    In all writing, it is like riding a swift steed — though it may possess a wild and unrestrained spirit, one must control it with bit and bridle, and must not let it run wild, straying from the proper path or indulging in aimless wanderings.

    From ancient times, wise kings and sage emperors still required diligent study; how much more so, then, for common people! This matter is found throughout the classics and histories. I cannot elaborate further, but I will briefly mention a few essential points of recent times in order to enlighten you.

    As for writings, their origin lies in the Five Classics: edicts, memorials, and military proclamations arise from the Shujing (Book of Documents); expository and argumentative writings arise from the Yijing (Book of Changes); songs, odes, and praises arise from the Shijing (Book of Poetry); ritual sacrifices, mourning, and elegies arise from the Liji (Book of Rites); memorials, admonitions, and inscriptions arise from the Chunqiu (Spring and Autumn Annals). Court regulations, military oaths and decrees, the proclamation of benevolence and righteousness, the elucidation of virtue and merit, governing the people and establishing the state—all are indispensable. As for cultivating the mind and spirit, offering gentle admonition in a relaxed manner, and savoring the flavor of such writings, these too are pleasant pursuits. When one has spare energy, then one may study them.

    The Spirit of Xiang Yu Manifests to Zhang Hongjiu in Wuxi

    Zhang Hongjiu, a merchant from Wuxi, was traveling to Wuhu when he passed through the Uj River. A fierce storm arose, and his boat collided with a rock, breaking it. Water flooded into the boat, and the boatman wept and called out to Xiang Yu for help. Suddenly, a silver light like a piece of cloth appeared, diagonally blocking the bottom of the boat. The water suddenly stopped surging, and the people were able to reach the shore. The next morning, they looked at the boat and found that the bottom of the cabin was already pierced. There was a large white fish that blocked the hole with its body, preventing water from entering. The boatman lifted the boat and rowed, and it sailed away smoothly. From then on, the incense and offerings to Xiang Yu became much more abundant than before. This happened in the 40th year of the Qianlong reign.

    312

    ○医肺痈用白蒋秀君精医理，宿粤东古庙中。庙多停枢，蒋胆壮，即在柩前看书。夜，灯忽绿，柩之前和，橐然落地，一红袍者出立蒋前，曰：“君是名医，敢问肺痈可冶乎，不可治乎？”曰：“可治。”“治用何药？”曰：“白。”红袍人大哭曰：“然则我当初误死也。”伸手胸前，探出一肺，如斗大，脓血淋漓。蒋大惊，持手扇击之。家僮齐来，鬼不见，而柩亦如故。

    　　○朱十二杭州望仙桥许姓住楼，相传有缢死鬼。屠户朱十二者恃其勇，取杀猪刀登楼，秉烛卧。三鼓后，烛光青色，果一老妪披发持绳而至。朱斫以刀，妪套以绳。刀斫绳，绳断复续；绳绕刀，刀亦如烟。格斗良久，老妪力渐衰，骂曰：“朱十二，我非怕你，你福分内尚有十五千铜钱未得，故我且饶你。待你得后，试我金老娘手段！”言毕拖绳走。朱下楼告知众人，视其刀，有紫血且臭。年馀，朱卖屋得价钱十五千，是夕果卒。

    Explanation

    312

    ○医肺痈用白蒋秀君精医理，宿粤东古庙中。庙多停枢，蒋胆壮，即在柩前看书。夜，灯忽绿，柩之前和，橐然落地，一红袍者出立蒋前，曰：“君是名医，敢问肺痈可冶乎，不可治乎？”曰：“可治。”“治用何药？”曰：“白。”红袍人大哭曰：“然则我当初误死也。”伸手胸前，探出一肺，如斗大，脓血淋漓。蒋大惊，持手扇击之。家僮齐来，鬼不见，而柩亦如故。

    Translation:
    Pig Butcher Jiang Xiu Jun was skilled in medical theory and stayed in an ancient temple in Guangdong. The temple had many coffins, and Jiang was bold enough to read in front of the coffins. One night, the lamp suddenly turned green, and a heavy sound came from the coffin, followed by a red-robed figure emerging and standing in front of Jiang. The figure said, "You are a renowned physician. Dare I ask if a lung abscess can be cured, or cannot be cured?" Jiang replied, "It can be cured." "What medicine is used for treatment?" "White ." The red-robed figure wept bitterly, "Then I was mistakenly killed before." It reached into its chest and pulled out a lung, as big as a winnowing basket, covered in pus and blood. Jiang was greatly shocked and hit the figure with his hand fan. The household servants came running, but the ghost disappeared, and the coffin was as it was before.

    　　○朱十二杭州望仙桥许姓住楼，相传有缢死鬼。屠户朱十二者恃其勇，取杀猪刀登楼，秉烛卧。三鼓后，烛光青色，果一老妪披发持绳而至。朱斫以刀，妪套以绳。刀斫绳，绳断复续；绳绕刀，刀亦如烟。格斗良久，老妪力渐衰，骂曰：“朱十二，我非怕你，你福分内尚有十五千铜钱未得，故我且饶你。待你得后，试我金老娘手段！”言毕拖绳走。朱下楼告知众人，视其刀，有紫血且臭。年馀，朱卖屋得价钱十五千，是夕果卒。

    Translation:
    In Hangzhou, near the Wuxian Bridge, there was a family named Xu living in a building. It was said that there was a ghost of a hanged woman. The pig butchering man Zhu Shier, relying on his courage, took a pig-killing knife and went up to the building, holding a candle and lying down. After the third drumbeat, the candlelight turned blue, and indeed, an old woman with disheveled hair and a rope appeared. Zhu struck with the knife, and the old woman wrapped the rope around it. The knife cut through the rope, but it broke and reformed; the rope wrapped around the knife, which became as thin as smoke. They fought for a long time, and the old woman's strength gradually weakened. She cursed, "Zhu Shier, I am not afraid of you, but you still have fifteen thousand copper coins within your fate, so I will spare you for now. Wait until you get them, and then I will test the power of my Lady Jin!" With that, she dragged the rope away. Zhu went downstairs and told everyone. When they looked at the knife, it had purple blood on it and smelled bad. A year later, Zhu sold the house for fifteen thousand copper coins and died that night.

    313

    ○鬼攀日线才能托生乩仙娄子春，自首宋未进士文丞相友也，修炼之术，在九幽使者家处馆四百年。主人司人间生死事，降王爵一等。子春言人间祸福事，甚验。有问轮回之说者，子春云：“轮回非一言可尽，凡死法有数种，生法亦有数种。德大者，成神佛；有来因而无业谪者，仍归原位；虽无德无来因而气未散者，随投人身；其馀散尽者，生即死，死更死矣！然微魂小魄，如风炉炊烟，一时未能消化，往往团为一气，在氤氲鼓荡之中。有时被风吹至阴山下，寒冷异常，惟冬至日有阳光一线，流照阴山，群鬼蠕蠕然，僵而复动，攀日线而行，得至中国，复投人身。投做一人之身，常合群鬼而来，非止一人之魂也。其堕落于线外者，仍归阴山，再待来岁冬至矣。”

    Explanation

    313

    The Spirit Medium Liu Zichun, who claims to be a friend of Wen Chengxiang, a scholar-official of the Song dynasty who did not pass the imperial examinations, is a medium through whom ghosts can be reborn. He has stayed at the residence of the Nine Hells Messenger for four hundred years, where the host oversees human matters of life and death and is ranked one level below a king. Liu Zichun speaks about human misfortunes and blessings, and his words are very accurate. When asked about the doctrine of reincarnation, he says, "Reincarnation cannot be fully explained in one statement. There are several ways to die, and several ways to be reborn.
