Those with great virtue become gods or Buddhas; those who have a cause but no karmic punishment return to their original position; those without virtue or a cause but whose qi (vital energy) has not dissipated are reborn into human bodies; the rest, whose qi has completely dissipated, are born and die immediately, and die again! However, faint souls and small spirits, like smoke from a wind stove, cannot be digested immediately and often gather into a single mass in a state of confusion and agitation. Sometimes they are blown by the wind to the foot of Yin Mountain, where it is extremely cold. Only on the winter solstice is there a line of sunlight that flows over Yin Mountain. The ghosts move slowly, stiffen, and then move again, clinging to the sun's thread to travel, and reach China to be reborn into human bodies. They are reborn into a single human body, but often come together with a group of ghosts, not just one soul. Those who fall outside the sun's thread return to Yin Mountain and wait for the next winter solstice."

    314

    或问：“有来世为人者乎？”曰：“此类甚多，辟如草木，其无旧根而生者，即是初世为草之草；犹之非投胎而来者，即是初世为人之人。”问：“鬼有化物者乎？”曰：“有。大凡娼优化虫蝶，恶人化蛇虎。”问：“雷击之鬼何化？”

    　　曰：“化蚯蚓。”《谭子化书》言：“凡被雷击死者，捣蚯蚓汁覆其脐可活。”

    　　斯言盖有所本。

    　　○死夫卖活妻杭州陶氏，家道小康。老主人绍元，曾为某州刺史，死已久矣。有仆人李福，夫妻同役其家，福病死逾年。忽一日，福妻了陈氏中风发狂，召集其家大呼：“我老太爷也。李福在阴间将妻陈氏卖与我为妾，汝等如何不放他来？”家人大骇，延医视之。陈氏手批医颊，医不敢近。亡何竟死。陈氏恰一粗婢耳，毫无姿色。

    　　○恶鬼吓诈不遂仁和秀才陈渠，性颇严正，生一女，幼而好道，日持斋诵经。闻人为议婚，便涕泣不食，渠厌苦之，父女不相见。

    Explanation

    314

    Final Translation

    Reincarnation and Transformation

    Someone asked, "Are there those who are reborn as humans?" The response was, "Such cases are numerous, like plants and trees, those that grow without a pre-existing root are those that were originally plants in their first incarnation; similarly, those who are not born through reincarnation are those who were humans in their first incarnation." Another question was, "Can ghosts transform into other forms?" The answer was, "Yes. Generally, prostitutes transform into insects or butterflies, and evil people transform into snakes or tigers." A further question was, "What do ghosts struck by lightning transform into?"

    The response was, "They transform into earthworms." The Tánzǐ Huàshū (谭子化书) states, "Anyone struck by lightning and killed can be revived by applying the juice of crushed earthworms to their navel." This statement is based on something.

    A Dead Husband Sells a Living Wife

    In Hangzhou, there was a family named Tao, whose household had a modest but comfortable life. The elderly master, Shào Yuán, was once a governor of a certain state and had been dead for a long time. There was a servant named Lǐ Fú, who worked with his wife in the household. Lǐ Fú died of illness more than a year ago. Suddenly one day, Lǐ Fú's wife, Chén Shì, suffered from a stroke and went mad. She gathered the family and shouted, "I am the old master. Lǐ Fú in the underworld sold his wife, Chén Shì, to me as a concubine. Why didn't you let him come back?" The family was greatly shocked and called a doctor to examine her. Chén Shì slapped the doctor's face, and the doctor dared not approach. Shortly thereafter, she died. Chén Shì was just a low-ranking female servant, with no beauty at all.

    A Malevolent Ghost Fails to Deceive

    Chén Zhōngqú, a scholar from Rénhé, was known for his strict and upright character. He had a daughter who was young and fond of the Dao. She daily fasted and recited scriptures. When she heard that someone was proposing marriage, she would weep and refuse to eat. Chén Zhōngqú became tired of her and no longer saw his daughter.

    315

    年三十余，忽病重呓语，口称：“我江西布客张四。汝前世为船户，我雇当船往四川，汝谋财杀我，并抉我目，剥我皮，沉我江中，故我来索命。”陈心念谋财之盗，容或有之；剥皮之事，盗未必为。问：“是何年事？”曰：“雍正十一年。”陈大笑曰：“雍正十一年，我女已三岁矣，焉有尚为船户之事？”女忽自批其颊曰：“陈先生好利害！是我错寻你女儿了。与我钱三千，我即去。”陈怒曰：“恶鬼妄诈人，我方取桃枝打汝，焉得与汝钱？”女又自批其颊曰：“陈先生好利害！汝既说我是恶鬼，我将肆恶鬼手段，索汝女命去，毋悔。”陈曰：“此女不孝，我甚厌之；汝同她去，我甚喜。但汝并非冤家，敢如此吓诈，想吾女阳数已绝矣。汝能立索其命，方信汝手段；若三日后死，则是吾女之大数使然，非汝手段也。”言毕，女蹶然起，不复作鬼语。后两月余，女才死。

    Explanation

    315

    When he was in his thirties, he suddenly fell seriously ill and began to mutter incoherently, claiming, "I am Zhang Si, a merchant from Jiangxi. In a past life, you were a boatman. I hired you to take me to Sichuan, but you murdered me for money, plucked out my eyes, skinned me, and threw me into the river. That is why I have come to demand my life." Chen thought that a thief who killed for money might exist, but the act of skinning was likely not done by a thief. He asked, "What year was this?" The ghost replied, "The eleventh year of Yongzheng." Chen laughed loudly and said, "The eleventh year of Yongzheng, my daughter was already three years old, how could she still be a boatman?" The ghost suddenly slapped her own cheek and said, "Mr. Chen, you are so sharp! I must have mistaken your daughter. Give me three thousand coins, and I will leave." Chen became angry and said, "A wicked ghost deceiving people, I am just about to take a peach branch to beat you, how can I give you money?" The ghost slapped her cheek again and said, "Mr. Chen, you are so sharp! Since you call me a wicked ghost, I will use the wicked methods of a ghost to take your daughter's life, do not regret it." Chen said, "This daughter is disobedient, I am very disgusted with her; if you take her with you, I would be very happy. But you are not my enemy, how dare you frighten and deceive me like this? I believe my daughter's fated lifespan has ended. If you can immediately take her life, I will believe in your power; if she dies three days later, it is her great fate, not your doing." After he finished speaking, the ghost suddenly stood up and no longer spoke in ghostly language. Two months later, the daughter died.

    316

    ○道士作祟自毙杭州赵清尧好弈，闻落子声，必与对枰。偶游二圣庵，见道人貌陋，与客方弈，而棋甚劣，自称“炼师”。赵意薄之，不与交言，随即辞出。

    Explanation

    316

    A Taoist Priest Engages in Malevolent Acts and Dies by His Own Hand

    In Hangzhou, there was a man named Zhao Qingyao who enjoyed playing chess. Whenever he heard the sound of pieces being placed on the board, he would inevitably engage in a match. Once, while visiting the Two Saints Temple, he saw a Taoist priest with an unattractive appearance, who was playing chess with a guest. The priest's skills were very poor, and he introduced himself as a "Lian Shi" (炼师), a title for a Taoist priest. Zhao looked down upon him, refused to engage in conversation, and immediately left.

    317

    是夕，上床就寝，有鬼火二团绕其帐上，赵不为动。俄有青面锯齿鬼持刀揭帐，赵厉声呵之，旋即消灭。次夕，满床作啾啾声，如童子学语，初不甚分明，细听之，乃云：“我棋劣自称炼师，与汝何干，而敢轻我？”赵方知道是道士为崇，愈加不恐。旋又闻低声云：“汝大胆，刀剑不畏，我将以勾魂法取汝性命。”

    　　遂咒云：“天灵灵，地灵灵，当门顶心下一针。”赵闻之，觉满身肉然如欲颤者，乃强制其心，总不一动，兼以手自塞其耳，然临卧则咒声出于枕中。

    　　赵坚忍月余，忽见道士涕泣跪于床前曰：“我以一念之嗔，来行法怖汝，要汝央求，好取些财帛。不料汝总不动心，我悔之无及。我法不行于人者，反殃其身，故我昨日已死；魂无所归，愿来服役，作君家樟柳神，以赎前愆。”赵卒不答。明日，遣人往二圣庵观之，道士果自刭。嗣后，赵君一日前之事必知之。或云：道士为服役也。

    Explanation

    317

    That evening, Zhao went to bed, and two balls of ghostly fire circled above his canopy. Zhao remained unmoved. Soon, a ghost with a green face and saw-like teeth appeared, holding a knife and tearing the canopy. Zhao shouted fiercely at it, and it immediately disappeared. The next evening, the bed was filled with chirping sounds, like a child learning to speak. At first, the sounds were not very clear, but upon careful listening, they said, "I am a poor chess player and call myself a炼师. What do you have to do with me, and dare to belittle me?" Zhao finally realized it was a Taoist priest causing trouble and became even less afraid. He then heard a low voice say, "You are bold, you fear neither knife nor sword. I will use the soul-calling method to take your life."

    He then recited an incantation: "Heaven is auspicious, Earth is auspicious. A needle in the center of the forehead." Upon hearing this, Zhao felt his whole body trembling as if it wanted to shake. He forced himself to remain completely still, covering his ears with his hands, but when he lay down, the incantation came from his pillow.

    Zhao endured firmly for over a month. Suddenly, he saw the Taoist priest kneeling before the bed, weeping, and said, "I came to frighten you with magic out of a moment of anger, to make you beg, to get some money. I did not expect you to remain unmoved. I regret it too late. My magic, when it doesn't work on people, harms me instead. Therefore, I died yesterday. My soul has no place to go, I wish to serve as your樟柳神, to atone for my past sins." Zhao finally did not respond. The next day, someone was sent to the Two Saints Temple to check, and the Taoist priest indeed committed suicide. Afterwards, Master Zhao knew everything that happened the day before. Some say: the Taoist priest served as a樟柳神.

    318

    ●卷九○木箍颈庄怡园在关东见猎户有以木板箍其颈者，怪而问之，曰：“我兄弟二人，方驰出猎，行大野间，忽见一人长三尺许，白须幅巾，揖于马前。兄问：”何人？‘摇头不语，但以口吹其马，马惊不行。兄怒，抽箭射之。其人奔窜，兄逐之，久而不返。我往寻兄，至一树下，兄仆于地，颈长数尺，呼之不醒。我方惊惶，幅巾人从树中出，又张口吹我。我觉颈痒难耐，搔之，随手而长，蠕蠕然若变作蛇颈者，急抱颈驰马逃归，始免于死。然颈已痿废不能振起，故以木板箍之而加铁焉。“或曰：此三尺许人，乃水木之精游光毕方类也，能呼其名，则不为害。见《抱朴子》。

    　　○掘冢奇报杭州朱某，以发冢起家，聚其徒六七人，每深夜昏黑，便持锄四出。嫌所掘老多枯骨，少金银，乃设乩盘，预卜其藏。一日，岳王降坛曰：“汝发冢取死人财，罪浮于盗贼，再不悛改，吾将斩汝。”朱大骇，自此歇业。

    Explanation

    318

    Final Translation

    Chapter 90: The Wood Collar of Zhuang Yiyuan

    In Guandong, I saw a hunter wearing a wooden collar around his neck. I found it strange and asked him about it. He said, "My brother and I were just out hunting, riding through the wilderness. Suddenly, we saw a man about three feet tall, with a white beard and a square headband, bowing before our horses. My brother asked, 'Who are you?' He shook his head without speaking and only blew on our horses, causing them to be startled and refuse to move. My brother became angry and shot an arrow at him. The man ran away, and my brother chased him for a long time without returning. I went to look for my brother and found him lying on the ground under a tree, his neck several feet long, and he could not be awakened. I was terrified, and the man with the square headband emerged from the tree, blowing on me again. I felt an unbearable itch in my neck and scratched it. As I scratched, it grew, wriggling like a snake's neck. I quickly grabbed my neck and rode the horse back home, thus escaping death. However, my neck had become paralyzed and could not be raised, so I wore a wooden collar with iron reinforcement."

    Some say that this three-foot-tall man was a spirit of water and wood, similar to the youguang and bifang. If you can call out its name, it will not harm you. This is recorded in Baopu Zi.

    Chapter 90: Strange Reckoning for Tomb Robbing

    In Hangzhou, there was a man named Zhu who made a living by tomb robbing. He gathered a group of six or seven followers and, every night when it was dark, they would take their shovels and go out to dig. They were unhappy with the fact that they mostly found old, dried bones and few treasures, so they set up a divination plate to predict where the treasures might be hidden. One day, the spirit of Yue Fei descended to the altar and said, "You rob tombs and take the wealth of the dead, your crime is worse than that of thieves. If you do not reform, I will cut you down." Zhu was greatly frightened and stopped his business from then on.

    319

    年余，其党无所归，乃诱其再祷于乩神以试之。如其言，又一神降曰：“我西湖水仙也。保ㄈ塔下有石井，井西有富人坟，可掘得千金。”朱大喜，与其徒持锄往。遍觅石井不得，正徘徊间，若有耳语者曰：“塔西柳树下非井耶？”视之，已填枯井也。掘三四尺，得大石椁，长阔异常，与其党六七人共扛之，莫能起。相传净寺僧有能持飞杵咒者，诵咒百余，棺椁自开，乃共迎僧，许以得财烹分。僧亦妖匪，闻言踊跃而往。诵咒百声，石椁豁然开。中伸一青臂出，长丈许，攫僧入椁，裂而食之，血肉狼藉，骨坠地有声。朱与群党惊奔四散。次日往视井，井不见。然净寺竟失一僧，皆知为朱唤去。徒众控官，朱以讼事破家，自缢于狱。

    Explanation

    319

    After a year, Zhu's followers had no place to turn, so they lured him to pray again to a spirit medium to test him. As he said, another spirit descended and said, "I am a water deity of West Lake. There is a stone well under the tower, and to the west of the well, there is a wealthy person's grave. You can dig up and find a thousand gold pieces." Zhu was very happy and went with his followers, carrying shovels. They searched for the stone well everywhere but could not find it. While they were hesitating, as if someone whispered to them, saying, "Isn't the well under the willow tree to the west of the tower?" They looked at it and saw that it was already a filled dry well. They dug three or four feet deep and found a large stone coffin, unusually long and wide. With six or seven of his followers, they tried to carry it together, but they could not lift it. It was said that a monk from the Pure Temple could perform a flying pestle spell. They chanted the spell over a hundred times, and the coffin opened by itself. They then went together to welcome the monk, promising to share the wealth after cooking it. The monk was also a sorcerer or a criminal. He eagerly went after hearing this. He chanted the spell a hundred times, and the stone coffin suddenly opened. An arm emerged from inside, about a zhang long, and grabbed the monk, pulling him into the coffin. It tore him apart and ate him, scattering the blood and flesh everywhere. The bones fell to the ground with a clattering sound. Zhu and his followers fled in all directions in panic. The next day, they went to look at the well, but the well was no longer there. However, the Pure Temple had lost one monk, and everyone knew it was Zhu who had called him away. The followers reported to the authorities, and Zhu was ruined by lawsuits. He hanged himself in prison.

    320

    朱尝言所见棺中僵尸不一；有紫僵、白僵、绿僵、毛僵之类。最奇者在六和塔西边掘坟，有圈门石户，广数丈，中有铁索悬金饰朱棺，斧之，乃犀皮所为，非木也。中一尸冕旒如王者，白须伟貌，见风悉化为灰。侍卫甲裳似层层茧纸所为，非丝非绢。又一陵中朱棺甚大，非绋索所悬，有四铜人如宦官状，跪而以首承棺，双手捧之，土花青绿，不知何代陵寝。

    Explanation

    320

    Zhu once said that the mummies found in coffins were not uniform; there were purple mummies, white mummies, green mummies, and hairy mummies. The most unusual case was found in a tomb west of the Six Harmonies Pagoda. The tomb had a circular stone door several zhang wide, inside which hung a red coffin adorned with gold, suspended by an iron chain. When the coffin was cut open, it turned out to be made of rhinoceros hide, not wood. Inside the coffin was a mummy wearing a royal cap and a decorative veil, resembling a king or emperor, with a white beard and a majestic appearance. When exposed to wind, the mummy turned entirely into ashes. The mummy's attendants wore armor-like clothing that resembled layer upon layer of silk paper, neither made of silk nor silk fabric. In another tomb, a large red coffin was found, not suspended by ropes, with four copper figures resembling eunuchs, kneeling and supporting the coffin with their heads and hands. The tomb was covered in greenish-blue lichen or moss, indicating its age and unknown origin.

    321

    ○一目五先生浙中有五奇鬼，四鬼尽瞽，惟一鬼有一眼，群鬼恃以看物，号“一目五先生”。

    　　遇瘟疫之年，五鬼联袂而行，伺人熟睡，以鼻嗅之。一鬼嗅则其人病，五鬼共嗅则其人死。四鬼伥伥然斜行踯躅，不敢作主，惟听一目先生之号令。

    　　有钱某宿旅店中，群客皆寐，己独未眠，灯忽缩小，见五鬼排跳而至。四鬼将嗅一客，先生曰：“此大善人也，不可。”又将嗅一客，先生曰：“此大有福人也，不可。”又将嗅一客，先生曰：“此大恶人也，更不可。”四鬼曰：“然则先生将何餐？”先生指二客曰：“此辈不善不恶、无福无禄，不啖何待？”四鬼即群嗅之，二客鼻声渐微，五鬼腹渐膨亨矣。

    Explanation

    321

    Five-Eyed Gentlemen of Zhejiang

    In the Zhejiang region, there are five strange ghosts. Four of these ghosts are completely blind, while only one has one eye. The other ghosts rely on this one-eyed ghost to see, and they are known as the "Five-Eyed Gentlemen."

    During years of plague, the five ghosts walk together, waiting for people to fall asleep, and they sniff with their noses. If one ghost sniffs a person, that person falls ill; if all five ghosts sniff a person, that person dies. The four blind ghosts walk aimlessly and hesitantly, dare not make decisions, and only follow the commands of the one-eyed master.

    There was a man named Qian staying in an inn. All the guests were asleep, but he alone was awake. Suddenly, the lamp shrank, and he saw the five ghosts jump and come. The four ghosts were about to sniff one guest, but the master said, "This is a great virtuous person, we cannot do it." They were about to sniff another guest, but the master said, "This is a great fortunate person, we cannot do it." They were about to sniff another guest, but the master said, "This is a great wicked person, even more so, we cannot do it."

    The four ghosts then asked, "Then what will the master eat?" The master pointed to two guests and said, "These people are neither good nor bad, neither fortunate nor blessed, why wait to eat them?" The four ghosts immediately sniffed them, and the two guests' breathing gradually became faint. The five ghosts' bellies gradually became swollen.

    322

    ○梦乞儿煮狗陈秀才清波，处馆绍兴。夜间梦游土地庙，庙后有数乞儿，状貌狞恶，拥土炉剥黄狗而烹之。狗似新受棍伤者，血犹淋漓，陈心恶之。忽门外有衣冠人来骂曰：“我家狗被汝偷食，我将告官。”语未毕，群丐起而殴之，衣冠者倒地死，陈惊醒。越三日，梦青衣皂隶持城隍牌票示之曰：“狗主人被恶丐打死，其鬼已控城隍。牒内写君作证，故来相招。”陈视票，果有已名，且有听审日期，觉而恶之，然自念此事与己无干，不过暂往阴司作证，因恐馆归，以二梦语其亲徐某，且托曰：“我死当复生，诚恐阴阳隔路，一时灵魂迷矣，乞君购白雄鸡书我姓名，临期到城隍庙招呼，免我迷路。”徐以为梦幻难凭，笑允之，始终不信也。

    Explanation

    322

    Tchen Ch'ing-p'o, a scholar residing in Shaoxing, had a dream in which he wandered to a land temple at night. Behind the temple, he saw several beggars with fierce and ugly appearances, who were roasting a yellow dog over a makeshift fire. The dog appeared to have fresh stick wounds, and its blood was still flowing. Tchen felt a strong aversion to this scene. Suddenly, a well-dressed man came out of the temple and shouted, "My dog was stolen by you and eaten! I will report you to the authorities!" Before he could finish speaking, the beggars attacked him and killed him. Tchen woke up in a fright. Three days later, he had another dream in which an official in a black robe presented him with a summons from the city god, saying, "The owner of the dog was killed by the vicious beggars, and his spirit has already filed a complaint with the city god. The document states that you are to testify, so we are here to summon you." Tchen looked at the document and saw his name listed, along with the date of the hearing. He woke up feeling uneasy, but he reasoned that this matter had nothing to do with him and that he would only need to go to the underworld temporarily to testify. He was concerned about returning to his teaching position, so he told his relative Xu about the two dreams and asked him to buy a white rooster, write his name on it, and come to the city god temple on the appointed day to guide his soul, so that he would not get lost. Xu thought the dreams were just fantasies and laughed, agreeing to do as he asked, but he never believed them in the first place.

    323

    至某月某日，陈果无疾而逝。家人泣报于徐，徐急买白鸡书陈姓名而往，适城隍庙搭台演戏，众人蜂拥，至日仄方能到神座下，大呼招魂。及归家，六月盛暑，尸已腐矣。

    　　○一棺藏十八人乾隆四年，山西蒲州修城，掘河滩土，得一棺，方扁如箱。启之，中有九，一藏二人，各长尺许，老幼男妇如生，不知何怪。

    　　○真龙图变假龙图嘉兴宋某，为仙游令，平素峭洁，以“包老”：自命。某村有王监生者，奸佃户之妻，两情相得，嫌其本夫在家，乃贿算命者告其夫以“在家流年不利，必远游他方，才免于难”，本夫信之。告王监生，王遂借本钱，令贸易四川。三年不归，村人相传：某佃户被王监生谋死矣。宋索闻此事，欲雪其冤。一日，过某村，有旋风起于轿前。迹之，风从井中出。差人撩井，得男子腐尸，信为某佃，遂拘王监生与佃妻，严刑拷讯。俱自认谋害本夫，置之于法。邑人称为“宋龙图”，演成戏本，沿村弹唱。

    Explanation

    323

    On a certain day, Chen passed away peacefully without any illness. His family wept and reported this to Xu, who hastily bought a white chicken and wrote Chen's name on it before going to the City God Temple, where a stage was set up for a performance. The crowd was so dense that he only reached the god's seat at noon, where he shouted loudly to summon the soul. By the time he returned home, in the hot summer of June, the body had already rotted.

    ○ A Coffin Containing Eighteen People
    In the fourth year of the Qianlong reign (1739), during the repair of the city walls in Pu Prefecture, Shanxi Province, a coffin was discovered when digging riverbank soil. The coffin was square and flat like a box. Upon opening it, they found nine coffins inside, each containing two people, all about a foot long, old and young, men and women, all looking alive, and no one knew what kind of strange phenomenon this was.

    ○ The Transformation of a True Dragon Map into a False One
    In Jiaxing, there was a magistrate named Song of Xianyou, who was usually strict and clean, and prided himself on being like Bao Zheng. In a certain village, there was a scholar named Wang who seduced the wife of a tenant farmer. They were mutually attracted, but disliked that the husband was at home. Wang then bribed a fortune-teller to tell his husband that staying at home would bring bad luck and that he must travel far away to avoid trouble. The husband believed it. Wang then borrowed capital and sent him to trade in Sichuan. He did not return for three years, and the villagers spread the rumor that a certain tenant farmer was murdered by Wang the scholar. Song heard of this matter and wanted to clear the tenant's name. One day, passing through a certain village, a whirlwind arose in front of his sedan chair. Following the wind, it was found to come from a well. Deputies searched the well and found a decomposed male body, believing it to be the tenant, and then arrested Wang the scholar and the tenant's wife, subjecting them to severe interrogation. They both confessed to plotting to kill the husband and were punished according to the law. The people of the county called him 'Song Longtu,' and the story was turned into a play and performed throughout the village.

    324

    又一年，其夫从四川归。甫入城，见戏台上演王监生事，就观之，方知己妻业已冤死。登时大恸，号控于省城。臬司某为之审理，宋令以故勘平人致死抵罪。

    　　仙游人为之歌曰：“瞎说奸夫害本夫，真龙图变假龙图。寄言人世司民者，莫恃官清胆气粗。”

    　　○莆田冤狱福建莆田王监生，素豪横，见田邻张妪田五亩，欲取成方，造伪契，贿县令某，断为己有。张妪无奈何，以田与之，然中心忿然，日骂其门。王不能堪，买嘱邻人殴杀妪，而召其子视之；即缚之，诬为子杀其母，擒以鸣官。众证确凿，子不胜毒刑，遂诬伏。将请王命，登时凌迟矣。

    Explanation

    324

    Another year passed, and his husband returned from Sichuan. As soon as he entered the city, he saw a play being performed on the stage about Wang Jiansheng. He watched it and only then realized that his wife had already been wrongfully killed. Overwhelmed with grief, he immediately appealed to the provincial capital. The provincial governor (臬司) handled the case, and Song County Magistrate was held accountable for the wrongful death of an innocent person.

    The people of Xianyou composed a song saying: "Blindly accusing the adulterer of harming the husband, the true dragon chart becomes a false dragon chart. Offering advice to those who govern the people, do not rely on your clear reputation and boldness."

    In the case of the Putian冤狱, Wang Jiansheng of Putian, Fujian, was always arrogant and domineering. He saw that his neighbor, Old Lady Zhang, had five acres of land and wanted to take possession of it by creating a fake contract. He bribed County Magistrate (某) to rule it as his own. Old Lady Zhang had no choice but to give the land to him, but in her heart, she was filled with anger and would scold him every day. Wang could not bear it and bribed a neighbor to beat and kill Old Lady Zhang. He then called his son to see it, immediately tied him up, and falsely accused him of killing his mother. He took him to the authorities. The evidence was solid and undeniable. The son could not withstand the torture and eventually confessed falsely. He was about to request the king's decree, and he was immediately subjected to the death penalty by slicing.

    325

    总督苏昌闻而疑之，以为子纵不孝，殴母当在其家，不当在田野间众人属目之地。且遍体鳞伤，子殴母，必不至此。乃檄福、泉二知府，会鞫于省中城隍庙。

    　　两知府各有成见，仍照前拟定罪。其子受绑将出庙门，大呼曰：“城隍！城隍！

    　　我一家奇冤极枉，而神全无灵响，何以享人间血食哉？“语毕，庙之西厢突然倾倒。当事者犹以庙柱素朽，不甚介意。甫牵出庙，则两泥皂隶忽移而前，以两梃夹叉之，人不能过。于是观者大噪，两府亦悚然重鞫，始白其子冤，而置王监生于法。从此，城隍庙之香火亦较盛焉。

    　　○水鬼畏嚣字赵衣吉云：“鬼有气息：水死之鬼羊臊气，岸死之鬼纸灰气。凡人闻此二气，皆须避之。”又云：“河水鬼最畏‘嚣’字，如人在舟中闻羊臊气，则急写一‘嚣’字，可以远害。”

    Explanation

    325

    Governor Su Chang heard of the incident and doubted it, thinking that even if the son was disobedient, he should have beaten his mother at home, not in an open field where many people could see. Moreover, the son was covered in bruises; if he had beaten his mother, it wouldn't be this severe. He then issued a decree to the governors of Fu and Quan, ordering them to convene at the provincial city god temple for a joint investigation.

    The two governors had their own biases and still planned to sentence him according to the previous decision. As the son was bound and about to leave the temple, he shouted, "City God! City God! My family has suffered an extraordinary injustice, and the deity shows no response. How can you enjoy human sacrifices?" After speaking, the western wing of the temple suddenly collapsed. Those in charge still thought the temple columns were old and did not pay much attention. As soon as he was led out of the temple, two clay officials suddenly moved forward and blocked the path with two spears, preventing anyone from passing. The crowd then made a great commotion, and the two governors, startled, re-investigated the case, clearing the son's name and punishing Wang Jiansheng according to the law. From then on, the incense offerings at the city god temple also became more frequent.

    ○ Water Ghosts Fear the Character 'Xiao'

    Zhao Yiji said: "Ghosts have odors: those who died in water have a smell like sheep's dung, and those who died on land have a smell like ashes. Anyone who smells these two odors should avoid them." He also said: "Water ghosts are most afraid of the character 'Xiao.' If someone in a boat smells the odor of sheep's dung, they should quickly write the character 'Xiao' to ward off harm."

    326

    ○狐仙知科举钱方伯琦、蔡观察应彪未第时，有友吴某招饮。其家素奉狐仙。二人与群客至其家，候至日晚，腹已枵矣，不见酒肴，心以为疑。少顷，主出，有愧色，曰：“今日饮诸公，肴已全备，忽为狐仙摄去，奈何？”众客疑吴惜费，以狐为推。

    　　蔡公曰：“主人若果治具，必有水浆痕迹，盍往厨房视之？”往验，则余火未熄，盘碗姜豉之物尚在，始知吴非诳言。众客欲散，独蔡公大呼曰：“果狐仙在此，我有一言奉问：今年乙卯秋闱，我辈皆下场人，如有一个中者，狐仙还我酒肴；如无一人中者，狐仙竟全啖之。我等亦没兴在此饮酒。”言毕，出。未久，主人大笑来曰：“恭喜诸公，酒肴都全还在案矣，今年必有中者。”于是群客欢饮而罢。是年，钱公登第，蔡迟一科。

    Explanation

    326

    ○ Fox Spirit and the Imperial Examination

    When Mr. Qian Fangboqi and Mr. Cai Guancha Yingbiao had not yet passed the imperial examinations, a friend named Mr. Wu invited them to a drinking party. Wu's family had always worshipped the fox spirit. The two men and their guests arrived at Wu's home, and they waited until late afternoon, but their stomachs were already empty, and they saw no food or drink, which made them feel puzzled. Shortly after, the host came out, looking embarrassed, and said, "Today, I prepared a feast for all of you, but the dishes were suddenly taken away by the fox spirit. What can I do?" The guests suspected that Wu was stingy and used the fox spirit as an excuse.

    Mr. Cai said, "If the host really prepared the food, there must be traces of water and food in the kitchen. Why don't we go check?" They went to the kitchen to verify, and they found that the fire was still burning, and the bowls and plates with ginger and soy sauce were still there. They then realized that Wu was not lying. The guests wanted to leave, but only Mr. Cai shouted, "If the fox spirit is really here, I have a question to ask: this year, during the autumn examination, we are all candidates. If any one of us passes, the fox spirit will return our food and drink; if none of us pass, the fox spirit will eat everything. We won't be in the mood to drink here." After he finished speaking, he left. Not long after, the host came laughing and said, "Congratulations, gentlemen! The food and drink are all still on the table. This year, at least one of you will pass." Thus, the guests drank merrily and left. That year, Mr. Qian passed the examination, and Mr. Cai passed one year later.

    327

    ○鬼争替身人因得脱会稽王二，以缝衣为业，手挈女裙衫数件，夜过吼山，见水中跳出二人，倮身黑面，牵之入河。王不能自主，随行数步。忽山顶松树间飞下一人，垂眉吐舌，手持大绳，套其腰，曳之上山，与黑面鬼彼此争夺。黑面鬼曰：“王二是我替身，汝何得夺之？”持绳鬼曰：“王二是成衣师父，汝等河水鬼赤屁股在水中，并无衣服要做，何所用之？不如让我。”王亦昏迷，听其互拉；然心中略有微明，私念倘遗失女裙衫，则力不能赔，因挂之树上。适其叔自他路归，月下望见树有红绿女衣，疑而近前视之，三鬼遂散。王二口耳中全是青泥填塞，扶之归，竟脱于难。

    Explanation

    327

    A Man Escapes Due to Ghosts Fighting for a Substitute Body

    Wang Er, a man from the region of Kuaiji, made a living by sewing clothes. One night, as he was passing by Roaring Mountain carrying several pieces of women's skirts and blouses, he saw two naked, black-faced ghosts jump out of the water and drag him into the river. Wang Er could not resist and was pulled a few steps. Suddenly, a man flew down from the top of a pine tree, with drooping eyebrows and a protruding tongue, holding a large rope. He looped the rope around Wang Er's waist and pulled him up the mountain, where the two naked, black-faced ghosts were also tugging at him. The ghosts and the man with the rope began to fight over Wang Er.

    The naked, black-faced ghost said, "Wang Er is my substitute body; why do you want to take him away?" The man with the rope replied, "Wang Er is a master tailor; you river ghosts with bare bottoms in the water have no clothes to make, so what use do we have for them? It would be better for me to have them." Wang Er was in a daze and let them pull him. However, his mind had a slight clarity, and he thought privately that if he lost the women's skirts and blouses, he would not be able to compensate. So, he hung them on a tree. Just as his uncle returned from another path, he saw under the moonlight that there were red and green women's clothes on the tree and approached to see them. The three ghosts then dispersed. Wang Er's mouth and ears were completely filled with green mud, and his uncle helped him return home, and thus he escaped the danger.

    328

    ○城隍神酗酒杭州沈丰玉，就幕武康。适上宪有公文饬捕江洋大盗，盗名沈玉丰，幕中同事袁某，与沈戏，以朱笔倒标“沈丰玉”三字，曰：“现在各处拿你。”沈怒，夺而焚之。

    　　是夜，沈方就枕，梦鬼役突入，锁至城隍庙中。城隍神高坐喝曰：“汝杀人大盗，可恶！”呼左右行刑。沈急辨是杭州秀才，非盗也。神大怒曰：“阴司向例：凡阳间公文到来，所拿之人，我阴司协同缉拿。今武康县文书现在，指汝姓名为盗，而汝妄想强赖耶？”沈具道同事袁某恶谑之故，神不听，命加大杖，沈号前呼冤。左右鬼卒私谓沈曰：“城隍神与夫人饮酒醉矣，汝只好到别衙门申冤。”

    Explanation

    328

    Shen Fengyu, a secretary in Wukang, was serving in Hangzhou. Just as he was about to rest, an official document arrived from the higher authorities, ordering the capture of notorious bandits. The name of the bandit was Shen Yufeng, and his colleague Yuan, who worked in the same office, played a cruel joke on him by writing "Shen Fengyu" in reverse order with red ink and saying, "Now they are coming to catch you." Shen became angry and snatched the document, burning it.

    That night, as Shen was just lying down to rest, he had a dream where ghost servants suddenly entered and locked him to the City God Temple. The City God, sitting high and shouting, said, "You murderous bandit, how despicable!" He called for the execution of the punishment. Shen quickly protested, saying he was a scholar from Hangzhou, not a bandit. The City God became very angry and said, "It is the established practice of the underworld: whenever official documents from the mortal world arrive, the person to be apprehended is to be cooperatively apprehended by us. Now the document from Wukang County is here, pointing to your name as a bandit, and you foolishly try to deny it?" Shen explained the cruel joke played by his colleague Yuan, but the City God did not listen and ordered the punishment to be increased. Shen shouted out in protest, crying out for justice. The ghost servants privately told Shen, "The City God and his wife are drunk, so you can only appeal to another office."

    329

    沈望见城隍神面红眼眯，知已沉醉，不得已，忍痛受杖。杖毕，令鬼差押往某处收狱。

    　　路经关圣庙，沈高声叫屈。帝君唤入，面讯原委。帝君取黄纸朱笔判曰：“看尔吐属，实系秀才，城隍神何得酗酒妄刑？应提参治罪。袁某久在幕中，以人命为儿戏，宜夺其寿。某知县失察，亦有应得之罪，念其因公他出，罚俸三月。

    　　沈秀才受阴杖，五脏已伤，势不能复活，可送往山西某家为子，年二十登进士，以偿今世之冤。“判毕，鬼役惶恐叩头而散。

    　　沈梦醒，觉腹内痛不可忍，呼同事告以故，三日后卒。袁闻之，急辞馆归，不久吐血而亡。城隍庙塑像无故自仆。知县因滥应驿马事，罚俸三月。

    Explanation

    329

    Shen saw the City God with a red face and squinting eyes, knowing he was drunk, and unwillingly, he endured the pain of the beating. After the beating was completed, he ordered the ghost officers to take him to a certain place for imprisonment.

    As he passed by the Temple of Guan Gong, Shen shouted loudly in protest. The Lord God called him in and personally questioned the details. The Lord God took yellow paper and a red pen to make a judgment: "Seeing your eloquence, you are indeed a scholar. How could the City God get drunk and abuse his power? He should be brought up for punishment. Yuan, who has been in the office for a long time, treats human life as a game, and his years of life should be taken away. The magistrate failed to observe, and he also deserves punishment. Considering he was on official business elsewhere, he will be fined three months' salary.

    Shen, the scholar, received the beating in the afterlife, and his five internal organs were injured. He could not revive and can be sent to a family in Shanxi as their son. At age twenty, he will pass the Jinshi examination to repay the injustice of this life." After the judgment was made, the ghost officers, frightened, knelt and dispersed.

    Shen woke from the dream, feeling unbearable pain inside. He called his colleagues to tell them the reason. Three days later, he died. Yuan heard the news, hastily resigned, and returned home. Soon after, he died spitting blood. The statue of the City God in the temple fell down for no reason. The magistrate was fined three months' salary for excessive use of postal services.

    330

    ○地藏王接客裘南湖者，吾乡沧晓先生之从子也，性狂傲，三中副车不第，发怒，焚黄于伍相国祠，自诉不平。越三日，病；病三日，死。魂出杭州清波门，行水草上，沙沙有声。天淡黄色，不见日光。前有短红墙，宛然庐舍。就之，乃老妪数人，拥大锅烹物。启之，皆小儿头足，曰：“此皆人间堕落僧也，功行未满，偷得人身，故煮之，使在阳世不得长成即夭亡耳。”裘惊曰：“然则妪是鬼耶！”妪笑曰：“汝自视以为尚是人耶！若人也，何能到此？”裘大哭，妪笑曰：“汝焚黄求死，何哭之为？须知伍相国！吴之忠臣，血食吴越，不管人间禄命事。今来唤汝者，伍公将汝状转牒地藏王，故王来唤汝。”裘曰：“地藏王可得见乎？”曰：“汝可自书名纸往西角佛殿投递，见不见未可定。”指前街曰：“此卖纸帖所也。”

    Explanation

    330

    A Woman with Two Heads and Four Arms

    During the reign of Emperor Ling, a woman from Luoyang was born with two heads and four arms.

    331

    裘往买帖，见街上喧嚷扰扰，如人间唱台戏初散光景。有冠履者，有科头者，有老者、幼者、男者、女者，亦有生时相识者。招之，绝不相顾，约略皆亡过之人，心愈悲。向前，果有纸店，坐一翁，白衫葛巾，以纸付裘。裘乞笔砚，翁与之。

    　　袭书“儒士裘某拜”。翁笑曰：“儒字难居，汝当书某科副榜，转不惹地藏王呵责。”裘不以为然。

    Explanation

    331

    Qiu went to buy a post, and saw the street bustling and noisy, like the aftermath of a theatrical performance. There were people with hats and shoes, bareheaded ones, old and young, men and women, and even acquaintances from life. When he called out to them, they paid no heed, and it was clear that they were all dead. His heart grew even sadder. As he moved forward, he indeed found a paper shop, where an old man was sitting, dressed in a white shirt and a gauze headband. He gave the paper to Qiu. Qiu asked for a brush and inkstone, and the old man gave them to him.

    Qiu then inscribed, "Rujishi Qiu bows." The old man smiled and said, "The character 'ru' (scholar) is hard to bear; you should write 'a certain rank in the supplementary list' instead, so as to avoid the rebuke of the Earth Store Bodhisattva." Qiu did not agree with this suggestion.

    332

    睨壁上有诗笺，题“郑鸿撰书”，兼挂纸钱甚多。裘素轻郑，乃谓翁曰：“郑君素无诗名，胡为挂彼诗笺？且此地已在冥间矣，要纸钱何用？”翁曰：“郑虽举人，将来名位必显。阴司最势利，故吾挂之，以为光荣。纸钱正是阴间所需，汝当多备，贿地藏王侍卫之人，才肯通报。”裘又不以为然。

    　　径至西角佛殿，果有牛头夜叉辈，约数百人，胸前绣“勇”字补服，向裘狰狞呵詈。裘正窘急间，有抚其肩者，葛巾翁也。曰：“此刻可信我言否？阳间有门包，阴间独无门包乎？我已为汝带来。”即代裘将数千贯纳之。“勇”字军人方持帖进。闻东角门闯然开矣，唤裘入。跪阶下，高堂峨峨，望不见王，纱窗内有人声曰：“狂生裘某！汝焚牒伍公庙，自称能文，不过作烂八股时文，看高头讲章，全不知古往今来多少事业学问，而自以为能文，何无耻之甚也！帖上自称‘儒士’，汝现有祖母年八十余，受冻忍饥，致盲其目，不孝已甚，儒当若是耶！”

    Explanation

    332

    As he glanced at the wall, he saw a poem written on a note, inscribed with the words "composed by Zheng Hong," and there were many pieces of paper money hanging as well. Qiu had always looked down on Zheng and said to the old man, "Zheng has never been known for his poetry, so why hang his poem? Moreover, we are already in the underworld, what use is paper money here?" The old man replied, "Zheng may be a scholar, but his fame and position will surely rise in the future. The underworld is the most mercenary place, so I hang it to gain honor. Paper money is exactly what is needed in the underworld; you should prepare plenty of it, bribe the guards of King Dizang, and they will only then agree to inform him." Qiu did not believe this.

    He went directly to the western corner of the Buddhist hall and indeed saw several hundred demons with bull heads and fierce appearances, all wearing ceremonial robes embroidered with the character "Yong" on their chests, scolding Qiu. Just as Qiu was in a difficult and urgent situation, someone touched his shoulder; it was the old man in the hemp hat. He said, "Do you believe my words now? There are gifts for officials in the world of the living, but not in the underworld? I have already brought them for you." He immediately handed over several thousand strings of cash on behalf of Qiu. The soldiers with the "Yong" character just then held a document and entered. Hearing the eastern corner gate suddenly open, Qiu was called in. He knelt at the bottom of the steps, the high hall was majestic, and he could not see the king, but there was a voice from inside the gauze window saying, "Wild scholar Qiu! You burned a petition at the temple of Wu Gong, claiming to be able to write, but you only produce stale eight-legged essays, reading commentaries, and have no knowledge of the achievements and learning of the past and present, yet you consider yourself able to write, how shameless you are! The document claims you are a 'Confucian scholar,' but you currently have an eighty-year-old grandmother who suffers from cold and hunger, causing her to go blind, and your filial impiety is extreme, should a Confucian scholar act like this?"

    333

    禁曰：“时文之外，别有学问某实不知。若祖母受苦，实某妻不贤，非某之罪。”

    　　王曰：“夫为妻纲，人间一切妇人罪过，阴司判者总先坐夫男，然后再罪妇人。

    　　汝既为儒士，如何卸责于妻？汝三中副车，以汝祖父阴德荫庇，并非仗汝之文才也。“

    　　言未毕，忽闻殿外有鸣锣呵殿声甚远，内亦撞钟伐鼓应之。一“勇”字军人虎皮冠者报“朱大人到。”王下阁出迎。裘踉跄下殿，伏东厢窃视，乃刑部郎中朱履忠，亦裘戚也。裘愈不平，骂曰：“果然阴间势利！我虽读烂时文，毕竟是副榜；朱乃入粟得官，亦不过郎中，何至地藏王亲出迎接哉！”“勇”字军人大怒，以杖击其口，一痛而苏。见妻女环哭于前，方知死已二日，因胸中余气未绝，故不入殓。

    Explanation

    333

    禁曰：“时文之外，别有学问某实不知。若祖母受苦，实某妻不贤，非某之罪。”

    禁说：“除了时文之外，我确实不知还有其他学问。如果祖母受苦，那是因为我的妻子不贤惠，而不是我的过错。”

    　　王曰：“夫为妻纲，人间一切妇人罪过，阴司判者总先坐夫男，然后再罪妇人。

    国王说：“丈夫是妻子的纲纪，人间所有妇人的过错，在阴间判罪时总是先惩罚丈夫，然后再惩罚妻子。

    　　汝既为儒士，如何卸责于妻？汝三中副车，以汝祖父阴德荫庇，并非仗汝之文才也。“

    你既然是儒士，怎么能把责任推给妻子呢？你的第三名成绩，是因为你祖父的阴德庇护，并不是依靠你的文才。”

    　　言未毕，忽闻殿外有鸣锣呵殿声甚远，内亦撞钟伐鼓应之。一“勇”字军人虎皮冠者报“朱大人到。”王下阁出迎。裘踉跄下殿，伏东厢窃视，乃刑部郎中朱履忠，亦裘戚也。裘愈不平，骂曰：“果然阴间势利！我虽读烂时文，毕竟是副榜；朱乃入粟得官，亦不过郎中，何至地藏王亲出迎接哉！”“勇”字军人大怒，以杖击其口，一痛而苏。见妻女环哭于前，方知死已二日，因胸中余气未绝，故不入殓。

    话还没说完，忽然听到殿外传来远处的锣声和呵殿声，殿内也撞钟击鼓回应。一位身穿虎皮帽的“勇”字军人通报：“朱大人到了。”国王下阁迎接。裘踉跄着下殿，躲在东厢偷偷看，原来是刑部郎中朱履忠，也是裘的亲戚。裘更加不满，骂道：“果然阴间也是势利眼！我虽然读烂了时文，终究只是副榜；朱履忠不过是捐官得来的郎中，怎么能让地藏王亲自出来迎接呢！”“勇”字军人大怒，用棍子打了他的嘴，他痛得苏醒过来。看到妻子和女儿们在他面前哭泣，才知道自己已经死了两天，因为胸中还有余气，所以没有入殓。

    334

    此后南湖自知命薄，不复下场，又三年卒。

    　　○治鬼二妙娄真人劝人遇鬼勿惧，总以气吹之，以无形敌无形。鬼最畏气，转胜刀棍也。

    　　张岂石先生云：“见鬼勿惧，但与之斗，斗胜固佳，斗败，我不过同他一样。”

    　　○狐读时文四川临邛县李生，年少家贫。偶闲坐，一老叟至，揖而言曰：“小女与君有缘，知君未娶，愿偕秦晋之婚。”李曰：“我贫，无以为娶。”叟曰：“郎但许我，娶妻之费，郎勿忧。”生方疑且惊，俄而香车拥一美人至，年十七八，妆奁甚华，几案军施之物，无不携来。叟具花烛，呼婿及女行交拜撒帐之礼，曰：“婚事毕，吾去矣。”

    Explanation

    334

    After this, Nanhu realized his fate was thin and no longer participated in exams. He died three years later.

    ○ Two Wonders in Dealing with Ghosts: Lou the Real Person advised people not to fear ghosts, always using breath to blow at them, using the intangible to counter the intangible. Ghosts fear breath the most, and it can turn out to be better than weapons.

    Mr. Zhang Qishi said, "When you see a ghost, don't be afraid. Just fight with it. If you win, it's good. If you lose, I'm just like him."

    ○ Fox Reads Contemporary Essays: Li Sheng from Linqiong County, Sichuan, was young and poor. He happened to be sitting idly when an old man came, bowed, and said, "My daughter has a connection with you. She knows you are not married and wishes to marry you in a Qin-Jin alliance." Li said, "I am poor and cannot afford to marry." The old man said, "Just agree to me, and the cost of marriage, you don't need to worry about." Li was just doubtful and surprised. Soon, a fragrant carriage brought a beautiful woman. She was age seventeen or eighteen, and the dowry was very splendid. All the furniture and decorations were brought along. The old man prepared candles and called the groom and bride to perform the bowing and scattering of the bed ceremony. He said, "The wedding is over, I will leave now."

    335

    生挽女解衣就床，女不可，曰：“我家无白衣女婿。须汝得科名，吾才与汝成婚。”生曰：“考期尚远，卿何能待？”曰：“非也。只须看君所作文章，可以决科，便可成婚，不必俟异日。”李大喜，尽出其平时所作四书文付女。女翻视良久曰：“郎君平日读袁太史稿乎？”曰：“然。”女曰：“袁太史文雄奇，原利科名，家读。然其人天分高，非郎所能学也。”因取笔为改数句曰：“如我所作，像太史乎？”曰：“然。”曰：“汝此后为文，先向我问作意，再落笔，勿草草也。”李从此文思日进，壬午举于乡。

    Explanation

    335

    The man helped the woman undress and get into bed, but she refused, saying, "My family would not accept a white-clad son-in-law. You must pass the imperial examinations, and only then will I marry you." The man replied, "The exam period is still far off, how can you wait?" She said, "No, I can decide by looking at the articles you write. If you can pass the exams, we can marry now, without waiting for another day." Li was delighted and showed her all the essays he had written based on the Four Books. She examined them for a long time and said, "Have you read the works of Yuan Mei?" He said, "Yes." She said, "Yuan Mei's writing is powerful and original, and it is very helpful for the exams. I have read it at home. However, his talent is exceptional, and it is not something you can easily imitate." She then took a pen and made some changes, saying, "Does my version resemble Yuan Mei's?" He said, "Yes." She said, "From now on, when you write, ask me first for the intention of the essay, and do not write hastily." From then on, Li's writing skills improved day by day, and in 1732, he passed the provincial examination.

    336

    此女在其家，事姑孝，理家务当，至今犹存，人亦忘其为狐矣。此事临邛知州杨潮观为予言。

    　　○何翁倾家通州何翁，生三子，皆庸俗。长子尤陋。娶妇王氏美，内薄其夫，郁郁不得志死。死后鬼常凭次妇史氏为厉，何翁苦之，具牒城隍庙。

    　　越数日，忽一鬼凭次妇言曰：“请亲翁答语。”何错愕，问：“为谁？”曰：“我史某，尔次妇之父也。死后为郡神掌案吏，不复留心家事。昨见翁牒，方知我女为王氏鬼所苦。我恳本官，已将王氏发配云南，嗣后可无患。惟是我女适翁家时，我已去世，家业萧条，愧无妆奁，至今耿耿。兹在冥司积白金五百两，当送女室。翁可于本月十六日子时备香烛果帛，同次子祭厨房之西南隅，焚帛锄土即得矣。”并戒：“是夕备素筵一席，我将邀二三同辈来庆翁也。”

    336

    A Woman Who Is Still Alive

    This woman, in her family, was filial to her mother-in-law and managed household affairs properly. She is still alive today, and people have also forgotten that she is a fox. This matter was told to me by the governor of Linqiong, Yang Chaoguan.

    ○ He, a man from Tongzhou named He, had three sons, all of whom were ordinary and vulgar. The eldest son was especially unattractive. He married a beautiful woman named Wang, who belittled her husband and, in her depression, died. After her death, her ghost often possessed the second wife, Shi, causing trouble. He was troubled by this and prepared a petition to the City God Temple.

    After several days, suddenly a ghost possessed the second wife and said, "Please, sir, answer." He was startled and asked, "Who are you?" The ghost replied, "I am Shi, your second wife's father. After death, I became a clerk under the city god and no longer paid attention to family matters. Yesterday, I saw your petition and learned that my daughter was being troubled by the ghost of Wang. I pleaded with the official, and they have already sent Wang's ghost to Yunnan. Thereafter, there will be no more trouble. However, when my daughter married into your family, I had already passed away, and our family was in decline, so I felt ashamed to have no dowry for her. Now, in the underworld, I have accumulated 500 taels of silver, which I will send to my daughter's room. You can prepare incense, candles, fruits, and cloth on the 16th day of this month at midnight, and with your second son, worship in the southwest corner of the kitchen. Burn the cloth and dig the soil, and you will receive it." The ghost also instructed, "On that night, prepare a vegetarian feast, and I will invite a few friends to celebrate with you."

    337

    翁如其言，及期锄土，竟得空坛，父子怏怏。至夕，鬼又凭妇曰：“翁运可谓蹇矣！我多年蓄积，一旦为犬子夺去，奈何？”先是，何翁有姊适徐氏，生一儿，名犬子。姊夫及姊亡，犬子零丁，挈千金依舅氏，舅待之薄。未几，犬子亦亡，其资竟为何有。犬子怨之，故先期来夺取五百金，盖鬼事鬼知也。

    　　越半载，次妇归宁，暮回家进门，忽倒地大哭，极口骂何翁不绝，举家惊。

    　　听其言，乃王氏自配所逃回。方谋舁入内室，而三媳房中婢奔出告曰：“三娘子在房晚妆，忽将妆台打碎，拍桌大呼，势甚凶猛，不解何故？”何翁夫妇入视，则又有鬼凭焉，乃王氏之解差鬼，骂曰：“何老奴才，太没良心！自家儿媳，全不顾恤，忍心控害，押赴远方。且倚仗尔亲翁史某作掌案吏势，叫我走此万里苦差，分文不给，如何得至云南？今王氏感我一路恩情，将身配我。我与伊回不得家乡，进不得衙门，只好借尔家做洞房花烛。

    Explanation

    337

    The old man followed the advice, dug the earth by the appointed time, and found an empty jar, leaving both father and son feeling disappointed. By evening, the ghost possessed the woman again and said, "Your fortune can be called adverse! I had accumulated wealth for many years, but it was all taken away by the son-in-law. What can be done about it?" Previously, Mr. He had a sister who married into the Xu family, and they had a son named Dogson. After her husband and sister died, Dogson, being left alone, took a thousand gold pieces and relied on his maternal uncle's household, but the uncle treated him poorly. Not long after, Dogson also died, and his wealth eventually belonged to someone else. Dogson resented this, so he came in advance to seize five hundred gold pieces, for ghostly matters are known to ghosts.

    After half a year, the second wife returned to her parents' home and came back in the evening. Suddenly, she fell to the ground and cried, continuously cursing Mr. He, which shocked the entire family.

    Upon hearing her words, it turned out that Ms. Wang had fled back from her marriage place. Just as they were planning to carry her into the inner room, a maid from the third wife's room ran out and reported, "The third wife was putting on her evening makeup when she suddenly smashed the dressing table, slammed the table, and shouted fiercely. I don't understand why." Mr. He and his wife went to see, and they found that another ghost had possessed her, this time the ghost of the executioner who had accompanied Ms. Wang. The ghost cursed, "Old Mr. He, you wretch, you have no heart at all! You completely disregarded your own daughter-in-law, cruelly accused her, and sent her to a distant place. Furthermore, you relied on your maternal uncle, Mr. Shi, who was a clerk, to exert power, making me travel this long and arduous journey without giving me a single coin. How could I reach Yunnan? Now, Ms. Wang is grateful for my kindness along the way and has given herself to me. I and she cannot return to our hometown or enter the government offices, so we can only borrow your house for our bridal chamber."

    338

    快温酒来，与我解寒！”何氏次、三两媳本对房居，此后王凭次妇，则差凭三媳；王凭三媳，则差凭次妇，终日不安。翁奔告神庙，神不复灵。翁大费资财，遍求方士，如此者二年。江西道士兰方九，应招而来。先作符十数张，遍贴其宅之前后门。再入室仗剑步罡。两妇先于房作笑骂状，次作惊窜状，后作哀恳状。忽屋角响声如雷，两妇伏地。兰持小瓶曰：“鬼入！鬼入！”旋封其口，而两妇醒。兰命起王氏墓，斧其棺，面目如生，尸僵出血，乃焚灰与小瓶合埋，用石镇之，其祟永绝。而何翁从此倾家。

    Explanation

    338

    "Bring me some warmed wine quickly to warm me up!" The second and third wives of Mr. He originally lived in separate rooms. Afterward, the second wife of Mr. Wang alternated with the third wife; the third wife alternated with the second wife, causing constant unrest. The old man rushed to the temple to seek help, but the gods no longer responded. He spent a lot of money to seek out alchemists and magicians, and this went on for two years. A Taoist priest from Jiangxi named Lan Fangjiu was invited to help. He first created several talismans and placed them on the front and back doors of the house. Then he entered the room, holding a sword and stepping through the ritual pattern. The two women first laughed and cursed in the room, then fled in fear, and finally pleaded. Suddenly, a thunderous sound came from the corner of the house, and the two women fell to the ground. Lan took a small bottle and said, "The ghost has entered! The ghost has entered!" He then sealed the mouth of the bottle, and the two women woke up. Lan ordered the Wang family tomb to be dug up, the coffin to be broken, and the face of the corpse to be as lifelike as before, but the body was stiff and bleeding. He then burned the ashes and the ghost in the small bottle, burying them together and placing a stone to seal the area, thus permanently exorcising the evil spirit. And from then on, Mr. He lost everything.

    339

    ○江轶林江轶林，通州士人也，世居通之吕泗场，娶妻彭氏，情好甚笃。彭归江三年，轶林甫弱冠，未游庠。一夕，夫妇同梦轶林于其年某月日游庠，彭氏即于是日亡。

    　　学使临通州，吕泗场距通州百里，轶林以梦故，疑不欲往。彭促之曰：“功名事重，梦不足凭。”轶林强行。及试，果获售，案出，即梦中月日也。轶林大不怿。

    　　越二日，果闻彭讣。试毕急回家，彭死已二七矣。

    　　通俗：人死二七，夜设死者衣衾于柩侧，举家躲避，言魂来赴尸，名曰：“回煞”。轶林痛彭之死，即于回煞夜舁床柩旁，潜处其中，以冀一遇。守至三更，闻屋角微响，彭自房檐冉冉下，步至柩前，向灯稽首，灯即灭。灭后，室中自明如昼。轶林惟恐惊彭，不敢声。彭自灵前循柩走至床，揭帐低声呼曰：“郎君归未？”轶林跃出，抱持大哭。哭罢，各诉离情，解衣就寝，欢好无异生前。

    Explanation

    339

    ○ Jiang Yilin, a scholar from Tongzhou, lived all his life in Lüsi Field, Tongzhou, and married Peng, with whom he had a very close relationship. Peng had been married to Jiang for three years when Yilin was just entering his early twenties and had not yet passed the imperial examination. One night, both husband and wife had a dream in which Yilin passed the examination on a certain date in that year, and Peng died on that very day.

    　　The provincial examiner arrived in Tongzhou, and Lüsi Field was a hundred li away. Yilin, due to the dream, hesitated to go. Peng urged him, saying, "The pursuit of fame and fortune is important; dreams are not to be trusted." Yilin went ahead anyway. When he took the exam, he indeed passed, and the results were announced on the exact date of the dream. Yilin was greatly disappointed.

    　　Two days later, he received the news of Peng's death. After finishing the exam, he hurried home, only to find out that Peng had died fourteen days ago.

    　　In common parlance: When a person dies, on the fourteenth day after death, the family sets out the deceased's clothes and bedding next to the coffin and hides away, saying that the soul will return to the body, a practice known as "huishah" (回煞). Grieving over Peng's death, Yilin carried his bed next to the coffin on the night of the "huishah" ceremony, hiding in it in hopes of a meeting. He waited until the third watch of the night when he heard a faint sound from the corner of the room. Peng slowly descended from the eaves, walked to the coffin, bowed deeply towards the lamp, and the lamp immediately went out. After the lamp went out, the room became bright as if it were daytime. Yilin was afraid of startling Peng and did not make a sound. Peng walked from the altar, followed the coffin to the bed, uncovered the curtain, and whispered, "Has my husband returned?" Yilin jumped out, embraced her, and wept bitterly. After crying, they recounted their separation, undressed, and went to bed, enjoying each other's company just as they did in life.

    340

    软林从容问曰：“闻说人死有鬼卒拘束，回煞有煞神与偕，尔何得独返？”彭曰：“煞神即管束之鬼卒也，有罪则羁绁而从。冥司念妾无罪，且与君前缘未断，故纵令独回。”轶林曰：“尔无罪，何故早死？”曰：“修短数也，不论有罪无罪。”

    　　轶林曰：“卿与我前缘未断，今此之来，莫非将尽于此夕乎？”答曰：“尚早。

    　　前缘了后，犹有后级。“言未毕，闻户外风起，彭大惧，以手持轶林曰：”紧抱我！护持我！凡作鬼最怕风，风倘着体，即来去不能自主，一失足被他吹到远处去矣。“鸡鸣言别，轶林依依不舍。彭曰：”无庸，夜当再会。“言讫而去。由此每夜必来。来，检阅生时奁物，为轶林补缀衣服。

    　　两月余，忽欷泣曰：“前缘了矣！此后当别十七年，始与群续后缘。”言讫去。轶林美少年，家丰于财，里中愿续婚者众，轶林概不允。待至十七年，以彭氏貌物色求婚，历通、泰、仪、扬、俱不得，仍归吕泗。

    Explanation

    340

    Soft Forest asked calmly, "I've heard that when a person dies, there are ghost attendants who restrain them, and during the return of the煞, there are煞deities accompanying them. Why can you return alone?" Peng replied, "The煞deities are the ghost attendants who control and bind. If one has committed a sin, they are restrained and follow. The underworld bureaucracy considered me innocent and also because our past karmic connection is not yet severed, so they allowed me to return alone." Yi Lin said, "If you have no sin, why did you die so early?" Peng answered, "Life and death are determined by fate, regardless of whether one has committed a sin or not."

    Yi Lin said, "Your past karmic connection with me is not yet severed. Coming now, is this not the end tonight?" Peng replied, "It is still early. After the past karmic connection is resolved, there will still be a future stage." As she finished speaking, she heard the wind outside and became very afraid. She held Yi Lin tightly and said, "Hold me tightly! Protect me! All ghosts are most afraid of the wind. If the wind touches the body, one cannot move freely and can be blown far away with one step." They said their goodbyes at the crowing of the rooster, and Yi Lin was reluctant to part. Peng said, "There is no need to worry, we will meet again at night." She finished speaking and left. From then on, she came every night. When she came, she reviewed the belongings from life and mended Yi Lin's clothes.

    After two months, she suddenly wept and said, "The past karmic connection is over! We will part for seventeen years and then continue our future karmic connection." She finished speaking and left. Yi Lin was a handsome young man from a wealthy family. Many in the neighborhood wished to marry him, but Yi Lin refused all of them. After seventeen years, he searched for someone resembling Peng to propose marriage, but found none in Tong, Tai, Yi, and Yang. He returned to Lusi.

    341

    吕泗故边海，有海舶自山东回者，载老翁夫妇来，言“本世族，止生一女，依叔为活。其叔欲以其女结婚豪族，翁颇不愿，故来避地。女亦欲嫁一江南人”。

    　　人为翁言轶林，翁甚欲之；言诸轶林，轶林必欲一见其女乃可。翁许之，见则宛然一彭也。问其年，曰：“十七矣。”其生时月日，即彭死之两月后也。轶林欣然订娶，欢好倍常。性情喜好，仿佛彭之生前。或叩以前生事，笑而不言。轶林字曰“蓬莱仙子”，隐喻彭仙再来也。子曰彭儿，女曰彭媳，欢聚者十七载，夫妇得疾先后卒。

    　　○裹足作俑之报杭州陆梯霞先生，德行粹然，终身不二色。人或以戏旦妓女劝酒，先生无喜无愠，随意应酬。有犯小罪求关说者，先生唯唯。当事者重先生，所言无不听。

    Explanation

    341

    吕泗故边海，有海舶自山东回者，载老翁夫妇来，言“本世族，止生一女，依叔为活。其叔欲以其女结婚豪族，翁颇不愿，故来避地。女亦欲嫁一江南人”。

    　　人为翁言轶林，翁甚欲之；言诸轶林，轶林必欲一见其女乃可。翁许之，见则宛然一彭也。问其年，曰：“十七矣。”其生时月日，即彭死之两月后也。轶林欣然订娶，欢好倍常。性情喜好，仿佛彭之生前。或叩以前生事，笑而不言。轶林字曰“蓬莱仙子”，隐喻彭仙再来也。子曰彭儿，女曰彭媳，欢聚者十七载，夫妇得疾先后卒。

    　　○裹足作俑之报杭州陆梯霞先生，德行粹然，终身不二色。人或以戏旦妓女劝酒，先生无喜无愠，随意应酬。有犯小罪求关说者，先生唯唯。当事者重先生，所言无不听。

    Final Translation:

    In the coastal region of Lüsi, there was a sea vessel returning from Shandong that carried an old man and his wife. They said, "We are from a distinguished family, and we only have one daughter who lives with her brother-in-law. Her brother-in-law wants to marry her to a wealthy family, but we are quite unwilling, so we came here to seek refuge. Our daughter also wishes to marry a man from Jiangnan."

    Someone told the old man about Yilin, and the old man very much wanted it. He told Yilin about his daughter, and Yilin insisted on seeing her before agreeing. The old man agreed, and when they met, she was just like Peng. When asked about her age, she said, "I am seventeen." Her birth month and day were two months after Peng's death. Yilin happily agreed to the marriage, and their affection was even more intense than usual. Her temperament and preferences were similar to Peng's before he died. When asked about her past life, she smiled but did not speak. Yilin named her "Penglai Fairy Lady," a metaphor for Peng's return as a fairy. He called his son Peng'er and his daughter-in-law Peng's wife. They lived happily together for seventeen years, but both husband and wife fell ill and died one after the other.

    ○ The story of the "bound feet" in Hangzhou, Mr. Lu Tixia, was a man of pure virtue, remaining faithful to one woman for life. If someone offered to entertain guests with female performers, Mr. Lu showed no joy or anger, responding casually. If someone committed a minor offense and sought his intervention, Mr. Lu would always comply. Those in power respected Mr. Lu, and they would listen to everything he said.

    342

    或訾先生自贬风骨，先生笑曰：“见米饭落地，拾置几上心才安，何必定自家吃耶？凡人有心立风骨，便是私心。吾尝奉教于汤潜庵中丞矣。中丞抚苏时，苏州多娼妓，中丞但有劝戒，从无禁捉。语属吏曰：”世间之有娼优，犹世间之有僧尼也。僧尼欺人以求食，娼妓媚人以求食，皆非先王法。然而欧公《本论》一篇既不能行，则饥寒怨旷之民作何安置？今之虐娼优者，犹北魏之灭沙门毁佛像也，徒为胥吏生财。不揣其本而齐其末，吾不为也。‘一日者，先生梦皂隶持帖相请，上书“年家眷弟杨继盛拜”。先生笑曰：“吾正想见椒山公。”遂行至一所，宫殿巍然；椒山公乌纱红袍，下阶迎曰：“继盛蒙玉帝旨，任满将升，此坐需公。”先生辞曰：“我在世间不屑为阳官，故隐居不仕，今安能为阴间官乎？”椒山笑曰：“先生真高人，薄城隍而不为！”

    Explanation

    342

    Some criticized Mr. Zhen for lowering his moral integrity, but he smiled and said, "Seeing rice fall on the ground, picking it up and placing it on the table makes one's mind at ease; why must one necessarily eat it oneself? If ordinary people have the intention to establish moral integrity, it is already a form of self-interest. I once received instruction from Governor Zhen Tang Qian'an. When Governor Zhen was governing Suzhou, there were many courtesans in Suzhou. There were only admonishments, but no prohibitions or arrests. He told his subordinates, 'The existence of courtesans in the world is like the existence of monks and nuns in the world. Monks and nuns deceive people to eat, and courtesans flatter people to eat. All are not in accordance with the laws of the ancient kings. However, since Ouyang Xiu's Ben Lun (Discourse on the Fundamentals) could not be implemented, what should be done with the poor and homeless people? Those who persecute courtesans today are like the Northern Wei who destroyed monasteries and Buddhist statues. They merely enrich the officials. Not grasping the root and only focusing on the branches, I will not do that.' One day, Mr. Zhen dreamed of a bailiff holding a summons inviting him, with the inscription, 'The junior cousin of the same surname, Yang Jisheng, pays respects.' Mr. Zhen smiled and said, 'I was just thinking about seeing Mr. Jiao Mountain.' He then went to a place, and the palace was majestic. Mr. Jiao Mountain, wearing a black cap and a red robe, came down the steps to greet him, saying, 'Jisheng has been instructed by the Jade Emperor, and after completing his term, he will be promoted; this position needs you.' Mr. Zhen declined, saying, 'I do not wish to serve as an official in the world, so I have chosen to live in seclusion. How could I now serve as an official in the underworld?' Mr. Jiao Mountain laughed and said, 'You are truly a noble person, and you disdain the position of City God!'

    343

    语未毕，有判官向椒山耳语。椒山曰：“此案难判，须奏玉帝再定。”先生问：“何案？”曰：“南唐李后主裹足案也。后主前世本嵩山净明和尚，转身为江南国主。宫中行乐，以帛裹其妃窈娘足为新月之形，不过一时偶戏。不料相沿成风，世上争为弓鞋小脚，将父母遗体矫揉穿凿，以致量大校小，婆怒其媳，夫憎其妇，男女相贻，恣为淫亵。不但小女儿受无量苦，且有妇人为此事悬梁服卤者。

    Explanation

    343

    While the words were not yet finished, a judge whispered to Peishan. Peishan said, "This case is difficult to decide; it must be reported to the Jade Emperor for further decision." The master asked, "What case?" The judge replied, "It is the case of Li Houzhu of Southern Tang wrapping his feet. In his previous life, the Later Master was the Pure and Bright Monk of Mount Song, and in this life he became the ruler of the Jiangnan kingdom. In the palace, they engaged in amusement by wrapping his concubine Yao娘's feet in silk to form the shape of a new moon, which was only a temporary amusement. Unfortunately, it became a trend over time, and in the world, people competed to have small, bow-shaped feet, forcing their parents' bodies into unnatural shapes, resulting in the measurement of large and small. Mothers-in-law were angry with their daughters-in-law, and husbands hated their wives. Men and women passed the practice on to each other, indulging in lewd behavior. Not only did little girls suffer immeasurable pain, but there were women who hanged themselves or committed suicide over this.

    344

    上帝恶后主作俑，故令其生前受宋太宗牵机药之毒，足欲前，头欲后，比女子缠足更苦，苦尽方薨。近已七百年，忏悔满，将还嵩山修道矣。不料又有数十万无足妇人奔走天门喊冤，云：”张献忠破四川时，截我等足堆为一山，以足之至小者为山尖，虽我等劫运该死，然何以出乖露丑一至于此！岂非李王裹足作俑之罪？求上帝严罚李王，我辈目才瞑。‘上帝恻然，传谕四海都城隍议罪。文到我处，我判：“孽由献忠，李后主不能预知，难引重典。请罚李王在冥中织屦一百万，偿诸无足妇人，数满才许还嵩山。’奏草虽定，尚未与诸城隍会稿，先生以为何如？”

    Explanation

    344

    The Heavenly Deity disapproved of the last emperor for pioneering something evil, thus causing him to suffer the poison of the "chuanji yao" (牵机药) from Emperor Taizong of Song before his death. His feet wanted to move forward, but his head wanted to retreat, more painful than a woman's bound feet, and he endured all the suffering before finally passing away. It has been nearly seven hundred years since then, and after completing his penance, he will return to Mount Song to cultivate Dao. Unbeknownst to him, tens of thousands of footless women rushed to the gates of Heaven to cry out their grievances, saying, "When Zhang Xianzhong invaded Sichuan, he cut off our feet and piled them into a mountain, using the smallest feet as the peak. Though we were destined to die, why should we be so exposed and humiliated to this extent? Isn't this the fault of the last emperor for pioneering the practice of foot-binding? We ask the Heavenly Deity to severely punish the last emperor, only then will our eyes be closed in peace." The Heavenly Deity felt compassion and ordered the city gods across the land to discuss the punishment. When the decree reached me, I judged: "The sin originates from Zhang Xianzhong. The last emperor could not have foreseen this, and it is difficult to invoke severe punishment. I propose that the last emperor be punished by weaving shoes in the afterlife for a million times, to compensate the footless women, and only after completing this task will he be allowed to return to Mount Song." The draft of the proposal is finalized, but it has not yet been reviewed by the city gods. What do you think, sir?

    345

    先生曰：“习俗难医，愚民有焚其父母尸以为孝者，便有痛其女子之足以为慈者，事同一例也。”椒山公大笑。先生辞出，醒竟安然。

    　　嗣后，椒山公不复来请，寿八十余，卒。常笑谓夫人曰：“毋为吾女儿裹足，恐害李后主在阴司又多织一双屦也。”

    　　○判官答问谢鹏飞，以仁和禀生为阴间判官，昼如平人，夜则赴冥司勾当公事。友朋多托查寿数，不肯。人疑其惧泄天机，曰：“非也。阳间有司衙门惟犯罪涉讼者才有文簿可查，否则百姓林林总总，谁有工夫为造保甲册？官府听其自来自去耳，阴间亦然。君辈不涉讼，不犯冥拘，气数来则生，气数尽则死，我实无册可查。”

    Explanation

    345

    The Master said, "Customs are hard to change; there are foolish people who burn their parents' bodies to show filial piety, and others who bind their daughters' feet to show kindness—both are the same example." Mr. Jiao Shan laughed heartily. The Master left, and upon waking, he was completely unharmed.

    Afterward, Mr. Jiao Shan no longer came to ask for advice. He lived to be over eighty and died. He often told his wife with a smile, "Do not bind the feet of my daughter, for it might cause Li Yu in the underworld to weave another pair of shoes."

    As for the Judge who answered questions for Xie Pengfei, he served as an official in the underworld by day, appearing as an ordinary person, and at night he went to the realm of the dead to handle official duties. Many friends asked him to check their life spans, but he refused. People suspected he feared revealing heavenly secrets, but he said, "Not so. In the mortal world, government offices only keep records for those who commit crimes or are involved in lawsuits; otherwise, the common people are too numerous for anyone to keep track of in a register. Officials allow them to come and go as they please, and the same is true in the underworld. You all do not involve yourselves in lawsuits or be summoned by the underworld, so when your time comes, you are born, and when it ends, you die. I truly have no records to consult."

    346

    问：“瘟疫死者可查乎？”曰：“引阳九百六阴阳小劫，应死者府县考试，有点名簿，恰可以查。然皆庸庸小民，方入引册；若有来历之人，便不在小劫数中来去，犹之阳间有官者不考童生也。”问：“疫外尚有大劫数乎？”曰：“水火刀兵是大劫数，此则贵显者难逃矣。”问：“冥司神孰尊？”曰：“既曰冥司，何尊之有？尊者，上界仙官耳。若城隍、土地之职，如人间府县俗吏，风尘奔走甚劳苦，贤者不屑为。昔白石仙人终朝煮白石，不肯上天，人问故，曰：”王宇清严，符录麻起，仙官司事者甚劳苦，故愿逍遥于山巅水涯，永为散仙。‘亦此意也。“

    　　○蒋太史蒋太史士铨官中书时，居京师贾家胡同。十一月十五日，儿子病，与其妻张夫人在一室中分床卧，梦隶人持帖来请，不觉身随之行。至一神庙，入门小憩。

    Explanation

    346

    Translation:

    Question: "Can the records of those who died from the plague be checked?"
    Answer: "During the period of small calamities known as the 'Nine Years of Yang and Six Years of Yin,' those destined to die undergo examinations at the local and county levels, and there are lists of names that can be checked. However, these are all ordinary commoners who are included in the register; if someone has a background or lineage, they are not subject to these small calamities, just as officials in the mortal world do not take the examination for young scholars."
    Question: "Are there other major calamities besides the plague?"
    Answer: "Floods, fires, and wars are major calamities, and these are difficult for the powerful and eminent to escape."
    Question: "Which deities in the underworld are the most revered?"
    Answer: "If it is already called the underworld, what need is there to ask about reverence? The revered ones are the celestial officials. As for the roles of city gods and land deities, they are like local and county officials in the mortal world, very tired from running around, and the virtuous do not care to do so. Once upon a time, the White Stone Immortal spent his days cooking white stones, not wanting to go to heaven. When asked why, he said: 'The celestial realm is clear and strict, with numerous and complex documents and records, and the celestial officials are very busy and hardworking. Therefore, I wished to be free and roam on the mountains and water, forever a wandering immortal.' This is also the meaning."

    **○ Jiang Taishi Jiang Taishi Shiquan, when serving as a censor, lived in Jiajia Hutong in the capital. On the fifteenth day of the eleventh month, his son fell ill, and he slept in the same room with his wife, Lady Zhang, on separate beds. In a dream, a servant came with a summons, and he did not realize that he was following the servant. They arrived at a temple, and he entered to rest for a while."

    347

    见门内所塑泥马，手抚之，马竟动，扬其鬣。隶扶蒋骑上，腾空而行，下视田亩，如棋盘纵横。俄而，雨然，心忧湿衣，仰见红油伞，有一隶擎而覆之。

    　　未几，马落一大殿阶下，宏敞如王者居。殿外二井，左扁曰：“天堂”，右扁曰“地狱”。蒋望天堂上轩轩大明，地狱则黑深不可测。所随隶亦不复见。殿旁小屋有老妪拥镬炊火，问：“何所煮？”曰：“煮恶人。”开锅盖视之，果皆人头。地狱井边有人，衣蓝缕，自往投入。妪曰：“此王爷将囚寄狱也。”蒋问：“此非人间乎？”曰：“何必问！见此光景，亦可知矣。”蒋问：“我欲一见王爷可乎？”曰：“王请君来，自然接见，何必性急？君欲先窥之亦可。”因取一高足几登蒋。蒋从殿隙窥王：王年三十余，清瘦微须，冕旒盛服，执笏北向。妪曰：“此上玉帝表也。”

    Explanation

    347

    Seeing the clay horse inside the door, he touched it with his hand, and the horse suddenly moved, raising its mane. A servant helped Jiang ride onto it, and they flew through the air. Looking down, the fields below resembled a chessboard with纵横交错的田地. Soon, the rain fell heavily, and Jiang was worried about his wet clothes, looking up to see a red oil-paper umbrella, held by a servant to cover him.

    Not long after, the horse landed at the bottom of a large hall's steps, spacious and grand like a king's residence. Outside the hall were two wells, with signs on the left and right reading "Heaven" and "Hell," respectively. Jiang looked up at the bright and open space of Heaven, while Hell was dark and deep, incomprehensible. The servant who had been with him was no longer visible. Near the hall, a small house had an old woman holding a pot over a fire. She asked, "What are you cooking?" The woman replied, "We are boiling evil people." When they opened the lid, they found heads inside. At the edge of the Hell well, a person dressed in tattered clothes threw himself in. The old woman said, "The king is temporarily imprisoning the prisoner." Jiang asked, "Is this not the human world?" The woman replied, "Why ask? Seeing this scene, you can tell." Jiang then asked, "Can I meet the king?" The woman said, "The king has invited you, so he will naturally receive you. Why be in such a hurry? If you wish to peek at him first, that is also possible." She then took a high stool and placed it under Jiang. Jiang peered through a gap in the hall to see the king: he was around thirty years old, slender with a light mustache, wearing a crown and splendid robes, holding a tablet and facing north. The old woman said, "This is a memorial presented to the Jade Emperor."

    348

    王焚香俯伏叩首毕，随闻正门豁然开，召蒋入。蒋趋进，见王服饰尽变：着本朝衣冠，白布缠头，以两束布从两耳拖下，若《三礼图》所画古人冕服状。坐定曰：“冥司事繁，我任满当去，此坐乞公见代。”音似常州武进人。蒋曰：“我母老子幼，事未了，不能来。”王有愠色，曰：“公有才子之名，何不达乃尔！令堂太夫人自有太夫人之寿命，与公何干？尊郎君自有尊郎君之寿命，与公何干？世上事要了就了，要不了便不了。我已将公姓名奏明上帝，无可挽回。”

    　　言毕，自掀其椅，背蒋坐，若不屑相昵者。蒋亦怒发，取其几上木界尺拍几厉声曰：“不近人情，何动蛮也！”大喝而醒，觉一灯荧然，身在床上，四肢如冰，汗涔涔透重衾矣。喘息良久，始能起坐，呼夫人告之。夫人大哭。蒋曰：“且住，恐惊太夫人。”因凭几坐，夫人伺焉。

    Explanation

    348

    The king burned incense, bowed down, and kowtowed. Suddenly, he heard the main gate open widely, and summoned Jiang inside. Jiang hurried forward and saw the king's attire had completely changed: he was wearing the clothing and headdress of the current dynasty, with white cloth wrapped around his head, and two strips of cloth hanging down from his ears, resembling the ancient crown and ceremonial robes depicted in the Three Rituals Diagram. The king sat down and said, "The affairs of the underworld are complicated, and I will leave when my term is up. This seat, I ask you to take over." His voice sounded like someone from Wujin, Changzhou. Jiang replied, "My mother is old, and my son is young; there are still things left to be done, so I cannot come." The king's face darkened with displeasure and said, "You have the reputation of a talented scholar, yet how unwise are you! Your mother, the respected elder lady, has her own lifespan, which has nothing to do with you. Your son has his own lifespan, which also has nothing to do with you. Things in the world are meant to be resolved if they can be, and left alone if they cannot. I have already reported your name to the Heavenly God, and there is no turning back."

    Upon finishing his words, the king picked up his chair, turned his back to Jiang, and sat down as if he didn't want to be close to him. Jiang also became enraged, took a wooden ruler from the table, and struck the table loudly, shouting, "Unreasonable, why act so barbarically!" He shouted and woke up, seeing a dim light, his body on the bed, his limbs as cold as ice, and sweat dripping through heavy blankets. He panted for a long time before finally able to sit up, calling out to his wife and telling her. The wife cried loudly. Jiang said, "Hold on, I'm afraid of scaring the grandmother." He leaned against the table and sat down, with the wife watching.

    349

    漏下四鼓，沉沉睡去，不觉又到冥间。殿宇恰非前处，殿下设五座位，案积如山，四座有人，专空第五座。一吏指告曰：“此公座也。”蒋随行至第三座视之，本房老师冯静山先生也，急前拱揖。冯披羊皮袍，卸眼镜欣然曰：“足下来好好，好。此间簿书忙极，非足下助我不可。”蒋曰：“老师亦为此言乎？门生母老子幼，他人不知，老师深知，如何能来？”冯惨然曰：“听足下言，触起我生前心事矣。我虽无父母，而妻少子幼，亦非可来之人。现在阳间妻子，不知作何光景？”言且泣涕如雨下。少顷，取巾拭泪曰：“事已如此，不必多言。保奏汝者，常州老刘也，本属可笑，汝速归料理身后事。今日已十五，到二十日是汝上任日也。”拱手作别而醒，窗外鸡已鸣，太夫人亦已闻知，抱持哭矣。

    Explanation

    349

    At the fourth drumbeat of the night, I fell into a deep sleep and was unaware that I had arrived in the underworld. The hall was not the same as before; below it, five seats were set up, with piles of documents as high as mountains. Four seats were occupied, while the fifth remained empty. A clerk gestured and said, “This is your seat.” I followed him to the third seat and saw that it was my former teacher, Mr. Feng Jingshan. I hurriedly bowed. Mr. Feng was wearing a sheepskin coat and had taken off his glasses, smiling warmly. “You’ve come just in time, very good,” he said. “The paperwork here is overwhelming, and I can’t manage without your help.” I replied, “Teacher, you said this too? My mother is old, and my child is young. Others might not understand, but you know. How can I come?” Mr. Feng looked sad and said, “Hearing your words, it brings back my own troubles in life. Though I have no parents, my wife is young, and my child is still small. I am not the one who can come here either. How are my family in the mortal world now?” He wept tears like rain. After a while, he wiped his tears with a handkerchief and said, “Things are as they are, no need to talk more. The one who recommended you for this position is the old man from Changzhou. It’s quite laughable, but you must hurry back and arrange your affairs after death. Today is the fifteenth, and the twentieth is the day you take office.” He bowed and we parted. I woke up to find the rooster had already crowed, and my mother was holding me, weeping.

    350

    蒋素与藩司王公兴吾交好，乃往诀别，且托以身后。王一见惊曰：“汝满面涂锅煤，昨日大病耶？何鬼气之袭人也？”蒋告以梦。王曰：“勿怖，惟礼斗诵《大悲咒》可以禳之。汝归家如我言，或可免也。”蒋太夫人平时奉斗颇虔，乃重建坛，合家持斋祈祷，兼诵咒语。至期，是冬至节日，诸亲友来贺，环而守之。

    　　至三更，蒋见空中飞下轿一乘，旗数竿，舆夫数人，若来迎者，乃诵《大悲咒》逼之。渐近渐薄，若烟气之消释焉。逾三年，始中进士，入翰林。

    Explanation

    350

    Jiang Su had a close friendship with Wang Gong Xingwu, a provincial official. He went to bid farewell and entrusted his afterlife affairs to him. Wang Gong Xingwu saw Jiang Su with a very dark and sooty face and asked, "Did you have a serious illness yesterday? Why is such a ghostly presence affecting you?" Jiang Su told him about his dream. Wang Gong Xingwu said, "Do not be afraid. Reciting the Great Compassion Mantra can ward off such things. If you follow my advice at home, you might be able to avoid it."

    Madame Jiang was very devout in her worship of the Tao. She rebuilt the altar, and the whole family fasted, prayed, and chanted mantras. On the day, it was the Winter Solstice holiday, and all relatives and friends came to congratulate him, surrounding him to protect him.

    At the third watch of the night, Jiang Su saw a sedan chair flying down from the sky, with several flags and several bearers, as if to welcome him. He recited the Great Compassion Mantra to ward it off. As it approached, it became thinner and thinner, like smoke dissipating. After more than three years, he finally passed the imperial examination and entered the Hanlin Academy.

    351

    ○李敏达公扶乩李敏达公卫，未时，遇乩仙，自称零阳子，为判终身云：“气概文饶似，勋员国同。欣然还一笑，掷笔在秋红。”旁小注曰：“秋红，草名。”当其时，无人解者。后公为保定总督，颏总河朱藻而薨。后人方悟：朱者，红也；藻者，草也。

    　　○吕道人驱龙河南归德府吕道人，年百余岁，鼻息雷鸣。或十余日不食，或一日食鸡子五百，吹气人身，如火炙痛。或戏以生饼覆其背，须臾焦熟可食矣。冬夏一布袄，日行三百里。

    　　雍正间，王朝恩为北总河，筑张家口石坝不成，糜帑数万，忧懑不食。适吕至曰：“此下有毒龙为祟。”王问：“汝能驱之否？”曰：“此龙修炼二千年，魄力甚大。梁武帝筑浮山堰崩，伤生灵数万，此龙孽也。公欲坝成，须贫道亲下河与斗，庶几逐龙去而坝可成。然贫道福命薄，虑为所伤，必须仗对圣天子威灵、大人福力护持之。”曰：“若何而可？”曰：“请王命牌，油纸裹缚贫道背上。

    Explanation

    351

    Translation

    ○李敏达公扶乩
    Li Mindada Gong Wei, at the hour of Wei, encountered a spirit medium who called himself Lingyangzi. He made a judgment about Li's entire life: "Your boldness and literary talent are similar to those of the illustrious officials of the state. With a smile, you will return to a place of joy, and cast your pen in the autumn red." A note beside it said, "Autumn red, the name of a grass." At that time, no one understood it. Later, Li became the Governor-General of Baoding and died before the death of Zhu Zao, the Chief River Commissioner. People later realized: Zhu is red, and Zao is grass.

    ○吕道人驱龙
    In Guandefu, Henan, there was a Daoist master named Lü, who was over a hundred years old. His breath was as loud as thunder. Sometimes he did not eat for over ten days, and sometimes he ate five hundred eggs in one day. When he blew air on a person, it felt like being burned by fire. Once, someone covered his back with a raw pancake, and it was instantly cooked and edible. He wore a single cotton robe all year round and could travel three hundred miles in a day.

    During the reign of Emperor Yongzheng, Wang Zhao'en was the Chief River Commissioner of the northern section. He failed to construct a stone dam at Zhangjiakou, wasting several tens of thousands of taels of silver, and was so distressed that he stopped eating. Lü arrived and said, "There is a toxic dragon causing harm here." Wang asked, "Can you drive it away?" Lü replied, "This dragon has been修炼 for two thousand years and has great power. During the reign of Emperor Wu of Liang, the collapse of the Fushan embankment caused the deaths of tens of thousands of people, and this dragon is responsible for that calamity. If you want the dam to be completed, I must personally descend into the river to fight it, and only then can we drive the dragon away and complete the dam. However, my good fortune is thin, and I am afraid of being injured. Therefore, I must rely on the divine authority of the emperor and the support of high-ranking officials to protect me." Wang asked, "What should be done?" Lü said, "Please have the emperor issue an order, and wrap it in oil paper and tie it to my back."

    352

    用河道总督印钤封，大人手书姓名加封之，乃可。“如其言，道士遂仗剑入水。

    　　顷刻黑风起，雷电大作，波浪掀天。至明日夜半，道士来署，提血剑，腥涎满身，背伛偻，曰：“贫道胁骨为龙尾击断矣。然贫道亦斩龙一臂，臂坠水，仅留一爪献公。龙受伤奔东海去，明日坝可成也。”王大喜，呼酒劳之，欲延蒙古医为之接骨。曰：“不必。贫道运真气养之，半年后可平复也。”次日，王公上工下扫，石坝果成。所藏龙爪，大如水牛角，嗅作龙诞香，悬之，蚊蝇远避。

    Explanation

    352

    The document was sealed with the seal of the River Chief, and the official personally wrote their name and sealed it again, only then could it be considered valid. "As instructed, the Taoist then entered the water with his sword.

    A moment later, a black wind arose, and thunder and lightning erupted, with waves surging to the heavens. By the next midnight, the Taoist returned to the office, carrying a bloody sword, covered in bloody saliva, and with a bent back. He said, "My rib was broken by the dragon's tail. However, I also cut off one of the dragon's arms, which fell into the water, leaving only a claw to present to you. The dragon, injured, fled to the East Sea, and the dam can be completed tomorrow." The official was greatly pleased, called for wine to reward him, and wanted to invite a Mongolian doctor to set his bones. The Taoist replied, "Not necessary. I will use my true energy to heal myself, and it will be healed in six months." The next day, the official went to work and supervised the construction, and the stone dam was indeed completed. The dragon's claw, as large as a water buffalo's horn, smelled like dragon musk. When hung, mosquitoes and flies stayed away.

    353

    吕自言与李自成交好，曾为系草鞋带。又与贾士芳同受业于王先生某。先生常言：“汝愿，故道可成；贾好利，又自作聪明，必不善终，然亦须名动天子。”

    　　嵇文敏公为总河入都陛见，家人不得家信，问吕，吕曰：“汝家大人，已被大木撑入眼矣。”举家惊，恐有目疾。已而授东阁大学士，方知“目”旁“木”乃“相”字耳。乾隆四年，吕入都，诸王公延之治疾，脱手愈。徐文穆公第六子虚阳不闭，吕一见曰：“公子面上血不华色，不过梦遗耳。”今闭目卧地袒胸，手一铁针，长尺余，直刺其心，拔之，血随针出，如一条红丝。取口唾拭其创处，旁人骇绝，而公子不知，是夕病痊。王太守孟亭患腰痛，求道人。道人曰：“俟天晴日来治。”至期，手撮日光揉之，热透五脏而愈。问导引之术，不肯言，乃引其僮私问之。曰：“无他异也，每早至旷野，红日始出，见道人向日作虎跳状，手招日光纳口中，且吸且咽，如是者再。”

    Explanation

    353

    Lv claimed to be friends with Li Zicheng, having once tied the laces of his straw shoes. He also studied with Jia Shifang under Mr. Wang. Mr. Wang often said, "If you have a wish, the path can be successful; Jia likes profit and acts cleverly on his own, so he will not have a good end, but he must still make a name known to the emperor."

    Ji Wenmin, the Grand Canal Commissioner, entered the capital for an audience with the emperor. His family did not receive any letters from him. They asked Lv, who said, "Your honorable father has already been poked into the eye by a big official." The whole family was shocked, fearing an eye disease. Later, he was appointed as Grand Secretary of the Eastern Pavilion. Only then did they know that "eye" with "wood" on the side is the character "相."

    In the fourth year of Qianlong, Lv entered the capital, and various princes and nobles invited him to treat diseases. He cured them easily. The sixth son of Xu Wenmu, Xu Wuchang, suffered from a condition where his virtual yang was not closing. Lv saw him and said, "Young master, your face does not have a healthy color; it's just nocturnal emission." Now, Xu Wuchang closed his eyes, lay on the ground, and exposed his chest. Lv took a steel needle, about a foot long, and pierced it directly into his heart. He pulled it out, and blood followed the needle out, like a red thread. He took saliva from his mouth to wipe the wound. The bystanders were terrified, but Xu Wuchang did not know. That night, the illness was cured.

    Wang Taishou, the magistrate of Mengting, suffered from back pain and sought a Taoist priest. The priest said, "Wait until the weather is clear and come for treatment." On the appointed day, the priest took the sunlight in his hand and rubbed it. The heat penetrated the five organs, and the illness was cured. Wang asked about the guidance and leading techniques, but the priest would not say. Then, Wang led his servant to ask privately. The priest said, "There is nothing special. Every morning, I go to the open field, and when the red sun rises, I see the hermit facing the sun and jumping like a tiger. He takes the sunlight into his mouth, sucks and swallows it, doing this twice."

    354

    ○盘古以前天相传阴沉木为开辟以前之树，沉沙浪中，过天地翻覆劫数，重出世上，以故再入土中，万年不坏。其色深绿，纹如织锦。置一片于地，百步以外，蝇蚋不飞。

    　　康熙三十年，天台山崩，沙中涌出一棺，形制诡异：头尖而尾阔，高六尺馀。识者曰：“此阴沉木棺也，必有异。”启其前和，中有人，眉目口鼻与木同色，臂腿与木同纹理，恰不腐坏。忽开眼仰 视空中，问曰：“此青青者何物耶？”众曰：“天也。”惊曰：“我当初在世时，天不若是高也。”语毕，目仍瞑。人争扶起之，合邑男女群来看盘古以前人。忽然风起，变为石人。棺为邑宰某所得，转献制府。予疑此人是前古天地将混沌时人也。纬书云：“万年之后，天可倚杵。”

    Explanation

    354

    Before Pangu, it was said that the "dark and heavy wood" existed as a tree before the creation of the world. This wood, submerged in sand and waves, passed through the cycles of destruction and rebirth, re-emerging into the world and being buried again, remaining uncorrupted for thousands of years. Its color is deep green, and its patterns resemble woven silk. Placing a piece of it on the ground, insects would not fly within a hundred steps.

    In the thirtieth year of Emperor Kangxi's reign, a landslide occurred in Tiantai Mountain, and a strange coffin emerged from the sand. The coffin had an unusual shape, with a pointed head and a broad tail, standing over six feet tall. Knowledgeable people said, "This is a coffin made of dark and heavy wood, and it must be extraordinary."
