When the coffin was opened, a person was found inside, with features and limbs that matched the wood's color and patterns, perfectly preserved. Suddenly, the person opened their eyes and looked up at the sky, asking, "What is this green thing?" The crowd replied, "It is the sky." The person was surprised and said, "When I was alive, the sky was not so high." After speaking, the person closed their eyes again. People tried to revive the person, and the entire town gathered to see someone from before Pangu's time. Suddenly, a gust of wind arose, and the person turned into a stone figure. The coffin was taken by a local official and presented to a high-ranking official. I suspect this person was from a time when the world was about to become chaotic. According to the Wei Shu (纬书), "After ten thousand years, the sky can be leaned on like a staff."

    355

    此人言天不若今之高，信矣。

    　　●卷十○禹王碑吞蛇屠赤文任陕西两当县尉，有厨人张某者，善啖多力，身体修伟，面无左耳。

    　　询其故，自言四川人，三世业猎，家传异书，能抓风嗅鼻，即知所来者为何兽，某幼亦业此。曾猎于邛崃山。其地号“阴阳界”，阳界尚平敞，阴界尤险峻，人迹罕至。一日，往猎阳界，无所得，遂裹粮入阴界。行五十里许，天已暮，远望十里外高山上有火光烧来，烛林谷如赤日，怪风吹而至。某不知何物，抓风再嗅，书所未载，心大惶恐，急登高树顶上觇之。

    Explanation

    355

    This person says the sky is not as high as it is today, and indeed it is true.

    Mr. Ren, who served as the magistrate of Liangdang County in Shaanxi, had a cook named Zhang, who was skilled in eating and had great strength. He was tall and strong, but he had no left ear.

    When asked about it, he said he was from Sichuan, where his family had been hunting for three generations. They had a tradition of passing down unusual books that allowed them to catch the wind and sniff the nose, thereby knowing what kind of animal was coming. When he was young, he also engaged in this practice. Once, he hunted in Qinglai Mountain, a place known as the "Yin-Yang Boundary." The Yang side was still relatively flat and open, while the Yin side was especially dangerous and rugged, rarely visited by people. One day, he went hunting on the Yang side but found nothing, so he brought food and entered the Yin side. After walking about fifty miles, it was already evening. From a distance, he saw a mountain ten miles away with firelight coming towards him, illuminating the forest valley like a red sun. Strange winds blew towards him. Not knowing what it was, he caught the wind and sniffed again, but the phenomenon was not recorded in books. He was greatly frightened and quickly climbed to the top of a tall tree to observe.

    356

    俄而火兴渐近，乃一大石碑，碑首凿猛虎形，光如万炬，燃照数里。碑能踯躅自行，至树下见有人，忽跃起三四丈，似欲吞啮者，几及我身。我屏息不敢动，碑亦缓缓向西南去。某方幸脱险，俟其去远，将下树矣。忽望见巨蛇千万条，大者身如车轮，小者亦粗如斗，蔽空而来。某自念此身必死于蛇腹，惊怕更甚，不料诸蛇皆腾空冲云而行，离树甚远，我蹲树上，竟无所损。惟一小蛇行少低，向我耳旁擦过，觉痛不可忍，摸之，耳已去矣，血涔涔流下。但见碑尚在前，蹲立火光中不动，凡蛇从碑旁过者，空中辄有脱壳堕下，乱落如万条白练，但闻去吸贪然有声。少顷，蛇尽不见，碑亦行远。

    Explanation

    356

    Shortly after, a fire rose up, approaching closer, revealing a large stone tablet. The top of the tablet was carved in the shape of a fierce tiger, its light as bright as a thousand torches, illuminating for several miles. The tablet could move on its own, stumbling as it went, until it reached a tree and saw someone. Suddenly, it jumped up three or four zhang, as if wanting to swallow or bite, almost reaching my body. I held my breath and dared not move, and the tablet also slowly moved southwestward. I was just fortunate to escape danger, waiting until it was far away, and was about to descend from the tree. Suddenly, I saw countless huge snakes, the larger ones with bodies as big as carriage wheels, the smaller ones also thick as a wine measure, covering the sky and coming. I thought that my body would surely die in the snakes' bellies, and was even more terrified. I did not expect all the snakes to fly into the clouds, very far from the tree. Crouching on the tree, I was not harmed at all. Only a small snake flew a little lower, scraping past my ear, and I felt pain that could not be endured. When I touched it, my ear was gone, and blood dripped down continuously. But I saw the tablet still ahead, standing still in the firelight. Any snake passing by the tablet would shed its skin in mid-air and fall, falling like thousands of white silk ribbons, but heard a sound of sucking and swallowing. After a while, all the snakes disappeared, and the tablet also moved far away.

    357

    某待至次日，方敢下树，急觅归路，迷不可得。途遇一老人，自称：“此山民也，”“子所见者为禹王碑。当年禹王治水，至邛崃山，毒蛇阻道，禹王大怒，命庚辰杀蛇，立二碑镇压，誓曰：”汝他日成神，世世杀蛇，为民除害。‘今四千年矣，碑果成神。碑有一大一小，君幸遇其小者，得不死；其大者出，则火燃五里，林木皆灰。二碑俱以蛇为粮，所到处挈以随行，故蛇俯首待食，不暇伤人。

    Explanation

    357

    I waited until the next day, then dared to climb down the tree. Hurriedly seeking the way back, I became lost. On the road, I met an old man who introduced himself as "a mountain dweller." "What you have seen is the Yu Wang Stele," he said. "When Yu the Great was controlling the waters, he came to Qingyang Mountain, where poisonous snakes blocked the path. Yu the Great was greatly angered, and ordered Gengchen to kill the snakes, setting up two steles to suppress them. He swore, saying, 'In the future, you will become a god, and generation after generation, you will kill snakes, to rid the people of harm.' Now, four thousand years have passed, and the stele has indeed become a god. There are two steles, one large and one small; you were fortunate to encounter the smaller one and thus did not die. When the larger one appears, it causes fire to burn for five miles, and all the trees turn to ash. Both steles feed on snakes, and wherever they go, they carry them along, so the snakes lower their heads to be eaten and do not have time to harm people."

    358

    子耳际已中蛇毒，出阳界见日则死。“因于衣襟下出药治之，示以归路而别。

    　　○黑柱绍兴严姓，为王氏赘婿。严归家，岳翁遣人走报其妻急病，严奔视之。天已昏黑，秉烛行路，见黑气如庭柱一条，时遮其烛。烛东则黑气柱亦东，烛西则黑柱亦西，拦截其路，不容前往。严大骇，乃到相识家借一奴添二烛而行，黑柱渐隐不见。到到家，岳翁迎出曰：“婿来已久，何以又从外入？”严曰：“婿实未来。”举家大惊，奔入妻房，见一人坐床上与其妻执手，若将同行者。严急向前握妻手，而其人始去，妻亦气绝。

    Explanation

    358

    The boy's ear had been poisoned by a snake, and if he left the underworld and saw the sun, he would die. "He took out a medicine from his collar to treat it and showed him the way back before parting.

    ○ Black Pillar, a man named Yan from Shaoxing, was a son-in-law of the Wang family. When Yan returned home, his father-in-law sent someone to rush and report that his wife was gravely ill. Yan hurried to see her. It was already dark, and he held a candle while walking. He saw a black vapor like a pillar in the courtyard, sometimes blocking his candle. When he moved the candle east, the black vapor pillar also moved east; when he moved the candle west, the black pillar also moved west, blocking his path and not allowing him to proceed. Yan was greatly frightened, so he went to a friend's house to borrow a servant and two more candles to continue. The black pillar gradually disappeared. When he arrived home, his father-in-law came out to greet him and said, "You have been here for a long time, why did you come in from outside?" Yan replied, "I really haven't been here." The whole family was greatly shocked, rushed into the bedroom, and saw a person sitting on the bed holding hands with his wife, as if they were about to go together. Yan hurriedly went forward and grasped his wife's hand, and the person left, and his wife also died.

    359

    ○猴怪杭州周云衢孝廉，有女嫁盐商吴某之子。吴以往屋颇窄，使居园中书舍。婚三月矣，忽周女患奇疾：始而心痛，继而腹背痛，继而耳目口鼻无不痛者，哀号跳掷，人不忍见。遍召医士，莫名其病，但见白、黑气二条缠女身，如绳带捆缚之状。云衢与吴翁斋醮无效，不得已，自为牒文投城隍神及关神处。半月未见灵应，又投文催之。果一日云衢与其女及婿俱白昼偃卧，若死去者，两日而苏。家人问之。据云衢云，城隍神得我牒文，即拘此妖。妖坑不得，直至催牒再至关神处，神批：“发温元帅擒讯。”讯得为祟者乃一雌猴，其白、黑二气则黑、白二蛇也。

    Explanation

    359

    A Monkey Spirit in Hangzhou

    There was a scholar named Zhou Yunqu, who had a daughter married to the son of a salt merchant named Wu. Due to the narrowness of his house, Wu was made to live in a book room in the garden. Three months after their marriage, Zhou's daughter suddenly fell ill with a strange disease: she began with heart pain, then moved to her back and abdomen, and finally to her ears, eyes, mouth, and nose, all of which were in pain. She cried out in agony and leaped about, making it unbearable to watch. They called upon many doctors, but none could diagnose her condition. All they saw were two white and black vapors缠绕着她的身体，就像绳带捆绑的样子。周云衢和吴翁进行了斋醮仪式，但没有效果，不得已，他们自己写了一份牒文投给城隍神和关神。半个月过去了，仍然没有灵验的回应，于是又写了一份牒文催促。果然有一天，周云衢和他的女儿以及女婿都在白天躺下，好像死去了一样，两天后才苏醒。家人询问他们。据周云衢说，城隍神收到了他的牒文，立刻拘捕了这个妖怪。妖怪逃脱了，直到催牒再次投给关神处，关神批示：“发温元帅擒讯。”经过审问，发现作祟的是一个雌猴，而那两条白、黑气则是两条黑白蛇。

    360

    元至正七年，猴与其雄偷果于达鲁花赤余氏之园，其时女为余家小婢，撞见以石掷之。雄走出，适遇猎户张信，以箭毙之。雌猴惊逸，修道手括苍山中。今猎户张托过为吴翁之子，婢托生为周氏之女，故来报仇。元帅问：“汝既有仇，何以不早报而必待至四百年后耶？”猴云：“此女七世托生为文学侍从之官，或为方伯中丞，故人不能相犯。因其前世居官无状，仍罚为女身，值逢所嫁之人又即猎户，故我两仇齐发。”问：“黑、白二气何来？”供称“吴园中物，被猴牵帅而至者。”元帅怒曰：“周女前生作婢，掷石驱猴，是其职分所当为；吴某前生为猎户，射杀一猴，亦人间常事。汝又不仇吴而仇其妻，甚为悖乱，且与园中蛇何与，而助纣为虐耶？”掷剑喝曰：“先斩妖党！”随见皂衣人取二蛇头呈验。

    Explanation

    360

    In the seventh year of the Zhizheng era of the Yuan Dynasty (1347), a monkey and its mate stole fruit from the garden of the 达鲁花赤 (a Mongol administrative official) family, whose name was Yu. At that time, the female monkey was a small servant in the Yu household and saw them, throwing a stone at them. The male monkey fled, and just then, he encountered a hunter named Zhang Xin, who killed him with an arrow. The female monkey was startled and fled to the mountains of Kuocang to practice spiritual cultivation. Now, the male monkey has been reborn as the son of Mr. Wu, and the female monkey has been reborn as the daughter of the Zhou family, so they have come to take revenge. The commander asked, "If you have such a grudge, why did you not take revenge earlier, but wait until four hundred years later?" The monkey replied, "This woman was reborn seven times as a literary official or a scholar, or as a provincial governor, so people could not harm her. Because she was a corrupt official in her past life, she was still punished and reborn as a woman, and she married a hunter, so both our grudges were fulfilled." The commander asked, "Where do the black and white qi come from?" The monkey replied, "They are from the Wu garden, brought here by the monkeys." The commander was angry and said, "The Zhou woman was reborn as a servant in her past life and threw a stone at the monkeys, which was her duty. Mr. Wu was reborn as a hunter in his past life and shot a monkey, which was a common human act. You do not hate Mr. Wu but his wife, which is very chaotic, and what does this have to do with the snakes in the garden, and why do you help the evil forces?" He threw his sword and shouted, "Execute the demons first!" Then, a black-clad person presented the heads of two snakes for verification.

    361

    元帅谓猴曰：“汝罪亦宜斩，但念尔修炼多年，颇有神通，将成正果，斩汝可惜。速改过悔罪，治好周女之病，我便赦汝。”一面详复关帝。猴狰狞不服，两目如电，奋爪向前，似若扑犯元帅者。俄闻空中大声曰：“伏魔大学有令，妖猴不服，即斩妖猴。”言毕，瓦上琅琅有刀环声响，猴始惧，叩头服罪。

    　　元帅呼周女到案下，令猴治病。猴抉其眼耳口鼻中，所出横刺、铁针、竹{聂}十馀条，女痛稍苏，惟心痛未解。猴不肯治，元帅又欲斩猴。猴云：“女心易治，但我有所求，须吴翁许我，我才替治。”问：“何求？”曰：“我爱吴园清洁，欲打扫西首云楼三间，使我居住。”吴翁许之。猴伸手女口，直到胸前，探出小铜镜一方，犹带血丝缕缕，女病旋愈。元帅命昊氏父子领女回家，遂各苏醒。

    Explanation

    361

    The general said to the monkey, "Your crime also deserves execution, but considering you have practiced for many years, you have some supernatural abilities, and are about to achieve enlightenment, it would be a pity to execute you. Quickly repent your sins, cure Zhou's daughter's illness, and I will pardon you." At the same time, he detailed the situation to Guan Di. The monkey was fierce and defiant, his eyes like lightning, and he raised his claws as if to attack the general. Soon, a loud voice was heard from above, saying, "The Supreme School of Subduing Demons has decreed that the rebellious monkey shall be executed." After the words were spoken, there was a clinking sound of sword rings on the tiles. The monkey then became afraid and knelt down to confess his crimes.

    The general called Zhou's daughter to the scene and ordered the monkey to treat her. The monkey probed her eyes, ears, mouth, and nose, removing several horizontal needles, iron needles, and bamboo spikes. The girl's pain slightly subsided, but her heart pain remained unresolved. The monkey refused to treat her, and the general wanted to execute him again. The monkey said, "Her heart is easy to treat, but I have a request. It must be agreed upon by Mr. Wu, and then I will treat her." The general asked, "What is your request?" The monkey replied, "I love the cleanliness of Mr. Wu's garden and wish to clean and live in the three western cloud pavilions." Mr. Wu agreed. The monkey reached into the girl's mouth, up to her chest, and pulled out a small copper mirror, still with threads of blood. The girl's illness was immediately cured. The general ordered the Wu family to take the girl home, and everyone then woke up.

    362

    此乾隆四十四年七月间事也。据吴翁云，温元帅幞巾纱帽，如唐人服饰，貌温然儒者，白面微须，非若世间所画青面瞪目状。猴在神前装束甚华，自称“小仙”。

    　　○鞭尸桐城张、徐二友，贸易江西。行至广信，徐卒于店楼，张入市买棺为殓。棺店主人索价二千文，交易成矣。柜旁坐一老人遮拦之，必须四千。张忿而归。

    　　是夜，张上楼，尸起相扑，张大骇，急避下楼。次日清晨，又往买棺，加钱千文。棺主人并无一言，而作梗之老人先在柜上骂曰：“我虽不是主人，然此地我号‘坐山虎’，非送我二千钱，与主人一样，棺不可得。”张素贫，力有不能，无可奈何，旁皇于野，又一白须翁，着蓝色袍，笑而迎曰：“汝买棺人耶？”曰：“然。”曰：“汝受坐山虎气耶？”曰：“是也。”白须翁手一鞭曰：“此伍子胥鞭楚王尸鞭也。今晚尸起相扑，汝持此鞭之，则棺得而大难解矣。”言毕不见。

    Explanation

    362

    This event took place in the seventh month of the forty-fourth year of the Qianlong era. According to Wu翁, General Wen wore a Tang-style headband and hat, dressed like a gentle scholar with a pale face and slight facial hair, unlike the fierce warriors depicted in the world. A monkey dressed elaborately before the deity and called itself "Little Immortal."

    ○ The two friends from Tongcheng, Zhang and Xu, were trading in Jiangxi. They traveled to Guangxin, where Xu died in a storehouse. Zhang went into the market to buy a coffin to bury Xu. The coffin shop owner asked for two thousand coins, and the transaction was completed. An old man sat next to the counter, obstructing the transaction, demanding four thousand coins. Zhang left in anger.

    That night, Zhang went upstairs, and the corpse rose and fought with him. Zhang was terrified and quickly retreated downstairs. The next morning, he returned to buy the coffin, adding an extra thousand coins. The coffin shop owner said nothing, but the old man who had caused trouble was already on the counter, shouting, "Although I am not the owner, this place is known as 'Sitting Mountain Tiger,' and you must give me two thousand coins, just like the owner, or you won't get the coffin." Zhang was usually poor and could not afford it, feeling helpless, he wandered around the fields. Another old man with a white beard, wearing a blue robe, greeted him with a smile and said, "Are you the one buying the coffin?" Zhang replied, "Yes." The old man asked, "Have you been intimidated by the 'Sitting Mountain Tiger'?" Zhang said, "Yes." The old man handed him a whip and said, "This is the whip used by Wu Zixu to whip the body of King Chu. Tonight, when the corpse rises and fights, you must hold this whip, and you will get the coffin without any trouble." After saying this, the old man disappeared.

    363

    张归，上楼，尸又跃起。如其言，应鞭而倒。

    　　次日，赴店买棺，店主人曰：“昨夜坐山虎死矣，我一方之害除矣，汝仍以二千文原价来抬棺可也。”问其故，主人曰：“此老姓洪，有妖法，能役使鬼魅，惯遣死尸扑人。人死买棺，彼又在我店居奇，强分半价。如是多年，受累者众。

    　　昨夜暴死，未知何病。“张乃告以白须翁赠鞭之事，二人急往视之，老人尸上果有鞭痕。或曰：白须而着蓝袍者，此方土地神也。

    Explanation

    363

    Zhang Gui went upstairs, and the corpse leaped up again. As he said, it fell over in response to the whip.

    The next day, he went to the store to buy a coffin. The store owner said, "Last night, the mountain tiger died, and the great harm to one region is gone. You can still come to carry the coffin at the original price of two thousand cash." Zhang Gui asked why, and the owner replied, "This old man surnamed Hong has magic, can command ghosts and spirits, and is accustomed to sending dead bodies to attack people. When a person dies and buys a coffin, he hoards goods in my store and forcefully demands half the price. Like this for many years, many people have been burdened."

    "Last night, he died suddenly, and it is unknown what illness he had." Zhang Gui then told him about the story of the elderly man with white hair giving a whip. The two hurried to see it, and indeed, there were whip marks on the old man's corpse. Someone said, "The elderly man with white hair wearing a blue robe is the land god of this place."

    364

    ○梁朝古冢淮徐道署，在宿迁城中。宿，故百战地，是处皆兵燹之余，署中多怪。康熙中，有某道升浙江臬司，临去留一朱姓幕友在署，俟后官交代。衙署旷荡，每夕，人语哗然。又一夕，月下闻语者聚中庭槐树下。朱于窗隙窥之，见庭中人甚多，面目不甚了了，大率衣冠奇古。一少年乌巾白衣椅柱凝思，不共诸人酬答。诸人呼曰：“陆郎，如此风月，何独惆怅？”少年答曰：“暴骸之事近矣，不能无愁。”

    　　语毕，诸人皆为咨嗟。有长髯高冠者出曰：“郎勿虑，此厄我先当之，赖有平生故人在此，自能相庇。”朗吟云：“寂寞千余岁，高槐西复东。春风寒白骨，高义望朱公。”少年举手谢曰：“当年受德至深，不图枯朽之余，犹叨仁庇。”因复共谈，似皆北魏、齐、梁时事。既而邻鸡远唱，诸人倏然散矣。朱胆壮，安寝如故。

    Explanation

    364

    In the office of the Huaixi Circuit Governor, located in Suqian City, there was an ancient tomb from the Liang Dynasty. Suqian had been a battlefield for many wars, and this place was filled with the remnants of such conflicts, making the office a site of numerous strange occurrences. During the reign of Emperor Kangxi, a certain governor was promoted to the position of Chief Justice of Zhejiang Province. Before leaving, he left behind a Mr. Zhu, a private secretary, to wait for the new official to take over. The office was vast and open, and every night, people could hear loud conversations. One evening, under the moonlight, Mr. Zhu heard voices gathered around a locust tree in the courtyard. Peering through a crack in the window, he saw many people in the courtyard, their faces not very clear, but they were mostly dressed in ancient and peculiar attire. Among them was a young man wearing a black cap and white robe, sitting alone and lost in thought, not interacting with the others. The others called out to him, saying, "Lu, with such a beautiful moon, why are you so melancholy?" The young man replied, "The time for exposing bones is approaching, and I cannot help but feel sad."

    After the conversation, everyone sighed. A man with long beard and a tall hat stepped forward and said, "Do not worry, I will face this calamity first. Thanks to my old friend here, we can protect each other." He then recited, "For over a thousand years, the tall locust tree has swayed from west to east. In the spring breeze, the cold bones lie, while the noble virtue of Zhu Gong is revered." The young man raised his hand in gratitude and said, "I have received great kindness in the past, and I did not expect that even in my decayed state, I would still receive your benevolence." They then continued to talk, discussing events from the Northern Wei, Northern Qi, and Liang Dynasties. Soon, the distant crowing of roosters signaled the approach of dawn, and the people suddenly dispersed. Mr. Zhu felt braver and slept soundly as usual.

    365

    阅数日，新官孙某来受交代。朱生匆匆出署，将觅船赴浙。忽差役寄东君札来止之曰：“某到金陵见督院后，接楚中讣音，已丁外艰，不赴浙西新任，竟归矣。先生行止，自定可也。”朱遂稍停。闻新任淮徐道孙公署中一友得急疾殂，乃托宿迁令某荐扬。一说而就。随携行李入署。时将署中旧住之屋改作客座，另置诸友于他所。幕中公务甚繁，朱不复忆前事。

    　　孙公新，来，大修衙署，一日，与朱闲坐，家人走报云：“适开前池，得一石碑，不知何代物？”孙公拉朱同往观之，见碑上书“梁散骑侍郎张公之墓”，正当两槐之间。朱恍忆前月下事，力为劝止，并述所见，云：“当更有一墓。”

    Explanation

    365

    After several days, the new official, Mr. Sun, arrived to take over his post. Mr. Zhu hurriedly left the office, intending to find a boat to head for Zhejiang. Suddenly, a servant sent a letter from the new official, stopping him and saying, "I arrived in Jinling to meet the governor-general, and then received news from Chu that my father had passed away. I have lost a parent and will not take up the new post in western Zhejiang; I am returning home. You may decide for yourself, Mr. Zhu." Mr. Zhu then paused slightly. He heard that a friend of the new official, Mr. Sun of the Huai-Xu circuit, had died of a sudden illness in his office. Mr. Zhu then entrusted the magistrate of Suqian to recommend and praise him. With one mention, it was done. He then entered the office with his luggage. At that time, the old house in the office was being converted into guest quarters, and his friends were placed in another location. The official duties in the office were very busy, and Mr. Zhu no longer remembered past events.

    Mr. Sun, the new official, arrived and began to renovate the government offices. One day, he sat idly with Mr. Zhu, and a servant came to report, "We just opened the front pond and found a stone tablet. We don't know what dynasty it is from." Mr. Sun took Mr. Zhu with him to see it. On the tablet, it was written, "The tomb of Zhang Gong, a Scattered Riding Attendant of the Liang Dynasty," and it was located between two willow trees. Mr. Zhu suddenly recalled the events of the previous moon and strongly advised against it, recounting what he had seen, saying, "There should be another tomb."

    366

    言未终，而荷锸者云：“又得骇骨一具。”孙始信其说非妄，命工人仍加工掩平如旧，池不改作矣。盖前碑乃长髯高冠之墓；而后所得，乌巾少年之骨也。

    　　○狮子大王贵州人尹廷洽，八月望日早起，行礼土地神前。上香讫，将启门，见二青衣排闼入，以手推尹仆地，套绳于颈而行。尹方惶遽间，见所祀土地神出而问故。

    　　青衣展牌示之，上有“尹廷洽”字样。土神笑不语，但尾尹而行里许。道旁有酒饭店，土神呼青衣入饮，得间语尹曰：“是行有误，我当卫君前行。倘遇神佛，君可大声叫冤，我当为君脱祸。”君颔之，仍随青衣前去。约行大半日，至一所，风波浩渺，一望无际。青衣曰：“此银海也。须深夜乃可渡，当少憩片时。”俄而，神亦曳杖来，青衣怪之。土神曰：“我与渠相处久，情不能已于一送，前路当分手耳。”

    Explanation

    366

    Before the words were finished, the person carrying the spade said, "Another frightening skeleton was found." Sun then believed that the story was not false, and ordered the workers to continue working to cover it up as before. The pond was not altered. The previous tombstone was for the one with a long beard and a tall hat; and what was later found were the bones of a young man wearing a black cap.

    ○ Lion King Yin Tingqie, a man from Guizhou, got up early on the 15th day of the eighth month and performed rituals before the Earth God. After offering incense, he was about to open the door when he saw two blue-robed servants rush in, pushing Yin to the ground and tying a rope around his neck. As Yin was in a state of panic, he saw the Earth God he had worshipped come out and ask for the reason.

    The blue-robed servant displayed a tablet, which had the words "Yin Tingqie" written on it. The Earth God smiled but did not speak, but followed Yin for a few li. There was a restaurant by the roadside, and the Earth God called the blue-robed servant to drink. Taking the opportunity, the Earth God told Yin, "This journey is a mistake, and I will guard you as we go forward. If you encounter a god or a Buddha, you can loudly cry out your innocence, and I will help you escape the calamity." Yin nodded in agreement, and continued to follow the blue-robed servant. After about half a day of walking, they arrived at a place where the waves were vast and boundless, stretching far into the distance. The blue-robed servant said, "This is the silver sea. It can only be crossed at night, so we should rest for a while." Soon after, the Earth God came with a cane, and the blue-robed servant was surprised. The Earth God said, "I have been with him for a long time, and I cannot help but bid farewell here. We will part ways ahead."

    367

    正谈说间，忽天际有彩云旌旗，侍从纷然，土神附耳曰：“此朝天诸神回也。

    　　汝遇便可叫冤。“尹望见车中有神，貌狞狞然，目有金光，面阔二尺许，即大声喊冤。神召之前，并饬行者少停，问何冤尹诉，为青衣所摄。神问：”有牌否？“

    　　曰：“有。”“有尔名乎？”曰：“有。”神曰：“既有牌，又有尔名，此应摄者，何冤为？”厉声叱之，尹词屈不知所云。

    　　土神趋而前跪奏：“此中有疑，是小神令其伸冤。”神问：“何疑？”曰：“某为渠家中ニ，每一人始生，即准东岳文书知会，其人应是何等人，应是何年月日死，共计在阳世几载，历历不爽。尹廷洽初生时，东岳牒文中开‘应得年七十二岁’。今未满五十，又未接到折算支书，何以忽尔勾到？故恐有冤。”神听说，亦迟疑久之，谓土神曰：“此事非我职司，但人命至重，尔小神尚肯如此用心，我何可漠视。

    Explanation

    367

    As they were talking, suddenly a colorful cloud with flags appeared in the sky, accompanied by a bustling crowd of attendants. The earth god whispered into someone's ear, saying, "These are the gods returning from heaven. If you encounter them, you can call for justice." Yu saw a god in the carriage, with a fierce appearance, golden eyes, and a face about two feet wide. He immediately shouted loudly for justice. The god called him forward and instructed the others to stop. He asked what injustice Yu had suffered and why he had been captured by the green-clad officials. The god asked, "Is there a record?" Yu replied, "Yes." "Is your name on it?" the god asked. Yu replied, "Yes." The god said, "If there is a record and your name is on it, there is no reason for you to be summoned. What injustice are you claiming?" He scolded Yu harshly, and Yu became speechless and unable to defend himself. The earth god approached and knelt to present his concerns, saying, "There is a doubt in this matter. It was I who ordered him to seek justice." The god asked, "What is the doubt?" The earth god replied, "I am his family deity. For every person born, we receive a notification from the Eastern Peak (Mount Tai) document, specifying what kind of person they should be, what date they should die, and how many years they should live in the mortal world. Everything is recorded accurately. When Yu Tingqia was born, the Eastern Peak document stated that he should live to be seventy-two years old. Now he is not yet fifty, and we have not received any official documents justifying his summoning. Why has he been summoned suddenly? Therefore, I fear there might be an injustice." The god listened and hesitated for a long time, then said to the earth god, "This matter is not within my jurisdiction, but human life is precious. Even though you, a minor deity, are so concerned, how can I neglect it?"

    368

    惜此间至东岳府往还辽远，当从天府行文至彼方速。”乃唤一吏作牒，口授云：“文书上只须问民魂尹廷洽有勾取可疑之处，乞飞天符下东岳到银海查办，急急勿迟。”尹从旁见吏取纸作书，封印不殊人世，但皆用黄纸封讫，付一金甲神持投天门。又呼召银海神，有绣袍者趋进。命：“看守尹某生魂，俟岳神查办，毋误”。绣袍者叩头领尹退，而神已倏忽入云雾中矣。此时尹憩一大柳树下，二青衣不知所往，尹问土神：“面阔二尺者是何神耶？”曰：“此西天狮子大王也。”

    Explanation

    368

    Regretting that the distance between here and the Eastern Peak Palace is far, one should dispatch a document from the Heavenly Court to expedite matters there. He then summoned a clerk to draft a letter, and orally instructed him, saying, "The document should only inquire about any suspicious matters involving the spirit of the commoner Yin Tingqia, and request the flying heavenly talisman to descend to the Eastern Peak and the Silver Sea for investigation, urgently without delay." Yin, standing nearby, saw the clerk take paper to write, seal, and imprint it, much like in the mortal world, but all were sealed with yellow paper. The clerk then handed it to a golden-armored deity, who carried it and threw it through the gates of heaven. He also summoned the deity of the Silver Sea, and one dressed in embroidered robes approached quickly. He commanded, "Guard the living spirit of Yin, await the Eastern Peak deity's investigation, do not be mistaken." The embroidered-robed deity bowed and escorted Yin away, while the deity had already suddenly entered the clouds and mist. At this time, Yin rested under a large willow tree, and the two green-robed attendants were nowhere to be seen. Yin asked the earth deity, "Who is the deity with a face two feet wide?" The earth deity replied, "This is the Lion King of the Western Heaven."

    369

    少顷，绣衣者谓土神曰：“尔可令尹某往暗处少坐，弗令夜风吹之；我往前途迎引天神，闻呼可急出答应。”尹随土神沿岸行约半里许，有破舟侧卧滩上，乃伏其中。闻人号马嘶及鼓吹之音，络绎不绝，良久始静。土神曰：“可以出矣。”

    　　尹出，见绣衣人偕前持牒金甲人引至岸上空阔处，云：“立此少待，岳司即到。”

    　　须臾，海上数十骑如飞而来，土神挟尹伏地上。数十骑皆下马，有衣团花袍、戴纱冠者上坐，余四人着吏服，又十余人武士装束，余悉狰狞如庙中鬼面，环立而侍。上坐官呼海神，海神趋前，问答数语，趋而下，扶尹上。尹未及跪，土神上前叩头，一一对答如前。上坐官貌颇温良，闻土神语即怒，目竖眉，厉声索二青衣。土神答：“久不知所往。”上坐者曰：“妖行一周，不过千里；鬼行一周，不过五百里。四察神可即查拿。”有四鬼卒应声腾起，怀中各出一小镜，分照四方，随飞往东去。

    Explanation

    369

    In a short while, the brocade-clad figure said to the earth god, "You may have Master Yin go sit in a dark place for a moment, do not let the night wind blow upon him. I will go ahead to meet and guide the heavenly deities; when called, he can quickly come out and respond." Master Yin followed the earth god along the shore for about half a li, and there was a broken boat lying sideways on the beach. He then hid inside it. He heard people shouting, horses neighing, and the sound of drums and music, which came continuously but eventually quieted down after a long while. The earth god said, "He may now come out."

    Master Yin came out and saw the brocade-clad figure accompany a person holding a document in a golden armor, leading him to an open space on the shore, saying, "Stand here and wait for a moment; the Lord of the Mountains will arrive shortly."

    Soon, dozens of riders came from the sea like they were flying. The earth god made Master Yin crouch down. The riders all dismounted, and there was a person in a floral-patterned robe and a gauze hat sitting on top, with four others in official robes and about ten more in warrior attire. The rest were terrifyingly ugly, like the faces of ghosts in temples, standing around as attendants. The person on top called out to the sea god, who approached and had a brief conversation before returning and helping Master Yin up. Master Yin had not yet knelt down when the earth god knelt and bowed, answering questions as before. The person on top had a gentle appearance, but upon hearing what the earth god said, he became angry, his eyes bulging and his eyebrows raised, shouting loudly for two blue-clad figures. The earth god replied, "I have no idea where they went." The person on top said, "An evil spirit can travel a full circuit within a thousand li, and a ghost can travel a full circuit within five hundred li. The Four Observing Gods can immediately investigate and capture them." Four ghost soldiers responded and flew up, each taking out a small mirror from their chests, shining them in all directions, and then flying east.

    370

    少顷，挟二青衣掷地上云：“在三百里外枯槐树中拿得。”上坐官诘问误勾缘由，二青衣出牌呈上，诉云：“牌自上行，役不过照牌行事。倘有舛误，须问官吏，与役无干。”上坐官诘云：“非尔舞弊，尔何故远？”青衣叩首云：昨见狮子大王驾到，一行人众皆是佛光；土神虽微员，尚有阳气；尹某虽死，未过阴界，尚系生魂，可以近得佛光。鬼役阴暗之气，如何近得佛光，所以远伏。及狮王过后，鬼役方一路追寻，又值朝天神圣接连行过，以故不敢走出，并未知牌中何弊。“上坐官曰：”如此，必亲赴森罗一决矣。“令力士先挟尹过海，即呼车骑排衙而行。尹怖甚，闭目不敢开视，但觉风雷击荡，心魂震骇。

    Explanation

    370

    A short while later, two green-clad attendants were carried and thrown to the ground, and it was said, "It was retrieved from a dried-up willow tree three hundred miles away." The official seated above interrogated about the reason for the mistake. The two green-clad attendants presented a card, and they explained, "The card came from above, and we merely followed the card's instructions. If there is any error, it must be asked of the officials, not the attendants." The official questioned, "It is not your fault, why did you hide far away?" The attendants bowed and said, "Yesterday, we saw the Lion King arrive, and all the people were filled with Buddha's light. The earth god, though minor, still has yang energy. Mr. Yin, though dead, has not crossed into the underworld, still a living soul, and can approach Buddha's light. The spirits of the dead, with their dark energy, how can they approach Buddha's light, so they hid far away. After the Lion King passed, the spirits began to follow, but then the celestial deities passed by one after another, so they dared not venture out, and we do not know what was wrong with the card." The official seated above said, "In that case, we must personally go to the realm of the dead to resolve this." He ordered the strong men to carry Mr. Yin across the sea, and then called for chariots and horsemen to form a procession and proceed. Mr. Yin was extremely terrified, closed his eyes, and dared not open them, but only felt the wind and thunder striking, his heart and soul were shaken.

    371

    少顷，声渐远，力士行亦少徐。尹开目即已坠地。见官府衙署，有冕服者出迎，前官入，分两案对坐。堂上先闻密语声，次闻传呼声，青衣与土神皆趋入。

    　　土神叩见毕，立阶下；青衣问话毕，亦起出。有鬼卒从庑下缚一吏入，堂上厉声喝问，吏叩头辩，若有所待者然。又有数鬼从庑下擒一吏，抱文卷入，尹遥视之，颇似其族叔尹信。既入殿，冕服者取册查核。许久，即掷下一册，命前吏持示后吏，后吏惟叩首哀求而已。殿内神喝：“杖！”数鬼将前吏曳阶下，杖四十；又见数鬼领朱单下，剥去后吏巾服，锁押牵出。过尹旁，的是其族叔，呼之不应。

    Explanation

    371

    A short while later, the sound gradually faded, and the strong man's pace also slowed down slightly. Yin opened his eyes and had already fallen to the ground. He saw the official quarters, where a person in a ceremonial cap and robe came out to greet. The previous official entered, and they sat facing each other at two tables. First, a low whispering sound was heard from the hall, then a loud shouting sound. The servant and the earth god both hurriedly entered.

    The earth god paid his respects and stood below the steps; after finishing the questioning, the servant also stood up and left. A ghost soldier tied one official from the eaves and brought him in. From the hall, a stern voice shouted questions. The official knelt and pleaded, as if waiting for something. Then several ghosts from the eaves seized another official, carrying official documents with them. Yin looked at him from afar and saw that he resembled his uncle Yin Xin. After entering the hall, the person in the ceremonial cap and robe took a register to check. After a long time, he threw down a register, instructing the first official to show it to the second official. The second official only knelt and begged for mercy. From inside the hall, a divine voice shouted, "Flog!" Several ghosts dragged the first official down the steps and flogged him forty times. Then several ghosts led a red slip down and stripped the second official of his hat and robe, locking and dragging him out. As he passed by Yin, it was indeed his uncle, but Yin did not respond.

    372

    叩何往，鬼卒云：“发往烈火地狱去受罪矣。”

    　　尹正疑惧间，随呼尹入殿。前花袍官云：“尔此案已明。本司所勾系尹廷治，该吏未尝作弊。同房吏有尹姓者，系廷治亲叔，欲救其侄，知同族有尔名适相似，可以朦混，俟本司吏不在时，将牌添改‘治’字作‘洽’字，又将房册换易，以致出牌错误。今已按律治罪，尔可生还矣。”回头顾土神云：“尔此举极好，但只须赴本司详查，不合向狮子大王路诉，以致我辈均受失察处分。今本司一面造符申覆，一面差勾本犯，尔速引尹廷洽还阳。”土神与尹叩谢出，遇前金甲者于门迎贺曰：“尔等可喜！我辈尚须候回文，才得回去。”

    Explanation

    372

    The Ghost Officer asked, "Where are you going?" The spirit replied, "To the Hell of Blazing Fire to suffer punishment."

    Yin Zheng, filled with doubt and fear, called Yin into the hall. The official in the flowered robe said, "Your case has been clarified. The office summoned Yin Tingzhi, and the official never cheated. There is an official named Yin in the same office, who is Tingzhi's uncle, and he wanted to save his nephew. Knowing that there was someone in the same clan with a similar name, he could pass it off as you. When the official of the office was not around, he altered the character '治' to '洽' on the document and also exchanged the office register, causing the issuance of the wrong document. Now he has been punished according to law, and you can return to life."

    Turning around, he looked at the Earth God and said, "Your actions were excellent, but you only needed to go to the office for a detailed investigation. It was inappropriate to appeal to the Lion King, which caused us all to be punished for negligence. Now the office will create a talisman to report the matter and also summon the culprit. You should quickly guide Yin Tingzhi back to the living world."

    The Earth God and Yin bowed and thanked him as they left, meeting the Golden Armor Figure at the door who welcomed them and said, "You are fortunate! We still need to wait for the reply before we can return."

    373

    尹随土神出走，并非前来之路，城市一如人间。饥欲食，渴欲饮，土神力禁不许。城外行数里，上一高山，俯视其下：有一人僵卧，数人守其旁而哭。因叩土神：“此何处？”土神喝曰：“尚不省耶！”以杖击之，一跌而寤，已死两昼夜矣。棺椁具陈，特心头微暖，故未殓耳。遂坐起稍送茶水，急唤其子趋廷治家视之。归云：“其人病已愈二日，顷复死矣。”

    Explanation

    373

    The earth deity left, and it was not the way he came. The city was just like the human world. Hungry and wanting to eat, thirsty and wanting to drink, the earth deity strongly prohibited it. Went several miles outside the city, climbed a high mountain, and looked down below: there was a person lying stiffly, with several people standing by his side and crying. Therefore, he asked the earth deity, "Where are we?" The earth deity shouted, "Still don't understand?" Hit him with a stick, and he fell and woke up. He had already been dead for two days and two nights. The coffin and bier were fully prepared, only slightly warm in the chest, therefore not yet buried. Then sat up and gave him a little tea and water. Quickly called his son to the court to manage the household and see him. He returned and said, "That person's illness had already recovered two days ago, but recently died again."

    374

    ○绿毛怪乾隆六年，湖州董畅庵就幕山西芮城县。县有庙，供关、张、刘三神像。庙门历年用铁锁锁之，逢春秋祭祀，一启钥焉。传言中有怪物，供香火之僧亦不敢居。

    　　一日，有陕客贩羊千头，日暮无托足所，求宿庙中，居民启锁纳之，且告以故。贩羊者恃有膂力，曰：“无妨。”乃开门入，散群羊于廊下，而已持羊鞭秉烛寝；心不能无恐，三鼓，眼未合。闻神座下豁然有声，一物跃出。贩羊者于烛光中视之：其物长七八尺，头面具人形，两眼深黑有光，若湖桃大，颈以下绿毛覆体，茸茸如蓑衣；向贩羊者睨且嗅，两手有尖爪，直前来攫。贩羊者击以鞭，竟若不知，夺鞭而口啮之，断如裂帛。贩羊者大惧，奔出庙外，怪追之。贩羊人缘古树而上，伏其梢之最高者。怪张眼望之，不能上。

    374

    In the sixth year of the Qianlong era, Dong Chang'an from Huzhou served as an official in Ruicheng County, Shanxi Province. The county had a temple that housed statues of the deities Guan, Zhang, and Liu. The temple door had been locked with an iron lock for many years, and it was only opened during the Spring and Autumn festivals for sacrifices. It was said that there was a monster inside, and even the monks who offered incense and fire were too afraid to stay.

    One day, a merchant from Shaanxi came with a thousand sheep and, at dusk, having no place to rest, requested to stay in the temple. The residents opened the lock and let him in, and they also told him the reason. The merchant, relying on his strength, said, "It's no problem." He then opened the door and entered, scattering the flock of sheep under the eaves. He already held the sheep whip and was sleeping with a candle, but his heart could not help but be afraid. At the third watch of the night, his eyes did not close. Suddenly, a sound was heard from beneath the deity's seat, and a creature jumped out. In the candlelight, the merchant saw it: the creature was about seven or eight feet long, with a face resembling a human, two deep black eyes that had a glow, as large as a peach from Lake Tai, and from the neck down, it was covered in green fur, soft and fluffy like a straw raincoat. It stared and sniffed at the merchant, with sharp claws on its hands, and directly came forward to grab. The merchant hit it with the whip, but as if it did not know, it grabbed the whip and bit it with its mouth, breaking it like tearing silk. The merchant was filled with great fear and ran out of the temple. The creature chased after him. The merchant climbed up an ancient tree and hid at the highest branch. The creature opened its eyes and looked at him, but could not climb up.

    375

    良久，东方明，路有行者，贩羊人树下觅怪，怪亦不见。乃告众人，共寻神座，了无他异，惟石缝一角，腾腾有黑气。众人不敢启，具牒告官。芮城令佟公命移神座掘之。深丈许，得朽棺，中有尸，衣服悉毁，遍体生绿毛，如贩羊人所见。乃积薪焚之，啧啧有声，血涌骨鸣。自此怪绝。

    　　○张大帝安溪相公坟在闽之某山。有道士李姓者利其风水，其女病瘵将危，道士谓曰：“汝为我所生，而病已无全理，今将取汝身一物，在利吾门。”女愕然曰：“惟翁命。”曰：“我欲占李氏风水久矣，必得亲生儿子之骨埋之，方能有应。但死者不甚灵，生者不忍杀，惟汝将死未死之人，才有用耳。”女未及答，道士即以刀划取其指骨，置羊角中，私埋李氏坟旁。自后，李氏门中死一科甲，则道士门中增一科甲；李氏田中减收十斛，则道士田中增收十斛。人疑之，亦不解其故。

    Explanation

    375

    For a long time, the east began to brighten, and a traveler on the road, a sheep seller searching for a strange thing under a tree, also did not see it. The sheep seller then informed the people, and they searched for the divine seat together. There was no other unusual thing, only a corner of the rock crevice, with rising black vapor. The people dared not open it, so they submitted a formal document to the official. The magistrate of Rui County, Mr. Tong, ordered the divine seat to be moved and dug up. It was dug to a depth of about a zhang, and a rotten coffin was found, containing a corpse. The clothes were all destroyed, and green hair grew all over the body, just as the sheep seller had seen. They then piled up firewood and burned it, making a hissing sound, with blood surging and bones making a noise. From this point on, the strange occurrences ceased.

    The grave of Duke Anxi of Zhang the Great Emperor was in a certain mountain in Fujian. A Taoist priest named Li took advantage of its feng shui. His daughter was sick with consumption and near death. The Taoist priest said to her, "You are my offspring, and your illness has no hope of recovery. Now I will take something from your body to benefit my family." The daughter was startled and replied, "Only as you command." The Taoist priest said, "I have wanted to take advantage of the feng shui of the Li family for a long time. I must get the bone of a son born of flesh and blood to bury it, and it will be effective. But the dead are not very effective, and the living are unwilling to kill. Only you, who is about to die but not yet dead, are useful." Before the daughter could answer, the Taoist priest immediately cut off her finger bone with a knife, put it in a goat horn, and secretly buried it beside the Li family's grave. Afterward, when one member of the Li family died who passed the imperial examinations, one member of the Taoist's family passed the imperial examinations. When the Li family's fields decreased by ten dou, the Taoist's fields increased by ten dou. People doubted it, but did not understand the reason.

    376

    值清明节，村人迎张大帝像，为赛神会，彩旗导从甚盛。行至李家坟，神像忽止，数十人舁之不可动，中一男子大呼曰：“速归庙！速归庙！”众从之，舁至庙中，男子上坐曰：“我大帝神也，李家坟有妖，须往擒治之。”命其徒某执锹，某执锄，某执绳索。部署定，又大呼曰：“速至李家坟！速至李家坟！”众如其言，神像疾趋如风。至坟所，命执锹、锄者搜坟旁。良久，得一羊角，金色，中有小赤蛇，蜿蜿奋动。其角旁有字，皆道人合族姓名也。乃命持绳索者往缚道士，鸣之官，讯得其情，置之法。李氏自此大盛，而奉张大帝甚虔。

    Explanation

    376

    On Tomb Sweeping Day, villagers carried the image of Zhang the Great Emperor to a ritual gathering to honor the deities, with a grand procession led by colorful flags. As they approached the Li Family Cemetery, the deity's image suddenly stopped, and despite the efforts of dozens of people, they could not move the palanquin. A man in the crowd shouted, "Quickly return to the temple! Quickly return to the temple!" The crowd followed his command, carrying the image back to the temple. The man then sat down and declared, "I am the spirit of Zhang the Great Emperor. There is an evil presence at the Li Family Cemetery, and I must go to capture and deal with it." He ordered one of his followers to take a shovel, another a hoe, and a third a rope. After the preparations were made, he shouted again, "Quickly go to the Li Family Cemetery! Quickly go to the Li Family Cemetery!" The crowd obeyed, and the deity's image moved swiftly like the wind. Upon arriving at the cemetery, he ordered the people with the shovel and hoe to search the area around the tomb. After a long time, they found a golden ram's horn with a small red snake wriggling inside. On the side of the horn were inscriptions with the names of a Taoist priest and his entire family. Zhang the Great Emperor then ordered the person with the rope to capture the Taoist priest, who was reported to the authorities. After an investigation, the priest was punished according to the law. From that day on, the Li family flourished greatly, and they worshipped Zhang the Great Emperor with great reverence.

    377

    ○紫姑神尤琛者，长沙人，少年韶秀。偶过湘溪野，庙塑紫姑神甚美，爱之，手摩其面而题壁云：“藐姑仙子落烟沙，玉作阑干冰作车。若畏夜深风露冷，槿篱茅舍是郎家。”

    　　是夜三鼓，闻有叩门者，启之，曰：“紫姑神也。妾本上清仙女，偶谪人间，司云雨之事。蒙郎见爱，故来相就。若不以鬼物见疑，愿荐枕席。”尤狂喜，携手入室，成伉俪焉。嗣后每夜必至，旁人不能见也。手一物与尤曰：“此名‘紫丝囊’，吾朝玉帝时织女所赐，佩之能助人文思。”生自佩后即入泮，举于乡，成进士，选四川成都知县。女与同行，助其为政，发奸摘伏，有神明之称。

    Explanation

    377

    尤琛，长沙人，少年时英俊秀美。偶然路过湘溪野，见到庙中塑的紫姑神像非常美丽，便爱上了她，用手抚摸她的脸庞，并在墙上题字道：“藐姑仙子落烟沙，玉作阑干冰作车。若畏夜深风露冷，槿篱茅舍是郎家。”

    那晚三更时分，听到有人敲门，开门一看，说是紫姑神。她说：“我是上清仙女，偶然被贬人间，负责云雨之事。承蒙郎君喜爱，所以前来相会。若不以鬼物相疑，愿与郎君同枕共眠。”尤琛狂喜，携手入室，成为夫妻。此后每夜必来，旁人看不见。她递给尤琛一件东西说：“这是‘紫丝囊’，我朝见玉帝时织女所赐，佩戴它能助人文学灵感。”尤琛佩戴后即入泮，举于乡，成进士，选为四川成都知县。紫姑神与他同行，助他为政，揭露奸邪，铲除隐恶，有神明之称。

    378

    忽一日谓尤曰：“今日置酒，与郎为别，妾将行矣。妾虽被谪谴，限满原可仍归仙籍。以私奔故，无颜重上天曹；地府又以妾本上界仙人，不敢收之鬼。

    　　自念此身飘荡，终非了计，虽托足君门，尚无形质，不能为君生育男女。昨将此情苦求泰山神君，神君许将妾名收置册上，照例托生。十五年后，可以重续爱缘，永为夫妇，未知君能勿娶，专相待否？“尤唯唯，不觉涕下。女亦凄然，大恸而去。自此，尤作官不如前时之明，因挂误革职。人有求婚者，毅然拒之，年四旬，犹只身也。如是者十五年。

    Explanation

    378

    One day, the woman said to You, "Today I have prepared wine to bid farewell to you. I will be leaving. Although I have been demoted and punished, once the term is over, I could still return to my divine status. However, because of my elopement, I have no face to return to the heavenly bureaucracy. The underworld, recognizing my former status as a celestial being, does not dare to include me among the dead.

    Reflecting on my current state, I realize that living like this is not a solution. Although I have found refuge with you, I lack a physical form necessary for reproduction. Yesterday, I appealed to the God of Mount Tai, who agreed to register my name for rebirth according to custom. After fifteen years, we can reunite and become husband and wife. Do you think you can remain faithful and wait for me?"

    You nodded silently, and tears welled up in his eyes. The woman was also deeply saddened and left in a state of great sorrow. From this point, You's performance in office declined, and he was dismissed due to errors. Despite marriage proposals, he firmly rejected them, remaining unmarried until he was forty. This situation continued for fifteen years.

    379

    房师某学士，愍其鳏居，为议婚。生又坚拒，并道所以。学士大骇，曰：“若果然，则吾堂兄女是已。吾堂兄女生十五年，不能言，但能举笔作字。每闻人议婚，必书‘待尤郎’三字，得毋即汝乎？”拉尤至兄家，请其女出见。女隔帘书“紫丝囊在否？”尤解囊呈验，女点首者三，遂择日成婚。合卺之夕，女仰天一笑，即便能言。然从此绝不记前生原委，如寻常夫妇。

    　　○魏象山余窗友魏梦龙，字象山，后余四科进士，由部郎迁御史。己卯典试云南，殁于途，归柩于西湖昭庆寺。其年十月，沈辛田观察亦厝其先人之柩于此寺，见前屋厝柩旁列“云南大主考”金字牌，知为魏君。魏故辛田所善也。俄而吊客来，孝子当扶杖行礼。辛田弟清藻忽不见，觅之，昏昏然卧魏柩前，神色惨沮。扶归，则寒热大作，病势沉重。医者下药，方开“人参三钱”。辛田心狐疑，未敢用参。

    Explanation

    379

    A certain scholar, who was the student's mentor, pitied his widowed state and proposed a marriage for him. The student firmly refused and explained his reasons. The scholar was greatly startled and said, "If indeed it is so, then it must be my elder brother's daughter. My elder brother's daughter has been fifteen years old and cannot speak, but can only write with a pen. Every time she hears people discussing marriage, she always writes the three characters 'awaiting Youlang.' Isn't it you?" The scholar took the student to his elder brother's house and asked his daughter to come out and meet. The girl wrote through the curtain, "Is the purple silk bag there?" The student opened the bag and presented it for verification. The girl nodded three times, and they then chose a date to get married. On the night of the wedding, the girl laughed up to the sky and immediately became able to speak. But from then on, she never remembered anything about her past life, like any ordinary couple.

    ○ Wei Xiangshan was my classmate and friend, Wei Menglong, whose courtesy name was Xiangshan. He passed the imperial examinations four years after me and was promoted from a departmental official to a censor. In the year of Ji Mao, he was appointed as the chief examiner for the provincial examination in Yunnan and died on the way. His coffin was returned to the Zhaqing Temple on West Lake. That same October, Shen Xintian, the observer, also placed his father's coffin in this temple. Seeing the gold-lettered plaque "Chief Examiner of Yunnan" beside the coffin in the front room, he knew it belonged to Wei. Wei was a good friend of Xintian. Soon after, mourners arrived, and the filial son should have supported a cane to perform the rites. Xintian's younger brother, Qingzao, suddenly disappeared. When they searched for him, they found him lying dazedly in front of Wei's coffin, with a grim and depressed expression. When they helped him back, he developed a severe fever and fell seriously ill. The doctor prescribed medicine, and the prescription included "three qian of ginseng." Xintian was uncertain and hesitant to use ginseng.

    380

    至床前视弟，弟跃起坐如平时，拱手笑曰：“沈五哥，别久矣，佳否？”辛田怪而呵之。旁有二女眷观疾，清藻又手挥之曰：“两嫂请回避。愿假纸笔，我有所言。”与之纸，熟视笑曰：“纸小，不足书也。”为磨墨而以长幅与之，乃凭几楷书曰：“梦龙白：梦龙奉命典试云南，从豫章行至樊城，感冒暑热。奴子吴升，不察病原，误投人参三钱，遂至不起。甚矣，人参之不可轻服也！樊城令某，经理丧事颇尽心力，使灵柩得还家，而诸弟啧有烦言，诬其侵蚀衣箱银两，殊不识好歹。家中所存，只破书几卷，诸弟尚忍言析乎？覆巢完卵，还望诸弟照应之。”

    　　书毕，掷管而卧。须臾又起，提笔将“人参不可轻服”数字旁加密圈。辛田大惊，不敢为弟下人参。请魏家人来，以所书示之，皆骇叹，汗泪交下。

    Explanation

    380

    To the bedside to see his younger brother, the younger brother leaped up and sat as usual, bowing and smiling said, "Older Brother Shen, it has been a long time, are you well?" Xin Tian found it strange and scolded him. There were two female relatives watching the illness, Qingcao waved his hand and said, "Aunties, please step aside. I wish to borrow paper and pen, I have something to say." He was given paper, and after looking at it and smiling, he said, "The paper is too small, not enough to write on." To grind ink and give him a long scroll, he then leaned against the desk and wrote in regular script, "Dream Long writes: Dream Long was ordered to preside over the examination in Yunnan, from Yuchang to Fancheng, caught a cold due to heat. Servant Wu Sheng, did not understand the cause of the illness, mistakenly gave three qian of ginseng, thus he did not recover. How serious it is, ginseng should not be taken lightly! The magistrate of Fancheng, someone, managed the funeral with great effort, to ensure the coffin returned home, but the other brothers had complaints, accused him of embezzling clothes and silver, truly does not know right from wrong. At home, there is only a few old books, can the brothers still speak of dividing the property? Hope the brothers will take care of the remaining eggs."

    After finishing writing, he threw down the brush and lay down. After a short while, he got up again, picked up the brush and added more circles around the words "ginseng should not be taken lightly." Xin Tian was greatly alarmed and dared not give the younger brother ginseng. He asked the Wei family members to come, and showed them what he had written, and they were all shocked and wept, sweat and tears mingling.

    381

    寻弟病愈。问其索纸作书状，全不省记，但云：“病重时，见短身多须而衣葛者入房，便昏然不晓人事矣。”沈年幼，不及见魏君，所云者果魏君貌也。沈后中辛卯探花，卒不永年而死。

    　　○王莽时蛇冤临平沈昌，余戊午同年举人，年少英俊。忽路间遇僧授药三丸曰：“汝将有大难，服此或可少瘳，临期吾再来视汝。”言毕去。沈素不信因果事，以药掷书厨上，勿服也。亡何，病大重，忽作四川人语曰：“我峨嵋山蟒蛇，寻汝二千年，今方得汝。”自以手扼其吭，气将尽，家人忆路间僧语，即速觅书厨上药，只存一丸，以水吞下，恍然记历代前生事。

    　　沈在王莽时，姓张名敬，避莽乱，隐峨嵋山学仙，有同志人严昌为耦耕之友。

    　　刘歆谋起兵应汉事败，裨将王均亦逃奔峨嵋，事二人为弟子。山洞有蟒，大如车轮，每出游，必有风雷，禾稼多伤。张欲除其害，命王削竹刺插地，以毒药敷之。

    Explanation

    381

    The brother's illness soon improved. When asked to write a letter, he had no memory of the event, but said, "When I was seriously ill, I saw a short, bearded man wearing hemp clothes enter the room, and then I became dazed and lost consciousness." Shěn was too young to have met Wei Jun, and what he described matched Wei Jun's appearance. Shěn later passed the imperial examination in the year Xīnmǎo and became a high-ranking official, but he did not live long and died.

    ○During the reign of Wang Mang, a snake from Mount Emei, named Shěn Chāng, was a young and handsome scholar who passed the imperial examination in the year Wǔwǔ. One day, he suddenly met a monk on the road who gave him three pills and said, "You will face a great calamity. Taking these pills might help slightly, and I will come again at the time." After the monk left, Shěn, who usually did not believe in karma, threw the pills onto the bookshelf and did not take them. Soon after, his illness became very serious, and he suddenly began speaking in the dialect of Sichuan, saying, "I am a python from Mount Emei, and I have been seeking you for two thousand years. Now I have found you." The python strangled his throat, and he was about to die. His family members remembered the monk's words and quickly searched for the pills on the bookshelf. Only one pill remained, and Shěn swallowed it with water, suddenly remembering his past lives.

    Shěn lived during the reign of Wang Mang, where his name was Zhang Jing. To avoid the chaos of Wang Mang's rule, he hid on Mount Emei to learn Taoist arts, and had a fellow practitioner named Yan Chang, who was his companion in farming. Liu Xin, who plotted to raise an army to support the Han dynasty, failed, and his subordinate general Wang Jun also fled to Mount Emei, becoming a disciple of the two. There was a python in the mountain cave, as large as a cart wheel, and whenever it went out, there was always wind and thunder, causing much damage to crops. Zhang wanted to eliminate this danger and ordered Wang to insert bamboo thorns into the ground and apply poison to them.

    382

    蛇果出，为竹所刺，死。蛇修炼有年，将成龙者，其出穴自挟风雷而行，非有心害人，为王杀后，思报主谋者之冤。而王均莽死后随出山佐光武中兴，拜骁骑将军，遣人迎张敬入洛，亦拜征虏将军，蛇不能报。再世为北魏高僧；三世为元将某，有战功，蛇又不能报；惟今世仅作孝廉，故蛇来，将甘心焉。其原委历历，口皆自言。家人问：“路僧为谁？”曰：“即严昌先生也。先生辞光武之聘，早登仙道，与吾有香火缘，故来相救。”言终，沐浴整衣冠卒。

    　　开吊日，前僧果来，泣拜毕，语其家人曰：“毋苦，毋苦。了此一重公案，行当仍归仙道耳。”语毕，忽不见。

    　　○牙鬼杭州朱亮工妻张氏，患伤寒甚剧。忽作山西人语，咆哮索命，击毁盘碗，且云：“恩自恩，仇自仇，不能作抵。”亮工在家，索命者不至；出，则瞀乱如前。

    Explanation

    382

    The snake emerged and was pierced by bamboo, dying. The snake, having cultivated for many years, was about to become a dragon, and when it emerged from its cave, it carried wind and thunder with it, not with the intention to harm people. After being killed by the king, it thought to avenge the wrong of the mastermind. After the death of Wang Mang, it followed out of the mountain to assist Guangwu in the revival, being appointed as a cavalry general, sent someone to welcome Zhang Jing into Luoyang, also appointed as a general to subdue the barbarians. The snake could not avenge. In the second life, it became a high monk of the Northern Wei; in the third life, it became a general of the Yuan, with military achievements, but the snake could not avenge again. Only in this life, it became a filial and clean official. Thus, the snake came, determined to satisfy its desire. The details were clear, all spoken by its own mouth. The family asked, "Who is the monk on the road?" It said, "It is Mr. Yan Chang." Mr. Yan Chang declined the invitation of Guangwu, early ascended to the path of immortality, with us having a connection of incense and fire, thus came to save me. After finishing speaking, it bathed, dressed, and died.

    On the day of the funeral, the previous monk indeed came, after weeping and bowing, he said to the family, "Do not grieve, do not grieve. This heavy case is resolved, I will soon return to the path of immortality." After finishing speaking, he suddenly disappeared.

    A Tooth Demon: Zhang, the wife of Zhu Lianggong of Hangzhou, suffered from a severe case of typhoid fever. Suddenly, she began speaking in the dialect of Shanxi, roaring for revenge, smashing plates and bowls, and saying, "Gratitude is gratitude, enmity is enmity, they cannot be offset against each other." When Zhu Lianggong was at home, the one seeking revenge did not come; when he went out, he was as disoriented as before.

    383

    亮工乃具牒诉本郡城隍神。张氏沉沉熟睡，如赴鞫者。

    　　良久，苏曰：“冤雪矣，冤去矣。”手摩其臀曰：“被神杖，甚痛。前生予与亮工俱山西贩布男子，官牙刘某吞布价而花销之。予告官比追，刘不胜其苦，当予前作赴水状，欲予怜而救之。予怒曰：”汝虽死，吾仍索欠不饶。‘刘赧于转身，竟溺水死。亮工前生姓俞名容，闻之，劝予曰：“牙人死固当，然棺殓之费，我二人当分给之。’予怒未息，竟不肯；俞乃捐囊中金三两，为棺殓焉。今此牙鬼来报予仇，而不料俞之为吾今生夫也，故不敢见之。昨蒙城隍神讯得刘牙侵蚀人银，自己寻死，本无冤抑，乃敢作闹于朱氏恩人之舍，责三十板，锁解酆都道。予前生以索债故，见死不救，见尸不殓，居心不忍，亦责十五板，然病势渐除矣。”

    Explanation

    383

    Liang Gong then prepared a formal document to appeal to the local city god. Zhang was deeply asleep, as if she were being summoned for trial.

    After a long while, she woke up and said, "The injustice has been cleared, and the wrong has been removed." She rubbed her buttocks and said, "I was struck by a divine rod, and it was very painful. In a past life, I and Liang Gong were both cloth traders from Shanxi. An official broker named Liu swallowed up the cloth price and spent it. I reported to the authorities to recover the money, and Liu, unable to bear the suffering, pretended to jump into the water to elicit my sympathy and rescue. I angrily said, 'Even if you die, I will still demand the debt and not let you off.' Liu was ashamed to turn around and ended up drowning. In a past life, Liang Gong's name was Yu Rong, who heard this and advised me, 'The broker's death was indeed deserved, but the cost of his coffin and burial should be shared by us two.' My anger was not yet subsided, so I refused. Yu then donated three taels of gold from his bag for the coffin and burial. Now, this broker's ghost comes to seek revenge, but did not expect Yu to be my husband in this life, so he dared not see me. Last night, under the judgment of the city god, it was found that Liu, the broker, embezzled people's silver and committed suicide, which was not unjustified, but he still caused trouble in Zhu's benefactor's house, so he was punished with thirty strokes and locked up to be sent to the underworld. In my past life, because I was demanding the debt, I did not save the dead and did not bury the body, which was not compassionate, so I was also punished with fifteen strokes, but the illness is gradually subsiding."

    384

    亡何，其押解之鬼差附病者身曰：“为汝家事作八百里远行，须以纸钱酒饭享我。”家人惧，为大设斋醮，方始寂然。

    　　○妖梦三则柘城李少司空子继迁成进士。司空及太夫人殁后，继迁患危疾，梦太夫人教服参，因以告医。医曰：“参与病相忌，不可服。”是夜，复梦太夫人云：“医言不可听，汝求生非参不可。我有参几许，在某处，可用。”探之，果得。服之，夜半发狂死。

    Explanation

    384

    Shortly after, the ghostly official attached itself to the sick person and whispered, "For your family's sake, I must undertake an eight-hundred-li journey; you must offer me paper money, wine, and food as a sacrifice." The family, fearing the ghost, held a large ritual offering, and only then did it become quiet.

    ○ Three Strange Dreams: Li Shao Sikong, a scholar from Zhazhou, passed the imperial examinations. After the death of Sikong and his respected mother-in-law, Li Jiqian fell gravely ill and dreamed that his mother-in-law instructed him to take ginseng. He informed the doctor, who said, "Ginseng is incompatible with your illness and should not be taken." That night, he dreamed again that his mother-in-law said, "Do not heed the doctor's words; if you wish to live, you must take ginseng. I have some ginseng, and it is stored in a certain place, where you can find it." They searched and indeed found the ginseng. After taking it, he went mad in the middle of the night and died.

    385

    陆射山峰征君，梦尊人孝廉公云：“吾窀穸内为水所浸，甚苦。皋亭山顶有地一区，系某姓，求售，曷往买而移葬，吾神所依也。”访之果合，因以重价得之。及改葬，旧穴了无水，且暖气如蒸，悔已无及。迁葬后，征君日就困踬，子孙流离。

    　　江宁报恩寺僧房，每科场年，凭为举子寓所。六合张生员者，住某僧房有年，其寺主老僧悟西已死。张以不第心灰，数科不至。忽一日，悟西托梦其徒曰：“速买舟过江，延张相公来应试，张相公今岁登科。”其徒告张，张喜，渡江应试。发榜后，仍不第，张愠甚，因设祭怼之。夜梦悟西来云：“今年科场粥饭，冥司派老僧给散。一名不到，老僧无处开销。相公命中尚应吃三场十一碗冷粥饭，故令愚徒相延，以免我遣，非敢诳也。”

    Explanation

    385

    Lusheshan Peak Recluse dreamed of his father, the scholar-official, who said, "The tomb within my resting place has been flooded with water, causing great distress. There is a plot of land on the top of Gaoting Mountain, belonging to a certain surname, which is for sale. Why not go and buy it and move the burial? This is where my spirit will rest." Upon investigation, it was found to be true, and he bought it at a high price. When the reburial took place, the old tomb site had no water, and the new site was warm like steam. Regret came too late. After the relocation, the recluse gradually became weak and troubled, and his descendants were scattered.

    The monk's quarters at the Baoyin Temple in Jiangning serve as a place for candidates to stay every year during the imperial examinations. Zhang, a student from Liuhe, had been staying in one of the temple rooms for several years. The temple's abbot, the old monk Wuxi, had already died. Zhang, having failed the exam, became disheartened and did not come for several exams. One day, Wuxi appeared to his disciple in a dream and said, "Quickly buy a boat to cross the river and invite Scholar Zhang to take the exam. Scholar Zhang will pass this year." The disciple told Zhang, who was pleased and crossed the river to take the exam. After the results were announced, he still did not pass, and Zhang was very angry. He set up an offering to blame Wuxi. That night, Wuxi came to him in a dream and said, "This year's exam is like food. The underworld assigned the old monk to distribute it. If one name is missing, the old monk has no place to spend. Scholar, according to your fate, you should still eat three meals and eleven bowls of cold rice. Therefore, I had my foolish disciple invite you, to avoid my being sent away. I dare not deceive you."

    386

    ○凯明府全椒令公音布，能诗倜傥，与余交好。庚寅分校南闱，疽发背卒。公母怀孕时，将至期，祖某为内务府总管，晚见庭下有巨人，长过屋脊，叱之，渐缩小。

    　　每叱一声，辄短数尺。拔剑追之，化作短人，奔树下而灭。取火烛之：乃一土偶人，长尺许，面扁阔，耸肩，左手少一小指。因拾置几上，而婢报某娘子房生一男矣。三日后抱视之：左手少一小指，状貌酷肖土偶。举家大惊，乃取土偶供祖庙中，礼事甚虔。

    Explanation

    386

    ○Kai Mingfu Quanjiao Ling Gong Yinbu, a man skilled in poetry and known for his free-spirited nature, was a close friend of mine. In the year Geng Yin, he served as a co-examiner in the southern examination hall and died from a severe boil on his back. His mother was pregnant, and as the due date approached, his grandfather, who held the position of Grand Secretary of the Imperial Household, saw a giant in the courtyard late one evening. The giant was taller than the ridge of the roof. The grandfather shouted at it, and it gradually shrank.

    Each time he shouted, the giant shortened by several feet. He drew his sword and chased after it, and it transformed into a short person, rushing to the tree and disappearing. Taking fire to illuminate it, he found it was a clay figurine, about a foot long, with a flat and broad face, hunched shoulders, and missing a little finger on the left hand. He picked it up and placed it on the table, and the maid reported that a boy was born in the room of a certain lady. Three days later, when they looked at the child, his appearance strikingly resembled the clay figurine. The whole family was greatly surprised, and they then took the clay figurine to the ancestral temple, worshipping it with great reverence.

    387

    及凯卒后，送神主入庙，见土偶为屋漏故雨滴其背，穿成三孔，仆于坐下。

    　　凯死时，背疮三孔皆穿。家人悔奉祀不虔，已无及矣。

    　　○羞疾湖州沈秀才，少年入泮，才思颇美。年三十余，忽得羞疾：每食，必举手搔其面曰：“羞，羞。”如厕，必举手搔其臀曰：“羞，羞。”见客亦然。家人以为癫，不甚经意。后渐羸，医治无效。有时清楚，问其故，曰：“疾发时，有黑衣女子捉我手如此，迟则鞭扑交下，故不得不然。”家人以为妖，适张真人过杭州，乃具牒焉。张批：“仰归安县城隍查报。”后十余日，天师遣法官来曰：“昨据城隍详称：沈秀才前世为双林镇叶生妻，黑衣女子者，其小姑也。叶饶于财，小姑许配李氏，家贫，叶生爱妹，延李郎在家读书，须李入泮，方议婚期。

    Explanation

    387

    After General Cai's death, when the spirit tablet was escorted into the temple, a clay figure was seen with water dripping from its back due to a leak in the roof, forming three holes, which fell down beside it.

    When General Cai died, there were three holes in his back from a sore. The family regretted not being sincere in their worship, but it was too late.

    Shame Disease: Shen, a scholar from Huzhou, entered the school at a young age and had a good mind. In his thirties, he suddenly developed a shame disease: every time he ate, he would raise his hand to scratch his face and say, "Shame, shame." When using the toilet, he would raise his hand to scratch his buttocks and say, "Shame, shame." He would do the same when seeing guests. The family thought he was crazy and did not pay much attention. Later, he gradually became emaciated, and medical treatment was ineffective. Sometimes, he was clear-headed, and when asked the reason, he said, "When the illness发作, a black-clad woman would grab my hand like this; if I delayed, she would beat me with a whip and rod, so I had no choice but to do it." The family thought it was an evil spirit, and when Zhang the True Man passed through Hangzhou, they prepared a document to present to him. Zhang wrote on the document: "Order the county city god of Guian to investigate and report." After more than ten days, the Heavenly Master sent a judge to say, "Yesterday, according to the detailed report of the city god, Mr. Shen, the scholar, was in his previous life the wife of Ye Sheng from Shuanglin Town, and the black-clad woman was his younger sister-in-law. Ye was wealthy, and his younger sister-in-law was promised to the Li family, who were poor. Ye loved his sister, so he invited Li to live in his house to study, and only when Li entered the school would they discuss the wedding."

    388

    一日者，小姑步月，见李郎方夜读，私遣婢送茶与郎。婢以告嫂，嫂次日向人前手戏小姑面曰：“羞羞。‘小姑忿，遂自缢，诉城隍神，求报仇索命。神批其牒云：”闺门处女，步月送茶，本涉嫌疑，何得以戏谑微词索人性命？不准。’小姑不肯已，又诉东岳。东岳批云：城隍批词甚明，汝须自省。但沈某前身既为长嫂，理宜含容，况姑娘小过，亦可暗中规戒，何得人前恶谑？今若勾取对质，势必伤其性命，罪不至此。姑准汝自行报仇，俾他烦恼可也。所查沈某冤业事，须至牒者。“天师曰：”此业尚小，可延高僧替小姑超度，俾其早投人身，便可了案。“如其言，沈病遂痊。

    Explanation

    388

    One day, the younger sister took a moonlit walk and saw Li Lang reading by night. She secretly sent a maid to deliver tea to him. The maid informed the older sister, who the next day teased the younger sister in front of others, saying, "Shameful." The younger sister, angered, committed suicide and appealed to the City God, seeking revenge and demanding a life. The City God responded in his decree, "A young woman in the privacy of her home, delivering tea under the moonlight, naturally raises suspicion. How can you justify taking a life with mere playful words? Not granted." The younger sister was not satisfied and appealed to the Eastern Peak. The Eastern Peak responded, "The City God's decree is clear; you must reflect on your actions. However, since Se Mou was formerly the elder sister, she should have been more forgiving. Moreover, the younger sister's minor offense could have been addressed discreetly. Why did you publicly mock her? If you were to summon her for cross-examination, it would inevitably harm her life, which is not a crime. I allow you to seek revenge yourself, but only to cause her trouble. The matter concerning Se Mou's karmic debt must be resolved through a formal decree." The Taoist priest said, "This karmic debt is minor; you may invite a high monk to help the younger sister attain liberation, allowing her to be reborn as a human, and the case will be resolved." Following his advice, Se Mou's illness was cured.

    389

    ○卖浆者儿杭州汪成瑞家，延钱塘贡生方丹成为西席，数日不至馆。问之，云：“替人作状告东岳。”问：“何事？”云：其邻张姓者妻病祈神，有卖浆叟往观。归，其子忽高坐呼其名索水吃。叟怒责之，子曰：“我非汝子，我是城隍司之勾神，今日与伙伴数人至张家勾取张氏妇魂。因其家延请五圣在堂，未便进内，久立檐下。渴甚，是以附魂汝子，向汝求水。”叟与之水。其子年仅十四五，所饮水不下石余。少顷，闻音乐声，曰：“张氏送神，吾去矣。叟赐我火炬数枝。”叟曰：“夜静难觅。”曰：“吾之火炬，即纸索耳，非世上火炬也。”焚与之，乃起谢曰：“受叟惠，无以报，吾有一事相告：令郎自今日后无使近水，否则将犯水厄。”语毕，其子即昏睡，而邻家张氏哭声举矣。叟虽异其事，尚秘之不宣。

    Explanation

    389

    A Boy from the Water Seller's Family

    In Hangzhou, the family of Wang Chengrui invited Fang Dancheng, a scholar from Qiantang, to serve as their private tutor. For several days, Fang did not come to the house. When they asked him why, he said, "I have been helping someone write a complaint against the East Peak." They asked, "What is the matter?" He replied: A neighbor, whose wife was ill and praying to the gods, invited a water seller to watch. When the seller returned home, his son suddenly sat up and called out the seller's name, demanding water to drink. The seller became angry and scolded him. The boy said, "I am not your son; I am a summoning spirit from the City God's office. Today, I came with several companions to the Zhang family to summon the soul of their wife. Because their home has invited the Five Saints to the hall, it was not convenient to enter, so we have been standing under the eaves for a long time. We are very thirsty, so I have possessed your son to ask you for water." The seller gave him water. The boy was only fourteen or fifteen years old and drank more than a stone of water. Shortly after, they heard music, and the boy said, "The Zhang family is sending off the gods, and I must go now. Please give me several torches." The seller said, "It is difficult to find them at night." The boy said, "My torches are just paper strips; they are not the torches of the world." The seller burned some paper strips and gave them to him. The boy then stood up and thanked him, saying, "I have received your kindness, and there is no way to repay you. I have one thing to tell you: Your son should not be allowed near water from today onward, otherwise, he will suffer a water-related disaster." After saying this, the boy fell into a deep sleep, and the Zhang family began to cry. The water seller found the event strange but kept it secret and did not reveal it.

    390

    次日下午，其子忽狂叫云：“甚热！我往浴于河。”叟不许，其子竟去。叟急拉回家，而狂躁愈甚，指地上石云：“如此好水，何不令我浴？”叟见其光景甚怪，惧不能提防，遍告诸邻，相同看视。

    　　西邻唐姓者，向信鬼神之事，里中祀东岳帝，唐主其事，或代亲友以祈禳，屡屡应验。闻浆叟言，又见其子之狂态，因告曰：“汝子为鬼所凭，何不求东岳神耶？”问：“作何求法？”曰：“帝君圣诞日，各执事俱齐，汝具牒呈焚香炉内，我鸣钟鼓相助。令有力者抱令郎在堂下，听候审讯发落，或可驱除恶鬼。”

    Explanation

    390

    The following afternoon, the boy suddenly shouted wildly, "It's so hot! I'm going to bathe in the river." The old man refused, but the boy went anyway. The old man hurriedly pulled him back home, but the boy became even more agitated, pointing to a stone on the ground and saying, "Such good water, why not let me bathe?" Seeing the boy's strange appearance, the old man feared he could not guard against it and informed all the neighbors to come and watch.

    The neighbor to the west, surnamed Tang, had previously believed in matters of ghosts and spirits. In the village, they worshiped the Eastern Emperor (Dongyue), and Tang was in charge of this matter. Sometimes, he would pray on behalf of relatives and friends to ward off evil, and these prayers were often verified. Hearing the old man's account and seeing the boy's wild behavior, Tang advised, "Your son is possessed by an evil spirit. Why not seek help from the Eastern Emperor?" The old man asked, "What method should I use?" Tang replied, "On the Eastern Emperor's birthday, all officials will be present. You should prepare a document and present it in the incense burner. I will sound the bells and drums to assist. Have someone strong hold your son in the hall, await the interrogation and judgment, and perhaps the evil spirits can be expelled."

    391

    浆叟以为然。

    　　三月二十八日清晨，叟斋戒往抱其子从辕门外匍匐喊冤；唐在殿上令会中执事者取其词状，大呼：“着速报司查拿。”浆叟抱儿上殿，众环拥之。甫及门，儿已昏迷，满口流涎，众惶恐。少顷苏醒，叟挟之归，至夜始能言，云：“我在街戏，见一人甚蓝缕，相约往浴。日日相随不离，至东岳庙时，尚随在后。忽见殿前速报司神奔下擒他，方惧而逃，恰已为其所获，并将我带上殿。见帝君持呈状细阅，向一戴纱帽者语缕缕，不甚明。惟闻说我父母无罪，何得捉伊儿作替代。

    　　将跟我之鬼锁押枷责，放我还阳。“嗣后，浆叟子竟无恙。

    　　○谢经历广州经历谢坤，绍兴人，甥陆某，选广东巡检，携母、妻及子至粤，甥舅相聚甚欢。赴任后，作书与舅氏，挽其转求上官，调一美缺。谢为转请于大府，得调澳门。其他虽所入胜昔，而逼近海隅，不无烟瘴。甥又作书与舅，复请再调。

    Explanation

    391

    The Old Man Found It Reasonable.

    On the morning of March 28th, the old man, after performing a ritual purification, went to carry his son out from the gate, crawling on his hands and knees to cry out his grievance. Tang, seated on the dais, ordered the officials present to take the old man's petition and shouted, "The Speedy Reporting God, seize him!" The old man carried his son up to the hall, and the crowd surrounded them. As they approached the entrance, the boy fell into a coma, foaming at the mouth, and the crowd grew anxious. After a short while, he regained consciousness. The old man took him home, and by nightfall, the boy could speak again, saying, "While playing in the street, I saw a man who was very poor. He invited me to bathe with him. Day after day, he followed me without leaving. When we reached Dongyue Temple, he was still behind me. Suddenly, I saw the Speedy Reporting God rush down from the front of the hall to catch him. I was terrified and tried to escape, but he had already caught me and brought me up to the hall. I saw the Heavenly Lord holding a petition and reading it carefully, speaking in detail to a man wearing a silk cap, though I could not fully understand what was being said. All I heard was that my parents were innocent, and why should they capture my child as a substitute?

    The ghost that was following me was locked in stocks and punished, and I was allowed to return to the world of the living." After this, the old man's son was unharmed.

    ○ The Experience of Xie Kun, the Magistrate of Guangzhou, a native of Shaoxing, and his nephew Lu, who was selected as a patrol inspector of Guangdong, brought his mother, wife, and son to the province. The uncle and nephew were very happy to be reunited. After Lu took up his post, he wrote a letter to his uncle, asking him to intercede with the higher authorities to transfer him to a more desirable position. Xie interceded on his behalf and managed to get him transferred to Macau. Although the salary was better than before, the area was close to the coast and had unhealthy miasma. Lu wrote another letter to his uncle, requesting another transfer.

    392

    谢憎其贪妄，不答。不两月，又接札云：“甥病矣，乞舅速救之，迟则性命不保。”

    　　谢虽恶甥之渎，而念姊已年迈，或有不测，势将如何；又惮长官见恶，难以进言。

    　　正踌躇间，当午假寐，见甥忽至前曰：“舅误我。我嘱舅至再，舅不一报。今甥受瘴死矣，母、妻及子已在城外水次，舅速迎之。”言毕而号。谢惊寤，即见人踉跄入门云：“陆甥于数日前已死，家眷扶柩至矣。”谢始悟梦见者即甥魂也，迎其眷至署，厝甥柩于僧寺，为作佛事。僧人宣疏，请斋主拈香，忽见朝衣冠者自屏后走出行礼，僧不知何人。其子拜佛，见其父在上，乃奔前相呼，随即杳然灭去，僧众皆惊。谢书室中素心兰开，外孙戏折一枝，谢挞之，忽见甥来怒曰：“舅奈何以一花责我儿，我当尽坏之！”片刻间，将兰叶均分为二。

    Explanation

    392

    Xie hated his nephew's greed and arrogance and did not reply. Within two months, he received another letter saying, "My nephew is ill; I beg Uncle to save him quickly, or his life will not be safe."

    Although Xie hated his nephew's impudence, he thought his sister was already old, and perhaps something unpredictable would happen, so he wondered what would happen then; he also feared being seen as hated by his superior and found it difficult to present his words.

    Just as he was hesitating, at noon, pretending to sleep, he saw his nephew suddenly come forward and say, "Uncle has mistaken me. I instructed Uncle twice, but Uncle did not reply once. Now my nephew has died of malaria; my mother, wife, and son are already at the water by the city. Uncle, quickly welcome them." After saying this, he cried. Xie was startled awake and immediately saw a person stumbling into the door and saying, "Lu nephew has already died several days ago; the family is carrying the coffin and has arrived." Xie began to understand that the one he dreamed of was his nephew's spirit. He welcomed his family to the office and placed the nephew's coffin in the temple, doing Buddhist rituals. The monk announced the prayer, inviting the host to pick up the incense. Suddenly, someone in court attire walked out from behind the screen to perform the ritual, and the monk did not know who it was. His son worshiped the Buddha and saw his father above, then ran forward to call to each other, and immediately disappeared and vanished. All the monks were surprised. In Xie's study room, the white-hearted orchid was in bloom, and his grandson playfully plucked a branch. Xie scolded him, and suddenly saw his nephew come angrily and say, "Uncle, how can you blame my son for picking a flower? I will destroy it all!" In an instant, he divided the orchid leaves into two equal parts.

    393

    居月馀，谢归其丧。解缆时，同里人附一柩于船尾，谢家人不知也。出粤界后，舟子欺其孤孀，与家人争殴。忽见陆甥跳舱中出，后随一少年，助陆将舟子五六人痛打，舟子哀求方已。家人惊疑，问舟子，云：“吾主人素所识，其少者不知何来。”舟子惶愧曰：“船头内附装一小柩，前恐府上人不许，是以匿之。

    　　今助殴者，想即此鬼耶。“从此一路，舟人倍小心矣。舟抵家，家人为开丧设主，从此寂然。

    　　○赵文华在阴司说情杭人赵京，祖籍慈溪。有弟某，性方严。婚后，妇家婢颇慧，未尝假以颜色，京私与狎，弟妻不知。无何，婢孕，妇翁疑婿，婢亦驾词诬婿，婿不能自明，恚投环死。

    Explanation

    393

    After staying for a month, the body was returned for burial. When untying the ropes to set sail, a fellow villager attached a coffin to the stern of the boat, which the family did not know about. After leaving the Guangdong border, the boatman took advantage of the widow and fought with the family. Suddenly, Lu's nephew jumped out of the cabin, followed by a young man, who helped Lu severely beat the boatman and five or six others. The boatman begged for mercy, and they stopped. The family was surprised and suspicious, and they asked the boatman. The boatman said, "My master is someone I know well, but I don't know where the young man came from." The boatman, feeling ashamed and guilty, said, "There was a small coffin inside the boat. I was afraid the people in your household would not allow it, so I hid it. The one who helped in the fight, I think it must be this ghost." From then on, the boatmen were extra cautious. When the boat reached home, the family prepared for the funeral and set up the spirit tablet, and then everything became quiet.

    ○ Sizhuang, a man from Hangzhou, whose ancestral home was in Cixi, had a younger brother who was strict and upright. After marriage, a servant girl in his wife's family was quite intelligent, but he never showed her any favor. Sizhuang secretly became intimate with her, and his younger brother's wife did not know about it. Soon after, the servant girl became pregnant. The father-in-law suspected his son-in-law, and the servant girl also fabricated words to slander him. The son-in-law could not clear his name, and in anger, he hanged himself to death.

    394

    越二年，京父寿辰，宾朋宴集，京与婢忽仆地呓语，经宿始苏，云：“摄至冥府，与婢械系大门外。俄闻发鼓升堂，鬼役ㄏ其首掷阶下，有冕旒者上坐，引弟质讯。京与婢皆伏罪，不敢置辩。将定谳矣，忽报：”赵尚书至。‘红柬上书’年家眷弟赵文华顿首拜。‘冥官肃衣冠出迎，命：“带人犯械系故处。’举头见柱上一联云：”人鬼只一关，关节一丝不漏；阴阳无二理，理数二字难逃。‘后署’会稽陶望龄题‘。正熟视间，报：“赵尚书出矣。’冥官唤京与婢谕云：‘本案应照因奸致死罪减三等判，以赵尚书说情，姑放回阳。且赵某身为男子，通婢事有何承认不起？而竟至轻生，亦殊可鄙。故且宽汝，放回阳间。’”举家不知赵文华何故庇京。一日，询诸宗老，始知文华其七世祖也，因谄严相，子孙丑之，故皆讳言，无知者。

    Explanation

    394

    Two years later, on the occasion of Jing's father's birthday, guests gathered for a feast. Suddenly, Jing and his maid fell to the ground, muttering incoherently, and remained unconscious for a full night before regaining consciousness. Jing recounted what had happened: "I was taken to the underworld, where I and the maid were shackled outside the main gate. Soon, the sound of a drum was heard, and the judge ascended the hall. A ghostly servant struck my head and threw it down the steps. There was a figure wearing a crown and flowing robes seated on the throne, who called my younger brother for interrogation. Both Jing and the maid admitted their guilt and dared not argue. Just as the final judgment was about to be pronounced, someone reported, 'Secretary of State Zhao has arrived.' A red envelope bore the inscription, 'Respectfully greeting my esteemed relative, Zhao Wenhua.' The underworld official dressed formally and went out to greet him, then ordered, 'Take the accused and re-shackle them in their original place.' Lifting his head, Jing saw a couplet on the pillar which read, 'Between humans and ghosts there is but one barrier; every joint is sealed tight; between the realms of yin and yang, there is no second principle; the logic of fate is inescapable.' Below the couplet was inscribed, 'Written by Tao Wangling of Kuaiji.' Just as Jing was studying it intently, someone reported, 'Secretary of State Zhao has left.' The underworld official then called Jing and the maid and said, 'This case should be judged with a reduction of three degrees from the crime of causing death through adultery, but since Secretary Zhao has interceded, you will be temporarily released back to the mortal world. Moreover, Zhao Wenhua, as a man, what is there to not admit about having an affair with a maid? And yet he took his own life, which is highly contemptible. Therefore, I will show leniency and release you back to the mortal world.' The entire family did not know why Zhao Wenhua protected Jing. One day, they inquired among the clan elders and learned that Zhao Wenhua was Jing's seventh-generation ancestor, who had flattered a strict prime minister and was despised by his descendants, so they all avoided mentioning him, and those who were unaware remained ignorant."

    395

    ○毁陈友谅庙赵公锡礼，浙之兰溪人，初选竹山令，调繁监利。下车之日，例应谒文庙及城隍神。吏启：“有某庙者，当拈香。”公往视：庙有神像三人，雁行坐，俱王者衣冠，状貌颇庄严。问：“何神？”竟无知者。公欲毁其庙，吏不可，曰：“神素号显赫，历任官参谒颇肃，毁之恐触神怒，祸且不测。”公归搜志乘祀典，不载此神，乃择日朝吏民于庙，手铁锁系神颈曳之。神像瑰伟，非掊击不能去。

    Explanation

    395

    Zhao Gong Xili, a magistrate from Lanxi, Zhejiang, was initially appointed as the magistrate of Zhushan and later transferred to Jianli. On the day of his arrival, according to custom, he should pay homage to the Confucian temple and the city god. An official informed him: "There is a temple, and one should offer incense." The magistrate went to see: the temple had three statues of deities, arranged in a line, all dressed in the attire of kings, with a very dignified appearance. He asked: "Who are these deities?" No one knew. The magistrate wanted to destroy the temple, but officials opposed it, saying: "The deity has always been known for its power and influence, and officials who have served before have paid homage with great respect. Destroying it might anger the deity, leading to unpredictable disaster."

    The magistrate returned to search through historical records and ritual texts, which did not record this deity. He then chose a day to hold a ceremony at the temple for officials and the people, personally tying a chain around the deity's neck and dragging it. The deity's statue is grand and impressive, and it could not be removed without force.

    396

    公曳之，应手而倒，三像碎于庭中。新其屋宇，改奉关帝。久之，竟无他异。公心终不怿，乃行文天师查之。得报牒云：“神系元末伪汉王陈友谅弟兄三人，兵败，死鄱阳湖，部曲散去，为立庙荆州。建于元至正某年，毁于国朝雍正某年赵大夫之手，合享血食四百年。”

    　　●卷十一○通判妾徽州府署之东，前半为司马署，后半为通判署，中间有土地祠，乃通判署之衙神也。乾隆四十年春，司马署后墙倒，遂与祠通。

    　　其夕，署中老妪忽倒地，若中风状。救之苏，呼饥；与之饭，啖量倍于常。

    396

    The official pulled it, and it fell with a response; the three images shattered in the courtyard. The building was renovated, and the worship of Guan Yu was changed.
