After a long time, there was no other unusual occurrence. The official remained dissatisfied and thus dispatched a letter to the Celestial Master for investigation. Received a reply document stating: "The deity is associated with the three brothers of the false Han king Chen Youliang at the end of the Yuan dynasty. They were defeated in battle and died in Poyang Lake, and their followers dispersed. A temple was built in Jingzhou for them. It was built in the year of some date in the Zhi Zheng era of the Yuan dynasty and destroyed by the hand of some official named Zhao in the year of some date in the Yongzheng era of the Qing dynasty. They shared offerings and sustenance for four hundred years."

    ● Volume Eleven, Chapter Ten: The Lady of the Deputy Magistrate in Huizhou Prefecture

    The eastern part of the Huizhou Prefecture office was divided into two sections: the front half was the office of the Military Governor, and the back half was the office of the Deputy Magistrate. In the middle, there was a land temple, which served as the deity of the Deputy Magistrate's office. In the spring of the 40th year of the Qianlong era, the wall of the Military Governor's office collapsed, and it became connected to the temple.

    That night, an elderly woman in the office suddenly fell to the ground, as if she had suffered a stroke. When she was rescued, she came to, and cried out that she was hungry; when given food, she ate twice the usual amount.

    397

    左足微跛，语作北音，云：“我哈什氏也，为前通判某妾，颇有宠，为大妻所苦，自缢桃树下。缢时希图为厉鬼报仇，不料死后方知命当缢死，即生前受苦，亦皆数定，无可为报。阴司例：凡死官署者，为衙神所拘，非墙屋倾颓，魂不得出。

    　　我向栖后楼中，昨日袁通判到任来，驱我入祠，此后饥馁尤甚；今又墙倾，伤我左腿，困顿不可耐。特凭汝身求食，不害汝也。“自是妪昼眠夜食，亦无所苦，往往言人已往事，颇验。

    　　先是司马有爱女卒于家，赴任时置女灵位某寺中，岁时遣祭，皆妪所不知。

    　　司马见其能言冥事，问：“尔知我女何在？”答曰：“尔女不在此，应俟我访明再告。”翌日，语司马云：“尔女在某寺中甚乐，所得钱钞，大有赢余，不愿更生人间，惟今春所得衣裳太窄小，不堪穿着。”司马大骇，推问衣窄之故。因遣家人往祭时，所制衣途中为两段，家人潜买市上纸衣代之故也。

    Explanation

    397

    Left-foot slightly limping, speaking with a northern accent, she said: "I am of the Ha Shi family, a concubine of a previous magistrate, favored but troubled by the main wife, I hanged myself from a peach tree. At the time of hanging, I intended to take revenge as a vengeful ghost, unfortunately, it was only after death that I learned my fate was to die by hanging, even the suffering I endured in life was predetermined, there was nothing to be done. The rules of the underworld: those who die in official quarters are bound by the spirits of the office, unless walls and houses collapse, the soul cannot leave.

    I had been residing in the rear building, but yesterday when Magistrate Yuan took office, he drove me into the shrine, and since then, I have been particularly hungry. Now, with the walls collapsing and injuring my left leg, I am in great distress. I rely on your body to find food, I will not harm you." From then on, the old woman slept during the day and ate at night, with no other discomfort, often speaking of past events of people, quite accurate.

    Previously, Magistrate had a beloved daughter who died at home. When taking office, he placed the daughter's spirit tablet in a certain temple, sent offerings at regular intervals, all unknown to the old woman.

    Magistrate saw that she could speak of matters of the afterlife and asked, "Do you know where my daughter is?" She replied, "Your daughter is not here, I should investigate further before telling you." The next day, she told Magistrate, "Your daughter is very happy in a certain temple, the money and paper she received, quite a surplus, she does not wish to be reborn in the human world, only the clothes she received this spring are too tight and small, not suitable to wear." Magistrate was greatly alarmed and inquired about the reason for the tightness of the clothes. It turned out that the clothes made were cut in half during the journey, and the family secretly bought paper clothes from the market to replace them.

    398

    未几，新通判莅任，方修衙署，动版筑，妪曰：“屋成，我当复归原处，但一入，又不知何年得出，敢向诸公多求冥钱，夜焚墙角下，我得之赂衙神，便可逍遥宇内焉。”司马如其言，焚之。次日，妪有喜色曰：“主人甚贤，无以为别，我善琵琶，且能歌，能饮酒，当歌一曲谢主人。”司马设为醴置琵琶，妪弹且歌云：“三更风雨五更鸦，落尽夭桃一树花。月下望乡台上立，断魂何处不天涯。”

    　　音调凄惋，歌毕，掷琵琶瞑目坐。众再叩之，蹶然起，语言笑貌，依然蠢老妪，足亦不跛矣。

    　　内幕崔先生常与问答。其言饥时，崔云：“此与府厨近，何不赴厨求食？”

    　　答云：“府署神尤严，不敢入。”其言袁通判见驱时，崔云：“袁通判上任大病，尔何必避？”答云：“他虽病，未至死，将来还要升官，我敢不避？”袁通判者，余弟香亭也。

    Explanation

    398

    Not long after, the new magistrate assumed office and began the construction of the government office. The old woman said, "When the house is completed, I will return to my original place, but once I enter, I do not know when I will be able to leave. I dare to ask for much spirit money from you all. Burn it at night at the corner of the wall; I will use it to bribe the office god, and then I can wander freely in the universe." The host followed her words and burned the spirit money. The next day, the old woman had a joyful expression and said, "The master is very kind; I have no other way to thank you. I am good at playing the lute and can sing and drink alcohol. I will sing a song to thank the master." The host prepared wine and placed the lute. The old woman played and sang:

    "Three o'clock rain and wind, five o'clock crows, all the early peach blossoms have fallen. Standing on the moon-viewing platform for looking back home, wherever the soul is severed, it is as far away as the horizon."

    The tune was mournful, and after she finished singing, she threw the lute and sat with closed eyes. When the others tried to speak to her again, she suddenly stood up, and her words, laughter, and appearance were still those of a foolish old woman, and her feet were no longer lame.

    Behind the scenes, Mr. Cui often engaged in conversation with the old woman. When asked about her hunger, Mr. Cui said, "This place is close to the kitchen of the government office. Why don't you go there to find food?"

    She replied, "The god of the government office is especially strict, and I dare not enter." When asked why she fled from Mr. Yuan, the new magistrate, Mr. Cui said, "Mr. Yuan was very ill when he took office. Why do you have to avoid him?" She replied, "Although he is sick, he has not died yet, and he will rise to a higher position in the future. How dare I not avoid him?" Mr. Yuan, the new magistrate, was my younger brother Xiangting.

    399

    ○刘贵孙凤阜阳王尹，遣家人刘贵偕役孙凤至江宁公干。凤素强悍，好管世上不平事。

    　　正月二日，贵邀凤晨饮淮清桥，凤于稠人中戟手骂曰：“新岁非索债之时，酒店非肆殴之地，渠可欺，我不可欺！”为扯拽卫护之状。同伴不解其故，方欲问之，凤忽瞑目云：“彼负我债，我迟至数十年，踪迹七千馀里，今才获之。干汝何事，乃为放去？汝既放彼，汝当代偿。”语毕，自批其颊，众共持之。俄而口涎目瞪，颓然倒地，众舁之旋寓。

    　　少顷苏云：“我入店见市中一人，额有血痕，状类乞丐，手ㄏ一儒生讨债，捶吐交下。儒生不胜痛，遍向市人求救，无一应者。我心不平，忿然大骂。其人惊释手，儒生趋避我右。其人来夺，我拳挥之。格斗间，儒生遂走，不知所往。

    Explanation

    399

    Liu Gui and Sun Feng, dispatched by Feiyang Wang Yin, were sent to Jiangning on official business. Sun Feng was usually strong and fierce, and he liked to meddle in unfair matters in the world.

    On the second day of the first month, Liu Gui invited Sun Feng to drink at Huiqing Bridge in the morning. In the midst of a crowd, Sun Feng pointed an index finger and scolded fiercely, saying, "The New Year is not a time for collecting debts, and a tavern is not a place for beating someone up. He can be deceived, but I cannot be deceived!" He made a gesture of pulling and protecting. His companions did not understand the reason and were just about to ask him, but Sun Feng suddenly closed his eyes and said, "He owes me a debt. I have waited for decades, traveling over seven thousand miles, and only now have I found him. What business is it of yours to let him go? Since you let him go, you will have to pay for it." After finishing his words, he slapped himself on the cheek. The others held him. Soon, he fell down limply, and they carried him back to the inn.

    After a short while, he regained consciousness and said, "I went into the store and saw a man in the market with bloodstains on his forehead, looking like a beggar, holding a scholar to collect a debt, beating and spitting at him at the same time. The scholar could not bear the pain and asked everyone in the market for help, but nobody responded. Feeling unjust, I scolded fiercely out of anger. The man released his hand in surprise, and the scholar avoided me to the right. The man came to take him, and I punched him. During the fight, the scholar ran away, and his whereabouts were unknown."

    400

    不料索债人遂为我祟，然彼时不备，故为所欺。今若再来，当痛捶之。“因以马鞭自卫。众见其无恙，稍稍散去，惟贵与同处。

    　　抵暮。凤语贵曰：“其人至门外矣。”方执鞭欲起，而手足皆若被缚，批颊詈骂如前。贵窘揖凤而言曰：“汝为何人？渠负汝何债，我当代偿。”凤曰：“我名王保定，儒生名朱祥，前世负我身价，非钱债也。本今无干，凤不合强预他人事，故我怒而凌之。承汝代偿，果丰，足我勾当，我即去；否则，并将及汝。”

    　　贵大恐，广集同伴，买冥镪数万。烧毕，乃向贵拱手作谢状曰：“十年后再获儒生，还须拉凤作证。”于是凤苏起，而神色散瘁，无复从前矫健矣。

    　　○狐诗汝宁府察多狐，每岁修葺，则狐四出为闾阎害，工竣则息。学使至，多所为扰。卢公明楷到任，祭之乃安，从此成例。学使至，皆祭署后小阁，相传狐所居。

    Explanation

    400

    Unexpectedly, the creditor had become a spirit to torment me, but at that time I was unprepared, so I was deceived. If he comes again now, I shall beat him severely. Therefore, I used a horsewhip for self-defense. The crowd saw that I was unhurt and gradually dispersed, leaving only Guì with me.

    By dusk, Feng spoke to Guì, saying, "That person has arrived at the gate." As Guì was about to stand up with the whip, his hands and feet were as if bound, and he slapped his cheek and cursed as before. Guì felt awkward and bowed to Feng while saying, "Who are you? What debt does he owe you? I will repay on his behalf." Feng replied, "My name is Wang Baoding. The scholar's name is Zhu Xiang. In a past life, he owed me my price, not a monetary debt. Originally, this matter is not my concern, but Feng should not have interfered in others' affairs, so I was angry and tormented him. If you repay on his behalf, and it is sufficient, it will suffice for my affairs, and I will leave; otherwise, it will also involve you."

    Guì was greatly frightened and gathered many companions, buying tens of thousands of paper money. After burning it, he bowed and made a gesture of thanks, saying, "In ten years, if another scholar is obtained, you will still need to bring Feng as a witness." Then Feng woke up, but his appearance was scattered and exhausted, no longer as agile as before.

    The fox composed poetry in Ruying Prefecture. There were many foxes there, and each year when repairs were made, the foxes would cause harm to the common people, but they would cease once the work was completed. When the provincial examiner arrived, they often caused disturbances. Upon Lu Gong Mingkai's arrival, he performed a ritual to appease them, and from then on, it became a custom. Whenever the provincial examiner arrived, they would all perform a ritual at the small pavilion behind the office, which was said to be where the foxes resided.

    401

    后学使至，有二仆不知，榻其上。晨起，人闻呼号声，往视，则二仆裸缚阁下，臂上各写诗二句。其一臂云：“文人祭我汝安床，汝试思量妨不妨。”一臂云：“前日享侬空酒果，今朝借尔代猪羊。”

    　　○大小绿人乾隆辛卯，香亭与同年邵一联入都。四月二十一日，至栾城东关，各店车马填集，惟一新开店无客，遂投宿焉。邵宿外间，香亭宿内间。

    　　漏初下，各就榻燃灯，隔壁遥相语。忽见长丈许人，绿面绿须，袍靴尽绿，自门入，其冠擦顶纸，ㄏㄏ有声。后又一小人，高不满三尺，头甚大，亦绿面绿衣冠 ，共至榻前，举袖上下作舞状。香亭欲呼而口噤，耳中闻邵语言，竟不能答。正惶惑间，见榻旁几上又倚一人，麻面长髯，头戴纱帽，腰束大带，指长人曰：“此非鬼也。”指大头者曰：“此鬼也。”又向二人挥手作语。二人点头，各向香亭拱手。

    Explanation

    401

    After the new prefect arrived, two servants, unaware of the situation, lay on the bed. In the morning, people heard cries, went to see, and found the two servants naked and bound under the eaves, with two lines of poetry written on their arms. One arm read: "Scholar, you worshipped me and placed your bed here; think about whether it is appropriate or not." The other arm read: "Yesterday, you offered me empty wine and fruit; today, you borrow me to replace pigs and sheep."

    ○ The Green Men of Large and Small, Qianlong Xinmao. Xiangting and his contemporary, Shao Yilian, traveled to the capital. On the twenty-first day of the fourth month, they reached the eastern gate of Luancheng. All the shops were filled with carriages and horses, but only one newly opened inn had no guests, so they decided to stay there. Shao stayed in the outer room, while Xiangting stayed in the inner room.

    At the first sound of the water clock, they each lit lamps on their beds and spoke to each other across the wall. Suddenly, they saw a man about ten feet tall, with a green face and green beard, wearing a green robe and green boots, enter through the door. His hat brushed the ceiling, making a rustling sound. Following him was a small man, no more than three feet tall, with an unusually large head, also with a green face and green clothing. Together, they came to the foot of the bed and raised their sleeves, dancing up and down. Xiangting wanted to call out, but his mouth was sealed, and he could only hear Shao's words in his ear, unable to respond. Just as he was in a state of confusion, he saw another person leaning against a table beside the bed. This person had a scraggly face and a long beard, wore a black hat, and had a broad sash tied around his waist. He pointed to the tall man and said, "This is not a ghost." Then he pointed to the man with the large head and said, "This is a ghost." He waved his hand to the two men and spoke to them. The two men nodded and each bowed to Xiangting.

    402

    每一拱手，则倒退一步，三拱三退出，纱帽者亦拱手而没。香亭遽起，方欲出户，邵亦狂呼突起奔而入，口称“怪事”不绝。香亭谓邵：“亦见大小绿人耶？”邵摇首曰：“否，否。方就枕时，觉床侧小屋内阴风习习，冷侵毛发，不能成寐，因与公相语。继呼公不答，见屋内有大小人面若盂若盎者数十，来去无定。初疑眼花，不之怪。忽大小人面层叠堆门限中，上下皆满，又一巨面大如磨盘，加于众面之上，皆视我而笑，乃投枕起，不知所谓绿人也。”香亭亦告以所见，遂此不秣马而行。

    Explanation

    402

    Every time they bowed with clasped hands, they took a step back. After bowing and stepping back three times, the man wearing a silk cap also bowed and disappeared. Xiangting suddenly stood up, about to go out the door, when Shao also shouted frantically and rushed in, continuously calling out, "Strange thing!" Xiangting asked Shao, "Have you also seen the small and large green people?" Shao shook his head and said, "No, no. When I was just lying down to sleep, I felt a cold breeze from a small room beside the bed, which seeped into my hair, and I couldn't fall asleep. I spoke to you, but when I called you, you didn't respond. I saw many small and large faces like bowls and jars inside the room, moving around without a fixed pattern. At first, I suspected it was an optical illusion and didn't find it strange. Suddenly, the faces were stacked up at the threshold, filling the space above and below. Then a giant face as large as a millstone appeared above the others, all looking at me and smiling. I threw the pillow aside and got up, not knowing what the green people were." Xiangting also recounted what he had seen, and thus they left without feeding the horses.

    403

    及时，闻二仆夫啧啧私语云：“昨宵所宿鬼店也，投宿者多死，否则病疯佯狂。县官疲于相验，禁闭已十馀年。昨一宿无恙，岂怪绝耶，抑二客当贵耶？”

    　　○红衣娘刘介石太守，少事乩仙，自言任泰州分司时，每日祈请，来者或称仙女，或称司花女，或称海外瑶姬，或称瑶台侍者。吟诗鄙俚，不成章句；说休咎，一无所应。

    　　署后藕花洲上有楼，相传为秦少游故迹。一夕，登楼书符，乩忽判“红衣娘”

    　　三字。问以事，不答，但书云：“眼如鱼目彻宵悬，心似酒旗终日挂。月光照破十三楼，独自上来独自下。”太守见诗，觉异，请退。次夕复请，又书：“红衣娘来也。”太守问：“仙属何籍？诗似有怨。且十三楼非此地有也，何以见咏？”

    Explanation

    403

    At that time, two servants were heard whispering in low tones, saying, "The ghost inn we stayed at last night. Most who stayed there died, or else became sick, mad, or feigned madness. The county officials were exhausted from investigating, and it had been sealed off for over ten years. Last night, we stayed there without any harm, was it because the place is no longer haunted, or because the two guests are destined for greatness?"

    ○The Red Robe Maiden, Liu Jieshi, the magistrate, served a spirit medium when he was young. He said that when he was serving as the deputy magistrate of Taizhou, he would pray to the spirit medium every day. Those who came sometimes called themselves fairy maidens, sometimes flower maidens, sometimes fairy maidens from the sea, and sometimes attendants of the Jade Terrace. They recited poetry that was coarse and vulgar, not forming coherent verses; they spoke of good and bad omens, but nothing they said came true.

    Behind the magistrate's office, on the Lotus Island, there is a tower, which is said to be the site of Qin Shaoyou's former residence. One night, he climbed the tower and wrote a talisman, and the spirit medium suddenly wrote the three characters "Red Robe Maiden." When he asked about matters, the spirit medium did not respond, but wrote the following: "Eyes like fish eyes hanging through the night, a heart like a wine flag hanging all day. Moonlight pierces the thirteen-story tower, coming up alone and going down alone." The magistrate saw the poem, found it strange, and asked to withdraw. The next night, he again asked, and the spirit medium wrote: "The Red Robe Maiden is coming." The magistrate asked, "What is the spirit maiden's origin? The poem seems to express resentment. Moreover, the thirteen-story tower is not in this place, why is it mentioned?"

    404

    又书曰：“十三楼爱十三时，楼是楼非那得知。寄语藕花洲上客，今宵灯下是佳期。”书毕，觇动不止。太守惧，弃盘奔就寐榻，见二婢持绿纱灯，引红衣娘冉冉至矣。拔剑挥之，随手而灭。自是每夕必至，不能安寝。数月后迁居始绝。

    　　○秀民册丹阳荆某，应童子试。梦至一庙，上坐王者，阶前诸吏捧册立，仪状甚伟。

    　　荆指册询吏：“何物？”答曰：“科甲册。”荆欣然曰：“为我一查。”吏曰：“可。”荆生平以鼎元自负，首请《鼎甲册》，遍阅无名；复查《进士孝廉册》，皆无名。不觉变色。一吏曰：“或在《明经秀才册》乎！”遍查亦无。荆大笑曰：“此妄耳。以某文学，可魁天下，何患不得一秀才！”欲碎其册，吏曰：“勿怒，尚有《秀民册》可查。秀民者。皆有文而无禄者也。人间以鼎甲为第一，天上以秀民为第一。此册为宣明王所掌，君可向王请之。”

    Explanation

    404

    Again, the Book of Changes says: "Thirteen floors love thirteen times, is it the floor or not? How can it be known? Send a message to the guest on Lotus Flower Isle, tonight under the lamp is a good time." After writing, the movement does not stop. The magistrate, fearing, abandons the tray and rushes to the sleeping couch, where he sees two maids holding green silk lamps, leading a red-clothed woman who appears slowly. He draws his sword and strikes, and it vanishes at once. From then on, every night it would come, preventing him from sleeping peacefully. After several months, moving to a new residence finally put an end to it.

    As for the Book of the Talented People, Jing from DanYang, was preparing for the child examination. He dreamed of entering a temple, where a king sat on the throne, and officials stood before the steps, holding a register, with impressive postures.

    Jing pointed to the register and asked the officials, "What is this?" They replied, "This is the register of scholars." Jing happily said, "Let me take a look." The official said, "Yes." Jing, who had always been proud of his status as a top scholar, first asked for the "Register of Top Scholars," but found no name among the entries; then he checked the "Register of Jinshi and Ju Ren," but still found no name. He could not help but change color. One official said, "Perhaps you are in the 'Register of Talented People'!" He checked thoroughly but still found no name. Jing laughed loudly, "This is nonsense. With my talent, I can be the top scholar in the world, why worry about not being a talented person!" He wanted to tear up the register, but the official said, "Do not be angry, there is still a 'Register of Talented People' to check. Talented people are those who have talent but no official position. In the world, the top scholar is considered the first, but in heaven, the talented person is considered the first. This register is held by the Exalted and Bright King. You can ask for it from the king."

    405

    如其言，王于案上出一册，黄金丝穿白玉牒，启第一页，第一名即“丹阳荆某”。荆大哭，王笑曰：“汝何痴也！汝试数从古有几个名状元、名主试乎？韩文公孙衮中状元，人但知韩文公，不知有衮；罗隐终身不第，至今人知有罗隐。

    　　汝当归而求之实学可耳。“荆问：”科第中皆无实学乎？“王曰：”即有文才，又有文福，一代不过数人，如韩、白、欧、苏是也。此其姓名，别在紫琼宫上，与汝尤无分也。“荆未对，王拂衣起，高吟曰：”一第区区何足羡，贵人传者古无多。“荆惊醒怏怏，卒不第以终。

    Explanation

    405

    According to his words, the king took a book from the table, bound with golden thread and white jade sheets. Opening the first page, the name at the top was "Jing of DanYang." Jing burst into tears, and the king smiled and said, "How foolish you are! Try counting how many famous top scorers and chief examiners there have been in history? Han Yu's son Sun Gun became the top scorer, but people only know of Han Yu, not of Gun; Luo Yin failed the exams all his life, yet people still know of Luo Yin.

    You should return and seek real learning instead." Jing asked, "Are there no real learning in the exams?" The king replied, "If there is literary talent and literary fortune, there are only a few in each generation, such as Han Yu, Bai Juyi, Ouyang Xiu, and Su Shi. Their names are inscribed in the Purple Jade Palace, and they have no connection with you." Jing remained silent. The king rose and left, reciting aloud, "A single top scorer is trivial, and those remembered by the elite are rare in ancient times." Jing woke up disappointed and ultimately failed the exams.

    406

    ○妓仙苏州西碛山后有雪隘峰，相传其上多仙迹，能舍身而上，不死即得仙。有王生者，屡试不第，乃抗志与家人别，裹粮登焉。更上，得平原，广百亩许，云树蓊郁中，隐隐见悬崖上有一女子，衣装如世人，徘徊树下。心异之，趋而前，女亦出林相望。迫视，乃六七年前所狎苏州名妓谢琼娘也。彼此素相识，女亦喜甚，携生至茅庵。

    　　庵无门，地铺松针，厚数尺，履之绵软可爱。女云：“自与君别后，为太守汪公访拿，褫衣受杖，臀肉尽脱。自念花玉之姿，一朝至此，何颜再生人间？因决计舍身，辞别鸨母，以进香为词，至悬崖奋身掷下，为萝蔓纠缠，得不死。有白发老妪食我以松花，教我以服气，遂不知饥寒。初犹苦风日，一岁后，霜露风雨，都觉无怖。老母居前山，时相过从。昨老母来云，今日汝当与故人相会。以故出林闲步，不意获见君子。”因问：“汪太守死否？”生曰：“我不知。

    Explanation

    406

    In Suzhou, west of Xiqi Mountain, there is a Snowy Narrow Peak, where legends say there are many traces of immortals. One can achieve immortality if they do not die while ascending. There was a man named Wang who had failed the imperial examinations several times. He decided to set his mind on a higher goal and bid farewell to his family. He packed provisions and climbed the mountain. He ascended further and found a vast plain, about a hundred acres in size. In the dense forest of clouds and trees, he faintly saw a woman on a cliff. She was dressed like a commoner and was standing by a tree. He was surprised and approached her. The woman also came out of the forest to meet him. Upon closer inspection, she was the famous courtesan Xie Qiongnyang, whom he had known six or seven years ago. They recognized each other, and the woman was very happy. She took him to a thatched hut.

    The hut had no door, and the ground was covered with pine needles, several feet thick, soft and comfortable underfoot. The woman said, "After parting from you, I was visited by Magistrate Wang, who questioned and punished me. My buttocks were severely beaten, and my flesh was torn away. I thought, 'How can I face the world again with this beautiful body?' So I decided to sacrifice myself. I bid farewell to my madam and used the excuse of going to pay respects. I jumped off the cliff, but I was caught by vines and did not die. An old woman fed me pine flowers and taught me to practice breathing exercises. I no longer feel hungry or cold. At first, I was afraid of the wind, but after a year, frost, dew, wind, and rain no longer frightened me. My mentor lives on a nearby mountain and visits me occasionally. Yesterday, my mentor told me that today I would meet an old friend. That's why I went for a walk in the forest and unexpectedly met you." She then asked, "Is Magistrate Wang still alive?" Wang replied, "I don't know."

    407

    卿仙家，亦报怨乎？”女曰：“我非汪公一激，何能至此！当感不当报。但老母向我云：”偶游天庭，见杖汝之汪太守被神笞背，数其罪。故疑其死。“生曰：”妓不当杖乎？“女曰：”惜玉怜香而心不动者，圣也；惜玉怜香而心动者，人也；不知玉不知香者，禽兽也。且天最诛人之心，汪公当日为抚军徐士林有理学名，故意杀风景以逢迎之，此意为天所恶。且他罪多，不止杖妾一事。“生曰：”我闻仙流清洁，卿落平康久矣，能成道乎？“女曰：”淫虽非礼，然男女相爱，不过天地生物之心。放下屠刀，立地成佛，不比人间他罪难忏悔也。“

    Explanation

    407

    The divine one asked, "Do you also harbor resentment?" The woman replied, "If it were not for the provocation by Mr. Wang, how could I have reached this point! I should feel grateful rather than seek retribution. But my mother once told me: 'Occasionally visiting the heavenly court, I saw the magistrate Mr. Wang, who beat you, being whipped by the gods, counting his sins. Therefore, I suspected his death.'" The man said, "Shouldn't a courtesan be beaten?" The woman said, "Those who cherish beauty and fragrance without being moved are saints; those who are moved by beauty and fragrance are human beings; those who do not know beauty and fragrance are beasts. Moreover, heaven most punishes human hearts. Mr. Wang, at the time, was known for his reputation in the school of rational learning under the governor-general Mr. Xu Shilin, intentionally destroying the scenery to curry favor with him. This intention was abhorred by heaven. Moreover, he has many other sins, not just the beating of me." The man said, "I have heard that those of the divine realm are pure, yet you have been in the brothels for a long time. Can you achieve enlightenment?" The woman said, "Though lust is not proper conduct, yet the love between men and women is simply the heart of heaven and earth giving birth. If one puts down the butcher's knife, one becomes a Buddha immediately. It is not as difficult to repent as other sins in the mortal world."

    408

    生具道来寻仙本意，且求宿庵中。女曰：“君宿何妨，但恐仙未能成也。”

    　　因为生解衣置枕，情爱如昔，而语不及私。生摸视其臀，白腻如初，女亦不拒。

    　　然心稍动，则女色益庄，门外猿啼虎啸，或探首于窦，或进爪于门，若相窥者。

    　　生不觉息邪心，抱女端卧而已。夜半，闻门外呵咤声，舆马驺从，贵官显者往来不绝。生怪之，女曰：“此各山神灵酬酢，每夕多有，慎勿触犯。”

    Explanation

    408

    The man fully explained his original intention to seek immortality and also requested to stay in the hermitage. The woman said, "Why not stay here, but I fear you might not succeed in becoming an immortal."

    Because the man undressed and placed the pillow, his affection was as it used to be, but the conversation did not touch on personal matters. The man touched her buttocks, which were as white and smooth as before, and the woman did not resist.

    However, when his heart slightly moved, the woman became even more serious. Outside the door, monkeys were crying and tigers were roaring, some were peeking through the holes, and some were reaching their claws through the door, as if they were watching.

    The man unconsciously calmed his evil thoughts and held the woman, lying quietly. At midnight, he heard shouts outside the door. Carriages, horses, and attendants came and went without end, noble officials and prominent figures. The man was surprised and asked the woman, "What is this?" The woman replied, "These are the spirits of the mountains exchanging courtesies, and it happens every night. Be careful not to offend them."

    409

    及天明，女谓生曰：“君诸亲友已在山下访寻，宜速返。”生不肯行，女曰：“仙缘有待，君再来未晚。”送至崖，一推而堕。生回望，见女立云雾中，情殊依依，逾时影才灭。生踉跄奔归，见其兄与家人持楮镪哭尊于山下，谓生已死二十七日矣，故来祭尊。访汪太守，果以中风亡。

    　　○李百年无锡张塘桥华协权者，与好事数人设乩盘于家。其降鸾者曰仲山王问。仲山，胡明进士，锡之闻人也。众因与酬答，出语蹇涩，诗亦不甚韵，每召辄至。时华方构一楼，请仙题其扁。仙曰：“无锡秦园有扁曰‘聊逍遥兮容与’，此可用乎？”

    　　众疑此语出屈子，而必曰秦园，不似仲山语也。

    　　一日者，与众答问方欢，忽书：“吾欲去矣。”问：“何之？”曰：“钱汝霖家见招赴席。”乩遂寂然。钱汝霖者，亦里中人，所居去张塘桥不二三里，众因怪而侦之，则是日以病故祷神也。

    Explanation

    409

    As dawn broke, the fairy said to the man, "Your friends and relatives have already come to search for you in the valley below; you should return quickly." The man refused to go, and the fairy said, "There is a destined meeting with the fairy; it is not too late for you to come again." She led him to the cliff and pushed him down. As he looked back, he saw her standing in the mist, her expression filled with deep affection. After a while, her image finally faded away. The man staggered back home and saw his brother and family holding paper money and mourning at the foot of the mountain, believing that he had died twenty-seven days ago and came to pay their respects. They then went to visit Governor Wang, who indeed had died of a stroke.

    ○ Li Bai, a man from Zhangtang Bridge in Wuxi, named Hua Xiequan, set up a medium's platform at home with several enthusiasts. The medium who descended was called Zhongshan Wang Wen. Zhongshan was Hu Ming, a successful scholar-official and a well-known figure in Wuxi. The group interacted with him, and his speech was stiff and difficult to express, and his poetry lacked rhythm. Whenever they summoned him, he would appear. At the time, Hua was building a tower and asked the medium to inscribe a nameplate for it. The medium said, "There is a nameplate in Qin Garden in Wuxi that reads 'Chatting leisurely, wandering at ease.' Can this be used?"

    The group doubted that these words came from Qu Yuan but wondered why he specifically mentioned Qin Garden, as it did not seem like something Zhongshan would say.

    One day, while they were happily answering questions, the medium suddenly wrote, "I want to leave." They asked, "Where are you going?" The medium replied, "I am invited to a banquet at Mr. Qian Rulin's house." The medium's platform then fell silent. Mr. Qian Rulin was also a local person, living no more than two or three miles from Zhangtang Bridge. The group found this strange and investigated, and it turned out that he had prayed to the gods because of an illness that day.

    410

    明日，仙复至，华因问：“昨夜饮钱家乎？”曰：“然。”“盛馔乎？”曰：“颇佳。”众嘲之曰：“钱乃祷神，非请仙也，所请者城隍土地之属，岂有高人王仲山而往赴席乎？”仙语塞，乃曰：“吾非王仲山，乃山东李百年耳。”问：“百年何人？”曰：“吾于康熙年间在此贩棉花，死不得归，魂附张塘桥庵。庵有无主魂，与我共十三人，皆无罪孽，无羁束。里中之祷者，皆吾辈享之。”华曰：“所祷城隍诸神，俱有主名，若既无名，何得参与其间？”曰：“城隍诸神岂轻向人家饮食？所祷者都是虚设。故吾辈得而享焉。”华曰：“无名冒食，天帝知之恐加罪，奈何？”曰：“天上岂知有祷乎，是皆愚民习俗之所为。即鬼祟索食，间或有之，究无关于生死也。况我非索之，而彼自设之，而我享之，何忤于天帝？即君家茶酒，亦非我素之也。”曰：“既如此，子何必托名于王仲山耶？”

    Explanation

    410

    The next day, the immortal returned, and Hua asked, "Did you drink at the house of Money last night?" "Yes," he replied. "Was the feast grand?" "Quite good," he said. The others mocked him, saying, "Money was praying to the gods, not inviting the immortals. The deities being prayed to are usually city gods and land deities, not high-ranking figures like Wang Zhongshan, so how could you attend their banquet?" The immortal was speechless and then said, "I am not Wang Zhongshan; I am Li Bai Nian from Shandong." "Who is Li Bai Nian?" they asked. "I sold cotton during the Kangxi period and died, unable to return home. My soul attached itself to the Ouyang Bridge Temple. There are thirteen souls in the temple, all without masters, and none of us have committed sins or are bound. The people who pray here are all our offerings." Hua asked, "The city gods and land deities being prayed to all have proper names. If you have no name, how can you participate in such offerings?" "City gods and land deities do not lightly accept offerings from people. Those being prayed to are often imaginary or symbolic. Thus, we can enjoy them." Hua said, "Spirits without names accepting offerings, the heavenly emperor might know and punish you. What will you do?" "The heavens do not know about these prayers; they are the result of the ignorant customs of the common people. Even if spirits demand offerings, it happens rarely, and ultimately does not affect life and death. Besides, we are not seeking food or drink; we are simply enjoying the offerings provided. Why would we offend the heavenly emperor? Even the tea and wine at your home are not what we seek." "If that's the case, why do you claim to be Wang Zhongshan?"

    411

    曰：“君家檐头神执符来请，彼不敢上请真仙，所请者皆我辈也。十三人中，惟我稍识几字，故聊以应命。使直书姓名曰‘李百年’，君等肯尊奉我乎？我见此处人家扁额多仲山王问书，知为名人，故托其名来耳。”问：“‘聊逍遥兮容与’六字何出？”曰：“吾但于秦家园见之，不知所出。道听涂说，见笑大方矣。”

    Explanation

    411

    The spirit on your eaves holds a talisman and comes to request, but he dares not petition the true immortals; those he petitions are all of our kind. Among thirteen people, only I slightly recognize a few characters, so I casually fulfill the command. If I were to write the name directly as 'Li Bai Nian,' will you all respect and follow me? I see that the nameplates here are mostly written by Zhongshan Wang, knowing he is a famous person, so I pretend to be him. Someone asks, "Where do the six characters 'liao xiao yao xi rong yu' come from?" I reply, "I only saw it in the garden of Qin's home, but I don't know its origin. Heard on the road and said in the street, laughed at by the knowledgeable."

    412

    华曰：“子既无羁束，何不归山东？”曰：“关津桥梁，是处有神，非钱不得辄过。”华曰：“吾今以一陌纸钱送汝归，何如？”曰：“唯唯，谢谢。既见惠，须更以一陌酬于桥神，不然，仍不获拜赐也。”

    　　时华之侄某在旁曰：“吾早暮过桥上，汝得无祟我乎！”曰：“顷吾言之矣，鬼安能为祟？”于是焚楮绽送之，而毁其乩焉。

    　　○医妒轩辕孝廉，常州人，年三十无子，妻张氏奇妒，孝廉畏如虎，不敢置妾。其座主马学士某怜之，赠以一姬。张氏怒，以为干我家事，我亦设计扰其家。会学士丧偶，张访得某村女世以悍闻，乃贿媒妪说马娶为夫人。马知其意，欣然往聘。

    Explanation

    412

    Hua said, "Since you are no longer bound or restrained, why don't you return to Shandong?" The ghost replied, "At checkpoints, bridges, and other travel obstacles, there are deities everywhere, and without money, one cannot pass through." Hua said, "I will now send you back with a unit of paper money. What do you think?" The ghost responded, "Agreed, thank you. Since I have received this gift, I must repay one unit to the bridge deity, otherwise, I cannot accept the gift."

    At that time, Hua's nephew, who was present, said, "I pass over the bridge every morning and evening. Will you not cause me trouble?" The ghost replied, "I just said that. How can a ghost cause trouble?" Hua then burned the paper money to send the ghost away and destroyed the divination board.

    ○ A scholar named Xuanyuan, from Changzhou, was thirty years old and had no children. His wife, Zhang, was extremely jealous, and the scholar feared her like a tiger,不敢置妾. His teacher, Scholar Ma, felt pity for him and gave him a concubine. Zhang was angry, thinking it interfered with her family affairs, and she also planned to disturb his household. When Scholar Ma lost his wife, Zhang found a woman from a certain village who was known for her ferocity and bribed a matchmaker to persuade Ma to marry her as his wife. Ma understood her intention and happily went to propose.

    413

    婚之日，妆奁中有五色棒一条，上书“三世传家捣稿砧”者也。合卺毕，群姬拜见。夫人问：“若辈何人？”曰：“妾也。”夫人叱曰：“安有堂堂学士家而有礼当置妾者乎？”即棒群姬。马命群姬夺其棒，齐殴之。夫人力不胜，逃入房，骂且哭。群姬各击锣鼓乱其声，如无闻焉者。夫人不得已，扬言将自尽，则侍者备一刀一绳，曰：“老爷久知夫人将有此举，故备此不堪之物奉赠。”已而群姬各敲木鱼诵往生咒，愿夫人早升仙界，声嘈嘈然。夫人寻死之说，又如无闻焉者。夫人故女豪，自分虚疑恫喝，计已尽施，无益，乃转嗔作喜，请学士入，正色曰：“君真丈夫也，我服矣。我所行诸策，亦祖奶奶家传，吓世间妄庸男子，非所以待君。嗣后请改事君，君亦宜待我以礼。”学士曰：“能如是乎，夫复何言！”即重行交拜礼，命群姬谢罪叩头，并取田房帐簿，一切金币珠翠，尽交夫人主裁。一月之间，马氏家政肃雍，内外无闲言。

    Explanation

    413

    On the wedding day, there was a colorful rod in the bride's dowry chest, inscribed with the words "Three Generations Pass Down the Family's Tradition of Beating the Mortar." After the wedding ceremony, the concubines paid their respects. The lady asked, "Who are you?" They replied, "We are your concubines." The lady scolded, "How can a respectable scholar's family have a reason to take concubines?" She then beat the concubines. Ma ordered the concubines to take the rod and beat her together. The husband, unable to resist, ran into the room, cursing and crying. The concubines each beat drums and gongs to drown out the sound, as if they hadn't heard anything. The lady, unable to bear it, threatened to commit suicide. Then the attendants prepared a knife and a rope. They said, "The master has long known that the lady would do this, so he prepared these unsuitable things to give her." Later, the concubines each knocked on wooden fish and recited the mantra for the afterlife, wishing the lady to ascend to the heavenly realm soon. The sound was noisy. The lady, being a woman of great courage, knew that her threats and intimidation had already been used to no avail, and turning her anger into joy, she invited the scholar to come in. She said solemnly, "You are truly a man, I am convinced. The strategies I used are also passed down from my ancestors, to scare the foolish men of the world, not to treat you. From now on, please treat me as your wife, and you should also treat me with respect." The scholar said, "If you can do this, what more can I say!" They then performed the formal bowing ceremony again, and she ordered the concubines to apologize and kowtow. She also took the land and property ledgers, all the gold, coins, and jewelry, and handed them over to the lady to manage. Within a month, the Ma household's affairs were orderly and harmonious, with no complaints inside or outside.

    414

    张氏于学士成亲日，即使人往探，召而问之，闻见群妾矣。曰：“何不棒之？”

    　　曰：“斗败矣。”曰：“何不骂且哭？”曰：“锣鼓声喧无所闻。”曰：“何不寻死？”曰：“早备刀绳，且诵往生咒送行矣。”“然则夫人如何？”曰：“已服礼投降。”张大怒，骂曰：“天下有如此不中用妇人乎？殊误乃娘事！”

    　　初，学士赠姬时，群门生具羊酒往贺轩辕生，有平素酗酒者与焉。饮方酣，张氏自屏后骂客。客皆隐忍，酗酒者直前握张氏发，批其颊曰：“汝敬轩辕兄，是我嫂也；汝不敬轩辕兄，是我仇也。门生无子，老师赠妾，为汝家祖宗三代计耳！我今为汝家祖宗代治汝，敢多一言者，死我拳下！”群客争前攘劝，始得脱，然裙裂衣损，几露其私焉。张素号牝夜叉，一旦凶威大损，愈恨马学士，计惟毒苦其所赠姬以抒愤。而姬阴受学士教，一味顺从，虽进门，不与轩辕生交一言，以故张虽笞詈屡加，未忍致之于死。

    Explanation

    414

    On the day the scholar married, Zhang sent someone to investigate and called the concubine, asking her, "Did you see many other concubines?" The concubine replied, "They were defeated in a fight." Zhang asked, "Why didn't you beat them?" The concubine answered, "The sound of drums and gongs was so loud that I couldn't hear anything." Zhang then asked, "Why didn't you scold and cry?" The concubine replied, "They had already prepared a knife and rope, and chanted a sutra for the afterlife to accompany them." Zhang then asked, "What about the wife?" The concubine said, "She has already submitted to the etiquette." Zhang flew into a rage and cursed, "Is there such a useless woman in the world? She greatly disappoints your mother!"

    At first, when the scholar gave the concubine as a gift, a group of disciples brought sheep and wine to congratulate Xuan Yuan. Among them was a habitual drunkard. As they were just getting drunk, Zhang came out from behind the screen and cursed the guests. The guests all endured it silently, but the drunkard stepped forward, grabbed Zhang's hair, and slapped her face, saying, "If you respect Xuan Yuan, then I am your brother-in-law; if you do not respect Xuan Yuan, then I am your enemy. The disciple has no son, the teacher gave a concubine, it is for the sake of your family for three generations! I am now representing your ancestors to deal with you. Anyone who dares to speak more will die at my hands!" The guests rushed forward to stop him, and he finally escaped, but her skirt was torn and her clothes were damaged, almost exposing her private parts. Zhang was always called a female night demon, and her fierce reputation was greatly diminished. She became even more resentful of the scholar and planned to torment the concubine given by the scholar to vent her anger. But the concubine secretly followed the scholar's instructions and was completely obedient. Although she entered the door, she did not speak to Xuan Yuan once. Therefore, although Zhang repeatedly beat and scolded her, she did not dare to kill her.

    415

    居亡何，学士手百金赠轩辕生曰：“明春将会试，生宜持此盘费早入都。”

    　　生以为然，归辞张氏。张氏虑其居家狎妾，喜而许之。生甫登舟，马遣人迎至家，扃后园中读书，而阴遣媒妪说张氏：“趁轩辕生外出，盍卖其妾？”张曰：“此吾心也。然卖必远方，方无后患。”妪曰：“易，易。”俄而，有陕西卖布客丑且胡，背负三百金来，呼姬出见，喝彩不已，即成交易。张氏余怒未消，褫其衫履，一簪不得着身。姬乘竹轿过北桥，大呼：“我不远出。”跳身河中，学士早备小舟，迎至园，与轩辕生同室矣。张氏闻姬投河死，方惊疑，而陕客已蹋门入曰：“我买人非买鬼。汝家卖妾，未曾说明，何得逼良为贱，欺我异方人？速还我银！”怒且骂。张氏无以答，畀原银三百两去。

    Explanation

    415

    After some time, the scholar handed him a hundred taels of silver and said, "Next spring, you will attend the metropolitan examination. You should hold onto this travel fund and arrive in the capital early."

    The scholar thought this was a good idea and returned to seek permission from Zhang. Zhang, fearing that he would become too familiar with her concubine at home, happily agreed. As soon as he boarded the boat, Ma sent someone to bring him back home, where he was confined to the back garden to study. Meanwhile, Ma secretly sent a matchmaker to persuade Zhang, saying, "While轩辕生 is out, why not sell your concubine?" Zhang replied, "That is indeed my intention. However, she must be sold far away to avoid any future troubles." The matchmaker said, "That is easy, easy."

    Soon after, a merchant from Shaanxi, who was ugly and foreign, arrived carrying three hundred taels of gold. He called for the concubine to be brought out, and he praised her endlessly, and the transaction was immediately completed. Zhang's lingering anger had not yet subsided, so she stripped the concubine of her clothes and shoes, leaving her not even allowed to wear a hairpin. The concubine, riding in a bamboo sedan chair, passed over the northern bridge and shouted, "I am not going far." She then leaped into the river. The scholar had already prepared a small boat and welcomed her back to the garden, where she shared a room with轩辕生. When Zhang heard that the concubine had jumped into the river and died, she was shocked and confused. However, the Shaanxi merchant had already kicked open the door and said, "I bought a person, not a ghost. Your family sold your concubine without explaining, how could you force a virtuous woman into servitude and deceive a stranger from afar? Quickly return my silver!" He was angry and cursing. Zhang had no answer and gave him back the original three hundred taels of silver.

    416

    越一日，有白发蓝缕男妇两老人号哭来曰：“马学士将我女赠汝家为妾，女今安在？生还我人，死还我尸！”张氏无以答，则撞头拼命，打碗掷盘，满屋无完物矣。张苦求邻佑，赠以财帛，劝解去。又一日，武进县捕役四五人，狞狞然持朱字牌来，曰：“事关人命，请犯妇张氏作速上堂。”投铁链几上，鉴然有声。

    　　张问故，初犹不言，以银贿之，方言：“某姬之父母在县告身死不明事也。”张愈恐，私念：我丈夫在家，则一切事让他抵当，何至累我一妇人出乖露丑，堂上受讯耶？方深悔从前待夫之薄，御妾之暴，行事之误，女身之无用。自怨自恨间，忽有戴白帽踉跄奔呼而至者曰：“轩辕相公到芦沟桥，暴病死矣！我骡夫也，故来报信。”张氏大恸，不能言。诸捕役曰：“他家有丧事，我辈且去。”张氏成服治丧。未数日，捕役又至。张氏乃招讼师谋缓其狱，典妆奁、卖屋，贿书差捺搁此案。讼事小停，家已荡然，日食不周矣。

    Explanation

    416

    The next day, two elderly man and woman with white hair and tattered clothes came wailing, saying, "Scholar Ma gave my daughter to your family as a concubine. Where is she now? Return her to me, alive or dead!" Zhang had no answer, so she hit her head in despair, smashed bowls and plates, and the entire house was in ruins. Zhang begged the neighbors for help, offering them money and cloth, and they persuaded them to leave. The next day, four or five constables from Wujin County came, fierce and holding red boards, saying, "This involves a matter of life and death. Please have the guilty woman Zhang come to the court quickly." They threw an iron chain onto the table, making a clanging sound.

    Zhang asked why, but at first they did not speak. She bribed them with silver, and they finally said, "The parents of a certain concubine are reporting her death in Wujin County." Zhang was even more afraid, thinking to herself, "If my husband were at home, he could handle everything. Why should I, a woman, be disgraced and exposed in court?" She deeply regretted her past treatment of her husband, her harsh treatment of her concubine, her mistakes in action, and the uselessness of her daughter's body. As she lamented and hated herself, a messenger wearing a white hat came running, saying, "High Official Xuan Yuan died suddenly at Lugou Bridge! I am the mule driver, so I came to deliver the news." Zhang wept bitterly, unable to speak. The constables said, "They have a funeral to attend to. We will leave for now." Zhang put on mourning clothes and prepared for the funeral. Not long after, the constables came again. Zhang then hired a lawyer to delay the case, pawning her jewelry and selling her house, bribing the scribe to postpone the proceedings. The lawsuit temporarily stopped, but the family was already ruined, and daily food was scarce.

    417

    前媒妪又来曰：“夫人一苦至此，又无公子可守，奈何？”张心动，取生年月日命瞎姑算之。瞎姑曰：“命犯重夫，穿金戴珠。”张氏语媒妪曰：“改嫁命也，我敢违命乎！但我自行主婚，必须我先一见所嫁者而后可。”妪引一美少年盛饰与观，曰：“此某公子也，候选员外郎。”张大喜，摒挡衣饰，未满七七，即嫁少年。

    　　方合卺，忽房内一丑妇持大棒出，骂曰：“我正妻大奶奶也。汝何处贱婢，敢来我家为妾？我断不容！”直前痛殴之。张悔被媒绐，又私念“此是我当日待妾光景，何乃一旦身受此惨，报复之巧，殆天意耶？”饮泣不能声。诸宾朋上前劝丑妇去曰：“且让郎君今日成亲，有话明日再说。”于是诸少年秉花烛引张氏入卧室。

    Explanation

    417

    The matchmaker came again and said, "The lady has suffered so much, and she has no son to protect her, what shall we do?" Zhang was moved, and she took her birth date to have the blind fortune-teller calculate her fate. The blind fortune-teller said, "Her fate is to have many husbands, wearing gold and pearls." Zhang said to the matchmaker, "It is fate for me to remarry, how dare I defy my fate! But I will arrange the marriage myself, and I must see the person I am marrying first." The matchmaker led a handsome young man, dressed in fine attire, for her to see, and said, "This is the young master, who is being considered for the position of junior official." Zhang was overjoyed, and she prepared her clothes and belongings. Before the seven days of mourning were over, she married the young man.

    As they were about to consummate the marriage, suddenly a hideous woman came out of the room with a big stick and shouted, "I am the first wife, the madam. You, where did you come from? How dare you come to my house as a concubine? I will not allow it!" She went straight up and beat Zhang severely. Zhang regretted being deceived by the matchmaker and secretly thought, "This is the same way I treated my concubine. How could I suddenly suffer such cruelty? Is this a form of divine retribution?" She wept silently without making a sound. The guests stepped forward and persuaded the ugly woman to leave, saying, "Let the young master marry today, and we can talk about other matters tomorrow." Then the young men carried the bridal torches and led Zhang into the bedroom.

    418

    甫揭帘，见轩辕生高坐床上，大惊，以为前夫显魂，晕绝于地，哭诉曰：“非我负君，实不得已也。”轩辕生笑摇手曰：“勿怕，勿怕，两嫁还是一嫁。”

    　　抱上床，告以自始至终中马老师之计。张初犹不信，继而大悟，且恨且惭。于是修德改行，卒与某村妇同为贤妻。

    　　○风水客袁文荣公父清崖先生，贫士也。家有高曾未葬，诸叔伯兄弟无任其事者。先生积馆谷金买地营葬，叔伯兄弟又以地不佳，时日不合，将不利某房为辞，咸捉搦之。先生发愤，集房族百余人祭家庙，毕，持香祷于天曰：“苟葬高曾有不利于子孙者，惟我一人是承，与诸房无碍。”众乃不敢言，听其葬。葬三年，而生文荣公。公面纯黑，颈以下白如雪，相传乌龙转世，官至大学士。

    418

    As soon as she lifted the curtain, she saw Xuan Yuan sitting high on the bed, and was greatly startled, thinking it was the spirit of her former husband. She fainted and fell to the ground, crying out, "It is not I who wronged you, indeed it was unavoidable." Xuan Yuan laughed and shook his hand, saying, "Do not fear, do not fear, two marriages are still one marriage."

    He carried her onto the bed and informed her that she had been involved in Master Ma's plan from beginning to end. Zhang Chu still did not believe it, but then suddenly realized it, feeling both anger and shame. She then cultivated her virtue and changed her behavior, eventually marrying a woman from a certain village and becoming a good wife together.

    ○ Feng Shui Guest, Yuan Wenrong's father, Mr. Qingya, was a poor scholar. His family had not yet buried his great-grandfather, and none of his uncles or brothers were willing to take on the task. Mr. Qingya saved money from his teaching to buy land for the burial. His uncles and brothers then argued that the land was not good, the timing and day were not auspicious, and it would be unfavorable for a certain branch of the family, and they all tried to stop him. Mr. Qingya became determined, gathered over a hundred relatives to worship at the family temple, and after the ceremony, held incense and prayed to heaven, saying, "If burying my great-grandfather is harmful to my descendants, I will bear it alone, and it will not affect the other branches of the family." The others then dared not speak, and allowed him to proceed with the burial. Three years after the burial, Yuan Wenrong was born. He had a completely black face, and below his neck was as white as snow. It is said that he was reborn as a black dragon, and later became a university scholar.

    419

    文荣公薨，子陛升将葬公，惑于风水之说。常州有黄某者，阴阳名家也，一时公卿大夫奉之如神。黄性迂怪，又故意狂傲，自高其价，非千金不肯至相府。

    　　既至，则掷碗碎盘，以为不屑食也；折屋裂帐，以为不屑居也。陛升贪其术之神，不得已，曲意事之。

    　　慈溪某侍郎，坟在西山之阳，子孙衰弱，黄说袁买其明堂为葬地。立券勘度毕，从西山归，已二鼓矣。入相府，见堂上烛光大明，上坐文荣公，乌帽绛袍，旁有二僮侍，如平生时，陛升等大骇，皆俯伏。文荣公骂曰：“某侍郎我翰林前辈。汝听黄奴指使，欲夺其地。昔汝祖葬高曾，是何等存心！汝今葬我，是何等存心？”某不敢答。公又怒睨黄，叱曰：“贼奴！以富贵利达之说诱人财，坏人心术，比娼优媚人取财更为下流。”令左右唾其面，二人皆惕息不能声。文荣公立身起，满堂灯烛尽灭，了无所见。

    Explanation

    419

    The Lord Wen Rong passed away, and his son, Ji Sheng, was about to bury him, but he was confused by the teachings of feng shui. In Changzhou, there was a man named Huang, who was a master of yin-yang, and at that time, all the ministers and officials revered him as if he were a deity. Huang was eccentric and strange, and he deliberately acted arrogantly, valuing himself highly, and would not come to the prime minister's mansion unless paid a thousand gold pieces.

    After arriving, he threw bowls and broke plates, saying it was beneath him to eat; he tore down houses and split up tents, saying it was beneath him to live. Ji Sheng coveted his magical skills and, unable to refuse, treated him with great deference.

    A certain Attendant of the Left in Cixi had his tomb on the sunny side of West Mountain, and his descendants were weak and declining. Huang persuaded Yuan to buy the area for his own burial. After signing the contract and measuring the land, they returned from West Mountain, and it was already the second drumbeat of the night. They entered the prime minister's mansion, where they saw a bright light from the candles on the main hall, with Lord Wen Rong seated above, wearing a black hat and a red robe, flanked by two attendants, just as he had been in life. Ji Sheng and the others were greatly frightened and all prostrated themselves. Lord Wen Rong scolded, saying, "That Attendant of the Left was my senior colleague in the Hanlin Academy. You listen to the commands of this Huang slave, wanting to take his land. In the past, when your ancestor buried your great-grandparents, what kind of heart did they have? Now you want to bury me, what kind of heart do you have?" The attendant dared not answer. The lord then angrily glared at Huang and shouted, "Thief! Using the teachings of wealth, power, and success to lure people's money and corrupt their morals is even more despicable than a prostitute or entertainer using charm to gain wealth." He ordered the attendants to spit on Huang's face, and both men were so terrified that they could not speak. As Lord Wen Rong stood up, all the lights and candles in the hall went out, and nothing could be seen.

    420

    次日，陛升面色如土，焚所立券，还地于某侍郎家。黄受唾处，满身白蚁，缘领啮襟，拂之不去，久乃悉变为虱。终黄之世，坐卧处虱皆成把。

    　　○吕兆鬣吕公兆鬣，绍兴人，以进士为陕西韩城令。严冬友侍读与交好，闲话间问：“公名兆鬣，义实何取？”吕曰：“我前生乃北通州陈氏家马也，花白色，鬣长三尺馀，陈氏畜我有恩。一日者，我在厩中闻陈氏妻生产，三日胎不得下，其戚某曰：”此难产之胎，必得某稳婆方能下之；可惜住某村，隔此三十里，一时难致，奈何？‘又一戚曰：“遣奴骑长鬣马去，立请可来。’言毕，果一苍头奴来骑我。

    Explanation

    420

    The next day,陛升's face turned ashen, and he burned the contract he had drawn up, returning the land to the home of a certain Attendant Official. Where Huang was spat upon, white ants crawled all over him, climbing up his collar and gnawing at his sleeves, and they could not be brushed away. After a long time, they all turned into lice. Until the end of Huang's life, lice gathered in handfuls wherever he sat or lay.

    ○吕兆鬣
    吕公兆鬣, a native of Shaoxing, became the magistrate of Hancheng in Shaanxi through the imperial examination. Yan Dongyou, a Reader in the imperial library, was his close friend. One day, during their idle conversation, Yan asked, "Your name is兆鬣, what is the significance of it?"吕 said, "In my previous life, I was a horse belonging to the Chen family in Beipingzhou. I was a flower-white horse with a mane over three feet long. The Chen family treated me with kindness. One day, while I was in the stable, I heard that Chen's wife was giving birth, but the child could not be delivered after three days. One of her relatives said, 'This is a difficult delivery, only a certain midwife can deliver the baby; it's a pity that she lives in a certain village, thirty miles away, and it's hard to get her here at once, what shall we do?' Another relative said, 'Send a servant to ride the long-maned horse and ask her to come immediately.' After he finished speaking, a young servant came and rode me."

    421

    我自念平日食主人刍豆，今主母有急，是我报恩时，即奋鬣行。遇一涧绝险，两崖相隔丈许，纡其途，原可缓到，而一时救主心切，遂腾身跃起，跌入深崖中，骨折而死。苍头以抱我背故，不触峰崖，转得不死。我死后，登时见白须翁引我至一衙门，见乌纱神上坐，曰：”此马有良心，在人且难得，而况畜乎！‘差役书一牒，若古篆文，缚置我蹄上，曰：“押送他一好处。’遂冉冉而升，不觉已入轮回，为绍兴吕氏家儿。周岁后，头上发犹分两处，如马鬣然，故名兆鬣也。”

    Explanation

    421

    I reflect on the fact that in the past I was fed the owner's straw and beans, and now that the mistress is in distress, this is my time to repay the favor. I immediately charge forward. I encounter a perilous ravine, with cliffs on either side separated by a distance of several feet. Taking a winding path would have allowed me to arrive slowly, but in my urgency to save my master, I leapt and fell into the deep ravine, breaking my bones and dying. The servant, having held me on his back, did not touch the peaks and cliffs and thus survived. After my death, I immediately saw an old man with white hair leading me to a hall, where a black-hatted god sat above. He said, "This horse has a sense of duty, which is rare even among humans, let alone animals!" An attendant wrote a document, as if it were ancient seal script, and bound it to my hoof, saying, "Send him to a good place." I then slowly ascended, unaware that I had entered the cycle of rebirth, becoming a child of the Lu family in Shaoxing. After a year, his hair still split into two parts, like a horse's mane, and thus he was named Zhao Lie.

    422

    ○张又华安庆生员陈庶宁，就馆于淮宁。重九登高，出南门，过一墓，若有青烟起者。

    　　谛视之，觉冷风吹来，毛骨作噤。归馆中。

    　　夜梦至僧舍，明窗净几，竹木萧然。东壁上松江笺一小幅，上有诗，题是《牡丹》，首句云“东风吹出一枝红”，意不以为佳，视纸尾，署“张又华”三字。正把玩间，有推门入者：瞪眼而红鼻，身甚矮，年四十馀，曰：“我即张又华也。汝在此读我诗，何以有轻我之意？”陈曰：“不敢。”解释良久。红鼻者自指其面曰：“汝道我人耶，鬼耶？”陈曰：“君来有冷气，殆鬼也。”曰：“汝以为我是善鬼耶，恶鬼耶？”陈曰：“能咏诗，当是善鬼。”红鼻者曰：“不然，我恶鬼也。”即前攫之，冷气愈甚，如一团冰沁入心坎中。陈避竹榻旁，鬼抱持之，以手掐其外肾，痛不可忍，大惊而醒，肾囊已肿如斗大矣。从此寒热往来，医不能治，遂卒馆中。

    Explanation

    422

    Zhang Youhua, a scholar from Anqing, was residing in a guesthouse in Huaining. On the Double Ninth Festival, he ascended a high place, leaving through the south gate and passing by a tomb where he noticed what seemed to be a greenish smoke rising.

    Upon closer inspection, he felt a cold wind blowing, causing his hair to stand on end and his bones to freeze. He returned to the guesthouse.

    That night, he dreamed of arriving at a Buddhist temple, where the windows were bright and the desks clean, with the sound of bamboo and wood rustling softly. On the east wall hung a small piece of high-quality paper from Songjiang, bearing a poem titled "Peony." The first line read, "The east wind blows out a red branch," which he did not find particularly impressive. Looking at the bottom of the paper, he saw the name "Zhang Youhua" written in three characters. As he was admiring the poem, a figure pushed open the door: a short man with wide-open eyes and a red nose, around forty years old, who said, "I am Zhang Youhua. Why do you read my poem with such disdain?" Chen replied, "I dare not." They explained the situation for a long time. The red-nosed man pointed to his face and asked, "Do you think I am a person or a ghost?" Chen said, "You come with a cold aura, you must be a ghost." The man asked, "Do you think I am a good ghost or a bad one?" Chen replied, "Since you can recite poetry, you must be a good ghost." The red-nosed man said, "No, I am a bad ghost." He then rushed forward and grabbed Chen, the cold aura intensifying as if a lump of ice were being pressed into his chest. Chen hid beside a bamboo bed, but the ghost held him tightly and pinched his external genitalia, causing unbearable pain. He woke up in shock, and his scrotum was swollen to the size of a large bowl. From then on, he suffered from alternating chills and fever, and despite medical treatment, he eventually died in the guesthouse.

    423

    淮宁令为之殡殓，义甚笃，然心终疑中何冤谴，偶问邑中老吏：“汝知此间有张又华乎？”曰：“此安庆府承发科吏书也，死已二年。平生罪恶多端，而好作歪诗，某曾认识之：赤红鼻，短身材。死，葬在南门外。”即陈所吹冷风处也。

    Explanation

    423

    The magistrate of Huainan provided burial and mourning rites for someone, showing great righteousness, but his heart always suspected some injustice or divine retribution. He happened to ask an old official in the county, "Do you know if there is a person named Zhang Youhua here?" The official replied, "He was a clerk in the Chéngfā Department of Anqing Prefecture. He has been dead for two years. He committed many sins in his lifetime, and he liked to write bad or improper poetry. I once knew him: he had a red nose and short stature. He died and was buried outside the south gate." This is exactly where he claimed to have blown cold wind.

    424

    ○官癖相传南阳府有明季太守某殁于署中，自后其灵不散，每至黎明发点时，即乌纱束带上堂南向坐，有吏役叩头，犹能颔之作受拜状。日光大明，始不复见。雍正间，太守乔公到任，闻其事，笑曰：“此有官癖者也，身虽死，不自知其死故耳。我当有以晓之。”乃未黎明即朝衣冠，先上堂南向坐。至发点时，乌纱者远远来，见堂上已有人占坐，不觉趑趄不前，长吁一声而逝。自此怪绝。

    　　○铸文局句容杨琼芳，康熙某科解元也。场中题是“譬如为山”一节，出场后，觉通篇得意，而中二股有数语未惬。夜梦至文昌殿中，帝君上坐，旁列炉灶甚多，火光赫然。杨问：“何为？”旁判官长须者笑曰：“向例：场屋文章，必在此用丹炉鼓铸。或不甚佳者，必加炭之锻炼之，使其完美，方进呈上帝。”杨急向炉中取观，则己所作场屋文也，所不惬意处业已改铸好矣，字字皆有金光，乃苦记之。

    Explanation

    424

    ○官癖相传南阳府有明季太守某殁于署中，自后其灵不散，每至黎明发点时，即乌纱束带上堂南向坐，有吏役叩头，犹能颔之作受拜状。日光大明，始不复见。雍正间，太守乔公到任，闻其事，笑曰：“此有官癖者也，身虽死，不自知其死故耳。我当有以晓之。”乃未黎明即朝衣冠，先上堂南向坐。至发点时，乌纱者远远来，见堂上已有人占坐，不觉趑趄不前，长吁一声而逝。自此怪绝。

    Translation:

    There was a story about an official from the late Ming Dynasty who died in his office in Nanyang Prefecture. After his death, his spirit did not disperse. Every morning at dawn, when the official duties began, he would appear wearing a black cap and tied with a sash, sitting facing south on the hall. When the clerks and officials bowed to him, he would nod in acknowledgment of their bows. Only when the sunlight became bright would he disappear. During the Yongzheng era, Governor Qiao took office and heard of this incident. He laughed and said, "This is someone with an obsession for office; even though he is dead, he does not know he is dead. I will teach him a lesson." So, before dawn, he dressed in his official robes and sat on the hall facing south. When the official duties began, the ghost approached from afar and saw that someone was already occupying the seat. He hesitated and then let out a long sigh and disappeared. Since then, the strange occurrences ceased.

    　　○铸文局句容杨琼芳，康熙某科解元也。场中题是“譬如为山”一节，出场后，觉通篇得意，而中二股有数语未惬。夜梦至文昌殿中，帝君上坐，旁列炉灶甚多，火光赫然。杨问：“何为？”旁判官长须者笑曰：“向例：场屋文章，必在此用丹炉鼓铸。或不甚佳者，必加炭之锻炼之，使其完美，方进呈上帝。”杨急向炉中取观，则己所作场屋文也，所不惬意处业已改铸好矣，字字皆有金光，乃苦记之。

    Translation:

    In the casting bureau in Jurong, there was Yang Qiongfang, the first-place winner in a certain imperial examination during the Kangxi era. The topic for the examination was a section from the Analects of Confucius titled "Like Building a Mountain." After completing the exam, he felt satisfied with his entire essay but found a few sentences in the middle that did not meet his standards. That night, he dreamed that he entered the Wen Chang Hall, where the god of literature was seated above, and many furnaces were lined up beside him, emitting bright flames. Yang asked, "What is this?" A long-bearded judge smiled and said, "By tradition, essays written in the examination hall must be refined here using a magical furnace. If they are not very good, they are further refined with charcoal until they are perfect, and then presented to the Heavenly Emperor." Yang quickly looked into the furnace and saw his own examination essay. The parts that did not satisfy him had already been improved, and every word was glowing with golden light. He then painfully committed the revised text to memory.

    425

    一惊而醒，意转不乐，以为此心切故耳，安得场中文如梦中文耶！

    　　未几，贡院中火起，烧试卷二十七本，监临官按字号命举子入场重录原文。

    　　杨入场，照依梦中火炉上改铸文录之，遂中第一。

    　　○染坊椎华亭民陈某，有一妻一妾，妻无子而妾生子，妻妒之，伺妾出外，暗投其子于河。邻有开染坊妇在河中椎衣，见小儿泛泛然随流来，哀而救之。抱儿入室，哺以乳粥，忘其敲衣之椎尚在河也。陈妻虽沉儿，犹恐儿不死，复往河边察视，不见儿，但见椎浮在水，笑曰：“吾洗衣正少此物。”遂取归，悬之床侧。

    　　亡何，有偷儿入室，攫其被，陈妻惊喊。偷儿急取床边椎击之，正中脑门，浆溃而死。陈氏旦报官，取验凶器，乃天生号染坊椎也。拘染坊人讯之，其妻备述抱儿弃椎之原委，官乃取其儿还陈氏，而另缉正凶。

    Explanation

    425

    One moment, I will provide the final translation.

    426

    ○血见愁吴文学耀延，少游京师，寓徽州会馆。馆中前厅三楹最宏敞；旁有东、西厢，亦颇洁净；最后数椽，多栽树木。有李守备者，先占前厅，吴因所带人少，住东厢中。守备悬刀柱间，刀突然出鞘，吴惊起视刀。守备曰：“我曾挂此刀出征西藏，血人甚多，颇有神灵。每出鞘，必有事，今宜祭之。”呼其仆杀鸡取血买烧酒，洒刀而祭。

    　　日正午，吴望见后屋有蓝色衣者逾墙入，心疑白撞贼，往搜，无人。吴惭眼花，笑曰：“我年末四十，而视茫茫耶？”须叟，有乡试客范某携行李及其奴从大门入，曰：“我亦徽州人，到此觅栖息所。”吴引至后房，曰：“此处甚佳，但墙低，外即市街，虑有贼匪，夜宜慎之。”范视守备刀笑曰：“借公刀防贼。”

    Explanation

    426

    Blood-Seeing-Chaos Wu Scholar Yao Yan, when young, traveled to the capital and stayed at the Anhui Guildhall. The main hall of the guildhall, with its three bays, was the most spacious and grand; beside it were the eastern and western wings, which were also clean and tidy; at the back were several small rooms, filled with trees. There was a Captain Li who had already occupied the main hall. Since Wu was traveling with a small entourage, he stayed in the eastern wing. The captain hung a sword between the pillars, and suddenly, the sword unsheathed itself. Wu was startled and got up to look at the sword. The captain said, "I once hung this sword when I went to campaign in Tibet, and it killed many people. It has a lot of spiritual power. Every time it unsheathes, something is bound to happen. Now it is appropriate to offer a sacrifice to it." He called his servant to kill a chicken, take its blood, and buy some rice wine, and they sprinkled the blood on the sword and offered a sacrifice.

    At noon, Wu saw someone wearing blue clothes climb over the wall into the back room. He suspected a thief and went to search, but found no one. Wu felt ashamed and dizzy, and said with a smile, "I am not yet forty, and my vision is already dim?" A moment later, there was a guest named Fan, who came in through the main gate with his luggage and servant. He said, "I am also from Anhui, and came here to find a place to stay." Wu led him to the back room and said, "This place is very good, but the wall is low, and outside is the market street. Be cautious of thieves at night." Fan looked at the captain's sword and said with a smile, "Let me borrow your sword to guard against thieves."

    427

    守备解与之。乘烛而寝，未二鼓，范见墙外一蓝衣人开窗入。范呼奴起，奴所见同，遂拔刀砍之，似有格斗者。奴尽力挥刀，良久，觉背后有抱其腰而摇手者曰：“是我也，勿斫！勿斫！”声似主人。奴急放刀回顾，烛光中，范已浑身血流，奄然仆地矣。

    　　吴与守备闻呼号声，往视之，得其故，大骇，曰：“奴杀主人，律应凌迟。

    　　范奴以救主之故，而为鬼所弄，奈何？盍趁其主人之未死，取亲笔为信，以宽奴罪。“急取纸笔与范。范忍痛书”奴误伤“，三字未毕，而血流不止。吴之苍头某曰：”墙下有草名‘鬼见愁’，何不采傅之？“如其言，范血渐止，竟得不死。吴与守备念同乡之情，共捐费助其还乡。

    Explanation

    427

    The guard handed it to him. He slept with the candles lit, before the second drumbeat, when Fan saw a blue-clothed person outside the wall opening the window and entering. Fan called out to his servant to wake up, and the servant saw the same thing. They then drew their swords and struck, it seemed as if there was a fight. The servant used all their strength to swing the sword, for a long time, when they felt someone hugging their waist from behind and shaking their hand, saying, "It's me, don't strike! Don't strike!" The voice sounded like that of the master. The servant quickly put down the sword and looked back, in the candlelight, Fan was covered in blood and collapsed on the ground.

    Wu and the guard heard the cries and went to see what had happened, finding out what had occurred, they were greatly alarmed, saying, "The servant killed the master, according to the law, should be executed by slicing."

    Fan's servant did it out of a desire to save the master, but was tricked by a ghost, what can be done? Why not take advantage of the master not being dead yet, get a personal note as proof, to lessen the servant's punishment." They quickly took paper and ink to give to Fan. Fan endured the pain to write "the servant mistakenly injured," not finished writing three characters, but the blood kept flowing. Wu's servant said, "There is grass under the wall called 'ghost-fear,' why not pick and apply it?" As he said, Fan's blood gradually stopped, and eventually, he did not die. Wu and the guard thought of their hometown feelings, together donated money to help him return home.

    428

    未半月，吴苍头溲于墙下，有大掌批其颊曰：“我自报冤，与汝何干，而卖弄‘血见愁’耶！”视之。即蓝衣人也。

    　　○龙阵风乾隆辛酉秋，海风拔木，海滨人见龙斗空中。广陵城内外风过处，民间窗棂帘箔及所晒衣物吹上半天。有宴客者，八盘十六碟随风而去，少顷，落于数十里外李姓家，肴果摆没，丝毫不动。尤奇者，南街上清白流芳牌楼之左，一妇人沐浴后簪花傅粉，抱一孩移竹榻坐于门外，被风吹起，冉冉而升，万目观望，如虎丘泥偶一座，少顷，没入云中。明日，妇人至自邵伯镇。镇去城四十馀里，安然无恙。云：“初上时，耳听风响甚怕。愈上愈凉爽。俯视城市，但见云雾，不知高低。落地时，亦徐徐而坠，稳如乘舆。但心中茫然耳。”

    Explanation

    428

    Within a few days, a low-ranking servant of Wu urinated at the base of a wall, and a large hand slapped his cheek, saying, "I am avenging myself; what business is it of yours, and why are you flaunting 'blood-seeing-grief'?" He looked up, and it was the man in blue clothes.

    ○ Dragon Array Wind, Autumn of Xin You during the Qianlong era, a sea wind uprooted trees, and people by the sea saw dragons fighting in the sky. In the city of Guangling, the wind passed through, and the windows, doors, and clothes hung out to dry were blown up into the sky. A host preparing a banquet saw eight plates and sixteen dishes carried away by the wind. Shortly after, they fell into the home of someone named Li dozens of miles away, where the food and fruits were neatly arranged, not a single thing moved. More strangely, on the left side of the famous archway in the southern street, a woman who had bathed, adorned herself with flowers, and applied makeup, holding a child, moved a bamboo bed to sit outside the door. She was blown up by the wind, slowly rising, and everyone watched, like a clay figure at Tiger Hill. Shortly after, she disappeared into the clouds. The next day, the woman came from Shao Bo Town, which is over forty miles from the city, and she was safe and sound. She said, "At first, I heard the wind blowing and was very afraid. The higher I went, the cooler it became. Looking down at the city, I only saw clouds and mist, and did not know how high or low I was. When I landed, I also descended slowly, as steady as a carriage. But my heart was still confused."

    429

    ○彭杨记异彭兆麟，掖县人，同邑增广生杨继庵，其姑丈也。兆麟业儒，年二十馀，病卒。越数年，杨亦卒。

    　　后有密高人胡邦翰者，与彭、杨素未谋面，因其仲兄久客于辽，泛海往寻，游学至兆麟馆，留与同居，凡两月馀。治装欲归，谓兆麟曰：“今归将赴郡应试，可为君作寄书邮。”兆麟曰：“昨已将家书付便羽矣，如至掖县，第代传一口信可也。”及将行，又曰：“去此百馀里，余姑丈杨庵在彼设帐授徒，烦便道代为致候。”胡因往，又一见继庵焉。

    　　比赴郡试至彭家，言其与兆麟及继庵相见颠末，其家人因二人死已二十年，以胡为妄。胡曰：“彼曾为予言，巷口关帝庙壁有手迹遗书，试往庙中。”发壁阅之，与辽馆所书笔迹不殊。复忆别时曾告以其妻及二女乳名。兆麟妻贾氏年已四十馀，二女已嫁，非亲党无知者，乃与胡言一一相符，其家方信，而胡亦始知其所遇之皆鬼也。胡是年入泮，未几亦亡。

    Explanation

    429

    Translation:

    The Strange Tale of Peng and Yang

    Peng Zhaolin was from Yexian, and Yang Jia'an, a student who had passed a preliminary examination, was his father-in-law. Peng Zhaolin studied Confucian classics, and when he was in his twenties, he died of illness. Several years later, Yang Jia'an also passed away.

    Later, there was a hermit named Hu Banghan, who had never met Peng or Yang before. Because his older brother had been a long-term resident in Liaodong, Hu sailed across the sea to look for him, traveling to study and eventually arriving at Peng Zhaolin's residence, where he stayed with him for more than two months. As Hu prepared to return home, he told Peng Zhaolin, "I am now returning to take the county examination, and I can send a letter for you." Peng Zhaolin replied, "I have already entrusted a messenger with my family letter. If you go to Yexian, just deliver a brief message for me." As Hu was about to leave, he added, "There is another hundred miles from here, and my father-in-law, Yang Jia'an, is setting up a school to teach students there. Please deliver my greetings on your way." Hu went on to visit Yang Jia'an again.

    When Hu arrived at Peng's home to take the county examination, he recounted the details of his encounters with Peng Zhaolin and Yang Jia'an. Peng's family, who had been dead for twenty years, thought Hu was delusional. Hu said, "They once told me that there was a handwritten note on the wall of the Guan Yu temple at the alley entrance. Try going to the temple." Upon opening the wall and reading the note, the handwriting matched that of the note left in the Liaodong residence. Hu then recalled that he had been told the names of Peng Zhaolin's wife and two daughters. Peng Zhaolin's wife, Jia Shi, was already in her forties, and his two daughters were married. No one else in the family knew these details, but they all matched what Hu had said, and the family finally believed him. Hu also realized that he had encountered ghosts. That year, Hu passed the examination to become a student at a local school, but he died shortly thereafter.

    430

    后数年，又有自辽东来者，兆麟寄一马并其死时所服衣来，其家愈惊，绝之不受。先是兆麟疾革，谓其家曰：“我死勿殓，可得复活。”既死，家人以为乱命，置不论，竟殓焉。葬三日，家人见其墓穿一孔，如有物自内出者。其年高密某姓不知兆麟之已死，延兆麟于家，教其幼子。历八九载，从不言归。后某子将赴郡应试，强与之俱。抵郡城马邑地方，谓某子曰：“此处有葭莩亲，予就便往视之。汝先行，至郭外候我。”某子至所约处，久待不至，日渐暮，投宿他所。

    　　旦至师家，口称弟子某。其家犹谓其生时曾拜门墙者。询之，方知事在死后，相与骇怪，莫知所以。其徒涕零而别。岂兆麟之客辽东，即从此而去耶！

    Explanation

    430

    Several years later, there were again those who came from Liao Dong, and Zhao Lin sent a horse along with the clothes he wore at the time of his death. His family became even more surprised and refused to accept the items. Previously, Zhao Lin's illness was critical, and he said to his family, "Do not bury me when I die; I can be revived." After he died, his family thought it was a foolish command and ignored it, finally burying him. Three days after the burial, the family saw a hole in the grave, as if something was coming out from inside. That year, a certain surname from Gao Mi did not know that Zhao Lin had already died and invited Zhao Lin to their home to teach their young son. For eight or nine years, he never mentioned returning. Later, the young man was about to take an examination in the county and forced him to go with him. They arrived at the county city of Ma Yi, and Zhao Lin told the young man, "There are distant relatives here; I will take the opportunity to visit them. You go ahead, and wait for me outside the city gate." The young man arrived at the agreed place, waited for a long time without him arriving, the day grew late, and he found lodging elsewhere.

    The next morning, he arrived at the teacher's house and introduced himself as a disciple. The family still thought he was someone who had studied under the teacher in life. Upon inquiry, they learned that the events took place after Zhao Lin's death, and they were all shocked and bewildered, not knowing what to make of it. His disciple wept and parted ways. Could it be that Zhao Lin's journey to Liao Dong was the reason for his departure?

    431

    此乾隆二十八年事，贵池令林君梦鲤所言。林，掖人也。

    　　○冤鬼戏台告状乾隆年间，广东三水县前搭台演戏。一日，演《包孝肃断乌盆》。净方扮孝肃上台坐，见有披发带伤人跪台间作申冤状，净惊起避之，台下人相与哗然，其声达于县署。县令某着役查问，净以所见对。县令传净至，嘱净：“仍如前装上台，如再有所见，可引至县堂。”

    　　净领命行事，其鬼果又现。净云：“我系伪作龙图，不若我带汝赴县堂，求官申冤。”鬼首肯之。净起，鬼随之至堂。令询净：“鬼何在？”净答：“鬼已跪墀下。”令大声唤之，毫无见闻。令怒，欲责净。净见鬼起立外走，以手作招势。净禀令，令即着净同皂役二名尾之，视往何处灭，即志其处。净随鬼野行数里，见入一冢中：冢乃邑中富室王监生葬母处。净与皂将竹枝插地志之，回县覆令。

    Explanation

    431

    This event took place in the 28th year of the Qianlong Emperor's reign, as reported by Lin Mengli, the magistrate of Guichi County. Lin is from Yecheng.

    The Ghost of a Wronged Person Appeals on the Stage
    During the Qianlong era, a stage was set up in Sanshui County, Guangdong Province, for performances. One day, a play titled "Bao Xiaosu and the Black Earthenware Pot" was being performed. The actor playing Bao Xiaosu had just taken his place on stage when he saw a ghost with disheveled hair and injuries kneeling in the middle of the stage, pleading for justice. The actor was startled and backed away. The audience was shocked and made a commotion, and the noise was heard by the county magistrate. The magistrate sent someone to investigate, and the actor reported what he had seen. The magistrate then instructed the actor to dress as before and go back on stage, and if anything else was seen, to bring it to the magistrate's office.

    The actor followed orders and went back on stage. The ghost appeared again. The actor said, "I am just pretending to be Bao Xiaosu. Let me take you to the magistrate's office to seek justice." The ghost nodded in agreement. The actor stood up, and the ghost followed him to the magistrate's office. The magistrate asked the actor, and the actor replied that the ghost had knelt in the courtyard. The magistrate shouted loudly, but nothing was seen. The magistrate became angry and wanted to punish the actor. The actor saw the ghost stand up and walk away, gesturing with its hand. The actor reported to the magistrate, who then ordered the actor to follow with two constables, to see where the ghost disappeared and mark the spot. The actor followed the ghost for several miles in the wilderness and saw the ghost enter a tomb. The tomb belonged to a wealthy family in the county, where Wang Jiansheng buried his mother. The actor and the constables marked the spot with a bamboo stick and returned to the county to report to the magistrate.

    432

    令乘舆往观，传王监生严讯。监生不认，请开墓以明己冤。令从之。至墓，开未二三尺，即见一尸，颜色如生。令大喜，问监生。监生呼冤，云：“其时送葬人数百，共观下土，并无此尸。即有此尸，必不能尽掩众口，数年来何默默无闻，必待此净方白耶？”令韪其言，复问：“汝视封土毕归家否？”监生曰：“视母棺下土后即返家，以后事皆土工为之。”令笑曰：“得之矣。速唤众土工来！”见其状貌凶恶，喝曰：“汝等杀人事发觉矣，毋庸再隐！”众土工大骇，叩头曰：“王监生归家后，某等皆歇茅蓬下，有孤客负囊来乞火，一伙伴觉其囊中有银，与众共谋杀而瓜分之，即举铁锄碎其首，埋王母棺上，加土填之，竟夜而成冢。王监生喜其速成，复厚赏之，并无知者。”令乃尽致之法。

    Explanation

    432

    The official ordered the carriage to go and inspect the matter, and summoned Wang Jiansheng for a strict interrogation. Wang Jiansheng denied the accusations and requested to open the tomb to prove his own innocence. The official agreed. Upon arriving at the tomb, they had not dug more than two or three feet when they found a body, whose appearance was as if it were still alive. The official was greatly pleased and asked Wang Jiansheng about the situation. Wang Jiansheng cried out for injustice, saying, "At that time, hundreds of people were attending the funeral and watched as the coffin was lowered into the ground. There was no such body present. If there were such a body, it would not have been able to completely cover up the truth. Why has this remained unknown for several years, and only now been revealed?" The official agreed with Wang Jiansheng's words and asked again, "Did you return home after the earth was piled over your mother's coffin?" Wang Jiansheng replied, "I watched as my mother's coffin was lowered into the ground and then immediately returned home. All subsequent matters were handled by the laborers." The official laughed and said, "I have it now. Quickly call the laborers here!" Seeing their fierce and evil appearance, he shouted, "Your murder has been discovered! There is no need to hide it anymore!" The laborers were greatly frightened and knelt down, saying, "After Wang Jiansheng returned home, we all rested under a thatched hut. A lone traveler carrying a bag came and asked for fire. One of our companions noticed that the traveler's bag contained silver, and we conspired to kill him and divided the silver among ourselves. We then lifted an iron spade and crushed his head, buried the body on top of Wang's mother's coffin, added soil to fill the area, and completed the mound through the night. Wang Jiansheng was pleased with the quick completion and rewarded us generously. No one knew about the crime." The official then punished them all according to the law.

    433

    相传众工埋尸时自夸云：“此事难明白，如要得申冤，除非龙图再世。”鬼闻此言，故籍净扮龙图时，便来申冤云。

    　　○奇鬼眼生背上费密，字此度，四川布衣，有“大江流汉水，孤艇接残春”之句，为阮亭尚书所称，荐与杨将军名展者。从征四川，过成都，寓察院楼中。人相传此楼有怪，杨与李副将俱不听，拉费同宿。费不能无疑，张灯按剑，端坐帐中。

    Explanation

    433

    It is said that when the workers buried the body, they boasted saying, "This matter is difficult to understand. If one wants to seek justice, unless Judge Song is reborn." The ghost heard this and, when it was reincarnated as Judge Song, came to seek justice saying, "This matter is difficult to understand. If one wants to seek justice, unless Judge Song is reborn."

    As for the ghost with the strange eye on its back, its name was Fei Mi, courtesy name Zhi Du, a commoner from Sichuan. He wrote the line "The great river flows into the Han River, a solitary boat meets the remnants of spring," which was praised by the Grand Secretary Ruan Ting. Ruan Ting recommended him to General Yang named Zhan. Fei Mi followed Yang to campaign in Sichuan and passed through Chengdu, where he stayed in the office building of the Inspectorate. People said that this building was haunted, but both Yang and his deputy general Li did not believe it, and they insisted on staying with Fei Mi. Fei Mi could not help but have doubts, so he lit a lamp, held his sword, and sat upright in his tent.

    434

    三鼓后，楼下橐橐有声，一怪蹑梯而上。灯下视之：有头面无眉目，如枯柴一段，直立帐前。费拔剑斫之，怪退缩数步，转身而走，有一眼竖生背上，长尺许，金光射人。渐行至杨将军卧所，揭其帐，转背放光射之。忽见将军两鼻孔中，亦有白气二条，与怪所吐之光相为抵拒。白气愈大，则金光愈小，旋滚至楼下而灭。杨将军终不知也。未几，又闻梯响，怪仍上楼，趋李副将所。副将方熟睡，鼾声如雷。费以为彼更勇猛，尤可无虞，忽闻大叫一声，视之，七窍流血死矣。

    　　●卷十二○挂周仓刀上绍兴钱二相公，学神仙炼气之术，能顶门出元神遍历十洲三岛。所遇诸魔，不一而足：或恶状狰狞，或妖饶艳冶。钱俱不为动，如是者十年。一日，诸魔聚而谋曰：“再迟一月逢甲子日，钱某大道成矣，我辈作速下手。”众以为然，趁其打坐时，牵抱手足，放大瓮中，压之云门山脚下。是夕，钱家失去二相公，遍寻无踪，以为真仙去矣。

    Explanation

    434

    After the third drumbeat, there was a soft, rhythmic sound coming from the楼下, and a strange creature began to climb the ladder. Under the light, the creature was seen to have a head and face but no eyebrows or eyes, resembling a piece of dried wood, standing upright in front of the tent. The protagonist drew his sword and struck at the creature, which retreated several steps and turned to flee. On its back, there was an eye growing vertically, about a foot long, emitting a golden light that shone on people. The creature gradually moved toward the place where General Yang was sleeping, pulled back the tent, and turned its back, emitting light that shone at General Yang. Suddenly, two white vapors emerged from General Yang’s nostrils, clashing with the creature’s golden light. As the white vapors grew larger, the golden light diminished, and it rolled down to the楼下 and disappeared. General Yang remained unaware of the events. Shortly after, the sound of the ladder was heard again, and the creature climbed the ladder once more, heading toward the place where Deputy General Li was. Deputy General Li was sleeping soundly, his snoring like thunder. The protagonist thought that Deputy General Li was more courageous and thus not in danger. Suddenly, a scream was heard, and upon looking, it was discovered that Deputy General Li was dead, with blood flowing from all his orifices.

    Chapter Twelve: Hanging Zhou Cang’s Sword

    In Shaoxing, there was a man named Qian, who studied the techniques of immortality and alchemy. He was capable of emitting his primordial spirit from his topknot and traversing the Ten Continents and Three Islands. During his journey, he encountered various demons, each with different appearances: some were grotesquely fierce, while others were妖娆艳冶. Qian remained unmoved by any of them, and this continued for ten years. One day, the demons gathered and plotted, saying, “If we delay another month until the day of Jiazi, Qian will achieve the Great Way, and we must act quickly.” They agreed, and taking advantage of the time when Qian was seated in meditation, they grabbed his hands and feet, placed him in a large瓮, and pressed him down at the foot of Yumen Mountain. That night, Qian’s family lost their son, and despite a thorough search, there was no trace of him. They believed that their son had truly become an immortal and departed.

    435

    半年后，月明中见二相公坐花园高树上大呼求救，乃取梯扶下。问其故，自言：“为魔所窘，幸平生服气有术，故不致冻馁而死。”问：“何以得归？”曰：“某月日，我在瓮中，有红云一道，伏魔大帝从西南来。我大声呼冤，且诉诸魔恶状。帝君曰：”作祟诸魔，诚属可恶，然汝不顺天地阴阳自生自灭之理，妄想矫揉造作，希图不死，是逆天而行，亦有不合。‘顾谓一将曰：“周仓，汝送他还家。’周将军唯唯。周长丈余，所持刀亦长丈余，取红绳缚我刀上，挂此树顶而去。我亦不料即我家园树也。”

    Explanation

    435

    Half a year later, under the bright moon, I saw the young man sitting on a high tree in the garden, shouting for help loudly. I then took a ladder to help him down. When I asked him why he was there, he explained: "I was troubled by demons, but fortunately, I have practiced breath control all my life, so I did not die from cold and hunger." I asked, "How did you get back?" He replied, "On a certain day of a certain month, I was in a jar. A streak of red cloud appeared, and the Emperor who subdues demons came from the southwest. I shouted loudly for injustice and complained about the evil deeds of the demons. The Emperor said, 'The demons causing trouble are truly despicable, but you are going against the natural order of heaven and earth, desiring to live forever, which is against the natural course of life and death.' He then turned and said to a general, 'Zhou Cang, take him back home.' General Zhou replied affirmatively. Zhou is over ten feet tall, and the knife he holds is also over ten feet long. He took a red rope to tie me to the knife, hung me on the top of this tree, and left. I did not expect it to be the tree in my own garden."

    436

    二相公自后随行逐队，饮酒御内，不敢复学神仙术矣。

    　　○驱云使者宣化把总张仁，奉缉私盐，过一古庙，将投宿焉。僧不可，曰：“此中有怪。”

    　　张恃其勇，竟往设帐，吹烛卧。至二鼓，满室尽明。张起怒喝，灯光外移；追之，见神灯万盏，投松下而灭。明早，往探松下，有大石洞。张命里人持锄掘之，得大锦被，中裹一尸：口吐白烟，三目四臂，似僵非僵。张知为怪，聚薪焚之。

    Explanation

    436

    The two princes followed in the procession, drinking alcohol and being in the imperial palace, and dared not learn the art of immortality again.
