    The driver of clouds messenger, Proclamation Captain Zhang Ren, carried out the task of intercepting illegal salt and passed by an ancient temple, where he intended to stay for the night. The monk would not allow it, saying, "There are strange things in this place."

    Relying on his courage, Zhang Ren went ahead and set up camp, lit the candle, and lay down. By the second drum beat, the entire room was lit. Zhang stood up and shouted angrily, and the lights moved outside. He chased after them and saw thousands of divine lamps, which were extinguished under the pine tree.

    The next morning, he went to investigate under the pine tree and found a large stone cave. He ordered his men to take shovels and dig it, and they found a large brocade blanket, wrapped in a body: mouth spouting white smoke, three eyes and four arms, seemingly stiff but not quite. Zhang knew it was a strange thing and gathered firewood to burn it.

    437

    后三日，白昼坐，有美少年盛服而至，曰：“我天上驱云使者，以行雨太多，违上帝令，谪下凡间，藏形石洞中，待限满后，依旧上天。偶于某夜出游，略露神怪，是我不知韬晦，原有不是。然汝烧我原身，亦太狠矣。我现在栖神无所，不得已，借王子晋侍者形躯来与汝索吵。汝作速召道士持诵《灵飞经》四十九日，我之原身犹可从火中完聚。汝本命应做提督一品官，以此事不良，上帝削籍，只可终于把总矣。”张唯唯听命，少年腾空而去。后张果以把总终。

    　　○吾头岂白斫者蒋心馀太史修《南昌府志》，夜梦段将军来拜，见一伟丈夫，兜鍪戎服，叉手不揖，披其颈骂曰：“吾头岂白斫者！”蒋惊醒，知有冤抑。查新志，并无其人；查旧志，有段将军，乃史阁部麾下副将，死于扬州者。急为补入《忠义传》中。

    Explanation

    437

    After three days, sitting in broad daylight, a handsome young man in fine attire arrived and said, "I am the cloud-driving messenger from heaven. Because I rained too much, I violated the decree of the Heavenly Emperor and was demoted to the mortal world. I hid my form in a stone cave and will return to heaven after my term is up. I occasionally went out one night and slightly revealed my supernatural nature. It was my fault for not knowing how to hide my true nature; there was something wrong with me. However, you burned my original form, which was also too harsh. I now have no place to house my spirit, so I had no choice but to borrow the form of Prince Zijun's attendant to come and complain to you. You must quickly summon a Taoist priest to recite the Lingfei Jing for forty-nine days. My original form can still be restored from the fire. Your destiny was to become a first-class supervisor, but because of this matter, the Heavenly Emperor stripped you of your rank, and you can only end up as a captain." Zhang obediently followed the command, and the young man flew away into the sky. Later, Zhang indeed ended up as a captain.

    ○ The head of the white executioner

    Jiang Xinyu, the historian, was compiling the Nanchang Prefecture Gazetteer. At night, he dreamed of General Duan coming to pay respects. He saw a tall and imposing man in a helmet and military uniform, who crossed his hands without bowing and tore open his neck to curse, "Is my head meant to be cut off in vain!" Jiang woke up in shock, realizing there was an injustice. He checked the new gazetteer and found no such person; he checked the old gazetteer and found General Duan, who was a deputy general under General Shi, and died in Yangzhou. He quickly added him to the Loyal and Righteous Biography.

    438

    ○石言吕著，建宁人，读书武夷山北麓古寺中。方昼阴晦，见阶砌上石尽人立。寒风一过，窗纸树叶飞脱着石，粘挂不下，檐瓦亦飞着石上。石皆旋转化为人，窗纸树叶化为衣服，瓦化冠帻，颀然丈夫十馀人，坐踞佛殿间，清淡雅论，娓娓可听。吕怖骇，掩窗而睡。

    　　明日起视，毫无踪迹。午后，石又立如昨。数日以后，竟成泛常，了不为害，吕遂出与接谈。问其姓氏，多复姓，自言皆汉、魏人，有二老者则秦时人也。所谈事，与汉、魏史书所载颇有异同。吕甚以为乐，午食后，静待其来。询以托物幻形之故，不答；问何以不常住寺中，亦不答；但答语曰：“吕君雅士，今夕月明，我共来角武，以广君所未见。”是夜，各携刀剑来，有古兵器，不似戈戟，而不能加名者。就月起舞，或只或双，飘瞥神妙，吕再拜而谢。

    Explanation

    438

    Shi Yan Lü Zhu, a native of Jianning, studied in an ancient temple at the northern foot of Mount Wuyi. During the day, the sky was overcast, and he saw the stones on the steps all stand up like people. As the cold wind passed, the window paper and leaves flew off and stuck to the stones, unable to hang, and the tiles on the eaves also flew onto the stones. The stones all quickly transformed into people, the window paper and leaves turned into clothes, and the tiles turned into hats and headbands. Tall men, about ten of them, sat or lounged in the Buddha hall, engaging in refined and elegant discussions, speaking in a smooth and pleasant manner. Lü was terrified and closed the window to sleep.

    The next morning, when he looked, there was no trace left. In the afternoon, the stones stood up again as they had before. After several days, it became commonplace and no longer caused any harm. Lü then went out to talk with them. He asked them their names, and many had double surnames. They said they were all from the Han and Wei dynasties, and two of the older men were from the Qin dynasty. The things they talked about differed somewhat from what was recorded in Han and Wei historical texts. Lü greatly enjoyed it. After lunch, he waited quietly for them to come. He asked them why they could transform objects into human forms, but they did not answer. He asked why they did not stay in the temple all the time, but they did not answer. They only replied, "Lü is a refined gentleman. Tonight, the moon is bright, and we will come to practice martial arts together, to broaden your horizons." That night, each of them came carrying swords and spears, some of which were ancient weapons that did not resemble modern spears and could not be named. They danced under the moonlight, sometimes alone and sometimes in pairs, their movements swift and divine. Lü bowed twice and thanked them.

    439

    又一日，告吕曰：“我辈与君周旋日久，情不忍别，今夕我辈皆托生海外，完生前未之事，当与君别矣。”吕送出户，从此阒寂，吕凄然如丧良友。取所谈古事，笔之于书，号曰《石言》，欲梓以传世，贫不能办，至今犹藏其子大延处。

    　　○鬼借官衔嫁女新建张雅成秀才，儿时戏以金箔纸制盔甲鸾笄等物，藏小楼上，独制独玩，不以示人。忽有女子年三十余，登楼求制钗钏步摇数十件，许以厚谢。秀才允之，问：“安用此？”曰：“嫁女奁中所需。”张以其戏，不之异也。明日，女来告张曰：“我姓唐，东邻唐某为某官，我欲倩郎君求其门上官衔封条一纸，借同姓以光蓬荜。”张戏写一纸与之。次夕，钗钏数足，女携饼饵数十、钱数百来谢。

    Explanation

    439

    On another day, they told Lü, "We have been interacting with you for a long time, and it is with great reluctance that we part. Tonight, we will all be reborn overseas to complete what we could not do in life, and we must part from you." Lü saw them out to the door, and from then on, there was silence. Lü felt sad as if losing a good friend. He wrote down the ancient stories they had talked about, calling it The Stone's Words, and intended to publish it for future generations, but he was too poor to afford it. It is still kept by his son, Dadian.

    ○ A Ghost Borrows an Official Title to Marry Her Daughter

    In the newly built Zhang Yacheng's study, when he was a child, he played by making armor, headdresses, and other items from gold foil paper, storing them on the small upper floor. He made and played with them alone, without showing them to others. Suddenly, a woman in her thirties came up to the floor and asked him to make dozens of hairpins, bracelets, and head ornaments, offering generous thanks. The scholar agreed, asking, "What are these for?" She replied, "They are needed for my daughter's dowry." Zhang thought it was just a game and did not find it strange. The next day, the woman came to Zhang and said, "My surname is Tang. My neighbor Tang, who holds an official title, has asked me to borrow his official title and seal to honor our humble abode." Zhang jokingly wrote a piece of paper with the title and seal for her. The next evening, she returned with dozens of pastries and hundreds of coins to thank him.

    440

    及旦视之，饼皆土块，钱皆纸钱，方知女子是鬼。

    　　数日后半夜，山中烛光灿烂，鼓乐喧天，村人皆启户遥望，以为人家来卜葬者。近视之，人尽披红插花，是吉礼也。山间万冢，素无居人，好事者欲追视之，相去渐远，惟见灯笼题唐姓某官衔字样，方知鬼亦如人间爱体面而崇势利，异哉！

    　　○雷祖昔有陈姓猎户，畜一犬，有九耳。其犬一耳动则得一兽，两耳动则得两兽，不动则无所得，日以为验。一日，犬九耳齐动，陈喜必大获，急入山。自晨至午，不得一兽。方怅怅间，犬至山凹中大叫，将足爬地，颠其头若招引状。陈疑掘之，得一卵，大如斗，取归置几上。

    　　次早，雷雨大作，电光绕室。陈疑此卵有异，置之庭中。霹雳一声，卵豁然而开，中有一小儿，面目如画。陈大喜，抱归室中，抚之为子。长登进士第，即为本州太守，才干明敏，有善政。至五十七岁，忽肘下生翅，腾空仙去。至今雷州祀曰：“雷祖”。

    Explanation

    440

    By dawn, he looked at it, and found that the cakes were all mud clumps, and the money was all paper money. He then realized that the woman was a ghost.

    A few days later, in the middle of the night, in the mountains, the candlelight was brilliant, and the drums and music were deafening. The villagers all opened their doors and looked far away, thinking that someone had come to choose a burial site. Upon closer inspection, everyone was dressed in red and adorned with flowers; it was a ceremony of good fortune. There are thousands of tombs in the mountains, and there has never been anyone living there. Those who are curious wanted to follow and see; they gradually moved further away; they only saw lanterns with the name 'Tang' and some official titles written on them. He then realized that ghosts, like people, love to show off and respect power and wealth. How strange!

    Once upon a time, there was a hunter named Chen who kept a dog with nine ears. Whenever one ear of the dog moved, he would catch one animal; if two ears moved, he would catch two animals; if none moved, he would catch nothing. Chen was very happy and expected a big catch, so he rushed into the mountains. From morning until noon, he did not catch a single animal. Just as he was feeling disappointed, the dog ran to a depression in the mountain, barked loudly, and pawed the ground as if calling him. Chen suspected that there was something underground and dug it up, finding an egg as large as a winnowing basket. He took it home and placed it on the table.

    The next morning, there was a heavy thunderstorm, and lightning flashed around the room. Chen suspected that the egg was unusual and placed it in the courtyard. A loud thunderclap, and the egg suddenly split open, revealing a small child with a painted face. Chen was very happy, took the child into the house, and raised him as his own son. The child grew up to become a successful candidate for the imperial examinations and was appointed as the governor of the state. He was talented, intelligent, and had good governance. At the age of fifty-seven, he suddenly sprouted wings on his elbows and flew into the sky, becoming a deity. Today, in Leizhou, he is worshipped as the God of Thunder.

    441

    ○镇江某仲某仲，镇江人，兄弟三人。伯无子，仲有子，七岁看上元灯，失去，不知所往。仲闷甚，携资贸易山西，并冀访子耗。去数载未归，飞语谓仲已死。仲妻不之信，乞叔往寻。

    　　伯利仲妻年少可鬻，诡称仲凶耗已真，旅榇将归，劝仲妻改适，仲妻不可，蒙麻素于髻，为夫持服。伯知其志难夺，潜与江西贾人谋，得价百余金，令买仲妻去，戒曰：“个娘子要强取。黑夜命舆来，见素髻者挽之去，速飞棹行也。”

    　　归语其妻，意甚自得。伯故避去，仲妻见伯状，知有变，甫黑即自经于梁，悬梁作声，伯妻闻之奔救，恐虚所卖金也。抱持间，仲妻素髻坠地，伯妻髻亦坠。适贾人轿至，伯妻急走出迎，摸地取髻，误戴素者。贾人见素髻妇，不待分辨，径抢以行。伯归，悔无及，噤不能声。

    Explanation

    441

    A Certain Man from Zhenjiang

    A certain man from Zhenjiang had three brothers. The eldest had no son, while the middle brother had a son who, at the age of seven, went to watch the lanterns during the Lantern Festival and was lost, with no one knowing where he had gone. The middle brother was very depressed and, taking capital with him, went to trade in Shanxi, hoping to find news of his son. After several years, he did not return, and rumors spread that the middle brother had died. The middle brother's wife did not believe these rumors and asked her uncle to go find him.

    The eldest brother saw an opportunity to profit from the young and saleable wife of the middle brother. He falsely claimed that the middle brother had indeed died, and his coffin was about to return. He persuaded the middle brother's wife to remarry, but she refused. She wore mourning attire on her head, adhering to the proper mourning customs for her husband. The eldest brother, knowing that her resolve was difficult to change, secretly conspired with a merchant from Jiangxi. He received a price of over a hundred gold pieces and ordered the merchant to buy the middle brother's wife. He instructed the merchant, "You must forcibly take this woman. Summon a carriage at night, and when you see the woman with the white hair accessory, take her away and quickly row away."

    Returning home, the eldest brother told his wife, feeling very pleased with himself. He deliberately avoided her, and the middle brother's wife, seeing his demeanor, knew something was amiss. Just as it became dark, she hanged herself on the beam, making a sound as she did so. The eldest brother's wife heard the sound and rushed to save her, fearing that the money paid might be for nothing. While holding her, the middle brother's wife's white hair accessory fell to the ground, and the eldest brother's wife's hair accessory also fell. Just then, the merchant's sedan chair arrived. The eldest brother's wife quickly ran out to greet him, picking up the hair accessory from the ground and mistakenly wearing the white one. The merchant saw the woman with the white hair accessory and, without waiting to distinguish her, forcibly took her and went. The eldest brother returned, regretting it too late and being silent and unable to speak.

    442

    仲自晋归，途如厕，见布袱裹五百金在地，心计此必先登厕者所遗，去应不远，盍俟诸。未几，遗金者果至，遂与之。其人感德，分以金，不受；乃邀仲偕行。数日，抵其家，具鸡黍，命一子一女出拜。仲视其子，宛然己子也；问之，良是。盖仲子失去时，为人所卖，遗金者无子，买为己子，十余年矣。仲持之泣下，遗金者曰：“若携子去，我女即许汝子为媳妇。”

    　　仲归，将渡江，见一人落于水，呼救，无应者，群攫其资。仲恻然，亟呼曰：“孰肯救者，我募以金！”救起视之，是季弟也。季承嫂命寻仲，伯并利其死；曩之落水，有挤之者，伯所使也。仲知其情，携弟与子归。入门，伯见之，亡去。

    Explanation

    442

    The man returned from Jin, and on the road, he encountered something unexpected. He saw a cloth bundle containing five hundred pieces of gold lying on the ground. He thought to himself that this must have been left behind by someone who had already used the toilet, and the owner must not be far away. So, he decided to wait for him. Soon enough, the owner of the gold arrived, and the man returned it to him. The owner was deeply grateful and offered to share the gold, but the man refused. Instead, the owner invited the man to travel with him. After several days, they arrived at the owner's home, where a simple but hearty meal of chicken and millet was prepared. The owner then ordered his son and daughter to come out and bow to the man. The man looked at the boy and recognized him as his own son. When he asked, the boy confirmed it was true. It turned out that when the man's son had been lost, he was sold by someone. The owner of the gold had no children and had bought the boy as his own, raising him for over ten years. The man was moved to tears, and the owner said, "If you take your son with you, I will allow my daughter to marry him."

    The man then returned home, and as he was about to cross the river, he saw a person fall into the water and call for help. No one responded, and a group of people were trying to seize the person's belongings. The man felt compassion and immediately called out, "Whoever is willing to save this person, I will reward them with gold!" Someone came forward and rescued the person. Upon seeing the person, the man recognized him as his younger brother. The younger brother had been sent by his sister-in-law to find the man, but the older brother had hoped he would die. It was the older brother who had pushed him into the water. The man knew the truth and took his younger brother and son back home. When they entered the house, the older brother saw them and fled.

    443

    ○银隔世走归原主夏镇属滕县。有蒋翁者，勤俭成家，生一子，失教，长而游荡，家渐落，蒋翁以为忧。有关帝庙陈道士，河南固始人，素与蒋翁善，乃私携五百金嘱道士云：“吾子不肖，谅不能守业，后日必为饿殍。今以此金付汝，我死后，俟其改悔，以此济之。倘终不悛，汝即以此金修庙。”道士应允，藏金瓦罐，上覆破磬，埋殿后，无有知者。

    　　后数月，翁死，子益无忌，家业尽废，妻归外家，至无栖身之地，交游绝迹，始萌悔念。道士时周恤之，蒋亦渐习操作。道士见其改过，乃告以其父遗金，将掘出畀之。乃携镢至藏金处，遍觅，已失所在，相与大骇。蒋归告其匪类，因共哗然，嗾控于官。官讯之，道士不讳，官断赔偿。道士罄其蓄，犹不满十分之二，里人多不直道士，道士遂舍庙去。

    Explanation

    443

    In the town of Xia, which belongs to Teng County, there lived an elderly man named Jiang who had established his household through diligence and frugality. He had one son whom he neglected in education, and as the boy grew up, he became a wanderer, causing the family's fortunes to decline. Jiang was deeply worried about this.

    There was a Taoist priest named Chen who worshipped at the Guan Yu Temple. Chen was from Gushi in Henan Province and had long been friendly with Jiang. One day, Jiang privately gave Chen five hundred gold coins and instructed him, saying, "My son is unworthy; I believe he cannot maintain the family business. In the future, he will surely starve to death. Now, I entrust this gold to you. After my death, if he repents, use this to help him. If he never reforms, then use this gold to renovate the temple." The Taoist priest agreed and hid the gold in a clay jar, covered it with a broken bell, and buried it behind the hall, keeping it a secret.

    A few months later, Jiang died. His son became even more reckless, ruining the family business completely. His wife returned to her family home, and he had nowhere to live, severing all social connections. It was then that he began to feel remorse. The Taoist priest took care of him, and Jiang gradually learned to work. Seeing that Jiang had reformed, the Taoist priest told him about his father's hidden gold and planned to dig it up and give it to him. They carried a hoe to the place where the gold was buried, searched everywhere, but found that it had already disappeared. They were all greatly alarmed.

    Jiang returned to tell his gang, and they all clamored loudly, inciting a complaint against him to the authorities. The official interrogated them, and the Taoist priest did not deny anything. The official ruled for compensation. The Taoist priest spent all his savings, but it still did not reach two-tenths of the amount. The villagers mostly did not support the Taoist priest, and he then left the temple.

    444

    云游数年，过直隶莲池禅寺，挂单将行，值寺僧为某观察公诵《寿生经》作佛事。有老仆抱公子戏于山门，公子遽牵道士衣，投怀不舍。家人不能解，因命道士抱送公子归。观察厚赠道士遣去，而公子啼哭追之。不得已，留道士于后园小庵，饮食之。一日，道士欲诵经为观察公子祈福，需木鱼钟磬，家人以破磬付之，道士惊云：“此我之磬也。”家人白其主。诘之，道士云：“磬覆瓦罐，内贮五百金。”问：“安所得金？”乃具述蒋翁遗金之事。观察恍然，知其子为蒋翁转世，此金即翁所藏而走归原主者也。告以生此子三日，掘地埋胞衣，因得此金。以无所用，付之布肆中，取息已五年矣。怜道士之无辜受赔，且与其儿有宿缘，因此以此金子母赠道士，并遣使送归夏镇，致书于滕邑令，将此事镌石以纪之。

    Explanation

    444

    Wandering for several years, I passed through the Lianchi Zen Temple in Zhili, where I registered temporarily before leaving. Coincidentally, the temple monks were reciting the Shousheng Jing (Sutra of Longevity and Life) for a certain official as part of a Buddhist ritual. An elderly servant was playing with the official’s son at the temple gate, and the child suddenly grabbed the Taoist’s robes, clinging to him with great affection. The family couldn’t understand the child’s behavior and asked the Taoist to take the child back home. The official generously rewarded the Taoist and sent him on his way, but the child cried and chased after him. Reluctantly, the family kept the Taoist in a small hermitage in the back garden and provided him with food and drink. One day, the Taoist wanted to recite scriptures to pray for blessings for the official’s son and needed ritual instruments such as a wooden fish, bell, and cymbals. The family gave him a broken cymbal, and the Taoist was shocked, saying, “This is my cymbal!” The family informed the official, who questioned the Taoist. The Taoist explained, “The cymbal was covered with a clay pot, which contained five hundred taels of gold.” The official asked, “Where did you get the gold?” The Taoist then recounted the story of Mr. Jiang’s lost gold. The official suddenly realized that his son was the reincarnation of Mr. Jiang, and the gold was the treasure Mr. Jiang had hidden and now returned to its rightful owner. The official explained that the gold was found three days after the birth of his son, when they dug up and buried the placenta. Since they had no use for it, they had entrusted it to a cloth shop, where the interest had accumulated over five years. Feeling sympathy for the Taoist’s innocent loss and recognizing their karmic connection, the official decided to give the Taoist the principal and interest of the gold. He also sent a messenger to return the Taoist to Xiazhen and wrote a letter to the magistrate of Teng County, requesting that the event be inscribed on stone as a memorial.

    445

    ○人熊浙商某，贩洋为生，同伴二十余人，被风吹至一海岛，因结伴上岛闲步。走里许，遇一人熊，长丈余，以两手围其伴，愈围愈逼。至一大树下，熊取长藤将人耳逐个穿通，缚树上，乃跳去。诸人俟其去远，各解所佩小刀割断其藤，趋奔回船。俄见四熊抬一大石板，板上又坐一熊，比前熊更大。前熊仍跳跃而来，状若甚乐者。至树侧，见空藤委地，怅然如有所失。石板上熊大怒，叱四熊群起殴之，立毙而去。众在舟中望之，各惊喜，以为再生。山阴吴某耳孔有一洞，沈君萍如戚也，问其故，历历言之如此。

    Explanation

    445

    A Merchant from Zhejiang named "Ren Xiong" made a living by trading overseas. He was accompanied by more than twenty companions who were blown to an island by the wind. They decided to go ashore together for a leisurely walk. After walking about a mile, they encountered a bear about ten feet long, which encircled their companions with its paws, getting closer and closer. They reached a large tree, where the bear took a long vine and pierced each person's ear, tying them to the tree. Then, it jumped away. The others waited until the bear was far away, used their small knives to cut the vines, and rushed back to the ship. Soon, they saw four bears lifting a large stone slab, with another bear sitting on top, larger than the first. The first bear still jumped over, appearing very happy. When they reached the side of the tree, they saw the empty vines lying on the ground, and the bears looked disappointed as if they had lost something. The bear on the stone slab became very angry, scolded the other four bears, and they attacked it, killing it instantly and then leaving. The group on the boat watched from afar, feeling surprised and relieved, thinking they had been reborn. A person named Wu from Shanyin had a hole in his ear. Mr. Shen Pingru, a relative, asked him about it, and he recounted the story in detail.

    446

    ○绳拉云山东济宁州有役王廷贞，术能求雨。常醉酒高坐本官案桌上，自称天师。刺史怒之，笞二十板。未几，州大旱，祷雨不下。合州绅士都言其神，刺史不得已召而谢之。良久许诺，令闭城南门，开城北门，选属龙者童子八名待差使，搓绳索五十二丈待用。己乃与童子斋戒三日，登坛持咒。自辰至午，云果从东起，重叠如铺绵。王以绳掷空中，似上有持之者，竟不坠落。待绳掷尽，呼八童子曰：“速拉！速拉！”八童子竭力拉之，若有千钧之重。云在西则拉之来东，云在南则拉之来北，使绳如使风然。已而大雨滂沱，水深一尺，乃牵绳而下。每雷击其首，辄以羽扇摭拦，雷亦远去。

    Explanation

    446

    A Rainmaker in Shandong's Jining Prefecture

    In the prefecture of Jining, Shandong, there was a low-ranking official named Wang Tingzhen, who claimed to possess the ability to summon rain. He often got drunk and sat arrogantly on the table of his superior's desk, calling himself a Celestial Master. The governor, angered by his audacity, punished him with twenty lashes. Not long after, the prefecture suffered a severe drought, and the traditional prayers for rain failed to bring any relief. The local gentry, desperate for a solution, all spoke of Wang's supposed divine powers. The governor, having no other choice, summoned Wang and apologized to him. After a long hesitation, Wang agreed to perform the ritual. He instructed the closing of the southern city gate and the opening of the northern gate, selected eight children born under the zodiac sign of the dragon to assist him, and had a rope fifty-two zhang long prepared. Wang and the children fasted and abstained for three days, then ascended the altar and chanted spells. From morning until noon, clouds indeed rose from the east, forming layers like a blanket. Wang threw the rope into the air, as if it were held by an unseen force, and it did not fall. When the rope was fully thrown, he called out to the eight children, "Pull quickly! Pull quickly!" The children strained with all their might, as if the rope weighed a thousand jin. When the clouds were to the west, they pulled them towards the east; when the clouds were to the south, they pulled them towards the north, making the rope move as if it were the wind. Soon, a heavy rain poured down, filling the streets with water a foot deep. Wang then pulled the rope down. Whenever lightning struck his head, he would use a feather fan to deflect it, and the lightning would move far away.

    447

    嗣后邻县苦旱，必来相延。王但索饮，不受币，且曰：“一丝之受，法便不灵。”每求雨一次，则家中亲丁必有损伤，故亦不乐为也。刺史即蓝芷林亲家。

    　　芷林为余言。

    　　○烧狼筋蓝府有狼筋一条，凡家中失物，烧之，则偷者手足皆颤。有女子公子失金钗一只，不知谁偷，乃齐奴婢甘姆数十人，取筋烧之。数十人神气平善，了无他异，但见房门布帘闪颤不已。揭视之，钗挂其上，盖妇公子走过时，钗为帘所勾留耳。

    Explanation

    447

    From then on, when a neighboring county suffered from drought, they would definitely come to invite [the figure]. The figure would only ask for a drink and would not accept money or gifts, and he would say, "If even a thread is accepted, the law will no longer be effective." Each time he prayed for rain, there would be some damage to his family members, so he did not like to do it. The official was the son-in-law of Lan Zhi Lin.

    Lan Zhi Lin told me.

    There was a piece of wolf sinew in the house. Whenever any item was lost in the house, if it was burned, the thief's hands and feet would tremble. A young lady and her young master lost a gold hairpin, but they did not know who had stolen it. So, they gathered dozens of servants and slaves, took the sinew, and burned it. The dozens of people present appeared calm, with no other strange occurrences, but only saw the door and curtain of the room keep shaking. Upon inspection, the hairpin was hanging on it. It turned out that when the lady and the young master passed by, the hairpin got caught by the curtain.

    448

    ○王老三江西陶悔庵行五，妻某氏，偶与姑口角，忽腾身而坐屋瓦上，大笑不止。再三招之，始下，口作北京男子音曰：“我天津卫王老三，谁人不知？年一百三十岁矣！从北迁南，住此已七十年。此屋是翰林蒋士铨故居，我犹见其初生时也。”

    　　家人闻之大骇，问：“汝鬼耶，狐耶？”曰：“我非鬼非狐，乃半仙也。我所住处被汝家五爷拆毁，使我无安身之所。我权立瓦檐七日，既冻且饿，不得不借寓你家娘子身上，速买面来疗饥。”与之面，一啖五斤。五爷者，悔庵也，问：“五爷并未拆房，何得云尔？”曰：“所拆者东厢庭柱下是也。”先是悔庵得古钱千文，欲其生青绿，故掘柱下埋之，不知即此怪所居。问：“既恼五爷，何以不附五爷身上？”曰：“彼手内有印，我畏之，故不敢。”悔庵因而自视其手，有纹正方，平素亦不自知也。

    448

    Wang Laosan, the fifth son of Tao Hui'an from Jiangxi, whose wife was surnamed [1], accidentally quarreled with her mother-in-law. Suddenly, he leaped up and sat on the roof tiles, laughing uncontrollably. After being called repeatedly, he finally came down and spoke in the voice of a Beijing man, saying, "I am Wang Laosan of Tianjin, who is known to everyone! I am 130 years old! I moved from the north to the south 70 years ago and still live in this house, which was the former residence of the scholar Jiang Shiquan. I even saw him when he was born."

    The family heard this and were greatly alarmed, asking, "Are you a ghost or a fox spirit?" He replied, "I am neither a ghost nor a fox spirit, but a half-immortal. The place where I lived was destroyed by your fifth master, leaving me without a place to stay. I temporarily stayed on the eaves for seven days, freezing and starving, and had to temporarily reside in your wife's body. Quickly buy some noodles to relieve my hunger." They gave him noodles, and he ate five pounds in one meal. The fifth master, Hui'an, asked, "Fifth Master did not demolish the house, why say so?" He replied, "The place he demolished was under the pillar of the east wing." Before, Hui'an had found a thousand ancient coins and wanted them to turn green, so he dug under the pillar to bury them, not knowing that it was the place where this supernatural being lived. Hui'an then looked at his hand, saw a square pattern, which he did not know before.

    Notes:
    [1] The wife's surname is not provided in the original text.

    449

    陶太夫人责之曰：“汝既自称半仙，便当知男女有别，何以缠扰我家娘子？”

    　　某氏即作男子揖状曰：“我自知非礼，但不附你家娘子身上，恐所求不遂。因知男女有别，故我夜间不许他睡，教他张着眼，所以避嫌疑也。且我高年修道，岂复再有邪念耶？”问：“何求？”曰：“送我迁居。”问：“作何送法？”曰：“请五爷用有印之手，用红纸写王三先生之神位，贴向东湖水边松树上，则我去矣。”如其言。又曰：“我尚需衣冠才去。”乃向纸店买纸衣冠焚之。又大笑曰：“我布衣也，并未入学，又未捐官，何必用此金顶帽哉？速换！速换！”视店中纸冠，果有金顶，乃去之。悔庵亲持纸牌送贴东湖松树上，闻空中呼谢者再，从此家中平安。

    Explanation

    449

    Final Translation

    The Grand Lady Tao scolded him, saying, "Since you claim to be a semi-immortal, you should know the difference between men and women. Why do you persistently bother my wife?"

    The man immediately made a bow in the manner of a man and said, "I know it is not proper, but if I do not attach myself to your wife, my request might not be fulfilled. Because I know the difference between men and women, I do not allow him to sleep at night and make him keep his eyes open, so as to avoid suspicion. Moreover, at my advanced age, I have no more wicked thoughts!" She asked, "What is your request?" He replied, "Send me to move to a new residence." She asked, "How should this be done?" He said, "Please have the fifth elder use his official seal, write the spirit tablet of Mr. Wang San on red paper, and post it on a pine tree by the eastern lake, and then I will leave." She followed his instructions. He then said, "I still need my clothes and hat to leave." She went to a paper shop and bought a paper robe and hat to burn. He laughed loudly and said, "I am just a commoner, I have never been to school, nor have I purchased an official position, why should I use this golden-capped hat? Change quickly! Change quickly!" Looking at the paper hats in the shop, he saw that they indeed had golden caps, and so he changed them. Regretful An personally held the paper tablet and posted it on the pine tree by the eastern lake. They heard in the air, calling thanks twice, and from then on, the house was peaceful and safe.

    450

    问其妻，曰：“我与姑口角时，忽见空中有短而髯者，以手堤我至瓦上，此后我不知矣。”怪在家作闹时，人问休咎，有中有不中，问多则不答，曰：“我答何难，但你辈亦须哀怜娘子，省费些中气。”闲亦作诗数句，文理粗俗，未落款但云“王三先生高兴”六字而已。

    　　○择风水贾祸湖南孝感县张息村明府，葬先人于九山。事毕，别买隙地五亩许，将造宗祠。工人动土竖柱，得一朱棺，盖已朽坏，中露一尸，骷髅甚大，体骨长过中人，胸贯三铁钉，长五六寸，腰有铁索环绕数匝。工人不敢动，告知明府。一时宾客尽劝掩埋，另择竖柱之所。张不可，曰：“我用价买地，本非强占，且风水所关，尺寸不可移。此古墓也，可以迁葬。”乃自作祭文，具牲牢祭之。祭毕，仍令迁棺。

    Explanation

    450

    When he asked his wife, she said, "When I had an argument with my mother-in-law, I suddenly saw a short, bearded figure in the air who took me by the hand and lifted me onto the roof. After that, I don’t know what happened." When the ghost caused trouble at home, people asked about good or bad fortune, and sometimes it was right, sometimes wrong. When asked too much, it didn’t answer, saying, "What’s difficult about answering? But you all must also pity my wife and save some of your vital energy." Sometimes it would also write a few lines of poetry, but the style was crude and vulgar, without a signature, only saying "Mr. Wang San is pleased" in six characters.

    ○ Selecting a Grave Site for Disaster

    The magistrate of Xiao'an County in Hunan, Zhang Xicun, buried his ancestors on Jiuzhou Mountain. After the burial, he bought another five acres of land nearby to build a clan hall. When the workers dug the ground and erected pillars, they found a red coffin, which was already decayed, revealing a large skeleton with bones longer than those of an average person. The chest was pierced by three iron nails, each about five or six inches long, and the waist was encircled by several coils of iron chain. The workers were too afraid to touch it and reported it to the magistrate. At that time, all the guests advised him to bury it and choose another place for the pillars. Zhang refused, saying, "I bought the land with money, not by force, and the feng shui is crucial, so it cannot be moved even by an inch. This is an ancient tomb; it can be moved." He then wrote an offering text himself, prepared sacrificial animals, and offered them. After the offering, he still ordered the coffin to be moved.

    451

    工人锹方下，遽仆地喷血，骂曰：“我唐朝节度使崔洪也，以用法过严，军人作乱，缚我钉死。国家衰乱，不能为我忿诛凶，葬此八百馀年。张某何人，敢擅迁我墓？必不能相恕也！”言毕，工人起而张明府病矣。诸宾客群为祈请，病竟不减，舁归数日而卒。

    　　○飞僵颖州蒋太守在直隶州遇一老翁，两手时时颤动作摇铃状，叩其故，曰：余家住某村，村居仅数十户。山中出一僵尸，能飞行空中，食人小儿。每日未落，群相戒闭户匿儿，犹往往被攫。村人探其穴，深不可测，无敢犯者。闻城中某道士有法术，因纠积金帛，往求捉怪。道士许诺，择日至村中设立法坛，谓众人曰：“我法能布天罗地网，使不得飞去，亦须尔辈持兵械相助，尤需一胆大人入其穴。”

    Explanation

    451

    The worker was just beginning to dig when he suddenly collapsed, spitting blood, and cursed, saying, "I am Cui Hong, a military governor of the Tang Dynasty. Because I was too strict in my administration, the soldiers rebelled, bound me, and nailed me to death. The state has fallen into decline and chaos, unable to avenge my wrongful execution. I have been buried here for more than eight hundred years. Who is Zhang, the local official, to dare move my tomb? I will not forgive him!" After speaking, the worker stood up, and the local official became ill. The guests gathered to pray for his recovery, but his condition did not improve. He was carried back home and died a few days later.

    A certain old man encountered a magistrate in Yingshou Prefecture who lived in Zhili Prefecture. The old man's hands were constantly trembling, as if he were shaking a bell. When asked about the cause, he said, "My family lives in a village in a certain place, where there are only several dozen households. There is a zombie in the mountains that can fly through the air and eat the children of people. Every day before sunset, the villagers warn each other to close their doors and hide their children, but they are still often taken. The villagers explored the zombie's lair, which was so deep that no one dared to enter. They heard that there was a Taoist priest in the city with magical powers, so they pooled their gold and silk and went to ask him to capture the monster. The Taoist priest agreed and chose a day to set up a ritual altar in the village, telling everyone, 'My magic can spread a celestial net, preventing it from flying away. You must also help by holding weapons, and especially, you need a brave man to enter its lair.'

    452

    众人莫敢对，余应声而出，问：“何差遣？”法师曰：“凡僵尸最怕铃铛声，尔到夜间伺其飞出，即入穴中持两大铃摇之，手不可住。若稍息，则尸入穴，尔受伤矣。”漏将下，法师登坛作法，余因握双铃。候尸飞出，尽力乱摇，手如雨点，不敢小住。尸到穴门，果狰狞怒视，闻铃声琅琅，逡巡不敢入。前面被人围住，又无逃处，乃奋手张臂与村人格斗。至天将明，仆地而倒，众举火焚之。余时在穴中，未知也，犹摇铃不敢停如故。至日中，众大呼，余始出，而两手动摇不止，遂至今成疾云。“

    　　○雨僵尸野合有壮士某，客于湖广，独居古寺。一夕，月色甚佳，散步门外，见树林中隐隐有戴唐巾者飘然来者，疑其为鬼。旋至松林最密中，入中古墓，心知为僵尸。

    　　素闻僵尸失棺上盖便不能作祟，初夜，先匿于树林中，伺尸出，将窃取其盖。

    Explanation

    452

    No one dared to answer, and I stepped forward immediately, asking, "What is the task?" The monk said, "All zombies are most afraid of the sound of bells. When you see the zombie fly out at night, enter the hole and hold two large bells, shaking them continuously without stopping. If you pause, the zombie will enter the hole, and you will be injured." As the night was about to end, the monk ascended the altar to perform a ritual, and I held the two bells. When the zombie flew out, I shook the bells with all my might, my hands moving like raindrops, not daring to stop for a moment. The zombie reached the entrance of the hole, indeed looking fierce and angry, but upon hearing the loud ringing of the bells, it hesitated and did not dare to enter. In front, it was surrounded by people, and it had no place to escape, so it raised its hands and arms to fight with the villagers. By the time dawn approached, the zombie fell to the ground and collapsed, and the crowd lit a fire to burn it. At that time, I was still in the hole and did not know what had happened, so I continued to shake the bells without stopping as before. By noon, the crowd shouted, and I finally came out, but my hands continued to shake uncontrollably, and thus, I have been ill ever since.

    Rain and the Zombie's Wild Conjunction

    There was a valiant warrior who was a guest in Huguang, living alone in an ancient temple. One night, the moonlight was exceptionally beautiful, and he took a walk outside the temple gates. He saw a figure in a Tang-style hat appearing and disappearing in the trees, suspecting it to be a ghost. Soon, the figure disappeared into the densest part of the pine forest, entering an ancient tomb, and he knew it was a zombie.

    He had always heard that a zombie loses its power if its coffin lid is removed. On the first night, he hid in the trees, waiting for the zombie to emerge, planning to steal the lid.

    453

    二更后，尸果出，似有所往。尾之，至一大宅门外，其上楼窗中先有红衣妇人掷下白练一条牵引之。尸攀援而上，作絮语声，不甚了了。壮士先回，窃其棺盖藏之，仍伏于松深处。夜将阑，尸匆匆还，见棺失盖，窘甚，遍觅良久，仍从原路踉跄奔去。再尾之，至楼下且跃且鸣，有声；楼上妇亦相对，以手摇拒，似讶其不应再至者。鸡忽鸣，尸倒于路侧。

    　　明早，行人尽至，各大骇。同往楼下访之，乃周姓祠堂。楼停一柩，有女僵尸，亦卧于棺外。众人知为僵尸野合之怪，乃合尸于一处而焚之。

    　　○鬼幕宾毗陵王生，年四十余，游幕关中。时虚庵庄公知县事，延至幕中。是年秋，与署中友暨庄逵吉诸人同至城隍庙看菊，苦无佳者。王生偶拾一枝，遣仆送妇。逵吉阻之，以为神前之物，不可轻动。王戏曰：“某一生直道，神明必不见怪。如欲加谴责，我为之代办公事一二件何如？”

    Explanation

    453

    After the second watch of the night, the entity indeed emerged, as if it were going somewhere. The protagonist followed it, and they arrived at the gate of a large mansion. From the window of the upper floor, a red-clad woman first threw down a piece of white silk to pull it. The entity climbed up, making a low, indistinct sound. The brave warrior returned first, secretly taking the coffin lid and hiding it, then remaining concealed deep among the pines. As the night nears its end, the entity hurriedly returned, seeing that the coffin lid was missing and greatly embarrassed, it searched everywhere for a long time, then staggered back along the original path. The protagonist followed it again, and they reached the mansion, where the entity jumped and called out, making a sound; the woman on the upper floor also responded in kind, shaking off with her hands, seeming surprised that it should not have returned. Suddenly, the rooster crowed, and the entity fell to the side of the road.

    The next morning, the travelers all arrived, greatly startled. Together, they went to the lower floor to investigate, and it turned out to be the Zhou clan hall. A coffin was stopped on the upper floor, containing a female zombie, which also lay outside the coffin. Everyone knew it was a strange occurrence of a zombie's wild union, so they gathered the bodies in one place and burned them.

    A Ghostly Guest from Pi Ling, Wang Sheng, was in his forties and traveled to the region of Guanzhong. At the time, Xuan'an Zhang Gong was in charge of the affairs of a certain county and invited him to join his entourage. That autumn, Wang Sheng and his friends from the office, along with Zhang Kuaiji, went together to the City God Temple to admire chrysanthemums, but they found none particularly fine. Wang Sheng happened to pick a branch and sent a servant to deliver it to his wife. Zhang Kuaiji stopped him, saying that it was an object before the deity and should not be moved lightly. Wang Sheng joked, "I have always been straightforward, and the gods and spirits would not find fault with me. If you wish to punish me, how about letting me handle a couple of official matters for you?"

    454

    明年三月三日，王生无疾而终，各以为骇。更余忽醒曰：“予独坐，见一使者持一名柬至邀余，即同步出门外登舆。行里许，至城隍庙。神降阶迎，行宾主礼，曰：”先生折我菊花，许我办案，兹有某县积案，迟延日久，尚未审结，恭邀先生一商。‘少顷，吏捧积年案卷至，主人退出。余阅诸情节，皆属易办，惟有误勾某罪人一案，余批云：“骨肉未寒，犹可还阳。否则东岳行查檄至，城隍将受处分矣。’神出视大喜，云：”先生所见，甚合我意。‘茶罢，仍送至丹墀，曰：“尚有一事奉托，如晤包少府，渠承办工程木料，日内可到矣。’余唯唯别出，登舆而归，取床头青蚨三百，犒其从者而醒。”

    Explanation

    454

    The following year on the third day of the third month, Mr. Wang died without illness, and everyone was surprised. After a while, he suddenly woke up and said, "I was sitting alone when I saw a messenger holding an invitation card come to invite me. I immediately followed the messenger out of the door and onto the carriage. After traveling a few miles, we arrived at the City God Temple. The god descended the steps to welcome, performed the rituals of host and guest, and said, 'There are unresolved cases in a certain county that have been delayed for a long time and have not yet been settled. I humbly invite you to discuss.' After a while, officials brought the accumulated case files, and the host left. I reviewed all the details, and they were all easy to handle, except for the case of mistakenly summoning a certain criminal. I wrote, 'The flesh and bones are not yet cold, so it is still possible to revive. Otherwise, if the Eastern Peak sends a summons, the City God will be punished.' The god came out and was very pleased, saying, 'What you have seen is very much in line with my intentions.' After tea, they still sent me to the red steps and said, 'There is still one matter to entrust. If you meet Mr. Bao, he is handling the construction materials, which should arrive within a few days.' I nodded in agreement and left, returning to the carriage and going home. I took three hundred coins from the bedside table to reward his followers and woke up."

    455

    越三日，仙游大水，木料皆出黑口镇矣。包少府者，醴泉同知包某也。至今人呼王生而为“鬼幕宾。”

    　　○雷震蟆妖严陵宋淡山于乾隆丁亥夏见遂安县民家雷震其屋，须臾天霁，一无所损，惟室中恒有臭气。旬日后，诸亲友以ヅυ之戏环聚于庭，天花板内忽有血水下滴。

    　　启板视之，见一死虾蟆，长三尺许，头戴综缨帽，脚穿乌缎靴，身着玄纱褙褡，宛如人形。方知雷击者，即此是虾蟆也。

    Explanation

    455

    Three days later, a flood struck Xianyou, and all the wood was carried to Heikou Town. The official known as Baoshoufu was actually a deputy governor named Bao from Lique. To this day, people still refer to Wang Sheng as "Ghost Guest."

    ○ During the summer of the Dinghai year of Qianlong, in Yancheng, Song Danshan witnessed a house in Suian County being struck by lightning. Soon after, the sky cleared, and nothing was damaged except for a persistent foul odor in the room. Ten days later, friends and relatives gathered in the courtyard for a game, and suddenly, blood water dripped from the ceiling.

    Upon opening the board, they saw a dead toad, about three feet long, wearing a hat adorned with ribbons, black satin boots, and dark silk robes, resembling a human form. They then realized that the object struck by lightning was indeed this toad.

    456

    ○梦中破案曹州刘姓，以典当为业。虞城张某，为经理其事已二载矣，少有蓄积。岁暮欲归，主人留至元旦，乘一青骡去，相订上元日返曹州。至期不至，刘因遣人促之来。至其家，则云：“未尝归也。”两家致讼，控至抚按，勒限饬县捕拿。延至六月矣，公差惶遽无措。

    　　一夕，访于城南，见有老人偕一年少相谓曰：“月色甚佳，何不向凉亭一行？”

    　　曹州南城十数里，旧有凉亭，公差私议：“二人于此时往，倘城门闭，何由而入？”

    　　心异之，遂先至彼相伺。未几，二人果至。听所言，皆邻里间琐事。有顷，少年忽云：“城内刘姓事至今未明，余心窃计，乃西门外卖饼孙姓利其财物，因而害之也。”翁问故，少年云：“饼店在此已数载，今春倏闭，是以疑之。”翁叱云：“此事大有干系，何得妄语？”意甚拂然。旋云：“夜深，可归矣。”

    Explanation

    456

    ○ A Case Solved in a Dream

    The Liu family of Caozhou were engaged in pawnbroking. Zhang from Yu Cheng, who had been managing their affairs for two years, had accumulated some savings. At the end of the year, he wanted to return home, but his employer kept him until New Year's Day. He then left on a gray donkey, agreeing to return to Caozhou on the Lantern Festival day. However, he did not return on the agreed date, so Liu sent someone to urge him to come back. When he arrived at his home, he said, "I never returned home." This led to a dispute between the two families, and they filed a complaint with the provincial governor and the censor, who imposed a deadline and ordered the county to apprehend him. By June, the officials were in a panic and at a loss.

    One evening, someone visited the south of the city and saw an old man with a young person saying, "The moonlight is very beautiful, why not go to the cool pavilion?"

    There used to be a cool pavilion a dozen miles south of the southern wall of Caozhou. The officials privately discussed, "If these two people go there at this time, if the city gates are closed, how can they enter?"

    Finding it strange, they went there first to wait. Soon after, the two people indeed arrived. Listening to what they said, it was all trivial neighborhood matters. After a while, the young person suddenly said, "The matter of the Liu family in the city is still unclear to this day. I secretly think it was the Sun family selling pancakes outside the west gate who benefited from his property and thus harmed him." The old man asked the reason, and the young person said, "The bakery has been here for several years, but it suddenly closed this spring, so I suspected it." The old man scolded, "This matter is very serious, how can you speak recklessly?" He was very displeased. Soon after, he said, "It's late, let's go home."

    457

    公差尾其后，行甚速，至南城，门已闭，见二人从门隙入。差亟呼司阍启钥入城，则两人尚在前行。至小弄，少年与翁别，入门，门亦未启也。复随翁行二十余家，亦未启扉而入。差大惊，叩其户。半晌翁出，持纸捻，披衣，极困惫之状。差曰：“适间与少年凉亭看月，何遽睡耶？”翁神色迟疑曰：“看月有之，乃梦中事也。”差复胁之往诣少年，少年出，亦如翁状，乃拘入县署，述梦中语。

    　　次早，遣二人至某村迹孙姓所居，则青骡宛系门首也，因锁拿到县，一讯而服，遂起赃问抵偿焉。

    　　此乙巳夏间事。曹州守吴忠诰向为绥德州牧，与严道甫善，告道甫也。

    　　○马变鱼园地变鹅雍正初年，伍相国为盛京将军送马五百匹诣黑龙江。将至不数里，忽一马振鬣长嘶，众马随之。至江口，尽跃入水，化而为鱼。

    Explanation

    457

    A public official followed behind, walking very quickly. He arrived at the southern city gate, which was already closed, and saw two people entering through a gap in the gate. The official urgently called out to the gatekeeper to unlock the gate and enter the city, but the two people were still moving forward. They reached a small alley where a young man parted ways with an old man, entering a door that was also not opened. The official, greatly surprised, knocked on the door. After a while, the old man came out, holding a paper wick, wearing his clothes, and looking extremely tired. The official said, "Just now, I was watching the moon with the young man in a pavilion. Why did you sleep so soon?" The old man, with a hesitant expression, replied, "Watching the moon is true, but it was a dream." The official then forced the old man to go to the young man, who also came out in the same state, and they were taken into the county office to recount the dream.

    The next morning, two people were sent to a certain village to trace the residence of the surname Sun. They found the blue mule still tied at the door, and the person was locked up and taken to the county. After one interrogation, he confessed, and the stolen goods were found, and compensation was demanded.

    This was an event that occurred during the summer of the year 1725 (乙巳). The governor of Caozhou, Wu Zhonggao, who previously served as the prefect of Suide, and was friends with Yan Daofu, told Yan Daofu about the event.

    In the early years of the Yongzheng era, Wu Xiangguo, serving as the general of Shengjing, sent 500 horses to Heilongjiang. As they were about to arrive, not far from the destination, suddenly one horse shook its mane and neighed loudly, and the other horses followed. They reached the river mouth, all jumping into the water and transforming into fish.

    458

    严道甫馆德州卢氏时，卢有戚罗氏，偶以二百钱买一鹅，带至济南应试。到时，鹅价甚贵，有以五百文售之者。罗忽动牟利之念，忆家有园地十五亩，若质钱买鹅，可获三倍之利。试毕回家，售地得价，四出买鹅，得三百馀只，复驱以往。

    　　行二日，至齐河，过城外长桥，有头鹅带铃者引颈长鸣，振翼而飞，众鹅相率以上。观者数十人，群相拍手。须臾之间，望之如白云一片，随风而灭。

    　　罗惭悔交集，无可奈何。搜索囊中，尚馀前次买鹅钱数百文，作盘费以归。

    　　自叹祖遗园地，化鹅而去矣。

    　　○聋鬼乾隆四十九年，杭州半山陆家牌楼河中淌一浮尸来，村民霍茂祥，素行善事，为敛钱买棺殡诸市上。夜梦蓝衣人来曰：“我临平人张某，教馆为业，不幸失足落水。蒙君殡我，无以为报。我能预知休咎，替人禳解。倘有灵应，须以牲牢谢我，君可得香火钱。”霍醒，告之里人，果有求必应。不数日，香火如云。

    Explanation

    458

    Yan Daofu was staying in the home of Lu in Dezhou when Lu's relative Luo, a merchant, accidentally bought a goose for two hundred coins and brought it to Jinan for the imperial exams. Upon arrival, the price of geese had risen significantly, with some selling for as much as five hundred coins. Luo suddenly conceived the idea of making a profit, recalling that he owned fifteen acres of garden land. If he pawned the land to buy geese, he could triple his investment. After finishing the exams, he returned home, sold the land, and spent the proceeds buying over three hundred geese, which he then drove back to Dezhou.

    Two days later, they reached Qihe and passed by a long bridge outside the city. A goose with a bell around its neck suddenly let out a loud cry, flapped its wings, and flew up into the sky. The other geese followed suit, and dozens of spectators clapped in amazement. In an instant, the flock disappeared into the sky like a patch of white clouds, vanishing with the wind.

    Luo was filled with shame and regret, feeling helpless. He searched through his bag and found the remaining coins he had used to buy the geese earlier, which he used as travel expenses to return home.

    He lamented that his ancestral land had turned into geese and disappeared.

    ○ In the 49th year of the Qianlong reign (1784), a floating corpse drifted down the river near Lu's house in the Half Mountain area of Hangzhou. A villager named Huo Maoliang, who was known for his charitable deeds, collected money to buy a coffin and buried the body in the market. That night, Huo dreamed of a man in blue robes who said, "I am Zhang, a private tutor from Linping, who unfortunately fell into the water and drowned. You buried me, and I have no way to repay you. I can foretell good and bad omens and offer solutions to problems. If you make offerings to me, you will receive incense money." Huo woke up and told his fellow villagers, and indeed, any requests made to the spirit were fulfilled. Within a few days, the number of offerings was like a cloud.

    459

    霍夜又梦张来曰：“我左耳聋，有来通诚者，须向右耳告我。”于是，次日人来祈祷者，听霍之言，多向棺右致祭，叫呼似有应声答者。材民奉之若狂，呼为“灵棺材”。霍家取香火钱，因以致富。

    　　未几，仁和令杨公路过，见烧香者汹汹蚁聚。杨怒其惑众，命焚其棺，鬼遂绝。

    　　○棺床陆秀才遐龄，赴闽中幕馆。路过江山县，天大雨，赶店不及，日已夕矣。望前村树木浓密，瓦屋数间，奔往叩门，求借一宿。主人出迎，颇清雅，自言沈姓，亦系江山秀才，家无余屋延宾。陆再三求，沈不得已，指东厢一间曰：“此可草榻也。”持烛送入。陆见左停一棺，意颇恶之，又自念平素胆壮，且舍此亦无他宿处，乃唯唯作谢。其房中原有木榻，即将行李铺上，辞主人出，而心不能无悸，取所带《易经》一部灯下观。至二鼓，不敢熄烛，和衣而寝。

    Explanation

    459

    Huo had another dream one night where Zhang appeared and said, "I am deaf in my left ear. If anyone wishes to communicate with me, they must speak to my right ear." The next day, people who came to pray listened to Huo's words and began to offer prayers to the right side of the coffin, as if they were receiving responses. The people revered the coffin as if it were a deity and called it the "spirit coffin." Huo's family became wealthy by collecting offerings from those who prayed to the coffin.

    Not long after, the magistrate Yang of Renhe passed by and saw a large crowd gathered to burn incense. The magistrate was angry about the people being misled and ordered the coffin to be burned, thus ending the phenomenon.

    Lu Xiucai Xialing's Journey to Fujian
    Lu Xiucai Xialing, a scholar traveling to a position in Fujian, was passing through Jiangshan County when it started to rain heavily. He couldn't find a place to stay and the sun was setting. Seeing a nearby village with dense trees and a few tile-roofed houses, he ran to knock on the door and asked to stay for the night. The host, who was well-mannered and scholarly, introduced himself as Shen and said he was also a scholar from Jiangshan but had no extra rooms for guests. Lu persisted and Shen, reluctantly, pointed to a room in the east wing and said, "This can serve as a temporary resting place." He held a candle and led Lu inside. Lu saw a coffin resting on the left side of the room and felt uneasy. However, he remembered his usual courage and decided to stay, as there was no other place to go. He laid out his belongings on the wooden bed in the room, bid farewell to the host, and left, though his heart was filled with unease. He took out a copy of the I Ching and began to read by the light. By the second drumbeat, he dared not extinguish the candle and slept in his clothes.

    460

    少顷，闻棺中有声，注目视之，棺前盖已掀起矣，有翁白须朱履，伸两腿而出。陆大骇，紧扣其帐，而于帐缝窥之。翁至陆坐处，翻其《易经》，了无惧色，袖出烟袋，就烛上吃烟。陆更惊，以为鬼不畏《易经》，又能吃烟，真恶鬼矣。恐其走至榻前，愈益谛视，浑身冷颤，榻为之动。白须翁视榻微笑，竟不至前，仍袖烟袋入棺，自覆其盖。陆终夜不眠。

    　　迨早，主人出问：“客昨夜安否？”“强应曰：”安，但不知屋左所停棺内何人？“曰：”家父也。“陆曰：”既系尊公，何以久不安葬？“主人曰：”家君现存，壮健无恙，并未死也。家君平日一切达观，以为自古皆有死，何不先为演习，故庆七十后即作寿棺，厚糊其里，置被褥焉，每晚必卧其中，当作床帐。“

    Explanation

    460

    In a short while, Lu heard a sound coming from inside the coffin, . He focused his attention and saw that the lid of the coffin had already been lifted. An old man with a white beard and red shoes extended his legs and emerged. Lu was greatly startled, tightly gripping his canopy and peering through the seams. The old man walked to where Lu was sitting, flipping through the I Ching, showing no sign of fear. He took out his pipe from his sleeve and smoked from the candle. Lu was even more alarmed, thinking that the ghost did not fear the I Ching and could smoke, truly a malevolent spirit. Fearing that the ghost would come to the foot of the bed, he stared more intently, shivering all over, causing the bed to move. The white-bearded old man smiled at the bed and did not come closer, putting his pipe back into his sleeve and returning to the coffin, covering himself again. Lu could not sleep all night.

    In the morning, the host came out and asked, "Did the guest have a good night?" "Lu reluctantly replied, "Good, but I don't know who is in the coffin left in the room. It is your esteemed father." The host said, "Since it is your esteemed father, why has he not been buried for so long?" The host said, "My father is still alive, healthy and unharmed, and has not died. My father has always had a broad perspective, believing that since ancient times, everyone dies, why not practice it in advance? Therefore, after celebrating his seventieth birthday, he made a coffin for his birthday, lined it thickly with something, and placed bedding inside. He sleeps in it every night, using it as a bed and canopy."

    461

    言毕，拉赴棺前，请老翁起，行宾主之礼，果灯下所见翁，笑曰：“客受惊耶！”

    　　三人拍手大剧。视其棺：四围沙木，中空，其盖用黑漆绵纱为之，故能透气，且甚轻。

    　　○炮打蝗虫祟祯甲申，河南飞蝗食民间小儿。每一阵来，如猛雨毒箭，环抱人而蚕食之，顷刻皮肉俱尽，方知《北史》载灵太后时蚕蛾食人无算，真有其事也。开封府城门被蝗塞断，人不能出入。祥符令不得已，发火炮击之，冲开一洞，行人得通。

    　　未饭顷，又填塞矣。

    　　○僵尸手执元宝雍正九年冬，西北地震，山西介休县某村地陷里许。有未成坑者，居民掘视之：一家仇姓者全家俱在，尸僵不腐，一切什物器皿完好如初；主人方持天平兑银，右手犹执一元宝，把握甚牢。

    Explanation

    461

    After finishing speaking, they were led to the coffin. They invited the old man to rise and performed the rituals of host and guest. Indeed, the old man seen under the lamp smiled and said, "Are you startled, guest?"

    The three of them clapped their hands greatly. They looked at the coffin: it was surrounded by sandwood and hollow inside. The lid was made of black lacquered gauze, which allowed air to pass through and was very light.

    In the year 1644, during the Ming dynasty, swarms of locusts in Henan ate children in the countryside. Each swarm came like a fierce rain of poisonous arrows, surrounding people and devouring them like silkworms. In a short time, their skin and flesh were completely gone. It was then that they realized the Northern History recorded countless people being eaten by silkworms, and this was indeed a true event.

    The city gates of Kaifeng were blocked by locusts, and people could not enter or leave. The magistrate of Xiangfu, having no other choice, fired cannons to break through the locusts, creating a hole through which people could pass. Before the meal was over, the locusts had filled the hole again.

    In the ninth year of the Yongzheng reign (1731), an earthquake struck the northwest region of China. In Jiaoxi county, Shanxi province, the ground sank by a mile. There was a pit that had not fully collapsed, and residents dug to see what was inside. They found the entire family of a man surnamed Chou, all of whom were stiff and not decomposed. All their household items were intact, as if nothing had happened. The owner was holding a balance to weigh silver, and his right hand still tightly gripped a gold ingot.

    462

    ○张飞棺萧松浦从四川归云：保宁府巴州旧刺史之厅东有张飞墓石穴，至今未闭。一朱棺悬空，长九尺，叩之，声铿铿然。

    　　乾隆三十年，有陈秀才某，梦金甲神自称：“我汉朝将军张翼德也，今世俗驿公文，避家兄长之讳，而反犯我之讳，何太不公道耶？”彼此大笑而寤。盖近日公文改“羽递”为“飞递”故也。

    　　○误尝粪常州蒋用庵御史，与四友同饮于徐兆潢家。徐精饮馔，烹河豚尤佳。因置酒请六客同食河豚。六客虽贪河豚味美，各举箸大啖，而心不能无疑。忽一客张姓者斗然倒地，口吐白沫，噤不能声。主人与群客皆以为中河豚毒矣，速购粪清灌之。张犹未醒。五人大惧，皆曰：“宁可服药于毒未发之前。”乃各饮粪清一杯。

    　　良久，张竟苏醒，群客告以解救之事。张曰：“小弟向有羊儿疯之疾，不时举发，非中河豚毒也。”于是五人深悔无故而尝粪，且嗽且呕，狂笑不止。

    Explanation

    462

    Translation

    Zhang Fei's Tomb

    Xiao Songpu, returning from Sichuan, reported: In the east of the old prefectural governor's hall in Baxiu, Ba Prefecture, there is a stone chamber of Zhang Fei's tomb, which remains unsealed to this day. Inside is a red coffin hanging in the air, nine chi long. When tapped, it makes a resonant sound.

    In the thirtieth year of Qianlong's reign, there was a scholar named Chen, who dreamed of a golden-armored deity who introduced himself as: "I am General Zhang Yide of the Han Dynasty. Today, secular official documents avoid the taboo of my elder brother but instead offend my own, how unfair is this?" They both laughed and woke up. It is because recent official documents changed "feathered delivery" to "flying delivery."

    Mistakenly Tasting Feces

    Zhang Yong'an, a censor from Changzhou, drank with four friends at the home of Xu Zhaohuang. Xu was excellent in food and drink, and the pufferfish he cooked was especially delicious. He therefore set up a banquet and invited six guests to eat pufferfish together. The six guests, though greedy for the delicious taste of pufferfish, each picked up their chopsticks and ate heartily, but they could not help but have doubts. Suddenly, one guest named Zhang fell to the ground, spat foam from his mouth, and was unable to speak. The host and the other guests all thought he had been poisoned by pufferfish and quickly purchased fecal water to pour down him. Zhang still did not wake up. The five were greatly afraid and all said: "Rather than take medicine before the poison had taken effect." They then each drank a cup of fecal water.

    After a long time, Zhang finally woke up. The other guests informed him of the rescue. Zhang said: "I have had epilepsy, which sometimes occurs, not due to pufferfish poisoning." Therefore, the five deeply regretted tasting feces for no reason and coughed and vomited, laughing uncontrollably.

    463

    ○借尸延嗣萧公文登，宰阳湖。伊邻施妪，其夫早卒，抚其遗腹子某，长大娶妻李氏，姑媳甚欢。年余，媳妇忽病亡。妪家贫，痛媳亡不能再娶以延夫祀，呼天吁地。

    　　次日将殓，媳忽从炕上跃起呼姑曰：“我来做汝家媳妇，不要再哭。”妪方庆媳再生，喜不自胜。其子私语母曰：“何声音之不似吾妻也？眼光又直视，恐非真李氏再生，得毋野鬼凭之为祟乎？”邻里皆惊，遂环守之。

    　　三四日中，闭目仰卧，给汤粥，饮啜如常，惟姑呼之则应，夫与之语则避而不答。至七日后方起，梳洗毕，敛衽告姑曰：“我海宁州某村方氏女也，行二，年十九岁，待聘未字。因病死，至冥府，适汝家李氏媳妇在焉。随有矮鬼无数、长鬼一个环跪阎君乞诉，求放李氏还阳。阎君怒叱，将众矮鬼逐出，长鬼责二十板。长鬼受责后，仍再四哀求云：”小人父祖以来，皆守本分，不敢为恶，罪不至于绝嗣。

    Explanation

    463

    Mr. Xiao from Wendeng, who governed Yanghu, had a neighbor, Mrs. Shi, whose husband had died early. She raised their posthumous child, who grew up and married a woman named Li. The mother-in-law and daughter-in-law were very happy. After a year, the daughter-in-law suddenly fell ill and died. Mrs. Shi, being poor, grieved deeply that she could not remarry to continue her husband's lineage, and cried out to heaven and earth.

    The next day, when they were preparing for burial, the daughter-in-law suddenly leapt up from the bed and called out to her mother-in-law, "I will be your daughter-in-law again; do not cry anymore." Mrs. Shi was overjoyed that her daughter-in-law had been reborn and could not contain her happiness. Her son whispered to his mother, "Why does her voice not sound like my wife's? Her gaze is fixed and direct; perhaps it is not the true Li family reborn. Could it be that a wild ghost has possessed her and caused harm?" The neighbors were all shocked and gathered around to watch.

    For three or four days, she lay on her back with her eyes closed, drinking soup and porridge as usual, but only responded to her mother-in-law's calls, avoiding her husband's words. Seven days later, she rose, combed her hair, and washed herself, then bowed and told her mother-in-law, "I am the second daughter of the Fang family from a certain village in Haining Prefecture, nineteen years old, awaiting marriage but not yet married. I died because of illness and went to the underworld, where I found your family's Li family daughter-in-law. There were countless short ghosts and one tall ghost kneeling in a circle before Yan Emperor, pleading to release the Li family daughter-in-law back to the living. Yan Emperor angrily rebuked them, expelled the many short ghosts, and scolded the tall ghost with twenty strokes. After receiving the scolding, the tall ghost still repeatedly pleaded, saying, 'Since my ancestors, I have always followed my duty, did not dare to do evil, and the sin was not to the extent of cutting off the lineage.'

    464

    妻辛苦万状，方得娶一媳妇，今又病亡，何能有力续娶？岂不令一家绝嗣乎！乞放媳还阳，得生子以延一脉。‘阎君怒稍霁，命判官检簿，细阅毕，问长鬼曰：“尔媳李氏阳寿已绝，不能放还，姑念尔世无过恶，尔妻又能守节抚孤，若令乏嗣，无以劝善。方氏女虽年命该尽，生前亦颇好善，可令借李尸复活，则尔无媳而得媳矣。’长鬼拜谢。阎君指长鬼告子曰：”此尔翁也。着他领尔借尸还魂，生子延祀。‘予逐随翁到此。翁指示予曰：“此尔姑也。’将我推跌在地。开眼不见翁，只见婆婆立我身旁，我故只认得婆婆一人，余皆不识也。我家父母俱存，有一个兄弟，年十六岁，望遣人告知，以免父母啼哭。

    Explanation

    464

    My wife endured immense suffering and hardship, and only after great effort did I manage to marry a wife. Now she has died of illness, and how can I afford to marry another? Won't this leave our family without an heir? I beg that my wife be allowed to return to life, so that she can bear a child and continue our lineage. The Judge of the Underworld's anger slightly eased, and he ordered the recorder to check the records. After carefully reading them, he asked the spirit of the deceased man, "Your wife, Li, has reached the end of her earthly life and cannot be allowed to return. However, considering that you have no sins or wrongdoings in your life, and that your wife has been loyal and cared for your orphaned child, if the family is left without an heir, it would not encourage good deeds. A woman from the Fang family, although her life is supposed to end, was also quite good during her life. She can be made to use Li's body to come back to life, so you will not have a wife but will gain one." The spirit of the deceased man bowed in gratitude. The Judge of the Underworld pointed to the spirit of the deceased man and told me, "This is your father-in-law. Let him guide you to borrow a body and return to life, so that you can bear a child and continue the family line." I followed my father-in-law to this place. He pointed to me and said, "This is your mother-in-law." He then pushed me to the ground. When I opened my eyes, I no longer saw my father-in-law but only my mother-in-law standing beside me. I only recognized my mother-in-law, and I did not recognize anyone else. My parents are still alive, and I have a brother who is sixteen years old. I hope someone can inform them so that my parents do not cry.

    465

    姑遣子探访，果如所云。告以故，其父与弟同至妪家。方氏见即相抱而哭，父返退缩，不敢向前，曰：“声音举止虽与吾女相像，而面貌不同，何也？”女对父泣曰：“我假李氏体以生，非我本来面目，喜得再见生身之父与同胞之弟。

    　　母亲忍心不来看我，父与弟又疑而不肯相认，生不如死矣。“

    　　悲痛间，其母遣邻妪来探问，女儿即呼某妈妈：“汝从何处处来？我母亦来看我乎？”父方抚而慰之，叩以往事，丝毫不爽，始真信其再生也。姑遂款留其父与弟在家。至晚，令子与媳同室而处。媳辞曰：“我处女也，虽冥数已定，乞俟吾母来，择吉日成夫妇礼，不可苟合。”亲邻群称善。父亦喜甚，遣其子归迎母来，始合卺焉。

    Explanation

    465

    The aunt sent her son to visit, and indeed, it was as she had said. She told him the reason, and her father and brother came together to the old woman's home. Upon seeing each other, they embraced and cried. The father turned back and shrank, not daring to approach, saying, "Though your voice and manner resemble my daughter's, the appearance is different. Why is that?" The girl replied to her father, weeping, "I was born with the body of the Li family, not my original appearance. I am happy to see my birth father and brother of the same womb again.

    My mother has no heart to come and see me, and my father and brother doubt and will not recognize me. I would rather be dead."

    In the midst of sorrow, the mother sent a neighbor woman to inquire. The daughter immediately called out to the neighbor, "Where are you coming from? Is my mother also coming to see me?" The father then comforted her, asking about past events, and not a single detail was wrong. Only then did he truly believe she had been reborn. The aunt welcomed the father and brother to stay at home. By evening, she ordered the son and daughter-in-law to live together in the same room. The daughter-in-law replied, "I am a maiden. Though fate is already determined, I ask you to wait for my mother to come and choose a good day to complete the marriage ceremony. It cannot be done hastily." The relatives and neighbors all praised her. The father was also very pleased, and he sent his son back to fetch the mother. Finally, they drank the wine of union.

    466

    三年后，举一子。子生百日，亲朋来贺，忽向姑曰：“已为汝家传后有人，我寿算久尽，要去矣。”瞑目而逝。人相传冥官破例办事，犹阳官之因公挪移云。

    　　●卷十三○关神下乩明季，关神下乩坛批某士人终身云：“官至都堂，寿止六十。”后士人登第，官果至中丞。国朝定鼎后，其人乞降，官不加迁，而寿已八十矣。偶至坛所，适关帝复降。其人自以为必有阴德，故能延寿，跪而请曰：“弟子官爵验矣，今寿乃过之，岂修寿在人，虽神明亦有所不知耶？”关帝大书曰：“某平生以忠孝待人，甲申之变，汝自不死，与我何与？”屈指计之，崇祯殉难时，正此公年六十时也。

    Explanation

    466

    Three years later, he had a son. When the child was one hundred days old, relatives and friends came to congratulate them. Suddenly, the woman spoke to her mother-in-law: "It has already ensured your family has someone to carry on the lineage; my lifespan has long been exhausted, and I must depart." She closed her eyes and passed away. People said that the underworld officials acted out of the ordinary, much like earthly officials who move due to official duty.

    Chapter Thirteen: Guan Yu's Divination in the Late Ming Dynasty

    During the late Ming dynasty, Guan Yu, the deity, descended to a divination altar and gave a lifetime prophecy to a certain scholar: "You will reach the rank of a high-ranking official, but your lifespan will stop at sixty." Later, the scholar passed the imperial examinations and indeed reached the position of Zhongcheng (a high-ranking official). After the Qing dynasty established its rule, the scholar surrendered, and although his official position did not advance, he lived to be eighty years old. Once, he visited the altar, and Guan Yu descended again. The scholar thought that he must have accumulated good deeds, which allowed him to extend his life. He knelt and asked: "My official position and rank have been fulfilled, but my lifespan has exceeded this. Is it that longevity lies in the hands of people, even the gods do not know everything?" Guan Yu wrote in large letters: "I have always treated others with loyalty and filial piety. During the Jia Shen upheaval, you survived on your own, what does that have to do with me?" Counting on his fingers, he calculated that it was exactly when Emperor Chongzhen met his tragic end that the scholar turned sixty.

    467

    ○遇太岁煞神祸福各异徐坛长侍讲未遇时，赴都会试，如厕，见大肉块，遍身有眼，知为太岁。侍讲记某书云“鞭太岁者脱祸”，因取大棍与家丁次第笞击。每击一处，则遍身之眠愈加闪烁。是年成进士。蒋文肃公家中开井，得肉一块，方如桌面，刀刺不入，火灼不焦，蜿蜒而动，徐化为水。是年，文肃公卒。任香谷宗伯未遇时，走田埂上，遇一人口含一刀，两刀持两刀，披发赤面，伛身而过。宗伯行未半里，见赤面人入丧者之家，知是煞神。宗伯后登第。苏州唐姓者，立孝子坊，忽于衣帽中得白纸帖书一“煞”字，如胡桃大。是年，其家死者七人。

    　　○归安鱼怪俗传：张天师不过归安县。云前朝归安知县某，到任半年，与妻同宿，夜半闻撞门声，知县起视之。少顷，登床谓妻曰：“风扫门耳，无他异也。”其妻认为己夫，仍与同卧，而时觉其体有腥气，疑而未言。然自此归安大治，狱讼之事，判若神明。

    Explanation

    467

    Translation

    Encounter with Tai Sui and Sha Shen

    When Xu Tan, the Chief Lecturer, was not yet successful, he traveled to the capital for the imperial examinations. While using the restroom, he saw a large piece of meat with eyes all over its body and recognized it as Tai Sui. The Chief Lecturer remembered a book that said, "Beating Tai Sui can ward off misfortune," so he took a large stick and had his servant beat the meat block in sequence. Each time they struck a part of it, the eyes on the meat seemed to flicker more intensely. That year, he passed the imperial examinations.

    Jiang Wensu, the Minister of Justice, had a well dug in his home and found a piece of meat as large as a table. The meat was impervious to knife thrusts and fire, moving in a winding manner before gradually turning into water. That year, Jiang Wensu died.

    Ren Xianggu, the Grand Secretary, was not yet successful when he was walking along a field path and encountered a man with a knife in his mouth, holding two knives, with disheveled hair and a red face, leaning forward as he passed by. After walking half a mile, Ren saw the red-faced man enter the house of a grieving family and realized it was the sha shen. Later, Ren passed the imperial examinations.

    In Suzhou, a man surnamed Tang erected a filial son archway. Suddenly, he found a white paper note with the character "sha" written on it, as large as a walnut, in his clothes or hat. That year, seven people in his family died.

    The Fish Monster of Guian

    A popular saying goes: Zhang Tian Shi did not pass through Guian County. It is said that during the previous dynasty, a certain magistrate of Guian County, after taking office for half a year, shared his bed with his wife. One night, he heard the sound of someone knocking on the door. He got up to look and, after a while, returned to bed and told his wife, "It was just the wind sweeping past the door; there is nothing unusual." His wife mistook him for her husband and continued to sleep with him, but she occasionally smelled a fishy odor and doubted it but did not speak up. However, from then on, Guian County was well-governed, and legal cases were handled with the clarity and fairness of a divine judge.

    468

    数年后，张天师过归安，知县不敢迎谒。天师曰：“此县有妖气。”令人召知县妻，问曰：“尔记某年月日夜有撞门之事乎？”曰：“有之。”曰：“现在之夫，非尔夫也，乃黑鱼精也。尔之前夫已于撞门时为所食矣。”妻大骇，即求天师报仇。

    　　天师登坛作法，得大黑鱼，长数丈，俯伏坛下。天师曰：“尔罪当斩，姑念作令时颇有善政，特免汝死。”乃取大瓮囚鱼，符封其口，埋之大堂，以土筑公案镇之。鱼乞哀，天师曰：“待我再过此则释汝。”天师自此不复过归安云。

    　　○张忆娘苏州名妓张忆娘，色艺冠时，与蒋姓者素交好。蒋故巨室，花朝月夕，与忆娘游观音、灵岩等山，辄并辔而行。忆娘素明慧，欲托身于蒋，而蒋姬媵绝多，不甚属意，因与徽州陈通判者有终身之托。陈娶过门，蒋不得再通，大恚，百计离间之，诬控以奸拐。忆娘不得已，度为比丘，衣食犹资于陈。蒋更使人要而绝之，忆娘贫窘，自缢而亡。

    Explanation

    468

    Several years later, Zhang the Taoist Master passed through Gui'an, and the magistrate dared not come out to greet him. The Taoist Master said, "This county has an evil aura." He ordered someone to summon the magistrate's wife and asked, "Do you remember a certain year, month, day, and night when there was a knocking at the door?" She replied, "Yes, I do." The Taoist Master said, "The man you are with now is not your husband, but a black fish spirit. Your previous husband was already eaten by him during the knocking at the door." The wife was greatly frightened and immediately asked the Taoist Master to take revenge.

    The Taoist Master ascended the altar to perform rituals and caught a large black fish, several meters long, which lay prostrate at the foot of the altar. The Taoist Master said, "Your crime should be punished by death, but considering you had some good governance when you were in office, I will spare your life." He then took a large jar to imprison the fish, sealed its mouth with a talisman, buried it in the main hall, and built a public desk to suppress it. The fish begged for mercy, and the Taoist Master said, "I will release you when I pass through here again." The Taoist Master did not pass through Gui'an again.

    ○ Zhang Yimeng, a famous courtesan from Suzhou, was renowned for her beauty and talent. She had a long-standing friendship with someone surnamed Jiang, who was a wealthy and influential person. On flower festivals and moonlit nights, Jiang and Zhang Yimeng would often ride together to places like Guanyin and Lingyan Mountains. Zhang Yimeng was always bright and wise and wanted to entrust herself to Jiang, but Jiang had many concubines and did not pay much attention to her. She then made a lifelong commitment with Chen, a magistrate from Huizhou. When Chen married her, Jiang could no longer have contact with her and became very angry. He used every means to create discord between them and falsely accused her of adultery and abduction. Zhang Yimeng had no choice but to become a Buddhist nun, and her food and clothing were still provided by Chen. Jiang sent someone to kill her, and Zhang Yimeng, poor and destitute, hanged herself to death.

    469

    居亡何，蒋早起进粥，忽头晕气绝，至一官衙，二弓丁掖之前，旁有人呼曰：“蒋某，汝事须六年后始讯，何遽至此？”呼者之面貌，乃蒋平日门下奔走士也，曾遣以问忆娘者，死三年矣。蒋惊醒，自此精气恍惚，饮食少进。

    　　有元妙观道士张某，精法律，为筑坛持咒作禳解法。三日后，道士曰：“冤魄已到，我不审其姓氏，试取大镜泼以明水，当有一女子现形。”召家人视之，宛然忆娘也。道士曰：“吾所能力制者，妖孽狐狸之类。今男女冤谴，非吾所能驱除。”竟拂衣去。蒋为忆娘作七昼夜道场，意欲超度之，卒不能遣。延苏州名医叶天士，赠以千金。药未至口，便见纤纤白手按覆之，或无故自泼于地。蒋病益增，六年而殁。

    Explanation

    469

    After a while, Jiang woke up early to drink porridge, but suddenly he became dizzy and lost consciousness. He arrived at an official building, where two archers supported him. Someone nearby called out, "Jiang, your matter will not be addressed until six years have passed. Why have you come so soon?" The person calling out had a familiar appearance; it was one of Jiang's former servants or attendants who had once been sent to inquire about Yin娘. This person had been dead for three years. Jiang woke up in shock and became mentally confused, eating very little.

    There was a Taoist priest named Zhang at the Yuanmiao Temple who was skilled in legal matters. He built an altar, chanted spells, and performed rituals to ward off evil. Three days later, the Taoist priest said, "The spirit of vengeance has arrived. I do not know its name, so let us take a large mirror and wash it with clear water. A woman will appear in the mirror." The priest summoned the family to look, and the woman appeared in the mirror as Yin娘. The Taoist priest said, "I can only control spirits or supernatural beings like foxes. The current situation involving a man and woman's mutual vengeance is beyond my ability to resolve." He then left in a huff.

    Jiang conducted a seven-day and night ritual for Yin娘, intending to help her achieve liberation, but ultimately failed to resolve the issue. He invited the famous physician Ye Tianshi from Suzhou and gave him a thousand gold pieces. The medicine did not reach Jiang's mouth, as a delicate white hand covered it, or it was accidentally spilled on the ground. Jiang's illness worsened, and he died six years later.

    470

    蒋氏从孙漪园，犹藏忆娘小照：戴乌纱髻，着天青罗裙，眉目秀媚，以左手簪花而笑，为当时杨子鹤笔也。

    　　○飞星入南斗苏松道韩青岩，通天文，尝为予言：“宰宝山时，六月捕蝗，至野田中。四鼓起，坐胡床，督率书役，见客星飞入南斗，私记占验书：”见此灾者，一月之内当暴亡。法宜剪发寸许，东西禹步三匝，便可移祸他人，尔时我即麾去书役，依法行之。居亡何，署中司书记者李某无故以小刀剖腹而死，我竟无恙。李乃我荐卷门生，年少能文，不料为我替灾，心为怅然。“余戏谓韩曰：”公言占验之术固神矣，然如我辈全不知天文，往往夜坐见飞星来往甚多。倘有入南斗者，竟不知厌胜法，为之奈何？“曰：”君辈不知天文者，虽见飞星入南斗，亦无害。“

    Explanation

    470

    Peng's grandson, Yiyuan, still keeps a small portrait of Yinniang: She wears a black hat with her hair tied up in a bun, a light blue silk skirt, and has beautiful and charming features. She holds a flower in her left hand and smiles, painted by Yang Zihuo at the time.

    ○ Flying Star Enters the Southern Dipper

    Han Qingyan, the governor of Suzhou and Songjiang, was knowledgeable in astronomy and once told me: "When I was serving as the governor of Baoshan County, in June, I went to the fields to capture locusts. At the fourth watch of the night, I sat on a trundle bed, supervising the clerks. I saw a guest star fly into the Southern Dipper and privately recorded the divination book: 'Seeing this disaster, one will die suddenly within a month. The method is to cut off a small amount of hair, walk three times east and west, and it can transfer the disaster to others.' At that time, I immediately dismissed the clerks and followed the method. Soon after, the clerk in charge of records in the office, Mr. Li, died unexpectedly by cutting open his stomach with a small knife, while I remained unharmed. Li was my recommended student, young and talented, but I did not expect him to take on my disaster, and I felt very disappointed." I jokingly said to Han: "Your divination technique is indeed amazing, but what if we, who do not know astronomy, often sit at night and see many flying stars? What should we do if one of them enters the Southern Dipper and we do not know the method to ward off evil?" He replied: "Those who do not know astronomy, even if they see a flying star enter the Southern Dipper, will not be harmed."

    471

    余曰：“然则公又何苦知天文，多此一事，而自祸福人耶？”韩大笑，不能答。

    　　○杨妃见梦康熙间，苏州汪山樵先生讳俊选陕西兴平县，宿马嵬驿中。梦一女子，容貌绝世，明翠羽，投牒而言曰：“妾有墓地为人所侵，幸明府哀而察之。”汪惊醒，询土人，曰：“此间惟有杨娘娘墓道，唐时改葬后，墓址原有数十亩宽，自宋明以来，为樵牧所侵，渐无馀地。”汪为清理，果有旧碑记存墓侧土中，题“大唐贵妃杨氏墓”。乃为别置界石，兼买树百株植其上，春秋设二祭焉。

    Explanation

    471

    I said:

    "Then why did you go to such lengths to know astronomy, add this one more thing, and predict people's fortunes and misfortunes?" Han Yu laughed and could not answer.

    ○ During the Kangxi period of the Qing dynasty, Mr. Wang Shanyao, a scholar from Suzhou, served in Xingping County, Shaanxi Province. While staying at the Ma嵬 post station, he had a dream of a woman of extraordinary beauty, dressed in fine attire, who presented a petition and said, "My tomb has been encroached upon by others. I hope you, the magistrate, will take pity on me and investigate the matter." Wang woke up in shock and asked the locals, who replied, "The only tomb in this area is that of Yang Guifei. After her reburial during the Tang dynasty, the tomb originally covered dozens of acres. Since the Song and Ming dynasties, it has been gradually reduced in size by woodcutters and shepherds." Wang cleared the area and found an old stone tablet buried in the ground, inscribed with "Tomb of the Noble Consort Yang of the Great Tang." He then set up boundary stones, bought a hundred trees to plant, and held two annual sacrifices.

    472

    ○曹能始记前生明季曹能始先生，登进士后，过仙霞岭，山光水色，恍如前世所游。暮宿旅店，闻邻家有妇哭甚哀，问之，曰：“为其亡夫作三十周年耳。”询其死年月日，即先生之生年月日也。遂入其家，历举某屋某径，毫发不爽。其家环惊，共来审视。曹亦凄然涕下，曰：“某书屋内有南向竹树数十株，我尚有文稿未终篇者，未知犹存否？”其家曰：“自主人捐馆后，恐夫人见书室而神伤，故至今犹关锁也。”曹命开之，则尘凝数寸，遗稿乱书，宛然具在，惟前妻已白发盈头，不可复认矣。曹以家财分半与之，俾终馀年。

    Explanation

    472

    A Record of Cao Nengshi's Past Life

    In the late Ming dynasty, Master Cao Nengshi, after passing the imperial examination as a Jinshi, passed through Xianxia Mountain. The light of the mountains and the color of the water seemed as if he had traveled here in a previous life. In the evening, he stayed at an inn and heard a woman weeping very sadly next door. He asked about it, and she said, "It was for the thirtieth anniversary of her deceased husband's death." When he inquired about the date of her husband's death, it turned out to be the same as the date of his own birth. He then went to her home and described specific details of her house and paths, with no mistake. They were all surprised and came to examine it together. Moved to tears, Cao Nengshi said, "In a certain study room, there are dozens of bamboo trees facing south. I still have unfinished manuscripts, and I wonder if they are still there?" The woman replied, "After the master left this world, we were afraid that the widow would be emotionally distressed seeing the study room, so it has been locked up until now." Cao ordered it to be opened, and found that dust had accumulated several inches thick, with unfinished manuscripts and scattered books still there as they were. But the former wife had already turned gray-haired and could no longer be recognized. Cao divided half of his wealth with her, so that she could live out the rest of her years.

    473

    余按《文苑英华》白敏中书滑州太守崔彦武事：崔记前生为杜明福妻，骑马直抵杜家，而明福老矣。乃说旧事，取所藏金钗于垣中，施宅为寺，号明福寺。

    　　与此相类。

    　　○江南客寓涤斋先生为诸先生时，在京师贾家胡同。有店号“江南客寓”，厅屋三间，中一间甚洁，住者绝少；先生居之，了无他异。一日外出，托所亲某管其衣物。

    　　夜睡至三鼓，忽室中尽明，时并无灯烛，所亲骇，揭帐视之，见一长人黑色，手提其头，血淋漓，对面直立不动，呼曰：“尔何得居此？”所亲狂奔，出告店主。

    Explanation

    473

    I have examined the Wen Yuan Ying Hua, which records an incident involving Bai Minzhong, the governor of Huazhou, and Cui Yanwu. Cui recorded that in a past life she was the wife of Du Mingfu, and she rode a horse directly to Du's home. By then, Du Mingfu was old. She then recounted old stories and took out the gold hairpin hidden in the wall. She donated the house to be turned into a temple and named it Mingfu Temple.

    This is similar to the following story:

    When Mr. Dizhai was among the scholars in Beijing, he stayed in Jia Hutong. There was a shop called "Jiangnan Guesthouse." The hall had three rooms, with one in the middle being very clean, and very few people stayed there. Mr. Dizhai lived there, and there were no other unusual events. One day when he went out, he entrusted a close friend to take care of his clothes. He slept until the third drumbeat of the night. Suddenly, the room became completely bright, even though there were no lamps or candles. His friend was startled and lifted the curtain to look. He saw a tall black person holding its head, with blood flowing, standing straight in front of him without moving. It shouted, "How did you get here?" His friend ran out and told the innkeeper.

    474

    主人曰：“此屋素不安静，尔乃必欲居之，奈何？”

    　　次日，先生归，告之故。先生曰：“此必有鬼欲申冤耳，我在此，何不现形耶？”大书一状，向空焚之，以为尔果有冤，当于今晚赴诉。是夕，先生复睡，未一更，所见果如所说，但持一血头，跪而不立。先生问：“何人何冤？”持头者以手指口，竟无一语。次日，亦不复见。

    　　先生又常于园中月下见黑物一团，大如浴盆，追奔树下，以脚蹋之，随脚而灭。次日，视其靴袜，黑如烟煤，并足皆黑。

    　　○荆波宛在本朝佟国相巡抚甘肃，按站行至伏羌县，梦神呼云：“速走！速走！”佟不以为意。次晚，梦如初，且云：“欲报我恩，但记‘荆波宛在’可耳。”佟惊起。

    　　亟走三日，而伏羌县沉为湖，卒不解救者为何神。后出巡至建昌野渡，有关公庙上书“荆波宛在”四字，佟入拜谒，大为修葺，今焕然犹存。

    Explanation

    474

    The master said, "This house has always been restless, yet you insistently want to live in it, what can be done?"

    The next day, the scholar returned and told him the reason. The scholar said, "There must be a ghost seeking justice. If I am here, why do you not show your form?" He wrote a complaint and burned it in the air, thinking that if the ghost truly had a grievance, it should appear tonight to sue. That night, the scholar slept again, and before the first watch, what he saw was exactly as described: the ghost was holding a bloody head, kneeling but not standing. The scholar asked, "Who are you, and what grievance do you have?" The ghost pointed to his mouth, but said nothing. The next day, it was not seen again.

    The scholar also often saw a black mass, as large as a bathtub, in the moonlight in the garden. He chased it to under a tree and stomped on it with his foot, and it disappeared with the footstep. The next day, he looked at his boots and socks, which were black like soot, and his feet were also black.
