If this is true, I ask the gods to guide me again." The other examiners laughed even more, and when Zhang was about to discard the article, they poked his hat again with the stick. Zhang did not read the article again but directly took it to the main examiner, who was already asleep. He knocked on the door and requested an audience, explaining that the gods had guided him to find a good article. The main examiner, Se Jinxi, read the article and said, "This article is very good and deserves a place in the top ranks. Why do you need to use supernatural means to educate?" The other examiners remained silent. When the results were announced, Zhang saw that the article was indeed selected. The other examiners laughed and told Zhang, "We played a trick on you." Zhang replied sternly, "This is not that I was fooled by you, but rather that you were fooled by the gods." The other examiners were also convinced by his words.

    511

    ○狸称表兄六合老梅庵多狸，夜出迷人，在窗外必呼人字，称曰表兄。人相戒不答，则彼自去。有夏姓少年读书庵中，月夜闻呼，疑为人也，开窗答之。见一妇人招手，而貌颇粗恶，意欲相拒。竟被拥抱入室，扯脱下衣，大吸其势，精尽乃去。据云其力甚大，不能自主，且毛孔腥臊，所经之处，皆有馀臭，经月始散。

    　　○陆大司马坟杭州陆大司马家方卜葬时，其子某听形家言，以千金买清波门外地。初下窆时，启得一棺，形制甚伟。众戚友咸劝毋动旧棺，别穿一穴。陆不可，曰：“我以重价买地，彼何人敢占我耶？”掘而弃之。

    Explanation

    511

    Translation

    The Cat Calling "Cousin"

    At Liuhe Old Plum Hermitage, there were many cats. At night, they would appear and bewitch people, always calling out a person's name when they appeared at the window, saying "cousin." People warned each other not to respond, and if no one answered, the cat would leave on its own. One summer, a young man named Xia was studying at the hermitage. One moonlit night, he heard the call and, thinking it was a human, opened the window to respond. He saw a woman beckoning to him, but her appearance was quite rough and ugly. He tried to resist her, but he was suddenly embraced and dragged into the room. She tore off his clothes and performed a forceful act on him, ejaculating and then leaving. He reported that the cat's strength was immense, making it impossible for him to resist, and that its pores emitted a foul, pungent odor. The area where the cat had been was left with a lingering foul smell that took a month to dissipate.

    The Tomb of General Lu

    In Hangzhou, the family of General Lu was in the process of selecting a burial site for the general. His son, following the advice of a geomancer, bought land outside the Qingbo Gate for a thousand gold pieces. When they first began to lower the coffin, they discovered an old, well-made coffin. The relatives and friends all advised against disturbing the old coffin and suggested creating a new burial site. However, the son of General Lu refused, saying, "I bought this land for a high price; who dares to claim it?" They dug up the old coffin and discarded it.

    512

    是夕，陆得病，自批其颊，口称葛老太太，云：“汝夺我安宅，以而父为尚书耶？我儿子亦前明侍郎也。”问：“为谁？”曰：“葛寅亮。于谊为乡亲，于科名为前辈。葬汝父，抛我骨，汝父安乎？”陆大司马夫人率全家注请延僧斋醮，烧纸钱十万，葛老太太似有允意。忽又作侍郎公语曰：“伤我母坟，不可逭也。”

    　　少顷，又作族祖梯霞先生口吻，从中说情。侍郎终不允，卒索其命去。

    　　当鬼崇时，陆有戚舒十九者，新馆选翰林归，在旁劝曰：“陆某以价买坟，何名为夺？鬼在陆口骂曰：”后生小子，新得一官，敢来亻言？恐自身难保耳！“

    　　陆亡后月馀，舒亦亡。

    　　○鬼受禁上虞令邢某，与妻素不睦，因口角批其颊，妻怒自缢。三日后，见形为祟，伺邢与妾卧，便吹冷风揭帐，或灭其灯。邢怒，请道士持咒作法，摄鬼于东厢，而以符封之，加官印焉，鬼竟不至。

    Explanation

    512

    That night, Lu fell ill, slapped his own cheek, and called out to the spirit of Ge Lǎotài, saying, "You have taken my peaceful home, do you think your father was a minister? My son was also a minister in the previous dynasty." He asked, "Who are you?" She replied, "I am Ge Yinliang. We are distant relatives by kinship and senior by scholarly achievement. Burying your father while throwing away my bones, is your father at peace?" Lady Lu, the wife of the Grand Marshal, led the entire family in inviting monks for a ritual ceremony, burning ten thousand paper money. Ge Lǎotài seemed to agree. Suddenly, she spoke in the voice of the minister, saying, "Injuring my mother's tomb, this cannot be avoided."

    A short while later, she spoke in the voice of the clan ancestor Ti Xī, mediating. The minister finally did not agree, and took his life and departed.

    During the period of ghostly harassment, Lu had a relative named Shū, who was newly appointed as a Hanlin scholar and returned home. He advised, "Lu bought the tomb with money, how can it be called taking? The ghost scolded him in Lu's mouth, saying, 'Young man, newly appointed to an official position, dare to speak disrespectfully? Perhaps you yourself cannot be safe!'"

    A month after Lu's death, Shū also died.

    ○ A Ghost is Bound by a Ban

    The magistrate of Shangyu, Xing, had always been at odds with his wife. One day, they quarreled, and he slapped her cheek. Enraged, she hanged herself. Three days later, she appeared as a ghost, tormenting Xing when he was with his concubine. She would blow cold air, lift the curtains, or extinguish his lamp. Xing became angry and invited a Daoist to perform a ritual, capturing the ghost in the eastern wing and sealing it with a talisman, adding an official seal. The ghost did not come.

    513

    亡何，邢调知钱塘，后任上虞者来开厢房，鬼得出，遂附一小婢身作祟如故。

    　　后任官呼鬼语曰：“夫人与邢公有仇，与小婢无涉，何故害之？”鬼曰：“非敢害丫鬟，我借附他身以便求公。”问：“何求？”曰：“送我到钱塘邢某处。”

    　　曰：“夫人何不自行？”曰：“我枉死之鬼，沿路有河神拦截，非公用印文关递不可，并求签两差押送。”问：“差何人？”曰：“陈贵、滕盛。”二人者，皆已故役也。后任官如其言，焚批文解送之。

    　　邢公方在寝室晚膳，其妾忽倒于地大呼曰：“汝太无良！汝逼我死，乃禁我于东厢受饥饿耶！我今已归来，不与汝干休。”自此，钱塘署中日夜不宁。邢不得已，再请道士作法，加符用印，封移钱塘狱中。鬼临去呼曰：“汝太丧心！前封我于东厢，犹是房舍；今我何罪，而置我于狱乎？我有以报汝矣。”

    Explanation

    513

    Shortly after, Xing Tiao became the magistrate of Qiantang. The next official who took over Shangyu opened the annex room, allowing the ghost to be released. The ghost immediately attached itself to a young girl and caused trouble as before.

    The next official called out to the ghost and said, "Madam, you have a grudge against Xing Tiao, not against the young girl. Why are you harming her?" The ghost replied, "I am not harming the maid. I am using her body to seek Xing Tiao." The official asked, "What do you seek?" The ghost said, "Send me to Xing Tiao in Qiantang."

    The official asked, "Why can't you go yourself?" The ghost replied, "I am a wrongfully dead spirit. Along the way, river gods intercept me. I cannot pass through without an official seal text and must be accompanied by two appointed officials." The official asked, "Who are the officials?" The ghost said, "Chen Gui and Teng Sheng." Both were deceased officials. The official followed the ghost's instructions, burned the warrant, and escorted it.

    At that moment, Xing Tiao was having dinner in his bedroom. His concubine suddenly fell to the ground and cried out, "You are too wicked! You forced me to die and confined me in the annex room to starve! Now that I have returned, I will not let it go!" From then on, the Qiantang office was restless day and night. Xing Tiao had no choice but to again invite a Taoist priest to perform a ritual, adding talismans and seals, and sealing the ghost in a prison in Qiantang. As the ghost was leaving, it called out, "You are too heartless! Before, you confined me in the annex room, which was still a house; now, what crime have I committed that you are putting me in a prison? I will seek revenge on you!"

    514

    未逾月，狱有重犯自缢死，邢因此被劾罢官。大惧，誓将削发为僧，云游天下。同寅官有捐资助其衣钵者，未及行而病卒。

    　　○狐鬼入腹李鹤峰侍郎之子，字医山，辛巳翰林，能诗文，兼好宋儒理学。灯下读书，忽两女子绝美，来与戏狎，李不为动。少顷，李晚膳毕，忽腹中呼曰：“我附魂茄子上，汝啖茄即啖我也，我已居汝腹中，汝复何逃？”即灯下女子声。李自此两目瞠然，若迷若痴，或以手自批其颊；或大雨，首顶一石跪雨中，衣裳淋漓，不敢入内；或对人膜拜，拉之不起。面色黄瘦，日渐不支。

    　　鬼常借李君手作字与人酬答。其同年蒋君士铨往视之，问：“汝貌甚佳，何不来诱我而必从李君耶？”李手书二字曰：“无缘。”蒋又问：“汝绝世佳人，何为居腹中污秽之地？”李手书二字骂曰：“下足。”

    Explanation

    514

    Within a month, a serious criminal hanged himself in prison, leading to Xing being impeached and dismissed from office. Filled with fear, he vowed to shave his head and become a monk, wandering the world. Officials of the same rank donated to support his robes and begging bowl, but before he could leave, he died of illness.

    ○ A Fox Spirit Enters the Stomach

    Li Hefeng, a high-ranking official, had a son named Yi, who was a scholar in the year of Xinsi (1761). Yi was skilled in poetry and prose, and also fond of the Neo-Confucian philosophy of the Song dynasty. One evening, while reading by the lamp, two extremely beautiful women suddenly appeared and tried to play with him, but Yi did not move. A little while later, after finishing his evening meal, he suddenly heard a voice call out from his stomach: "I have attached my soul to the eggplant. If you eat the eggplant, you eat me too. I am already in your stomach, how can you escape?" It was the voice of the woman under the lamp. From then on, Yi's eyes became wide and blank, as if he was in a daze. Sometimes he would slap his own cheeks, or during heavy rain, he would kneel in the rain with a stone on his head, his clothes soaked, and he dared not go inside. Sometimes he would kneel and worship people, and could not be pulled up. His face became yellow and thin, and he gradually lost strength.

    The ghost often used Yi's hand to write and respond to people. His contemporary, Jiang Junshi, went to see him and asked, "You are very beautiful, why didn't you come to tempt me but had to go through Li?" Yi wrote two characters with his hand: "No fate." Jiang asked again, "You are a beauty of this world, why do you reside in the filthy place of my stomach?" Yi wrote two characters and scolded: "Lowly feet."

    515

    时江西巡抚吴公与侍郎善，乃招李往，为延张天师，设坛于滕王阁。斋三日，诵咒三日，其法官悬牌曰：“三月十五日拿妖。”临期，观者如堵，天师上坐，法官旁坐，令李跪，张其口向法师。法师伸两指入其口，撮而掷之，一小狐如猫从口中出，呼曰：“我为姊探信，不料被擒，姊慎毋出。”腹中应声曰：“唯。”

    　　方知腹中尚有一妖。

    　　天师封符于坛，投之大江。李微觉神清，而腹中叹息之声大作，曰：“我与汝有宿世冤。因寻汝不着，故拉仙姑同来，不料反为彼祸，使我心转不安。我愈不饶汝矣。”言毕，腹痛不止。天师问法官：“李翰林可救乎？”法官取镜照其腹曰：“此是翰林前生冤鬼，非妖也。法不能治。”天师以告中丞，中丞亦无奈何，仍送李还家养病，遂卒。

    Explanation

    515

    During the reign of Governor Wu of Jiangxi, who was friendly with a Deputy Minister, Governor Wu invited Li to come, and they invited Master Zhang, a Taoist priest, to set up an altar at the Tengwang Pavilion. They fasted for three days and chanted spells for three days. The priest hung a sign saying, "Catch the demon on the fifteenth day of the third month." On the day, spectators gathered like a wall. Master Zhang sat above, and the priest sat beside him. They ordered Li to kneel and open his mouth toward the priest. The priest inserted two fingers into his mouth, plucked something, and threw it out. A small fox like a cat came out of his mouth, calling out, "I was sent to find news for my sister, but unexpectedly I was captured. Sister, be careful not to come out." The voice from inside replied, "Yes."

    They only then realized there was still a demon inside.

    Master Zhang sealed a talisman on the altar and threw it into the Yangtze River. Li felt slightly clearer, but the sighing sound from inside became very loud, saying, "I have a karmic grudge with you from past lives. Because I couldn't find you, I dragged the fairy lady along. Unfortunately, it turned into a disaster for her, making my mind even more uneasy. I will not spare you." After finishing, the stomach pain did not stop. Master Zhang asked the priest, "Can Li be saved?" The priest took a mirror and looked at his stomach, saying, "This is a ghost from Li's past life, not a demon. Magical tools cannot cure it." Master Zhang informed the governor, who also had no solution, and sent Li back home to rest and recuperate, and he died.

    516

    ○怪诈人父李玉双孝廉家有婢，名春云，颇有姿，年十五，李欲纳为妾，与其妻有成说矣。春云白日见瓦上一男子下，拥其髻而嗅之曰：“汝发甚香，当大贵，宜从我，勿从主人。主人处馆穷儒，虽中举，不过一教官终耳。你向主人言，命其让我，且供我酒馔，我便赘汝家。”玉双闻之大怒，然亦无如何。是夜，怪竟来与婢配合。婢求主人具酒馔，如其言，则日夜安宁；否则，飞砖掷瓦之祸毕作。玉双不得已，与人谋将此屋招人承买。玉双馆于望仙桥施氏，不常在家。一日者，商人孙耕文来看屋，敲门，有苍须老翁衣灰鼠袍出迎，摇手曰：“此屋是我祖遗，并未出卖，勿听小儿玉双妄语，私相授受，将来要受讼累。”孙大骇，走告玉双，责以“父在，子不得自专。”玉双曰：“先君亡已十余年，家中并无此翁。”乃知为怪所挪揄，冒认为父，彼此大笑。

    Explanation

    516

    A Ghostly Father in Ruyuang's Home

    Ruyuang, a scholar of the Imperial Examinations, had a maid named Chunyun, who was quite beautiful and fifteen years old. He intended to make her his concubine, and he had already agreed with his wife. One day, Chunyun saw a man descending from the roof during daylight, grabbing her hair and smelling it. He said, "Your hair is very fragrant, and you will become very wealthy. You should follow me, not your master. Your master is a poor scholar who, even if he passes the exams, will only become a minor official. Tell your master to let me in, and provide me with food and drink, and I will marry into your family." Ruyuang heard this and became very angry, but he could do nothing about it. That night, the ghost came and had relations with the maid. The maid asked her master to provide food and drink as the ghost had requested, and if he did, they would be peaceful day and night; otherwise, the ghost would cause havoc by throwing bricks and tiles. Ruyuang had no choice but to conspire with someone to sell the house. Ruyuang was staying at the home of Shi at the Wangxian Bridge and was not often at home. One day, a merchant named Sun Gengwen came to look at the house. When he knocked on the door, an elderly man with gray hair and wearing a gray rat fur robe came out to greet him. He waved his hand and said, "This house is my ancestor's legacy and has not been sold. Do not listen to the foolish words of Ruyuang, who is trying to sell it privately. In the future, you will have to deal with legal troubles." Sun was greatly shocked and went to tell Ruyuang, rebuking him, "Your father is still alive, and you cannot act on your own." Ruyuang said, "My father has been dead for over ten years, and there is no such old man in my home." It turned out that the ghost was mocking Ruyuang, pretending to be his father, and they both laughed.

    517

    自后，人知屋有怪，屡卖不成。玉双乃命婢父母领女还家，勿索身价。婢嫠面剪发，誓不肯归。其母虑为怪所害，以绳缚之，捆载还家，另嫁一士人。怪竟不来。

    　　○皂荚下二鬼丹阳南门外吕姓者，有皂荚园，取利甚大。每结实时，吕氏父子守之，防有偷者。一夕月下，其父坐石上看树，树下有蓬发然从土中出，惧而不视，呼其子往曳之。有红衣女子闯然起，父惊仆地，其子狂奔入室。女追之，至大门，忽僵立不动，一足在门外，一足在门内。子大呼，家人持刀杖齐集，畏其冷气射人，俱不敢近。女子从容起行，伛身入床下，遂不见。其子持姜汤灌醒其父，扶以归，招邻人共掘床下，果一朱棺中有红衣女尸，如夜所见。嗣后，父子不敢看园守树矣。

    Explanation

    517

    From then on, people knew that the house was haunted, and it repeatedly failed to sell. Yu Shuang then ordered the servant's parents to take her daughter home, without demanding a dowry. The servant, with a shaved face and cut hair, swore not to return. Her mother, fearing that the strange phenomenon would harm her, tied her up and loaded her onto a vehicle to return home, where she was married to another scholar. The strange phenomenon did not come again.

    ○ Two Ghosts Under the Soapberry Tree

    A person surnamed Lu living south of the DanYang Gate had a soapberry orchard that made a lot of profit. Every time the fruits ripened, the Lu family's father and son guarded it to prevent thieves. One night under the moonlight, the father sat on a stone watching the trees. Under the tree, a head of disheveled hair emerged from the ground. Frightened and not looking, he called his son to pull it. Suddenly, a red-clothed woman stood up. The father was shocked and fell to the ground, and his son ran wildly into the room. The woman chased him to the main gate, where she suddenly stood stiffly and did not move, with one foot outside the door and one foot inside. The son shouted loudly, and family members gathered with knives and sticks, afraid that her cold breath would harm them, and none dared to approach. The woman calmly stood up and walked, bent her body, and entered under the bed, and then disappeared. The son held ginger soup to revive his father, helped him back home, and called neighbors to dig under the bed. Indeed, there was a red coffin with a red-clothed female corpse inside, just as seen at night. Thereafter, the father and son no longer dared to guard the orchard and trees.

    518

    逾三日，皂荚树下又有仆于地者，吕氏子亦灌醒之，问其由来，曰：“我西邻也，见君家皂荚甚多，无人看守，故来偷窃。不意见树下有无头人以手招我，我故骇而仆地。”其子又集人掘之，得黑棺，埋一无头尸，皆僵不腐。聚而焚之，其怪遂绝。

    　　○中山王江宁布政司署，为徐中山王故府，中有宁安殿，供奉中山王像。一几一椅，灰高数寸，例不敢拭，拭者有灾。帐幕桌帏，俱以黄绫为之。乾隆四十年，方伯某上任之日，即往行香，心念中山王爵虽贵，亦人臣也，帷幔黄色，似乎太僭，命以红绫易之。是夕，火光照耀。急往视之，则一帐一帷，俱已焚尽，而几案丝毫无伤。细查并无引火之物，于是悚然怖惧，仍以黄色绫易之。

    Explanation

    518

    After three days, there was another person who fell to the ground under the soapberry tree. The son of the Lu family also revived him by watering him and asked him about the reason. The person replied, "I am from the west neighbor. I saw that your family has many soapberries, and no one is guarding them, so I came to steal. I did not expect to see a headless person under the tree waving at me, so I was frightened and fell to the ground." The son then gathered people to dig it up and found a black coffin, burying a headless body. All were stiff but not rotten. They gathered and burned it, and the strange phenomenon thus ceased.

    ○ The Jiangning Provincial Administration Office of the Prince of Zhongshan was the former residence of Prince Xu of Zhongshan. There is a Ning'an Hall inside, where the statue of Prince of Zhongshan is enshrined. A table and a chair, with ash several inches high, it is customary not to wipe them, as those who do will suffer misfortune. The curtains and tablecloths are all made of yellow silk. In the 40th year of the Qianlong reign, on the day the provincial governor took office, he immediately went to pay respects. He thought that although the Prince of Zhongshan was noble, he was still a subject, and the yellow curtains seem too presumptuous, so he ordered them to be replaced with red silk. That night, the firelight shone. He hurriedly went to see it, and then one curtain and one tent were completely burned, but the table and chair were undamaged. Upon careful inspection, there was no source of fire, and he was then filled with fear. He then ordered them to be replaced with yellow silk again.

    519

    ○状元不能拔贡状元黄轩自言：作秀才时，屡试高等。乙酉年，上江学使梁瑶峰爱其才，以拔贡许之。临试之日，头晕目眩，握笔一字不能下。梁不得已，以休宁县生员吴鹤龄代之，及榜出后，病乃霍然。从此灰心于功名，自望得一县佐州判官心足矣。

    　　后三年，竟连捷，以至廷试第一。而吴鹤龄远馆溧水，以伤寒病终，终于贡生。

    　　○谨权量方敏悫公署直隶按察使时，饶阳民妇侯萧氏拒奸被杀，有周秋者迹可疑，而狡诈不肯吐实，悬案二载。公阅案牍尽三鼓，坐而假寐，梦一人持素纸，下宽上窄，缺左角，中有方孔，孔下有“谨权量”三字。寤后细思：“周”字下宽左缺，而“谨权量”三字皆“土”字在下，移土之文于方孔之上，则成“周”字，且月令“谨权量”三字乃秋政也，凶人为周秋无疑矣。一讯而服。此事载公行状中。

    Explanation

    519

    Final Translation

    ○The Top Scholar Who Could Not Be Selected for Imperial Service: Zhuangyuan Huang Xuan recounted: "When I was a scholar, I consistently achieved high scores in exams. In the year Bǐyǒu, the provincial examiner of Jiangnan, Mr. Liang Yao Feng, admired my talent and promised me a place as a selected scholar. On the day of the exam, I became dizzy and disoriented, unable to write a single word with the pen. Mr. Liang had no choice but to appoint Wu Heling from Xiuning County as my substitute. After the results were announced, my illness suddenly disappeared. From then on, I lost all hope of further advancement and was content with just becoming a county magistrate or state judge.

    Three years later, I unexpectedly passed consecutively and ultimately ranked first in the imperial examination. However, Wu Heling was appointed to a distant post in Lishui and died of a severe cold, remaining a selected scholar until his death."

    ○The Case of the Woman Resisting Rape: When Fang Minque was serving as the Censorate of Zhili, a case involving a woman from Raoyang named Hou Xiaoshi who resisted rape and was killed came to light. There was a man named Zhou Qiu whose behavior was suspicious, and he was cunning and refused to confess. The case remained unresolved for two years. After reviewing the case files until late at night, Fang Minque sat down to rest and fell into a light sleep. He dreamed of a person holding a white sheet of paper, which was wider at the bottom and narrower at the top, with a missing left corner and a square hole in the middle. Below the hole, there were the three characters "谨权量" (jǐn quán liáng). Upon waking, he carefully considered: "The character '周' (zhōu) has a wide bottom and a missing left side, and the three characters '谨权量' (jǐn quán liáng) all have '土' (tǔ) at the bottom. Moving the '土' character to the top of the square hole forms the character '周' (zhōu), and according to the monthly calendar, '谨权量' (jǐn quán liáng) refers to the autumn administration. Therefore, the culprit must be Zhou Qiu." Upon questioning, Zhou Qiu confessed. This case is recorded in Fang Minque's biography.

    520

    ○拘忌有侍郎某，性多拘忌，每遇人谈有“死丧”二字，必作喷嚏以啐散之；路逢殡柩，则急往亲友家，解下衣帽，扑散数次，以为将晦气撒在人家，与己无与矣。

    　　又薛生白常往李侍郎家看病，清晨往，待至日午始地侍郎以面向内，以背向外，两公子扶之而行；坐定诊脉，口答病源，终不回顾。薛大骇，疑其面有恶疾，故不向客。问其家人，家人云：“主人貌甚丰满，并无恶疾，所以然者，以某日喜神方在东，故不肯背之而出。又是日辰巳有冲，故必正午方出耳。”

    Explanation

    520

    A certain vice-minister, known for his excessive caution, would sneeze and spit whenever someone mentioned the words "death" or "funeral," believing it would ward off bad luck. If he encountered a funeral procession on the road, he would rush to a relative's house, take off his clothes and hat, and shake them several times, thinking that he was scattering the bad luck onto someone else, thus avoiding it himself.

    Dr. Xue Shengbai often visited Vice-Minister Li's house for consultations. In the morning, he would arrive, but the vice-minister would not leave until noon, facing inward and with his back to the door, supported by his two sons. Once seated, the vice-minister would diagnose the pulse and answer questions, but he would never turn around. When asked why, it was explained that the vice-minister's face was full and healthy, with no signs of illness. The reason for his behavior was that on that day, the auspicious direction was to the east, so he would not turn his back to it. Additionally, the hours of Chen and Si were in conflict, so he would only leave at noon.

    521

    ○奇术康熙间，成其范善风角。三藩之变，成为中书，凡千里外用兵之事，日有所奏，皆奇验，以此官至理藩院侍郎。常赴席东华门张参领家，已坐定矣，忽脱冠带置几上，谓主人曰：“我腹痛，将如厕。”出门呼其舆夫，飞奔而归。舆夫问故，摇手曰：“我与汝三人皆此日劫数中人，我不敢不到，故留衣冠以厌之。”

    　　言未毕，东华门火药局火发，延烧数十家，张参领家已为灰烬。

    　　又有计小堂者，以妖言惑众，充发黑龙江。至旅店中，饭桌仄小，解差三人不能同坐，小堂以手扯之，顷刻桌长三尺。差役曰：“汝以当得罪，尚不悛改，而作此狡狯乎！”小堂怒而起，拉其所乘马送入墙内，仅留一尾在外摇摆。差哀求，乃拔其尾而出之。至配所，与某将军交善。一日，忽来泣曰：“缘尽矣，不知何时再见。”挥手作别。将军留之不可，但见小堂冉冉升空而去。将军速到彼帐中访之，则已死矣。

    Explanation

    521

    During the reign of Emperor Kangxi, Cheng Qifan was skilled in wind divination. During the Revolt of the Three Feudatories, he became a Grand Secretary. He could predict military events happening thousands of miles away, and his predictions were always accurate. Because of this, he was promoted to the position of Vice Minister of the Board of Rituals. He often attended a banquet at the home of Zhang, a government official, near the Eastern Gate of the Forbidden City. After they had sat down, he suddenly removed his hat and belt and placed them on the table, telling the host, "I have a stomachache and need to go to the bathroom." He then called for his carriage and rushed back home. The carriage driver asked him why he was in such a hurry, and Cheng Qifan waved his hand and said, "All three of us are destined to be affected by calamity today. I dare not miss it, so I left my clothes behind to ward it off."

    Before he finished speaking, the gunpowder depot near the Eastern Gate of the Forbidden City caught fire, spreading to burn dozens of houses, and Zhang's home was reduced to ashes.

    There was also a man named Ji Xiaotang who used deceptive and fraudulent methods to deceive the masses and was exiled to Heilongjiang. When he arrived at an inn, the table was too narrow for three of the guards to sit together. Ji Xiaotang stretched the table with his hand, and in an instant, it became three feet long. The guards said, "You have already committed a crime, yet you still do not repent and are doing this trickery!" Ji Xiaotang became angry and stood up, pulling the horse he was riding into the wall, leaving only its tail outside, swaying back and forth. The guards pleaded with him, and he finally pulled the tail out. When he arrived at the place of exile, he became friends with a certain general. One day, the general suddenly came to him and wept, saying, "Our karmic connection has ended, and I do not know when we will meet again." He waved goodbye, and the general tried to keep him, but he could not. All they could see was Ji Xiaotang slowly ascending into the sky and disappearing. The general quickly went to his tent to visit him, but he had already died.

    522

    ○狐仙自缢金陵评事街张姓屋西书楼三间，相传有缢死鬼，人不敢居，封锁甚密。一日，有少年书生盛衣冠而来，求寓其家。张辞以家无空屋，书生愠曰：“汝不借我，我自来居，日后冒犯无悔！”张闻其言，知为狐仙，诡云：“西边书房三间，可以奉借。”因此房有鬼，私心欲狐仙居为之驱除，然口不言其故。书生喜，揖谢而去。次日，闻楼中有笑语声，连日不断。张知狐仙已来，日具鸡酒供之。未半月，楼上寂然无声，张疑狐仙己去，将重封锁其门。上楼视之，有黄色狐自缢于梁上。

    　　○高白云四川高白云先生，名辰，辛未翰林，长于天文占验之学，尝就馆于岳大将军家。宰娄县，观星象，知山东氛恶，已而果有王伦之事。未遇时，请乩仙问终身，仙赠诗云：“少时志业蛟潜壑，老去功名凤峙冈。”先生不解。后由祠部主事升凤阳府同知，未到任，卒。其子扶榇来江宁，厝于仪凤门外，方悟乩仙第二句之应。

    Explanation

    522

    A Fox Spirit's Suicide in Jinling

    In the Pingshi Street of Jinling, there was a three-story study belonging to a family with the surname Zhang. It was said that a ghost who had hanged himself haunted the place, and people were too afraid to live there, so the house was sealed very tightly. One day, a young scholar came dressed in fine clothes, seeking lodging in the house. The homeowner declined, saying that there were no vacant rooms, and the scholar angrily replied, "If you won't lend me a room, I will come and live here myself, and I will not regret offending you!" The homeowner, upon hearing this, knew that the scholar was a fox spirit and deceptively said, "The three-story study to the west can be lent to you." The homeowner privately wanted the fox spirit to live there to drive away the ghost, but he did not say the reason. The scholar was pleased and bowed to thank him before leaving. The next day, he heard laughter and conversation coming from the study, which continued for several days. The homeowner knew that the fox spirit had come and prepared chicken and wine for him every day. After half a month, the study became completely silent, and the homeowner suspected that the fox spirit had left and was about to reseal the door. When he went upstairs to check, he saw a yellow fox hanging himself on the beam.

    High Baiyun

    Mr. Gao Baiyun, from Sichuan, was named Chen and passed the imperial examination in the year Xinwei, becoming a member of the Hanlin Academy. He was skilled in the study of astronomy and divination. He once served as a tutor in the household of General Yue. While governing Lou County, he observed celestial phenomena and knew that there was a bad omen in Shandong, and indeed, there was later an event involving Wang Lun. Before he achieved success, he consulted a spirit medium about his fate, and the spirit gave him a poem: "In youth, his aspirations were like a dragon hiding in a deep valley; in old age, his achievements were like a phoenix standing on a hill." Mr. Gao did not understand the meaning. Later, he was promoted from a position in the Cishi Department to deputy governor of Fengyang Prefecture, but he died before he could take up the post. His son carried his coffin to Jiangning and buried him outside the Yi Feng Gate, and only then did he realize the meaning of the second line of the poem.

    523

    ○梁观察梦应广东梁兆榜观察，其族某，素奉佛，妻有娠，梦观音大士谓曰：“汝生子，可名兆榜，将来是三甲第八名进士。”惊醒，果生一男，夫妇甚喜，以兆榜名之，即为捐监，以待入场。及年长，顽蠢异常，不能识字，留监照无用，乃以与族侄，使下场，即观察也。果庚午、辛未连捷，会试，出侍郎双公门。将殿试时，双公欲为送表联于读卷官，观察辞曰：“门生先有梦兆，已定为三甲第八名进士。殿试前列，似难以人谋也。”双公笑而不信。殿试榜发，竟得二甲六十八名，双公愈笑其诞，观察亦疑梦之不足凭矣。是科进呈十卷，第一名为某相国之子，上改拔杭州吴鸿为状元，嫌二甲八十名太多，命分二十卷，置三甲，于是梁公仍为三甲第八名进士。双公叹曰：“《易》称‘圣人先天而天不违，’斯言信矣。”

    Explanation

    523

    Observation of the Dream of Liang: A Story from Guangdong

    A certain member of the Liang family in Guangdong, who had long practiced Buddhism, was pregnant when he dreamed of the Bodhisattva Guanyin, who said to him, "Your son can be named Zhao Bang, and he will become the eighth-ranked jinshi (imperial scholar) in the third category." Startled awake, he indeed gave birth to a boy, and the couple were very happy. They named him Zhao Bang and immediately contributed to the Imperial College to gain entry for him. As he grew older, he became unusually dull and stupid, unable to read or write. His certificate from the Imperial College was useless, so they gave him to a cousin to take his place in the examination. This cousin became the official known as Liang Zhao Bang. Indeed, in the years of Gengwu and Xinwei, he passed the examinations consecutively, advanced to the imperial examination, and studied under Grand Secretary Shuang Gong. Before the final imperial examination, Grand Secretary Shuang Gong wanted to send a letter of recommendation to the examination official, but Liang Zhao Bang declined, saying, "My student has a dream that has already determined he will be the eighth-ranked jinshi in the third category. It seems difficult to achieve a top position through human effort." Grand Secretary Shuang Gong laughed but did not believe him. When the final examination results were announced, Liang Zhao Bang was ranked sixty-eighth in the second category. Grand Secretary Shuang Gong laughed even more at this, and Liang Zhao Bang also began to doubt the reliability of his dream. That year, ten scrolls were presented to the emperor, and the first-ranked candidate was the son of a certain chancellor. The emperor changed the selection and chose Wu Hong of Hangzhou as the zhuangyuan (top scorer). The emperor felt that there were too many candidates in the second category, so he divided them into twenty scrolls and placed them in the third category. Thus, Liang Zhao Bang remained the eighth-ranked jinshi in the third category. Grand Secretary Shuang Gong sighed and said, "The Yijing says, 'A sage acts ahead of time, but heaven does not violate,' and this saying is indeed true."

    524

    ○大胞人壬辰二月间，余过江宁县前，见道旁爬一男子，年四十馀，有须，身面缩小，背负一肉山，高过于顶，黄胀膨亨，不知何物。细视之，有小窍，而阴毛围之，方知是肾囊也。囊高大，两倍于其身，而拖曳以行，竟不死。乞食于途。

    　　○钱文敏公梦辛稼轩而生钱文敏公维城，初名辛来，以其尊人梦辛稼轩而生公故也。改名后乃字稼轩，以存梦谶。乙丑科前四月，梦行天榜：状元李某，己为探花，榜眼不著姓名。后榜发，公为状元，而李某竟在二甲，以知县用，亦不可解。

    Explanation

    524

    During the second month of the Renchen year, I passed by Jiangning County and saw a man crawling by the roadside. He was over forty years old, had a beard, and his body and face had shrunk. On his back was a mountain of flesh, taller than his head, yellow, swollen, and bloated. I did not know what it was. Upon closer inspection, I noticed a small opening surrounded by pubic hair, and then realized it was his scrotum. The scrotum was so large, twice the size of his body, and he dragged it along as he moved, yet he did not die. He begged for food on the road.

    As for Qian Wenshen, he was said to have been born in a dream of Xin Qiji. He was originally named Xin Lai because his father had dreamed of Xin Qiji giving birth to him. After changing his name, he took the courtesy name Jiaxuan to preserve the dream's prophecy. Four months before the Yichou imperial examinations, he dreamed of the imperial list: the top-ranked candidate was Li, and he himself was third. The second-ranked candidate's name was not recorded. After the list was announced, he became the top-ranked candidate, while Li ended up in the second tier and was appointed as a magistrate, which is also inexplicable.

    525

    ○鬼入人腹焦孝廉妻金氏，门有算命瞽者过，召而试之。瞽者为言往事甚验，乃赠以钱米而去。是夜，金氏腹中有人语曰：“我师父去矣，我借娘子腹中且住几日。”

    　　金家疑是樟柳神，问：“是灵哥儿否？”曰：“我非灵哥，乃灵姐也。师父命我居汝腹中为祟，吓取财帛。”言毕，即捻其肠肺，痛不可忍。

    　　焦乃百计寻觅前瞽者，数日后遇诸途，拥而至室，许除患后谢以百金，瞽者允诺，呼曰：“二姑速出！”如是者再。内应曰：“二姑不出矣。二姑前生姓张，为其家妾，被其妻某凌虐死。某转生为金氏。我之所以投身师父作樟柳神者，正为报此仇故也。今既入其腹中，不取其命不出。”瞽者大惊曰：“既是宿孽，我不能救。”遂逃去，焦悬符拜斗，终于无益。每一医至，腹中人曰：“此庸医也，药亦无益。”

    Explanation

    525

    A Ghost Enters a Woman's Abdomen

    Jiao, the scholar-official, had a wife named Jin. A blind fortune-teller passed by their door, and they summoned him to test his abilities. The fortune-teller accurately predicted past events, and they rewarded him with money and rice before he left. That night, Jin heard a voice in her abdomen say, "My master has gone. I will stay in your belly for a few days."

    The Jiaos suspected it was a樟柳神 and asked, "Are you Lingge?" The voice replied, "I am not Lingge, but Lingjie. My master commanded me to cause trouble in your belly and scare you into giving money." After saying this, the ghost pinched Jin's intestines and lungs, causing her unbearable pain.

    Jiao searched tirelessly for the fortune-teller and found him several days later. He invited the fortune-teller to his home and promised to pay him a hundred gold pieces if he could rid them of the ghost. The fortune-teller agreed and called out, "Second Aunt, come out!" He repeated this twice. Inside, a voice responded, "Second Aunt will not come out. She was named Zhang in her past life and was a concubine in her family. She was cruelly killed by her wife, who was reborn as Jin. I became a樟柳神 under my master to seek revenge. Now that I am in her belly, I will not leave unless I take her life." The fortune-teller was greatly alarmed and said, "If this is a matter of past karma, I cannot help." He then fled. Jiao hung talismans and worshipped the Big Dipper, but it was of no avail. Every time a doctor came, the ghost said, "This is a mediocre doctor. The medicine will not help."

    526

    且听入喉。或曰：“此良医也，药恐治我。”便扼其喉，药吐而后已。又曰：“汝等软求我尚可，若用法律治我，我先啮其心肺。”嗣后，每闻招僧延道，金氏便如万刃刺心，滚地哀号，且曰：“汝受我如此煎熬，而不自寻一死，何看性命太重耶？”

    　　焦故彭芸楣侍郎门生，彭闻之，欲入奏诛瞽者。焦不欲声扬，求寝其事。金氏奄奄垂毙。此乾隆四十六年夏间事。

    　　○牛僵尸江宁铜井村人畜一牝牛，十馀年生犊凡二十八口，主人颇得其利。牛老，不能耕，宰牛者咸请买之。主人不忍，遣童喂养，俟其自毙，乃掩埋土中。是夜，闻门外有击撞声，如是者连夕，初，不意即此牛。月馀，为祟更甚，闻吼声蹄响。

    Explanation

    526

    And listen as it enters the throat. Someone said, "This is a good doctor, but the medicine might treat me." Then he choked his throat, making him vomit the medicine and stop. Another said, "If you beg me gently, I might still comply, but if you use the law against me, I will bite out your heart and lungs first." Afterward, whenever she heard about hiring monks or Taoist priests, Jin Shi felt as if ten thousand blades were piercing her heart and rolled on the ground wailing, and said, "You endure such torment from me and do not seek your own death, why do you value your life so much?"

    Jiao was a former student of Peng Yunmei, the Censorate Minister. Upon hearing this, Peng wanted to petition the emperor to punish the blind man. Jiao did not want it to become public and asked to drop the matter. Jin Shi lay on the brink of death. This was an event in the summer of the forty-sixth year of the Qianlong reign.

    A man in Tongjing Village, Jiangning, kept a female cow. Over ten years, she gave birth to twenty-eight calves, and the owner greatly benefited from it. When the cow grew old and could no longer plow, butchers all requested to buy her. The owner could not bear to sell her and sent a child to feed her, waiting for her to die naturally before burying her in the ground. That night, there were banging sounds outside the door, continuing for several nights. Initially, it was not thought to be the cow. After a month, the disturbances became more severe, and roaring and hoof sounds were heard.

    527

    于是一村之人皆疑此牛作怪，掘验之：牛尸不坏，两目闪闪如生，四蹄爪皆有稻芒，似夜间破土而出者。主人大怒，取刀断四蹄，并剖其腹，以粪秽沃潴之。嗣后寂然，再启土视之，牛朽腐矣。

    　　○袁州府署大树江西袁州府署后园，有大树高十馀丈，每夜有两红灯悬其巅。或近视之，必有泥沙抛掷；春夏则蜈蚣蛇蝎下焉，人以故不敢狎亵。乾隆年间，有敏姓者来为太守，恶其为妖，召匠数人持刀斧伐树。宾僚妻子，无不谏者，太守不为动，自坐胡床，督匠伐树。树上飞下白纸一张，上有字数行，坠太守怀中。太守视之，色变而起，趣挥匠散。至今大树犹存，然终不知纸上作何语，太守亦终不为人言。

    　　○燧人钻火树四川苗洞中人迹不到处，古木万株，有首尾阔数十围、高千丈者。邛州杨某，为采贡木故，亲诣其地，相度群树。有极大楠木一株，枝叶结成龙凤之形。将施斧锯，忽风雷大作，冰雹齐下，匠人惧而停工。

    Explanation

    527

    1. First Story

    Thus, the people of the village all suspected that this cow was causing trouble. They dug it up to investigate. The cow's body did not decay, and its eyes were shining as if it were alive. Its four hooves and claws were all covered with rice straw, as if it had broken through the soil at night. The owner became very angry, took a knife and cut off its four hooves, and also cut open its belly, pouring filth into it. Afterward, it was quiet. When they dug up the soil again to look at it, the cow was rotten.

    2. Second Story

    A Big Tree in the Rear Garden of the Yizhou Prefecture Office

    In the rear garden of the Yizhou Prefecture Office in Jiangxi Province, there was a big tree over ten zhang tall. Every night, two red lights hung at its top. If someone got close to it, there would definitely be mud and sand thrown at them; in spring and summer, centipedes, scorpions, and other venomous creatures would fall from it, and people therefore dared not approach or disrespect it. During the Qianlong era, a man surnamed Min came to be the governor. Disliking that it was an omen, he called several craftsmen with axes to chop down the tree. His attendants, officials, and family members all advised against it, but the governor was not moved by their advice and sat on a trundle bed, supervising the craftsmen as they chopped down the tree. Suddenly, a white sheet of paper flew down from the tree, with several lines of writing, landing in the governor's lap. The governor looked at it, his face changed color, and quickly dismissed the craftsmen. To this day, the big tree still stands, but no one knows what was written on the paper, and the governor never spoke about it.

    3. Third Story

    The Tree of Fire Drilling by the Fire God

    In the remote areas of Sichuan's Miao caves, where human footprints are rarely seen, there are thousands of ancient trees, some with trunks and branches spanning dozens of zhang wide and reaching a thousand zhang tall. Yang of Qiong Prefecture, for the purpose of collecting tribute wood, personally visited the site and surveyed the trees. There was one extremely large nanmu tree whose branches and leaves formed the shapes of dragons and phoenixes. Just as he was about to use his axe and saw, suddenly, there was a great storm of wind and thunder, and hailstones fell together. The craftsmen were terrified and stopped work.

    528

    其夜刺史梦一古衣冠人来，拱手语曰：“我燧人皇帝钻火树也。当天地开辟后，三皇递兴，一万余年，天下只有水，并无火，五行不全。我怜君民生食，故舍身度世，教燧人皇帝钻木出火，以作大烹。先从我根上起钻，至今灼痕犹可验也。有此大功，君其忍锯我乎？”刺史曰：“神言甚是，但神有功亦有过。”神问：“何也？”曰：“凡食生物者，肠胃无烟火气，故疾病不生，且有长年之寿。

    　　自水火既济之后，小则疮痔，大则痰壅，皆火气熏蒸而成，然后神农黄帝尝百草、施医药以相救。可见燧人皇帝以前，民皆无病可治，自火食后，从此生民年寿短矣。且下官奉文采办，不得大木，不能消差，奈何？“神曰：”君言亦有理。我与天地同生，让我与天地同尽。我有曾孙树三株，大蔽十牛，尽可合用消差。但两株性恭顺，祭之便可运斤；其一株性倔强，须我谕之，才肯受伐。“

    Explanation

    528

    That night, the Magistrate dreamed of an ancient figure in formal attire who approached him with a respectful bow and said, "I am the Fire-Maker Emperor, the tree that taught Emperor Sui Ren to drill wood and create fire. After the heavens and earth were formed, the Three Sovereigns arose in succession, spanning over ten thousand years. At that time, there was only water in the world, no fire, and the five elements were incomplete. I pitied the people, who lived without fire, so I sacrificed myself to guide the world, teaching Emperor Sui Ren to drill wood and make fire for cooking. I began by drilling at my roots, and the burn marks from that act can still be seen today. Having accomplished such great deeds, could you, as your lord, bear to saw me?"

    The Magistrate replied, "The divine words are indeed true, but the divine being has both merit and fault." The figure asked, "What do you mean?" The Magistrate explained, "All who consume living creatures have intestines that lack the smoky or fiery energy, so they do not suffer from diseases and enjoy long lives.

    After the balance between water and fire was achieved, minor ailments like hemorrhoids and major ones like phlegm congestion arose from the fumes of fire. Then, Emperors Shennong and Huangdi tasted hundreds of herbs and administered medicine to heal the people. It is clear that before Emperor Sui Ren, the people had no diseases to treat, but since the advent of fire, the lifespan of the people has shortened. Moreover, your humble servant is in charge of gathering timber and cannot fulfill the order without obtaining large trees, what shall we do?"

    The figure said, "Your words are also reasonable. I was born with the heavens and earth, and I will let myself perish with them. I have three grandchild trees, large enough to shelter ten oxen, all of which can be used to fulfill the order. Two of them are gentle and obedient, and if they are offered sacrifice, they can be felled. The third one is stubborn and strong-willed, and I must instruct it before it will consent to being cut down."

    529

    次日，如其言设祭施锯，果都平顺。及运至川河，忽风浪大作，一木沉水中。

    　　万夫曳之，卒不起。

    　　○鬼怕冷淡扬州罗两峰自言能见鬼，每日落，则满路皆鬼，富贵家尤多。大概比人短数尺，面目不甚可辨，但见黑气数段，旁行斜立，呢呢絮语。喜气暖，人旺处则聚而居，如逐水草者然。扬子云曰：“高明之家，鬼瞰其室。”言殊有理。鬼逢墙壁窗板，皆直穿而过，不觉有碍。与人两不相关，亦全无所妨。一见面目，则是报冤作祟者矣。贫苦寥落之家，鬼往来者甚少，以其气衰地寒，鬼亦不能甘此冷淡故也。谚云“穷得鬼不上门”，信矣。

    Explanation

    529

    The next day, following his instructions, they set up a sacrifice and applied a saw, and indeed everything went smoothly. When it was transported to the river, suddenly the wind and waves grew fierce, and one piece of wood sank into the water. Ten thousand men pulled it, but it did not rise.

    ○ Ghosts fear loneliness. Luo Liufeng from Yangzhou claimed he could see ghosts. Every evening, the roads were full of ghosts, especially in wealthy families. Generally, they were a few feet shorter than humans, their faces were not very distinguishable, but only black vapors could be seen. They walked sideways and stood slanted, whispering to each other. Happy and warm, they gathered where people were active, like those who follow water and grass. Yang Ziyun said, "In a noble house, ghosts look down upon the room." His words are indeed very reasonable. When ghosts encounter walls and window boards, they pass through directly, not feeling any obstruction. They have no relation to humans and cause no harm. Once they see faces, then they are those who seek revenge or cause mischief. Poor and desolate homes see few ghosts, because the qi is weak and the place is cold, and ghosts cannot tolerate such loneliness. The proverb says, "Poverty drives ghosts away," and indeed it is true.

    530

    ○鬼避人如人避烟两峰云：鬼避人如人之避烟，以其气可厌而避之，并不知其为人而避之也。

    　　然往往被急走之人横冲而过，则散为数段，须团凑一热茶时，方能完全一鬼，其光景似颇吃力。

    　　○卖蒜叟南阳县有杨二相公者，精于拳勇，能以两肩负粮船而起。旗丁数百以篙刺之，篙所触处，寸寸折裂，以此名重一时。率其徒行教常州，每至演武场传授枪棒，观者如堵。

    　　忽一日，有卖蒜叟龙钟伛偻，咳嗽不绝声，旁睨而揶揄之，众大骇，走告杨。

    　　杨大怒，招叟至前，以拳打砖墙，陷入尺许，傲之曰：“叟能如是乎！”叟曰：“君能打墙，不能打人。”杨愈怒，骂曰：“老奴能受我打乎？打死勿怨！”叟笑曰：“老人垂死之年，能以一死成君之名，死亦何怨！”乃广约众人，写立誓券，令杨养息三日。

    Explanation

    530

    Final Translation

    ○ Ghosts Avoid People Like People Avoid Smoke

    Two peaks said: "Ghosts avoid people like people avoid smoke, repelled by the unpleasantness of human presence, without knowing that they are avoiding humans."

    However, when someone runs quickly and crosses paths, the ghost's form breaks apart into several pieces. It takes as long as a cup of hot tea to gather, and only then can the ghost be fully restored, a process that seems quite strenuous.

    ○ The Garlic Seller

    In Nanyang County, there was a martial arts master named Yang Er, who was exceptionally skilled in martial arts. He could lift a grain boat with both shoulders. Hundreds of flag bearers attacked him with poles, and wherever the poles touched, they broke into pieces, earning him great fame at the time. He led his disciples to teach martial arts in Changzhou, and whenever he demonstrated at the martial arts field, the audience was packed tightly like a wall.

    One day, an elderly man selling garlic, bent and hunched over, coughing continuously, glanced sideways and mocked him. The crowd was greatly startled and rushed to tell Yang.

    Yang became furious and called the old man forward. With a punch, he cracked a brick wall, and said arrogantly, "Can you do that too?" The old man replied, "You can punch walls, but you cannot punch people." Yang became even angrier and shouted, "Can you old fool take a beating from me? If I kill you, don't complain!" The old man smiled and said, "I am nearing death. I am willing to die to make your name famous, so what do I have to regret?" He then gathered the crowd, wrote and signed a covenant, and ordered Yang to rest for three days.

    531

    老人自缚于树，解及露腹，杨故取势于十步外奋拳击之。老人寂然无声，但见杨双膝跪地叩头曰：“晚生知罪了。”拔其拳，已夹入老人腹中，坚不可出。

    　　哀求良久，老人鼓腹纵之，已跌出一石桥外矣。老人徐徐负蒜而归，卒不肯告人姓氏。

    　　○借棺为车绍兴张元公，在阊门开布行。聘伙计孙某者，陕人也，姓诚谨而勤，所经算无不利市三倍，以故宾主相得。三五年中，为张致家资十万。屡乞归家，张坚留不许，孙怒曰：“假如我死，亦不放我归乎？”张笑曰：“果死，必亲送君归，三四千里，我不辞劳。”

    　　又一年，孙果病笃，张至床前问身后事，曰：“我家在陕西长安县钟楼之旁，有二子在家。如念我前情，可将我灵柩寄归付之。”随即气绝。张大哭，深悔从前苦留之虐。又自念十万家资皆出渠帮助之力，何可食言不送？乃具赙仪千金，亲送棺至长安。

    Explanation

    531

    The Old Man Bound Himself to a Tree

    The old man bound himself to a tree, unfastened his bindings, and revealed his belly. Yang, from a distance of ten steps, threw a punch at him. The old man remained completely silent. Yang, seeing this, knelt on both knees and bowed, saying, "I, a younger generation, know I am guilty." When Yang tried to pull back his fist, it got stuck in the old man's belly, firmly and could not be removed.

    After pleading for a long time, the old man expanded his belly and released Yang, who fell out of a stone bridge. The old man slowly carried garlic back, finally refusing to reveal his name.

    ○ Borrowing a Coffin for a Carriage

    In Shaoxing, Zhang Yuan Gong opened a cloth shop at the Chuan Gate. He hired a partner named Sun, who was from Shaanxi. Sun was honest, diligent, and skilled in calculations, always making three times the profit. Therefore, the master and servant got along well. Within three to five years, Sun helped Zhang accumulate a wealth of ten thousand taels. Sun repeatedly asked to return home, but Zhang insisted on keeping him. Sun became angry and said, "If I die, will you not let me return?" Zhang laughed and said, "If you die, I will personally send you back, even if it's three or four thousand miles, I will not hesitate."

    A year later, Sun indeed fell seriously ill. Zhang went to his bedside and asked about his afterlife arrangements. Sun said, "My home is near the Bell Tower in Chang'an County, Shaanxi Province. I have two sons at home. If you remember my past kindness, please send my coffin back to them." Soon after, Sun passed away. Zhang wept bitterly, deeply regretting his previous harsh treatment. He also thought that ten thousand taels of wealth were all due to Sun's help, how could he break his promise and not send him back? Therefore, he prepared a thousand taels of mourning money and personally sent the coffin to Chang'an.

    532

    叩其门开，长子出见。告以尊翁病故原委，为之泣下，而其子夷然，但唤家人云：“爷柩既归，可安置厅旁。”既无哀容，亦不易服，张骇绝无言。少顷，次子出见，向张致谢数语，亦阳阳如平常。张以为此二子殆非人类，岂以孙某如此好人，而生禽兽之二子乎！

    　　正惊叹间，闻其母在内呼曰：“行主远来，得毋饥乎？我酒馔已备，惜无人陪，奈何？”两子曰：“行主张先生，父执也，卑幼不敢陪侍。”其母曰：“然则非汝死父不可。”命二子肆筵设席，而己持大斧出，劈棺骂曰：“业已到家，何必装痴作态！”死者大笑，掀棺而起，向张拜谢曰：“君真古人也，送我归，死不食言。”张问：“何作此狡狯？”曰：“我不死，君肯放我归乎？且车马劳顿，不如卧棺中之安逸耳。”张曰：“君病既愈，盍再同往苏州？”曰：“君命中财止十万，我虽再来，不能有所增益。”留张宿三日而别，终不知孙为何许人也。

    Explanation

    532

    Knocking on the door, it opened, and the eldest son came out to see. He was told about the illness and death of his father, and Zhang wept at the news, but the eldest son remained calm, just calling the family and saying, "The coffin of your father has returned, can be placed beside the hall." He showed no signs of grief and did not change his clothes. Zhang was so shocked he could not speak. After a while, the second son came out to see, said a few words of thanks to Zhang, and was calm and composed as usual. Zhang thought these two sons seemed not human, how could such a good person like Sun have such bad children!

    Just as Zhang was amazed, he heard his mother call from inside, saying, "The guest has come from afar, are you not hungry? The wine and food are ready, it's a pity there's no one to accompany, what shall we do?" The two sons said, "The guest Zhang Mr., is a father's friend, we are too young to accompany." His mother said, "Then only your dead father can do it." She ordered the two sons to set up the feast, and she herself took out a large axe, sliced open the coffin and scolded, saying, "You've already come home, why pretend to be foolish and act strange!" The dead person laughed, lifted the coffin lid and got up, bowed to Zhang in thanks, saying, "You are truly a person from ancient times, sent me back, did not break my word even after death." Zhang asked, "Why do such tricks?" The dead person replied, "I wouldn't die, would you let me return? And the journey was tiring, it's more comfortable to lie in the coffin." Zhang said, "Your illness has healed, why not go to Suzhou together again?" The dead person said, "Your destiny has only ten thousand taels of silver, even if I come back again, I cannot add anything." He kept Zhang for three days and then parted, finally, they didn't know who Sun was.

    533

    ○孙伊仲常州孙文介公玄孙伊仲，赴江阴应试，舟泊于野。天将夕牟，路见古衣冠者问：“何去？”曰：“应试。”其人咤曰：“功名富贵，可袭取乎？水源木本，可终绝乎？此之不知，应试何为？”言毕不见。伊仲恍惚如梦，归至舟中。欲不应试，同人劝行，不得已，仍至江阴。患疟甚剧，莽热时，见古衣冠者又来曰：“尔无父，我无子，风雨霜露，哀哉伤心。”伊仲悚然，即买舟南归。以此言告本族，方知文介公本无子，嗣其宗人为子，后其家子孙皆嗣子所出，而嗣子之墓久不可考矣。赵恭毅公孙刑部郎中某代访得消息，墓为沈氏所占，乃为助钱议赎还之。此乾隆四十三年事。

    Explanation

    533

    Sun Yizhong, the great-grandson of Grandmaster Sun Wenjie from Changzhou, went to Jiangyin for the examination, and his boat docked in the countryside. As the evening was approaching, he saw an anciently dressed man on the road who asked, "Where are you going?" He replied, "To take the examination." The man shouted, "Can fame, wealth, and honor be inherited? Can the source of water and the root of trees be completely cut off? If you don't understand this, why are you taking the exam?" After speaking, the man disappeared. Yizhong felt dazed as if in a dream and returned to his boat. He wanted to abandon the examination, but his companions persuaded him to go. Reluctantly, he went back to Jiangyin. He suffered from a severe case of malaria. When he was delirious with fever, he saw the anciently dressed man again who said, "You have no father, and I have no son. The wind, rain, frost, and dew, how sad and heart-wrenching." Yizhong was startled and immediately bought a boat to return to the south. He told this to his family, and they learned that Grandmaster Sun Wenjie originally had no son, but adopted a relative from the same clan as his son. Later, all his descendants were from the adopted son, and the tomb of the adopted son had long been untraceable. A certain Minister of the Department of Punishment, a descendant of Duke Zhao Gongyi, found out about the situation and helped with money to negotiate the return of the tomb, which had been taken over by the Shen family. This was the 43rd year of the Qianlong reign.

    534

    ●卷十五○姚端恪公遇剑仙国初桐城姚端恪公为司寇时，有山西某以谋杀案将定罪。某以十万金赂公弟文燕求宽，文燕允之，而惮公方正，不敢向公言，希冀得宽，将私取之。

    　　一夕者，公于灯下判案，忽梁上男子持匕首下，公问：“汝刺客耶，来何为？”

    　　曰：“为山西某来。”公曰：“某法不当宽。如欲宽某，则国法大坏，我无颜立于朝矣，不如死。”指其颈曰：“取。”客曰：“公不可，何为公弟受金？”曰：“我不知。”曰：“某亦料公之不知也。”腾身而出，但闻屋瓦上如风扫叶之声。

    　　时文燕方出京赴知州任。公急遣人告之。到德州，已丧首于车中矣。据家人云：“主人在店早饭毕，上车行数里，忽大呼‘好冷风！’我辈急送绵衣往视，头不见，但血淋漓而已。”端恪题刑部白云亭云：“常觉胸中生意满，须知世上苦人多。”

    Explanation

    534

    Chapter Fifteen: Grand Secretary Yao Meets the Sword Immortal

    In the early days of the Ming Dynasty, Grand Secretary Yao of Tongcheng was serving as the Minister of Justice. There was a man from Shanxi who was about to be sentenced for a murder plot. The man bribed Grand Secretary Yao's younger brother Wenyan with ten thousand taels of gold to seek leniency. Wenyan agreed, but was afraid of Grand Secretary Yao's integrity, so he dared not tell him, hoping to get leniency and secretly take the money.

    One evening, as Grand Secretary Yao was judging a case under the lamp, suddenly a man on the beam holding a dagger came down. Grand Secretary Yao asked, "Are you an assassin? What are you here for?"

    The man replied, "I am here for the person from Shanxi." Grand Secretary Yao said, "According to the law, the person should not be lenient. If I were to show leniency, the national law would be greatly undermined, and I would have no face to stand in court. It is better to die." He pointed to his neck and said, "Take it." The man said, "You cannot do that. Why did your younger brother accept the bribe?" Grand Secretary Yao said, "I do not know." The man said, "I also expected that you would not know." He leaped out, and only the sound of wind sweeping leaves on the roof tiles was heard.

    At that time, Wenyan was just leaving the capital to take up his post as governor. Grand Secretary Yao quickly sent someone to inform him. When he arrived in Dezhou, his head had already been severed in the carriage. According to the family, it is said that the master finished breakfast at the inn, got into the carriage, and after traveling a few miles, suddenly shouted, "It's so cold!" We hurriedly sent warm clothes to see him, but his head was nowhere to be seen, only blood was everywhere.

    Grand Secretary Yao inscribed at the White Cloud Pavilion in the Ministry of Justice, saying:
    "Always feel that there is plenty of life in my heart. Remember that there are many suffering people in the world."

    535

    ○吴髯扬州吴髯行九，盐贾子也，年二十，将往广东某藩司署中赘娶。舟至滕王阁下，白昼见一女与公差来舟中，云：“寻君三世，今日得见面矣。”吴髯茫然不知所来。家人知为冤鬼，日以苕帚打其见处，无益也。从此吴髯言语与平时迥异。

    　　由江西以及广东，二鬼皆不去。

    　　入赘之日，女鬼忽入洞房，索其坐位，与新人争上下，惟新人与吴髯闻其声，云“我本汉阳孀妇，与吴狎昵，逐订婚姻，以所畜万金与至苏州买屋开张布字号，订明月日来汉阳迎娶。不意吴挟金去，五年竟无消息。我因自经死，到黄泉哭诉，汉阳城隍移查苏州城隍，回批云：”此人已生湖南。‘寻至湖南诉城隍，又查明已生扬州，及至扬州，而吴又来广东。追至江西，始得相逢。今日婚姻之事，我不能阻，但须同享荣华“等语。新人大骇，白之藩台。不得已，竟虚其位待之，始得安然。鬼差口索杯箸求食，乃另设席相待。

    535

    Wu Ran, a salt merchant from Yangzhou, was the ninth son of his family and was twenty years old when he set out to marry into a family in Guangdong. As the boat passed under Tengwang Pavilion, during the day, a woman and an official boarded the vessel. She said, "I have been searching for you for three generations, and today I finally get to see you." Wu Ran was bewildered and did not know where she had come from. His family recognized her as a wronged ghost and hit the place where she appeared with a broom every day, but it was of no use. From then on, Wu Ran's speech was greatly different from his usual self.

    From Jiangxi to Guangdong, the two ghosts did not leave.

    On the day he married into the family, the female ghost suddenly entered the bridal chamber, demanded a seat, and competed with the new bride for status. Only the new bride and Wu Ran heard her voice, and she said, "I was a widow from Hanyang, and I had an intimate relationship with Wu. We agreed to marry, and I gave him ten thousand taels of silver to buy a house in Suzhou and start a cloth business. We agreed on a date to come to Hanyang for the wedding. Unexpectedly, Wu took the money and left, and there was no news of him for five years. I hanged myself and went to the underworld to complain. The city god of Hanyang sent a message to the city god of Suzhou, who replied that 'this person has been reborn in Hunan.' I went to Hunan to complain to the city god, and they found out he had been reborn in Yangzhou. When I got to Yangzhou, Wu had already come to Guangdong. I followed him to Jiangxi, where we finally met. Today, the marriage cannot be stopped, but we must share in prosperity."

    The new bride was very frightened and told the official. Reluctantly, they left an empty seat for her, and only then could they rest easy. The ghost's messenger asked for a cup and chopsticks for food, so they prepared another meal for her.

    536

    阅一月，吴髯告归，买舟回扬，鬼亦索舆甚迫，欲随其舆以登舟。扬州士人早知此事而不信，于吴髯抵扬之日，填街塞巷，以待其归。见其四舆入城，前果二空舆，肩舆者亦觉其若有人坐。一时好事者作《再生缘》传奇。

    　　阅半月，吴髯妻与女鬼约修道场七日，焚冥镪于琼花观中，劝之去，女鬼欣然诺之。其时鬼差已去，道场中设女魂牌于殿之西侧，每日吴髯妻设席亲祭。至第七日，大雨，遣家人往供。家人失足跌于路，即供以泥污之馔。鬼大嚷不止。

    　　吴髯责其家人，而髯妻又约以九日道场。圆满之。故女鬼向髯妻称谢，谓吴髯曰：“后十年来，再索汝命，我且暂去。”

    Explanation

    536

    After one month, Wu Ran reported back home, bought a boat to return to Yangzhou, and the ghost also demanded a sedan chair very urgently, wanting to follow him onto the boat. The people of Yangzhou had known about the incident but did not believe it, and on the day Wu Ran arrived in Yangzhou, they filled the streets and alleys to see him return. They saw four sedan chairs enter the city, and in front were indeed two empty sedan chairs, and those carrying the sedan chairs also felt as if someone was sitting in them. At that time, those interested in such things created a legend called "Regenerated Affinity."

    After half a month, Wu Ran's wife arranged for a seven-day ritual, burned paper money in the Qionghua Temple, and persuaded the ghost to leave, and the ghost happily agreed. At that time, the ghost's officials had already left, and in the ritual, a tablet for the female soul was set up on the west side of the hall, and every day Wu Ran's wife set up a table and personally offered sacrifices. On the seventh day, it rained heavily, and she sent family members to offer, but the family members lost their footing and fell on the road, and they offered food stained with mud, and the ghost continued to shout loudly.

    Wu Ran blamed his family members, and his wife arranged for a nine-day ritual, which was completed. Therefore, the ghost thanked Wu Ran's wife and said to Wu Ran, "In ten years, I will come again to claim your life, and I will temporarily leave."

    537

    髯惧，舍身为城隍役。至期，则白日睡去。至今扬之人皆知吴九胡子为活勾差。

    　　○麻林长随麻林与李二交好，李以贫死，而林家资颇厚。一夕，梦李登其床责之曰：“我与汝平日两弟兄颇莫逆，今我死，无子孙，汝不以一豚蹄见祭我坟，何忍心也？”林唯唯许诺。李起身出户，而林犹觉胸腹上有物相压者，疑李魂未散。急起视之，乃一小猪压被上，尿矢淋漓，方知李魂附猪而来也，心大省悟。即缚小猪卖之，得二千文，为备酒肉，亲至其坟祭之。

    　　○鹤静先生厉樊榭未第时，与周穆门诸人好请乩仙。一日，有仙人降盘书曰：“我鹤静先生也，平生好吟，故来结吟社之欢。诸君小事问我，我有知必告；大事不必问我，虽知亦不敢告。”嗣后，凡杭城祈晴祷雨、止疟断痢等事问之，必书日期，开药方皆验；其他休咎，则笔卧不动。每日祈请，但书“鹤静先生”四字。向空焚之，仙辄下降，有所唱和。诗尤清丽，和“雁”字至六十首，如是一年。

    Explanation

    537

    The Bearded Man, fearing, offered himself as a servant to the city god. At the appointed time, he would sleep during the day. To this day, people in Yangzhou all know that Wu Jiu Hu Zi is a living official.

    ○ Ma Lin, a long-serving attendant of Ma Lin, was friendly with Li Er, who died of poverty, while Ma Lin had considerable wealth. One night, Ma Lin dreamed that Li Er climbed onto his bed and rebuked him, saying, "In our daily lives, we were very close like two brothers. Now that I am dead and have no descendants, you do not offer a pig trotter as a sacrifice to my tomb, how can you have such a heartless attitude?" Ma Lin nodded in agreement. Li Er then got up and left the room, but Ma Lin still felt something pressing on his chest and abdomen, suspecting that Li Er's spirit had not dispersed. He quickly got up and looked, and saw a small pig pressing on the bed, with urine and feces all over it. Only then did he realize that Li Er's spirit had attached itself to the pig, and he greatly understood. He immediately tied up the little pig and sold it, obtaining two thousand coins, which he used to prepare wine and meat, and personally went to Li Er's tomb to make a sacrifice.

    ○ Mr. He Jing, Li Fan Xie, before passing the imperial examination, often invited a spirit medium with Zhou Mu Men and others. One day, a fairy descended and wrote on a plate, saying, "I am Mr. He Jing, who enjoys poetry in my lifetime, so I come to join the joy of the poetry society. If you have any trivial matters to ask me, I will certainly tell you if I know; for major matters, you do not need to ask me, even if I know, I will not dare to tell." After that, whenever there were requests in Hangzhou for prayers for sunshine and rain, stopping malaria and diarrhea, etc., the fairy would always write the date and prescribe medicine, all of which proved effective; for other good or bad omens, the pen would remain still. Every day when they prayed, they only needed to write the four characters "Mr. He Jing" and burn them towards the sky, and the fairy would descend immediately, engaging in poetic exchanges. His poems were especially clear and beautiful, and he composed up to sixty poems on the theme of "wild geese," like this for a whole year.

    538

    樊榭、穆门请与相见，拒而不许，诸人再四恳求，曰：“明日下午在孤山放鹤亭相候。”诸公临期放舟伺之，至日昃，无所见，疑其相诳，各欲起行。忽空中长啸一声，阴风四起，见伟丈夫须长数尺，纱帽红袍，以长帛自挂于石牌楼上，一闪而逝。疑是前朝忠臣殉节者也。自此乩盘再请亦不至矣。惜未问其姓名。

    　　○门户无故自开孙叶飞先生掌教云南五华书院，正月十三夜，院门无故自开，枢限皆脱，以为大奇。次日，城中轰传家家门户昨晚皆无故自开，不知是何妖异。伺之月馀，大小平安，了无他故。

    　　○黄陵玄鹤陕西黄帝陵向有两玄鹤，相传为上古之鸟，朔望飞鸣，居人可望不可即。乾隆初年，又有二小鹤同飞，羽色示黑。一日，忽空中飞下大雕，以翅扑小鹤，几为所伤。老鹤知之，双来啄雕，格斗良久，云雷交至。雕死崖石上，其大可覆数亩。土人取其翅当作屋瓦，荫庇数百家。

    538

    Fancan and Mumen requested to meet him, but he refused their request. The others repeatedly pleaded, saying, "We will meet you tomorrow afternoon at the Crane Release Pavilion on Solitary Hill." On the appointed day, the scholars set out on boats to wait for him, but as the sun began to set, they saw nothing. They suspected that they had been deceived and were about to leave. Suddenly, a long howl echoed from the sky, followed by a gust of cold wind. They saw a tall and dignified man with long hair, wearing a silk hat and a red robe, hanging himself with a long piece of cloth on a stone tablet, and then disappearing in an instant. They suspected that he was a loyal minister from a previous dynasty who had died for his principles. From then on, the spirit board no longer responded to their requests. It is regrettable that they did not ask for his name.

    ○ Unexplained Opening of Doors

    Mr. Sun Yefei, who served as the principal of the Wuhua Academy in Yunnan, was teaching on the night of the 13th day of the first lunar month. The academy's doors opened unexplainably, and the hinges came loose, which was considered a great wonder. The next day, the entire city was abuzz with the news that every household's doors had opened unexplainably the previous night, and no one knew what kind of supernatural phenomenon this was. They waited for over a month, and everything remained peaceful, with no further incidents.

    ○ The Mythical Cranes of the Yellow Emperor's Mausoleum

    In the area of the Yellow Emperor's mausoleum in Shaanxi, there were two mythical cranes, said to be birds from ancient times. They would fly and call out during the new and full moons, visible to the residents but unreachable. In the early years of the Qianlong era, two smaller cranes began flying alongside them, their feathers showing black. One day, a large eagle suddenly flew down from the sky and attacked the smaller cranes with its wings, nearly injuring them. The older cranes sensed the danger and flew down to peck at the eagle. They fought for a long time, and thunder and lightning accompanied the battle. The eagle died on a cliff, and its size was such that it could cover several acres. The local people took its wings and used them as roof tiles, providing shelter for hundreds of families.

    539

    ○土地迎举人休宁吴衡，浙江商籍生员。乾隆乙酉乡试，榜发前一日，其家老仆夜卧忽醒，喜曰：“相公中矣！”问：“何以知之？”曰：“老朴夜梦过土地祠，见土地神驾车将出，自锁其门，告我曰：”向例省中有中式者，土地例当迎接。我现充此差，故将启行。汝主人，即我所迎也。‘“吴闻之，心虽喜，终不信。已而榜发，果中第十六名。

    　　○孙烈妇歙县绍村张长寿妻孙氏，父某，工武艺。孙自幼从父学年及笄，归长寿。长寿家贫，娶妇弥月即客浙西。有贼数人窥妇年少，夜往撬其门，将行不良。妇左手执烛，右手持梃与贼斗，贼被创仆地而逃。又一年，长寿病死，妇从容执丧事。

    Explanation

    539

    The Land Deity Welcomes the Successful Candidate

    In the year of the rabbit, during the provincial examination under the reign of Emperor Qianlong, a servant of Wu Heng from Shouning, Anhui, who was a scholar from a merchant's household in Zhejiang, woke up suddenly at night the day before the announcement of the list of successful candidates. He exclaimed with joy, "Master has succeeded!" When asked how he knew this, he replied, "Last night, I dreamed of passing by the land deity's shrine. I saw the land deity driving a carriage and about to leave, locking the door himself. He told me, 'According to tradition, when there are successful candidates in the province, the land deity is supposed to welcome them. I am currently on this duty, so I will set off. Your master is the one I am welcoming.'"

    Wu Heng heard this and, although he was happy, he did not fully believe it. Later, when the list was announced, he indeed ranked 16th.

    The Chaste Widow Sun

    Sun, the chaste widow from the village of Shao in Shexian, was the wife of Zhang Changshou. Her father, whose name is not mentioned, was skilled in martial arts. Sun learned martial arts from her father from a young age and married into Zhang's family. Zhang was poor, and after a month of marriage, he went to Zhejiang West as a guest. Several bandits, coveting the young wife, came at night and tried to pry open the door, intending to commit an indecent act. The wife, holding a candle in her left hand and a club in her right, fought the bandits. The bandits were wounded and fell to the ground, fleeing in terror. A year later, Zhang died of illness, and the wife calmly performed the funeral rites.

    540

    既葬，闭户自缢。邻人以妇强死，惧其为祟，集僧作佛事超度之。夜将半，僧方诵经，见妇坐堂上叱曰：“我死于正命，并非不当死而死者，何须汝辈秃奴来此多事！”僧皆惊散。后村有妇某与人有私，将谋弑夫者，忽病狂呼曰：“孙烈妇在此责我，不敢！不敢！”嗣后合村奉孙如神。

    　　○小芙黟北王氏妇梦美女子认己为男子而与之合，曰：“我番禺陈家婢小芙也。子前生为仆，与我有约而事露，我忧郁死，爱缘未尽，故来续欢。”妇醒即病颠，屏夫独居，时自言笑，皆男子亵语，忘己之为女身也。久之，小芙白昼现形，家人百计驱之，莫能遣。会邻舍不戒于火，小芙呼告王氏，得免于难。王家德之，听其安居年馀。一夕谓妇曰：“我缘已尽，且得转生矣。”抱妇大哭，称“与哥哥永诀”，妇颠病即已，后竟无他。

    Explanation

    540

    After the burial, she shut herself in and hanged herself. The neighbors thought the woman had died against her will and were afraid she might become a spirit to cause trouble, so they gathered monks to perform Buddhist rituals to help her soul. It was nearly midnight when the monks were just beginning to recite sutras, and they saw the woman sitting in the hall and shouting, "I died by my own will; I was not someone who should not have died. Why do you need to bother with us bald slaves here?" The monks were all startled and scattered. Later, in a village, a woman who had been involved in an affair with someone and was planning to murder her husband suddenly became ill and shouted wildly, "The virtuous lady Sun is here scolding me! I dare not! I dare not!" Afterward, the entire village revered Sun as if she were a deity.

    ○ A woman from the Wang family in the north of Yi dreamed of a beautiful woman who identified herself as a man and had intercourse with her, saying, "I am Xiao Fu, a servant girl from the Chen family in Fanyu. In my previous life, I was a servant and had an agreement with you, but it was exposed, and I died of depression. Our karmic bond is not yet complete, so I came to continue our relationship." The woman woke up and immediately became mentally deranged, isolating herself from her husband and living alone. She often spoke and laughed, using language that was intimate and typically associated with men, forgetting that she was a woman. After a long time, Xiao Fu appeared in broad daylight, and the family tried every means to drive her away but could not. When a neighboring house caught fire, Xiao Fu called out to the Wang family, and they were able to avoid the disaster. The Wang family was grateful and allowed her to stay for over a year. One night, she told the woman, "My karmic bond is now complete, and I am about to be reborn." She hugged the woman and cried, saying, "Farewell, my brother." The woman's mental illness ended, and she had no further issues afterward.

    541

    ○鬼宝塔杭人有邱老者，贩布营生。一日取帐回，投宿店家，店中人满。前路荒凉，更无止所，与店主商量。主人云：“老客胆大否？某后墙外有骰子房数间，日久无人歇宿，恐藏邪祟，未敢相邀。”邱老曰：“吾计半生所行，不下数万里，何惧鬼为？”于是主人执烛，偕邱老穿室内行至后墙外，视之：空地一方，约可四五亩，贴墙矮屋数间，颇洁净。邱老进内，见卓椅床帐俱全，甚喜。主人辞出，邱老以天热，坐户外算帐。

    　　是夕淡月朦胧，恍惚间似前面有人影闪过，邱疑贼至，注目视之，忽又一影闪过，须臾，连见十二影，往来无定，如蝴蝶穿花，不可捉摸。定睛熟视，皆美妇也。邱老曰：“人之所以畏鬼者，鬼有恶状故也。今艳冶如斯，吾即以美人视鬼可矣。”遂端坐看其作何景状。

    Explanation

    541

    A Ghost Treasure Tower

    There was an elderly man named Qiu from Hangzhou who made a living selling cloth. One day, after returning with his accounts, he checked into an inn, only to find it full. The road ahead was desolate, and there was nowhere else to stop, so he negotiated with the innkeeper. The innkeeper said, "Are you a brave old guest? Behind my back wall, there are several dice houses that have been empty for a long time, perhaps harboring evil spirits, and I dare not invite you." Qiu replied, "I have traveled half my life, not less than tens of thousands of miles, what do I fear from ghosts?" Thus, the innkeeper held a candle and accompanied Qiu through the rooms to the back wall, where they looked out: a piece of empty ground, about four or five acres, with a few low houses attached to the wall, quite clean. Qiu entered inside and saw tables, chairs, beds, and curtains all complete, and he was very pleased. The innkeeper bade farewell and left, and Qiu, because it was hot, sat outside to calculate accounts.

    That evening, the moon was dim and hazy. In a moment of daze, it seemed like there were human shadows flickering in front of him. Qiu suspected bandits, and he watched intently, but suddenly another shadow flickered, and in a short while, he saw twelve shadows, moving without a fixed pattern, like butterflies flitting through flowers, impossible to grasp. Focusing his gaze, he saw that they were all beautiful women. Qiu said, "People fear ghosts because ghosts have ugly appearances. Now, they are so beautiful, I will regard them as beautiful women." He then sat upright to see what kind of scene they would make.

    542

    未几，二鬼踞其足下，一鬼登其肩，九鬼接踵以登，而一鬼飘然据其顶，若戏场所谓“搭宝塔”者然。又未几，各执大圈齐套颈上，头发俱披，舌长尺余。

    　　邱老笑曰：“美则过于美，恶则过于恶，情形反覆，极似目下人情世态，看汝辈到底作何归结耳！”言毕，群鬼大笑，各还原形而散。

    　　○棺盖飞钱塘李甲，素勇，夕赴友人宴，酒酣，座客云：“离此间半里，有屋求售，价甚廉，闻藏厉鬼，故至今尚无售主。”李云：“惜我无钱，说也徒然。”客云：“君有胆能在此中独饮一宵，仆当货此室奉君。”众客云：“我等作保。”即以明晚为订。“

    　　次午，作队进室，安放酒肴，李带剑升堂，众人阖户反锁去，借邻家聚谈候信。李环顾厅屋，其旁别开小门，转身入，有狭弄，荒草蒙茸；后有环洞门，半掩半开。李心计云：我不必进去，且在外俟其动静。乃烧烛饮酒。

    Explanation

    542

    Not long after, two ghosts sat on his feet, one ghost climbed onto his shoulder, nine ghosts followed one after another to climb, and one ghost lightly sat on his head, just like the theatrical performance known as "Building a Pagoda." Soon after, each held a large hoop and put it around their neck, their hair all hanging down, and their tongues longer than a foot.

    Old Qiu laughed and said, "If beauty is too beautiful, and ugliness is too ugly, the situation repeatedly changes, it is very much like the current human emotions and social conditions. See what you will ultimately conclude!" After he finished speaking, the group of ghosts laughed, each returned to their original form, and dispersed.

    ○ A Flying Coffin Lid in Qiantang, Li Jia was usually brave. One evening, he went to a friend's banquet. When he was drunk, the guests at the table said, "Half a li away from here, there is a house for sale. The price is very cheap, but it is said to be haunted by fierce ghosts, so it still has no buyer." Li said, "I regret that I have no money; even talking about it is futile." The guests said, "If you have the courage to drink alone in this place for one night, I will sell this room to you." The other guests said, "We will guarantee it." They made it a deal for tomorrow night.

    The next noon, they entered the room in a group, placed wine and food, Li drew his sword and went upstairs, and the others locked the door and left, borrowing a neighbor's house to gather and wait for news. Li looked around the hall and room, there is another small door beside it. He turned and went in, there is a narrow alley overgrown with wild grass; at the back, there is a circular door, half open and half closed. Li thought to himself, "I don't have to go in; I will wait outside for any movement." He lit a candle and drank.

    543

    至三更，闻脚步声，见一鬼高径尺，脸白如灰，两眼漆黑，披发，自小门出，直奔筵前。李怒挺剑起，其鬼转身进弄，李逐至环洞门内。顷刻狂风陡作，空中棺盖一方似风车儿飞来，向李头上盘旋。李取剑乱斫，无奈头上愈重，身子渐缩，有泰山压卵之危，不得已大叫。其友伴在邻家闻之，率众入，见李将被棺盖压倒，乃并力抢出，背负而逃。后面棺盖追来，李愈喊愈追，鸡叫一声，盖忽不见。于是救醒李甲，连夜抬归，次日，共询房主，方知后园矮室停棺，时时作祟，专飞盖压人，死者甚众。

    　　于是鸣于官，焚以烈火，其怪乃灭。李病月余始愈。常告人曰：“人声不如鸡声，岂鬼不怕人，反怕鸡耶？”

    　　○油瓶烹鬼钱塘周轶韩孝廉，性豪迈。某年暑甚，偕七八人暮夜泛湖。行至丁家山下，一友曰：“吾闻净慈寺长桥左侧多鬼，曷往寻之？或得见其真面，可供一笑。”

    543

    Final Translation

    At the third watch of the night, they heard footsteps and saw a ghost standing about a foot tall, with a face as white as ash and pitch-black eyes, its hair loose and unkempt. The ghost emerged from a small door and rushed directly to the banquet table. Li Jia, enraged, stood up and drew his sword. The ghost turned around and entered a narrow passage. Li chased it into a circular cave entrance. Suddenly, a violent wind started up, and a coffin lid flew through the air like a windmill, circling above Li's head. Li swung his sword wildly, but the weight on his head grew heavier, and his body began to shrink, as if crushed by a mountain. Helpless, he shouted for help. His friends, hearing him, rushed to his aid. They saw Li about to be crushed and lifted him out, carrying him away. The coffin lid continued to pursue them, and Li shouted louder as it pursued more intensely. A rooster crowed, and the coffin lid suddenly disappeared. The friends woke Li up and carried him back home at night. The next day, they inquired of the house owner and learned that the small room in the back garden contained a coffin that often caused mischief by flying and crushing people, resulting in many deaths. They reported the incident to the authorities and burned the coffin with intense fire, which eliminated the supernatural threat. Li was ill for over a month before recovering. He often told people, "Human voices are not as effective as rooster calls. Could it be that ghosts are not afraid of humans but are afraid of chickens?"

    Oil Bottle Ghost-Burning in Qiantang: Zhou Yihan, a Scholar of High Spirit

    In a certain year during a particularly hot summer, Zhou Yihan, a scholar of high spirit, sailed with several companions late into the night on a lake. They rowed to the foot of Ding Family Hill, and one of his friends said, "I have heard that there are many ghosts on the left side of the long bridge at Jingci Temple. Why don't we go there to find them? Perhaps we might see their true faces and get a laugh out of it."

    544

    众相怂恿上岸，同行桥边，见扳夜网者挈鱼而走。孝廉熟视，是其管坟人也，乃云：“此网借我一用，明早奉还。”管坟人允之，遂付仆从肩驮此网而行。众友询故，孝廉云：“余将把南屏山下鬼一网打尽。”各大笑，遂拣山僻小路步去。

    　　是夜月明如昼，见前林中有一妇，红衬白裙，举头看月。众友云：“此时夜深，必无女娘在外，是鬼无疑。谁敢作先锋者？”孝廉愿往，大步前进。相去半箭许，冷风吹来，妇人回身，满面血流，两眼倒挂。孝廉战栗，僵立不行，连声呼：“网来！网来！”众人向前，一网打去，不见形迹，网中仅得枯木尺许。携归，敲管坟者门，借利锯寸寸锯开，有鲜血淋漓。乃买主人点灯油一瓶，携上船尾，然火烹油，将锯断枯木送入瓶中，一时飞起青烟，竟成焦炭。

    Explanation

    544

    The crowd urged each other to go ashore, and they walked along the bridge. They saw a night fisherman carrying his catch and leaving. The Scholar carefully observed and recognized him as the manager of a grave. He then said, "May I borrow this net for a moment? I will return it by tomorrow morning." The grave manager agreed, and the Scholar had his servants carry the net. The friends asked why he needed the net, and the Scholar replied, "I intend to catch all the ghosts under Nanping Mountain in one net." They all laughed, and then chose a secluded and small path to proceed.

    That night, the moon was as bright as daylight. They saw a woman in front of the forest, dressed in a red shirt and white skirt, looking up at the moon. The friends said, "It is so late at night, there must be no woman out here. She must be a ghost. Who dares to go first?" The Scholar volunteered and took large steps forward. After a short distance, a cold wind blew, and the woman turned around, her face covered in blood, and her eyes hanging upside down. The Scholar trembled, stood stiffly and could not move, and repeatedly called out, "Bring the net! Bring the net!" The others moved forward, cast the net, but saw no trace of her. The net caught only a piece of dry wood about a foot long. They carried it back, knocked on the grave manager's door, borrowed a sharp saw, and cut the wood inch by inch, revealing fresh blood. They then bought a bottle of lamp oil from the owner, carried it to the stern of the boat, lit it, and poured the oil over the sawn dry wood. Instantly, blue smoke rose, and the wood turned into charcoal.

    545

    众人达旦入城告亲友云：“昨夜油瓶烹鬼，大是奇事。”

    　　○无门国吕恒者，常州人，贩洋货为业。乾隆四十年，为海风所吹，舟中人尽没，惟吕抱一木板，随波掀腾，飘入一国。人民皆楼居，楼有三层者、五层者；祖居第三层，父居第二层，子居第一层，其最高者则曾高祖居之。有出入之户，无遮阑之门。国人甚富，无盗窃事。

    　　吕初到时，言语不通，以手指画。久之，亦渐领解。闻是中华人，颇知礼敬。

    Explanation

    545

    People entered the city until dawn to inform their relatives and friends, saying, "Last night, we cooked a ghost with an oil bottle, which is a very strange event."

    ○ A man named Lü Heng from Wumen Country, originally from Changzhou, was engaged in the business of trading foreign goods. In the fortieth year of the Qianlong era, he was blown off course by a sea wind, and all the other people on the ship perished. Only Lü Heng managed to cling to a piece of wood, riding the waves and eventually drifting into a country. The people of this land all lived in tall buildings, with some having three stories and others five. The most senior ancestors resided on the third floor, fathers on the second, and sons on the first, with the highest floor reserved for the great-great-grandfather. The buildings had entrances and exits but no doors or barriers. The people were very wealthy, and there were no incidents of theft.

    When Lü Heng first arrived, he could not communicate due to language differences and used gestures to convey his meaning. Over time, he gradually understood the language. Upon learning that he was from China, the locals showed him great respect and courtesy.

    546

    其俗分一日为两日，鸡鸣而起，贸易往来；至日午则举国安寝，日斜时起，照常行事，至戌时又睡矣。问其年，称十岁者，中国之五岁也；称二十者，中国之十岁也。吕所居处，离国王尚有千里，无由得见。官员甚少，有仪从者，呼为“巴罗”，亦不知是何职司。男女相悦为婚，好丑老少，各以类从，无搀越勉强致嗟怨者。刑法尤奇，断人足者亦断其足，伤人面者亦伤其面，分寸部位，丝毫不爽。

    　　奸人子女者，使人亦奸其子女。如犯人无子女，则削木作男子势伏，其臀窍。

    　　吕居其国十有三月，因南风之便，附船还中国。据老洋客云：“此岛号”无门国“，从古来未有通中国者。

    　　○宋生苏州宋观察宗元之族弟某，幼孤依叔，叔待之严。七岁时，赴塾师处读书，偷往戏场看戏，被人告知其叔，惧不敢归，逃于木渎乡作乞丐。有李姓者，怜而收留之，俾在钱铺佣工，颇勤慎，遂以婢郑氏配之。如是者九年，宋生颇积资财。

    Explanation

    546

    In this country, the day is divided into two parts: people rise at dawn and engage in trade and commerce; at noon, the entire nation rests, and they resume their activities in the afternoon, continuing as usual until the ninth hour of the evening when they sleep again. When asked about their age, those who claim to be ten years old are actually five years old by Chinese reckoning, and those who claim to be twenty are actually ten years old. The narrator, Lu, lived far from the king, a thousand miles away, and had no opportunity to meet him. There were very few officials, and those who had attendants were called "Baro," though their exact duties were unknown. Marriages were based on mutual attraction, with people of similar appearance and age marrying each other, and there were no forced marriages that led to complaints. The legal system was particularly unusual: if someone's foot was cut off, the punishment would be the cutting off of the offender's foot; if someone's face was injured, the punishment would be the injuring of the offender's face, with the exact location and severity being precisely matched.

    If someone seduced another's children, the punishment would be to seduce the offender's children. If the offender had no children, a wooden figure would be carved to represent a man, and the buttocks would be marked.

    Lu stayed in the country for thirteen months and returned to China by ship, taking advantage of the south wind. According to an old foreigner, the island was known as "No Gates Country" and had never been in contact with China since ancient times.

    ○ C, a relative of Song Zongyuan, the observer of Suzhou, was orphaned at a young age and lived with his uncle, who treated him strictly. At the age of seven, C went to a tutor to study but secretly went to the theater to watch a play. He was discovered by his uncle, who was afraid to return home and fled to the town of Muxu, where he became a beggar. A man surnamed Li took pity on him and took him in, allowing him to work in a money shop. C was diligent and careful, and Li eventually married him to his maid Zheng. After nine years, C had accumulated a considerable amount of wealth.

    547

    到城内烧香，遇其叔于途，势不能瞒，遂以实告。叔知其有蓄，劝令还家，别为择配。生初意不肯，且告叔云：“婢已生女矣。”叔怒曰：“我家大族，岂可以婢为妻？”逼令离婚。李家闻之，情愿认婢为女，另备妆奁陪嫁。叔不许，命写离书寄郑，而别娶于金氏。郑得书大哭，抱其女自沉于河。

    　　越三年，金氏亦生一女。其叔坐轿过王府基，忽旋风刮帘而起，家人视之，痰涌气绝，颈有爪痕。是夜，金氏梦一女子披发沥血诉曰：“我郑氏婢也。汝夫不良，听从恶叔之言，将我离异。我义不再嫁，投河死。今我先报其叔，当即来报汝。夫与汝无干，汝无怖也。但汝所生之女我不能饶，以女易女，亦是公道报法。”妻醒，告宋生。生大骇，谋之友。友曰：“玄妙观有施道士，能作符驱鬼，俾其作法牒之酆都可也。”乃以重币赂施。施取女之生年月日写黄纸上，加天师符，押解酆都，其家果平静。

    Explanation

    547

    He went to burn incense in the city, and on the way met his uncle, who could not be deceived, so he told the truth.
