His uncle, knowing he had something hidden, persuaded him to return home and find another suitable match. Initially, he refused, and told his uncle, "The maid has already given birth to a daughter." His uncle angrily said, "Our family is of high status; how can we marry a maid?" He forced a divorce. The Li family, upon hearing this, willingly acknowledged the maid as their daughter and prepared a dowry for her. His uncle did not allow it and ordered a divorce document to be sent to Zheng, while he remarried Madam Jin. Zheng received the document, cried, and threw himself and his daughter into the river.

    After three years, Madam Jin also gave birth to a daughter. His uncle was riding in a sedan chair when he passed by the foundation of the Prince's mansion. Suddenly, a whirlwind swept the curtain and rose. The family saw him foam at the mouth and die, with claw marks on his neck. That night, Madam Jin dreamed of a woman with loose hair and dripping blood, who said, "I was Madam Zheng's maid. Your husband was unworthy and listened to his evil uncle's words, causing me to be divorced. I honored my duty and would not remarry, so I threw myself into the river and died. Now I first report to his uncle, and I will come to report to you. Your husband has nothing to do with this; do not be afraid. But your daughter, I cannot spare. By exchanging daughters, it is also a fair way of retribution." She woke up and told Cao Sheng. He was greatly frightened and consulted his friend. The friend said, "The Xuanmiao Temple has a Daoist named Shi who can write talismans to exorcise ghosts. Let him perform a ritual to send the spirit to Fengdu." They bribed Shi with valuable gifts. Shi wrote the girl's birth date and year on yellow paper, added a talisman from the Heavenly Master, and sent her to Fengdu. The family indeed became peaceful.

    548

    三年后，生方坐书窗，白日见此婢来骂曰：“我先拿汝叔迟拿汝者，为恶意非从汝起，且犹恋从前夫妻之情故也。今汝反先下手，牒我酆都，何不良至此？

    　　今我牒限已满，将冤诉与城隍神。神嘉我贞烈，许我报仇，汝复何逃？“宋生从此痴迷，不省人事。家中器具，无故自碎；门撑棍棒，空中乱飞。举家大惧，延僧超度，终于无益。十日内宋生死；十日外其女死；金氏无恙。

    　　○尸香二则杭州孙秀姑，年十六，为李氏养媳。李翁挈其子远出，家只一姑，年老矣。

    　　邻匪严虎窥秀姑有色，借乞火为名，将语挑之。秀姑不从。乃遣所嬖某作饵，搔头弄姿，为蛊惑计。秀姑告其姑，姑骂斥之。严虎大怒詈曰：“女奴不承抬举，我不淫汝不止！”朝夕飞砖撬门。李家素贫，板壁单薄，绝少亲友，严又无赖，邻人无敢撄其锋，于是婆媳相持而哭。

    Explanation

    548

    Three years later, Song Sheng was sitting at his study desk when, in broad daylight, this maid came to scold him, saying, "I caught your uncle before catching you, out of malice and not because of you, and still clinging to the former marital affection. Now you have taken the initiative and filed a case against me in Fengdu, how wicked can you be?

    Now my case has reached its limit, and I will appeal my grievance to the City God. The god commends my loyalty and purity and allows me to take revenge. How can you escape?" From then on, Song Sheng became delirious and lost consciousness. The household items shattered for no reason, and door supports and sticks flew around in the air. The whole family was greatly frightened and invoked monks for a ritual to help the soul, but ultimately, it was of no help. Within ten days, Song Sheng died; ten days later, his daughter died; Jin Shi remained unharmed.

    Two Cases of Corpse Fragrance

    Sun Xiugū, a sixteen-year-old girl from Hangzhou, was adopted as the daughter-in-law of the Li family. The Li father took his son far away, leaving the household with only an elderly aunt. A neighboring ruffian named Yan Hu coveted Xiugū's beauty and used the pretext of borrowing a light to seduce her. Xiugū refused. Yan Hu then sent a favored woman to entice her with seductive gestures, planning to bewitch her. Xiugū told her aunt, who scolded her. Yan Hu flew into a rage and shouted, "You insignificant wench! I won't stop until I have my way with you!" He threw bricks and pried at the door day and night. The Li family was poor, their walls thin, and they had few friends. Yan Hu was also a scoundrel, and the neighbors dared not confront him. Thus, the grandmother and granddaughter held each other and wept.

    549

    一日者，秀姑晨起梳头，严与其嬖登屋上，各解裤挺其阳以示之。秀姑不胜忿，遂密缝内外衣重重牢固，而私服盐卤死。其姑哀号，欲告官，无为具呈者。

    　　忽有异香从秀姑所卧处起，直达街巷，行路者皆愕眙相视。严虎知之，取死猫死狗诸秽物罗置李门外，以乱其气，而其香愈盛。适有总捕厅某路过，闻其香而怪之，查问街邻，得其冤，乃告知府县，置严虎于法，而旌秀姑于朝。至今西湖上牌坊犹存。

    　　荆州府范某乡居，家甚富，而早卒，子六岁，倚其姊以居。姊年十九，知书解算，料理家务甚有法。族匪范同欺其弟幼，屡来贷借，姊初应之；继为无厌之求，姊不能应。范同大怒，与其党谋去其姊，为吞噬计，乃俟城隍赛会时，沉其姊于河。又缚沉一钱店少年，以两带束其尸，报官相验，云：“平素有奸，惧人知觉，故相约同死。”县官信之，命棺殓掩埋而已。范氏家产尽为族匪所占。

    Explanation

    549

    One day, Xiu Gu, in the morning, combed her hair. Yan and his favorite concubine climbed onto the roof, and each unbuttoned their pants and erected their penis to show her. Xiu Gu was overwhelmed with anger and secretly sewed her inner and outer clothes layer upon layer, firmly, and then privately drank salt brine to die. Her aunt wailed in grief, wanting to report to the authorities, but no one was available to prepare a formal complaint.

    Suddenly, a strange fragrance arose from where Xiu Gu lay, reaching the streets and alleys, and passersby all stared in astonishment. Yan Hu knew about it and took dead cats, dead dogs, and various filthy things, arranging them outside Li's door to confuse the smell, but the fragrance became even stronger. Just then, a certain official from the General Police Hall passed by, smelled the fragrance, and found it strange. He investigated the neighbors and discovered her injustice. He then informed the prefecture and county, punished Yan Hu according to the law, and commended Xiu Gu in the court. To this day, a monument still exists on West Lake.

    In Jingzhou Prefecture, a certain Fan lived in the countryside. His family was very wealthy, but he died early. His son, six years old, relied on his sister to live. The sister, nineteen years old, knew books and could calculate, and managed household affairs very well. Clan outlaws, Fan Tong, took advantage of his young brother, often coming to borrow. The sister initially agreed, but later made insatiable demands, and the sister could not comply. Fan Tong was furious and planned with his gang to get rid of her, intending to devour her. He waited until the City God Festival and drowned her sister in the river. He also tied up and drowned a young man from a money shop, bound his body with two belts, and reported to the authorities for inspection, saying, "They had an affair, feared being discovered, so they agreed to die together." The county magistrate believed it and ordered a coffin and burial, that was all. Fan's family property all became occupied by the clan outlaws.

    550

    逾年，荆州太守周钟宣到任，过范女坟，有异香从其坟起。问书役，中有知其冤者，为白其事，乃掘男女两坟验之。尸各如生，手足颈项皆有捆缚伤痕。于是拘讯范同，则数日前已为厉鬼祟死矣。太守具酒食香纸躬祭女坟，表一碣曰：“贞女范氏之墓。”冤白后，两尸俱腐化。

    　　○储梅夫府丞是云麾使者储梅夫宗丞能养生，七十而有婴儿之色。乾隆庚辰正月，奉使祭告岳渎，宿搜敦邮亭。是夕，旅店灯花散采，倏忽变现，如莲花，如如意，如芝兰，喷烟高二三尺，有风雾回旋。急呼家童观之，共为诧异，相戒勿动。是夕，梦见群仙五六人招至一所，上书“赤云冈”三字，呼储为云麾使者。诸仙列坐松阴联句，有称海上神翁者首唱曰：“莲炬今宵献瑞芝。”次至五松丈人续曰：“群仙佳会飘吟髭。”又次至东方青童曰：“春风欲换杨柳。”旁一女仙笑曰：“此云麾使者过凌河句也，汝何故窃之？”相与一笑。忽灯花作爆竹声，惊醒。

    Explanation

    550

    The following year, the governor of Jingzhou, Zhou Zhongxuan, arrived at his post and passed by the grave of Fan Woman. A strange fragrance emanated from her grave. He questioned the clerks, and among them was one who knew of her innocence, who explained the situation. They then dug up the graves of both men and women to investigate. The bodies were still like the living, with binding marks on their limbs and necks. Consequently, Fan Tong was arrested and interrogated, and it was found that he had been killed by a malevolent ghost several days prior. The governor prepared wine, food, incense, and paper money to personally offer at the woman's grave, erecting a stele that read, "The Tomb of the Chaste Woman Fan." After the truth was revealed, both bodies decomposed.

    ○Chu Meifu, a vice chancellor and envoy of the cloud flag, was skilled in preserving life and at seventy years old, he still had the appearance of a baby. In the first month of the year 1770 during the Qianlong era, he was sent on a mission to offer sacrifices to the mountains and rivers and stayed at a post station. That evening, the lamp flower in the inn scattered colors, suddenly transforming into shapes like lotus, wish-fulfilling jewel, and orchid and calamus, spraying smoke two or three feet high with wind and mist swirling. He quickly called the servants to watch, and all were astonished, warning each other not to touch it. That night, he dreamed of being invited by five or six immortals to a place with the words "Chiyun Gang" written above, and they called him the envoy of the cloud flag. The immortals sat under the pines and composed poetry together. One called the divine old man of the sea sang first, saying, "The lotus torch tonight offers auspicious fungus." Next, the old man of the five pines continued, saying, "The gathering of immortals makes the beard fly with verses." Then the Eastern Green Child said, "Spring wind wants to change the willows." A nearby female immortal laughed and said, "This is the line of the envoy of the cloud flag crossing the Leng River. Why did you steal it?" They laughed together. Suddenly, the lamp flower made a firecracker sound, and he woke up startled.

    551

    ○唐配沧武昌司马唐配沧，杭人也，素有孝行，卒于官。后五年其长子在亭远馆四川，长媳郭氏在杭病剧，忽作司马公语云：“冥司念我居官清正，敕为武昌府城隍。

    　　念尔等新作人家，我既无遗物与汝辈，斯妇颇勤俭，特来救护。但须至狮子桥觅刘老娘来，托他禳解。“

    　　伊次子字开武者往觅得，邀至家中，即杭俗所称“活无常”也。问：“此病汝能救否？”答云：“我奉冥司勾捉，何敢私纵？今尔家太爷去向阎罗王说情，或得生亦未可定。”因问：“你见太爷何在？”答云：“此刻现在向灶神说情。”

    　　少顷曰：“太爷出门，想至冥府去了。”病者静卧不言，逾时曰：“太爷来。”

    　　病者即大声曰：“汝已得生，无虑也。”是时，视病者有亲友在座，郭氏作司马语，各道款洽，宛如生前。

    Explanation

    551

    ○Tang Peicang, the Military Governor of Wuchang, was a native of Hangzhou and was known for his filial piety. He died in office. Five years after his death, his eldest son, Wang Zaiting, was stationed far away in Sichuan. His eldest daughter-in-law, Guo, fell gravely ill in Hangzhou. Suddenly, she spoke as if she were Tang Peicang, saying: "The underworld has recognized my integrity while in office and has appointed me as the guardian deity of Wuchang. Considering your family is newly established, I have no material possessions to leave you. This woman is very diligent and thrifty, so I have come to protect you. But you must go to Lion Bridge to find Old Lady Liu and ask her to perform a ritual to resolve the issue."

    His second son, named Kaiwu, went to find Old Lady Liu, who turned out to be a living version of the deity known as "Wu Chang." He asked, "Can you save this illness?" She replied, "I am acting on behalf of the underworld, so I dare not release you privately. Now, your father has gone to plead with Yama, the king of hell, and you might still be able to live, but it's not certain. Where is your father now?" She answered, "He is currently pleading with the Kitchen God."

    After a while, she said, "Your father has left, and I think he has gone to the underworld." The sick woman lay quietly without speaking. After some time, she said, "My father has returned."

    The sick woman then spoke loudly, "You have been saved; there is no need to worry." At this time, there were friends and relatives present to see the sick woman, and Guo, speaking as Tang Peicang, greeted each of them warmly, just as if he were alive.

    552

    其次子因跪请云：“父既为神，应预知休咎，儿辈将来究作何结局？”司马厉声曰：“做好人，行好事，自有好日，何得预问？”又云：“我今日为家私事勤劳庙中夫役，速焚纸钱，并给酒饭酬之。”语毕，病者仍复原音，病亦自愈。

    　　此乾隆二十年五月事，至今郭氏尚存。

    　　○裘文达公为水神裘文达公临卒语家人曰：“我是燕子矶水神，今将复位。死后，汝等送灵柩江西，必过此矶，有关帝庙，可往求签。如系上上第三签者，我仍为水神。否则，或有谴谪，不能复位矣。”言终卒。家人闻之，疑信参半，苍头某信之独坚，曰：“公为王太夫人所生。太夫人本籍江宁，渡江时，曾求子于燕子矶水神庙。夜梦袍笏者来曰：”与汝儿，并与汝一好儿。‘果逾年生公。“公妻熊夫人挈柩归，至燕子矶，如其言，卜于关帝庙，果有第三签，遂举家大哭，烧纸钱蔽江，立木主于庙旁。旁有尹文端公诗碣。

    Explanation

    552

    The Second Son knelt and asked, "Since you are now a god, you should already know good or bad fortune. What will be the ultimate fate of my children?" The Master spoke sternly, "Do good deeds, perform good actions, and there will naturally be good days. Why should you ask in advance?" He continued, "I am working hard today for family matters, laboring with the temple workers. Burn the paper money quickly and give them food and drink as a reward." After speaking, the sick person returned to their original voice, and the illness healed on its own.

    This happened in May of the 20th year of the Qianlong era, and to this day, the Guo family still exists.

    Qiu Wendada, as the Water God, spoke to his family on his deathbed, "I am the water god of Yanzijisi, and I am about to return to my position. After I die, you will send my coffin to Jiangxi. You will surely pass by this矶 (a small hill or cliff), where there is a Temple of Guan Yu, where you can go to draw a lot. If it is the third lot marked as the best, I will remain the water god. Otherwise, I may be punished and unable to return to my position." He spoke these words and then died. The family heard this and were half-believing, half-doubting. A certain servant believed it firmly, saying, "He was born to Lady Wang, the Grand Lady. Lady Wang, originally from Jiangning, prayed for a son at the Temple of the Water God of Yanzijisi when she crossed the river. One night, she dreamed of someone in official robes and a tablet who said, 'I will give you a son and a good son.' Indeed, a year later, he was born." His wife, Lady Xiong, carried the coffin back home. When they reached Yanzijisi, as he had said, they drew a lot at the Temple of Guan Yu, and it was indeed the third lot. They then cried as a family, burned paper money until it covered the river, and set up a wooden tablet beside the temple. Beside it, there was a stele with poetry by Yin Wenzhong.

    553

    予往苏州，阻风于此，乃揖其主而题壁曰：“燕子矶边泊，黄公垆下过。摩挲旧碑碣，惆怅此山阿。短鬓皤皤雪，长江渺渺波。江神如识我，应送好风多。”

    　　次日果大顺风。

    　　○庄生叶祥榴孝廉云：其友陈姓家延西席庄生。八月间日暮，诸生课毕，陈姓弟兄弈于书斋，庄旁观之，倦，起身归家。

    　　庄家离陈姓里许，须过一轿。庄生上桥失足跌地，急起趋家，扣门不应，仍返陈氏斋。陈弟兄弈局未终，乃闲步庭院。见轩后小门内有园亭，巨蕉无数，心叹主人有此雅室不作书斋。再数步，见小亭中孕妇临蓐，色颇美，心觉动。既而曰：“此东人内室见此不退，非礼也。”趋出，仍至斋中小坐。见主人棋为乃弟暗攻，主人他顾，若不觉者，代为通知。主人张皇似惊，仍复不睬。庄复大声呼曰：“不依我，全盘输了！”且以手到局上指告。陈氏兄弟惊惶趋内，灯为之熄。

    Explanation

    553

    I went to Suzhou, but was delayed by the wind. I then bowed to the host and wrote on the wall: "Moored at Yanzi Ridge, passed by Huang Gong's wine shop. Rubbing and examining old inscriptions, melancholy about this mountain. My white hair like snow, the vast waves of the Yangtze River. If the river god knows me, he should send more favorable winds."

    The next day, there was indeed a strong favorable wind.

    Mr. Zhuang Xiangliu, a scholar-official, said: His friend, the Chen family, invited Zhuang as a private tutor. In the evening of August, after the students had finished their lessons, the brothers of the Chen family were playing chess in the study. Zhuang watched them, feeling tired, and got up to go home.

    Zhuang's house was about a carriage ride away from the Chen family. As he crossed a bridge, he lost his footing and fell to the ground. He hurriedly got up and went home, but there was no response when he knocked on the door. He returned to the Chen family's study. The brothers' chess game was not yet finished, so he took a leisurely walk in the courtyard. He saw a garden pavilion inside a small door behind the hall, with many large banana plants. He thought that the owner had such a beautiful room but did not use it as a study. After a few more steps, he saw a pregnant woman giving birth in a small pavilion. She was quite beautiful, and his heart was moved. He then said, "If the owner's inner room saw this and did not retreat, it would be improper." He hurried out and sat in the study. He saw the master's chess pieces being attacked by his younger brother, but the master looked elsewhere, as if he did not notice. Zhuang took it upon himself to inform the master. The master was startled and seemed surprised, but he still ignored it. Zhuang shouted loudly, "If you don't follow me, you will lose the entire game!" And he pointed to the board with his hand. The Chen brothers hurried inside in panic, and the light went out.

    554

    庄不得已，仍回家。至桥，复又一跌，起，赴家扣门，阍者纳焉。庄以前吹扣门不应之事罪其家人，家人曰：“前未闻也。”

    　　庄次日赴馆，见灯盏在地，棋局尚存，恍然若梦。少顷，主人出曰：“昨夜先生去后，鬼声大作，甚至灭火，真怪事。”庄骇然，告以曾来教棋。东人曰：“吾弟兄并未见先生复至。”庄曰：“且有一证：我到尊府花园，见有临蓐夫人。”

    　　陈笑曰：“我家并无花园，何有此妇？”庄曰：“在轩后。”庄即拉陈同至轩后，有小土门，内仅菜园半亩，西角有一猪圈，育小猪六口，五生一毙，庄悚然大悟：盖过桥一跌，其魂已出；后一跌，则魂仍附体。倘不戒于淫，则堕入畜生道矣。

    Explanation

    554

    Braced with no other choice, Zhuang returned home. Upon reaching the bridge, he fell again. He got up and went home to knock on the door, which was opened by the gatekeeper. Zhuang blamed his family for not answering the door earlier, but they replied, "We didn't hear anything before."

    The next day, Zhuang went to the place where he had been teaching chess. He found the lamp on the ground and the chessboard still set up, and he suddenly felt as if he were in a dream. Soon after, the owner came out and said, "After Master left last night, ghostly sounds became very loud, even putting out the lamp—it was truly a strange occurrence." Zhuang was startled and told him that he had come to teach chess. The owner said, "My brothers and I did not see the teacher come back." Zhuang replied, "I have evidence: when I arrived at your garden, I saw a woman in labor."

    The owner laughed and said, "My family has no garden, how could there be such a woman?" Zhuang said, "It was behind the pavilion." Zhuang then took the owner to the back of the pavilion, where there was a small earthen gate. Inside was a half-acre vegetable garden, with a pigsty in the southwest corner containing six young pigs, five of which were alive and one dead. Zhuang suddenly realized with great enlightenment: it was because of the first fall over the bridge that his soul had left his body; the second fall allowed his soul to return to his body. If he had not abstained from lust, he would have fallen into the realm of animals.

    555

    ○褐道人国初，德侍郎某与褐道人善。道人精相术，言公某年升官，某年得红顶，某年当遭雷击，德公疑信参半。后升官一如其言，乃大惧，恳道人避雷击之法。道人故作难色。再四求之，始言：“只有一法。公于是日约朝中一二品官十余位，环坐前厅大炕上，公坐当中，过午时则免。”德公如其言。

    　　至是日，天气清朗，将午，起黑云，风雨毕至，雷声轰轰，欲下复止。忽家人飞报：“老太太被雷摄至院中。”德公大惊，与各官急趋往扶，则霹雳一声，将炕击碎。视其中，有一大蝎，长二尺许，太夫人故无恙也。寻褐道人，已不见矣。始知道人即蝎精也，以术愚人，实以自卫，智亦巧矣。非雷更巧，则德公竟不知为其所用也。

    Explanation

    555

    ○ Brown-robed Man, Early Qing Dynasty. Duke De was close friends with the brown-robed man. The brown-robed man was skilled in physiognomy and predicted that Duke De would be promoted in a certain year, receive a red hat (indicating a high rank) in another year, and be struck by lightning in yet another year. Duke De was half-believing and half-doubting these predictions. Later, he was indeed promoted as predicted, which greatly frightened him. He repeatedly requested the brown-robed man to find a way to avoid being struck by lightning. The brown-robed man acted reluctant but eventually said, "There is only one way. On that day, you must invite more than ten officials of the first and second ranks from the court to sit around a large kang in the main hall. You should sit in the center, and if it passes noon, you will be safe." Duke De followed his advice.

    On that day, the weather was clear, but just before noon, dark clouds gathered, and a storm broke out with thunder rumbling. Suddenly, a family member rushed in to report, "Lady Grandmother has been struck by lightning and taken to the courtyard." Duke De was greatly alarmed and rushed with the officials to help. Just as they arrived, a loud clap of thunder struck the kang, shattering it. Upon inspection, they found a large scorpion, about two feet long, in the center of the kang. Lady Grandmother was unharmed. When they searched for the brown-robed man, he was nowhere to be found. It was then revealed that the brown-robed man was actually a scorpion spirit who used his skills to deceive people and protect himself. If not for the cleverness of the thunder, Duke De would never have known he was being used.

    556

    ○佟<角奇>角京师傅九者，出正阳门，过一巷，路狭人众，挨肩而行。一人劈面来，急走如飞，势甚猛。傅不及避，两胸相撞，竟与己身合而为一，顿觉身如水淋，寒噤不止，急投一缎店坐定。忽大言曰：“你拦我去路，可恶已极。”于是自批其颊，自捋其须。家人迎归，彻夜吵闹。或言：“有活无常佟犄角者能治之。”正将延请，而傅九已知之，骂曰：“我不怕铜犄角、铁犄角也。”

    　　未几佟至，目视曰：“汝何处鬼，来此害人？速供来。不实供，叉汝下油锅！”傅瞪目不言，但切齿咋咋有声。其时男女观者如堵。佟倾油一锅，烧柴煎之，手持一铜叉，向傅脸上旋绕作欲刺状。傅果战惧，自供：“我李四也，凤阳人。迫于饥寒，盗发人坟，被人捉着。一时仓猝，用铁锹拒捕，连伤二人。坐法当斩，今日绑赴菜市。我极力挣脱逃来，不料此人拦住，心实忿忿，故与较论。”

    Explanation

    556

    ○Master Feng Jiqi, upon leaving the Zhengyang Gate, passed through a narrow alley where people were crowded, walking shoulder to shoulder. Suddenly, a man approached him face-to-face, running as fast as a bird, with great momentum. Feng did not have time to avoid him, and their chests collided, as if they had merged into one. He suddenly felt as if he were drenched in water, shivering uncontrollably. He quickly entered a silk shop and sat down. Suddenly, he shouted loudly: "You blocked my way, how despicable!" Then he slapped his own cheek and pulled his beard. His family came to take him home, and he continued to make noise all night. Someone said, "There is a living and non-living Feng Jiqi who can deal with this." Just as they were about to invite him, Feng Jiqi had already heard of it and shouted, "I am not afraid of copper Jiqi or iron Jiqi!"

    Soon, Feng Jiqi arrived, glaring at him and saying, "Where are you, a ghost, coming from to harm people? Quickly confess. If you do not confess truthfully, I will stab you into the oil pot!" Feng stared at him without speaking, but clenched his teeth and made a grunting sound. At that time, there were many people watching. Feng Jiqi poured oil into a pot, lit it, and started to fry something, holding a copper fork and waving it over Feng's face as if to stab him. Feng was indeed terrified and confessed, "I am Li Si, a man from Fuyang. Forced by hunger and cold, I stole and desecrated graves, and was caught. In a moment of panic, I used an iron shovel to resist, injuring two people. According to the law, I should be sentenced to death, and I was bound to be executed at the vegetable market. I struggled and escaped, but this person blocked my way, and I was really angry, so I argued with him."

    557

    佟曰：“然则速去勿迟。”乃倚叉而坐。傅大哭曰：“小人在狱中两脚冻烂，不能行走，求赐草鞋一双。且求秘密，不教官府知道，再来捉拿。”傅家人即烧草鞋与之。乃伏地叩头，伸脚作穿状。观者皆笑。佟问：“何往？”曰：“逃祸须远，将奔云南。”佟曰：“云南万里，岂旦夕可至？半路必为差役所拿。不如跟我服役，可得一吃饭处也。”傅叩头情愿。佟出囊中黄纸小符焚之，傅仆地不动，良久苏醒，问之茫然。是日刑部秋审，访之，果有发墓之犯，已枭示矣，盖恶鬼犹不自知其已死也。

    　　佟年五十馀，寡言爱睡，往往睡三四日不起。至其家者，重门以内，无寸芥纤埃。云其平日所服役者，皆鬼也。

    Explanation

    557

    佟说：“那么快走，别迟疑。”于是他倚着叉子坐下。傅大哭道：“我在监狱中双脚冻烂，无法行走，请求赐予一双草鞋。而且请求保密，不让官府知道，再来捉拿我。”傅的家人立刻烧了草鞋给他。傅于是伏地叩头，伸脚作穿鞋状。观看的人都笑了。佟问：“你要去哪里？”傅说：“逃祸需要远走，我将前往云南。”佟说：“云南万里之遥，怎能一日两日到达？半路上必定会被差役捉拿。不如跟我一起服役，可以得到一个吃饭的地方。”傅叩头表示愿意。佟拿出袋中的黄纸小符烧了，傅倒在地上一动不动，过了很久才苏醒，问他时他茫然不知。当天刑部进行秋审，调查后发现确实有一个盗墓的罪犯已经被处决并示众了，原来恶鬼还不知道自己已经死了。

    佟五十多岁，寡言少语，喜爱睡觉，常常睡上三四天不起床。到他家的人，屋内没有一丝尘埃。据说他平日所役使的都是鬼。

    558

    ○淘气永州守恩公之奴，年少狡黠，取名淘气。服事书房，见檐前流萤一点，光大如鸡卵，心异之。时天暑，赤卧床上，觉阴处蠕蠕有物动。摸视之，即萤火也。

    　　笑曰：“幺麽小虫，亦爱此物耶！”引被覆身而睡。夜半，有人伸手被中，扪其阴，且捋其棱角，按其马眼。其时身欲转折，竟不能动，似有人来交接者。良久，精遗矣。

    　　次日，身颇倦惫，然冥想其趣，欲其再至，不以告人。日暮浴身，裸以俟之。

    　　二更许，萤火先来，光愈大，照见一女甚美，冉冉而至。奴大喜，抱持之，遂与绸缪。叩其姓氏，曰：“妾姓姚，父某，为明季知府，曾居此衙。妾年十八，以所慕不遂，成瘵而死。生时酷爱梨花，断气时嘱老母即葬此园梨树下。爱卿年少，故来相就。”奴方知其为鬼，举枕投之，大呼而出，径叩宅门。宅中妇女疑为火起，争起开门，见其赤身，俱不敢前。主人自出，叱而问之，奴以实告，乃命服以朱砂，且为着裤。

    Explanation

    558

    The Young Servant of Yongzhou, Known as Taoqi

    Taoqi, the young and cunning servant of Governor En of Yongzhou, was given the name Taoqi. He served in the study and one day saw a single firefly fluttering under the eaves, its light as large as a chicken egg, which surprised him. It was a hot day, and he lay naked on the bed, feeling something moving in the dark. When he touched it, he found it to be the firefly.

    He laughed and said, "What a tiny insect, does it also love this thing?" He pulled the blanket over himself and fell asleep. At midnight, someone reached into the blanket, touching his private parts, stroking his sides, and pressing his testicles. At that moment, he tried to move but could not, as if someone was coming to join him. After a while, he released his semen.

    The next day, his body was quite tired, but he thought about the fun of it and wanted it to happen again, so he did not tell anyone. At dusk, he bathed and waited naked.

    Around the second watch, the firefly came first, and its light became even brighter, illuminating a very beautiful woman who slowly approached. The servant was delighted, hugged her, and they became intimate. He asked her name, and she replied, "My name is Yao. My father was a magistrate during the late Ming Dynasty and once lived in this office. I was eighteen years old and died of consumption because my desires were unfulfilled. When I was alive, I loved pear blossoms, and when I died, I asked my mother to bury me under a pear tree in this garden. Since you are young, I came to be with you." The servant then realized she was a ghost, threw a pillow at her, shouted, and ran to knock on the door. The women in the house thought there was a fire, rushed to open the door, and saw him naked, but were too afraid to approach. The master came out, scolded him, and asked what happened. The servant told the truth, and the master ordered him to dress with cinnabar and put on pants.

    559

    次日，掘梨树下，果得一朱棺，剖而视之，女色如生，乃焚而葬之。奴自此恂恂，不复狡黠。伙伴笑曰：“人不可不遇鬼，淘气遇鬼，不复淘气矣。”

    　　○白莲教京山富人许翁，世居桑湖畔。娶新妇某，妆奁颇厚。有偷儿杨三者，羡之年余。闻翁送其子入京，新妇有孕，相伴惟二婢，乃夜入其室，伏暗处伺之。

    　　至三更后，灯光下见有一人，深目虬须，负黄布囊，爬窗而入。杨念：吾道中无此人。屏息窥之。其人袖出香一枝，烧之于灯，置二婢所，随向妇寝处喃喃诵咒。妇忽跃起，向其人赤身长跪。其人开囊，出一小刀，剖腹取胎，放小磁罐中，背负而出，妇尸仆于床下。杨大惊，出户尾之。至村口一旅店，抱持之，大呼曰：“主人速来，吾捉得一妖贼！”众邻齐至，视其布囊，小儿胎血犹涔涔也。

    559

    The next day, they dug under the pear tree and indeed found a red coffin. Upon cutting it open and looking inside, the woman's appearance was as if she were still alive. They then burned her and buried her. The servant became timid and no longer acted cunningly. His companions laughed and said, "One cannot avoid meeting ghosts; if a mischievous person meets a ghost, they will no longer be mischievous."

    ○ A wealthy man named Xu from Jing Mountain, who had lived for generations by the side of Sang Lake, married a new wife who had a very rich dowry. There was a thief named Yang San who had envied this for more than a year. He heard that Xu had sent his son to the capital and that his new wife was pregnant, with only two maids accompanying her. So he entered her room at night, hiding in the dark and waiting for her.

    After the third watch of the night, under the light, he saw a person with deep-set eyes and a curly beard, carrying a yellow cloth bag, climb through the window. Yang thought, "I don't know such a person." He held his breath and watched her. The person pulled out a branch of incense from his sleeve, burned it on the lamp, and placed it where the two maids were. Then he moved to the woman's sleeping area and muttered spells. The woman suddenly jumped up, stood up naked, and knelt down before him. The person opened the bag, took out a small knife, cut open her abdomen, and took out the fetus, placing it in a small porcelain jar. He carried it on his back and left, and the woman's body fell on the floor. Yang was very surprised, went out the door, and followed him. At the village entrance, at a roadside inn, he grabbed him and shouted loudly, saying, "Come quickly, master! I caught an evil thief!" All the neighbors came together, looked at his cloth bag, and saw that the baby's blood was still dripping.

    560

    众大怒，持锹锄击之。其人大笑，了无所伤；乃沃以粪，始不能动。

    　　及旦，送官刑讯，曰：“我白莲教也，伙伴甚多。”方知汉、湘一带胎妇身死者，皆受此害。狱成，凌迟其人，赏偷儿银五十两。

    　　○服桂子长生吕琪从其兄官岭南司马，署有古井，夏夜纳凉，见井中有声然，升起数红丸，大如弹棋，疑有宝。次早，遣人缒下探焉，得隔年桂子数十粒，鲜赤可爱。

    　　琪戏以井水服焉，日七枚，七日而尽。顿觉精神强健，如服参者然，年九十馀。

    　　○伊五披甲人伊五者，身矮而貌陋，不悦于军官。贫不能自活，独走出城，将自缢。

    　　忽见有老人飘然而来，问：“何故轻生？”伊以实告。老人笑曰：“子神气不凡，可以学道。予有一书授子，够一生衣食矣。”伊乃随行数里，过一大溪，披芦苇而入，路甚曲折，进一矮屋，止息其中，从老人受学。七日而术成，老人与屋皆不见。伊自此小康。

    Explanation

    560

    The crowd became furious and attacked him with shovels and hoes. The man laughed, and was completely unharmed; they then drenched him with manure, and he could finally not move.

    By dawn, he was taken to the authorities for interrogation, and said, "I am a follower of the White Lotus Society, and I have many companions." It was then discovered that pregnant women in the Han and Xiang regions who died were all victims of this. After the trial, the man was executed by slicing, and the thief was rewarded with fifty taels of silver.

    ○ Consuming the osmanthus seeds for longevity

    Lü Qi followed his brother to serve as a military governor in Lingnan. The office had an ancient well. On a summer night, seeking coolness, he saw a sound in the well, and several red pills rose, as large as a go stone. He suspected there was treasure. The next morning, he sent someone down to investigate, and found dozens of osmanthus seeds from the previous year, fresh and red, lovely.

    Qi jokingly drank the well water with the seeds, seven per day, and finished them in seven days. He suddenly felt strong and healthy, like one who has taken ginseng, and lived to be over ninety years old.

    ○ Yi Wu, the armored man

    Yi Wu, short and ugly, was not favored by the officers. Poor and unable to support himself, he left the city alone, intending to hang himself. Suddenly, an old man appeared gracefully, and asked, "Why are you thinking of suicide?" Yi told the truth. The old man laughed and said, "You have an extraordinary spirit; you can learn the Dao. I have a book to give you, which will provide for your needs for life." Yi followed him for several miles, passing a large stream, and entered a small house through reeds, the path very winding. After seven days, the art was completed, and the old man and the house disappeared. Yi then became moderately well-off.

    561

    其同辈群思咀嚼之，伊无难色，同登酒楼，五六人恣情大饮，计费七千二百文。众方愁其难偿，忽见一黑脸汉登楼拱立曰：“知伊五爷在此款客，主人遣奉酒金。”解腰缠出钱而去。数之，七千二百也，众大骇。

    　　与同步市中，见一人乘白马急驰而过。伊纵步追之，叱曰：“汝身上囊可急与我。”其人惶恐下马，怀中出一皮袋，形如半胀猪脬，授伊竟走。众不测何物，伊曰：“此中所贮小儿魂也。彼乘马者，乃过往游神，偷攫人魂无算。倘不遇我，又死一小儿矣。”俄入一胡同，有向西人家门内哭声嗷嗷，伊取小囊向门隙张之，出浓烟一缕，射此家门中，随闻其家人云：“儿苏矣。”转涕为笑。众由是神之。

    Explanation

    561

    His peers thoughtfully considered it, but he showed no difficulty. Together, they went to a wine shop, where five or six people drank heartily, and the total cost was seven hundred and twenty coins. Everyone was worried about how to pay, when suddenly a dark-faced man climbed up to the wine shop and stood respectfully, saying, "Knowing that the fifth sir is entertaining guests here, the owner has sent me with the money for the wine." He untied the money tied around his waist and left with it. When they counted it, it was seven hundred and twenty coins, and everyone was greatly shocked.

    While walking in the market, they saw a man riding a white horse galloping past. He ran after him and shouted, "The contents of your bag can be given to me immediately." The man, in a panic, dismounted and took out a leather bag from his chest, which looked like a half-inflated pig bladder, and gave it to him and then ran away. Everyone couldn't guess what it was, and he said, "This bag contains the souls of children. That man riding the horse is a passing spirit, who has stolen countless souls. If I hadn't met him, another child would have died." Soon they entered a lane, and inside a house facing west, there was a loud crying sound. He took the small bag and opened it towards the gap in the door, and a thick wisp of smoke shot out into the house. Then they heard the family members say, "The child has revived." They turned from tears to laughter. Everyone was amazed by this.

    562

    适某贵公有女为邪所凭，闻伊名，厚礼招致。女在室已知伊来，形象惨沮。

    　　伊入室，女匿屋隅，提熨斗自卫。伊周视上下，出曰：“此器物之妖也，今夕为公除之。”漏三下，伊囊中出一小剑，锋芒如雪，被发跣足，仗之而入，众家人伺于院外。寻闻室中叱咤声，击扑声，与物腾掷声，诟詈喧闹声，良久寂然，但闻女叩首哀恳，不甚了了。伊呼灯甚急，众率仆妇秉烛入。伊指地上一物相示曰：“此即为祟者。”视之，一藤夹膝也。聚薪焚之，流血满地。

    　　○诸廷槐嘉定诸廷槐家有再醮仆妇李姓者，忽鬼扼其喉，口称：“是汝前夫。我病时，呼茶索药，汝多不睬，以至气忿而亡。冥王以我阳数未尽，受糟蹋死，与枉死一般，不肯收留。游魂飘荡，受尽饥寒。汝在此饱食暖衣，我心不服，故扼汝喉，使汝陪我忍饥。”廷槐知为鬼所凭，上前手批其颊，鬼呼痛逃去。廷槐视其掌，黑如锅煤。

    Explanation

    562

    A certain nobleman had a daughter who was possessed by evil spirits. Hearing of someone's reputation, he invited them with generous gifts. The daughter, already in the room, knew the person was coming and looked sad and depressed. When the visitor entered the room, the daughter hid in a corner of the house and held a flatiron for self-defense. The visitor looked around thoroughly and then said, "This is a spirit of an object. I will eliminate it tonight for you." At the third watch, the visitor took out a small sword from their bag, with a sharp edge like snow, and entered the room with loose hair and bare feet, carrying the sword. The family members waited outside the courtyard. Soon, they heard shouting inside the room, hitting and beating sounds, and sounds of things being thrown around, along with loud cursing and noise. After a long time, it became quiet, but only heard the daughter kneeling and pleading, though not very clearly. The visitor called for a light very urgently, and the family and servants carried candles and entered. The visitor pointed to an object on the ground and said, "This is the one causing the possession." They looked at it, and it was a wooden knee brace. They gathered wood and burned it, and blood flowed all over the ground.

    ○ In Jiading, there was a man named Zhu Tinghuai. His household had a remarried female servant named Li, who suddenly had a ghost choke her throat. The ghost said, "You are my former husband. When I was sick, I called for tea and medicine, but you paid little attention, leading to my anger and death. The Underworld King, seeing that my earthly lifespan was not yet complete, but I died from abuse, treated it the same as a wrongful death and would not accept me. My soul wanders, enduring hunger and cold. You here are well-fed and warm, and I am不服, so I choke your throat to make you suffer with me." Zhu Tinghuai knew she was possessed by a ghost, so he went forward and slapped her cheek. The ghost cried out in pain and fled. Zhu Tinghuai looked at his hand, which was black like soot from a pot.

    563

    少顷，鬼又作闹，廷槐再打，妇无惧色，手亦不黑矣。骂曰：“你家主人初次打我，出我不意，故被他打痛。今我已躲入汝背脊骨窍中，虽用掌心雷打我，亦不怕也。”于是众家人代为请曰：“汝妻不过妇道有亏，事汝不周，并非有心杀汝，无大仇可报。况汝所生子女，赖渠改嫁后夫替你抚养，也算有良心。汝何不略放松手，俾其少进饮食。”鬼唯唯。妇觉咽喉一清，登时吃饭三碗。众人知其可动，乃曰：“主人替你超度何如？”鬼又唯唯。遂设醮延僧，诵《往生咒》。

    　　鬼去而复至曰：“和尚不付度牒，我仍不能托生也。”乃速焚之，鬼竟去而妇安矣。

    　　当作闹时，最畏主人之少子，曰：“此小相公头有红光，将来必贵，我不愿见之。”或问：“可是诸府祖宗功德修来乎？”曰：“非也，是他家阴宅风水所荫。”问：“何由知？”曰：“我与鬼朋友数人常在坟间乞人祭扫之余，烛不敢上诸府坟，因陇上有热气一条，如火冲出故也。”

    Explanation

    563

    In a short while, the ghost caused trouble again, and Tinghuai beat it once more. The woman showed no fear and her hands were no longer black. She scolded, saying, "Your master beat me for the first time, catching me off guard, so I was beaten painfully. Now I have hidden myself in the hollow of your spine, even if he uses the palm heart thunder to beat me, I am not afraid." The household servants then spoke on her behalf, saying, "Your wife merely lacks the proper conduct of a woman, serving you inadequately, and did not intentionally kill you; there is no great grudge to be avenged. Moreover, your children, who relied on her remarriage to a new husband to raise them, can also be said to have a conscience. Why don't you slightly relax your hand and allow her to have some food and drink?" The ghost agreed. The woman felt her throat clear and immediately ate three bowls of rice. Everyone knew she could be moved and said, "What about your master helping you cross over to the other world?" The ghost agreed again. They then held a ritual and invited monks to recite the Rebirth Mantra.

    The ghost left but returned, saying, "The monk did not give me a certificate of ordination, so I still cannot reincarnate." They quickly burned it, and the ghost finally left, and the woman was safe.

    When causing trouble, the ghost most feared the master's youngest son, saying, "This young master has a red light on his head, and he will surely be noble in the future; I do not wish to see him." Someone asked, "Is it because of the merits and virtues cultivated by the ancestors of all the estates?" The ghost replied, "No, it is because of the geomancy of his ancestral tomb that benefits him." Someone asked, "How do you know?" The ghost said, "I and several ghost friends often beg for offerings after the people have finished sweeping the graves. We dare not light candles on the ancestral tombs of all the estates because there is a streak of heat on the mounds, like fire bursting out."

    564

    ○王都司山东王某，作济宁都司。忽一日，梦南门外关帝庙周仓来曰：“汝肯修帝庙，可获五千金。”王不信。次夜，又梦关平将军来曰：“我家周仓最诚实，非诳人者，所许五千金，现在帝君香案脚下。汝须黑夜秉烛来，五千金可得。”王喜且惊，心疑香案下地有藏金，分应我得者，乃率其子持皮口袋往，以便装载。

    　　及至庙中，天已黎明，见香案下睡一狐，黑而毛，两目金光闪闪。王悟曰：“得毋关神命我驱除此妖耶？”即与其子持绳索捆缚之，装放口袋中，负之归家。

    　　口袋中作人语曰：“我狐仙也，昨日偶醉，呕唾圣帝庙中，触怒神明，故托梦于君，教来收拾我。我原有罪，但念我修炼千年，此罪尚小，君不如放我出袋，彼此有益。”王戏问：“何以见谢？”曰：“以五千金为寿。”王心记周仓、关平两将军之言验矣，即释放之。

    Explanation

    564

    The Warden of Jinan, Wang from Shandong, served as the Warden of the Jinan Commandery. One day, he dreamed that Zhou Cang from the Guandi Temple outside the southern gate came to him and said, "If you are willing to rebuild the temple, you will receive five thousand taels of gold." Wang did not believe him. The next night, he dreamed again that General Guan Ping came to him and said, "My companion Zhou Cang is the most honest person, and he does not deceive others. The five thousand taels of gold he promised are now at the foot of the altar of the Lord Guan. You must come at night with a candle, and you will receive the five thousand taels of gold." Wang was both pleased and surprised, and he suspected that there might be hidden treasure under the altar, which should belong to him. So, he took his son with him and went to the temple with a leather bag to carry the treasure.

    When they arrived at the temple, it was already dawn. They saw a black fox sleeping under the altar, its eyes shining with golden light. Wang realized, "Is it not the Lord Guan who has commanded me to drive away this demon?" He immediately tied the fox with ropes, put it in the leather bag, and carried it back home.

    Inside the bag, a human voice spoke, "I am a fox spirit. Yesterday, I was drunk and vomited in the Guandi Temple, angering the gods. Therefore, I had a dream sent to you, instructing you to come and deal with me. I have committed a sin, but considering that I have practiced for a thousand years, this sin is small. You might as well release me from the bag, and it will be beneficial for both of us." Wang jokingly asked, "How will you thank me?" The fox replied, "With five thousand taels of gold as a gift." Wang remembered that the words of the two generals, Zhou Cang and Guan Ping, had come true, so he released the fox.

    565

    顷刻，变成一白须翁，唐巾飘带，言词温雅，蔼然可亲。王乃置酒设席，与谈过去未来事，且问：“都司穷官，如何能得五千金？”狐曰：“济宁富户甚多，俱非行仁义者，我择其尤不肖者，竟往彼家抛砖打瓦，使他头疼发热，心惊胆战。

    　　自然彼必寻求符，延请道士。君往说‘我能驱邪’，但书花押一个，向空焚之，我即心照而去，又闹别家。如此一月，则君之五千得矣。但君官爵止于都司，财量亦止五千金。过此以往，不必妄求。吾报君后，亦从此逝矣。“

    　　未几，济宁城内外疫疠大作，鸡犬不宁，但王都司一到，便即安宁，遂得五千金。舍二百金修圣庙，祭奠周关两将军。乞病归里，至今小康。

    Explanation

    565

    In a moment, the figure transformed into an elderly man with a long white beard, dressed in a Tang-style headband and flowing ribbons, speaking in gentle and refined words, affable and approachable. The official then prepared wine and set up a feast, engaging in conversation about past and future events. He asked, "How can a low-ranking official like me acquire five thousand gold coins?" The fox replied, "There are many wealthy households in Jining, but none of them practice benevolence and righteousness. I will choose the most unscrupulous among them, go to their homes, and throw bricks and tiles, causing them to suffer headaches, fevers, and heart palpitations.

    Naturally, they will seek talismans and invite Taoist priests. You go and say, 'I can exorcise evil spirits,' just write a stylized signature, burn it toward the sky, and I will leave with mutual understanding, moving on to another household. In this way, for a month, you will acquire your five thousand gold coins. However, your official rank will not exceed that of a military officer, and your wealth will not surpass five thousand gold coins. Beyond that, there is no need to seek further. After I fulfill my promise to you, I will also depart from this world."

    Soon after, an epidemic broke out in Jining, causing unrest among people and animals. But as soon as the official arrived, the situation calmed, and he acquired five thousand gold coins. He set aside two hundred gold coins to repair the Temple of the Sage and to honor the generals Zhou and Guan. He then requested leave due to illness and returned home, where he has since enjoyed modest prosperity.

    566

    ●卷十六○杭大宗为寄灵童子万近蓬奉斗甚严，每秋七月，为盂兰之会，与施柳南刺史同设道场。施能见鬼，凡来受祭者，俱能指为何人，且与言语。方立坛时，先书列死者姓名，向坛焚化。

    　　万，故杭大宗先生弟子，忘书先生名。施见是夕诸公俱集，有人短白须，披丧纱袍，不冠而至，骂曰：“近蓬我弟子，今日设会，独不请我何也？”施素不识杭，不觉目瞪。旁一人曰：“此杭大宗先生也。”施向前揖问：“先生何来？”

    　　曰：“我前生是法华会上点香者，名寄灵童子，因侍香时见烧香女美，偶动一念，谪生人间。在人间心直口快，有善无恶，原可仍归原位。惟以我好讥贬人，党同伐异，又贪财，为观音所薄，不许即归原位。”因自指其手与口曰：“此二物累我。”问：“先生在阴间乐乎？”曰：“我在此无甚苦乐，颇散荡，游行自如。”

    Explanation

    566

    Chapter Sixteen: Hang Dazong as the Reincarnated Spirit Child

    Wan Jinping, a former disciple of Master Hang Dazong, did not write down Master Hang's name. Liu Nan, the prefect, saw many people gather that night, including a man with short white hair, wearing a mourning robe, and arriving without a hat. The man cursed, saying, "Jinping is my disciple. Today, you have set up this gathering, but why did you not invite me?" Liu Nan did not recognize Master Hang and was taken aback. Another person said, "This is Master Hang Dazong." Liu Nan bowed and asked, "Where are you from, Master?"

    The man replied, "In my previous life, I was a participant in the Lotus Sutra ceremony, known as the Spirit Child. I was responsible for lighting incense and saw a beautiful woman burning incense, which momentarily stirred my thoughts. As a result, I was exiled to be born in the human world. In my human life, I was straightforward and spoke my mind, doing good deeds without evil. Originally, I could have returned to my original position. However, because I liked to criticize and belittle others, favored my own party and attacked others, and was greedy for wealth, I was disapproved by Guanyin and not allowed to return to my original position. These two things—my hands and mouth—are a burden to me." He pointed to his hands and mouth. "Where am I now? Not much suffering or happiness, quite free and unrestrained, moving around freely."

    567

    问：“先生何不仍投人身？”杭以手作拍势，笑曰：“我七十七年人身，倏忽过去，回头想来，有何趣味？”曰：“先生何不仍求观音收留？”曰：“我坠落亦因小过，容易超度。可告知近蓬，替我念《秽迹金刚咒》二万遍，便可归原位。”

    　　问：“陈星斋先生何以不来？”曰：“我不及彼，彼已仍归桂宫矣。”语毕，上座大啖，笑曰：“施柳南一日不出仕，我辈田允兄大有吃处。”“田允”兄者，俗言鬼字也。

    　　○西江水怪徐汉甫在江西见有咒取鱼鳖者。日至水滨，禹步持咒，波即腾沸，鱼鳖阵至，任择取以归。其法不可多取，约日需若干，仅给其值而已。

    Explanation

    567

    Final Translation

    "Why didn't you reincarnate as a human again?" Hang waved his hand in a gesture of dismissal, smiling, "Seventy-seven years as a human, it passed away quickly. Looking back, what fun or enjoyment was there?" "Why don't you ask Guanyin to take you in again?" "I fell due to minor faults, and it's easy to be liberated. Inform Jinqing to recite the Dharmapala Vajra Sutra 20,000 times for me, then I can return to my original position."

    "Why didn't Mr. Chen Xingzhai come?" "I am not as good as him; he has already returned to the Palace of Osage Orange." After speaking, he sat down and indulged in food, laughing, "Shi Liunan refused to serve for a day, and we, the 'ghosts,' would have plenty to eat." "Ghost" is a colloquial term for "ghost."

    ○ In the West River, Xu Hanfu saw someone who could use spells to catch fish and turtles. Each day, they would go to the water's edge, perform the ritual steps, and hold the spell. The waves would immediately churn, and fish and turtles would appear in an array. They could choose any they wanted to take. The method does not allow for too many to be taken; it is only given according to the amount needed for the day.

    568

    一日，偶至大泽，方作法，忽水面涌一物，大如猕猴，金眼玉爪，露牙口外，势欲相攫。其人急以蒙首走。物奔来，跃上肩，抓其额，人即仆地，流血晕绝。

    　　众咸奔救。物见众至，作声如鸦鸣，跃高文许遁去。人不敢捕，伤者亦苏。土人云：“此水怪也，以鱼鳖为子孙。吾食其子孙，故来复仇耳。其爪利，遇物破脑，非蒙首而得众力，则毙其爪下矣。”

    　　○仲能唐再适先生观察川西时，有火夫陈某，粗悍嗜饮。一夕方醉卧，觉有物据其腹，视之，乃一老翁，髯发皆白，貌亦奇古，朦胧间不甚了了。陈以同伴戏己，不甚惊怖。时初秋，适覆单衾，因举以裹之，且挟以卧。晓曳衾，内有一白鼠，长三尺馀，已压毙矣。始悟据腹老人即此怪。按此即《玉策记》所云“仲能”，善相卜者，能生得之，可以预知休咎。

    Explanation

    568

    One day, he happened to arrive at a large marsh, just about to perform a ritual, when suddenly something emerged from the water. The creature was as large as a macaque monkey, with golden eyes and jade-like claws, showing its teeth outside its mouth, seeming to want to grab or attack. The person quickly covered their head with a cloth and ran. The creature rushed forward and leaped onto the person's shoulder, grasping its forehead, and the person fell to the ground, bleeding and unconscious.

    Everyone rushed to help. The creature saw the crowd and made a sound like a crow, then jumped high and disappeared. The person was too afraid to catch it, and the injured person also regained consciousness. The locals said, "This is a water monster, which considers fish and turtles as its descendants. I eat its descendants, so I came to take revenge. Its claws are sharp and deadly; if it encounters something, it breaks its brain. Without covering the head and the help of others, one would be killed by its claws."

    During the time when Mr. Tang Zai Shi, the observer of Sichuan, was in the western part of Sichuan, there was a fireman named T, who was rough, fierce, and fond of drinking. One night, just as he was drunk and sleeping, he felt something sitting on his stomach. When he looked, it was an old man with a beard and white hair, and an odd, ancient appearance. In the haze, it was not very clear, and T thought his companions were playing a prank on him, not very frightened. It was early autumn, and he had just covered himself with a single blanket. He picked it up and wrapped it in the blanket, then lay down with it. In the morning, he pulled the blanket, and inside was a white rat, more than three feet long, which had already been crushed and killed. He finally realized that the old man sitting on his stomach was this monster. According to the Yuce Ji, this is what is called 'Zhong Neng,' a skilled fortune-teller who, if captured alive, can predict good or bad omens.

    569

    ○雀报恩周之庠好放生，尤爱雀，居恒置黍谷于帘下饲之。中年丧明，饲雀如故。忽病气绝，惟心头温，家人守之四昼夜。苏云：初出门，独行旷野，日色昏暗，寂不逢人。心惧，疾弛数十里，见城外寥寥无烟火。俄有老人杖策来，视之，乃亡父也，跪而哀泣。父曰：“孰唤汝来？”答曰：“迷路至此。”父曰：“无伤。”

    　　导之入城。至一衙署前，又有老人纶巾道服自内出，乃亡祖也。相见大惊，责其父曰：“尔亦糊涂，何导儿至此！”叱父退，手挽之庠行。有二隶卒貌丑恶，大呼曰：“既来此，安得便去？”与其祖相争夺。忽雀亿万自西来，啄二隶，隶骇走。祖父翼之出，群雀随之，争以翅覆之庠。约行数十里，祖以杖击其背曰：“到家矣。”遂如梦觉，双目复明。至今无恙。

    Explanation

    569

    A Story of Gratitude from the Birds

    Zhou Zhiyang was a man who loved to release animals from captivity, and he had a particular fondness for birds. He would habitually place millet and grain under his curtains to feed them. In his middle years, he lost his eyesight, but he continued to feed the birds as before. One day, he suddenly fell ill and seemed to die, but his heart remained warm. His family watched over him for four days and nights. When he revived, he recounted: "At first, I went out the door and found myself alone in the wilderness. The sun was dim, and there was no one to be seen. Frightened, I hurried for dozens of miles and saw that the city outside was desolate, with no signs of life. Suddenly, an old man approached with a cane. When I looked at him, I realized it was my deceased father. I knelt before him and wept. My father asked, 'Who called you here?' I replied, 'I lost my way here.' My father said, 'There is no harm.'"

    He led me into the city. We came to the front of a government office, and another old man emerged from inside, wearing a scholar's cap and a robe. It was my deceased grandfather. We were startled to see each other, and my grandfather scolded my father, saying, 'You are also foolish, why did you bring my son here?' He then ordered my father to step back and took my hand, leading me along. There were two low-ranking officials nearby, both ugly in appearance, who shouted, 'Since you are here, how can you leave so easily?' They began to argue with my grandfather. Suddenly, countless birds flew in from the west, pecking at the two officials, who were frightened and ran away. My grandfather and father then led me out, and the birds followed, competing to cover me with their wings. We walked for about dozens of miles, and my grandfather struck my back with his cane, saying, 'We have arrived home.' I then woke up as if from a dream, and my eyesight returned. Since then, I have been well.

    570

    ○全姑荡山茶肆全姑，生而洁白婀娜，年十九。其邻陈生美少年，私与通，为匪人所捉。陈故富家，以百金贿匪。县役知之，思分其赃，相与牵扭到县。县令某自负理学名，将陈决杖四十。女哀号涕泣，伏陈生臀上愿代。令以为无耻，愈怒，将女亦决杖四十。两隶拉女下，私相怜，以为此女通体娇柔如无骨者，又受陈生金，故杖轻扑地而已。令怒未息，剪其发，脱其弓鞋，置案上传观之，以为合邑戒。且贮库焉，将女发官卖。

    　　案结矣，陈思女不已，贿他人买之，而己仍娶之。未一月，县役纷来索贿，道路喧嚷。令访闻大怒，重擒二人至案。女知不免，私以败絮草纸置裤中护其臀。

    Explanation

    570

    Full Lady's Tea House on Quan Mountain, Quan Gu, was born white, delicate, and graceful, and was nineteen years old. Her neighbor, Chen Sheng, a handsome young man, privately had relations with her and was caught by bandits. Chen, being a wealthy family, bribed the bandits with a hundred gold pieces. The county officials knew about it and thought to share in the bribe, so they together dragged him to the county office. The county magistrate, who prided himself on his reputation as a Confucian scholar, ordered Chen to be beaten with forty strokes of the stick. The woman wailed and wept, fell on Chen's buttocks, and offered to take his place. The magistrate thought it was shameful, became even angrier, and ordered the woman to be beaten with forty strokes of the stick as well. The two servants pulled the woman down, secretly pitied her, thinking that this woman was completely delicate and soft, as if she had no bones, and had received money from Chen, so the beating was light and just fell to the ground. The magistrate's anger was not yet spent, so he cut her hair, removed her arch shoes, placed them on the table for public viewing, to serve as a warning to the whole county, and stored them in the treasury, selling the woman publicly.

    The case was concluded, but Chen could not stop thinking about the woman, so he bribed someone else to buy her, and still married her. Not even a month later, the county officials came in a rush to demand bribes, and the streets were noisy. The magistrate heard of it and became greatly angry, severely captured the two of them and brought them to court. The woman knew she could not escape, so she secretly placed old rags and paper in her trousers to protect her buttocks.

    571

    令望见曰：“是下身累累者，何物耶？”乃下堂扯去裤中物，亲自监临，裸而杖之。陈生抵拦，掌嘴数百后，乃再决满杖。归家月余死，女卖为某公子妾。

    　　有刘孝廉者，侠士也，直入署责令曰：“我昨到县，闻公呼大杖，以为治强盗积贼，故至阶下观之。不料一美女剥紫绫裤受杖，两臀隆然，如一团白雪，日炙之犹虑其消，而君以满杖加之，一板下，便成烂桃子色。所犯风流小过，何必如是？”令曰：“全姑美，不加杖，人道我好色；陈某富，不加杖，人道我得钱。”

    　　刘曰：“为父母官，以他人皮肉，博自己声名，可乎？行当有报矣！”奋衣出，与令绝交。

    　　未十年，令迁守松江，坐公馆，方午餐，其仆见一少年从窗外入，以手拍其背者三，遂呼背痛不食。已而背肿尺许，中有界沟，如两臀然。召医视之，医曰：“不救矣，成烂桃子色矣。”令闻，心恶之，未十日卒。

    Explanation

    571

    The magistrate saw and said, "This person with numerous lumps on the lower body, what is it?" He then went down to the hall and pulled out the object in his pants, personally overseeing and whipping him/her naked. Chen Sheng resisted, and after slapping his/her face hundreds of times, he then decided to give a full beating. He died at home after a month, and the woman was sold as a concubine to a certain young master.

    There was a man named Liu, a scholar-official and a chivalrous man, who directly entered the office and ordered, saying, "I arrived at the county yesterday and heard that you were calling for a severe beating, thinking it was for dealing with robbers and habitual thieves, so I came down to observe. Not expecting a beautiful woman to be stripped of her purple silk pants and receive a whipping, her two buttocks were prominent, like a lump of white snow, even the sun's heat would worry about melting it, and you gave a full whipping, one strike, and it turned into the color of rotten peaches. The minor offense of being flirtatious, why treat it so severely?" The magistrate said, "If I do not whip the beautiful woman, people will say I am fond of beauty; if I do not whip the wealthy Chen, people will say I am greedy for money."

    Liu said, "As a magistrate, using others' skin and flesh to gain one's own reputation, is this acceptable? There will be retribution!" He left in a huff and severed ties with the magistrate.

    Not even ten years later, the magistrate was transferred to govern Songjiang, sitting in the public hall, just having lunch. His servant saw a young man enter from the window, who slapped his back three times. He then called out that his back hurt and refused to eat. Soon after, his back swelled by a foot or so, with a boundary line, like two buttocks. He summoned a doctor to examine him, who said, "It cannot be saved, it has turned into the color of rotten peaches." The magistrate heard and felt disgust, and he died within ten days.

    572

    ○奇勇国初有二巴图鲁：一溺地地陷一尺，能自抓其发拔起身在空中高尺许，两足离地，移时不下。一在关外，被敌劫营，黑暗中已为敌断其首矣，刀过处，急以右手捺住头，左手挥刀，犹杀数十人而后死。

    　　○红毛国人吐妓红毛国多妓。嫖客置酒召妓，剥其下衣，环聚而吐口沫于其阴，不与交媾也。

    　　吐毕放赏，号“众兜钱”。

    　　○西贾认父钱塘铨部主事吴名一骐者，初举孝廉，入都会试，僦居旅次。有西贾王某来，云其父临终言，往生浙地某处为吴氏子。其终年即铨部生年也。又云昨晚其母又复示梦云：“汝父已至都中，现寓某处，汝何不往？”以故到此访问，乞一睹颜色。铨部因事属怪异，不肯出见。王贾痛哭遥拜而去。王贾甚富，并无所希冀而来者，以故人笑吴公之迂。吴作吏部主事数年死，死年二十八。

    Explanation

    572

    At the beginning of the Qing dynasty, there were two Ba Tulu (Mongolian for 'valiant warrior'). One sank into the ground, the earth sinking a foot, but he could grab his own hair and pull himself up into the air, about a foot high, with his feet off the ground, remaining there for some time. Another was outside the pass, attacked by the enemy in the darkness, and had his head cut off. As the blade passed, he quickly pressed his head with his right hand and slashed with his left, killing dozens of people before dying.

    The Dutch people have many prostitutes. Patrons would set up a feast and summon prostitutes, strip them, and gather around to spit on their genitals, without engaging in sexual intercourse. After spitting, they would give rewards, calling it 'zhongdou money'.

    A Western merchant recognized his father. The protagonist, Wu Yiqi, was initially recommended as a filial and honest man, entered the capital to take the imperial examination, and rented a place to stay. A Western merchant named Wang came, saying that his father, on his deathbed, said he had been reborn in a certain place in Zhejiang as the son of the Wu family. His father's age at death was the same as the year the official was born. He also said that last night his mother appeared in a dream, saying, 'Your father has already come to the capital, currently residing at a certain place, why don't you go?' Hence, he came here to visit and request to see him. The official, finding the matter strange, did not come out to see him. The merchant cried and bowed from afar before leaving. The merchant was very wealthy and came with no expectations. Therefore, people laughed at the official for being so naive. Wu served as a departmental official for several years and died at the age of twenty-eight.

    573

    ○徐步蟾宫扬州吴竹屏臬使，丁卯秋闱在金陵扶乩问：“中否？”乩批“徐步蟾宫”四字。吴大喜，以为馆选之征。乃榜发，不中。是年解元，乃徐步蟾也。

    　　○歪嘴先生湖州潘淑聘妻未娶，以瘵疾亡。临终请岳翁李某来，要其未嫁之女守志，翁许之。潘卒后，翁忘前言，女竟改适。将婚之夕，鬼附女身作祟。有教读张先生者闻之，意不能平，竟上女楼，引古礼折之，以为女虽已嫁，而未庙见，尚归葬于女氏之党。况未嫁之女，有何守志之说。鬼不能答，但走至张前张口呵之，一条冷气如冰，臭不可耐。从此，女病愈，而张嘴歪矣。李德之，延请在家。合村呼“歪嘴先生”。

    Explanation

    573

    A Gentleman Named "Slow Steps to the Moon Palace"

    In the autumn of Ding Mao, Wu Zhuping, the Deputy Governor of Yangzhou, performed a divination in Jinling (Nanjing) to ask, "Will I pass?" The divination result was the four characters "徐步蟾宫" (slow steps to the moon palace). Wu was greatly pleased, believing it to be an omen of being selected for a prestigious position. However, when the results were announced, he did not pass. That year, the top scorer (解元) was indeed named Xu Buxian.

    The Man with the Crooked Mouth

    In Huzhou, Pan Shupin, who was betrothed but had not yet married, died from tuberculosis. On her deathbed, she called her father-in-law, Mr. Li, and asked him to make her unmarried daughter observe her vows of chastity. Mr. Li agreed. After Pan's death, Mr. Li forgot his promise, and the daughter eventually remarried. On the eve of her wedding, a ghost possessed the daughter and caused mischief. Mr. Zhang, a teacher, heard about this and felt it was unjust. He went up to the girl's room and used ancient rituals to argue that since the girl had already been married but had not yet performed the temple visit, she should still be buried in her family's ancestral grounds. Moreover, for an unmarried girl, what could be said about observing chastity? The ghost could not answer and instead walked up to Zhang, opened its mouth, and exhaled a cold, unbearable stench. From that day on, the girl recovered from her illness, but Zhang's mouth became crooked. Mr. Li felt remorseful and invited Zhang to stay at his home. The entire village called him "Mr. Crooked Mouth."

    574

    ○鬼衣有补褂痕常州蒋某，在甘肃作县丞。乾隆四十五年，甘肃回回作乱，蒋为所害，三年音耗断矣。其侄某，开参店于东城。忽一日午后，蒋竟直入，布裹其头，所穿衣有钉补褂旧痕，告其侄曰：“我于某月日为乱兵所害，尸在居延城下，汝可遣人至其处棺殓载归。”指其仆曰：“此小儿亦是劫数中人，我现在阴间雇用之，每年给工食银三两。”其侄大惊，唯唯听命。鬼命小僮取火吃烟，旋即不见。侄即遣人载其棺归，启视之：头骨斫作数块，身着红青缎褂，隐隐有补褂一方痕迹。

    　　○孙方伯孙涵中方伯为部郎时，居京师之樱桃斜街，房宇甚洁。忽有臭气一道，从窗外达于中庭。嗅而迹之，乃从后苑井中出。夜三鼓，众人睡尽，有连呼其老仆姓名者。听之，隐隐然亦出自井中。孙公怒而填之，怪亦竟绝。

    Explanation

    574

    Translation of the Source Text

    Story 1: The Ghost with Patched Coat Marks

    A man from Changzhou named Jiang served as a county magistrate in Gansu. In the 45th year of the Qianlong era, the Hui people in Gansu rebelled, and Jiang was killed. For three years, no news of him reached his family. His nephew, who ran a ginseng shop in the eastern city, was suddenly visited one afternoon by Jiang. He entered directly, wrapped his head in cloth, and wore a coat with visible patches. He told his nephew, "I was killed by the rebels on a certain date, and my body lies under the wall of Juyan City. You should send someone to retrieve my body and bring it back." Pointing to his servant, he said, "This boy is also destined to meet his end in this turmoil. I have hired him in the underworld, and I pay him three taels of silver annually for his work." The nephew was greatly shocked but nodded in agreement and obeyed. The ghost then had a young servant bring fire and smoke, and he disappeared immediately. The nephew sent someone to retrieve the body, which they found under the wall of Juyan City. Upon opening and looking inside the coffin, they saw that the headbone was cut into several pieces, and the body was wearing a red and blue silk coat, faintly showing a patch on the coat.

    Story 2: The Ghost of the Old Servant

    Mr. Sun, a high-ranking official, was a low-ranking official in the imperial bureaucracy when he lived on Cherry Slope Street in Beijing, where his house was very clean. One day, there was a foul odor that came from outside the window and reached the courtyard. They followed the smell and found it coming from the well in the rear garden. At the third watch of the night, when everyone was asleep, there was a voice calling out the name of the old servant. The voice was faintly heard coming from the well. Mr. Sun was angry and had the well filled in, and the ghostly occurrences ceased.

    This translation aims to capture the essence and details of the original text while maintaining a natural and coherent flow in English.

    575

    ○卖冬瓜人杭州草桥门外有卖冬瓜人某，能在头顶上出元神。每闭目坐床上，而出神在外酬应。一日，出神买鲞数片，托邻人带归交其妻。妻接之，笑曰：“汝又作狡狯耶！”将鲞挞其头。少顷，卖瓜者神归，以顶为鲞所污，徨床侧，神不能入，大哭去，尸亦渐僵。

    　　○柳如是为厉苏州昭文县署，为前明钱尚书故宅。东厢三间，因柳如是缢死此处，历任封闭不开。

    　　乾隆庚子，直隶王公某莅任，家口多，内屋少，开此房居妾某氏，二婢作伴；又居一妾于西厢，老妪作伴。未三鼓，闻西厢老妪喊救命声，王公奔往，妾已不在床上。寻至床后，其人眼伤额碎，赤身流血，觳觫而立，云：“我卧不吹灯，方就枕，便一陈阴风吹开帐幔，遍体作噤。有梳高髻披大红袄者揭帐招我，随挽我发，强我起。我大惧，急逃至帐后，眼目为衣架触伤。老驱闻我喊声，随即奔至，鬼才放我，走窗外去。”合署大骇，虑东厢之妾新娶胆小，亦不往告。

    Explanation

    575

    There was a man selling winter melons outside Caobridge Gate in Hangzhou, who had the ability to project his spirit from his head. He would often close his eyes and sit on his bed, while his spirit would go out to socialize. One day, his spirit went out to buy several slices of dried fish, entrusting a neighbor to bring them back to his wife. When the wife received the dried fish, she laughed and said, "Are you being tricky again?" She then struck the dried fish against his head. Soon after, the man's spirit returned, but his head was stained by the dried fish. He wandered around the bed, unable to return his spirit, and cried out loudly before departing, with his body gradually stiffening.

    In Zhaowen County Office in Suzhou, the place was haunted by the ghost of Liu Rushi, the former residence of a high-ranking official from the Ming dynasty. The eastern wing, which was three rooms, was sealed off because Liu Rushi had hanged herself there, and previous officials had closed it due to the haunting.

    In the year 1780 during the reign of Emperor Qianlong, a high-ranking official from the province of Zhili, Wang Gong, took up his post. He had a large family but few inner rooms, so he opened this room for his concubine, who was accompanied by two maids. Another concubine lived in the western wing, accompanied by an elderly woman. Before the third drumbeat (around 11:00 PM), they heard the elderly woman in the western wing scream for help. Wang Gong rushed over, but the concubine was no longer on the bed. When he searched behind the bed, he found the woman with her eyes and forehead injured, naked and bleeding, standing there trembling in fear. She said, "I was lying down without blowing out the lamp, and just as I was about to go to sleep, a cold wind opened the bed curtains, and I felt a chill all over my body. A woman with a high bun and a red robe pulled the curtains and called me. She then grabbed my hair and forced me to get up. I was very frightened and quickly ran behind the bed, where my eyes and face were hit by a clothes rack. The old woman heard my screams and rushed over, and the ghost only let me go after she arrived, escaping through the window." The entire office was greatly frightened and, fearing that the new concubine in the eastern wing, who was timid, might be affected, they did not inform her.

    576

    次日至午，东厢竟不开门。启入，则一姬二婢俱用一条长带相连缢死矣。于是王公仍命封锁此房，后无他异。

    　　或谓：柳氏为尚书殉节，死于正命，不应为厉。按《金史。蒲察琦传》：琦为御史，将死崔立之难，到家别母。母方昼寝，忽惊而醒。琦问：“阿母何为？”

    　　母曰：“适梦三人潜伏梁间，故惊醒。”琦跪曰：“梁上人乃鬼也。儿欲殉节，意在悬梁，故彼鬼在上相候。母所见者，即是也。”旋即缢死。可见忠义之鬼用引路替代，亦所不免。

    　　○捧头司马如皋高公岩，为陕西高陵令，其友某往探之。去城十里许，日已薄暮，恐不能达，见道旁废寺：正室封扃；西偏屋二楹，内有小门通正室，门亦封扃。某以屋尚整洁，遂借宿焉。沽酒少饮，解衣就寝。其仆出与守寺道人同宿东边之耳房。

    Explanation

    576

    The next day until noon, the eastern room remained closed. When they entered, they found that one concubine and two maidservants had all hanged themselves together with a single long sash. Thus, the prince ordered the room to be sealed again, and there were no further strange occurrences.

    Some say: Lady Liu, who died for her husband the Shangshu, died by a righteous death, and should not become a ghost. According to the History of the Jin Dynasty in the biography of Pu Cha Qi: Qi, as a censor, was about to die in the disaster involving Cui Li, and went home to bid farewell to his mother. His mother was napping during the day and suddenly woke up startled. Qi asked, "Why, Mother?" His mother said, "I just had a dream where three people were hiding in the rafters, so I woke up startled." Qi knelt and said, "The people on the beams are ghosts. I intend to die for my country, and my plan is to hang myself, so those ghosts are waiting up there. What you saw is exactly that." He immediately hanged himself. It can be seen that even ghosts of loyal and righteous people use substitutes to guide, which is unavoidable.

    ○ Holding his head,司马如皋高公岩, who was the magistrate of Gaoling in Shaanxi, had a friend visit him. About ten miles from the city, it was already late afternoon, and he feared he would not arrive in time, so he saw an abandoned temple by the side of the road. The main hall was sealed; the two rooms on the west side had a small door connecting to the main hall, and that door was also sealed. He thought the room was still tidy, so he decided to stay there. He bought some wine and drank a little, then undressed and went to bed. His servant went out and stayed in the eastern annex with the temple caretaker.

    577

    时当既望，月明如昼，某久不成寐。忽闻正室履声橐橐，小门砉然顿开，见有补褂朝珠而无头者就窗下坐，作玩月状。某方惊，其人转身内向，若有见于某者，旋即走还正室中。某急起开门遁，而门外锁已为其仆倒扣去。某大呼，喑不能声，其仆弗应。某无措，遂夺窗出。窗外有墙缭之，又不克越，近窗高树一株，乃缘之而上。俯视窗下，则其人已捧头而出，仍就前坐，以头置膝，徐伸两指拭其眉目，还以手捧之安置顶上，双眸炯炯，寒光射人。是时，某已魂飞，不复省人事矣。

    　　次晨仆入，不见主人，遍寻之，得于树上。急拨其腕，交抱树柯，坚不可解。

    　　久之始苏，犹谓鬼之来攫己也。问之道人，云：“二十年前，宁夏用兵，有楚人为同知者，解粮误期，为大帅所戮。柩行至此，资斧告绝，遂寄寺中。今或思归，见形于客乎！”某白高，高因捐俸为赉柩资，并寓书于楚，令其子领归。

    Explanation

    577

    At the time of the full moon, the moonlight was as bright as daylight, and I had been unable to sleep for a long time. Suddenly, I heard the sound of footsteps from the main room,橐橐作响, and the small door opened with a sharp sound. I saw a headless figure in a traditional official robe and neck beads sitting by the window, pretending to admire the moon. I was startled, and the figure turned inward, as if seeing me. It then quickly walked back into the main room. I hastily got up, opened the door, and fled, but the lock on the door had already been removed by my servant. I cried out, but could not make a sound, and my servant did not respond. I was at a loss, so I forced my way out through the window. Outside the window, there was a wall surrounding it, and I could not cross it. There was a tall tree near the window, so I climbed it. Looking down at the window below, I saw that the figure had already pulled its head out, returned to the previous sitting position, placed its head on its knees, slowly extended two fingers to wipe its eyebrows and eyes, and then used its hand to lift it back up and place it on top of its head. Its eyes were bright and cold, glaring at people. At that time, I had already lost my soul, and I no longer knew what was happening.

    The next morning, my servant entered and did not see the master. He searched everywhere and found me on the tree. He quickly pulled my wrist, and I was tightly embraced by the tree branches, making it impossible to separate them.

    After a long time, I regained consciousness, still thinking that a ghost had come to snatch me. I asked a Taoist, who said, "Twenty years ago, during a military campaign in Ningxia, there was a person from Chu who was a deputy governor. He failed to deliver grain on time and was executed by the general. The coffin was brought here, and the funds ran out, so it was temporarily stored in a temple. Perhaps he is now thinking of returning home and has appeared to you!" I reported this to the high-ranking official, who then donated his salary to cover the expenses of the coffin and wrote a letter to Chu, instructing his son to bring it back.

    578

    ○驱鲎吴兴卞山有白鲎洞，每春夏间即见，状如匹练，起空中游漾无定。所过之下，蚕茧一空，故养蚕时尤忌之。性独畏锣鼓声。明太常卿韩绍曾命有司挟毒矢逐之，有《驱鲎文》载郡志，近年来作患尤甚。

    　　乾隆癸卯四月，有范姓者具控于城隍。是夜，梦有老人来曰：“汝所控已准，某夜当命玄衣真人逐鲎。但鲎鱼司露有功，被害者亦有数，彼以贫故，当示之罚。

    　　尔等备硫磺烟草在某山洞口相候可也。“

    　　范至期集数十人往。夜二鼓，月色微明，空中风作，见前山有大蝙蝠丈许飞至洞前，瞬息，诸小群集者不下数十。每一蝙蝠至，必有灯一点，如引导状。范悟曰：“是得非所谓玄衣真人乎！”即引火纵烧烟草。俄而洞中声起，如潮涌风发，有匹练飞出，蝙蝠围环若布陈然，彼此搏击良久，乡民亦群打锣鼓，放爆竹助之。约一时许，匹练飘散如絮，有青气一道向东北而去，蝙蝠亦散。

    Explanation

    578

    White Crab Drive in Wu County

    Bian Mountain in Wu County has a White Crab Cave. Every summer and autumn, white crabs appear, their appearance resembling a ribbon, floating aimlessly in the air. Wherever they pass, all the silkworm cocoons are empty, making them particularly dreaded during the silkworm-raising season. They are uniquely afraid of the sound of gongs and drums. During the Ming dynasty, the Grand Master of the Temple, Han Shao, once ordered officials to use poisoned arrows to drive them away. There is a Driving Away White Crab text recorded in the local gazetteer, and in recent years, the problem has become even more severe.

    In the fourth month of the year 1753 during the Qianlong reign of the Qing dynasty, a man surnamed Fan filed a complaint with the City God. That night, he dreamed of an old man who said, "Your complaint has been approved. On a certain night, I will order the Black-Clothed Immortal to drive away the white crabs. However, the white crabs have a role in managing dew or moisture, and those who suffer from them are few. They are poor, so they will be shown punishment. You should prepare sulfur and tobacco at the entrance of a certain mountain cave and wait there."

    Fan gathered dozens of people and went on the appointed day. At midnight, the moon was slightly bright, and the wind blew in the air. They saw a large bat, about a zhang in size, fly to the front of the cave. In an instant, dozens of smaller bats gathered around it. Each time a bat approached, there was a light guiding it. Fan realized, "Is this not the so-called Black-Clothed Immortal?" He immediately lit a fire and burned the sulfur and tobacco. Soon, a sound rose from inside the cave, like the surge of a tide and the blowing of the wind. A white ribbon-like creature flew out, and the bats surrounded it in a formation, engaging in prolonged combat. The villagers also beat gongs and drums and set off firecrackers to assist. After about an hour, the white ribbon-like creature dispersed like fluff, and a green vapor moved northeast, with the bats also scattering.

    579

    次早往视：林莽间绵絮千馀片，或青或白，触手腥秽，不可近。自是鲎患竟息。

    　　○海中毛人张口生风雍正间，有海船飘至台湾之彰化界。船止二十余人，赀货颇多，因家焉。逾年，有同伙之子广东人投词于官，据云：某等泛海开船，后遇飓风，迷失海道，顺流而东。行数昼夜，舟得泊岸，回视水如山立，舟不可行，因遂登岸，地上破船、坏板、白骨不可胜计，自分必死矣。不逾年，舟中人渐次病死，某等亦粮尽。

    　　余豆数斛，植之，竟得生豆，赖以充腹。一日者，有毛人长数丈，自东方徐步来，指海水而笑。某等向彼号呼叩首。长人以手指海，若挥之速去者。某等始不解，既而有悟，急驾帆试之。长人张口吹气，蓬蓬然东风大作，昼夜不息，因望见鹿仔港口，遂收泊焉。彰化县官案验得实，移咨广省，以所有资物按二十余家均分之，遂定案焉。

    Explanation

    579

    The next morning, they went to investigate: in the dense forest, there were thousands of pieces of cotton-like material, some green and some white, with a foul and putrid stench upon touch, making it impossible to approach. From then on, the calamity of the horseshoe crabs ceased.

    ○ A Hairy Man in the Sea, Creating Wind During the Reign of Emperor Yongzheng, there was a sea vessel that drifted to the area of Changhua in Taiwan. The ship had only about twenty to thirty people, but it carried a significant amount of wealth and goods, so they settled there. After a year, one of their partners, a man from Guangdong, filed a complaint with the authorities, stating: "We set sail across the sea, later encountered a typhoon and lost our way at sea, drifted eastward with the current. After several days and nights, the boat finally reached the shore. Looking back, the water was as high as mountains, making the boat unable to move, so we disembarked. On the ground, there were countless broken ships, splintered planks, and white bones, and we thought we would surely die. Within a year, the people on the ship gradually fell ill and died, and we also ran out of food.

    We had only a few measures of beans left, planted them, and eventually the beans sprouted, relying on them to fill our stomachs. One day, a hairy man several zhang tall slowly walked toward us from the east, pointing at the sea and laughing. We shouted and bowed to him. The tall man pointed at the sea as if waving us to leave quickly. We didn't understand at first, but then we realized, and hurriedly raised the sails and tried it. The tall man opened his mouth and blew, and a strong east wind suddenly arose, unceasingly day and night. We spotted Lukang Harbor and finally anchored there. The magistrate of Changhua County investigated and confirmed the truth, sent a report to Guangdong Province, and divided all the wealth and goods among the twenty or so families, and the case was thus resolved.

    580

    后有人云：此名海阐，乃东海之极下处，船无回理，惟一百二十年方有东风屈曲可上。此二十余人恰好值之，亦奇矣。第不知毛而长者又为何神也。

    　　○卞山地陷乾隆乙巳，湖州大旱，西门外下塘地陷数丈，民居屋脊与地相平，屋中人破瓦而出，什物一无损坏。河中忽亘起土埂，升出白光一道，望龙溪而去，怪风随之。溪中渔舟数十，俱为白光所迷。俄顷风定，舟俱聚一处，而白光亦不见矣。

    　　时有方老人者，年九十馀，自云少年时见渔舟捕得白鳝一条，重五六斤，不敢匿，献之乌程令某。适令前一夕梦见一白衣女子来告云：“某苕上水神也，为陈皇后守宫门，明日有厄求救。”次日见鳝而悟，仍命放入河中。今土中白光，得毋即此物欤！考西门外与迎禧门相连，南朝陈武帝之后为其父母营葬于卞山，起民夫开地道而出，葬后仍行封闭。然则地之陷亦有由矣。

    Explanation

    580

    After some time, there was a report: This place is called Hai Chan, the deepest point of the East Sea, where ships cannot return; only after one hundred and twenty years will there be an easterly wind that can lead them back. These more than twenty people happened to encounter it, which is also extraordinary. However, I do not know what deity the long-haired figure might be.

    ○ In the year of Qianlong's Bixi (1765), Huzhou suffered a severe drought. Outside the western gate, the land at the lower end of the canal collapsed several meters. The rooftops of houses were now level with the ground, and people inside emerged through broken tiles, with all household items undamaged. Suddenly, a ridge rose in the river, and a white light ascended, heading towards Longxi, followed by a strange wind. There were dozens of fishing boats in Longxi, all bewitched by the white light. In a short while, the wind stopped, and the boats gathered in one place, and the white light also disappeared.

    At the time, there was an elderly man named Fang, over ninety years old, who recounted that when he was young, he saw fishing boats catch a white eel weighing five or six pounds. He did not hide it and presented it to a certain magistrate of Wucheng. The magistrate had a dream the night before where a white-clad woman came and said, "I am a water spirit of the Tiao River, guarding the palace gate for Empress Chen. There will be a disaster tomorrow, please help." The next day, he saw the eel and realized the connection, and ordered it to be released back into the river. Could the white light in the earth be this very eel? Investigating that the western gate is connected to the Yinxih Gate, after the death of Emperor Wu of Chen, he arranged for his parents' burial at Bianshan, mobilizing laborers to dig a tunnel and exit, which was sealed again after the burial. Therefore, the collapse of the land also had its cause.

    581

    ○鬼逐鬼桐城左秀才某，与其妻张氏伉俪甚笃。张病卒，左不忍相离，终日伴棺而寝。

    　　七月十五日，其家作盂兰之会，家人俱在外礼佛设醮，秀才独伴妻棺看书。

    　　忽阴风一阵，有缢死鬼披发流血拖绳而至，直犯秀才。秀才惶急，拍棺呼曰：“妹妹救我！”其妻竟勃然掀棺而起，骂曰：“恶鬼，敢无礼犯我郎君耶！”挥臂打鬼，鬼踉跄逃出。妻谓秀才：“汝痴矣，夫妇钟情一至于是耶！缘汝福薄，故恶鬼敢于相犯，益同我归去投人身，再作偕老计耶？”秀才唯唯，妻仍入棺卧矣。秀才呼家人视之，棺钉数重皆断，妻之裙犹夹半幅于棺缝中也。不逾年，秀才亦卒。

    Explanation

    581

    A Scholar from Tongcheng

    A certain scholar from Tongcheng, whose name is not mentioned, was very devoted to his wife, Zhang. Zhang fell ill and passed away, but the scholar could not bear to be separated from her. He spent his days sleeping beside her coffin.

    On the fifteenth day of the seventh month, the family held a Ullambana Festival to honor their ancestors. While the family members were outside worshipping Buddha and performing rituals, the scholar remained alone, reading beside his wife's coffin.

    Suddenly, a gust of cold wind blew in, and a ghost of a hanged man, with disheveled hair and bloody rope, appeared and attacked the scholar. The scholar was terrified and beat the coffin, shouting, "Sister, save me!" His wife suddenly lifted the coffin lid and stood up, cursing, "How dare you, evil ghost, disrespect my husband?" She swung her arm and struck the ghost, who fled in a stumbling manner.

    The wife then said to the scholar, "You are foolish. How deeply do you love each other, to the point where you sleep beside her coffin? Because your good fortune is shallow, the evil ghost dares to attack you. Let's go together to reincarnate as humans and plan to grow old together in the next life." The scholar nodded in agreement, and the wife returned to the coffin and lay down again.

    The scholar called the family members to see, and they found that the coffin nails had been broken multiple times, and her skirt was still caught in the coffin seam. No more than a year later, the scholar also died.

    582

    ○柳树精杭州周起昆作龙泉县学教谕，每夜，明伦堂上鼓无故自鸣。遣人伺之，见一人长丈馀，以手击鼓。门斗俞龙素有胆，暗张弓射之，长人狂奔而去。次夜寂然。

    　　后两月，学门外起大风，拔巨柳一株。周命锯之为薪，中有箭横贯树腹，方知击鼓者此怪也。龙泉素无科目，是年中一陈姓者。

    　　○折叠仙浒市关有陈一元者，弃家学道。购一精舍，独坐其间，内加锁钥。初辟粥饭，继辟果蔬，但饮石湖之水。命其子每月饷水一壶，次月往视，则壶仍置门外而水已干，乃再实其壶以进焉。

    　　孙敬斋秀才闻而慕之，书一纸条贴壶盖上问可见否并请许见日期，心惴惴，恐不许也。次月往探，壶上批纸尾云：“二月初七日，可来相见。”孙大喜，临期，与其子偕往，见一元年仅四十许，而其子则已老矣。孙问：“修道从何下手？”

    Explanation

    582

    The Willow Spirit

    In Hangzhou, Zhou Qikun served as the headmaster of the Confucian school in Longquan County. Every night, the drum in the Minglun Hall would sound without cause. He sent someone to investigate and saw a man over a zhang tall striking the drum with his hand. The gatekeeper Yu Long, known for his courage, secretly drew his bow and shot at the apparition, causing it to flee wildly. The following night, everything was silent.

    Two months later, a strong wind arose outside the school, uprooting a large willow tree. Zhou ordered it to be cut into firewood, and an arrow was found horizontally piercing the tree's trunk, revealing that the drum-beater was this strange being. Longquan had never produced scholars before, but that year, one surnamed Chen passed the imperial examinations.

    In another place, there was a man named Chen Yüan in Xianhu Town who abandoned his family to learn the Dao. He purchased a secluded dwelling and sat alone within, locking it securely. He initially prepared simple meals, then moved on to fruits and vegetables, drinking only water from Shih Lake. He ordered his son to bring water once a month, and the next month, upon checking, the pot was still outside but the water had dried up. He then refilled the pot and brought it again.

    Scholar Sun Jingzai heard of this and admired Chen Yüan. He wrote a note and attached it to the lid of the pot, asking if he could be seen and requesting a date, with a trembling heart, fearing that it would not be allowed. On the appointed day, he went there with his son. They found Chen Yüan to be around forty years old, while his son had already grown old. Sun asked, "How does one begin to practice the Dao?"

    583

    曰：“汝且静坐片时，自数其心所思想处。”孙坐良久，一元问：“汝可起几许念头？”曰：“起过七十二念。”一元笑曰：“心无所寄，求静反动，理之常也。

    　　汝一个时辰起七十二念，不可谓无恨气，可以学道。“遂教以饮水之法曰：”人生本自虚空而来，因食物过多，致身体坚重，腹中秽虫丛起，易生痰滞。学道者先清其口，再清其肠。饿死诸虫以荡涤之，水为先天第一真气。天地开辟时，未有五行先有水，故饮水为修仙要诀。但城市水浑，有累灵府，必取山中至清之水，徐徐而吞，使喉中喀喀有响，然后甘味才出。一勺水，可度一昼夜。如是一百二十年，身渐轻清，并水可辟，便服气御风而行矣。“孙问一元：”何师？“曰：”余三十年前往太山烧香，遇一少年，貌其灵俊，能预知阴晴，因与一路偕行。

    Explanation

    583

    The speaker said, "Sit quietly for a moment and count the thoughts in your mind." Sun sat for a long time. One Yuan asked, "How many thoughts did you have?" Sun replied, "I had more than seventy-two thoughts." One Yuan smiled and said, "When the mind has no focus, seeking stillness only leads to more agitation, which is a common phenomenon.

    With seventy-two thoughts in just one hour, it cannot be said that your mind is calm enough for learning the Way." Then, One Yuan taught him the method of drinking water: "Human life originally comes from emptiness. Overeating leads to a heavy body, the proliferation of harmful substances in the abdomen, and the production of phlegm and blockages. Those who wish to learn the Way should first cleanse their mouth, then their intestines. By starving the harmful substances, the body can be purified. Water is the first true primordial energy. When the universe was formed, water existed before the five elements, so drinking water is a crucial practice for cultivating immortality. However, city water is turbid and can burden the spirit; one must take the purest water from the mountains, drink it slowly, ensuring it is properly swallowed and the sweetness is tasted.
