A single spoonful of water can sustain one for a day and night. After a hundred and twenty years, the body will gradually become light and pure, able to fly without water, and ride the wind.

    Sun asked One Yuan, "Who is your teacher?" One Yuan replied, "Thirty years ago, I went to Mount Tai to burn incense. I met a young man who was spiritually gifted and could predict the weather. We traveled together."

    584

    少年背负一锦匣，每至下店，必向匣絮语片时，然后安寝。心大惊疑，凿壁窥之：见少年放匣几上，整冠再拜，一老人从匣中笑坐而起，双眸炯炯，白须飘然。两人相与密语，听不可解，但闻‘有窃盗者、有盗窃者’八字而已。夜三更，少年请曰：“先生可安寝乎？‘老人颔之，遂将老人折叠如纸绢人一般，装入匣中矣。

    Explanation

    584

    A Young Man with a Brocade Box

    A young man carried a brocade box, and every time he reached an inn, he would speak to the box for a short while, then settle down to sleep. The narrator was greatly astonished and suspicious, so he peered through a hole in the wall to look. He saw the young man place the box on the table, adjust his cap and bow twice, and then a man emerged from the box, sat smiling, and stood up. The man had bright and sharp eyes, and his white beard fluttered gracefully. The two engaged in a secret conversation, the words of which were unclear, but the narrator could only make out the phrases "there are thieves and there are those who steal." At the third watch of the night, the young man asked, "May the master rest peacefully?" The old man nodded in agreement, and the young man then folded the old man like a paper figure and put him back into the box.

    585

    次日，少年知余窥见，故告我来历，许我为弟子而传以道也。“孙抱一元试之，连所坐椅，仅三十斤。孙以两女未嫁故，乞假而归，假满再往。

    　　余见之于震泽张明府署中，具道如此。是戊申二月初十日也。

    　　○仙人顶门无发癸巳秋，张明府在毗陵遇杨道人者，童颜鹤发，惟顶门方寸一毛不生。怪而问之，笑曰：“汝不见街道上两边生草，而当中人所践踏之地不生草乎？”初不解所谓，既而思之，知宰门地方故是元神出入处，故不生发也。道人夜坐僧寺门外，僧招之内宿，决意不可。次早视之，见太阳东升，道人坐墙上吸日光。其顶门上有一小儿，圆满清秀，亦向日光舞蹈而吞吸之。

    　　○香虹吴江姜某，一子一女，其子娶新妇刘氏。刘性柔婉，不能操作。有婢香虹者，素诡谲，因与其女日夜媒蘖其短，刘恨不能伸。来时嫁资颇丰，为其姑逼索且尽。

    　　未期年，染病床褥。姑谓其痨也，不许其子与见。刘抑郁死。

    Explanation

    585

    The next day, the youth realized that I had seen him and thus told me about his origins, allowing me to become his disciple and impart his teachings to me. "Sun Baoyuan tested him, including the chair he was sitting on, which weighed only thirty pounds. Sun requested leave to return home because his two daughters had not yet married, and after the leave period ended, he returned.

    I saw him at the office of Zhang Mingfu in Zhenze and heard the entire story. This was on the tenth day of the second month in the year Wu Shen.

    ○ In the autumn of Gui Si, Zhang Mingfu met an elderly Taoist in Changzhou named Yang. The Taoist had a youthful appearance and white hair, but a square inch of his forehead had no hair. Zhang found this strange and asked him, to which the Taoist smiled and said, "Don't you see that grass grows on both sides of the street, but not where people walk in the middle?" At first, I did not understand what he meant, but after thinking about it, I realized that the forehead is the place where the soul enters and exits, so no hair grows there. The Taoist sat outside a temple at night, and a monk invited him inside to stay, but he firmly refused. The next morning, I saw him sitting on a wall outside the temple, absorbing sunlight. On his forehead, there was a little boy who was round, full, and handsome, and he also danced toward the sunlight while absorbing it.

    ○ In Wujiang, there was a man named Jiang, who had one son and one daughter. His son married a new wife named Liu, who was gentle and soft-spoken but unable to perform household chores. There was a maid named Xiang Hong, who was always deceitful and cunning, and she constantly slandered Liu's shortcomings with her daughter. Liu was angry but could not retaliate. When she came, her dowry was quite substantial, but her mother-in-law forced her to give it all away.

    Within a year, Liu fell ill and was bedridden. Her mother-in-law claimed she had tuberculosis and did not allow her son to see her. Liu died of depression.

    586

    忽一日，其女登床自批其颊，历数其生平之恶，且云：“姑使我不与郎见，亦是姻缘数尽，然尔辈用心何太酷耶？”如是数日。为设醮，亦不应。姜与其妻婉求之，乃曰：“翁待吾厚，姑亦老悖，此是香虹之过，我不饶他。”香虹在侧忽瞪目大呼，两手架空而行，若有人提之者，坠下则已毙矣。其女依然无恙。此乾隆五十三年正月事。

    　　○阎王升殿先吞铁丸杭州闵玉苍先生，一生清正，任刑部郎中时，每夜署理阴间阎王之职。至二更时，有仪从轿马相迎。其殿有五，先生所以莅，第五殿也。每升殿，判官先进铁弹一丸，状如雀卵，重两许，教吞入腹中，然后理事，曰：“此上帝所铸，虑阎罗王阳官署事有所瞻徇，故命吞铁丸以镇其心，此数千年老例也。”先后照例吞丸。审案毕，便吐出之。三涤三视，交与判官收管。所办事晨起辄忘；即记得者，亦不肯向人说，但劝人勿食牛肉，多诵《大悲咒》而已。

    Explanation

    586

    One day, the woman climbed onto the bed and slapped her own cheeks, recounting her past misdeeds, and said, "It is also the end of our marital fate, but how harsh your intentions have been!" She did this for several days. They set up an altar, but she did not respond. Mr. Jiang and his wife gently pleaded with her, and she said, "My father treated me well, but my mother is old and foolish. This is the fault of Xianghong, and I will not forgive him." Suddenly, Xianghong, who was beside her, opened her eyes wide and shouted. She raised her hands into the air as if someone was lifting her, and when she fell, she was already dead. The woman remained unharmed. This was the first month of the 53rd year of the Qianlong era (1788).

    Mr. Min Yucang, a man of integrity who served as a Langzhong in the Ministry of Punishment, would often act as the King of Hell at night. At around two o'clock in the morning, he would be greeted by attendants and a procession of carriages. His palace had five halls, and he would preside over the fifth hall. Before ascending the hall, the judge would present him with an iron ball, about the size of an egg, weighing about two pounds, and instruct him to swallow it. Afterward, he would handle the affairs, saying, "This is cast by God, to prevent the King of Hell from being biased in his earthly duties. This is an old rule that has been in place for thousands of years." He would follow the tradition and swallow the ball. After finishing the trial, he would spit it out. He would then wash and inspect it three times before handing it over to the judge for safekeeping. The things he did in the morning were quickly forgotten, and even if he remembered, he did not tell anyone. But he only advised people not to eat beef and to recite the Great Compassion Mantra.

    587

    到任三月，忽一日晨起召诸亲友而告曰：“吾今而知小善之不足为也。昨晚吾表弟李某死，生魂解到，判官将其生平作官恶迹，请寄地狱审定拟罪，再详解东岳。余心恻然，将狱牌安放几上，再三目李。李自诉平生不食牛肉，作官时禁私宰尤严，似可以此功德抵销他罪。余未作声，判官驳云：此之谓”恩足以及禽兽，而功不至于百姓“也。子不食牛肉，何以独食人肉？‘李云：”某并未食人肉。’判官曰：“民脂民膏，即人肉也。汝作贪官，食千万人之膏血，而不食一牛之肉，细想小善可抵得大罪否？‘李不能答。余知李素诵《大悲咒》，为阴司所最重，因手书’大悲咒‘三字在掌上以示之。李竟茫然，不能诵一字。余为代诵数句，满堂判官胥役一齐跪听，西方赫然似有红云飞至者。然而铁丸已涌起于胸中，左冲右撞，肠痛欲裂矣。余不得已急取狱牌加朱，放李狱中，肠内铁丸始定，方理别案而归。”

    Explanation

    587

    After three months in office, one morning the official summoned his relatives and friends and said, "I now realize that small good deeds are insufficient. Last night, my cousin Li died, and his soul was brought before the judgment. The judge reviewed his life as an official and the records of his evil deeds, preparing to sentence him in hell, and then send him to Mount Tai for further judgment. My heart was moved with compassion, and I placed the judgment slip on the table, looking at Li again and again. Li claimed that he had never eaten beef and had been especially strict in banning private slaughter during his time as an official, suggesting that this could offset his sins. I remained silent, and the judge countered, saying, 'This is what is meant by "kindness is enough to reach animals, but not the people." You did not eat beef, but why did you eat human flesh?' Li replied, 'I never ate human flesh.' The judge said, 'The people's wealth and labor are as if they were human flesh. You, as a corrupt official, consumed the blood and fat of thousands of people, but did not eat even one cow's meat. Can small good deeds truly offset such great sins?' Li could not answer. I knew that Li had always recited the Great Compassion Mantra, which is highly regarded in the underworld. So, I wrote the words 'Great Compassion Mantra' on my palm and showed them to him. Li was茫然, unable to recite even one word. I then recited a few lines on his behalf, and all the judges and officials in the hall knelt down to listen, as if a red cloud flew in from the west. However, an iron ball surged up in my chest, colliding left and right, causing my intestines to ache as if they would tear apart. I could not help but quickly take the judgment slip, mark it with red ink, and send Li to hell. Only then did the iron ball in my intestines settle, and I returned to handle other cases."

    588

    诸亲友因问：“到底牛肉可食乎？”先生曰：“在可食不可食之间。”人问故，曰：“此事与敬惜字纸相同，圣所未戒，然不过推重农重文之心，充类至义之尽，故禁食之者，慈也。然‘天地不仁，以万物为刍狗’，此语久被老子说破。

    　　试想春蚕作丝，衣被天子，以至于庶人。其功比牛更大，其性命比牛更多，而何以烹之煮之，抽其腹肠而炙食之，竟无一人为之鸣冤立禁者，何耶？盖天地之性人为贵，贵人贱畜，理所当然，故食牛肉者，达也。“

    　　○万佛崖康熙五十年，肃州合黎山顶忽有人呼曰：“开不开？开不开？”如是数日，无人敢答。一日，有牧童过，闻之，戏应声曰：“开。”顷刻砉然，风雷怒号，山石大开，中现一崖，有天生菩萨像数千，须眉宛然。至今人呼为“万佛崖”。

    Explanation

    588

    The friends and relatives then asked, "Is beef actually edible?" The master replied, "It is in the middle of whether it is edible or not." When they asked why, he said, "This matter is similar to the practice of respecting and cherishing written characters. The sages did not prohibit it, but they emphasized the importance of agriculture and literature. By extending this analogy to its fullest extent, they banned the consumption of beef as an act of benevolence. However, 'Heaven and Earth are not benevolent; they treat all things as straw dogs,' a statement that Laozi made long ago.

    Consider the silkworms that produce silk, which clothe emperors and commoners alike. Their contribution is greater than that of cattle, and their lives are more numerous. Yet, we boil and cook them, extract their intestines, and roast them, without anyone raising a voice to protest or establish a prohibition. Why is this? Because human life is considered precious, and the value of humans is placed above that of animals, which is a natural and reasonable principle. Therefore, eating beef is an act of understanding."

    Additional Note on the Second Part

    In the year 50 of the reign of Emperor Kangxi, a cliff known as "Wanfo Cliff" appeared suddenly on the top of He Li Mountain in Su State. People began to hear voices calling out, "Open or not open? Open or not open?" For several days, no one dared to respond. One day, a herdsboy passed by and heard the voices. He playfully responded, "Open." Immediately, there was a loud cracking sound, and the wind and thunder roared. The mountain split open, revealing a cliff with thousands of naturally formed statues of Bodhisattvas, their eyebrows and beards clearly visible. To this day, the place is still known as "Wanfo Cliff."

    589

    章淮树观察过其地亲见之。

    　　○大力河孙某作打箭炉千总，其所辖地阴雨两月。忽一日雨止，仰天见日光，孙喜，出舍视之。顷刻，烟沙蔽天，风声怒号，孙立不牢，扑地乱滚，似有人提其辫发而颠掷之者，腿脸俱伤。孙心知是地动，忍而待之。食顷，动止，起视，人民与自家房屋全已倾圮。有一弟逃出未死，彼此惶急。

    　　孙老于居边者，谓弟曰：“地动必有回潮，不止一次，我与汝须死在一处。”

    　　乃各以绳缚其身，两相拥抱。言未毕，而怪风又起，两人卧地，颠播如初。幸沙不眼，见地裂数丈：有冒出黑风者，有冒出火光如带紫绿二色者，有涌黑水臭而腥者，有现出人头大如车轮、目夹夹斜视四方者，有裂而仍合者，有永远成坑者。兄弟二人竟得无恙，乃埋葬全家，掘出货物，各自谋生。

    Explanation

    589

    Chapter Huishu, an observer, personally visited the place and witnessed the event.

    In the area of Dalihe, there was a man named Sun who served as a Thousand-Total at Dajianlu. The region he governed experienced rain for two months. Suddenly, one day, the rain stopped, and he looked up to see the sun shining. Sun was delighted and went out to see what had happened. In an instant, smoke and sand obscured the sky, and the wind howled fiercely. Sun was thrown to the ground and rolled around, as if someone was pulling his braids and tossing him about. His legs and face were all injured. Sun realized it was an earthquake and endured it. After a while, the movement stopped, and he got up to look around. The people and his own house were completely destroyed. One of his brothers had escaped and was still alive, and they were both in a state of panic.

    Sun, being experienced in living on the frontier, said to his brother, "Earthquakes always come with aftershocks, and they won't stop. We must die together." They tied themselves together with ropes and embraced each other. Before he finished speaking, a strange wind arose again. The two of them lay on the ground and were tossed about as before. Fortunately, the sand did not blind their eyes, and they saw the ground split open several feet. There were black winds spewing out, flames like a purple and green belt, black water gushing out, stinking and fishy, human heads appearing, as large as cart wheels, their eyes bulging and looking sideways at all sides, cracks forming and then closing again, and permanent pits being formed. Despite all this, the two brothers were ultimately unharmed. They buried their entire family, dug out goods, and each sought to make a living.

    590

    先三月前，有疯僧持缘簿一册，上写“募化人口一万”。孙恶其妖言，将擒之送县，僧已立一杨柳小枝上，曰：“你勿送我到县，送我塞大力河水口可也。”

    　　言毕不见。是年地动日，四川大力河水冲决，溺死万余人。

    　　●卷十七○白骨精处州地多山，丽水县在仙都峰之南，土人耕种，多有开垦到半山者。山中多怪，人皆早作早休，不敢夜出。时值深秋，有田主李某到乡刈稻，独住庄房。土人恐其胆怯，不敢以实告，但戒昏夜勿出。一夕，月色甚佳，主人闲步前山，忽见一白物踊而来，棱テ有声，状甚怪。因急回寓，其物已追踪而至。幸庄房门有半截栅栏可推而进，怪不能越。主人进棚胆壮，月色甚明，从栅缝中细看，乃是一髑髅咬撞栅门，腥臭不可当。

    Explanation

    590

    Three months prior, there was a mad monk holding a book for alms, on which was written, "To solicit ten thousand people for alms." The landowner hated his strange words and intended to capture him and send him to the county. The monk had already stood on a small branch of a willow tree and said, "You don't have to send me to the county; you can send me to block the mouth of the Dali River."

    After speaking, he disappeared. On the day of the earthquake that year, the Dali River in Sichuan province burst its banks, drowning over ten thousand people.

    Chapter Seventeen: The White Bone Spirit
    Chuzhou is a region with many mountains, and Lishui County is south of Xiandu Peak. Local people farm, and many have cultivated up to the middle of the mountain. There are many strange things in the mountains, and people work early and rest early, daring not to go out at night. It was deep autumn, and there was a landowner named Li who went to the village to cut rice, living alone in a cottage. The locals were afraid he would be timid and did not dare to tell him the truth, but warned him not to go out at dusk or night. One night, the moonlight was very beautiful, and the owner strolled leisurely in front of the mountain. Suddenly, he saw a white object jumping and leaping towards him, making a clacking sound, the appearance was very strange. He hurried back to his lodging, and the object had already followed him. Fortunately, the cottage door had a half-barrier that could be pushed open, and the strange object could not cross. The owner felt braver after entering the cottage, and the moonlight was very bright. He looked closely through the gap in the fence and saw that it was a skull biting and bumping against the gate, the smell was unbearable.

    591

    少顷鸡鸣，见其物倒地，只白骨一堆。天明，亦不复见。问之土人，曰：“幸足下遇白骨精，故得无恙。若遇白发老妇，假开店面，必请足下吃酒。凡吃其烟者，从无生理。月白风清之夜，常出作祟，惟用苕帚可以击倒之。亦终不知何怪。”

    　　○鼋壳亭乾隆二十年，川东道白公，以千金买一妾，挂帆回任，宠爱异常。舟过镇江，月夜泊舟，妾推窗取水，为巨鼋所吞。主人悲恨，誓必得鼋而后已，传谕各渔船协力搜拿，有能得巨鼋者赏百金。船户争以猪肚羊肝套五须钩为饵，上系空酒坛，浮于水面，昼夜不寐。

    Explanation

    591

    A short while later, the rooster crowed, and they saw the object fall to the ground, revealing only a pile of white bones. By dawn, it was also no longer seen. They asked the locals, who said, "Fortunately, you encountered the white bone demon, so you were unharmed. If you had met an old woman with white hair, pretending to open a shop, she would have invited you to drink wine. Anyone who eats its smoke has no chance of survival. On clear moonlit nights, it often causes mischief, but only a broom can knock it down. Ultimately, we do not know what kind of monster it was."

    ○Yuanke Pavilion
    In the twentieth year of Qianlong's reign, Bai Gong, the governor of the Sichuan Eastern Circuit, bought a concubine for a thousand gold pieces and set sail back to his post, cherishing her exceptionally. As their boat passed through Zhenjiang on a moonlit night, the concubine leaned out the window to fetch water, only to be swallowed by a giant turtle. The master was filled with grief and anger, vowing to capture the turtle at all costs. He ordered all fishing boats to work together to search for it, promising a hundred gold pieces to anyone who could catch the giant turtle. The fishermen competed to use pig stomachs and sheep livers as bait on five-pronged hooks, attaching empty wine jars to the lines and floating them on the water, staying awake day and night.

    592

    两日后，果钓得大鼋，数十人拽之不能起，乃以船缆系巨石磨盘，用四水牛拖之，跃然上岸，头如车轮。群以利斧斫之，滚地成坑，喳喳有声，良久乃死。

    　　破其腹，妾腕间金镯尚在。于是碎其身，焚以火，臭闻数里。一壳大数丈，坚过于铁，苦无所用，乃构一亭，以鼋壳作顶，亮如明瓦窗。至今在镇江朝阳门外大路旁。

    　　○怪怕讲理苏州富翁黄老人者，年过八十，独处一楼。忽见女子倚门而望，老人壮年曾有爱女卒于此楼，疑是女魂，置之不问。次晚又见，则多一男子矣。至第三日，一男一女，跨身梁间，两目下注。老人故作不见，俯首看书。其男子乃下，直立老人旁。老人笑问曰：“足下是鬼耶，此来甚差！我年已八十馀，死乃旦夕事，不久与君为同类，何必先蒙过访？若是仙耶，何不请坐一谈？”怪不答，但长啸，四面楼窗齐开，阴风袭人。老人唤家人上楼，怪亦不见。

    Explanation

    592

    Two days later, they successfully caught a large turtle. Dozens of people pulled it, but could not move it. They then tied a ship's cable to a large stone millstone and used four water buffalo to drag it. It suddenly leaped onto the shore, its head as big as a cart wheel. The crowd hacked at it with sharp axes. It rolled on the ground, creating a pit and making a crunching sound, and after a long time, it died.

    They cut open its belly and found a gold bracelet on the wrist of the concubine still inside. They then crushed its body and burned it with fire. The smell spread for several miles. Its shell was several zhang wide and harder than iron, but it had no practical use. They then built a pavilion, using the turtle's shell as the roof, which was as bright as a glazed window. It still stands today by the main road outside the city gates of Zhenjiang.

    In Suzhou, there was an elderly wealthy man named Huang, over eighty years old, who lived alone in a building. One day, he suddenly saw a woman leaning against the door looking out. He had had a beloved daughter who had died in this building when he was young. He suspected it was the spirit of his daughter and ignored it. The next evening, he saw it again, but there was also a man. On the third day, a man and a woman stood on the beam, looking downward. The old man pretended not to see them and bent his head to read a book. The man then descended and stood directly next to the old man. The old man smiled and asked, "Are you a ghost? It's not a good time for you to come. I am already over eighty, and death is imminent; soon I will be of the same kind as you, so why bother visiting me first? If you are a god, why don't you sit down and talk with me?" The ghost did not answer but let out a long howl. The windows on all sides of the building opened simultaneously, and a cold wind blew in. The old man called his family to come upstairs, and the ghost disappeared.

    593

    后数月，二媳一孙皆死，仅存一小婢。老人恐此女身后无依，乃赠与西席华君为妾，生三子。现在浙江临海县华公署中。此事华秋槎明府为余言。

    　　○娄真人错捉妖松江御史张忠震，甲辰进士。书房卧炕中，每夜鼠斗，作闹不止。主人厌其烦，烧爆竹逐这，不去；打以火枪，亦若不知。张疑炕中有物，毁之，毫无所见。

    　　书室后为使女卧房，夜见方巾黑袍者来与求欢。女不允，旋即昏迷，不省人事。

    　　主人知之，以张真人玉印符放入被套覆其胸。是夕鬼不至，次日又来作闹，剥女下衣，污秽其符。

    　　张公怒，延娄真人设坛作法。三日后，擒一物如狸，封入瓮中，合家皆以为可安。是夜，其怪大笑而来曰：“我兄弟们不知进退，竟被道士哄去，可恨！谅不敢来拿我。”淫纵愈甚。主人再谋之娄，娄曰：“我法只可行一次，第二次便不灵。”张无奈何，每晚将此女送入城隍庙中，怪乃去。一回家，则又至矣。

    Explanation

    593

    After several months, two of the daughter-in-laws and one grandson died, leaving only a small maid. The old man, fearing that this woman would have no support after his death, gave her to Master Huajun of the Western Scholar as a concubine. She bore him three sons, and they now live in the official residence of Huagong in Linhai County, Zhejiang Province. This matter was told to me by Mingfu Huoqiucha.

    ○ Master Lou Mistakenly Captured a Demon

    Zhang Zhongzhen, a jinshi of the Jia Chen year, served as an imperial censor in Songjiang. In the heated platform of his study, every night the mice would fight, causing endless trouble. The owner, tired of the disturbance, burned firecrackers to drive them away, but they did not leave. He also hit them with a fire gun, but they seemed unaware. Zhang suspected there was something in the kang and destroyed it, but saw nothing.

    Behind the study was the sleeping room of the maid. At night, she saw a man in a square cap and black robe coming to seek her pleasure. She refused, and he immediately made her unconscious, rendering her unconscious.

    The owner, knowing this, placed a jade seal talisman of Zhang the Taoist Master inside a pillowcase and covered it over her chest. That night, the ghost did not come, but the next day it came again, stripping the maid's lower clothes and defiling the talisman.

    Offended, Zhang invited Master Lou to set up a platform and perform rituals. After three days, they caught an animal like a weasel, sealed it in a jar, and the whole family thought they were safe. That night, the demon laughed loudly and came saying, "My brothers don't know how to retreat, they were tricked by the Taoist priest, how hateful! They won't dare to come after me again." The demon's licentious behavior became even more severe. The owner consulted Master Lou again, who said, "My method can only be used once; it won't work a second time." Zhang had no choice but to send the maid to the City God Temple every night, and the ghost would then leave. Once she returned home, the ghost would come again.

    594

    越半年，主人深夜与客奕棋，天大雪，偶推窗漱口，见窗外一物，大如驴，脸黑眼黄，蹲伏阶下。张吐水正浇其背，急跳出窗外逐之，怪忽不见。次早，女告主人曰：“昨夜怪来，自言被主人看见，天机已露，请从今日去矣。”自此怪果绝。

    　　○陈姓妇啖石子天台县西乡赛会迎神，神袍微皱，有妇人姓陈者为扶熨之。晚归，见金甲神自称将军拥众至，仪卫甚盛，云：“汝替我整衣，有情于我，今娶汝为妻。”带点心与啖，皆河子石也。妇人啖时，甚觉软美。小者从大便出，大者仍从口内吐出，吐出则坚硬如常石子矣。父兄俟其来时，使有勇者与格斗。良久，妇人曰：“伤其锤柄矣。”次日至野庙中， 有五通神所执金锤有伤，乃毁其庙，神亦寂然。

    Explanation

    594

    After half a year, the master played chess with a guest at midnight. It snowed heavily, and he accidentally opened the window to rinse his mouth. He saw something outside the window, as big as a donkey, with a black face and yellow eyes, crouching at the bottom of the stairs. Zhang was spitting water onto its back, and quickly jumped out of the window to chase it. The strange creature suddenly disappeared. The next morning, the woman told the master, "Last night, the strange creature came and said that it had been seen by the master, revealing the heavenly secret, and asked to leave from today." From then on, the strange creature indeed disappeared.

    A woman surnamed Chen ate stone chips during the festival welcoming the gods in the west of Tiantai County. The god's robe was slightly wrinkled, and a woman surnamed Chen helped to smooth it. In the evening, she returned home and saw a golden-armored god who called himself General and led a crowd, with a grand entourage. He said, "You helped me with my clothes, I have feelings for you, and now I will marry you." He brought some snacks and fed her, which were all river pebbles. The woman found them very soft and delicious when she ate them. The smaller ones came out through the large intestine, and the larger ones were still expelled from the mouth, and they became hard like ordinary pebbles. Her father and brothers waited for her to come back and had a brave person fight with her. After a long time, the woman said, "I injured the handle of his hammer." The next day, they went to a wild temple. There was a golden hammer held by the Five Tongs God that was injured. They then destroyed the temple, and the god became silent.

    595

    ○天台县缸天台县署中，到任官空三堂而不居，让与一缸居之，相传为前朝故物。缸有神灵，能知人祸福。凡县尹到任，必行三跪九叩礼祭之，否则作祟。官当升迁，则缸先凭空而起，若有系之者；当降革，则缸先下降，渐入土中。平是缸离地寸许，从不着土。余心疑焉。

    　　壬寅春，游天台山，地主钟公醴泉邀饮署内，酒后言曰：“署中二古物，盍往一观？”书室西有老桂参天，旁悬一匾，乃明天启四年邑宰陈命众题额。转过三堂，则缸神所居，其大如鼓，一黄沙粗缸耳，中有小穴。吏云：“此神口也，牲血涔涔，皆历年来所享鸡豕。”余以扇击之，声铿然；以竹片试其底，毫不能入，并非离在者。钟公骇然，余笑曰：“我击之，我试之，缸当祸我，不祸君也。”

    Explanation

    595

    In Tiantai County's administrative building, newly appointed officials would vacate the three halls and not reside there, instead allowing a barrel to occupy the space. This barrel is said to be an ancient relic from a previous dynasty. It is believed to possess a divine spirit capable of predicting people's fortune and misfortune. Whenever a county magistrate arrives at their post, they must perform a ritual of kneeling three times and bowing nine times to worship the barrel; otherwise, it will cause trouble. If the official is to be promoted, the barrel will first rise into the air, as if being lifted; if the official is to be demoted or dismissed, the barrel will first descend, gradually sinking into the ground. Normally, the barrel is about an inch off the ground and never touches the soil. I was skeptical of this.

    In the spring of the Renyin year, I visited Tiantai Mountain, and my host, Zhong Gong Liqian, invited me to drink inside the administrative building. After the meal, he said, "There are two ancient objects in the office; why don't we go and see them?" To the west of the study room, there is an ancient osmanthus tree towering overhead, with a plaque hanging beside it. This plaque was inscribed in the fourth year of the Tianqi era during the Ming Dynasty by the county magistrate Chen Mingzhong. Turning past the three halls, we came to the barrel, which is as large as a drum, a rough clay barrel with a small hole in it. The official said, "This is the mouth of the spirit, and the blood of chickens and pigs offered over the years drips continuously." I hit the barrel with a fan, and it made a clear, ringing sound. I then tested the bottom with a piece of bamboo, but it could not penetrate at all, indicating that it was not suspended. Zhong Gong was greatly startled, and I laughed and said, "I hit it, I tested it; the barrel should bring misfortune to me, but it won't bring misfortune to you."

    596

    已而寂然。此缸载《天台县志》中。

    　　○木姑娘坟京师宝和班，演剧甚有名。一日者，有人骑马来相订云：“海岱门外木府要唱戏，登时须去。”是日班中无事，遂随行。至城外，天色已晚。过数里荒野之处，果见前面大房屋，宾客甚多，灯火荧荧然微带绿色，内有婢传呼云：“姑娘吩咐，只要唱生旦戏，不许大花面上堂，用大锣大鼓，扰乱取厌。”管班者如其言。自二更唱起，至漏尽不许休息，又无酒饭犒劳。帘内妇女，堂上宾客，语嘶嘶不可辨，于是班中人人惊疑。大花面顾姓者不耐烦，竟自涂脸扮《关公借荆州》一出，单刀直上，锣鼓大作。顷刻，堂上灯烛灭尽，宾客全无。取火照之，是一荒冢，乃急卷箱而归。

    Explanation

    596

    After a while, everything fell silent. This story is recorded in the Tiantai County Gazetteer.

    The Baohé Troupe of the capital city was renowned for its performances. One day, a messenger arrived on horseback to make a request: "The Wooden Mansion outside the Sea and Mount Gate requires a performance, and you must depart immediately." That day, the troupe had no other engagements, so they followed the messenger. Upon reaching the outskirts of the city, it was already late. After traveling through a desolate area for several miles, they indeed saw a large house ahead, with many guests and faint green lights. A maid inside called out: "The Lady has instructed that only performances featuring male and female leads are allowed. No heavy makeup or loud drums and gongs are permitted, as they would disturb and annoy us." The troupe leader followed her instructions. From the second watch, they began performing, and they were not allowed to rest until the end of the night. There was no food or drink to reward them. The women behind the curtains and the guests in the hall spoke in hushed tones, making it impossible to understand their words. Everyone in the troupe was filled with unease and suspicion. One of the performers, surnamed Gu, who wore heavy makeup, could no longer tolerate the situation. He hastily put on makeup and performed The Borrowing of Jingzhou (关公借荆州), boldly advancing onto the stage with loud drums and gongs. Suddenly, the lights in the hall went out completely, and all the guests vanished. When they lit the lanterns again, they found themselves standing before an abandoned tomb. The troupe quickly packed their belongings and fled.

    597

    明早询土人，曰：“某府木姑娘坟也。”

    　　○雷诛王三常州王三，积恶讼棍也。太守董怡曾到任，首名访拿，王三躲避。其弟名仔者，武进生员，正在娶亲，新人入门，而差役拘王三不得，遂拘其弟往，管押班房。王三知家属已去，则官事稍松，乃夜入弟室，冒充新郎，与弟妇成亲。

    　　次日，差役带其弟上堂。太守见是柔弱书生，愍其无辜，且知其正值新婚，作速遣还。宽限一月访拿王三。其弟入室慰劳其妻，妻方知此是新郎，昨所共寝者非也，羞忿缢死。其岳家要来吵闹，而赧于发扬，且明知非新郎之罪，乃曰：“我家所赔赠衣饰，须尽入棺中，我才罢休。”新郎舅姑哀痛不已，一一从命。

    　　王三闻之，又动欲念，伺其攒殡之处，往发掘之。开棺，妇色如生，乃剥其下衣，又与淫污。污毕，取其珠翠首饰藏裹满怀，将奔上路。忽空中霹雳一声，王三震死，其妇活矣。

    Explanation

    597

    In the morning, he asked the local people, who said, "It is the grave of the wooden maiden of a certain prefecture."

    ○ A man named Wang San from Changzhou was a wicked and litigious rascal. When Governor Dong Yi arrived at his post, Wang San was first listed for investigation and arrest, but he managed to evade capture. His younger brother, named Zai, was a student from Wujin who was in the process of getting married. When the new bride entered the house, the officials could not find Wang San, so they arrested his brother instead and detained him in the jail. Wang San, knowing that his family had left, felt that the official matter was somewhat relaxed, and he sneaked into his brother's room at night, impersonating the groom, and had sexual intercourse with his younger brother's wife.

    The next day, the officials brought his brother to court. The governor, seeing that he was a weak and innocent scholar, pitied him and knew that he was newly married, so he quickly sent him back home. He granted a one-month extension to investigate and arrest Wang San. The younger brother went into the room to comfort his wife, who only then realized that this was the new groom, and that the person she had slept with the previous night was not him. She hanged herself out of shame and anger. Her father-in-law wanted to come and cause a disturbance, but was ashamed to publicize it and knew that it was not the new groom's fault. He said, "All the clothes and jewelry that my family gave as gifts must be placed in the coffin, and then I will stop." The new groom's mother and father grieved endlessly and complied with everything.

    Upon hearing this, Wang San was aroused by desire again. He waited at the place where the coffin was placed and dug it up. When he opened the coffin, the woman looked as if she were alive. He stripped off her undergarments and had sexual intercourse with her. Afterward, he took her pearl and jade jewelry, wrapped it in his arms, and was about to run away. Suddenly, a thunderclap resounded in the air, and Wang San was shocked to death. The woman came back to life.

    598

    次早，管坟人送信于其弟家，迎归完娶。太守闻之，命斫王三骨而扬其灰。

    　　○铁匣壁虎云南昆明池旁农民掘地得铁匣，匣上符篆不可识，旁有楷书云“至正元年杨真人封”。农民不知何物，椎碎其匣，中有壁虎寸许，蠕蠕然似死非死。童子以水沃之，顷刻，寸许者渐伸渐长，鳞甲怒生，腾空而去。暴风烈雨，天地昏黑，见一角黑蛟与两黄龙空中攫斗，冰雹齐下，所损田禾民屋无算。

    　　○图公为神乾隆己丑，两淮盐院图公思阿到任，清操卓然，每日用三百文。遇商人和平坦易，慈爱谆谆，人以为百馀年来无此好盐政也。年七十三殁。前三日，遍召幕客戚友曰：“吾将归去，君等助我摒挡鹾务，以便交代后人。”众咸疑之，以为谰语。公笑曰：“吾岂斯人者哉！”临期，自草遗本毕，沐浴冠带，趺坐而逝。

    Explanation

    598

    The next morning, the caretaker of the grave sent a message to his brother's house, and they returned to complete the wedding. The prefect, upon hearing of this, ordered the chopping of Wang San's bones and the scattering of his ashes.

    A Ge

    599

    三七之期，群商往哭，其妾某夫人遣人问曰：“诸位老爷可知道天下有思州府否？”曰：“有，此州在广西省。未知夫人何故问之？”曰：“妾昨夜梦老爷托梦云：”我将往思州府作城隍，上帝所命。‘“于是众商哗然，知图公果为神，又不知何缘宦此远方也。

    　　○随园琐记余姨母王氏得疾将死，忽转身向里卧，笑吃吃不止。其女问之，曰：“我闻袁家甥将补廪，故喜。”时余犹附生也。姨卒之次年，竟以岁试第三补廪。

    　　先君子亡时，侍者朱氏亦病，呼曰：“我去！我去！太爷在屋瓦上唤我。”

    　　时先君虽卒，而朱氏病危，家人虑其哀伤，并未告知，俄而亦死。方信古人升屋复魂之说，非无因也。

    Explanation

    599

    After the period of mourning, the merchants went to pay their respects. One of the deceased's concubines, a certain Lady Wang, sent someone to ask, "Do the gentlemen know if there is a Sizhou prefecture in the world?" They replied, "Yes, this prefecture is in Guangxi Province. But why does Lady Wang ask such a question?" She said, "Last night, I had a dream where my husband appeared to me and said, 'I will be appointed as the city god of Sizhou, as commanded by God.'" Upon hearing this, the merchants were startled and realized that the deceased was indeed a deity, but they wondered why he would be appointed to such a distant place.

    In the "Suiyuan Miscellaneous Notes," it is recorded that my aunt, Lady Wang, fell ill and was near death. Suddenly, she turned to lie facing inward and laughed continuously. Her daughter asked her why she was laughing. She replied, "I heard that my cousin from the Yuan family will be promoted to a廪生, so I am happy." At the time, I was still a candidate for the imperial examinations. The following year, after my aunt's death, my cousin was actually promoted to a廪生 in the third position in the annual examination.

    When my father passed away, his servant Zhu also fell ill. She called out, "I'm going! I'm going! My father is calling me from the roof tiles." At that time, my father had already died, and the family was concerned about her grief, so they did not inform her. Soon after, she also died. This confirmed the ancient belief that spirits can return to the house, which is not without reason.

    600

    阍人朱明死矣，复苏，张目伸手索纸钱曰：“我有应酬之用。”为烧之，自始瞑。

    　　甲戌秋，余病危，见白面小僮戴缨帽跪床下，持一单幅，上书“家政条条，人口寥寥”八字。余念此鬼戏我也，我亦戏之。是午饮胡椒汤，胸次稍宽，乃口号续云：“可怜小鬼，只怕胡椒。”僮一笑去矣。当热重时，觉床中有六七人纵横杂卧，或我不欲呻吟而被教之，或我欲静卧而彼摇之。热减，则人渐少，热减尽，仍然一我而已。方信三魂六魄之说，亦属有之。

    　　至于梦兆，有不可解者。余祖旦釜公好道术，梦至一山顶，有八人饮酒，如俗所画八仙状貌。余祖至，群仙不起。余祖戏曰：“八个仙人，十五只脚。”李跛大怒，持杖将击。群仙呼曰：“速谢罪！”拉余祖跪谢，而杖已至腰，曰：“与汝三年。”惊醒后，腰上凸起如鸡卵，群医罔效，溃裂三年，竟卒。余戏谓：“跛奴与我家不共戴天。”每见跛像，必痛詈之，亦复不能作祟。

    Explanation

    600

    The gatekeeper Zhu Ming died, but revived, opened his eyes, stretched out his hands, and asked for paper money, saying, "I have a need for it." I burned it for him, and he then closed his eyes.

    In the autumn of Jia Xu, I was gravely ill and saw a white-faced young boy wearing a hat with tassels kneeling at the foot of my bed, holding a single scroll with the words "household affairs, sparse population" written on it. I thought this ghost was playing a trick on me, so I played along as well. That noon, I drank pepper soup, and my chest felt a bit more relaxed. I then improvised a verse and continued, "Poor little ghost, you're afraid of pepper." The boy smiled and left. When the fever was at its worst, I felt there were six or seven people lying in all directions on the bed, some teaching me to stop groaning when I didn't want to, and some shaking me when I wanted to lie quietly. As the fever subsided, the number of people decreased, and when the fever was completely gone, I was left alone. Only then did I believe in the saying of three souls and six spirits, which also seemed to have some truth to it.

    As for dream omens, there are some that are inexplicable. My ancestor, Dan Fu Gong, was fond of Daoist practices, and in a dream, he reached the top of a mountain where eight people were drinking, resembling the Eight Immortals as depicted in popular art. When my ancestor arrived, the immortals did not rise to greet him. My ancestor joked, "Eight immortals, fifteen feet." Li Pao, one of the immortals, became very angry and was about to strike with his staff. The other immortals shouted, "Hurry and apologize!" They pulled my ancestor to his knees to apologize, but the staff had already hit him on the waist, saying, "Give you three years." After waking up, I had a lump on my waist as big as an egg. All the doctors were ineffective, and it ruptured and split for three years before I finally died. I joked, "The lame servant and my family are enemies." Every time I saw a depiction of a lame person, I would curse it fiercely, and it also could not cause any harm.

    601

    姊夫王贡南祈梦于少保坟，梦一僧，状狞恶，持棍追击。贡南狂奔，见前面群僧数十，围坐草上。贡南求救，众僧拉贡南入草中，而四围膜手向外。追僧至，索贡南不得，喝曰：“无情种子，留他作甚？大众闪开，领吾一棍。”贡南惊醒，至今无验。

    　　余幼时，梦束数百万笔为大桴，身坐其上浮于江，亦至今无验。又立春日，梦关帝绿袍长须立空中，以左手擒我，右手持雷，从脐击入，如烈火钻灼。痛醒，腹犹热也。或以为关帝戊午生，余亦戊午得科之故，终属强解。

    　　壬子乡试，将赴科考，是日五更，梦遇门斗李念先于路，摇手曰：“勿去，勿去。相公科考不取，遗才不取，须大收方取耳。”是时科考，遗才最宽，余自问必不至此，后一如其言。因念补廪录科，事甚小而机先动，及后登进士，入词林，改县令，杳无预兆，何也？

    Explanation

    601

    Wang Gongnan, my brother-in-law, prayed for a dream at the tomb of a former chancellor. In his dream, he saw a monk who appeared fierce and ugly, carrying a stick to chase and attack him. Wang Gongnan fled wildly and saw in front of him dozens of monks sitting in a circle on the grass. He begged for help, and the monks pulled him into the grass, with their hands outstretched outward. When the chasing monk arrived, he could not find Wang Gongnan, and shouted, "Heartless seed, what use is he? Everyone step aside, let me hit him with a stick." Wang Gongnan awoke in fear, and to this day, the dream has not been verified.

    When I was young, I dreamed of tying together millions of brushes to form a large log, on which I sat floating on a river, and this dream also has not been verified. On the day of the Start of Spring, I dreamed of Guan Yu, standing in the air with a green robe and long beard. He grabbed me with his left hand and held a thunderbolt in his right hand, striking it into my navel like a burning fire drilling into me. I woke up in pain, and my abdomen was still warm. Some believe that since Guan Yu was born in the year of Wu Wu, and I also passed the imperial examination in the year of Wu Wu, this is ultimately a forced interpretation.

    In the year Renzi, during the provincial examination, I was about to take the imperial examination. On that early morning, I dreamed of meeting Li Nianxian, a low-ranking official, on the road. He shook his hand and said, "Do not go, do not go. You will not pass the imperial examination, nor will you be selected as a reserve candidate; you must wait for the major recruitment to be selected." At that time, the selection of reserve candidates was very broad, and I thought I would not reach this point, but later, as he said, it happened. Reflecting on being selected as a reserve candidate and entering the official school, it was a very small matter, but the sign came first. Later, I passed the imperial examination, entered the imperial academy, and became a county magistrate, with no prior signs. Why is this so?

    602

    ○广西鬼师广西信奉鬼师，有陈、赖二姓，能捉生替死，病家多延之。至则先取杯水覆以纸，倒悬病者床上，翌日来视，其水周时不滴者，云可救。或取雄鸡一只，贯白刃七八寸入鸡喉，提向病人身，运气诵咒。咒毕，鸡口不滴血者，亦云可救。

    　　拔刃掷地，鸡飞如故。若滴下点水及鸡血者，辞去勿救。其可救者，设一坛，挂神鬼像数十幅，鬼师作妇人妆，步罡持咒，锣鼓齐作。至夜，染油纸作灯，至野外呼魂，其声幽渺。邻人有熟睡者，魂即应声来。鬼师递火与之，接去后，鬼师向病家称贺，则病者愈，而来接火之人死矣。解之之术，但夜闻锣鼓声，以两脚踏土上，便无所妨。陈、赖二家以此致富，其堂宇层层阴黑，供鬼神像甚多。

    Explanation

    602

    In Guangxi, there is a belief in ghost masters, with the Chen and Lai families being particularly renowned. These ghost masters are said to have the ability to "catch life and replace death," and many sick families invite them to help. Upon arrival, they first pour water into a cup, cover it with paper, and hang it over the sick person's bed. The next day, if the water has not dripped, they claim the patient can be saved. Alternatively, they may take a rooster, insert a blade several inches into its throat, and hold it near the patient while chanting incantations. If the rooster does not bleed after the incantation, they also claim the patient can be saved.

    If the blade is removed and the rooster flies away as if nothing happened, but if the water or blood drips, the ghost master will leave without attempting to save the patient. For those who can be saved, an altar is set up, and dozens of images of gods and ghosts are hung. The ghost master dresses like a woman, walks in ritual patterns, and chants incantations while drums and gongs are played. At night, they dye paper with oil to make lamps and go to the wilderness to call the soul, producing an eerie sound. If a neighbor is asleep, their soul will respond and come. The ghost master then passes a flame to the person, and after they receive it, the ghost master congratulates the family, and the patient recovers, but the person who received the flame dies. To avoid this, one should stand on the ground and stomp when hearing drum and gong sounds at night. The Chen and Lai families have become wealthy through these practices, and their dwellings are dimly lit and shadowy, with many images of ghosts and gods on display.

    603

    余婶母患病，呼赖鬼师视之。赖持剑捕鬼，房中有物，如大蝙蝠，投入床下。

    　　赖用掌心雷击之，火倒出烧赖须。赖大怒，令煎一锅桐油，书符烧之。以手搅锅中油，闻床下鬼啾啾求饶，久之而绝，婶病果愈。

    　　一日者，陈鬼师为某家呼魂，见蓝衣女冉冉来。逼视之，即其所生女来接火。

    　　陈大惊，掷火于地，以掌击其背。急归视女，女方睡惊觉，云：“梦中闻爷呼，故来。”所衣蓝布衫上，手掌油迹宛然。

    　　桂林魏太守女病危，夫人延陈鬼师视之，陈索百金为谢。太守素方严，拘而杖之，将置之狱。鬼师笑曰：“杖我毋后悔。”方杖鬼师，女忽于床上呼曰：“陈鬼师命二鬼杖我臀，拉我入狱！”夫人大恐，力劝放之，许以重谢，陈曰：“业为祟鬼所惊，吾力不能。”女竟死。

    Explanation

    603

    My Aunt was ill, and we summoned the ghost exorcist, Lai, to examine her. Lai held a sword to catch the ghost, and there was an object in the room that looked like a large bat, which flung itself under the bed.

    Lai struck it with palm thunder, and the fire spouted out, burning his beard. Lai became very angry and ordered the boiling of a pot of tung oil. He wrote a talisman and burned it. As he stirred the oil in the pot with his hand, he heard the ghost under the bed chirping pitifully for mercy. After a while, it died, and my Aunt's illness indeed recovered.

    One day, Chen, the ghost exorcist, was summoned by a certain family to summon the soul. He saw a woman in blue robes coming slowly. Upon closer inspection, it was his own daughter who had come to receive the fire.

    Chen was very surprised and threw the fire to the ground, striking her back with his palm. He rushed home to see his daughter, who was sleeping and had been startled awake. She said, "I heard Father calling in my dream, so I came." On her blue robe, the palm oil marks were still clearly visible.

    The daughter of the governor of Guilin was critically ill. The governor's wife invited Chen, the ghost exorcist, to come and look. Chen demanded a hundred taels of gold as payment. The governor, who was always upright and strict, detained and beat him with a stick, intending to put him in jail. The ghost exorcist laughed and said, "Beat me, don't regret it." As he was in the process of beating the ghost exorcist, the daughter suddenly called out from the bed, "Chen, the ghost exorcist, ordered two ghosts to beat my buttocks and drag me into prison!" The wife was greatly frightened and strongly urged him to be released, promising a generous reward. Chen said, "I have already been frightened by the evil spirits. I have no power to change it." The daughter ultimately died.

    604

    ○马家坟伊都拉，年二十一，入直羽林。假日，猎芦沟桥之西，见群雀飞入林际，因驰马纵鹰攫之。雀惊散，少年将往收鹰，见深林内有人臂鹰而立，以右手刷其羽毛。谛视之，自手至足，皆枯骨也。骇而奔告诸仆从，弹以鸟枪，枯骨人不见。

    　　伊收鹰。行里许，望见高楼大厦，以为贵人庄院，各下马。见老妇人冉冉来，戴大髻，衣杏黄袍，锦靴素袜，婢数人，向伊呼曰：“汝非某家郎乎？余为汝中表姑。既至此，何不过我？”伊趋前问起居曰：“某以当差内府，不识大人居址，请往候安。”老妇先行，招诸仆从曰：“汝辈俱来少息。”入等，堂宇深邃，老妇趺坐榻上，与语近事，甚悉。呼其女出见，曰：“汝妹也，年十八矣。”伊见其貌美，心为之动。老妇曰：“郎君远猎，得毋渴乎？”食以瓜，大倍于常，并赐诸从者，皆叩头谢出。侍者引至左旁，与女子坐语良久。

    Explanation

    604

    A young man from Ma Family Tomb, named Yidula, aged twenty-one, served in the elite military unit of the imperial guard. On a day off, he went hunting west of Lugou Bridge and saw a group of sparrows flying into the forest. He then galloped on horseback and released his hawk to catch the sparrows. The sparrows were startled and scattered, and the young man, intending to retrieve his hawk, saw a person standing in the deep forest with a hawk on their arm, brushing the hawk's feathers with their right hand. Upon closer inspection, from hand to foot, the person was all dry bones. Terrified, he ran to tell his servants, who shot at the skeleton with a bird gun, but the skeleton person disappeared.

    The young man retrieved his hawk. After walking about a mile, he saw tall buildings and assumed they belonged to the mansion of a noble person. They all dismounted from their horses. An elderly woman came slowly, wearing a large bun, a yellow robe, brocade boots, and white socks, accompanied by several maids. She called out to the young man, saying, "Are you not the young man from a certain family? I am your cousin aunt. Since you are here, why not visit me?" The young man hurried forward and asked about her health, saying, "I serve in the imperial household and do not know the address of the noble person. Please allow me to visit to pay respects." The old woman led the way and called the servants, saying, "You all come to rest for a moment." They entered the courtyard, where the hall was deep and spacious. The old woman sat cross-legged on the couch and talked about recent events, which she knew very well. She called her daughter to come out and meet, saying, "She is your sister, and she is eighteen years old." The young man was moved by her beautiful appearance. The old woman said, "Young man, you have been far hunting, are you not thirsty?" She offered him melons, which were much larger than usual, and also gave them to the servants, who all bowed and thanked before leaving. An attendant led the young man to the left side, where he sat and talked with the girl for a long time.

    605

    俄而，一靴服丈夫冠珊瑚顶孔雀翎昂然自外入，少年起，执手问讯。坐定，丈夫曰：“顷于树林内得鹰绝佳，甚爱之，忽有何人放火枪，几为所中，鹰逸去，可惜！”伊闻之，始悟为鬼，默不敢语。因诡请如厕，出门上马而驰，仆从六七人，各色若死灰。行数十步，回望之，松楸宿草而已。询之士人，曰：“此马家坟也。昔有马将军者，以阵亡，暨其夫人并一女同葬于此。”

    Explanation

    605

    Soon after, a dignified figure entered, wearing a hat adorned with coral and peacock feathers, dressed in boots and robes. The young man stood up and bowed slightly in greeting. Once seated, the dignified figure said, "Just now, I found an eagle in the woods that was absolutely superb, and I cherished it greatly. Suddenly, someone fired a gun, nearly hitting it, and the eagle flew away, what a pity!" Upon hearing this, the young man finally realized that the figure was a ghost, and he remained silent, afraid to speak. He then deceptively asked to use the toilet, went out, mounted his horse, and galloped away. Six or seven servants followed, each with a pallid, lifeless appearance. After riding for several dozen steps, he looked back and saw only ancient tombstones and overgrown grass. He asked a scholar, who replied, "This is the Ma family tomb. Long ago, there was a General Ma who died in battle, and his wife and daughter were buried here together."

    606

    ○天厨星曹能始先生饮馔极精，厨人董桃媚尤善烹调。曹宴客，非董侍则满座为之不欢。曹同年某督学蜀中，乏作馔者，乞董偕行。曹许之，遣董。董不往，曹怒逐之。董跪而言曰：“桃媚，天厨星也，因公本仙官，故来奉侍。督学凡人，岂能享天厨之福乎？尔来公禄将尽，某亦行矣。”言毕，升空向去，良久影逝。不逾年，曹竟不禄。

    　　○梦中联句曹少时过太平书坊，得《椒山集》归。夜阅之，倦，掩卷卧。闻叩门声，启视，则同学迟友山也。携手登台，仰见明月，友山赋诗云：“冉冉乘风一望迷。”

    　　曹云：“中天烟雨夕阳低。来时衣服多成雪。”迟云：“去后皮毛尽属泥。但见白云侵冷月。”曹云：“何曾黄鸟隔花啼。”迟云：“行行不是人间象。”曹云：“手挽蛟龙作杖藜。”吟罢，友山别去。学士归语其妻，妻不答；转呼仆，仆亦不应。复坐北窗，取《椒山集》掀数页，回顾已身卧竹床上，大惊，始知梦也。

    Explanation

    606

    Mr. Cao Nengshi, the Celestial Kitchen Star, was renowned for his exquisite culinary skills, and his cook, Dong Taomei, was particularly adept at cooking. Whenever Cao hosted guests, if Dong was not serving, the entire gathering would be unhappy. One of Cao's contemporaries, who was the governor of Sichuan, lacked a competent cook and asked Dong to accompany him. Cao agreed and sent Dong. However, Dong refused to go, and Cao angrily dismissed him. Dong knelt down and said, "Taomei is the Celestial Kitchen Star. Because you are originally a celestial official, I came to serve you. The governor is just an ordinary person, how could he enjoy the blessings of the Celestial Kitchen? Your good fortune will soon end, and I will also leave." After speaking, he ascended into the sky and departed, and after a long while, his shadow disappeared. Within a year, Cao died.

    When Cao was young, he passed by a bookstore in Taiping and obtained the Jiashan Collection before returning home. That night, he read it, grew tired, and put the book aside to sleep. He heard a knock at the door and, upon opening it, saw his fellow student, Youshan. They walked together to a platform, looked up at the bright moon, and Youshan recited a poem: "Gradually riding the wind, one view is lost."

    Cao replied, "Smoke and rain in the middle sky, the sunset is low. When coming, the clothes are mostly turned to snow." Youshan continued, "After leaving, the skin and fur are all turned to mud. Only see white clouds invade the cold moon." Cao added, "How often have yellow birds been heard singing through the flowers?" Youshan responded, "Step by step, it is not a human image." Cao then said, "Hand-wrapping a dragon as a walking stick." After finishing the poem, Youshan left. The scholar returned home and told his wife, but she did not answer. He then called the servant, but the servant also did not respond. He sat again at the north window and picked up the Jiashan Collection, flipping through several pages. Looking back, he found himself lying on a bamboo bed. He was greatly surprised and realized it was a dream.

    607

    惊醒，起视《椒山集》，宛然掀数页，而次日友山讣至。

    　　○碧眼见鬼河南巡抚胡公宝泉，眼碧色，自幼能见鬼物。九岁，犹不言，尚记前生事。

    　　能言后，不复记矣。自言人间街衢堂怀，在在有鬼，惟朝廷午门内无人，菜市口刑人处，鬼尤丛集。遇人气盛，避之而行；衰弱，则摩肩而过。或有所揶揄者，其人必病。午前犹不甚出，午后道路纷纷。然其举止，率皆卑琐龌龊，无昂伟正大者。

    　　公一生不肯入庙，神佛见之，往往起立。尝述所经历者：尊莫尊于东岳大帝，卤簿繁盛；奇莫奇于金将军，遍体金色，毛孔闪闪，生万道金光；丑莫丑于狭面神，身长三尺，面长四尺，阔止五六寸，令人对之欲呕。他如如来、仙子、关公、蒋侯，皆未之见也。

    Explanation

    607

    Startled awake, I rose to look at The Collection of Pepper Mountain, still finding several pages flipped, and the next day, news of Friend Mountain's death arrived.

    ○ Mr. Hu, the governor of Henan, with blue eyes, could see ghostly things since childhood. At nine years old, he still did not speak but still remembered past-life events.

    After he could speak, he no longer remembered. He once described his experiences: in the streets and alleys of the human world, there are ghosts everywhere, except within the noon gates of the court, where no one is present. At the execution ground of the vegetable market, ghosts are especially numerous. When encountering people with strong energy, they avoid them, but when encountering weak people, they pass by shoulder to shoulder. If someone is mocked by them, that person will surely become ill. Before noon, they are not very active, but after noon, the roads are crowded. However, their behavior is generally lowly, petty, and unclean, with none being noble, majestic, or upright.

    Throughout his life, he refused to enter temples; gods and Buddhas often stood up upon seeing him. He once described his experiences: none were more revered than the God of Mount Tai, with a splendid entourage; none were more strange than the Golden General, whose body was entirely golden, with pores that sparkled and emitted countless rays of golden light; none were more ugly than the Narrow-Faced God, who was three feet tall, with a four-foot face that was only five or six inches wide, making one feel nauseous upon seeing him. As for the Buddha, fairies, Guan Yu, and Jiang Ziya, he had never seen them.

    608

    幼时过土地祠，旁塑牛头鬼，公践其角。鬼随归家，以角抵公卧床，震撼不已。随患疟，牛压其胸，太夫人祭之方去。人问：“胡公官贵，何神佛见之尚起立，而牛头贱鬼乃敢揶揄之耶？”余答之曰：“惟是神是佛，正直聪明，故知其为贵人正人而敬之。牛则无知也，何敬之有？”

    　　公抚河南时，朔日行香，未至庙，忽低头持扇遮面。司道迎接打恭，岸然不答。公素谦，一旦改常，司道大疑。越一日，乘间问曰：“公某日行香如有意拒绝我等者，得毋有所开罪乎？”公曰：“非也。前日见庙前有天蓬神两位被河神锁系，求我说情。我若允许，则彼原有罪；如不允，则天蓬神缠扰不清，故佯为不见而过之耳。”

    608

    When I was young, I passed by a land temple where a cow-headed ghost was sculpted beside it. I stepped on its horn. The ghost followed me home and used its horn to push against my sleeping bed, shaking it continuously. I then suffered from malaria, as if a cow was pressing on my chest. My mother-in-law offered a sacrifice to it, and it finally left. Someone asked, "Why does Mr. Hu hold such a high position? Even gods and Buddhas rise in respect when they see him, but why does a lowly cow-headed ghost dare to mock him?" I replied, "Only gods and Buddhas are upright and intelligent, so they know he is a noble and upright person and respect him. A cow is unaware; how can there be respect for it?"

    When Mr. Hu was governing Henan, on the first day of the month, he went to offer incense. Before reaching the temple, he suddenly lowered his head and held his fan in front of his face. The local officials greeted him with bows, but he remained aloof and did not respond. Mr. Hu was always humble, but suddenly changed his usual behavior, which greatly puzzled the officials. The next day, taking the opportunity, they asked, "Mr. Hu, on the day you went to offer incense, did you seem to intentionally reject us? Could it be that we have offended you?" Mr. Hu said, "No, it is not that. The other day, I saw two Heavenly Marshal deities in front of the temple, being bound by the River God. They asked me to intercede. If I allowed it, then they would have committed a sin; if I did not allow it, then the Heavenly Marshal deities would continue to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" - "to纠缠不清" -

    609

    ○龙母常熟李氏妇，孕十四月，产一肉团，盘曲九折，莹若水晶。惧，弃之河，化为小龙，擘空而去。逾年，李妇卒，方殓，雷雨晦冥，龙来哀号，声若牛吼。里人奇之，为立庙虞山，号“龙母庙”。乾隆壬午夏，大旱，牲玉既罄，卒无灵，桂林中丞以为大戚，其门下士薛一瓢曰：“何不登堂拜母乎”牲牢祷龙母庙，翌日雨降。

    　　○清凉老人五台山僧，号清凉老人，以禅理受知鄂相国。雍正四年，老人卒。西藏产一儿，八岁不言。一日剃发，呼曰：“我清凉老人也，速为我通知鄂相国。”乃召小儿入。所应对，皆老人前世事，无舛。指待者仆御，能呼其名，相识如旧。鄂公故欲试之，赐以老人念珠，小儿手握珠叩头曰：“不敢，此僧奴前世所献相国物也。”鄂公异之，命往五台山坐方丈。

    Explanation

    609

    The Dragon Mother was a woman from the Li family in Changshu who, after being pregnant for fourteen months, gave birth to a mass of meat that was coiled and folded nine times, shining like crystal. Fearing this, she threw it into the river, where it transformed into a small dragon and soared into the sky. A year later, the woman from the Li family died, and when they were preparing her for burial, a thunderstorm and darkness ensued, and the dragon came to mourn, its sound like a bull's roar. The locals found this strange and built a temple on Mount Yu, naming it the Dragon Mother Temple. During the summer of the year Renwu in the Qianlong era, a severe drought occurred, and after exhausting sacrificial animals and jade, there was still no response. The Governor of Guilin considered it a great disaster, and his subordinate, Xue Yipiao, suggested, "Why not go to the temple and worship the mother?" Sacrifices were offered at the Dragon Mother Temple, and the next day, rain fell.

    The Cool-Breeze Elder was a monk from Wutai Mountain, known as the Cool-Breeze Elder, who was known for his understanding of Zen principles by the Duke of E. In the fourth year of Yongzheng, the elder passed away. In Tibet, a child was born who could not speak until he was eight years old. One day, he shaved his head and called out, "I am the Cool-Breeze Elder; quickly inform the Duke of E of this." The Duke summoned the child, and everything the child said pertained to the elder's past life, without any error. He could name the servants and attendants present, recognizing them as if they were old acquaintances. The Duke wanted to test him, giving him the elder's prayer beads, and the child, holding the beads and bowing, said, "I dare not; these were given to the Duke by the elder's servant in a previous life." The Duke was amazed and ordered the child to go to Wutai Mountain to take up residence as the abbot.

    610

    将至河间，书一纸与河间人袁某，道别绪甚款。袁，故老人所善，大惊，即骑老人所赠黑马来迎。小儿中道望见，下车直前抱袁腰白：“别八年矣，犹相识否？”又摩马鬣笑曰：“汝亦无恙乎！”马为悲嘶不止。是时，道旁观者万人，皆呼生佛，罗拜。

    　　小儿渐长大，纤妍如美女。过琉璃厂，见画店鬻男女交媾状者，大喜，谛玩不已。归过柏乡，召妓与狎。到五台山，遍召山下淫妪与少年貌美阴巨者终目淫，亲临观之，犹以为不足；更取香火钱往苏州聘伶人歌舞，被人劾奏。疏章未上，老人已知，叹曰：“无曲躬树而生色界天，误矣！”即端坐趺跏而逝，年二十四。

    Explanation

    610

    As he was about to arrive at Hejian, he wrote a letter to Yuan, a man from Hejian, expressing a very heartfelt farewell. Yuan, an old man who was a good friend of his, was greatly surprised and immediately rode the black horse given to him by the old man to welcome him. The child saw him halfway along the road, got off the carriage and ran forward, embracing Yuan's waist and said, "It has been eight years since we parted, do you still recognize me?" He then ran his hand along the horse's mane and said with a smile, "Are you also well?" The horse neighed mournfully and would not stop. At that time, ten thousand people on the roadside all called him a living Buddha and worshiped in a circle.

    The child gradually grew up, becoming as slender and beautiful as a young woman. Passing through the Lufu factory, he saw a shop selling paintings of men and women engaged in sexual intercourse and was delighted, examining it closely and would not stop. Returning through Baixiang, he called a prostitute and engaged in intimate relations. Reaching Wutai Mountain, he called all the promiscuous women and handsome young men with large genitalia in the area to engage in sexual activity all day, personally witnessing it, still thinking it was insufficient; then took money from the temple and went to Suzhou to hire actors and musicians for performances, was accused and reported. The petition had not yet been submitted, but the old man already knew and sighed, "It is a mistake to bend the body and grow in the realm of desire!" He then sat cross-legged and passed away, at the age of twenty-four.

    611

    吾友李竹溪与其前世有旧，往访之。见老人方作女子妆，红肚袜，裸下体，使一男子淫己，而己又淫一女，其旁鱼贯连环而淫者无数。李大怒，骂曰：“活佛当如是乎！”老人夷然应声作偈曰：“男欢女爱，无遮无碍。一点生机，成此世界。俗士无知，大惊小怪。”

    　　○徐崖客湖州徐崖客者，孽子也，其父惑继母言，欲置之死。崖客逃，云游四方，凡名山大川，深岩绝涧，必攀援而上，以为本当死之人，无所畏。

    　　登雁荡山，不得上，晚无投宿处，旁一僧目之曰：“子好游乎？”崖客曰：“然。”僧曰：“吾少时亦有此癖，遇异人授一皮囊，夜寝其中，风雨虎豹蛇虺俱不能害。又与缠足布一匹，长五丈，或山过高，投以布，便攀援而上。即或倾跌，但手不释布，紧握之，坠亦无伤。以此游遍海内。今老矣，倦鸟知还，请以二物赠公。”徐拜谢别去。嗣后，登高临深，颇得如意。

    Explanation

    611

    My friend Li Zhuxi has a connection with his previous life, and I went to visit him. I saw an old man putting on women's makeup, wearing red gartered stockings, and exposing his lower body. He made a man have sex with him, and he himself had sex with a woman. There were countless people having sex in a line or a chain around them. Li became very angry and shouted, "Should a living Buddha act like this?" The old man calmly responded with a verse: "Male pleasure and female love, without obstruction or hindrance. A single spark of life creates this world. Common people are ignorant and make a big fuss about nothing."

    ○ Xu Yake, a wanderer from Huzhou, was a bastard son. His father was misled by his stepmother and wanted to kill him. Xu Yake fled and wandered everywhere, climbing all famous mountains and rivers, deep caves, and steep ravines. Thinking he was someone who should have died, he had nothing to fear.

    He tried to climb Yandang Mountain but couldn't, and he had no place to stay at night. A nearby monk looked at him and said, "Do you like to travel?" Xu Yake said, "Yes." The monk said, "When I was young, I also had this habit. I met a strange person who gave me a leather bag. At night, I slept inside it, and neither wind, rain, tigers, leopards, snakes, nor other venomous creatures could harm me. He also gave me a piece of cloth for binding feet, five zhang long. If a mountain was too high, I threw the cloth and climbed up. Even if I fell, as long as I didn't let go of the cloth and held it tightly, I wouldn't be injured. With these, I traveled all over the country. Now I am old, and like a tired bird, I know it's time to return. Please accept these two items as a gift." Xu bowed and thanked him before leaving. Afterward, when climbing high mountains and deep valleys, he found it quite easy.

    612

    入滇南，出青蛉河外千余里，迷道，砂砾渺茫，投囊野宿。月下闻有人溲于皮囊上者，声如潮涌。偷目之，则大毛人，方目钩鼻，两牙出颈外数尺，长倍数人。又闻沙上兽蹄杂沓，如万群獐兔被逐狂奔者。俄而，大风自西南起，腥不可耐，乃蟒蛇从空中过，驱群兽而行，长数十丈，头若车轮。徐惕息噤声而伏，天明出囊，见蛇过处两旁草木皆焦，己独无恙。饥无乞食处，望前村有若烟起者，奔往，见二毛人并坐，旁置镬，芋甚香。徐疑即月下遗溲者，跪而再拜，毛人不知；哀乞救饥，亦不知；然色态甚和，睨徐而笑。徐乃以手指口，又指其腹，毛人笑愈甚，哑哑有声，响震林谷，若解意者，赐以二芋，。徐得果腹，留半芋，归视诸人，乃白石也。

    Explanation

    612

    Entering the southern region of Yunnan, Xu traveled more than a thousand miles beyond the Qingling River. He lost his way, finding himself in a desolate and barren landscape covered with sand and gravel. Desperate, he sought shelter in the wild by hiding in a bag. At night, he heard someone urinating on the skin bag, and the sound was like a tidal wave. Peeping at it, he saw large, hairy creatures with square eyes and hooked noses, two teeth protruding several feet from their necks, taller than several people. He also heard the hooves of beasts on the sand, like thousands of deer and rabbits being chased and running wildly. Soon, a strong wind arose from the southwest, and the smell was unbearable. It turned out to be a python passing through the air, driving the herd of beasts. The python was several dozen zhang long, with a head like a cartwheel. Xu held his breath, remained silent, and lay down. At dawn, he emerged from the bag and saw that the grass and trees on both sides of the python's path were scorched, but only he was unharmed. Hungry and with nowhere to beg for food, he saw a village ahead where smoke was rising and ran towards it. He saw two hairy people sitting together, with a pot nearby, steaming yams smelled very fragrant. Xu suspected they were the ones who had urinated on the skin bag at night, kneeled and bowed twice, but the hairy people did not understand. He appealed for help to save him from hunger, but they also did not understand. However, their demeanor was very gentle, and they glanced at Xu and laughed. Xu pointed to his mouth with his finger, then pointed to his stomach, and the hairy people laughed even more, making a sound like "ya ya," which echoed through the forest and valley, as if they understood. They gave him two yams, and Xu was able to eat enough. He left half a yam, and when he returned and looked at the yams, they turned out to be white stones.

    613

    徐游遍四海，仍归湖州。尝告人曰：“天地之性人为贵。凡荒莽幽绝之所，人不到者，鬼神怪物亦不到。有鬼神怪物处，便有人矣。”

    　　○虎衔文昌头陕西兴安州民某六月娶妻，天大暑，路远，新妇以红巾裹首，不胜闷热，暴死车中。其父母悲甚，买棺殓之。，不便仍舁至家，乃厝之城外古庙后。棺不甚坚厚，会大雨，凉气浸入棺中，女复活，哼咛有声。庙中僧师徒二人闻而视之，启其棺，嫣然美妇也。扶起，以汤药灌苏，抱女入寺。其徒思独占此女，嘱师买酒，饮半醉，持斧斫杀之，即以女棺盛共师尸置庙后，而负女逃居别村文昌祠，蓄发为伙居道士。

    　　逾年，夜，忽有虎跳入祠中，将所塑文昌帝君头衔去，而遗下乳虎三只。村邻喧传，争来看虎，女之父母亦至。突见其女，以为鬼也，抱哭良久。女不能隐，具陈始末，且告以占妻杀僧事。其父母控官，讯鞫得实，掘验僧尸，置其徒于法，女交父母领归。此事严侍读冬友从陕西归亲为予言。

    Explanation

    613

    Travelling leisurely around the four seas, he eventually returned to Huzhou. Once, he told others, "Among all things in heaven and earth, human beings are the most precious. In places that are desolate, wild, and secluded, where no one goes, ghosts, gods, and monsters do not go either. Where there are ghosts, gods, and monsters, there must be people."

    ○ A Tiger Takes the Head of Wen Chang Di Jun

    In June, a man from Xing'an Prefecture in Shaanxi Province married a wife. The weather was very hot, and the journey was long. The new bride wrapped her head with a red scarf but could not bear the heat and stuffiness, and she died suddenly in the carriage. Her parents were very sad and bought a coffin to prepare her body. It was inconvenient to carry her back home, so they placed her in a tomb behind an ancient temple outside the city. The coffin was not very sturdy or thick. When it rained heavily, cool air seeped into the coffin, and the woman revived, making soft sounds. Two monks, master and disciple, in the temple heard and saw her. They opened the coffin and saw a beautiful young woman. They helped her up and gave her medicine to revive her, then carried her into the temple. The disciple wanted to possess this woman alone and asked the master to buy wine. They drank until half-drunk, and the disciple took an axe and killed her. They immediately placed the master's body in the woman's coffin and put it behind the temple, and carried the woman to escape to another village and live in the Wen Chang Temple, growing their hair and living as a group of Daoist priests.

    After a year, at night, suddenly a tiger jumped into the temple and took the head of the statue of Wen Chang Di Jun, leaving behind three cubs. The villagers spread the news and gathered to see the tiger. The woman's parents also came. They suddenly saw their daughter and thought she was a ghost, hugging and crying for a long time. The woman could not hide and told the beginning and end of the story, and informed them of the matter of taking her as a wife and killing the monk. Her parents reported to the authorities, and after investigation, the truth was found. They dug up the monk's body for examination and punished the disciple according to the law. The woman was returned to her parents. This story was told to me by Yan Shidu Dongyou, who returned from Shaanxi to visit his family.

    614

    ○采战之报京师人杨某，习采战之术，能以铅条入阴窍而呼吸进退之，号曰“运剑。”

    　　一鼓气，则铅条触壁，铿然有声；或吸烧酒至半斤。妓妾受其毒淫者众矣。

    　　忽自悔非长生之道，乃广求丹灶良师。相传阜城门外白云观，元时为邱真人所建，每年正月十九日，必有真仙下降，烧香者毕集。杨往伺焉，见一美尼偕众烧香，衣褶能逆风而行，风吹不动，意必仙也，向前跪求。尼曰：“汝非杨某学道者乎？”曰：“然。”曰：“我道须择人而传，不能传汝俗子。”杨愈惊，再拜不已。尼引至无人之所，与丹粒二丸，曰：“二月望日，候我于某所。此二丹与汝，可先吞一丸，临期再吞一丸，便可传道。”杨如其言，归吞一粒，觉毛孔中作热，不复知寒，而淫欲之念，百倍平时，愈益求偶。坊妓避之，无敢与交者。

    Explanation

    614

    The Reward of Sexual Practices

    In Beijing, there was a man named Yang who practiced the art of "cai zhan" (sexual practices). He could insert a lead rod into his body and manipulate it through breathing techniques, which he called "yun jian" (wielding a sword).

    With one breath, the lead rod would touch the walls of his body, producing a clear sound; sometimes he would inhale half a pound of alcohol. Many prostitutes and concubines were harmed by his practices.

    Suddenly, he realized that this was not a path to longevity, so he began to widely seek out alchemists and good teachers. It was said that outside the Fucheng Gate, there was the White Cloud Temple, built by Master Qiu during the Yuan Dynasty. Every year on the 19th day of the first lunar month, true immortals were believed to descend, and many people gathered to burn incense. Yang went to wait there, and he saw a beautiful nun among the crowd burning incense. Her clothes moved against the wind, and the wind could not move them. He thought she must be an immortal and knelt before her, begging for guidance.

    The nun said, "Are you Yang, the one who practices these arts?" He replied, "Yes." She said, "My teachings must be passed on to the right person; I cannot teach you, an ordinary man." Yang was even more surprised and continued to bow repeatedly. The nun led him to a secluded place and gave him two pills of elixir, saying, "On the 15th day of the second lunar month, meet me at a certain place. These two pills are for you. Swallow one now, and swallow the other at the appointed time, and I will teach you the way."

    Yang followed her instructions and returned home to swallow one of the pills. He felt heat in his pores and no longer felt cold. His sexual desires increased a hundredfold compared to usual, and he became even more eager to find a partner. The prostitutes avoided him, and no one dared to consort with him.

    615

    至期，吞丹而往，尼果先在一静室，弛其下衣曰：“盗道无私，有翅不飞。

    　　汝亦知古人语乎？求传道者，先与我交。“杨大喜，且自恃采取之术，耸身而上。

    　　须臾，精溃不止，委顿于地。尼喝曰：“传道传道，恶报恶报。”大笑而去。五更苏醒，乃身卧破屋内，闻门外有买浆者，匍匐告以故。舁至家中，三日死矣。

    Explanation

    615

    By the appointed time, Yang swallowed the pill and went to the monk. The monk was indeed already in a quiet room, having loosened her lower garments and said, "The way of stealing is without bias; having wings, one does not fly. Do you also know the sayings of the ancients? One who seeks to learn the way must first associate with me." Yang was greatly pleased and, relying on his own technique of taking, ascended with a flourish. In a moment, he lost control of his essence and collapsed on the ground. The monk shouted, "Transmit the way, transmit the way; evil retribution, evil retribution." She laughed and left. Yang awoke at the fifth watch of the night, finding himself lying in a dilapidated house. He heard someone selling drinks outside and, crawling out, explained the situation. He was carried home and died after three days.

    616

    ○木皂隶京师宝泉局有土地祠，旁塑木皂隶四人，炉头铜匠，咸往祀焉。每夜，众匠宿局中，年少者梦中辄被人鸡奸，如魇寐然，心恶之而手足若有所缚，不能动，亦不能叫呼。旦起，摸谷道中，皆有青泥。如是月馀，群相揶揄，终不知何怪。

    　　后祀土地，见一隶貌如夜来奸淫人者，乃诉之官，取铁钉钉其足，嗣后怪绝。

    　　○王清本湖北巡抚陈公葬其父文肃公于祖茔，卜有日矣，其弟绳祖梦有持贴来拜者，上书“王清本”三字。入门，则十三人也，坐无一语。俄而，十二人辞去，独留一人告公曰：“此十二人皆河神也。”公惊醒。次日，到坟伐其树之碍路者，树文有“王清本”三字，数之，十二枝也，大骇，遂命停斧。其木今尚存于家。此事严侍读为余言，并云：“偶阅《五色线》说部，果载河神名王清本。”

    Explanation

    616

    Translation:

    In the capital city, at the Imperial Mint (宝泉局), there was a shrine to the land god (土地祠), beside which were four wooden statues of servants (木皂隶). The furnace-head copper craftsmen (炉头铜匠) all went to worship there. Every night, when the craftsmen stayed overnight at the mint, the younger ones would dream of being subjected to anal rape (鸡奸), as if in a nightmare (如魇寐然). They felt a deep aversion to it, and their hands and feet seemed to be bound, preventing them from moving or calling out. In the morning, when they woke up, they found green mud (青泥) in their anus. This continued for over a month, and they were often ridiculed by their peers, but they never knew what was causing the strange occurrences.

    Later, when they worshipped the land god, they saw one of the servants who resembled the person who had committed the acts of rape in their dreams. They reported this to the authorities, who then nailed an iron nail into the servant's foot. After this, the strange occurrences ceased.

    Another story involves Wang Qingben, a river god, who was mentioned in the book "Wu Se Xian" (五色线). The story goes that during the burial of Wen Su Gong (文肃公), the father of Governor Chen (陈公) of Hubei Province, in their ancestral tomb, Governor Chen's younger brother, Sheng Zu (绳祖), had a dream where someone with a note (持贴) came to pay respects (拜). The note bore the name "Wang Qingben." Upon entering, there were thirteen people, but none spoke. Soon after, twelve of them left, leaving one person who told Sheng Zu, "These twelve are all river gods." Sheng Zu woke up in shock. The next day, when he went to the tomb to cut down trees that were obstructing the path, he found the trees had the name "Wang Qingben" carved on them, and there were twelve branches. He was greatly alarmed and ordered the cutting to stop. The tree still exists in his family today. This story was told to me by Yan Shidu (严侍读), who also mentioned that he had read about it in the book "Wu Se Xian," which indeed records the name of the river god Wang Qingben.

    617

    ○女化男耒阳薛姓女名雪妹，许字黄姓子，嫁有日矣。忽病危，昏聩中有白须老人拊其身，至下体，女羞涩支拒，白须翁迫以物纳之而去。女大啼，父母惊视之，已转为男身矣，病亦霍然。邹令张锡组署耒阳篆，陶悔轩方伯以会审来，唤验之，果然，面貌声音，犹作女态，但肾囊微隙，宛然阴沟也。薛本二子，得此为三，改雪妹名为雪徕。

    　　○井泉童子苏州缪孝廉涣，余年家子也。其儿喜官，年十二，性顽劣，与群儿戏溲于井中。是夜得疾，呼为井泉童子所控，府城隍批责二十板。旦起视之，两臀青矣，疾小痊。越三日，复剧，又呼曰：“井泉童子嫌城隍神徇同乡情而罪大罚小，故又控于司路神，神云：”此儿污人食井，罪与蛊毒同科，应取其命。‘“是夕遂卒。问：”城隍何人？“曰：”周公范莲，庚戌翰林，苏州人，为河南某郡太守，正直慈祥。每杖人，不忍看，必以扇掩其面。“

    Explanation

    617

    ○ A Woman Transformed into a Man

    In Laiyang, there was a girl surnamed Xue named Xue Mei, who was engaged to a boy surnamed Huang. Her wedding was to take place soon. Suddenly, she fell gravely ill. In her delirium, she felt a white-bearded old man touching her body, reaching down to her lower regions. She blushed and resisted, but the old man forced something into her and then left. She cried out loudly, and her parents, alarmed, looked at her. To their astonishment, she had transformed into a man, and her illness disappeared suddenly. The magistrate of Laiyang, Zhang Xizuo, and the high-ranking official Tao Huixuan, who came for a joint review, summoned her for examination. Indeed, she appeared and sounded feminine, but there was a slight gap in her scrotum, resembling a male anatomy. The Xue family originally had two sons; now they had a third, and they changed Xue Mei's name to Xue Lai.

    ○ The Well-Water Child

    In Suzhou, there was a scholar-official named Miao Xiaolian, a friend of my family. His son, who was twelve years old, was fond of officialdom and had a mischievous nature. He played with other children and urinated in a well. That night, he fell ill and cried out that he was being accused by the well-water child. The city god of the prefecture ordered him to be beaten twenty times with a stick. In the morning, his parents saw that his buttocks were bruised, and his illness slightly improved. Three days later, his condition worsened again, and he cried out once more: "The well-water child complained to the road god because the city god was lenient due to their hometown connection and imposed a light punishment. The road god said: 'This boy has defiled the well water, and his crime is equivalent to poisoning; he should be put to death.'" That night, the boy died. When asked, "Who is the city god?" he replied, "It is Fan Lian, a scholar-official from the year of Geng Xu, a native of Suzhou, who served as the governor of a certain county in Henan. He is just and kind. Whenever he beats someone, he cannot bear to look at them and always covers his face with a fan."

    618

    ○射天箭苏州陶夔典之弟某，年十六，好仰空发矢，号曰“天箭”。忽一日射毕投弓大叫曰：“我太湖水神，朝天过此，被汝射伤我臀，罪当万死！”举家跪求，卒不能救，病一日而死。夔典为余曰：“弟诚顽劣，然以鬼神之灵而不能避儿童之箭，亦不可解。”

    　　○神秤张玉奇，武进县户房书吏也。解钱粮至苏州，过横林地方，白日仆地。越一日苏，自言被金甲人擒去，至大院落呼曰：“大师父，恶人来矣。”上坐青面獠牙者，云：“既是恶人，着即拘禁。”金甲人跪请曰：“玉奇有朝廷公事在身，未便羁留，且放还阳，候其事毕，再行审讯未迟。”青面者许之，张遂活。

    Explanation

    618

    Final Translation

    ○射天箭

    A certain younger brother of Tao Kui Dian from Suzhou, aged sixteen, enjoyed shooting arrows into the sky and called them "heavenly arrows." One day, after shooting, he threw down his bow and shouted, "I am the water god of Taihu Lake, passing by on my way to heaven, and you shot me in the buttocks! I deserve to die a thousand deaths!" His entire family knelt and begged for his life, but they could not save him. He fell ill and died the next day. Kui Dian told me, "My younger brother was indeed mischievous, but that a spirit of such power could not avoid an arrow from a child is inexplicable."

    ○神秤

    Zhang Yu Qi, a clerk in the household department of Wujin County, was delivering money and grain to Suzhou when he passed through Henglin. One day, he suddenly fell to the ground. After a day, he revived and said that he had been captured by a golden-armored figure, who brought him to a large courtyard and called out, "Master, an evil person has come!" Sitting on the throne was a figure with a green face and sharp teeth, who said, "Since he is an evil person, detain him immediately." The golden-armored figure knelt and pleaded, "Yu Qi has official duties in the court and cannot be detained. Please release him back to the living world, and we can conduct further questioning after his affairs are completed." The green-faced figure agreed, and Zhang Yu Qi was thus saved.

    619

    解粮至苏，掣批归，仍过横林，宿旅店中，梦金甲人又来，将玉奇引见大师父，即青面者。大师父判曰：“取玉奇生平功过簿来，称其轻重，再行治罪。”

    　　左右取一秤至，金星照耀，其权以紫金石为之。凡善事用红标签，恶事用黑标签，分投秤盘。顷刻间，红轻黑重矣，张战栗不已。俄而，有人取红签文书一卷投之，则秤盘中诸黑尽为所压，红签重不可量。青面者曰：“有此大功德，可放还阳，增寿一纪。”

    　　玉奇惊醒，以此语人。人问：“可认得是何文书？”曰：“我所承办，岂有不认！此常州刘藩司名某者抄家案也。”刘被抄时，所籍田产，佃户陈欠甚多，县令某欲按数比追。玉奇阳承奉其言，而夜中故意不戒于火，尽焚之，以此被杖，其事遂已。想压秤者，是此事也。“玉奇至今尚存。

    Explanation

    619

    Grain was transported to Suzhou, and upon receiving an official document, I continued through Henglin and stayed in an inn. In my dream, a person in golden armor came again and led me to meet the master, who had a green face. The master declared, "Bring forth the record of merits and faults of Yu Qi, weigh its lightness and heaviness, and then punish accordingly."

    On both sides, a scale was brought forward, shining with the golden star, and its balance was made of purple gold stone. All good deeds were marked with red labels, and all bad deeds were marked with black labels, separated and placed on the scale pans. In an instant, the red was light and the black was heavy, causing me to tremble uncontrollably. Soon, someone took a red-tagged document and threw it into the scale, and all the black labels in the scale pan were overwhelmed, making the red labels too heavy to measure. The green-faced master said, "With such great virtue, you may be released back to life and your lifespan increased by twelve years."

    Yu Qi awoke in shock and told people about it. Someone asked, "Can you recognize what document it was?" He replied, "It was a case I handled; how could I not recognize it! It was the case of the confiscation of the family of Liu, the provincial governor of Changzhou." When Liu was confiscated, his registered land and property had many overdue debts from tenant farmers, and a county magistrate wanted to pursue them according to the amount. Yu Qi pretended to follow his words but intentionally did not guard against fire at night, burning it all, and for this reason, he was beaten, and the matter was resolved. I suspect the document that weighed down the scale was related to this case. Yu Qi still exists to this day.

    620

    ○庄明府庄明府斤，未官时，馆广西横州刺史署中。昼卧书室，梦青衣人持帖云：“城隍神奉请。”庄随行至一衙署，城隍神降阶迎，叙寒温华，道：“为某案事，君作中证，故屈来质对，无干碍也。”庄唯唯，即告以当年作中原委。城隍笑颔之，呼童置酒，神南向，庄西向，曰：“敝署有幕四人，可许作陪否？”庄首肯，左右即请四先生来，皆非素相识者，彼此相揖，不交一言。四先生依城隍而坐，离庄甚远，阶下红灯四盏，光荧荧然。

    　　宴毕，庄知为阴府，因问：“终身之事，可预知否？”城隍神亦无难色，命左右取四簿至，上贴红签，有“横死、夭、死、老寿”四柱名目。庄本身注在老寿簿上，有妻某、子某、妾某云云。庄其时尚无子无妾也。庄辞别，城隍神命青衣者依原路送还。

    Explanation

    620

    A County Magistrate Named ZHUANG

    Before he became an official, ZHUANG resided in the office of the prefect of Guangxi Hengzhou. One day, while lying down in his study, he dreamed of a servant dressed in green who held a note and said, "The city deity invites you." ZHUANG followed the servant to an office, where the city deity descended the steps to welcome him. They exchanged pleasantries, and the deity said, "For a certain case, you are to serve as a mediator, so I have summoned you to discuss it. There is no interference." ZHUANG nodded politely and immediately provided a full account of the matter. The deity smiled and nodded in approval, then called for wine to be prepared. The deity faced south, and ZHUANG faced west. The deity said, "My office has four officials. May I ask if they may accompany us?" ZHUANG nodded in agreement, and the officials were summoned. None of them were people ZHUANG had met before; they exchanged bows but did not speak. The four officials sat near the deity, far from ZHUANG, with four red lamps glowing below.

    After the banquet, ZHUANG realized he was in the underworld and asked, "Is it possible to know my fate in life?" The city deity showed no hesitation and ordered the officials to bring four ledgers. On each ledger was a red label with the titles "sudden death, premature death, death, and longevity." ZHUANG's name was recorded in the longevity ledger, along with the names of his wife, son, and concubine. At the time, ZHUANG had no son or concubine. ZHUANG bid farewell, and the city deity ordered the servant to return him along the same path.

    621

    出衙，见街上搭台演戏，观者加堵，庄问：“何班？”青衣者曰：“郭三班也。”中有白须老人冯某，是庄旧邻，死久矣，一见，便来握手，且托云：“我葬某地，棺为地风所吹，现在倾仄。君归告我儿孙，改善为安。”

    　　庄自粤归，如其言，告知冯家。启坟视之，棺果斜朽。十馀年来，庄之遭际，历历如梦。惟所云为某中证事，不肯向人言。

    　　○净香童子桂林相国陈文恭公幼时扶乩，仙判牒云：“人原多道气，吏本是仙才。”后文恭历任封疆，位至宰相，似乩仙语未满其量。

    　　公卒后数年，苏州薛生白之子妇病，医治不效，乃扶乩求方，乩判云：“薛中立，可怜有承气汤而不知用，尚得为名医之子乎？”服之果愈。问：“乩仙何人？”曰：“我叶天士也。”盖天士与生白在生时各以医争名，而中立者，生白之子，故谑之。从此，苏人求方者毕集。乩所判药，应手而痊。

    Explanation

    621

    Leaving the government office, the author saw a stage set up on the street for a performance, with the audience packed so densely. The author asked, "Which troupe is this?" A person in green attire replied, "It's the Guo San Class." Among the crowd, there was an elderly man with a white beard, named Feng, who was an old neighbor of the author, having been dead for a long time.
