Upon seeing the author, he came forward to shake hands and entrusted him with a message: "I am buried at a certain place, and my coffin has been blown by underground wind, now tilting. Please tell my descendants when you return to make improvements for safety."

    The author returned from Guangdong and, as instructed, informed Feng's family. They opened the tomb and found the coffin indeed tilted and decayed. Over the past ten years, the author's experiences had been as clear as a dream. However, the matter mentioned in the previous statement, the author was unwilling to speak about it to others.

    When the official Chen Wengong, known as the Pure Fragrance Child and Governor of Guilin, was young, he performed a divination ritual using a board and a pendulum. The divine judgment written on a tablet said, "People originally have a lot of divine energy, and officials are inherently talented like immortals." Later, Chen Wengong served in various high-ranking positions and reached the position of chancellor, suggesting that the words of the diviner did not fully encompass his potential.

    After the official's death, several years later, the wife of a son of Xue Shengbai from Suzhou fell ill, and medical treatment was ineffective. They then performed a divination ritual to seek a prescription. The diviner's judgment said, "Zhongli, it is pitiful that you have the Chengqi Decoction but do not know how to use it. How can you be the son of a famous doctor?" After taking the medicine, she indeed recovered. They asked, "Who is the diviner?" The answer was, "I am Ye Tianshi." It is said that Ye Tianshi and Xue Shengbai competed for fame as doctors in their lifetime, and Zhongli is the son of Xue Shengbai, hence the teasing. From then on, people from Suzhou came to seek prescriptions in large numbers. The medicines prescribed by the diviner healed immediately.

    622

    一夕告别，大书云：“我为大公祖净香童子所召，不得不往。”众骇然问：“净香童子何以有公祖之称？”曰：“陈文恭公已复净香童子之位矣。”陈，故苏州巡抚也。

    　　○棺尸求祭常州御史吴龙见，文端公之曾孙也。其弟某，馆于李氏，厅宇甚宽，旁有古棺，穗帷尘满，吴亦习见，不以为怪。一夕月明时，棺中囊然有声，则前和开矣，中伸一首出，纱帽白髯，手指其腹，自称饥渴求祭。吴许之，白髯者向棺中取淡黄色袍服相畀，曰：“此明朝万历皇帝所赐也，今以为谢。”吴不敢受。夜渐阑，棺合缝如故。吴次日告主人，为建斋醮。据云：此棺乃李氏高祖，名杰，前明侍郎。以子孙甚多，惑于风水，故未葬耳。

    　　○沈椒园为兵岳部司嘉兴盛百二，丙子孝廉，受业于沈椒园先生。沈殁数年，盛梦游一处，见椒园乘八轿，仪从甚盛。盛趋前拱揖，沈摇手止之，随入一衙门。盛往投帖求见，阍者传谕：“北东岳府也，主人在此作部曹，未便进见。”

    Explanation

    622

    One night, he bid farewell and wrote in large characters: "I have been summoned by the Great Ancestor Jingxiangtongzi and cannot but go." The others asked in astonishment, "Why does Jingxiangtongzi have the title of Great Ancestor?" He replied, "Chen Wengong has been restored to the position of Jingxiangtongzi." Chen Wengong was the former Governor of Suzhou.

    ○ A coffin seeks offerings. Wu Longjian, the great-grandson of Wen Duan Gong, a magistrate of Changzhou, stayed at Li's residence. The hall was very spacious, and beside it was an ancient coffin, its curtain covered with dust. Wu was familiar with this sight and did not find it strange. One moonlit night, the coffin made a bag-like sound, and the front part opened. A head emerged, wearing a scholar's cap and a white beard, pointing to his abdomen and claiming to be hungry and thirsty, seeking offerings. Wu agreed, and the bearded one reached into the coffin and took out a pale yellow robe, saying, "This was granted by Emperor Wanli of the Ming Dynasty, and I offer it as a token of gratitude." Wu did not dare to accept it. As the night waned, the coffin closed as it was before. The next day, Wu informed the host and established a ritual of fasting and invocation. It was said that this coffin belonged to Li's great-grandfather, named Jie, who was a high-ranking official of the previous dynasty. Having many descendants, he was bewitched by geomancy, hence not yet buried.

    ○ Shen Jiao Yuan, a military official in charge of the southern region, was a student of Mr. Shen Jiao Yuan from Jiaxing. In the year of Bingzi, Sheng Bai'er, a scholar-official, studied under Mr. Shen Jiao Yuan. Several years after Shen's death, Sheng dreamed of wandering to a place where he saw Shen riding in an eight-man sedan chair, with a grand retinue. Sheng hurried forward to bow, but Shen waved his hand to stop him and then entered an office. Sheng went to leave a note requesting an audience, but the gatekeeper conveyed the message, "This is the Eastern Temple of the North, and the master is currently serving as a departmental official and cannot receive visitors."

    623

    盛知公为神，乃踉跄出。见柳阴下有人彷徨独立，谛视之，椒园表弟查某也，问：“何以在此？”曰：“椒园表兄招我入幕，我故来，及到此，又不相见，未知何故？我有大女明姑，冬月将出嫁，我要过此期才能来，而此意无由自达，奈何？”盛曰：“若如此，我当再扣先生之门，如得见，则并达尊意何如？”查曰：“幸甚。”盛仍诣辕门，向阍者述所以又来求见之故，阍为传入。顷之，阍者出曰：“主人公事忙，万不能见。可代致意查相公，速来速来，不能待至冬月。即查大姑娘，亦随后要来，不待婚嫁也。”盛以此语复查，相与欷而醒。

    　　是时春二月也，急往视查，彼此述梦皆合，查怃然不乐。其时查甚健，无恙。

    Explanation

    623

    Sheng realized that the man was a deity and stumbled out. Under the shade of a willow tree, he saw someone standing alone in a state of indecision. Upon closer inspection, he recognized his cousin Zha Mou from Jiao Yuan. Sheng asked, "Why are you here?" Zha Mou replied, "My cousin from Jiao Yuan invited me to join his entourage, so I came. But upon arriving, I was not met, and I do not know why. I have an eldest daughter named Minggu, who is to be married in winter. I must wait until after this period to come, but I cannot convey this message on my own. What am I to do?" Sheng said, "If that is the case, I will try to seek an audience with the master again. If I am able to see him, I will also convey your honorable intention. How is that?" Zha Mou said, "How fortunate!" Sheng then went to the main gate and explained to the gatekeeper why he had come to seek an audience again. The gatekeeper admitted him. Shortly after, the gatekeeper came out and said, "The master is busy and absolutely cannot meet. You may convey a message to Mr. Zha, telling him to come quickly, for he cannot wait until winter. Even Miss Zha, the eldest daughter, is to come shortly, without waiting for her wedding." Sheng relayed this message to Zha Mou, and they sighed together before waking up.

    It was then the second month of spring. Sheng hurried to see Zha Mou. They both recounted their dreams, and they found them to be in agreement. Zha Mou was filled with a sense of loss and sadness. At that time, Zha Mou was very healthy and had no ailments.

    624

    至八月间，查以疟亡；九月间，查女亦以疟亡。椒园，余社友，同举鸿词科。

    　　●卷十八○陕西茶客陕西茶客某，贩茶江南，归宿阌乡旅店。其东厢先有居者，山东二布客也。

    　　彼此晚膳毕，闭门睡矣。客梦有怪物，披发，赤短须凹面，撞门入，手持铁索，取东厢二布客锁之。随锁茶客，三人共索如鱼贯然，缚门外柳树上，怪又撞入他店去。二布客铁链甚紧，不能动；茶客链稍松，苦挣得脱。惊醒，以为梦也。告店主，亦不甚怖。次日五更，店主大喊，东厢二客死矣。半里外饭店中，亦死一骡夫。

    　　○山娘娘临平孙姓者新妇为魅所凭，自称“山娘娘”，喜敷粉着艳衣，白日抱其夫作交媾秽语。其夫患之，请吴山施道士作法。方设坛，其妻笑曰：“施道士薄薄有名，敢来治我？我将使之作王道士斩妖矣！”王道士斩妖者，俗演戏笑道士之无法者也。即以手按其妇腹下，秽血喷之，法果不灵。

    Explanation

    624

    In August, Chā died of malaria; in September, Chā's daughter also died of malaria. Jiao Yuan, my fellow society member, passed the imperial examination for literary talent together with me.

    Chapter Eighteen: The Tea Merchant from Shaanxi

    A certain tea merchant from Shaanxi, who traded tea in Jiangnan, stayed overnight at an inn in Wénxiāng. The east room of the inn was previously occupied by two cloth merchants from Shandong.

    After having their evening meal, they closed the door and went to sleep. The tea merchant dreamed of a monster with long hair, a red, short beard, and a concave face. The monster broke into the door, holding an iron chain, and locked the two cloth merchants from the east room. It then locked the tea merchant, chaining the three together like fish in a line and tying them up on a willow tree outside the door. The monster then broke into another store. The two cloth merchants' iron chains were very tight, and they couldn't move; the tea merchant's chain was a bit loose, and he struggled to break free. He woke up, thinking it was a dream. He told the innkeeper, who was not very scared. The next day at dawn, the innkeeper shouted that the two guests from the east room had died. Half a mile away in a restaurant, a mule driver also died.

    Chapter Eighteen: The Mountain Goddess

    In Linping, a newly married woman surnamed Sun was possessed by a demon who called herself "Mountain Goddess." She liked to powder her face and wear bright, colorful clothes. During the day, she hugged her husband and spoke filthy words about sexual intercourse. Her husband was troubled by this and asked Wu Shan to perform a ritual with the Daoist Master Shi. As the altar was being set up, his wife laughed and said, "Master Shi has only a slight reputation; dare he come to treat me? I will make him perform the ritual of Master Wang, the Daoist who slays demons!" The ritual of Master Wang, who slays demons, is a popular play that mocks Daoists who lack skill. She immediately placed her hand on her wife's abdomen, and filthy blood spewed out, proving that the ritual was ineffective.

    625

    道士曰：“我有辟秽符在枕中。”命其徒取而张之，再坐坛作法。妻有惧色，亦坐几上，挥帚作法，彼此斗良久。其夫见三目神擒一白猴，大五尺许，投阶前，猴俯伏。道士取而掷之，屡掷屡小，缩如初生小猫。乃取入瓦坛中，封以符印，旋有黑气从坛中出。次日投江中，妇病遂愈。

    　　○瓜洲公子杭州大方伯地方，有胡姓姑嫂二人，同居一楼。清明日，嫂见瓦上有搭柳为桥者，疑是儿戏，用竿挑去之。晚间，有羽衣男子突至卧床前，曰：“我瓜洲公子也，与汝姑嫂有缘，故折柳做鹊桥，从瓦上度来，以应清明佳节，汝何得拆去？”

    　　言毕，住房中，凭二女为祟。其家请道士念《玉皇经》解禳之。道士方至，怪以溺器掷之，经卷淋漓。道士逃去。胡翁遣老媪五人守夜调护，则五媪发皆成辫，丝丝相接，非拖曳不能行。如是者月余。

    Explanation

    625

    The Daoist priest said, "I have a talisman to ward off impurities in my pillow." He ordered his disciple to retrieve and display it, then sat on the altar again to perform rituals. The wife looked fearful and also sat on the table, waving a broom as part of the ritual, and they contended for a long time. The husband saw a three-eyed deity capture a white monkey, about five feet tall, and throw it down the stairs, where the monkey bowed down. The priest took the monkey and threw it repeatedly, causing it to shrink each time, until it was the size of a newborn kitten. He then placed it in a clay jar, sealed it with a talisman and an official seal, and soon black smoke emerged from the jar. The next day, he threw the jar into the river, and the wife's illness was cured.

    ○ A nobleman from Guazhou, residing in a grand mansion in Hangzhou, had two sisters-in-law named Hu, who lived together in one building. On the Qingming Festival, the older sister saw a willow bridge on the roof and thought it was a child's play, so she removed it with a pole. That evening, a man dressed in feathered clothing suddenly appeared at her bedside and said, "I am the nobleman from Guazhou, and I have a connection with you and your sister-in-law. Therefore, I made a bridge out of willow branches to cross over, in accordance with the Qingming Festival. How could you remove it?"

    After speaking, he stayed in the house and caused mischief through the two women. The family invited a Daoist priest to recite the Yuhuang Jing to exorcise the evil spirits. When the priest arrived, the man threw a urinal at him, causing the scriptures to become wet. The priest fled. The old man Hu sent five old women to guard the night and care for them, but the women's hair turned into braids, connected in threads, and they could not walk without dragging. This continued for more than a month.

    626

    其女久有婿家，遂择日嫁之，怪曰：“某家无缘，我不能往，在此徒挟一美，亦觉萧索，请从此辞。”因谓胡翁曰：“我在此闹汝久，甚愧无以为报。我有妹甚美，愿赠汝为妾，未知汝肯纳否？”胡请见，怪许之，命中堂垂帘观之，果望见绝色女子。胡不觉心动，急请婚期。怪曰：“我愿以汝为妹夫，而妹嫌汝老丑，心颇不肯。汝能将颐下须尽去之，则姻事成矣。”胡年五十余，肥而多髯，惑其言，一旦尽剃之，怪在空中大笑而去，妹竟不来。

    　　○王白斋尚书为潮鸣寺僧余同年王白斋，少年美秀。初入学时，年才十七。偶游鸣寺，见影堂老僧像，不觉毛发渐洒，还家遂病。嗣后过寺不敢入。及探花及第时，梦老僧以线香五十四枝与之，曰：“我有三弟子：一梦麟，一钱维城，一汝也。汝将来司刑名时，当超度某案，再来归依原位。”白斋秘而不言。后果为大司寇，寿五十四而终，卒不知所超度者何案也。

    Explanation

    626

    Final Translation

    Her daughter had long been engaged, and they chose a day to marry her. The supernatural entity said, "There is no connection with that family; I cannot go there. Here I am just holding onto one beauty, and also feel desolate. Please let me leave." Then it said to the old man Hu, "I have troubled you for a long time here, and I am very ashamed and have nothing to repay you. I have a sister who is very beautiful, and I wish to give her to you as a concubine. I don't know if you will accept her." Hu requested to see her, and the supernatural entity agreed. In the main hall, she lowered the curtain to see her, and indeed, she saw a beautiful woman. Hu was not aware that his heart was moved, and quickly requested the wedding date. The supernatural entity said, "I wish to make you my brother-in-law, but my sister finds you old and ugly, and she is quite unwilling. If you can remove all the hair under your chin, then the marriage would be successful." Hu was over fifty years old, fat and with many whiskers. Deceived by its words, he shaved it all off one day. The supernatural entity laughed loudly in the air and left, and the sister never came.

    ○ Wang Baizhai, the Grand Secretary, was a contemporary of the monk from Chao Ming Temple. Wang Baizhai was handsome and graceful when he was young. When he first entered school, he was only seventeen. Once, he visited the temple and saw the image of an old monk in the shadow hall, and he felt his hair stand on end. He returned home and fell ill. Afterward, he was afraid to enter the temple. When he passed the imperial examination and became a scholar, he dreamed that the old monk gave him fifty-four sticks of incense and said, "I have three disciples: one is Meng Lin, one is Qian Weicheng, and one is you. When you take charge of criminal cases in the future, you should liberate a certain case and return to your original position." Wang Baizhai kept this secret and did not speak of it. Later, he became the Grand Secretary of Criminal Affairs and lived to be fifty-four, but he never knew what case he had liberated.

    627

    ○白天德湖州东门外有周姓者，其妻踏青入城，染邪归。其家请道士孙敬书诵《天篷咒》，用拷鬼棒击之，妖附其妻供云：“我白天德也。为祟者，我弟维德，与我无干。”孙书符唤维德至，问：“汝与周家妇何仇？”曰：“无仇。我路遇，爱其美，故与结缘。方爱之，岂肯害之！”问：“汝向住何处？”曰：“附东门玄帝庙侧，偷享香火已数百年。”孙曰：“东门庙是玄帝太子之宫。当时创立，原为镇压合郡火灾，故立庙离宫东首。汝何得妄玄元帝庙耶？”妖云：“治火灾当治其母，不当治其子，犹之伐木者当克其本，不克其枝。汝作道士而五行生克之理茫然不知，尚要行法来驱我耶？”拍其肩大笑去。周氏妻亦竟无恙。

    Explanation

    627

    A Spirit Named Bai Tiande in Huzhou

    In Huzhou, there was a man surnamed Zhou who lived outside the eastern city gate. His wife went into the city to enjoy the spring scenery and returned, having been infected by an evil spirit. Her family invited the Taoist priest Sun Jingshu to recite the Tianpeng Spell and used a ghost-flogging stick to strike the spirit. The spirit possessed her wife and confessed, saying, “I am Bai Tiande. The one causing harm is my younger brother Weide, and I am not involved.” Sun wrote a talisman and summoned Weide, asking, “What enmity do you have with Mrs. Zhou?” The spirit replied, “I have no enmity. I met her on the road, admired her beauty, and thus formed a connection. While I admire her, how could I harm her!” Sun asked, “Where have you been living before?” The spirit answered, “I have been attached to the side of the Xuan Emperor Temple at the eastern gate, secretly enjoying the offerings for several hundred years.” Sun said, “The eastern gate temple is the palace of the Prince of the Xuan Emperor. It was originally established to suppress fires throughout the prefecture, so the temple was built east of the palace. How dare you claim the Xuan Emperor Temple as your own?” The spirit retorted, “To control fires, one must address the root cause, not the symptoms, just as a woodcutter should cut down the tree trunk, not the branches. You, as a Taoist priest, do not understand the principles of the Five Elements and their interactions, yet you dare to use your rituals to drive me away?” The spirit then slapped his shoulder, laughed, and left. Mrs. Zhou remained unharmed.

    628

    ○髑髅乞恩杭州陈以夔，善五鬼搬运法，替人圆光，颇有神效。其友孙姓者宿其家，夜半，床下走出一白发翁，跪而言曰：“乞致意陈先生，还我髑髅，使我全尸。”

    　　孙大骇，急起，以灯照床下，则骷髅一具存焉，方知陈驱役鬼物，皆向败棺中取其天灵盖来施符用咒故也。孙初劝之，陈犹隐讳；取床下骨示之，陈乃无言，即送还原处。未几，陈为群鬼所击，遍身青肿死。

    　　○锡锞一锭阴间准三分用杭州龚薇垣生员，原任甘泉令龚明水之从子也。病中梦游阴府，街巷店铺，与阳间无异，惟黄沙迷漫，不见日月。见店铺中有司柜者，故所识也，趋往问路。

    Explanation

    628

    A Skull's Plea for Kindness

    In Hangzhou, there was a man named Chen Yikui who was skilled in the Five Ghosts Transport Method, a technique used to perform divinations and fortune-telling that had quite a magical effect. One night, a friend of his, whose name was Sun, stayed at Chen's house. At midnight, an elderly man with white hair walked out from under the bed and knelt, saying, "Please convey my message to Mr. Chen. Return my skull to me so that I may have a complete body."

    Sun was greatly alarmed and quickly got up, lighting a lamp to look under the bed. There, he saw a skull present. Only then did he realize that Chen was summoning and controlling ghostly beings, all of which were taken from the top of decayed coffins to cast spells and use incantations. Initially, Sun had advised Chen, but Chen still kept it secret. When Sun showed him the bones from under the bed, Chen remained silent and immediately returned them to their original place. Not long after, Chen was attacked by a group of ghosts and died with his entire body bruised and swollen.

    A Tin Ingot Worth Three Parts in the Underworld

    In Hangzhou, there was a scholar named Gong Weiyuan, a descendant of Gong Mingshui, who had originally served as the magistrate of Ganquan. While sick, he dreamed of visiting the underworld. The streets and shops there were no different from the mortal world, except that yellow sand obscured everything, and the sun and moon could not be seen. He saw someone serving as a shopkeeper, someone he knew before, and rushed over to ask for directions.

    629

    司柜者笑曰：“此间无路。汝至此，尚欲何往？”再问不答。薇垣不得已，彷徨道中。

    　　有乘四轿呵殿而来者，近视之，己之岳翁某也，趋而问焉。翁惨然曰：“此非人间，汝何至此？”薇垣方知其身已死，因自述病中原委，并问其父母寿算。岳翁曰：“此事非我所司，汝叔父明水先生现在王府教书，汝可往问。但王府尊严，侍卫甚众，非重用门包不能通报。”薇垣问：“门包何物？”曰：“亦不过阳世通用之锡锞耳。凡阳世烧锡锞一锭，阴间准作三分用。或有破损湿烂者，仅准一二分用。”薇垣闻言，急走往王府，忘其身未带锡锞。

    　　至一宫门，侍卫者如林，见薇垣，果伸手索贿，而薇垣无以应也，但口称“家叔明水在此教书，烦为通报”。侍卫者怒，骂曰：“一老腐头巾在府，已甚可厌，怎禁得又添一小腐头巾来！”挥杖击之，一惊而醒，家人已环泣于旁。后数月，薇垣忽无故缢死。

    Explanation

    629

    The Gatekeeper smiled and said, "There is no path here. You have come this far, but where do you intend to go?" When asked again, he did not answer. Wei Yuan could not help but wander aimlessly.

    There was someone arriving in a four-person sedan chair, shouting and demanding attention. Upon closer inspection, it was his father-in-law, who approached and asked him. The father-in-law looked sorrowful and said, "This is not the human world. How did you come here?" Wei Yuan then realized that he had died. He recounted the details of his illness and inquired about the lifespan of his parents. The father-in-law said, "This matter is not within my jurisdiction. Your uncle, Mr. Ming Shui, is currently teaching at the Prince's Mansion. You may go and ask him. However, the Prince's Mansion is very strict, and there are many guards. Without a substantial gift, you will not be able to get through." Wei Yuan asked, "What is this gift?" The father-in-law replied, "It is simply the common tin ingots used in the world. Any tin ingot burned in the world counts as three parts in the underworld. If it is damaged or damp, it is only counted as one or two parts." Upon hearing this, Wei Yuan hurried to the Prince's Mansion, forgetting that he had not brought the tin ingots with him.

    When he reached a palace gate, the guards were as numerous as trees. Seeing Wei Yuan, they indeed reached out to demand a bribe. But Wei Yuan had nothing to offer, so he could only say, "My uncle, Mr. Ming Shui, is teaching here. Please inform him of my arrival." The guards became angry and shouted, "One old scholar is already in the mansion, and he is quite annoying. How can we tolerate another young scholar?" They struck him with their staffs, and he awoke in a start, finding his family weeping around him. A few months later, Wei Yuan suddenly hanged himself without cause.

    630

    ○鸡卵担粪杭州清泰门外有观音堂徐姓者，其妻为五通神所据，每朔望，至其家饮啖，有事必预为通知。妻故穷苦，佐其夫粪田。神怜之，代为担粪。以两空壳鸡卵为桶，盛粪石许，细竹管挑之，较多于木桶盛者。而所灌田尤肥。

    　　○狐丹常州武进县有吕姓者，妇为狐所凭。化作美男子，戴唐巾，为人言休咎，有验有不验。来问卜者，狐或外出，则命书一笺焚之，存其灰于坛中。狐来，口吐物，红色，如小镜然，大不过寸许，持向坛中照灰，便能朗诵所焚之语，丝毫无误。照毕，仍吞入腹中。或曰：此狐丹也。狐有批答，辄令妇口授之，虑其遗忘，则以手掐妇手指之中节，便能记忆。虽长篇韵语，俱能成通，过此则依然不识字也。

    Explanation

    630

    ○ Chicken Egg Manure Carrying
    In Hangzhou, outside the Qingtai Gate, there was a temple dedicated to Guanyin, and a man named Xu lived there. His wife was possessed by the Wutong God. Every new moon and full moon, the deity would visit their home to drink and eat, and would always inform them in advance if there were any matters. The wife was poor and helped her husband fertilize the fields. The deity pitied her and took on the task of carrying the manure. Using two empty chicken eggshells as buckets, it could carry as much manure as a stone, and with a thin bamboo pole, it could carry more than a wooden bucket. The fields it fertilized were especially fertile.

    ○ Fox Elixir
    In Wujin County, Changzhou, there was a man named Lu, whose wife was possessed by a fox. The fox transformed into a handsome man, wearing a Tang-style cap, and predicted good or bad fortune for people. Some predictions were accurate, and some were not. When people came to consult the oracle, if the fox was out, it would write a note and burn it, storing the ashes in an altar. When the fox returned, it would spit out a red object, about the size of a small mirror, no larger than an inch. Holding it towards the ashes in the altar, it could then recite the burned words without any error. After looking, it would swallow it back into its belly. Someone said: "This is the fox's elixir." When the fox had a reply, it would make the woman recite it. To prevent forgetting, it would pinch the middle joint of the woman's finger, and she could then remember. Even long rhymed passages could be recited fluently, but beyond this, she remained illiterate.

    631

    有某秀才，为妇中表亲，欲与狐唱酬，嘱转致狐。狐曰：“有一对，秀才能属对，即与酬答可也：”红白桃花映纸窗，花无二色。‘“妇以告，秀才不能对，惭而退。此狐至今犹存其家，钱竹初明府为予言。

    　　○处州溺妇奇狱处州乡民陈瑞送妻还其母家，路过半塘桥，妇溲于厕，久而不返。陈往寻不得，望前村攒屋中红裙外露，急往视之，果其妻裙也。似被人曳入棺中，露半幅于外。心疑僵尸作祟，将斧出之以救其妻。访问棺主，有张某云：“此我家姑母棺也。姑母死时，年三十余，其子又亡，无力营葬，久攒于此。”陈请开棺，初不许，陈哀求至再，始许之。劈开，则一白须男子，手持某妻之裙，而不见其妻之身。于是，陈以失生妻控官，张以失死姑控官，官不能断，至今悬为疑狱。

    Explanation

    631

    There was a certain scholar who was a cousin-in-law of a woman. He wished to engage in poetic exchange with a fox and asked someone to relay his message. The fox said, "I have a couplet: 'Red and white peach blossoms reflected on a paper window, the flowers have no second color.' If the scholar can respond, I will reciprocate." The woman reported this to the scholar, but he could not respond and felt ashamed, retreating. This fox is still present in the scholar's home, as told to me by Magistrate Qian Zhuchu.

    In Zhejiang Province, there was a strange case involving a drowned woman. A local man named Chen Rui sent his wife back to her mother's house. As they passed over Ban Tang Bridge, the wife went to the restroom and did not return for a long time. Chen went to look for her but could not find her. He saw a red skirt visible outside a group of closely built houses in a nearby village and rushed to check, finding it to be his wife's skirt. It seemed as if she had been dragged into a coffin, with half of her body visible outside. Suspecting that a ghost was causing trouble, Chen used an axe to split open the coffin to rescue his wife. He asked the owner of the coffin, who identified it as belonging to his grandmother and explained that she had died at the age of thirty or so, and her son had also died, leaving them unable to afford proper burial, so they had kept her coffin there for a long time. Chen requested to open the coffin, but it was initially refused. After persistent pleading, the owner finally agreed. When the coffin was split open, they found an elderly man with a white beard holding the wife's skirt, but there was no sign of the wife's body. Chen then filed a lawsuit for the loss of his living wife, and Zhang filed a lawsuit for the loss of his deceased grandmother. The officials could not make a decision, and the case remains unresolved to this day.

    632

    ○道家有全骨法杭州龙井初开时，商人叶姓者司其事。有倪某者，为叶择开工日期。后十年，叶身故，倪忽暴病，有群鬼附其身，语音不一，曰：“还我骨！还我骨！”声啾啾然，楚、越、吴、鲁音皆有也，最后有自称陈朝傅将军者曰：“我助萧摩诃南征北讨，葬此千年，汝何得与叶某擅伤我骨？”家人环求曰：“此官府所命，主人力不能抗，将军何不相谅耶？”将军曰：“此虽公事不可违，然汝与叶某理宜将掘骨暴棺事告知官府。官府不从，便与汝无罪。今汝等并不告官，而擅将我等数十人骨混行抛掷，以致男装女头，老接少脚，至今丛残缺散，鬼如何安？”家人请用佛法解禳，将军曰：“佛无能为，惟道家有全骨法，汝往求之。”

    Explanation

    632

    A Taoist method for preserving bones

    During the initial opening of Longjing in Hangzhou, a merchant surnamed Ye was in charge of the matter. There was a man named Ni who helped Ye choose the date for the commencement of work. Ten years later, Ye passed away, and Ni suddenly fell gravely ill. A group of ghosts possessed his body, and their voices were varied, shouting: "Return my bones! Return my bones!" The voices chirped and whispered, with accents from Chu, Yue, Wu, and Lu. Finally, one of the ghosts introduced itself as General Fu of the Chen dynasty, saying: "I assisted Xiao Moxie in his southern and northern campaigns, and have been buried here for a thousand years. How could you, along with Ye, have the audacity to injure my bones without permission?" The family pleaded, "This was ordered by the authorities, and Ye had no power to resist. Why can't you understand, General?" The general replied, "Though this is an official matter that cannot be avoided, you should have informed the authorities about the excavation of bones and the exposure of coffins. If the authorities do not agree, then you would be blameless. But now, you have not informed the authorities and have擅自 thrown about the bones of dozens of people, causing men's bodies to be attached to women's heads and old bodies to be connected to young feet. As a result, the bones are scattered and incomplete, and how can the ghosts find peace?" The family asked to use Buddhist methods to dispel the spirits, but the general said, "Buddhism is powerless; only Taoism has the method to preserve bones. You should go and seek it."

    633

    于是，叶家人访有礼斗人施柳南、万近蓬等，往而拜求，遂设坛于龙井。作法七日，见西湖神灯赫然，散满水上，或叠高为塔，或横排为雁字，或团聚如大车轮，或散作流萤万点。须臾，斗母下降，霞佩璎珞，严妆不可逼视。牵二囚来，即叶某与倪姓也，皆跪阶前。鬼数十争来笞击，斗母喝曰：“此亦汝等劫数，毋庸仇怨。我命九幽使者尽提残骨，为汝等补还可也。”少顷，髑髅数十具皆有白气索绕，旋滚成团，其缺处皆圆满矣。将军长丈余，披金甲，率群鬼拜谢斗母。

    　　叶亦解锁，合掌膜拜而去，倪病遂愈。此事近蓬为余言。

    Explanation

    633

    At this point, the family of Ye sought out the ritual practitioners Shi Liunan and Wan Jinping, and went to them to make a request. They set up an altar at Longjing and performed rituals for seven days. They witnessed the divine lanterns of West Lake, shining brightly and scattered across the water, sometimes stacking high like a pagoda, sometimes arranged in the shape of a wild goose formation, sometimes gathering together like a large cartwheel, and sometimes dispersing like countless fireflies. Soon, the Mother Star descended, adorned with a cloud belt and jade ornaments, her appearance so majestic that it was too radiant to look upon. She led two prisoners here, who were Ye and Ni. Both knelt before the steps. Dozens of ghosts rushed to beat them. The Mother Star shouted, "There is no need for enmity. I will command the messengers of the nine depths to retrieve all the remains and compensate you." Shortly after, dozens of skulls were all surrounded by white vapor, rolling into a ball, and the missing parts were all made complete. A general over a zhang tall, wearing golden armor, led the ghosts to bow and thank the Mother Star. Ye was also released, clasped his hands, bowed in worship, and departed. Ni's illness was then cured. This story was told to me by Wan Jinping.

    634

    ○批地藏王颊两江总督于成龙未遇时，梦至一宫殿，上书“地藏王府”四字，殿上老僧跏趺闭目。于心念地藏王主人间生死事，家有老仆某，愿而勤，久病不起，因长揖告诉，求为延寿。再三言，僧默然不应。于怒，直前手批其颊。老僧开眼笑，屈一指示之。醒而告人，皆云：“地藏王一指，当是延寿一纪。”已而仆病愈，果又生人间十二年。

    　　○儒佛两不收杭州杨生兆南，业儒，兼通禅学。殁后一年，托梦于其妻曰：“人死必有所归。我故儒士，司魂者送我于文昌所，帝君出题试我，我不能作，帝君不收；司魂者再送我佛菩萨处，佛出经问我，我不能解，佛又不收。徨阴间，无歇足之地。不得已，将以某月日投生张某家。自念我一生好佛，汝须往告张家，勿以荤乳我，免再堕落。”张故兆南友也。临期视之，果生一男，盘膝而生。哭三年不止，张氏啖以荤，哭遽止，而儿遂犯惊痫之疾。此乾隆四十三年事。

    Explanation

    634

    Translation

    ○ Slapping the Cheek of Ksitigarbha Bodhisattva

    When Governor-General Yu Chenglong of the Two Rivers Region was obscure, he had a dream where he came to a palace inscribed with "Ksitigarbha's Mansion." On the dais, an old monk sat in the lotus position with closed eyes. Yu thought about Ksitigarbha Bodhisattva's role in overseeing human life and death. He remembered that there was an old servant in his household, who was diligent and kind but had been ill for a long time and could not get up. He bowed respectfully and told the monk, asking for the servant's life to be extended. He repeated his request several times, but the monk remained silent and unresponsive. Enraged, Yu stepped forward and slapped the monk's cheek. The old monk opened his eyes and smiled, pointing with one finger. Upon waking, Yu told others, and they all said, "Ksitigarbha Bodhisattva's single finger means the servant's life will be extended by twelve years." Indeed, the servant recovered and lived for another twelve years.

    ○ Neither Confucianism nor Buddhism Accepts

    In Hangzhou, there was a scholar named Yang Zhengnan who was well-versed in both Confucianism and Zen Buddhism. One year after his death, he appeared in a dream to his wife and said, "When a person dies, they must have a destination. I was a Confucian scholar, and the soul guide sent me to the office of the God of Literature, where the god set a question to test me. I could not write it, so the god did not accept me. The soul guide then sent me to the presence of the Buddha and Bodhisattvas. The Buddha asked questions based on the scriptures, but I could not understand them, so the Buddha did not accept me either. I wandered aimlessly in the underworld, finding no place to rest. Reluctantly, I will be reborn into the family of Zhang on a certain date. I remember that I always favored Buddhism. You must go to the Zhang family and tell them not to feed me meat, so as to avoid further suffering." Zhang was a friend of Yang Zhengnan. When the time came, they observed that a boy was indeed born, sitting cross-legged. He cried continuously for three years. The Zhang family fed him meat, and his crying stopped, but the child developed a condition of epilepsy. This was the 43rd year of the Qianlong reign.

    635

    ○鸟门山事绍兴东关有张姓者，妻病延医，行过鸟门山，遇白须叟相随而行。时天已晚，觉此叟足不贴地，映夕阳无影，心疑为鬼。问其踪迹，叟亦不讳，曰：“我非人，乃鬼也，然有求于君，非害君者。我有骸骨葬鸟门山之西，被凿石者终日钻斫，山石就倾，我坟中朽棺业已半露，不久将坠入河中。幸君哀我，为改葬之。君前去到新桥地方，有五个溺水鬼坐而待君，我为君先往驱除之。”出怀中朱家糕与张食曰：“明日请到朱家，以朱家包糕纸为证。”张与偕行至新桥，果有黑气五团踞桥坐。叟先往折树枝打之，声啾啾然，尽落于水。张到医家，叟再拜别去。

    Explanation

    635

    In the eastern suburbs of Shaoxing, there was a man surnamed Zhang. His wife fell ill, and he went to seek a doctor. On his way, he passed through Bird Mountain and encountered an old man with a white beard walking beside him. By then, it was already late in the day, and Zhang noticed that the old man's feet did not touch the ground, and he cast no shadow under the sunset. He suspected that the old man might be a ghost. When he asked about the old man's origins, the old man did not hide anything and said, "I am not a human; I am a ghost. However, I have a request of you, and I am not here to harm you. My remains are buried on the west side of Bird Mountain. The stonecutters have been drilling and chiseling the mountain all day, causing the mountain stones to be about to collapse. My rotten coffin has already half-exposed, and it will soon fall into the river. May you pity me and re-bury my remains. When you go to the New Bridge area, there will be five drowning ghosts sitting on the bridge waiting for you. I will go ahead to drive them away." The old man took out a red family cake from his pocket and gave it to Zhang to eat, saying, "Please come to the Red Family tomorrow, and use the Red Family's cake wrapper as proof." Zhang accompanied the old man to the New Bridge, and indeed, there were five black clouds sitting on the bridge. The old man went first to break branches and hit them, making a chirping sound, and they all fell into the water. Zhang arrived at the doctor's house, and the old man bowed twice and left.

    636

    次日，张往朱家买糕，出其纸，果朱店中招贴也，告以原委，店主人悄然曰：“君所见叟，姓莫名全章，故余戚也。渠改葬之事，何不托我而托君？想与君有缘。君命中不应死于五水鬼，故神灵命此叟为君驱除耶？”引张往鸟门山，视其墓棺，离水仅尺许，乃别择地改葬焉。

    　　○杨二杭州杨二，素以拳棒为事。夏夜，坐后园假山上乘凉，见石罅中出一小头，先露其发，再露其面。杨大骇，持棍击之，头不见。次日宿楼中，闻楼下有着屐声往来历落，疑为贼，然心念偷儿无着屐之事。有顷，屐声缘梯而上，则一白衣人带甬长帽，手持四方灯笼，嘻嘻然向杨而笑。杨击以铁尺，白衣人坠于楼下，作怒声曰：“好打好打！待我唤伙计来，好好收拾你！”

    Explanation

    636

    The next day, Zhang went to Zhu's shop to buy cakes. He found a poster there, which indeed belonged to Zhu's shop. He was told the whole story by the shop owner, who quietly said, "The old man you saw, his surname is unknown, and his given name is Full Zhang. He is my relative. Why did you entrust the matter of reburial to you instead of me? Perhaps you and he have a connection. Your fate does not mean you should die from the five water demons, so the gods have commanded this old man to expel them for you." The shop owner led Zhang to Bird Gate Mountain, where they inspected the grave and coffin, which were only a few inches away from the water. They then chose another place for reburial.

    In Hangzhou, there was a man named Yang Er, who was skilled in martial arts. One summer night, he sat on a pavilion in the back garden to cool off. He saw a small head emerge from a crack in the stone, first revealing its hair, then its face. Yang was greatly startled and struck it with a stick, but the head disappeared. The next day, while staying in a building, he heard footsteps on the stairs below, which sounded like they were walking back and forth. He suspected it was a thief, but he thought that thieves would not wear clogs. Soon, the sound of clogs climbed up the stairs, and a man in white robes wearing a long hat and holding a square lantern appeared, laughing at Yang. Yang struck the man with an iron ruler, and the man in white robes fell to the floor below, shouting angrily, "Hit me hard! Wait until I call my colleagues to deal with you!"

    637

    次日，杨召其徒告之，诸无赖噪曰：“彼有伙计，我等亦伙计，请护持老兄登楼打鬼。”于是治肴痛饮，各持器械登楼，鬼竟不至。鸡鸣时，诸无赖各倦卧。

    　　平明起，寻杨二不见。觅之，已死于楼下竹榻上。

    　　○吴秉中吴秉中，居葵巷，故予旧宅邻也，延汪名天生先训其子侄。月夜至馆中闲谈，见墙上有一老翁，长尺许，白发锐头，坐而效其所为。吴吃烟，叟亦吃烟；吴拱手，叟亦拱手。以为大奇，呼汪先生观之，先生所见无异。其侄锡九往观，无所见。是年秋，秉中与汪俱死，而锡九至今独存。

    Explanation

    637

    The next day, Yang summoned his disciples and told them. The ruffians shouted, saying, "He has partners, we have partners too, please protect our elder brother as he goes upstairs to fight the ghost." So they prepared food and drank heavily, each carrying weapons and climbing up the stairs. The ghost did not appear. At the time of the rooster's crow, the ruffians were all tired and lying down.

    At dawn, they got up and searched for Yang Er but did not find him. They looked for him, and found him already dead on a bamboo mat downstairs.

    ○ Wu Bingzhong, who lived in Kuixiang, was a neighbor of my former residence. He invited Wang Tiansheng to teach his nephews. One moonlit night, Wu went to the study to chat. He saw an old man about a foot tall, with white hair and a sharp head, sitting and imitating what he was doing. When Wu smoked, the old man smoked; when Wu bowed, the old man bowed. Wu thought this was very strange and called Wang Mr. to see it. Mr. Wang saw nothing unusual. His nephew Xijiu went to see, but saw nothing. That autumn, both Wu and Wang died, while Xijiu is still alive to this day.

    638

    ○土窟异兽闽商陈某，与诸客泛海，遇飓风，飘至一山脚下，见山崖平坦可步，相率樵采。初进，路甚仄，行一二里，即觉开旷。时天色将暮，闻海风萧飒，林鸟啾啁，不敢深入，乃归。

    　　次日，风更甚，舟不行，舟中人悔昨未穷其境，约再往，拉陈与偕。迹前径行八九里，有一溪，水色澄绿，旁有土山，不甚高，穴中似有物喘息。众惧窜走，陈恃胆力，上在树隐身觇之。

    　　食顷，其物出穴外，大倍水牛而形似象，顶生一角，晶莹犀利，盘踞石上长啸，声裂竹木。陈惊惧几坠，但见虎豹猿鹿各以其属至，俯伏其下，不止千计。

    　　其物择肥者践之，用舌舐其腹，吸其血，百兽皆股栗不敢动。食三四兽，复曳尾入穴。客乃下，寻旧径归，与众言所见，终未知山与兽何名也。

    　　○鸡脚人闽商杨某，世以洋贩为业，言其祖于康熙中偕客出洋，遇旋风吹入海汊。其水面四高，惟中港独低，又在海水之下。杨舟盘涡而下，人船惧无恙。

    Explanation

    638

    Strange Beasts in Earth Caves

    A merchant from Fujian named Chen, traveling by sea with several guests, encountered a typhoon and drifted to the foot of a mountain. They saw that the cliff was flat and walkable, and proceeded to gather firewood. At first, the path was very narrow, but after walking one or two miles, they immediately felt open and spacious. As the sky was about to turn dark, they heard the sea wind howling and the chirping of birds in the forest, and did not dare to go deeper, so they returned.

    The next day, the wind was even stronger, and the boat could not move. The people on the boat regretted not fully exploring the area yesterday and agreed to return. They followed the previous path and walked eight or nine miles. There was a stream, the water color was clear and green, and there was a small hill nearby, not very high. In the cave, it seemed there was something breathing. The group was afraid and ran away, but Chen relied on his courage and climbed up a tree to hide and observe.

    After a while, the creature came out of the cave. It was much larger than a water buffalo and had a shape similar to an elephant. It had a single horn on its head, shiny and sharp, and sat on a rock, roaring loudly. The sound was so loud that it could split bamboo and wood. Chen was so frightened that he almost fell. He saw tigers, leopards, monkeys, and deer coming in their respective groups, cowering beneath it, numbering more than a thousand.

    The creature chose the fatter ones to trample and licked their bellies with its tongue, sucking their blood. All the beasts trembled with fear and did not dare to move. After eating three or four beasts, it dragged its tail back into the cave. The guests then descended and returned along the old path, telling the others what he had seen. Finally, they did not know the names of the mountain and the beast.

    People with Chicken Feet

    A merchant from Fujian named Yang, whose family has been involved in overseas trade, said that his ancestor, during the reign of Emperor Kangxi, traveled with guests overseas and encountered a whirlwind that blew them into a small branch of the sea. The water surface was high all around, but the middle channel was low and even below the sea level. Yang's boat spiraled down into the channel, and although the people on the boat were afraid, they were unharmed.

    639

    至港底，见山川草木，田畴蔬谷，一如人世，惟无庐舍。岸侧有船依泊，内有数十人，亦中州来者，见杨等，欢如骨肉。因言此水惟闰年月有一日独高与海水平，舟始可归，然只一食顷耳，稍迟则又不得上矣。其人先被飓风吹至时，亦曾有人居此港，后遇闰水得归。彼迟不及，留此六年，皆屡遇闰而失其时，故未得去。

    　　杨同舟客有四十人，带有谷菜诸种，咸分土耕种。其地颇沃而收倍，且不须人灌溉，终日与前舟人款接往来，几忘身在世外也。惜无黄历考日时，每食讫，咸登舟待水满而已。

    　　一日，杨与客闲步野外，望隔溪有人行近溪口，皆长丈余，无衣，身有毛，脚如鸡爪，胫如牛膝。见杨，啾唧作对语状，音不可晓。归与彼舟人言之，亦言来时曾于溪口见之，缘溪满不得渡。倘其来此，吾辈宁有孑遗耶？！

    Explanation

    639

    Upon reaching the bottom of the harbor, they saw mountains, rivers, grass, trees, fields, vegetables, and grains, all as in the human world, but without any houses. By the shore, there were ships moored, with dozens of people inside, also from the Central Plains. Seeing Yang and his companions, they rejoiced as if they were family. They explained that this water only rises to meet the sea level on the extra day of a leap year month, and the boat can then return, but only for the time it takes to eat a meal. If it's slightly delayed, it won't be able to go up again. When those people were first blown here by a typhoon, there were also people living in this harbor. Later, they encountered the leap tide and returned. They were too late and stayed here for six years, repeatedly encountering the leap tide but missing the timing, and thus could not leave.

    Yang and his forty fellow travelers carried various types of grains and vegetables. They all divided the land and cultivated it. The land was quite fertile and yielded double, and did not require human irrigation. They spent the whole day interacting and coming and going with the people from the previous boat, almost forgetting that they were in the otherworld. Regretfully, there was no calendar to check the day and time. Every time after eating, they all boarded the boat and waited for the water to rise.

    One day, Yang and his guests took a leisurely walk in the wild. They saw people walking near the creek mouth on the other side of the stream. All were over ten feet tall, naked, with hair on their bodies, feet like chicken claws, and shins like ox knees. Seeing Yang, they chattered and gestured as if conversing, but the sound was incomprehensible. They returned and told the people from the previous boat, who also said they had seen them at the creek mouth when they came, but because the stream was full and they couldn't cross, they were worried. If they come here, will any of us survive?

    640

    后六年八月，遇风水满，与前舟人同归。杨家有老仆曾随行者，今已八十余，尚在，能道其详。按台湾有鸡爪番，常栖宿树上，此岂其苗裔欤？

    　　○海和尚潘某，老于渔，业颇饶。一日，偕同辈撒网海滨，曳之，倍觉重于常，数人并力舁之。出网，中并无鱼，惟有六七小人趺坐，见人辄合掌作顶礼状，遍身毛如猕猴，髡其顶而无发，语言不可晓。开网纵之，皆于海面行数十步而没。土人云：“此号‘海和尚’，得而腊之，可忍饥一年。”

    　　○一足蛇谢大痴言：其友某在黔日，往一村，见民家多悬一物，鳞甲莹然，已腊而干之矣。言此去五里有山，为樵采地。山脚为往来路径，旁有枯树一株，极大。树内藏一蛇，人首驴耳，耳能扇动有声，鳞如松皮，只一足，如龙爪，吐舌甚长，跃行迅疾。近人辄以口喷毒气，令人迷仆，然后以舌入人鼻，吸血饮之。村人募丐者，予以金，除其患，无有应者。

    Explanation

    640

    Six years later, in the eighth month, they encountered a sudden rise in water level and returned with the previous boatmen. The Yang family had an old servant who had accompanied them on the journey; he is now over eighty years old, still alive, and able to recount the details. According to legend, there are the Chicken Claw Tribe in Taiwan, who often rest in trees. Could these be their descendants?

    ○ A certain Mr. Pan, who has been fishing for a long time and is quite prosperous, one day went with his peers to cast nets along the seashore. When they pulled the nets, they found them unusually heavy. Several people together managed to haul them up. Upon opening the nets, there were no fish inside, but instead, six or seven small figures were sitting cross-legged. They greeted people by joining their hands in a gesture of respect, had hair like a macaque, were bald on top, and spoke in an incomprehensible language. When they released the figures, they all walked on the sea surface for several steps before disappearing. The locals said, "These are called 'Sea Monks.' If captured and dried, they can sustain hunger for a year."

    ○ Xie Dachi recounted: A friend of his, while in Guizhou, visited a village where he saw many households hanging an object with shiny scales, already dried and preserved. He was told that five miles from there was a mountain used for gathering firewood. At the foot of the mountain was a path frequently traveled, beside which stood a large, withered tree. Inside the tree lived a snake with a human head and donkey ears, whose ears could flap and make a sound. Its scales were like pine bark, it had only one foot shaped like a dragon's claw, a very long tongue, and moved quickly. When it approached people, it would spray toxic gas from its mouth, causing them to become dazed and fall, and then it would insert its tongue into their nostrils to suck blood. The villagers tried to recruit beggars, offering them money to deal with the problem, but no one responded.

    641

    逾年，有二丐应命，索重酬，众为醵金如其数。其人取唾涎厚涂其身，裸而诱之。蛇果至，则急趋道旁田内。蛇追及之，陷于泥中，不能动。然后二丐跃起，以长竿扎刀尽力斫之，断其首，乃死。村民家有被其害者，争分其肉。

    　　○方蚌有人在闽出海口樵采，至一山，见山涧内悉卧方蚌：大者丈许，小者亦长数尺，重叠，以千百计。其人惊，方欲去，忽一蚌开口，其壳内有蓝面人，如夜叉状，卧其中。见人，手足皆动，作攫拿势，欲起而不得脱，盖其躯生壳上，即借蚌壳为背，故不能脱壳而出。少顷，众蚌悉张口，皆有夜叉如前状，其人仓皇急窜，闻背后剥剥有声，众蚌皆旋滚随之。及舟，舟中人斫以巨斧，获其一，并壳俱碎，夜叉亦死。带归示人，俱无知者。

    Explanation

    641

    After a year, two beggars responded to the call, demanding a substantial reward. The villagers pooled their money as requested. The beggars coated their bodies with saliva and lured the snake with their nakedness. When the snake arrived, they quickly rushed to the side of the road into the fields. The snake pursued them, becoming stuck in the mud and unable to move. Then the beggars leaped up, wielding long poles with blades and struck the snake with all their might, severing its head and killing it. Villagers who had been harmed by the snake competed to divide the meat.

    ○ A person from Fujian, who went out to sea for firewood, reached a mountain and saw countless flat clams lying in the stream: some as large as a few feet, others as long as several feet, piled up in layers, numbering in the hundreds. The person was startled and was about to leave when suddenly one clam opened, revealing a blue-faced figure inside, resembling a night demon, lying within. Upon seeing a person, the figure's limbs moved, making a grabbing motion, trying to rise but unable to escape. Its body grew on the shell, using the clam shell as a back, so it could not emerge from the shell. Shortly after, all the clams opened, each containing a night demon as described. The person fled in panic, hearing a peeling sound behind them as the clams rolled and followed. Upon reaching the boat, the people on board struck one of the clams with a large axe, breaking both the shell and the night demon inside. They brought the remains back to show others, but no one knew what had happened.

    642

    ○山和尚有李姓者客中州，遇大水，登山避之。水势骤涨，其人更上山顶。时已暮，见矮草屋，乃山民耕在夜巡者所居，内悉藉以草，旁置一竹梆，其人宿焉。中夜，闻踏水声，视之，见一黑短胖和尚游水面将至。其人大呼，此怪稍却，少顷又前。

    　　其人窘急，取梆大击。山民都集，怪遂去，终夜不复至。次日水退，询山人，云：“山和尚也，斯人孤弱，便食人脑。”

    　　○赠纸灰杭州捕快某，偕其子缉贼，每过夜子不归。其父心疑，遣徒伺之。见其子在荒草中谈笑，少顷，走至攒屋内，解下衣，抱一朽棺作交媾状。其徒大呼，其子惊起，不得已，系裤带随其徒归，然精犹淋漓不止。抚其阳，冷如冰雪，直至小腹。其母问之，曰：“儿某夜乞火小屋，见美妇人挑我，与我有终身之计，以故成婚月馀，且赠我白银五十两。”母骂曰：“鬼安得有银？”少年取怀中包掷几上，铿然有声，视之，纸灰也。访诸邻人，云：“攒屋中乃一新死孀妇。”

    Explanation

    642

    A Mountain Monk and a Gift of Ashes

    There was a man surnamed Li, a guest in the Central Plains, who encountered a flood and climbed a mountain to avoid it. The water level rose suddenly, and the man climbed even higher to the top of the mountain. It was already evening when he saw a small thatched house, which was the dwelling of a mountain farmer who patrolled at night. Inside, everything was covered with grass, and beside it was placed a bamboo drum. The man stayed there for the night. In the middle of the night, he heard the sound of someone walking on the water. Looking, he saw a short, fat monk with dark skin floating on the water, approaching. The man shouted loudly, and the strange being retreated slightly, but soon came forward again. The man was in a desperate situation and struck the bamboo drum hard. The mountain farmers gathered, and the strange being left, never returning for the rest of the night. The next day, when the water receded, he asked the mountain farmers. They said, "It was a mountain monk, a weak and solitary being who eats human brains."

    A Gift of Ashes

    In Hangzhou, a police officer named Mr. Zhang accompanied his son in pursuing a thief. Every night after midnight, his son would not return home. His father became suspicious and sent a subordinate to watch him. The subordinate saw the son talking and laughing in the wild grass. After a short while, the son walked to a pile of old houses, took off his clothes, and embraced a decayed coffin, making a coitus position. The subordinate shouted loudly, and the son was startled awake. Unable to avoid it, he tied his pants and followed the subordinate back home, but his semen continued to flow. When they touched his penis, it was as cold as ice, extending down to his abdomen. His mother asked him about it, and he said, "One night, I borrowed a fire from a small house and saw a beautiful woman who tempted me, promising to have a lifelong relationship with me. As a result, we were married for over a month, and she also gave me fifty taels of silver." His mother scolded him, saying, "How can a ghost have silver?" The young man took a package from his chest and threw it onto the table, making a clanging sound. When they looked inside, it was ashes. They asked the neighbors, who said, "The pile of old houses was where a recently deceased widow lived."

    643

    ○汤翰林钱塘汤翰林其五，未遇时，应试贡院，僦屋而居，苦其狭小。见旁有大宅，封锁甚固，杳无人居。访之邻人，云：“此杭州太守柴公屋也，有恶鬼作祟，以故无人承买。”汤素有胆，曰：“借居可乎？”邻人笑其狂，亦无阻者。汤遂开锁启门入，见楼上有二桌四椅，楼西有竹箱。虽久无人居，而尘埃不积。汤心喜，即挈行李登楼，手一壶一棍，秉烛读书。

    　　至三鼓，阴风起于窗外，灯焰缩小，有披发女子赤身喷血而进。汤挥以棍，女惘然曰：“贵人在此，妾误矣。”仍从窗出。汤喜鬼已出，将解衣安寝。忽楼西厢内簌簌有声，视之，则此女从西厢出，手持裙袄艳色衣并梳蓖等物，若将膏沐者。汤愈无恐，且饮且读书。

    Explanation

    643

    The Fifth Hanlin Scholar of Tang, while not yet successful, was taking the examination at the imperial examination hall. He rented a house nearby, finding it too narrow and small. He saw a large mansion beside him, which was tightly sealed and completely uninhabited. He asked the neighbors, who told him, "This is the house of Governor Zhai of Hangzhou. It is plagued by evil spirits, so no one has bought it." Tang, known for his courage, said, "May I stay here?" The neighbors laughed at his madness but did not stop him. Tang then unlocked and opened the door, entering the house. On the second floor, he saw two tables and four chairs, and on the west side, a bamboo chest. Although the house had been empty for a long time, no dust had accumulated. Tang was pleased and immediately brought his luggage upstairs, holding a pot and a stick, and began reading by candlelight.

    At the third drumbeat, a cold wind rose from outside the window, and the flame of the lamp shrank. A woman with long hair, naked, and spitting blood entered. Tang hit her with his stick, and she said in a dazed manner, "A noble person is here; I made a mistake." She still went out through the window. Tang was happy that the ghost had left and was about to undress and go to sleep. Suddenly, there was a rustling sound inside the western room. Looking inside, he saw the same woman emerging from the western room, holding a skirt, a bright-colored dress, and other grooming items, as if she were about to apply makeup. Tang became even less afraid and continued to drink and read.

    644

    有顷，女子梳妆毕，着艳衣。冉冉至前跪诉曰：“妾负奇冤，非公不能为我白者。妾姓朱，名笔花，杭州柴太守妾也。正妻妒而狡，知太守爱妾，不敢加害。

    　　值妾产子时，贿收生婆于落胎后将生桐油涂我产宫，溃烂而亡。妾儿名某，正妻取以为子，至今虽长成，并不知为妾之子。十年后，君为湖北主考，子当出公门下，公须以妾冤告之。妾尸犹埋此楼之东墙井边，有八角砖为记，可命其来此改葬生母。“并指竹箱曰：”此皆妾藏首饰奁具处也。妾亡时，太守哀痛之至，临去吩咐家人，勿持我箱还家，恐触目心伤故也。后有来窃取者，妾以阴风喝退之，今此中尚存三百金，可以奉赠。“汤为惨然，唯唯而已，后一如其言。楼上怪从此绝，而屋亦转售。

    Explanation

    644

    After a short while, the woman finished dressing and put on a gorgeous dress. She slowly approached and knelt down to speak, saying, "I bear an extraordinary injustice, and only you can clear my name. My name is Zhu Bi Hua, and I was the concubine of the governor of Hangzhou. The principal wife was jealous and cunning, knowing that the governor loved me, she did not dare to harm me directly.

    When I gave birth to a child, she bribed the midwife to smear tung oil on my birth canal after a miscarriage, causing me to die from infection and decay. My son is named 'Mou,' and the principal wife took him and raised him as her own. Even though he has grown up, he does not know he is my son. Ten years later, you will be the chief examiner in Hubei, and my son will be among those taking the examination. You must inform him of my unjust death. My body is still buried near the eastern wall of this building by a well, marked by an octagonal brick. You can instruct him to come here to properly reburial his mother." She pointed to a bamboo box and said, "This is where I kept my jewelry and other personal items. When I died, the governor was so grief-stricken that he instructed the servants not to bring my belongings home, fearing that seeing them would cause him too much pain. Later, anyone who tried to steal from the box was driven away by my spirit with a supernatural wind. Now, there are still 300 taels of gold inside, which I offer as a gift." The narrator was somber and nodded in agreement, following her instructions as promised. From then on, the strange occurrences on the upper floor ceased, and the house was sold.

    645

    ○黑苗洞湖南房县，在万山之中。西北八百里，皆丛山怪岭，苗洞以千数，无人敢入。

    　　有采樵者误入洞内，迷路不能出，见数黑人浑身生毛，语兜离似鸟，以草结巢，栖于树巅。见樵人，喜，以藤缚其手足，挂于树梢。樵者自分死矣。

    　　俄而，一老妪从他巢中来，白发高颡，略似人形，言语犹作楚声，谓樵者曰：“汝何误入此洞耶？我亦房县城中人。康熙某年年荒，乞食迷入此洞。诸黑苗初欲食我，后摸我下体，知为女，遂留居巢中为妻。”指二黑毛人曰：“此我儿也，尚听我说话，我当救汝。”樵人感谢。老人妪腾身上树，亲解其缚，袖中出栗枣数枚曰：“为汝疗饥。”随向二黑毛人耳语良久，语呶呶莫辨，手树枝一条，缚布巾于上曰：“有尔等同类欲害我乡邻者，以此示之，俾知我意。”

    Explanation

    645

    Black Miao Cave in Fang County, Hubei, lies amidst countless mountains. Eight hundred miles to the northwest, the terrain is densely forested with strange and rugged peaks, and there are thousands of Miao caves, none of which dare to be entered.

    One day, a woodcutter accidentally entered the cave and became lost, unable to find his way out. He saw several dark-skinned people covered in hair, speaking in a language that sounded like bird calls, building nests with grass, and living in the treetops. When they saw the woodcutter, they were pleased and tied his hands and feet with vines, hanging him from a tree branch. The woodcutter thought he was doomed.

    Soon after, an old woman came from another nest. She had white hair and a high forehead, resembling a human form, and spoke in a language with a Chu accent. She said to the woodcutter, "Why did you accidentally enter this cave? I too am from Fang County. During a famine in the Kangxi era, I was begging for food and accidentally entered this cave. The Black Miao initially wanted to eat me, but later, upon touching my lower body, they realized I was a woman and kept me as a wife." She pointed to two black-haired people and said, "These are my children. They still listen to what I say, and I will save you." The woodcutter expressed his gratitude. The old woman climbed up the tree, personally untied his bonds, and pulled out a few chestnuts and dates from her sleeve, saying, "Here, to relieve your hunger." She then whispered to the two black-haired people for a long time, her words indistinct, and handed them a branch with a cloth tied to it. She said, "If there are any of your kind who wish to harm my neighbors, show this to them so they know my intention."

    646

    二毛人送樵人，行三日许，才得原路归。路上人皆曰：“此黑苗洞也，迷入者都被其啖，从无归者。”

    　　○空中扯辨芜湖江口巡司衙门弓兵赵信，年三十馀，尚未娶妻。忽一日往野庙中，留连笑语，不肯归家。人问之，则曰：“吾赘于某氏矣。”极夸其妻之美、家之富。

    　　次日又往，嬉笑如常。人与同行，毫无所见，知为鬼所弄，乃嘱其父母苦禁之，闭门而通饮食焉。赵在房呼曰：“我来我来，勿扯我辫！”

    Explanation

    646

    An elderly person guided a woodcutter, traveling for three days before finally returning to the original path. People on the road all said, "This is the Black Miao Cave. Those who get lost in it are all eaten by the inhabitants, and none have ever returned."

    In the air, Zhao Xin, a bowman in the local patrol office at Wuhu's river mouth, was in his thirties and still unmarried. One day, he went to a wild temple, lingered, laughed, and refused to return home. When people asked him, he said, "I have married into a family." He greatly praised his wife's beauty and his family's wealth.

    The next day, he went again, laughing and joking as usual. When people accompanied him, they saw nothing, realizing he was being tricked by spirits. They advised his parents to strictly confine him, providing food and drink through the door. Zhao called out from his room, "I'm coming, I'm coming, don't pull my hair!"

    647

    家人在窗眼中密窥之，见其头上辫发直竖空中，似有人提之者，于是防范愈严。三日后，声响寂然。开户视之，竟以辫发自缢床栏干上。

    　　○蓬头鬼泾县于道士能白日视鬼。常往城中赵氏家饮酒，密语主人曰：“君家西楼内夹墙有鬼蓬头走出，东窥西探，状如窃贼，必是冤谴有所擒捉，但未知应在府中何人？”主人曰：“何以验之？”道士曰：“我明日早来，看鬼藏何处，即便告君。君可唤家人一一走过，看鬼作何形状，便见分晓。”主人以为然。

    　　次日，道士来曰：“鬼在西厅案桌脚下。”主人召集家丁往来桌前，鬼皆不理；其女六姑娘过，鬼向之大笑。道士曰：“此其是矣，然且勿通知令爱，虑其惊怖也。”主人问：“可禳解否？”曰：“此生前孽，无可禳也。”自后闻抛砖掷瓦之声，月馀不绝。俄而，六姑娘以产亡，家果平静。

    Explanation

    647

    The family members secretly peeked through the window eye and saw that the queue on his head stood straight up in the air, as if someone was holding it. They then took even stricter precautions. Three days later, there was no sound at all. When they opened the door, they found that he had hanged himself with his queue on the bed rail.

    The Daoist priest from Jing County, who could see ghosts during the day, often went to Mr. Zhao's home in the city to drink. He whispered to the host, "There is a ghost with disheveled hair emerging from the partition wall inside the western pavilion of your home. It peeks east and west, looking like a thief. It must be a wronged soul being captured, but it is unknown which person in the household it will affect." The host asked, "How can we verify this?" The Daoist priest said, "I will come early tomorrow to see where the ghost is hiding and inform you. You can call the family members one by one to walk past, and see what the ghost does, and then we will know for sure." The host agreed.

    The next day, the Daoist priest came and said, "The ghost is under the table in the west hall." The host gathered the family members and had them walk past the table, but the ghost paid no attention. When the host's daughter, Sixth Miss, passed by, the ghost laughed at her. The Daoist priest said, "This must be it, but do not inform your daughter, as you might frighten her." The host asked, "Can this be dispelled or resolved?" The Daoist priest replied, "This is a sin from a past life, and there is nothing that can be done to dispel it." After that, they heard sounds of throwing bricks and tiles for more than a month without ceasing. Soon after, Sixth Miss died during childbirth, and the household indeed became peaceful.

    648

    ○借丝绵入殓芜湖赵明府必恭，宰湖南衡阳，伤寒病剧，气已绝矣。家人棺殓，绵絮无一不周，因其心口尚温，故尔未殓。赵梦行黄沙中，茫茫然不见天日，过一小河，天渐天朗，有庙题曰“准提观音庵”。走入，见老僧趺坐煮素面甚香，觉腹中饥，向僧乞食。僧喝曰：“汝何必在此乞食？可作速还家，家中有面等汝！”

    　　赵踉跄走出，遇乡邻吴某拱手谢曰：“蒙君见惠，使我体暖。”赵不解所云，惊而醒，果闻素面如庵中之香，盖家人守尸，镇日不饭，故煮面充饥。赵即索食，家人曰：“老爷病月馀，汤水不沾，何能吃面耶？”赵必欲取食，家人无如何，与一瓯，竟饮啖如常，而病亦愈。心中想吴某谢暖之说，乱梦无征，绝不向家人言及。

    Explanation

    648

    The Governor Zhao Mingfu Biquan of Hengyang in Hunan Province, who had fallen gravely ill with typhoid fever and whose breath had already ceased, was being prepared for burial. His family had carefully wrapped him in every piece of cotton and wool, but since his chest was still warm, they had not yet buried him. In his dream, Zhao Mingfu Biquan walked through a vast expanse of yellow sand, with no sight of the sky, and crossed a small river where the sky gradually cleared. He saw a temple inscribed with "Zun Ti Guanyin Temple." Entering the temple, he saw an old monk sitting cross-legged, cooking vegetarian noodles that smelled very fragrant. Feeling hungry, he asked the monk for food. The monk shouted, "Why should you beg here? You can quickly return home; your family has noodles waiting for you!"

    Zhao Mingfu Biquan stumbled out and met a neighbor named Wu, who bowed and thanked him, saying, "I am grateful for your kindness; it made my body warm." Zhao Mingfu Biquan did not understand what he meant and woke up, finding the vegetarian noodles as fragrant as those in the temple. It turned out that the family had been guarding the body and not eating all day, so they cooked noodles to eat. Zhao Mingfu Biquan insisted on eating, and the family, unable to refuse, gave him a bowl. He finally ate and drank as usual, and his illness also improved. He thought about Wu's words of gratitude, but as it was just a dream, he did not mention it to the family.

    649

    后二年，赵眷属还芜，将昔年作殓之绵装箱带归。适吴某死，当盛夏，无处买绵，其家殓时来借丝绵，乃即与之。

    　　又三年，赵罢官归，偶与家人谈及前事，方知千里之外，两年之前，此绵应归吴用，生魂早来谢矣。

    　　○洞庭君留船凡洞庭湖载货之船，卸货后，每年必有一整齐精洁之船，千夫拉曳不动。舟人皆知之，曰：“此洞庭君所留也。”便听其所之，不复装货。舵工水手，俱往别船生活。至夜，则神灯炫赫，出入波浪中；清晨，仍归原泊之处。年年船只轮换当差，从无专累一家者，亦从无撞折损伤者。

    　　○缆将军失势鄱阳湖客舟遇风，常有黑缆如龙扑舟而来，舟必损伤，号“缆将军”，年年致祭。雍正十年，大旱，湖水干处，有朽缆横卧沙上。农人斫而烧之，涎尽血出。

    Explanation

    649

    Two years later, Zhao's relatives returned to Wu, taking the silk used for burial back in a box. Just as Wu died, during the hot summer, there was no place to buy silk. When his family came to borrow silk for the burial, it was then given to them.

    Three years later, Zhao returned home after being dismissed from office. During a casual conversation with family members about past events, it was only then known that the silk should have been used for Wu two years ago, and that Wu's soul had already come to thank them.

    The Lord of Dongting Leaves a Ship
    Every year, there is a ship in Dongting Lake that is left by the Lord of Dongting. Even a thousand people cannot move it. All boatmen are aware of this phenomenon, saying, "This is the ship left by the Lord of Dongting." They let it go where it will and do not load it again. The helmsman and crew all go to other boats to live. At night, a divine lantern appears, moving in and out of the waves; in the morning, it returns to its original mooring place. Every year, the ships involved in this phenomenon are rotated, with no single family being permanently burdened, and no damage occurring.

    The Loss of Power of the Cable General
    A guest boat on Poyang Lake often encounters wind, and there is frequently a black cable like a dragon rushing towards the boat, causing damage. This phenomenon is called the "Cable General" and is worshipped annually. During the tenth year of Yongzheng's reign, during a severe drought, an old cable was found lying on the sand in the dried-up lake. Farmers cut and burned it, with blood appearing after the saliva is gone.

    650

    从此，缆将军不复作祟，而舵工亦不复致祭矣。

    　　○吴二姑娘全椒金棕亭进士，寓扬州马氏玲珑山馆。孙某，年十七，文学颇佳，相随读书，祖孙隔房而寝。夜间懵呼声，以为魇也，起视唤之，孙即醒悟。棕亭还卧己房。未几又魇，棕亭再往，其孙业已起坐床上，对棕亭，以两手向上，曰：“请屈一指。”则一指弯。曰：“请屈五指。”则五指弯。自后或叉手，或拱手，作态万状。棕亭呵之，泣求还家见母，乃呼轿送归。

    　　病者自取衣冠靴带着之，请祖父母上坐，拜别曰：“儿即登仙去矣。”举家惶惑，莫知所为。日午，神气稍定，私拉乃祖耳语曰：“无他，一小狐狸闹我耳。”

    　　语毕，瞀乱如初。自称：“吴二姑娘与我前世有缘，”或云：“妹子吴三姑娘也来了。姊妹二人要同嫁我。”随作淫秽语，令人难闻。拉棕亭向前，呵气一口，其冷如冰，从鼻管直到丹田，毛发皆噤。

    Explanation

    650

    From then on, the cable general ceased his mischief, and the rudderman also stopped making offerings.

    ○ Wu Ernyang, a female spirit, was once the wife of Jin Zongting, a scholar and official from Quanjiao, who stayed at the Lenglong Shanguan in Yangzhou. His grandson, aged seventeen, was quite talented in literature and studied alongside him. They slept in separate rooms. One night, he heard incoherent muttering, thinking it was a nightmare, he got up and called to him, and the grandson woke up. Jin Zongting returned to his own room. Soon after, the grandson had another nightmare, and Jin Zongting went to see him again. The grandson was already sitting on the bed, facing Jin Zongting, and raised his hands, saying, "Please bend one finger." One finger bent. He said, "Please bend five fingers." All five fingers bent. Afterwards, he made various gestures, sometimes crossing his hands, sometimes bowing, acting in countless ways. Jin Zongting rebuked him, and the grandson cried, asking to return home to see his mother. Jin Zongting then called for a carriage to send him back.

    The sick grandson took his clothes and shoes, dressed himself, and invited his grandparents to sit, bowing farewell and saying, "I am about to ascend to immortality." The whole family was in confusion, not knowing what to do. By noon, his spirit was a bit calmer, and he privately pulled his grandfather's ear, whispering, "There's nothing else; a little fox is bothering me."

    After he finished speaking, he became confused again. He claimed, "Wu Ernyang has a karmic connection with me from a past life," or said, "My sister Wu Sanlang has also come. The two of us want to marry me together." He then spoke obscene words, which were hard to hear. He pulled Jin Zongting forward, exhaled a breath, which was as cold as ice, from his nasal passage to his lower abdomen, making all his hair stand on end.

    651

    镇江蒋春农中翰赠天师符一张，方欲张挂，而病者遽来抢夺，幸系绫本，爪掐不伤。棕亭张符向之，又被吹冷气一口，符飞窗外，绫竟碎裂。棕亭不得已，求祷城隍庙、关帝庙。数日，忽病者呼：“接驾接驾，伏魔大帝至矣。”

    　　棕亭悚然，率家人齐跪。病者呼棕亭名骂曰：“金兆燕，汝身为进士，而脱帽露顶，不穿公服迎我，有是理乎！”棕亭叩头谢罪。少顷，复呼：“接驾，接驾，孔圣人至矣。”棕亭又叩头迎接。文、武二圣，相与共语，嚅嚅不可辨，皆在病者口中作山东、山西两处人口吻，如是者自午及申。举家长跪哀求，不敢起立，腿脚皆肿。病者厉声曰：“妖魔已斩，封尔孙为上真诸侯，吾当去也！”棕亭叩送毕，进病者粥。病者向空招手曰：“吃粥！吃粥！”狂言如故。棕亭大悟，文、武二圣，皆妖冒充。责病者曰：“我年逾六十，从未受人欺哄，今乃为汝揶揄耶！”病者缩首内向掩口而笑，作得意状，颠狂月余。

    Explanation

    651

    The scholar-official Jiang Chun'ong presented a Daoist talisman to Zongting, intending to hang it up. However, the patient suddenly snatched it, fortunately the talisman was on a silk scroll, and the nails did not damage it. Zongting was about to place the talisman, but it was blown away by a cold breath from the patient, and the silk scroll was torn into pieces. Faced with this situation, Zongting had no choice but to pray at the Temple of the City God and the Temple of Guan Yu.

    After several days, the patient suddenly shouted, "Welcome! Welcome! The Emperor Who Subdues Demons has arrived!"

    Zongting was startled and led his family to kneel together. The patient called out Zongting's name and scolded, "Jin Zhaoyan, you are an imperial scholar, yet you dare to remove your official cap and greet me without wearing your official robes. Is this proper?"

    Zongting knelt down and apologized. Shortly after, the patient shouted again, "Welcome! Welcome! Confucius, the Sage of Literature, has arrived!" Zongting knelt down again to welcome him. The Two Sages, Confucius and Guan Yu, conversed together, mumbling in a way that was difficult to understand. Their voices came from the patient's mouth, alternating between the dialects of Shandong and Shanxi, like this until the afternoon.

    The entire family knelt in awe, unable to stand up, their legs and feet swollen. The patient then shouted loudly, "The demons have been defeated, I will confer the title of 'Shangzhen Hou' (a high-ranking official or noble) upon your descendant, and now I must leave!" Zongting knelt to send them off. He then brought the patient some porridge. The patient waved his hand towards the air and said, "Eat the porridge! Eat the porridge!" His words were still delirious as before.

    Zongting suddenly realized that the Two Sages were actually demons impersonating them. He rebuked the patient, "I am over sixty years old, and I have never been deceived by anyone. How could I be mocked by you now?" The patient turned his head inward, covered his mouth, and laughed, showing a look of satisfaction. The patient continued to act wildly and madly for more than a month.

    652

    有林道士者来，言拜斗可以禳遣。棕亭于是设坛斋醮，终日诵经。如是七日，病者神气渐清，乃急为完姻，入赘岳家，妖果不至。此乾隆四十七年三月间事，棕亭先生亲为余言。

    　　○石狮求救命广东潮州府东门外，每行人过，闻唤救命声。察之，四面无人，声从地下出。

    　　疑是死人更活，持锄掘之。下土三尺许，有石狮子被蟒围其颈，众大骇，即击杀蟒，而扛石狮于庙中。土人有所祈祷，灵验异常。或不敬信，登时降祸。自此香火大盛。

    Explanation

    652

    A Taoist priest named Lin came and said that worshipping the Big Dipper could avert misfortune. Mr. Zongting then set up an altar, performed a ritual, and recited scriptures throughout the day. After seven days, the patient's spirit gradually cleared, so they hastily completed the marriage, and the man became a son-in-law to his wife's family. The evil did not come. This was an event that occurred in the third month of the 47th year of the Qianlong Emperor's reign, and Mr. Zongting personally told me about it.

    A stone lion in the eastern gate of Chaozhou Prefecture in Guangdong province would call for help every time a passerby went by. Upon investigation, there were no people around, and the sound came from underground. They suspected that the dead had come back to life and dug with a hoe. Three feet below the surface, there was a stone lion with a python coiled around its neck. The crowd was terrified, killed the python, and carried the stone lion to the temple. The local people prayed to the stone lion, which was unusually effective. Those who did not show respect or faith would face immediate misfortune. From then on, the temple's incense offerings became very popular.

    653

    太守方公闻之，以为妖异，将毁其庙，民众哓哓，几激成变。太守不得已，诡言迎石狮入城，将别为立庙，众方应允。舁至演武场，钅追碎石狮，投之河中，了无他异。太守方公名应元，湖南巴陵人。

    　　余按晋元康中，吴郡怀瑶家地下闻吠声，掘之，得二犬。长老云：“此名犀犬，得者其家富昌。”事载《异苑》。

    　　○旱魃乾隆二十六年，京师大旱。有健步张贵为某都统递公文至良乡，漏下出城，行至无人处，忽黑风卷起，吹灭其烛，因避雨邮亭。有女子持灯来，年可十七八，貌殊美，招至其家，饮以茶，为缚其马于柱，愿与同宿。健步喜出望外，绸缪达旦。鸡鸣时，女披衣起，留之不可，健步体疲，乃复酣寝。梦中觉露寒其鼻，草刺其口。天色微明，方知身卧荒冢间，大惊牵马，马缚在树上，所投文书，已误期限五十刻。

    Explanation

    653

    Final Translation

    Governor Fang Gong heard of the matter and considered it a strange and supernatural phenomenon. He intended to destroy its temple, but the people protested loudly, almost inciting a disturbance. Having no choice, Governor Fang Gong lied and said he would welcome the stone lion into the city, planning to build a separate temple for it, and the people agreed. The stone lion was carried to the military drill ground, crushed, and thrown into the river, and nothing else unusual happened. Governor Fang Gong's name was Fang Yingyuan, and he was from Balin, Hunan.

    According to my research, during the Yuan Kang period of the Western Jin dynasty, a family in Wu Prefecture heard barking underground and dug it up, finding two dogs. The elders said, "These are called rhinoceros dogs. Whoever possesses them will have a prosperous family." This story is recorded in The Strange Realm.

    Drought Demon

    In the 26th year of the Qianlong era, the capital suffered a severe drought. There was a swift-footed man named Zhang Gui who was delivering official documents for a certain general to Liangxiang. After the drum struck for the night watch, he left the city and walked to an uninhabited area. Suddenly, a black wind arose and blew out his candle. He took shelter at a post station, where a woman came with a lamp. She was about seventeen or eighteen years old and extremely beautiful. She invited him to her home, gave him tea, tied his horse to a pillar, and wished to share the night with him. The swift-footed man was overjoyed beyond expectation and spent the night in close companionship. At cockcrow, the woman put on her clothes and got up, but could not be persuaded to stay. The swift-footed man was exhausted and fell asleep again. In his dream, he felt the cold dew on his nose and grass pricked his mouth. The sky was just beginning to lighten, and he realized he was lying in a deserted grave. He was greatly alarmed and pulled his horse, which was tied to a tree. The document he was supposed to deliver was now fifty kē late.

    654

    官司行查至本都统，虑有捺搁情弊，都统命佐领严讯，健步具道所以。都统命访其坟，知为张姓女子，未嫁与人通奸，事发，羞忿自缢，往往魇祟路人。

    　　或曰：“此旱魃也。猱形披发一足行者，为兽魃；缢死尸僵出迷人者，为鬼魃。获而焚之，足以致雨。”乃奏明启棺，果一女僵尸，貌如生，遍体生白毛。

    　　焚之，次日大雨。

    　　○蝎怪佟明府宰芮城，有乡民夏间袒背坐石上，持面一碗，食未毕，忽大呼仆地而绝。众人视之，背正中有洞，深数寸，黑气泉涌，不知何疾也。具呈报官，疑为卖面人所毒。佟公往验，见所坐石旁有罅，黑血流入罅中，其下若有呼嘬声，乃命掘石。下三尺许，石穴中有蝎，如鹅大，方仰首饮血，尾弯环作金色。乡民争持犁锄击之，蝎死而尾不损。以验死者之背，伤痕宛然，乃以蝎尾贮库。至今犹存。

    Explanation

    654

    Officials conducted an investigation up to the current governor-general, considering there might be suppression or obstruction of the truth. The governor-general ordered the assistant commander to conduct a strict interrogation, and the messenger reported the reason in detail. The governor-general then ordered the grave to be investigated, and it was known that she was an unmarried Zhang surname woman who had an affair with someone, and when the affair was discovered, she hanged herself out of shame and anger. She often haunted passersby.

    Some said, "This is a drought demon. A demon with a monkey-like shape, long hair, and one foot is a beast demon; a ghost demon is one who dies by hanging, stiffens, and leads people astray. If captured and burned, it can bring rain." They reported and the coffin was opened, revealing a female zombie that looked as if alive and covered in white hair all over the body.

    It was burned, and the next day there was heavy rain.

    In the county of Rui under the jurisdiction of Mingfu Tong, a local magistrate, a villager sat with his back exposed on a stone during summer, holding a bowl of noodles. Before finishing eating, he suddenly cried out and fell dead. Upon examination, there was a hole in the center of his back, several inches deep, with black gas gushing out. No one knew what illness it was. The villager reported to the official in detail, suspecting that he was poisoned by the noodle seller. Mingfu Tong went to investigate, saw a crack beside the stone where black blood flowed in, and there seemed to be a sucking sound below. He then ordered the stone to be dug up. About three feet down, in a stone cave, there was a scorpion as big as a goose, drinking blood with its head raised, its tail was bent in a golden ring. The villagers fought to hit it with plows and hoes, the scorpion died but its tail remained intact. To examine the dead man's back, the wounds were clearly visible. Then, the scorpion's tail was stored in the treasury. It is still preserved today.

    655

    ○蛇王楚地有蛇王者，状类帝江，无耳目爪鼻，但有口；其形方如肉柜，浑浑而行，所过处草木尽枯；以口作吸吞状，则巨蟒恶蛇尽为舌底之水，而肉柜愈觉膨然大矣。

    　　有常州叶某者，兄弟二人，游巴陵道上，见群蛇如风而趋，若有所避。已而腥风愈甚，二人怖，避树上。少顷，见肉柜正方，如猬而无刺，身不甚大，从东方来。其弟挟矢射之，正中柜面，柜如不知，负矢而行。射者下树，将近此物之身，欲再射之。拔其矢，而身已仆矣，良久不起。乃兄下树视之，尸化为黑水。

    　　洞庭有老渔者曰：“我能擒蛇王。”众大骇，问之，曰：“作百余个面馒头，用长竿铁叉叉之送当其口。彼略吸，则去之而易新者，如是数十次。其初馒头霉烂如泥，已而黑，已而黄，已而微。伺馒头之色白如故，而后众人围而杀之，如豚犬耳，不能噬人。”众试之，果如其言。

    Explanation

    655

    The King of Snakes in Chu Land

    In the Chu region, there was a king of snakes whose appearance resembled the mythical creature Di Jiang. It had no ears, eyes, claws, or nose, but only a mouth. Its shape was square, like a meat cabinet, and it moved in a hazy, undefined manner. Wherever it passed, plants and trees withered. When it opened its mouth in a sucking and swallowing manner, all large and venomous snakes were turned into water under its tongue, and the meat cabinet-like body became even more swollen.

    There were two brothers from Changzhou who were traveling on the road to Ba Ling. They saw a group of snakes rushing like the wind, as if avoiding something. Soon after, the foul wind became even stronger, and the two brothers were frightened, climbing a tree. After a while, they saw the square meat cabinet-like figure, resembling a porcupine without spines, with a body that was not very large, coming from the east. The younger brother shot it with an arrow, hitting the cabinet directly. The cabinet seemed unaware and continued moving with the arrow stuck in it. The shooter climbed down the tree, approached the creature, and wanted to shoot it again. When he pulled out the arrow, the body collapsed and did not get up for a long time. His elder brother climbed down the tree to look at it, and the body turned into black water.

    An old fisherman from Dongting Lake said, "I can capture the king of snakes." The crowd was greatly startled and asked him. He said, "Make over a hundred steamed buns and use a long pole with an iron fork to place them in front of its mouth. If it slightly sucks, remove the old bun and replace it with a new one. Do this dozens of times. The initial buns will turn moldy like mud, then black, then yellow, and finally slightly. Wait until the bun's color returns to white, and then the crowd can surround and kill it, like killing a pig or dog, and it cannot harm people." The crowd tried it, and indeed, it was as he said.

    656

    ○颜渊为先师判狱杭州张秀才，夏月痢死，家贫无棺，从其叔乞助。叔居海宁，往返五日而苏，言至天帝所听谳，已入死案。既而曰：“诸生也”遣一官押至学宫。“请二先师出曰：”是人已有成案，然必得二师决之。“一师曰：”罪轻而情重，当死。“一师曰：”虽然，事尚可矜，渠非首谋，姑与减等，五年后改行则已。其父官岭南，有功德于民，姑押令见渠父。“命原押官押至岭南名宦祠见其父。父大呼曰：”非吾子也！“拒而不见。母夫人从室旁出泣曰：”父不汝子矣！汝当速归改过。但汝死久，恐尸坏，可归则归，否则仍返帝所，自有处分。万勿借他人尸也！“遣鬼仆同至家，觇家人肯认否。及至家，见尸尚横卧未坏，旁有一灯一饭，押者推仆尸上，尸遽动，妻子哭而惊视之，鬼仆呼曰：”认矣，可以报主母矣！“遂去。已活，人争问隐事，不言。后未五年，竟死。

    Explanation

    656

    Yan Yuan acted as a judge in a case involving Zhang Xiucai, a scholar from Hangzhou. In the summer, Zhang died from dysentery, and his family was too poor to afford a coffin. He begged his uncle for help. The uncle lived in Hai Ning, and it took five days for Zhang to return home and revive. He recounted that he had been brought to the presence of the Heavenly Emperor for judgment, and his case had already been decided with a verdict of death. However, the Emperor then said, "He is a scholar," and sent an official to take him to the Confucian academy. The official asked the two sages to come out and said, "This person has a pre-existing case, but it must be decided by the two of you." One sage said, "The crime is minor but the circumstances are severe, so he should die." The other sage said, "Although that is true, the case is somewhat pitiable, as he was not the main instigator. We will reduce the sentence, and if he reforms his behavior within five years, he will be spared. His father is an official in the south and has done much good for the people. We will take him to see his father." The official was then ordered to take Zhang to the temple dedicated to famous officials in the south to see his father. The father shouted, "This is not my son!" and refused to see him. The mother came out from the side of the room and wept, saying, "Your father no longer recognizes you! You should return quickly and reform your ways. But since you have been dead for a long time, I fear your body may decay. If you can return, do so; otherwise, go back to the Heavenly Emperor, who will deal with you accordingly. Do not use someone else's body!" She sent a ghost servant to accompany Zhang back home to see if the family would recognize him. Upon arriving home, they saw that the body was still lying there, not yet decayed, with a lamp and a meal beside it. The official pushed Zhang onto the body, and it suddenly moved. His wife and children cried out in surprise. The ghost servant called out, "He has been recognized! I can now report to the lady!" and left. Zhang had revived, and people asked him about his secret experiences, but he did not speak. Before the five years were over, Zhang died.

    657

    其从兄名纲者，毛西河友也，告西河曰：“大清兵下杭州，潞王北去，其宫眷留匿塘西孟氏家。吾弟为王某所诱，谋出首取赏，既而悔之，不列名。后同王某出首者五人，皆暴死。吾弟死而复苏，然狡性不改，与朱道士争一鹤，乃私窜道士名于海寇案中，竟致之死。负先师之训，违慈母之教，宜其终不永年也。”

    　　问：“学宫先师姓名，曾言何人？”曰：“其一颜渊，其一子服景伯。”

    　　○豆腐架箸四川茂州富户张姓者，老年生一儿，甚爱之。每出游，必盛为妆饰。年八岁，出观赛会，竟不返。遍寻至某溪中，已被杀矣，裸身卧水，衣饰尽剥去。张鸣于官，凶手不得，刺史叶公身宿城隍庙求梦。夜梦城隍神开门迎叶，置酒宴之，几上豆腐一碗，架竹箸其上，旁无余物，终席无一言。叶醒后解之，不得其故。后捕快见人持金锁入典铺者，获而讯之，赃证悉合。其人姓符，方知竹架腐上，成一“符”字。

    Explanation

    657

    His paternal uncle, named Gāng, who was a friend of Máo Xīhé, told Máo Xīhé: "The Qing army conquered Hangzhou, and the Prince of Lu fled north. His family members were hidden in the home of a person from the Meng family in Tangxi. My younger brother was lured by someone named Wang, plotting to turn himself in for a reward. Afterwards, he regretted it and did not put his name on the list. Later, five people who turned themselves in with someone named Wang all died suddenly. My younger brother died and then revived, but his deceitful nature did not change. He disputed with a Taoist priest over a crane, secretly adding the Taoist's name to a case involving pirates, ultimately causing his death. He betrayed the teachings of my former teacher and disobeyed the teachings of my kind mother. It is fitting that he did not live a long life."

    The question was asked: "What are the names of the teachers in the school, and who did  (a name) mention?" The answer was: "One was Yan Hui, and the other was Zi Fu Jingbo."

    ○ A Woman with Two Heads and Four Arms

    A wealthy Zhang family member from Maozhou in Sichuan, who was elderly, had a son whom he loved very much. Whenever he went out, he would dress the child up elaborately. At eight years old, the child went to watch a festival and never returned. The search led to the discovery of the body in a stream. The child was naked and lying in the water, all his clothes were taken away. The family reported the incident to the authorities, but the murderer was not found. The governor, Ye Gong, stayed at the City God Temple to seek a dream. At night, he dreamed that the City God opened the door to welcome him and set a feast. There was a bowl of tofu with bamboo chopsticks on it, nothing else on the table, and the entire meal was silent. After waking up, Ye Gong could not understand the meaning of the dream. Later, a policeman saw someone enter a pawnshop with a gold lock. They caught and interrogated him, and all the evidence matched. The person's surname was Fu. It was then realized that the bamboo chopsticks on the tofu formed the character 'Fu.'

    658

    ○蒋金蛾通州兴仁镇钱氏女，年及笄，适农民顾氏为妇。病卒，忽苏，呼曰：“此何地？我缘何到此？我乃常熟蒋抚台小姐，小字金娥。”细述蒋府中事，啼哭不止，拒其夫曰：“尔何人，敢近我？须遣人送我回常熟。”取镜自照，大恸曰：“此人非我，我非此人！”掷镜不复再照。
