    　　钱遣人密访蒋府，果有小姐名金娥，病卒年月相符，遂买舟送至常熟。蒋府不信，遣家人至舟中看视。妇乍见，能呼某某名姓。一时观者如堵。蒋府恐事涉怪诞，赠路费促令回通。妇素不识字，病后忽识字，能吟咏，举止娴雅，非复向时村妇样矣。

    Explanation

    658

    Jin'e, the daughter of the Qian family from Xingren Town in Tongzhou, reached the age of marriage and married a farmer named Gu. After falling ill and suddenly passing away, she revived and cried out, "Where am I? How did I end up here? I am the young lady of the Jiang family, known by the pet name Jin'e." She recounted in detail the events of the Jiang household, weeping uncontrollably and refusing her husband, saying, "Who are you to dare approach me? You must send someone to escort me back to Changshu." Taking a mirror to look at herself, she wept bitterly, saying, "This person is not me, and I am not this person!" She threw down the mirror and would no longer look at herself again.

    The Qian family sent someone to secretly investigate the Jiang household, and indeed, there was a young lady named Jin'e who had died at the same time. They then bought a boat to send her back to Changshu. The Jiang family did not believe her story and sent family members to the boat to see her. Upon seeing them, the woman could call out their names. At that moment, the viewers were as dense as a wall. The Jiang family feared that the matter involved the bizarre or supernatural and gave travel expenses to hurry her back to Tongzhou. The woman had never known how to read or write before, but after the illness, she suddenly learned to read and write, could recite poetry, and her behavior was graceful and refined, no longer the same as a village woman.

    659

    有何义门先生之侄号权之者，向曾聘蒋府女，未娶女卒因事来通，妇往见何，称为姑父。与谈旧事，一切皆能记忆，遂呼何为义父。何劝妇仍与原夫为婚，妇不肯，欲为尼，不果。此事在乾隆三十二年。

    　　○还我血刑部狱卒杨七者，与山东偷参囚某善。囚因事发，临刑，以人参赂杨，又与三十金，嘱其缝头棺殓。杨竟负约，又记人血蘸馒头可医瘵疾，遂如法取血，归奉其戚某。甫抵家，忽以两手自扼其喉大叫：“还我血！还我银！”其父母妻子烧纸钱延护救之，卒喉断而死。

    　　●卷十九○周世福山西石楼县周世福、周世禄兄弟相斗，刀戳兄腹，肠出二寸。后日久，肚上创平复如口，能翕张，肠拖于外，以锡碗覆之，束以带，大小便皆从此处出。如此三载馀方死。死之日，有鬼附家人身詈其弟云：“汝杀我，乃前生数定也，但早了数年，使我受多少污秽。”

    Explanation

    659

    There was a nephew of Mr. Yimen named Quan Zhi. He once engaged a girl from the Jiang family, but she died before they could marry. Due to some matter, he came to visit. The woman went to see him and called him her father-in-law. They talked about past events, and he remembered everything. She then called him her adoptive father. He advised her to still marry her original husband, but she refused and wanted to become a nun, but did not succeed. This event took place in the 32nd year of the Qianlong era.

    There was also a prison guard named Yang Qi from the Ministry of Justice who was friends with a prisoner from Shandong who stole ginseng. The prisoner was about to be executed due to the discovery of his crime. He gave Yang Qi ginseng and thirty gold pieces, asking him to sew the head into the coffin. Yang Qi broke the promise and remembered that human blood mixed with steamed buns could cure consumption. He followed the method to take blood and brought it back to his relative. As soon as he arrived home, he suddenly choked himself and cried out, "Return my blood! Return my money!" His family burned paper money and tried to save him, but he died by cutting his own throat.

    In the nineteenth volume, there was a man named Zhou Shifu in Shilou County, Shanxi Province. He and his brother Zhou Shilu fought with a knife, and the knife stabbed his brother's abdomen, causing two inches of his intestines to protrude. After a long time, the wound on his belly healed and became as smooth as an opening, capable of opening and closing. His intestines were dragged out externally, covered with a tin bowl, and secured with a belt. He used this opening for both urination and defecation. This condition lasted for more than three years before he died. On the day of his death, a ghost possessed a family member and scolded his brother, saying, "You killed me, it was destined by our past lives, but it happened several years too soon, making me suffer so much filth."

    660

    ○韩宗琦余甥韩宗琦，幼聪敏，五岁能读《离骚》诸书，十三风举秀才。十四岁，杨制军观风拔取超等，送入敷文书院，掌教少宗伯齐召南见而异之，曰：“此子风格非常，虑不永年耳。”

    　　己卯八月初一日清晨，忽谓其母曰：“儿昨梦得甚奇，仰见天上数百人奔波于云雾之中，有翻书簿者，有授纸笔者，状亦不一。既而闻唱名声，至三十七名，即儿名也，惊应一声而醒。所呼名字，一一分明，醒时犹能记忆，及晓披衣起，俱忘之矣”。自以为天榜有名，此科当中。

    　　及至乡试，三场毕，中秋，月明如昼，将欲缴卷，闻有人呼曰：“韩宗琦，好归去也！”如是者三，其声渐厉，若责其迟滞者。甥应曰：“诺。”及缴卷时，四顾无人，踉跄归。次日，问诸同考友，皆曰：“无之。倘我辈即欲同归，必另有称呼，岂敢竟呼兄名？”

    Explanation

    660

    My uncle's nephew, Han Zongqi, was exceptionally intelligent from a young age. At the age of five, he was able to read the Li Sao and other books. At thirteen, he passed the fengju xiucai examination. At fourteen, during an inspection, General Yang selected him as the top candidate and sent him to the Fushuwen Academy. The Minister of Rites, Qi Shaonan, saw him and remarked, "This boy has an extraordinary style, but I fear he may not live long."

    On the first day of the eighth month in the year Jiyou, early in the morning, Han suddenly told his mother, "I had a very strange dream last night. I saw hundreds of people bustling through clouds and mist, some flipping through books and others handing out writing materials, all in different forms. Then I heard my name being called, and it was the thirty-seventh name, which was my own. I was startled awake with a start. The names called were all clear, and I could still remember them when I woke up, but by the time I got up and dressed, I had forgotten them all." He believed that he was named on the heavenly list and that he would surely succeed in this examination.

    When he reached the provincial examination, after completing the three rounds, it was the Mid-Autumn Festival, and the moon was as bright as day. Just as he was about to submit his papers, he heard someone call out, "Han Zongqi, go home!" This happened three times, and the voice grew increasingly stern, as if scolding him for being slow. Han replied, "Yes." When he submitted his papers, he looked around and saw no one, and he hurriedly returned home. The next day, he asked his fellow examinees, and they all said, "No, we didn't hear anything. If we wanted to go home together, we would have used a different term, how could we just call your name?"

    661

    揭榜后，名落孙山，甥怅怅不乐。旋感病，遂不起。临终苦吟“举头望明月，低头思故乡”二句，张目谓母曰：“儿顿悟前生事矣。儿本玉帝前献花童子。因玉帝寿诞，儿献花时偷眼观下界花灯，诸仙嫌儿不敬，即罚是日降生人间，今限满促归，母无苦也。”卒年十五，盖俗传正月初九为玉帝生日云。

    　　○徐俞氏邓州牧徐廷璐，与妻俞氏伉俪甚笃。俞卒，徐恸甚，凡其粉泽衣香，一一位置若平时，取其半臂覆枕上。至一七，营奠于庭，有小婢惊呼：“夫人活矣！”

    　　徐趋视，见夫人着半臂端坐床上，子女家人奔集，咸见之。徐走前欲抱，其影奄然澌灭，而半臂犹僵立，良久始仆。

    Explanation

    661

    After the results were posted, the nephew failed and was deeply saddened. Soon after, he fell ill and did not recover. At the moment of his death, he recited the lines "Lifting my head to gaze at the bright moon, lowering it to think of my hometown" and opened his eyes to speak to his mother: "I suddenly understood the events of my past life. I was a flower boy before the Jade Emperor. During the Jade Emperor's birthday celebration, I looked down at the lanterns of the mortal world while offering flowers, and the immortals found me disrespectful, so they punished me to be born on this day in the human world. Now my time is up, and I must return; do not be sad, Mother." He died at the age of fifteen, as it is a folk belief that the Jade Emperor's birthday is on the ninth day of the first lunar month.

    ○ Xu Yushi, the wife of Xu Tinglu, the magistrate of Dengzhou, had a very close relationship with her husband. After Xu Yushi's death, Xu Tinglu was extremely grief-stricken. He arranged all her cosmetics, perfumes, and clothes as if she were still alive, placing one of her half-sleeves over the pillow. On the seventh day, he set up an altar in the courtyard for a memorial service. A young maid suddenly cried out in surprise: "Madam is alive!"

    Xu Tinglu rushed to see, and saw his wife wearing one of her half-sleeves sitting upright on the bed. Her children and family members gathered around, and all saw her. Xu Tinglu went forward to embrace her, but her image vanished suddenly and completely, leaving only the half-sleeve standing stiffly. It remained this way for a long time before finally falling.

    662

    一夕，徐设席，欲与夫人对饮者，执杯泣曰：“素劳卿戒饮，今谁戒我耶！”

    　　语未毕，手中杯忽失所在，侍立婢仆遍寻不得。少顷，杯覆席间，酒已无余。

    　　有妾语人曰：“此后夫人不能诟我矣。”至夕，见夫人直登卧榻批其颊，颊上有青指痕，三日始灭。自是，举室畏敬，甚于在生时。

    　　○琵琶坟董太史潮，青年科第，以书画文辞冠绝时辈，性磊落。而有国风之好。常与诸名士集陶然亭散步吟诗，独至城堙下，忽闻琵琶声。踪迹之，声出数椽败屋，乃十七八美女子，着淡红衣，据窗理弦索。见董，略无羞避，挥弦如故。董徘徊不能去。同人怪董久不至，相率寻之，见董方倚破牖痴立，呼之不应。群啐之，董惊悟，而女子形声俱寂。始道其故，众入室搜索，败瓦颓垣，绝无人迹，有蓬颗一区，俗所称“琵琶坟”也。乃掖董归。未几，以疾归常州，卒于家。

    Explanation

    662

    One night, he gently set up the table, intending to drink with his wife. Holding the cup, he wept and said, "I have always asked you to abstain from drinking; who will now ask me to abstain?"

    Before he finished speaking, the cup in his hand suddenly disappeared. The servants standing by searched everywhere but couldn't find it. After a short while, the cup overturned on the table, and the wine was already gone.

    One of the concubines told someone, "After this, my wife won't scold me anymore." By evening, she saw his wife directly climb onto the bed and slap her face. There were blue finger marks on her cheek, which took three days to fade. From then on, the entire household feared and respected him, even more so than when he was alive.

    ○ The Pipa Tomb

    Dong Taishi Chao, a young scholar who passed the imperial examinations, was unmatched among his contemporaries in calligraphy, painting, and literary skills. He had a straightforward and open personality and a fondness for national customs. He often gathered with famous scholars at Tao Ran Pavilion to walk and recite poetry. One day, he went alone to the city wall and suddenly heard the sound of a pipa. He followed the sound, which came from a dilapidated building with several rooms. Inside was a beautiful girl of seventeen or eighteen, wearing a light red dress, sitting by the window and tuning her strings. Seeing Dong, she showed no embarrassment or avoidance and continued to play as before. Dong lingered and couldn't leave. His companions were surprised that he hadn't returned for a long time and went to look for him. They saw Dong leaning against a broken window, standing in a daze. They called to him but he didn't respond. They poked him, and Dong was startled and came to his senses. But the girl's form and sound had vanished. He then explained what had happened. The others searched the room, which was filled with broken tiles and crumbling walls, with no sign of anyone. There was a patch of weeds, which the locals call the "Pipa Tomb." They supported Dong back home. Not long after, he fell ill and returned to Changzhou, where he died at home.

    663

    ○曹阿狗归安程三郎，妻少艾而贤，里党称三娘子。方夏日晓妆，忽举动失常，三郎疑为遇祟，以左手批其颊。三娘子呼曰：“勿打我，我邻人曹阿狗也。闻家中设食，同人来赴。既至，独无我席，我惭且馁，知三娘子贤，特凭之求食耳，勿怖。”

    　　其邻曹姓，大族也，于前夕果延僧人诵《焰火经》。阿狗者，乃曹氏无赖，少年未婚而卒者也。以阿狗无后，实未为之设食，闻此言亦骇，同以酒浆楮镪至三娘子前致祝。三娘子曰：“今夕当专为我设食，送我于河，此且祭祀，必有阿狗名乃可。”曹氏惧，如其言送之，三娘子遂愈。

    　　○钱仲玉钱生仲玉，少年落魄，游兰溪署中。值上元夕，同人咸出观灯，仲玉中怀郁郁，独不往，步月庭除，叹曰：“安得五百金，使我骨肉团聚乎！”语毕，闻阶下应声曰：“有，有。”仲玉疑友人揶揄之，遍视，不见人，乃还斋坐。

    Explanation

    663

    ○ Cao Ah Dog, a man of low repute, was married to Cheng Sanlang. His wife, young and beautiful, was also virtuous, and she was known as Sanlangzi in the local community. One summer morning, while she was dressing, she suddenly behaved erratically. Sanlang suspected she had encountered a ghost and slapped her cheek with his left hand. Sanlangzi called out, "Don't hit me! I am your neighbor, Cao Ah Dog. I heard that you were preparing food and invited others to join. When I arrived, there was no place set for me. I felt ashamed and hungry, knowing that Sanlangzi is virtuous, so I relied on her to ask for food. Don't be afraid."

    Her neighbor, a member of the large Cao clan, had indeed invited monks to recite the Yanhuo Jing (Flame Scripture) the night before. Ah Dog, a young and disreputable member of the Cao family who died unmarried, was not given food because he had no descendants. Upon hearing this, they were also startled and brought wine, water, and paper money to Sanlangzi to offer their wishes. Sanlangzi said, "Tonight, you should prepare food specifically for me and send me to the river. This is a ritual, and it must include the name of Ah Dog." The Cao family, fearing the consequences, followed her instructions and sent her to the river. Sanlangzi then recovered.

    ○ Qian Zhongyu, a young man who had fallen on hard times, was staying at the Lanxi magistrate's office. On the Lantern Festival, his companions all went out to see the lanterns. Zhongyu, feeling melancholy, did not go, but instead walked around the courtyard under the moonlight and sighed, "Where can I find five hundred gold pieces to bring my family together?" As soon as he finished speaking, he heard a voice from the stairs below say, "Yes, yes." Zhongyu suspected that someone was mocking him and looked around, but saw no one. He returned to his room and sat down.

    664

    闻窗外谡谡声，一美女搴帏入曰：“郎勿惊，妾非人，亦非为祸者也。佳节异乡，共此岑寂。适闻郎语，笑郎以七尺男子，何难得五百金哉？”仲玉曰：“然则顷云‘有有’者即卿耶？”曰：“然。”钟玉曰：“在何处？”女笑曰：“勿急，勿急。”即拉仲玉手同坐曰：“妾汪六姑也，葬此，为污泥所侵，求君改葬高处，必当如君言以报。”问：“何病亡？”女以手遮面曰：“羞不可言。”

    　　固问之，曰：“妾幼解风情，而生长小家，所居楼临街，偶倚窗，见一美少年方溺，出其阴，红鲜如玉，妾心慕之，以为天下男子皆然。已而嫁卖菜佣周某，貌即不佳，体尤琐秽，绝不类所见少年，以此怨思成疾，口不能言，遂卒。”仲玉闻之，心大动，弛下衣，拉女手使摸。而人声忽至，女遽拂衣起曰：“缘未到。”

    　　仲玉送至墙下，女除一银臂钏与之曰：“幸勿忘。”言毕而没。仲玉恍然如梦，视银钏，竟在手中，乃秘之。

    Explanation

    664

    Hearing the rustling sound outside the window, a beautiful woman drew aside the curtain and entered, saying, "Young man, do not be startled. I am not a human, nor am I a bringer of disaster. It is a festive occasion in a foreign land, and we share this desolate atmosphere. I just heard your words and laughed, thinking how hard it is for a man of average height to obtain five hundred gold coins?" Zhong Yu replied, "Then, the one who just now said 'have have' was you?" She answered, "Yes." Zhong Yu asked, "Where is she?" The woman smiled and said, "Do not rush, do not rush." She then took Zhong Yu's hand and sat down together, saying, "I am Wang Liu's sixth daughter. I was buried here and invaded by mud. I ask you to rebury me in a higher place, and I will certainly repay you as you say." Zhong Yu asked, "What illness did you die of?" The woman covered her face with her hand and said, "It is too shameful to speak of."

    Zhong Yu insisted on asking, and she said, "When I was young, I understood the ways of love, but I grew up in a small family. My dwelling was a building facing the street. Once, leaning on the window, I saw a handsome young man urinating. His private parts were red and fresh like jade, and I longed for him, thinking that all men in the world were like that. Later, I married a vegetable vendor named Zhou, whose appearance was not good, and his body was especially unclean. He was not at all like the young man I had seen. Because of this, I developed grief and resentment, became ill, could not speak, and eventually died." Zhong Yu heard this and was greatly moved. He loosened his clothes and pulled the woman's hand to touch him. Suddenly, people's voices approached, and the woman quickly brushed off her clothes and stood up, saying, "The time is not right."

    Zhong Yu escorted her to the wall, and the woman took off a silver bracelet and gave it to him, saying, "Please do not forget." After she spoke, she disappeared. Zhong Yu woke up as if from a dream, looked at the silver bracelet, and found it in his hand, so he kept it secret.

    665

    次夕人静，独步墙阴，遍视不复见，乃语主人，并出臂钏以证。主人异之，起土三尺许，得女尸，衣饰尽朽，肌色如生，与仲玉所见无异，右臂一钏犹存。

    　　仲玉解衣覆之，为备棺衾，移葬高阜。

    　　其夕，梦女来谢曰：“感郎信义，告郎金所，郎卧榻向左三尺，旧有人埋五百金，明当取之。”如其言，果得金如数。

    　　○虾蟆蛊朱生依仁，工书，广西庆远府陈太守希芳延为记室。方盛暑，太守招僚友饮。

    　　就席，各去冠，众见朱生顶上蹲一大虾蟆，拂之落地，忽失所在。饮至夜分，虾蟆又登朱顶而朱不知，同人又为拂落，席间肴核。尽为所毁，复不见。朱生归寝，觉顶间作痒。次日，顶上发尽脱，当顶坟起如瘤，作红色。皮忽迸裂，一蟆自内伸头瞪目而望，前二足踞顶，自腰以下在头皮内，针刺不死。引出之，痛不可耐，医不能治。有老门役曰：“此蛊也，以金簪刺之当死。”试之果验，乃出其蟆。

    Explanation

    665

    The following evening, when it was quiet, Zhong Yu walked alone in the shadow of the wall, looking all around but did not see it again. Then he told the owner and showed the arm bracelet as proof. The owner was surprised and dug up about three feet of earth, finding a woman's body. Her clothes and decorations were all rotten, but her skin color was like that of a living person, just as Zhong Yu had seen. A bracelet on her right arm still existed.

    Zhong Yu took off his clothes to cover her and prepared a coffin and shroud, moving the burial to a high place. That night, he dreamed the woman came to thank him, saying, "Grateful for your trust and righteousness, I will tell you where the gold is. Three feet to the left of your bed, there used to be someone who buried five hundred taels of gold. I will get it tomorrow."

    As he said, he indeed found the gold in the amount mentioned.

    ○ The Mantis Shrimp Poison

    Zhu Sheng Yi Ren, skilled in calligraphy, was invited by Chen Taishou Xifang of Qingyuan Prefecture in Guangxi to serve as his secretary. During the intense heat of summer, the taishou invited his colleagues and friends for a drinking session.

    As they sat down to the table, everyone removed their hats, and the others saw a large mantis shrimp sitting on Zhu Sheng's head. They brushed it off the ground, but it suddenly disappeared. By nightfall, the mantis shrimp returned to Zhu Sheng's head, but he was unaware. His companions brushed it off again, and the food and drink on the table were completely destroyed. The mantis shrimp then disappeared once more. Zhu Sheng returned home and felt itching on his head. The next day, all the hair on his head fell out, and a lump rose on his scalp, turning red. Suddenly, the skin burst open, and a mantis shrimp emerged, stretching its head and glaring with its eyes. Its front two legs were on his head, while the rest of its body was inside his scalp. Even when pierced with a needle, it did not die. When they pulled it out, he suffered excruciating pain, and doctors could not treat him. An old servant said, "This is a poison. Piercing it with a gold hairpin should kill it." They tried it, and it worked. They then removed the mantis shrimp.

    666

    而朱生无他恙，惟顶骨下陷，若仰盂然。

    　　○礅怪高睿功，世家子也。其居厅前有怪。每夜人行，辄见白衣人长丈余蹑后，以手掩人目，其冷如冰。遂闭前门，别开门出入。白衣人渐乃昼见，人咸避之。睿功偶被酒坐厅上，见白衣人登阶倚柱立，手拈其须，仰天微睇，似未见睿功在坐者。睿功潜至其后，挥拳奋击，误中柱上，挫指血出，白衣人已立丹墀中。睿功大呼趋击，时方阴雨，为苔滑扑地。白衣人见而大笑，举手来击，腰不能俯；似欲以足蹴，而腿又长不能举；乃大怒，环阶而走。睿功知其无能为，直前抱持其足而力掀之，白衣人倒地而没。睿功呼家人就其初起处掘，深三尺，得白瓷旧坐礅一个，礅上鲜血犹存，盖睿功指血所染也。击而碎之，其怪遂绝。

    Explanation

    666

    While Zhu Sheng had no other ailments, only his top bone was sunken, as if an inverted bowl.

    ○ A Stool-Strange Tale of Gao Ruigong, a Son of a Well-Known Family. There was a strange phenomenon before his residence's hall. Every night when people walked, they would see a white-clad figure over ten feet tall following behind, covering their eyes with hands, as cold as ice. So they closed the front door and opened a different door for entry and exit. The white-clad figure gradually began to appear during the day, and people all avoided it. Rui Gong happened to be sitting in the hall after drinking alcohol, and he saw the white-clad figure climb the steps and lean against the pillar standing there, with fingers playing with his beard, looking up at the sky with a slight gaze, as if he did not see Rui Gong sitting there. Rui Gong quietly approached from behind and swung his fist to attack, accidentally hitting the pillar, and his finger was cut, blood flowing out. The white-clad figure was already standing in the red-paved courtyard. Rui Gong shouted loudly and rushed to attack, but it was raining heavily, and the slippery moss caused him to fall to the ground. The white-clad figure saw this and laughed loudly, raising his hand to strike, but he could not bend his waist; it seemed as if he wanted to kick with his feet, but his legs were too long to lift; he then became very angry and ran around the steps. Rui Gong knew he was unable to do anything, so he went straight forward and grabbed his feet, using all his strength to lift him. The white-clad figure fell to the ground and disappeared. Rui Gong called his family to dig at the place where the figure first appeared, digging three feet deep and finding an old white porcelain stool. There was still fresh blood on the stool, which was likely stained by Rui Gong's finger blood. He hit and broke it, and the strange phenomenon ceased.

    667

    ○六郎神斗广西南宁乡里，祀六郎神。人或语言触犯，则为祟。尤善媚女子，美者多为所凭。凡受其害者，以纸镪一束，饭一盂，用两三乐人，午夜祀之，送至旷野，即去而之他。其俗无夕不送六郎也。

    　　有杨三姑者，年十七，美姿容。日将夕，方与父母共坐，忽嫣然睨笑。久之，趋入房，施朱傅粉，娇羞百态。父母往问，砖石自空掷下，房门遂闭，惟闻两人笑语声。知为六郎，亟呼乐人送之。六郎不肯去。及晨，女出如常，云：“六郎美少年，头戴将巾，身披软甲，年可二十七八，与我甚恩爱，不必送他去。”父母无如何。

    　　越数夕，忽仓皇奔出曰：“又一六郎来！大胡子，貌甚狞恶，与前六郎争我相殴。前六郎非其敌也，行当去矣。”俄闻室中斗声甚剧，似无物不损者，父母乃召乐人双送之。两人俱去，三姑亦无恙。

    Explanation

    667

    The God of Six Gentlemen is worshipped in the villages of Guangxi Nanning. If someone offends the deity, they will be haunted. The deity is particularly adept at charming women, and beautiful women are often possessed by it. Those who are harmed by the deity are offered a bundle of paper money and a bowl of rice, along with two or three musicians, at midnight. The offerings are sent to the wilderness, and the deity leaves. It is customary to send the deity every night.

    There was a girl named Yang San’gu, who was seventeen years old and very beautiful. As the day was ending, she was sitting with her parents when she suddenly gave a coy, sidelong smile. After a while, she hurried into the room, applied rouge and powder, and displayed various coquettish and shy postures. Her parents went to ask her, and bricks and stones fell from the air, closing the room door. Inside, they could only hear the sounds of two people laughing and talking. They recognized it as the God of Six Gentlemen and quickly called musicians to send the deity away. However, the deity did not leave. The next morning, the girl came out as usual and said, “The God of Six Gentlemen is a handsome young man, wearing a military cap and soft armor, about twenty-seven or twenty-eight years old. He is very affectionate with me, so there is no need to send him away.” Her parents had no choice.

    After several nights, the girl suddenly ran out in a panic and said, “Another God of Six Gentlemen has come! He has a large beard and a very fierce appearance. He is fighting with the previous one over me. The previous one is no match for him, and he will leave soon.” Soon after, they heard loud fighting sounds from inside the room, as if everything was being damaged. The parents then called two musicians to send both deities away. Both deities left, and the girl was unharmed.

    668

    ○返魂香余家婢女招姐之祖母周氏，年七十余，奉佛甚虔。一夕寝矣，见室中有老妪立焉。初见甚短，目之渐长，手纸片堆其几上，衣蓝布裙，色甚鲜。周私忆，同一蓝色，何彼独鲜？问：“阿婆蓝布从何处染？”不答。周怒骂曰：“我问不答，岂是鬼乎！”妪曰：“是也。”曰：“既是鬼，来捉我乎？”曰：“是也。”周愈怒，骂曰：“我偏不受捉！”手批其颊，不觉魂出，已到门外，而老妪不见矣。

    　　周行黄沙中，足不履地。四面无人。望见屋舍，皆白粉垣，甚宏敞，遂入焉。

    　　案有香一枝，五色，如秤杆长，上面一火星红，下面彩绒披覆层叠，如世间婴孩所戴刘海搭状。有老妪拜香下，貌甚慈，问周何来，曰：“迷路到此。”曰：“思归乎？”曰：“欲归不得。”妪曰：“嗅香即归矣。”周嗅之，觉异香贯脑，一惊而苏，家中僵卧已三日矣。或曰：“此即聚窟山之返魂香也。”

    Explanation

    668

    Translation:

    The Reviving Fragrance

    In my family, the grandmother of the servant girl Zhaogui, Zhou, was over seventy years old and devoutly worshiped Buddhism. One night, after she had gone to bed, she saw an old woman standing in her room. At first, the old woman appeared very small, but as Zhou watched, she grew taller. The old woman had paper pieces piled on her table, wore a blue cloth skirt, and her color was very bright. Zhou privately wondered, "Why is her blue cloth so vivid when ours is the same color?" She asked, "Grandma, where did you dye your blue cloth?" The old woman did not answer. Zhou angrily shouted, "If you don't answer my question, are you a ghost?" The old woman replied, "Yes, I am." Zhou asked, "If you are a ghost, are you here to catch me?" The old woman said, "Yes, I am." Zhou became even angrier and shouted, "I won't let you catch me!" She slapped the old woman's cheek. Suddenly, Zhou felt her soul leaving her body, and she found herself outside the door, while the old woman had disappeared.

    Zhou walked through the yellow sand, her feet not touching the ground. There was no one around her. She saw houses with white plaster walls, very grand and spacious, and she entered one.

    On a table, there was a fragrant branch, multicolored and as long as a scale, with a red spark on top and colorful silk draped over the bottom, resembling the tassel worn by a child. An old woman was kneeling before the fragrance, her appearance very kind. She asked Zhou where she had come from, and Zhou replied, "I got lost and came here." The old woman asked, "Do you want to go home?" Zhou said, "I want to go but cannot." The old woman said, "If you smell the fragrance, you will go home." Zhou smelled the fragrance and felt an unusual scent fill her head. She was startled and woke up, having been unconscious at home for three days. Some said, "This is indeed the Reviving Fragrance from Juku Mountain."

    669

    ○观音作别方姬奉一檀香观音像，长四寸。余性通脱，不加礼，亦不禁也。有张妈者，奉之尤虔，每早必往佛前，焚香稽首毕，方供扫除之役。余一日早晨，呼盥面汤其急，而张方拜佛不已，余怒，取观音像掷地，足蹋之。姬泣曰：“昨夜梦观音来别我，云：”明日有小劫，我将他适矣。‘今果被君作蹋，岂非数也！“乃送入准庵。会想：佛法全空，焉得作如此狡狯，必有鬼物凭焉。嗣后，乃不许家人奉佛。

    　　○兔儿神国初，御史某年少科第，巡按福建。有胡天保者爱其貌美，每升舆坐堂，必伺而睨之。巡按心以为疑，卒不解其故，胥吏亦不敢言。居无何，巡按巡他邑，胡竟偕往，阴伏厕所窥其臀。巡按愈疑，召问之。初犹不言，加以三木，乃云：“实见大人美貌，心不能忘，明知天上桂，岂为凡鸟所集，然神魂飘荡，不觉无礼至此。”巡按大怒，毙其命于枯木之下。

    Explanation

    669

    Translation

    Section 1

    When Guanyin bid farewell, Fang Ji presented a small sandalwood statue of Guanyin, four inches long. My nature is open and relaxed, so I did not impose strict rituals or restrictions. There was a servant named Zhang Ma, who was particularly devout. Every morning, she would go before the Buddha, burn incense, and bow before attending to her cleaning duties. One morning, I called for water for washing my face in a hurry, but Zhang Ma was still praying. This angered me, and I took the Guanyin statue, threw it on the ground, and stepped on it. Fang Ji wept and said, "Last night, I dreamed that Guanyin came to say goodbye to me, saying, 'There will be a small disaster tomorrow, and I will leave.' Indeed, you have trampled on me, is this not fate?" I then sent her to the Zun An temple. Reflecting on this, I thought: Buddhist teachings are all about emptiness, how could there be such trickery? There must be some supernatural influence at play. Afterwards, I forbade my family from worshipping Buddha.

    Section 2

    In the early days of the country, a young censor named Hu Tianbao loved the beauty of the circuit inspector. Whenever the inspector ascended the sedan chair and sat on the dais, Hu Tianbao would wait and gaze at him. The inspector felt uneasy but could not understand why. The officials also dared not speak of it. Soon after, when the inspector went on a tour to another county, Hu Tianbao followed, secretly hiding in the latrine to peek at his buttocks. The inspector became even more suspicious and summoned him for questioning. Initially, Hu Tianbao remained silent, but under the threat of three wooden stocks, he confessed: "I really see the beauty of the great man, and I cannot forget it. I know that the moon is not for common birds, but my spirit is so distracted that I did not realize I was being so disrespectful." The inspector was furious and killed him under a withered tree.

    670

    逾月，胡托梦于其里人曰：“我以非礼之心于犯贵人，死固当，然毕竟是一片爱心，一时痴想，与寻常害人者不同。冥间官吏俱笑我、揶揄我，无怒我者。

    　　今阴官封我为兔儿神，专司人间男悦男之事，可为我立庙招香火。“闽俗原为聘男子为契弟之说，闻里人述梦中语，争醵钱立庙。果灵验如响。凡偷期密约，有所求而不得者，咸往祷焉。

    　　程鱼门曰：“此巡按未读《晏子春秋》劝勿诛羽人事，故下手太重。若狄伟人先生颇不然。相传先生为编修时，年少貌美。有车夫某，亦少年，投身入府，为先生推车，甚勤谨，与雇直钱，不受，先生亦爱之。未几病危，诸医不效，将断气矣，请主人至，曰：”奴既死，不得不言。奴之所以病至死者，为爱爷貌美故也。‘先生大笑，拍其肩曰：“痴奴子！果有此心，何不早说矣？’厚葬之。”

    Explanation

    670

    After a month, the ghost of Hu appeared in a dream to someone from his village and said, "I offended a noble person with an improper heart, and death was indeed my due. However, it was ultimately an act of love, a fleeting fancy, unlike the actions of those who harm others. The officials in the afterlife all laughed at me, mocked me, but did not anger me.

    Now, the officials in the underworld have appointed me as the Rabbit God, in charge of matters of men loving men in the human world. You can build a temple for me and offer incense and candles." The custom in Fujian originally involved hiring a man as a sworn younger brother. Upon hearing the account of the dream from the villagers, they competed to raise money and build a temple. It proved to be as effective as an echo. Anyone who has secret meetings, unfulfilled desires, or things they cannot obtain, all go to pray to me.

    Cheng Yumen said, "This censor did not read the Spring and Autumn Annals of Yanzi which advises against killing innocent people, hence he acted too harshly. But Mr. Di Weiren was quite different. It is said that when Mr. Di was a proofreader, he was young and handsome. There was a coachman, also a young man, who joined the household and pushed the carriage for Mr. Di, being very diligent. He was offered payment, but refused it. Mr. Di also loved him. Soon after, he fell gravely ill, and the doctors were unable to help, and he was about to die. He asked the master to come, and said, 'Since I am going to die, I must speak. The reason I became so ill and died was because I loved Master's beauty.' Mr. Di laughed heartily, patted his shoulder, and said, 'Foolish servant! If you truly had this heart, why didn't you say so earlier?' He was buried with great ceremony."

    671

    ○玉梅香亭家婢玉梅，年十余岁，素勤。忽懒，终日昏睡，笞之亦不改。每夜喃喃，如与人私语。问之，不肯说，褫下衣验其阴，已非处子，且溃烂矣。拷讯乃云：“夜有怪，状如黑羊，能作人语。阳具如毛锥，痛不可当。戒我勿告人，如告人，当拉我去，置之死地。”众骇然。

    　　伺婢卧，夜窃听焉。初作猫饮水声，继而呻吟，香亭率众持棍入，烛照无人，问：“怪何在？”婢指床下曰：“此绿服者是也。”果见眼光两道，闪耀处，帐色皆绿。棍击之，跳起冲窗去，满房帐钩箱锁之类，锵锵有声。

    　　次日失婢所在，遍觅不得。薄暮，灶下人见风飘红布裙一条在柴房西角处，往寻得婢，痴迷不醒。灌以姜汁，苏曰：“怪昨夜来云：”事为汝主所知，不得不抱汝去。‘遂藏我于柴房中，约今夜仍来。“问：”听得猫饮水声，何耶？“

    　　曰：“怪每淫我，先舐后交，口舐差乐也。”香亭即日呼媒者，将玉梅转售他家，怪竟不往。

    Explanation

    671

    A Maid Named Yu Mei, in the Xiangting Household

    Yu Mei, a maid in the Xiangting household, was over ten years old and had always been diligent. Suddenly, she became lazy, sleeping all day, and even beating her did not change her. Every night, she muttered, as if speaking privately to someone. When asked, she refused to say anything. They stripped off her clothes to examine her vagina, and found that she was no longer a virgin, and her vagina was also ulcerated. After being interrogated, she said: "At night, there was a monster, shaped like a black sheep, that could speak human language. Its penis was like a needle, unbearable to bear. It warned me not to tell anyone. If I told anyone, it would drag me away and kill me." Everyone was terrified.

    They waited until the maid was asleep and listened secretly at night. At first, it made the sound of a cat drinking water, then moaned. Xiangting led the crowd with sticks and entered, but found no one under the candlelight. They asked, "Where is the monster?" The maid pointed to the bed and said, "This green-clad creature is it." Indeed, there were two beams of light, shining green, making the curtains green. The stick hit it, and it jumped up and rushed out the window. All the curtains, hooks, boxes, and locks in the room made a clanging sound.

    The next day, the maid disappeared, and they could not find her. At dusk, a person in the kitchen saw a red cloth skirt floating in the wind at the west corner of the shed. They went to find the maid, who was dazed and unconscious. They poured ginger juice on her, and she regained consciousness and said, "The monster came last night and said, 'The matter was known by your master, so it had to take you away.' It hid me in the shed and promised to come again tonight." They asked, "Why did I hear the sound of a cat drinking water?"

    She replied, "The monster often raped me, first licking and then having intercourse, and the licking was somewhat enjoyable." Xiangting immediately called a matchmaker to sell Yu Mei to another family, and the monster did not come.

    672

    ○卢彪余幼时同馆卢彪，一日至馆，神色沮丧，问之，曰：“我昨日往西湖扫墓，归迟，城门闭矣，宿某店家。夜月甚明，鸡鸣即起，踏月进城。至清波门外，小憩石上。见远远一女子来，向余侠拜。余疑其非人，口诵《大悲咒》拒之。女如畏闻而不敢近者，我逼而诵之。我愈近女，女愈远我，我惊，乃狂奔数里。将入瓮城，见东方渐白，卖鱼人挑担往来，以为此时尚复何惧，何不重至旧处一探踪迹？行至前路，不料此女高坐石上，如有所待。望见我便大笑，奔前相扑，冷风如箭，毛发尽颤。我惶急，再诵《大悲咒》拒之。女大怒，将手向上一伸，两条枯骨侧侧有声，面上非青非黄，七窍流血。我不觉狂叫仆地，枯骨从而压之，我从此昏昏无知矣。后有行路者过，扶起，以姜汁灌我，才得苏醒还家。”余急与诸窗友置酒为卢压惊，视之耳鼻两窍及辫发中尚有青泥填塞，星星如小豆。或云：“皆卢所自塞也，故两手亦皆泥污。”

    Explanation

    672

    Lu Biao, a fellow student of mine from my youth, one day came to the academy looking dejected. I asked him what was wrong, and he replied, "Yesterday, I went to West Lake to visit my ancestors' graves. I returned late, and the city gates were already closed. I stayed at a storehouse. The night was very bright, and I woke up at the crow of the rooster, treading on the moonlight to enter the city. I reached Qingbo Gate and took a short rest on a stone. I saw a distant woman coming, and she bowed to me. I suspected she was not human and recited the Great Compassion Mantra to reject her. She seemed to be afraid of the sound and did not dare to approach me. I pressed closer and continued to recite the mantra. The closer I got to her, the farther she moved away. I was frightened and ran madly for several miles. As I approached the瓮城, I saw the east gradually turning white, and fish sellers were carrying their goods back and forth. I thought, 'What is there to fear now?' and decided to return to the old place to see if she was still there. When I reached the previous path, I did not expect to see this woman sitting on a stone, as if waiting for something. She saw me, laughed, and ran towards me to attack. The cold wind was like arrows, and my hair and skin all trembled. I was in a panic and recited the Great Compassion Mantra again to reject her. She became furious, raised her hands, and two dry bones made a sound. Her face was neither blue nor yellow, and blood flowed from all seven orifices. I could not help but scream wildly and fell to the ground. The dry bones pressed down on me, and I lost consciousness. Later, a passerby helped me up, gave me ginger juice to drink, and I regained consciousness and returned home." I quickly gathered my fellow students to set up a feast to comfort Lu Biao. Upon examining him, I found that his ears, nose, and hair were still clogged with green mud, like small beans. Some said, "All of this was blocked by Lu himself, so his hands were also dirty with mud."

    673

    ○孔林古墓雍正间，陈文勤公世倌修孔林。离圣墓西十馀步，地陷一穴，探之：中空，广阔丈馀，有石榻；榻上朱棺已朽，白骨一具甚伟，旁置铜剑，长丈馀，晶莹绿色，竹简数十页，若有蝌蚪文者。取视，成灭。鼎俎尊彝之属，亦多破缺漫漶。

    Explanation

    673

    A Mysterious Discovery in the Confucius Cemetery

    During the reign of Emperor Yongzheng, Chen Wenqin, a high-ranking official and scholar, undertook the restoration of the Confucius Cemetery. While working near the main Confucius tomb, about ten steps to the west, the ground suddenly collapsed into a hole. Upon investigation, they discovered a hollow space that was over a zhang (approximately 3.3 meters) wide. Inside, there was a stone bed, on which lay a decayed red lacquered coffin. Beside the coffin was a set of very large white bones. A copper sword, over a zhang long and crystal clear with a green hue, was placed next to the bones. Dozens of pages of bamboo slips, written in what appeared to be seal script, were also found. When the bamboo slips were taken for viewing, they mysteriously disappeared. Many of the ritual vessels and utensils, such as tripods, sacrificial stands, and ritual vessels, were also found, but many were broken, damaged, and faded.

    674

    文勤公以为此墓尚在孔子之先，不宜惊动，谨加砖石封砌之，为设少年之奠焉。

    　　○史阁部降乩扬州谢启昆太守扶乩，灰盘书《正气歌》数句，太守疑为文山先生，整冠肃拜。问神姓名，曰：“亡国庸臣史可法。”时太守正修葺史公祠墓，环植松梅，因问：“为公修祠墓，公知之乎？”曰：“知之。此守土者之责也，然亦非俗吏所能为。”问自己官阶，批曰：“不患无位，患所以立。”谢无子，问：“将来得有子否？”批曰：“与其有子而名灭，不如无子而名存。太守勉旃。”问：“先生近已成神乎？”曰：“成神。”问“何神？”曰：“天曹稽察大使。”书毕，索长纸一幅，问：“何用？”曰：“吾欲自题对联。”与之纸，题曰：“一代兴亡归气数，千秋庙貌傍江山。”笔力苍劲，谢公为双勾之，悬于庙中。

    Explanation

    674

    The diligent official, Wen Qin, believed that this tomb, which predated Confucius, should not be disturbed. He carefully sealed it with bricks and stones and set up a memorial for the young.

    ○ The magistrate of Yangzhou, Xie Qikun, performed a divination ritual. On the ash tray, he wrote several lines of the Zhengqi Ge (Song of Righteousness). The magistrate suspected that the spirit was Wen Tianxiang, the "Mr. Wenshan," and he straightened his cap and bowed solemnly. When he asked the spirit its name, it replied, "A mediocre minister of a fallen state, Shi Kefa." At the time, the magistrate was repairing the shrine and tomb of Shi Gong, planting pines and plums around it. He then asked, "Do you know that I am repairing your shrine and tomb for you?" The spirit replied, "I know. It is the duty of one who guards the land, but it is also beyond the capabilities of an ordinary official." When asked about its own rank, the spirit wrote, "Do not worry about not having a position, but worry about whether you have the ability to hold it." The magistrate, who was childless, asked, "Will you have a son in the future?" The spirit replied, "It is better to have no son and have one's name preserved than to have a son and have one's name forgotten. Do your best, magistrate." The magistrate then asked, "Have you recently become a deity?" The spirit replied, "Yes, I have become a deity." When asked what kind of deity, it replied, "An inspector general of the Heavenly Bureau." After writing, the spirit asked for a long sheet of paper and asked, "What is it for?" It replied, "I wish to write a couplet myself." The magistrate gave it the paper, and the spirit wrote, "The rise and fall of one generation are determined by fate, while the temple's glory stands beside the mountains and rivers." The brushwork was firm and vigorous. The magistrate traced it in double outline and hung it in the temple.

    675

    ○悬头竿子某令宰宝山时，有行商来告抢夺者，被抢处系一坍港泊舟所也。令往视其地，见水路可通城中，而乘舟者倒在此处雇夫起行，心疑之，众莫言其故。

    　　一把总来见曰：“此地原可通舟，所以客来必起拨者，港口穷民籍挑驮之力为糊口计故也。”令问抢夺事，曰：“不敢言，须宽把总罪，才敢言。”令曰：“律有自首免罪之条，汝告我，即为自首矣，何妨？”曰：“诸抢夺者，皆把持垄断人也，把总儿子亦在其中。。前月某商到此，见水路可通，不肯起拨，因而打吵，事实有之。”乾隆三十年新例：拿获强盗者，破格超迁。令定案时，心想迁官，竟以获盗具详；把总知情，照窝家倒立决。一时斩者六人，令超迁安庆知府。

    Explanation

    675

    During the time when the official named 悬头竿子 was in charge of Baoshan, a traveling merchant came to report a robbery. The location of the robbery was a collapsed harbor where boats were moored. The official went to inspect the site and saw that the waterway could connect to the city. However, those who had arrived by boat had to hire local laborers to depart, which raised his suspicion, though no one dared to explain the reason.

    A local officer, known as 把总, came to see him and said, "This area originally allowed boats to pass through. That is why travelers must hire local laborers; the poor people at the harbor rely on their strength to carry goods as a means of making a living." The official asked about the robbery, and the officer replied, "I dare not speak of it. You must first show leniency towards my punishment, and then I will tell you." The official said, "According to the law, there is a provision that allows for the reduction of punishment if one confesses. By telling me, you are confessing, so what is there to fear?" The officer then admitted, "The robbers were all people who monopolized the area, and even the son of the 把总 was involved. Last month, a certain merchant arrived here and, seeing that the waterway was passable, refused to hire local laborers, leading to a fight. This is indeed what happened."

    In the 30th year of the Qianlong era, a new law was enacted: those who captured bandits would be promoted beyond the usual ranks. When the official was finalizing the case, he thought of his own promotion and thus reported the capture of the bandits in detail. The 把总, having been involved, was sentenced to be executed by being tied upside down. At that time, six people were executed, and the official was promoted to the position of governor of Anqing.

    676

    后六年，署松太道。巡海至宝山抢夺处，见六竿子挂髑髅尚存。问跟役曰：“前累累者何物耶？”役曰：“此六盗也，大人以此升官而忘之耶？”令不觉悚然，怒曰：“死奴！谁教汝引我至此？速归！速归！”舁至衙，骂司阍者曰：“此内室也，汝何敢放某把总擅入！”言毕而背疮发，一疮六头，如相啮者。家人知为不祥，烧纸钱、请高僧忏悔，卒以不起。

    　　○陈紫山余乡会同年陈紫山，名大仑，溧阳人也。入学时，年才十九。偶病剧，梦紫衣僧，自称“元圭大师”，握其手曰：“汝背我到人间，盍归来乎？”陈未答，僧笑曰：“且住，且住。汝尚有琼林一杯酒，瀛台一碗羹，吃了再来未迟。”屈其指曰：“别又十七年了。”言毕去。陈惊醒，一汗而痊。己未中进士，入翰林，升读学士。

    Explanation

    676

    Six years later, he was appointed as the governor of Song and Tai Prefectures. While patrolling the sea, he came to the site where a robbery had occurred and saw six poles with skulls still hanging from them. He asked his attendant, "What are these things?" The attendant replied, "These are the skulls of six bandits, sir. Did you rise to your position by ignoring this?" The official was startled and angrily said, "Dead slave! Who told you to bring me here? Go back! Go back!" They carried him back to the office, where he scolded the gatekeeper, saying, "This is the inner chamber, how dare you let a certain captain enter without permission?" After speaking, a sore on his back broke out, with one sore having six heads, as if they were biting each other. His family knew this was inauspicious and burned paper money, inviting high monks to confess, but he finally did not recover.

    ○ Chen Zishan

    Chen Zishan, my fellow county student from the same year, was named Da Lun and was from Liyang. When he entered school, he was only nineteen years old. Once, he suddenly fell seriously ill and dreamed of a purple-robed monk who called himself "Master Yuan Gui." The monk held his hand and said, "You brought me to the human world, why not return?" Chen did not answer, and the monk laughed, "Wait, wait. You still have a cup of wine from the Jade Forest and a bowl of soup from the Sea of Tranquility. It’s not too late to come back after enjoying them." The monk bent his fingers and said, "It has been seventeen years since then." After speaking, he left. Chen woke up in shock, sweating profusely, and recovered. In the year of the Earth Pig, he passed the imperial examination, entered the Hanlin Academy, and was promoted to the rank of scholar-official.

    677

    三十八岁，秋痢不休，因忆前梦十七年之期，自知不起。常对家人笑曰：“大师未来，或又改期，亦未可知。”忽一日早起，焚香沐浴，索朝衣冠着之，曰：“吾师已来，吾去矣。”同年金质夫编修素好佛者，在旁喝曰：“既牵他来，又拖他去。一去一来，是何缘故？”陈目且瞑，强起张目答曰：“来原无碍，去亦何妨。人间天上，一个坛场。”言毕，跏趺而逝。

    　　○忌火日曹来殷太史在京师昼寝，梦伟丈夫来拜，自称“黄昆圃先生”。拉至一处，宫阙巍然，中有尊神，面正方，着本朝衣冠，请曹入见，曰：“吾三人皆翰林衙门官，只行前后辈礼，不行僚属礼。”坐定目曹曰：“卿十一岁时曾行一大好事，上帝知之，故特召卿到此受职，卿可即来。”曹茫然不记幼所行何事，再三辞，力陈“家寒子幼，故不愿来”。尊神甚不悦，旁顾昆圃先生曰：“再向彼劝掖之。”

    Explanation

    677

    At the age of thirty-eight, he suffered from a persistent autumnal illness, and recalling a dream from seventeen years ago, he knew he would not recover. He often laughed at his family and said, "Perhaps the master has not come yet, or the period may have been changed, it is uncertain." One morning, he rose early, burned incense, and bathed, asking for his official robes and putting them on. He said, "My master has come, I am leaving." A fellow scholar named Jin Zhi Fu, who was a compiler and had always been fond of Buddhism, shouted beside him, "Having brought him here, now dragging him away. One comes and one goes, what is the reason for this?" With his eyes almost closed, he forced himself to open them and replied, "Coming originally causes no hindrance, going away also poses no problem. Whether in the human world or the celestial realm, it is all one arena." Saying this, he passed away in the traditional posture of meditation.

    On a day when fire was taboo, Cao Lai Yin, a historian in the capital, took a nap during the day and dreamed of a great man who came to pay homage, introducing himself as "Mr. Huang Kunpu." He led Cao to a place where the palace was grand and majestic, with a revered deity in the center, wearing the official attire of the current dynasty. The deity invited Cao to see him and said, "We three are all officials in the Hanlin Academy, we only follow the etiquette of seniority, not the etiquette of subordinates." After they sat down, the deity looked at Cao and said, "When you were eleven years old, you did a great good deed, the Heavenly Emperor knew about it, so he specially summoned you here to receive your position, you can come now." Cao was at a loss and did not remember what good deed he had done as a child, and he repeatedly declined, strongly stating, "My family is poor and my children are young, so I do not want to come." The deity was very displeased and looked at Mr. Huang Kunpu, saying, "Try to persuade him again."

    678

    语毕，不顾而入。

    　　先生拉曹笑曰：“我深知翰林衙门亦甚清苦，卿何恋恋不肯来耶？”曹复哀求。先生曰：“我且为卿说情，似亦可免，但卿此后逢火日不可出门，慎无忘也。”

    　　曹问：“尊神何人？”曰：“张京江相公。”问：“何地？”曰：“天曹都察院。”

    　　曹惊醒。后每出门，必检视黄历，遇火日，虽庆吊事，皆不行。数年后，不甚记忆。

    　　乾隆三十三年腊月二十三日，严冬友舍人邀曹至程鱼门家作诗会，俗以此日祀灶，遂以为题。席间酒数巡，曹伥然如睡去者，目瞑身仆。群客大惊，疑诗中有侮灶神之语，故神为祟，乃群向灶礼拜祈请。至三更时，曹始苏，自言“见黑袍人送我回来”。次日，取黄历视之，二十三日，火日也。

    Explanation

    678

    After finishing speaking, he entered without looking back.

    The master pulled and smiled at Cao, saying, "I know that the Hanlin Academy is also very austere. Why are you so reluctant to come?" Cao begged again. The master said, "I will plead on your behalf; it seems that it can be avoided, but you must not go out on fire days afterward. Be careful not to forget!"

    Cao asked, "Who is the divine being?" "Zhang Jingjiang," he replied. "Where is it?" "The Heavenly Court of Inquiry," he said.

    Cao woke up in shock. Afterward, every time he went out, he would check the almanac, and on fire days, even celebratory or mourning events would not be held. After several years, he did not remember it very clearly.

    On the twenty-third day of the twelfth lunar month in the thirty-third year of the Qianlong era, Yan Dongyou, a scholar, invited Cao to a poetry gathering at Cheng Yumen's house. It was customary on this day to worship the stove god, so they chose it as the theme. During the gathering, after several rounds of wine, Cao suddenly fell asleep, his eyes closed and body slumped. The guests were greatly alarmed, suspecting that there were disrespectful words about the stove god in the poem, and thus the god had caused a disturbance. They all knelt before the stove and prayed. By the third watch, Cao awoke and said, "I saw a black-robed person escort me back." The next day, he checked the almanac and saw that the twenty-third day was indeed a fire day.

    679

    ○朱法师同馆翰林朱氵之父朴庵先生，陕西人也，少时课徒为业。偶至一村，村人传呼曰：“朱法师来矣！”具酒馔求书姓名，以为镇压。朱笑曰：“我乃蒙童之师，非法师也。且素无法术，不能镇怪。汝辈何为？”众人曰：“此村有狐仙为民患者三年。昨日空中语曰：”明日朱法师来，我当避之。‘今日先生来，果姓朱，故疑为法师。“朱写姓名与之，某村果安。

    　　未几。朱别过一村，其村人之欢迎者如前，且曰：“狐仙有语，二十年后，与朱法师相见于太学之崇志堂。”朱其时尚未乡举也。

    　　后中壬子科举人，选国子监助教。监中祭器久被狐窃去，司祭者皇皇然，索而弗获，方议赔偿，朱记前语，为文祭之。一夕，俎豆之属，尽横陈于崇志堂，丝毫无损。屈指算之，距到某村已二十年。

    Explanation

    679

    Zhu Bo'an, the father of Zhu Yichun, a fellow scholar in the Hanlin Academy, was a native of Shaanxi Province. In his youth, he made a living by teaching students. Once, he happened to visit a village, and the villagers called out, "Master Zhu has arrived!" They prepared wine and food and asked him to write down his name, believing it would serve as a protective charm. Zhu smiled and said, "I am merely a teacher of young children, not a spiritual master. Moreover, I have never possessed any magical powers and cannot pacify evil spirits. What are you doing?" The villagers replied, "This village has been plagued by a fox spirit for three years. Yesterday, the fox spirit spoke from the air, saying, 'Tomorrow, when Master Zhu arrives, I will avoid him.' Today, the master has come, and indeed he has the surname Zhu, so we suspect him to be the spiritual master." Zhu wrote down his name for them, and the village indeed became peaceful.

    Soon after, Zhu passed through another village, where the villagers greeted him as they had before, and they said, "The fox spirit has spoken: in twenty years, I will meet Master Zhu at the Hall of Aspiring in the Imperial College." At that time, Zhu had not yet passed the provincial examination.

    Later, Zhu passed the imperial examination in the year of Renzi and was selected as an assistant teacher in the Imperial College. The ritual vessels in the college had been stolen by a fox for a long time, causing the ritual officiants to be in a state of panic. They searched but could not find them and were just about to discuss compensation. Zhu remembered the fox spirit's words and wrote an offering to the spirit. One night, the ritual vessels were found lying in a row in the Hall of Aspiring, completely undamaged. Counting on his fingers, he realized that it had been twenty years since he visited that village.

    680

    ○城门面孔广西府差常宁，五鼓有急务出城。抵门，犹未启钥，以手扪之，软腻如人肌肤。差大骇，乘残月一线，定睛视之，则一人面塞满城门，五官毕具，双眼如箕，惊而返走。天明，逐队出城，亦无他异。

    　　○竹叶鬼丰溪吴奉我，作宦闽峤，谢病归里。舟过豫章，天暑热，假空馆于百花洲，屋宇宽敞，颇觉适意。屋内外常有声如鬼啸，家人独行，往往见黑影不一。一夕，吴设榻乘凉于阑干侧，闻墙角芭蕉丛中有声，走出无数人，长者、短者、肥者、瘠者，皆不过尺许。最后一人稍大，荷大笠帽，不见戎其面。旋绕垣中，若数十个不倒翁。吴急呼人至，倏忽不见，化作满地流萤。吴捉之，一萤才入手，戛然有声，馀萤悉灭。取火烛之，一竹叶而已。

    Explanation

    680

    A Face on the City Gate

    A messenger named Changning from Guangxi Province was tasked with an urgent matter and had to leave the city at the fifth drum strike in the early morning. Upon reaching the gate, he found it still locked. He reached out to touch it and felt a soft, smooth surface like human skin. The messenger was greatly startled and, under the faint light of the waning moon, focused his eyes to see a face fully occupying the city gate, with all facial features intact, including eyes as large as winnowing baskets. He was so frightened that he turned and ran away. By dawn, he left the city in groups, and there were no other unusual occurrences.

    The Bamboo Leaf Ghost

    Wu Fengxi, an official serving in the mountainous regions of Fujian, returned home due to illness. While passing through Yuzhang (ancient name for Nanchang), Jiangxi, during the hot summer days, he temporarily stayed in an empty house on the beautiful Baihua Isle. The house was spacious and quite comfortable. However, there were often sounds like ghost wails inside and outside the house. When family members walked alone, they often saw various black shadows. One night, Wu set up a bed to enjoy the coolness beside the railing. He heard a rustling sound coming from the banana grove near the wall. Suddenly, countless people emerged, some tall, some short, some fat, some thin, all no more than a foot tall. The last person was slightly larger, wearing a large bamboo hat, and his face was not visible. They spun around the wall like dozens of unbreakable toys. Wu quickly called for someone to come, but they disappeared suddenly, transforming into countless fireflies. Wu tried to catch one, and as soon as he touched it, a sharp sound was heard, and all the other fireflies vanished. He lit a fire to illuminate it and found it was just a bamboo leaf.

    681

    ○驴大爷某贵官长子，性凶暴，左右稍不如意，即扑责致死，侍女下体，以非刑。

    　　未几病死，见梦于平昔亲信之家奴云：“阴司以我残暴，罚我为畜，明晨当入驴腹中。汝速往某胡同驴肉铺中，将牝驴买归，以救我命。稍迟，则无及矣。”言甚哀。奴惊寤，心犹疑之，乃复睡去。又梦告之曰：“以我与尔有恩，俾尔救援，尔宁忘平日眷顾耶？”奴亟赴某胡同，见一牝驴将次屠宰。买归园中，果生一驹，见人如相识者。人呼“大爷”，则跃而至。

    Explanation

    681

    A Certain Nobleman's Eldest Son, Known as "Old Donkey Master"

    The eldest son of a certain nobleman was extremely cruel and violent. If anyone around him did not please him, he would beat them to death. He also subjected his maidservants to cruel punishments.

    After a short time, he fell ill and died. In a dream, he appeared to a household servant who had been his close confidant and said, "The underworld is punishing me for my cruelty by turning me into an animal. Tomorrow morning, I will enter the belly of a donkey. Go quickly to a certain alley where there is a donkey meat shop and buy a female donkey. Bring it back to save my life. If you delay, it will be too late." His words were filled with sorrow. The servant was startled awake, but still doubted what he had heard, so he fell back asleep. He dreamt again and the nobleman said to him, "Because of the kindness I showed you, I am asking you to rescue me. Could you forget the care I showed you in the past?" The servant hurried to the alley and saw a female donkey that was about to be slaughtered. He bought it and brought it back to the garden, where it gave birth to a foal. The foal recognized people as if it knew them. When people called "Master," it would jump over to them.

    682

    有画士邹某，居其园侧，一日闻驴鸣，其家人云：“此我家大爷声也。”

    　　○熊太太康熙间，内城伍公某者，三等侍卫也，从上打围木兰。以逐取猎犬故，坠深涧中，自分死矣。饿三日，有人熊过涧，乃抱以上，自分以为将啖己也，愈惊。

    　　熊抱入山洞，采果喂之，或负羊豕与食。伍见而攒眉，熊为采树叶。烧熟以食之。

    　　久之，渐无怖意。每小便。熊必视其阴而笑，方知熊故雌也，遂与成夫妇。生三子，勇力绝人。

    　　伍欲出山，熊不许；其子求还家，熊许之。长子名诺布，官蓝翎侍卫，乃以巨车迎父母还家，家人号曰“熊太太”。人求见者，熊不能言，能叉手答礼。就养其家十余年，先伍公卒。学士春台亲见之，为余言。

    Explanation

    682

    There was a painter named Zou who lived near the garden. One day, he heard a donkey braying, and his family said, "This is the sound of my master."

    In the Kangxi era, there was a certain Wu official in the inner city, a third-rank imperial guard, who accompanied the emperor on a hunting expedition in Mulan. Because he was chasing after his hunting dog, he fell into a deep ravine and thought he was going to die. He starved for three days until a bear crossed the ravine and picked him up, carrying him to the top. He thought the bear would eat him and became even more frightened.

    The bear carried him into a cave and gathered fruits to feed him, sometimes bringing sheep or pigs to share. Wu frowned upon seeing this, so the bear gathered leaves, cooked them, and fed him.

    After some time, he gradually lost his fear. Every time he urinated, the bear would look at his genitals and laugh, and he realized the bear was actually female. They then became husband and wife and had three sons, all of whom were extraordinarily strong.

    Wu wanted to leave the mountain, but the bear did not allow it. His son requested to return home, and the bear agreed. The eldest son was named Nobu and became a third-rank imperial guard. He used a large carriage to bring his parents back home, and the family called her "Bear Lady." People who wanted to see her could not speak, but she could bow in greeting. She lived with the family for over a decade, dying before Wu. Scholar Chun Tai personally witnessed this and told me about it.

    683

    ○冤鬼错认杭城艮山门外俞家桥杨元龙，在湖墅米行中管理帐目。湖墅距俞家桥五里，元龙朝往夕返，日以为常。偶一日，因米行生理热闹，迟至更余方归。至得胜坝桥，遇素识李孝先偕二人急奔。元龙呼之，李答云：“不知二人何事，要紧拉我往苏州去？”杨询二人，皆笑而不答。元龙拱手别李，李嘱云：“汝过潮王庙里许小石桥边，有问汝姓名者，须告以他姓，不可言姓杨；若言姓杨，须并以名告之。切记，切记！”元龙欲问故，孝先匆匆行矣。

    　　元龙前行至桥，果有二人坐草中问其姓名。元龙方答姓杨，二人即直前扭结云：“久候多时，今日不能放你了。”元龙以手拒之，奈彼伙渐众，为其扯入水中；始悟为鬼，并记前语，即大呼曰：“我杨元龙并未与各位有仇！”中有一鬼曰：“误矣，放还可也。”方叫唤间，适有卖汤圆者过桥，闻人叫声，持灯来照，见元龙在水中，急救之。元龙起视，即邻人张老，告以故，张老送元龙归家。

    Explanation

    683

    A Ghost Mistakenly Identifies Yang Yuanlong of Yu Jiaqiao, Outside the Gen Shan Gate of Hangzhou

    Yang Yuanlong, who managed accounts at a rice shop in Hu Shu, was mistakenly identified by a ghost. Hu Shu is five li away from Yu Jiaqiao, and Yuanlong would go there in the morning and return in the evening, making this his daily routine. One day, because the rice shop was busy, he returned home late, well past midnight. As he reached De Sheng Ba Bridge, he encountered Li Xiaoxian, whom he knew well, running with two others. Yuanlong called out to Li, who replied, "I don't know what these two are up to, but they're dragging me to Suzhou!" Yuanlong asked the two people, but they only laughed and did not answer. Yuanlong bowed to Li and bid him farewell. Li warned him, "When you pass the Xu Xiaoshi Bridge near Chao Wang Temple, if anyone asks for your name, tell them a different surname, not Yang. If you must say Yang, give them your full name. Remember this, remember this!" Yuanlong wanted to ask why, but Li hurried away.

    As Yuanlong continued on, he reached the bridge and indeed found two people sitting in the grass asking for his name. As soon as Yuanlong answered that his surname was Yang, the two people immediately grabbed him and dragged him into the water, saying, "We've been waiting for you a long time, and today we won't let you go!" Yuanlong tried to resist, but more and more people joined in, pulling him into the water. He then realized they were ghosts and remembered Li's warning. He shouted, "I, Yang Yuanlong, have no grudge against any of you!" One of the ghosts said, "You're mistaken, we can let you go." Just as he was shouting, a vendor selling tangyuan (glutinous rice balls) passed by the bridge, heard the cries, and came with a lamp to see what was happening. Seeing Yuanlong in the water, the vendor rescued him. Yuanlong got up and saw that it was his neighbor Zhang Laoren, whom he told what had happened. Zhang Laoren then escorted Yuanlong back home.

    684

    次早，元龙往视孝先，见孝先方殓。询之，其家云：“昨晚中风死矣。”盖遇李时，即李死时也，但不知往苏州何事。

    　　○代州猎户代州猎户李崇南，郊外驰射，见鸽成群，发火枪击之，正中其背，负铅子而飞。李在惊，追逐至一山洞，鸽入不见。李穿洞而进，则石室甚宽，有石人数十，雕镂极工，头皆斫去，各以手自提之；最后一人，枕头而卧，怒目视李，睛闪闪如欲动者。李大怖，方欲退出，而带铅子之鸽率鸽数万争来咬扑。李持空枪且击且走，不觉坠入池内，水红热如血，其气甚腥。鸽似甚渴者，争饮于池，李方得脱逃。出洞，衣上所染红水，鲜明无比，夜间映射灯月之下，有火光照灼。终不知此山鸽究属何怪。

    Explanation

    684

    The next morning, Yuan Long went to visit Xiao Xian and found him in the process of being prepared for burial. Upon inquiring, Xiao Xian's family informed him that he had died of a stroke the previous night. It so happened that Yuan Long's visit coincided with the time of Li Chongnan's death, but the reason for Li's trip to Suzhou remained a mystery.

    In another account, Li Chongnan, a hunter from Dai Prefecture, was out hunting in the outskirts when he saw a flock of pigeons. He fired his gun and hit one squarely in the back, causing the pigeon to carry the bullet and fly away. Startled, Li chased the pigeon into a mountain cave, where the pigeon disappeared. Curious, Li entered the cave and discovered a spacious stone chamber containing numerous stone carvings of people. The carvings were highly detailed, but their heads had been severed, and they held their own heads in their hands. The last figure in the chamber was lying on a pillow, glaring at Li with eyes that seemed to flicker as if they were about to move. Overcome with fear, Li tried to leave, but thousands of pigeons carrying bullets rushed at him, biting and pecking. Holding an empty gun, Li shot and ran, accidentally falling into a pool of red, hot water that smelled strongly of blood. The pigeons, appearing extremely thirsty, drank from the pool, allowing Li to escape. As he left the cave, the red water stains on his clothes glowed vividly under the light of the lamp and moon, as if illuminated by a fire. Ultimately, the nature of the strange pigeons in the mountain remained a mystery.

    685

    ○金刚作闹严州司寇某，有戚徐姓者，能持《金刚经》。司寇卒后，徐作功德，为诵经，日八百遍。一夕病重，梦鬼役召至阎罗殿，上坐王者谓曰：“某司寇办事太刻，奉上帝檄，发交我处。应讯事甚多，忽然金刚神闯门入，大吵大闹，不许我审，硬向我要某司寇去。我系地下冥司，金刚乃天上神将，我不敢与抗，只好交其带去。金刚竟将他释放。我因人犯脱逃，不能奏复上帝，只得行查至地藏王处，方知是汝在阳间多事，替他念《金刚经》所致。地藏王晓得公事公办，无可挽回，故替我拦住金刚神，不许再来作闹，仍将某公解回听审。所以召汝者，将此情节告知，不许再为诵经。姑念汝也是一片好意，无大罪过，故仍放汝还阳。然妄召尊神，终有小谴，已罚减阳寿一纪矣。”徐大惊而醒。未十年竟卒。

    Explanation

    685

    A Vajra Causing a Commotion: The Grand Judge of Yanzhou

    A relative of the surname Xu, who was able to recite the Diamond Sutra, had a kinsman who was a Grand Judge in Yanzhou. After the judge's death, Xu performed good deeds and chanted the Diamond Sutra daily, reciting it eight hundred times each day. One night, while gravely ill, Xu dreamed that ghost officials summoned him to the Yama Court, where a ruler seated above said to him, "This Grand Judge was too harsh in his dealings, and I received a decree from the Heavenly Emperor to bring him to my place for judgment. There were many cases to be heard, but suddenly the Vajra burst in, causing a great commotion, refusing to allow me to proceed with the trial, and forcibly demanding that I hand over the Grand Judge to him. As an official of the underworld, I dare not oppose a heavenly deity, so I had no choice but to hand him over. The Vajra then released the Grand Judge. Because the prisoner escaped, I could not report back to the Heavenly Emperor, so I had to investigate the matter with the Bodhisattva Ksitigarbha, who informed me that it was you who, while in the mortal world, caused trouble by reciting the Diamond Sutra on his behalf. Ksitigarbha understood that the matter had been handled according to proper procedure, and there was no way to reverse it, so he stopped the Vajra from causing further disturbance and returned the Grand Judge to me for judgment. That is why I called you here—to inform you of this situation and to forbid you from reciting the Diamond Sutra again. Out of consideration for your good intentions, I will not impose a severe punishment, but I have reduced your lifespan by one jī as a penalty for improperly summoning a revered deity." Xu awoke in great shock. Within ten years, he passed away.

    686

    吴西林曰：“金刚乃佛家木强之神，党同伐异，闻呼必来，有求必应，全不顾其理之是非曲直也，故佛氏坐之门外，为壮观御武之用。诵此经者，宜慎重焉。”

    　　○烧头香凡世俗神前烧香者，以侵早第一枝为头香，至第二枝，便为不敬。有山阴沈姓者，必欲到城隍庙烧头香，屡起早往，则已有人先烧矣，闷闷不乐。其弟某知之，预先通知庙祝：毋纳他人，俟其先到，再开门纳客。庙祝如其言。沈清晨往，见烧香者未至，大喜，点香下拜，则仆地不起矣。

    　　扶舁归家，大呼曰：“我沈某妻也。我虽有妒行，然罪无死法。我夫不良，趁我生产时，嘱稳婆将二铁针置产门中，以此陨命。一家之人，竟无知者。我诉城隍神，神说我夫阳寿未终，不准审理。前月关帝过此，我往喊冤，城隍说我冲突仪仗，又缚我放香案脚下。幸天网恢恢，我夫来烧头香，被我捉住，特来索命。”

    Explanation

    686

    Wu Xilin said, "The Diamond is a wooden and strong deity of the Buddhist faith, biased and hostile towards differing views, coming when called and responding to requests, completely disregarding the rightness or wrongness of the matter. Therefore, the Buddhists place it outside the gate for the purpose of impressive display and martial defense. Those who recite this sutra should be cautious."

    ○ Burning the First Incense

    In the secular world, those who burn incense before the gods consider the first incense stick offered early in the morning as the head incense. If the second incense stick is offered, it is considered disrespectful. There was a person from Shanyin with the surname Shen who was determined to be the first to burn incense at the City God Temple. He got up early many times, but someone always beat him to it, leaving him depressed and unhappy. His younger brother learned of this and notified the temple attendant in advance: do not admit others until he arrives first, then open the door to admit visitors. The temple attendant followed his instructions. Shen went to the temple early in the morning, saw that no one had arrived to burn incense yet, and was overjoyed. He lit the incense and bowed, but then fell to the ground unconscious.

    He was carried home and shouted, "I am the wife of Mr. Shen. Although I have a jealous nature, my crime does not warrant death. My husband was unscrupulous, taking advantage of my childbirth to instruct the midwife to place two iron needles in my birth canal, causing my death. None of the family members knew about it. I appealed to the City God, but the City God said my husband's earthly life had not ended, so he would not allow the case to be heard. Last month when Guan Di passed by, I went to appeal, but the City God said I disrupted the procession and tied me to the incense altar. Fortunately, the vast net of heaven has its ways, and my husband came to burn the first incense, where I caught him and came to demand my life."

    687

    沈家人毕集拜求，请焚纸钱百万，或请召名僧超度。沈仍作妻语曰：“汝等痴矣！我死甚惨，想往叩天阍，将城隍纵恶、沈某行恶之事，一齐申诉，岂区区纸钱超度所能饶名者乎？”言毕，沈自床上投地，七窍流血死。

    　　○树怪费此度从征西蜀，到三峡涧，有树孑立，存枯枝而无花叶，兵过其下辄死，死者三人。费怒，自视之，其树枝如鸟爪，见有人过，便来攫拿。费以利剑斫之，株落血流。此后行人无恙。

    　　○广信狐仙徐芷亭方伯初守广信府，有西厢房锁闭多年，云中有狐。徐夫人不信，亲往观之。闻鼾呼声，启户无人，声从一榻中出。夫人以棍敲之，空中有人语云：“夫人莫打。我吴子刚也，居此百余年，颇有去意。屡欲移居，而门神拦我。夫人可为我祭之，且代为乞情，则我让出朝廷公廨矣。”

    Explanation

    687

    The entire family of Shen gathered to pray and beg, requesting to burn a hundred million paper money, or to summon famous monks for salvation. Shen still spoke as if he were the wife, saying, "You are all foolish! I died very painfully, and I want to go and knock at the heavenly gate to complain about the city god allowing evil to prevail and Shen's acts of evil. How could mere paper money and salvation suffice to spare my name?" After speaking, Shen fell from the bed to the ground, bleeding from all seven orifices and died.

    In another instance, during Fei Zhi's campaign in Western Shu, he came to the Three Gorges Ravine, where there was a tree standing alone, with only withered branches and no flowers or leaves. Whenever soldiers passed under it, they died; three soldiers died. Fei Zhi became angry and looked at it himself. The tree branches looked like bird claws, and when someone passed by, they would come and grab. Fei Zhi cut it down with a sharp sword, and the trunk fell, blood flowing. Afterward, travelers were safe.

    In yet another story, Xu Zhiting, the governor of Guangxin Prefecture, had a west wing room that had been locked for many years, rumored to be haunted by a fox. Xu's wife did not believe it and went to see for herself. She heard snoring and roaring sounds, but when she opened the door, there was no one there. The sounds came from a bed. The lady hit it with a stick, and someone in the air said, "Madam, please do not hit me. I am Wu Zigan, and I have lived here for over a hundred years, and I have the intention to leave. I have wanted to move many times, but the door gods have blocked me. Madam, you can offer a sacrifice for me and ask them to let me go, and I will give up the government office."

    688

    夫人大骇，具酒肴向竹床陈设，兼祭门神，告以原委。又闻空中语曰：“我受夫人恩，愧无以报，谨来贺喜。府上老爷即日升官。奉嘱者，七月七日，切勿抱官官到红梅园嬉戏，其日恐有恶鬼在园作祟。”言毕寂然。

    　　到期，方伯表兄某过园，见树上有两红衣儿以手招人。就视之，并无形影，但闻崩颓之声，则假山石倒矣，几为所压。九月间，徐公升赣南道。此事徐公子秉鉴为我言。

    　　○白石精天长林司坊名师者，家设乩坛，有怪物占为坛主，自名“白石真人”，人问休咎颇验。常教林君修仙，须面上开一眼，便可见上帝宫室，云中神仙。林从此痴迷，时以小刀向鼻间刻划。人夺其刀，便怒骂。

    Explanation

    688

    The lady was greatly frightened and prepared wine and food on the bamboo bed, also worshipping the door gods and telling them the whole story. She then heard a voice from the air say, "I have received the lady's kindness and feel ashamed that I have nothing to repay. I come to congratulate you. Your husband will be promoted to a higher position soon. The one who was entrusted to you is on the seventh day of the seventh month. Do not take any officials to the Red Plum Garden for fun, as there might be evil spirits causing trouble in the garden." After the voice disappeared, a certain cousin of the provincial governor passed by the garden and saw two red-clothed children waving hands on the tree. He looked at them, but there were no visible figures. But he only heard the sound of collapse, and the artificial mountain collapsed, almost crushing him. In September, Mr. Xu was promoted to the position of governor of Gannan. This matter was told to me by Mr. Xu's son, Bingjian.

    ○ A certain master named Lin, who lived in the village of Linshi in Tianchang, set up a spirit-medium altar at home. A monster claimed the position of the altar's master, naming itself "White Stone Immortal." People who consulted the altar about auspicious or inauspicious signs found it quite accurate. The monster often taught Master Lin to practice immortality, saying that he must open an eye on his face to see the palaces of the Heavenly Emperor and the immortals in the clouds. From then on, Master Lin became obsessed with this idea and would often use a small knife to carve marks on his nose. When people tried to take the knife away from him, he would become angry and curse them.

    689

    忽一日，乩盘书云：“我土地神也。现在缠汝者是西山白石之精，神通绝大，我受其驱使。渠不能作字，凡乩上，皆强我代书。今日渠往西天参佛，故我特来通知，速拆乩盘，具呈于本县城隍，庶免此难。但切不可告知此怪，是土地神来泄漏也。”适蒋太史苕生自金陵来，知其故，立毁其盘，并以三十金买天师符一张，悬林室中，怪果不至。

    　　后十年，林君亡矣，符尚挂中堂，有线香倒下，烧其符上朱砂，字画尽，而衬纸不坏。其时蒋在京师，未得林讣，适天师来朝，告蒋曰：“贵亲家林君死矣。”

    　　问：“何以知之？”曰：“某月日，我所遣符上神将已来归位故也。”后得之林家烧符之信，方觉骇然。

    　　当扶乩时，蒋在座，则盘中不动。蒋去后，人问乩，书云：“此老有文光射人，我不喜见之。”据土地云：“白石精在林家作祟者，要摄取林之魂，供其役使故耳。”

    Explanation

    689

    One day, the divination disk wrote: "I am the land deity. The one currently troubling you is the spirit of the white stone in the West Mountain, with immense supernatural power. I am being forced to act by it. It cannot write, so all that appears on the divination disk is written by me on its behalf. Today, it has gone to the Western Heaven to consult the Buddha, so I have come to inform you. Quickly destroy the divination disk and present a report to the local city god of this county, in order to avoid this difficulty. But do not tell this monster that it was the land deity who disclosed this information." At that time, Mr. Jiang, a historian from Nanjing, arrived and learned of the situation. He immediately destroyed the divination disk and purchased a Taoist talisman for thirty gold pieces, hanging it in Mr. Lin's room. The spirit did not appear thereafter.

    Ten years later, Mr. Lin passed away, but the talisman remained hanging in the hall. One day, a burning incense stick fell and burned the red cinnabar on the talisman, destroying the characters and paintings, while the backing paper remained unharmed. At that time, Mr. Jiang was in the capital city and had not yet received news of Mr. Lin's death. Just then, a Taoist priest arrived and told Mr. Jiang, "Your relative, Mr. Lin, has passed away."

    Mr. Jiang asked, "How do you know this?" The Taoist priest replied, "On a certain day, the spirit that I sent on the talisman has returned to its position." Later, when Mr. Jiang received news that Mr. Lin's family had burned the talisman, he was greatly shocked.

    When Mr. Jiang was present during the divination, the disk remained still. After Mr. Jiang left, someone asked the divination disk, which wrote: "This old man has a literary brilliance that shines on others, and I do not like to see him." According to the land deity, "The white stone spirit that was causing trouble in Mr. Lin's household wanted to capture his soul to serve as a slave."

    690

    ○鬼圈蒋少司马时庵公子某，与数友在京师游愍忠寺。时届清明，踏青荒地，见精舍数间，中有琵琶声。趋往，则一女背面坐，手弹弦索。逼视之，女回头，变青面狰狞者，直来相扑，阴风袭人，各惊走归。时尚下午，彼此以为眼花，且恃有四人之众，各持木棍再往，则有四黑人坐而相待，手持铜圈套人。受其套者，无不倾跌，棍无所施。正仓皇间，有放马者数人驱马冲来，怪始不见。四人归，各病十馀日。

    　　○东医宝鉴有法治狐萧山李选民，少年惆傥。烧香佛庙，见美女在焉，四顾无人，逐与通语。女自言姓吴，幼无父母，依舅而居。舅母凌虐，故在此礼佛，愿得佳耦。李以言挑之，女唯唯，遂与归家，情好甚笃。久之，李体日羸。觉交接时吸取其精，与寻常夫妇不同。且十里以内之事，必先知之。心知为狐，驱之无法。

    Explanation

    690

    ○ In a place called Ghost Circle, there was a young nobleman named Mr. Jiang, the son of a lesser general, who was known as the "Prince of the Time of the Temple." One day, he and several friends were visiting the Temple of Loyalty and Filial Piety in the capital city. It was the Qingming Festival, and they were enjoying the spring outing in a desolate area when they saw several small pavilions and heard the sound of a pipa being played inside one of them. They hurried over to investigate, and they saw a woman sitting with her back to them, playing the stringed instrument. As they got closer, the woman turned around, and her face turned green and grotesque, transforming into a terrifying visage. She came straight at them, and a cold wind struck them, causing them to run back in fear. It was still early afternoon, and they thought they must have been seeing things. They relied on their numbers, four of them, each holding a wooden stick, and went back to the spot. There, they found four dark figures sitting and waiting for them, holding copper rings that could ensnare people. Anyone caught by these rings would fall down, and the sticks were useless. Just as they were in a state of panic, several people driving horses came charging through, and the strange beings disappeared. The four men returned home and fell ill for more than ten days.

    ○ In the Eastern Medical Canon, there is a method for treating fox spirits. There was a young man named Li Xuanmin from Xiaoshan, who was known for his bold and carefree nature. One day, he was burning incense in a Buddhist temple and saw a beautiful woman there. He looked around and saw no one else, so he approached her and started talking. The woman said her name was Wu and that she had no parents, living with her uncle. Her aunt was cruel to her, so she was there praying to the Buddha, hoping to find a good match. Li tried to flirt with her, and she responded politely, so he took her home. They became very close, and their relationship was very intimate. After some time, Li's body became increasingly weak. He noticed that during their intimate moments, she was absorbing his essence, which was different from that of ordinary couples. Moreover, she always knew about events within ten miles of their home before they happened. He realized she was a fox spirit, but he couldn't find a way to drive her away.

    691

    一日，拉其友杨孝廉至三十里外，以情告之。杨曰：“我记《东医宝鉴》中有治狐术一条，何不试之？”遂偕往琉璃厂，觅得是书，求东洋人译而行之，女果涕泣去。

    　　此事余在西江谢蕴山太史家亲见，杨孝廉为余言之，惜未问其《东医宝鉴》中是何卷页。

    　　○乩言抚州太守陈太晖，未第时，在浙乡试，向乩神问题，批云：“具体而微。”

    　　后中副车，方知所告者，非题也。有求对联者，书“努力加餐饭，小心事友生”

    　　十字。问：“次句何出？”曰：“秀才读时文，不能杜诗，可怜可笑。”陈方与友游鉴湖观莲，乩问：“昨日鉴湖之游乐乎？”有咏红莲者，以诗求和，乩上题云：“红衣落尽小姑忙，从此朝来叶亦香。莫恼韶光太匆迫，花开三日即为长。”

    Explanation

    691

    One day, he took his friend Yang Xiaolian, a scholar-official, thirty li away and informed him of the situation. Yang said, "I remember there is a fox-curing technique in Dongyibaojian. Why not try it?" They then went together to Lüliu Factory, where they found the book and asked a Japanese person to translate and apply it. The woman indeed cried and left.

    I witnessed this event firsthand at Xie Yunshan's house in Xijiang, and Yang Xiaolian told me about it. I regret not asking which volume and page in Dongyibaojian it was.

    The spirit medium said about Governor Chen Taihui of Fuzhou, who had not yet passed the imperial examinations when he was taking the provincial exams in Zhejiang. He asked the spirit medium a question, and it replied, "It is specific but small."

    Later, he passed as a second-place candidate and realized that what he had asked about was not the exam question. Someone asked for a couplet, and he wrote, "Strive to eat more, be careful in serving friends." He asked, "Where does the second line come from?" The spirit medium replied, "Scholars cannot read Du Fu's poetry, pitiable and laughable." Chen was touring the West Lake with a friend to admire lotuses, and the spirit medium asked, "Was the trip to the West Lake yesterday enjoyable?" Someone recited a poem about red lotuses, and the spirit medium responded with a poem: "The red robes have fallen, the young girl is busy. From now on, the leaves will also be fragrant in the morning. Do not be annoyed by the fleeting nature of time, for the blooming of the flower lasts only three days."

    692

    云门山氓有被鬼作闹者，诣乩盘求救，乩书：“我不能救，请某村余二太爷来救。”如其言，请余二太爷至，余向其家东北角厉声曰：“你们要往四川也，该速去了！”空中应曰：“极是。”从此恨竟寂然。余二太爷者，某村之学究也，问其所以驱鬼者是何言语，笑而不答。问乩，乩亦无言。

    　　●卷二十○移观音像山西泽州北门外有庙供观音，时时有黄蜂从其座下石缝中出，纷纷数万，白日为晦。土人移观音像，掘蜂穴，以火熏之。见一朱棺，有底无面，中有妇人突然而起，将红袖一挥，颈拖双带而走。众瞠视，听其所往。其裙上满绣蝴蝶，飘飘然竟入市中李姓家而灭。李方娶妇，众人告以故。李以为妄，大骂众人荒诞。

    　　未三日，其家新妇缢死。

    Explanation

    692

    A villager from Yunmen Mountain was troubled by ghosts, so he went to seek help at an oracle board. The board wrote: "I cannot save you; please have the respected elder Mr. Yu from a certain village come to save you." Following the advice, the villager invited Mr. Yu, who then went to the northeast corner of the villager's home and shouted sternly, "You must go to Sichuan soon!" The voice from the air responded, "That is correct." From that moment, the disturbance ceased. Mr. Yu, the respected elder from the village, was asked why he used those words to drive away the ghosts, but he smiled and did not answer. When asked the oracle board, it remained silent.

    In the twentieth chapter, there is a temple in the north of Zezhou, Shanxi, dedicated to the Bodhisattva Guanyin. Often, tens of thousands of yellow bees would emerge from a crevice beneath the Bodhisattva's seat, causing the daylight to dim. The locals moved the statue of Guanyin, dug up the bees' nest, and burned it with fire. They then saw a red coffin with a bottom but no lid, from which a woman suddenly rose. She waved her red sleeve and walked away with her neck dragging two ribbons. Everyone stared in amazement, watching her direction. Her dress was adorned with butterflies, and she floated into the home of a man surnamed Li in the market, where she disappeared. Li had just married a new wife, and the people told him the story. Li thought it was nonsense and scolded everyone for being absurd.

    Three days later, Li's new wife hanged herself.

    693

    ○山阴风灾己丑年，蒋太史心余掌教山阴。有扶乩者徐姓盘上大书“关神下降”。蒋拜问其母太夫人年寿，神批云：“尔母系再来人，来去自有一定，未便先漏天机。”

    　　复书云：“屏去家僮，有要语告君。”如其言。乃云：“君负清才，故尔相告。

    　　今年七月二十四日，山阴有大灾，尔宜奉母避去。“蒋云：”弟子现在寄居，绝少亲戚，无处可避。且果系劫数中人，避亦无益。“乩盘批”达哉“二字，灵风肃然，神亦去矣。

    　　临七月之期，蒋亦忘神所言，二十四日晨起，天气清和，了无变态。过午二刻，忽大风西来，黑云如墨，人对面不能相见，两龙斗于空中，飞沙走石；石如碗大者，打入窗中以千百计；古树十余丈者，折如寸草；所居蕺山书院石柱尽摇，至申刻始定。墙倾处压死两奴，独一七岁小儿存米桶中呻吟不死。问之，曰：“当墙倒时，见一黑人长丈余，擒我纳桶内。”其母则已死桶外矣。是年，临海居民死者数万人。

    Explanation

    693

    In the year of the Ox, Jiang XinYu, the chief instructor of Shanyin, was staying in Shanyin. A diviner named Xu performed a fújī session, and on the board appeared the words "Guan Yu descends." Jiang bowed and asked about the longevity of his mother, the Lady Taifu. The spirit replied: "Your mother is a '再来人' (a person who returns), and her coming and going are predetermined; it is not appropriate to reveal heavenly secrets prematurely."

    The spirit then wrote: "Remove your servants, and I will tell you something important." Jiang followed the instructions. The spirit said: "You possess a clear talent, so I am informing you.

    "On the twenty-fourth day of the seventh month this year, there will be a great disaster in Shanyin. You should take your mother and flee." Jiang replied: "I am currently staying in a temporary residence, with few relatives, and nowhere to escape. Moreover, if I am destined to perish, fleeing will be of no use." The spirit on the board wrote "达哉" (Indeed so), and a sudden, eerie wind arose, after which the spirit departed.

    As the seventh month approached, Jiang forgot the spirit's warning. On the twenty-fourth day, he woke up to clear weather with no signs of change. By mid-afternoon, a strong wind came from the west, bringing dark clouds like ink, so thick that people could not see each other face-to-face. Two dragons fought in the sky, sending flying sand and rolling stones; stones as large as bowls rained into the windows in the hundreds. Ancient trees over ten zhang tall were broken like grass. The stone pillars of the Jishan Academy where Jiang lived swayed violently, and it was not until the late afternoon that the storm subsided. The collapsed wall crushed two servants, but a seven-year-old boy survived, crying in a rice bucket. When asked, he said: "When the wall fell, I saw a black figure over a zhang tall who grabbed me and put me into the bucket." His mother, however, had already died outside the bucket. That year, thousands of residents in Linhai perished in the disaster.

    694

    ○谢檀霞连者，昭州人，好洁耽吟。友人某邀与同贾楚中，友入肆会计，独守舟次。泊湘源数日，爱江水净碧，凡衣裳襟带，都促奴子再三浣濯，而自吟不辍。

    　　夜梦身立水上，有好女子蹴波与语，自称：“谢檀霞，元时人，年十八夭死。父母怜我癖爱此间山水，遂葬于此。今冢没水噬，遗骨久付泥沙。生时好洁耽吟，与君同癖，宜寿而夭，故得全其神气，不复轮回，生死介在仙鬼之间。君明日当死于风涛中，妾怜其癖之同也，敢以预告，君可速附他舟回家。”惊醒，即治装，觅下水船抵家。归后足不出户，旅闻湘源陷风涛，死数千人，惴惴而已。

    Explanation

    694

    Xie Tanxia, a man from Zhaozhou, loved cleanliness and was fond of reciting poetry. A friend invited him to join in trade in Chu. The friend went to the shop to count money, leaving Xie Tanxia alone on the boat. They stayed at Xiangyuan for several days. He loved the clear and green waters of the river, so all his clothes, belts, and other items were washed repeatedly by the servants. He continuously recited poetry.

    One night, he had a dream where he stood on the water, and a beautiful woman kicked up waves and spoke to him. She introduced herself as Xie Tanxia, a person from the Yuan dynasty who died at the age of eighteen. Her parents loved her passion for the local scenery and buried her there. Her tomb was submerged in water, and her remains had long been buried in mud and sand. When she was alive, she loved cleanliness and recitation, just like him.
