She should have lived a long life but died young, so she retained her spirit and did not reincarnate, existing between the realms of immortals and ghosts. She warned him that he would die in a storm the next day. She pitied his shared passion and dared to foretell this to him. He should quickly board another boat and return home.

    Xie Tanxia woke up and prepared his belongings, finding a boat to return home. After returning, he did not leave his house. He heard that Xiangyuan was hit by a storm, killing thousands of people, and was very anxious.

    695

    年余，忽梦吏数人突至其家，责以免脱之罪，谓“冥王赫怒，将重按其事。”

    　　皇遽甚，许梦冥钱若干，方允缓期。数夕后，鬼使复至，索钱加倍，亦允许。

    　　正当焚送之期，方昼寝，忽见檀霞自外入，笑曰：“我来贺君脱难，寻君居址不得，广为问讯。不图野水之劫，人数太多，容易蒙混。又喜各府判官新旧交代，我已遣人将君姓名注销，自今以后，杳无死期。我是数百年英魂，飘泊无耦，愿共晨夕。授子服气之法，不必交媾，如人世之夫妇也。”且曰：“鬼差索诈，不必理他，有我在此。”后遂白日降形其家，周旋如妻妾，不饮不食。

    Explanation

    695

    After more than a year, he suddenly dreamed that several officials rushed to his home, accusing him of the crime of escape and saying, "The King of Hell is furious and will re-investigate the matter." He was greatly alarmed and agreed to offer a certain amount of money to the spirits in a dream, which only resulted in a temporary delay. A few nights later, the ghostly messenger returned, demanding double the amount, which he also agreed to. During the time when the money was being burned and sent, he was just taking a daytime nap when he suddenly saw Tan Xia enter from outside, smiling and saying, "I come to congratulate you on escaping trouble. Unable to find your residence, I asked around widely. Unaware that the disaster at the wild water involved too many people, making it easy to mix in. I am also happy that the officials in charge of various departments are changing; I have already sent someone to cancel your name. From now on, there will be no end to your life. I am a spirit of a woman who has been wandering for hundreds of years without a companion, and I wish to spend every day and night with you. I will teach you the method of cultivating qi, no need for intercourse, like a couple in the human world." She added, "The ghostly messengers demanding money, don't bother with them, I am here to protect you." Later, she began to appear in his home during the day, behaving like a wife or concubine, and did not eat or drink.

    696

    久之，亦能辟谷，每言祸福辄应，闾里以此敬而奉之。檀霞嫌人世无味，仍偕重游湘中，不知所终。

    　　○引鬼报冤浙江盐运司快役马继先，积千金，为其子焕章营买吏缺。焕章吏才更胜乃翁，陡发家资巨万。继先暮年娶妾马氏，颇相得。继先私蓄千金指示妾云：“汝小心服侍，终我天年，我即将此物相赠，去留听汝。”越五六年，继先病，复语其子云：“此女事我甚谨，我死后，所蓄可俱付之。”

    　　继先死，焕章顿起不良，即与其姑丈吴某曾为泉州太守者商曰：“不意我翁私蓄尚多，命与此女，殊为可惜。”吴云：“此事易为。乃翁死后，我来助汝逐之。”过后日，焕章诱此妾出屋伴灵，私与其妻硬取箱箧，搬入内室，将乃翁卧房封锁，此妾在外，尚不知也。

    Explanation

    696

    After a long time,  was also able to practice辟谷, and whenever he spoke of good or bad fortune, it would always come true. The people of the neighborhood respected and revered him because of this. Tánxiá found the world to be tasteless and, still accompanied by , revisited the area around the Xiang River, but no one knows what happened to them afterward.

    ○引鬼报冤

    Zhejiang Salt Transportation Administration low-ranking official Mǎ Jìxiān accumulated a thousand gold pieces and used them to buy a position for his son Huànzhāng. Huànzhāng, with his administrative talent, surpassed his father, and suddenly amassed a fortune of millions. In his later years, Jìxiān took a concubine named Mǎ, and they got along very well. Jìxiān privately saved a thousand gold pieces and told the concubine: "Be careful in your service, until the end of my life, I will give you this treasure, and you can decide whether to stay or leave." After five or six years, Jìxiān fell ill and again told his son: "This woman has been very careful in her service to me. After my death, all my savings can be given to her."

    After Jìxiān's death, Huànzhāng suddenly became malicious and immediately discussed with his father-in-law, Wú, who had once been the governor of Quanzhou: "I didn't expect my father had so much private savings, and he left it to this woman, which is truly a pity." Wú said: "This is easy to handle. After my father-in-law's death, I will help you drive her away." The next day, Huànzhāng lured the concubine out of the house to accompany the spirit, secretly took the chests with his wife, moved them into the inner room, and sealed off his father's bedroom. The concubine was still outside and did not know about it.

    697

    继先回煞后，此妾欲归内室，吴突自外入，厉声曰：“姨娘无往！我看汝年轻，决不能守节，不若即今日收拾回娘家，另择良配。我叫汝小主人赠汝银两可也。”随呼焕章：“兑银五十两来。”焕章趋出曰：“已备。”妾欲进内，焕章止之，曰：“既是姑爷吩咐，想必不错。汝之箱箧行李，我已代汝收拾停妥，毋烦再入。”妾素愿，惧吴之威，含泪登舆去。焕章深谢吴之劳。

    　　又数月，节届中元。妾带去之资及衣饰已为父母兄弟荡尽，欲趁此节哭奠主人，仍归马氏守节。七月十二日，备香帛祭器至马家哭奠，焕章之妻骂曰：“无耻贱人，去而复返！”不容入内，命其坐外厅之侧轩暂过一夜，祭毕即去，如再逗留，我决不容！妾彻夜哭，五鼓方绝声。次早往视，已悬躯于梁矣。焕章买棺收敛，其母家惧吴声势，亦无异言。

    Explanation

    697

    After the ritual of hui sha (return of the soul) for Jiexian, this concubine wished to return to her inner quarters. Wu suddenly entered from outside and sternly said, "Auntie, do not go! I see you are young and will surely not remain loyal. It would be better if you gather your belongings today and return to your parents' home to find a good match. I will have your young master give you some silver." He then called Huanchang, "Get fifty taels of silver here." Huanchang hurried out and said, "It is ready." The concubine wanted to enter, but Huanchang stopped her and said, "Since the young master has instructed, it must be correct. Your luggage and belongings, I have already packed for you. There is no need to re-enter." The concubine, who had always wished to remain loyal, feared Wu's authority and wept as she climbed into the carriage. Huanchang deeply thanked Wu for his efforts.

    Several months later, the Ghost Festival arrived. The concubine had spent the money and clothing she took with her on her parents and brothers, leaving her with nothing. She wished to take advantage of the festival to mourn her master and then return to the Ma family to remain loyal. On the twelfth day of the seventh month, she prepared incense, silk, and ritual objects and went to the Ma family to mourn. Huanchang's wife scolded, "Shameless and lowly person, you left and now you return!" She did not allow her to enter and ordered her to sit in a side pavilion in the outer hall for one night. After the mourning, she must leave. If she lingers again, she will not be allowed! The concubine cried all night and stopped only at the fifth watch. The next morning, when they went to check, she had hanged herself from a beam. Huanchang bought a coffin and prepared her for burial. The concubine's family, fearing Wu's influence, made no unusual remarks.

    698

    焕章因屋有缢死鬼，将屋转售章姓，别构华室自居。章翁自小奉佛诵经，夜见此女作悬梁哭泣状。翁久知此事，心为不平，且恶焕章之嫁祸，乃祝曰：“马姨娘，我家买屋用价不少，并非强占。姨娘与马焕章、吴某有仇，与我家无干。

    　　明晚二更，我亲送汝至焕章家何如？“鬼嫣然一笑而没。

    　　次晚，为此女设位持香，送至焕章门，低声曰：“姨娘旁立，待我叩门。”

    　　即叩门问司阍：“汝主人归否？”对曰：“尚未。”乃又私祝曰：“姨娘请自入，仇可复矣。”司阍者不解章之喃喃何语，笑其痴。章归家，终夜不寐。

    　　天未明，即趋马家听信，见司阍者已立门外，章曰：“汝起何早？”司阍者曰：“昨夜主人归，方至门，即疾作，刻下危甚。”章惊而返。下午复探，马已死矣。过数日，吴太守亦亡。焕章无子，其资均为他人所有；吴没后，家亦不振。

    Explanation

    698

    Huanzhang, having sold his house to a man surnamed Zhang due to a ghost of a woman who had hanged herself there, built a new splendid residence for himself. Old Master Zhang, who had been devoutly following Buddhism and reciting scriptures since childhood, saw this woman at night hanging from a beam and crying. Old Master Zhang, who had long known about this matter, felt it was unjust and hated how Huanzhang shifted blame. He prayed, saying, "Auntie, my family bought the house for a considerable price and did not forcefully take it. Auntie, if you have a grudge against Huanzhang and Wu, it has nothing to do with my family. The next night at midnight, I will personally escort you to Huanzhang's home. What do you think?"

    The ghost smiled gently and disappeared. The next night, Old Master Zhang set up an altar and held incense, escorted her to Huanzhang's door, and whispered, "Auntie, please stand by me. I will knock on the door." He knocked on the door and asked the gatekeeper, "Has your master returned yet?" The gatekeeper replied, "Not yet." Old Master Zhang then whispered, "Auntie, please go in. Your grudge can be avenged." The gatekeeper did not understand Old Master Zhang's muttering and laughed at his foolishness. Old Master Zhang returned home and stayed awake all night.

    Before dawn, he hurried to Ma's house to listen for news. He saw the gatekeeper already standing outside the door. Old Master Zhang asked, "Why are you up so early?" The gatekeeper replied, "Last night, the master returned home just as he reached the door and fell ill. He is in grave danger now." Old Master Zhang returned home in shock. In the afternoon, he went back to check, and Ma had already died. Several days later, Governor Wu also died. Huanzhang had no children, and his assets were all taken by others. His family also fell into decline.

    699

    ○灵鬼两救兄弟武昌太守汪献琛之弟名延生者，暑月暴亡。后乾隆二十八年秋日，其堂兄希官亦得危疾，数夜不寐。医者开方，以补剂治之。其母方煎药，病者忽发声曰：“大婶娘毋再误也！我昔误于庸医，今希哥又遭此难，我不忍坐视其死。”言毕，即将药碗掷地。希母问曰：“汝何人凭我儿？”曰：“我即延生也，死未一年，婶娘不能辨我声音耶？”希母曰：“汝死后作何事？”曰：“阴司神念我性直，且系屈死，命我为常州城隍司案吏。因本宫移文浙省城隍，会议总督到任差务要事，命我赍文来此，我故得来一探希哥，不意渠已卧病，几为庸医所杀。此刻我往城隍衙门，将公事了结再来。”语毕，即闭目卧，竟夜安眠。

    Explanation

    699

    In the summer months, the younger brother of Wu Chang's governor, Wang Xianchen, named Yan Sheng, died suddenly. Later, in the autumn of the 28th year of the Qianlong era, his elder brother, Xi Guan, also fell gravely ill and could not sleep for several nights. The doctor prescribed a tonic to treat him. As the mother was preparing the medicine, the patient suddenly spoke, saying, "Aunt, do not make the same mistake again! I was once misdiagnosed by an incompetent doctor, and now Xi Guan is facing this danger. I cannot bear to see him die." After speaking, he threw the medicine bowl to the ground. Xi Guan's mother asked, "Who is possessing my son?" He replied, "I am Yan Sheng. I have not been dead for a year, and Aunt, cannot you recognize my voice?" Xi Guan's mother asked, "What have you been doing since your death?" He answered, "The gods of the underworld, recognizing my honesty and the fact that I died unjustly, appointed me as an official in the City God's office of Changzhou. Because our temple has sent a letter to the City God of Zhejiang Province, discussing important matters concerning the governor-general's appointment, I was sent here with the document. That is why I came to visit Xi Guan, but I did not expect him to be ill and almost killed by an incompetent doctor. Now I must go to the City God's office to finish my duties and return." After speaking, he closed his eyes and lay down, sleeping peacefully all night.

    700

    次早醒，问之，茫然无知。至晚，忽作延生声曰：“惫矣，速具水浆来解渴。”

    　　希母与之。又云：“可呼八兄来，我有话说。”八兄者，即其胞兄也。既至，慰问若生时，且云：“八兄，汝何贪戏若此？前在祖宗祠堂池内自荡小舟，几为石柱碰毙。其时幸我在旁，使柱旁倒，不然难逃此厄。柱下有古冢一丘，因我父浚池不察，使他枯骨日浸水中，故欲来报怨。我再三求之，彼方允诺。八兄须为迁葬。”又呼其妹三人至前曰：“大妹二妹，有福不妨，小妹禄甚薄，不若随我去，交与母亲照管，何苦在此常受庶母之气？”大笑拱手作别状，曰：“再会再会。”

    　　言毕，希忽仰卧如初。越数日，病愈，不半年，其幼妹果亡。

    　　二十九年冬，希哥梦延生至曰：“兄今愈矣。弟办完此差，小有功绩，可望受职。从此别矣，后会难期。”语竟而去，希哥悲呼而醒。

    Explanation

    700

    The next morning, he woke up and asked, but received a confused and ignorant response. By evening, he suddenly heard a voice that suggested prolonging life, saying, "I am exhausted, quickly prepare water and drink to quench my thirst."

    His mother gave him water and drink. The voice then said, "You can call my older brother, I have something to say." The older brother was his biological brother. When he arrived, he was comforted as if he were alive, and said, "Older brother, why are you so greedy for play? Before, in the pond of the ancestral temple, you were rowing a small boat by yourself and almost collided with a stone pillar and died. At that time, fortunately, I was beside you and made the pillar fall over, otherwise, you would have faced this disaster. Under the pillar, there is an ancient tomb, because my father dug the pond without noticing it, making the dry bones daily immersed in water, so they want to come and seek revenge. I repeatedly asked them, and they finally agreed. Older brother, you must relocate the burial." He then called his three sisters and said, "Big sister and second sister, you have good fortune, but little sister has little luck, it is better to follow me and be taken care of by our mother, why suffer the mistreatment of a stepmother here?" He laughed loudly, saluted with clasped hands, and made a parting gesture, saying, "See you again."

    After he finished speaking, he suddenly lay on his back as if nothing had happened. A few days later, his illness was cured, but within half a year, his younger sister indeed died.

    In the winter of the twenty-ninth year, his brother had a dream where the voice that suggested prolonging life appeared and said, "Brother, you are now cured. I have completed this task and have a small achievement, I can hope to receive a position. From now on, this is a farewell, future meetings will be difficult to expect." After the words were spoken, the voice left, and his brother cried out in sorrow and woke up.

    701

    ○木画永城尉陆敬轩，浙之萧山人，修署截木。署旧有柳树一株，锯之，板中现天然画一幅。如淡墨写成：左右峰石悬崖，崖上松一株，山树一株，枝叶倒垂，松上缠藤累累；中有一叟扶杖立，商冠长袖，须眉如活，左手纳袖中着胸前，右足前行露舄左舄隐衣下，回顾若听泉状。尉宝之，携归其家。时乾隆辛酉十月十三日事。

    　　○滚经台贵州平越府署内有石台，高七尺，藏佛经十六幅，全书梵字，读之不可解。

    　　相传太守讯狱，有事关重大而犯人不伏者，则取经铺地，令犯人在经上滚过。理直者了然无害，理屈者登时目瞪身僵。数百年来，官恃以断狱，而狱囚亦无敢轻滚经台者。张文和公第五子景宗，性素愎，抵任后以为妖，拆台焚经。是年两子死，次年公亡。

    　　○菜花三娘子阳湖某秀才，美丰姿，春夜独坐书房中，闻扣门声。启视之，有女自称“菜花三娘子，特来相伴。”随后有四姊妹，如媵从然。生惊其美，遂留宿焉。

    Explanation

    701

    Final Translation

    Wooden Painting of the Yongcheng Magistrate Lu Jingxuan

    Lu Jingxuan, the magistrate of Yongcheng, was a native of Xiaoshan in Zhejiang. While repairing the office, he cut down an old willow tree. When the wood was sawed, a natural painting appeared on the board. It was as if written with light ink: on both sides were peaks and cliffs, with a pine tree on the cliff and a mountain tree, both with branches and leaves hanging down, and the pine tree entwined with vines. In the middle stood an old man holding a cane, wearing a merchant's cap and long sleeves, with eyebrows and beard as if alive. His left hand was in his sleeve resting on his chest, his right foot forward showing his shoe, the left shoe hidden under his robe, looking back as if listening to the sound of water. The magistrate treasured it and took it home. This was the thirteenth day of the tenth month in the year Xinyou of Qianlong.

    Rolling Scripture Platform

    In the office of Pingyue Prefecture in Guizhou, there is a stone platform seven feet high, containing sixteen rolls of Buddhist scriptures, all written in Sanskrit, which cannot be understood. It is said that when the prefect questioned a prisoner about a serious case and the prisoner did not admit guilt, he would lay out the scriptures on the ground and make the prisoner roll over them. If the prisoner was innocent, he would be unharmed; if guilty, he would immediately become stiff and motionless. For hundreds of years, officials relied on this to judge cases, and prisoners also dared not lightly roll over the scripture platform. Zhang Wenhé's fifth son, Jingzong, was always stubborn. After taking office, he considered it a superstition and dismantled the platform and burned the scriptures. That year, two of his sons died, and the next year, he himself died.

    Three Misses of Vegetable Flower

    In Yanghu, a certain scholar with handsome features, on a spring night, was sitting alone in his study and heard knocking on the door. He opened the door and saw a woman who introduced herself as "Three Misses of Vegetable Flower" and said she had come to keep him company. Then four sisters followed, like attendants. The scholar was amazed by their beauty and invited them to stay the night.

    702

    日久身病，遣之不能去，其父具牒诉于本县之张王庙。是夜梦张王拘犯听审，责三娘子蛊惑良人，各杖十五，押逐出衙。五妇行未数步，皂肃持杖追至，向三娘子索钱，曰：“非我用情轻打，则汝等娇嫩之臀伤矣，焉能行路？”各女皆于裙带中出钱谢之。

    　　越三日，三娘子复来曰：“我与汝缘法未尽，不能舍汝。汝再告张王，王亦无奈我何。汝同学有王先生某者，其人迂腐可憎。汝不许往告，亦不许其入门。”

    　　生父母恶之，重具牒诉于张王庙，神果不灵，乃速招王生。生处馆远方，越数日方到。到时，生已死矣。王先生，亦邑中廪生，年未三十。

    　　○神和病赵云菘探花年十六时，戚人张某患神和病，有女鬼相缠，形神鹄立，奄奄欲毙。其母遍祷诸神，卒无效验，惟赵坐其榻，鬼不敢至。赵去，鬼笑曰：“汝能使赵探花常坐此乎？”母苦求赵公，赵不得已往，秉烛相伴。至第三夜，不胜其倦，略闭目，病人情已遗矣，越数日而卒。

    Explanation

    702

    After a long time, the person fell ill, and despite being sent away, they could not leave. Their father prepared a petition and appealed to the local Zhang Wang Temple. That night, he dreamt that Zhang Wang detained the accused and heard the case. Zhang Wang blamed the ghost San Niangzi for bewitching an innocent person, and each was beaten with fifteen strokes of a stick before being forcibly expelled from the temple. The five women had not taken a few steps when the temple officials, holding sticks, pursued them, demanding money from San Niangzi. They said, "If I did not use my discretion and lightly beat you, your delicate buttocks would have been injured, and how could you walk?" Each woman took money from her skirt and thanked them.

    Three days later, San Niangzi returned and said, "My karmic fate with you is not yet exhausted, so I cannot leave you. If you appeal to Zhang Wang again, the god will still be unable to do anything to me. You have a classmate named Mr. Wang, a man who is stuffy and hateful. You must not go to appeal, nor allow him to enter."

    The parents of the person detested this, and prepared another petition to appeal to the Zhang Wang Temple. The god indeed proved ineffective, so they quickly summoned Mr. Wang. Mr. Wang, who lived far away, arrived after several days. By the time he arrived, the person had already died. Mr. Wang, also a scholar from the county, was under thirty years old.

    ○ Divine Harmony Illness

    When Zhao Yunsong, the top scholar at the age of sixteen, was a relative of Zhang, Zhang suffered from a divine harmony illness, with a female ghost纠缠着他。他的身体和精神都像鹤立一样僵硬，奄奄一息。他的母亲遍祷诸神，但最终无效。只有赵坐在他的床上，鬼不敢靠近。赵离开后，鬼笑着说：“你能让赵探花常坐在这里吗？”母亲苦苦请求赵，赵不得已前往，秉烛相伴。到了第三夜，赵无法忍受疲劳，稍微闭上眼睛，病人的病情已经离开，过了几天后去世。

    703

    ○鼠食牛句容村民养一牡牛，忽有七鼠从牛后窍入，食其心肺，牛竟死。村民逐鼠，得其一，遍体白毛，重十斤。烹食之，肥过鸡豚。

    　　○代神判断萧十洲参戎，致政归养，舟泊巫峡。是夜梦有若差官状者持令箭骑马沿江问：“孰是萧大老爷船？”跃入船头，喘犹未定，怀中取出公文一角，面书“金龙四大王封”六字，随押七犯跪旁，请判“斩”字。萧骇曰：“此地方官之事，余武职，且退归林下之员，不敢越俎。”差官答曰：“公文上有公衔名，请照例办。”

    　　顷刻间，灯烛辉煌，传呼升堂。开门，阶下仪仗吏卒排立，俨然坐公堂上，非舟中也。差官先唱“绞犯六名”，毕，后唱“斩犯一名”，乃六七岁童子。萧问曰：“渠尚未成丁，何罪遽斩？”吏摇手曰：“罪名已定，毋须置议，请速判之。”

    Explanation

    703

    A Cow Devoured by Mice

    In Jurong, a villager raised a bull, which suddenly had seven mice enter through its hindquarters and eat its heart and lungs, causing the bull to die. The villagers chased the mice and caught one, which was covered in white fur and weighed ten pounds. They cooked and ate it, finding it fatter than chickens and pigs.

    A Divine Judgment

    Xiao Shizhou, a retired military officer, docked his boat at Wuxia Gorge. That night, he dreamed of a messenger with an official document and a command arrow, riding a horse along the riverbank and asking, "Which is the boat of Sir Xiao?" The messenger jumped onto the boat's bow, still breathing heavily, and took out a piece of official document from his chest, writing "Seal of the Great King of the Golden Dragon" on it. He then brought seven prisoners to kneel beside, asking for a judgment of execution. Xiao exclaimed in shock, "This is the business of local officials. I am a military officer and now a retired official, dare not overstep my bounds." The messenger replied, "The official document has your title and name, please handle according to procedure."

    In a moment, the lights and candles shone brightly, and the official was called to ascend the hall. The door opened, and officials and soldiers lined up below the steps. Xiao sat solemnly on the public hall, not in the boat. The messenger first announced "six hanged criminals," and then "one executed criminal," who was a child of six or seven years old. Xiao asked, "He is not yet an adult, what crime deserves immediate execution?" The official shook his head and said, "The crime is already determined, no need for discussion, please judge quickly."

    704

    随送标条。判讫，遂押众犯而去。公梦觉，心恶之。

    　　次晨，大雾弥江，公戒勿解缆。巳刻，向其母太夫人闲话间述前梦未竟，忽有一只上水货船触石撞沉，呼救甚惨，乃急命舟子捞救。仅救起三客，业僵死矣，如法灌救，良久方活，其舵工七名皆已淹毙。后复捞获无头童男一尸，认其衣服，即舵工之子也。

    　　余按此事与无锡华师道梦中相同：华梦阴官差役请华到衙门判“斩”字。华以未审罪名，不肯落笔。有被发妇再四哀求云：“公若不肯下判，则此案又拖累三年矣。”华终不肯，云：“我不知其所以应斩之罪，如何忍心落笔？”遂喝拒而醒。隔三年，师道卒。师道字半江，精篆隶之学，在淮上程莼江家处馆，与余交好。

    Explanation

    704

    A formal document was sent along with the case. After the judgment was made, the many criminals were escorted away. The official awoke from his dream, feeling deeply troubled by it.

    The next morning, a thick fog covered the river, and the official warned not to untie the ropes. At the fourth watch, while chatting with his mother, the Lady-in-Waiting, he was recounting his previous dream but had not finished. Suddenly, an upstream cargo ship struck a rock and sank, crying for help in a very pitiful manner. The official quickly ordered the boatmen to rescue them. Only three guests were rescued, but they were already dead. They were resuscitated according to the proper method, and after a long time, they revived. The seven helmsmen were all drowned. Later, they retrieved the body of a headless young boy. By recognizing his clothes, they identified him as the son of the helmsman.

    I note that this event is similar to a dream experienced by Mr. Hua Shidao of Wuxi: In his dream, an official from the underworld summoned Hua to the courthouse to write the character "斩" (execution). Hua, not having examined the charges, refused to write it. A woman with her hair loose repeatedly begged, saying, "If you do not write the judgment, this case will drag on for another three years." Hua still refused, saying, "I do not know the crime that warrants execution, how could I bear to write it?" He then shouted and rejected her, waking up. Three years later, Mr. Shidao died. Mr. Shidao, whose courtesy name was Banjiang, was skilled in seal and clerical script, and served as a tutor in the household of Mr. Cheng Chunjiang in Huai'an. He was a close friend of mine.

    705

    ○鬼门关朱梁江，名衣，太仓州诸生也。戊子科赴江宁乡试，寓中患热症，甚危，亲友买舟送归。行次丹徒，朱卧舱中，忽尔晕绝。

    　　见三青衣人导之登岸，其路直而窄，黑暗无光，两足甚轻飘。行约十数里，忽有一物来，紧傍身左；走十数里，又一物来，紧傍身右。走十数里，到一城，巍巍然双门谨闭，城额横书“鬼门关”三字。二青衣扣门不应，再扣之，旁边突出一鬼，貌甚狰狞，与二青衣互相争斗。遥见红灯一对，四轿中坐一官长，传呼而来。近视之，似太仓州城隍神。神问：“你是何姓名？”对：“系下场太仓州学生员。”神曰：“你来尚早，此处不可久停。”命撤所导之灯送归，见城门洞启，轿甫入而门仍闭矣。

    Explanation

    705

    At the Ghost Gate, Zhu Liangjiang, whose name was Yi, was a scholar from Taicang Prefecture. In the year of Wu Zi, he traveled to Nanjing for the provincial examination, staying at an inn where he fell ill with a severe heat illness, which was very dangerous. His friends and relatives bought a boat to send him back home. While traveling to Dantu, Zhu was lying in the cabin when he suddenly fainted.

    He saw three men in blue robes leading him to the shore. The path was straight and narrow, dark and without light, and he felt very light and floating. He walked for several miles and suddenly encountered something on his left side; he continued for several more miles and encountered something on his right side. After walking for several more miles, he arrived at a city, majestically with both gates tightly closed, and the city gate had the characters "Ghost Gate" written across it. Two men in blue robes knocked on the gate, but there was no response. They knocked again, and suddenly a ghost appeared from the side, looking very fierce, and engaged in a struggle with the two men in blue robes. Far away, he saw a pair of red lanterns, and a high-ranking official was sitting in a four-person sedan chair, being called for. Up close, he saw that the official resembled the City God of Taicang. The City God asked, "What is your name?" Zhu replied, "I am a scholar from Taicang Prefecture." The City God said, "You are here too early; this place cannot be stayed in for long." He ordered the guide lamps to be removed and sent back. Zhu saw the city gate wide open, and as the sedan chair had just entered, the gate closed again.

    706

    持灯者云：“速随我向东走。”觉非前来之路。行二三里，至大江边，白浪滚滚。持灯者将渠推入江心，大呼救命而苏。时舟已抵太仓城外，盖死去已三日矣。因心窝尚温，故从者促舟子日夜趱行，至家病愈。此事萧松浦所言。

    　　萧客珠崖时，曾过檐耳，四面叠{山律}，中通一道，壁上镌“鬼门关”三字，旁刻唐李德裕诗，贬崖州司户经此所题。诗云：“一去一万里，十来九不还。

    　　家乡在何处，生渡鬼门关。“字径五尺大，笔力遒劲。过此，则毒雾恶草，异鸟怪蛇，冷日愁云，如入鬼域真非人境矣。

    　　○冤魂索命乾隆戊寅，萧松浦与沈毅庵同客番禺幕中，分办刑名。时菱塘有刃伤事主盗案，获犯七名，赃证确凿。萧照律拟斩，解府司勘转。臬司某疑七犯皆问大辟，得毋过刻，驳审减轻。萧亦不愿办此重案，借此推辞。案归毅庵办矣。

    Explanation

    706

    The lamp-bearer said, "Hurry and follow me to the east." It felt like it wasn't the path they came from. They traveled two or three miles and reached the edge of a great river, where white waves were rolling. The lamp-bearer threw him into the middle of the river, and he cried for help and revived. By then, the boat had already arrived outside Taicang city, and he had been dead for three days. Because his chest was still warm, the followers urged the boatman to travel day and night, and he returned home and recovered. This is what Xiao Songpu said.

    When Xiao was a guest in Zhu Ya, he once passed through Yan Er, a place surrounded by overlapping mountains with a path running through the middle. On the wall was engraved the three characters "Ghost Gate," next to which was engraved a poem by Tang dynasty Li Deyu, written when he was exiled to Yanzhou and passed through here. The poem reads, "A journey of ten thousand miles, nine out of ten do not return. Where is my hometown? Born to cross the Ghost Gate." The characters were five feet tall, and the brushwork was powerful and vigorous. Beyond this, there were poisonous mists, evil grasses, strange birds, and venomous snakes, cold sun and sorrowful clouds, as if entering a ghostly realm that was truly not human territory.

    In the year Wu Yin of Qianlong, Xiao Songpu and Shen Yian were guests in the Panyu government office, divided the responsibility for criminal cases. At the time, there was a knife injury case involving the owner of a lotus pond, and seven suspects were caught, with solid evidence. Xiao proposed the death penalty according to the law and sent it to the provincial governor for review. The provincial governor wondered if sentencing all seven to death was too harsh and requested a lighter sentence. Xiao also did not want to handle this serious case and used this as an excuse to avoid it. The case was assigned to Shen Yian.

    707

    毅庵居处，与萧仅隔一板壁。夜间披阅案牍，闻毅庵斋中若嘶嘶有声甚微，起而瞰之，见毅庵俯首案上，笔不停书；其旁立有三四鬼，手捧其头。又见无数矮鬼环跪于地。萧急呼毅庵视之，忽血腥扑鼻，灯烛俱灭，身亦晕跌窗外，童仆急扶归卧。

    　　次日，毅庵及同人叩其故，萧告以所见。毅庵曰：“吾知之矣。昨宵所办，菱塘盗案也。原拟情真罪当，七犯皆无可生之法。因奉驳审，不得不从中减轻二名。内谢阿挺、沈阿痴两犯，本在外接赃，并未入内。因护赃格斗，刃伤事主，且有别案，君故皆拟斩。予欲改轻其罪，以迎合臬司。君所见跪地无数矮鬼，殆二犯之祖宗也；其环传之无头鬼，非二犯已伏法诛之伙盗，即被杀害之怨鬼来索命也。余不敢枉法以活人，使死鬼含冤于地下，请仍照原拟顶详可也。”其案遂定。

    Explanation

    707

    Yi'an's residence was separated from Xiao's by only a partition wall. One night, while Xiao was reading official documents, he heard a faint, high-pitched sound in Yi'an's study. Getting up to look, Xiao saw Yi'an bent over his desk, writing continuously. Beside him stood three or four small ghosts, holding his head. Xiao also saw countless small ghosts kneeling in a circle on the ground. Xiao called out to Yi'an, and suddenly a strong smell of blood filled the air. All the lamps and candles went out, and Xiao fainted, falling outside the window. The servant quickly helped him back to bed.

    The next day, Yi'an and his colleagues asked Xiao about what had happened. Xiao told them what he had seen. Yi'an said, "I know what it was. Last night, I was handling the Lingtang theft case. Originally, the guilt was clear, and the punishment was just; all seven criminals had no way to escape punishment. Because the case was reviewed, I had no choice but to reduce the punishment for two of them. Inside, Xie Ating and Shen Achizi, who were originally involved in receiving stolen goods and did not enter the house, fought to protect the stolen goods, injuring the victim with a blade, and they also had other cases. Therefore, you planned to sentence them to death. I want to reduce their punishment to please the provincial governor. The countless small ghosts you saw kneeling on the ground are likely their ancestors; the headless ghosts you saw are either bandits who have been executed by law or vengeful ghosts of those who were killed and are seeking revenge. I dare not pervert the law to save a life, letting the dead ghosts suffer injustice in the underworld. Please still follow the original plan and submit a detailed report." The case was then settled.

    708

    ○扫螺蛳徐公浩观察山西，有老狐化作道士，时入其署与语。某县令太仓王姓者，中飞语，观察信之，将褫其官。老狐缓颊，谓其人祖宗功德不可量也。后观察廉得其诬，事遂已。令来谒观察，问：“君祖宗作何好事？”对以五世祖耕海滨，海潮至，青螺随潮入岸；潮退，螺不能归原处，被人捉卖。祖夫妻各持帚扫青螺入海，自三更至黎明为度，如是者六十年。狐所谓功德，或指此耶！

    　　观察有小婢曰彩云，狐见之曰：“不可使为婢，此女有根基，将来是观音大士作媒，嫁与洞庭君者。”迟数日，彩云持其父所书扇倚柱看，观察见文理粗通，问知其父为诸生，祖翰林，且感老狐之言，命作第三孙女，远近皆知有三姑娘。

    Explanation

    708

    A Certain Official Observing Affairs in Shanxi

    Xu Gong Hao, an official observing affairs in Shanxi, encountered an old fox that had transformed into a Taoist priest. The fox would often visit Xu's office and converse with him. A county magistrate from Taicang, surnamed Wang, was accused by false rumors. Xu, believing the accusations, intended to strip Wang of his official position. The old fox interceded on Wang's behalf, arguing that his ancestors had immense merit. Later, Xu investigated and found the accusations false, and the matter was resolved. When Wang came to visit Xu, Xu asked, "What good deeds did your ancestors perform?" Wang replied that his great-great-great-grandfather farmed by the sea. When the tide came in, green snails would wash ashore. When the tide receded, the snails could not return to their original place and were collected and sold. Wang's grandparents each held a broom and swept the snails back into the sea, doing so from midnight until dawn, for sixty years. Perhaps the fox's reference to "merits of ancestors" was alluding to this act.

    Observant of the old fox, Xu had a servant girl named Caiyun. The fox saw her and said, "Do not let her remain a servant. This girl has a foundation; in the future, she will be matched by Guanyin Bodhisattva and married to the Lord of Dongting Lake." A few days later, Caiyun held a fan written by her father against a pillar to read it. Xu noticed that she could understand the text and, upon learning that her father was a scholar and her grandfather a member of the Hanlin Academy, felt moved by the old fox's words. He arranged for Caiyun to be his third granddaughter, and the news spread far and wide, with everyone knowing about the third daughter.

    709

    阅半载，有巨公以礼寄观察，并赠一画轴，云：“闻公三姑娘未字人，可许与申太守大年之子。奉赠大士像甚灵，悬斋头祷求，当有验也。”申，湖北人，悟洞庭君之说。大士像又与媒札同至，乃为成其婚。狐之前知如此。

    　　○周太史驱妖周用修，江西瑞昌县楼下村人，年五十余，早丧妻，有子有媳，生计颇自给。

    　　一日，有妪年五十许，入其家，登楼呼其长子妇至曰：“吾尔姑也，尔毋惧。”

    　　妇诧甚，于归时并未见有姑也。用修闻之，欲相见，不许；其子欲见，亦不许。

    　　然饮啖寝兴，无异常人，举家亦安之。无何，有谇语飞入其耳，怒亡去，用修家遂困。所存布菽，贮之柜，扃锁甚固，启视一空，邑人但时见老妪在用修门首日市布菽。如是三年，家困甚，请于官，召巫治之，皆不验。

    Explanation

    709

    After half a year, a high-ranking official sent a letter of congratulations to the observer and presented a scroll painting, saying, "I have heard that your third daughter is not yet married. She can be betrothed to the eldest son of Governor Shen of Hubei. I present to you a statue of a bodhisattva, which is very powerful. Hang it in your hall and pray for it, and it will surely be effective." Governor Shen, a native of Hubei, understood the words of the Lord of Dongting Lake. The statue of the bodhisattva arrived along with the marriage contract, and thus the marriage was arranged. The fox's foreknowledge was indeed accurate.

    ○ Zhou the Historian Exorcises an Evil Spirit

    Zhou Yongxiu, a historian from the village of Xiaolou in Ruiyang County, Jiangxi Province, was over fifty years old, had lost his wife early, had a son and a daughter-in-law, and was self-sufficient in his livelihood.

    One day, an old woman about fifty years old entered his home, climbed the stairs, and called his daughter-in-law to her, saying, "I am your aunt. Do not be afraid."

    The daughter-in-law was very surprised, as she had not seen an aunt when she married. Zhou heard about it and wanted to meet the old woman, but she refused. His son also wanted to see her, but she refused him as well.

    However, the old woman ate, drank, slept, and woke up just like a normal person, and the whole family accepted her. Soon, harsh words entered Zhou's ears, and she angrily left. Zhou's family then became impoverished. Their stored cloth and beans, kept in a cabinet and locked tightly, were found empty when opened. The townspeople only saw the old woman daily buying cloth and beans at Zhou's entrance. For three years, the family became very poor. They asked the authorities, who summoned a shaman to treat the problem, but none of them worked.

    710

    宗人厚辕以庶吉士在假，至其家，先一夕怪去，至期又去。用修异之，乞厚辕为驱除。厚辕朱书黄纸檄其土地神及社神曰：“阴与阳同一理，无阴司则已，若果有，则以一区区楼下村有二神在此，而听此妖祟人，竟莫之同乎？限三日驱之。不能，则五日。七日，若再不能，是无神也，焉用血食为？当令焚尔庙，毁尔像矣。”檄焚后，厚辕即渡江访友。

    　　阅半月，仍过楼下村，在肩舆小睡，似见漫山塞谷皆老少男妇，人上立人者，几千万辈，拥道来观。二老人须长二尺，立舆旁，默无语。厚辕惊觉，催肩舆入城。诸族人贺曰：“君焚檄后三日，怪去，竟不复来。”言未已，用修至，搏颡于地，求为草善后文，再焚于二神祠，怪遂绝。

    Explanation

    710

    Hou Yuan, a scholar-official on leave, returned to his home. The previous night, strange occurrences had happened, and on the expected date, they happened again. Yongxiu found this unusual and asked Hou Yuan to drive them away. Hou Yuan wrote a formal proclamation in red ink on yellow paper, addressed to the land and community gods, saying:

    "The principles of Yin and Yang are the same. If there is no realm of the dead, then it is fine. But if there is, then how can it allow such a small village to be plagued by these evil spirits, without taking any action? I give you three days to expel them. If you cannot, then five days. If you still cannot in seven days, then you are no gods, and what use are you of blood and food offerings? I will order your temples to be burned and your statues destroyed."

    After the proclamation was burned, Hou Yuan crossed the river to visit a friend. After half a month, he passed through the village again, taking a nap in a sedan chair. He seemed to see a vast crowd of people, both young and old, with figures standing on top of each other, numbering in the millions, gathered to watch. Two elderly men with beards two feet long stood beside the sedan chair, silent and motionless. Hou Yuan was startled awake and urged the sedan chair to enter the city. The family members congratulated him, saying, "After you burned the proclamation for three days, the strange occurrences stopped and did not return." Before they finished speaking, Yongxiu arrived, knelt down, and asked Hou Yuan to write a follow-up document to be burned at the two shrines. After this, the strange occurrences ceased.

    711

    ○良猪江南宿州睢溪口民被杀，投尸于井，官验无凶手。忽一猪来至马前，啼甚惨，从役驱之不去。官曰：“畜有所诉乎？”猪跪前蹄若叩首状，官命随之行。猪起前导，至一室，排户入，猪奔卧榻前，以嘴啮地，出刀，血迹尚新。执其人讯之，果杀人者。乡人义之，各出费养猪于佛舍，号曰“良猪”。十余年死，寺僧为龛埋焉。

    　　○雷打扒手乌程彭某，妻病子幼，卖丝度日。一日负一捆丝赴行求售，因估价不合，置之柜上。时出入卖丝者甚众，行家以其货少，他顾生理。彭转瞬，丝即失去，因牵行主鸣官。行主云：“我数万金开行，肯骗此数千文丝乎？”官以为有理，不究。

    Explanation

    711

    Translation:

    In the vicinity of Suixi in Suozhou, Jiangnan, a man was murdered, and his body was thrown into a well. The officials investigated but found no culprit. Suddenly, a pig approached the horse, crying out in a pitiful manner. The attendant tried to drive it away, but it would not leave. The official said, "Does the animal have something to report?" The pig knelt on its front legs as if bowing, and the official ordered that it be allowed to lead the way. The pig led them to a room, pushed the door open, and ran to the front of a bed, biting the ground with its mouth, revealing a knife with fresh bloodstains. The man with the knife was arrested and interrogated, and it was confirmed that he was the murderer. The villagers, moved by the pig's actions, each contributed money to raise it in a Buddhist temple, calling it "The Good Pig." After living for over ten years, the pig died, and the temple monks buried it in a niche.

    In Wucheng, a man named Peng, whose wife was ill and his child was young, sold silk to make a living. One day, he carried a bundle of silk to a shop to sell it, but the price was not agreed upon, so he placed it on a counter. At the time, many people were selling silk in the shop, and the shopkeeper, thinking his goods were few, paid no attention to them. As Peng turned away, his silk was taken, and he pulled the shopkeeper to report the matter to the authorities. The shopkeeper said, "I have tens of thousands of gold pieces in this shop; would I steal a few strands of silk?" The official, believing the shopkeeper's words, did not investigate further.

    712

    卖丝者闷闷回家。适其子嬉戏门外，见父卖丝归，以为必带果饵，迎上索取。

    　　彭正失丝怀忿，任脚踢之。儿登时死。彭悔，急自投河亦死，其妻不知也。邻人见其子卧于门，扶之，方知气已绝，连呼病妇，告以儿亡。妇痛子情急，登时坠楼死。官验后，嘱邻人为之埋葬。

    　　赵三日，雷雨大作，震死三人于卖丝者之门。少顷，一剃头者复苏，据云：“前扒手孙某在某行扒出一捆丝，对门谢姓见之，欲与分价，方免出首。丝在我店卖出，派分我得钱三百，彼二人各得二千。旋闻卖丝者投河，官验后无事矣。

    　　不料今日同遭雷击，彼等均已击死，我则打伤一腿。“验之果然。

    　　○北门货绍兴王某与徐姓者，明季在河南避张、李之乱，所过处尸横遍野。一夕遇李兵，二人自度必死，避城内乱尸中。夜半，灯烛辉煌，自城头而下，疑贼兵巡城。

    Explanation

    712

    The silk seller returned home in dejection. Just as his son was playing outside, he saw his father returning from selling silk and expected him to bring treats, so he approached and demanded them.

    Corporal Peng, angry at losing the silk, kicked him with his foot. The child died immediately. Corporal Peng regretted his action and, in a hurry, jumped into the river and also died. His wife did not know about these events. The neighbors found the child lying at the door, lifted him up, and realized that he was already dead. They then called the sick wife and informed her of the child's death. The wife, overcome with grief, fell from the building and died immediately. The official conducted an investigation and instructed the neighbors to perform the burial.

    On the third day of Zhao, a sudden and intense thunderstorm occurred, killing three people at the silk seller's door. Shortly after, a barber revived and said: "Before, the pickpocket Sun stole a bundle of silk from a certain shop. The neighbor, Mr. Xie, saw this and wanted to share the money, so he did not report it. The silk was sold in my shop, and I received three hundred coins, while the other two each received two thousand. Shortly after, I heard that the silk seller had jumped into the river, and the official's investigation found no issues.

    Unbeknownst to me, we were all struck by lightning today, and they have all died, while I have only injured my leg." The examination confirmed the truth of his account.

    ○ At the Northern Gate, Wang from Shaoxing and Xu were in Henan during the Ming Dynasty to avoid the chaos caused by Zhang and Li. Everywhere they went, they saw bodies lying everywhere. One night, they encountered the troops of Li, and both thought they would die. They hid among the corpses in the city. In the middle of the night, the city was illuminated with bright lights, and they suspected that the Li troops were patrolling the city.

    713

    渐近，乃城隍灯笼。愈惊惧，不敢作声。少顷，闻从者曰：“有生人气。”又一吏呼曰：“一个北门货，一个不在数。”神渐远去。次早，贼兵出城，二人起走，紧记夜所闻，认南路而行。傍晚，又抵一城，恰是北门。突遇贼捕兵，徐被杀，王遁归家。后子孙甚众。

    　　○泥刘海仙行走如皋北门内湖南常德太守徐文度家，买一泥塑刘海仙，长六寸许，置于堂前神龛内有年矣。一日，文度欲睡，忽闻堂前有剥啄声，命婢携灯照视。其婢惊奔入告曰：“龛内泥刘海忽然下地行走！”公初不信，视婢惊怖之状，乃出堂谛视，而泥刘海果朱朱而行。咸以为妖，欲毁弃之。公语曰：“汝等且勿惧，此像既能行走，或有灵应之征，不可毁弃。”仍令供奉龛内。迄今二十馀载，绝无他故。其子湘浦，现任两浙副使。

    Explanation

    713

    As they gradually approached, they saw the lanterns of the City God. They became even more frightened and dared not make a sound. After a short while, they heard the attendants say, "There is a living human's breath." Another official called out, "One from the northern gate, one not counted." The deity gradually moved away. The next morning, the bandit soldiers left the city. The two people got up and walked, firmly remembering what they had heard at night, and headed south along the road. In the evening, they arrived at another city, which was precisely the northern gate. They suddenly encountered bandit soldiers, and one was slowly killed, while Wang fled back home. Later, his descendants were numerous.

    ○ A clay statue of Liu Hai, the Taoist deity, was purchased by Xu Wende, the governor of Changde, Hunan, who lived in the North Gate of Rugao. The statue, about six inches long, had been placed in the shrine in front of the hall for many years. One day, as Xu Wende was about to sleep, he suddenly heard a knocking sound in front of the hall. He ordered his maid to bring a lamp to investigate. The maid ran in frightened and reported, "The clay statue of Liu Hai in the shrine suddenly got down and walked!" At first, Xu Wende did not believe it, but seeing the maid's terrified expression, he went out to the hall to carefully examine it, and indeed, the clay statue of Liu Hai was walking. Everyone thought it was a demon and wanted to destroy it. Xu Wende said, "Do not be afraid. Since this statue can walk, it may have signs of spiritual response, and should not be destroyed." He still ordered it to be placed in the shrine. Until now, for more than twenty years, there has been no other incident. His son Xiangpu is currently serving as the Deputy Governor of Zhejiang.

    714

    ○驴雪奇冤乾隆四十三年春，保定清苑县民李氏女嫁与西乡张家庄张氏子为室，相距百余里。李女归宁月余，新郎跨驴来迎，令妻骑驴而己步行于后。路经某村，离家仅二十里，缘此村居民素与新郎熟识，必多调笑，且驴亦熟识归路，张乃令妻先行。

    　　至六七里许，有三岔歧路，过西为张家庄大路，过东则任邱县界。有一少年控车自西道辘辘而来，系任丘豪富刘某，将张妻驴冲向任邱道上，相逼而行。天渐晚。张妻心慌，问少年曰：“此地离张家庄几何？”少年答曰：“娘子误矣。

    Explanation

    714

    ○ The Strange Case of the Donkey Snow in the 43rd Year of Qianlong. In the spring of the 43rd year of Qianlong, a woman from the Li family in Qingyuan County, Baoding, married a man from Zhangjiabang in the western part of the county, which is about a hundred li away. After her wedding, the woman visited her parents' home for a month. Her husband, riding a donkey, came to fetch her, and he arranged for her to ride the donkey while he walked behind. They passed through a certain village, which was only twenty li from their home. Because the villagers were familiar with the groom and would likely tease him, and because the donkey was well-acquainted with the way home, the groom instructed his wife to go ahead.

    　　After traveling about six or seven li, they reached a fork in the road. Going west led to the main road to Zhangjiabang, while going east led to the border of Renqiu County. A young man, controlling a carriage, came from the west, rattling along. He was Liu, a wealthy and powerful man from Renqiu. He overtook the donkey ridden by the groom's wife and forced her to follow him. As the day grew late, the woman became anxious and asked the young man, "How far is this from Zhangjiabang?" The young man replied, "Madam, you are mistaken."

    715

    张家庄须向西而去。此是任岳大路，相距数十里。天晚难行，当为娘子择庄借宿，天明即遣人送往，何如？“张妻无奈，勉强允从。

    　　至前庄，系刘之佃户孔某家，备房安歇。其时适孔佃之女亦新婚归宁，孔谓女曰：“今晚业主借宿，不能违命。汝当暂回夫家，侯业主去后，再来迎汝。”

    　　女从而归，其房为刘张共宿之所，刘之车夫宿于房外，张之骑驴系于檐下。

    　　次日将午，不见启户，孔佃窥于窗隙，见两尸在炕，头俱在地，檐下系驴亦失。孔佃与车夫颤栗莫制。佃乃密语车夫曰：“汝家河南，离此甚远，何不载彼衣物速行窜归？一经到官，则尔我身命难保矣！”车夫从之。是晚，即野瘗两尸，御车载物而去。

    Explanation

    715

    Zhang's village must be headed westward. This is the main road leading to Renyue, separated by several tens of miles. It is difficult to travel at night, so we should choose a village to stay for the night and send someone to escort her early in the morning. What do you think? Mrs. Zhang, helpless and reluctantly agreed.

    Upon arriving at the previous village, it was Mr. Kong's house, a tenant of Liu, who prepared a room for rest. At that time, it just happened that the daughter of the tenant was also newly married and returning to her parents' home. Kong told his daughter, "Tonight, the owner is staying for the night, and we cannot disobey. You should temporarily return to your husband's home and wait until the owner leaves, then come and fetch you."

    The daughter followed his instructions and returned. The room was where Liu and Zhang had stayed together, with Liu's driver staying outside the house and Zhang's riding donkey tied under the eaves.

    The next day, just before noon, no one opened the door. The tenant peeped through the window crack and saw two bodies on the kang (a traditional Chinese bed), with their heads on the ground. The donkey tied under the eaves was also missing. The tenant and the driver trembled uncontrollably. The tenant then whispered to the driver, "Your home is in Henan, far from here. Why not load their belongings and quickly flee back to your home? Once reported to the authorities, our lives would be in danger!" The driver agreed. That evening, they buried the two bodies in the wild and drove the carriage with the belongings and left.

    716

    刘母见子久出不归，杳无音耗，即在任邱县控追车夫；张郎追妻不见，疑有别故，复又赶至清苑控告其岳父母。县官疑有冤，饬捕密访。其时有嗜赌无赖之郭三鬻驴于市，恰与张供毛色相符。向郭盘诘，始知郭三向与孔佃之女有私，孔女归宁，郭从后窗潜入，见有二人共寝，一时气忿，杀此二人，并盗此驴。县今复唤孔佃，根诘尸首所在，亲往起尸。开土三尺，赫然一死人，乃秃头老和尚也。

    　　复又深掘，得所杀两尸。张冤既雪，刘死有踪，而和尚之尸又属疑案。正怀疑间，天忽阴雨，乃避雨古庙，寂无人迹。询诸邻保，云：“此庵向有师徒二僧，后以师出云游，徒亦他往矣。”即同邻保往视僧尸，咸云：“此即云游之僧也。”遂缉拿其徒。访至河南归德地界，已蓄发娶妻，开张豆腐店。究其师死之由，缘僧徒所娶之妇，向与其师有奸。后徒渐长，复与此妇私通。其徒每有不平，故共谋杀其师，弃庙远窜，遂成夫妇。乃置之法。

    Explanation

    716

    D Liu's mother, seeing that her son had been out for a long time without returning and had no news, immediately filed a lawsuit against the coachman in Renqiu County; Zhang, upon not finding his wife, suspected there was another reason, and again rushed to Qingyuan to accuse his in-laws. The county magistrate suspected there was a wrong and ordered a secret investigation. At that time, there was a gambling-addicted rogue named Guo San selling a donkey in the market, whose appearance matched Zhang's description. When questioned, it was discovered that Guo San had been having an affair with Kong Tian's daughter. After Kong's daughter returned home, Guo sneaked into the house through the back window and saw two people sleeping together. Filled with anger, he killed these two people and stole the donkey. The county magistrate then summoned Kong Tian again, thoroughly interrogated him about the location of the bodies, and personally went to exhume them. After digging three feet into the ground, a dead body suddenly appeared, a bald old monk.

    Further digging revealed the bodies of the two people who had been killed. Zhang's冤 was cleared, and Liu's death had a trace, but the monk's body remained a doubtful case. While in doubt, the sky suddenly turned overcast and rainy, so they took shelter in an ancient temple, which was deserted. When they asked the neighbors, they said, "This temple once had a master and his disciple, but after the master went on a cloud tour, the disciple also left." They then went with the neighbors to see the monk's body, and everyone said, "This is the wandering monk." They then arrested the disciple. Upon searching in the area of Guide, Henan, he had already grown hair and married, opening a tofu shop. Investigating the cause of the master's death, it turned out that the monk's disciple's wife had previously had an affair with the master. Later, as the disciple grew older, he resumed his relationship with the woman. Whenever the disciple felt wronged, they conspired to kill the master, abandoned the temple, and fled far away, eventually becoming husband and wife. They were then punished according to the law.

    717

    ○张大令嘉兴张大令者，辛巳进士，海陵查太守虞昌之业师，素行正直。忽一日，平明而起，索冠带甚急，道有当事贵人要来相会。遂着蟒衣补褂，迎至大门外。升中堂，作揖逊坐，口喃喃对语，旁人听者，语不可解。初若欣喜，继而悲叹，又继而辞让。取茶两杯，一自饮，一置空中，杯亦不脱落。作态良久，乃送至大门外，再揖始归。家人问：“何客？”曰：“嘉兴府城隍也。彼升任去，举我代其职，故先来见访。且告我此地一二年内，有两贵人横死，遭劫者不少。我不便泄天机也。”言毕端坐，不饮不食，三日遂亡。俄而，巡抚王、陈两公事发。

    Explanation

    717

    A Certain Magistrate from Jiaxing

    The Magistrate from Jiaxing, who passed the imperial examination in the year 辛巳, was the teacher of Zhuang Chang, the governor of Hailing. He was known for his upright conduct. One day, at dawn, he suddenly got up in great haste, preparing his official robes. A high-ranking official was expected to visit. He put on his dragon robe and official jacket, went to the main gate to welcome the guest. He entered the main hall, performed a formal bow, and invited the guest to sit down. He spoke softly and indistinctly, and those nearby could not understand what he was saying. At first, he seemed joyful, then he sighed in sorrow, and finally declined something politely. He took two cups of tea, drank one, and placed the other in mid-air without it falling. He maintained this posture for a long time, then sent the guest to the main gate and performed a final bow before returning. His family asked, "Who was the guest?" He replied, "The city god of Jiaxing Prefecture. He is being promoted to a higher position, and he has proposed me to take over his role, so he came to visit me first. He also told me that within the next one or two years, two high-ranking officials will die tragically, and many will suffer misfortune. I cannot reveal divine secrets." After speaking, he sat upright, neither eating nor drinking, and died after three days. Soon after, the governors Wang and Chen were involved in a scandal.

    718

    ○镜水湘潭有镜水，照人三生。有骆秀才往照，非人形，乃一猛虎也。有老篙工往照，现作美女，云鬟霞，池开莲花，瓣瓣皆作青色。

    　　○蔡掌官虎丘蔡掌官，以古董为业，年少貌美。饮倪康民家，倪遣小奴持灯送归。于无人之处，见掌官与人作揖，口喃喃细语。奴问：“与何人说话？”曰：“好友李三哥唤我，我便同他去，你不必跟我。”语未毕，跳入河中。奴急救起之，拉归家，告知蔡之父母。亲友咸大惊，都来问蔡。蔡如醉可痴，口无所言，但见刀即摩其喉，见绳则试其颈，若以为天下至乐之境，无如横死者。家人锁闭之，虽小衣衫裤，皆不缝带，但穴一洞通饮食而已。

    　　清明日，全家人坟，蔡从窗外逸出，两日不归。家人知其必死，四处寻觅，至白莲桥空野，忽见掌官倚桑树大呼曰：“我在此，不必再寻矣！”家人喜，奔趋视之，则已缢死树上。呼者，乃其魂也。缢带系偷染坊店地上所晒布为之。

    Explanation

    718

    ○ Mirror Water in Xiangtan, there is a Mirror Pool, which reflects a person's three lives. A scholar named Luo went to look into the pool, and instead of seeing a human form, he saw a fierce tiger. An old boatman also went to look into the pool, and his reflection appeared as a beautiful woman with cloud-like hair and a pearl headdress, with the pond opening lotuses whose petals were all blue.

    ○ Cai Zhianguan, a young and handsome antique dealer from Tiger Hill, drank at Ni Kangmin's house. Ni sent a small servant with a lamp to escort him home. In an isolated place, the servant saw Cai bowing to someone and whispering softly. The servant asked, "Who are you speaking to?" Cai replied, "My good friend Li San has called me; I will go with him, you need not follow." Before he finished speaking, he jumped into the river. The servant rushed to pull him out and brought him back home, informing Cai's parents. Friends and relatives were greatly alarmed and all came to ask about Cai. Cai seemed dazed and spoke nothing, but he would touch his throat with a knife and test his neck with a rope, as if the greatest joy in the world was to die by hanging. His family locked him up, even small clothes and pants were not sewn, but only a hole was left for eating and drinking.

    On the day of Qingming Festival, the entire family went to the graves, and Cai escaped from the window, not returning for two days. His family knew he would surely die and searched everywhere. They finally found him at the desolate area near Bai Lian Bridge, where they suddenly saw Cai leaning against a mulberry tree and shouting, "I am here, there is no need to search anymore!" The family was overjoyed and rushed to see him, only to find that he had already hanged himself on the tree. The one who called out was his soul. The rope used for hanging was made from stolen cloth from a dyeing shop.

    719

    ○沈文崧高邮沈公文崧，宰山左沾化时，有相好同官某，亲老无子，将奉差西藏，公慨然代往，闻者无不惊其高义。跋涉三年余，始回内地。途中冰雪苦寒，往往月余无人烟。有仆二人，名夏祥者，侍公最忠。每至住营帐时辄不见，少顷，必手捧粟至，炊熟奉公，不知其粟可自来也。

    　　一日晦雾，行至险坂，下临深涧万丈，二仆俱堕涧中。公马足已陷。忽见云雾中有大士像，手持青莲，向公指导。俄顷，身已过涧至平地，痛失二仆，逡巡不前。久之曛黑。闻人语声，急呼之，则夏祥至矣。问：“何来？”称：“堕涧后，有绿毛人长丈余，自涧中负出。”主仆相抱大哭。

    　　公归后，将此事语高文良公，高为动色，绘大士图，书年月以纪之。后三十余年，沈之孙名均安者，知江西赣县；高之孙名士钅黄者，官赣县司马。初不相识，既而询及世系，彼此爽然，始知大士图犹在高处，传为至宝，至此乃以归沈。

    Explanation

    719

    Shen Wensong, a magistrate from Gao Post in Zhanhua County, Shandong, had a close colleague who was sent on a mission to Tibet. This colleague had elderly parents without children and was about to embark on the journey. Shen Wensong, moved by the colleague's situation, decided to go in his place. Everyone was amazed by his noble virtue. After traveling arduously for more than three years, he finally returned to the mainland. The journey was fraught with bitter cold and heavy snow, and often there was no human habitation for more than a month. Shen Wensong had two servants, one of whom was named Xia Xiang, who was the most loyal to him. Whenever they set up camp, Xia Xiang would often disappear, but soon after, he would return with grain, cook it, and serve it to Shen Wensong. It seemed as if the grain appeared by itself.

    One day, as they were traveling through thick fog, they reached a steep and dangerous slope that overlooked a deep ravine thousands of feet deep. Both servants fell into the ravine. Shen Wensong's horse's hooves were already stuck. Suddenly, he saw an image of a Bodhisattva in the mist, holding a blue lotus and guiding him. In a moment, he crossed the ravine and reached flat ground, but he was painfully aware of having lost his two servants. He hesitated to move forward. As dusk approached, he heard human voices and called out urgently. It was Xia Xiang who came to him. Shen Wensong asked, "Where have you been?" Xia Xiang replied, "After falling into the ravine, a green-haired person over ten feet tall carried me out from the ravine." The master and servant embraced each other and cried loudly.

    After returning home, Shen Wensong told this story to Gao Wenliang, who was deeply moved. Gao Wenliang painted a picture of the Bodhisattva and recorded the date and year of the incident. More than thirty years later, Shen's grandson, named Jun'an, became the governor of Gan County in Jiangxi Province. Gao's grandson, named Shihuang, served as the Military Governor of Gan County. Initially, they did not know each other, but after inquiring about their family lineages, they suddenly realized their familial connection. They discovered that the picture of the Bodhisattva, which had been considered a treasure, was still in Gao's possession and was finally returned to Shen.

    720

    ○蓝姑娘王中丞丁忧后，居杭州羊市公馆。灶下婢忽仆地，良久苏醒，瞪目作旗人语曰：“我镶红旗某都统家蓝姑娘也，口渴腹肌，可致意大人，作速供养我。”王亲临问曰：“尔既系旗人，何故到我汉人家来？”鬼曰：“我与群姊妹清明日出门看会，不料布政使国大老爷路过，仪从甚盛，将我姊妹一冲而散，我避不及，只得避到大人家来。”中丞曰：“汝避国大人不避我，独不知国大人尚是我之属员乎？他冲汝，汝何不到他家作祟？”鬼曰：“我畏之。”中丞曰：“然汝辈作鬼者亦势利，只怕现任官，不怕去任官耶？”曰：“不然。去任者果做好官，我亦怕他。”中丞大不喜，不得已，且供饭焚纸钱与之，婢病旋愈。未一年，中丞及于难。

    Explanation

    720

    After Blue Girl, the Vice-Provincial Governor Wang observed the mourning period for his deceased superior and resided in a public hall in Hangzhou's Sheep Market. Suddenly, one of the kitchen maids collapsed to the ground. After a long time, she awoke, her eyes wide open, speaking in the language of the Manchu people: "I am Blue Girl from the household of a certain Duting of the Banner of the Embroidered Red, and I am thirsty and hungry. Please inform the respected official and quickly provide me with sustenance." The Vice-Provincial Governor personally approached and asked, "Since you are a Manchu, why have you come to my Han Chinese household?" The ghost replied, "I was out with my sisters on Qingming Festival to visit a gathering, but unexpectedly, the High Provincial Official Guo the Great Lord passed by with a grand procession, which swept us all apart. I could not avoid it and had to seek refuge in your household." The Vice-Provincial Governor said, "You avoided the High Provincial Official but not me. Do you not know that he is still one of my subordinates? If he swept you away, why didn't you cause mischief in his household?" The ghost said, "I fear him." The Vice-Provincial Governor said, "Yet, you ghosts are also opportunistic, fearing only those in office, not those who have retired?" The ghost replied, "Not so. If a retired official was a good one, I would also fear him." The Vice-Provincial Governor was greatly displeased but had no choice but to provide food and burn paper money for the ghost. The maid's illness soon recovered. Within a year, the Vice-Provincial Governor met with disaster.

    721

    ○鼠胆两头山东桂未谷广文，精篆隶之学，藏碑板文字甚多。每夜被鼠咬破，心恶之，设法擒鼠。以为鼠胆汁可以治聋，乃生剥之。果得一胆，如蚕大，两外有头，蠕蠕行动。鼠死半日，胆尚活也。卒不解其故，惧而弃之沟中，亦无他异。或云：“首鼠两端，此之谓也。”然擒他鼠验之，并胆俱无。

    Explanation

    721

    Mr. Guangwen of Gui Weigu in Shandong, skilled in seal script and clerical script, had a large collection of inscriptions on steles and plaques. Every night, the mice would bite and damage them, and he hated this deeply. He tried to catch the mice. Thinking that the bile of mice could cure deafness, he skinned them alive. Indeed, he found one gallbladder, as big as a silkworm, with two heads outside, moving slowly. The mouse died half a day later, but the gallbladder was still alive. Finally, he could not understand the reason, and out of fear, he threw it into a ditch. There were no other strange occurrences. Some say: "This is what is meant by being indecisive and wavering." But when he tried this with other mice, there was no gallbladder found.

    722

    ○西海祠神嘉兴钱汝器，太傅文端公第七子也，选陕西武功令。抵任后，不数月，以疾卒。卒之前一日，旦起告家人具汤沐，朝服北向九拜，复东向九拜。家人问故，曰：“北向所以谢主恩也。东向者，余出都时，过蒲州，宿西门外禹庙，梦禹王召我为水神，居西海祠。余固辞不获，定于明日当去。”次早，果端坐而逝，时壬九月十七日也。

    　　先是有郭生者，人，明慧善歌，为钱所眷，孙君渊如亦善之，旋以他事逸去。后孙在朝邑令庄虚庵所，接郭生书云：“九月过解州，梦钱七公子来，仪卫甚盛，告余云：”将赴任西海祠，如申旦之约，无间幽明，当访我于蒲州南郭外。言讫而寤。若梦中言果真，公子当不在人间矣。“

    Explanation

    722

    The Western Sea Shrine Deity, Qian Ruoqi from Jiaxing, was the seventh son of the posthumously titled Wendi Gong, a Grand Tutor. He was appointed as the magistrate of Wugong in Shaanxi. After assuming his post, he fell ill and died within a few months. On the day before his death, he woke up early and told his family to prepare for a ritual bath. Dressed in court attire, he bowed nine times facing north, then nine times facing east. His family asked why, and he replied, "The bows facing north were to thank the imperial favor. The bows facing east were because when I left the capital, I passed through Puzhou and stayed at the Great Yu Temple outside the west gate. In my dream, the Great Yu King summoned me to be a water god, residing at the Western Sea Shrine. I firmly declined but was unable to refuse, and I decided to leave the next day." The following morning, he indeed passed away while sitting upright, on the seventeenth day of the ninth month of the year Ren.

    Previously, there was a man named Guo from a certain place, who was intelligent, wise, and skilled in singing. He was favored by Qian, and Mr. Sun Junyuan also favored him. Soon, he left due to other matters. Later, Sun was at the residence of the magistrate of Chaoyi, Zhuang Xuan'an, and received a letter from Guo which said, "In September, I passed through Jiezhou and dreamed that Qian's seventh son came, with a grand retinue. He told me, 'I am going to take up my post at the Western Sea Shrine, as we agreed at dawn, without regard for the distinction between the living and the dead. I will visit you outside the south gate of Puzhou.' He spoke and then woke up. If the words in the dream are true, the young master must no longer be among the living."

    723

    时孙正访生消息不得，接此信，即日脂车渡河，至蒲州相访。果有西海祠，建于至元十二年，现在重修落成。方徘徊间，忽郭生自廊庑出，相与叙述前事，共相悲喜。因酾酒洁羞，为文祭云：“昔者巨卿死友，厥有素车之驰；子文酒徒，无损成神之骨。恭闻故实，不谓逢君。”阳湖洪孝廉亮吉亦吊以诗云：“少年有愿须先偿，既入神籍何能狂？”

    　　○猢狲洒曹学士洛为予言：康熙甲申春，与友人潘锡畴游黄山。至文殊院，与僧雪庄对食，忽不见席中人，仅各露一顶，僧曰：“此云过也。”

    　　次日，入云峰洞，见一老人，身长九尺，美须髯，衲衣草履坐石床。曹向之索茶，老人笑曰：“此间安得茶？”曹带炒米，献老人。老人曰：“六十余年未尝此味矣！”曹叩其姓氏，曰：“余姓周，名执，官总兵，明末隐此，百三十年。

    Explanation

    723

    At that time, Sun Zheng was unable to find any news about the student. Upon receiving this letter, he immediately prepared his carriage to cross the river and visit Pu Prefecture. Indeed, there was a Temple of the Western Sea, built in the twelfth year of the Zhiyuan era, and it has now been restored and completed. As they were lingering there, suddenly Guo emerged from the corridor, and they recounted past events together, sharing both sorrow and joy. They then poured wine, prepared the offering, and wrote an inscription for the sacrifice: "In ancient times, Ju Qing, the deceased friend, had his simple carriage driven; Ziwén, the wine companion, did not diminish the bones of the deified. I respectfully hear of the old story, not expecting to meet you." Yanghu's scholar-official Hong Liangji also mourned with a poem: "A young man must fulfill his wishes first; once he enters the divine register, how can he be wild?"

    The monkey-spirit scholar Cao Xuéshi Luò Wēi told me: In the spring of the year Jia Shen during the Kangxi era, I traveled to Mount Huang with my friend Pan Xichou. We arrived at the Wenshu Temple, where I dined with Master Xuezhuang, but suddenly the people at the table disappeared, leaving only their hats visible. The monk said, 'This is just a passing cloud.'

    The next day, we entered Yunfeng Cave and saw an old man, nine feet tall, with beautiful whiskers, wearing a patched robe and straw sandals, sitting on a stone bench. Cao asked for tea, and the old man laughed, saying, 'How could there be tea here?' Cao offered him roasted rice, and the old man said, 'I haven't tasted this flavor in over sixty years!' Cao asked about his name, and he replied, 'My surname is Zhou, my given name is Zhi. I was a general and hid here at the end of the Ming dynasty, over a hundred and thirty years ago.'

    724

    此猿洞也，为虎所据，诸猿患之，招余杀虎。殪其类，因得居此。“床置二剑，光如沃雪，台上供河洛二图、六十四卦，地堆虎皮数十张。笑谓曹曰：”明日诸猿来寿我，颇可观。“言未已，有数小猿至洞前，见有人，惊跳去。老人曰：”自虎害除，猿感我恩，每日轮班来供使令。“因呼曰：”我将请客，可拾薪煨芋。“猿跃去，少顷，捧薪至，煮芋与曹共啖。曹私忆此间得酒更佳，老人已知，引至一崖，有石覆小凹，澄碧而香，曰：”此猢狲酒也。“酌而共饮。老人醉，取双剑舞，走电飞沙，天风皆起。舞毕还洞，枕虎皮卧，语曹云：”汝饥，可随手取松子橡栗食之。“食后，体觉轻健。先是，曹常病寒，至是病减八九。

    Explanation

    724

    This is the monkey cave, which was occupied by a tiger. The monkeys were troubled by this, so they invited me to kill the tiger. I killed all of its kind, and therefore, I was able to live here. There are two swords placed on the bed, their light as bright as melted snow. On the platform, there are the two diagrams of the Yellow River and Luo River, and the sixty-four hexagrams. The ground is piled with dozens of tiger skins. Laughing, I said to Cao, "Tomorrow, the monkeys will come to celebrate my birthday, it will be quite impressive." Before I had finished speaking, several small monkeys came to the front of the cave, saw someone, and jumped away in fright. The old man said, "Since the tiger's harm was removed, the monkeys are grateful for my kindness, and they come in shifts every day to serve me." He then called out, "I am going to entertain guests, you can gather firewood and cook yams." The monkeys jumped away, soon after, they brought firewood, cooked yams, and ate with Cao. Cao privately remembered that getting wine here would be even better. The old man already knew, led him to a cliff, where there was a stone covering a small depression, clear and fragrant, saying, "This is monkey wine." We drank it together. The old man got drunk, took the two swords and danced, the lightning moved, the sand flew, and the wind rose. After finishing the dance, he returned to the cave, lay down on the tiger skin, and said to Cao, "If you are hungry, you can take pine nuts and acorns at will." After eating, the body felt light and healthy. Previously, Cao often suffered from cold, but now his illness has reduced by eight or nine parts.

    725

    最后引至一崖，有长髯白猿以松枝结屋而坐，手索书一卷，诵之琅琅，不解作何语，其下千猿拜舞。曹大喜，急走归告雪庄。拉之同往，洞中止存石床，不见老人。

    　　○张秀才杭州张秀才某，馆京师某都统家。书舍在花园中，离正宅百步。张素小胆，唤馆僮作伴，灯上即眠，已年余矣。

    Explanation

    725

    Finally, they were led to a cliff, where a bearded white monkey was sitting in a house made of pine branches. The monkey held a scroll and recited it clearly, but the language was unknown. Below, thousands of monkeys bowed and danced in reverence. Cao was greatly pleased and hurriedly went back to report to Xuezhuang. He pulled him along to go, but when they arrived at the cave, only a stone bed remained, and the old man was not seen.

    In Hangzhou, there was a scholar named Zhang, who was staying at the home of a certain general in the capital. His study was located in a garden, about a hundred paces away from the main house. Zhang was naturally timid, so he called a servant from the household to keep him company. After the lamp was lit, he would go to sleep, and this had been going on for more than a year.

    726

    八月中秋，月色大明，馆僮在外饮酒，园门未关。张立假山石上玩月，见一妇人披发赤身，远远而至。谛视之，肤体甚白，而自脸至身，皆有泥污垢瘢。张大惊，以为此必僵尸破土而出者也。双睛炯然，与月光相射，尤觉可畏。急取木弋撑房门，而已登床窃视之。

    　　未几，砉然有声，门撑推断，而此妇昂然进矣。坐张所坐椅上，将案头书帖尽撕毁这，飒飒有声。张已骇绝。更取其界尺大敲桌上，仰天长叹。张神魂飞越，从此不省人事矣。昏迷中，觉有摩其下体者，骂曰：“南蛮子，不堪！不堪！”

    　　摇步而去。

    　　次早，张僵卧不起，呼之不应，馆僮及学生急请都统来视，灌以姜汁始苏，具道昨宵情形。都统笑曰：“先生毋骇，此非鬼也。吾家人仆妇丧偶，积思成疯，已锁禁二年矣。昨偶然锁断，故逸出作闹，致惊先生。”张不信。都统亲拉至锁妇处窥观，果昨所见也。病乃霍然。

    Explanation

    726

    On the Mid-Autumn Festival of August, the moonlight was exceptionally bright. The servant was drinking outside, and the garden gate remained unlocked. Zhang stood on a rock in the artificial mountain to enjoy the moon, when he saw a woman with her hair loose and naked, approaching from a distance. Upon closer inspection, her skin was very white, but from her face to her body, she was covered in mud and scars. Zhang was greatly alarmed, thinking she must be a corpse that had risen from the earth. Her eyes were bright and sharp, shining in the moonlight, making her even more terrifying. He quickly took a wooden stick to prop the door open, and had already climbed onto the bed to secretly watch her.

    Soon, there was a sudden sound, and the door prop was pushed aside. The woman entered boldly. She sat on the chair Zhang was sitting on and tore up all the books and letters on the desk, making a rustling sound. Zhang was utterly terrified. She took a ruler and hit the table hard, sighing long into the sky. Zhang's spirit flew away, and he lost consciousness and did not respond. In a state of unconsciousness, he felt someone touching his lower body. She shouted, "Southern barbarian, you're no good! You're no good!" and walked away, swaying.

    The next morning, Zhang lay motionless and did not get up. He did not respond when called. The servant and students urgently called the commander to come and see. They gave him ginger juice to drink, and he finally regained consciousness. He described the events of the previous night in detail. The commander laughed and said, "Don't be afraid, Mr. Zhang, it's not a ghost. One of my family's servants, who lost her husband, became insane from grief and has been locked away for two years. She accidentally broke her lock last night and escaped to cause trouble, which startled you." Zhang did not believe him. The commander personally took him to see the locked woman, and it was indeed the same one he had seen the previous night. The illness disappeared suddenly.

    727

    张颇以“不堪”二字自惭馆僮闻而笑曰：“幸而相公此物不堪，家中人有中疯妇意者，都被其索闹不休，有咬伤掐痛其阴几至断者。”

    　　○周将军墓二事山西宁武有周将军遇吉之墓，百馀年来，河水啮其旁，坟渐倾泻。土人张某哀之，具牲牢致祭，默祷曰：“将军威灵，当思所以护墓之法。”次夕，天大雷雨，百里内闻有兵马腾中踔之声。次日，将军坟旁忽涌出一山，高十丈馀，阑截冲水处，至墓前，便绕道曲流矣。人咸异之。

    　　乾隆四十五年，其他山水暴至。有周某者，将军之族孙也，负母而奔，黑夜踉跄，全不认路。其母在伊背上骂曰：“汝有妻有子，妻可以生儿，可以传代，汝俱弃之，而独负我龙钟之母，不太愚乎！”其子不顾，牢负其母狂奔而已。次日天明，始知身与母俱立将军墓上，土高丈许，水不能淹。虽行一夜，并无三里之远也。归家视妻子，皆无恙，云：“水来时，似有人扶我上屋者，故得生全。”

    Explanation

    727

    Zhang Po was ashamed to use the term "unable to bear" and a servant heard him and laughed, saying, "Fortunately, the item you are referring to is indeed unable to bear, and those who have the mind of a crazy woman at home are constantly demanding and causing trouble, some even biting and pinching so severely that their private parts are almost severed."

    ○ Two Stories about the Tomb of General Zhou In Ningwu, Shanxi, there is the tomb of General Zhou Yuj, who has been there for more than a hundred years. The river has gnawed at its side, and the tomb has gradually been eroded. A local man named Zhang, moved by this, prepared offerings and performed a ritual, silently praying, "General Zhou, your divine presence, please think of a way to protect the tomb." The next evening, there was a great thunderstorm, and within a hundred miles, the sound of marching troops could be heard. The next day, a hill suddenly emerged beside the general's tomb, over ten zhang high, blocking the direct path of the water. By the time it reached the tomb, the water took a detour and flowed in a winding path. People were all amazed by this.

    In the 45th year of the Qianlong reign, other floods came. There was a Zhou by the same name, a descendant of the general, who carried his mother and fled. In the dark night, he stumbled and did not recognize the way. His mother, on his back, scolded him, saying, "You have a wife and children, your wife can give birth to children, and can pass on the family line. You have abandoned them all, and yet you only carry me, your old and frail mother, aren't you being foolish?" His son paid no heed and carried his mother fiercely, running madly. The next day, when it was light, he realized that he and his mother were standing on the general's tomb, the earth rising up over a zhang, and the water could not reach them. Although they had run all night, they had not traveled more than three li. Upon returning home, he saw that his wife and children were all safe, saying, "When the water came, it seemed as if someone helped me up onto the house, so I was able to survive."

    728

    其旁邻人，已无孑遗矣。

    　　●卷二十一○娄罗二道人娄真人者，松江之枫乡人。幼孤，从中表某养大。与其婢私，中表怒逐之。

    　　娄盗其橐金五百，逃入江西龙虎山。方过桥，有道人白发，曳杖立，笑曰：“汝来乎？汝想作天师法官乎？须知法官例有使费，非千金不可，五百金何济？”娄大骇曰：“吾实带此数，金少奈何？”道人曰：“吾已为汝豫备矣。”命侍者担囊示之，果五百金。娄跪谢称仙。道人曰：“吾非仙，吾乃天师府法官也，姓陈名章，缘尽当去，为待子故未行。有三锦囊，汝佩之，他日有急难大事，可开视之。”言毕，趺坐桥下而化。娄入府见天师，天师曰：“陈法官望汝久矣，汝来陈法官死，岂非数耶！”

    Explanation

    728

    Beside him, there were no surviving neighbors.

    Chapter Twenty-One: The Two Daoists, Lou and Luo

    Liu, the True Person, was from Maple Village in Songjiang. He was orphaned at a young age and was raised by a relative. He had an illicit relationship with his maid, which angered the relative who then expelled him.

    Liu stole five hundred pieces of gold from his relative and fled to Longhu Mountain in Jiangxi. As he was crossing a bridge, an old man with white hair, dragging a staff, stood there and smiled, saying, "Are you here? Do you want to become a judge in the Celestial Master's court? You must know that judges usually have expenses, and without a thousand gold pieces, it is not possible. What good are five hundred gold pieces?" Liu was greatly startled and said, "I really have this amount, but what if the money is insufficient?" The old man replied, "I have already prepared it for you." He ordered a servant to carry a bag and show it to Liu, and indeed, there were five hundred gold pieces. Liu knelt and thanked him, calling him a deity. The old man said, "I am not a deity; I am a judge in the Celestial Master's court. My name is Chen Zhang. My fate is to leave, but I am waiting for you, so I have not gone. I have three brocade bags for you to wear. In case of future urgent and difficult matters, you can open and look at them." After speaking, he sat cross-legged and died at the foot of the bridge. Liu entered the court and met the Celestial Master, who said, "Judge Chen has been waiting for you for a long time. When you came, Judge Chen died. Was this not fate?"

    729

    故事：天师入京朝贺，法官从行。雍正十年，天师入朝，他法官同往，娄不能与。夜梦陈法官踉跄而来，涕泣请曰：“道教将灭，非娄某不能救。须与偕入京师，万不可误！”天师愈奇娄，乃与之俱。时京师久旱，诸道士祈请无效，世宗召天师谕曰：“十日不雨，汝道教可废矣。”天师惶恐伏地，窃念陈法师梦中语，遽奏请娄某升坛。娄开锦囊，如法作咒，身未上而黑云起，须臾雨г足。世宗悦，命留京师。

    　　十一年，诛妖人贾士芳。贾在民间为祟，召娄使治。娄以五雷正法治之，拜北斗四十九日，妖灭。是年地震，娄先期奏明。皆锦囊所载三事也。今娄尚存，锦囊空而术亦尽矣。娄所服丸药，号“一二三”。当归一两，熟地二两，枸杞三两。

    Explanation

    729

    A Tale: The Celestial Master entered the capital to pay homage, accompanied by judges. In the tenth year of Yongzheng's reign, the Celestial Master went to court, with other judges accompanying him, but Lou could not join. In a dream, Judge Chen staggered to come, tearfully pleaded, saying, "Daoism will be extinguished unless Lou can save it. You must go to the capital with me, and must not delay!" The Celestial Master found Lou even more remarkable and thus went with him. At that time, the capital had been suffering from a long drought, and the prayers of all Daoists were ineffective. Emperor Shizong summoned the Celestial Master and said, "If it does not rain in ten days, your Daoism can be abolished." The Celestial Master was frightened and prostrated himself, secretly recalling what Master Chen had said in the dream, and hastily requested Lou to ascend the altar. Lou opened the brocade bag, performed the spell according to the method, and before he ascended, dark clouds arose. Soon, the rain was sufficient. Emperor Shizong was pleased and ordered him to stay in the capital.

    In the eleventh year, the sorcerer Jia Shifang was executed. Jia caused mischief among the people, and Lou was summoned to deal with it. Lou used the Five Thunder Righteous Law to deal with it, worshiped the North Star for forty-nine days, and the demon was destroyed. That year, there was an earthquake, and Lou reported in advance. All three events were recorded in the brocade bag. Now, Lou still exists, but the brocade bag is empty, and the techniques are also exhausted. The pill medicine Lou took is called "one-two-three." It consists of one ounce of angelica, two ounces of cooked rehmannia, and three ounces of goji berries.

    730

    又有罗真人者，冬夏一衲，佯狂于市。儿童随之而行，取生米麦求其吹，吹之即熟。晚间店家燃烛无火，亦求罗吹，吹之即炽。京师九门，一日九见其形。

    　　忽遁去无迹，疑死矣。

    　　京师富家多烧暖炕，炕深丈许，过三年必扫煤灰。有年妇者扫坑，炕中间鼾声，大惊召众观之，罗真人也。崛然起曰：“借汝家坑熟卧三年，竟为尔辈扫出。”

    　　众请送入庙，曰：“吾不入庙。”请供奏之，曰：“吾不受供。”“然则何归？”

    　　曰：“可送我至前门外蜜蜂窝。”即舁往蜂窝。窝洞甚狭，在土山之凹，蜂数百万，嘈嘈飞鸣。罗解上下衣赤身入，群蜂围之，穿眼入口，出入于七窍中，罗怡然不动。

    　　人馈之食，或食或不食，每食，必罄其所馈。或与斗米饭、鸡卵三百，一啖而尽，亦无饱色。语呶呶如枭，不甚可解。某贵人馈生姜四十斤，啖之，片时俱尽。居窝数年，一日脱去，不知所往。

    Explanation

    730

    There was a Daoist master named Luo, who wore the same robe in winter and summer and pretended to be mad in the marketplace. Children followed him, taking raw rice or wheat and asking him to blow on it, and it became cooked. At night, when the shop owner tried to light a candle without a flame, they also asked Luo to blow, and it ignited. In the capital city's nine gates, he appeared nine times in one day.

    Suddenly, he disappeared without a trace, and one suspected he had died.

    In the capital, wealthy families often have heated beds, which are about a zhang deep. After three years, the coal ash must be swept away. There was an elderly woman who was sweeping the bed when she heard snoring in the middle of it. She was greatly surprised and called the crowd to see it; it was Luo the real person. He suddenly stood up and said, "I borrowed your bed to sleep for three years, and finally, you have swept me out."

    The crowd asked to send him to the temple, but he said, "I do not enter the temple." They offered him food, but he said, "I do not accept offerings." "So where shall I go?" they asked.

    He replied, "You can send me to the honeycomb outside the front gate." They immediately carried him to the honeycomb. The hole in the honeycomb was very narrow, located in the depression of the earthen hill. There were millions of bees, making loud buzzing sounds. Luo removed his upper and lower clothes and entered naked. The bees surrounded him, entering his eyes and mouth, going in and out of his seven orifices. Luo remained calm and motionless.

    People offered him food, and he sometimes ate and sometimes did not. Whenever he ate, he would finish everything offered. Sometimes he was given a dou of rice and three hundred eggs, which he ate all in one go, showing no signs of being full. He spoke loudly and incessantly, like a screech owl, and it was not very understandable. A certain nobleman offered him forty catties of ginger, which he ate all in a short time. He lived in the honeycomb for several years, and one day, he disappeared, and no one knows where he went.

    731

    ○蛇含草消木化金张文敏公有族侄寓洞庭之西碛山庄，藏两鸡卵于厨舍，每夜为蛇所窃。伺之，见一白蛇吞卵而去，颈中膨亨，不能遽消，乃行至一树上，以颈摩之，须臾，鸡卵化矣。张恶其贪，戏削木片装入鸡卵壳中，仍放原处。蛇果来吞，颈胀如故。

    　　再至前树摩擦，竟不能消。蛇有窘状，遍历园中诸树，睨而不顾，忽往亭西深草中，择其叶绿色而三叉者摩擦如前，木卵消矣。

    　　张次日认明此草，取以摩停食病，略一拂试，无不立愈。其邻有患发背者，张思食物尚消，毒亦可消，乃将此草一两煮汤饮之。须臾间，背疮果愈，而身渐缩小，久之，并骨俱化作水。病家大怒，将张捆缚鸣官。张哀求，以实情自白，病家不肯休。往厨间吃饭，入内，视锅上有异光照耀。就观，则铁锅已化黄金矣，乃舍之，且谢之。究亦不知何草也。

    Explanation

    731

    The Snake-Ingested Grass that Dissolves Wood and Transforms Gold

    Zhang Wenshen, a prominent official, had a nephew who lived in the Western Qiji Mountain Villa by Dongting Lake. One day, the nephew hid two chicken eggs in the kitchen, only to find them stolen every night by a snake. He watched as a white snake swallowed the eggs and left, its neck swelling so much that it couldn't quickly dissipate. The snake then walked to a tree and rubbed its neck against it. In a moment, the chicken eggs were transformed.

    Disliking the snake's greed, Zhang playfully cut a piece of wood and put it inside the chicken egg shell, then placed it back in the same spot. The snake came to swallow it, and its neck swelled just as before. It went back to the tree to rub against it again, but still couldn't dissipate. The snake looked distressed, wandering through all the trees in the garden, looking at them but not paying attention. Suddenly, it went to the deep grass west of the pavilion, choosing a green, three-lobed leaf to rub against it as before. In a moment, the wooden egg was transformed.

    The next day, Zhang recognized the grass and took it to rub against those who had stopped eating due to illness. After a slight brushing, all were instantly cured. One of his neighbors had a case of furunculosis. Zhang thought that if food could be transformed, poison could also be transformed, so he took one or two of this grass, boiled it into a decoction, and drank it. In a moment, the back sore was indeed healed, but the body gradually shrank, and after a long time, the bones all turned to water. The patient's family became very angry, tied Zhang up, and went to the authorities. Zhang pleaded, telling the truth, but the patient's family would not let it go.

    They went to the kitchen to eat, entered, and saw a strange light shining on the pot. They looked closer and found that the iron pot had turned into gold. They let him go and thanked him. In the end, they didn't know what kind of grass it was.

    732

    ○蔡京后身崇祯时，某相公常自言为蔡京后身，以仙官堕地狱，每世间诵《仁王经》，耳目为一亮。又罚作扬州寡妇，守空房四十年。故癖好尤奇。好观美妇之臀，美男之势，以为男子美在前，女子之美在后，世从易之，非好色者也。常使妇衣袍褶，男饰裙钗，而摸其臂势，以为得味外味。又常戏以取姬妾优童数十，以被蒙其首，而露其下体，互猜为某郎某姬，以为笑乐。有内阁供事石俊者，微有姿，而私处甚佳，公甘为咂弄。有求书者，非石郎磨墨不可得也。号臂曰：“白玉绵团”，势曰：“红霞仙杵”。

    　　○天镇县碑天镇县隶云中，其地有玄帝庙。庙有古碑，其上炮铳铅铁大小丸甚多，皆陷入石内。邑人云：前明时，闯兵来，邑人拒战不胜。俄见此碑自庙飞出，盘旋军阵。凡敌所放火炮，咸着于上，我军无失衄，而敌赖以退。今谓之“天成碑”，现存于庙。

    Explanation

    732

    ○ The Reincarnation of Cai Jing

    In the reign of the Chongzhen Emperor, a certain high official often claimed to be the reincarnation of Cai Jing, having fallen from the rank of a celestial official to hell. Every time he recited the Rensang Sutra in the world, his ears and eyes were suddenly brightened. He was also punished to become a widowed woman in Yangzhou, guarding an empty room for forty years. Therefore, his peculiarities were particularly strange. He liked to look at the buttocks of beautiful women and the genitalia of handsome men, believing that the beauty of men lies in the front, and the beauty of women lies in the back. The world often changes this, but he was not a lover of beauty. He often made women wear men's robes and men wear women's skirts, and touched their arms and genitals, believing it to be a taste beyond taste. He also often played a game with dozens of concubines and young boys, covering their heads with blankets and exposing their lower bodies, guessing which was which, and found it amusing. There was a man named Shi Jun who served in the cabinet, who had a slight appearance but was very good in private. The official willingly indulged in him.
