Those who sought his writings could only obtain them if Shi Jun ground the ink. He called the arm "White Jade Cotton Ball" and the genitalia "Red Cloud Immortal Pestle."

    ○ The Stele of Tianzhen County

    Tianzhen County, under the jurisdiction of Yunzhong, has a temple dedicated to the Black Emperor. The temple has an ancient stele on which there are many large and small lead and iron balls from cannon fire, all embedded in the stone. The people of the county said: During the early Ming dynasty, the rebel army came, and the people of the town resisted but were defeated. Suddenly, this stele flew out from the temple, circling the military formation. All the enemy's fireballs landed on it, and our troops suffered no losses, and the enemy relied on this to retreat. It is now called the "Heavenly Formation Stele" and still exists in the temple.

    733

    ○抬轿郎君杭州世家子汪生，幼而聪俊，能读《汉书》。年十八九，忽远出不归，家人寻觅不得。月余，其父遇于荐桥大街，则替人抬轿而行。父大惊，牵拉还家，痛加鞭。问其故，不答，乃闭锁书舍中。未几逃出，又为人抬轿矣。如是者再三。

    　　祖父无如何，置之不问，戚友中无肯与婚。然《汉书》成诵者，终身不忘。遇街道清净处，郎诵《高祖本纪》，琅琅然一字不差。杭州士大夫亦乐召役之，胜自己开卷也。自言两肩负重则筋骨灵通，眠食俱善，否则闷闷不乐。此外亦无他好。

    　　○杨笠湖救难杨笠湖为河南令，上宪委往商水县赈灾。秋暑甚虐，午刻事毕，纳凉城隍庙。

    　　坐未定，一人飞奔而来，口称：“小民张相求救”。问：“何事？”曰：“不知。”

    Explanation

    733

    The Carrier of Sedan Chairs

    Wang, a son of a wealthy family in Hangzhou, was intelligent and talented from a young age, able to read the Book of Han. At around eighteen or nineteen, he suddenly left home and did not return, and his family could not find him. After more than a month, his father met him on Jiance Bridge Street, where he was carrying a sedan chair for someone else. The father was greatly surprised, pulled him back home, and severely whipped him. When asked why, Wang did not answer, so he was locked up in a study. Soon after, he escaped again and resumed carrying sedan chairs. This happened repeatedly.

    His grandfather could do nothing about it, leaving the matter unattended, and no relatives or friends were willing to marry him. However, he could recite the Book of Han and never forgot it. Whenever he encountered quiet streets, he would loudly recite the Biography of Emperor Gaozu, reading every word perfectly without error. Scholars and officials in Hangzhou also enjoyed summoning him to work, finding it more convenient than opening a book themselves. He said that when carrying heavy loads on both shoulders, his bones and muscles became lively and responsive, and his sleep and food were both good; otherwise, he felt depressed and unhappy. He had no other interests besides this.

    Yang Lihu Rescues a Distress

    Yang Lihu, serving as the magistrate of Henan, was entrusted by the higher authorities to travel to Shangshui County to provide relief during a disaster. The autumn heat was very oppressive, and at noon, after finishing his duties, he sought coolness in the temple of the city god.

    He had not sat down for long when a man rushed over and said with his mouth, "The commoner Zhang Xiang seeks your help." He asked, "What is the matter?" The man replied, "I do not know."

    734

    左右疑有疯疾，群起逐之。其人长号不出，曰：“我昨夜得一梦，见此处城隍神与已故县主王太爷同坐。城隍向我云：”汝有急难，可求救于汝之父母官。‘我即向王太爷叩头。王曰：“我已来此，无能着力，汝须去求邻封官杨太爷救，过明午则无害矣。’故今日黎明即起，闻大爷姓杨，又在此庙，故来求救。”言毕，叩头不肯去。扬无奈何，笑曰：“我已面准，汝有难即来可也。”问其姓名，命家人记之。

    　　数日后，散赈过其地，讯其邻人，曰：“张某是日得梦入城后，彼卧室两间无故塌倒，毁伤什物甚多，惟本人以入城故免。”

    　　○冯侍御身轻冯侍御养梧先生自言初生时，身如小猫，称之，重不满二斤，家人以为必难长成。后过十岁，形渐魁梧，登进士，入词林，转御史。生二子，一为布政使，一为翰林。先生为儿时，能踏空而行十馀步，方知李邺侯幼时能飞，母恐其去，以葱蒜压之，其事竟有。

    Explanation

    734

    The people around him suspected he had a mental illness and all got up to chase him. He shouted loudly but did not leave, saying, "Last night I had a dream where I saw the city god and the late magistrate Wang Taishan sitting together here. The city god told me, 'You have urgent difficulties; you can seek help from your superior official.' I immediately knelt before Wang Taishan. Wang said, 'I have come here, but I can do nothing to help you. You must go seek help from the neighboring official Yang Taishan. If you do, you will be safe by noon tomorrow.' Therefore, I got up at dawn today, heard that the elder brother's name is Yang, and since he is in this temple, I came to seek help." After finishing, he knelt and would not leave. Yang, having no choice, smiled and said, "I have already agreed. If you have difficulties, just come." He asked for his name and instructed his family to record it.

    A few days later, when distributing relief supplies, they passed through his area and questioned his neighbors, who said, "On that day, Zhang Mou had a dream of entering the city, and after waking up, his two bedrooms collapsed without reason, destroying many items. Only he was spared because he had entered the city."

    ○Mr. Yang, a high-ranking official, once said that when he was born, he was as light as a kitten, weighing less than two pounds, and his family thought he would surely not grow up well. After ten years, his body gradually became robust, and he passed the imperial examination, entered the imperial library, and later became a censor. He had two sons, one of whom became a provincial governor, and the other became a scholar in the imperial library. When he was a child, he could walk on air for more than ten steps, just like Li Yehou, who was said to be able to fly when he was young. His mother, fearing he would fly away, used garlic to weigh him down, and the story turned out to be true.

    735

    ○江都某令江都某令，以公事将往苏州。临行，往甘泉李公处作别，面托云：“如本县有尸伤相验事，望代为办理。”李唯唯。已而闻其三鼓后仍搬行李回署，李不解何事，探之，乃有报相尸者。商家汪姓两奴口角，一奴自缢。汪有富名，某以为奇货，命其停尸大厅，故不往验，待其臭秽，讲贯三千两，始行往验。验时又语侵主人，以为喝令，重诈银四千两，方肯结案。

    　　李公见而尤之，以为太过。某曰：“我非得已，我欲为小儿捐一知县故耳。

    　　现在汪银七千两，已差人送入京师，我并不存家中。“未几，其子果选甘肃某县，升河州知州。乾隆四十七年，为冒赈事发觉，斩立决，孙二人尽行充发，家产籍没入官。某惊悸，疽发背死。

    Explanation

    735

    A Certain Magistrate from Jiangdu

    A certain magistrate from Jiangdu was about to travel to Suzhou on official business. Before his departure, he went to see Mr. Li in Ganquan to say goodbye and said face-to-face, "If there are any cases of dead bodies in my county that need to be examined, I hope you can handle them on my behalf." Mr. Li agreed politely. Later, he heard that the magistrate still moved his luggage back to the office after the third drum strike (indicating late night). Mr. Li did not understand what was happening and inquired about it. He found out that there was a report of a dead body examination. Two servants of a merchant named Wang had quarreled, and one of them hanged himself. Wang had a reputation for being wealthy, and the magistrate considered it a valuable opportunity. He ordered the body to be kept in the main hall, so he did not go to examine it, waiting for it to stink, and asked for 3,000 taels of silver before finally going to examine it. During the examination, he insulted the owner again, considering it an order, and demanded an additional 4,000 taels of silver, only then closing the case.

    Mr. Li saw this and criticized him, thinking it was excessive. The magistrate said, "I had no choice; I wanted to donate a county magistrate position for my son."

    "Now I have 7,000 taels of silver from Wang," he said, "I have sent someone to deliver it to the capital, and I do not keep it at home." Soon after, his son was indeed selected for a county in Gansu and promoted to the position of magistrate of Hezhou. In the 47th year of the Qianlong reign (1782 AD), due to the exposure of fraud in relief distribution, he was executed immediately, and his two grandsons were exiled, with his property confiscated by the government. The magistrate was terrified and developed a boil on his back, which led to his death.

    736

    ○执虎耳云南大理县南乡民李士桂，家世业农。家畜水牛二只，至夜，一牛不归，士桂往寻。昏黑中，月色初上，见田中有兽卧焉，酣声雷鸣，以为己牛，骂曰：“畜生，如何此刻不回家！”随即骑上。将攀其角，角不见，但耸毛耳两只，遍身狸色斑然，方知是虎，急不敢下。

    　　虎被人骑，惊醒，腾身起，咆哮叫跳。士挂私念下背必为所啖，于是竭生平之力，紧握其耳，至于穿破耳轮，手愈牢固，抵死不放。虎性猛烈，腾山跃水，为棘刺所伤，次日晨刻，力尽而毙；士桂亦僵仆虎背，气息奄然。家人寻得，抱持归家，竟获重生。两脚上为虎爪所攫，肉尽骨见。医逾年，才得平复。

    　　○十八滩头湖南巡抚某，平时敬奉关帝。每元旦，先赴关庙行香求签，问本年休咎，无不应验。乾隆三十二年正月一日，诣庙行礼毕，求得签有“十八滩头说与君”之句，因有戒心。是年，虽浅水平路，必舍舟坐轿。秋间，为候七一案，天使按临。

    Explanation

    736

    Translation:

    A Farmer from the Southern Village of Dali County, Yunnan, Named Li Shiguai

    Li Shiguai, a farmer from the southern village of Dali County in Yunnan, whose family had long been engaged in agriculture, kept two water buffaloes at home. One night, one of the buffaloes did not return, so Shiguai went out to look for it. In the darkness, as the moon began to rise, he saw an animal lying in the field, snoring deeply like thunder. Thinking it was his own buffalo, he cursed, "Beast, why aren't you home now!" He immediately mounted the animal. About to grab its horns, he found no horns, but instead saw two erect ears and a body covered with spots like a leopard. Only then did he realize it was a tiger and was too frightened to dismount.

    The tiger, being ridden by a human, woke up, leaped up, roared, and jumped around. Shiguai privately thought that his back would surely be eaten, so he exerted all his strength to tightly grip the tiger's ears. Until the ear was pierced through, his grip became even tighter, and he held on until death. The tiger, with its fierce nature, leaped over mountains and rivers, was injured by thorns, and exhausted itself and died the next morning. Shiguai also collapsed on the tiger's back, barely breathing. His family found him, carried him home, and he eventually recovered. His legs were torn by the tiger's claws, flesh stripped to the bone. After more than a year of medical treatment, he finally recovered.

    In the Eighteen Rapids, Hunan, Governor of Hunan Province, who usually revered Guan Yu, every New Year's Day, first went to the Guan Temple to burn incense and draw lots, asking about the good or bad fortune of the year, and there was no failure to be answered. In the 32nd year of Emperor Qianlong's reign, on the first day of the first lunar month, after performing the rites at the temple, he drew a lot with the phrase "I will tell you about the Eighteen Rapids." He thus had a sense of caution. That year, even on shallow waters and flat roads, he abandoned boats and rode in palanquins. In autumn, for the case of "Hou Qi," the imperial envoy arrived.

    737

    从某湖过某地，行舟则近而速，起旱则远而迟。使者欲舟行，公不可，乃以关神签语诵而告之，使者勉从而心不喜。

    　　未几，贵州铅厂事发，有公受赃事。公不承认，而司阍之李奴必欲扳公，说：“此银实送主人，非奴所撞骗。”时李已受刑，两足委顿，奴主争辩不休。使者厉声谓公曰：“十八滩头之神签验矣！李字，‘十八’也；委顿于地，‘瘫’也，说此银送与主人，是送与君也。关圣帝君早知有此劫数，公何辩之有？”公悚然，遂认受赃而案定。

    　　○三姑娘钱侍御琦巡视南城，有梁守备年老，能超距腾空，所擒获大盗以百计。公奇之，问以平素擒贼立功事状。梁跪而言曰：“擒盗未足奇也，某至今心悸且叹绝者，擒妓女三姑娘耳，请为公言之：雍正三年某月日，九门提督某召我入，面谕曰：”汝知金鱼胡同有妓三姑娘势力绝大乎？“曰：”知。“”汝能擒以来乎？“曰：”能。“”需役若干？“

    Explanation

    737

    From Lake X to Place Y, traveling by boat is close and fast, while traveling by land is far and slow. The envoy wanted to travel by boat, but the official refused, and instead recited the divine sign from the Guan Di temple to inform him. The envoy reluctantly complied but was not pleased.

    Not long after, the lead factory in Guizhou was implicated, and there was an accusation of bribery against the official. The official did not admit it, but the gatekeeper's servant, Li, insisted on implicating the official, saying, "This silver was actually given to the master, not me who tricked him." At that time, Li had already been punished and his feet were paralyzed, and the servant and master continued to argue. The envoy sternly said to the official, "The divine sign from the Eighteen Rapids has verified it! Li's name is 'Eighteen,' and being paralyzed on the ground is 'paralysis.' He said this silver was given to the master, which means it was given to you. Guan Di, the Lord of Heaven, knew of this calamity early on, what can you argue?" The official was startled and finally admitted to the bribery, thus settling the case.

    Lady San, the Censorate Officer Qian, was inspecting the southern city. There was an old military commander, Liang, who could leap and fly, capturing over a hundred bandits. The official was amazed and asked about his usual feats of capturing bandits. Liang knelt and said, "Capturing bandits is not remarkable, but what still terrifies and amazes me is capturing the courtesan Lady San. Please let me tell you: in the month of a certain year in the third year of Yongzheng, the Nine Gates Commander summoned me and said, 'Do you know that in Jin Yu Hutong there is a courtesan named Lady San who has immense power?' I replied, 'Yes.' He then asked, 'Can you capture her?' I said, 'Yes.' He then asked, 'How many men do you need?'"

    738

    曰：“三十。”提督与如数，曰：“不擒来，抬棺见我。”三姑娘者，深堂广厦，不易篡取者也。梁命三十人环门外伏，己缘墙而上。时已暮，秋暑小凉，高篷荫屋。梁伏篷上伺之。

    　　漏初下，见二女鬟从屋西持朱灯引一少年入，跪东窗低语曰：“郎君至矣。”

    　　少年中堂坐良久，上茶者三，四女鬟持朱灯拥丽人出，交拜妮语，肤色目光，如明珠射人，不可逼视。少顷，两席横陈，六女鬟行酒，奇服炫妆，纷趋左右。三爵后，绕梁之音与笙箫间作。女目少年曰：“郎倦乎？”引身起，牵其裾从东窗入，满堂灯烛尽灭，惟楼西风竿上纱灯双红。

    　　梁窃意此是探虎穴时也，自篷下，足蹋寝户入。女惊起，赤体跃床下，趋前抱梁腰，低声辟耳曰：“何衙门使来？”曰：“九门提督。”女曰：“孽矣，安有提督拘人而能免者乎？虽然，裸妇女见贵人，非礼也，请着衣，谢明珠四双。”

    Explanation

    738

    He replied, "Thirty." The governor-general gave as many as requested and said, "If you don't bring him to me, I will carry my coffin to see you." The lady known as "Third Miss" lived in a deep hall and broad house, not easily to be taken by force. Liang ordered thirty men to lie in ambush around the gate and climbed up the wall himself. It was already evening, and the autumn heat was slightly cool, with a high awning shading the house. Liang lay hidden on the awning waiting for them.

    The water clock began to drip, and he saw two maidservants from the west of the house holding red lamps leading a young man in. They knelt at the east window and whispered, "The young master has arrived."

    The young man sat in the middle of the hall for a long time, tea was served three times, and four maidservants holding red lamps escorted a beautiful woman out. They exchanged greetings and soft words. Her skin and eyes shone like pearls, dazzling to look at. A short while later, two tables were set out, and six maidservants served wine, wearing strange clothes and dazzling makeup, and hurriedly attended to the young man. After three cups of wine, the music around the beam and the sound of flutes and pipes alternated. The woman looked at the young man and said, "Are you tired?" She stood up, took his robe, and led him into the east window. All the lights and candles in the hall went out, only the red silk lanterns on the wind vane on the west side of the tower remained lit.

    Liang secretly thought this was the time to probe the tiger's den, and he jumped down from the awning and entered through the bedroom door. The woman jumped up, naked, leaped off the bed, ran forward, hugged Liang's waist, and whispered, "Who is this officer from the government?" Liang replied, "The governor-general of the nine gates." The woman said, "Ah, this is a disaster! How could the governor-general capture someone and escape? However, it is not proper for a naked woman to see a noble person. Please dress, and I will give you four pairs of pearls as a gift."

    739

    梁许之，掷与一裨、一裙、一衫、一领袄。女开箱取明珠四双，掷某手中。

    　　女衣毕，乃从容问：“公带若干人来？”曰：“三十。”曰：“在何处？”

    　　曰：“环门伏。”曰：“速呼之进，夜深矣，为妾故累，若饥渴，妾心不安。”

    　　顾左右治具，诸婢烹羊炮兔，咄嗟立办。三十人席地大嚼，欢声如雷。梁私念床中客未获，将往揭帐。女摇手曰：“公胡然？彼某大臣公子也，国体有关，且非其罪，妾已教从地道出矣。提督讯时，必不怒公；如怒公，妾愿一身当之。”

    　　天黎明，女坐红帷车与梁偕行，离公署未半里，提督飞马朱书谕梁曰：“本衙门所拿三姑娘，访闻不确，作速释放，毋累良民，致干重谴。”梁惕息下车，持珠还女。女笑而不受。前婢十二人骑马来迎，拥护驰去。明日侦之，室已空矣。

    Explanation

    739

    Liang agreed, and threw to her a piece of undergarment, a skirt, a shirt, and a jacket. The woman opened a box and took out four pairs of pearls, throwing them into someone's hand.

    After the woman finished dressing, she calmly asked, "How many people did you bring?" Liang replied, "Thirty." She then asked, "Where are they?"

    Liang answered, "They are hiding around the door." She said, "Quickly call them in. It's already late at night, and because of me, you are tired. If you are hungry or thirsty, I feel uneasy."

    The attendants were instructed to prepare food, and the maids cooked lamb and roasted rabbits, which was done in a moment. Thirty people ate heartily on the ground, and the cheers were like thunder. Liang privately thought that the guest in the bed had not been caught and planned to reveal him. The woman shook her hand and said, "Why are you doing this? He is the son of a certain minister, and it concerns national dignity. Moreover, he has no fault. I have already taught him to escape through the underground passage. When the commander-in-chief interrogates, he will certainly not blame you; if he does, I am willing to take it all upon myself."

    At dawn, the woman sat in a red-curtained carriage and traveled with Liang. Not far from the government office, the commander-in-chief rode a horse and wrote a red-lettered message to Liang, stating that the investigation was not accurate and they should release the three girls immediately to avoid involving innocent people and severe punishment. Liang nervously got off the carriage and gave the pearls back to the woman. The woman smiled and refused to accept them. Twelve maids rode horses to welcome them and escorted them away. The next day, they investigated, and the room was already empty.

    740

    ○搜河都尉予亲家张开士，牧宿州，奉旨开河掘地得鼋，大如车轮，项系金牌，镌“正德二年皇帝敕封搜河都尉”十二字。鼋两眼深碧色，背壳绿毛寸许。民间聚观，告之官，官念前代老物，命放之。是夜，风雨飒至，河不掘而成者三十馀丈。

    　　○科场事五条乾隆元年正月元日，大学士张文和公梦其父桐城公讳英者独坐室中，手持一卷。文和公问：“爷看何书？”曰：“《新科状元录》。”“状元何名？”公举左手示文和公曰：“汝来此，吾告汝。”文和公至此，曰：“汝已知之矣，何必多言？”公惊醒，卒不解。后丙辰状元，乃金德瑛。移“玉”字至“英”字之左，此其验也。公得子迟，祈梦于京师之前门关帝庙。梦帝以竹竿与之，旁无枝叶，心颇不喜。有解者贺曰：“公得二子矣。”问：“何故？”曰：“孤竹君之二子，此传记也。破‘竹’字为两‘个’字，此字法也。”已而果然。

    Explanation

    740

    1. 搜河都尉予亲家张开士，牧宿州，奉旨开河掘地得鼋，大如车轮，项系金牌，镌“正德二年皇帝敕封搜河都尉”十二字。鼋两眼深碧色，背壳绿毛寸许。民间聚观，告之官，官念前代老物，命放之。是夜，风雨飒至，河不掘而成者三十馀丈。

    Inspector of Rivers, my relative Zhang Kaishi, was stationed in Suzhou. Under imperial command, he was digging a river and found a turtle as large as a cart wheel, with a gold medal hanging from its neck, engraved with twelve characters saying "Enthroned by the Emperor in the second year of Zhengde as Inspector of Rivers." The turtle had deep blue eyes and green hair on its shell about an inch long. The locals gathered to watch, and reported it to the authorities. The official thought of ancient relics and ordered it to be released. That night, strong wind and rain came, and the river was naturally excavated for more than thirty zhang.

    2. 科场事五条乾隆元年正月元日，大学士张文和公梦其父桐城公讳英者独坐室中，手持一卷。文和公问：“爷看何书？”曰：“《新科状元录》。”“状元何名？”公举左手示文和公曰：“汝来此，吾告汝。”文和公至此，曰：“汝已知之矣，何必多言？”公惊醒，卒不解。后丙辰状元，乃金德瑛。移“玉”字至“英”字之左，此其验也。公得子迟，祈梦于京师之前门关帝庙。梦帝以竹竿与之，旁无枝叶，心颇不喜。有解者贺曰：“公得二子矣。”问：“何故？”曰：“孤竹君之二子，此传记也。破‘竹’字为两‘个’字，此字法也。”已而果然。

    Five points about the imperial examination: On the first day of the first month of the first year of Qianlong, Grand Secretary Zhang Wenhe dreamed that his father, the Duke of Tongcheng, was sitting alone in the room, holding a scroll. Zhang Wenhe asked, "Father, what book are you reading?" The Duke replied, "The New List of Top Scorers." "What is the name of the top scorer?" Zhang Wenhe asked. The Duke raised his left hand and said, "You come here, I will tell you." Zhang Wenhe said, "You already know it, why speak more?" He woke up in shock but finally could not understand. Later, the top scorer in the year Bingchen was Jin Deying. Moving the character "玉" to the left of "英," this was the proof. Zhang Wenhe had a late son and prayed for a dream at the Guandi Temple in the front gate of Beijing. He dreamed that Guandi gave him a bamboo pole with no branches or leaves, which he was not very pleased about. Someone explained and congratulated him, saying, "You will have two sons." He asked, "Why?" The person replied, "The two sons of Guzhujun, this is recorded in the annals. Splitting the character '竹' into two '个' characters, this is the method of the characters." And indeed, it came true.

    741

    王士俊为少司寇，读殿试卷，梦文昌抱一短须道士与之。后胪唱时，金状元德瑛如道士貌，出其门。

    　　刘大魁丙午下场，请乩，乩仙批云：“壬子两榜。”刘不解，以为壬子非会试年，或者有恩科耶？后丙午中副榜，至壬子又中副榜。

    　　缪焕，苏州人，年十六入泮，遇乩仙，问科名，批云：“六十登科。”缪大恚，嫌其迟。后年未三十竟登科，题乃《六十而耳顺》也。

    　　有三人祈梦于于肃愍庙，两人无梦，一人梦肃愍谓曰：“汝往观庙外，照墙则知之。”其人醒，告二人。二人妒其有梦，伪溲焉者，即于夜间取笔向墙上书“不中”二字。天尚未明，写“不”字不甚连接。次早，三人同往视之，乃“一个中”三字，果得梦者中矣。

    　　○百四十村阁学周公煌，四川人，自言其祖樵也，孤身居峨嵋山，年九十九未婚。每日入山打薪，卖与山下吴姓鬻豆腐翁。吴夫妻二人，一女，每日买周薪为炊，交易甚欢。

    741

    Wang Shijun, serving as the Minister of Justice, read the imperial examination papers and dreamed of the God of Literature holding a short-bearded Taoist priest and giving it to him. Later, when the list of successful candidates was announced, Jin Zhaoyuan De Ying, who resembled the appearance of the Taoist priest, came from his school.

    Liu Dakui took the exam in the year Bingwu and asked the spirit board. The spirit wrote, "two lists in the year Renzi." Liu did not understand and thought that Renzi was not a year for the imperial examination, perhaps there would be a special imperial examination? Later, in the year Bingwu, he passed the supplementary list, and again in the year Renzi, he passed the supplementary list.

    Miao Huan, a person from Suzhou, entered the school at the age of sixteen and encountered a spirit. He asked about academic success, and the spirit wrote, "passed the exams at the age of sixty." Miao was very angry and disliked the delay. Later, before he turned thirty, he passed the exams, and the topic was "At sixty, one can hear things without being offended."

    Three people sought a dream at the temple of Yu Sumin. Two of them had no dreams, but one dreamed that Yu Sumin told him, "Go outside the temple and look at the wall, then you will know." The person woke up and told the other two. The other two were jealous that he had a dream and pretended to go to the bathroom. At night, they took a pen and wrote "not successful" on the wall. Before dawn, the "not" character was not very connected. The next morning, the three went to look at it, and it turned out to be "one is successful." Indeed, the person who had the dream was successful.

    Zhou Gong Huang, a scholar from the village of Baisi Shiqi, a person from Sichuan, said that his ancestor was a woodcutter who lived alone in Emei Mountain, remained unmarried at the age of ninety-nine. Every day, he went into the mountain to cut firewood and sold it to a man named Wu who sold tofu. Wu and his wife, with one daughter, bought Zhou's firewood for cooking every day, and they had very happy transactions.

    742

    吴年六旬，告周曰：“明日是吾生辰，叟早来饮酒。”周诺之，已而不至，吴之妻曰：“周叟颇喜饮，今不来卖薪，又不来称祝，毋乃病乎，盍往视之？”

    　　吴翌日往访，见周颜色甚和，问：“昨何不来？”叟笑曰：“我昨入山，将伐薪作寿礼，不意过一深溪，见黄白色累累，得无世所称金银者乎？余竭力运之，现堆床下。若下山，则谁为守者？”吴视之，果金银，因代为谋曰：“叟不可居此矣。叟孤身住空山而挟此物，保无盗贼虑耶？”周曰：“微君言，吾亦知之，盍为我入城寻一屋在人烟稠密处？”吴如其言，且助之迁居。

    　　未几，周又至，面赧然有惭色，手百金赠吴，揖曰：“吾有求于公。吾明年百岁矣，从未婚娶，自道将死，遑有他想？不料获此重资，一老身守之，复何所用？意欲求公作媒，代聘一妇。”吴睨其妻，相与笑吃吃不休，嫌其不知老也。

    Explanation

    742

    Wu, at the age of sixty, told Zhou, "Tomorrow is my birthday, Old Man, come early to drink." Zhou agreed, but he did not come. Wu's wife said, "Old Man Zhou enjoys drinking; if he isn't selling firewood today, nor offering blessings, could it be that he is ill? Why don't we go and see him?"

    The next day, Wu went to visit Zhou and found his complexion very pleasant. He asked, "Why didn't you come yesterday?" Zhou smiled and said, "I went into the mountains yesterday to cut firewood for my birthday gift. Unexpectedly, I passed by a deep stream and saw yellow and white objects in clusters. Could they be the gold and silver that the world recognizes? I exerted all my effort to carry them, and they are now piled under my bed. If I go down the mountain, who will guard them?" Wu looked and indeed saw gold and silver. He then advised, "Old Man, you cannot stay here. Living alone in an empty mountain with these valuables, how can you not worry about thieves?" Zhou said, "Without your words, I would have known too. Why don't you help me find a house in a densely populated area of the city?" Wu agreed and assisted Zhou in moving to a new location.

    Not long after, Zhou returned, with a blush and a look of shame, handing Wu a hundred gold coins and bowing, "I have a request for you. I will be a hundred years old next year, and I have never married. I thought I would die soon, so I had no other thoughts. Unexpectantly, I gained this substantial wealth. With just me to guard it, what use is it? I intend to ask you to act as a matchmaker and arrange for me to marry a woman." Wu looked at his wife, and both laughed incessantly, feeling that Zhou was unaware of his age.

    743

    周曰：“非但此也。我聘妻，非处子不可。若再醮二婚，非老人郑重结发之意。

    　　倘嫌我老者，请万金为聘，以三千金谢媒。“吴虽知其难，而心贪重谢，强应曰：”诺。“老人再拜去。月余，无人肯与老人婚。老人又来催促，吴支吾无计。

    　　时吴女才十九岁，忽跪请曰：“女愿婚周叟。”夫妇愕然。女曰：“父母之意，不过嫌周老，怜女少耳。女闻人各有命。儿如薄命，虽嫁年相若者，未必不作孀妇；儿如命好，或此叟尚有余年，幸获子嗣，足支门户，亦未可定。且父母无子，只生一女，女恨不能作男儿孝养报恩。如彼以万金来此，而又以三千金作谢，是生女愈于生男，而女心亦慰。女想此叟如许年纪，获此横财，恐天意未必遽从此终也。”吴夫妇以女言告叟，叟跪地连叩头呼岳父母者再。嫁，生一子，读书补廪，孙即阁学公也。

    Explanation

    743

    Mr. Zhou said, "It is not only this. I must marry a virgin. If she has been married before, it does not align with the solemn commitment of tying hair together at the wedding. If you find me old, please offer a dowry of ten thousand gold pieces and give three thousand gold pieces to the matchmaker." The Wu family knew it was difficult but were tempted by the generous reward and reluctantly agreed, saying, "Yes." The old man bowed twice and left. After a month, no one was willing to marry the old man. The old man came again to urge them, and the Wu family had no good response.

    At that time, the Wu daughter was only nineteen years old. She suddenly knelt down and said, "I wish to marry Mr. Zhou." The couple were surprised. The daughter said, "The parents' concern is simply that they find Mr. Zhou old and pity me for being young. I have heard that everyone has their own fate. If I am unlucky, even if I marry someone my age, I might still become a widow. If I am fortunate, perhaps this old man still has many more years to live, and we might have children to support the family, which is not certain. Moreover, our parents have no sons, only one daughter, and I regret that I cannot be a son to honor and repay them. If he comes here with ten thousand gold pieces and gives three thousand gold pieces as a reward, it is better to have a daughter than a son, and my heart will also be comforted. I think this old man, at his age, gaining such unexpected wealth, heaven's will might not end here." The Wu couple told Mr. Zhou what their daughter said. Mr. Zhou knelt down and bowed repeatedly, calling them "father-in-law" and "mother-in-law." They married, and he had a son who studied and passed the imperial examination, and his grandson became a scholar in the imperial court.

    744

    老人年一百四十岁，吴女先卒，年已五十九矣。老人殡葬制服，哭泣甚哀。

    　　又四年，老人方卒。所居村，人题曰“百四十村”。

    　　○人畜改常《搜神记》有“鸡不三年，犬不六载”之说，言禽兽之不可久畜也余家人孙会中，畜一黄狗，甚驯。常喂饭，狗摇尾乞怜，出入必相迎送，孙甚爱之。一日，手持肉与食，狗嚼其手，掌心皆穿，痛绝于地，乃棒狗杀之。

    　　扬州赵九善养虎，槛虎而行。路人观者先与十钱，便开槛出之，故意将头向虎口摩擦，虎涎满面，了无所伤，以为笑乐。如是者二年有馀。一日，在平山常下索钱，又将头擦虎口，虎张口一啮而颈断。众人报官，官召猎户以枪击虎杀之。

    Explanation

    744

    An Elderly Man of 140 Years

    An elderly man lived to be 140 years old. His Wu wife died first, at the age of fifty-nine. The family observed proper funeral rites, and he mourned deeply.

    Four more years later, the old man finally died. The village where he lived was inscribed as "the village of one hundred and forty."

    ○ Changes in Human and Animal Behavior

    The Shen Yi Ji (Records of the Supernatural) states, "Chickens should not be kept for more than three years, and dogs for more than six years," suggesting that domestic animals may become dangerous or ill-tempered if kept too long.

    My relative, Sun Hui, kept a yellow dog that was very tame. He often fed it, and the dog would wag its tail and beg for affection. Whenever Sun went out, the dog would greet him and see him off. Sun loved the dog very much. One day, while holding meat to feed it, the dog bit his hand, tearing the palm and causing him to fall to the ground in agony. He then beat the dog to death.

    Zhang Jiu from Yangzhou was skilled at raising tigers, walking with them in a cage. Passersby would give him ten coins, and then he would release the tiger from its cage. He would deliberately rub his head against the tiger's mouth, covered in saliva, but suffer no harm. This was a source of amusement for the crowd. This went on for over two years.

    One day, at Ping Mountain, he asked for money again, rubbed his head against the tiger's mouth, and the tiger bit his neck, severing it. The crowd reported this to the authorities, who summoned hunters to shoot and kill the tiger with guns.

    745

    人皆曰：“鸟兽不可与同群。”余曰：不然，人亦有之。乾隆丙寅，余宰江宁，有报杀死一家三人者。余往相验，凶手乃尸辛之妻弟刘某。平日郎舅姊弟甚和，并无嫌隙。其姊生子，年甫五岁。每舅氏来，代为哺抱，以为惯常。是年五月十三日，刘又来抱甥，姊便交与。刘乃掷甥水缸中，以石压杀之；姊惊走视，便持割麦刀砍姊，断其头；姊夫来救，又持刀刺其腹，出肠尺馀，尚未气绝。余问有何冤仇，伤者极言平日无冤，言终气绝。问刘，刘不言，两目斜视，向天大笑。余以此案难详，立时杖毙之，至今不解何故。

    　　又有寡妇某，守节二十馀年，内外无间言。忽年过五十，私通一奴，至于产难而亡。其改常之奇，皆虎狗类矣。

    Explanation

    745

    People often say, "Birds and beasts cannot be in the same group as humans." I say, that's not true, humans can also be like that. In the year Qianlong Bingyin (1766 AD), I served as magistrate in Jiangning, and there was a report of the killing of three people in a family. I went to examine the case, and the murderer was the wife's younger brother of the deceased, named Liu. In daily life, the husband and wife's younger brother were very harmonious, with no grievances or conflicts. Her sister had a child who was just five years old. Every time the husband came, he would take care of the child, which was considered normal. On the thirteenth day of May in that year, Liu came again to take care of the nephew, and the sister handed the child over to him. Liu threw the child into the water jar and crushed him with a stone; the sister ran to see what happened and then took a sickle and cut off her head. Her husband came to save her, but Liu stabbed him in the abdomen, causing his intestines to protrude more than a foot, and he was not yet dead. I asked if there was any grudge, and the injured person strongly stated that there was none, dying before he could finish speaking. When I asked Liu, he did not speak, looked sideways, and laughed at the sky. I found the case too complex to understand, so I immediately had him beaten to death; I still do not know why.

    There was also a widow who maintained her chastity for more than twenty years, with no complaints from inside or outside. Suddenly, when she was over fifty, she had an affair with a servant, and she died during childbirth. Such unusual changes in behavior are like those of tigers and dogs.

    746

    ○梦葫芦尹秀才廷一，未第时，每逢下场，必梦神授一葫芦，放榜不中。自后遇入闱心恶，而每次必梦葫芦，然屡梦则葫芦愈大。雍正甲辰科，入闱前夕，尹恐又梦，乃坐而待旦，欲避梦也。其小奴方睡，大呼“梦见一个葫芦，与相公称等身。”

    　　尹懊恨不祥，亦无可奈何。已而榜发，尹竟中三十二名。其三十名姓胡，其三十一名姓卢，皆甚少年，方悟初梦之小葫芦，盖二公尚未长成故也。

    　　○乩仙示题康熙戊辰，会试举子求乩仙示题，乩仙书“不知”二字。举子再拜求曰：“岂有神仙而不知之理？”乩仙乃大书曰：“不知不知又不知。”众人大笑，以仙为无知也。是科，题乃“不知命，无以为君子也”三节。又甲午乡试前，秀才求乩仙示题，仙书“不可语”三字。众秀才苦求不已，乃书曰：“正在‘不可语’上。”众愈不解，再求仙明示之，仙书一“署”字，再叩之，则不应矣。已而，题是“知之者，不如好之者”一章。

    Explanation

    746

    Translation

    Dreaming of a Gourd

    Scholar Yin Tingyi, before he passed the imperial examinations, would dream of a deity giving him a gourd every time he took the exam. When the results were announced, he did not pass. Afterward, whenever he faced the exam hall, he felt anxious and would always dream of a gourd. However, the more he dreamed, the larger the gourd became. In the year 1734 during the reign of Emperor Yongzheng, on the eve of entering the exam hall, Yin feared he would dream again and sat waiting for dawn, hoping to avoid the dream. His young servant, who was asleep, suddenly shouted, "I dreamed of a gourd as tall as Master."

    Yin was deeply regretful and felt unlucky, but there was nothing he could do. When the results were eventually announced, Yin surprisingly ranked 32nd. The 30th and 31st places were held by men surnamed Hu and Lu, respectively, both of whom were very young. It was then that Yin realized the small gourd in his initial dreams, as these two men had not yet grown to full size.

    The Oracle of the Spirit Medium

    In 1698 during the reign of Emperor Kangxi, candidates for the metropolitan examination sought guidance from a spirit medium. The medium wrote the word "不知" (not knowing). The candidates bowed and asked, "How could a deity not know?" The medium then wrote, "不知不知又不知" (not knowing, not knowing, not knowing). Everyone laughed, thinking the deity was ignorant. For that examination, the topic was "不知命，无以为君子也" (Those who do not know their destiny cannot be considered true gentlemen).

    In 1704, before the provincial examination, scholars sought guidance from the same spirit medium. The medium wrote the word "不可语" (cannot be spoken). The scholars persisted in their request, and the medium wrote, "正在‘不可语’上" (right on the 'cannot be spoken' part). The scholars were even more confused and asked the medium to clarify. The medium wrote a single character "署" (office) and did not respond to further questions. When the results were announced, the topic was "知之者，不如好之者" (Those who know it are not as good as those who love it).

    747

    ○神签预兆秦状元大士将散馆，求关圣签，得“静来也好把此心扪”之句，意郁郁不乐，以为神嗤其有亏心事也。已而，试《松柏有心赋》，限“心”字为韵，终篇忘点“心”字，阅卷者仍以高等上。上阅之，问：“‘心’韵何以不明押？”秦俯首谢罪，而阅卷者亦俱拜谢。上笑曰：“状元有无心之赋，主司无有眼之人。”

    　　○奇骗骗术之巧者，愈出愈奇。金陵有老翁持数金至北门桥钱店易钱，故意较论银色，哓哓不休。一少年从外入，礼貌甚恭，呼翁为老伯，曰：“令郎贸易常州，与侄同事，有银信一封托侄寄老伯。将往尊府，不意侄之路遇也。”将银信交毕，一揖而去。

    　　老翁拆信谓钱店主人曰：“我眼昏，不能看家信，求君诵之。”店主人如其言，皆家常琐屑语，末云：“外纹银十两，为爷薪水需。”翁喜动颜色，曰：“还我前银，不必较论银色矣。儿所寄纹银，纸上书明十两，即以此兑钱何如？”

    Explanation

    747

    Translation

    A Divine Sign Foretelling Qin, the ZHUANGYUAN, Would Graduate from the Hanlin Academy, He Sought a Sign from Guan Yu and Received the Phrase, "It Is Good to Examine One's Heart When Calm," Feeling Deeply Melancholy, He Believed the God Was Mocking Him for Having a Guilty Conscience. Soon After, He Took an Examination on the Topic of "The Pine and Cypress Having a Heart," with the Character "心" as the Rhyme, but Forgot to Include the Character in His Essay. Despite This Oversight, the Examiners Still Ranked Him Highly. The Emperor Read His Essay and Asked, "Why Was the Rhyme '心' Not Clearly Used?" Qin Bowed in Apology, and the Examiners Also Bowed in Gratitude. The Emperor Smiled and Said, "The ZHUANGYAN Has Written an Essay Without the Heart, and the Examiners Are Blind."

    A Cunning Deception That Becomes More Ingenious. In Nanjing, an Old Man Carried Several Gold Coins to a Money Shop at the North Gate Bridge to Exchange Them, Deliberately Arguing Over the Quality of the Silver Coins, Talking Nonstop. A Young Man Entered from Outside, Showing Great Respect, Addressing the Old Man as "Old Master," and Said, "Your Son Traded in Changzhou, and I Worked with Him. He Entrusted Me with a Letter Containing Silver, to Deliver to You. I Was Going to Your Residence, but Unexpectedly Met You Here." After Delivering the Letter and Silver, the Young Man Bowed and Left.

    The Old Man Unsealed the Letter and Told the Owner of the Money Shop, "My Eyes Are Failing, I Cannot Read My Family's Letter, Please Recite It for Me." The Shop Owner Did as He Asked, and the Letter Contained Only Ordinary Household Matters, Ending with, "There Are Ten Taels of Silver Attached, for Your Living Expenses." The Old Man's Face Brightened, and He Said, "Return My Previous Silver, There Is No Need to Argue Over the Quality. The Silver My Son Sent, Clearly Stated Ten Taels on the Paper, Let Us Exchange It for Money Right Now."

    748

    主人接其银称之，十一两零三钱，疑其子发信时匆匆未检，故信上只言十两；老人又不能自称，可将错就错，获此余利，遽以九千钱与之。时价纹银十两，例兑钱九千。翁负钱去。

    　　少顷，一客笑于旁曰：“店主人得毋受欺乎？此老翁者积年骗棍用假银者也。

    　　我见其来换钱，已为主人忧，因此老在店，故未敢明言。“店主惊，剪其银，果铅胎，懊恼无已。再四谢客，且询此翁居址。曰：”翁住某年，离此十里余，君追之犹能及之。但我翁邻也，使翁知我破其法，将仇我，请告君以彼之门向，而自往追之。“店主人必欲与俱，曰：”君但偕行至彼地，君告我以彼门向，君即脱去，则老人不知是君所道，何仇这有？“客犹不肯，乃酬以三金，客若为不得已而强行者。

    Explanation

    748

    The owner took the silver and weighed it, finding it to be eleven taels and three cash. Suspecting that his son had sent the letter hastily without checking, the letter only mentioned ten taels. The old man could not claim it for himself, so it might be better to accept the mistake and gain this extra profit. He immediately gave him nine thousand cash. At the time, ten taels of fine silver was equivalent to nine thousand cash. The old man left with the money.

    A short while later, a guest smiled beside him and said, "Is the store owner not being deceived? This old man has been a long-time fraudster using fake silver. I saw him come to exchange money and was already worried for the owner. Because this old man is in the store, I did not dare to speak openly." The store owner was shocked, cut the silver, and found it was lead. He was extremely disappointed. He repeatedly thanked the guest and asked for the old man's address. The guest replied, "The old man lives in a certain year, about ten miles away, and you can still catch up to him. But I am a neighbor of the old man, and if he finds out I exposed his trick, he will hate me. Please tell you the direction of his door, and I will go chase him myself." The store owner insisted on going with him. The guest said, "You just go with me to that place, tell me the direction of his door, and then leave. Then the old man will not know that you told me, and there will be no reason to hate you." The guest still refused, so the store owner offered him three gold pieces. The guest reluctantly agreed and went along.

    749

    同至汉西门外，远望见老人摊钱柜上，与数人饮酒，客指曰：“是也，汝速往擒，我行矣。”店主喜，直入酒肆，ㄏ老翁殴之曰：“汝积骗也，以十两铅胎银换我九千钱！”众人皆起问故，老翁夷然曰：“我以儿银十两换钱，并非铅胎。

    　　店主既云我用假银，我之原银可得见乎？“店主以剪破原银示众。翁笑曰：”此非我银。我止十两，故得钱九千。今此假银似不止十两者，非我原银，乃店主来骗我耳。“酒肆人为持戥称之，果十一两零三钱。众大怒，责店主，店主不能对。

    　　群起殴之。

    　　店主一念之贪，中老翁计，懊恨而归。

    　　○骗术巧报骗术有巧报者。常州华客，挟三百金，将买货淮海间。舟过丹阳，见岸上客负行囊，呼搭船甚急。华怜之，命停船相待。船户摇手，虑匪人为累。华固命之，船户不得已，迎客入，宿于后舱尾。将抵丹徒，客负行囊出曰：“余为访戚来。

    Explanation

    749

    Together they arrived at the western gate of Han, and from afar they saw an old man sitting on a money cabinet, drinking with several people. The guest pointed and said, "That's the one. You go quickly and catch him; I will go." The shopkeeper was pleased and entered the wine shop directly, beating the old man and saying, "You have accumulated frauds! You exchanged ten taels of lead-silver for my nine thousand coins!" The crowd all got up to ask why. The old man remained calm and said, "I exchanged ten taels of my silver for coins, and it was not lead-silver.

    The shopkeeper said I used fake silver. Can I see my original silver?" The shopkeeper showed the cut-up original silver to the crowd. The old man smiled and said, "This is not my silver. I only had ten taels, so I got nine thousand coins. This fake silver seems to be more than ten taels, so it is not my original silver. It is the shopkeeper who is tricking me." The people in the wine shop held the scale to weigh it, and it turned out to be eleven taels and three coins. The crowd was very angry, reprimanding the shopkeeper, who could not respond.

    They all got up to beat him.

    The shopkeeper's greed led him to fall into the old man's trap, and he returned home regretful and angry.

    ○ Cleverly Reported Trickery There is a cleverly reported trickery. A guest from Changzhou, carrying three hundred gold pieces, was going to buy goods in Huaihai. As the boat passed through Danyang, he saw a passenger on the shore carrying a travel bag and calling out urgently for a boat. The guest felt pity for him and ordered the boat to stop and wait. The boatman waved his hand, fearing that the person might be a bandit. The guest insisted, and the boatman had no choice but to welcome the passenger on board, who stayed in the back cabin. As they approached Dantu, the passenger took his travel bag and said, "I came here to visit a relative."

    750

    今已至戚处，可以行矣。“谢华上岸去。顷之，华开箱取衣，箱中三百金尽变瓦石，知为客偷换，懊恨无已。

    　　俄而天雨，且寒风又逆，舟行不上，华私念：金已被窃，无买货资，不如归里摒挡，再赴淮海。乃呼篙工拖舟返，许其直如到淮之数。舟人从之，顺风张帆而归。

    　　过奔牛镇，又见有人冒雨负行李淋漓立，招呼搭船。舵工睨之，即窃银客也，急伏舱内，而伪令水手迎之。天晚雨大，其人不料此船仍回，急不及待，持行李先付水手，身跃入舱。见华在焉，大骇，狂奔而走。发其行囊，原银三百宛然尚存，外有珍珠数十粒，价可千金。华从此大富。

    　　○香亭记梦香亭于乾隆壬辰冬赴都谒选，绕道东昌。十二月五日，宿冠城县东关客店。

    Explanation

    750

    Now that we have reached the place of kinship, we can proceed. Xie Hua went ashore. Soon after, Hua opened the box to retrieve clothing, only to find that the three hundred gold pieces inside had all turned into tiles and stones. Realizing it was stolen and replaced by a guest, Hua felt an unending sense of regret.

    Suddenly, it began to rain heavily, and the cold wind blew against us, making it difficult for the boat to move forward. Hua privately thought, "The gold has already been stolen, and I have no money to buy goods. It would be better to return home and prepare, then go to Huaihai again." He then called the boatman to drag the boat back, promising the same amount as if he had reached Huaihai. The boatman agreed, and with the wind at their back, they set sail home.

    Passing through Bunnian Town, they saw someone braving the rain, carrying luggage, standing drenched, and calling out to board the boat. The helmsman glanced at him and recognized him as the thief of silver. He quickly hid in the cabin and pretended to instruct the crew to welcome him. It was late, and the rain was heavy. The person did not expect the boat to return and was too eager to wait. He handed his luggage to the crew first and then jumped into the cabin. Seeing Hua there, he was greatly frightened and ran away wildly. Opening his luggage, the original three hundred silver pieces were still there, along with dozens of pearls worth a thousand gold pieces. From then on, Hua became greatly wealthy.

    ○ A Dream at Xiangting

    During the winter of the year Renchen in the reign of Emperor Qianlong, Xiangting traveled to the capital to participate in the imperial examinations, taking a detour through Dongchang. On the fifth day of the twelfth month, he stayed at a guesthouse in the eastern suburb of Guancheng County.

    751

    夜梦至一园亭，竹石萧疏，迥非人境。儿上横书一卷，字作蝇头小楷。阅之，载一事，云：“新野之渠有巨鱼，化为丽姝，名曰”乔如“。有李氏子惑焉，至三百六十日，而李氏子以溺死。宋氏子又惑焉，历三十六日，而宋氏子亦死。有杨氏子知其为怪也，故纳之，而特嬖之，绝其水饮，而乔如无所施术。三年，生三子，悉化为鱼。六年，杨氏子遍体生鳞甲，而乔如益冶艳。一夕暴风雨，乔如抱持杨氏子，两身合为一身，各自一首，鼓同飞，投洞庭湖。日出时，杨饮水；日入时，乔如饮水。杨氏子犹知与乔如交欢，不知为鱼在水也，而竟得不死寿。此之谓物其物，化其化。”

    　　自此以下，字模糊不可辨。钟鸣梦醒，枕上默诵，不遗一字。

    　　○敦伦李刚主讲正心诚意之学，有日记一部，将所得行事，必据实书之。每与其妻交媾，必楷书“某月某日，与老到敦伦一次。”

    Explanation

    751

    At night, I dreamed of arriving at a garden pavilion, where the bamboo and stones were sparse, far from a human realm. On a child's upper part, there was a scroll written horizontally, the writing very small and neat, like fly's head small regular script. I read it, and it described a legend: In the channel of Xinye, there was a giant fish that transformed into a beautiful woman, named "Qiao Ru." A man named Li was bewitched by her and died after 360 days. Another man named Song was also bewitched and died after 36 days. A man named Yang knew it was a strange phenomenon and took her in, treating her specially and cutting off her water supply, so she had no means to use her magic. After three years, she gave birth to three sons who all transformed into fish. After six years, Yang's body was covered with scales, and Qiao Ru became even more beautiful. One night of violent wind and rain, Qiao Ru held Yang, their two bodies merged into one, each with a head, and they flew together, diving into Dongting Lake. During the day, Yang drank water; at night, Qiao Ru drank water. Yang still knew to be intimate with Qiao Ru, unaware that he was a fish in the water, and thus gained immortality. This is called transforming into one's own nature and transforming into one's own transformation.

    From this point onward, the writing became blurred and indistinguishable. The bell rang, and I woke from my dream, reciting silently on my pillow, not missing a single word.

    ○ Rigid Master Li, who taught the principles of sincerity and earnestness, kept a diary. He recorded all his actions truthfully. Every time he had intimate relations with his wife, he would write in neat script, "On the [month] [day], I engaged in the duty of conjugal relations once."

    752

    ○一字千金一咳万金商邱宰某，申详一案，有卑职勘得“毫无疑义”四字。臬使某怒其专擅，驳饬不已，并提经承宅门，将行枷责。杨急改“似无疑义”四字，再行申详，乃批允核转。然往返盘费、司房打点已至千金。

    　　汶上令某，见巡抚某。偶患寒疾，失声一咳。某怒其不敬，必欲提参。央中间人私献万金方免。人相传为“一字千金，一咳万金”。

    　　○菩萨答拜余祖母柴太夫人常为余言，其外祖母杨氏老而无子，依其女洪夫人以终，年九十七而卒。居一楼奉佛诵经，三十年足不履地。性慈善，闻楼下笞奴婢声，便傍徨不能食。或奴婢有上楼者，必分己所食与食。九十以后拜佛，佛像志立答拜，太夫人大怖，时余祖母年尚幼，必拉之作伴，曰：“汝在此，佛不答我也。”卒前三日，索盆濯足。婢以向所用木盆进，曰：“不可，我此去踏莲花，须将浴面之铜盆来。”俄而，旃檀之气自空缭绕，端跏趺而逝。逝后，香三昼夜始散。

    Explanation

    752

    A Single Word Worth a Thousand Gold, A Single Cough Worth Ten Thousand Gold

    In Shangqiu, an official submitted a detailed report on a case, in which the subordinate had found the phrase "without any doubt." The provincial governor was furious at his unauthorized action, repeatedly rebuked him, and even planned to publicly reprimand him at the main entrance of his residence. Yang quickly changed the phrase to "seems without any doubt," resubmitted the report, and it was finally approved and forwarded for review. However, the total travel expenses and bribes paid to the office staff amounted to a thousand gold.

    A Magistrate of Wenshang once met a provincial governor. He accidentally coughed due to a cold illness. The governor was angry at his lack of respect and insisted on impeaching him. A mediator was asked to privately offer ten thousand gold to avoid the impeachment. People began to say that "a single word is worth a thousand gold, a single cough is worth ten thousand gold."

    The Buddha Answers the Bow

    My grandmother, Lady Chai, often told me that her grandmother, Lady Yang, who was old and childless, lived with her daughter, Lady Hong, until she passed away at the age of ninety-seven. She spent thirty years in a room worshipping Buddha and reciting scriptures, never stepping on the ground. She was kind-hearted and would not eat if she heard the sound of beating slaves downstairs. If a slave or servant came up the stairs, she would always share her food with them. After the age of ninety, when she bowed to Buddha, the Buddha statue seemed to answer her bow. Lady Chai was very frightened. At that time, my grandmother was still young, so she would always take her along, saying, "You stay here, and the Buddha won't answer my bow." Three days before her death, she asked for a basin to wash her feet. A maid brought the wooden basin she had used before, but she said, "No, I will step on lotus flowers in the afterlife, so bring the copper basin used for washing my face." Soon after, the fragrant aroma of sandalwood wafted through the air, and she passed away in a lotus position. After her death, the fragrance lingered for three days and nights before dissipating.

    753

    ○暹罗妻驴暹罗俗最淫。男子年十四五时，其父母为娶一牝驴，使与交接。夜睡缚驴，以其势置驴阴中养之，则壮盛异常。如此三年，始娶正妻，迎此驴养之终身，当作侧室。不娶驴者，亦无妇子肯嫁之也。

    　　○倭人以下窍服药倭人病不饮药。有老倭人能医者，熬药一桶，令病者覆身卧，以竹筒插入谷道中，将药水乘热灌入，用大气力吹之。少顷，腹中汩汩有声。拔出竹筒，一泻而病愈矣。

    753

    Final Translation

    ○ Siamese Marriage to Donkeys

    The customs of Siam are extremely licentious. When young men are around fourteen or fifteen years old, their parents arrange for them to marry a female donkey, and they are made to have intercourse with it. At night, the donkey is tied up, and the boy's penis is placed in the donkey's vagina to "cultivate" it. This practice makes the boy exceptionally strong. After three years, he is allowed to marry a human wife, and the donkey is kept as a concubine for life. Those who do not marry a donkey are also considered undesirable by women and children.

    ○ Japanese Administration of Medicine Through the Lower Orifice

    Japanese people do not take medicine orally. There was an elderly Japanese healer who could treat illnesses. He boiled a large quantity of medicine and had the patient lie face down. He inserted a bamboo tube into the patient's rectum and poured the hot medicine into it. Then, he blew air into the rectum with great strength. After a short while, the patient's stomach made gurgling sounds. When the bamboo tube was removed, the patient had a bowel movement, and the disease was cured.

    754

    ○狮子击蛇戈侍御涛云：某太翁名锦，为某邑令。适西洋贡狮子经过其邑。狮子于路有病，与解员在馆驿暂驻。狮子蹲伏大树下，少顷，昂首四顾，金光射人，伸爪击树，树中断，鲜血迸流，内有大蛇决折而毙。先是，驿中马多患病，往往致死，自此患除。厚待贡使。至京，献于阙廷，象见之不跪。狮子震怒。长吼一声，象皆俯伏。奉旨放归本国，后数日，陕抚奏至，云：“京中放狮，本日午时已过潼关。”

    　　○贾士芳贾士芳，河南人，少似痴愚。有兄某读书，命士芳耕作。时时心念，欲往游天上。一日，有道人问曰：“尔欲上天耶？”曰：“然。”道士曰：“尔可闭目从我。”遂凌虚而起，耳畔但闻风涛声。少顷，命开目，见宫室壮丽，谓士芳曰：“尔少待，我入即至。”良久出谓曰：“尔腹馁耶？”授酒一杯。贾饮半而止，道人弗强，曰：“此非尔久留处。”仍令闭目，行如前风涛声。

    Explanation

    754

    Translation

    ○Lion Strikes Snake: A Tale Told by Shiyu Tao

    A certain old man named Jin served as the magistrate of a county. Just as a tribute lion from the Western Regions passed through his county, the lion fell ill along the way and was temporarily lodged in the post station with the escort officials. The lion crouched under a large tree. After a short while, it lifted its head and looked around, its golden light shining on people. It extended its claws and struck the tree, which split in half, with blood gushing out. Inside, a large snake was cut and broken, and it died. Previously, many horses in the post station had often fallen ill and died. From then on, the problem was eliminated. The old man treated the tribute envoy generously. When the lion arrived in the capital, it was presented to the imperial court. The elephants saw it but did not kneel. The lion became furious. It roared loudly, and the elephants all knelt down. By imperial decree, the lion was released back to its own country. After several days, the governor of Shaanxi reported, saying, "The lion was released in the capital, and by noon today, it had already passed through Tongguan Pass."

    ○Jia Shifang

    Jia Shifang was from Henan. As a child, he seemed dull and foolish. He had an older brother who was studying, and Jia Shifang was ordered to work in the fields. He often thought about traveling to heaven. One day, a Taoist asked him, "Do you wish to ascend to heaven?" Jia Shifang replied, "Yes." The Taoist said, "You may close your eyes and follow me." They rose into the air, and all Jia Shifang could hear was the sound of wind and waves. After a short while, the Taoist told him to open his eyes. He saw magnificent palaces and said to Jia Shifang, "Wait a moment, I will return shortly." After a long time, the Taoist came out and asked, "Are you hungry?" He gave Jia Shifang a cup of wine. Jia Shifang drank half of it and stopped. The Taoist did not insist, saying, "This is not a place where you can stay for long." He then told Jia Shifang to close his eyes again and continued their journey, with the sound of wind and waves as before.

    755

    少顷开目，仍在原处。步至伊兄馆中，兄惊曰：“尔人耶？鬼耶？”曰：“我人耳，何以为鬼？”曰：“尔数年不归，曩在何处？”曰：“我同人至天上，往返不过半日，何云数年？”其兄以为痴，不之顾，与徒讲解《周易》。士芳坐于旁，闻之起摇手曰：“兄误矣！是卦繇词九五阳刚与六二相应，阴阳合德，得位乘时，水火相济，变为正月之卦。过此以往，刚者渐升，柔者渐降。至上九，数不可极，极则有悔，悔则潜藏，以待剥复之机矣。”其兄大惊，曰：“汝未读书，何得剖析《易》理如此精奥！”信其果遇异人。远近趋慕，叩以祸福，无不响应。田中丞奏闻，蒙召见。卒以不法伏诛。

    Explanation

    755

    A short while later, he opened his eyes and found himself still in the same place. He walked to his brother's study, and his brother was surprised and asked, "Are you a human or a ghost?" He replied, "I am a human, how can I be a ghost?" His brother then asked, "You haven't returned for several years, where were you before?" He answered, "I went to heaven with others, and the round trip took no more than half a day, so how can it be several years?" His brother thought he was foolish and ignored him, continuing to teach his students about the Yijing (Book of Changes). Sitting nearby, Shi Fang listened and then interrupted, shaking his head and saying, "Brother, you are mistaken! The hexagram's line meanings indicate that the fifth line (九五) is yang and strong, corresponding to the second line (六二). Yin and yang unite in virtue, achieving a proper position at the right time, and water and fire complement each other, transforming into the hexagram for the first month. Beyond this, the strong gradually rise, and the weak gradually descend. At the topmost line (上九), numbers cannot be taken to the extreme; when taken to the extreme, there is regret, and regret leads to concealment, awaiting the cycles of decline and renewal." His brother was greatly surprised and said, "You haven't studied, how can you analyze the Yijing principles so profoundly?" Believing that he had indeed encountered a strange person, people from near and far came to seek him, asking about fortune and misfortune, and none went unanswered. The county magistrate reported this to the court, and he was summoned for an audience. Ultimately, he was punished for his illegal actions.

    756

    或云：贾所遇道人，姓王名紫珍，尤有神通，尝烹茶，招贾观之，指曰：“初烹时，茶叶乱浮，清浊不分，此混沌象也。少顷，水在上，叶在下，便是开辟象矣。十二万年，不过如此一霎耳。”

    　　嵇文敏公总督河道时，贾常在署中，人多崇奉之。有不相敬者，贾必拉至无人之处，将其生平隐事妻子所不知者一一语之，其人愧服乃已。又常问人：“可畏鬼否？”曰畏鬼便已，如云不畏，则是夜必有奇形恶状者入房作闹。

    Explanation

    756

    It is said that the Daoist hermit Jia encountered a man named Wang Zizhen, who possessed extraordinary powers. Once, while brewing tea, he invited Jia to watch and pointed out, "At first, the tea leaves float chaotically, making it impossible to distinguish between clear and turbid water. This represents the state of chaos. Soon, the water rises above the leaves, and they settle below, symbolizing the act of creation. Twelve million years, in this context, is but a fleeting moment."

    When Ji Wenmin, the governor of the river region, was in office, Jia often stayed in his office, and many people revered him. Those who did not show respect, Jia would take to a secluded place and reveal their hidden secrets, even those unknown to their wives. The person would feel ashamed and submit. He also frequently asked others, "Are you afraid of ghosts?" If they said yes, that was fine. But if they claimed not to be afraid, that night there would surely be a strange and terrifying figure entering their room to cause trouble.

    757

    ○石男“石妇”二字，见《太玄经》，其来久矣。至于半男半女之身，佛书亦屡言之，近复有所谓“石男”者。扬州严二官，貌甚美，而无人与狎。其谷道细如绿豆，下秽如线香。昼食粥一盂，酒数杯，蔬菜须而已，多则腹中暴胀，大便时痛苦异常。

    　　○须长一丈黄龙眉，震泽县人，官热河四旗厅巡检，须长一丈有奇，绕腰两匝，馀垂至地。

    　　○禁魇婆粤东崖州居民，半属黎人，有生黎、熟黎之分。生黎居五指山中，不服王化；熟黎尊官长，来见则膝行而入。

    　　黎女有禁魇婆，能禁咒人致死。其术取所咒之人或须发，或吐馀槟榔，纳竹筒中，夜间赤身仰卧山顶，对星月施符诵咒。至七日，某人必死，遍体无伤，而其软如绵。但能魇黎人，不能害汉人。受其害者擒之鸣官，必先用长竹筒穿索扣其颈项下，曳之而行，否则近其身必为所禁魇矣。据婆云：不禁魇人，则过期已身必死。

    Explanation

    757

    Translation

    Stone Man and Stone Woman

    The terms "Stone Man" and "Stone Woman" are found in the Taixuanjing, and their origins are long-standing. As for individuals with bodies that are half male and half female, these are also frequently mentioned in Buddhist texts. Recently, there has been talk of "Stone Men." In Yangzhou, there was a man named Yan Er, who was very handsome but no one was intimate with him. His anus was as thin as a green bean, and his excrement was as fine as incense. During the day, he ate one bowl of porridge, a few cups of wine, and only vegetables. If he ate more, his abdomen would swell suddenly, and defecation would be extremely painful.

    Long Beard

    A man from Zhenze County, serving as an inspector in the four banners hall in Rehe, had a beard as long as a yellow dragon's eyebrow. His beard was over a meter long, wrapped around his waist twice, with the rest hanging down to the ground.

    Witch Who Casts Spells to Cause Death

    The residents of Yazhou in Guangdong Province are half Li people. There is a distinction between wild and civilized Li people. The wild Li live in Wuzhi Mountain and do not submit to the king's rule; the civilized Li respect their leaders and crawl on their knees when they come to see them.

    Among the Li women, there are witches who can cast spells to cause death. Their method involves taking the hair or beard of the person they are cursing, or the leftover betel nut, and placing it in a bamboo tube. At night, they lie naked on their back on the mountain top, facing the stars and moon, and perform rituals and recite spells. After seven days, the cursed person will die, with no visible wounds, but their body will be soft like cotton. These witches can only harm the Li people, not the Han people. Those who are harmed by her capture her and report to the authorities. They must first use a long bamboo tube with a rope tied around her neck and drag her away. Otherwise, approaching her will surely result in being cursed. According to the witch, if she does not curse someone, she will die herself after the deadline.

    758

    婆中有年少者，及笄便能作法，盖祖传也。其咒语甚秘，虽杖杀之，不肯告人。有禁魇婆，无禁魇公，其术传女不传男。

    　　○割竹签黎民买卖田土，无文契票约，但用笔签一片。售价若干，用刀划数目于签上，对劈为二，买者卖者各执其半以为信。日久转卖，则取原主之半签合而验之。其税签如税契，请官用印于纸，封其竹签之尾，春秋纳粮，较内地加丰焉。

    　　○黎人进舍黎民婚嫁，不用舆马，吉日，新郎以红布一匹往岳家裹新妇，负背上而归。

    　　其俗，未成亲之先，婿私至翁家与其妻苟合，谓之“进舍”。若能生子而后负妇者，则群以为荣。邻里交贺，各以白纸封番钱几元，至其门首，抛竹筐中，其主人以大瓮贮酒陈于门前，瓮内插细竹筒数条。贺客至，各伏筒瓮而饮。饮毕，又无迎送拜跪之礼。余在肇庆府署中，州刺史陈桂轩为余言。

    　　○海异海中水上咸下淡，鱼生咸水者，入淡水中即死；生淡水中者，入咸水中即死。

    Explanation

    758

    Female Shamans and Traditional Practices

    Among the female shamans, there are those who, upon reaching adulthood, can perform rituals, a skill passed down through generations. Their incantations are highly secret, and even if they were beaten to death, they would not reveal them. There are shamanesses who practice exorcism, but no male shamans perform this practice; it is passed down to daughters but not sons.

    ○ Bamboo Slips for Land Transactions

    Common people who buy and sell land do not use written contracts or agreements; instead, they use a piece of paper or a slip. The price is marked on the slip with a knife, and the slip is split in half. The buyer and seller each hold one half as proof. After a long time, if the property is resold, the original owner's half of the slip is retrieved and verified by joining the two halves. The tax slip, similar to a tax contract, requires the official to stamp the paper and seal the end of the bamboo slip. Taxes are paid in the spring and autumn, and they are higher compared to inland areas.

    ○ Traditional Wedding Practices of the Li People

    The Li people do not use carriages and horses for weddings. On a favorable day, the groom goes to the bride's family with a piece of red cloth, wraps the bride, and carries her on his back back home.

    Their custom is that before the wedding, the groom secretly visits the bride's family and has intimate relations with her, which is called "jin she" (entering the house). If the couple has a child before the wedding, the community considers this honorable. Neighbors congratulate the couple, each giving a wrapped amount of money, which is thrown into a bamboo basket at the couple's door. The couple places a large jar of wine in front of their door, with several thin bamboo tubes inserted into the jar. Guests drink from the tubes in the jar. After drinking, there is no formal welcoming, sending off, or bowing ceremony. I was in the governor's office in Zhaoqing, and the prefect of Xizhou, Chen Guixuan, told me about this.

    ○ Sea Anomalies

    In the sea, the water is salty at the surface and fresh at the bottom. Fish that live in salty water die if they enter fresh water, and fish that live in fresh water die if they enter salty water.

    759

    若水煮饭，水干而米不熟，必用淡水煮才熟。水清者，下望可见二十馀丈，青红黑黄，其色不一。人小便，则水光变作火光，乱星喷起。鱼常高飞如鸟雀，有变虎者，有变鹿者。

    　　○喝呼草快子竹惠州山中有草，喝之则卷，号“喝呼草”。罗浮山有“快子竹”，竹形小而质劲，截之可以为箸。不许人作声，若作声呼之，便遁入土中，觅不可得。

    　　○蚺蛇藤琼、雷两州，蚺蛇大如车轮，所过处，腥毒异常，遇者辄死。性淫而畏藤，土人多以妇人裤并藤条置腰间，闻腥气知蛇至，先以妇裤掷去，蛇举头入裤吮嗅不已；然后以藤抛击，蛇便缩伏，凭人捆缚。缚归，钉之树上，用刀剖腹，蛇似不知；将至胆处，乃作爱护之状。胆畏人取，逃上逃下，未易捉取，直至蛇死腹裂，胆落地上，犹跃起丈馀，渐渐力尽势低。取挂檐间，其胆衣内汁犹终日奔腾上下，无一隙停留。俟晾干后，才可入药。

    Explanation

    759

    In the waters of Ruo, when cooking rice, the water dries up before the rice is cooked, and only fresh water can fully cook the rice. The clear water allows one to see twenty or more zhang deep, with varying colors of blue, red, black, and yellow. When a person urinates, the water's light changes to firelight, and stars seem to erupt. Fish often fly high like birds, and some transform into tigers or deer.

    ○ The "Hēhū Grass" and "Kuàizǐ Bamboo" In the mountains of Huizhou, there is a grass called "Hēhū Grass," which folds up when called upon. On Luofu Mountain, there is a type of bamboo known as "Kuàizǐ Bamboo," which is small but strong, suitable for making chopsticks. This plant does not allow people to make noise; if someone does, it will disappear into the ground and cannot be found.

    ○ The "Ránshé" Vine In the states of Qióng and Léi, there is a large snake called "Ránshé" that is as big as a cart wheel. Wherever it goes, it leaves a strong, toxic odor that causes anyone who encounters it to die. The snake has a promiscuous nature but is afraid of vines. Locals often wear women's pants and vine strips around their waists. They use the strong odor to detect the snake and throw a woman's pants to distract it. The snake then raises its head into the pants and sniffs and licks it repeatedly. Then, they use the vine to strike the snake, causing it to retreat and become easy to bind. Once captured, the snake is bound and nailed to a tree, then cut open with a knife, but the snake seems unaware. As it nears its bile, it reacts protectively. The snake's bile is difficult to obtain as it moves up and down, until the snake is dead and its belly is split open, and the bile falls to the ground, still jumping a few feet before gradually losing its strength. The bile is then hung under the eaves, and its juice continues to move throughout the day without stopping. After drying, it can be used as medicine.

    760

    ○网虎江西鄱阳湖渔人收网，疑其太重，解而视之，斑然虎也，惜已死矣。

    　　○福建解元裘文达公殿典试福建，心奇解元之文，榜发后，亟欲一见。昼坐公廨，闻门外喧嚷声，问之，则解元公与公家人为门包角口。公心薄之，而疑其贫，禁止家人索诈，立刻传见。其人面目语言，皆粗鄙无可取。心闷闷，因告方伯某，悔取士之失。

    　　方伯云：“公不言，某不敢说。放榜前一日，某梦文昌、关帝与孔夫子同坐，朱衣者持《福建题名录》来，关帝蹙额云：”此第一人平生恶武断，何以作解头？‘文昌云：“渠官阶甚大，因无行，已削尽矣。然渠也勇喜斗，一闻母喝即止，念此尚属孝心，姑予一解，不久当令归土矣。’关帝尚怒，而孔子无言，此亦奇事。”

    Explanation

    760

    A Tiger in a Net

    In the Poyang Lake of Jiangxi, a fisherman reeled in his net, suspecting it was unusually heavy. Upon opening it, he found a striped tiger inside. Regretfully, he noted that the tiger was already dead.

    A Scholar with Unremarkable Appearance

    Qi Wen Da Gong, the top scholar from Fujian, was appointed to preside over the examinations in Fujian. He was impressed by the top scholar's essay and eagerly wanted to meet him after the results were announced. During the day, while sitting in his office, he heard noisy voices outside. Upon inquiring, he discovered that the top scholar and his family were quarreling over a door envelope. The official felt disdain for the situation and suspected the top scholar was poor. He forbade his family from extorting money and immediately summoned the top scholar. The top scholar's appearance and speech were all crude and unremarkable. Feeling depressed, the official told the provincial governor about his regret over the mistake in selecting scholars.

    The provincial governor said, "If you don't say it, I dare not speak. The day before the results were posted, I dreamed of Wen Chang, Guan Di, and Confucius sitting together. A red-clothed person held the 'Fujian Examination List' and came. Guan Di frowned and said, 'This first person has always disliked arbitrary decisions, how could he be the top scholar?' Wen Chang replied, 'He had a high rank, but because of his lack of virtue, it was all stripped away. However, he is brave and enjoys fighting, but if he hears his mother scold him, he stops immediately. Considering this, he still shows filial piety, so I will give him one more chance, but he will soon return to the earth.' Guan Di was still angry, but Confucius remained silent. This is also a strange event."

    761

    未几某亡。

    　　○顾四嫁妻重合永城吕明家佃人顾四，乾隆丙子岁荒，鬻其妻某氏，嫁江南虹县孙某，生一女。次年岁丰，顾又娶后妻，生子成。成幼远出，为人佣工，流转至虹县地方，赘孙姓家。两年，妻父殁，成无所依，遂携其妻并妻母回永城。顾四出见，儿之岳母，已之故妻也。时顾后到先一月殁，遂为夫妇如初。

    　　○千里客万历年间，绍兴商冢宰起第，卜云“千里客来居此宅”。当时讶之。至国初，王侍御兰膏先生任盐政归，买此宅居之。王别号“千里”，即江宁王检校大德父也。

    　　○赵子昂降乩邓宗洛秀才云：伯祖开禹公少时赘海宁陈大司空家，众人请仙，公亦问终身，乩判云“予赵子昂也”五字，宛然赵书。公在帝微笑云：“两朝人物。”乩随判诗一首云：“莫笑吾身事两朝，姓名久已著丹霄。书生不用多饶舌，胜尔寒毡叹寂寥。”后公年八十，由岁贡任来安训导，十年而终。

    Explanation

    761

    Not long after, someone died.

    Gu Si, a tenant farmer in the village of Zhonghe, Yongcheng, belonging to the family of Lü Ming, sold his wife in the year Bingzi of the Qianlong era (1766) during a famine, and she was married to someone in Hong County, Jiangnan. She gave birth to a daughter. The next year, when the harvest was good, Gu Si remarried and had a son named Cheng. When Cheng was young, he left home to work as a laborer and eventually settled in Hong County, marrying into the Sun family. Two years later, his father-in-law died, and Cheng had no place to rely on, so he brought his wife and his mother-in-law back to Yongcheng. Gu Si saw his former wife, who had become his daughter-in-law, and they resumed their marital relationship.

    In the Wanli period, the Grand Councillor of the Shaoxing merchant family, Zang Zongluo, had a divination that said, "A guest from a thousand miles will live in this house." At the time, people were surprised by this prediction. At the beginning of the Qing dynasty, Mr. Wang Lan Gao, the Magistrate of Justice, bought this house and lived in it. Wang's courtesy name was "Qianli," and he was the father of Wang Jiandiao of Jiangning.

    Zhao Ziyang descended to a medium. Mr. Deng Zongluo said that his great-grandfather, Kai Yu, had married into the family of Chen, the Grand Secretary of Hai Ning, when he was young. When people asked the medium about his fate, the medium wrote, "I am Zhao Ziyang," in a style that resembled Zhao's calligraphy. Zhao smiled and said, "A figure of two dynasties." The medium then wrote a poem: "Do not laugh at me for serving two dynasties; my name has long been inscribed in the heavens. Scholars should not talk too much; better than your cold mat of loneliness." Later, Kai Yu, at the age of eighty, was selected as an annual tribute and served as an educational supervisor in Laian, where he died after ten years.

    762

    ○神仙不解考据乾隆丙午，严道甫客中州。有仙降乩巩县刘氏，自称雁门田颖，诗文字画皆可观，并能代请古时名人如韩、柳、欧、苏来降。刘氏云，有坛设其家已数载矣。

    　　中州仕宦者，咸敬信之。颖本唐开、宝间人，曾撰张希古墓志，石在西安碑林，毕中丞近移置吴中灵岩山馆。

    　　一日，降乩节署，甫至，即以此语谢其护持之功。此事无知者，因共称其神奇。时严道甫在座，因云：“记墓志中云：”左卫马邑郡尚德府折冲都尉张君。‘考唐府兵皆隶诸卫，左右卫领六十府。志云尚德府为左卫所领，固也，但《唐书。地理志》马邑郡所属尚无德府，未知墓志何据？“仙停乩半响，云：”当日下笔时，仅据行状开载，至唐《地理志》，为欧尤所修，当俟晤时问明，再奉复耳。“

    Explanation

    762

    In 1786, during the reign of Emperor Qianlong, Yan Daofu was a guest in the Central Plains region. One day, a spirit descended in the form of a divination board to the Liu family in Gong County, claiming to be Tian Ying from Yanmen. This spirit was skilled in poetry, calligraphy, and painting, and could also summon famous historical figures like Han Yu, Liu Zongyuan, Ouyang Xiu, and Su Shi. The Liu family mentioned that they had set up an altar in their home for several years.

    The officials of the Central Plains region all respected and believed in this spirit. Tian Ying claimed to be a person from the Tang dynasty during the Kaiyuan and Tianbao periods, and he had written the epitaph for Zhang Xigu, which is inscribed on a stone now located in the Stele Forest Museum in Xi'an. Recently, Governor Bi had moved this stone to the Lingyin Mountain Pavilion in Wuzhong.

    One day, the spirit descended to the government office, and as soon as he arrived, he thanked them for their protection. No one knew about this event, and it was widely regarded as a miraculous occurrence. At the time, Yan Daofu was present and remarked, "I remember the epitaph says: 'The Left Guard, Marquis of Shangde, and the Deputy Commander of the Marquis of Shangde, Zhang Jun.' According to the Tang dynasty's military system, all palace guards were under the command of the Left and Right Guards, which led sixty prefectures. The epitaph states that the Marquis of Shangde was under the Left Guard, which is correct, but the 'Geographical Records' of the Tang dynasty does not list a Marquis of Shangde under the jurisdiction of Ma County. I wonder what basis the epitaph has for this?"

    The spirit paused for a moment before responding, "At the time of writing, I only relied on the biography, but the 'Geographical Records' of the Tang dynasty were revised by Ouyang Xiu and others. I will ask them when I meet them and then provide a detailed explanation."

    763

    然自是节署相请，乩不复降。即他所相请，有道甫在，乩亦不复降。

    　　○产公广西太平府僚妇生子，经三日，便澡身于溪河，其夫乃拥衾抱子坐于寝榻，卧起饮食，皆须其妇扶持之，稍不卫护，生疾一如孕妇，名曰“产公”，而妻反无所苦。查中丞俭堂云。

    　　○乌鲁木齐城隍乌鲁木齐于乾隆四十一年筑城，得至德提残碑，中有“金蒲”字，知其地唐时为金蒲城，今《唐书》作“金蒲城”，误也。并建有城隍庙，兴工三日，都统公亮梦有人儒冠而来云：“姓纪，名永宁，陕西人。昨奉天山之神奏为皮地城隍，故尔来谒。”公心异之。

    Explanation

    763

    From then on, when the local officials invited, the spirit would no longer descend. Even if invited elsewhere, if Dao Fu was present, the spirit would also not descend.

    A man from Guangxi, who was born with a condition similar to pregnancy, was born to a woman from Taiping Prefecture. After three days, he bathed in a stream. His husband then held him in his arms and sat on the bed, needing his wife to support him in all his movements, eating, and sleeping. If she did not take care of him properly, he would become ill just like a pregnant woman. He was called "Chang Gong," while his wife experienced no discomfort. According to Zha Zhongcheng Jian Tang.

    In the year 1776 during the reign of Emperor Qianlong, the City God of Urumqi was built. An ancient stone tablet inscribed with "Zhi De" was discovered, which mentioned "Jin Pu." It was known that the area was called Jin Pu City during the Tang Dynasty, but the Book of Tang incorrectly recorded it as "Jin Pu City." A City God temple was also constructed, and after three days of construction, General Gong Liang dreamed of a man in a scholar's cap coming and saying, "My surname is Ji, and my given name is Yongning. I am from Shaanxi. Yesterday, I was appointed as the City God of Pidi by the god of Tianshan, so I came to pay respects." The general found it strange.

    764

    时毕公秋帆抚陕，因以札来询。毕公饬州县查，现在纪姓中，未有名永宁者。

    　　适严道甫修《华州志》，有纪以家谱来求登载其远祖。检之，则名永宁者居然在焉。乃明中叶生员。生平亦无他善，惟嘉靖三十一年的震时，曾捐资掩埋瘗伤死者中四十馀人而已。因以复明公。书至，适于是日庙方落成也。

    　　○黑霜四海本一海也，南方见之为南海，北方见之为北海，证之经传皆然。严道甫向客秦中，晤诚毅伯伍公，云：雍正间，奉使鄂勒，素闻有海在北界，欲往视，国人难之。固请，乃派西洋人二十名，持罗盘火器，以重毡裹车，从者皆乘橐驼随往。

    　　北行六七日，见有冰山如城郭，其高入天，光气不可逼视。下有洞穴，从人以火照罗盘，蜿而入。行三日乃出，出则天色黯淡如玳瑁，间有黑烟吹来，着人如砂砾。洋人云：“此黑霜也。”每行数里，得岩穴则避入，以硝磺发火，盖其地不生草木，无煤炭也。逾时复行。

    Explanation

    764

    At that time, Governor Bi Gong of Qiu Fan was in charge of Shaanxi, and he sent a letter to inquire. He ordered the counties to investigate, and it was found that there was no one named Ji Yongning in the Ji family.

    At that time, Yan Daofu was compiling the Huazhou Gazetteer, and a person from the Ji family came to request the inclusion of his distant ancestors in the gazetteer. Upon checking, it was found that the name Ji Yongning was indeed there. He was a student during the mid-Ming period. He had no other good deeds, but during the earthquake in the 31st year of the Jiajing era, he donated money to bury and cover the injured and dead, more than forty people. He reported this back to Governor Bi Gong. The letter arrived on the day the temple was just completed.

    The four seas are originally one sea; in the south, it is seen as the South Sea; in the north, it is seen as the North Sea. This is confirmed by the classics and histories. Yan Daofu, who had been a guest in the Qin region, met with Duke Wu of Chengyi, and said: "During the reign of Emperor Yongzheng, he was sent on a mission to E'le, and he had always heard that there was a sea in the northern border, so he wanted to go and see it, but the people of the country opposed it. He firmly requested, and so twenty Westerners were sent, carrying compasses and firearms, with the carriage wrapped in thick felt, and all the followers riding camels.

    After traveling north for six or seven days, they saw an ice mountain like a city wall, reaching high into the sky, with a light and vapor that could not be looked at directly. There were caves below, and the followers used fire to illuminate the compass, and they entered the cave windingly. After traveling for three days, they emerged, and the sky was dark like tortoiseshell, with occasional black smoke blowing in, hitting people like sand and gravel. The foreigners said, 'This is black frost.' Every few miles, they would seek shelter in caves, using saltpeter and sulfur to start a fire, as the area did not grow grass or trees, and there was no coal. After a while, they continued their journey.

    765

    如是又五六日，有二铜人对峙，高数十丈，一乘龟，一握蛇，前有铜柱，虫篆不可辨。洋人云：“此唐尧皇帝所立，相传柱上乃‘寒门’二字。”因请回车，云：“前去到海，约三百里不见星日，寒气切肌，中之即死。海水黑色如漆，时复开裂，则有夜叉怪兽起来攫人。至是水亦不流，火亦不热。”公因以火着貂裘上试之，果不燃，因太息而回。

    　　入城，检点从者，五十人冻死者二十有一。公面黑如漆，半载始复故，随从人有终身不再白者。

    　　○中印度后藏西南四千余里，有务鲁木者，即佛经所云中印度也，世尊居之。金银宫阙，与佛书所云无异。宫门外有池，方广百里，白莲如斗，香气着衣，经月不散，云即阿暂池也。天时寒暖，皆如三四月，粳稻再熟。无金银，皆以货物交易。达赉喇嘛五岁一往觐。

    Explanation

    765

    Like this for another five or six days, there were two bronze figures facing each other, each towering dozens of feet high, one riding a turtle and the other holding a snake. In front of them was a bronze pillar with insect-like characters that could not be deciphered. The foreigners said, "This was established by Emperor Yao of the Tang Dynasty, and it is said that the characters on the pillar are 'Cold Gate.'" They asked to turn the carriage around, saying, "Going forward to the sea, about three hundred miles without seeing stars or the sun, the cold penetrates the skin, and being struck by it is to die. The seawater is black as ink, and from time to time it cracks, with ogre-like monsters rising to grab people. At this point, the water does not flow, and the fire does not heat." The official tested the fire on the fur robe, and indeed it did not burn, so he sighed and returned.

    Upon entering the city, they inspected the followers, and out of fifty, twenty-one froze to death. The official's face was black as ink, and it took half a year before returning to normal, with some of the followers never turning white again.

    ○ In the southwestern region of Central India, about four thousand miles away, there is a place called Wulum, which is the Central India mentioned in Buddhist scriptures, where the World Honored One resides. The palaces of gold and silver are no different from what is described in Buddhist texts. Outside the palace gates is a pool, a square hundred miles wide, with white lotuses as large as a winnowing basket, their fragrance clinging to clothes, not dissipating for months, said to be the Pool of Coolness. The weather is cold and warm, all like March or April, with rice ripening twice. There are no gold or silver, and all transactions are made through goods. The Dala Lama pays homage once every five years.

    766

    闻雍正初年，鄂罗索发兵万余，驱猛象数百来斗，欲夺其地。世尊持禁咒，遣毒蟒数千往御。鄂罗索惧，请受约束，蟒蛇瞬息不见。世尊云：“此嗔心所致也，不嗔则无有矣。”因谕以此地人少，每十年当以童男女五百来献，令其自相配偶至今犹然。诚毅伯伍公云。

    　　○来文端公前身是伯乐来文端公自言伯乐转世，眸子炯炯有光，相马独具神解。兼管兵部及上驷院时，每值挑马，百十为群，瞥眼一过，其毛病纤悉，无不一一指出，贩马者惊以为神。年七十后，常闭目静摄。每有马过，静听蹄声，不但知其良否，即毛色疾病，皆能知之。上所乘马，皆先命公选视。

    　　有内侍卫数人，精选三马，百试无差，将献上。公时已老，眼皮下垂，以两指撑眼视之，曰：“其一可用，其二不可用。”再试之，果蹶矣。

    Explanation

    766

    During the early years of Yongzheng's reign, Eroso mobilized more than ten thousand troops and drove hundreds of elephants to fight, aiming to seize their land. The World-Honored One (Buddha) held a banishing spell and sent thousands of venomous pythons to confront them. Eroso was afraid and asked to submit to constraints. The pythons vanished instantly. The Buddha said, "This is due to anger; if there is no anger, there will be none." Therefore, he advised that since the population here is small, every ten years 500 boys and girls should be offered, to let them marry each other, and this has continued until now. According to the Honorable and Martial Lord Wu.

    ○ The former incarnation of Lai Wende was Bo Le. Lai Wende himself claimed to be reborn as Bo Le, with eyes that gleamed with light, uniquely able to discern the qualities of horses. While overseeing the Ministry of War and the Imperial Stud, whenever selecting horses, he would look over a hundred or so in a group, and with a quick glance, he could point out all their flaws in detail, leaving horse traders amazed at his divinity. After the age of seventy, he often closed his eyes for meditation. Whenever a horse passed by, he would listen quietly to the sound of its hooves and not only determine its quality but also its coat color and any illnesses. All the horses the emperor rode were first selected and inspected by him.

    There were several imperial guards who had carefully chosen three horses, which had been tested a hundred times without fail, and were about to present them to the emperor. By then, Lai Wende was old, with drooping eyelids, and he used two fingers to prop open his eyes to look at them. He said, "One of them can be used, the other two cannot." They tried again, and indeed, the two that he deemed unusable stumbled and fell.

    767

    一日坐内阁，史文靖公乘马至阁门外下，偶言所乘枣骝马甚佳，公曰：“佳则佳矣，但公所乘乃黄膘马也，何得相诳？”文靖云：“适所言诚误，但公何以知之？”公笑而不言。

    　　又一日，梁文庄公入阁少迟，自言所乘马伤水，艰于行步。“公曰：”非伤水，乃误吞水蛭耳。“文庄乃请兽医针治，果下水蛭数升而愈。

    　　公常语侍读严道甫云：“二十时，荷校于长安门外三十余日，玩索《易》象乾坤二卦，得相马之道。其神解所到，未能以口授人也。”

    　　○福建试院树神纪太史晓岚视学闽省，试院西斋有柏一株，干霄蔽日，幕中友人于深夜常见友人来往其下，章服一如本朝制度，惟袍是大红。纪意树神为祟，乃扫室立主以祀，并作对句悬于楹间云：“参天黛色常如此，点首朱衣或是公。”自是怪遂绝。

    Explanation

    767

    One day, while sitting in the cabinet office, Shi Wenjing, a high-ranking official, arrived on horseback and dismounted at the gate. He casually mentioned that the chestnut-colored horse he was riding was excellent. The speaker replied, "It is indeed good, but the horse you are riding is actually a yellow-brown horse. How could you deceive me?" Shi Wenjing responded, "I did make a mistake just now, but how did you know?" The speaker smiled and said nothing.

    On another day, Liang Wen庄, another high-ranking official, arrived at the cabinet office a bit late. He said that the horse he was riding was lame and had difficulty walking. The speaker said, "It is not injured by water, but rather it mistakenly swallowed leeches." Liang Wen庄 then asked a veterinarian to treat the horse with needles, and indeed, several leeches were removed, and the horse recovered.

    The speaker often told his tutor, Yan Daofu, "When I was twenty, I carried a yoke outside the Changan Gate for more than thirty days. During that time, I studied the I Ching and the two fundamental trigrams, Heaven and Earth, and gained insight into the art of judging horses. The depth of my understanding is something I cannot fully articulate."

    ○ During his time as an examiner in Fujian, Ji Xiaolan, a famous scholar, noticed that the examination hall in the west pavilion had a tall pine tree that reached high into the sky and shaded the sun. Late at night, his colleagues often saw figures in official robes walking beneath the tree, dressed in the same style as the current dynasty, but their robes were a deep red. Ji Xiaolan believed that the tree was inhabited by a spirit and decided to appease it. He swept the room, set up an altar, and wrote a couplet to hang on the pillars: "The dark green color of the tree reaches high into the sky, just as it always does. The red-capped figure nodding might be you." After this, the strange occurrences ceased.

    768

    ○于云石金坛于云石，官翰林时，迎其父就养入都。一日，行至中途，天色已晚，四无人烟，寻一旅店，遂往投宿。店主以人满辞，于以前路无店，固求留宿。店主踌躇久之，曰：“店后只有空屋数椽，小儿幼年曾读书其处，不幸夭亡，我不忍往观，故封闭之。客如不嫌，请暂住一夜如何？”

    　　于从之，即开门入，见四壁尘蒙，蛸满户，案有残书数卷。偶得时文稿一本，翻阅之，与其子云石所作文无异；入后数篇，与乡、会试中式之卷亦相同意，甚讶然。忽寓外有光射入，见对面石壁上恍惚有“于云石”字迹，即秉烛出现，乃“千霄石”三字也。转身进内，蹦然有声，石壁遂倒，字亦随灭。一夜惊疑不寐。

    Explanation

    768

    于 Yunshi, a native of Jintan, served as a Hanlin scholar. One day, while traveling to the capital to welcome his father for support, he reached a point where it was already evening, and there was no one around. He found a small inn and requested to stay overnight. The innkeeper declined due to being fully booked, but Yunshi insisted, saying there were no other places ahead.
