After some hesitation, the innkeeper finally agreed, saying, "There is only an empty house with several rooms behind the store. A young child used to study there, but unfortunately passed away. I cannot bear to go see it, so it has been sealed up. If you don't mind, please stay for one night."

    Yunshi agreed and entered the house, finding it dusty with spiders and insects everywhere, and a few old books on the table. He chanced upon a copy of a current essay draft, which was the same as the essays written by his son Yunshi; the subsequent several pieces also agreed with the essays that passed the provincial and metropolitan exams, which greatly surprised him. Suddenly, light shone in from outside the room, and he saw the characters "Yu Yunshi" faintly on the opposite stone wall. He immediately lit a candle and saw it, which turned out to be the three characters "Qianxiao Shi" (Thousand Clouds Rock). He turned around and went inside, and a loud noise was heard, causing the stone wall to collapse, and the characters also disappeared. He spent the night in fear and unable to sleep.

    769

    晓行抵都，与子备述其事。云石闻言，不觉失色，须叟仆地。急唤家人救治，不苏而绝。

    　　●卷二十二○王昊庐宗伯是莲花长老王昊庐宗伯，未第时，自黄冈赴京应试。路过庐山，宿于莲花宫内，因次日仍欲启行，未晚便睡。梦身坐大殿之上，面供斋果，下有袈裟百辈环拜诵佛，因随手取面前枣子，偶啖数枚，遂醒。醒时，口中有馀味。正惊讶间，忽见住房外灯烛辉煌，几筵肆设，众僧方膜拜，宛然梦中光景。启户问之，是日乃此庵肆已故净月上人忌辰，众方祭祀。宗伯大异，起视所供盘中之枣，其顶微缺，如少二三枚者，恍悟自已前身乃此庵长老也。故终身奉佛甚虔。先是，宗伯父用予公崇祯翰林。殉节庐山，故自号“昊庐”，取“昊天罔极”之义，讳泽宏。

    Explanation

    769

    In the morning, he traveled to the capital and told his son in detail about the events. Upon hearing the news, Yunshi suddenly turned pale and immediately fell to the ground. His family rushed to call for help, but he did not revive and died.

    Wang Haolu, a high-ranking official, had not yet passed the imperial examinations when he traveled from Huanggang to the capital for the test. Passing through Mount Lu, he stayed overnight in the Lotus Palace. Because he still intended to depart the next day, he went to bed before it was late. In his dream, he saw himself seated on a high platform in a grand hall, with offerings of fruits and vegetables in front of him. Below, a hundred monks in saffron robes were circling around, bowing and chanting. He casually picked up some dates in front of him and ate a few, then woke up. When he woke up, he still had the taste of the dates in his mouth. Just as he was surprised, he suddenly saw the outside of his house lit up with lamps and candles, with tables and mats set up, and the monks were just bowing in worship, exactly as he had seen in his dream. He opened the door and asked, and it turned out that it was the anniversary of the death of the late Elder Master Jingyue of this temple, and the monks were just holding a memorial service. Wang Haolu was greatly surprised, and he got up to look at the dates on the table, noticing that the tops were slightly missing, as if two or three were missing. Suddenly, he realized that his previous life was that of the elder monk of this temple. Therefore, he devoted himself to Buddhism with great sincerity.

    Previously, his father, Gong Yongyu, was a scholar-official during the Chongzhen era and died a martyr in Mount Lu. Therefore, he called himself "Hao Lu," taking the meaning of "the boundless sky," and his name was Zemeng.

    770

    ○鬼买儿洞庭贡生葛文林，在庠有文名。其嫡母周氏亡后，父荆州续娶李氏，即文林生母也。于归三日后，理周氏衣箱，有绣九枝莲红袄一件，爱而着之。

    　　食次即昏迷，自批其颊曰：“余前妻周氏也。箱内衣裳是我嫁时带来。我平日爱惜，不忍上身。今汝初来，公然偷着，我心不甘，来索汝命。”家人环跪，替李求情，且云：“娘子业已身故，要此华衣何用？”曰：“速烧与我，我等要着。我自知气量小，从前妆奁，一丝不能与李氏，皆速烧与我，我才肯去。”家人不得已，如其言，尽焚之。鬼拍手笑曰：“吾可以去矣。”李即霍然病愈。家人甚喜。

    　　次日李方晨妆，忽打一呵欠，鬼又附其身曰：“请相公来。”其夫奔至，乃执其手曰：“新妇年轻，不能理家事，我每早来代为料理。”嗣后，午前必附魂于李身，查问薪米，呵责奴婢，井井有条。如是者半年，家人习而安之，不复为怪。

    770

    First Draft:
    ○ Ghost Buying a Child

    Dongting, a tribute scholar named Ge Wenlin, was known for his literary talent while studying at the academy. After his stepmother Zhou passed away, his father, surnamed Jingzhou, remarried a woman named Li, who was also Ge Wenlin's biological mother. Three days after her return from marriage, she organized Zhou's clothing box and found a red袄embroidered with nine lotus flowers, which she loved and wore.

    While eating, she suddenly fell into a trance and slapped her own cheek, saying, "I am your former wife, Zhou. The clothes in the box were brought by me when I got married. I cherished them and was reluctant to wear them. Now that you are new here, you openly wear them. I am not satisfied, and I am here to take your life." Family members knelt around her, pleading for Li, and said, "Madam has already passed away, what use is this fine clothing?" She replied, "Burn it for me quickly. We want to wear them. I know I have a small capacity. I could not give any of my dowry to Li. Burn it all for me, then I will leave." The family had no choice but to follow her words and burned everything. The ghost clapped her hands and laughed, "I can leave now." Li immediately recovered from her illness. The family was very happy.

    The next day, Li was about to put on her morning makeup when she suddenly yawned. The ghost reappeared, possessing her body and said, "Ask my husband to come." Her husband rushed over, and she took his hand, saying, "I am young and cannot manage household affairs. I will come every morning to help manage them." Afterward, before noon, the ghost would always possess Li's body, inquiring about firewood and rice, scolding the servants, and doing everything in an orderly manner. Like this for half a year, the family became accustomed to it and no longer found it strange.

    First Draft Ready.

    771

    忽一日谓其夫曰：“我要去矣。我柩停在此，汝辈在旁行走，震动灵床，我在棺中骨节俱痛，可速出殡，以安我魂。”其夫曰：“尚无葬地，奈何？”曰：“西邻卖爆竹人张姓者有地在某山，我昨往看，有松有竹，颇合我意。渠口索六十金，其心想三十六金，可买也。”葛往观，果有地有主，丝毫不爽，遂立契交易。

    　　鬼请出殡日期，葛曰：“地虽已有，然启期告亲友，尚无孝子出名，殊属缺典。”鬼曰：“此说甚是。汝新妇现有身矣，但雌雄未卜，与我纸钱三千，我替君买一儿来。”言毕去。至期，李氏果生文林。

    　　三日后，鬼又附妇身如平时，其姑陈氏责之曰：“李氏新产，身子孱弱，汝又来纠缠，何太不留情耶？”曰：“非也。此儿系我买来，嗣我血食，我不能忘情。新妇年轻贪睡，倘被渠压死奈何？我有一言嘱婆婆：俟其母乳毕后，婆婆即带儿同睡，我才放心。”其姑首肯之，李妇打一呵欠，鬼又去矣。

    Explanation

    771

    One day, the ghost said to her husband, "I must go now. My coffin is here, and you all walking around are shaking the spirit bed, causing me pain in my bones in the coffin. You should quickly bury me to rest my soul." Her husband replied, "There is still no burial ground, what shall we do?" The ghost said, "The person named Zhang, who sells firecrackers, has land in a certain mountain. I went to look yesterday, and there are pines and bamboos, which I quite like. He asked for sixty gold coins, but I think thirty-six gold coins, it can be bought." Gu went to see, and indeed, the land was available, and everything was as the ghost had said, so they signed a contract and completed the transaction.

    The ghost then asked for the date of the burial. Gu said, "The land is already secured, but we still need to inform relatives and friends and name a filial son to carry out the burial, which is a serious omission." The ghost said, "This is true. Your new wife is pregnant, but we don't know the gender yet. Give me three thousand paper money, and I will buy a son for you." After saying this, the ghost left. On the appointed day, Li gave birth to Wenlin.

    Three days later, the ghost again possessed the wife as usual. Her grandmother Chen reproached her, saying, "Li just gave birth, and she is weak. Why do you keep bothering her, showing no mercy?" The ghost said, "It is not so. This child is bought by me, to inherit my sacrifices, and I cannot forget my feelings. The new wife is young and sleeps a lot, if she is crushed by him, what will happen? I have one request for you: after her breastfeeding is finished, you will take the child to sleep with you, then I will be at ease." The grandmother agreed, and Li yawned, and the ghost left again.

    772

    择日出丧，葛怜儿甫满月，不胜粗麻，易细麻与着。鬼来骂曰：“此系齐衰，孙丧祖之服。我嫡母也，非斩衰不可。”不得已，易而送之。临葬，鬼附妇身大哭曰：“我体魄已安，从此永不至矣。”嗣后果断。

    　　先是，周未嫁时，与邻女结拜三姊妹，誓同生死，其二妹先亡。周病时曰：“两妹来，现在床后唤我。”葛怒，拔剑斫之。周顿足曰：“汝不软求，而斫伤其臂，愈难挽回矣。”言毕而亡，年甫二十三。

    　　○鬼抢馒头文林言：洞庭山多饿鬼。其家蒸馒头一笼，甫熟揭盖，见馒头唧唧自动，逐渐皱缩，如碗大者，顷刻变小如胡桃。食之，味如面筋，精华尽去。初不解其故，有老人云：“此饿鬼所抢也，起笼时以朱笔点之，便不能抢。”如其言，点者自点，缩者仍缩。盖一人之点，不能胜群鬼之抢也。

    Explanation

    772

    Choosing a day for the funeral, Ge Lian'er, who had just turned one month old, could not bear the rough hemp, so they changed to fine hemp and dressed her. A ghost came and scolded, saying, "This is Qi衰, the mourning attire for a grandson mourning his grandfather. I am her legitimate mother, and only Zhan衰 is acceptable." Reluctantly, they changed and sent her. At the time of the burial, the ghost possessed the woman's body and wept, saying, "My body and spirit are now at rest, and from now on, I will never come back." Indeed, she did not return.

    Before that, Zhou, before she married, had sworn with her neighbor's daughters as three sisters, vowing to share life and death. Her two sisters had died first. When Zhou was ill, she said, "My two sisters are here, calling me from behind the bed." Ge became angry and drew his sword to cut her. Zhou stomped her feet and said, "You did not gently request, but instead cut her arm, making it even harder to reverse the situation." After speaking, she passed away at the age of twenty-three.

    ○ Ghosts Stealing Steamed Buns Wen Lin said: Dongting Mountain has many hungry ghosts. One of their family steamed a batch of buns, and just as they were fully cooked, the lid was lifted, and they saw the buns moving and making sounds on their own, gradually shrinking and wrinkling. Those as large as bowls instantly shrunk to the size of walnuts. They tasted like gluten, and all the essence was gone. Initially, they did not understand the reason. An old man said, "This is because the hungry ghosts are stealing them. When lifting the steamer, mark them with a red pen, and they cannot be taken." Following his advice, they marked the buns, but those that were marked still shrank, while those that were not marked continued to be taken. It seems that the mark of one person cannot overcome the taking of many ghosts.

    773

    ○荷花儿余姚章大立，康熙三年举人。家居授徒，忽有二冤鬼，一女一男，白日现形。

    　　初扼其喉，继推之地，以两手高撑，梏而不开，若空中有绳系之者。先作女声曰：“我荷花儿也。”继作男声曰：“我王奎也。”皆北京口气。

    　　家人问：“何冤？”曰：“章大立前身姓翁，亦名大立，前朝隆庆时为刑部侍郎。其时我主人周世臣，官锦衣指挥，家贫无妻，只荷花儿与王奎一婢一奴相伴。有盗入室杀世臣去，我二人报官。官遣张把总入室捕盗，疑我二人因奸弑主。

    　　刑部严刑拷讯，我二人不胜楚毒，遂自诬服。刑部郎中潘志伊疑之，狱久不决。

    　　及大立为侍郎，忽发大怒，别委郎中王三锡、徐一忠再讯，二人迎合，竟照前议定罪。志伊苦争不能得，遂剐我二人于市。越二年，别获真盗，都人方知我二人之冤。传入宫中，天子怒，仅夺大立官职，而调一忠、三锡于外。请问：凌迟重情，可是夺职所能蔽辜否？我故来此索命。“

    Explanation

    773

    In the village of Yuyao, there lived a scholar named Zhang Dali, who passed the provincial examination in the third year of the Kangxi reign. He taught students at home when suddenly two vengeful spirits appeared, one female and one male, manifesting themselves in broad daylight.

    Initially, they strangled his throat, then pushed him to the ground, locking him in place with both hands held high and unable to move, as if there were ropes tied to him in the air. First speaking in a female voice, she said, "I am Huahua." Then, in a male voice, he said, "I am Wang Kui." Both spoke with a Beijing accent.

    The family asked, "What grievance do you have?" They replied, "In a former life, Zhang Dali's surname was Wang, and he was also named Dali. During the Longqing era of the previous dynasty, he served as a Vice Minister of Justice. At that time, our master Zhou Shizhen was a high-ranking officer in the imperial guard, poor and without a wife, only Huahua and Wang Kui, a maid and a servant, were with him. A thief entered the house and killed Shizhen. We two reported to the authorities. The official sent Captain Zhang into the house to catch the thief, suspecting that we two committed adultery and killed the master.

    The Ministry of Justice subjected us to severe torture, and we two could not endure the torture, and thus falsely confessed. The Deputy Minister of Justice Pan Zhiyi suspected us, and the case remained unresolved for a long time.

    When Dali became a Vice Minister, he suddenly became very angry, appointed Deputy Ministers Wang Sansi and Xu Yizhong to re-investigate. The two agreed and finally decided the case according to the previous agreement. Zhiyi tried hard to argue but failed, and had us two beheaded in the market. Two years later, the real thief was caught, and the people of the capital finally knew our injustice. The news reached the palace, and the emperor was angry, only stripped Dali of his official position, and transferred Yizhong and Sansi to outer regions. Ask: Is the crime of dismemberment a serious offense? Can it be covered by just stripping of office? Therefore, I have come here to seek revenge."

    774

    家人问：“何以不报王、徐之冤？”曰：“彼二人恶迹更多。一已变猪，一囚酆都狱中。我不必再报。惟大立前身颇有清官之号，又居显秩，故尔迟迟。今渠已投第三次人身矣，禄位有限，方能报复。且明季朝纲不整，气数将绝，阴司鬼神亦多昏聩。我等屡诉不准，不许出京，岂若当今大清之世，冥司阴官，亦洗心革面耶！”家人跪求说：“召名僧为汝超度何如？”曰：“我果有罪，方要名僧超度。我二人丝毫无罪，何用名僧超度？况超度者，不过要我早投人身耳。我想就投人身，遇着大立，也要报仇，渠必死我二人之手。然而旁观者不解来历，即我与大立既已隔世，虽报其人，两边都不晓来历，无以垂戒作官之人。故我二人每闻阴司唤令轮回，坚辞不肯。今冤报后，可以轮回矣。”言毕，取几上小刀自割其肉，片片坠下。作女声问曰：“可像剐耶？”作男声问曰：“可知痛耶？”

    Explanation

    774

    The family member asked, "Why have you not avenged the wrongs of Wang and Xu?" The response was, "Those two individuals have committed even more evil deeds. One has been transformed into a pig, and the other is imprisoned in the underworld. There is no need for further revenge. However, Dali, in his previous life, had a reputation as a clean official and held a high position, which is why the revenge has been delayed. Now, he has taken his third human life, and his career prospects are limited, making it possible to take revenge. Moreover, during the late Ming dynasty, the political system was in chaos, and the end was near; the officials of the underworld were also corrupt. We have repeatedly appealed without success and were not allowed to leave the capital. How is it different now in the Qing dynasty, where the officials of the underworld have also reformed their hearts and faces?" The family member knelt and pleaded, "Why not call a famous monk to perform a ritual for you?" The response was, "If I had committed sins, I would need a famous monk for the ritual. The two of us are entirely innocent, so why would we need a famous monk's ritual? Besides, the purpose of the ritual is merely to facilitate a quick reincarnation. I intend to reincarnate and, upon encountering Dali, seek revenge; he will surely die by our hands. However, bystanders do not understand the origins, and since I and Dali are separated by lifetimes, even if we take revenge, neither side will know the origins, and it cannot serve as a warning to officials. Therefore, we have always refused to be reborn when called by the underworld. Now, after the revenge is complete, we can be reborn." After speaking, he took a small knife from the table and began to cut his own flesh, pieces falling one by one. He then asked in a feminine voice, "Is this like being executed by slicing?" And in a masculine voice, "Do you know it hurts?"

    775

    血流满席而死。

    　　○欧阳澈宋浙西有陈东、欧阳澈庙，当时士民怜其忠，故私立而祠之也。后王伦从金国来，见面恶之，命有司拆毁。明季有富而好义者李士贵，又立庙于艮山门外，乡民祈求颇灵。

    　　一日，李梦神人布袍革履叩门求见，曰：“我欧阳澈也，当日位卑而言高，获罪系我自取，幸上帝怜我忠诚，命我司杭州水旱之事。杭州城地方甚大，我一人难以办理。我有友二人，一樊安邦，一傅国璋，皆布衣有气节。可塑二人像于我侧，助我安辑地方。”李允许，既而笑问曰：“陈东先生安在，何不相助为理？”

    Explanation

    775

    The blood flowed all over the seat, and he died.

    ○ There are temples dedicated to Euanghui and Chen Dong in the western part of Zhejiang during the Song Dynasty. At that time, the scholars and common people admired their loyalty, so they privately built and worshipped at these temples. Later, Wang Lun came from the Jin Kingdom and disliked Euanghui, ordering the officials to demolish the temples. In the late Ming Dynasty, a wealthy and righteous person named Li Shigui rebuilt the temple outside the Gate of Genshan in Hangzhou, and the villagers found it very responsive to their prayers.

    One day, Li had a dream where a spirit person in a cloth robe and leather shoes knocked on the door to request an audience. The spirit said, "I am Euanghui. At that time, I was of low status but spoke boldly, and the punishment I received was self-inflicted. Fortunately, the Heavenly Emperor pitied my loyalty and appointed me to manage the affairs of drought and flood in Hangzhou. The city of Hangzhou is very large, and I alone find it difficult to manage. I have two friends, Fan Anbang and Fu Guozhang, both of whom were ordinary people of high moral character. You can make statues of them beside me to help me manage the area." Li agreed. The spirit then smiled and asked, "Where is Mr. Chen Dong? Why doesn't he help us manage things?"

    776

    曰：“李伯纪相公现司南岳，聘陈先生作记室去矣。”士贵于次日即增两像于旁。

    　　○浮尼戊戌年，黄河水决。河官督治者每筑堤成，见水面有绿毛鹅一群翱翔水面，其夜堤必崩。用鸟枪击之，随散随聚，逾月始平。虽老河员，不知鹅为何物。后阅《桂海稗编》载前明黄萧养之乱，黄江有绿鹅为祟，识者曰：此名浮尼，水怪也，以黑犬祭之，以五色粽投之，则自然去矣。如其言，果验○雷火救忠臣全椒金光辰，以御史直谏触崇祯皇帝之怒，召对平台，将重惩之。忽迅雷震御座，乃免之。嘉靖怒刘魁、杨爵、周怡直谏，杖置狱中。有神降乩言三人冤，乃赦之。后因熊浃言乩仙不足信，重捕入狱。亡何，高元殿火起，帝祷于灵台，火光中有呼三人姓名称忠臣者，乃急传诏释之，且复其官。

    Explanation

    776

    The Chancellor Li Boji, now serving as the governor of Nan Yue, has invited Mr. Chen to serve as his secretary. The gentleman added two more images next to the existing ones the following day.

    In the Wu Xu year, the Yellow River broke its banks. Officials overseeing the repair of the river would build new levees, but every time they completed them, they saw a group of green-feathered geese flying over the water. That night, the levee would inevitably collapse. They tried firing arrows at the geese, but they would disperse and then regroup. It took over a month to calm down. Even experienced river officials did not know what these geese were. Later, while reading the Miscellaneous Records of Guangxi, they found an account of the rebellion of Huang Xiao Yang in the early Ming dynasty, which mentioned that the Yellow River had green geese causing mischief. An expert identified them as "浮尼," a water monster, and suggested offering a black dog as a sacrifice and throwing a five-colored zongzi at it to make them leave. Following this advice, the problem was resolved.

    During the reign of Emperor Chongzhen, Jin Guangchen, a censor, dared to speak frankly and angered the emperor. He was summoned to the platform for an audience and was about to be severely punished. Suddenly, a thunderbolt struck the throne, and he was then spared. During the reign of Emperor Jiajing, Liu Kui, Yang Jue, and Zhou Yi, who spoke frankly, were beaten and imprisoned. A spirit descended on the divination board and said the three were innocent, and they were then pardoned. Later, because Xiong Jia said the divination spirit was not trustworthy, they were re-arrested and imprisoned. Shortly after, a fire broke out at the Gao Yuan Hall. The emperor prayed at the Spirit Altar, and in the firelight, someone called out the names of the three men, calling them loyal ministers. He immediately issued an edict to release them and restored their positions.

    777

    ○滑伯河南滑邑署中有滑伯墓甚大，邑令到任，必先祭奠，朔望行香。滑伯之神时时出现，圭璋衮冕而出者，官必升迁；深衣便服而出者，官多不详。余门生吕炳星宰滑州，忽一日见滑伯衣胄立于墓上，是年，升香河同知。墓前古木甚多，木叶落时，风吹四散，从未有落墓上者，亦奇。

    Explanation

    777

    In the county office of Huazhou, there is a large tomb of Huabo. Whenever the magistrate arrives, they must first pay homage and perform rituals on the first and fifteenth days of the lunar month. The spirit of Huabo often appears, and when it emerges wearing ritual objects and attire, officials are sure to be promoted; when it appears in formal or casual clothing, officials often do not fare well. One of my students, Lü Bingxing, who served as the governor of Huazhou, once saw Huabo standing on the tomb wearing armor and helmet. That year, he was promoted to the position of Tongzhi of Xianghe. There are many ancient trees in front of the tomb, and when the leaves fall, the wind blows them away in all directions, never allowing them to fall on the tomb—this is also quite unusual.

    778

    ○盘古脚迹西洋锡兰山，高出云汉，其颠有巨人脚迹，入石深二尺，长八尺，云是盘古皇帝开天落地之脚迹。其国人多裸形，有穿衣者，皮肉必烂。

    　　○珠重七两《明史》：永乐十五年，苏禄国贡大珠，重七两有零。

    　　○采胆入酒占城国取生人胆入酒与家人饮，且以浴身，曰：“通身是胆。”每伺人于道，出其不意杀之，取胆以去。若其人惊觉，则胆先裂，不足用矣。置众胆于器，必以中华人胆居上。王在位三十年，则避位入深山，以兄弟子侄代，而已持斋受戒告于天曰：“我为君无道，愿虎狼食我，或病死。”居一年无恙，则复位如初。

    　　○胆长三寸福王之败，有起义兵者吴汉超，宣城生员也。兵溃，逃出城，念其母在，乃入见大帅曰：“首事者我也。”杀之，剖其腹，胆长三寸。

    Explanation

    778

    Final Translation

    The Footprints of Pangu on Western Ceylon

    The footprints of Pangu are said to be on Western Ceylon, towering above the Milky Way. At the summit of the mountain, there are giant footprints, deeply embedded in the stone, two feet deep and eight feet long. It is said that these are the footprints of Pangu, the emperor who separated heaven and earth. The people of the country often go naked, and those who wear clothes suffer from skin ailments.

    A Pearl Weighing Seven Taels

    According to The History of Ming: In the fifteenth year of Yongle (1417), the country of Sulu offered a large pearl as a tribute, weighing seven taels and some more.

    Collecting Bile in Wine

    In the kingdom of Champa, they collect the bile of living humans and mix it into wine, which they drink with their family, and also use it for bathing. They say, "My whole body is bile." They often ambush people on the road, killing them unexpectedly and taking their bile. If the person is startled and wakes up, the bile will first rupture, making it useless. When they collect many biles in a container, they always place the bile of a Chinese person on top. If the king has been in power for thirty years, he will abdicate and retreat to a deep mountain, appointing his brothers and nephews as his successors, while he himself fasts and takes vows, declaring to heaven, "I have ruled without virtue; I wish for tigers and wolves to devour me, or for me to die of illness." If he remains unharmed after a year, he will return to the throne as before.

    Bile Three Inches Long

    During the defeat of the Prince of Fu, there was a rebel leader named Wu Hanchao, a scholar from Xuancheng. After his army was defeated, he fled the city, thinking of his mother, and went to see the general, saying, "I was the one who started the rebellion." He was then killed, and his abdomen was opened, revealing bile that was three inches long.

    779

    ○湖神守尸明季大学士贺逢圣，在武昌为张献忠所逼，投墩子湖死。自夏至秋，有神托梦于湖之居民某云：“我奉上帝命，守贺相尸殊苦，汝可捞而视之，有黑子在其左手者是也。”某觉而异之，俟于湖，赫然尸出，乃殓而葬之。尸在水中百有七十日，面如生。

    　　○僵尸抱韦驮宿州李九者，贩布为生。路过霍山，天晚，店客满矣，不得已，宿佛庙中。

    　　漏下两鼓，睡已熟，梦韦驮神抚其背曰：“急起，急起，大难至矣！躲我身后，可以救你。”李惊醒，踉跄而起。见床后厝棺砉然有声，走出一尸，遍身白毛，如反穿银鼠套者，面上皆满，两眼深黑，中有绿眼，光闪闪然，直来扑李。李奔上佛柜，躲韦驮神背后。僵尸伸两臂抱韦驮神而口咬之，嗒嗒有声。李大呼，群僧皆起，持棍点火把来。僵尸逃入棺中，棺合如故。

    Explanation

    779

    Translation

    During the late Ming dynasty, Grand Councillor He Fengsheng, in Wuchang, was forced by Zhang Xianzhong to jump into Dunszi Lake and die. From summer to autumn, a deity appeared in a dream to a certain resident of the lake, saying, "I have been ordered by the Heavenly Emperor to guard Grand Councillor He's body, which has been very difficult. You can go and look for it; the one with a black spot on his left hand is the correct one." The resident woke up and found this strange, so he waited by the lake. Suddenly, the body appeared, and he then had it embalmed and buried. The body had remained in the water for 170 days and its face was still as if it were alive.

    In another incident, a zombie attacked the Buddhist deity Vajrapani. Li Jiu, a cloth merchant from Suozhou, was traveling through Huoshan. It was late, and all the inns were full, so he had no choice but to stay in a Buddhist temple. Just after two drums had struck, he was deeply asleep when he dreamed that Vajrapani touched his back and said, "Hurry up, hurry up, a great calamity is coming! Hide behind me, and I can save you." Li Jiu woke up in a panic and stumbled out of bed. He saw a sudden, sharp sound coming from the coffin behind his bed, and a corpse emerged, covered in white hair like a silver squirrel turned inside out, with its face completely covered and two deep black eyes with a green glow. The corpse charged straight at Li Jiu. Li Jiu ran to the Buddha cabinet and hid behind Vajrapani. The zombie stretched out its arms to embrace Vajrapani and bit him, making a dull, repetitive sound. Li Jiu shouted, and all the monks woke up, holding sticks and torches. The zombie retreated into the coffin, and the lid closed as if nothing had happened.

    780

    次日，见韦驮神被僵尸损坏，所持杵折为三段，方知僵尸力猛如此。群僧报官，焚其棺。李感韦驮之恩，为塑像装金焉。

    　　○穷鬼祟人富鬼不崇人西湖德生庵后门外厝棺千余，堆积如山。余往作寓，问庵僧：“此地尝有鬼祟否？”僧曰：“此间皆富鬼，终年平静。”余曰：“城中那得有如此许多富人？

    　　焉能有如此许多富鬼？且久攒不葬，不富可知。“僧曰：”所谓富者，非指其生前而言也，凡死后有酒食祭祀、纸钱烧化者，便谓之富鬼。此千余棺虽久攒不葬，增于每年四节必募缘作道场，设盂兰会烧纸钱千万，鬼皆醉饱，邪心不生。公不见世上人抢劫诈骗之事，皆起于饥寒。凡病人口中所说，目中所见，可有衣冠华美、相貌丰腴之鬼乎？凡作祟求祭者，大率皆蓬头历齿，蓝缕穷酸之鬼耳。“余甚是其言，果住月余，虽家僮婢子，当阴霾之夜，无闻鬼啸者。

    Explanation

    780
    The next day, I saw that the statue of the Bodhisattva Vajrapani had been damaged by a zombie, and the staff he held was broken into three pieces. This made me realize just how powerful the zombie was. The monks reported the incident to the authorities, who then burned the zombie's coffin. Li was grateful for the kindness of the Bodhisattva Vajrapani and had a new statue made and gilded.

○ Ghosts of the Poor Torment People, but Ghosts of the Wealthy Do Not

Behind the back door of the De Sheng Temple near West Lake, there were over a thousand coffins piled up like a mountain. I stayed there as a guest and asked the temple monk, "Have there ever been any ghosts tormenting people here?" The monk replied, "Here, all the ghosts are wealthy ghosts, and the place remains peaceful all year round." I said, "How could there be so many wealthy people in the city?

How could there be so many wealthy ghosts? And since they have not been buried for a long time, it is clear they are not wealthy." The monk said, "What we mean by 'wealthy' is not referring to their lives before death. Any ghost who receives offerings of food and drink, and paper money burned after death, is considered a wealthy ghost. These over a thousand coffins, though not buried for a long time, are given offerings every year during the four major festivals. They conduct religious ceremonies and hold Ullambana Festivals, burning millions of paper money. The ghosts are all well-fed and satisfied, so evil thoughts do not arise. Have you not seen that in the world, people commit robbery and fraud because they are hungry and cold? Have you ever heard of a ghost with beautiful attire and a plump appearance? Those who cause mischief and seek offerings are mostly ghosts with shabby clothes, missing teeth, and poor, destitute spirits." I agreed with his words and stayed there for over a month. Even the household servants and maids did not hear any ghostly howling on gloomy nights.
    781

    ○雷神火剑乾隆戊申八月，河库道司马公遣两仆还家，一名祝升，年三十；一名寿子，年十六。二人雇船行至宝应刘家堡地方，天渐阴晦，寿子忽喜曰：“前面搭台喝戏，有金盔金甲神在场上，甚热闹。”旁人皆不见，笑曰：“前面河水滔滔，绝无戏台。汝孩子气，一心想看戏耶？”祝升同一篙工争曰：“果然有戏，诸君何独不见？”言未毕，有恶见吹折桅杆，满船昏黑，震雷一声，击杀寿子、祝升于船头，并杀篙工于船尾。雷雨稍定，舱中人大惊，泊船报县，请官相尸。

    Explanation

    781

    In the month of August in the year 1768 during the reign of the Qing Emperor Qianlong, the official in charge of river and reservoir affairs, the River and Reservoir Censorate, sent two servants back home. One was named Zhu Sheng, aged thirty; the other was named Shouzi, aged sixteen. The two men hired a boat and traveled to the Dujia Fort area in Baoying. As the sky gradually became overcast and gloomy, Shouzi suddenly exclaimed with joy, "There’s a stage set up ahead for a play, with a god wearing a golden helmet and armor on the field—it’s very lively!" The others around him saw nothing and laughed, saying, "Ahead there is only the rushing river, and no stage. Are you still a child, wanting to see a play so badly?" Zhu Sheng and the boatman argued, "There really is a play! Why can’t you see it?" Before they finished speaking, a frightening wind blew down the mast, and the entire boat was plunged into darkness. A deafening thunderclap struck, killing Shouzi and Zhu Sheng at the bow of the boat, and also killing the boatman at the stern. When the thunder and rain subsided slightly, the people in the cabin were greatly alarmed. They anchored the boat and reported the incident to the county, requesting an official to examine the bodies.

    782

    俄而祝升苏曰：“我与寿子正在船看戏，忽见前面万道金光，不见河路，地上俱铺雪白银砖。台上宫殿巍峨，中坐冕旒神，方面白须，旁立金盔金甲者数十。

    　　一金甲神向冕旒者鞠躬白事，语不可辨，但见冕旒神点首，金甲者遂趋出，上船擒我与寿子、篙工三人去跪殿上。抽腰下挂剑，红光照耀，将寿子头上横穿过去，又将篙工胸上横穿过去。我看见光景不好，侧身要逃，被别个金甲神扯住，用金瓜锤当头一打，我遂昏绝，以后便不知人事了。“

    Explanation

    782

    Soon after, the shaman suddenly regained consciousness and said, "Master Shouzi and I were on the boat watching a play when we suddenly saw countless rays of golden light in front of us, obscuring the river path. The ground was covered with snow-white silver bricks. A majestic palace stood on the stage, with a crowned deity seated in the center, having a square face and white beard. Dozens of figures stood beside him, wearing golden helmets and armor.

    One of the golden-armored deities bowed to the crowned deity and reported something. The words were indistinguishable, but the crowned deity nodded. The golden-armored deity then hurried out and came aboard the boat, taking me, Master Shouzi, and the boatman to kneel before the throne. He drew a sword hanging at his waist, and its red light shone brightly, thrusting it horizontally through Master Shouzi's head and then through the boatman's chest. I saw the situation was dire and tried to escape by turning sideways, but another golden-armored deity grabbed me and struck me on the head with a golden mace. I then fainted and lost consciousness afterward."

    783

    县官万公来验，即取此段口供，申详立案。验寿子、篙工两尸，果有细眼穿喉胸二处，买棺殓埋。因祝尚活，在船中不便医治，乃撑船至大王庙停泊，扛祝升入庙。祝望见大王，惊曰：“刚才上坐者，即此神也。”又旁睨曰：“诸位神道，都在殿上，何不救救我耶？”言毕，食粥一碗，仍气绝矣。

    　　是年冬，余同刘霞裳游沐阳，过刘家堡，泊船大王庙。往看诸神，皆寻常金装木偶，无他灵异。刘向神问：“寿子年幼，有何恶而犯天诛？”神不答。余笑曰：“痴秀才！此所谓民可使由之，不可使知之耳！幽明一理，何必对神饶舌耶？”

    　　○水精孝廉广东纪孝廉，童时误入蛇腹。黑无所见，但闻腥气。扪其壁，滑氵达不可近。

    783

    The local official, Mr. Wan, came to investigate the case. He immediately took the confession and formally reported and recorded it. Upon examining the bodies of the boatman and the boatman's assistant, he found two puncture wounds in the throat and chest. He bought a coffin and buried them. Since Zhu was still alive, it was inconvenient to treat him on the boat, so they rowed the boat to the Great King Temple and anchored it. They carried Zhu into the temple. Zhu saw the Great King and was startled, saying, "The person who was sitting there just now is this deity." He then glanced around and said, "All the deities are on the dais, why didn't you save me?" After speaking, he ate a bowl of porridge and then died.

    That winter, I traveled with Liu Xichang to Muyang, passing through Liujiabu and anchoring the boat at the Great King Temple. We went to see the various deities, who were all ordinary gold-decorated wooden statues. Liu asked the deity, "The boatman was young, what evil did he commit to deserve divine punishment?" The deity did not answer. I laughed and said, "Foolish scholar! This is what is meant by 'the people can be made to follow, but not to understand.' The same principle applies to the visible and invisible worlds. Why talk to the gods?"

    ○ Scholar-official of Guangdong, Jin Xianlian, as a child mistakenly entered the belly of a snake. He saw nothing but darkness and could only smell the stench. Touching the walls, he found them smooth and slippery, making it impossible to approach.

    784

    幸身边有小刀，因挖其壁。渐见微明，就明钻出，困卧于地。邻人见之，携归其家。是日，村郊三十里外有大蛇死焉。孝廉为毒气所伤，通身皮脱如水精，肠胃皆见，从幼至壮不改。乡举后，同年皆见之，呼为“水精孝廉”。

    　　○水鬼移家王某居杭州城之东园，地多鱼池，东西相接，中隔一埂。季夏日正午，立埂上乘凉，见东池忽有一道浮沤，阔尺许，似潮涌来而氵砉氵砉有声。及近埂岸，有尺半长一段黑气从东池飞入西池而寂，鼻中作羊膻气。顺问之邻人，云：“是水鬼移家也。”

    　　○负妻之报杭城仙林桥徐松年，开铜店。年三十二，骤得瘵疾。越数月，疾渐剧，其妻泣谓曰：“我有两儿俱幼，君或不讳，我不能抚，我愿祷于神，以寿借君。君当抚儿，待其长娶媳，可以成家，君不必再娶矣。”夫许之，妇投词于城隍，再祷于家神，妇疾渐作，夫疾渐瘳，浃岁而卒。

    Explanation

    784

    Translation

    Fortunately, he had a small knife nearby, so he dug into the wall. Gradually, a faint light appeared, and he emerged into the light, collapsing exhausted on the ground. A neighbor saw him and took him home. On that day, thirty miles outside the village, a large snake died there. The scholar-official was injured by the poisonous gas, and his entire body's skin peeled off like crystal, revealing his intestines. From childhood to adulthood, the condition did not change. After passing the local examination, his peers all saw him and called him "Water Crystal Scholar."

    ○ Water Ghosts Moving Their Home

    Wang lived in the East Garden of Hangzhou City, where there were many fish ponds, east and west connected by a dike. On a summer afternoon at noon, standing on the dike to cool off, he saw a floating foam in the eastern pond, about a foot wide, like a tide surging with a sound. When it approached the dike, a black vapor about a foot and a half long flew from the eastern pond to the western pond and disappeared, with a smell of mutton in the nose. He asked the neighbors, who said, "It is the water ghosts moving their home."

    ○ The Sacrifice of a Wife

    Xu Songnian, who opened a copper shop in Xianlin Bridge, Hangzhou City, was thirty-two years old when he suddenly contracted tuberculosis. After several months, the disease worsened, and his wife cried and said, "I have two young sons. If you do not survive, I cannot take care of them. I wish to pray to the gods to give me my life in exchange for yours. You should take care of the children, wait until they grow up and marry, and you can establish a family. You do not need to remarry." The husband agreed, and the wife submitted a petition to the City God and prayed again to the family gods. The wife's disease gradually worsened, while the husband's disease gradually improved. She died after a year.

    785

    松年竟违其言，续娶曹氏。合卺之夕，床褥间夹一冷人，不许新郎交接，新妇惊起，盖前妻附魂于从婢以闹之也。口中痛责其夫，共寝五六月，斋祷不灵，松年仍以瘵殁。

    　　○四小龟扛一大龟而行杭城横塘镇有孤静庵，一老僧焚修其后殿。见有四小龟共扛一大龟，径尺许，循墙依槛，团团而走，回环不止。老僧唪经毕，清罄一声，龟方敛迹。数年后，老僧圆寂，龟亦不复再见。雍正年事。

    　　○鬼送汤圆杭州王生绳玉，课蒙于横良钟氏。钟第三子字有条，年已二十，自瞒其年，称十六，问：“弟子此时尚可读书否？”王答以：“果能志坚，书何不可读耶？”

    Explanation

    785

    Song Nian ultimately violated his words and remarried the Cao family. On the wedding night, a cold person was found between the bedding, preventing the groom from interacting with the bride. The new bride was startled and woke up, as it turned out that the former wife's soul had attached itself to a maidservant to cause trouble. The ghost verbally scolded her husband harshly, and they cohabited for five or six months. Prayers and fasting were ineffective, and Song Nian still died of consumption.

    In Hangzhou's Hengtang Town, there is a hermitage called Gushi Temple. An old monk practiced Buddhism in the rear hall. He saw four small turtles carrying a large turtle, about a foot in diameter, moving along the wall and railing, circling endlessly. When the old monk finished reciting sutras and struck the bell, the turtles disappeared. Several years later, the old monk passed away, and the turtles were never seen again. This was during the reign of Emperor Yongzheng.

    A Ghost Sends Tangyuan (Glutinous Rice Balls)

    In Hangzhou, Mr. Wang Shengyuan, who taught children at the Zong family in Hengliang, had a student named Zhong, the third son of the family. Zhong was actually twenty years old but hid his age, claiming to be sixteen. He asked, "Is it still possible for me to study now?" Mr. Wang replied, "If you can maintain your resolve, why can't you study books?"

    786

    有条大喜，讽诵不辍。其父俗贾也，不以为然，迫之赴吴门贸易。有条郁郁而往，日赴市廛，夜仍阖户，隐身帷帐中，私自钻研。满房贴“岁不我与”四字。越四月，疾亟而归。时近重九，抵家遂卒。柩停于家。

    　　次年七夕前一日，王睡梦中，闻内屋启门声，步至书舍排闼入。见有条左手秉烛，右手执碗，碗内腾腾热气，至王床前，启帐笑曰：“先生肚饥耶？特送点心来。”王坐起接其碗，见内浮汤圆四个，兼有铜铫。遂忘其为鬼，竟挑食之。

    　　及三而饱，尚留其一，随手交还有条，有条复为下帐闭门而去。

    　　王忽大悟，惊曰：“有条殁已周岁，今夕胡为而来？”方举念间，体中寒热顿作，自夜及明，循环三次。惫甚，不能起，乃呼舆归家。家中拦门鬼以百十计，男女大小他乡本郡之鬼无所不有，大约鸠形鹄面披衣曳履之穷鬼为最多，恰无怪状奇形之可怖者。

    Explanation

    786

    Yǒu Tiáo was greatly delighted and recited without interruption. His father, a common merchant, did not think much of this and urged him to go to Wumen for trade. Yǒu Tiáo went reluctantly, visiting the market every day and closing the door at night, studying in secret behind curtains. He covered the walls with the phrase "Time does not wait for me." After four months, he fell seriously ill and returned home. It was close to the Double Ninth Festival when he arrived home and passed away. The coffin remained at home.

    The following year, on the day before the Qixi Festival, while asleep, Wang heard the sound of a door opening in an inner room. He walked to the study and pushed open the door to enter. He saw Yǒu Tiáo holding a candle in one hand and a bowl in the other, with steam rising from the bowl. Yǒu Tiáo approached Wang's bed, lifted the curtain, and smiled, saying, "Are you hungry, Master? I’ve brought some snacks for you." Wang sat up, took the bowl, and saw four floating glutinous rice balls, along with a small copper pot. He forgot that Yǒu Tiáo was a ghost and began to eat them.

    After eating three, he still had one left, which he handed back to Yǒu Tiáo. Yǒu Tiáo then lowered the curtain, closed the door, and left.

    Wang suddenly realized, shocked, "Yǒu Tiáo has been dead for a year. Why is he here tonight?" Just as he was thinking this, he felt a sudden chill and fever. The illness recurred three times, from night until dawn. Exhausted, he could not move and called for a carriage to return home. At home, there were hundreds of spirits blocking the door—men, women, children, and ghosts from different places. Most were poor and disheveled, but none had terrifying or monstrous appearances.

    787

    王有妹嫁翟家，来视兄疾，鬼在病人口中云：“汝是郑家桥翟家娘子，亦来此耶！”王弟访之，果翟邻家结发之妻新缢死者也。

    　　王父为延医投药，掖起病人命服，众鬼挤肩揎背，持其手，使不得服。如是者再四，王心厌焉，竟违父命，终不饮药。次晨，另延一医诊视，问：“曾投药否？”父语以故，医索方视之，惊曰：“幸而未饮，否则今日不能出声矣！”另立一方，鬼不复来夺。从此众鬼阗门塞屋，日掩天光，夜蔽灯火，或坐或立，或言或笑，聚集十余日。家中持经放焰口、毫无效验。一女鬼呼曰：“汝家该延老僧宏道来，我辈便去。”如其言，往请宏道。甫到门，众鬼轰然散矣。病亦渐安。

    Explanation

    787

    The nobleman had a sister who was married to the Zhai family. She came to visit her brother's illness, and a ghost in the patient's mouth said, "Are you the wife of the Zhai family from Zhengjia Bridge?" The nobleman's younger brother went to investigate, and it turned out to be the recently hanged wife of the Zhai family's neighbor, who had been married since childhood.

    The nobleman's father invited a doctor to prescribe medicine and helped the patient put on his official robe. The ghosts crowded around, pushing and shoving, holding his hands to prevent him from taking the medicine. This happened four times. The nobleman's heart grew weary, and he ultimately disobeyed his father's command, refusing to take the medicine.

    The next morning, another doctor was invited to examine him. The doctor asked if any medicine had been given, and the father explained the reason. The doctor looked at the prescription and exclaimed in surprise: "Fortunately, he hasn't taken it; otherwise, he wouldn't be able to speak today." The doctor prescribed a new formula, and the ghosts no longer came to prevent him from taking it.

    From then on, the ghosts crowded the door and filled the house, blocking out the sunlight during the day and the lights at night, sitting, standing, talking, or laughing, gathering for more than ten days. The family held rituals with scriptures and lit incense, but nothing worked. A female ghost called out: "Your family should invite the old monk Hongdao; we will leave." As she said, they went to invite Master Hongdao. As soon as he arrived at the door, the ghosts suddenly dispersed. The patient's condition gradually improved.

    788

    袁子曰：同是念经放焰口，而有验有不验，此之谓有治人，无治法也。不知鬼食之不宜人食，而以奉其先生，此之谓愚忠愚孝也。

    　　○忠恕二字一笔写黄照，歙县人，原任福山同知，罢官后主讲韶州书院。尝书“忠恕”二大字，勒石讲堂，款落“新安后学某敬书”。

    　　忽一日，梦黑衣者二人执灯至曰：“奉命召汝。”黄即随往。至一处，历阶而升，闻呼曰：“止。”黄即立定，黑衣人分左右立，中隔一层白云。闻有人曰：“汝为大清官员，何以生今反古，书‘忠恕’二字，款落‘新安’？宜速改正。”

    Explanation

    788

    Yuan Zi said: Although the same scripture is recited and the same ritual of feeding the hungry ghosts is performed, some are effective while others are not. This is called having capable people but no fixed rules. Not knowing that food for ghosts is unsuitable for humans, yet to offer it to their master, this is called foolish loyalty and filial piety.

    ○ The two characters "loyalty" and "forgiveness" written by Huang Xizhao, a native of She County, who formerly served as the magistrate of Fushan, after being dismissed from office, became the headmaster of Shaoguan Academy. He once wrote the two large characters "loyalty" and "forgiveness" and had them engraved on the stone in the lecture hall, with the inscription "Respectfully written by a student of New An, someone."

    One day, he suddenly dreamed that two black-clad figures carrying lamps came and said, "We have been ordered to summon you." Huang followed them immediately. They arrived at a place, climbed the steps, and heard someone calling, "Stop." Huang stood still, and the black-clad figures stood on either side, with a layer of white clouds in between. Someone said, "You are an official of the Great Qing Dynasty. Why do you live in the present but act like the ancients, writing the two characters 'loyalty' and 'forgiveness,' with the inscription 'New An'? You should quickly correct this."

    789

    黄惊醒，急将前所刻“新安”二字改写“歙县”。

    　　越数日，又梦前黑衣人引至原处，仍闻云中人语曰：“汝改书勒石固善，但亦知‘忠恕’二字之义是一气读否？汝可于古帖中求之。”黄醒，检阅十七帖，见“忠恕”二字行书乃是“忠心如一”四字，恍然大悟。复将壁间石刻毁去，仿贴中行书，另写勒石。今现存韶州书院。

    　　○土雨乾隆十四年，李元叔秀才自京就馆沈阳，越明年夏四月，回京师，渡辽水。

    　　是日往北台子，站路过远，昏黑不得抵宿。时乘四套车投一深林中，闻树叶上蔌蔌作雨声，沾洒衣上，视之皆土也。未几，四马攒蹄，退后不敢前。骡脚大呼曰：“有鬼蹲踞当道，车拉不动！”乃取开路铁锄抓土撒之，口中作咒语，车始得行。

    Explanation

    789

    Huang woke up in a start, quickly changing the previously carved "Xian'an" to "She County."

    After several days, he dreamed again of the black-clothed person leading him to the original place, and he still heard the voice of someone in the clouds say, "You have done well to change and carve it into stone, but do you also know if the meaning of the two characters 'zhong' (loyalty) and 'shu' (forgiveness) is meant to be read together as one phrase? You can find the answer in ancient calligraphy." Huang woke up and reviewed the Seventeenth Postscript, seeing that the running script for 'zhong' and 'shu' was actually the four characters 'zhongxin ruyi' (loyalty and unity). He suddenly realized the truth. He then destroyed the stone carving on the wall, copied the running script from the postscript, and carved it anew. It now exists in the Shaozhou Academy.

    In the fourteenth year of Qianlong, Scholar Li Yuanshu from Beijing came to teach in Shenyang. The following summer, he returned to the capital and crossed the Liao River.

    That day, he was heading to Beitaizi, but the journey was too far and it was already dark, so he could not reach his destination. At that time, he was traveling in a four-horse carriage and stopped in a deep forest. He heard the sound of raindrops on the leaves, splattering onto his clothes, and upon looking, he saw that it was all soil. Soon after, the four horses gathered their hooves, retreating backward and refusing to move forward. The horseman shouted, "There is a ghost squatting in the road, and the carriage cannot be pulled!" They then took an iron hoe to scatter the earth, muttering incantations, and the carriage finally moved forward.

    This translation aims to capture the essence of the original text while ensuring it reads naturally in English. It maintains the narrative flow and the cultural and historical context, and handles idiomatic expressions and proper nouns consistently.

    790

    不数步，见一火，茶杯大，傍车而行，其光上下远近不定，照里许而灭。土人云：“凡鬼物出，皆先有土雨。”

    　　○降庙粤西有降庙之说。每村中有总管庙，所塑之像，美丑少壮不同。有学降庙法者，法将成，则至庙中卜卦降神。初至，插一剑于庙门之中，神降则拔剑而回；神不降，则用脚踢倒之。能随足而起则生，如不起，则为神诛矣。

    　　其法将一碗净水写一“井”字圈绕之，地上亦写一“井”字圈绕之，八仙桌中间亦写一“井”字圈绕之，召童子四人，手上各写一“走”字圈绕之，将桌面反对碗口之上，四童以指抬桌，其人口念咒云：“天也转，地也转，左叫左转，右叫右转，太上老君急急如令转。若还不转，铜叉叉转，铁叉叉转。若再不转，土地、城隍代转。”唱毕，桌子便转，然后请药方，无不验者。

    Explanation

    790

    Not many steps away, a fire was seen, the size of a teacup, moving alongside a vehicle. Its light flickered, appearing at different heights and distances, illuminating for a mile or so before extinguishing. The locals said, "Whenever ghostly apparitions appear, there is always a prior rain of earth."

    ○ Invoking Spirits in Temples

    In Guangxi, there is a practice known as "invoking spirits in temples." Each village has a temple dedicated to a local deity, known as a "total commander temple." The statues within these temples vary in appearance, being beautiful or ugly, young or old. Those who learn the method of invoking spirits in temples, when nearly complete, go to the temple to cast a divination and invoke the spirit. Upon arrival, they insert a sword into the temple door. If the spirit descends, they draw the sword and return; if the spirit does not descend, they kick the sword over with their foot. If the sword rises with the foot, it is a sign of life; if it does not rise, it is a sign of being punished by the spirit.

    The method involves writing a "well" character in a circle around a bowl of clean water, writing a "well" character in a circle on the ground, and writing a "well" character in a circle in the middle of an eight-tray table. Four young boys are summoned, and a "walk" character is written in a circle on each boy's hand. The table is placed upside down over the bowl, and the four boys lift the table with their fingers. They recite a spell with their mouths: "The sky turns, the earth turns. Call left to turn left, call right to turn right. Lord Lao Tzu, turn quickly as commanded. If it still does not turn, use copper forks to turn, use iron forks to turn. If it still does not turn, the local land god and city god will turn it." After singing, the table turns, and then they ask for a prescription, which is always effective.

    791

    ○陇西城隍神是美少年康熙间，陇西城隍塑黑面而髯者，貌颇威严，忽于乾隆间改塑像为美少年。

    　　或问庵僧，僧曰：“闻之长老云，雍正七年，有谢某者，年甫二十，从其师在庙读书。夜间先生出外，谢步月吟诗。见一人来祷，乃隐于神后伺之，闻其祝云：”今夜若偷物有获，必具三牲来献。“方知是贼也。心疑神乃聪明正直之人，岂可以牲牢动乎？次日，贼竟来还愿，生大不平，作文责之。神夜托梦于其师，将降生祸。师醒后问生，生抵赖。师怒，搜其箧，竟有责神之稿，怒而焚之。

    Explanation

    791

    The Deity of Longxi as a Handsome Young Man

    During the reign of Emperor Kangxi, the deity of Longxi was depicted with a black face and a beard, having a rather stern appearance. Suddenly, during the reign of Emperor Qianlong, the statue was changed to that of a handsome young man.

    Someone asked a temple monk about this change, and the monk replied: "I heard from the elder monks that in the seventh year of Emperor Yongzheng's reign, there was a young man named Xie, who was just twenty years old. He was studying in the temple under his teacher. One night, when his teacher went out, Xie strolled under the moonlight and recited poetry. He saw someone come to pray and hid behind the deity to observe. He heard the person pray, saying: 'If I steal something tonight and get it, I will definitely offer three sacrificial animals.' Realizing that the person was a thief, Xie doubted that the deity, being a wise and upright person, could be moved by such offerings. The next day, the thief came to fulfill his vow, which greatly aggrieved Xie. He wrote an essay to criticize the thief. That night, the deity sent a dream to his teacher, warning that disaster would befall him. Upon waking, the teacher asked Xie about it, but Xie denied it. The teacher became angry and searched his box, finding a draft criticizing the deity. In anger, the teacher burned it.

    792

    是夜，神踉跄而至曰：“我来告你弟子不敬神明，将降以祸，原不过吓吓他。

    　　你竟将他文稿烧化，被行路神上奏东岳，登时将我革职拿问。一面将此城隍之位奏明上帝，即将汝弟子补缺矣。“欷而退。

    　　未三日，少年卒。庙中人闻呼驺声，云是新城隍到任。嗣后，塑像者易黑胡之貌为美少年。

    　　○城隍赤身求衣张观察挺修湖城隍庙，以檀香雕三丈法身，绣衮为袍衣之，供奉三日矣。忽夜梦一巨人，头带平天冠，而身无衣服，赤两股直立帐前。公惊醒心动，急欲赴庙查看，而庙中道士已来报神衣被窃矣。乃为另制，且命拿贼云。

    Explanation

    792

    Translation

    On that night, a ghost appeared, staggering and said, "I come to inform you that your disciple disrespects the gods, and I will bring disaster upon him. Originally, I just wanted to scare him.

    You have burned his writings, and the road god reported this to the Eastern Peak. Immediately, they dismissed me and questioned me. They also reported the position of city god to the Supreme Deity, and your disciple will be appointed to fill the vacancy." With a sigh, he withdrew.

    Within three days, the young man died. The people in the temple heard the sound of the chariot's attendants, saying that the new city god had taken office. Thereafter, the sculptor changed the image from a black-bearded man to a handsome young man.

    The observer suddenly dreamed of a giant, wearing a flat-topped crown, but naked except for his upper body, standing upright in front of the tent. He awoke in a state of shock and alarm, hurrying to the temple to check, but the temple's Taoist priest had already come to report that the god's robe had been stolen. So he ordered another one made, and instructed the thief to be caught.

    Final Translation

    On that night, a ghost appeared, staggering and said, "I come to inform you that your disciple disrespects the gods, and I will bring disaster upon him. Originally, I just wanted to scare him.

    You have burned his writings, and the road god reported this to the Eastern Peak. Immediately, they dismissed me and questioned me. They also reported the position of city god to the Supreme Deity, and your disciple will be appointed to fill the vacancy." With a sigh, he withdrew.

    Within three days, the young man died. The people in the temple heard the sound of the chariot's attendants, saying that the new city god had taken office. Thereafter, the sculptor changed the image from a black-bearded man to a handsome young man.

    The observer suddenly dreamed of a giant, wearing a flat-topped crown, but naked except for his upper body, standing upright in front of the tent. He awoke in a state of shock and alarm, hurrying to the temple to check, but the temple's Taoist priest had already come to report that the god's robe had been stolen. So he ordered another one made, and instructed the thief to be caught.

    793

    ○水怪吹气杭州程志章由潮州过黄岗，渡海汊。半渡，开大风，有黑气冲起，中有一人浑身漆黑，惟两眼眶及嘴唇其白如粉，坐船头上以气吹舟中人。舟人共十三人，顷刻貌尽变黑，与之相似，其不变者三人而已。少顷，黑气散，怪亦不见。开船，风浪大作，舟覆水中，死者十人，皆变色者也，其不变色之三人独免。

    　　○坛响杭州北门外三清院林道士能擒妖，在兴化收妖坛中，放三清神座下。逾年，钱生袖海与友孔传经饯行，上南京乡试，本醉后坛云：“我友中则坛响。”果响一声。客散，生夜看书，见白衣人坐槛上与之拱手。生用界尺打之，抚掌大笑而退。是年孔君果中。

    　　○贞女诉冤陆作梅作浔州太守，有和奸自尽一案，县详到府，文卷在案上，将批如详核转矣。其晚，幕友房中起大风，宛然一女子，立而不言，五更始去。幕友告太守，适太守奉调上省，谓其子曰：“汝胆大，今晚可至幕友房伺之。”

    Explanation

    793

    ○ Water Monster Blows Gas

    Cheng Zhizhang from Hangzhou traveled from Chaozhou through Huanggang and crossed a sea channel. Halfway across, a strong wind arose, and a dark cloud rose from the sea. In the midst of the cloud, there was a person who was entirely blackened, with only the eye sockets and lips as white as powder, sitting on the bow of the ship and exhaling a gas that affected the passengers. There were thirteen people on the boat, and in an instant, their faces turned completely black, resembling the figure. Only three people remained unchanged. Shortly after, the dark cloud dissipated, and the figure disappeared. The boat set sail again, but the wind and waves grew fierce, causing the boat to capsize. Ten people died, all of whom had changed color, while the three who remained unchanged survived.

    ○ Altar Resonance

    In the Sanqing Temple located north of Hangzhou, Taoist priest Lin was skilled at capturing demons. He had captured a demon in a ritual circle in Xinghua and placed it under the altar of the Three Pure Ones. A year later, Qian Sheng from Xiuhai bid farewell to his friend Kong Chuanjing, who was heading to Nanjing for the provincial examination. After drinking, Qian made a remark about the altar: "If my friend passes the exam, the altar will make a sound." As predicted, the altar made a sound. After the guests left, Qian read at night and saw a white-clad figure sitting on a railing, bowing to him. Qian hit the figure with a ruler, and the figure clapped and laughed before leaving. That year, Kong Chuanjing indeed passed the exam.

    ○ Virtuous Woman Seeks Justice

    Lu Zuomei served as the governor of Xunzhou. There was a case involving a woman who committed suicide due to adultery. The case was reported to the prefecture, and the case file was on the desk, ready to be reviewed and forwarded. That evening, a strong wind arose in the chamber of a retainer, and a woman-like figure stood silently. The figure left at the fifth watch of the night. The retainer informed the governor, who was summoned to the provincial capital. The governor told his son, "You are bold; tonight, you may go to the retainer's chamber to investigate the matter."

    794

    晚间，公子遵父命，宿幕友书房。果如前风起，幕友又见此女，即告公子，而公子无见也，因大声问曰：“汝何为者？”女曰：“吾即几上案中人也，因拒奸至死。父母受贿，证成和奸，污我名节。曩诉之县，县亦受贿，不为申理，所以来此诉冤。”公子唯唯，即以共言写家信驰告太守。太守从省归，适经是县，因札致幕友，将原案发回本县。

    　　未几，县令来迎。太守不宿公馆，先往在隍庙行香，谓令曰：“吾访闻前奸案事有冤，信乎？”县据其父母口供，抗词请质。太守无奈何，即宿城隍庙中，传犯人及邻证人等于大殿后陪宿，阴伏人于殿后察之。至三更余，邻证等各自言语，有骂其父母之无良，怜其女之贞烈者，听者取笔书之。

    Explanation

    794

    In the evening, the young master followed his father's command and stayed overnight in the guest's study. As if the wind had risen before, the guest saw this woman again and immediately told the young master, but the young master did not see her. The guest then loudly asked, "Who are you?" The woman replied, "I am the person on the table. I died because I resisted adultery. My parents accepted bribes and testified to consensual adultery, tarnishing my reputation. I previously sued in the county, but the county also accepted bribes and did not investigate, so I came here to seek justice." The young master nodded and wrote a letter to the prefect, reporting the case. When the prefect returned from the provincial capital, he happened to pass through this county and sent a letter to the guest, ordering the original case to be sent back to the county for re-investigation.

    Not long after, the county official came to welcome the prefect. The prefect did not stay at the official residence but first went to the city god temple to worship. He told the county official, "I have heard that there was an injustice in the previous adultery case. Is that true?" The county official insisted on his parents' testimony and requested to be questioned. The prefect had no choice but to stay at the city god temple, summoning the accused, witnesses, and neighbors to accompany him overnight in the back of the temple. He secretly had people stationed in the back of the temple to observe them. After the third watch, the neighbors and witnesses began to speak among themselves, some cursing their parents for their wickedness and pitying the woman's loyalty and purity. The listeners took notes with pens.

    795

    至天明，先盘诘邻证，取夜间所书示之，俱服。遂以强奸致死定案。旌其女入节孝祠。

    　　○杨成龙成神处州太守杨成龙，性正直，作官五十年，颇有政声。壬寅春，余游天台，招余饮酒，历叙办山东数大案，有古循吏风，余许作传，以表章之。不料别后告老，就养于伊子深州署中，无疾而卒。先是，太守宰历城时，买沙板一副，置张秋僧舍。身亡后，其子文必欲遣人取归，然后入殓，以慰乃父之心。

    　　忽其幼孙某头晕仆地，旋起坐，厉声曰：“文，汝太糊涂！当此六月天，我尸在床，待从张秋取棺来，则吾尸坏矣。深州木材尽可用，何必远取？现在处州八来迎我作彼处城隍，我俟汝丧事小定，即往到任。我无他语，大凡人在世上，肯做好官，必有好报，汝紧记之。明年三月十四日，二孙所生之子，将来可以绍我之志，取名绍志可也。若葬我，当在唐务山中做癸丁山向。”幼孙言毕，沉沉睡去，俄而嬉戏如初。文悚然，一遵父命。

    Explanation

    795

    Until dawn, the neighbors were first interrogated as witnesses, and the notes written at night were shown to them; all confessed. The case was therefore settled as a rape resulting in death. Her daughter was honored by being placed in the Temple of Filial Piety and Chastity.

    The magistrate of Chuzhou, Yang Chenglong, was known for his integrity and had served as an official for fifty years, earning a good reputation for governance. In the spring of Renyin, I visited TianTai and was invited to drink by him. He recounted handling several major cases in Shandong, displaying the style of ancient upright officials. I agreed to write a biography to honor and commend him. Unexpectedly, after our parting, he retired and settled in the residence of his son in Shenzhou, where he died peacefully without illness.

    Previously, when the magistrate was governing Licheng, he bought a set of sandstone slabs and placed them in a temple in Zhangqiu. After his death, his son Jiwén insisted on sending someone to fetch them back for the burial, to comfort his father's spirit.

    Suddenly, his young grandson felt dizzy and fell to the ground, but soon got up and sat, speaking loudly: "Jiwén, you are too foolish! In this June, my body is in bed, waiting for someone to fetch the coffin from Zhangqiu, then my body will decay. The wood in Shenzhou is sufficient for use, why go far to fetch it? Now the officials from Chuzhou are coming to invite me to be their city god. I will wait until your funeral arrangements are somewhat settled, then I will go to take office. I have nothing else to say. Generally speaking, people in the world, those who are willing to be good officials will surely receive good rewards. You must remember this. Next year on March 14th, the son born to your second grandson will be able to carry on my aspirations; you can name him Shaozhi. If you bury me, you should bury me in Tangwu Mountain, facing the direction of Gui Ding." After the grandson finished speaking, he fell into a deep sleep and soon resumed playing as before. Jiwén was startled and followed his father's instructions strictly.

    796

    次年，果生绍志，月日无爽。

    　　○周仓赤脚相传东台白驹场关庙周仓赤脚，因当日关公在襄阳放水淹庞德时，周仓亲下江挖坑故也。戊申冬，余过东台，与刘霞裳入庙观之，果然赤脚，又见神座后有一木匣，长三尺许。相传不许人开，有某太守祭而开之，风雷立至。

    　　○张飞治河大学士嵇文敏公总督南河，将筑堤东岸。梦有兜鍪而短须者直入一揖，随即上坐曰：“某堤须筑某所，裁保无虞。若在此，不能成功。”嵇颔之。已而思其人状貌乃一武夫，言复椎鲁，何以公然与宰相抗礼？意颇不怿，叱咤而醒。次日上工，次过张桓侯庙，小住啜茶，上塑神像，宛然梦中人，乃命停工。

    　　○神佑不必贵人章观察家奴陈霞彩，居上元义直巷中，与其外妇同宿。夜闻风雨声，似震雷击物。初不介意，天明揭帐，则卧榻后山墙夜崩，榻之前后左右，皆砖堆数尺，惟留一榻不打坏。青衣青楼，亦得神佑如此。

    Explanation

    796

    The following year, indeed a child named Shaozhi was born, with the date being without error.

    In the temple of Guan Yu in the White Horse Town of Dongtai, there is a tradition that Zhou Cang had bare feet. This tradition states that Zhou Cang had bare feet because he personally went down to the river to dig a pit when Guan Yu flooded the area to defeat Pang De in Xiangyang. In the winter of the year Wu Shen, I passed through Dongtai and went into the temple with Liu Xiacang to observe it. Indeed, he had bare feet, and we also saw a wooden box behind the deity's seat, about three feet long. It is said that no one is allowed to open it. A certain governor opened it during a sacrifice, and immediately a storm and thunder came.

    The university scholar Ji Wenmin, who was the governor of the southern river region, was about to build a dike on the eastern bank. He dreamed of a man wearing a helmet and with a short beard who entered directly and bowed. The man then sat down and said, "The dike must be built at a certain place to ensure there will be no trouble. If it were built here, it would not succeed." Ji nodded. Later, he thought the man's appearance was that of a warrior, and his speech was simple and unrefined. Why would he openly show respect to a prime minister? He was quite displeased, shouted, and woke up. The next day, he went to work and passed by the Zhang Huanhou Temple. He stopped briefly to drink tea, and the statue of the deity on the upper part was exactly like the man in his dream. He then ordered the work to stop.

    A servant named Chen Xiacai, who was under the supervision of Zhang Guancha, lived in the Yizhi Lane of Shangyuan. He slept with his concubine. At night, he heard the sound of wind and rain, which sounded like thunder hitting something. Initially, he did not pay attention. When morning came, he lifted the curtain and saw that the mountain wall behind his bed had collapsed at night. The bricks were piled several feet high all around the bed, but only the bed was left undamaged. The green-robed woman in the green building also received divine protection in this way.

    797

    ○成神不必贤人李海仲秀才，秋试京师，在苏州雇鸭嘴船。行至淮上，见舱前来王某求附舟，旧时邻也，因与同行。

    　　洎晚，王笑问：“君胆大否？”秀才愕然，漫应曰：“大。”王曰：“惧君生畏，故以胆问。君既胆大，我不得不以实告。我非人，乃鬼也。我别君六年矣，前年岁荒，为饥寒所迫，掘坟盗财，被捕拿获，罪已斩决。今作鬼依旧饥寒，故往京中索逋，仗君乞带。”李问：“往索何人之债？”曰：“汪某。渠作刑部司官，许拟斩文书到部时为驳减等，故馈以五百金。不料渠全无照应，终不能保全性命，故往祟之。”汪某者，李戚也。李大骇，晓之曰：“汝罪宜诛，部议不枉，汪舍亲不应骗汝财物，我带汝往，说明原委，令渠还汝，以解此仇可也。但汝已死，要银何用？”王曰：“我虽无用，尚有妻子在家，居与君邻。我索得后，可代我付之。”李唯唯。

    Explanation

    797

    A man named Li Hai Zhong, a scholar, went to take the imperial examination in the capital city. He hired a boat shaped like a duck's beak in Suzhou. As they traveled along the Huai River, he saw a man named Wang come to the boat and ask to join them. They were old neighbors, so Li agreed to let him come aboard.

    By evening, Wang smiled and asked, "Are you brave?" The scholar was startled and casually replied, "Yes." Wang said, "I asked about your courage because I feared you might be afraid. Since you are brave, I must tell you the truth. I am not a living person; I am a ghost. I have been separated from you for six years. Last year, during a famine, I was driven by hunger and cold to rob graves and steal property. I was caught and sentenced to death by beheading. Now, as a ghost, I still suffer from hunger and cold, so I am going to Beijing to demand payment and rely on you to take me along." Li asked, "Whom are you demanding payment from?" Wang replied, "Wang. He is an official in the Ministry of Punishment and promised to reduce my sentence when the execution order reached the ministry, so he gave me five hundred gold pieces. But he did nothing, and I could not save my life, so I am going to torment him." Wang is a relative of Li. Li was greatly startled and said to him, "Your crime deserves punishment, and the ministry's decision was not unjust. Your relative Wang should not have deceived you out of your property. I will take you to him, explain the situation, and make him pay you back to resolve this grudge. But since you are dead, what do you need the money for?" Wang said, "Although I am dead, I still have a wife and children at home, who live near you. After I get the money, you can pay it on my behalf." Li nodded in agreement.

    798

    又数日，将到京师，王请先行，曰：“我且到令亲处作祟，令渠求救无方，君再往说之，方肯听君。否则渠系贪财之人，君虽有言，渠不听也。”言毕不见。

    　　李入都觅寓，迟三日，往汪家，汪果得风狂之病，举家求神问卜，毫无效验。李方至门，病人口语曰：“汝家救星到矣！”家人争迎问李，李告以原委。汪妻初意要烧纸钱数万为偿，病人大笑曰：“以真钱还真钱，天下无此便宜之事！速兑五百金交李老爷，我便饶你。”其家如其言，汪病果愈。

    Explanation

    798

    After several more days, they were approaching the capital. The king requested to go first, saying, "I will go to your relative's place to cause trouble. He will have no way to seek help. You must go again and speak to him, then he will listen to you. Otherwise, he is a greedy person. Even if you speak, he will not listen." After saying this, he disappeared.

    Li entered the capital to find lodging. After three more days, he went to Wang's house. Wang indeed contracted a mental illness. The entire family sought divine help and consulted fortune-tellers, but there was no effect. When Li arrived, the patient said, "The savior of your family has arrived!" The family members rushed to welcome and ask Li. Li explained the whole story. Wang's wife initially wanted to burn tens of thousands of paper money as compensation. The patient laughed and said, "To return real money with real money, there is no such easy deal in the world. Quickly exchange five hundred gold coins and give them to Mr. Li. Then I will spare you." His family followed his words, and Wang's illness indeed recovered.

    799

    又数日，来李处催与同归，李不肯曰，“我未下场。”鬼曰：“君不中，不必下场也。”李不听。毕三场后，鬼又催归。李曰：“我要等榜。”鬼曰：“君不中，不必等榜也。”榜发无名，鬼来笑曰：“君此时可以归乎？”李惭沮，即日起身。鬼与同船，一切饮食，嗅而不吞，热物被嗅，登时冷矣。

    　　行至宿迁，鬼曰：“某村唱戏，盍往观乎？”李同至戏台下。看数出，鬼忽不见，但闻飞沙走石之声，李回船待之。天将黑，鬼盛服而来曰：“我不归矣，我在此做关帝矣。”李大骇曰：“妆何敢做关帝？”曰：“世上观音、关帝，皆鬼冒充。前日村中之戏，还关神愿也。所还愿之关神，比我更无赖，我故大怒，与决战而逐之。君独不闻飞沙石之声乎？”言毕拜谢而去。李替带五百金付其妻子。

    Explanation

    799

    Several days later, the ghost came to Li's place to urge him to return together. Li refused, saying, "I haven't taken the exams yet." The ghost replied, "You won't pass, so there's no need to take the exams." Li did not listen. After finishing the three exams, the ghost urged him to return again. Li said, "I want to wait for the results." The ghost said, "You won't pass, so there's no need to wait for the results." When the list was posted and his name was not on it, the ghost came laughing and said, "Can you return now?" Li felt ashamed and left immediately. The ghost traveled with him on the same boat, sniffing at everything but not swallowing it; hot objects were sniffed and instantly cooled.

    They arrived at Suqian, and the ghost said, "There's a village where they're performing opera. Shall we go and watch?" Li agreed and went to the stage. They watched several acts, and then the ghost suddenly disappeared, but they could hear the sound of flying sand and stones. Li returned to the boat to wait for him. As it was getting dark, the ghost came dressed in fine clothes and said, "I won't be returning. I'm becoming Guan Yu here." Li was greatly startled and said, "How dare you become Guan Yu?" The ghost replied, "In this world, Guan Yu and Kuan Yin are all ghosts pretending. The opera we saw yesterday in the village was to fulfill a wish for Guan Yu. The Guan Yu who fulfilled the wish was even more unscrupulous than me, so I got angry and fought him, driving him away. Haven't you heard the sound of flying sand and stones?" After saying this, the ghost bowed and left. Li left 500 gold coins for his wife and children.

    800

    ○中一目人康熙甲戌科，丹徒裴公之仙偕数友人入都会试。都中有善召乩者，延之问中否。仙至，判一“贵”字。众不解，再叩之，则曰：“皆判明矣。”榜发后，惟裴公中会元，馀皆落第。裴公眇一目，始悟向所判“贵”字，乃“中一目人”也。

    　　○女鬼告状镇江包某，年少美丰姿，娶室王氏。包世业贾，常与同事者往来闾巷。乾隆庚子秋日，偕数友为狎邪之游，日暮乃返。王氏方同一老妪入厨下治晚餐，闻叩门声，命老妪往启，见一少妇盛妆而入，直赴内室，问之不答。妪疑为姻戚，往告王氏。王急趋至室，则包在焉，因大笑老妪目昏，误认主人为妇人也。

    Explanation

    800

    A One-Eyed Person in the Kangxi Jiaxu Exam

    In the year 1694 during the reign of Emperor Kangxi, Mr. Pei from Dantu, known as Pei Xian, accompanied several friends to take the metropolitan examination in the capital. There was a renowned medium in the capital who was skilled in summoning spirits. They invited him to predict whether they would pass the exam. When the medium arrived, he wrote a single character "贵" (precious). The others did not understand its meaning and asked him again. He then said, "The outcome has been decided." After the results were announced, only Mr. Pei passed as the top scholar, while the rest failed. Mr. Pei was one-eyed, and only then did he realize that the character "贵" was a pun for "中一目人" (a one-eyed person).

    A Ghost Reporting a Case in Zhenjiang

    A young man named Bao from Zhenjiang, who was handsome and well-proportioned, married a woman named Wang. Bao's family had long been engaged in trade, and he often socialized with colleagues in the neighborhood. On an autumn day in 1760 during the reign of Emperor Qianlong, he went out with several friends for licentious activities and returned late in the evening. At that time, Wang was with an elderly woman preparing dinner in the kitchen. When they heard a knock on the door, Wang sent the old woman to open it. The old woman saw a young woman dressed elaborately enter and go straight into the inner room without responding to any questions. Suspecting she might be a relative or close friend, the old woman went to inform Wang. Wang hurriedly went to the room and found Bao there. She then laughed at the old woman's blurred vision, saying she had mistaken the owner for a woman.

    801

    忽包作女态裣衽而前，与王氏寒暄，且言：“包郎在某娼家饮酒时，我在门后专守，俟其出，方得同回。”王见其声音举动不类包郎，恐其疯狂，急召僮仆及邻里姻戚共来看视。包皆一一与见，礼仪周到，称谓无误，宛然一大家女也。

    　　或男子稍与相狎，鬼即怒曰：“我贞女也，谁近我，我即取其命！”众问：“你与包有何仇？”鬼曰：“妾与包实因恩爱成仇，曾控告于城隍神，前后共十九状，俱未见准。今又告于东岳帝君，始蒙批准，不日与包同往矣。”询其姓名，鬼曰：“我好人家儿女，姓名不可闻也。”“告包者何词？”鬼即连诵十九词，其词甚急，不能悉晓，大概控包负心，令彼无归之意。或又问：“汝即托包身而言，包今何在？”鬼微笑曰：“渠被我缚在城隍庙侧小屋中矣。”王氏泣拜，求放其夫，鬼不答。

    Explanation

    801

    Suddenly, Bao adopted a feminine demeanor, bowed deeply, and approached Wang, exchanging pleasantries with her and saying, "When Bao was drinking at a certain brothel, I waited for him in the doorway. Only after he left could we return together." Wang noticed that Bao's voice and movements were unlike those of her husband, fearing he had gone mad, she hastily summoned her servants and neighbors to come and see. Bao greeted each of them individually, with proper etiquette, using correct titles, and appeared to be a proper lady from a noble family.

    If a man slightly approached her in a familiar manner, the ghost immediately became angry and said, "I am a chaste woman. Whoever comes near me, I will take their life!" The crowd asked, "What is your grudge against Bao?" The ghost replied, "I and Bao truly turned from love into enmity. I once accused him before the city god, presenting nineteen complaints, all of which were not accepted. Now I have appealed to the God of Mount Tai, and He has finally granted my request. Soon, I will go with Bao." When asked about her name, the ghost said, "I am a child from a good family, and my name cannot be mentioned." "What did you accuse Bao of?" The ghost then quickly recited the nineteen accusations, speaking so fast that they could not be fully understood, but the gist was that Bao had betrayed her, leaving her with no place to return to. Someone else asked, "Since you are speaking through Bao's body, where is he now?" The ghost smiled and said, "He is bound in a small house beside the city god temple." Wang wept and begged the ghost to release her husband, but the ghost did not respond.

    802

    至夜分，众姻戚私语曰：“彼鬼曾言告城隍状不准，今缚包于城隍庙侧，何不往告于神，求其伸理？”于是共觅香烛楮镪，若将往者。鬼忽言曰：“今诸人既同来相求，且放彼归，自有东岳审断。”言毕倒地。

    　　少顷包苏，极称困顿，众环问所见，包曰：“初出某娼门，即见此妇相随。

    　　初尚或左或右，至教场，妇遽前扯拽往城隍庙左侧小屋内，黑暗中以绳缚我手足，置之于地，旁似有相守之人。适闻妇来曰：“今目放汝归。‘推我出户，一跌而醒，身已在家。此事明日东岳当传审矣。”再询其细，包惟酣睡而已。

    Explanation

    802

    Until midnight, the relatives and in-laws spoke privately, saying, "That ghost had said that the complaint to the city god was not accepted, and now the package is bound at the side of the city god's temple. Why not go to the god and ask for justice?" They then gathered incense, candles, and paper money, as if they were about to go. Suddenly, the ghost said, "Now that all of you have come together to seek help, let him go back, and there will be a judgment by the Eastern Peak." After saying this, the ghost fell to the ground.

    After a while, the package revived and complained of great exhaustion. The people gathered around and asked what he had seen. The package said, "At first, when I left a certain brothel, I saw this woman following me. At first, she was sometimes to the left or right, but when I reached the parade ground, the woman suddenly pulled me into a small house on the left side of the city god's temple. In the darkness, she tied my hands and feet and placed me on the ground, with someone nearby guarding me. Just then, I heard the woman say, 'Now let you go back.' She pushed me out the door, and I fell and woke up, already at home. This matter will be judged by the Eastern Peak tomorrow." When asked for more details, the package was just sound asleep.

    803

    次日午后起曰：“差人至矣，速具酒食。”自出厅向空座拱揖，语多不解。

    　　酒既设，复归卧床上，更许死矣，惟心头微热。王氏与诸人泣守之，见包面色时青时红时黄，变幻不测。三更后，胸前及喉颊间见红斑爪痕数处。次夜二鼓，发辫忽散乱。至晓始苏，索茶饭尽十数器，吞咽迅速，观者骇然。少定，呼“取酒食款差役”，王氏如前设之；又命取纸钱六千，须去其破缺者，以四千焚于厅前，二千焚于门侧巷内。复自起至大门作拜送状，反室熟睡两日乃能起。悉言所见：自女鬼解缚放回后，次日下午，有二差役来传，其一不识，其一陈胜，亦贾人子，儿时与包为同窗友。陈家贫，娶妇时，包曾助以钱数千文，今已殁三载。

    Explanation

    803

    The next day in the afternoon, he said, "The officials have arrived, prepare wine and food quickly." He came out of the hall and bowed toward an empty seat, but most of his words were incomprehensible.

    After the wine was served, he returned to lie on the bed, seeming even more dead, but only his heart was slightly warm. Wang and others cried and guarded him, seeing Bao's face change from blue to red to yellow, shifting unpredictably. After the third watch, red spots and claw marks appeared on his chest and around his throat and cheeks. The next night at the second drumbeat, his braids suddenly became disheveled. He woke up at dawn, asking for tea and food, consuming ten or more dishes quickly, shocking those watching. After a brief calm, he called out, "Prepare wine and food to treat the officials," and Wang did so as before. He also ordered six thousand paper money, instructing that the damaged ones be removed, with four thousand burned in front of the hall and two thousand burned in the alley beside the door. He then got up and went to the main gate, making a gesture of farewell, before returning to his room to sleep deeply for two days before he could get up. He recounted everything he had seen: after the female ghost untied him and released him, the next afternoon, two officials came to announce something. One he did not recognize, and the other was Chen Sheng, also the son of a merchant, who had been his classmate and friend when they were children. Chen's family was poor, and when he married, Bao had helped him with several thousand coins. Now Chen had been dead for three years.

    804

    谓包曰：“此事已发速报司审办，尔我同窗好友，在生又承高谊，自当用情照应，不必上刑具。”同行至中途，见二役锁前女鬼，鬼大恚，以首触包，手抓仿包面颊，此包身所以有红斑爪痕之现也。女鬼詈二差卖法，差不得已，为包亦上锁同行。路愈远愈黑，阴风惨烈，辫发俱散。

    　　至一处，仿佛见衙署，差令坐地守候。旋见二红灯由内出，二差去包锁，带入跪于灯止处。见有公案文卷，一官上坐，红袍乌纱，以手捋须，问曰：“汝包某耶？”包应曰：“诺。”官即提女鬼至，讯答语颇多。女与包并跪阶下，相云尺许，绝不闻其一字。见官震怒，令批女鬼颊十五，即上枷锁，二役牵之，痛哭而去。

    　　包初跪案前，觉沮洳泥泞，阴风吹发，面上丝丝如刀刺，寒栗难当。迨批女颊时，陈役从旁悄言曰：“老兄官司已赢矣，吾为兄辫起发来。”包再举首，灯与官俱不复见。二役乃送之回，言明差钱四千文，其二千，则陈役所私得也。

    Explanation

    804

    The official said to Baok, "This matter has already been reported to the judicial office for handling. You and I, fellow students and good friends, have received your high regard in life; I should naturally use my feelings to support you, and there is no need to use restraints." They continued walking until they reached the middle of the road, where they saw two constables locking a woman ghost in front. The ghost was very angry and hit Baok with her head, grabbing and scratching his face, which is why Baok's body had red spots and scratch marks. The woman ghost cursed the two constables for selling justice, and the constables had no choice but to also lock Baok and go with her. The road became darker and darker as it went further, with a harsh and bleak cold wind blowing, and their braids were all loose.

    They reached a place where it seemed like there was a government office, and the constables ordered Baok to sit on the ground and wait. Soon, they saw two red lanterns coming out from inside, and the two constables removed Baok's restraints, leading him to kneel at the place where the lanterns stopped. There, they saw a public case and documents, with a judge sitting above, dressed in a red robe and black cap, stroking his beard. He asked, "Are you Baok?" Baok replied, "Yes." The judge immediately brought the woman ghost forward, and there were many questions and answers. The woman and Baok knelt side by side on the steps, about a foot apart, and Baok could not hear a single word. The judge became furious and ordered to slap the woman ghost's cheeks fifteen times, immediately putting her in shackles. The two constables led her away, crying painfully as they left.

    Baok initially knelt in front of the bench, feeling the ground was muddy and slippery, with the cold wind blowing through his hair, the threads of the wind felt like needles pricking his face, and it was so cold and uncomfortable. When the woman's cheeks were being slapped, Chen, the constable, whispered from the side, saying, "Brother, you have already won the case. I will braid your hair back up." Baok raised his head again, and the lanterns and the judge were no longer visible. The two constables then sent him back, clarifying that the fee was four thousand coins, two thousand of which were privately kept by Chen, the constable.

    805

    人问包：“曾识此女否？”包力言不识。揣其情，女鬼因慕包之色而亡，又欲招包以偕阴耦，逞私妄控，故为阴司所责谴。

    　　○丁大哥康熙间，扬州乡人俞二耕种为生。入城取麦价，铺户留饮，回时已迟，途径昏黑。行至红桥，有小人数十扯拽之。俞素知此地多鬼，然胆气甚壮，又值酒酣，奋拳殴击，散而复聚者数次。闻鬼语曰：“此人凶勇，非我辈所能制，必请丁大哥来，方能制他。”遂哄然去。俞心揣丁大哥不知是何恶鬼，但已至此，惟有前进。方过桥，见一鬼长丈许，黑影中仿佛见其面色青紫，狰狞可畏。愈念动手迟则失势难脱，不若乘其未至迎击之。解腰间布裹钱二千文迎面打去，其鬼随手倒地，触街石上，铿然有声。俞以足踏之，渐缩渐小，其质甚重，牢握归家。灯下照视，乃古棺上一大铁钉也，其长二尺，粗如巨指。入火熔之，血涔涔出。俞召诸友笑曰：“丁大哥之力量不如俞二哥也。”

    Explanation

    805

    Someone asked Ba, "Have you ever known this woman?" Ba firmly stated that he did not know her. Considering the situation, the female ghost had died because she was enamored with Ba's appearance, and she wanted to invite Ba to be her partner in the underworld. Acting out of selfishness and making false accusations, she was punished by the underworld officials.

    ○Big Brother Ding During the reign of Emperor Kangxi, a man named Yu Er from Yangzhou, who made a living by farming, went into the city to collect wheat money. The shop owner kept him to drink, and he returned late when the path was dark. As he walked to Red Bridge, dozens of small figures pulled and dragged him. Yu always knew that there were many ghosts in this place, but he was very brave, and he was drunk. He punched and beat them, and they scattered and gathered several times. He heard the ghost say, "This person is fierce and brave, not something we can control. We must call in Big Brother Ding to control him." They then dispersed in a hurry. Yu guessed that Big Brother Ding might be some kind of evil ghost, but he had already come this far, so he could only continue forward. When he was crossing the bridge, he saw a ghost about ten feet tall. In the dark shadow, he vaguely saw its face was blue and purple, and it looked terrifying. He thought that if he delayed, he would lose the advantage and be unable to escape. It would be better to attack him before he arrived. He took the money wrapped in cloth from his waist and hit it in front of him. The ghost fell to the ground and hit the street stone, making a clanging sound. Yu stepped on it with his foot, and it gradually shrunk and became smaller. It was very heavy, and he firmly held it and went home. He examined it under the lamp, and it turned out to be a large iron nail from an ancient coffin. It was two feet long and as thick as a large finger. He put it in the fire to melt it, and blood flowed out continuously. Yu called his friends and laughed, saying, "Big Brother Ding's strength is not as good as Second Brother Yu's."

    806

    ○汪二姑娘绍兴吴某行三，在赵州刺史署中主刑名。后又延一管书禀者，亦吴姓行三，苏州人。署有“老吴师爷”、“小吴师爷之称”。其馆舍对房而居，甚相亲洽。

    　　刺史有妾七八人，侍婢甚夥，亦皆妖艳，常出入于馆舍左右。二吴每评论某某当吾意，某某当君意，以为戏谑。

    　　一日，公事毕时，已三鼓，各回房就寝。小吴方坐床上吸烟，燃烛于帐外，命仆反掩门而去。少顷，举署皆寂，忽有人推门。小吴问为谁，不答。见一女子年可二十，容色甚美，急趋而进，至床前瞪目视。小吴惊问：“尔何人？何为至此？”女曰：“我汪二姑娘也，来寻绍兴吴三。误矣！误矣！”吴疑其为东家侍婢，与老吴有约，因笑指曰：“绍兴吴三在对房，我苏州吴三也。”女瞥然竟去。

    Explanation

    806

    Miss Wang the Second, the third daughter of a family from Shaoxing, served as the chief of criminal affairs in the office of the prefect of Zhaozhou. Later, he hired another clerk responsible for documents and reports, also a third son of a family named Wu from Suzhou. The office was known as "Old Mr. Wu" and "Young Mr. Wu." They lived in facing rooms and were very close and friendly.

    The prefect had seven or eight concubines and many female servants, all of whom were seductive and often came and went around the office or living quarters. The two Wus often commented that certain ones suited his taste, and certain ones suited the prefect's taste, as a joke.

    One day, after official business had concluded at the third watch, they each returned to their rooms to sleep. Young Mr. Wu was sitting on his bed smoking, lighting a candle outside his mosquito net, and ordered the servant to close the door behind him and leave. A short while later, the entire office was quiet, when suddenly someone pushed open the door. Young Mr. Wu asked who it was, but there was no answer. He saw a woman, about twenty years old, with a very beautiful appearance, who rushed in and stood before the bed, staring intently. Young Mr. Wu was startled and asked, "Who are you? Why are you here?" The woman replied, "I am Miss Wang the Second, looking for the third son of Shaoxing. I must have made a mistake! I must have made a mistake!"
