Young Mr. Wu suspected she was one of the prefect's female servants, who had an appointment with Old Mr. Wu, and laughed, pointing to the other room and saying, "The third son of Shaoxing is in the opposite room; I am the third son of Suzhou." The woman then swiftly left.

    This translation aims to capture the nuances of the original text while ensuring it reads naturally in English.

    807

    明日，向老吴戏谑曰：“昨夜大快活。”老吴不解。屡言之，老吴究问所以，小吴笑曰：“吾所目击，尚抵赖乎？”老吴益疑，再三问，小吴告以衣服形状，并汪二姑娘来寻绍兴吴三之语。老吴爽然失色曰：“彼何至此耶？”少定，告小吴曰：“此吾至亲也，亡去已十数年，不识何故寻我？”小吴惊异，见其颜色沮丧，不复再问。

    　　至晚，老吴默默无语，而畏惧之容愈甚，拉小吴至房同居。小吴力辞，老吴不得已，命二仆夹床而卧。小吴彻夜潜听，毫无声息。至晓，其二仆起，视老吴，则已死矣。

    Explanation

    807

    The next day, Xiao Wu teased Lao Wu, saying, "I was very happy last night." Lao Wu did not understand. Xiao Wu repeated this several times, and Lao Wu inquired about the reason. Xiao Wu laughed and said, "What I saw with my own eyes, can I still deny it?" Lao Wu became even more suspicious and asked repeatedly. Xiao Wu told him about the appearance of the clothes and mentioned that Wang Er had come looking for Wu San from Shaoxing. Lao Wu looked startled and said, "Why would they come here?" After a moment, he told Xiao Wu, "This is a close relative of mine, who has been missing for over ten years. I don't know why they are looking for me." Xiao Wu was astonished and saw that Lao Wu looked dejected, so he did not ask further.

    By evening, Lao Wu was silent and speechless, and his expression of fear grew even more intense. He took Xiao Wu to the room to stay together. Xiao Wu strongly refused, but Lao Wu had no choice and ordered two servants to lie beside the bed. Xiao Wu listened intently all night, but there was no sound at all. By morning, the two servants got up and saw that Lao Wu was already dead.

    808

    ○谢铜头镇江西门，旧在唐颓山，国初迁于北城外阳彭山，有佛寺，殿宇廊庑修洁，即丽春台古迹也。地近孔道，绅当道迎送饮饯，皆在此处。自城门迁后，路既隔远，此寺遂废，惟存大铜佛三尊，相传五代时所铸，约数万斤，露处山内。

    　　有谢某者，素贩铜为业，潜勾通书役销熔而朋分之，议定工费皆谢出，谢取其半，诸人分其半。销毁之日，四体皆化，惟佛头不坏。众皆疑惧。谢曰：“此易事耳。”登炉溺之，佛头竟毁。谢年四十余，尚无子。是时方欢笑间，佣工者至前，贺家中已生子矣。谢大喜，以为此佛劫数，当为我毁，遂名其子为“谢铜头”。家由此少裕，日以私铸制钱为事。

    　　数年后，其党以私铸见获，词连谢某。谢自以热灰揉瞎双目，到案时，言目瞽已久，仇扳显然竟得漏网。及铜头长成，仍事私铸，复为人所控。乾隆某年，父子对缚，斩于阳彭山下。

    Explanation

    808

    ○ Xie Tongtou's gate at West Gate of Jiangxi was originally located on Tang颓 Mountain, but at the beginning of the dynasty, it was moved to Yangpeng Mountain outside the northern city wall. There was a temple there with clean and well-maintained halls and corridors, which was the ancient site of Lichun Terrace. It was near a major thoroughfare, where officials and scholars welcomed and sent off guests with drinks and banquets. After the city gate was moved, the road became distant, and this temple was thus abandoned. Only three large copper statues remained, which were said to have been cast during the Five Dynasties period, weighing about tens of thousands of pounds, exposed in the mountain.

    There was a man named Xie who had been dealing in copper. He secretly conspired with the book clerks to melt them down and divide the proceeds, agreeing that all costs would be covered by Xie. Xie took half, and the others divided the rest. On the day of destruction, all parts melted, but only the Buddha's head remained intact. Everyone was suspicious and fearful. Xie said, "This is easy." He climbed into the furnace and drowned it, and the Buddha's head was finally destroyed. Xie was in his forties and still childless. At that moment, while they were laughing, a laborer came forward and congratulated him, saying his home had already given birth to a son. Xie was very happy, thinking this was the Buddha's fate, and that it was meant to be destroyed for him, so he named his son 'Xie Tongtou'. His family became somewhat prosperous, and he made a living by privately casting coins.

    Several years later, his accomplices were caught for private coinage, and the case implicated Xie. Xie rubbed his eyes blind with hot ash. When he appeared in court, he claimed he had been blind for a long time, and the obvious enmity made it clear he escaped the net. When Tongtou grew up, he continued to cast coins privately and was again reported. In a certain year of the Qianlong reign, father and son were bound together and beheaded at Yangpeng Mountain.

    809

    ○乌头太子胡某，世以丹徒江上洲田为业。乾隆十八年冬初，至洲收租，以所收稻晒于场上。有乌鸦群集食稻，吴取土块逐之，随手中一乌，哑然坠地，复奋起飞去。

    　　吴归庄房，晚餐后，忽闻风雨声，启户仰视，天色深黑，大雨如注，急入室，衣色全白，皆鸦粪矣。吴因忆人言禽粪着身者不吉，我今被污，殆将死乎？自此遂病雀爪风，手足抽掣，不便起卧，又不能持物饮食，需人扶喂，不堪其苦。然心甚明晰。因自念鸦食我稻，我逐之，有何过？乃敢祟我，将控之于神。屡动此念，实未能写状也。

    　　一日尽寝，梦以黄纸自写一状，将投于城隍庙。忽空中有黑云二片飞下，至地化青衣人向吴曰：“君前所击者，非鸦也，乃乌头太子也。君因得罪于彼，故患此恙。若再往告彼，罪益重矣！不如具酒食请罪于太子，可保全也。”吴不听，且怒曰：“彼食我稻，又妄祟我，我必告之！”

    Explanation

    809

    Wutou Tai Zi Hu Mou, whose family had made a living by farming the fields on the island in the river near Dantu, was a descendant of this lineage. In the early winter of the eighteenth year of the Qianlong era, he went to the island to collect rent and dry the harvested rice on the field. There were crows gathering to eat the rice, so Wu picked up a piece of soil and chased them. One of them fell into his hand, fell silently to the ground, and flew away again.

    After returning to his house, Wu had dinner and then, suddenly, heard the sound of wind and rain. He opened the door and looked up, and saw that the sky was very dark, with heavy rain pouring down. Hurriedly entering the room, he found his clothes completely white, all covered in crow droppings. Wu remembered what people said about bird droppings on the body being inauspicious. "Am I going to die?" he wondered. From then on, he fell ill with the disease called "sparrow claw wind," his hands and feet twitched, and he was unable to sit up or lie down. He could not hold objects or eat and needed someone to help him eat. He could not bear the pain, but his mind was very clear. He thought to himself, "The crows ate my rice, and I chased them. What wrong have I done? Dare they torment me? I will report them to the gods!" He often had this thought, but in reality, he could not write a complaint.

    One day, while sleeping, he dreamed of writing a complaint on yellow paper, intending to submit it to the City God Temple. Suddenly, two black clouds flew down from the sky and transformed into two people in green robes who said to Wu, "The one you struck earlier was not a crow, but the Wutou Tai Zi. You have offended him, which is why you are suffering from this illness. If you go to report him again, your sin will be even greater! It would be better to prepare some wine and food to apologize to the Wutou Tai Zi, and you can be saved." Wu did not listen and angrily said, "He ate my rice and then tormented me without reason. I must report him!"

    810

    须臾，空中又下黑云二片，化作少年，玄色冠巾，一人持黑伞随其后，向吴拱手曰：“君欲控乌头太子耶？控词何拟？”吴持与观之。少年曰：“君前击中太子，故有此疾，今知其误也，某为君缓颊于太子，可保君如旧，何须控告耶？”

    　　因取控词怀之飞去。吴遽前往夺，忽然惊醒。自此所患渐愈，两月后平复如常。

    　　○吴生两入阴间吴某，丹徒旧家子也，其祖、父俱在庠序。祖为人端直，乡闾推重，殁十数年，某始娶妇，琴瑟甚笃。乾隆丙子，其妇暴卒，吴追思不已。

    　　有朱长班者，合城皆知其走阴差，因吴治丧，彼朝夕来供役，吴因私问阴司事。朱言阴司与人世无异，无罪者安闲自适，有罪者始入各狱。吴遂恳其携往阴司，一与妻见。朱云：“阴阳道隔，生人尤不宜滥入。老相公侍我甚好，我岂肯作此狡狯？”吴嬲之不已，朱云：“此事我不为，相公果坚意欲往，可往城里太平桥侧寻丹阳常妈，许以重资，或可同往。”吴欣然。

    Explanation

    810

    A moment later, two dark clouds descended from the sky, transforming into a young man dressed in dark blue robes and a headgear, followed by someone holding a black umbrella. The young man bowed to Wu and asked, "Do you wish to accuse the Prince of the Black Head? What is the basis for your accusation?" Wu showed him the accusation. The young man said, "You struck the prince before, which is why you have this illness. Now that we know it was a mistake, I will intercede on your behalf with the prince and can ensure you return to normal. Why do you need to file a complaint?"

    He then took the accusation, put it in his chest, and flew away. Wu rushed to take it back, but suddenly woke up from his dream. From that day on, his illness gradually improved, and after two months, he returned to normal.

    ○ Wu Twice Entered the Underworld

    Wu, a son of an old family in Dan Tu, had both his grandfather and father as scholars. His grandfather was upright and respected by the community, having passed away more than ten years ago. Wu recently married, and his relationship with his wife was very harmonious. In the year 1746 (Qianlong 11th year), his wife died suddenly, and Wu could not stop missing her.

    There was a person named Zhu Changban, known throughout the city as a messenger of the underworld. Because Wu was handling the funeral arrangements, Zhu came to assist him day and night. Wu privately asked about matters in the underworld. Zhu said that the underworld is no different from the human world; the innocent live comfortably, while the guilty are imprisoned. Wu then asked Zhu to take him to the underworld to see his wife once more. Zhu replied, "The boundary between the living and the dead is distinct, and it is especially inappropriate for the living to enter the underworld without reason. The elder treated me very well, and I would not do such a trick." Wu persistently begged him, and Zhu said, "I won't do this, but if you are determined to go, you should go to the side of Tai Ping Bridge in the city and find Dan Yang Chang Ma, offering a substantial reward, and perhaps we can go together." Wu was very pleased.

    811

    次日，寻得常妈，初亦不允；许钱数千，始允之，且曰：“相公某日可择一静屋独宿，我即来相约，但衣履一切，不可使人稍为移动。稍移动，即不能还阳矣。”谆嘱再四而归。

    　　吴自妻殁后，即独宿于一厢屋内。至某日，吴私嘱其婶母曰：“侄今病甚，须早卧，望婶母为我锁房，切不可令人擅入动我衣履，此侄生死关头也。”婶母甚骇，问其故，不告，乃阴为检点之。吴既入房，然一灯于床前，心有此事，展转不寝，私念曰：“彼原未嘱我熟睡，但彼从何来招我耶？抑妄言耶？”

    　　二鼓后，见有黑烟一线自窗隙间入，袅袅然如蛇之吐舌也，吴心甚惧。少顷，其烟变成一黑团，大如斗，直扑吴面，遂昏晕。有人在耳边悄言曰：“吴相公同去。”声即常妪也。以手扶起，同由门隙而出，所过窗户皆无碍。见其婶母房门有火光数丛，盖与诸弟同宿于内。

    Explanation

    811

    The next day, Wu tracked down the old woman, but she initially refused. After offering several thousand coins, she finally agreed, and said, "On a certain day, you may choose a quiet room to stay alone, and I will come to meet you. But all your clothing and footwear must not be moved by anyone. If moved even slightly, you will not return to life." She repeated this instruction several times before returning.

    After his wife's death, Wu began to stay alone in a side room. On a certain day, Wu privately instructed his aunt, saying, "My nephew is very ill and must go to bed early. Please lock the room for me, and absolutely do not let anyone enter or move my clothes and shoes, as this is a critical moment for my nephew." His aunt was very frightened, asked the reason, but he did not tell her, so she secretly checked on him. After Wu entered the room, he lit a lamp in front of the bed. Having this matter on his mind, he tossed and turned without sleeping, thinking to himself, "He originally did not tell me to sleep deeply, but how could he come to summon me? Or is it just idle talk?"

    After the second drumbeat, Wu saw a thin line of black smoke enter through the window crack, winding like a snake's tongue. Wu felt very afraid. A short while later, the smoke turned into a black ball, as big as a winnowing basket, and directly hit Wu's face. He then fainted. Someone whispered in his ear, "Come with me, Scholar Wu." The voice was that of the old woman. She helped him up and together they exited through the door crack, passing through all the windows and doors unobstructed. They saw several clusters of firelight at his aunt's room door, where his brothers were staying together.

    812

    甫出大门，则另一天地，黄沙漫漫，不辨南北。途中所见街市衙署，与人世仿佛。行至一处，见一大池水，红色，妇女在内哀号。常指曰：“此即佛家所谓‘血污池’也，娘子想在其内。”吴左右顾，见其妻在东角，吴痛哭相呼，妻亦近至岸边，垂泪与语，并以手来拉吴入池。

    　　吴欲奔赴，常妪大惊，力挽吴，告之曰：“池水涓滴着人，即不能返。入此池者，皆由生平毒虐婢妾之故。凡殴婢妾见血不止者，即入此池，以婢妾身上流血之多寡为入池之浅深。”吴曰：“我娘子并无殴婢妾，何由至此？”妪曰：“此前生事也。”吴又问：“娘子并未生产，何入此池？”妪言：“我前已言明，此池非为生产故也，生产是人间常事，有何罪过？”言毕，牵吴从原路归。吴昏睡过午始起，面色黄白若久病者，数日方复。

    Explanation

    812

    As soon as Wu stepped out of the main gate, he found himself in a different world, a vast expanse of yellow sand stretching endlessly, making it impossible to distinguish north from south. Along the way, he saw street markets and government offices that resembled those in the human world. Upon reaching a certain place, he saw a large pool of red water, with women inside wailing in sorrow. The old woman pointed and said, "This is the so-called 'Blood Stained Pool' of Buddhism; your wife likely is inside." Wu looked around and saw his wife at the eastern corner. He cried out in pain, calling to her, and she too came close to the shore, crying and speaking to him, reaching out to pull Wu into the pool.

    Wu wanted to rush over, but the old woman was greatly alarmed, pulling him back forcefully and told him, "Even a drop of the pool water touching you will prevent you from returning. Those who enter this pool have all been punished due to their past cruel treatment of maids and concubines. Anyone who beats a maid or concubine to the point of continuous bleeding will enter this pool, with the depth of the pool determined by the amount of blood shed." Wu said, "My wife did not beat any maids or concubines; how could she be here?" The old woman replied, "This is a matter of her past life." Wu asked again, "My wife has not given birth; why is she in this pool?" The old woman explained, "I have already mentioned that this pool is not for childbirth. Childbirth is a common human event; what sin is there?" After saying this, she led Wu back along the original path. Wu fell into a deep sleep until after noon, waking up with a yellowish-white complexion like someone who had been ill for a long time, and it took several days for him to fully recover.

    813

    月馀，吴思妻转甚，走至常妪家，告以欲再往看之意，常甚难之。许以数倍之资，始为首肯。如前嘱婶母锁门，常妪复来相约。出门行里许，常妪忽撇吴奔去。吴不解其故，错愕间，见前有一老翁肩舆至，觌面乃其祖也。吴惶遽欲避，祖喝之曰：“汝何为至此？”吴无奈何，告以故。其祖大怒曰：“各人生死有命，汝乃不达若此！”手批其颊骂曰：“汝若再来，我必告阴官，立斩常妪。”遣舆夫送至河畔，舆夫从后推吴入河，大叫而醒。左颊青肿，痛不可忍，托病卧房中，十数日始愈。

    　　时吴有烟戚某翁病笃，吴谓其婶母曰：“某翁某日方死。”婶惊问之，吴告以两次所见，并言于一衙署前，见所挂牌上姓名月日，故知之也。自后吴神气委靡，两目蓝色，下午后即常见鬼，至今犹存。吴婶母，法嘉荪中表，法故悉其颠末，而为予言。

    Explanation

    813

    After a month, Fú's wife's condition worsened. He went to the old woman's house and told her about his intention to go again to see her. The old woman strongly objected. Fú offered several times the usual fee, and finally, she agreed. As before, he instructed his aunt to lock the door, and the old woman came again to make an appointment. He went out and walked about a li, when the old woman suddenly left him and ran away. Fú did not understand the reason and was in a state of surprise and confusion. In front of him, an old man appeared in a sedan chair, and it turned out to be his ancestor. Fú felt alarmed and wanted to avoid him. The ancestor shouted at him, "Why are you here?" Fú had no choice but to tell him the reason. The ancestor became very angry and said, "Everyone's life and death is determined by fate. You are so ignorant!" He hit Fú on the cheek and scolded, "If you come again, I will definitely inform the officials of the underworld and immediately have the old woman executed." He sent the sedan-chair bearers to take Fú to the riverbank. The bearers pushed Fú into the river from behind, and he cried out and woke up. His left cheek was bruised and swollen, and the pain was unbearable. He pretended to be ill and stayed in bed for several days before he recovered.

    At that time, a certain elderly man who was related to Fú by marriage was gravely ill. Fú told his aunt, "The elderly man will die on a certain day." The aunt was shocked and asked him. Fú told her about what he had seen twice and said that he had seen it in front of an official office, where he saw the names and dates posted on the board, so he knew. Afterward, Fú became listless, his eyes turned blue, and he began to see ghosts after noon. This continues to this day. Fú's aunt, who is a cousin of Fǎ Jiāsūn, knew all the details and told me about it.

    814

    ○狐道学法君祖母孙氏外家有孙某者，巨富也，国初，海寇之乱，移家金坛。一日，有胡姓携其子孙奴仆数十人，行李甚富，过其门，云是山西人，遇兵不能行，愿假尊屋暂住。孙有其言貌，知非常人，分一宅居之。暇日过与闲话，见其室中有琴剑书籍，所读书皆《黄庭》、《道德》等经，所谈者皆心性《语录》中语，遇其子孙奴仆甚严，言笑不苟。孙家人皆以“狐道学”称之。

    　　孙氏小婢有姿。一日，遇翁之幼孙于巷，遽抱之，婢不从，白于胡翁。翁慰之曰：“汝勿怒，吾将杖之。”明曰日将午，胡翁之门不启，累叩不应。遣人逾墙开门阅之，宅内一无所有，惟书室中有白金三十两置几上，书“租资”二字。

    Explanation

    814

    A Scholarly Trapper

    In the early Qing dynasty, a wealthy family named Sun had to move to Jintan due to the turmoil caused by sea bandits. One day, a man surnamed Hu, accompanied by his descendants, servants, and dozens of others, passed by the Sun family's door. He carried a great deal of possessions and claimed to be from Shanxi, stating that they had encountered soldiers and could not proceed, and thus wished to borrow a house for temporary residence. The Sun family, noting his demeanor and words, felt that he was not an ordinary person, and so they gave him a separate house to live in. On a leisure day, they visited him for casual conversation and found that his study was filled with musical instruments, swords, and books. The texts he read were the "Huangting Jing" and the "Daode Jing," and the topics he discussed were all from the "Lüshi Chunqiu" (Master Lü's Spring and Autumn Annals). He treated his descendants and servants very strictly, speaking and laughing without frivolity. The Sun family members all called him "the fox scholar."

    One of the Sun family's maids was beautiful. One day, she suddenly hugged the young grandson of the trapper in an alley. The maid refused to let go, and the incident was reported to the trapper, Hu. The trapper consoled her, saying, "Do not be angry; I will punish him." The next day at noon, the trapper's household did not open their door, and repeated knocks went unanswered. The Sun family sent someone to climb over the wall and open the door to check, finding the house completely empty. Only in the study were thirty taels of silver placed on the table, with the words "rental fee" written on it.

    815

    再寻之，阶下有一掐死小狐。

    　　法子曰：“此狐乃真理学也。世有口谈理学而身作巧宦者，其愧狐远矣。”

    　　●卷二十三○太白山神秦中太白山神最灵。山顶有三池：曰大太白、中太白、三太白。木叶草泥偶落池中，则群鸟衔去，土人号曰“净池鸟”。

    　　有木匠某坠池中，见黄衣人引至一殿，殿中有王者，科头朱履，须发苍然，顾匠者笑曰：“知尔艺巧，相烦作一亭，故召汝来。”匠遂居水府。三年功成，王赏三千金，许其归。匠者嫌金重难带，辞之而出，见府中多小犬，毛作金丝色，向王乞取。王不许，匠者偷抱一犬于怀辞出。路上开怀视之，一小金龙腾空飞去，爪伤匠者之手，终身废弃。归家后，忽一日雷雨下冰雹皆化为金，称之，得三千两。

    　　○太平闲吏王员外中斋，予告后卜居江宁，题一斋额曰：太平闲吏。后十年，员外卒，屋之东偏，售于太平守王克端；屋之西偏，售于太平守李敏第。

    Explanation

    815

    Translation

    Searching again, below the steps was a small fox that had been strangled to death.

    The sage said, "This fox represents true learning. In the world, there are those who talk about true learning but act as cunning officials; how far they fall short of the fox!"

    Chapter Twenty-Three: The God of Mount Taibai in Qin

    The god of Mount Taibai in Qin is the most powerful. At the summit of the mountain, there are three pools: the Great Taibai, the Middle Taibai, and the Third Taibai. Occasionally, leaves, grass, and mud fall into the pools, and flocks of birds carry them away. The local people call these birds "Net Pool Birds."

    There was a carpenter who fell into one of the pools and saw a person in yellow clothes lead him to a hall. In the hall, there was a king, bareheaded and wearing red shoes, with white beard and hair. He looked at the carpenter and smiled, saying, "Knowing your skillfulness, I have troubled you to build a pavilion, so I called you here." The carpenter then lived in the water palace. After three years, the work was completed, and the king rewarded him with three thousand gold pieces, allowing him to return. The carpenter thought the gold was too heavy to carry and declined the reward, leaving. He saw many small dogs in the palace with fur like gold threads and asked the king to take one. The king did not allow it, and the carpenter secretly took a dog in his arms and left. On the road, he opened his arms to look at it, and a small golden dragon flew into the sky, its claws injuring the carpenter's hand, leaving him permanently disabled. After returning home, one day during a thunderstorm, hail turned into gold. When weighed, it amounted to three thousand taels of gold.

    The Leisurely Official of Peace, Wang Wai, the Middle Study

    After being granted leave, Wang Wai chose to live in Jiangning and named his study "Leisurely Official of Peace." Ten years later, Wang Wai passed away. The eastern part of his house was sold to the governor of Taiping, Wang Keduan; the western part was sold to the governor of Taiping, Li Min.

    816

    ○楚雄奇树楚雄府Ф嘉州者卜夷地方有冬青树，根蟠大十里，远望如开数十座木行，其中桌椅床榻厨柜俱全，可住十馀户。惜树叶稀，不能遮风雨耳。其根拔地而出，枝枝有脚。

    　　○泗州怪碑泗州虹县有井，是禹王锁巫支祈处，铁索犹存。旁有石碑，头不可动。一那移其头，则碑孔内便流黄水如金色。

    　　○雁荡动静石南雁荡有两石相压，大可屋二间，下为静石，上为动石。欲推动之，须一人卧静石上撑以双脚，石轰然作声，移开尺许，如立而手推之，虽千万人，不能动石一步。其理卒不可解。

    　　○瓦屑庙石人无头大湖旁有瓦屑庙，庙不甚大，中坐石人二十馀，头皆斫落在地，亦有以手握之者。相传：张士诚被围，夜有石将军率部伍拒战甚勇。城破后，庙中石人头俱坠地矣。一云：明末，石人夜为民祟，故村民以铁锄击去其头。

    Explanation

    816

    Translation

    Strange Tree in Chuxiong

    In the Chuxiong Prefecture, there is a strange evergreen tree in the village of Buyi. Its roots spread over ten miles, and from a distance, it looks like dozens of wooden houses. The tree is so intricate that it has tables, chairs, beds, cabinets, and other furnishings, enough to accommodate more than ten households. Unfortunately, the leaves are sparse, providing little shelter from wind and rain. The roots emerge from the ground, and each branch appears to have feet.

    Strange Stone Tablet in Sizhou

    In Sizhou, Hongxian County, there is a well where Yu the Great is said to have chained the demon Wuzhiqi. The iron chains still remain. Beside the well, there is a stone tablet whose head cannot be moved. If one attempts to move its head, yellow water flows from the hole in the stone tablet, resembling gold.

    Moving and Still Stones in Nan Yan Tang

    In Nan Yan Tang, there are two stones pressing against each other, large enough to fit two rooms. The lower one is the still stone, and the upper one is the moving stone. To move the upper stone, one must lie on the still stone and support it with both feet. The stone makes a loud noise and moves back a foot or so. If one stands and pushes it with hands, even if there are thousands of people, they cannot move the stone an inch. The reason behind this remains inexplicable.

    Headless Stone Figures in Wa Xie Temple

    By the large lake, there is a Wa Xie Temple, which is not very large. Inside, there are more than twenty stone figures, all of whose heads have been cut off and fallen to the ground. Some of the stone figures are holding their heads in their hands. It is said that when Zhang Shicheng was besieged, a stone general led his troops to fight bravely at night. After the city was captured, the heads of the stone figures in the temple all fell to the ground. Another account says that during the late Ming Dynasty, the stone figures caused trouble for the people at night, so the villagers used iron shovels to strike off their heads.

    817

    ○十三猫同日殉节江宁王御史父某有老妾，年七十馀，畜十三猫，爱如儿子，各有乳名，呼之即至。乾隆己酉，老奶奶亡，十三猫绕棺哀鸣。喂以鱼飧，流泪不食，饿三日，竟同死。

    　　○鬼吹头弯林千总者，江西武举。解饷入都，路过山东，宿古庙中。僧言：“此楼有怪，宜小心。”林恃勇，夜张灯烛，坐以待之。半夜后橐橐有声，一红衣女踏梯上，先向佛前膜拜，行礼毕，望林而笑。林不在意，女被发目，向前扑林。林取几掷之，女侧身避几，而以手来牵。林握其手，冷硬如铁。女被握，不能动。乃以口吹林，臭气难耐。林不得已，回头避之。格斗良久，至鸡鸣时，女身倒地，乃僵尸也。明日报官焚之，此怪遂绝。然林自此颈弯如茄瓢，不复能正矣。

    　　○虾蟆教书蚁排阵余幼住葵巷，见乞儿索钱者，身佩一布袋、两竹筒。袋贮虾蟆九个，筒贮红白两种蚁约千许，到店市柜上演其法毕，索钱三文即去。

    Explanation

    817

    Thirteen Cats Die in Mourning

    In Jiangning, there was a certain Wang, a magistrate whose father had a concubine who was over seventy years old. She kept thirteen cats and loved them as her own children, each with a pet name, and they would come when called. In the year 1779, the concubine passed away, and the thirteen cats circled her coffin, mourning. Fed with fish, they wept and refused to eat, starving for three days until they all died.

    The Ghost with the Bent Neck

    Lin, a military officer from Jiangxi who was a martial arts candidate, was delivering money to the capital. Passing through Shandong, he stayed in an ancient temple. The monk warned him, "There is a monster in this building; be careful." Relying on his courage, Lin lit lamps and candles at night and sat waiting for it. After midnight, there was a tapping sound, and a red-cloaked woman climbed the stairs. She first knelt before the Buddha, performed the ritual, and then smiled at Lin. Lin paid no attention, and the woman, with disheveled hair and wild eyes, rushed forward to attack him. Lin picked up a table and threw it at her, and she dodged it but reached out to pull him. Lin grabbed her hand, which was cold and hard like iron. The woman was held and could not move. Then she blew in Lin's face, and the stench was unbearable. Lin had no choice but to turn away to avoid it. After a long struggle, at the crow of the rooster, the woman fell to the ground, and she was a zombie. The next day, the authorities were informed, and she was burned; this monster was thus eliminated. However, Lin's neck was bent like a gourd and he could never straighten it again.

    Frogs Teaching Ants to Form an Array

    When I was young, I lived in Kuilane. I saw beggars who begged for money, wearing a cloth bag and two bamboo tubes. The bag contained nine frogs, and the tubes contained about a thousand red and white ants. After performing their act at the store and market stalls, they asked for three coins and left.

    818

    一名“虾蟆教书”。其法设一小木椅，大者自袋跃出坐其上，八小者亦跃出环伺之，寂然无声。乞人喝曰：“教书！”大者应声曰：“阁阁，”群皆应曰“阁阁”，自此连曰“阁阁”，几聒人耳。乞人曰：“止。”当即绝声。一名蚂蚁摆阵。其法张红白二旗，各长尺许。乞人倾其筒，红白蚁乱走柜上。乞人扇以红旗曰：“归队！”红蚁排作一行；乞人扇以白旗曰：“归队！”白蚁排之作一行。乞人又以两旗互扇喝曰：“穿阵走！”红白蚁遂穿杂而行，左旋右转，行不乱步。行数匝，以筒接之，仍蠕蠕然各入筒矣。虾蟆蝼蚁，至微至蠢之虫，不知作何教法。

    　　○木犬能吠叶公文麟言在京师到某比部家，甫叩门，有狮毛恶犬咆哮而出，状若噬人者，叶大怖。主人随出喝之，犬卧不动。主人视客，笑吃吃不止。问：“何故？”曰：“此木犬也，外覆以狮毛，中设关键，遂能吠走。”叶不信，主人更出一鸡，黄羽绛冠，申颈报晓。披毛视之，亦木所为。

    Explanation

    818

    One is called "Frog Teaching Books." The method involves setting up a small wooden chair, and the larger one leaps out of the bag and sits on it, while the eight smaller ones also leap out and surround it, silent and motionless. The beggar shouts, "Teach books!" The larger one responds with a "ge ge," and the others follow suit, repeating "ge ge" until it becomes too noisy. The beggar says, "Stop," and they immediately fall silent. Another is called "Ants Arranging an Array." The method involves unfurling two red and white flags, each about a foot long. The beggar pours from his tube, and red and white ants scurry on the cabinet. The beggar waves the red flag and says, "Form a line!" The red ants line up in a row. The beggar waves the white flag and says, "Form a line!" The white ants line up in a row. The beggar then waves both flags and shouts, "Weave through the lines!" The red and white ants then weave through the lines, moving left and right, but in an orderly fashion. After several rounds, the beggar collects them back into the tube, and they return to their tubes, moving slowly. Frogs and ants, the most insignificant and foolish of creatures, it is unknown what teaching methods they use.

    ○ Wooden Dog Can Bark

    Mr. Wenlin of Ye said that when he visited the home of a certain clerk in Beijing, as soon as he knocked on the door, a fierce dog with lion's fur leaped out, growling as if it would bite him. Ye was greatly frightened. The host then came out and shouted at the dog, which lay down and did not move. The host looked at the guest and laughed uncontrollably. When asked why, he said, "This is a wooden dog, covered with lion's fur on the outside and containing a mechanism inside, so it can bark and move." Ye did not believe it. The host then brought out a chicken, which had yellow feathers and a red crest, and stretched its neck to announce the dawn. When examined, it was also made of wood.

    819

    ○铜人演西厢乾隆二十九年，西洋贡铜伶十八人，能演《西厢》一部。人长尺许，身躯耳目手足，悉铜铸成；其心腹肾肠，皆用关键凑接，如自鸣钟法。每出插匙开锁，有一定准程，误开则坐卧行止乱矣。张生、莺莺、红娘、惠明、法聪诸人，能自行开箱着衣服。身段交接，揖让进退，俨然如生，惟不能歌耳。一出演毕，自脱衣卧倒箱中。临值场时，自行起立，仍上戏毯。西洋人巧一至于此。

    　　○双花庙雍正间，桂林蔡秀才，年少美风姿。春日戏场观戏，觉旁有摩其臀者，大怒，将骂而殴之。回面，则其人亦少年，貌更美于己，意乃释然，转以手摸其阴。其人喜出望外，重整衣冠向前揖道姓名，亦桂林富家子，读书而未入泮者也。两人遂携手行赴杏花村馆，燕饮盟誓。此后出必同车，坐必同席，彼此熏香剃面，小袖窄襟，不知乌之雌雄也。

    Explanation

    819

    Translation

    Copper Figures Perform Romance of the Western Chamber

    In the twenty-ninth year of the Qianlong era, eighteen copper actors were presented as gifts from the Western countries. They were capable of performing the entire Romance of the Western Chamber. Each figure was about a foot tall, with bodies, ears, eyes, hands, and feet all cast in copper. Their internal organs, including the heart, intestines, and kidneys, were all connected by intricate mechanisms, similar to those of a self-striking clock. Each act required inserting a key to unlock, following a specific procedure. If opened incorrectly, their sitting, standing, and movements became chaotic. Zhang Sheng, Yingying, Hongniang, Huiming, and Fachong, among others, could open their own boxes and dress themselves. Their gestures, bows, and steps were as lifelike as real people, though they could only act, not sing. After one performance, they undressed and lay down in their boxes. When it was time for the next performance, they stood up on their own and returned to the stage. The craftsmanship of the Westerners reached such a level of sophistication.

    Temple of the Twin Flowers

    During the reign of Emperor Yongzheng, there was a young scholar named Cai from Guilin, who was young and handsome. On a spring day, he was watching a play at the theater when he felt someone touch his buttocks from the side. He was furious and was about to scold and beat the person. Turning around, he saw that the person was also young and even more handsome than himself. He felt relieved and then touched the other person's private area. The person was overjoyed beyond expectation and straightened his clothes, bowing forward to introduce himself. He was also a wealthy young man from Guilin who had studied but had not yet passed the entrance examination. The two then held hands and went to the Apricot Flower Village Hall to drink and swear an oath. Afterward, they always traveled together in the same carriage and sat together at the same table. They both perfumed themselves and shaved their faces, wearing tight sleeves and narrow collars. It was hard to tell which was the male and which was the female.

    820

    城中恶棍王秃儿伺于无人之处，将强奸焉。二人不可，遂杀之，横尸城角之阴。两家父母报官相验。捕役见秃儿衣上有血，擒而讯之，吐情伏法。两少年者平时恂恂，文理通顺，邑人怜之，为立庙，每祀必供杏化一枝，号“双花庙”。

    　　偶有祈祷，无不立应，因之香火颇盛。

    　　数年后，邑令刘大胡子过其地，问双花庙原委，得其详，怒曰：“此淫祠也，两恶少年，何祀之为？”命里保毁之。是夜，刘梦见两人一ㄏ其胡，一唾其面，骂曰：“汝何由知我为恶少年乎？汝父母官，非吾奴婢，能知我二人枕被间事乎？

    　　当日三国时，周瑜、孙策俱以美少年交好同寝宿，彼盖世英雄，汝亦以为恶少年乎？汝作令以来，某事受枉法赃若干，某年枉杀周贡生，汝独非恶人！而谓我恶乎？吾本欲立索汝命，因王法将加，死期已近，姑且饶汝！“袖中出一棍，长三尺许，系刘辫发上曰：”汝他日自知。“

    Explanation

    820

    A Villainous Ruffian and a Temple of the Twin Flowers

    In the city, a villainous ruffian named Wang Tueru lurked in a secluded place, intending to commit rape. The two youths refused, so he killed them, leaving their bodies lying dead in the shadow of the city corner. The parents of both families reported the incident to the authorities for an investigation. The constables saw blood on Wang Tueru's clothes, arrested him, and interrogated him. He admitted his guilt and accepted the law. The two youths, who were usually modest and respectful, well-versed in literature and reason, were pitied by the townspeople, who built a temple in their honor. Every time they worshipped, they offered a branch of apricot blossoms, calling it the "Temple of the Twin Flowers."

    Occasionally, prayers were made, and they were always answered immediately, leading to a surge in the popularity of the temple.

    Several years later, the county magistrate Liu Dabizi passed through the area and inquired about the origin of the Temple of the Twin Flowers. After learning the details, he became angry and said, "This is a shrine of debauchery! Two evil youths, why are they being worshipped?" He ordered the temple to be destroyed. That night, Liu dreamed of two figures: one grabbed his beard, and the other spat in his face, scolding him, "How do you know we are evil youths? You, the magistrate, are not our slave, yet you know the intimate details of our lives?

    In the days of the Three Kingdoms, Zhou Yu and Sun Ce, both young men of high moral and physical standards, were close friends who shared the same bed and slept together. They were heroes of their time. Do you also consider them evil youths? Since you became the magistrate, you have unjustly obtained wealth in certain matters, and in a certain year, you unjustly killed Zhou Gongsheng. Are you not an evil person yourself? And yet you call us evil? We originally wanted to immediately take your life, but because the law is about to be enforced, and your death is near, we will spare you!" A stick came out of his sleeve, about three feet long, and tied to Liu's braid, he said, "You will know in the future."

    821

    刘惊醒，与家人言，将复建庙祀之，而赧于发言。未几，以赃事被参，竟伏绞罪、方知一棍之征也。

    　　○假女贵阳县美男子洪某，假为针线娘教女子刺绣，行其技于楚、黔两省。长沙李秀才聘请刺绣，欲私之，乃以实告。李笑曰：“汝果男耶，则更美矣！吾尝恨北魏时魏主入宫朝太后，见二美尼，召而昵之，皆男子也，遂置之法。蠢哉魏主！

    　　何不封以龙阳而畜为侍从？如此不独己得幸臣，且不伤母后之心。“洪欣然就之。

    　　李甚宠爱。

    　　数年后，又至江夏，有杜某欲私之。洪欲以媚李者媚杜，而其人非解事者，遂控于官。解回贵阳，臬使亲验之：其声娇细，颈无结喉，发垂委地，肌肤玉映，腰围仅一尺三寸，而私处棱肥肉厚如大鲜菌。自言幼无父母，邻有孀母抚养之。

    Explanation

    821

    Liu awoke with a start and spoke to his family about rebuilding the temple for worship, but he was embarrassed to speak about it. Not long after, he was accused of corruption and eventually confessed to a capital crime, only then realizing the consequences of a single act.

    A handsome man named Hong from Guiyang pretended to be a needlework teacher and taught women embroidery, practicing his skills in the provinces of Chu and Qian. A scholar from Changsha hired him for embroidery and wanted to have a private relationship with him. Hong told him the truth. Li laughed and said, "If you are indeed a man, you would be even more beautiful! I once regretted that during the Northern Wei dynasty, the emperor visited the palace to pay respects to the empress dowager and saw two beautiful nuns, summoned them and favored them, only to find out they were men, and then punished them. What a foolish emperor!

    Why didn't he appoint them as court favorites and keep them as attendants? In this way, he would not only gain favored ministers but also not hurt the empress dowager's feelings." Hong happily agreed.

    Li deeply loved him.

    Several years later, he went to Jiangxia, where someone named Du wanted to have a private relationship with him. Hong wanted to please Du in the same way he pleased Li, but the person did not understand the situation, so he reported him to the authorities. He was brought back to Guiyang, and the provincial governor personally examined him: his voice was soft and delicate, his neck had no Adam's apple, his hair hung down to the ground, his skin was as smooth as jade, his waist was only thirteen inches around, and his private parts were thick and fleshy like a large fresh mushroom. He said he had no parents when he was young and was raised by a widow neighbor.

    822

    长与有私，遂不剃发，且与缠足，诡言女也。邻母死，乃为绣师教人。十七岁出门，今二十七岁。十年中所遇女子无算。“问其姓氏，曰：”抵我罪足矣，何必伤人闺阃？“讯以三木，始供吐某某。抚军欲拟长流，臬使争以为妖人，非斩不可，乃置极刑。

    　　死前一日，谓狱吏曰：“我享人间未有之乐，死亦何憾！然某臬使亦将不免。

    　　我罪止和奸，畜发诱人，亦不过刁奸耳，于律无死法。且诸女子与通奸，毕暗昧不明之事，尽可覆盖，何必逼我供招！宣诸章奏，各拟重杖，使数十郡县富贵人家女子玉雪肌肤困于朱木乎？“次日，赴市受戮，指其跪处曰：”后三年，讯我者在此矣。“已而臬使果以事诛，众咸异焉。

    Explanation

    822

    A man had a secret relationship, and thus he did not shave his head and even bound his feet, pretending to be a woman. After his mother died, he became a teacher of embroidery. At seventeen, he left home, and now he is twenty-seven. Over the ten years, he encountered countless women. "When asked about their surnames, he said: 'It is enough to atone for my crime; why should I harm anyone's private life?'" He was subjected to physical punishment with wooden restraints, and only then did he confess the names of those involved. The military governor wanted to exile him for a long time, but the provincial governor argued that he was a demon and that he could not be spared without beheading, so he was sentenced to death.

    On the day before his execution, he told the jailer: "I have enjoyed pleasures in the world that no one else has experienced; what regret can there be in dying! However, that provincial governor will also not escape punishment. My crime was only adultery, keeping my hair unshorn to seduce others, which is merely a form of deceitful adultery, and there is no law that mandates the death penalty for it. Moreover, all the women I had relations with were involved in secretive and unclear matters, which can be covered up. Why should you force me to confess? By announcing this in official documents and proposing severe punishments for each, are you going to subject dozens of girls from wealthy families in various counties to the humiliation of being bound and beaten?" The next day, he was executed in public. Pointing to the spot where he was to kneel, he said: "In three years, the person who will interrogate me will be here." Soon after, the provincial governor was indeed punished for some matter, and everyone found this remarkable.

    823

    余谓此事与《明史》所载嘉靖年间妖人桑相同，桑不报仇而洪乃报仇，何耶？

    　　○预知科名族弟袁楠，作秀才时，癸酉乡试，因有家难，场前奔走倦矣。入闱，进洪字三号。天已晚，即铺板熟睡。二鼓后，闻有人问：“何号是袁相公？”不觉惊起。

    　　其人乃同考秀才，素不相识者，问：“君姓袁，可名楠乎？”曰：“然。”其人拱手作贺曰：“君已中矣。”问：“何以知之？”曰：“我临安人，姓谢，与君同号。顷睡梦间，闻外喊题目纸声甚急。及取之，只一纸，首题是‘邦有道，危言危行’二句”。其时同号中有六七十人，嘈嘈争问：“题目何止一纸？‘外答曰：”此号只中洪字第三号袁某，应行一纸耳，君既坐此号，名姓皆符，故来相报。“袁谢而颔之。

    Explanation

    823

    I believe this event is similar to what is recorded in the Ming Shi (History of the Ming Dynasty) about the heretic Sang during the Jiajing period. Why is it that Sang did not seek revenge, while Hong did?

    ○ My younger brother, Yuan Nan, who was a scholar, was preparing for the provincial examination in the year Guiyou. Due to family difficulties, he became exhausted before the exam. Upon entering the examination hall, he was assigned the number "Hong Sanhao." It was already late, and he immediately laid down to sleep. After the second drumbeat, he heard someone ask, "Which number is Master Yuan?" He was startled awake.

    The person asking was a fellow candidate for the examination, someone he did not know. The person asked, "Do you have the surname Yuan, and is your name Nan?" Yuan Nan replied, "Yes." The person bowed and congratulated him, saying, "You have already passed." Yuan Nan asked, "How do you know?" The person replied, "I am from Lin'an, and my surname is Xie. I share the same exam number as you. Just now, in a dream, I heard the announcement of the exam questions, which were only one paper. The first question was 'If the state is governed rightly, speak frankly and act uprightly.'" At that time, there were about sixty to seventy people sharing the same exam number, and they were all asking in confusion, "How could there be only one paper of questions?" The person outside replied, "This number only passes the third candidate with the surname Yuan from the 'Hong Sanhao' number. Since you have this number and your name matches, I came to inform you." Yuan Nan thanked him and nodded in acknowledgment.

    824

    黎明，题纸出，果如其言，乃大喜，自命必中，纵笔疾书，文如宿构，榜发，竟登第。

    　　○胡鹏南胡公鹏南，巡视中城。一日，闻姊病，往视之。妹已昏迷，闻胡至，谡然而起曰：“弟来视我甚善，然弟宜速归。”胡不肯，姊起用手推之，家人子弟不解其故。胡既归，姊语家人曰：“我方死去，押差将送我至城隍府，路遇旌旗皂役曰：”旧城隍升去，新城隍到任，汝且将女犯押回。‘问：“新城隍何人？’曰：‘吏科给事中胡鹏南也。’我惊醒，不意鹏南即坐我床上，故我劝令还家，汝等可速往视之。”如其言，胡已沐浴朝服无疾而逝矣。胡乃春圃座师。

    Explanation

    824

    At dawn, the exam questions were released, and they turned out exactly as he had predicted. He was greatly pleased and declared himself certain to pass. He wrote freely and quickly, his essay as if pre-written. When the list of successful candidates was announced, he was finally listed among them.

    ○Tr. Tr. Hu Pengnan, the Venerable Master Hu, was the inspector of the central city. One day, he heard that his sister was ill and went to visit her. His sister had already fallen into a coma, but upon hearing that Hu had arrived, she suddenly sat up and said, "It's good that you came to see me, but you should return home quickly." Hu refused, and his sister got up and pushed him away. The family members did not understand why. After Hu returned home, his sister told the family, "I was just about to die, and an official was escorting me to the City God's office. On the way, we encountered a banner and officials who said, 'The old City God has ascended, and the new City God has taken office. You should escort the female prisoner back.' I asked, 'Who is the new City God?' They replied, 'It is the Censorial Officer of the Censorate, Hu Pengnan.' I was awakened in a start, and I did not expect that Pengnan would be sitting on my bed. That is why I advised him to return home. You should go and see him quickly." As she said, Hu had already bathed, dressed in court attire, and passed away peacefully. Hu was the teacher of Chunpu.

    825

    ○龙护高家堰乾隆二十七年，学使李公因培科考淮安。清晨，风雨怒号，生徒惊顾，不能唱名。正踌躇间，地大震，辕外旗竿，被龙攫入云中，不知所往，河水暴涨，与高家堰相齐。河督高公及各厅官面如土色，皆云西风一大，则淮扬休矣。方恐怖间，忽转东风，天低若盖，将压人头，见黑龙在云中拖尾取水，数卷后，顷刻之间，洪泽湖水低三丈，人心大安。龙之鳞甲金光四射，惟头身则不可见。此石埭县教官沈公雨潭所目击。

    Explanation

    825

    In the 27th year of Qianlong, the dragon protected the Gaojiayan embankment, and Mr. Li Yinpei, the provincial examiner, administered the provincial examination in Huai'an. In the early morning, fierce winds and rain howled, and the students were startled and looked around, unable to call out names. Just as they were hesitating, a massive earthquake occurred, and the flagpole outside the carriage was grabbed by a dragon and pulled into the clouds, disappearing from sight. The river water surged, reaching the level of the Gaojiayan embankment. Governor Gao and the officials of various bureaus had ashen faces, all saying that if the west wind intensified, Huaiyang would be doomed. Just as they were terrified, the wind suddenly turned east, and the sky seemed to lower like a lid, about to press down on people's heads. They saw a black dragon dragging its tail to fetch water, and after several turns, the water level of Hongze Lake dropped by three zhang in the blink of an eye, and people's hearts were greatly reassured. The dragon's scales shone with golden light, but only the head and body were not visible. This was witnessed by Mr. Shen Yutan, the county schoolmaster of Shidai.

    826

    ○雷公被污沈公又云：是年淮安有雷轰轰然将击孤贫院中一老妇。妇方解裤溲，心急甚，即以马桶泼之，随见金甲者绕屋而下。少顷，有雷神蹲老妇之旁，尖嘴黑身，长二尺许，腰下有黑皮如裙遮掩下体，瞪目无言，两翅闪闪摇动不止。居民报知山阳县官，官遣道士来画符建醮，以清水沃其头，至十馀石，次日复雨，才能飞去。

    　　○李文贞公梦兆李相公光地未贵时，祈梦于九龙滩庙。神赠诗一联云：“富贵无心想，功名两不成。”李意颇恶之。后中戊戌科进士，为宰相，方知“戊戌”两字皆似“成”

    　　字而非“成”字，“想”字去“心”恰成“相”字。

    　　○鬼求路引德龄安孝廉，知太仓州事。内幕某，浙人也，偶染时症。一夕，大呼曰：“归欤！归欤！胡不归？”察其音，陕人也。问：“何以不归？”曰：“无路引。”

    Explanation

    826

    ○ The Thunder God's Defilement Shen Gong further recounted: In that year, there was a thunderstorm in Huai'an, and the thunder was rumbling as if it was about to strike an elderly woman in a charity house for the poor. The woman was just relieving herself, feeling very urgent, so she immediately poured water from her portable toilet. Soon after, a divine being in golden armor circled the house and descended. Shortly, a thunder god sat beside the old woman, with a pointed beak and a black body, about two feet long, with black skin like a skirt covering its lower body, staring silently, and its wings flickering and moving incessantly. The residents reported this to the official of Shanyang County, who then sent a Taoist priest to draw talismans and set up altars. They poured clear water over the thunder god's head, using more than ten buckets, and the next day, it rained again, and the thunder god could finally fly away.

    ○ The Omen of Li Wenzheng Li Wenzheng, before he became prominent, prayed for a dream at the Nine Dragon Bay Temple. The deity gave him a couplet of poetry: "Wealth and rank are not in one's thoughts, neither merit nor reputation is achieved." Li Wenzheng was quite displeased with it. Later, he passed the imperial examination in the year of the Horse (戊戌) and became a prime minister, only then realizing that the characters "戊戌" both resembled the character "成" but were not "成," and that removing the heart radical from the character "想" exactly formed the character "相."

    ○ The Ghost's Request for a Death Certificate An Deqing, a scholar-official serving as the magistrate of Taicang Prefecture, had someone in his entourage from Zhejiang who accidentally contracted a seasonal illness. One night, the person suddenly shouted, "Return home! Return home! Why not return?" Upon listening to his accent, it was clear he was from Shaanxi. An Deqing asked, "Why haven't you returned?" The person replied, "I have no death certificate."

    827

    问：“何以死于此。”曰：“我宁夏人，姓莫，名容非，前太仓刺史赵酉远亲也。

    　　万里赍粮而来，为投赵故。赵刺史反拒不纳，且一文不赠，故穷馁怨死于此。“

    　　问：“何以不缠赵？幕友与汝宁有冤乎？”曰：“赵已他迁，鬼无路引不能出境，缠他人无益，故来缠幕友，庶几惊动主人，哀怜幕友，必与我路引。”德公闻而许之，召吏房作文书，咨明一路河神关吏，放莫容非魂归故乡。幕友病不医而愈。

    　　○石揆谛晖石揆、谛晖二僧，皆南能教也。石揆参禅，谛晖持戒，两人各不相下。谛晖住杭州灵隐寺，香花极盛。石揆谋夺之。会天竺祈雨，石揆持咒召黑龙行雨，人共见之，以为神。谛晖闻知，即避去，隐云栖最僻处，石揆为灵隐长老，垂三十年。身本万历孝廉，口若悬河，灵隐兰若之会，震动一时。

    Explanation

    827

    Translation:

    Asked, "Why did you die here?" he replied, "I am from Ningxia, my surname is Mo, and my given name is Rongfei. I am a distant relative of the former Tai仓刺史 Zhao You.

    I traveled thousands of miles bringing provisions for the purpose of visiting Zhao. The Tai仓刺史 Zhao refused to receive me and did not give even a single coin, therefore, I died here of hunger and resentment."

    Asked, "Why didn't you bother Zhao? Does the guest have a grudge against you?" he replied, "Zhao has moved elsewhere. Ghosts cannot leave the realm without a travel permit. Bothering others is of no use. Therefore, I came to bother the guest, hoping to arouse the master's pity and give me a travel permit." Dongong heard and agreed, calling the clerk's office to write a document, consulted the river gods and customs officials along the way, allowed Mo Rongfei's soul to return to his hometown. The guest's illness was cured without treatment.

    Shikui and Dihui are two monks, both are followers of the Southern School. Shikui practices meditation, Dihui observes precepts, the two do not yield to each other. Dihui resides at the Lingyin Temple in Hangzhou, the temple is very prosperous. Shikui planned to take it over. Just as the Tianzhu Temple was praying for rain, Shikui chanted a spell to summon the black dragon to bring rain. People all saw it and thought it was a miracle. Dihui heard about it and immediately left, hiding in the most secluded part of Yunqi. Shikui became the abbot of Lingyin Temple, for nearly thirty years. Originally a scholar-official of the Wanli era, speaks eloquently, the gathering at Lingyin Temple caused a sensation at the time.

    828

    有沈氏儿丧父母，为人佣工，随施主入寺。石揆见之大惊，愿乞此儿为弟子，施主许之。儿方七岁，即为延师教读。儿欲肉食，即与之肉，儿欲衣绣，即衣之绣。不削发也。儿亦聪颖，通举子业。年将冠矣，督学某考杭州，令儿应考，取名近思，遂取中府学第三名。

    　　月余，石揆传集合寺诸僧曰：“近思，余小沙弥也，何得瞒我入学为生员耶？”

    　　命跪佛前剃其发，披以袈裟，改名“逃佛”。同学诸生闻之大怒，连名数百人上控巡抚学院，道“奸僧敢剃生员发，援儒入墨，不法已甚！”有项霜泉者，仁和学霜也，率家僮数十篡取近思，为假辫以饰之，即以己妹配之，置酒作乐，聚三学弟子员赋《催妆诗》作贺。诸大府虽与石揆交，而众怒难犯，不得已，准诸生所控，许近思蓄发为儒。诸生犹不服，各汹汹然，欲焚灵隐寺殴石揆。大府不得已，取石揆两侍者，各笞十五，群忿始息。

    Explanation

    828

    A child of the Shen family, who had lost his parents, worked as a laborer for others and followed a benefactor into a temple. Shi Kui, upon seeing him, was greatly surprised and wished to adopt the child as a disciple. The benefactor agreed. The child was just seven years old, and immediately Shi Kui hired a teacher to educate him. If the child wanted meat, it was given to him; if he wanted embroidered clothes, he was given them. However, he did not shave his head. The child was also intelligent and proficient in the studies required for the imperial examinations. By the time he was about to reach adulthood, the supervisor of education in Hangzhou ordered the child to take the exam and named him Jinsi. He was ranked third in the prefectural school.

    A month later, Shi Kui gathered all the monks in the temple and said, "Jinsi, you are my young novice. How could you瞒 me and enter school as a scholar?" He ordered him to kneel before the Buddha, shave his head, dress him in a monk's robe, and change his name to "Escape Buddha." The fellow students heard of this and were greatly angry, joining names and petitioning the governor and the school superintendent, saying, "The wicked monk dares to shave the head of a scholar, mixing Confucianism with Buddhism, which is extremely unlawful!" There was Xiang Shuangquan, the head of the Renshou school, who led dozens of servants to forcibly take Jinsi, made a fake queue to cover his head, and immediately married his own sister to him. They held a banquet and made music, gathering students from three schools to compose a Cui Zhuang poem to congratulate. The officials, though they were friends with Shi Kui, found it difficult to face the public anger and reluctantly allowed the students' petition, permitting Jinsi to keep his hair and be a Confucian scholar. The students were still不服, each looking very angry, and wanted to burn Lingyin Temple and beat Shi Kui. The officials, unable to do otherwise, took two of Shi Kui's attendants and each beat them fifteen times. The public anger finally subsided.

    829

    后一月，石揆命侍者撞钟鼓召集合寺僧，各持香一炷礼佛毕，泣曰：“此予负谛晖之报也。灵隐本谛晖所住地，而予以一念争胜之心夺之，此念延绵不已，念己身灭度后，非有大福分人，不能撑持此地。沈氏儿风骨严整，在人间为一品官，在佛家为罗汉身，故余见而倾心，欲以此坐与之。又一念争胜，欲使佛法胜于孔子，故先使入学，以继我孝廉出身之衣钵，此皆贪嗔未灭之客气也。今侍儿受杖，为辱已甚，尚何面目坐方丈乎？夫儒家之改过，即佛家之忏悔也，自今以往，吾将赴释梵天王处忏悔百年，才能得道。诸弟子速持我禅杖一枝，白玉钵盂一个、紫衣袈裟一袭往迎谛晖，为我补过。”群僧合掌跪泣曰：“谛晖逃出已三十年，音耗寂然，从何地迎接？”曰：“现在云栖第几山第几寺，户外有松一株、井一口，汝第记此去访可也。”言毕，趺坐而逝，鼻垂玉柱二尺许。群僧如其言，果得谛晖。

    Explanation

    829

    One month later, Shi Kui ordered his attendants to strike the bells and drums to assemble all the monks of the temple. Each monk held a stick of incense and finished worshipping Buddha, then cried and said, "This is my retribution for wronging Di Hui. Lingyin Temple was originally Di Hui's place of residence, but I took it with a competitive mindset. This thought has persisted, and I now think that after my own passing, only someone with great fortune can maintain this place. Shen's son has a dignified and upright character; in the secular world, he is a high-ranking official, and in the Buddhist world, he is an arhat. Therefore, I was deeply impressed by him and wanted to give him this position. Another competitive thought arose, and I wanted to make Buddhism superior to Confucianism, so I first sent him to school to continue my scholarly lineage. All of this was due to unextinguished greed and anger. Now, my servant has been punished, and I feel great shame. How can I face the abbot's quarters? In Confucianism, repentance is the same as confession in Buddhism. From now on, I will confess for a hundred years in front of the Buddha and the celestial kings to achieve enlightenment. My disciples, quickly bring me a Zen staff, a white jade bowl, and a purple robe and cassock to welcome Di Hui and make up for my mistakes." The monks joined hands and cried, saying, "Di Hui has been missing for thirty years, with no news. How can we find him?" Shi Kui replied, "He is now at Yunxi Temple, which is located in a certain mountain and temple. Outside the temple, there is a pine tree and a well. Remember these details and go to visit." After he finished speaking, he sat in lotus position and passed away, with his nose hanging like a two-foot-long jade column. The monks followed his instructions and indeed found Di Hui.

    830

    沈后中进士，官左都御史，立朝有声，谥清恪。虽贵，每言石揆养育之恩，未尝不泣下也。

    　　谛晖有老友恽某，常州武进人，逃难外出披甲，有儿年七岁，卖杭州驻防都统家，谛晖欲救出之。会杭州二月十九日观音生日，满汉士女，咸往天竺进香，过灵隐必拜方丈大和尚。谛晖道行高，贵官男女膜手来拜者以万数，从无答礼。

    Explanation

    830

    Shen Hou passed the imperial examination and became a Jinshi, later serving as the Left Censor-in-Chief, where he was distinguished and well-regarded. He was posthumously honored with the title "Qingke" (Pure and Respectful). Despite his high status, he always spoke of the kindness of his foster father, Shikui, with tears.

    Dihui had an old friend named Yun, who was from Wujin, Changzhou. Yun had escaped during a time of trouble and joined the military, leaving behind a seven-year-old son who was sold into the household of the garrison commander in Hangzhou. Dihui wanted to rescue the child. Coincidentally, on February 19th, the day of Guanyin's birthday, both Manchu and Han men and women went to Bodhi Temple in Hangzhou to offer incense, and as they passed through Lingyin Temple, they had to pay respects to the abbot. Dihui, known for his high moral standing, received countless visits from high-ranking officials and their families, but he never returned their courtesies.

    831

    都统夫人某，从苍头婢仆数十人来拜谛晖，谛晖探知瘦而纤者恽氏儿也，矍然起，跪儿前，膜拜不止，曰：“罪过！罪过！”夫人大惊问故，曰：“此地藏王菩萨也，托生人间，访人善恶。夫人奴畜之，无礼已甚，闻又鞭扑之，从此罪孽深重，祸不旋踵矣！”夫人皇急求救，曰：“无可救。”夫人愈恐，告都统。

    　　都统亲来长跪不起，必求开一线佛门之路。谛晖曰：“非特公有罪，僧亦有罪，地藏王来寺而僧不知迎，罪亦大矣。请以香花清水供养地藏王入寺，缓缓为公夫妇忏悔，并为自己忏悔。”都统大喜，布施百万，以儿与谛晖。谛晖教之读书学画，取名寿平，后即纵之还家，曰：“吾不学石揆痴也。”后寿平画名日噪，诗文清妙。

    Explanation

    831

    A certain lady of the governor, accompanied by dozens of young male and female servants, came to pay respects to Dihui. Dihui discovered that the thin and slender one was the son of the Yuen family, and suddenly stood up, knelt in front of the child, and kept prostrating himself, saying, "Misfortune! Misfortune!" The lady was extremely alarmed and asked why. Dihui replied, "This is the Bodhisattva of Ksitigarbha, who has taken birth in the human realm to observe the good and evil of people. The lady treats him like a slave, which is already extremely disrespectful; and I have heard that she also beat and whipped him, so from now on, his sins will be deep and heavy, and misfortune will follow immediately!" The lady became extremely anxious and sought help, but Dihui said, "There is no way to save it." The lady grew even more fearful and informed the governor.

    The governor personally came, knelt for a long time without rising, and insisted on opening a path to the Buddhist door. Dihui said, "Not only is the governor guilty, but the monk is also guilty. The Bodhisattva of Ksitigarbha came to the temple, but the monk did not know to welcome him, which is also a great sin. Please offer incense, flowers, and clear water to welcome the Bodhisattva of Ksitigarbha into the temple, and slowly perform repentance for the governor and his wife, and also for oneself." The governor was greatly pleased, made a donation of a million, and gave the child to Dihui. Dihui taught him to read, write, and paint, and gave him the name Shouping. Later, he was allowed to return home, saying, "I will not learn the foolishness of Shi Kui." Afterward, Shouping's reputation as a painter grew, and his poetry and prose were clear and exquisite.

    832

    人或问恽、沈二人优劣，谛晖曰：“沈近思学儒不能脱周、程、张、朱窠臼，恽寿平学画。能出文、沈、唐、仇范围，以吾观之，恽为优也。”言未已，以戒尺自击其颈曰：“又与石揆争胜矣，不可，不可！”谛晖寿一百零四岁。

    　　○天上四花园嘉兴祝孝廉维诰为中书舍人，好扶乩，言休咎往往有应者。将死前一月，乩仙自称：“我天上看园叟也，特来奉迎。”祝问：“天上安得有园？”叟云：“天上花园甚多，不能言其数，但我所管领者，四园三主人耳。”问：“主人为谁？”曰：“冒辟疆、张广泗，其一则足下也。”祝问：“冒与张绝不相伦，何以共在一处？”曰：“君等三人皆隶仙籍，冒降生为公子，享福太多，现今未许复位，园尚荒芜。张福力最大，以作经略时杀降太多，上帝怒之，将置冥狱，幸而生前已罹国法，故犹许住园。君在世无过无功，今阳数将终，可来复位。”言毕，乩盘不动。是年，祝病亡。

    Explanation

    832

    Translation

    Some people asked about the merits of Yun and Shen. Dihui said, "Shen Jinsi studied Confucianism but could not break free from the traditions of Zhou Dunyi, Cheng Hao, Cheng Yi, and Zhu Xi. Yun Shouping studied painting and was able to transcend the boundaries set by Wen Zhengming, Shen Zhou, Tang Yin, and Qiu Ying. In my opinion, Yun is superior." While speaking, he hit his own neck with a ruler and said, "I am again competing with Shi Kui for superiority, which is not good, not good!" Dihui lived to be 104 years old.

    ○ Four Gardens in Heaven

    Zhu Xianlian from Jiaxing was a Zhongshu She Ren. He enjoyed using a spirit board, and his predictions often came true. One month before his death, the spirit board claimed to be a garden keeper in heaven, here to welcome him. Zhu asked, "How can there be gardens in heaven?" The old man said, "There are many gardens in heaven, too many to count, but I manage four gardens and three masters." Zhu asked, "Who are the masters?" The old man replied, "They are Mao Bijiang, Zhang Guangsi, and you." Zhu asked, "Mao and Zhang are not of the same class, why are they together?" The old man replied, "All three of you are listed in the celestial registry. Mao was born as a noble and enjoyed too much happiness, so he is not allowed to return to his position, and his garden is still desolate. Zhang had the greatest good fortune, but he killed too many surrendering soldiers when he was a military governor, angering the Heavenly Emperor, who would have sent him to hell. Fortunately, he was already punished by law in life, so he is still allowed to stay in the garden. You have neither virtue nor sin in life, and now your earthly fate is coming to an end, so you can return to your position." After speaking, the spirit board stopped moving. That year, Zhu died of illness.

    833

    ○碌碡作怪常州武生某，素有力。往金陵乡试，路过龙潭，见一妇坐门首，因口渴，向其索茶。妇以生不分男女，大骂闭门进去。生思不与茶则已，何至詈骂，气甚不平。见其田中卧碌碡一条，即用力擎起，架于树上而去。明日，妇开门见之，询邻人，皆曰：“此物非数人不能动，莫非树神所为乎！”因朝夕敬礼，有求必应。

    Explanation

    833

    A martial arts student from Changzhou, who was usually strong, went to Jinling for the provincial examination. On his way, he passed through Longtan and saw a woman sitting at the door. Because he was thirsty, he asked her for tea. The woman scolded him because she thought he didn't distinguish between men and women, and she closed the door. The student thought that not giving tea was enough, why did she scold, and he was very upset. He saw a large stone roller lying in her field and immediately lifted it up, placed it on a tree, and left. The next day, the woman opened the door and saw the stone roller. She asked her neighbors, and they all said, "This thing cannot be moved by several people, could it be the work of a tree spirit?" Therefore, she worshiped it every day and night, and all her requests were fulfilled.

    834

    或侮慢之，即有不利。如是者月余。

    　　生试毕归家，仍过其地。见所置碌碡尚在树间，其下香火罗列，禳祷者纷纷，心知为己所误，笑而不言。是晚，宿店中，思此事终是惑众，必转去说明方好。

    　　忽朦胧睡去，见有人告曰：“我某处鬼也，游魂到此，假托树神，以图血食。君新科贵人，故不敢隐瞒。若肯见容不说破，感恩非浅。”言毕不见。生遂不转去，径回常州。是科榜发，果中举人。

    　　○风流具长安蒋生，户部员外某第三子也，风流自喜。偶步海岱门，见车上妇美，初窥之，妇不介意；乃随其车而尾之，妇有愠色，蒋尾不已，妇转嗔为笑，以手招蒋。蒋喜出意外，愈往追车，妇亦回头顾盼若有情者。蒋神魂迷荡，不知两足之蹒跚也。

    　　行七八里，至一大宅，车中妇入。蒋痴立门外，不敢近，又不忍去。徘徊间，有小婢出手招蒋，且指示宅旁小门。蒋依婢往，乃溷圊所也。婢低语：“少待。”

    Explanation

    834

    If someone insults or treats him disrespectfully, there will be misfortune. Such a situation lasted for over a month.

    After passing the examination, the scholar returned home and passed by the place again. He saw the millstone he had placed still hanging in the tree, with incense and offerings arranged below, and people praying and making offerings in a crowd. Knowing it was due to his own mistake, he smiled but said nothing. That evening, he stayed at an inn, thinking that this matter ultimately deceives the people, and it is best to go back and explain. Suddenly, he fell into a drowsy sleep and saw someone who said, "I am a ghost from somewhere, wandering here as a spirit, pretending to be a tree god to seek blood offerings. Since you are a newly honored scholar, I dare not hide this. If you are willing to overlook this and not reveal it, my gratitude will be profound." After speaking, the person disappeared. The scholar then did not return, but went straight back to Changzhou. When the list of successful candidates was posted, he indeed passed the examination.

    A man named Jiang from Chang'an, the third son of a certain official in the Ministry of Revenue, was proud of his own charm. Once, while walking by the Sea and Dai Gate, he saw a beautiful woman in a carriage. At first glance, the woman did not take notice; he then followed her carriage, and the woman showed signs of displeasure. Jiang continued to follow, and the woman turned her anger into a smile, using her hand to beckon him. Jiang was delighted beyond expectation and went even further to chase the carriage, while the woman also looked back with a glance that seemed to have feelings. Jiang was so enchanted that he was unaware of his own stumbling.

    After walking for seven or eight li, they arrived at a large mansion, and the woman in the carriage entered. Jiang stood dazed outside the gate, not daring to approach but also unwilling to leave. While hesitating, a young maid reached out and beckoned Jiang, also pointing to a small side door of the mansion. Jiang followed the maid, and it turned out to be a latrine. The maid whispered, "Wait a moment."

    835

    蒋忍臭秽，屏息良久。日渐落，小婢出，引入，历厨灶数重，到厅院，甚堂皇，上垂朱帘，两僮倚帘立。蒋窃喜，以为入洞天仙子府矣，重整冠，拂拭眉目，径上厅。

    　　厅南大炕上坐一丈夫，麻黑大胡，箕踞两腿，毛如刺猬，倚隐囊怒喝曰：“尔何人？来此何为？”蒋惊骇身战，不觉屈膝。未及对，闻环佩声，车中妇出于室，胡者抱坐膝上，指谓生曰：“此吾爱姬，名珠团，果然美也。汝爱之原有眼力，第物各有主，汝竟想吃天龙肉耶？何痴妄乃尔！”言毕，故意将妇人交唇摩乳以夸示之。生窘急，叩头求去。胡者曰：“有兴而来，不可败兴而去。”问：“何姓？父何官？”生以实告。胡者笑曰：“而愈妄矣，而翁，吾同部友也，为人子侄而欲污其伯父之妾，可乎？”顾左右取大杖，“吾将为吾友训子。”一僮持枣木棍长丈余，一僮直前按其项仆地，裤剥下，双臀呈矣，生哀号甚惨。

    Explanation

    835

    Jiang endured the foul odors and held his breath for a long time. As the sun set, a young maid came out and led him in, passing through several kitchens, to the hall, which was very grand. Red curtains hung down, and two attendants stood leaning against the curtain. Jiang secretly rejoiced, thinking he had entered the palace of a celestial maiden. He straightened his hat, wiped his eyebrows and eyes, and walked straight to the hall.

    On a large kang in the southern hall sat a man with black, coarse hair, sitting with his legs spread out like a winnowing basket, his hair like that of a hedgehog, leaning against a hidden pillow and shouting angrily, "Who are you? What brings you here?" Jiang was terrified and shivered, not realizing he had bent his knee. Before he could answer, he heard the sound of jade ornaments and a woman from a carriage came out of the room. The man with coarse hair picked her up and sat her on his knee, pointing to Jiang and saying, "This is my beloved concubine, named Zhu Tuan, and indeed she is beautiful. You have good taste in loving her, but things have their owners. Do you really want to eat the meat of a dragon? How delusional are you!" After saying this, he deliberately kissed and rubbed the woman's lips and breasts to show off. Jiang was in a panic, bowed and begged to leave. The man with coarse hair said, "Having come with enthusiasm, one cannot leave in disappointment." He asked, "What is your surname? What office does your father hold?" Jiang truthfully answered. The man with coarse hair laughed and said, "You are even more delusional. Your father, my friend in the same department, as a nephew or younger relative, wanting to defile his uncle's concubine, is it possible?" He turned to the side and fetched a large stick, "I will discipline my friend's son." One attendant held a long zao wood stick, more than ten feet long, and another pushed Jiang to his knees, stripped off his pants, exposing his buttocks, and Jiang wailed in great pain.

    836

    妇人走下榻跽跪而请曰：“奴乞爷开恩。奴见渠臀比奴臀更柔白，以杖击之，渠不能当；以龙阳待之，渠尚能受。”胡者叱曰：“渠我同寅儿也，不可无礼！”妇又请曰：“凡人上庙买物，必挟买物之具，渠挟何具以来，请验之。”胡者喝验，两僮手摩其阴报曰：“细如小蚕，皮未脱棱。”胡者搔其面曰：“羞！羞！挟此恶具，而欲唐突人妇，尤可恶。”掷小刀与两僮曰：“渠爱风流，为修整其风流之具。”僮持小刀握生阴，将剥其皮。生愈惶急，涕雨下。妇两颊亦发赤，又下榻请曰：“爷太恶谑！使奴大惭。奴想吃饽饽，有五斗麦未磨，毛驴又病，不如着渠代驴磨面赎罪。”胡者问：“愿否？”生连声应诺，妇人拥胡者高卧。俩僮负麦及磨石至，命生于窗外磨麦，两僮以鞭驱之。

    　　东方大白，炕上呼云：“昨蒋郎苦矣，赐饽饽一个，开狗洞放归。”生出，大病一月。

    Explanation

    836

    The woman descended from the bed, knelt, and pleaded, saying, "I beg for your mercy, sir. I see that his buttocks are softer and whiter than mine. If you beat him, he cannot withstand it; if you treat him in a lewd manner, he can still endure it." The man scolded, "He is my colleague, and we cannot be disrespectful to him!" The woman then pleaded again, "Generally, when people go to a temple to buy things, they carry the tools needed to buy them. What tools did he bring, and please check them." The man ordered an inspection, and the two boys touched his genital area and reported, "It is as fine as a silkworm, and the skin is not fully developed." The man scratched his face and said, "Shame! Shame! Bringing such a disgusting tool and attempting to violate a woman is especially despicable." He threw a knife to the two boys and said, "He likes to be well-groomed, so let's fix his 'windy' equipment." The boys held the knife and grasped his genital area, preparing to peel it. The man became more panicked, and tears flowed. The woman's cheeks also turned red, and she knelt again, saying, "Sir, you are too cruelly teasing me, making me very ashamed. I want to eat buns, but there is five dou of wheat that needs to be ground, and the donkey is sick. It would be better to have him grind the flour as a form of atonement." The man asked, "Are you willing?" The man repeatedly agreed. The woman embraced the man and they lay down together. The two boys carried the wheat and millstone over, and they ordered the man to grind the wheat outside the window. The two boys drove him with whips.

    At dawn, the man on the bed called out, "Painful was the experience of Mr. Jiang last night. Grant me one bun, open the dog hole, and let me return." The man left, and fell seriously ill for a month.

    837

    ○骗人参京师张广号人参铺甚大。一日，有骑马少年负银一囊到店，先取百两与作样，而徐取参数包阅之，曰：“我主人性琐碎，买参不如其意，必加呵责，我又不善择参，可否存此样银于店，命老成伙计多带上等参同往主人处，凭其自择何如？”

    　　店家以为然，即收银遣店中叟负参数斤偕往，临行嘱曰：“谨持参，勿落他人手也。”

    　　进东华门，至一大府第，少年同登楼，楼上主人美须眉，披貂裘，戴蓝宝石顶，病奄然，倚枕踞床，自负参者曰：“所携参果辽东顶上者耶？”店叟唯唯。

    　　旁两僮捧参上，逐包开检，所批驳皆洞中行情。

    　　阅未毕，忽门外车马声甚喧，一客入。主人惶遽，命侍者下楼，辞以病不能会客，低语负参者曰：“此向我借债客也，断不可使上楼。彼上楼见我力能买参，则难以无钱相复矣。”客在楼下呼曰：“汝主病诈也，必是抱优童、娶小奶奶，不许我登楼。我偏欲上楼一看！”两侍者固拒之，争吵不已。

    Explanation

    837

    A Deceptive Scheme Involving Ginseng

    In the capital, there was a large ginseng shop named "Zhang Guang Ginseng Shop." One day, a young man riding a horse arrived at the shop with a bag of silver. He first took out a hundred taels to use as a sample, and then slowly examined several packages of ginseng, saying, "My master is very particular, and the ginseng I buy does not meet his expectations, so he will surely scold me. I am also not good at selecting ginseng. May I leave this sample silver with you in the shop and send an experienced employee with several top-grade packages of ginseng to my master's place, allowing him to choose for himself?"

    The shop owner thought this was a good idea and immediately took the silver, sending an elderly employee to accompany the ginseng to the master's place. Before leaving, the shop owner instructed the employee, "Handle the ginseng carefully and do not let it fall into the wrong hands."

    They entered through the Eastern Gate and arrived at a large mansion. The young man and the employee climbed the stairs together. On the upper floor, the master had beautiful eyebrows and a beard, wore a sable coat, and a blue sapphire hat. He was weak and sickly, leaning on a pillow and sitting on the bed. The young man said, "Is the ginseng I brought truly top-grade from Liaodong?" The elderly employee nodded in agreement.

    Two servants then brought the ginseng upstairs, opening and inspecting each package one by one. The rejected ginseng was all deeply understood in terms of market conditions.

    Before the inspection was complete, suddenly there was a loud noise of vehicles and horses outside the door. A guest entered. The master became alarmed and ordered the servants to go downstairs, explaining that he was too ill to receive guests. He whispered to the employee, "This is a guest who owes me money. It is absolutely not allowed for him to go upstairs. If he sees that I have the means to buy ginseng, it will be difficult for him to repay me without money." The guest shouted from downstairs, "Your master is pretending to be sick! He must be having a concubine or a new wife, so he does not allow me to go upstairs. I especially want to go upstairs and have a look!" The two servants firmly refused him, and the argument continued without end.

    838

    主人愈惶急，又低语负参者曰：“速藏参！速藏参！毋为恶客所见！床下竹箱可以安放。”以铜锁钥匙付之曰：“汝坐箱上护守参，我自下楼见彼，或能止其上楼，亦未可定。”踉跄下楼，与客始而寒暄，继而戏骂。客必欲上楼，主人又固拒之。客大怒曰：“汝不过防我借银耳！虑我见汝楼上有银故也。如此薄待我，我即去，永不再来！”主人阳为谢罪，送客出，僮仆亦随之出，许久寂然。

    　　负参者端坐箱上以待；良久不至，始有疑意。开锁取参，参不见。藏参之箱，一活底箱也，箱底板即楼板。方戏骂时，从楼下脱板取参，守参者不知也。

    　　○偷画有白日入人家偷画者，方卷出门，主人自外归。贼窘，持画而跪曰：“此小人家祖宗像也，穷极无奈，愿以易米数斗。”主人大笑，嗤其愚妄，挥叱之去，竟不取视。登堂，则所悬赵子昂画失矣。

    Explanation

    838

    The host became even more panicked and whispered to the servant carrying the ginseng, "Hurry up and hide the ginseng! Hurry up and hide the ginseng! Do not let the bad guest see it! There is a bamboo box under the bed where it can be stored." He handed over the copper lock and key and said, "You sit on the box to guard the ginseng, and I will go downstairs to see him. Perhaps I can stop him from going upstairs, but it's uncertain." He staggered down the stairs and exchanged pleasantries with the guest, then teased and scolded him. The guest insisted on going upstairs, and the host firmly refused him. The guest became very angry and said, "You're just afraid I'll borrow money, right? Worried that I might see the silver in your upstairs room, right? Treating me so poorly, I will leave and never come back again!" The host pretended to apologize and escorted the guest out, and the servants also followed out. After a long time, it was silent.

    The servant carrying the ginseng sat on the box waiting. For a long time, he did not arrive, and he began to feel suspicious. He opened the lock to take the ginseng, but the ginseng was not there. The box where the ginseng was hidden was a live-bottom box, and the bottom plate of the box was the floor of the upstairs room. While teasing and scolding, the thief removed the floorboard from downstairs to take the ginseng, and the person guarding the ginseng did not know.

    ○ There was a thief who stole paintings during the day. Just as he was about to leave with the painting, the host returned from outside. The thief was in a difficult situation and knelt with the painting, saying, "This is a portrait of my ancestors, and I am in dire straits. I am willing to trade it for a few measures of rice." The host laughed and mocked him for his foolishness, shouted at him, and sent him away, not even looking at it. When he went upstairs, the painting by Zhao Ziyang that was hanging was missing.

    839

    ○偷靴或着新靴行市上，一人向之长揖，握手寒暄，着靴者茫然曰：“素不相识。”

    　　其人怒骂曰：“汝着新靴便忘故人！”掀其帽掷瓦上去。着靴者疑此人醉，故酗酒。方彷徨间，又一人来笑曰：“前客何恶戏耶！尊头暴烈日中，何不上瓦取帽？”

    　　着靴者曰：“无梯奈何？”其人曰：“我惯作好事，以肩当梯，与汝踏上瓦何如？”

    　　着靴者感谢。乃蹲地上，耸其肩。着靴者将上，则又怒曰：“汝太性急矣！汝帽宜惜，我衫亦宜惜。汝靴虽新，靴底泥土不少，忍污我肩上衫乎？”着靴者愧谢，脱靴交彼，以袜踏肩而上，其人持靴径奔，取帽者高居瓦上，势不能下。市人以为两人交好，故相戏也，无过问者。失靴人哀告街邻，寻觅得梯才下，持靴者不知何处去矣。

    Explanation

    839

    A person wearing new shoes was walking in the market when someone bowed formally and greeted them with a handshake and small talk. The person wearing the shoes looked confused and said, "We have never met before."

    The person scolded angrily, "You wear new shoes and forget your old friends!" They grabbed the person's hat and threw it onto the roof tiles. The person wearing the shoes thought the other person was drunk and behaving oddly. As they were wandering aimlessly, another person came and laughed, "What kind of bad joke is this? Why don't you climb up to the roof to get your hat?"

    The person wearing the shoes said, "But there's no ladder, what should I do?" The other person replied, "I often do good deeds. Let me use my shoulder as a ladder. How about you climb onto the roof with me?"

    They crouched down and raised their shoulders. The person wearing the shoes tried to climb up, but the other person angrily said, "You're too impatient! You should take care of your hat, and I should take care of my shirt. Your shoes are new, but they have a lot of dirt on the soles. How can you let them dirty my shirt?"

    The person wearing the shoes felt ashamed and thanked them, then took off their shoes and handed them over. They climbed onto the roof with their socks on the other person's shoulders. The person who took the hat ran away with the shoes, leaving the person on the roof tiles, unable to come down. The people in the market thought the two were friends and were just playing a joke, so they didn't intervene. The person who lost the shoes begged the neighbors for help and found a ladder to come down. The person who took the shoes had already disappeared.

    840

    ○偷墙京中富人欲买砖造墙。某甲来曰：“某王府门外墙现欲拆旧砖换新砖，公何不买其旧者？”富人疑之曰：“王爷未必卖砖。”某甲曰：“微公言，某亦疑之，然某在王爷门下久，不妄言。公既不信，请遣人同至王府，候王出，某跪请，看王爷点头，再拆未迟。”富人以为然，遣家奴持弓尺偕往。故事：买旧砖者，以弓尺量若干长，可折二分算也。适王下朝，某甲拦王马头跪，作满洲语喃喃然。

    　　王果点头，以手指门前墙曰：“凭渠量。”甲即持弓尺率同往、奴量墙纵横算得十七丈七尺，该价百金，归告富人，富人喜，即予半价。

    Explanation

    840

    A Wealthy Man's Wall

    A wealthy man in the capital wanted to buy bricks to build a wall. A man named A came and said, "The wall outside a certain prince's mansion is currently being renovated, and they want to replace the old bricks with new ones. Why don't you buy the old ones?" The wealthy man doubted him and said, "The prince might not be selling the bricks." A replied, "Without your words, I would also doubt it, but I have been in the prince's service for a long time and do not speak falsely. If you do not believe me, please send someone with me to the prince's mansion to wait for the prince to come out. I will kneel and ask, and if the prince nods, then we can proceed with the demolition." The wealthy man thought this was reasonable and sent a servant with a measuring tape to go together. It is customary that when buying old bricks, one measures the length with a measuring tape and can deduct two parts from the total. Just as the prince descended from court, A blocked the prince's horse and knelt, muttering in a low, indistinct voice in the Manchu language.

    The prince indeed nodded and pointed to the wall in front of the mansion, saying, "Let him measure." A immediately took the measuring tape and led the way, and the servant measured the wall, calculating it to be seventeen zhang and seven chi in length, which amounted to a price of a hundred gold pieces. Upon returning to tell the wealthy man, he was pleased and immediately offered half the price.

    841

    择吉日，遣家奴率人往拆墙，王府司阍者大怒，擒问之，奴曰：“王爷所命也。”司阍者启王，王大笑曰：“某日跪马头白事者，自称某贝子家奴，主人要筑府外照墙，爱我墙式样，故来求丈量，以便如式砌筑。我以为此细事，有何不可，故手指墙命丈。事原有之，非云卖也。”富人谢罪求释，所费不赀，而某甲已逃。

    　　○鬼妒二则常德张太守之女，许周氏子，年十七以瘵疾亡。周别聘王氏女，年亦十七，甫缔姻，尚无婚期，王女忽中恶，以手批颊曰：“我张四小姐也。汝何人，敢夺我郎君？”周氏子闻之，告太守。太守夫人治家素严，闻之大怒，悬亡女画像骂曰：“汝与周郎连姻，尚未成亲，汝死，周郎再娶，亦礼之常，何以往害王家女，无耻若是！”骂毕，折桃枝击之。未数下，门外周郎奔来求饶，问：“何故？”

    Explanation

    841

    Choose a propitious day, and send a household servant to lead people to demolish the wall. The gatekeeper of the prince's mansion became greatly angry and arrested the servant to interrogate him. The servant said, "It was commanded by the prince." The gatekeeper reported this to the prince, who laughed and said, "On a certain day, the one who knelt before the horse and reported matters claimed to be a servant of a certain prince. The owner wanted to build a decorative wall outside the mansion and liked the style of our wall, so he came to request measurements in order to build it according to the same pattern. I thought this was a trivial matter and saw no harm in it, so I pointed to the wall and ordered the measurements. There was nothing wrong with the matter, and it was not about selling anything." The wealthy man apologized and sought release, spending an enormous amount of money, while the person known as "Mr. A" had already fled.

    　　○Two Stories of Ghostly Jealousy

    The daughter of the governor of Changde, Lady Zhang, was betrothed to a young man from the Zhou family. At the age of seventeen, she died from a wasting disease. The Zhou family then betrothed their daughter from the Wang family, who was also seventeen. They had just begun to arrange the marriage, but there was still no wedding date. Suddenly, the Wang family daughter fell ill with a seizure and slapped her own cheek, saying, "I am the Fourth Miss Zhang. Who are you, to dare to take my husband?" The young man from the Zhou family heard this and reported it to the governor. The governor's wife, who managed the household with great strictness, heard this and became very angry. She hung the portrait of the deceased daughter and scolded, "You were to be married to Zhou Lang, but you did not yet become his wife. After your death, Zhou Lang remarried, which is a normal practice according to the rites. Why did you go to harm the Wang family daughter in such a shameful way?" After she finished scolding, she broke off a branch of a peach tree and struck the portrait. Before she could strike a few times, Zhou Lang rushed in from outside to beg for mercy, asking, "Why is this happening?"

    842

    曰：“王女口称，张四小姐呼痛去矣，并求替他母亲说情，故婿特来。”王氏女竟愈。

    　　杭州马坡巷谢叟，卖鱼为业，生二女，俱有姿，有武生李某，见而习焉。李貌亦美，先有表妹王氏慕之，托人说婚，李却王氏，就婚于谢，王氏以瘵亡。谢嫁未逾月，忽披发佯狂，口称“我王氏也，汝一个卖鱼婆，何得我秀才？”取几上剪刀自刺其心曰：“取汝蜜罗柑。”谢叟夫妻往秀才家烧纸钱作斋醮跪求，卒不能救。问：“蜜罗柑何物？”曰：“你女儿之心肝也。”未几，女竟死。秀才又来求聘其妹，谢叟有戒心，不许妹悦其貌，曰：“我不畏鬼，如其来，我将挥刀杀之，为姊报仇。”谢不得已，仍嫁与之。婚后，鬼竟寂然，为秀才生一子而寡居。

    Explanation

    842

    The princess said, "Miss Zhang Four called out in pain and left, and requested that he plead for his mother, so the husband came specially." The Wang family daughter eventually recovered.

    In the Ma Slope Lane of Hangzhou, there was an elderly man named Xie who made a living by selling fish. He had two daughters, both of whom were beautiful. There was a martial arts student named Li who saw them and became familiar with them. Li was also handsome. Previously, he had a cousin named Wang who admired him, and she entrusted someone to propose marriage. Li refused Wang and married into the Xie family. Wang died of consumption. Xie had not been married for more than a month when suddenly, she untied her hair and acted mad, saying, "I am Wang. You, a fish-selling woman, how could you have my scholar?" She took a scissors from the table and stabbed her heart, saying, "Take your honey melon." Mr. and Mrs. Xie went to the scholar's house to burn paper money and perform a ritual to pray, but could not be saved in the end. They asked, "What is the honey melon?" She said, "It is your daughter's heart and liver." Not long after, the daughter died. The scholar came again to propose marriage to her sister. Mr. Xie had a wary heart and did not allow his sister to be charmed by his appearance, saying, "I do not fear ghosts. If he comes, I will kill him with a knife to avenge my sister." Xie, having no choice, still married him. After marriage, the ghost remained silent. She gave birth to a son for the scholar and remained a widow.

    843

    ○人面豆山江子七之乱，人死者多。平定后，田中黄豆生形如人面，老少男妇好丑不一，而耳目口鼻俱全，自颈以下皆有血影，土人呼为“人面豆”。

    　　○粉楦杭州范某，娶再婚妇，年五十馀，齿半落矣。奁具内橐橐有声，启视，则匣装两胡桃，不知其所用，以为偶遗落耳。次早，老妇临镜敷粉，两颊内陷，以齿落故，粉不能匀，呼婢曰：“取我粉楦来。”婢以胡桃进，妇取含两颊中，扑粉遂匀。杭州人从此戏呼胡桃为“粉楦”。

    　　○口琴崖州人能含细竹，装弦其上，以手拉之，上下如弹胡琴状，其声幽咽，号曰“口琴”。

    　　○芜湖朱生芜湖监生朱某，家富而啬，待奴仆尤苛。捐州牧入都，路出荏平，以一二文之微，痛笞其奴。奴怀恨，夜伺其睡，持所用锡溺壶击其顶门，脑裂而死。店主告官，置奴于法。

    Explanation

    843

    Translation:

    ○ Human-Faced Beans
    During the rebellion led by Jiang Ziqi in Shandong, many people perished. After the rebellion was quelled, yellow beans grew in the fields, taking on the shape of human faces. The faces varied in age, gender, and beauty, with all features—ears, eyes, mouth, and nose—complete. Below the neck, they bore blood-like shadows, and the locals called them "human-faced beans."

    ○ Powder Holder
    A man from Hangzhou named Fan, who married a second wife, was over fifty years old, and half of his teeth had fallen out. When he opened his bridal chest, he heard a rattling sound inside. Upon inspection, he found a box containing two walnuts, unsure of their purpose, and assumed they had been accidentally left behind. The next morning, as the elderly woman applied makeup by the mirror, her cheeks were sunken due to her missing teeth, and the powder did not apply evenly.
