She called her maid and said, "Bring me my powder holder." The maid brought the walnuts, and the woman placed them in her cheeks, allowing the powder to apply evenly. From then on, the people of Hangzhou jokingly referred to walnuts as "powder holders."

    ○ Oral Fiddle
    A person in Yanzhou could hold a thin bamboo tube in their mouth, string it, and pull it with their hands, creating a sound that rose and fell like a huqin (Chinese fiddle). The sound was soft and plaintive, and it was called an "oral fiddle."

    ○ Wuhu's Zhu
    Zhu, a wealthy and stingy supervisor of the Imperial College in Wuhu, treated his servants harshly. On his way to the capital to donate money for a prefectural office, he passed through Renping and severely beat his servant with just a few coins. The servant harbored resentment and waited for him to fall asleep one night. He then took the tin chamber pot Zhu used and struck him on the top of the head, causing his skull to split and leading to his death. The innkeeper reported the incident to the authorities, and the servant was punished according to the law.

    844

    后十年，芜湖赵孝廉会试，误投此店，灯下见赤身披血而立者曰：“我朱某也，欲有所求。”赵曰：“汝奴凌迟，汝冤已雪，汝复何求？”曰：“穷极求救。”

    　　曰：“汝身虽亡，汝家大富，汝虽为鬼，不合苦穷。”曰：“我死后方知，生前所有银钱，一丝不能带到阴间。奈阴间需用更甚于阳间，我客死于此，两手空空，为群鬼所不齿。公念故人之谊，烧些纸钱与我，以便与群鬼争雄。”问：“何不归？”曰：“凡人某处生，某处死，天曹都有定簿，非有大福力超度者，不能来往自如。横死者，阴司设阑干神严束之，故不能还故乡。”问：“纸钱纸也，阴司何所用之？”曰：“公此问误矣！阳间真钱亦铜也，饥不可食，寒不可衣，亦无所用，不过习俗所尚，人鬼自趋之耳。”言毕不见。赵哀之，为焚纸镪五千而行。

    Explanation

    844

    Ten years later, Zhao, a scholar-official from Wuhu, accidentally entered this inn while taking the metropolitan examination. In the dim light, he saw a figure standing naked and covered in blood, who said, "I am Mr. Zhu, and I have something to ask of you." Zhao replied, "Your servant was subjected to lingchi, and your wrong has been avenged. What more do you seek?" The ghost said, "I am in extreme poverty and seek help."

    Zhao said, "Though you are dead, your family is wealthy. Though you are a ghost, it is not fitting to be in poverty." The ghost replied, "I only knew this after my death. All the silver and money I had in life, not a single thread could be taken to the underworld. But the underworld requires more than the world of the living. I died as a stranger here, with nothing in my hands, and am looked down upon by other ghosts. Please, out of consideration for our old friendship, burn some paper money for me, so I can compete with the other ghosts." Zhao asked, "Why don't you return to your homeland?" The ghost said, "All people are born in a certain place and die in a certain place, and there are fixed records in heaven. Unless one has great merit and is liberated, one cannot move about freely. Those who die violently, the underworld gods strictly restrain them, so I cannot return to my homeland." Zhao then asked, "Paper money is just paper. What use does the underworld have for it?" The ghost replied, "Your question is mistaken! Real money in the world of the living is also copper; it cannot be eaten when hungry or worn when cold, and is also useless. It is just a custom that people and ghosts follow." After speaking, the ghost disappeared. Feeling sorry for him, Zhao burned 5,000 paper notes and continued on his way.

    845

    ○白日鬼有偷儿戚姓，技最工，攫取渐多，恐迹之者众，因僦义冢旁败屋居焉。有数鬼见梦曰：“若宜祀我，会且致富。”戚于梦中诺之，觉以为妄。亡何，鬼复见梦曰：“三日内祀我，出三日，则若于夜间所偷，予能白日取之。”戚倔强，觉而不祭。三日后，果大病，命其妻检视诸物，征鬼言验否。时日亭午，诸物忽自移动，若隐隐有运之者。欲起夺之，手足如缚，物尽而缚解，戚病亦痊。乃大悟，笑曰：“我烧闷香迷人，今乃为鬼所迷，世俗所称‘白日鬼’，其斯之谓欤？自此改行为善。

    　　○饶州府幕友慈溪袁如浩游幕西江，与宁都州程牧交好。乾隆三十一年，程公委署饶州府篆，邀如浩偕往。时郡署新遭回禄，前太守某被焚身死，程公到任，修葺尚未告成。

    Explanation

    845

    A Daytime Ghost and a Thief Named Qi

    There was a thief named Qi who was exceptionally skilled in his craft. Over time, he stole more and more, fearing that many would track him down. Therefore, he rented a dilapidated house near a burial ground to live in. A few ghosts appeared to him in a dream and said, "You should worship us, and you will soon become wealthy." In the dream, Qi agreed, but upon waking, he thought it was nonsense. Not long after, the ghosts appeared to him again in a dream and said, "Worship us within three days, and three days after that, we will be able to take what you stole at night during the day." Qi was stubborn and did not worship them upon waking. Three days later, he indeed fell gravely ill. He instructed his wife to check on their possessions to see if the ghosts' words were true. At noon, the objects suddenly moved on their own as if being carried by an invisible force. Qi tried to grab them, but his hands and feet felt as if they were bound. Once all the objects were taken, the bindings disappeared, and Qi's illness also healed. He then realized the truth and laughed, saying, "I burned incense to bewitch people, but now I have been bewitched by the ghosts. What the common people call 'daytime ghosts,' is this what they mean? From now on, I will change my ways and do good."

    A Retainer from Cixi and a Governor from Ningdu

    Yuan Ruohao, a retainer from Cixi, traveled to the western part of the region and formed a friendship with Cheng Mu, the governor of Ningdu. In the 31st year of Emperor Qianlong's reign, Cheng Mu was appointed to the prefecture of Raozhou and invited Yuan to accompany him. At that time, the prefectural office had recently suffered a fire, and the previous prefect had been burned to death. Cheng Mu had not yet completed the repairs when he took office.

    846

    夜间，如浩持灯往厕中，遇一人年三十许，衣月白衫，举头望月，若有所思，惟下体所着鞋袜，模糊莫辨。见如浩至，拱手问讯。审其音，杭州人也，自言周姓，字澹庵。如浩因署内并无是人，诘所自来，乃欷告曰：“我非人，乃鬼也，我系前任司钱谷幕友。上年饶郡被灾，太守某侵蚀赈粮，郡民聂某率领三十余人赴部告准，蒙发本省大宪审问，吊核赈册。不料，太守已早捏造印簿，升斗出入，皆有可凭。大宪为其所欺，遂将数人问成诬告，即行正法。此辈怨魂上诉都城隍，牒阎罗审讯，我系幕友，故被株连，又值公事甚忙，正在查办饶郡灾民册子，候至月余，始得审明，太守某冒赈是实，又冤杀数人，即遣鬼隶擒缚放入火中，以故在署烧死。我非同谋，罪虽获免，而皮囊已腐，不能还魂，只得稽留在此。因停厝处被瓦木匠溲溺，终日秽杂，坐卧不安，先生肯为我移至郊外，含恩不浅。”

    Explanation

    846

    At night, Ru Hao held a lamp as he headed to the toilet. There, he encountered a man around thirty years old, dressed in a light-colored shirt, who looked up at the moon, deep in thought. The lower half of his body, with its shoes and socks, was indistinct. Seeing Ru Hao approach, the man bowed and greeted him. Upon listening to his voice, Ru Hao discerned that he was from Hangzhou, and the man introduced himself as Zhou, courtesy name Dan'an. As there was no one by that name in the office, Ru Hao questioned where he had come from. The man then sighed and said, "I am not a living person, but a ghost. I was the former retainer in charge of finances. Last year, Rao County suffered a disaster, and the magistrate embezzled relief grain. A citizen named Nie led more than thirty people to report the matter to the authorities, who then ordered a high-ranking official in the province to investigate and verify the relief records. Unbeknownst to them, the magistrate had already forged official documents, with all transactions measured in units of grain, providing ample evidence. The high-ranking official was deceived by him and consequently convicted several people of false accusations, sentencing them to execution. These angry spirits appealed to the city god, who submitted a document to Yama for review. As I was a retainer, I was implicated in the matter. At the time, I was very busy with official business, investigating the records of disaster victims in Rao County. After waiting more than a month, the matter was finally investigated clearly, revealing that the magistrate had indeed embezzled relief funds and wrongfully executed several people. They were then captured and bound by ghost servants and thrown into a fire, which is why I was burned to death in the office. I was not a co-conspirator, and although my crime was forgiven, my physical body has decayed, and I cannot return to life. I am left here, with nowhere to rest. The place where my body is kept has been soiled by carpenters and masons, making it impossible for me to sit or lie down comfortably. Sir, would you be willing to move me to the outskirts? Your kindness would be greatly appreciated."

    847

    言讫不见。

    　　如浩次日寻至署后，果见黑漆棺一具停在墙边，诸工作人在旁喧嚷，遂告知主人，舁至城外，择地掩埋，作文祭之。

    　　○雷诛不孝湖南凤凰厅张二，赋性凶恶。父死，依母而居。母年七十余，视若老婢，少不如意，辄加呵叱。邻里忿极，欲鸣之官，母溺爱隐忍，反为调护。

    　　乾隆庚寅六月七日，值其生辰，留群不逞饮酒食面。家故贫，未娶，厨中仅母一人司炊。某酒酣索面，母云：“柴湿火不旺，姑少待。”某怒，赴内呵责，母急捧一碗战兢而至，因煌遽，忘下葱姜。某益怒，按碗劈面打母，母倒地仰天大哭。忽天光昼晦，云气如墨，雷声隐隐而起，某自知干天之怒，即扶母起，跪地谢罪。母亦代为跪求。某伏母后，抱持母足不放，雷电绕屋不去。母起立焚香，忽火光如流星飞入中堂，将某摄去，击死于街。邻里聚观，同声称快。

    Explanation

    847

    After he finished speaking, he was no longer seen.

    The next day, Ru Hao searched to the back of the office and indeed found a black lacquered coffin resting by the wall, with various workers nearby clamoring. He then informed the master, who had the coffin carried to the outskirts of the city, chose a place to bury it, and wrote an article to commemorate it.

    ○ Lightning Punishment for Filial Impiety: Zhang Er of Huanxiang Hall, Hunan

    Zhang Er, by nature cruel and evil, lived with his mother after his father's death. His mother, over seventy years old, was treated by him like an old servant. Whenever she did not meet his expectations, he would scold her. The neighbors were extremely angry and wanted to report him to the authorities, but his mother, out of deep love, endured silently and even tried to protect him.

    On the seventh day of the sixth month in the year 1760 during the Qianlong era, it was his birthday. He invited a group of miscreants to drink and eat noodles. His family was poor, and he had not yet married. There was only his mother in the kitchen, in charge of cooking. When he was drunk and asked for noodles, his mother said, "The firewood is damp and the fire isn't strong, please wait a moment." He became angry and went inside to scold her. His mother hurriedly brought a bowl of noodles, trembling, but due to her haste, forgot to add scallions and ginger. He became even angrier, grabbed the bowl, and slapped his mother in the face. His mother fell to the ground and cried out to the heavens. Suddenly, the sky darkened during the day, the clouds turned as dark as ink, and thunder began to rumble. Zhang Er knew he had incurred the wrath of heaven and immediately helped his mother up, knelt on the ground to apologize. His mother also knelt on his behalf to pray for forgiveness. He crouched behind his mother, tightly holding her feet, and the lightning and thunder surrounded the house without leaving. His mother stood up and burned incense. Suddenly, a flash of light like a shooting star flew into the main hall and carried him away, striking him dead on the street. The neighbors gathered to watch, and all shouted in joy.

    848

    朱孝廉名锦者适主敬修书院讲席，闻而趋视，见其面目焦黑，左太阳一孔如针大，作硫黄气。其身局缩如僵蚕，提起即长，放手即缩，盖骨节已震碎矣。背间有字，似篆非篆，不能识。

    　　○桂花相公江西丰城县署后有桂花相公祠。相公之里居姓氏弗可考，相传为明时人，作幕丰城令。有盗案株连数人，相公廉其冤，欲释之，令不从，遂大怒，触桂树而死。后人肖其像，为之立祠，称为“桂花相公”。相公甚灵异，宰斯土者，必先行香。凡有命案，发觉前一日，相公必脱帽几上，自露其顶。始而异之，积久如是，亦弗之怪。

    　　○落氵祭海水至澎湖渐低，近琉球则谓之“落氵祭”。落氵祭者，水落下而不回也。

    Explanation

    848

    Zhu Xiaolian, whose name was Jin, happened to be the head of the Jingxiu Academy. Upon hearing the news, he hurriedly went to see and found that the person's face was charred black, with a hole the size of a needle on the left temple, emitting a sulfur smell. His body was shrunk and contracted like a dead silkworm, stretching when lifted and shrinking when released, indicating that his bones and joints were shattered. There were characters on his back, resembling but not exactly seal script, which could not be identified.

    ○ The Guìhuā Xiānggōng Shrine is located behind the Fengcheng County Office in Jiangxi Province. The hometown and surname of the gentleman cannot be traced, but it is said he was a person from the Ming dynasty who served as an official in Fengcheng. There was a theft case that implicated several people, and the gentleman found them innocent and wanted to release them. The official did not comply, and the gentleman became very angry, hitting a camphor tree and dying. Later people made a statue of him and built a shrine for him, calling him "Guìhuā Xiānggōng." The gentleman is very miraculous, and officials who govern this land must first offer incense. Whenever there is a murder case, the day before it is discovered, the gentleman will remove his hat and expose his topknot. Initially, this was considered strange, but over time, it became a familiar occurrence, and people no longer found it unusual.

    ○ The sea level drops as it reaches Penghu, and near Ryukyu, it is called "Luo Zhi." "Luo Zhi" refers to the water falling and not returning.

    849

    有闽人过台湾，被风吹落氵祭中，以为万无生理。忽然大震一声，人人跌倒，船遂不动。徐视之，方知抵一荒滩，岸上砂石尽是赤金，有怪鸟见人不飞，人饥则捕食之。夜闻鬼声啾啾不一。

    　　居半年，渐通鬼语。鬼言：“我辈皆中国人，当年落氵祭，流尸到此，不知去中国几万里矣！久栖于此，颇知海性，大抵阅三十年落氵祭一平，生人未死矣者可以望归。今正当氵祭水将平时，君等修补船只，可望生还。”如其言，群鬼哭而送之，竞取岸上金沙为赠，嘱曰：“幸致声乡里，好作佛事，替我等超度。”

    　　众感鬼之情，还家后，各出资建大醮以祝谢焉。

    　　○铁公鸡济南富翁某，性悭吝，绰号“铁公鸡”，言一毛不拔也。忽呼媒纳妾，价欲至廉，貌欲至美，媒笑而允之。未几，携一女来，不索价，但取衣食充足而已。

    　　翁大喜过望，女又甚美，颇嬖之。

    Explanation

    849

    A man from Fujian passed through Taiwan and was blown by the wind into the river, thinking there was no hope of survival. Suddenly, there was a loud tremor, and everyone fell down, and the boat stopped moving. When they looked around, they realized they had reached a desolate beach. The sand and stones on the shore were all red gold. There were strange birds that did not fly when they saw people, and when people were hungry, they caught and ate them. At night, they heard the ghosts' sounds, which were varied and eerie.

    After living for half a year, they gradually understood the ghost language. The ghosts said, "We are all Chinese people. Years ago, we fell into the river, and our bodies floated to this place. We don't know how many miles we are from China! We have lived here for a long time and know the nature of the sea well. Roughly, after thirty years, the river will calm down, and those who are still alive can hope to return. Now is the time when the river water is about to calm down. You can repair your boats and hope to return to life." Following their advice, the ghosts cried and sent them off, competing to take the gold sand from the shore as a gift. They said, "Please tell our hometown, do good Buddhist deeds, and help us achieve liberation."

    The people were moved by the ghosts' feelings. After returning home, they each contributed money to build a grand ceremony to express their gratitude.

    ○ A wealthy man in Jinan, known by the nickname "Iron Rooster" for his extreme stinginess, suddenly called a matchmaker to arrange for a concubine. He wanted the price to be as low as possible and the woman to be as beautiful as possible. The matchmaker smiled and agreed. Soon, the matchmaker brought a girl, who did not ask for any price, only requesting that her clothing and food be sufficient.

    The man was overjoyed beyond expectation, and the girl was very beautiful, so he favored her greatly.

    850

    一日，女置酒劝翁曰：“君年已老，需此多钱无用，何不散之贫人，使感德耶？”翁大怒拒之，嗣后且防之，虑其花费。如是者半年，启其所藏，已空矣。

    　　翁知女所窃，拔刀问之，女笑曰：“君以为我为人乎？我狐也。君家从前有后楼七间，是我一家所居，君之祖父每月以鸡酒相饷，已数十年。自君掌家，以多费故罢之，转租取急，俾我一家无住宿处。怀恨在心，故来相报耳。”言讫不见。

    　　○夜星子京师小儿夜啼谓之“夜星子”，有巫能以桑弧桃矢捉之。某侍郎家，其曾祖留一妾，年九十馀，举家呼为老姨，日坐炕上，不言不笑，健饭无病，爱畜一猫，相守不离。

    　　侍郎有幼子尚襁褓，夜啼不止，乃命捉夜星子巫来治之。巫手小弓箭，箭竿缚素丝数丈，以第四指环之。坐至半夜，月色上窗，隐隐见窗纸有影，倏进倏却，仿佛一妇人，长七八尺，手执称矛，骑马而行。巫推手低语曰：“夜星子来矣。”

    Explanation

    850

    One day, the woman prepared wine and advised the old man, saying, "You are old, and having so much money is useless. Why not give it to the poor to earn their gratitude?" The old man became furious and rejected her, then guarded against her, fearing her spending. After half a year, when he opened his hidden treasure, it was already empty.

    The old man realized that the woman had stolen it and drew a knife to question her. She smiled and said, "Do you think I am human? I am a fox. Your family once had a seven-room back house, which was occupied by my family. Your ancestors provided us with chicken and wine every month for many years. Since you took over the family, due to excessive expenses, you stopped the provision and rented out the house, leaving my family without a place to stay. I harbored resentment in my heart and came to seek revenge." She finished speaking and disappeared.

    In the capital, a crying child at night is called "night star." There is a witch who can capture the spirit using a willow bow and peach arrows. In a certain minister's family, his great-grandfather left a concubine who was over ninety years old. The whole family called her "Old Auntie." She sat on the kang all day, neither speaking nor smiling, ate well, and was healthy. She loved a cat and never left her side.

    The minister had a young son who was still in the cradle, and he cried incessantly at night. So, he summoned the witch to deal with the situation. The witch held a small bow and arrows, with the arrow shafts tied with several feet of white silk, held by the fourth finger. She sat until midnight, when the moonlight shone through the window. She faintly saw a shadow on the window paper, resembling a woman about seven or eight feet tall, holding a spear and riding a horse. The witch whispered, "The night star has come."

    851

    弯弓射之，唧唧有声，弃矛反奔。巫破窗引线，率从逐之。

    　　比至后房，其丝竟入门隙。众呼老姨不应，乃烧烛入觅。一婢呼曰：“老姨中箭矣！”环视之，果见小箭钉老姨肩上，呻吟流血。所畜猫犹在胯下，所持矛乃小竹签也。举家扑杀其猫，而绝老姨之饮食。未几死，儿不复啼。

    　　○疡医大兴霍Υ、霍筠、霍，皆疡医子，筠犹秀逸出群，不屑屑本业，而喜读书。

    　　父以其梗家教，怒而责之，赖有邻翁姚学究者时来劝勉，因得肆力于举子业。不数年父死，Υ、各行其术，颇能自赡，独筠谋生计拙，日就穷困。

    　　时值试期，筠步行之通州，一老仆相随。因起身晚，行二十余里，日已西下，苦无宿店。忽见林际灯光自远而近，一妪奔走气喘。，老仆遮问曰：“此处有人家借宿否？”妪应曰：“正有急事去请外科，不得代借宿家。”筠急呼曰：“我晓外科，何不见请？”妪问：“先生如此少年，可曾娶妻否？”曰：“未也。”

    Explanation

    851

    Drawing the bow and shooting it, there was a sound, and he abandoned his spear and fled backward. The witch broke the window and led the string, leading her followers to chase after it.

    By the time they reached the back room, its thread finally entered the door crack. The crowd called out to Old Aunt but got no response, so they lit a candle and went in to look for her. A maid called out saying, "Old Aunt has been hit by an arrow!" Looking around, they indeed saw a small arrow stuck in Old Aunt's shoulder, groaning and bleeding. The cat she kept was still under her crotch, and the spear she held was actually a small bamboo skewer. The whole family beat and killed the cat, and cut off Old Aunt's food and drink. Not long after, she died, and the child no longer cried.

    ○ Sons of Surgeon Da Xing: Huo You, Huo Jin, and Huo Zhen, all sons of surgeons, with Jin being exceptionally talented and standing out, disdaining his own profession, but enjoying reading books.

    The father considered him stubborn in family education, becoming angry and scolding him, but thanks to the elderly neighbor Yao Xuejiu who often came to encourage him, he could fully devote himself to the scholar's trade. Not many years later, the father died, and You and Zhen each practiced their own methods, quite well supporting themselves, while only Jin was clumsy in making a living, gradually becoming poor and destitute.

    It was the time for the examination, and Jin walked to Tongzhou with an old servant following him. Because he got up late, he walked more than twenty miles, and the sun had already set, suffering from the lack of an inn for the night. Suddenly, he saw lights in the forest coming from far to near, and an old woman ran and panted. The old servant blocked and asked, "Is there a house here where we can stay for the night?" The old woman replied, "There is an urgent matter to go and see the surgeon, and cannot substitute for a house to stay for the night." Jin urgently called out, "I know about the surgeon, why not ask me instead?" The old woman asked, "Sir, you are so young, have you ever married?" He replied, "Not yet."

    852

    妪大喜，就请同行，筠心疑其所问非所答。

    　　俄至一庄，门庭壮丽，妪请少待，容先入白老夫人。少顷，妪率婢妇数十趋出曰：“老夫人奉请。”筠与老仆随妪行过十余间屋，始到上房。夫人已相待于中堂，年约三十余，珠环玉佩，光艳夺目，与筠行宾主礼，问姓字年齿及未婚原委。筠以实对，夫人之颜色甚怡，屏去侍婢谓筠曰：“身姓符，本籍河南，寄居于此。孀居无子，只生一女名宜春，年已十七，待字于家。忽患疮疾在私处，不便令人医治。尝与小女商量，必访得医生貌美年少者，乃请疗病，病愈即以小女相配。如先生者正是合式，但未知手段何如？”筠初念不过欲求一宿，及闻此语，喜不自胜。

    Explanation

    852

    The old woman was greatly pleased and immediately asked to accompany him. The protagonist was suspicious that the old woman's questions did not match her answers.

    Soon they arrived at an estate with a grand entrance. The old woman asked to be allowed to go in first and inform the old lady. A short while later, the old woman hurriedly came out with several maidservants and said, "The old lady respectfully invites you." The protagonist and the elderly servant followed the old woman through several rooms and finally arrived at the main hall. The lady was already awaiting them in the central hall. She was about thirty years old, adorned with pearl rings and jade pendants, radiant and dazzling. She greeted the protagonist with the etiquette of host and guest, asking about his name, age, and marital status. The protagonist answered truthfully, and the lady's expression was very pleased. She dismissed her maidservants and said to the protagonist, "My name is Fu. I am originally from Henan but am living here temporarily. I am a widow with no sons, and I only have a daughter named Yichun, who is seventeen years old and unmarried. She suddenly fell ill with a sore in a sensitive area, making it inconvenient for others to treat her. I once discussed with my daughter, and we decided to find a young and handsome doctor to treat her. If the disease is cured, we will marry her to the doctor. Someone like you is exactly suitable, but I am unsure of your skills." The protagonist had initially thought he would only seek a temporary stay, but upon hearing this, he was overjoyed.

    853

    夫人命唤蕊儿传语，亲携筠手而行，历曲室数重，始至闺闼。启帘入，见丽人拥锦衾而卧。夫人谓女曰：“郎君乃良医也，儿意可否？”女睨筠低语曰：“娘以为可便可耳。”夫人曰：“先生请看病，娘且暂去。”女羞涩不胜，蕊儿屡促之，乃斜卧向内，举袖障面。筠坐床侧，款款启衾，则双臀玉映，谷道茧细而霞深，惟私处蔽以红罗，疮大如钱。筠视毕，覆衾下床，夫人迎于门外，延至书斋，陈设精雅。筠麾诸婢出，碎扇上所系紫金锭，调以砚水，携入见夫人曰：“此药忌阴人手，须亲敷乃可。”夫人曰：“但得病愈，任郎所为。”筠复启衾，摩裟其臀，温存敷药，女但微笑，不作一语。

    Explanation

    853

    The lady commanded Rui'er to convey a message and personally took Jing by the hand, passing through several winding rooms before finally arriving at the inner chamber. Opening the curtain, they saw a beautiful woman lying under a luxurious quilt. The lady said to the woman, "Jing is a skilled physician; do you consent to his treatment?" The woman glanced at Jing and whispered, "Mother thinks it is acceptable." The lady replied, "Please examine her, Mother will leave for now." The woman, shy and embarrassed, could not bear it, and Rui'er repeatedly urged her to lie down, so she lay at an angle facing inward, raising her sleeve to cover her face. Jing sat by the side of the bed, gently opening the quilt, and saw her two buttocks shining like jade, the anal region thin and smooth with a deep, sunset-like glow, while her private parts were covered with red silk, and there was an ulcer as large as a coin. After examining her, Jing covered the quilt and got off the bed. The lady met him at the door and invited him to a study room with elegant furnishings. Jing dismissed the maids, crushed the fan to reveal the purple gold pill attached, mixed it with ink-water, and brought it in to present to the lady, saying, "This medicine should not be applied by a woman's hand; it must be applied personally." The lady said, "As long as the illness is cured, do as you see fit." Jing then opened the quilt again, gently massaging her buttocks and applying the medicine with care, and the woman merely smiled without saying a word.

    854

    越数日，疮愈。夫人举酒嘱筠曰：“郎君之少于小女，天使来也。”乃部署新室，涓吉合卺。新婚弥月，筠欲归家，夫人曰：“此间荒野，不足栖迟。京师阜城门外有故宅一所，郎往居之。”筠遂同行，辎重甚富。既至宅，皆画栋雕墙也。居数年，生子女二人。

    　　一夕，宜春忽泣向筠曰：“夙缘已尽，明日将别矣，四十年后当复相见。”

    　　天明，携手出门，彼此大恸。前已驻一犊车，望之甚小，夫人与宜春、蕊儿率女婢十数人乘之，车亦不觉隘，瞬息不见，宜春哭声尤恍然在耳也。

    　　筠后举孝廉，出为某县尹，究不知四十年后再见之说果何如耳。

    　　○产麒麟芜湖张姓者，卖腐为业，其妻孕十四月，生一麒麟，圆手方足，背青腹黄，通身翠毛如绣，左右臂有鳞甲，金光闪闪。坠地能走，喂饭能食，好事者以为祥瑞，方欲报官，而是晚死矣，距生时只七日。

    Explanation

    854

    After several days, the wound healed. The lady raised a cup of wine and said to Jun, "Your youth is a divine mandate for my daughter." She then arranged the new room and chose a good day for the wedding. After a month of marriage, Jun wanted to return home. The lady said, "This place is a wilderness, not suitable for living. There is an old house outside the Fucheng Gate in the capital; you should go and live there." Jun agreed and they set off together, with a considerable amount of baggage. Upon arriving at the house, it was all painted beams and carved walls. They lived there for several years and had two children.

    One night, Yichun suddenly wept and said to Jun, "Our past karmic ties have ended; we will part tomorrow. We will meet again in forty years."

    At dawn, they held hands and went out, both greatly distressed. In front, a small cart had already been stationed. It looked very small, but the lady and Yichun, Rui'er, along with about ten female servants, got on it. The cart did not feel cramped, and in an instant, it was gone. Yichun's crying sound was still faintly audible.

    Jun later passed the imperial examination and was appointed as the magistrate of a certain county. But he never knew what would happen in forty years.

    ○ A Man from Wuhu Named Zhang, Who Sold Stinky Tofu for a Living, His Wife Was Pregnant for Fourteen Months and Gave Birth to a Qilin. The Qilin had round hands and square feet, a blue back and yellow belly, green feathers like embroidery all over its body, and scales and armor on its left and right arms, with golden light flashing. It could walk immediately after birth and could eat when fed. Those who believed it was an auspicious sign wanted to report it to the authorities, but it died that night, only seven days after its birth.

    855

    ○生夜叉绍兴郑时若秀才妻卫氏生一夜叉，通体蓝色，口豁向上，环眼缩鼻，尖嘴红发，鸡距骆蹄，落胎即咬，咬伤收生婆手指。秀才大惧，持刀杀之。夜叉作格斗状。良久乃毙，血色皆青。其母亦惊死。

    　　○石膏因果嘉定张某，有名医之号，偶下药用石膏，误杀一人。过后自知，深以为悔，然亦不便语人，虽家中妻子，无人知者。一年后，张亦患病，延徐某来诊，定一方而去。临煮药时，张自提笔加“石膏一两”，子弟谏，不听。清晨服后，取方视之，惊曰：“此‘石膏一两’，谁人加耶？”其子曰：“爷亲笔所加，爷忘之乎？”张叹曰：“吾知之矣！汝速备后事可也。”作偈语曰：“石膏石膏，两命一刀。庸医杀人，因果难逃。”过午而卒。

    Explanation

    855

    Translation

    A Night Demon

    In Shaoxing, Zheng Shifang, a scholar, had a wife named Wei who gave birth to a night demon. The demon was entirely blue, with a mouth split upward, circular eyes, a retracted nose, a sharp mouth, red hair, chicken feet, and camel hooves. It bit immediately after birth, causing injury to the midwife's finger. The scholar was greatly afraid and held a knife to kill it. The night demon made a fighting posture. After a long time, it died, and its blood was all blue. Its mother also died in shock.

    Cause and Effect of Gypsum

    In Jiaxing, there was a doctor named Zhang, who was known for his medical skills. Once, he mistakenly killed one person with a dose of gypsum. Afterward, he knew it himself and deeply regretted it, but it was not convenient to tell others. Even in the family, no one knew about it. One year later, Zhang also fell ill and invited Xu to diagnose him. Xu prescribed a formula and left. When boiling the medicine, Zhang took a pen and added "one ounce of gypsum." His children advised him, but he did not listen. After taking it in the morning, he took the prescription and looked at it, shocked and said, "Who added this?" His son replied, "Grandfather added it with his own hand, Grandfather forgot it?" Zhang sighed and said, "I know it! Prepare the funeral arrangements quickly." He composed a verse and said, "Gypsum, gypsum, two lives, one knife. A mediocre doctor kills, the cause and effect cannot be escaped." He died after noon.

    856

    ○刘伯温后辈绍兴上虞县署后园有古墓，相传新令到任拜城隍神后，必往祭之，由来旧矣。

    　　乾隆间，有冉姓者宰其地，礼房吏以旧例请。冉问：“从前县令到任时，可有不祭者乎？”曰：“惟张某，性倔强，竟不行此礼，今现任湖北布政司。”冉曰：“我有志效张公。”竟不祭。

    　　一日，至厅审事，见有古衣冠客乘舆至，径上堂，冉竟不知为鬼，叱传事吏何以不报。语未毕，其人下车拉冉入书室，语哓哓不可辨，但闻冉若与人争辨者，亡何气绝，作鬼语曰：“我姓苏，名松，元末进士，为上虞县令，死乱葬此，刘伯温犹是我后辈也，汝大胆不祭！”或引张方伯故事折之，鬼云：“张某禄位盛时，我不能报。今其运尽，我将挖其眼矣。”冉家人环跪求恩，愿多备牲牢祭奠。

    Explanation

    856

    A Tomb of a Descendant of Liu Bowen

    Behind the county office of Shangyu in Shaoxing, there is an ancient tomb. It is said that new officials must go to worship this tomb after paying homage to the city god upon taking office, and this has been the custom for a long time.

    During the reign of Emperor Qianlong, there was a Mr. Ran who governed the area, and the official in charge of rituals requested according to the old custom. Mr. Ran asked, "In the past, were there any county magistrates who did not perform this ritual when they took office?" It was said that only Mr. Zhang, who was stubborn, did not perform this ritual, and he is currently serving as the Governor of Hubei. Mr. Ran said, "I have the intention to follow Mr. Zhang's example." He did not perform the ritual.

    One day, Mr. Ran went to the hall to handle official business and saw an anciently dressed guest arrive in a carriage and directly enter the hall. He did not realize it was a ghost and scolded the clerk for not reporting this. Before he finished speaking, the person got out of the carriage and pulled Mr. Ran into the study. The conversation was chaotic and incomprehensible, but it was heard that Mr. Ran was arguing with someone, and soon after, he died. He spoke in the voice of a ghost saying, "My name is Su Song. I was a Jinshi (imperial scholar) at the end of the Yuan dynasty and served as the county magistrate of Shangyu. I died in chaos and was buried here. Liu Bowen is still my descendant. You dare not perform the ritual!" Or he cited the story of Mr. Zhang to counter it. The ghost said, "When Mr. Zhang's position and fortune were at their peak, I could not repay him. Now that his luck is exhausted, I will gouge out his eyes." Mr. Ran's family surrounded him and knelt to beg for mercy, willing to prepare more sacrifices for the offering.

    857

    良久苏醒。冉惧，遂朝服祭之，寻果无恙。未几，张方伯竟以事挂误，遂至丧明。

    　　此事钱少詹辛楣先生为余言。

    　　○小那爷参领明公，与小那爷交好。明奉差他出，三年还都。行至南小街市，见那立市中，仲夏衣棉衣，戴暖帽。明心异之，下马执手，各道寒暄毕，那曰：“自与公别后，每为人欺，蒙公所赠骡，为某骑去不还，新居树木被畜牧伤扰，家人不理。幸公归，替我图之。”语毕，明公上马，那亦登车去。

    　　明公归语其事。家人云：“那死一年矣。”明公大骇，至那家问之，殓时衣服与途中所见同。问所赠骡，其子云：“在某家，据云先人所赠，故不敢索。”

    Explanation

    857

    After a long time, he awoke. Ran was afraid, so he worshiped him in formal attire, and soon enough, he was found to be fine. Shortly after, Mr. Zhang Fangbo was implicated in a mistake and eventually lost his sight.

    This matter was told to me by Mr. Qian Shaojian.

    ○Mr. Xiao Na, the commanding officer of Mr. Ming, and Mr. Xiao Na were friends. Mr. Ming was sent on a mission and returned to the capital after three years. On his way to the South Small Street Market, he saw Mr. Na standing in the market. It was midsummer, yet he was wearing cotton clothes and a warm hat. Mr. Ming was surprised and dismounted to take his hand. After exchanging pleasantries, Mr. Na said, "Since we parted ways, I have been mistreated by others. The mule you gave me was ridden off by someone and not returned. The trees in my new residence were damaged by livestock, and the servants did not manage it. Fortunately, you have returned to help me deal with it." After speaking, Mr. Ming mounted his horse, and Mr. Na also got into his carriage and left.

    Upon returning, Mr. Ming recounted the matter to his family. They said, "Mr. Na has been dead for a year." Mr. Ming was greatly startled and went to Mr. Na's house to inquire. The clothes Mr. Na wore at the time of his burial were the same as those he saw on the road. When asked about the mule, Mr. Na's son said, "It is in someone's possession. They say it was given by our ancestor, so we dare not claim it."

    858

    公呼某吓之，道破其诈，乃追骡还其子。视其墓，果被牧畜践损，为修葺封树而还。其夕梦那来谢云：“愧无以报，明午屠市中有一病骡，公买之，必获大利。”

    　　明公如其言，果得骡。医痊后，日行五百里。

    　　○水鬼坛武林门外西湖坝人家，有老仆日暮取水，远见水面一酒坛随流而泛，因思探取亦可贮物。俄而坛已至前，用手取之。不意腕入坛口，口渐缩小，拖伊入水。

    　　急呼人救，获免。

    　　○鬼市汪太守仆人李五，由路河赴京，畏暑，至晚步行，计天晓进城。夜半，见途中街市甚盛，肆中食物正熟，面饭蒸食，其气上腾。腹且馁，入肆中啖之，酬值而出。及晓，遥望京城，猛忆潞河至京四十里，其间不过花园打尖草舍一二家，何以昨夕有街市如此盛耶？顿觉胸次不快。俯而呕之，而蠕蠕然在地跳跃。谛视之，乃虾蟆也，蚯蚓蟠结甚多，心甚恶之，然亦无他患。又数岁乃卒。

    Explanation

    858

    The official called out to someone to scare him, revealing his deceit, and then chased the donkey back to its owner. When he looked at the grave, it was indeed trampled by livestock. He repaired and fenced the grave and returned. That night, he dreamed that the man came to thank him, saying, "I am ashamed that I have nothing to repay. At dawn tomorrow, there will be a sick donkey in the slaughterhouse market. If you buy it, you will surely gain great profit."

    The official followed his advice and indeed obtained the donkey. After being treated, it could travel five hundred miles a day.

    ○ A Water Ghost's Jar Near the West Lake Gate in Hangzhou, there was an old servant who went to fetch water at dusk. He saw from afar a wine jar floating downstream and thought that he could take it to store things. Soon the jar floated in front of him. Unaware that his wrist went into the jar's mouth, which gradually shrank, he was pulled into the water.

    He urgently called for help and was saved.

    ○ Ghost Market The servant Li Wu of Magistrate Wang traveled from the river to the capital, afraid of the heat, he walked at night, planning to enter the city at dawn. At midnight, he saw a very lively street market. The food in the shops was just cooked, steaming and rising. Feeling hungry, he went into a shop to eat, paid, and left. By dawn, he looked far into the capital and suddenly remembered that the distance from Lu River to the capital is forty li. There are only a few gardens and roadside inns along the way. How could there have been such a lively market last night? He suddenly felt uncomfortable in his chest. He bent down and vomited. A wriggling creature jumped on the ground. He looked closely and saw it was a frog, with many earthworms coiled around it. He found it very disgusting, but there was no other harm. He lived several more years before dying.

    859

    ○金娥墩金娥墩在无锡县城东南六十里，故南唐李煜妃墓地。娥能工词翰，进忠言，煜甚爱之。越数年，煜发兵晋陵，挈娥同行，遇吴越王兵，不得进，娥适死，因葬于此。乾隆初年，居民耕地得砖，上篆四字云：“唐王宝印。”至今墓间尚多。

    　　更可异者，每当风雨之夕，常有女鬼见形，且泣且歌，曰：“日侵削兮三尺土，山川已改兮众余侮。”

    　　○翻洗酒坛广信府徐姓，少年无赖，斗酒殴死邻人，畏罪逃去。官司无处查拿，家人以为死矣。五年后，其叔某偶见江上浮尸，即其侄也，取而葬之。又五年，徐忽归家，家人皆以为鬼。徐曰：“我以杀人故逃，不料入庐山中，遇仙人授我炼形分身之法，业已得道，恐家中念我，特浮一尸，以相安慰。今我尚有未了心事，故还家一走。”徐故未娶，其嫂半信半疑，且留住焉。

    Explanation

    859

    Jin'e Dun

    The Jin'e Dun is located sixty miles southeast of Wuxi city, and it was the burial site of a concubine of Li Yu, the last ruler of the Southern Tang dynasty. The concubine was skilled in writing poetry and prose, and she offered loyal advice to Li Yu, who greatly loved her. Several years later, Li Yu led an army to Jinling, taking the concubine along. They encountered the forces of the Wu-Yue king and were unable to advance. The concubine happened to die, and she was buried there. In the early years of the Qianlong reign, local residents found a brick while farming, inscribed with four characters: "Tang Wang Bao Yin." To this day, many such bricks remain at the tomb site.

    Even more strange is that whenever there is rain or wind at night, a female ghost often appears in physical form, weeping and singing:

    "The sun gradually erodes the three feet of earth,
    The mountains and rivers have changed, and many people have mocked me."

    A Man from the Xu Family in Guangxin Prefecture

    A man from the Xu family in Guangxin Prefecture, a young and unruly youth, killed a neighbor in a drunken brawl and fled out of fear of punishment. The authorities could not find him, and his family thought he had died. Five years later, his uncle happened to see a floating corpse on the river, which turned out to be his nephew. He took the body and buried it. Another five years passed, and the man suddenly returned home. His family all thought he was a ghost. He said, "I fled because I killed someone. Unexpectedly, I entered Mount Lu, where a Taoist immortal taught me the method of refining my form and dividing myself into multiple bodies. I have already attained enlightenment, but I feared that my family would miss me, so I especially floated a corpse to console them. Now I still have unfinished business, so I returned home for a visit."

    The man had not yet married. His aunt was half-believing, half-doubting, and she kept him in the house.

    860

    一日，溲于酒坛，嫂大怒骂之。徐曰：“洗之何妨？”嫂曰：“秽在坛里，如何可洗？”徐伸手入坛，拉其里出之，如布袋然，仰天大笑，蹑云而去。至今翻底坛尚存。徐昔所殴死邻家，早起在案上得千金。或云：“徐来作报，所云了心事者，即此之谓。”

    　　○雷诛吉湖州女子徐氏，生吃胎素，三岁后，即好念佛。攻至十四岁，忽被雷诛。乡人哗然，谓雷无灵。及殡时，见有篆文在背，识者以为“唐吉”三字。

    　　○狐仙亲嘴隐仙庵有狐祟人，庵中老朴王某恶而骂之。夜卧于床，灯下见一女子冉冉来，抱之亲嘴，王不甚拒；乃变为短黑胡子，胡尖如针，王不胜痛，大喊，狐笑而去。

    Explanation

    860

    One day, Xu urinated in a wine jar, and his sister-in-law angrily scolded him. Xu said, "What's wrong with washing it?" The sister-in-law replied, "The filth is inside the jar, how can it be washed?" Xu reached into the jar, pulled out the inside, like a cloth bag, laughed at the sky, and walked on clouds away. Even now, the bottom of the jar is still there. Xu had beaten to death a neighbor he had killed in the past, and in the morning, he found a thousand gold coins on the table. Some say, "Xu came to make a report, what was meant by 'settling his mind' is what this refers to."

    A woman named Xu from Huzhou was struck by lightning and died. She had been eating vegetarian food since birth and started reciting Buddhist scriptures at the age of three. By the time she was fourteen, she was suddenly struck by lightning. The villagers were shocked and said that the thunder had no power. When her body was being prepared for burial, they found the characters "Tang Ji" written on her back.

    In the Yin Xian Temple, there was a fox spirit that would torment people. An old man named Wang Pu in the temple hated the fox and scolded it. One night, as he lay in bed, he saw a woman slowly approach, embrace him, and kiss him. Wang did not resist much. Suddenly, the woman transformed into a short, black-haired man with a needle-like beard. Wang could not bear the pain and screamed. The fox laughed and disappeared.

    I hope this translation meets your requirements. If you need any further adjustments or have additional requests, please let me know.

    861

    次日，仆满嘴生细眼，若胃刺者然。

    　　○喇嘛西藏谟勒孤喇嘛王死，其徒卜其降生于维西某所。乾隆八年，众喇嘛乃持其旧器访之。

    　　至某所，有么些头人子，名达机，已七岁矣，忽指鸡雏问母曰：“雏终将依母乎？”其母曰：“雏终将离母也。”达机曰：“儿其雏乎？”有顷，谓其父母曰：“西藏有人至此迎小活佛，曷款留之。”父母以为妄，不听。达机力言之；其父出视，果有喇嘛数十辈，不待延请，竟造其室。达机见之，跏趺于地，为咒话良久。众喇嘛举所用钵、数珠、手书《心经》一册，各以相似者付之，令达机审辨，得其旧器服珠持钵，展经大笑。众喇嘛免冠罗拜。达机释钵执经起，遍摩众喇嘛顶，于是一喇嘛取僧衣帽进，达机自服之。群喇嘛以所携锦茵数十层置中庭，拥达机坐。

    Explanation

    861

    The next day, the servant's mouth was covered with tiny sores, as if pricked by needles.

    In the case of Lama Moge Gu, a Tibetan Buddhist king, his disciples divined that he would be reborn in a certain place in Weixi. In the eighth year of the Qianlong era, the lamas held a ceremony and brought the old relics to search for him.

    When they arrived at the place, there was a son of a Mosuo chief named Da Ji, who was already seven years old. He suddenly pointed to a chick and asked his mother, "Will the chick always stay with its mother?" His mother replied, "The chick will eventually leave its mother." Da Ji then said, "Am I the chick?" After a while, he told his parents, "There are people from Tibet here to bring the young reincarnated lama. Why don't we keep them here?" His parents thought this was absurd and did not listen. Da Ji strongly urged them, and his father went out to see. Indeed, there were several dozen lamas who, without waiting to be invited, entered his house. Da Ji saw them and sat cross-legged on the ground, engaging in a long conversation. The lamas presented the bowl, prayer beads, and a handwritten copy of the Heart Sutra, and each gave similar items to Da Ji, asking him to identify the original ones. Da Ji recognized the old relics and held the bowl, opened the sutra, and laughed. The lamas removed their hats and prostrated themselves in a row. Da Ji put down the bowl, took the sutra, and stood up, touching the heads of the lamas. One lama then brought a monk's robe and hat, and Da Ji put them on himself. The lamas placed dozens of layers of silk mats in the courtyard and escorted Da Ji to sit on them.

    862

    其父不知所为，众奉以白金五百，锦缯各数十端，为其父寿，曰：“此吾寺主活佛也，将迎归西藏。”其父以止此独子，不许。达机曰：“毋忧，明年某月日，父母将生一子承宗祧。我乃佛转世，不能留也。”其父不得已许之，亦合掌拜焉。众喇嘛拥达机于达摩洞佛寺，远近么些千百成群顶香皈拜，布施无算。

    　　留三日，去之西藏。

    　　明年，其父母果如期生一子。

    Explanation

    862

    The father, unsure of what to do, was presented with five hundred taels of silver and dozens of bolts of fine silk by the congregation, who wished him a long life, saying, "This is our temple's reincarnated Buddha, who will be brought back to Tibet." The father, having only this one son, refused. Daji said, "Do not worry; next year on a certain date, your parents will give birth to a son to continue the family line. I am a reincarnated Buddha and cannot stay." The father, having no choice, agreed and bowed in acceptance. The lamas escorted Daji to the Dharma Cave temple, where people from far and near, in groups of hundreds, came to worship and make offerings, giving without limit.

    He stayed for three days before leaving for Tibet.

    The following year, his parents indeed had a son as predicted.

    863

    ○梦中事只灵一半泾县胡讳承，方为诸生时，夜梦至一公府，若王侯之居。值其叔父在焉，其叔父惊曰：“此地府也，汝何以至？”承询其叔父：“有何职任？”叔父曰：“为吏尔。”承请查其禄命，叔父阅其籍曰：“一穷诸生耳。”承再三哀恳，求为之地。其叔父不得已，乃以他人禄命与之相易，曰：“此大弊也，若破，罪在不赦，可若何？”因以其所易籍示之：庚子科举人，雍正年恩科进士，任长垣县知县，某年月日终。且谓之曰：“尔乡试，须记有卦名。”因以手推之，一跌而寐。

    　　承庚子科首题“岁寒”一节，因用屯、蒙、剥、复等十卦成文，果得高魁。

    　　癸卯恩科成进士，又数年，授长垣县知县，一一不爽。无何届死期矣，因豫办交盘，且置酒与亲友作别，沐浴易衣，静坐而待。至黄昏后，忽呕血数升，以为必死矣。徐徐平复，竟不死，复活十馀年。至乾隆六年，寿终于南粮道任。梦寐之事，忽灵忽不灵如此。

    Explanation

    863

    Dreams are only half true. When Hu Cheng was still a student, he dreamed of arriving at a public office, which seemed like the residence of a prince or king. His uncle was there, and he was surprised, saying, "This is the underworld. How did you get here?" Hu Cheng asked his uncle, "What is your position?" His uncle replied, "I am just an official." Hu Cheng then asked his uncle to check his destiny. His uncle looked at his records and said, "You are just a poor student." Hu Cheng pleaded earnestly, asking his uncle to change his fate. His uncle, reluctantly, exchanged another person's destiny with him, saying, "This is a great sin. If it is exposed, the punishment will be severe, what shall we do?" He then showed him the exchanged records: a successful candidate in the Gengzi examination, a special imperial examination (恩科) in the reign of Emperor Yongzheng, serving as the magistrate of Changyuan County, and ending on a certain date. He also told him, "For your provincial examination, you must remember the name of the hexagram." He pushed with his hand, and Hu Cheng woke up abruptly.

    Hu Cheng, in the Gengzi examination, used the hexagrams of屯 (屯), 蒙 (蒙), 剥 (剥), and 复 (复) in his essay on the topic "岁寒" (the cold of the year), and indeed, he won the top prize.

    In the癸卯 (Gui Mao) special imperial examination, he became a successful candidate. After several years, he was appointed as the magistrate of Changyuan County, and everything matched exactly. Soon, his death date arrived, so he prepared for the handover, and set up a feast to bid farewell to his friends and relatives. He bathed, changed clothes, and sat quietly waiting. In the evening, he suddenly vomited several liters of blood, thinking he was surely going to die. Gradually, he recovered, and he did not die. He lived for more than ten years. In the sixth year of Emperor Qianlong's reign, he died in office as the Southern Grain Commissioner. Dreams are sometimes true and sometimes not, like this.

    864

    ●卷二十四○长乐奇冤福建长乐县民妇李氏，年二十五，生一子，越六月而夫亡，矢志抚孤。家只一婢、一苍头，此外虽亲族罕相见者，里党咸钦之。子年十五，就学外傅。

    　　一日，氏早纺绩，忽见白衣男子立床前，骇而叱之，男子趋床后没，氏惧，呼婢入房相伴。及午，子自外归，同母午餐，举头又见白衣男子在床前，骇而呼，男子复趋床下没。母语子曰：“闻白衣者财神也，此屋自祖居，至今百年余，得毋先人所遗金乎？”与婢共起床下地板，有青石大如方桌，上置红缎银包一个，内白银五铤。母喜，欲启其石，而力有未逮，乃计曰：“凡掘藏，宜先祀财神，儿曷入市买牲礼祭，而后起之。”儿即持银袱趋市买猪首。既成交，乃忆未经携钱，因出银袱与屠者曰：“请以五铤为质。”更以布袋囊猪首归。

    Explanation

    864

    Chapter Twenty-Four: The Mysterious Tragedy of Changle

    In Changle County, Fujian Province, there lived a woman named Li, who was twenty-five years old and had given birth to a son. Six months after her husband's death, she resolved to raise her child steadfastly. Her household consisted of only one maid and one old servant, and she rarely interacted with her relatives. Her fellow villagers respected her greatly. Her son was fifteen years old and was receiving private tutoring.

    One day, while Li was spinning thread, she suddenly saw a white-clad man standing at the foot of her bed. Startled, she shouted at him, and the man quickly disappeared behind the bed. Li was afraid and called her maid to enter the room to keep her company. By midday, her son returned from school, and they had lunch together. As they ate, Li saw the white-clad man again standing at the foot of the bed. She was startled and called out, and the man once more disappeared behind the bed. Li spoke to her son, saying, "I've heard that white-clad men are the god of wealth. This house has been in our family for over a hundred years. Could this be a legacy left by our ancestors?"

    Together, Li and her maid lifted the floorboards under the bed and found a large stone as big as a square table, on which there was a red silk pouch containing five silver ingots. Li was delighted and wanted to move the stone, but she lacked the strength. She then said, "When digging up hidden treasures, one should first offer sacrifices to the god of wealth. Why don't you go to the market to buy sacrificial offerings and then return to dig up the treasure?" The son took the silver pouch and went to the market to buy a pig's head. After making the deal, he realized he had not brought any money with him. He then took out the silver pouch and said to the butcher, "Please use the five ingots as collateral." He returned home with the pig's head in a cloth bag.

    865

    道经县署前，有捕役尾之，问：“小哥袋内盛何物？”曰：“猪头。”役盘诘再三，儿怒掷袋于地曰：“非猪头，岂人头耶？”倾囊出，果一人头，鲜血满地。儿大恐啼泣。役捉到官，儿以买自某屠告。拘屠者至，所言合，并以银袱呈上。经胥吏辗转捧上，皆红缎袱，及至案前开视，则缎袱乃一血染白布，中包人手指五枚。令大骇，重讯儿，儿以实对。

    　　令亲至其家启石坑，内一无头男子，衣履尽白，右五指缺焉。以头与指合之相符。遍究从来，莫能得其影响。因系屠与儿于狱，案悬莫结。此乾降二十八年事。

    　　○烧包粤人于七月半，多以纸钱封而焚之，名曰“烧包”，各以祀其先祖。张戚者，素无赖，而有胆。其仆三儿，卧病月馀，至七月十六日，忽自床蹶起，趋而出。

    Explanation

    865

    As the child passed by the county office, a captured officer followed closely behind him. The officer asked, "What is inside your bag, young master?" The child replied, "A pig's head." The officer questioned him repeatedly, and the child, becoming angry, threw the bag to the ground and said, "If it’s not a pig’s head, then what else could it be but a human head?" He then emptied the bag, revealing a human head covered in blood. The child was greatly frightened and began to cry. The officer arrested the child and brought him to the magistrate. The child claimed that he had bought the head from a butcher. The butcher was then arrested and brought before the magistrate, and his statements matched those of the child. The child also produced a silver cloth as evidence. The officials and clerks passed the cloth up to the magistrate, who found it to be a blood-stained white cloth wrapped in red silk. When the magistrate opened the cloth, he found it contained only five human fingers. The magistrate was greatly startled and questioned the child again, who then answered truthfully.

    The magistrate personally went to the child’s home and opened the stone pit. Inside, he found a man without a head, dressed entirely in white, with his right hand missing five fingers. When the head and fingers were matched, they fit together perfectly. Despite a thorough investigation, no leads were found. The butcher and the child were imprisoned, and the case remained unresolved. This was an event from the 28th year of the Qianlong reign.

    In Guangdong, people burn paper money on the mid-seventh month to honor their ancestors. Zhang Qis, who was always a troublemaker but had a lot of courage, had a servant named San Er, who had been sick for over a month. On the 16th day of the seventh month, San Er suddenly got up from his bed and rushed out.

    866

    戚追之，出城，至大河侧，三儿痴立点首呓语，若与人争状。戚掌其颊，三儿云：“为差人拘来，替人挑送包钱。”戚问：“差何在？”以手指曰：“前立浅渚间者是也。”戚果见一人，高帽青衣，若今之军牢皂隶状，手执鞭指挥。戚大呼擒之，一击而没。问：“包在何处？”三儿云：“在家堂板阁上，我因过重不肯担，乃拘我来。”戚归启家堂，果有纸灰十包。

    　　○金银洞高峰崖在广西思恩府城南百里，两峰壁立，崖上大书十三字云：“金七里，银七里，金银只在七七里。”字画遒劲，不知何年镌凿。崖下有土地祠，望气者咸称其地有金银气。百十年间，土人多方搜求，一无所得。星士某至土地祠内，徘徊数日，攫神像去。土人追及，询知像乃范金所为，然亦不知“七七里”为何义。

    Explanation

    866

    戚追之，出城，至大河侧，三儿痴立点首呓语，若与人争状。戚掌其颊，三儿云：“为差人拘来，替人挑送包钱。”戚问：“差何在？”以手指曰：“前立浅渚间者是也。”戚果见一人，高帽青衣，若今之军牢皂隶状，手执鞭指挥。戚大呼擒之，一击而没。问：“包在何处？”三儿云：“在家堂板阁上，我因过重不肯担，乃拘我来。”戚归启家堂，果有纸灰十包。

    戚追着那人，出了城，来到大河岸边，三个孩子呆立着，点头又低声说话，像是在与人争论。戚拍了其中一个孩子的脸颊，孩子说：“是差人把我抓来的，让我替人挑送包钱。”戚问：“差人在哪里？”孩子用手指着说：“前面站在浅滩上的那个人就是。”戚果然看见一个人，戴着高帽，穿着青衣，像是现在的军牢皂隶，手里拿着鞭子指挥。戚大声喊着要抓住他，那人一击便消失了。戚问：“包钱在哪里？”孩子说：“在我们家堂屋的板阁上，我因为太重不肯担，所以他们把我抓来了。”戚回到家里，打开家堂屋的板阁，果然有十包纸灰。

    金银洞高峰崖位于广西思恩府城南一百里处，两座山峰直立，崖上刻着十三个大字：“金七里，银七里，金银只在七七里。”字迹遒劲有力，不知是何年刻凿。崖下有一座土地祠，看风水的人都说那里有金银之气。一百多年来，当地人多方搜寻，却一无所获。有一天，一个星士来到土地祠内，徘徊了几天，偷走了神像。当地人追上他，询问后得知神像是用范金铸成的，但也不明白“七七里”是什么意思。

    867

    崖中旁峰数十丈，上有银洞。洞中白银累累，大者重数十斤。土人架木而登拾之，即百计不能出。或向外掷之，着地即失。或牵犬入，将银缚犬身向外牵之，犬即狂吠，比出，而身亦无银也。

    　　○猫怪靖江张氏，住城之南偏，屋角有沟，久弗疏瀹，淫雨不止，水溢于堂。张以竹竿通之，入丈许，竿不可出，数人曳之不动，疑为泥所滞。天晴复举之，竿脱然出，黑气如蛇，随竿而上，顷刻天地晦冥，有绿眼人乘黑戏其婢。每交合，其阴如刺，痛不可忍。张广求符术，道士某登坛治之。黑气自坛而上，如有物舐之者，所舐处舌如刀割，皮肉尽烂，道士狂奔去。

    Explanation

    867

    In a cliff with side peaks towering tens of feet high, there is a silver cave on top. Inside the cave, there is an abundance of silver, with some pieces weighing dozens of pounds. Local people use wooden structures to climb and collect the silver, but despite their efforts, they cannot get it out. They try to throw the silver out, but it disappears upon touching the ground. They also try to tie the silver to a dog's body and pull it out, but the dog starts barking wildly, and by the time the dog is brought out, it has no silver on it.

    ○ A strange cat in Jingjiang, owned by the Zhang family, lived in the southern part of the city. There was a ditch in the corner of their house that had not been cleaned for a long time. Continuous heavy rain caused water to overflow into the hall. The Zhangs used a bamboo pole to clear the ditch, but after inserting it about ten feet, the pole could not be pulled out. Several people tried to pull it, but it wouldn't budge, leading them to suspect that it was stuck in the mud. When the weather cleared, they tried again, and the pole came out easily. A black mist like a snake rose along the pole, and in an instant, the sky and earth became dark. A green-eyed man took advantage of the darkness to play with the Zhangs' maid. Whenever they were intimate, her vagina felt like it was being pricked, causing unbearable pain. The Zhangs sought various talismans and spells widely, and a Taoist priest named Mr. Zhang ascended the altar to perform a ritual. The black mist rose from the altar, as if something was licking it, and the places it licked were cut like with a knife, leaving the skin and flesh all torn up. The Taoist priest ran away in a frenzy.

    868

    道士素受法于天师，不得已，买舟渡江。张使人随之，将求救于天师。至江心，见天上黑云四起，道士喜拜贺曰：“此妖已为雷诛矣！”张归家视之，屋角震死一猫，大如驴。

    　　○梦马言乾隆十八年，山东高蔚辰宰河南延津县。昼寝书室，梦一马冲其庭立而人言，高射之，正中其心，马吼而奔。高惊醒，适外报某村妇卢罗氏夜被杀，以弋其阴，并杀二孩。高往验尸，伤如所报，而凶犯无以根究。因忆所梦，乃顺庄点名，冀有马姓者。点毕无有，问：“外庄有姓马者乎？”曰：“无。”

    Explanation

    868

    A Taoist priest, who had long been initiated by the Celestial Master, unwillingly bought a boat to cross the river. Zhang sent someone to follow him, intending to seek help from the Celestial Master. When they reached the middle of the river, they saw dark clouds gathering in the sky. The Taoist priest happily bowed and congratulated, saying, "This demon has already been struck down by thunder!" Zhang returned home to find a cat dead in the corner of the house, as large as a donkey.

    In a dream, a horse spoke in the eighteenth year of Qianlong, when Gao Weichen, from Shandong, was the magistrate of Yanting County in Henan. During the day, while sleeping in his study, he dreamed of a horse charging into his courtyard and speaking like a human. Gao shot at it, hitting it right in the heart, and the horse roared and ran away. Gao woke up in a panic, just as someone outside reported that a woman named Lu Luoshu from a certain village had been killed at night, with her genitalia mutilated, and two children were also killed. Gao went to examine the body, and the injuries were as reported, but the perpetrator could not be identified. Because he remembered his dream, he then went to the village to take a census, hoping to find someone with the surname Ma. After the census, there was no one with the surname Ma. He asked, "Is there anyone with the surname Ma in the outer village?" They replied, "No."

    869

    高将庄册翻阅，沉思良久，见有姓许名忠者，忽心计曰：“马属午，马立而言，则言午也；正中其心，当是许忠矣！”呼许曰：“杀此妇者汝也。”许惊愕叩首曰：“实是也。以奸不从，故杀之；两指被妇咬伤，故怒而其阴，并杀其子。但未识公何以知之？”高笑不答。视其手，血犹涔涔也。置于法，合郡以为神。

    　　○蒋静存麟昌蒋君，字静存，余同馆翰林也，诗也李昌谷，有“惊沙不定乱萤飞，羊灯无焰三更碧”之句。生时，其祖梦异僧担《十三经》掷其门，俄而长孙生，故小字僧寿。及长，名寿昌，以避国讳故，特改名。又自梦僧画麒麟一幅与之，遂名麟昌。十七岁举孝廉，十九岁入词林。二十五岁卒。性傲兀不羁，过目成诵，常曰：“文章之事，吾畏袁子才，而爱裘叔度，他名宿如沈归愚，易与耳。”卒后三日，其遗孤三岁，披帐号叫曰：“阿爷僧衣僧冠坐帐中。”家人争来，遂不见。

    Explanation

    869

    A High Official's Deduction and the Story of Jiang Jingcun

    A high-ranking official named Gao Zhaocang was reviewing documents and pondered deeply for a long time. He noticed a person named Xu Zhong and suddenly thought, "The horse belongs to the noon hour, and when the horse stands and speaks, it is 'speech noon.' This directly matches the heart; it must be Xu Zhong!" He called out to Xu, saying, "You are the one who killed this woman." Xu was startled and knelt, saying, "It is true. She refused to be unfaithful, so I killed her; two of my fingers were bitten by the woman, so I became angry and mutilated her genitals, and killed her son as well. But I do not know how you knew this?" Gao laughed but did not answer. He looked at Xu's hands, which were still bleeding. Xu was punished according to the law, and the entire county considered Gao a god.

    ○ Jiang Jingcun, courtesy name Lin Chang, was a Hanlin scholar who shared the same hall with me. He was also a poet, like Li Shangyin, and had the lines "Startled sand, uncertain, with flying fireflies, a lamb lamp without flame, blue at the third watch of the night." At the time of his birth, his grandfather dreamed of a strange monk carrying the Thirteen Classics and throwing them at his door. Soon after, his eldest grandson was born, so he was given the nickname Monk Longevity. When he grew up, his name was Shouchang, but he changed it to Lin Chang to avoid a national taboo. He also dreamed of a monk painting a qilin and giving him one picture, so he named himself Lin Chang. At seventeen, he was selected as a filial and upright official, and at nineteen, he entered the literary circle. He died at twenty-five. His temperament was proud and unrestrained, and he could memorize texts upon first reading. He often said, "In literary matters, I fear Yuan Mei, but love Qiu Shuduo; other famous scholars like Shen Guifuy are easy to deal with." Three days after his death, his three-year-old son unrolled the account book and called out, "Father is sitting in a monk's robe and hat in the tent." Family members came rushing, and then disappeared.

    870

    呜呼！静存终以僧为鸿爪之露，其为戒律轮回似矣。然吾与之谈，辄痛诋佛法而深恶和尚，何耶？

    　　○天妃神乾隆丁巳，翰林周奉命册立琉球国王。行至海中，飓风起，飘至黑套中，水色正黑，日月晦冥。相传入黑洋从无生还者，舟子主人正共悲泣，忽见水面红灯万点，舟人狂喜，俯伏于舱呼曰：“生矣！娘娘至矣！”果有高髻而金环者，甚美丽，指挥空中。随即风住，似有人曳舟而行，声隆隆然。俄顷，遂出黑洋。

    　　周归后，奏请建天妃神庙。天子嘉其效顺之灵，遂允所请。事见乾隆二十二年邸报。

    　　○宿迁官署鬼淮徐道姚公廷栋，驻札宿迁。封翁寿期，演剧于堂。堂旁墙极高，见墙外有人头数千，眼癸癸然，俱来观剧。初疑是皂隶辈，叱之不去，近之无有。明旦视之，墙外皆湖，无立人处。

    Explanation

    870

    Alas! Even a monk, who is supposed to be pure, can reveal his true nature through his actions, as if he were caught in the cycle of rebirth and suffering. Yet when I spoke with him, he often vehemently criticized Buddhism and despised monks. Why is that?

    ○ The Goddess Mazu, in the year Ding Si of the Qianlong era, the Hanlin scholar Zhou Zhen was commissioned to invest the King of Ryukyu. They sailed into the sea, when a hurricane arose, and they were blown into the Black Strait, where the water was pitch black, and the sun and moon were obscured. It was said that no one who entered the Black Ocean ever returned alive. The crew and the captain were weeping together in sorrow, when suddenly they saw thousands of red lights on the water, and the sailors were overjoyed, bowing down in the cabin and shouting, "We are saved! The Lady has come!" Indeed, there was a beautiful woman with a high coiffure and golden rings, who was directing the sky. Immediately, the wind stopped, and it seemed as if someone was dragging the boat along, with a loud rumbling sound. Soon, they emerged from the Black Ocean.

    After Zhou returned, he submitted a request to build a temple for the Goddess Mazu. The Emperor praised the divine power of loyalty and obedience, and granted his request. The matter is recorded in the official gazette of the 22nd year of the Qianlong era.

    ○ The Ghost in the Official Residence of Suqian, Governor Yao Tingdong of Huai and Xu regions, stationed in Suqian. During the celebration of an elderly man's birthday, plays were performed in the hall. The wall beside the hall was very high, and they saw thousands of heads outside the wall, with wide-open eyes, all coming to watch the play. At first, they thought it was the common people, and they shouted at them to go away, but they did not leave. When they got closer, there was nothing there. The next morning, they looked outside, and found that the area was surrounded by lakes, with no place for people to stand.

    871

    其幕友潘禹九遣奴往厨取酒，久而不至，迹之，已仆于地，口眼皆青泥，盘中酒菜之类，变作蚯蚓树叶。潘素不信鬼神，乃挺身至奴所行处，验其有无。署中二客诈为鬼状，私往吓之。潘笼一小灯，行未半道，两客见黑气一条绕灯而入，灯色绿如萤火，潘勿觉。二客悚然，噤不发声。潘将如厕，有大黑手遮其面，踉跄急归。二客迎之，共相骇异。手持灯渐重，火亦澌灭。家奴各持火来照，灯笼内有死野鸭一只，鸭大笼小，竟不知从何处窜入也。

    　　○广东官署鬼康熙壬戌武探花沈崇美为广东守备，署后花园有井，担水者率以为常。

    　　偶一夜，有女子呼水，担夫如其言与之，乃ㄏ其头入桶中。担夫疑署中婢与戏，詈群婢。群婢曰：“无之。”担夫引婢至取水处，有海棠一枝，白鸡成群，入树下不见。群婢笑曰：“非鬼也，藏神也，掘之必得金银。”遂令担夫具畚锸开土。未五六尺，得一棺，惧而止。忽一婢发狂大呼曰：“请主人！请主人！”

    Explanation

    871

    The Master's assistant, Pan Yujiu, sent a servant to fetch wine from the kitchen, but the servant did not return for a long time. Upon searching, it was found that the servant had collapsed on the ground, with green mud around his mouth and eyes. The food and drink in the dish had transformed into earthworms and leaves. Pan, who had never believed in ghosts or spirits, decided to investigate personally, following the servant's path to see if there was any truth to the matter. Two officials in the office, pretending to be ghosts, secretly went to scare him. Pan carried a small lantern and walked halfway, when the two officials saw a black mist swirling around the lantern, turning its light green like fireflies. Pan did not notice this. The two officials were so frightened that they could not speak. As Pan was about to go to the toilet, a large black hand covered his face, causing him to rush back in panic. The two officials greeted him, both shocked and surprised. The lantern in his hand became heavier, and the flame went out. The servants brought other lights to investigate, and they found a dead wild duck inside the lantern, which was too big to fit inside. It was unknown how it had gotten in.

    ○ During the reign of Emperor Kangxi, in the year 1692, Shen Chongmei, a successful candidate in the military examination, served as a military officer in Guangdong. Behind his office was a garden with a well, from which water carriers regularly fetched water.

    One night, a woman called for water, and the water carrier responded by pushing her head into the bucket. Suspecting that the maids were playing a prank, the water carrier cursed them. The maids denied it, and he led them to the place where water was fetched. There, he saw a branch of a crabapple tree and a flock of white chickens, which disappeared when he looked closer. The maids joked, "It's not a ghost, but a hidden spirit. If you dig here, you will find treasure." They instructed the water carrier to prepare shovels and picks to dig. After digging a few feet, they found a coffin, which frightened them, and they stopped. Suddenly, one of the maids went mad and called out, "Master! Master!"

    872

    沈公偕其妻往视，婢呼曰：“我嘉靖十七年巡按某公之第四妾也，遭主妇毒虐，缢死埋此。公家群婢犯我，我应索其命。第土浅地湿，棺中多水，主人肯改葬我，则掘者不为无功，将免其罚。大堂西偏，我生前埋金镯一只，宝珠数颗，可掘取为改葬费，亦不累主人金也。”言毕，婢子如常无病矣。

    　　主人为启其棺，水涔涔欲流；发堂之西偏，封镯宛然。为改葬高处。镯重三两六钱，形如蒜苗。

    　　○为儿索价葛礼部讳亮者为予言：其邻程某，拥重资，无子。晚年生儿，性聪慧，眉目莹秀，程爱如掌中珍。十二岁即多病，所费医药不赀。稍长，不事生业，好斗鸡走狗，产为之空，程忿甚。一旦，悬祖宗神像，将笞之。子忽作山东人语曰：“俺吴某也，前生为尔负债万金，今来索取，将尽。汝以我为子耶？大误！大误！

    Explanation

    872

    Shen Gong, accompanied by his wife, went to see the maid, who called out and said: "I was the fourth concubine of a certain official during the 17th year of the Jiajing era. I suffered from the cruelty of the mistress, hanged myself, and was buried here. The maids of your household offended me, and I should demand their lives. But the soil is shallow and damp, and there is much water in the coffin. If the master agrees to re-bury me, then the one who digs will not be without merit and will be exempt from punishment. In the west side of the main hall, I buried a gold bracelet before my death and several precious gems. These can be dug up as the cost for re-burying me, and it will not burden the master's wealth." After finishing speaking, the maid returned to normal and was not ill.

    The master opened the coffin, and water was dripping out. In the west side of the main hall, the sealed bracelet was still intact. They re-buried her in a higher place. The bracelet weighed three liang and six qian and was shaped like garlic sprouts.

    ○ Mr. Ge Li, the Minister of Rites, told me: His neighbor, Mr. Cheng, possessed substantial wealth but had no children. In his later years, he had a son who was intelligent and beautiful, with bright eyes and a refined appearance. Cheng loved him like a treasure in his palm. At twelve years old, the boy began to suffer from frequent illnesses, and the medical expenses were enormous. As he grew older, he did not engage in any productive work and enjoyed cockfighting and dog racing, which emptied their household of its resources. Cheng became very angry. One day, he hung up the images of his ancestors and was about to whip the boy. The boy suddenly spoke in a Shandong dialect and said, "I am Wu Mou from my previous life. I owe you ten thousand gold pieces, and now I am here to collect them, almost finished. Do you think I am your son? Great mistake! Great mistake!"

    873

    我昨揭帐，尚欠八十馀金，今亦不能相让。“奋衣前取其母髻上珠，踏碎之，然后死。程卒大穷而嗣绝。

    　　○鬼魂觅棺告主人姜静敷寓京师愍忠寺，寺旁为书室，室中有空棺，俗所谓寿器是也，寺邻某为其父老故置焉。姜月夜读书，窗户轰然大开，棺盖低昂不已。姜大骇，持烛视之，如有人指痕出没于棺上者，响良久乃已。次早，邻人叩门云：“某翁死，来取棺。”方悟初死之魂，夜间先来就棺也。

    　　苏州唐道原年七十卒，其子为买棺于海红坊寿器店。主人云：“昨夜有白须人坐某一棺上，烛之不见。”问其状貌，酷似道原，店主人素不相识也，乃即买其所坐者归。

    Explanation

    873

    I revealed the debt yesterday, and I still owe eighty or more gold coins, and now I cannot compromise. He took off his clothes and went forward to take the pearl from his mother's hair, then crushed it underfoot, and then died. Cheng died in extreme poverty, and his descendants were cut off.

    Jiang was reading by moonlight when the windows suddenly burst open, and the lid of the coffin kept rising and falling. Jiang was greatly frightened and held a candle to look, and it seemed as if there were fingerprints appearing and disappearing on the coffin. The sound continued for a long time before stopping. The next morning, a neighbor knocked on the door and said, "Mr. So-and-so has died, and we are here to take the coffin." Jiang then realized that the spirit of the recently deceased had come to the coffin the night before.

    In Suzhou, Tang Dao Yuan, who was seventy years old, died. His son bought a coffin from the Life Container Store in Haihong Lane. The store owner said, "Last night, an old man with a white beard sat on one of the coffins, and when I lit a candle, I could not see him." When asked about his appearance, he was very much like Dao Yuan, and the store owner had never met him before. He then immediately bought the coffin that the person had sat on and took it home.

    874

    金陵戴敬咸进士与梅式庵饮于吴朱明孝廉家，忽狂癫，握梅手呼曰：“要朱红，要加漆！”梅愕然在解。已而气绝，方知所托者，藏身物也。

    　　程原衡家管事李姓者夜醉堕楼死，举家未知。原衡睡醒，觉左耳阴冷异常，疑而回顾，灯光青荧，有黑人吹气入耳，似有所诉。惊起，呼家丁四照，见楼下尸，方知李魂来告主人求棺殓也。

    　　○匾怪杭州孙秀才，夏夜读书斋中，觉顶额间蠕蠕有物。拂之，见白须万茎出屋梁匾上，有人面大如七石缸，眉目宛然，视下而笑。秀才素有胆，以手捋其须，随捋随缩，但存大面端居匾上。秀才加杌于几视之，了无一物。复就读书，须又拖下如初。如是数夕，大面忽下几案间，布长须遮秀才眼，书不可读。击以砚，响若木鱼，去。又数夕，秀才方寝，大面来枕旁，以须搔其体。秀才不能睡，持枕掷之。大面绕地滚，须飒飒有声，复上匾而没。合家大怒，急为去匾，投之火，怪遂绝，秀才亦登第。

    Explanation

    874

    Jinling scholar Dai Jingxian, a Jinshi, was drinking with Mei Shizhao at the home of Wu Zhu Ming, a xiao lian. Suddenly, Dai became mad, grasped Mei's hand and shouted, "I need cinnabar, I need to be varnished!" Mei was surprised and unable to understand. Soon after, Dai died, and they realized that what he was entrusting was a place to hide his soul.

    Cheng Yuanheng's family manager, surnamed Li, died after falling from a building while drunk at night, but the whole family did not know. When Yuanheng woke up from sleep, he felt an unusually cold sensation in his left ear. Doubting, he looked back, and the light was greenish. There was a black person blowing air into his ear, as if there was something to say. Startled, he called out to the household servants to look around and saw the body downstairs. They realized that Li's soul had come to inform the master to request a coffin for burial.

    The Strange Phenomenon with the Plaque

    A Hangzhou scholar, Sun, was reading in his study during the summer night. He felt something wriggling between his forehead and top of his head. When he brushed it away, he saw thousands of white hairs emerging from the plaque on the roof beam. There was a face as large as a seven-stone jar, with eyebrows and eyes clearly visible, looking down and smiling. The scholar, who was usually brave, touched its beard with his hand. As he touched it, it would shrink, but the large face remained upright on the plaque. The scholar placed a stool on the desk to look at it, but there was nothing there. He resumed reading, but the beard dragged down again as before. For several nights, the large face suddenly descended onto the desk, spreading its long beard to cover the scholar's eyes, making it impossible to read. He struck it with the inkstone, and it made a sound like a wooden fish, then disappeared. After several more nights, while the scholar was sleeping, the large face came to the side of his pillow, scratching his body with its beard. The scholar couldn't sleep and threw the pillow at it. The large face rolled around on the ground, making a rustling sound with its beard, and then ascended back onto the plaque and disappeared. The entire family became very angry, quickly removed the plaque, and threw it into the fire. The strange phenomenon ceased, and the scholar also passed the imperial examination.

    875

    ○徐支手咸阳徐某。家巨富。初生一子，颇聪慧，六岁病痞死。旋生三子，貌皆相似，病亦如之。徐年已迈矣，至第三子死时，抚尸恸甚，用刀剖儿腹，出其痞，复断其左臂，骂曰：“毋再来诱我。”其痞形如三角菱，有口，能呼吸，悬之树间，风日吹干，每触油腥，口犹能动。未期年，徐又得子，貌如前，痞虽不作，而左手竟废，至今尚存，人呼为“徐支手”。

    　　○鱼怪会稽曹山{山全}入市得大鱼归，剖食之，馀半置纱厨内。至晚，厨中忽有光，举室皆亮。迫视，则所馀之鱼鳞甲通明，火光射目。曹大骇，盛以盘送于河，其光散入水中，随波摇荡，婉转间，成鱼而去。曹归家，屋中火发，东灭西起，衣物床帐烧毁都尽，而不及栋宇，凡三昼夜始息。食鱼之人，竟亦无恙。

    Explanation

    875

    ○ Xu Zhishou, a man from Xianyang, was extremely wealthy. His first son was very intelligent but died of a tumor at the age of six. Soon after, he had three more sons, all of whom looked similar and also died of tumors. By the time his third son died, Xu was already elderly. He was deeply grieved and used a knife to cut open his son's abdomen, removing the tumor and then amputating his left arm, cursing, "Do not come back to tempt me again." The tumor was shaped like a three-sided diamond, had a mouth, and could breathe. It was hung in a tree, dried by wind and sun, and even when touched by oil or fishy smells, its mouth would still move. Before a year had passed, Xu had another son who looked like the previous ones, but although he did not have a tumor, his left hand was paralyzed. He is still alive today, and people call him "Xu Zhishou."

    ○ A Fish Monster

    Cao Shanquan from Kuaiji bought a large fish and returned home, cutting it open to eat, and placing the remaining half in a gauze cabinet. At night, there was suddenly light in the kitchen, and the whole house became bright. Upon closer inspection, the remaining fish's scales were completely transparent, and the light was blinding. Cao was greatly frightened and placed the fish in a dish to send it to the river. The light dispersed into the water, moving with the waves, and eventually turned into a fish and swam away. When Cao returned home, there was a fire in the house, which started in the east and spread to the west, burning all clothes, beds, and curtains, but did not reach the main structure. The fire burned for three days and nights before it was extinguished. The person who ate the fish was unharmed.

    876

    ○盗鬼供状先君子在湖广臬司迟公维台署中，同事大兴人朱扬湖司钱谷。忽一日狂呼。

    　　趋视之，面如死灰，伏地昏迷。饮以姜汁，良久曰：“吾坐此校文案，日方正午，见地下砖响，有物蠕蠕然顶砖起。疑为鼠，以脚践之，砖亦平复。稍坐定，砖响如初，掀视之，有黑毛一团，类人头发，自土中起，阴风袭人，渐起渐大。先露两眼，瞪睛怒视，再露口颐腰腹。其黑如漆，颈下血淋漓，跃然而上举手抱我足曰：”汝在此乎！汝在此乎！吾前世山东盗也，法当死，汝作郯城知县，受我赃七千两，许为开脱。定案时，仍拟大辟，死不瞑目。今汝虽再世，而吾仇必报。‘言毕，即牵我入地。我大呼。彼见众客至，舍我走。“众视砖迹，犹宛然开。

    Explanation

    876

    The Confession of the Thieving Ghost

    My esteemed father was serving as a censor in the Huguang provincial administration under the supervision of Censor Chi Weitai. He worked alongside Zhu Yanghu, a native of Daxing, who was in charge of finances and grain storage. One day, my father suddenly shouted wildly.

    I rushed to see him, and his face was ashen; he had fallen to the ground and fainted. I gave him some ginger juice, and after a long while, he said: "I was sitting here reviewing documents when it was midday. I heard the bricks on the ground making a sound, and something moved slowly and flexibly, pushing the bricks up. I thought it was a mouse and stepped on it, and the bricks returned to their original state. After sitting down for a moment, the bricks made the same sound again, and I looked up to see a mass of black hair, like human hair, rising from the ground. A cold, eerie wind struck me, gradually growing stronger. First, two eyes appeared, glaring angrily, then the mouth, jaw, waist, and abdomen. It was as black as ink, with blood dripping from the neck. It suddenly jumped up and grabbed my feet, saying: 'Are you here? Are you here? I was a thief from Shandong in my past life, sentenced to death. You were the magistrate of Tancheng, accepted seven thousand taels of my bribe, and promised to release me. When the case was decided, you still sentenced me to death, and I died without closing my eyes. Now that you have been reborn, my revenge must be taken.' After finishing his words, he immediately dragged me into the ground. I shouted loudly. When he saw many guests arrive, he left me and ran away." The guests looked at the marks left by the bricks, which were still clearly visible.

    877

    嗣后，其鬼无日不至。有人共座，则不至。尤畏臬司迟公，闻迟公将至，便抱头远窜。公大书几上曰：“问恶鬼，汝作盗应死，敢与法吏仇乎？汝欲报仇，应仇于前生，敢仇于今世乎？速具供状来。”鬼夜墨书其侧，字迹歪斜，曰：“某不敢仇法吏，敢仇赃吏。某以盗故杀人，多受冥司炮烙，数十年，面目已成焦炭。每受刑必呼曰：”某当死，有许我不死者在也，郯城县某老爷受赃七千两，独不应加罪乎！‘呼六十余年，初不准理，今以苦海渐满，许我弛桎梏报冤，所具供状是实。“迟公无如何，不能朝夕伴朱，命多人守护之。

    Explanation

    877

    Afterward, the ghost was constantly present. When there was someone sitting together, it would not appear. It especially feared the provincial governor, Mr. Chi. Upon hearing that Mr. Chi was about to arrive, the ghost would clutch its head and flee far away. Mr. Chi wrote large on the desk and said, "Question the evil spirit: You, as a thief, should die. Dare you be enemies with the legal officials? You wish to take revenge. Should you take revenge in a previous life? Dare you take revenge in this world? Quickly prepare a confession and bring it here." The ghost wrote in ink on the side at night, with slanted and irregular handwriting, saying, "I do not dare to be enemies with the legal officials, but I dare to be enemies with corrupt officials. I killed someone because of theft and suffered many punishments from the underworld for several decades, my face having become like charcoal. Every time I was punished, I would cry out, 'I should die, but there is someone who promised not to let me die. The magistrate of Tancheng County accepted a bribe of seven thousand taels, should he not be punished? I cried out for over sixty years, initially not allowed, but now as the sea of suffering is gradually filling up, he allowed me to be freed from chains to seek revenge. The confession I prepared is true." Mr. Chi had no choice, could not accompany Zhu day and night, and ordered many people to guard him.

    878

    居月余，迟公生日演戏，诸客饮酒，强朱出现，朱曰：“吾待死之人，有何心情看戏？诸公爱我，可多命家人伴我。”如其言。席散往视，朱已缢于床。迟公及诸友俱责家人何以不管？佥云：“灯下吹来黑气一团，奴婢便各睡去。”或云：诸奴看戏，亦未必伴朱也。

    　　○时文鬼淮安程风衣，好道术，四方术士咸集其门。有萧道士琬，号韶阳，年九十馀，能游神地府。

    　　雍正三年，风衣宴客于晚甘园，萧在席间醉睡去，少顷醒，曰：“吕晚村死久矣乃有祸，大奇。”人惊问，曰：“吾适游地府间，见夜叉牵一老书生过，铁锁锒铛，标曰：”时文鬼吕留良，圣学不明，谤佛大过。‘异哉！“时坐间诸客皆诵时文，习《四书》讲义，素服吕者，闻之不信，且有不平之色。未几，曾静事发，吕果剖棺戮尸。

    Explanation

    878

    After staying for more than a month, on Mr. Chi's birthday, a play was performed, and the guests drank alcohol. Zhu was forced to appear. Zhu said, "I am a person awaiting death, what mood do I have to watch a play? If you all love me, you can have more of my family members accompany me." As he said, after the banquet, they went to see, and Zhu had hanged himself on the bed. Mr. Chi and his friends all blamed the family members for not taking care. They all said, "A dark gas came blowing under the lamp, and the maidservants and servants each went to sleep." Some said, "The servants watching the play did not necessarily accompany Zhu."

    In the same year, a Taoist named Xiao, known as Shao Yang, who was over ninety years old and could travel his spirit to the underworld, was present at a banquet in the late Gan Garden hosted by Cheng Fengyi of Huai'an. He fell asleep drunk at the table and soon woke up, saying, "Lü Wanchun has been dead for a long time and yet he has suffered, which is very strange." People were surprised and asked him, "We just saw Lü Wanchun in the underworld, bound with iron chains, marked with 'The ghost of Lü Liuliang, who did not understand the true teachings and slandered Buddhism too much.' It is indeed strange!" At the time, the guests at the table were all reciting classical texts and studying the Four Books commentary, and those who respected Lü Wanchun did not believe it and showed signs of dissatisfaction. Soon after, the case of Zeng Jing was exposed, and Lü Wanchun was indeed posthumously executed.

    879

    今萧犹存，严冬友秀才与同寓转运卢雅雨署中，亲见其醉后伸一手指，令有力者以利刃割之，了无所伤。

    　　○鬼弄人二则杭州沈济之，训蒙为业。一夕，梦金冠而髯者谓曰：“汝后园有理金一瓮，可往掘之。”沈曰：“未知何外？”曰：“有草绳作结，上穿康熙通宝钱一文，此其验也。”明早往园视之，果有草绳，且缚钱焉。沈大喜，持锄掘丈馀，卒无有，竟一怒而得狂易之疾。

    　　乾隆甲子，冯香山秀才梦神告曰：“今岁江南乡试题《乐则韶舞》。”冯次日即作此题，熟诵之。入闱，果是此题，以为必售，榜发无名。就馆广东，夜间独步，闻二鬼咿唔声。聆之，其闱中所作文也。一鬼诵之，一鬼拊掌曰：“佳哉，解元之文！”沈惊疑，以为是科解元必割截卷而偷其文字，辞馆入都，以状具控礼部。礼部为奏闻行查，江南解元薛观光，文虽不佳，并非冯稿也，获诬告之罪，谪配黑龙江。

    Explanation

    879

    Now, let's proceed with the translation of the source text into English, applying the insights from the pre-drafting research directly to handle idiomatic expressions, cultural allusions, syntactic ambiguities, and proper nouns as planned.

    Translation:

    Xiao Yu is still alive today. Yan Dongyou, a scholar, and his fellow lodger Lu Yayu, the magistrate of the transportation bureau, witnessed a supernatural event. After getting drunk, a bearded figure with a golden crown stretched out one finger and ordered a strong person to cut it with a sharp knife, but there was no injury.

    Two Tales of Ghostly Deception

    In Hangzhou, Shen Jizhi made a living teaching children. One night, he dreamed of a bearded figure wearing a golden crown who told him, "In your backyard, there is a pot of refined gold. You should go dig it up." Shen asked, "How will I know which one?" The figure replied, "There will be a rope tied into a knot, with a single 'Kangxi Tongbao' coin threaded through it. That will be the sign." The next morning, Shen went to the garden and found the rope, with the coin tied to it. Shen was overjoyed and began digging with a hoe, but after digging more than a meter deep, he found nothing. In a fit of anger, he developed a sudden and severe mental illness.

    In 1764, Feng Xiangshan, a scholar, dreamed of a deity who told him, "This year's provincial examination question will be 'If music is joyful, it is the Shao dance.'" Feng memorized and practiced this topic the next day. When he entered the examination hall, the question was indeed this one. He believed he would surely succeed, but his name was not on the list. He then went to Guangdong to teach. One night, he heard two ghosts reciting. Listening closely, it was the essay he had written. One ghost recited it, and the other clapped and said, "Excellent, this is the essay of the top scorer!" Feng was shocked and suspicious, thinking that the top scorer must have cut and stolen his text. He left his teaching position and went to the capital to file a complaint with the Ministry of Rites. The Ministry of Rites reported this to the emperor, who ordered an investigation. It was found that the top scorer, Xu Guangguan, had indeed written a poor essay, but it was not Feng's work. Feng was found guilty of false accusation and was exiled to Heilongjiang.

    End of Translation.

    880

    ○汉江冤狱曹震亭知汉江县，晚衙夜坐，见无头人手提一头，啾啾有声，语不甚了。曹大骇，遂病，病三日，死矣。家人欲殓，胸前尚温，过夜而苏，曰：被隶人引至阴府，见峨冠南面者，衣本朝服色，辕外人传呼：“汉江县知县曹学诗进。”曹行阳间属吏礼，向上三揖。神赐坐，问：“有人诉公，公知否？”曰：“不知。”

    　　神取几上牒词示曹。曹阅之，本县案卷也，起立曰：“此案本属有冤，为前令所定，已经达部，我申详三次，请再加审讯，为院所驳，驳牌现存。”神曰：“然则公固无罪也。”传呼冤鬼某进，阴风飒然，不见面目手足，但见血块一团叫跳呼号，滚风而至。神告以曹为申救之故，且曰：“汝冤终当昭雪，须另觅仇人。”

    Explanation

    880

    The Wrongful Case of Hanjiang

    Cao Zhen Ting served as the magistrate of Hanjiang County. One night, after his office hours, he sat late into the night. Suddenly, a ghostly apparition appeared, headless, holding another head, making a sound. The words it spoke were not very clear. Cao Zhen Ting was greatly startled and fell ill. He was sick for three days and then died. His family wanted to prepare his body for burial, but his chest was still warm. The next day, he woke up.

    He said, "I was led by a subordinate to the underworld. I saw someone wearing an elaborate hat facing south, dressed in the attire of the current dynasty. Someone outside the carriage called out, 'The magistrate of Hanjiang County, Cao Xue Shi, enter.' I performed the ritual of a subordinate in the mortal world and bowed three times towards the superior. The deity granted me a seat and asked, 'Is there someone who has accused you? Do you know about it?'"

    The deity took a document from the table and showed it to Cao. Cao read it, and it was a case from his county. He stood up and said, "This case originally had an injustice. It was decided by the previous magistrate and has already been reported to the higher authorities. I have submitted detailed reports three times, requesting a further investigation. It was rejected by the court, and the rejection order is still here."

    The deity said, "Then you are indeed innocent." The deity then called in the ghost who had accused him. A cold wind blew, and the ghost appeared as a mass of blood, calling out and jumping. The deity told Cao that he had intervened on his behalf and assured him that his冤would eventually be cleared, but he would need to find another enemy.

    881

    鬼伏地不肯去。神拱手向曹作送状，手挥隶人云：“速送速送。”曹猛然惊醒，不觉汗之沾衣也。自此辞官归家，长斋奉佛梦终其身。
