    　　○控鹤监秘记二则《控鹤监秘记》唐人张所纂，京江相公曾孙张冠伯家有抄数十页，皆载唐宫淫亵事，绝不类世所传《武后外传》。其略云：太后幸怀义数年，怀年骄恣不法，驰马南衙，为宰相苏良嗣批颊，后闻而衔之。一日置酒上阳宫，从容谓千金公主曰：“汝知朕左右无人乎，为此纡郁，奈何？”公主顿首曰：“臣欲奏天皇久矣，天皇不言，臣何敢先言。今陛下既知小宝之罪，臣窃以为天皇是何等佛，托身人间，广选男妃，自应择公卿旧家子弟姿禀粹者，置床第间，足以游养圣情，捐除烦虑。何事幸彼市井无赖之徒，为毒、昙献故事，被千秋万世拟秦、胡两后耶？”后曰：“微汝言，朕亦知之。近日宰相批怀义面，正欺其市井小人耳。若得公卿子通晓文墨者，南衙何敢辱之。”

    Explanation

    881

    A ghost lay on the ground, refusing to leave. The spirit bowed its hands toward Cao, making a gesture of dismissal, and waved to the attendant, saying, "Hurry to send him away." Cao suddenly woke up, and without realizing it, sweat had soaked his clothes. From that day on, he resigned from his position and returned home, observing long fasts and worshipping Buddha, living out the rest of his life in dreams.

    The Konghe Monastery Secret Record

    The Konghe Monastery Secret Record was compiled by Zhang Hong of the Tang Dynasty. Zhang Guanbo, a descendant of the former Prime Minister of Jingjiang, had a copy of the record, which contained dozens of pages detailing the lewd and scandalous affairs of the Tang palace, quite unlike the Extravagant Biography of Wu Zetian that is commonly known. The record briefly states: For several years, the empress favored Huaiyi, who grew arrogant and lawless. He galloped his horse in the southern palace, where he was slapped across the face by Chancellor Su Liangsi. Upon hearing this, the empress harbored resentment. One day, she held a banquet at the Shangyang Palace and spoke calmly to Princess Qianjin, saying, "Do you know that there are no trusted attendants around me, causing me distress? What should I do?" The princess bowed her head and said, "I have long wanted to report to the Heavenly Emperor, but since he has not spoken, how dare I speak first? Now that Your Majesty knows the crimes of Xiao Bao, I privately think that the Heavenly Emperor is what kind of Buddha, entrusting himself to the human world, should select young men from the noble families who are refined and virtuous, place them in the inner chambers, to satisfy His Majesty's emotions and alleviate His worries. Why should He favor those lowly and unscrupulous people of the common folk, following the stories of Mei Du and Tan Xian, being compared to the two empresses of Qin and Hu for thousands of generations?" The empress said, "Without your words, I would also know. Recently, the chancellor slapped Huaiyi's face, exactly because he is a lowly person of the common folk. If we could find a noble son who is well-versed in literature and writing, how could the southern palace dare to humiliate him?"

    882

    言毕叹。公主曰：“陛下勿叹。陛下知太宗时有凤阁侍郎张九成乎？其从子昌宗，年近弱冠，玉貌雪肤，眉目如画，其风采绝类巢刺王妃。”后默然，俯而未应。

    　　公主遽前跪起附耳语曰：“陛下毋过虑，儿兼知昌宗下体矣。儿于凝碧池置庄，春花盛时，驸马辄宴宾客，宴毕赐浴。浴时，儿于琉璃屏窥之，群臣无有佳于昌宗者。昌宗通体雪艳，无微痕半暇。瘦不露骨，丰不垂腴。其阴头丰根削，未起时，垂不甚长，浑脱类鹅卵。有洼棱高起五六分，鲜红柔润。”语未毕，太后色和，谩曰：“儿试耶？”公主曰：“儿非不涎之也，为后故不敢。然终不自信，故遣侍儿逼焉。”回顾侍者曰：“据实奏天皇，毋惭也。

    Explanation

    882

    After speaking, the Emperor sighed. The princess said, "Your Majesty, do not sigh. Do you know that during the reign of Emperor Taizong, there was a Fengge Shilang named Zhang Jiucheng? His nephew, Changzong, is nearly twenty years old, with a beautiful face and fair skin, and his eyes and eyebrows are like a painting. His appearance is extremely similar to that of Princess Chao Ci." The empress remained silent, bowed her head, and did not respond.

    The princess quickly stepped forward, knelt up, and whispered into the Emperor's ear, "Your Majesty, do not overthink this. I also know about Changzong's private parts. I set up a residence at Ningbi Pool, and during the time when spring flowers are in full bloom, my husband always entertains guests. After the banquet, he grants them a bath. During the bath, I peered through the琉璃screen and saw that none of the ministers are better than Changzong. Changzong's entire body is white and beautiful, with no tiny marks or half flaws. He is thin but not bony, and full but not overweight. His private parts are full at the base and slender, not very long when not erect, smooth and round like a goose egg. They have indentations raised about five or six points, bright red, soft, and smooth." Before she finished speaking, the empress's expression softened, and she said, "Did you try it?" The princess replied, "I am not drooling over it, but I dare not because of the empress. However, I am not confident, so I sent a maid to force it." She looked back at the maid and said, "Report the truth to the Emperor, and do not feel ashamed."

    883

    ”侍者跪起附耳如公主状，奏曰：“奴初遇昌宗时，似南海鲜荔枝，入口光嫩异常，棱张如伞，三四提后，花蕊尽开，神魂飞矣。昌宗迟速，亦不自为主张，婉转随奴意，事毕后，红玉颓然，奴触之，体犹噤也。”太后大喜，指公主曰：“儿诚解人。朕每闻世俗女子但好壮健，不选温柔，此村妪淫耳。夫壮健迟久，可以药力为也，海外慎恤胶，朕宫中有石许，无所用之。男阴佳处，全在美满柔和。怀义老奴，筋胜于肉，徒事憨猛，当时虽惬，过后朕体觉违和。御医沈南ギ肉差胜，然上下如一，头角蒙混，且皮弛，稍稍裹棱，非翘起不脱，故时觉不净。如卿所云，乃全才也。”

    Explanation

    883

    The servant knelt and leaned close to the ear, mimicking the manner of a princess, and reported, "When I first met Changzong, he was like fresh lychees from the South Sea, exceptionally smooth and tender when tasted, opening up like an umbrella, and after a few thrusts, the flower petals fully opened, leaving my soul flying away. Changzong neither hastened nor delayed, but went with the flow, gracefully adapting to my desires. After the act, his body collapsed in exhaustion, still stiff and rigid."

    The Empress was greatly pleased and pointed to the princess, saying, "You truly understand. I often hear that ordinary women only prefer strength, not gentleness. This is the behavior of a village woman, lacking refinement. Strength and endurance can be achieved through drugs. There is a cautionary drug called 'Shenxu gel' in my palace, but it is not used. The best part of a man's body is its fullness and gentleness. Huaiyi, the old servant, has more muscle than flesh, focusing only on brute force, which was pleasurable at the moment but left my body feeling out of sorts afterward. The imperial physician Shen Nang's meat is slightly better, but it is uniform throughout, lacking distinct features. Moreover, the skin is loose, requiring slight wrapping, and it won't come off unless lifted. As you said, he is truly all-around talented."

    884

    公主出，即命侍者召昌宗，衣以轻绡雾之衣，冠以玉清云仙之巾，浴兰芳、含鸡舌入宫。后果大幸，薛、沈辈不复召矣。

    　　当是时，后春秋高，学修养法，常含昌宗阴而睡。昌宗阴头丰肥，后口为之劳，终弗忍弃。后儿齿生，昌宗觉苦，乃荐易之。后口含易之，而以下体受昌宗，情尤酣艳。易之宠亚于昌宗，二人易班休沐。每归家，后遣人伺之，不许与妻交一语，上楼去梯。其母怜之，为置人壁间，方生国忠也。太后使昌宗骑木鹤，呼为子晋后身。又命修《三教珠英》，居控鹤监，与学士崔融、宋之问等酬唱。之问尤谄事二张，为持溺器，人笑之。

    Explanation

    884

    The princess went out and immediately ordered the attendant to summon Changzong. She dressed him in a light, gauzy, and ethereal robe and adorned him with a jade-clear cloud and fairy headband. He bathed in fragrant orchid and held a chicken tongue before entering the palace. As a result, he was greatly favored, and the Xue and Shen clans were no longer summoned.

    At that time, the Empress was of advanced age and studied methods of cultivation, often holding Changzong's genitalia while sleeping. Changzong's genitalia was full and plump, and the Empress's mouth was tired from it, but she could not bear to part with it. When the Empress's teeth came in, Changzong felt pain, and he then recommended Yi instead. The Empress held Yi in her mouth and received Changzong with her lower body, and the passion was especially intense. Yi's favor was second only to Changzong's, and the two alternated their days off. Every time they returned home, the Empress sent someone to watch them, not allowing them to speak to their wives, and they went up the stairs and removed the ladder. Their mother pitied them and placed someone in the wall, at the time when Guozhong was born. The Empress Dowager made Changzong ride a wooden crane, calling him the reincarnation of Zi Jin. She also ordered the compilation of the Sanjiao Zhuying (Three Teachings Pearl Light) and resided at the Control Crane Supervision, engaging in poetry exchanges with scholars like Cui Rong and Song Zhiwen. Song Zhiwen especially flattered the two Zhangs, holding the chamber pot, and people laughed at him.

    885

    之问曰：“卿知是何等溺乎？我为妇人，遇二张，亦不知何者为名节，况天后也。”后以龙锦千段赐公主，且曰：“朕闻古时公主多行不端，此选驸马者之罪也。自今以后，命画工写昌宗上下形体为式，如式者，方充驸马之选。庶几公主夫妻和乐，亦不虚生帝王家。”公主及侍儿虽人皆叩头呼万岁。中宗睿宗仿而行之。其时安乐公主虽骄奢，与武延秀恩好颇隆，无面首之侍，皆后力也。昌宗到貌寝，后主入宫，封一品崇让夫人。常戏曰：“夫人何修，得嫁六郎耶！”时有“一世修貌，二世修阴”之谣。

    Explanation

    885

    之问曰：“卿知是何等溺乎？我为妇人，遇二张，亦不知何者为名节，况天后也。”后以龙锦千段赐公主，且曰：“朕闻古时公主多行不端，此选驸马者之罪也。自今以后，命画工写昌宗上下形体为式，如式者，方充驸马之选。庶几公主夫妻和乐，亦不虚生帝王家。”公主及侍儿虽人皆叩头呼万岁。中宗睿宗仿而行之。其时安乐公主虽骄奢，与武延秀恩好颇隆，无面首之侍，皆后力也。昌宗到貌寝，后主入宫，封一品崇让夫人。常戏曰：“夫人何修，得嫁六郎耶！”时有“一世修貌，二世修阴”之谣。

    Translation:
    Zhi asked, "Do you know what kind of drowning this is? As a woman, I encountered the two Zhangs and did not know what constituted propriety or virtue, let alone as the Empress." The Empress then bestowed a thousand pieces of dragon-patterned silk upon the princess and said, "I have heard that ancient princesses often behaved improperly; this is the fault of those who selected their husbands. From now on, I shall order the painters to create a model of Changzong's upper and lower body. Only those who match this model shall be eligible for the position of prince consort. Perhaps this will ensure that the princess and her husband live in harmony and that her birth in the imperial family is not in vain." The princess and her attendants all knelt and cried, "Long live the Empress!" Emperor Zhongzong and Emperor Ruizong followed suit. At that time, Princess Anle, though骄奢, had a close and affectionate relationship with Wu Yanshou, without the company of male concubines, all due to the Empress's influence. Changzong, despite his less-than-ideal appearance, was granted entry to the palace by the Empress and was ennobled as First-Rank Lady of Respectful Yielding. He often joked, "What did Lady do to deserve marrying the sixth prince?" There was a proverb at the time: "In one life, one cultivates appearance; in the next, one cultivates the inner self."

    886

    亡何，五王起兵，入宫诛二张。横尸未收，百姓怨之，脔割股体为糜碎。宫人婉儿揣后意，于残骸中收得茎头半段，红润如生，手持献后。后泣曰：“是六郎也，契儿不能如是。”选第一府白玉盒盛之，曰：“朕万年后，以此为殉。”

    　　上官婉儿以祖仪得罪，没入掖廷，容貌瑰丽，兼工词翰，天后爱之，使侍侧治笔砚。后幸昌宗，不避婉儿。婉儿性黠，媚昌宗而远之，后尤喜。然昌宗每溲，婉儿顾盼，不能无情。

    　　天后命将作大匠于峡石为昌宗造园，屋舍皆黄金涂，白玉为阶。后燕奇香，拥真珠帐，幸昌宗。昌宗醉眠，阴软，后与为戏，拉茎上皮覆阴头，头棱高，皮格格不上，俄而挺然，根虽驽健，而头肉肥厚，如绵球成团，色若芙蓉，捻之类无精管者。后叹曰：“使人之意也消。”婉儿心动，裙下皆湿，不觉手近昌宗。

    Explanation

    886

    Shortly after, the five princes rose in rebellion, entered the palace, and killed the two Zhangs. The bodies lay uncollected, and the common people resented it, mutilating the flesh and limbs into minced pieces. The palace lady Wangǎn guessed the Empress's intention, found a half segment of the glans in the remains, and presented it to the Empress. The Empress wept and said, "This is the Sixth Prince, even his son could not do such a thing." She chose a first-rate jade box to hold it and said, "After my death, I will have this as a sacrifice."

    Wangǎn was punished for her family's conduct, conscripted into the imperial harem, and was loved by the Empress for her beauty and literary skills. The Empress ordered her to serve at her side and manage the writing utensils. The Empress favored Changzong without avoiding Wangǎn. Wangǎn was cunning and flattered Changzong while distancing herself, which the Empress especially delighted in. But whenever Changzong urinated, Wangǎn looked around and could not remain unaffected.

    The Empress ordered the chief architect to build a garden for Changzong at Xiasi, with buildings gilded with gold and steps made of white jade. The Empress enjoyed exotic fragrances and a pearl-embroidered canopy while favoring Changzong. Changzong was drunk and asleep, weak and soft, and the Empress played with him, pulling the skin over the glans, which was prominent but the skin did not fit smoothly. Soon it became erect, though the root was weak and healthy, and the glans was thick and fleshy, like a ball of cotton, the color of a lotus flower, and could be squeezed without a clear channel. The Empress sighed, "It made one's thoughts dissipate." Wangǎn's heart stirred, her skirt was wet, and she unconsciously reached out to Changzong.

    887

    后大怒，取金刀插其髻，曰：“汝敢近禁脔，罪当死！”六郎为哀求，始免。然额有伤痕，故于宫中常戴花钿也。

    　　吏部侍郎崔，以才貌年少，私侍婉儿。婉儿有外舍，极亭台之胜，招与宣淫。先通武三思，后通。问：“庐陵王三思何如？”曰：“庐陵王棱角混，韦皇后笑其食哀家梨，不削皮，何能知味？三思故自佳，然亦嫌肉薄耳。”问：“两后选男何法？”曰：“阴虽巨，以皮筋胜者不选。”问：“何故？”曰：“人之一身，舌无皮，故知味；踵皮厚，故履地。女阴纤膜，微蒙天生，男子之阴，亦去皮留膜，取极嫩处与之作合，又与棱角，使之扪摩，幼而蕊含，长而茄脱，以柔抵柔，故有氤氲化醇之乐。否则，拖皮带秽，进退麻漠，如隔一重甲矣。

    Explanation

    887

    The Empress flew into a rage, seizing a golden knife and piercing her hair bun, shouting, "How dare you approach my forbidden delicacy? Your crime deserves death!" The man known as "Sixth Brother" pleaded on her behalf, and she was spared. However, she bore a scar on her forehead, so she often wore decorative pins in the palace.

    Cui Yu, the Vice Minister of Personnel, was a young man of exceptional talent and beauty, and he secretly attended to the woman. She had a private retreat with extremely beautiful pavilions and terraces, where they engaged in secret trysts. She first had an affair with Wu Sansi, and later with Cui Yu. Cui Yu asked, "How does the Prince of Lu compare to Wu Sansi?" She replied, "The Prince of Lu is rough and unrefined. Empress Wei laughed at him for eating pears without peeling them, saying how could he know their taste? Wu Sansi is indeed good, but he is also criticized for lacking substance." He then asked, "What criteria do the two empresses use to select men?" She answered, "Even if a man is large, one who is not flexible and supple is not chosen." He asked, "Why is that?" She explained, "In a person, the tongue has no skin, so it can taste; the heel is thick-skinned, so it can walk on the ground. A woman's genitalia is a thin membrane, slightly covered by nature. A man's genitalia also has this membrane, and one should take the most sensitive part and engage in intimacy, adding some roughness to stimulate it. When young, it is like a bud; when grown, it is like a pod. Softness against softness brings a harmonious and pure pleasure. Otherwise, dragging skin and dirt, moving in a dull and unresponsive way, is like being separated by a layer of armor."

    888

    天后幸男子毕，不诟阴头离宫，冯小宝虽壮盛，头锐易离；六郎棱肥脑满，如鲜菌灵芝，虽宣泄，而阴头犹能填塞满宫，久而不脱，故欢爱之情，有馀不尽。六郎侍寝，后虽衰，仙液犹透重衾也。“曰：”如昭容言，天下优劣，岂独男子然耶！少忝官阶，为女子所悦，所遇岂无粲者。然下体亦正难言，往往有交无媾，木木然如瞽人投井，不知何往。尔时徒惫精神，少回味，道天下女子皆然。

    Explanation

    888

    The Empress Consort had completed her sexual relations with a man, and did not criticize the male genitalia detaching from the palace. Although Feng Xiaobao was strong and vigorous, his head was sharp and easily detached; Sixth Brother had a prominent forehead and a full brain, like fresh mushrooms and lingzhi, and even when releasing, his male genitalia could still fill the entire palace, remaining there for a long time without detaching. Therefore, the joy of love was never-ending. When Sixth Brother served in bed, the empress although old, the divine fluid still penetrated the heavy quilt. "As the Empress Dowager said, the superiority and inferiority of the world, how can it be only men who are like this? I held a minor official position and was favored by women. Those I met were not without outstanding ones. However, the lower body is indeed difficult to describe; often there is contact but no intercourse, stupidly like a blind person falling into a well, not knowing where to go. At that time, I wasted energy, with little aftertaste, saying that all women in the world are like this."

    889

    自蒙昭容恩接后，方知西子、毛嫱所以专宠六宫者，必别有胜人处。昭容花心粹，一交接，觉阴头触嫩处，如醍醐灌顶，毛发皆苏。手按昭容后窍，翕翕然跃，便知将宣泄，不敢摇身。俟谷道跃定后，再候意旨，故常得昭容欢。亦蒙昭容湛露之恩，深含细吐，山泽气交，次日上朝，不觉疲惫。想世间男子喜干，女子好久，皆如乞丐，食猪脂三斗，便道穷奢极欲，真初世人耳。“昭容笑曰：”卿言大快。然知音甚难，大抵男女交接，如匙之配锁，各有所宜，闻刘妃阴有横骨，非尖劲者不能入宫。卿阴头柔嫩，若遇之，不大苦卿耶！天后云：“肉重则进佳，棱高则退佳。‘真解人语。”方昵语间，安乐公主拥驸马武延秀至，颇有所闻。公主褫驸马裤，手其阴夸曰：“此何如崔郎耶？”昭容曰：“直似六郎，何止崔郎！此皆天后选婿之功，不可忘也。”是夕酣饮，观拔河之戏。次日为中宗生辰，至午始朝贺。

    Explanation

    889

    After receiving the favor of the Empress Dowager, I came to realize that the legendary beauties Xi Shi and Miaoqiang, who were favored by the six palaces, must have had something exceptional about them. The Empress Dowager's delicate and refined nature, when we were intimate, felt like a touch of tenderness that was refreshing and invigorating, as if a sudden enlightenment had struck me, causing my hair to stand on end. When I touched her posterior, I felt a vigorous movement, knowing that she was about to reach her climax, and I dared not move. After her climax subsided, I waited for her instructions, and thus I often pleased her. I too received the Empress Dowager's deep and lasting favor, experiencing a harmonious mingling of energies, and the next day when I attended court, I felt no fatigue. I thought that in this world, men enjoy intercourse, and women desire it, but if they indulge excessively, it is like a beggar eating three pecks of pork fat, indulging in extreme luxury and desire, truly like the people of the early world. The Empress Dowager smiled and said, "Your words are very agreeable. However, finding a true understanding is difficult. Generally, in intimate relations between men and women, it is like a key fitting a lock, each having its own suitability. I have heard that the Empress Consort Liu has a bony structure in her genitalia, and only someone with a sharp and strong penis can enter her. Your penis is soft and tender, if you were to encounter her, wouldn't it not be painful for you? The Empress Regnant said, 'A fleshy partner is better for penetration, while a more angular partner is better for withdrawal.' Truly insightful words." Just as we were speaking intimately, Princess Anle arrived with her husband Wu Yuanxiu, and they overheard our conversation. The princess stripped her husband's pants and touched his genitalia, boasting, "How does this compare to Cui Lang?" The Empress Dowager said, "It is exactly like Sixth Brother, not just Cui Lang! This is all due to the Empress Regnant's skill in selecting a husband, and it cannot be forgotten." That night, we indulged in drinking and watched the game of tug-of-war. The next day was the birthday of Emperor Zhongzong, and we began the court ceremony at noon.

    890

    当是时，虽通昭容，附三思，而心知韦氏必亡，故阴附临淄王。王起兵诛韦后，婉儿持灯来迎，亦斩于纛下。为同平章事，竟不能救。侍郎张说使子均收昭容尸，厚葬之，兼表请复昭容号，编序其文集，人莫不多说而恶也。

    Explanation

    890

    At that time, although  had an affair with the concubine and was aligned with Li Xian, he knew in his heart that the Wei family would inevitably fall, so he secretly supported Li Longji. When Li Longji raised an army to eliminate the Empress Wei, Zhang Shuo's wife came with a lamp to greet him, and was also beheaded in front of the military standard.  became a co-chief minister but ultimately failed to save her. Ouyang, the Attendant, sent his son Jun to retrieve the concubine's body and buried her with great ceremony. He also submitted a memorial requesting the restoration of her title and the compilation of her literary works. People praised Ouyang but criticized .

    891

    ○牛乞命天台县令钟公醴泉为余言：其尊人守贵州大定府，设局办船。日正午，忽有牛突入铅厂。数十人鞭之，不肯去。醴泉往观，牛伏在作叩头状，因问牵牛者曰：“此耕牛乎？宰牛乎？”曰：“宰牛。”问：“价若干？”曰：“七千。”钟曰：“以牛与我，以价与汝，何如？”牵牛者谢领钱去，牛蹶然起矣。

    　　○猪乞命奉天锦州府之南有天桥厂，海泊交易处。屠人缚一猪，将杀以入市。其猪乘间啮断绳索，奔至海客前，屈双足伏地。屠人执绳追至。海客询其市价，如数付与，以此猪舍于海会寺之龙神庙。人呼猪道人，则应。曰何得无礼，辄屈前双足，向人作叩首状。牙长数寸，脚爪环裹如螺，其大倍于常猪。

    　　○张世荦张世荦字遇春，杭州府诸生。每入试场，仿佛有人持其卷者，迨晓，则墨污被黜，积愤殊甚。

    Explanation

    891

    A Cow Pleads for Its Life

    The magistrate of TianTai County, Zhong Gong Li Quan, told me: His father, who was the governor of Dading Prefecture in Guizhou, had set up an office to manage shipbuilding. At noon, suddenly a cow rushed into the lead factory. Dozens of people lashed it, but it would not leave. Zhong Gong went to see, and the cow knelt down and made a bowing gesture. He then asked the cowherd, "Is this a work ox or a slaughter cow?" The cowherd replied, "It is a slaughter cow." Zhong Gong asked, "What is the price?" The cowherd said, "Seven thousand." Zhong Gong said, "Give me the cow, and I will give you the money. How does that sound?" The cowherd thanked him and took the money to leave, and the cow suddenly got up.

    A Pig Pleads for Its Life

    In the south of Fengtian Jinzhou Prefecture, there is a place called Tianqiao Factory, a location for maritime trade. A butcher tied up a pig, intending to slaughter it and sell it in the market. The pig took advantage of a moment and bit through the rope, running to the front of a sea merchant and bending its front legs to kneel on the ground. The butcher grabbed the rope and chased after it. The sea merchant asked about the market price, paid the amount, and took the pig to the Dragon God Temple at Haihui Temple. When people called the pig "Taoist," it responded. They said, "How can you be so rude, bending your front legs and bowing to people?" The pig had teeth several inches long, and its hooves were tightly curled like a snail shell, making it much larger than an ordinary pig.

    Zhang Shiluo

    Zhang Shiluo, also known as Yuchun, was a student from Hangzhou Prefecture. Whenever he entered the examination hall, he felt as if someone was holding his test paper. By morning, the paper was stained with ink, and he was disqualified. He accumulated great resentment over this.

    892

    乾隆甲子科入闱，加意防范。试卷誊真，至晚，另贮他所，坐号中留心伺察。

    　　睹一女子舒手探卷，急执之，厉声问曰：“予与汝何仇，七试而污我卷？”曰：“今岁君应中解元，我亦难违帝命，但君当为我剖雪前言，择地瘗我，以释冤谴。

    　　我即君对门钱店女也。当日邻人戏谓君与我有私，君实无之，乃不为辨明，且风情自命，假无为有，以资嘲谑。既嫁，而夫信浮言，不与我同处。我无以自明，气忿投缳。君污我名，我污君卷，迟君七科宜也。“言毕不见。张毛骨俱栗。甫出场，即访其家，告以故，而捐资助葬之，且为延僧超荐。是科揭晓，果中第一名。

    　　○洗心池洗心池在茅山乾元观西，石壁上有“洗心池”三字，笔法遒劲，隐而不见。

    　　欲见，则以池水沃之，虽大旱不涸。相传钱妙真独居燕洞宫修炼，或谤之，乃于此刳腹洗心以相示，故名。

    Explanation

    892

    In the Jiazi year of the Qianlong era, Zhang entered the examination hall with extra precautions. The examination papers were carefully copied, and by nightfall, they were stored in a different location. Zhang kept a close watch in his designated seat.

    Suddenly, he saw a woman reaching out to touch the papers. Zhang quickly grabbed her and sternly asked, "What do I have against you, that you have defiled my paper after seven examinations?" The woman replied, "This year, you should be the top scholar. I also cannot defy the emperor's will, but you must clear my name by finding a place to bury me, to release me from my curse.

    I am the daughter of the money shop across the street from you. Once, neighbors jokingly said that you and I had an affair, but you did not have one, yet you did not clarify it. You boasted about it, pretending to have no affair, using it as material for ridicule and jest. After I married, my husband believed the rumors and did not live with me. I had no way to clear my name, so I hanged myself out of anger. You defiled my name, and I defiled your paper, so it is appropriate that it takes you seven examinations to clear this." After speaking, she disappeared. Zhang was so frightened that his hair stood on end. As soon as he left the examination hall, he went to her family, told them the reason, and donated money to help with the burial, and also arranged for a monk to perform a memorial service. When the results were announced, he indeed became the top scholar.

    ○ Wash Heart Pool

    The Wash Heart Pool is located west of the Qianyuan Temple on Mount Mao. On the stone wall, the three characters "Wash Heart Pool" are inscribed in bold, vigorous strokes, yet they are hidden from view.

    To see them, one must pour the pool water over them. Even during a great drought, the pool does not dry up. It is said that Qian Miaozhen lived alone in the Yandong Palace for cultivation, and some slandered her. She then carved her abdomen and washed her heart here to show her purity, hence the name.

    This translation aims to be faithful to the original text while ensuring it reads naturally in English. It captures the supernatural elements, cultural context, and the emotional intensity of the story.

    893

    ○活死人墓道人江文谷于洗心池旁培小阜，叠石塞牖，跌坐于中，嘱其徒云：“每日向牖呼我，应则已，水应则入收遗蜕。”呼之三年又皆应，忽一日应曰：“可厌，吾去矣！”嗣后不应，启石视之，尸果僵，故称活死人墓。

    　　○屋倾有数总宪金公德瑛视学江西，考吉安府童生。五鼓点名毕，灯下见红衣妇人从考棚趋出，冉冉腾空而去。问之朴隶，皆有所见。公心恶之，即以《中庸》“必有妖孽”四字命题。日正午，诸生方握笔，忽考棚倾倒，压死三十六人。金公据实奏闻，上怜之，俱钦赐生员。

    　　余亲家史少司马抑堂任福建臬使时，与粮道王介祉等四人同坐花厅议事，闻梁上屋角沙沙有声。客欲起避，史公不可。已而声渐大，有鼠呼曰：“出出”者再。史亦心动，急与四客齐出，则花厅倒矣，几案皆碎。是日，省中府县俱来请安，史公笑谓曰：“设使四大员一时并命，则司道之印，诸公委署，不皆有分乎！”

    Explanation

    893

    The Living-Dead Person's Tomb

    A hermit named Jiang Wengu, at the Washin Pool, built a small mound and placed stones to block the windows, sitting in a meditative posture inside. He instructed his disciples: "Every day, call out to me through the window. If I respond, stop; if the water responds, enter to collect my remains." For three years, they called out, and he always responded. Suddenly, one day, he said, "I am tired of this, I am leaving!" After that, he no longer responded. When they opened the stones to look inside, his body was found stiff, hence the name "Living-Dead Person's Tomb."

    The Collapsing House

    During his inspection of education in Jiangxi, Governor Jin Deying examined the young scholars of Ji'an Prefecture. At dawn, after the roll call, he saw a woman in red robes emerging from the examination hall, slowly ascending into the sky. When he asked the low-ranking officials, they all reported seeing her. Jin Deying was displeased and immediately set a question based on the Zhongyong (Doctrine of the Mean): "There will surely be calamities." At noon, while the scholars were writing, the examination hall suddenly collapsed, killing thirty-six people. Jin Deying reported the incident truthfully, and the emperor, moved by it, granted all the surviving scholars the status of scholar-officials.

    My relative-in-law, Shi Yiting, the Deputy Governor of Fujian, was sitting in the flower hall with the grain commissioner Wang Jiezhi and three others to discuss matters. They heard a rustling sound from the corner of the ceiling. The guests wanted to leave, but Shi Yiting forbade them. The sound grew louder, and a rat called out, "Out, out," twice. Shi Yiting was also startled and quickly left with the four guests. The flower hall then collapsed, and all the tables and desks were broken. That day, the prefects and county magistrates of the province came to pay their respects, and Shi Yiting joked, "If the four of us had died at the same time, then the seals and duties of the provincial and prefectural offices, wouldn't you all have a share of them?"

    894

    ○沔布十三匹杭州胡某，程九峰中丞之表侄也。中丞巡抚湖北，胡往求馆，荐与荆州刺史某署中司书记事。半年后，胡妻在家病疟，忽为鬼所附，声如男子。听之，乃其夫也。口称：“到湖北后，蒙中丞公荐往荆州，宾主相得。不料未二月患病身死，有衣箱行李，新买沔阳布十三匹，现在署中，须着人往取。我客死饥寒，可供木主祭我，并广招名僧超度我。”家人闻之环泣，当即成服立主。以死无日月，未便报讣。

    　　亡何，妻病痊。家故贫，欲差人往楚迎丧，以无盘费，屡屡迁延。亡何，胡竟归里，举家骇然，以为鬼也。坐定谈说，方悟前所凭者，乃邪鬼借名索食求超度故也。顷之，衣箱到门，开之，果有布十三匹，的系胡过沔阳时所买。

    　　○牛鬼山守岁广西柳州有牛卑山，形如女阴，粤人呼阴为卑，因号牛卑山。每除夕，必男妇十人守之待旦，或懈于防范，被人戏以竹木梢抵之，则是年邑中妇无不淫奔。

    Explanation

    894

    Thirteen Bolts of Cloth from Mian, Mr. Hu of Hangzhou, was a distant nephew of Zhongcheng Cheng Jiufeng. When Zhongcheng was serving as the provincial governor of Hubei, Mr. Hu sought employment and was recommended to serve as the record-keeper in the office of a certain governor of Jingzhou. Half a year later, Mr. Hu's wife fell ill with malaria at home and was suddenly possessed by a ghost, her voice sounding like that of a man. Upon hearing it, they realized it was Mr. Hu. He said, "After arriving in Hubei, I was graciously recommended by Zhongcheng to go to Jingzhou, where the host and guest got along well. Unexpectedly, within two months, I fell ill and died. I have a trunk and luggage, including thirteen bolts of new cloth from Mianyang, currently in the office. Please send someone to retrieve them. I died as a guest, suffering from hunger and cold. Please provide a wooden tablet for me to be worshipped, and widely recruit famous monks to help me transcend." Upon hearing this, the family gathered around and wept, immediately completing the mourning attire and setting up a memorial tablet. Since the exact date of his death was unknown, it was not convenient to report the death.

    Not long after, Mr. Hu's wife recovered from her illness. The family was poor and wanted to send someone to bring back Mr. Hu's body from Hubei, but due to lack of funds, they repeatedly delayed the arrangements. Not long after that, Mr. Hu unexpectedly returned to his hometown, and the entire family was startled, thinking it was a ghost. After sitting down and talking, they finally realized that what had possessed his wife earlier was an evil spirit pretending to be Mr. Hu, demanding food and seeking salvation. Shortly thereafter, the trunk arrived at the door. When they opened it, indeed, there were thirteen bolts of cloth, which Mr. Hu had purchased when he passed through Mianyang.

    A Cow Vagina Mountain for New Year's Eve

    In Guangxi Province, there is a mountain called Niu Bei Shan, which has a shape resembling a female vagina. The people of Guangdong call a vagina "bei," hence the name Niu Bei Shan. Every New Year's Eve, ten men and women must guard the mountain until dawn. If they are lax in their vigilance, someone might playfully strike the mountain with a bamboo or wooden pole, and in that year, no women in the county would remain chaste.

    895

    有邑令某恶之，命里保将土块填塞。是年，其邑妇女小便梗塞，不能前后溲，致有伤命者。

    　　广东沙面上妓船如云，河泊大使专司船政。有总督某严禁之，随即海水溢漫，城不没者三板。地方绅贾俱以为言，乃收回禁约以试之，果令收而水退。至今妓船愈多。

    　　○鬼拜风钱塘孙学田，开盐店温州城中，与友钱晓苍往来甚狎。钱有楼三间，封锁颇密，相传有鬼，人不敢居。孙素有胆，与同人赌胜，铺床楼上，烧巨烛二枝，竟往居焉。

    　　夜二鼓，闻推门声，有艳装女子冉冉来。见烛光，意若畏之，敛衽再拜。每一俯首，则阴风从其袖生，一烛灭矣。孙掷以剑，鬼走下楼去。孙知将复来，所恃惟烛，乃以所灭烛重加点明，以身拥触而坐。鬼果再至，又作拜状，见孙上坐，欲却欲前。孙以剑掷，鬼变恶状，上前格斗，彼此相持不已。忽闻楼外鸡鸣，遂化黑气一团滚楼而下。温州人为之语曰：“人拜曲躬，鬼拜生风。但逢孙老，比鬼还凶。”

    Explanation

    895

    In a certain county, a magistrate disliked something and ordered the local officials to fill it with earth. That year, the women in the county suffered from urinary blockage, unable to urinate, resulting in some deaths.

    In Guangdong, the number of pleasure boats on the river was like clouds, and the river commissioner was in charge of boat administration. A certain governor-general strictly prohibited them, and immediately the sea water overflowed, leaving the city only three boards above being submerged. Local gentry and merchants all spoke out, and they then withdrew the prohibition to test it. Indeed, when the boats were allowed back, the water receded. Until now, there are more pleasure boats.

    A man named Sun Xuetian from Qiantang, who operated a salt shop in Wenzhou city, had a very close relationship with a friend named Qian Xiaocang. Qian had a three-story building with tightly locked doors and windows, and it was said that there was a ghost, so people dared not live there. Sun was always brave and bet with his colleagues, laying out a bed on the upper floor, lighting two large candles, and finally went to stay there.

    At the second drumbeat of the night, he heard the sound of the door being pushed open, and a woman dressed in fine clothes slowly approached. Seeing the candlelight, she seemed to be afraid of it and bowed twice. Every time she bowed her head, a cold wind came from her sleeves, and one candle went out. Sun threw a sword at her, and the ghost ran downstairs. Sun knew she would come back, relying only on the candles, so he relit the extinguished candle and sat hugging the candle. The ghost indeed came back again and bowed again, seeing Sun sitting above, she hesitated to move forward or backward. Sun threw a sword at her, and the ghost turned into a fierce form, fighting fiercely, neither giving up. Suddenly, they heard a rooster crowing outside the building, and the ghost then turned into a black gas and rolled down the building. The people of Wenzhou said: "When people bow, they bend their bodies; when ghosts bow, they create wind. But if you encounter Old Sun, he is even more ferocious than a ghost."

    896

    ○僵尸夜肥昼瘦俞苍石先生云：凡僵尸夜出攫人者，貌多丰腴，与生人无异。昼开其棺，则枯瘦如人腊矣。焚之，有啾啾作声者。

    　　○黑云劫王师征缅甸，有昆明县皂隶叶某，死三日复苏，言被鬼卒勾赴冥司，有大殿朱门如王者居，门外坐官吏甚多，皆手一簿，判记甚忙。判毕，则黑气一团，覆于簿上，有椎腰蹙额自称劳苦者。叶阳寿未尽，以不在应死之数，故仍放还。

    　　路间私问鬼卒：“彼官吏所执何簿？”曰：“人簿三，兽簿五。”问：“何为有簿？”曰：“从古人间征战之事，皆天上劫数先定，无可挽回。一切应死者，皆先写入黑云劫簿中，虽一骡一马，皆无错误。终竟兽多人少，故其簿有‘人三兽五’之说。”问：“应此劫者，省城中可有某官乎？”曰：“第一名即你家总督也。”其时督滇南者刘公藻，丙辰鸿词翰林，后自刎。

    Explanation

    896

    Zombies are Fat at Night and Thin During the Day

    Mr. Yu Cangshi said: All zombies that come out at night to snatch people often appear plump and well-fed, no different from living people. Opening their coffins during the day, they become dry and emaciated like mummies. Burning them, some make a chirping sound.

    Black Cloud Calamity

    During the imperial campaign against Burma, there was a low-ranking official named Ye from Kunming County who died and revived after three days. He said he was summoned by ghost soldiers to the underworld, where there was a grand hall with a red gate, resembling a royal residence. Outside the gate, many officials sat, each holding a book, and they were very busy recording judgments. After completing their judgments, a black cloud would cover the books, and some would bend over with a frowning face, claiming to be tired. Ye's lifespan had not yet ended, and he was not among those destined to die, so he was allowed to return.

    On the road, he privately asked a ghost soldier, "What books do those officials hold?" The soldier replied, "There are three human books and five animal books." Ye asked, "Why are there books?" The soldier explained, "From ancient times, all human conflicts and wars are predestined calamities decided in heaven, and there is no way to change them. All those who are destined to die are first written into the Black Cloud Calamity books. Even a single mule or horse is recorded without error. Ultimately, there are more animals than humans, so the books are said to have 'three human and five animal' entries." Ye then asked, "Are there any officials in the provincial capital who are affected by this calamity?" The soldier replied, "The first one is your governor." At that time, the governor of Dian (Yunnan Province) was Liu Gongzao, who passed the imperial examination in the year Bingshen and was appointed to the Hanlin Academy. He later committed suicide.

    897

    ○金秀才苏州金秀才晋生，才貌清雅，苏春进士爱之，招为婿，婚有日矣。

    　　金夜梦红衣小鬟引至一处，房舍精雅，最后有圆洞门，指曰：“此月宫也，小姐奉候久矣。”俄而一丽人盛妆出曰：“秀才与我有夙缘，忍舍我别婚他氏乎？”金曰：“不敢。”遂携手就寝，备极绸缪。嗣后，每夜必梦，欢好倍常，而容颜日悴。举家大惧，即为完姻。苏女亦有容色，秀才爱之如梦中人。嗣后夜间，酉戌前与苏氏交，酉戌后与梦中人交。久之，竟不知何者为真，何者为梦也。

    　　其父百般禳解，终无效。体本清赢，斫削逾年，成瘵疾而卒。

    　　与梦中女唱和甚多，不能全录，但记其《赠金郎一绝》云：“佳偶岂易寻，夺郎如夺彩。幸亏下手强，争先得为快。”

    　　○董观察董观察名榕，官赣南道时，所属上犹县某村素被山瀑冲没田庐，公为相度开河，引水入江，居民安堵。又改佛寺为濂溪书院，规模一新。

    Explanation

    897

    The Scholar Jin from Suzhou, Jin Sheng, was a man of exceptional talent and refined appearance. Su Chun, a successful candidate in the imperial examinations, admired him greatly and invited him to be his son-in-law. The wedding was set to take place soon.

    One night, Jin dreamt of a young girl in red leading him to a place with elegant houses. At the end, there was a round door, and she pointed and said, "This is the Moon Palace, and the young lady has been waiting for you for a long time." Soon after, a beautiful woman appeared in splendid attire and said, "Scholar, we have a predestined bond. How can you abandon me to marry someone else?" Jin replied, "I dare not." They then went to bed together, indulging in utmost intimacy. Afterward, Jin dreamt of her every night, and their affection became even more intense, but his face grew increasingly withered. His entire family was greatly afraid and decided to proceed with the marriage. Su Chun's daughter was also beautiful, and Jin loved her as much as he loved the woman from his dreams. Afterward, at night before 9 PM, he would be with Su Chun's daughter, and after 9 PM, he would be with the woman from his dreams. Over time, he could no longer distinguish between reality and dreams.

    His father tried every possible remedy, but nothing worked. Jin, who was naturally thin, gradually declined in health over a year, eventually developing consumption and dying.

    Jin had many exchanges of poetry with the woman from his dreams, but they cannot all be recorded here. Only one of her verses is noted: "A good match is not easily found; snatching the husband away is like snatching a prize. Fortunately, I acted quickly and took the lead."

    Dong Guancha

    Dong Guancha, whose name was Feng, served as the governor of Gannan. In one of his jurisdictions, a village in Shangyou County had long been devastated by mountain torrents that had destroyed their fields and homes. He carefully planned and opened a canal to divert the water into the river, allowing the residents to live in peace. He also transformed a Buddhist temple into the Lianxi Academy, giving it a new and grand scale.

    898

    亡何，丁太夫人忧，哀毁过度，欲以身殉。扶榇返里，至滕王阁下，维舟受唁，大吏亲来抚慰，观者无不谓董公真孝子，真好官。次早，方欲解缆，忽家仆等惊觅观察不得，急报守土官。沿江打捞，俱无踪迹。经一昼夜，尸竟逆流至丰城县沙岸上。验视之，犹白衣麻带，面目如生，乃具殓送至舟中。

    　　月余，公旧仆某偶至上犹，土人告以感公开河之恩，立庙祀公。仆欣然走至庙中拜觇神像，则俨然公之面目。询立像时日，即公堕水夕也。

    　　○狐仙开帐和州张某，作客扬州，寓兴教寺。寺中僧舍，素有狐仙，无人敢居。张性落拓，意往居焉。未三日，果有一翁，自称吴刚子求见。揖而与言，风采颇异，能知过去未来之事。因问：“可是仙乎？”曰：“不敢。”张故贫士，意欲交结之，以图富贵，遂设酒食，与之饮宴，吴亦答谢。

    898

    [English translation]

    Shortly after, he suffered the loss of his mother-in-law, and his grief was so excessive that he wished to follow her in death. He carried her coffin back home and anchored the boat at the Tengwang Pavilion to receive condolences. High officials personally came to comfort him, and all who saw him said that Governor Dong was indeed a filial son and a truly good official. The next morning, just as he was about to untie the ropes, suddenly the household servants could not find the governor. They immediately reported to the local officials, who searched along the river but found no trace. After a day and night, the body floated upstream to the sandy shore of Fengcheng County. When examined, he was still wearing white clothing and a hemp sash, with a face as if he were alive. They then prepared the body and sent it back to the boat.

    More than a month later, one of the official's old servants happened to go to Shangyou. The locals told him that they were grateful for the official's kindness in dredging the river and had built a temple to worship him. The servant happily went to the temple to pay respects and see the statue of the deity. It was exactly like the official's face. When asked about the date the statue was made, it was said to be the same night the official fell into the water.

    ○ A Fox Spirit Opens a Tent in Hezhou

    A certain Zhang from Hezhou, a guest in Yangzhou, stayed at Xingjiao Temple. The temple's monk's quarters were said to be haunted by a fox spirit, and no one dared to live there. Zhang, being a free-spirited person, decided to stay there. Not three days later, an old man appeared, introducing himself as Wu Gangzi, seeking to meet him. After greeting and speaking with him, Zhang found that the old man had a very unusual demeanor and could know past and future events. Zhang asked, "Are you a spirit?" The old man replied, "I dare not say so." Zhang, being a poor scholar, wanted to befriend the old man in hopes of gaining wealth and status, so he prepared a feast and entertained him, to which the old man also expressed gratitude.

    899

    未半月，张力竭矣，而吴之酒馔甚丰。张遂起贪念，终日嬲其设席。吴作主人，亦无吝色。如是者月余，吴忽不至。时遇霉雨，张开箱晒衣，则全箱空矣，中书一帐，并质钱帖数纸：“某日鸡鱼若干，某日蔬果若干。”皆典张之衣服而用之，笔笔开除，不空设一席，不妄消一文。

    　　○皮蜡烛上虞人钱姓者，为人佣工。夜归，见女路哭，问其故，曰：“夫亡无归，家居夏盖山，一时迷路，求为指示。”钱与谐戏，相随至一室中，成夫妇之好。如是者数月。主人见其貌日憔悴，再三问钱，钱言其故。主人曰：“此鬼也，再与交时，须取渠一物以为验。”钱如其言，伴与欢笑，而暗剪女发一束，女大惊走去。钱细视所居之地，全无房屋，其与此女淫处，精流蟹洞中，皆血也。发如烛而软黑若牛皮，刀斫火焚不坏。自此不敢出门，匿主人家。

    Explanation

    899

    Within a short period, Zhang's resources were exhausted, while Wu's supply of food and drink was abundant. Zhang then developed a greedy desire and persistently asked Wu to set up meals. Wu, acting as the host, showed no reluctance. This continued for over a month, until Wu suddenly stopped coming. During a period of heavy rain, Zhang opened his box to dry his clothes, only to find it completely empty, with inside a ledger and several notes: "On a certain day, so many chickens and fish; on another day, so many vegetables and fruits." All of these were expenses recorded meticulously, with no unnecessary meals set up and no money wasted.

    A man from Shangyu named Qian, who worked as a laborer, returned home one night and saw a woman crying on the road. He asked her why she was crying, and she replied, "My husband has died, and I have nowhere to go. I live in Xuegai Mountain, and I have lost my way. Please guide me." Qian accompanied her, and they soon arrived at a room where they became intimate. This continued for several months. The owner noticed that the woman's appearance became increasingly gaunt and repeatedly asked Qian about it. Qian told him the truth. The owner said, "This woman is a ghost. The next time you are with her, you must take something from her as proof." Qian followed the owner's advice and, while they were enjoying each other's company, secretly cut off a lock of the woman's hair. The woman was greatly alarmed and fled. Qian carefully examined the place where they had been, and found no house. The spot where they had engaged in intimacy was actually a crab hole, filled with blood. The hair was as soft and black as a candle and could not be destroyed by knives or fire. From then on, Qian dared not go out and hid in the owner's home.

    900

    未几，鬼入主人家，附其婢身作闹曰：“还我钱郎！不还我者，即将钱郎交与汝家。我暂去，明年来捉。”且云：“俟今秋汝寿尽时，当来降祸。”至期，竟不验。钱姓至今犹存。此事台州张秀墀为余言。

    　　○乍浦海怪乾隆壬辰八月甘三日，黎明大风雨，平湖、乍浦之海滨有物突起，自东南往西北，所过拔木以万计，民居屋上瓦多破碎。中间有类足迹大如圆桌子者，竟不知是何物。有某家厅房移过尺许，仍不倒坏。

    Explanation

    900

    Not long after, a ghost entered the household and possessed the body of the maid, causing a commotion and saying, "Return my Mr. Qian! If you do not return him, I will hand him over to your family. I will temporarily leave, but I will come to get him next year." The ghost also said, "When your life ends this autumn, I will bring misfortune." However, when the time came, it did not happen as expected. The surname Qian still exists to this day. This story was told to me by Zhang Xiuchi of Taizhou.

    On the morning of the third day of the eighth month in the Renchen year of the Qianlong era, a sudden and violent storm swept through the coastal areas of Pinghu and Zhapu. A strange creature suddenly appeared, rising from the southeast and moving toward the northwest. It uprooted countless trees in its path, and many of the tiles on the roofs of houses were shattered. Among the debris, there were footprints as large as a round table, but no one knew what the creature was. In one house, the main room was shifted several feet but remained standing.

    901

    ○天开眼增湖张学攵坡，一日偶在庭中，天无片云，忽闻砉刂然有声，天开一缝，中阔，两头小，其状若舟。睛光闪铄，圆若车轴，照耀满庭，良久方闭。识者以为此即“天开眼”云。

    　　○泥像自行平湖张氏，世居蒹葭围。其始迁祖名迪，字静庵，明洪武间人。殁时，其家泥塑静庵夫妇二像，高七八寸，供家庙中，所居屋归属长房。历四百馀年，长房子孙贫，屋倾圯，仅存数间，而其像犹在。

    　　张低故有宗祠，距静庵故居三里许。一日黎明，有乡人操舟者见两老人来雇渡船，遂载以行。问：“何往？”云：“将之张家祠堂。”既登岸，疾步如飞，舟人望之，见形躯渐小。无何，抵祠前，守祠僧闻扣门声，起视之，寂无所见，惟见两泥像在门枢下，一时惊以为异。其裔孙张舟九方重修祠宇，因加彩绘，别设一厨，供之祠中。

    Explanation

    901

    The sky opened its eye, and the lake Zhang Xue opened. One day, he happened to be in the courtyard, and the sky was clear with no clouds. Suddenly, he heard a sharp, sudden sound, and the sky opened a seam, which was wide in the middle and narrow at both ends, resembling a boat. The eye of the sky flashed brightly, round like a cart axle, illuminating the entire courtyard for a long time before closing. Those who understood it believed this was the "sky opening its eye."

    The clay images moved on their own, and the Zhang family of Pinghu lived in the reed enclosure. Their first ancestor, named Di, with the courtesy name Jing'an, was a person of the Ming Dynasty's Hongwu period. At his death, his family made clay images of Jing'an and his wife, about seven or eight inches tall, and placed them in the family temple. The house belonged to the eldest branch. After more than four hundred years, the eldest branch's descendants became poor, and the house collapsed, leaving only a few rooms, but the images remained.

    The Zhang family had an ancestral hall, three li away from Jing'an's former residence. One day at dawn, a boatman saw two elderly men come to hire a boat, so he took them on board. They asked, "Where are you going?" The elderly men replied, "We are going to the Zhang family temple." After they disembarked, they walked quickly like flying, and the boatman watched them, seeing their figures gradually shrink. Soon, they arrived at the temple, and the monk guarding the temple heard the knocking sound, got up to look, and saw nothing, but only the two clay images under the door hinge. The descendant Zhang Zhoujiu was just renovating the temple, so he added color and painting, set up a separate cabinet, and placed the images in the temple.

    902

    ○焚尸二则平湖南门外某乡掘出三穴，二穴已空，中一穴棺木依然，砖书“赵处士之墓”。

    　　尸年四十许，貌如生，穿云履，蟹青纟由袍，纟由如一钱厚，不坏。掘者马某覆出其尸而焚之，火不能旺，乃投诸水。是夜，鬼大哭，一村皆惊。好事者为扛起残尸，血缕缕如注，乃仍纳棺中，加土葬之，是夕遂安。马姓至今无恙，为典史皂役。

    　　平湖小西溪之西蒋姓，田家也，冬至前一日，日方西，烧父尸。方开棺，尸走出；追之，蒋击以锄，尸倒地，乃焚之。晚归，闻其父骂曰：“汝烧我甚苦，何不孝至此！”其人头肿如匏，及午而死。张熙河所目击也。

    Explanation

    902

    Two Cases of Burning Corpses

    In a certain village outside the south gate of Pinghu, three pits were dug. Two of the pits were empty, while the middle one still contained a coffin. The brick was inscribed with "The Tomb of Zhao the Scholar."

    The body was about forty years old, and its appearance was as if it were alive. It wore cloud-patterned shoes and a crab-blue silk robe, the silk as thin as one coin, and it was not decayed. The digger, Ma, uncovered the body and burned it. The fire did not burn strongly, so it was thrown into the water. That night, the ghost cried loudly, and the entire village was shocked. A kind-hearted person lifted the charred remains, and blood dripped down like a stream. It was then placed back into the coffin and buried. That night, everything returned to peace. The surname Ma has remained unharmed to this day, and he serves as a clerk and a minor official.

    In the village of Jiang, west of the small West Creek in Pinghu, there was a farmer's family. On the day before Winter Solstice, when the sun was setting, they burned their father's body. As they opened the coffin, the body emerged. They chased it, and Jiang struck it with a hoe. The body fell to the ground, and then they burned it. When they returned home at night, they heard their father's voice cursing, saying, "You burned me terribly, how unfilial can you be?" The person's head swelled like a gourd, and he died at noon. This was witnessed by Zhang Xihé.

    903

    ○美人鱼人面猪崇明打起美人鱼，貌一女子也，身与海船同大。舵工问云：“失路耶？”点其头。乃放之，洋洋而去。

    　　云栖放生处有人面猪，平湖张九丹先生见之。猪羞与人见，以头低下，拉之才见。

    　　○花魄婺源士人谢某，读书张公山。早起，闻树林鸟声啁啾，有似鹦哥。因近视之，乃一美女，长五寸许，赤身无毛，通体洁白如玉，眉目间有愁苦之状。遂携以归，女无惧色。乃畜笼中，以饭喂之。向人絮语，了不可辨。畜数日，为太阳所照，竟成枯腊而死。洪孝廉字麟闻之曰：“此名花魄，”凡树经三次人缢死者，其冤苦之气结成此物，沃以水，犹可活也。“试之果然。里人聚观者，如云而至。谢恐招摇，乃仍送之树上。须臾间，一大怪鸟衔之飞去。

    Explanation

    903

    A mermaid was set free in Chongming. She appeared as a beautiful woman, her body as large as a sea vessel. The helmsman asked, "Lost your way?" She nodded. He then released her, and she swam away gracefully.

    At the cloud-abode release place, there was a human-faced pig. Mr. Zhang Jiudan of Pinghu saw it. The pig was ashamed to be seen by people, lowering its head, and could only be seen when pulled.

    A scholar from Wuyuan named Xie, while studying at Zhang Gong Mountain, one morning heard the chirping of birds in the forest, which sounded like parrots. He looked closely and saw a beautiful woman, about five inches tall, naked and hairless, with a body as white as jade, and a look of sorrow in her eyes and brows. He took her home, and she showed no fear. He kept her in a cage and fed her rice. She chattered to people, but her words were indistinguishable. After keeping her for several days, she was exposed to the sun and turned into a dried-up corpse and died. Mr. Hong, a scholar of letters, heard of this and said, "This is called a flower spirit." Any tree that has witnessed three suicides can give rise to such a spirit. Watering it with water can still bring it back to life. He tried it, and it worked. The locals gathered to watch, like clouds. Xie was afraid of causing a stir, so he returned her to the tree. In a moment, a large strange bird picked her up and flew away.
